anchor_text: A famous opera singer, Giorgio Fini, loses his voice during a tour of the United States. So he goes to see a specialist, Pamela Taylor, who he falls in love with...
text_a: Mary Barrett is a young woman who wants to become an opera singer and therefore begins to receive lessons from the maestro Giulio Monteverdi. Little by little, and with opera music in the background, the two of you will fall in love.
text_b: Giorgio Fini is an opera singer who loses his voice while in the United States and is treated by Pamela Taylor, a vocal cord specialist with whom he soon falls in love.

anchor_text: Anna Heymes, in her 30's, is the wife of one of the highest officials in the Ministry of the Interior. For more than a month, she has suffered terrifying hallucinations and frequent bouts of amnesia, to the point of not recognizing her own husband's face and even beginning to doubt his honesty. Meanwhile, in the 10th arrondissement of Paris, Paul Nerteaux, a hardened police captain, is entrusted with an investigation into the deaths of three women of Turkish origin, who worked in clandestine workshops and whose bodies were found horribly mutilated. To help him infiltrate the Turkish population of the neighborhood, Nerteaux has no choice but to call on Jean-Louis Schiffer, one of his former colleagues known for his reputation as a ruthless and corrupt cop.
text_a: Anna Heymes is the wife of a senior official in the Ministry of the Interior. Her life goes on normally, but for the past month she has been suffering from terrifying hallucinations and amnesia. Her illness causes her to forget her husband's face and even to question his honesty. Meanwhile, on the other side of town, a police captain, Paul Nertheaux, is tasked with investigating a triple murder of Turkish women who worked in an underground workshop and whose bodies have been mutilated. Paul has no choice but to infiltrate the Turkish community and for this, he turns to Jean-Louis-Schiffer, a former colleague, a corrupt and violent ex-policeman.
text_b: Babette, a young French waitress, is forced to flee Paris following the rapid invasion of German troops. After a series of encounters and adventures, she arrives in London and goes to the French command to look for Lieutenant Gérard de Crécy, who had helped her during her escape. Gérard hired her as a cleaning lady at the Free French Headquarters. Major Fitzpatrick, a British officer, notices her resemblance to Hilda, the former mistress of German General von Arenberg, so he decides to use Babette and Gérard to capture the general and delay the enemy's plans. After an intensive course, the girl is parachuted to France together with Gérard and manages to trick von Arenberg, allowing him to be captured. Back in London, the two young men attend a party in honor of the authors of the venture, but the honors go to the one who came up with the plan, while the couple who risked their lives are ignored. Babette and Gérard, however, do not pay much attention to it: now in love, they take advantage of a moment of freedom and run away together.

anchor_text: Anna Heymes, a typical 31-year-old Parisian housewife, suffers from nightmares and hallucinations related to a series of grisly murders in the city. At the same time, a pair of cops, the unorthodox Schiffer and the more scrupulous Nerteaux, work to track down the serial killer. Anna's life changes dramatically when she discovers that she is actually Turkish, and somehow connected to the frightening series of murders and the Turkish mafia.
text_a: Anna Heymes is the wife of a senior official in the Ministry of the Interior. text_b: Jeanne, the daughter of French diplomat and political correspondent Alfred Ney, resides with her family in Crimea in 1917 Russia. Her father comes across a list of Bolshevik agents with the name of Jeanne's lover, Andreas Labov. The latter ends up executing Jeanne's father, who, terrified, still tries to save Andreas' life. But the Red Army arrives and soon surrounds the city...

anchor_text: In 1880, there was a fight between ranchers and farmers in Clayton. Land theft is the order of the day, murders like Coleman's of the boy's father, Tony Murphy, are the order of the day. He's now being raised by an aunt and admires circus performer Gary McGuire. Due to a childhood trauma (he was blamed by his father for the accidental death of his brother), although he does not face a duel, he is still considered a great hero who shot five bandits during a bank robbery. When the real perpetrator - the leader of the murdered and responsible for the bank robbery, Owl Roy - is back in the area, he makes Gary the laughing stock of the people. Nevertheless, Roy makes Gary his co-conspirator for his father's property; Coleman soon learns that Roy knows as well and then has Roy kidnapped and Gary captured. The sight of the tormented boy makes Gary overcome his trauma. He escapes in the middle of the night and attacks the Coleman Ranch on his own. His extraordinary abilities and the support of friends from the circus soon make him achieve significant advantages. He can kill Coleman and bring Owl Grey to justice.
text_a: Former gunfighter Dan Murphy has been happily married for some time, quietly and contentedly. One day, however, he is recognized by a sheriff; while trying to stop him, Dan's son dies. In desperation, Dan kidnaps the sheriff's son and flees with his wife. Once again, he's trying to build an honorable life. Dan's dream, however, is interrupted by two events: the enterprises of the power-hungry Carter brothers and the appearance of a man paid by the sheriff in search of his son. This forces Dan to take up arms again, first to eliminate the Carter brothers and their helpers. He must return the kidnapped child to the sheriff, who, now disillusioned and stubbornly staying in the area, visits him at his ranch and kills him in a duel.
text_b: In the 1880s, ranchers and farmers are fighting each other in the small town of Clayton. Robbery and theft are common, and murder is also common. Little Tony Murphy, orphaned by his father Jonathan, killed by outlaw Coleman, is raised by an aunt, and grows up admiring the skilled circus art gunman Gary McGuire. The latter, due to a childhood trauma (he was blamed by his father for the accidental death of his brother) is unable to face a duel, although he is considered a hero by fellow citizens, having eliminated five bandits who were committing a bank robbery years earlier. When the real culprit - Owl Roy, the originator and organizer of the bank robbery - returns to the area, Gary becomes the laughingstock. However, Roy reveals to Gary the truth about the events of the past: Tony's father guarded the entire loot and now the boy is the only one who knows where the stash is hidden. Soon Tony is kidnapped and held captive at Coleman's ranch, with Roy's connivance. The sight of the boy being tortured in order to know the hiding place, causes Gary to overcome his trauma. After unsuccessfully attempting, on the advice of the sheriff (who was injured in a shootout), to organize a posse, he attacks the Coleman Ranch alone. His extraordinary skills as a gunfighter and the unexpected support of circus friends will give him a significant advantage over his opponents. He'll save Tony, kill Coleman and bring Owl Roy to justice.

text_b: In 1880 ranchers and farmers clashed in the small town of Clayton. Robbery and murder are the order of the day. Tony Murphy, orphaned by his father Jonathan, killed by the outlaw Coleman, is taken in by his aunt, and grows up in the admiration of Gary McGuire, a circus gunman. The latter is marked by a childhood trauma: he was accused by his father of the accidental death of his brother. As a result, he is unable to face an opponent in a duel. Yet the townspeople revere him, because he eliminated five bandits who had robbed the bank. Owl Roy, the organizer of the bank robbery, comes to the area, and mocks Gary. Roy also suggests to Gary that it was Tony's father who had the loot from the robbery, and that only Tony knows where it is. Tony is kidnapped and held hostage at Coleman's ranch, with Roy's complicity. Seeing the torture inflicted on the boy, Gary manages to free himself from his trauma. Having tried in vain to organize a posse, on the advice of the recently wounded sheriff in the exchange of gunfire, he finds himself alone to attack Coleman's ranch. His skills with the revolver, and the unexpected support of his circus colleagues allow him to save Tony, kill Coleman, and bring Owl Roy to justice.

anchor_text: The young Roland, tired of the work routine, meets by chance his former schoolmate Lola, with whom he was unrequited in love. Lola is a dancer in a cabaret, and leads a licentious existence. She also has a son, born years earlier to a lover, Michel, who mysteriously disappeared and left the woman alone. Roland's old feelings for Lola are rekindled, but she is having an affair with Frankie, an American sailor, to whom she has no feelings at all. Roland proposes to Lola to establish a relationship, but the woman, on her way to Marseille, where a two-month work commitment awaits her, asks him to wait for her return before committing. However, before leaving, Michel suddenly returns, and Lola, who has never forgotten him, decides to return with him. Lola's youthful affair - her decision to run away with Michel when she was still a teenager - is meanwhile resumed by Cécile, a high school student who falls in love with Frankie, and decides to run away from home to follow him to Cherbourg.
text_a: Lola is a 16-year-old girl who lives in Paris with her divorced mother, Anne, and her two younger brothers. After a long summer, she can't wait to get back to school to see her friends and her fiancé, Arthur. This happiness at seeing her friends again, and especially Arthur, soon turns into great sadness as Arthur confesses to her that during the summer he betrayed her, ending their relationship. Supporting and comforting Lola are her best friends Charlotte and Stephane and her best friend Maël. Lola and Maël begin to see each other very often and over time a relationship blossoms that becomes much more than just a friendship. At the same time, Lola's mother meets a man named Lucas, an inspector in the anti-drug unit of the Paris police. After a while, the first jealousy and quarrels arise between the two boys. Lola, thinking she heard Maël making love in the bathroom with another girl, leaves him. Actually, it wasn't Maël in that bathroom, but his friend Charlotte with a boy from school named Paul-Henri. Lola and Maël do not speak to each other until, during a school trip to London, Charlotte reveals the truth to her. Upon learning the facts, Lola makes peace with Maël. Meanwhile in France, Lola's mother, finding her daughter's secret diary, does not resist the temptation to open it and read it, only to repent and confess everything to her daughter when she returns from the trip. Furious, Lola decides to go to live with her father, but after a few days, despite the anger she felt towards her mother, whom she misses in the meantime, she decides to return home. The two reconcile, Lola and Maël live their love story serenely and the mother continues her relationship with Lucas, happy to give herself another chance for a happy life.
text_b: Lola takes place in the Atlantic coastal city of Nantes, France. A young man, Roland Cassard, wastes his life until he has a chance encounter with Lola, a woman he met as a teenager before World War II, who is now a cabaret dancer. Although Roland is quite in love with her, Lola is worried about her former lover Michel, who abandoned her after impregnating her seven years earlier. Also vying for Lola's heart is American sailor Frankie, whose affection Lola does not return. Struggling to find work, Roland becomes involved in a diamond smuggling plot with a local barber. Cécile, a 13-year-old girl, crosses paths with Roland; in many ways she reminds him of Lola, whose real name is also Cécile. In the end, Michel returns to Nantes, apparently very successful and hoping to marry Lola, just as she leaves for Marseille to look for another job. In the end, he leaves with Michel, like he always said he would.

anchor_text: The film features several characters: Hannah and Paul, a couple about to renew their wedding vows; Meredith who agrees to go on a date with Trent; Mildred at the bedside of her son Mark dying of AIDS; Keenan and Joan who meet in a night club; Roger and Gracie living their love in secret and finally Hugh telling his romantic tribulations in bars. For eight days in Los Angeles, the fates of these characters will evolve, revealing several connections between their respective lives.
text_a: A group of people who belong to different generations and have very different lifestyles, but they all have one thing in common: they are looking for love and friendship in today's Los Angeles. Among them are Hannah and Paul, a couple whose coexistence will be threatened. Meredith, a vocational bachelor devoted to her work. Hugh, a loner, who goes from bar to bar telling sad love stories. Gracie and Roger, a couple who have found sexual fun in a clandestine relationship, or Joan and Keenan, two young people who spend their time daydreaming in Los Angeles nightclubs. All of these people will experience a transformation because of a chain of unforeseen events that will bring to light just how fleeting, complex, and often miraculous love can be depending on the circumstances.
text_b: In Houston, Aurora lives with her faithful housekeeper, Rosie, and has been caring for her motherless grandchildren for years. The oldest boy is in prison on his third drug charge, the second-oldest lives with a girl and has a young son, and the third-oldest, Melanie, is about to leave her grandmother's house to move in with her boyfriend, an aspiring actor. Rosie notices that Aurora is slipping into a state of depression and advises her to see a therapist. Aurora starts some sessions with Jerry like this.

text_b: Meredith, theater director, recently divorced from a failed marriage, struggles to let herself be loved by the nice Trent. Joan, an eccentric and jaded girl, falls in love with Keenan, a strange boy who seems to snub her. He's actually got a serious problem. Paul is dying of cancer, on the eve of his 40th wedding anniversary with his beloved wife Hannah, but the specter of an ex-girlfriend arrives to put a wall between them. Gracie and Hugh's love seems to have ended after years of marriage, but an extramarital affair with a priest convinces Gracie that Hugh is still the man she loves. At the wedding anniversary party between Paul and Hannah, the link between the events of the protagonists is discovered: Meredith, Joan and Gracie are in fact sisters and daughters of the couple.

anchor_text: In Jersey City, New Jersey, an African-American contract killer lives by the precepts of Hagakure, the code of honor of the samurai of medieval Japan. He lives alone, very simply, feeding his pigeons, calling himself Ghost Dog, literally in French ghost dog or invisible dog. His savior following an incident eight years earlier, whom he considers his Master, is part of the local Italian mafia. When the daughter of the Godfather becomes a witness to one of the contracts of Ghost Dog, it seems very embarrassing for the mobsters who decide to get rid of it as soon as possible.
text_a: In Brooklyn, New York, a group of Italian immigrants live modestly in a building that also houses their violent and ruthless landlord, a lawyer named Bossi. When someone knocks on his door to ask for a favor, Bossi chases the intrusive visitor by imitating the barking of an angry dog. An old woman once cursed him to bark all his life until he found someone who loved him. The curse is fulfilled and Bossi becomes a hungry, ugly stray dog. Then begins for him a life of sadness, like his former victims. Meanwhile, the residents of the building, surprised by Bossi's disappearance, breathe easier without the pressure to pay, with the complicity of Bossi's secretary. One day he meets a young man named Filippo who also feels lonely. They become friends. One afternoon, Filippo was attacked by a gang of older boys, and the dog saved him. Filippo, grateful, gives her a warm embrace. The term of the curse has been fulfilled and Bossi becomes human again, but his behavior towards other people will change.
text_b: Ghost Dog is the nickname of a hitman, who lives alone in a dirty New Jersey terrace, following the precepts of Bushido, the samurai code of conduct. He lives simply; the only pleasure he gives himself is raising his pigeons, serving his master Louie, an Italian-American mobster who had saved his life eight years earlier. But when, in the course of fulfilling one of his "contracts", he comes face-to-face with the godfather's daughter, Louie's boss, things get complicated.

anchor_text: Phil Broker (Jason Statham), a former DEA agent, moves with his daughter to Louisiana after the death of his wife. He finds himself confronted by local drug lord Gator Bodine (James Franco).
text_a: The story is about a former drug dealer who wants to rebuild his life by moving to Houston. Once there, a former drug-dealing associate visits him and leaves him a briefcase full of heroin. From that moment on, he must return to his former life by escaping various dangers.
text_b: A widowed former DEA agent, Phil Broker (Jason Statham), retreats to a small town for the sake of his 10-year-old daughter. The only problem is, you picked the wrong town.

anchor_text: It's Raquel's birthday, 41 years old, and she's been a faithful servant for 23 years to the Valdes family, a middle-class family in Santiago, Chile. She has too much work and she's sick, but she can't stand the possibility of sharing her work with any other maid. One day, an accident will prove to be a major turning point...
text_a: Raquel, the bitter and introverted nanny (Chilean euphemism for domestic servants) of the Valdés household, with whom she has worked for 23 years, sees her position endangered when they hire a new employee to help her during her convalescence. Rachel is dedicated to making life difficult for the new nannies. This mechanic is repeated over and over again until the appearance of Lucy, a provincial woman who will manage to penetrate Rachel's heart.
text_b: It tells the story of Raquel, a young woman who is attracted to her neighbor, Ares Hidalgo, an "enigmatic" boy whom she stalks from the shadows, but Ares does not know of his existence. However, she proposes to stop being like this: she is willing to fall in love with him anyway, even leaving aside that good and polite Raquel that everyone knows to impress him.[3]

anchor_text: Joanna Mills is a rural American girl determined to uncover the truth behind the supernatural and terrifying visions that haunt her. Joanna has a good job as a sales representative for a transportation company. Her personal life, on the other hand, is in trouble: she has argued with her father, she is haunted and threatened by her ex-boyfriend, she has few friends, and she believes that her whole life is getting out of hand. The girl sees in vision the brutal murder of a young woman she has never met, at the hands of a ruthless killer, a man who seems to want to make Joanna his next target. Determined to fight back, Joanna is driven by her nightmares to the murdered woman's hometown of La Salle, Texas. Once there, he's accompanied by a particular character, Terry, who seems to be linked to the woman in the visions. Rejected by Terry, Joanna discovers the killer's identity shortly before leaving town.
text_a: In less than 24 hours, two young women are brutally murdered in a luxury apartment building. Soon after, the owner, Andrea, invited two models, Jennifer and Marilyn, to move into the building, hoping that in return they would pose for an advertising campaign. But Jennifer is soon stalked by the serial killer. She discovers that the previous owner of her apartment has committed suicide. With all the suspicion focused on Andrea, Jennifer tries to uncover the killer before she becomes the next victim. The suspects are many: her former husband, a member of a satanic cult; a lesbian neighbor; the deformed son of an elderly widow; and the architect and owner of the building, who hates the sight of blood.
text_b: Joanna Mills (Sarah Michelle Gellar) is a young Midwesterner determined to uncover the hidden truth behind increasingly terrifying supernatural visions that have plagued her. Joanna is a highly successful professional who works as a sales representative for a trucking company. But her private life is complicated, she's estranged from her father, has few friends, and lives harassed by an ex-boyfriend who is obsessed with her. He's afraid he's losing control of his life. Joanna sees and feels the brutal murder of a woman she doesn't know. The ruthless killer seems to have taken her as his next victim. Determined to defend herself, Joanna is guided by her nightmares to the birthplace of the woman killed by the subject, La Salle, in Texas. There she is drawn to a stranger, Terry (Peter O'Brien), and discovers that there are secrets that cannot be hidden, that there are spirits that never die, and that the murder she is trying to solve may be her own.

anchor_text: The villagers have always shunned the northern forests. It is said to be haunted by monsters, ghosts, monsters and witches. Hansel and Gretel live with their father and mother in a little house on the edge of this dark forest. They barely have enough to eat, so they live a meager life. The mother sends Hansel and Gretel to fetch berries when the father is not at home. They're running into the forest, which is getting denser and denser. Soon the sunlight disappears behind the leaves. Hansel scatters breadcrumbs along the way so they can find their way home. The bread crumbs will soon be eaten by a hungry bird. They spend the night in the forest, where they hear eerie laughter from behind the trees. Even the mother, who has already noticed that her children are not coming home, tells the father. Desperate, they run into the forest to find their children. They call for them, but the dense forest barely lets a sound through. The next morning Hansel and Gretel wake up. You go on, and soon you come to a house made entirely of sugar and sweets. They're happy to go after this Zuckerberg guy. Suddenly, an old woman comes out of the house. It's the good grandmother Griselda who lives in this little house. She invites the children in and gives them plenty to eat. Then you both get a room for the night. In the night, Gretel wakes up, hears voices from the basement, and goes downstairs. Old Griselda was just being friendly. She's an evil witch who feeds on little children. She locks Hansel in a cage and Gretel has to cook him food every day. When Hansel grows fat, the witch ties him to a hook and wants to throw him into the oven. But Gretel soon finds the witch's wand and spells the witch into the furnace. Hansel and Gretel are free again.
text_a: A woodcutter lived with his wife and three daughters on the edge of a forest. He successively asks each of his daughters to bring him his lunch in the forest, to his workplace, and marks his way with seeds for this purpose. But the birds eat the seeds and the girls wander off one by one. Each of the three girls arrived at a house in the forest, where an old man lived with his animals: a rooster, a hen and a cow. She asks for hospitality and the old man consults his animals, who simply answer: Duks, which he interprets as an agreement on their part. At the old man's request, the girl prepares dinner, but the first two do not think about the animals. When they go up to bed, the old man drops them through a hatch in the cellar. The third girl has a good heart: before eating, she takes care of feeding the animals. She goes upstairs to bed and the old man falls asleep next to her. During the night, she hears a loud noise, but in the end nothing bad happens to her. When she awakens, she finds herself in a magnificent palace; the old man, who was a bewitched prince (the animals were his servants), has returned to his former form thanks to the good deed of the girl. He marries her and invites her parents to the wedding. As for the two bad sisters, they're sentenced to work in a coal mine until they get better.
text_b: In a house near a forest live two brothers, a boy and a girl, named Hansel and Gretel. Their parents are Stephane and Maria, who complains that they are poor. One day Stephane takes the two children to the village to settle accounts with the baker, who gives Gretel a bag containing cake crumbs. The next day Hansel and Gretel are sent by their mother to pick berries in the forest. The two children, however, notice that the berries are all gone on the path where they used to pick them. At that point the two go deep into the infamous northern forest, unbeknownst to them inhabited by demons and where many children had already disappeared. To trace their path and not get lost, Hansel and Gretel trace their path with the cake crumbs the baker had given them, but the birds eat them and Hansel and Gretel get lost in the cursed forest, and they arrive at a wonderful house made of pastries and marzipan. The two discover that Griselda lives in the house, a very friendly old woman who hosts them in her home. The two stop to sleep, but that night Gretel catches Griselda performing a satanic ritual and discovers that she is a witch. He goes to warn Hansel, but it's too late. The witch locks Hansel in a cage, intending to fatten and eat him, and enslaves Gretel. A few days later Gretel discovers that the marzipan babies outside the house were Griselda's previous victims who had been cooked and turned into marzipan. Gretel manages to steal Griselda's magic scepter by turning her into marzipan instead of Hansel. Then the house melts and all the witch's victims become human again. Hansel and Gretel are reunited with their parents who had come looking for them desperately. Griselda's magic scepter is turned into money, and Hansel and Gretel get rich.

anchor_text: A love story between two characters, the story of a commoner woman who succeeds thanks to her affairs with men of power to rise in French society in the latter part of the 18th century. Madame du Barry notably managed to integrate the court of Louis XV and become his favorite. Disillusioned love, present throughout the film, is the driving force behind the revolutionary action.
text_a: In a pornographic retelling of George Bernard Shaw's play Pygmalion (and its derivative My Fair Lady), the film tells the story of a sexologist (Dr. Seymour Love) who tries to turn a low-level prostitute (Dolores "Misty" Beethoven) into a sophisticated sex goddess. While the doctor instructs her to successfully seduce a homosexual artist, the girl falls in love with him instead. As the story progresses, Misty "rises" higher than Love ever hoped, but eventually abandons him. Misty later returns and resumes her relationship with Dr. Love, but this time with the roles reversed, as she is now the dominant one in the relationship.
text_b: Deciding between the love of the poor but honest Armand and that of the seducer, the rich Don Diego, Jeannette decides by counting the petals of a flower, the ribbons of her corset. Her career, which will lead her first to the splendours of Versailles, mistress of King Louis XV, and then to the guillotine, begins thus.

anchor_text: Truck driver Peter Karner narrowly escapes an attempted murder. He finds out that he's working on behalf of the ruthless drug dealer Manuzzo, transporting cocaine instead of washing powder. There are dramatic scenes on the highway where Manuzzo is shot. Karner can escape and hide in Zur Kobra's rest home with his girlfriend Alexa. While Commissioner Langhammer and Inspector Knecht are officially investigating the case, Karner decides to uncover the smuggling ring and its ringleader, a Mr. Green, on his own. All the tracks seem to lead to the Paradise Bar, where numerous shady characters and suspects hang out. Karner's life is in danger and Alexa is kidnapped by the criminals. At the last moment, the criminals can be overwhelmed and a certain Mr. Weird can be exposed as the mastermind behind Mr. Green. Fleeing through a car graveyard, he eventually dies in a junkyard.
text_a: Truck driver Peter Karner narrowly escapes an assassination attempt on his life. He discovers that he's transporting cocaine instead of laundry on behalf of the unscrupulous drug dealer Manuzzo. On the highway, there are dramatic scenes, during one of which Manuzzo is shot. Karner escapes and hides in Zur Kobra restaurant with his girlfriend Alexa. While Inspector Langhammer and Inspector Knecht are officially investigating the case, Karner decides to expose the smuggling ring and their backer, a Mr. Green, on his own. All the tracks seem to lead to the Paradise Bar, which is home to many characters and suspects. Karner is in mortal danger and Alexa is kidnapped by the criminals. At the last moment, the criminals can be overwhelmed and a certain Mr. Wunderlich is unmasked as Mr. Green. In his escape in a car wreck, he kills himself by falling into a press.
text_b: The characters in this film have no names and are indicated only by the role they play. Driver is a skilled car driver who is used by the criminal underworld to evade police pursuit after a robbery. Behind him is the Detective, who has never been able to frame his opponent. Determined to get him at any cost, the Detective blackmails some prejudiced people into committing a robbery, hoping to catch Driver with his hands in the bag. To avoid being deceived by the bandits, Driver uses a girl known by chance (the Player), who had shown him loyalty by refusing to recognize him during a confrontation. The two engage in a fight against the treacherous bandits, while the Detective is on the train to retrieve the loot. The protagonist goes to the station to retrieve his fee deposited in a safety deposit box, but the money is not there and the Player is on the run again. The police are once again unable to get their hands on him because, in spite of himself, he is once again found with clean hands.

anchor_text: For happily married professional marriage counselor Ava, a world collapses when she learns that her mother Betty wants a divorce after 30 years of marriage. Ava doesn't want to accept this, which is why she's doing everything she can to save her parents' marriage. However, this puts their own marriage to the test.
text_a: After years of marriage, Pete is the only man in the household, surrounded by his wife, Debbie, and their daughters, Charlotte and Sadie, aged 8 and 13. As his indie label flies, he and Debbie must find ways to let go and enjoy the rest of their lives... before they kill each other.
text_b: A marriage counselor, who happens to be newly married and whose job is to care about the marital happiness of other couples, finds her reality turned upside down when she discovers that her parents are getting a divorce.

anchor_text: Bond Ravi is a karate champion and a generous man who works at a medical center in Singapore. He meets Meera on the bus and falls in love with her. She's here to make a documentary about the village where Bond Ravi lives. As Bond Ravi leaves the village, he meets Major Chandrakanth and gets caught in a shootout.
text_a: Bond Ravi (Puneeth Rajkumar) is a generous man who has a tendency to help others. He's a karate champion who works at a medical camp in Singapore. He meets Meera (Priyamani) on a bus and ends up enchanted with her. One day, Meera visits the same village where Bond Ravi lives to make a documentary film. There will begin a story of danger and action, framed in the romance of Ravi and Meera.
text_b: Major Ram Prasad Sharma's father is killed by a group of rebels who are against the government, after his funeral, a general gives Ram a mission, which is to go incognito to the university where his daughter is to protect her from the rebels. Ram agrees because he must also look for his brother. At first Ram and Lucky don't get along but when Ram finds out that Lucky is his brother, a friendship starts between them. Ram falls in love with one of the teachers.

anchor_text: This is Detroit, 2018. Damien is a cop. His mission is to infiltrate the Brick Mansions ghetto to recover a weapon of mass destruction stolen by gangsters. He is guided by Lino, a local who knows the area like the back of his hand.
text_a: Alcatraz just reopened its doors. It is now a high-security prison with the latest surveillance technology. While FBI agent Sascha is locked away on a secret mission, a commando team storms the notorious prison to free a death row inmate. Sasha is going to have to deal with this gang of extremely dangerous criminals.
text_b: In 2018 in the city of Detroit an entire crime-ridden neighborhood is isolated by a fence erected by the police and controlled by soldiers. In the restricted area, which is being renamed "Brick Mansions", the crime intensifies further until a bomb is detonated. The police are sending one of their own undercover to defuse the device.

anchor_text: 922: Ibn Fadlān travels north as a representative of the Caliph of Baghdad to contact the king of Saqaliba. But before he arrives, he encounters the mighty warrior Buliwyf in a Viking village and witnesses the traditional burial of a deceased leader. One day, a young messenger from King Rothgar comes to report that the land is being overrun by monsters. These monsters come with the fog and eat people. A fortune teller announces that only thirteen warriors will be able to defeat the evil that comes with the fog. But the thirteenth warrior must not be a Viking. The warriors are chosen after the 12 moons, among them Buliwyf. Ibn Fadlān is reluctantly appointed the 13th warrior and travels with the twelve warriors to Scandinavia until they arrive in the kingdom of Rothgar. Soon, the thirteen warriors are faced with an uncanny superiority.
text_a: The story takes place in Mesopotamia in the seventh century A.D., just before the rise of Islam. The simple young woman Zabibah repeatedly flees from her violent husband to the nearby palace of the old, melancholic King Arab, who was her guest on a ride, and holds philosophical discussions with him about the state and society. They fall in love. When Zabibah's husband finds out about the meetings, he rapes her and then flees abroad to escape arrest. Instigated by him and other internal enemies of Arabia, the foreign powers go to war against the kingdom, but are repulsed with the support of the entire people. Both Arab and Zabibah and her husband are killed in the battle. This is followed by a popular assembly to celebrate Zabibah as a martyr and to pay tribute to the king's achievements. Nevertheless, it is decided to abolish monarchy and feudalism and establish a people's government.
text_b: Nine hundred and twenty-one. Ahmed Ibn Fadlan, cultured, refined, accustomed to the refinement of the Baghdad court, finds himself the caliph's ambassador. This is actually an excuse to send him into exile for seducing the wife of a relative of the ruler. His caravan, consisting of a long line of men and camel riders, stopped on the banks of the Volga River, where a group of Vikings camped. Considered a guest, Ahmed observes and learns the customs of this pagan civilization that seems so different from his own. This is when a messenger arrives announcing that far to the north, Lord Rothgar needs help to repel his enemies. A seer declares that thirteen men must unite for this journey, twelve from the same group... and a stranger. Ahmed reluctantly agrees to join these proud warriors, led by their leader Buliwyf, and finds himself embarked on a perilous quest to wild lands surrounded by icy seas. The thirteen warriors eventually reach the kingdom of Rothgar, at war with mysterious monsters that emerged from the fog and are called dead eaters...

anchor_text: Monty Wildhorn is a writer who became an alcoholic and depressed after the death of his wife. His nephew, worried, sends him to a quiet place so he can recover. He moved to Belle Isle, where he met a divorced mother and her three daughters. Little by little, Monty's inspiration and zest for life returns.
text_a: Gunman Jack Wade, nearly blind, returns to his hometown, which he left after committing murder. His son thinks he's responsible for his mother's death, but he'll have to change his mind. Wade faces one final duel, and he'll be backed by his son.
text_b: Monte Wildhorn is a famous Western novelist, but the death of his wife, six years earlier, blocked his inspiration and drove him to drink. For the summer he buys a lake house in picturesque Belle Isle and befriends the nearby O'Neill family, consisting of an attractive single mother and her daughters. Wildhorn especially bonds with his middle daughter, Finn, who will help him rediscover inspiration in writing.

anchor_text: A new school year begins at the elite Gurukul school. The principal of this institution is Narayan Shankar, who vigorously enforces his three principles: tradition, honor and discipline. The three new students Vicky, Sameer and Karan now come to this school, and all three fall in love. Vicky in Ishika, a student at the nearby girls' school. Sameer in his childhood sweetheart Sanjana, who lives nearby with her father, and Karan in Kiran, who is married to a missing military pilot. All three know that according to the current rules of Gurukul, they can hardly hope for a fulfillment of their love, as once the principal threw a student out of the school who had fallen in love with Shankar's daughter Megha. She then committed suicide. But for the new school year, music teacher Raj Aryan is also coming to Gurukul to bring the students the power of music, the power of love and faith in their own abilities. Raj and Shankar repeatedly clash. When the principal wants to fire Raj because of his beliefs, he reveals himself as the former student who was in love with Shankar's daughter. Raj is blaming Shankar's principles for the death of Megha. Eventually, the director realizes that he is wrong. In the end, Shankar resigns from the post of director and hands over the management of the elite school to music teacher Raj Aryan and asks him to take on the responsible task. Vicky, Sameer and Karan all find their great love and Megha finally has peace.
text_a: Mohabbatein is the story of a violin teacher (Shahrukh Khan) who through his passion wants to convey a more universal message, that of love. A music teacher, he tries to spread this sentiment among the students of Gurukul University, but he soon clashes with the headmaster of the institution (Amitabh Bachchan) whose values inspire terror and submission in all the students. The director of the institute is the father of the late Megha (Aishwarya Rai Bachchan) who committed suicide after being forbidden by her father to pursue her relationship with a young student. But who is this student really? What are the reasons for him to represent himself at the university so many years after the disappearance of his beloved? Will he seek revenge? Mohabattein is a love hymn that celebrates the pure relationship of two soul mates whose power, beyond death, manages to overcome all obstacles.
text_b: Raj (Salman Khan) moves from Goa to Mumbai. He has set two goals for himself: to become a star singer and to find a wife for life. In Mumbai, he lives with his boyfriend and tries in vain to start a singing career. After a series of setbacks, he decides to return to Goa, but then an incident happens that stops him: he rescues a woman in a wedding dress (Rani Mukerji) from a car accident and accompanies her to the hospital. When her relatives show up, there's a mix-up. The bride is Pooja Oberoi the daughter of an influential music producer who wanted to marry a poor man named Romi against her father's wishes. Raj is mistaken for Romi. With Pooja now in a coma and her fiancé seemingly no longer interested in her, Raj is asked by a friend of the house to pretend to be Romi until Pooja awakens from the coma. Raj takes advantage of the situation and likes to play the future son-in-law of the big producer. This allows him a singing career, and his second goal also seems to be achieved he falls in love with Pooja's best friend Jahnvi (Preity Zinta). Jahnvi loves him too, and they're just waiting for Pooja to come to her senses to explain everything to her family. But then the unexpected happens. Pooja has watched the whole story in a coma, and knows that Raj has done it for the sake of her heart-sick father, for the most part. Now she's in love too and wants to marry Raj. Jahnvi does not want to destroy Pooja's dream and renounces Raj, but he is ready to give up everything and return to Goa if he cannot be with Jahnvi.

anchor_text: Max Barber is a failed, indebted film producer who, in the new film he's producing, plans to have his leading man die in order to collect the insurance money. It's supposed to look like an accident, but to the producer's chagrin, the aging star Duke Montana turns out to be more robust than expected.
text_a: In Berlin in June 1934, Max frequents the back alleys of the Reich capital and lives with Rudy, a cabaret dancer. On 29 June, following the brutal murder of an SA he had just conquered by the SS, a tragic escape begins where Rudy is also killed and ends in Dachau. Max, wearing a yellow star, meets Horst, wearing the pink triangle. Together they will try to endure the terrible conditions of life in the concentration camp.
text_b: Max Barber is a Hollywood B-movie producer who needs a project to pay off his debt to a mob boss. He decides to start shooting a movie with action scenes, in order to cause the death of the protagonist, Duke Montana, a former western star, and thus be able to collect the insurance and definitively solve his economic problems. However, when filming begins, not everything goes according to plan.

anchor_text: Ernie Mott is a restless young man who walks through life aimlessly. He tries in vain to make a career as a musician and has an affair with the cellist Aggie Hunter. The influence of the divorced Ada Brantline gives him hope to change his life. Ernie helps his terminally ill mother to keep the small shop running. Through a chain of unfortunate circumstances, his mother ends up in prison, where she dies alone. My relationship with Ada is also falling apart. Ernie is left with no hope.
text_a: A young Londoner returns home after a long journey. Forced to stay against his will and uncertain about his future, he must find the direction to give his life: he does not know whether to choose honest work and honest marriage, or the relationship with a woman linked to a gangster and unclean earnings in the clique of her ex-husband.
text_b: A real loser avoids prison after making a deal with the Drug Enforcement Administration. He has to impersonate a major drug dealer, Bobby Z, in a prisoner swap with a Mexican drug dealer. But things will go wrong and the impostor will run off with the 12-year-old son of the real Bobby Z.

anchor_text: Stéphane Margelle is a wealthy industrialist and above all a seducer. He's taking his wife to the airport, and he happens to run into Julie. He offers to take her with him in order to seduce her. However, shortly after arriving at his apartment in the evening, his wife shows up because she could not take off due to a strike by airport staff. He pretends Julie's his daughter so he doesn't have to confess to the attempted adultery. Julie plays this role throughout the film.
text_a: Stéphane Margelle is a wealthy industrialist and above all a seducer. Convinced that his wife Sophie has flown in the day, he brings home Julie who does not know where to sleep. But a surprise strike prevents Sophie from leaving and she comes home unexpectedly in the middle of the night and surprises them. To get out of it, Stéphane pretends Julie is his daughter, whom he has forgotten to mention to her. Also, in this confusing discussion, Julie invents the fact that she is pregnant. These lies will lead to more and more and Stephen will get into more and more trouble.
text_b: Charles Desvallées has good reason to believe his wife is unfaithful. To be sure, he hires a private investigator. Learning the identity of the lover, the writer Victor Pégala, he goes home. After a brief conversation, he kills him. Discovering Helen's address in a notebook from Pegala, the police go to her home and question the unfaithful woman. Without saying anything between the two husbands, Helen guesses what happened and understands that her husband is the murderer. Helen and her husband exchange long, meaningful glances.

anchor_text: During the 1970s, San Diego was ruled by Ron Burgundy, a star anchorman, and his entire team: Brian Fantana, a reporter, Brick Tamland, a weatherman, and Field Kind, a sports reporter. But the arrival of journalist Veronica Corningstone on the Channel 4 team has upended the hierarchy. Hostilities between Ron and Veronica then flare up to become the paper's anchor.
text_a: Ron Burgundy is a famous anchorman who is part of the Channel Four San Diego team of reporters; he has won five Emmy Awards in his career. Much loved by women, mounted, spooky and serious as ever, he is fascinated by his new colleague, Veronica Corningstone, ready to do anything to blow his job.
text_b: Freshly separated from her partner, a beautician develops just above the apartment of a pre-op depressed trans woman. At first tense, the relations between the two people who are almost opposites, gradually evolve from misunderstandings to reconciliations.

anchor_text: The film is set in 1935 in Waxahachie, Texas. Edna Spalding lives on a farm with her husband Royce. Royce, the local sheriff, is called in one night about a black man rioting, and he's shot in the line of duty. From now on, Edna is on her own, caring for her children Possum and Frank, but at the same time, they are burdened with financial problems. Albert Denby, the banker, insists on the repayment of the outstanding loan and advises Edna to sell the farm. The widow refuses, and the farmer Moses, who has taken her in, advises her to grow cotton. She follows his advice, and Denby initially relents as well, on the condition that Edna take in and employ his stepbrother, Mr. Will, who was blinded in World War I. Early on, Will proves to be a skillful helper, but Edna continues to struggle with her financial difficulties. Her sister Margret Lomax, who runs a beauty salon, and her husband are also unable to support her financially. She has a young daughter to care for and is faced with the sexual escapades of her husband Wayne and her own poverty.
text_a: The film centers on two sharecroppers, Lennie and George, who in the 1930s, at the height of the Depression, live on the road, moving from ranch to ranch in search of work. One has the brain of a child but the strength of a potter; the other tries to protect him from life's obstacles. Difficulties that won't be long in coming, given how easily Lennie gets into trouble. Landing on one last farm, Lennie, attracted by the seductive wife of the chief's son, and unable to control his own strength, accidentally kills her. George, torn by grief, in order not to abandon his friend to the lynching, decides to end his existence.
text_b: It is set in 1935 in Waxahachie, Texas, a small town in the midst of the Great Depression. One Sunday afternoon, Sheriff Royce Spalding goes to investigate the rail yard shootings. A happily drunk black teenager, Wylie, is firing a revolver. He reaches an empty chamber and, thinking the gun is empty, points at the sheriff and kills him. Royce's body is taken home to his widow, Edna, and their children. A truck full of vigilantes drags Wylie's body behind them and pulls up in front of the Spalding house. Later, Wylie's friends take his body down from a tree. Both men are buried on the same day. Edna now has to raise her children alone and is heavily in debt to the bank, which is why the manager recommends that she sell her house and move out. She refuses to be homeless and decides to start planting cotton seeds to sell later. For this, Edna receives help from a black man named Moze, and also receives Mr. Will as a guest in her home, who is also blind.

anchor_text: The film is set in 1935 in Waxahachie, Texas. Edna Spalding lives on a farm with her husband Royce. Royce, the local sheriff, is called in one night about a black man rioting, and he's shot in the line of duty. text_a: The film centers on two sharecroppers, Lennie and George, who in the 1930s, at the height of the Depression, live on the road, moving from ranch to ranch in search of work. One has the brain of a child but the strength of a potter; the other tries to protect him from life's obstacles. Difficulties that won't be long in coming, given how easily Lennie gets into trouble. Landing on one last farm, Lennie, attracted by the seductive wife of the chief's son, and unable to control his own strength, accidentally kills her. George, torn by grief, in order not to abandon his friend to the lynching, decides to end his existence.
text_b: A young mother, Edna Spalding, after the death of her sheriff husband at the hands of a drunken African-American boy, is suddenly faced with the need to take care of all the problems of daily life on her own. After her husband's death she also learns that the large estate and the house itself have not yet been paid in full. Faced with the dual risk of having to sell everything and having to disperse her family, Mrs. Spalding, with the decisive help of African-American Moses, transforms her barren estate into a thriving cotton plantation. Other significant events are inserted on this plant: from the blind tenant, initially welcomed by Mrs. Spalding only for economic reasons, over which humanitarian and affective ones will soon prevail, to the violent tornado that collapses and destroys the entire area, to the racial conflict in which Moses, the architect of the Spalding family's fortune, will eventually be directly involved.

anchor_text: Zach is an aspiring artist who gave up his dream of going to art school to take care of his family. This consists of his sick father, his sister Jeanne, and their five-year-old son Cody. In his free time, Zach surfs, paints, or hangs out with his best friend Gabe or his "always on" girlfriend Tori. When Gabe's older brother Shaun returns to the house on a temporary basis, he and Zach become friends. Shaun encourages Zach not to just give up on his dreams and apply to art school one more time. The friendship develops into a love affair. Jeanne warns Zach that Shaun is gay when she learns that the two are spending a lot of time together. She also forbids Cody from having any contact with Shaun. The dispute with Jeanne and Zach's sense of duty to his family strains his relationship with Shaun to such an extent that he separates from him in the dispute. Zach gets a call that he's been accepted into art school after reapplying. The application was sent by Shaun without his knowledge. At the same time, Jeanne asks Zach to take care of Cody so that she and her boyfriend can move to another state, where both have found better jobs. He reconciles with Shaun and says that when he first applied, he cancelled out of a sense of duty to his family. With Shaun's help, he manages to convince Jeanne to let Cody live with them permanently.
text_a: Young California surfer Zach was forced to give up his dream of entering an art school to devote himself to his family, especially his grandson Cody, the son of his sister Jeanne. When Shaun, the older brother of his best friend Gabe, returns home, he and Zach quickly form a close friendship. Shaun urges Zach to take control of his life in order to better establish himself both in the family and in the art world. Zach is also impressed by the strong bond that Shaun has forged with Cody. However, as time passes and due to his homosexual relationship with Shaun, Zach comes into conflict with his family, partly also with his friend Gabe and even with his own identity. Also, having learned of the affair, Jeanne decides to protect Cody from the homosexual couple and forces her brother Zach to choose between the nephew and the lover. Zach then temporarily breaks up with Shaun, but it will also be thanks to the complicity and advice of his ex-girlfriend Tori that Zach will get back together with Shaun. So, when Jeanne decides to leave little Cody with her brother for a while, in order to move in with her new boyfriend who can't stand children or Cody, Zach, backed by his partner, will be able to impose on his sister by reversing the situation and forcing the latter to choose whether to continue to take care of the child or entrust him with Shaun.
text_b: Seventeen-year-old Veronica "Ronnie" Miller's parents have separated, and her father, Steve, has moved from New York to the country. Ronnie still blames him for leaving the family. She and her brother Jonah have to spend the summer holidays with their father. Steve lives a simple life and devotes his time to the completion of a mosaic window in the village church. It was destroyed in a mysterious fire that nearly killed Steve, Ronnie's father. Jonah enjoys spending time with his father crafting. With Ronnie, though, Steve just can't seem to get a hold of him. Ronnie meets the self-confident Will, to whom she is also repulsive, as she is to most people. On the beach, she finds a place with sea turtle eggs. To protect it from raccoons, she sleeps on the beach from now on. When Ronnie asks the marine aquarium for help, it turns out that Will is working there as a volunteer helper. Eventually, the two discover commonalities, and Ronnie can finally enjoy the holidays. But Will's family and friends aren't thrilled about meeting Ronnie, so their feelings for each other are put to the test. When Ronnie's father has a coughing fit and collapses, he has to go to the hospital. Ronnie finds out that he has been suffering from lung cancer for some time and wanted to spend probably the last summer of his life with his children. Also, it turns out Ronnie's father didn't set fire to the church, as the villagers suspected, but Will's friend Scott witnessed someone else set fire to the church. Ronnie starts playing the piano again and reconnects with her father. After Steve's death, Ronnie returns to New York and attends the Juilliard School, from which she has been watched since she was five years old and received a scholarship upon graduation. Her father taught there, too.

anchor_text: Kat Ellis, a 30-year-old New Yorker and somewhat neurotic airline executive, must travel to London for her younger sister Amy's wedding. But she has no desire to make it as a bachelor and to see her ex-fiancé there as a wedding witness. She decides to hire Nick Mercer to accompany her as her fiancé. However, as preparations for the festivities are in full swing, Kat discovers that she begins to find the young man much more than nice...
text_a: Kat Ellis (Debra Messing) will attend her sister's wedding in England, however, she does not want to go alone, as she wants to make her ex-boyfriend jealous and decides to hire the services of Nick Mercer (Dermot Mulroney), a professional gigolo, to pose as her boyfriend, as her ex-boyfriend will be the best man of the wedding for being the best friend of the groom. Upon arriving with the brand new "boyfriend", Kat will go through a series of critical moments such as discovering the real reason for her previous breakup, the tense reason for her dealings with her mother and will learn to love herself thanks to Nick.
text_b: Kate is a fine-dining chef at one of the finest restaurants in Manhattan, New York. She focuses all of her life and energy on her work and her very high ambitions to be the best in her field. Her younger sister (the mother of nine-year-old Zoé) is tragically killed in a car accident while visiting her father. Kate then takes care of her niece and realizes that she is unable to raise her properly on her own. The sympathetic and charismatic chef Nick, a lover of Italian cuisine and a great fan of operas by Giuseppe Verdi and Giacomo Puccini, then enters their lives and introduces them to the "taste of life" in his own way...

anchor_text: New York City-based Kat Ellis travels to London for the wedding of her younger sister Amy, where she acts as a bridesmaid. She finds out that the best man is her ex-fiancé Jeffrey. Ellis hires the call boy Nick Mercer, whom she learns about from an article in the New York Times. Have Mercer pose as her boyfriend and make her ex-boyfriend jealous. Ellis asks her mother for separate rooms for her and Mercer, but the older woman refuses, saying she is "not fussy". Ellis and Mercer are closing in. It turns out that during Jeffrey's relationship with Kat, he cheated on her with Amy. Ellis resents Mercer for finding out sooner than she did and not telling her. Mercer leaves the agreed wages and leaves the house, but returns later. Kat's father makes her realize that she loves Mercer, and persuades her to "get him back". Amy confesses the affair to her fiancé Edward Fletcher-Wooten, who forgives her and marries her. Ellis and Mercer end up together; Mercer goes out of business.
text_a: Kat Ellis (Debra Messing) will attend her sister's wedding in England, however, she does not want to go alone, as she wants to make her ex-boyfriend jealous and decides to hire the services of Nick Mercer (Dermot Mulroney), a professional gigolo, to pose as her boyfriend, as her ex-boyfriend will be the best man of the wedding for being the best friend of the groom. Upon arriving with the brand new "boyfriend", Kat will go through a series of critical moments such as discovering the real reason for her previous breakup, the tense reason for her dealings with her mother and will learn to love herself thanks to Nick.
text_b: Kate is a successful, skilled but overly serious chef, and social interaction is not her strong suit. One day, her sister dies in a car accident and Kate has to take care of little Zoe, her granddaughter. During his brief absence, the owner of the restaurant hires the Italian-American Nick as a second chef, who conquers everyone with his skill and cheerfulness and Kate feels threatened by him. Meanwhile, Kate is unable to properly care for Zoe, who is still very depressed about her mother's death. So she tries to take her granddaughter to work, and she's very fond of Nick there. One day Kate forgets her little niece, and in exchange for forgiving her aunt, she asks her to invite Nick to dinner, hoping that something will happen between the two of them. Shortly afterwards the man reveals to Kate that he had been offered his place; Kate misunderstands and after an argument, fires him, only to discover later that he had actually refused. When Zoe finds out that her aunt and Nick will never see each other again, she runs away from home, reaching the cemetery to find solace on the grave of her mother who she misses so much. In desperation, Kate contacts Nick, and together they find the little girl, making peace. After quitting her job, Kate opens a new restaurant with Nick, and the two are helped by little Zoe.

anchor_text: The film begins with the issue of mass incarceration of Americans in the United States. There's a short audio clip of Barack Obama saying that the United States has 5 percent of the world's population, but 25 percent of the world's prisoners. The film traces the history of this development, beginning with the passage of the 13th Amendment to the United States Constitution, which was supposed to abolish slavery. Since convicted criminals were allowed to continue doing forced labor, the post-Civil War legal system was designed to provide a steady supply of offenders to compensate for the lost labor of slaves. This approach was steeped in racism. Added to this were the Jim Crow laws, which were an expression of racism. Through the film The Birth of a Nation, the cultural accompaniment of this period is shown. After the Civil Rights Act of 1964, direct racist legislation was no longer possible. Crime rates did not decline, however, since the war on crime and drugs now dominated U.S. politics. This adjustment of the legal system was also marked by racism. A prominent example is the difference in punishment between crack cocaine and powder cocaine. Social minorities often could only afford crack cocaine and had to fear much higher prison sentences. The case of the Central Park Five will also be highlighted. It also shows how the penal system is linked to the prison system. For example, prisons are often privately operated and networked with manufacturing facilities. This gives incentive to all concerned that there are many inmates in the prisons.
text_a: The film begins with an audio of former U.S. President Barack Obama stating that although his country has 5% of the world's population, it has 25% of the world's prisoners.[6] It explores the economic history of slavery and the post-Civil War racist legislation and practices that replaced it in systems of racial control, such as the criminalization of minor offenses, by institutionalizing the hiring of convicts (which created an incentive to criminalize more).[7] It continues with a review of the legalization of segregation (the Jim Crow laws) and with the actions that, after the civil rights movement, were pushed by Republicans and Democrats under the justification of the war on drugs. Added to this are the interests of politicians and businessmen who encouraged the construction of prisons, which has mainly harmed African American and Latino populations.[8] He also details the role of ALEC (American Legislative Exchange Council), short for the American Legislative Exchange Council, who have been behind some proposed laws such as the Florida law defend your territory that helped acquit the assailant George Zimmerman after shooting and killing Trayvon Martin in 2012.[9]
text_b: In the Rebibbia penitentiary, a group of inmates perform William Shakespeare's play Julius Caesar for an audience. The film opens with the last scenes of the play, when Brutus asks his comrades for help in committing suicide. Here's a look back at the beginning of the trial six months ago. The middle part of the film shows the development of the performance in black and white. It's not until the premiere that the rest of the film is in color again. As a reminder that you can also show color, a landscape image on a cell wall changes color for a few seconds. The color scheme of the Taviani brothers illustrates the different dramaturgical levels of the film. The finished staging is shown in color. Black and white prison life and the relationships of the roles with the real life and the past of the convicts. The theater director Fabio Cavalli portrays himself in the film as recruiting a squad of 12 men from the prisoners: all "heavy boys" with one exception: the actor of Brutus had already been fired six years earlier and had meanwhile developed as a professional actor. Apparently, the theater is being restored, which is why the rehearsals are taking place at the prison's usual locations. As a result, the buildings, courtyards, and corridors of the penitentiary become the setting for the unfolding action. The prisoners also rehearse in their cells, reflecting on the content of the texts with their own lives.

anchor_text: The documentary opens with recent statistics: the United States has 5 percent of the world's population but 25 percent of the world's incarcerated people. The director demonstrates that slavery has been perpetuated since its abolition through actions such as the criminalization of certain behaviors. Freedmen, for example, were accused of vagrancy and forced to work for the State or to rent land to landowners. Other reasons that led to the mass incarceration of people of color in the United States in the late 20th century include the privatization of voting rights for African-Americans and the Republican War on Drugs. The director also examines the prison centers that have become real industrial complexes and points out the profits made by some companies as a result of these incarcerations.
text_a: The film begins with an audio of former U.S. President Barack Obama stating that although his country has 5% of the world's population, it has 25% of the world's prisoners.[6] It explores the economic history of slavery and the post-Civil War racist legislation and practices that replaced it in systems of racial control, such as the criminalization of minor offenses, by institutionalizing the hiring of convicts (which created an incentive to criminalize more).[7] It continues with a review of the legalization of segregation (the Jim Crow laws) and with the actions that, after the civil rights movement, were pushed by Republicans and Democrats under the justification of the war on drugs. Added to this are the interests of politicians and businessmen who encouraged the construction of prisons, which has mainly harmed African American and Latino populations.[8] He also details the role of ALEC (American Legislative Exchange Council), short for the American Legislative Exchange Council, who have been behind some proposed laws such as the Florida law defend your territory that helped acquit the assailant George Zimmerman after shooting and killing Trayvon Martin in 2012.[9]
text_b: The film deals with the rise and fall of the Black Panther Party for Self-Defense, known as the Black Panthers, during the era of the Black Power movement. Another focus is on the FBI's COINTELPRO program, a strategy that used illegal methods to combat the black civil rights movement. The film argues that U.S. government agencies (FBI/CIA) worked with organized crime to flood the inner cities - which were predominantly inhabited by blacks - with heroin and cocaine. This policy was intended to prevent the positive development of black communities. This film is dedicated to those who have contributed to the advancement of African-Americans.

anchor_text: Gregoire Canvel is a film producer. He loves his films as much as his family, even if reconciling the two seems increasingly difficult, outside of the parentheses represented by the weekends in the countryside; hyperactive and hung on his phone, even on vacation, charming, good-natured, he seems as solid and confident as possible. Yet around him the difficulties pile up. His production company, Moon Films, is in trouble. Then begins a race forward, from frustration to exhaustion to despair. Filled with debts, he shoots himself in the head.
text_a: Grégoire Canvel has it all. A wife he loves, three wonderful children and a profession that he is passionate about and to which he devotes almost all his time and energy, he is a film producer. Grégoire never stops, except for the weekends he spends with his family in the countryside, moments as precious as they are fragile. Grégoire is admired by everyone, but the reality is that his production company is not doing well and he wants to follow up to the last consequences. On a good day, he has no choice but to face reality, to face failure; accompanied by an enormous boredom that gradually degenerates into despair.[1]
text_b: Sarah Dedieu, an actress in her forties, is at the peak of her career. It has just been awarded a prestigious prize for interpretation. Going to rest at her house in the Var, she invites three other actresses. The four women, of different ages, will be exchanging views on their respective professions. And also about men, especially how men affect their lives and their art.

anchor_text: After the suicide of her husband Grégorie, a very dynamic film producer but full of debts, Sylvia tries in vain to keep the man's production company afloat. Meanwhile, Clémence, one of Sylvia and Grégorie's daughters, discovers that she has a brother, Moune, born from a parallel relationship with her father. After the company's bankruptcy, Sylvia leaves Paris with her daughters. Clémence won't be able to meet her half-brother.
