anchor_text: It's Seattle. Paul is a guy who's about to get married to his longtime girlfriend Karen. Unfortunately for him, the morning after the bachelor party he wakes up in the bed next to Becky, a young and shabby girl he met the night before. Assuming he has slept with her, Paul chases Becky away from seeing her again the same night at a family dinner when he discovers she is Karen's cousin. In the days that follow, Paul tries his best to hide the affair from his future wife, but he ends up getting himself into all kinds of trouble. Soon, this complicated situation is joined by Ray, Becky's ex-boyfriend, a very jealous policeman, and Pete, Paul's brother, who shows, not too veiled, that he has feelings for Karen. And Paul himself, in the run-up to the wedding, realizes that he's beginning to harbor a strong feeling for the wild Becky, much deeper than for his quiet fiancée.
text_a: The film tells the intertwined stories of three protagonists, each of whom in his own way tries to change his life. Television producer Denise quits her job, breaks up with her boyfriend Paul and wants to get rid of her city apartment to work on a farm and write a novel. Her estranged husband, Paul, a filmmaker himself, is in a life crisis. In his general uncertainty, he can't decide whether to quit his job and run away, or whether to stay and take over his ex-girlfriend's apartment. The prostitute Isabelle is tired of being constantly abused by pimps and deprived of half of her earnings. She's finally moving into the apartment that Denise vacated. Paul gets hit by a car and killed.
text_b: Karen (Selma Blair) and Paul (Jason Lee) are about to get married. At his bachelor party, Paul gets drunk and meets an eccentric dancer named Becky (Julia Stiles). The next day she wakes up next to him, and Paul kicks her out. After learning that Becky is his fiancée's cousin, Paul tells Karen a lie so as not to worry her about their impending wedding. The little lie will grow into a bigger one, and soon your whole life will be a series of misunderstandings. An important point is that the film revolves around how Paul's secret is protected by other men who don't even know him, but at the same time, they all understand the situation and simply cover it up. After a time of trying to hide the lie from Karen with the help of Becky, who in turn is stalked by her ex-boyfriend, a corrupt cop named Ray (Lochlyn Munro), Paul realizes that he has fallen in love with his fiancée's eccentric cousin.

anchor_text: Tom agrees to keep his employer's house, hoping to win the heart of Lisa, his boss's daughter. But the girl has other plans for the evening. Our unfortunate plans will then be disrupted by the arrival of unexpected characters...
text_a: When Tom is invited to his boss's house, he thinks it's a great opportunity to get to know his daughter, who he's lost his mind about. But things do not go as hoped, in fact, other guests of the house will interfere with his plan of approach.
text_b: Kathy is married to Peter and seems happy, but their relationship becomes routine. She wonders what would have happened if she had gone further with Tom, her college boyfriend. She asks her friend Emily to start a relationship with Tom and then tell him.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of the Donbass war through 12 episodes that make up "an unprecedented choral black comedy", as Paolo Mereghetti called it: among these, an ordinary German journalist arbitrarily accused of Nazism, a volunteer nationalist soldier who ends up being handcuffed and exposed to public ridicule, a wedding celebrated by the separatist authorities of New Russia that ends in a kind of farce, etc. According to the director, the film: "is not the story of a region, a country or a political system. It's about a world lost in post-truth and false identities. It involves all of us".
text_a: Events such as the Cossack heritage, the Russian Revolution (1917-1921), the occupation of Ukraine by Austro-German troops, the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk, the incorporation of Ukraine into the Soviet Union, Ukrainian collaborationism in World War II, the Volyn massacre, the 1990s, the Orange Revolution, the Ukrainian Revolution of 2014, the referendum on the status of Crimea, the Odessa clashes, the War in Donbass, the downing of flight MH17, and other significant events are discussed. Throughout the film, Russian President Vladimir Putin, former Ukrainian President Viktor Yanukovych and former Ukrainian Interior Minister Vitalij Zacharchenko, as well as American journalist Robert Parry, are interviewed, primarily by Oliver Stone, about events in Ukraine's recent state history.
text_b: The film explores in several sequences the hybrid war in Donbas between Ukraine and the self-proclaimed Donetsk People's Republic, supported by Russia. With its images it is a film that is pure militant cinema and that strives especially to convey to us the coldness of violence, either through the snowy landscapes or its palette of grayish colors stripped of all humanity.[2] In the Donbas, war is called peace, propaganda is erected as truth and hatred pretends to be love. Crossing the Donbas is a chain of risky adventures in which the grotesque and the tragic are mixed, as are life and death. It is not a story about a region, a country or a political system, but about a world lost between what lies after the truth and false identities.[4]

anchor_text: The film's scarcely interconnected thirteen scenes deal with the conflict between Ukraine and the Russian-backed Donetsk People's Republic in the mid-2010s. Since 2014, Ukrainian government forces have been fighting pro-Russian separatists. For the inhabitants of the Donbas Basin, war has become part of everyday life. Society will be torn apart as collateral damage between the great powers fighting here. However, although the structures are crumbling in the civil war and the situation is not improving, the eye of the media and thus of the potential participants is rarely focused very intensely on this region. Many residents of the region are trying to continue their normal lives at home in the midst of the chaos that has become commonplace. In revenge for an alleged murder of reputation, a politician dumps feces on the head of an editor-in-chief. Meanwhile, terrified people are hiding in a bunker from the relentless gunfire from above. In the middle is also the German journalist Michael Walter, whose task as a reporter is not made easy either at the entrance or on the spot.
text_a: The film explains the sources of contemporary Ukrainian nationalism, reviewing events such as the Cossack Hetmanate, the occupation of Ukraine by German-Austrian troops, the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk, the incorporation of Western Ukraine into the USSR, Ukrainian collaborationism with Nazi Germany in World War II, the Great Patriotic War, the massacre of Poles in Volhynia, the massacres of Jews in Babi Yar, the configuration of Ukraine in the 1990s, the Orange Revolution of 2004, the Euromaidan of 2013-2014 and events that took place until 2015 such as the referendum on the political status of Crimea of 2014, the clashes in Odessa of May 2014, the Dombas War and the Malaysia Airlines Flight 17 crash, among other events. It offers a historical perspective on the deep divisions in the region that led to the 2004 Orange Revolution, the 2014 uprisings, and the violent overthrow of democratically elected Yanukovych. Covered by Western media as a popular revolution, the documentary argues that it was a coup d'état prepared and staged by nationalist groups and the U.S. State Department. Investigative journalist Robert Parry reveals how, both in Ukraine and in other countries, since the 1980s, political NGOs, foundations and media companies funded by the United States have emerged to replace the CIA in promoting its geopolitical agenda abroad. It also highlights the decisive role of far-right and anti-Semitic Ukrainian political organisations, such as the Right Sector and the Svoboda party, and of far-right paramilitary organisations in Western Ukraine from the time of the Second World War such as the Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA) and Stepan Bandera.
text_b: The film explores in several sequences the hybrid war in Donbas between Ukraine and the self-proclaimed Donetsk People's Republic, supported by Russia. With its images it is a film that is pure militant cinema and that strives especially to convey to us the coldness of violence, either through the snowy landscapes or its palette of grayish colors stripped of all humanity.[2] In the Donbas, war is called peace, propaganda is erected as truth and hatred pretends to be love. Crossing the Donbas is a chain of risky adventures in which the grotesque and the tragic are mixed, as are life and death. It is not a story about a region, a country or a political system, but about a world lost between what lies after the truth and false identities.[4]

anchor_text: Conrad Valmont enjoys life at the family-owned and prestigious Manhattan hotel, where he's taken care of by the staff. Conrad has no goals, no committed relationships, and only one friend, Dylan Tate. This one takes him in when Conrad loses his right to stay at the hotel because of his parents' divorce. At his friend's apartment, Conrad meets Beatrice Fairbanks, who is already dating Dylan. So within a week, Conrad's life is turned upside down and he begins to search for the meaning of life and finds true love. The film is told from Conrad's perspective.
text_a: A child psychiatrist named Nathan R. Conrad (Michael Douglas) visits the day before Thanksgiving, at the request of a friend (Oliver Platt), Elisabeth Burrows (Brittany Murphy), a teenage girl with unexplained psychological disorders. After an unsuccessful session the psychiatrist returns home where he is met by his wife, Aggie Conrad (Famke Janssen), who has a temporary plastered leg. But the next day, when he wakes up, something will have happened, as his daughter has been kidnapped by a dangerous man, Patrick Koster (Sean Bean). The brutal kidnappers do not want money. They just want the prestigious Dr. Conrad is extracting a six-digit code from Elisabeth's mind. With the help of Detective Sandra Cassidy (Jennifer Esposito), Conrad will try to solve the case and save his daughter's life.
text_b: At the age of 11, Conrad Valmont's (Jason Bateman) parents leave for Paris for a 20-year "vacation" and leave their son in the hands of the staff of their luxury hotel. At the age of 30, Conrad has a fantastic life, he is surrounded by women and lives in wealth but his parents decide to divorce and this brings him to a new reality. Conrad finds himself penniless, evicted from his hotel and with only a $200 loan from his analyst in his pocket. He moves in with his friend Dylan (Billy Crudup) and meets Beatrice (Olivia Wilde), the girl of his dreams who, unfortunately, is dating Dylan. Conrad will have to juggle loyalty to his friend and the dream of a great love affair, all while lying about his financial situation.

anchor_text: 1942: The Alsatian Robert Klein lives as an art dealer in German-occupied Paris and does business with Jews in need. One day an issue of a Jewish newspaper is delivered to him. His efforts to have his name removed from the magazine's subscriber list put him in the crosshairs of the prefecture, which uses the subscriber list. Klein begins investigating and discovers that a Jewish resistance fighter has used his name and gone underground. Klein's attempts to prove his non-Jewish ancestry strengthen the police's suspicions. Thus, he too becomes a victim of the large-scale raid, in which thousands of Parisian Jews are gathered and arrested at the Vélodrome d'Hiver before their deportation to Germany. The lawyer with whom he is friends can no longer hand over the Aryan certificate that Klein has tried to obtain in his Alsatian homeland in time, and Klein finds himself in the wagon with the Jew with whom he did business at the beginning of the film, on the way to the concentration camp.
text_a: In Paris in the summer of 1942, during the Nazi occupation, thieves, apparently compelled by food rationing, steal meat and sausage from Monsieur Edmond Batignole's small butcher shop. Edmond suspected, instigated by the collaborator Pierre-Jean Lamour, Simon Bernstein, the son of a wealthy Jewish family of doctors who live in the same house. When Edmond challenges the boy's father, he inadvertently delays the Bernstein family's escape. Meanwhile, Pierre-Jean calls the local police, announces the escape plans and the whole family is eventually arrested. Afterward, though shocked by the events, Edmond resumes his occupation as a butcher. Meanwhile, Pierre-Jean, the fiancé of Edmond's daughter Micheline, arranges through his connections for Edmond to receive the large apartment of the deported Bernstein family. Also, Edmond, more or less involuntarily, becomes a court supplier to the German commander. While Edmond is having a small party for his new "client" in the "aryanised" apartment, Simon Bernstein, the little boy, suddenly reappears. He escaped deportation and is now looking for his family. Edmond hides him in the attic and supplies him with food. When the boy's hiding place threatens to be blown up, Edmond, driven by his bad conscience, struggles to find an escapee for Simon. As a result, Edmond himself now arouses the interest of the Germans. When Simon's cousins show up, everything blows up and Edmond kills Pierre-Jean just as he wants to hand the children over to the Germans. Edmond flees with the three children to the countryside, and a dangerous escape to Switzerland begins. Through the children's reckless statements and actions, Edmond even becomes the target of the Resistance, who considers him a traitor.
text_b: Monsieur Robert Klein is a forty-year-old Frenchman living in Paris in 1942 who is indifferent to war and people's pain. He is an art collector, he has a house full of luxuries and a young mistress: when he needs it, as a good lender, he gets at half price paintings that some Jew needs to get rid of. His security is compromised when he discovers that there is another Robert Klein, probably Jewish, who is trying to escape racial persecution by attributing his identity to him. Klein is frightened and goes to the police, arousing strong suspicions. Things get complicated when Klein, searching for his namesake, locates the poor house where he lives, and is welcomed by his lover into a castle. The man has good reason to believe that the stranger has set him up, perhaps to cover up his own terrorist activity, and he hopes to escape by presenting to the authorities documents indicating that he is of Aryan descent. However, as the days go by, the bait closes even more as it is not possible to locate the second Mr. Klein, all the clues lead to a dead end, and the police become increasingly aggressive. A lawyer offers him a way to leave France with a fake passport, but he does not use it, determined to discover his enemy, whose voice he has only heard over the phone.

anchor_text: In 1914, the peaceful life on Moreau's farm was disrupted by war. His son André joined the army, while a prisoner-of-war camp was set up on the farm, and his daughter Mona felt nothing but hatred towards Germany. Arriving at the camp, the German prisoners look approvingly at Mona, but the worsening news of the war keeps her distant and hostile. Oscar Muller, a prisoner working on the farm, then proves by his actions that he is a good man. As the bond between Mona and Oscar grows stronger, so does the disapproval of the neighborhood; even with the end of the war, tragic consequences seem inevitable.
text_a: In a French village on the eve of World War I, two families live as good neighbors. The boy (Robert Harron) from one family wants Marie (Lillian Gish), the daughter from the other family. While they are preparing for their wedding, war breaks out and the young man, though an American, decides to serve the country in which he lives and participate in combat. During the fighting, the village is bombed and the young man's family is killed. Von Storm, a German officer, assaults Marie, who manages to escape. On the battlefield, the boy is seriously wounded and is treated by the Red Cross. After his recovery, he enters the village disguised as a German officer, and is reunited with Marie, but both have to kill a German officer who discovered them. Von Storm finds the body and then the hiding place of the two lovers, entrenched in a room of the hostel. The Germans try to break through the door, while the French arrive to liberate the village.
text_b: In France in 1914, pastoral life on the Moreau farm is interrupted by war. Andre, the son, joins the army, in place of the farm a prisoner of war camp is built, and the daughter, Mona, feels great hatred towards Germany. Upon arrival, the German prisoners cast approving glances at Mona, but news of the worsening war keeps her hostile until the arrival of Oscar Muller, a camp prisoner who proves to be a good guy. As the bond between Mona and Oscar solidifies, the neighbors' vituperation grows, even with the end of the war. Tragic results seem inevitable.

anchor_text: De Roller, the High Commissioner of the French Republic on the island of Tahiti in French Polynesia, is a man of calculation and good manners. Between official receptions and shady establishments, he's constantly feeling the pulse of the local population. Furious riots could break out at any time. This is all the more true as a rumor persists that a submarine has been sighted whose ghostly appearance could herald a resumption of French nuclear tests.
text_a: "On the French Polynesian island of Tahiti, the High Commissioner of the Republic and French government official De Roller is a calculating man with impeccable manners. His somewhat broad perception of his role leads him to navigate the high-level "establishment" as well as dark places where he mingles with the locals. A persistent rumor has also been circulating: the sighting of a submarine with a ghostly presence could herald the return of French nuclear testing".
text_b: On the French island of Saint Pierre (off the coast of Canada), Neel, guilty of a savage murder, is sentenced to the guillotine. This one has to be brought in from the Caribbean island of Martinique, so it's gonna take a long time to get here. Meanwhile, under the custody of Captain Jean, and thanks to his wife, Neel becomes quite popular and indispensable on the island.

anchor_text: Charlie Rogers, a rebellious young singer, is riding his motorcycle across the United States. One day he stopped in a small town and got a part-time job at a club. To the rhythm of his songs, the place quickly becomes a place where everyone rushes.
text_a: The vagabond musician Charlie Rogers, thanks to his songs, manages to save the amusement park run by Maggie Morgan from bankruptcy. One day Charlie is falsely accused of stealing, so he leaves the amusement park and starts singing for the competition. However, he soon discovers that he is too fond of his old friends and returns to them thus bringing back the success of their amusement park.
text_b: Roger Swanson (Campbell Scott), a New York advertising and seduction expert, becomes the mentor of his 16-year-old nephew (Jesse Eisenberg) who wishes to lose his virginity.

text_a: Rick Richards returns home to the Hawaiian Islands after being fired from his job as an airline pilot. So he decides to team up with his friend Danny Kohana to fly tourists from island to island in their helicopter. Following a minor accident, Rick's pilot's license is suspended. He will, however, still have to get back on his helicopter to rescue his friend Danny and his daughter who are in danger. Rick will be able to rescue the two, and thanks to his heroism, their company will be able to continue its activities without any further obstacles.
text_b: It's 1964, and Charlie Rogers (Elvis) is riding around on his motorcycle as a globetrotter, a man of many talents who excels at nothing, until he finally gets a job at a traveling carnival. At first he handles physical chores, but soon he picks up a guitar and things change. He soon gives the impression that this talented young man is just what Maggie Morgan (Barbara Stanwyck), the fair's owner, needs to get out of bankruptcy.

anchor_text: The plot of the film is the story of the street girl Nikki Finn, who is falsely accused of murdering his boyfriend and therefore goes to prison. After her release, Nikki meets a man who is supposed to make sure she gets on her bus back to Philadelphia, and persuades him to help her catch those responsible for her imprisonment. In their search for the culprit, they fall in love.
text_a: Nikki Finn spent four years behind bars in a cell for a crime she didn't commit. So his primary obsession is to find the real culprit, as soon as he gets out of prison. She takes with her on her journey the lawyer assigned to escort her to the bus station and an endangered cougar.
text_b: The main plot of the film is based on the story of a mother and the consequences of a brutal rape she suffers and which is witnessed by her young daughter. A veteran cop (Cage) ends up becoming an unexpected avenger for both of them.

anchor_text: Young Sheriff John Higgins is chasing three robbers who are taking refuge at a remote express shack on the trail. That's where old Dan is already resting, being shot by the robbers. Before the robbers escape, they set up the scene as if Dan had been involved in the robbery. John believes that he himself is responsible for the death of his friend and father-in-law, but at the same time he is convinced that Dan had nothing to do with the robbery. He's putting down his sheriff's star and leaving town. In the wilderness, he scavenges for gold, befriends an Indian tribe, and takes action against outlaws when they cross his path. So it is that one day he rushes to the aid of Dan's daughter Bess, who returns to take over her father's farm and is attacked by robbers. Following Sheriff Williams' advice, Bess employs John as a foreman on the farm. In time, the two fall in love. At a dance event, Bess hears through John's rival Joe Dickson that John shot her father. Filled with hatred, she then chases him off the ranch. But now it turns out that Dickson had something to do with the robbery. John follows Dickson's men and finds out that the next morning they plan to steal the horses from Bess's ranch. With the help of the friendly Indian tribe and the sheriff, John can prevent this from happening and take down the gang. He coerces a gang member into a confession. That's how he finds out that Dan was actually killed by Dickson. John can overpower him and capture him. The film ends with Bess and John reconciling.
text_a: Sheriff John Higgins is in pursuit of bandits who have just stolen money. They take refuge with Dan Mathews, John's best friend. The sheriff arrives on the scene and manages to shoot a robber at the window. When he enters the room, it is Dan that John finds dead at the bottom of the window. Disgusted at killing his friend, John resigns from his position and decides to leave town. It's Ed Williams, a friend of John and Dan's, who inherits the sheriff's badge. Before he died, Dan had just withdrawn his savings from the bank to invest in his ranch with the intention of bequeathing it to his daughter Bess. Having found the money on his dead friend's body, John entrusts it to Ed to hand over to Bess when she shows up to inherit the ranch. Some time later, Bess does arrive in town, but the car carrying her is attacked by the same bandits who kill the driver to steal the money that was being transported. She manages to hide, and it's John who comes through, who helps her escape and retrieves the money. In the heat of the action, the girl believes that John is one of the bad guys. But she doesn't see his face dirty from the fight. Ed manages to convince John to become Bess's foreman to help keep her ranch afloat. He accepts and becomes closer and closer to the young lady. But she doesn't know two things about him: he's a thief she doesn't know, and he's the man who killed her father. The outlaw gang is using this secret to turn Bess against John. So they try to manipulate her and steal her cattle. Thanks to the serial number of a ticket from the first flight, the racks are identified. John manages to get a confession and learns that he didn't shoot his friend Dan.
text_b: Dan Evans is a farmer. The earth is ungrateful, stingy, and drought is a relentless enemy. One morning, accompanied by his two young sons, he was surprised by a stagecoach attack by the gang of the bandit Ben Wade. This one shoots the conveyor who pulled out his gun. Even if he's unarmed and alone against everyone, Dan says he's saving his sons. So he does not intervene and even allows thieves to take his own horses. On the advice of his wife, Dan decides to go into town and apply for a loan. He can't find a creditor, but he's offered a way to collect $200. All he has to do is escort Ben Wade, who's been arrested, with the help of Potter, the local drunk, to a remote station where they'll catch the 3:10 train to Yuma and its notorious penitentiary. As Wade and Evans wait in a hotel room for the time to board the train, Wade's men manage to locate the train, and Dan soon finds himself surrounded by ten rifles. With his gun pointed at Wade, he's still in the lead, but for how long? Little by little, a strange bond is formed between the educated and seductive criminal and the tough and courageous farmer. Eventually, moved by his bravery and tempted by a new challenge (the escape from the penitentiary), Wade teams up with Dan to escape the pursuers, and they both board the Yuma train in extremis.

anchor_text: Veronika (Sarah Michelle Gellar) is a woman who wakes up in a psychiatric clinic after a failed suicide attempt. She discovers that she has a weak heart and has only a few days to live.
text_a: A woman (Sarah Michelle Gellar) wakes up in a mental institution after a suicide attempt. There she discovers that because of the overdose, her heart has weakened and she has few days left to live, and as time passes, she discovers true love and a way to survive.
text_b: A man teaches a young girl how to become a lethal weapon. His mission is to get close to a group of sadistic teenagers who kill blonde women for fun, causing the hunt to begin. A gang of rich psychopaths who kill girls in the woods for fun choose as their new victim a young woman who turns out to be a professional killer.

anchor_text: Twenty-seven-year-old Veronika is bored with life. She's pretty, has a good-paying job and lives in New York. But one day she decides to take her own life. She's on an overdose of medication. However, Veronika is found and survives the suicide attempt. When she regains consciousness, she finds herself in the Villette Psychiatric Hospital, where she is told that her heart has been damaged by the overdose and that she will die within a few weeks. From then on, Veronika lives the life of a lunatic in the clinic and begins an emotional bond with a young man named Edward. Slowly, but surely, Veronika begins to reconnect with life in the knowledge that she will soon die. She escapes from the asylum with Edward to spend what she believes will be the last days of her life. However, towards the end of the film, it is revealed that Veronika is physically healthy and has survived her suicide attempt without any lasting medical consequences. The attending psychiatrist, Dr. Blake, has deliberately experimentally misdiagnosed Veronica's impending death in order to take away Veronica's lethargy with such a therapeutic trick. In his unorthodox view, in patients at risk of suicide, the bitterness of life caused by a body poison called "vitriol" can only be effectively treated by an awareness of death. Dr. Blake's experiment in Veronica's therapy is proving successful in the end.
text_a: Set in New York City, the film tells the story of Veronika Deklava, a 28-year-old woman who decides to end her life after living for years in the monotony of the world around her. When she gets home, she pours herself full of pills and abandons herself to her fate. He wakes up in a room with doctors around his bed. She thus learns that she survived the suicide, but also learns that her heart system has weakened as a result of it, and it will support her for a short time, perhaps a week. During this short period she remains in treatment at the Villate mental institution, under the guidance of Dr. Blake, where she meets other guests, including her roommate Claire and Mari; above all she meets Edward, who will have in the few days that she has left to live a very important role regarding the achievement of the "awareness of life" in the face of the "awareness of death".
text_b: Paulo Coelho addresses, through his characters, the problem of mental illness: depression, schizophrenia and panic syndrome. In this book, which takes place in Slovenia in 1997, Paulo Coelho narrates the experience of Veronika, a completely normal young woman. She's pretty, she's got plenty of suitors, and she's got a good job. His life passes without major shocks, without great joys or great sorrows, but he is not happy. That's why, on the morning of November 11th, Veronika, she decides to die. After failing in her suicide attempt and spending two weeks in intensive care, her family decides to admit her to a psychiatric hospital. Dreams and fantasies. Desire and death. Madness and passion. On her way to death, Veronika experiences new pleasures and finds a new meaning to life, a meaning that had remained hidden from her until now, when perhaps it is too late to turn back.

anchor_text: Zola (Taylour Paige), a waitress from Detroit, befriends one of her clients, Stefani (Riley Keough), who invites her to join her for a weekend in Florida where, as pole dancers, they can make some money. The trip quickly turns into a nightmare when Zola realizes that X (Colman Domingo), their driver, is also Stefani's pimp...
text_a: The waitress Zola meets a prostitute named Stefani in the restaurant where she works and the two become friends thanks to their common interest in pole dancing. Stefani invites Zola to join her on a road trip with the goal of earning a handsome sum by performing in numerous Florida strip clubs. Zola agrees and soon finds herself involved in a strange journey with Stefani, her boyfriend Derrek, and her violent Lennon X.
text_b: Nisi (Halle Berry) and Mickey (Natalie Desselle) are waitresses at a soul food restaurant in Decatur, Georgia. His plan is to open the world's first combination barber shop and soul food restaurant. To raise the necessary start-up money, they audition for a music video in Los Angeles. Through an unusual turn of events, they end up assisting a Beverly Hills butler and caring for an elderly millionaire, Mr. Blakemore (Martin Landau), who welcomes them to his mansion. They become Black American Princesses (BAP) "living big and taking control!" among the rich and famous.

anchor_text: The story takes place in 1953 during the purges of the authoritarian Rákosi regime. Two women, a mother and her daughter-in-law, are bound together by their love for the same man. A historical context, a universal tension between two beings make up the backdrop of a discourse on the "imaginative" function, the mental capacity and the social need to make one's own cinema.
text_a: Female Pleasure deals with the oppression of women and in particular their sexuality. The documentary follows five women with different religious backgrounds in various regions of the world in their struggle to have an equal relationship between the sexes.
text_b: In 1953, after Stalin's death, many political prisoners are released in Hungary. A woman, whose husband had been arrested some time earlier, makes her mother-in-law's last days happy, telling her that the man has left for the United States.

anchor_text: The married couple Anne and Jack White are successful in their careers. After the second attempt at artificial insemination, Anne is pregnant. The childless couple, Helen and Frank, kidnap the pregnant woman because Helen can't get pregnant after a miscarriage. You fake a car accident, Anne is declared dead. The kidnappers are targeting the unborn child: After the birth, the mother is to be killed. The kidnappers tell the expectant mother that she was implanted with Helen's fertilized egg at the women's clinic where Frank works. Anne is vacillating between rebellion and resignation. But the psychopathic kidnapper duo also has violent conflicts: Frank is beaten to death by his wife Helen. As he dies, he reveals to Anne that she is carrying her own child and not someone else's. Anne's husband Jack, meanwhile, has begun to investigate, but is knocked unconscious by Helen. After a failed escape attempt, Anne overpowers Helen in a fight and kills her. The injured spouses and the newborn are being transported from the rescue.
text_a: Jack White and his wife Anne have everything they need to be happy. They live an exemplary married life in a lavish home, surrounded by their best friends. Yet, there is one small symbol missing from this perfect life: a child. After several miscarriages, Anne decided to resort to in vitro fertilization. The experiment proves it: the pregnancy test is positive. The two lovers are happy until the day they are told, during a medical interview, that the test results are actually negative. The young woman is not pregnant, it's a new hope that collapses. One night, when she is alone at home, Anne is brutally kidnapped and taken away from home to a remote house in the countryside. She meets her two captors: Frank, the man in charge of Anne's fertility analysis studies, and her companion Helen, a sadistic and hysterical mental retard. The kidnappers tell their inmate that she was deceived by Frank's manipulations at work, and that she is actually pregnant. Frank and Helen are a married couple who once had an abortion and are now unable to have another child. They plan to appropriate Anne's after disguising her disappearance as a horrific car accident. Now alone in a squalid cellar, prey to insane torturers, the expectant mother will live her pregnancy in a veritable hell for nine months. Jack, on the other hand, is convinced that his wife is still alive and will devote all his energy to finding her.
text_b: France, 1963: Anne, who comes from humble backgrounds, is considered to be an enlightened student. When the young woman becomes pregnant, her promising future is threatened. Final exams are coming up, and her baby belly is starting to grow. She fears not being able to finish her university studies and losing everything with the birth of an illegitimate child. With the academic title sought, Anne wanted to escape the constraints of her social background. She is determined to have an abortion. However, in 1960's France, abortion is illegal. For Anne, it's the beginning of a career that could end in prison if she's found out.

anchor_text: Newly arrived in New York City to pursue her studies, Tracy felt very lonely. She did not have the exciting encounters she had hoped for, nor did she lead the hectic life she had longed for. Until the day she's welcomed by her soon-to-be half-sister Brooke, a delusional, fearless young woman. Seduced by Brooke's extravagances, Tracy finally discovers the Manhattan of her dreams...
text_a: Tracy Heart leads a seemingly quiet and normal life. The young woman still lives at home with her mother and brother in the suburbs of Sydney and works as a clerk in a video store, but in her heart she fights daily against her demons. An inveterate heroin addict, Tracy was subjected to forced and ruthless detoxification treatment by her mother, who has been supervising her ever since. Meanwhile, the girl continues to struggle with drugs, especially when she meets the famous football champion Lionel Dawson, whom Tracy used to supply with heroin.
text_b: Tracy, a college freshman in New York, is not having the exciting college experience, nor the glamorous metropolitan lifestyle that she envisioned. But when she is greeted in Times Square by her older half-sister, Brooke, she befriends her and is soon seduced by the girl's crazy plans.

anchor_text: Rosemary has been dead for eleven months now, but her memory lives on, for better or for worse, in all who knew her. And especially in those who were present on the day of his tragic suicide, which took place during a dinner at the elegant restaurant "Luxemburg". Exactly one year later, however, a series of anonymous letters arouses suspicion that this was not a suicide but a murder. All five of the dinner guests had a valid reason for seeing Rosemary dead, and none of them are who they say they are. The truth will come to light only after a hallucinatory and fatal reconstruction of the crime.
text_a: All the participants in the birthday party of the beautiful Mari-Rosa (Rosemary in the original) Barton remember her and try to understand the causes that led her to commit suicide that night. But her husband is warned: she did not commit suicide, she was murdered. Could a reproduction of that party help unmask the killer? Everyone present could have had reasons to kill her and Colonel Race, a friend of the family, is investigating to give a definitive answer to this crime.
text_b: Hercule Poirot's very efficient secretary, Miss Lemon, makes a small mistake while transcribing a letter. The reason for his discomfort is related to a problem concerning his sister, an equally efficient lady who runs a boarding house for foreign students at 26 Hickory Street in London. In recent months, strange and unpleasant events have occurred, which Mrs. Hubbard has been unable to handle properly: unexplained thefts and acts of vandalism. Mr. Poirot decides to help Mrs. Hubbard, but he is immediately thrown into the middle of so many seemingly independent situations. The problem is exacerbated when a murder occurs after Poirot visits the family boarding house. Poirot, however, does not give up and, with Detective Sharpe, manages to gather enough information to make sense of the events of Hickory Street. At last, Miss Lemon is reassured and Poirot can once again rely on her great efficiency.

anchor_text: American twins Allyson and Melanie Porter visit their grandfather Edward in Paris. He's the U.S. ambassador there, and he has little time for the two girls. That's why he's assigned his assistant, Jeremy Bluff, to supervise them, and they keep getting away from him. They fall in love with the city and two French boys, Jean and Michel. Allyson and Melanie scoot around the city's sights, such as Notre Dame, and meet many nice people. They take a shopping tour of boutiques and meet for dinner dates at the Eiffel Tower.
text_a: Maxime and Mélanie, two young people living in Brussels, are in love. Awkwardly but passionately, the two explore their sexuality. But then Mélanie becomes pregnant, and Maxime is not enthusiastic about it, because he feels too young and sees the pregnancy as an obstacle to his career as a football goalkeeper. But from time to time, he gets used to the idea of raising the child. But Melanie, under pressure from her mother, has since changed her mind.
text_b: The twins Melanie and Allyson Porter visit their grandfather Edward, the U.S. Ambassador to the Ville Lumière in Paris. The latter decides to entrust his two granddaughters to his young embassy employee, Jeremie, to show them around the city. The twins visit many museums like the Louvre, but they get very bored. While out for a walk, Mel and Ally meet two boys and fall in love. They don't eat anything for dinner that night because they're not used to French food. The next day in a restaurant they meet Brigitte, a model, and become friends with her. They keep seeing those boys and introduce Brigitte to Jeremie. Eventually Brigitte and Jeremie fall in love and Mel and Ally return to America.

anchor_text: Melanie and Allyson Porter are 13-year-old California middle school twin sisters who are mostly interested in fashion and boys. Right now, their main concern is the high school prom. They each have a named boyfriend, but none of the boys are able to tell them apart. Far from these worries, their mother, Barbara, is planning a future vacation to Paris...
text_a: Maxime and Melanie are 15 years old and in love. He hopes to become a professional footballer and be able to support them when he grows up. One day, Melanie finds out that she's pregnant. After consultation, despite their young age and despite the reluctance of their parents, they decide to keep the baby.
text_b: Melanie and Allyson are thirteen-year-old twins who arrive in Paris to spend a week at the home of their grandfather, the United States ambassador to France. An embassy employee takes them to museums and exhibitions, until they meet two boys their own age and start having fun.

anchor_text: Amantha Starr is the privileged daughter of a plantation owner from Kentucky. After his death, a shocking secret comes to light: her mother was her father's black slave. Now that she is legally considered property and not her deceased father's estate, she is being offered for sale to a New Orleans slave trader. On the voyage to New Orleans, she is harassed by her owner, the slave trader Calloway. Then she decides to end her life. Calloway can prevent this at the last moment and now leaves her alone, because such a beautiful, cultured, young woman, who passes as white, seems to him far too valuable to risk her death. Amantha is up for auction. When she shamelessly inspects a potential buyer in the auction hall, she is rescued by wealthy plantation owner Hamish Bond by offering an extraordinarily high sum for her. He gets the job. Expecting the worst, Amantha is surprised to find that her new owner treats her like a lady, not a slave. In his city villa, she meets his housekeeper and former lover Michele. Hamish has a very special relationship with his chief slave and plantation manager, Rau-Ru. Rau-Ru is grateful for Hamish's kindness, education, and trust, but still hates him. Amantha and Hamish fall in love. He is prepared to grant her freedom, but she chooses to remain with him. Their situation is complicated by the fact that Hamish is hiding a terrible secret from his past that troubles his conscience. Then the Civil War breaks out and New Orleans is captured by the Union Army. Hamish becomes a wanted man when, in protest, he and other plantation owners set fire to his fields to destroy the crop. Rau-Ru, who has meanwhile joined the Northern States Regiment, helps Hamish and Amantha escape.
text_a: Amantha Starr (Yvonne De Carlo) is the extremely spoiled daughter of a Kentucky plantation owner. Unfortunately, his death reveals a terrible secret: Amantha didn't know that her mother had been one of her father's black slaves. Betrayed by his father's new wife. She legally becomes property and is taken by a New Orleans slave trader to be sold. On the boat down the river, he frankly tells her that he intends to have sex with her, but he gives up when she tries to hang herself. The mixed race could pass for white having a higher financial value, he decided to preserve his merchandise so his gain. Amantha's being auctioned off, and now she's being mercilessly inspected by a potential buyer, a rude character. She is saved from further humiliation by Hamish Bond (Clark Gable), who outbid and buys her for an exorbitant price. Expecting the worst, Amantha is surprised that her new owner treats her like a lady and not a slave. At her master's property in the city, she meets the main slaves he owns, his housekeeper Michele (Carolle Drake) and his favorite slave Rau-Ru (Sidney Poitier), with contrasting personalities. He is indeed grateful for Hamish's kindness, the education he has given him and the trust he has placed in him, but he can never forget his social status. Time passes and Amantha and Hamish fall in love with each other. To make matters worse, Hamish is hiding the terrible secret of a past that troubles his conscience. Eventually the Civil War breaks out and New Orleans falls to the Union, with a price on Hamish's head. He and Amantha escape with the help of Rau-Ru, who had escaped and joined the Union army.
text_b: Qinawi is a young newspaper vendor at the Cairo train station, with a slight limp for which all women avoid him. He becomes obsessed with Hannuma, a beautiful liquor saleswoman, engaged to Abu Siri, a stocky luggage handler and union leader. However, Qinawi proposes to the girl. Rejected, his obsession turns to madness. Inspired by an unsolved murder, he buys a knife and plots to kill Hannuma. When the local police start a raid against the illegal vendors, Hannuma asks Qinawi to take their drinks and hide them. He tries to lure her to a warehouse later, but she asks a friend to go get them. In the darkness, Qinawi does not notice the substitution; she stabs the other woman repeatedly, then hides the body in a wooden box that supposedly holds Hannuma's gear. Then he gets Abu Siri to load the rigging onto a train for the impending wedding. However, the woman is not dead. They find her and alert the station. Abu Siri is accused of attempted murder, but the victim identifies his real attacker. Meanwhile, Hannuma goes to get his drinks. Qinawi chases her through the rail yard and holds her with a knife to the head to keep the surrounding crowd at bay. The owner of the newsstand tells Qinawi that he will be allowed to marry Hannuma and convinces him to put on his wedding dress. Qinawi obeys, only to moments later notice that he has put on a straitjacket.

anchor_text: Stu Miley is a cartoonist, he created the cartoon Monkeybone about a monkey named Monkeybone. Miley suffers from nightmares, which is why he's being treated by Dr. Julie McElroy, who he falls in love with. She inspired Miley to do the cartoon that made him successful. Miley has an accident that leaves him in a coma. In this state, he experiences a fantasy world where he meets Monkeybone. Monkeybone takes over Miley's body when he comes to Earth. Miley is arrested and meets writers such as Edgar Allan Poe and Stephen King in prison. He learns of a conspiracy of fictional characters who take on the bodies of well-known authors from the real world. A fictional character frees Miley from prison. He takes on a body and fights his opponent until both are killed by the arriving police. Death unites the personalities of Miley and Monkeybone into a body that is sent into the real world.
text_a: Cartoonist Stu Miley is the creator of a new comic book character growing in popularity, Monkeybone, a little monkey. Monkeybone is the complete opposite of its author: impudent, clever, obscene, greedy, and immoral. It's these qualities that ensure the character's success. Stu knows fame and he's about to marry Dr. Julie McElroy, the woman of his dreams. However, he was involved in a serious car accident that left him in a coma. He drifts into his imagination and enters Downtown, a strange world populated by bizarre creatures. It's where all people who stand between life and death usually end up. They have to make a decision, to find their way back to consciousness or to sink into eternal sleep. Stu has 12 hours to leave this hostile world before Death comes for him. He then gets help from Monkeybone.
text_b: At the beginning of the film, Kermit the Frog is sitting in a marshy landscape, singing the song The Rainbow Connection, when an agent happens to pass by in a rowboat. He is enthralled by Kermit's talents and encourages him to go to Hollywood to become famous there. Kermit is enthusiastic about the idea and is on his way. Along the way, he meets, among others, comedian Fozzie Bear, the band Dr. Teeth & The Electric Mayhem, stuntman Gonzo and his girlfriend Camilla, and piano-playing dog Rowlf, who join him. At a carnival, Kermit meets Miss Piggy, a recently shaved beauty queen, who falls in love with the frog and tries to win his heart. But there are also dangers lurking on the road to Hollywood. Doc Hopper, the owner of a frog-leg restaurant chain, is desperate to get Kermit as a mascot and publicity figure. Together with his assistant Max, he makes several unsuccessful attempts to catch the frog, including using the German scientist Professor Max Krassmann and the Frog Killer Snake on him. Eventually, Kermit and his entourage reach Los Angeles and break into the office of producer Lew Lord. He agrees to finance a film about the adventures of the Muppets, entitled The Muppet Movie.

anchor_text: Wayne and Eileen Hayes are finally living their American dream in peace: a successful career, grown-up children and a dream house. But their happiness suddenly turns into a nightmare when Wayne is kidnapped by a former collaborator, Arnold Mack, and held captive deep in a secluded forest. Very quickly, the victim, exhausted by the most difficult negotiations, realizes that he cannot rely on anyone. His lot is entirely in the hands of his captors, a man who has nothing to lose and everything to gain. Faced with the most important negotiations of his life, Wayne knows he's now negotiating for his own life.
text_a: Jim is a literature teacher. For work reasons he is forced to move with his family to the city where he spent his traumatic childhood. As children, Jim and his older brother Wayne were attacked in a railroad tunnel by a gang of thugs. Wayne accidentally lost his life at the hands of the ringleader, Jim fled while three of the four thugs were run over by a speeding train. Overwhelmed by terrible grief, little Jim and his parents left town for good. Now a grown man, he is forced to return with his family to the same place that marked his life forever and where his past awaits him: the three villains who killed his brother Wayne have returned for a reckoning.
text_b: Wayne Hayes (Robert Redford) and his wife Eileen (Helen Mirren) are living the American Dream: they have two children and run a successful business. They're looking for a place to retreat from the city and rest. But everything changes when the protagonist is kidnapped. He, negotiating with the kidnappers, and she, collaborating with the FBI, try by all means to end that situation. Throughout the film, his wife discovers that he is continuing the affair that he had promised to leave several months ago, which changes the relationship between the two again.

anchor_text: A contract killer named Raven is framed by nightclub owner Willard Gates, a go-between for rogue industrialist Alvin Brewster, the president of Nitro Chemical. On his way to Los Angeles, shooting his way to the traitors, Raven meets Ellen Graham, a nightclub singer, and takes her hostage. Graham is then hired by a senator to find out through Gates who is doing business with the Japanese to sell them poison gas. Ellen's fiance, the police Lt. Michael Crane, trying to track down Raven while simultaneously wondering if his girlfriend was kidnapped or a willing accomplice.
text_a: Ten people from different backgrounds are gathered in a huge house in a desert area, at the invitation of a Mr. Owen, a mysterious and invisible host. On the first night, after dinner, as the guests are gathered in the drawing room, a voice is heard, accusing each of them of a murder that has gone unpunished and vowing to avenge it. That's when Michael Raven, after drinking quite a bit of whiskey, collapses. We thought he was drunk, but he stopped living, and we found out he was poisoned. It'll just be the first of many. Is Owen one of the remaining guests?
text_b: Raven, a contract killer, is hired by Willard Gates to kill a man named Baker and steal documents from him. After exploiting him, Gates pays him in marked bills so the police can catch him. While being chased to arrest him, Raven manages to escape by vowing revenge and meets Ellen Graham, a nightclub entertainer who is actually the girlfriend of a police officer, Michael Crane, and a counterintelligence agent. The girl works for the government who's trying to expose Alvin Brewster, Gates's handler and the president of a major chemical company, who actually works for the Japanese. Ellen tries to save Raven and convince him to cooperate with the law to expose the real criminals. Realizing he has been betrayed, Raven kills the traitors but is himself shot down by the police.

On his way to Los Angeles, shooting his way to the traitors, Raven meets Ellen Graham, a nightclub singer, and takes her hostage. Graham is then hired by a senator to find out through Gates who is doing business with the Japanese to sell them poison gas. Ellen's fiance, the police Lt. Michael Crane, trying to track down Raven while simultaneously wondering if his girlfriend was kidnapped or a willing accomplice.

On the first night, after dinner, as the guests are gathered in the drawing room, a voice is heard, accusing each of them of a murder that has gone unpunished and vowing to avenge it. text_b: A contract killer, Phillip Raven (Alan Ladd) shoots an industrialist, a police informant, and soon realizes that he was paid with dirty money. Pursued by the police, Raven encounters Ellen Graham (Veronica Lake), the fiancée of the detective tracking him, on his trip to Los Angeles. However, Ellen who is a magician and nightclub singer will be of great help to him, as Raven wants to find her sponsor Willard Gates (Laird Cregar), a nightclub owner, and take revenge. Melville will use the film as inspiration for his "Samurai"

anchor_text: Right on the border between Arizona and Mexico, retired U.S. Marine Jim Hanson runs a small farm that's threatened with foreclosure because of his late wife's expensive cancer treatment. While on patrol, he encounters Rosa and her ten-year-old son Miguel, who have illegally crossed the border to escape a Mexican cartel. Rosa is mortally wounded in an attack by the gangsters. Dying, she asks Hanson to bring her son to his family in Chicago. In return, she hands him a bag full of money. After initial hesitation, Hanson accepts the assignment, and the unequal couple embarks on the one-thousand-and-a-half-mile journey, always pursued by the cartel's killers, who are aided along the way by corrupt cops. On top of that, they are also wanted by the immigration authorities, who want to turn Miguel over to the Mexican juvenile authorities.
text_a: During the war in the Pacific, a battalion of U.S. Marines lands on an island in the Philippines occupied by the Japanese. Under the command of Captain McDonald, the soldiers fight their way forward, but suffer heavy casualties in the process. McDonald is wounded in the shoulder, but he continues to lead his men into a patch of forest. They're setting up camp there. The Americans go in search of their enemies. During a pause in the fighting, the soldiers remember their civilian lives. The Japanese soldiers under the command of Captain Tanaka are also thinking of their families and their homeland. McDonald sends nervous young Cliff, whose father is a minister, and womanizer Egan on a scouting mission. They can transmit enemy positions by radio, but they'll be ambushed on their way back. Egan is killed, Cliff and the young Japanese Nakano are badly wounded. Cliff takes pity and gives Nakano his field bottle. In return, Nakano Cliff offers his cigarettes. At this moment, the sadistic Sergeant Honeywell reaches the two wounded and kills the young Japanese. Captain McDonald looks around and laments the madness of war.
text_b: Jim Hanson (Liam Neeson) leads a quiet and trouble-free life. A cowboy and rancher, this former Marine and Vietnam veteran lives alone on his ranch on the Arizona-Mexico border. One day he saw a woman and her child illegally crossing the border into the United States. They were chased by a group of armed men who wounded the mother, Rosa (Teresa Ruiz). After defending the mother and her eleven-year-old son, Miguel (Jacob Perez), he promises Rosa, who does not survive her injuries, to protect Miguel. They'll have to face the killers of a drug cartel run by Mauricio. Against the advice of his police daughter Sarah Pennington (Katheryn Winnick), Jim decides to take the young Mexican under his wing while Mauricio (Juan Pablo Raba) and his henchmen land on American soil to shoot Miguel. Alone against all, the ex-soldier is willing to do anything to protect him.

anchor_text: The members of the rap group "The Fat Boys" are portrayed in this film as a group of underqualified and overweight nurses from Brooklyn. You're being hired by Winslow Lowry for a job in Palm Beach, Florida. There they are to care for his ailing, wheelchair-bound great-grandson, Dennison. Turns out Winslow's got big betting debts with loan sharks. To pay off the gangsters, Winslow hopes that the unsuspecting caretakers will send his uncle to his grave as soon as possible. Curiously, however, the Fat Boys' unusual methods of treatment are causing Dennison to recover more and more.
text_a: Winslow Lowry, a so-called "good-for-nothing" full of debts, tries to hasten the death of his wealthy uncle (in order to take possession of the inheritance), by hiring the three most incompetent nurses he had found in the vicinity. These are played by members of the Fat Boys, who are unaware of Winslow's plan, and who are seriously trying to save their uncle's life. With their good-natured antics, they eventually manage to save the man, especially after discovering the nephew's plan and trying to stop it at all costs.
text_b: The film tells the story of five young men from Compton, California, who, armed only with outspoken, brutal hip hop lyrics and their own swagger, rebelled against the abuses of authority and formed N.W.A., one of the most influential rap groups of all time. And so, by recounting the life of the ghetto as no one had ever done before, they started a social revolution whose echo still resonates today. The events of the film take place between 1986 and 1995.

anchor_text: Ken Castle is the head of the reality show Slayers, in which death row inmates kill each other to win their freedom. The originality of this reality show is that the protagonists are not free of their movements, they are controlled remotely by online players like a simple video game. Kable, Simon's avatar, is the hero of this entertainment. However, he soon learns that he is being set up and that he will have to escape from this game.
text_a: The year is 2034. Mind control technology has become popular and the video game Slayers allows you to control prisoners in a series of prison fights. If anyone survives 30 fights, they win freedom. Simon (Logan Lerman) is a 17-year-old boy who controls the mind of convict Kable (Gerard Butler), the only one who has managed to win 28 fights. He only has to earn two more to be a free man, but things are not what they seem and an anti-system group decides to take action to raise awareness in the world. Here, nothing is what it seems, least of all Kable and billionaire video game financier Ken Castle. It's all right.
text_b: In a post-atomic world, Shannon, a well-known television champion of a game where he, as the prey, must kill all members of the group of hunters who pursue him to win, is helped in a challenge by Lilith, a telepathic mutant. In return, she asks him to transport her and a group of mutants to a new land, to escape the military's extermination, with a reward in gold bars. Shannon accepts the assignment and gathers a group of warriors as an escort. The journey will be very adventurous, between battles, deadly traps and various ambushes until reaching the final clash with the military authorities, in which the intervention of a child endowed with a great paranormal power will be crucial.

anchor_text: In the 19th century, a little boy, Pelle, and his elderly father, Lassefar, leave Sweden for Denmark. Lassefar promises his son they'll have a better life there. But as soon as they leave the ship that takes them to this new country, they are faced with the harsh fate of immigrants, exploited as cheap labor and despised for their status as foreigners. Lassefar's old age is even a major drawback in finding a job. Forced both of them to work as cowboys for a rich owner, so they continue to have a life of misery...
text_a: At the end of the 19th century, a ship full of Swedish emigrants bound for the United States arrives on the Danish island of Bornholm. Among them are the boy Pelle (Pelle Hvenegaard) and his father Lasse (Max von Sydow), who want to emigrate after the death of their mother. Since they cannot afford the fare, they try to find a job that will allow them to save money, as it is rumored that money can be earned there in a short time. They get a job on a farm, but because of their low social status, they only get to be exploited and mistreated.
text_b: The year is 1936, and the effects of the Great Depression are still being felt in Sweden. Anders lives with his family in the Korpen slum in Malmö. The father is an inveterate alcoholic. To survive, the mother cleans the house. In order to escape this machine, the son flees his neighborhood, his friends and his fiancée, hoping to become a writer one day.

anchor_text: In the 19th century, a little boy, Pelle, and his elderly father, Lassefar, leave Sweden for Denmark. Lassefar promises his son they'll have a better life there. But as soon as they leave the ship that takes them to this new country, they are faced with the harsh fate of immigrants, exploited as cheap labor and despised for their status as foreigners. Lassefar's old age is even a major drawback in finding a job. Forced both of them to work as cowboys for a rich owner, so they continue to have a life of misery...
text_a: The year is 1936, and the effects of the Great Depression are still being felt in Sweden. Anders lives with his family in the Korpen slum in Malmö. The father is an inveterate alcoholic. To survive, the mother cleans the house. In order to escape this machine, the son flees his neighborhood, his friends and his fiancée, hoping to become a writer one day.
text_b: The story takes place in the late 1950s and early 1960s on the island of Bornholm, Denmark. Being very poor, Lasse Karlsson and his son Pelle emigrate from Sweden to Denmark in search of a better life. Both find work on a farm, but are constantly discriminated against because they are Swedish. Over time, Pelle learns the language and gains the trust of some people, such as their master's wife and Erik, a man who works on the same farm. Pelle has a dream: to leave Scandinavia and travel the world with his father Lasse. Despite the hostility of others, Pelle will not give up hope.

anchor_text: Wealthy businessman Harold Van Pelham is a hypochondriac. Accompanied by his nurse and his servant Mr. Pipps, he embarks on a voyage to Paradiso, a small island off the coast of Chile. There, he finds himself caught up in the turmoil of a revolution instigated by the greedy American James Blake. Blake sees Harold as a threat to his intentions and has him arrested. In prison, Harold meets Colossus the Hen, who suffers from severe toothache. They both escape from prison, and Harold removes the infected tooth. Colosso follows Harold out of gratitude. Since Harold sees the excitement and fighting as bad for his heart, he wants to stop the revolution. With the help of Colosso and his nurse, Harold Blake can defeat the revolutionary forces. His actions convince Harold that he is healthy and strong. With Colosso and the nurse, he goes aboard the ship, where Mr. Pipps awaits him.
text_a: Harold Pelham is a flawless-looking City gentleman, married and a family man. One evening, as he drove out of his office in his Rover P5B, he was suddenly caught in a frenzy of speed. He loses control of his vehicle on the highway and is the victim of a serious accident. On the operating table, he appears to be clinically dead. Suddenly, two heartbeats appear on the monitor to the amazement of the doctors. When he wakes up, his whole life seems to have been turned upside down. He discovers a mistress, seems to have been to places he claims he's never been... A mysterious gray car follows him around all the time. Then he finds himself faced with a duplicate of himself... Is he a doppelganger? Has he lost his mind? Does he really exist?
text_b: Wealthy hypochondriac Harold goes to a tropical country for his health (actually excellent) and continually takes pills, given to him by his nurse. But he finds himself in a state of revolution and only realizes it when imprisoned alongside a giant. The two escape; Harold frees the nurse they had imprisoned and, with his cunning and the giant's strength, defeats the revolutionaries. Harold realizes that he loves the nurse and decides to stop taking pills. He's back home with his nurse wife, has a son, and runs off to party with the giant while he's directing traffic.

anchor_text: Film producer Steve Canfield wants Russian composer Peter Illyich Boroff to write music for his next film. However, comrades Brankov, Bibinski and Ivanov were sent to bring Boroff back to Moscow. Canfield, however, bribes her with worldly luxury and arranges for Boroff to work for him. His star Peggy Dayton is supposed to use her feminine charm to convince Boroff to work for Canfield. However, a woman will also be deployed from the Russian side. Comrade Ninotchka Yoschenko has been sent to bring the four men back to Russia. Even though she's focused on her work, Canfield can seduce her with his charm. He even asks her to marry him. However, when she and Boroff hear how much his music has been disfigured, the Russians travel home. However, Canfield does not give up and arranges for Brankov, Bibinski and Ivanov to be sent back to Paris, knowing that they will be taken over by the flair of this city. Ninotchka is sent after them again. Now Canfield has plenty of time to take care of her, so she eventually falls in love with him, too.
text_a: Moscow demands the presence in the homeland of the Russian composer Peter Boroff, all caught in Paris by a career marked by Western pomp. The American producer Steve Canfield - for whom Boroff has to compose the music for a film inspired by War and peace played by the aquatic diva Peggy Dayton - manages to bribe the three commissioners sent from Moscow, making them succumb to the flattery of a luxurious Parisian stay. In their search comes a very strict companion, Nina Yoshenko called Ninotchka who, however, soon falls into the net of the beautiful world, falling in love with Parisian clothes and silk socks (and also Canfield). Everything seems to be going for the best until the preview of a scene that should be included in the film: War and peace has become a musical with protagonist Giuseppina winking at the viewer and Napoleon. Boroff is horrified, and Nina has a knack for getting everyone back home without any resistance. It doesn't take long for the returnees to regret the good life in the West, dreaming of returning to Paris. Which is what the trio Bibinski, Brankov, Ivanov can do. Their new misconduct induces the party, following an anonymous letter denouncing them, to send Nina back to the West as well. It turns out that the anonymous author of the letter is Steve, who helped the three to open a typical Russian restaurant ("That's how we will give good publicity to our homeland", they maliciously declare), who managed with this ploy to get Nina, whom he declares his love, out of the USSR as well.
text_b: During Napoleon's campaign in Russia, the widowed officer Bolkonskij becomes engaged to the young Nataša, but when she falls in love with another he breaks off the bond. The war will make them lose and find in Moscow while the city burns, but really it is not fate and the girl is left alone again. She'll find solace in another man. # Differences with the novel The script of the film has been very condensed compared to the original novel; minor aspects have been omitted or attenuated, while great prominence is given to the figures of Nataša, Pierre and Andrej and their interpersonal relationships. Even some historical events have been cut (such as some minor battles) or speeded up (the meeting between Napoleon and Tsar Alexander I at Tilsit), while others have been deepened (the Battle of Beresina).

anchor_text: After serving a prison sentence for a crime he did not commit, Harry Barber returns to the corrupt city of Palmetto. He's hired by Madame Malroux to fake the kidnapping of her daughter-in-law, the heiress of a dying billionaire. But Harry quickly falls victim to this Machiavellian trap.
text_a: Harry Yquem, a stockbroker, buys for his wife, Florence, a very valuable piece of jewelry from a den of thieves and receivers. He notices a man who looks a lot like a picture he saw in his wife's belongings. Curious, he followed the man to his hotel and left a note asking him to meet him at his home. This man, William Krafft, comes to Harry's house...
text_b: Harry Barber (Woody Harrelson) is a typical middle-class man who wants to get out of financial trouble once he's out of prison. By chance he meets Rhea Malroux (Elisabeth Shue), the beautiful wife of a count, who suggests a business, which consists of a fake kidnapping of the daughter of Elisabeth Shue (Chloë Sevigny) in order to collect a ransom.

anchor_text: J. Harold Manners, a wasteful millionaire, stumbles one day into a poor neighborhood in his hometown. Immediately, he destroys the dining car of Brother Paul, a philanthropist who cares for the poor. Through a misunderstanding, Harold casually pays the philanthropist the then-high sum of $1,000 for the wrecked car. Brother Paul can use the money to open a homeless shelter named after his donor's husband. When Manners learns about this from the newspaper, he goes to the homeless shelter to tell Brother Paul that he does not agree with the use of his name. At the asylum, he meets the beautiful Hope, the daughter of Brother Paul, who works there, with whom he falls in love. To please Hope, Manners now volunteers for the homeless shelter, going from one problem to the next. Through his cleverness, Manners can ultimately help the homeless shelter, while also earning the respect of the roughnecks from the slum bars. Harold and his daughter Hope are finally getting married. However, Harold's wealthy friends, who consider his marriage to Hope a mistake, occasionally kidnap him. It's only after a long struggle that he can finally walk down the aisle with Hope.
text_a: Harold Fine, a well-known lawyer, after a few years of living together, agrees to marry Joyce, his 30-year-old secretary. But a chance meeting with Nancy, a friend of his brother who lives in a hippie community, changes his mind, and just at the time of the ceremony he flees into Nancy's arms, abandoning his job and going to live in his car. Forced by the police to return home, he soon finds it invaded by Nancy's friends, strange, dirty and with too free attitudes. Determined to get back on his feet, he is forgiven by Joyce, who convinces him to marry again. But once again, during the ceremony, the memory of the most beautiful moments spent with Nancy and especially the thought of freedom and love that await him persuade him to abandon Joyce.
text_b: Harold is very rich and he wastes his money buying new cars and destroying them in a day. A girl wants to open a mission, and Harold gives a man money because he thinks he's damaged something, but he thinks the money is for the mission, because there's nothing broken. So the mission is open. When Harold finds out, he is enraged and wants to remove his name from the mission but, for the love of the girl who wanted it, he does not remove it and instead cleverly sends many drunks from the notorious bar where they used to get drunk on the mission and then start going there deliberately, because they like it. Harold and the girl decide to get married but his old rich friends kidnap him to stop him. Then those who went to the mission drunk, believing that Harold had abandoned the girl, get drunk again. Then they find out about it and they pick him up drunk. Then Harold has to get the boys back to the church, but with their hangover, it's gonna be tough. After many vicissitudes they arrive at the church on time and the wedding takes place regularly.

anchor_text: Marina, a young woman heir to a long line of seers, thinks she has found the man of her life and future husband when she sees him in a dream. One day, her dream came true when she spotted Leo Lemke in his small fishing boat on the beach near her home. That same day, she proposed to Leo, and the newlyweds left for New York, where Leo owned a small butcher shop in Brooklyn. But Marina's arrival will upset the lives of some of the people who live or work in the neighborhood, as well as hers and Leo's.
text_a: Marina, a 23-year-old sexually inexperienced girl, lives with her father Spyros, a contemplative architect terminally ill with cancer, in an industrial city by the sea where he works in the local steel mill. Unable to relate to men, she lives her life through David Attenborough's wildlife documentaries, the songs of Suicide and the sex education lessons given by her best friend Bella. When an engineer begins a course of work at the steel mill, Marina has her first sexual relationship with him, although she remains very introverted. As Spyros nears death, Marina asks Bella to sleep with her father, as a last favor to a dying man, as well as to prevent her from sleeping with the engineer as well. Bella satisfies Marina, who in the meantime begins a sexual relationship with the engineer. The film ends with Spyros dying, and Marina and Bella scattering his ashes into the sea.
text_b: Marina (Demi Moore) is a young woman with special powers. It can predict some things that will happen in the future. For several nights she dreams of a man who will be her husband. When Leo (George Dzundza), a man from New York who has gone fishing, arrives on the beach, Marina is convinced that he is the man of her dreams. She goes with him to New York and they get married. The neighbors soon establish a good relationship with Marina and she creates a magical atmosphere that captivates them all. In time, Marina realizes that her marriage is not as she had imagined, but in the neighborhood there is a young man who, according to her, is the man of her dreams.

anchor_text: Carter Page is an escort boy who makes his living offering his presence to the wealthiest women in New York society. One day, the lover of one of them, married to a senator, is found dead by the latter and Page lies to the police by telling that he was the one who discovered him. This lie leads him into political machinations.
text_a: Carter Page III, a middle-aged gay man, is an escort for bored wealthy ladies. One of these women is Lynn Lockner, the unfaithful wife of a liberal senator. When the latter discovers that her lover has been murdered, she will involve her friend and companion Carter in an investigation that will lead them to the highest levels of the federal government.
text_b: Wayne Carter is a well-known bachelor with numerous lady connections. In his luxurious Park Avenue apartment, he receives his lovers, including the married Agatha Carraway. One day, he makes the acquaintance of the virtuous Helene Andrews, who has come to New York from a small town in the Midwest to bring her wayward sister to her senses. The two fall in love, but Mrs. Carraway's intrigues interfere with the smooth running of their romance. In the end, Wayne and Helene walk down the aisle.

anchor_text: Django Reagan is supposed to track down the perpetrator of a robbery, after he manages to get his hands on the stolen money. He sets out to find it in Montana, where a war is raging between ranchers and settlers.
text_a: After his father's assassination, Django is surprised to learn that his father, whom he thought was poor, has in fact left him half of the city as an inheritance. Seeking to seize it, he discovers that his father has been betrayed by Kuster, his partner, in order to seize the magot. Django then decides to take revenge for the honor of his family...
text_b: Django is a bounty hunter on the trail of Trevor Norton, the bank robber from Abilene. When he arrives at Rockstar, he's mistaken for the new sheriff, and he does nothing to prove otherwise. At Rockstar, he also falls in love with Sally, who happens to be the daughter of the man he's supposed to kill.
