anchor_text: Pretty Sonny dreams of escaping her dreary routine and living the life of an outlaw. When she meets the dreaded bandit Jed, she sees her chance coming. Jed is an unusual outlaw who only robs the rich and hides out in Bandera with the peaceful Somillo tribe. Sheriff Franciscus is his worst enemy, because he's been after him relentlessly ever since Jed escaped from his prison, leaving behind a trail of corpses. After initially bluntly rejecting Sonny's accompaniment, Jed finds himself forced to accept her due to Sonny's stubbornness and the threat posed by Franciscus. After several joint robberies, the two marry and then rob the priest. When Franciscus manages to stop the pair, they demand amnesty and promise him a share of the spoils. Franciscus sets their love nest on fire, but blinds himself in the process; Sonny and Jed escape. At a banquet hosted by the noble Don García Moreno, Jed takes his wife hostage and brings her to Bandera. Jealous Sonny then leads Sheriff Franciscus to Jed's hideout. Jed is badly wounded and is being nursed back to health by Sonny. Almost slavishly, he now has to follow Sonny reverse ratio compared to the beginning.
text_a: Pretty Sonny dreams of escaping her dreary daily life and leading a life of outlawry. When she meets the fearsome bandit Jed, she sees her chance come. Jed is an outlaw out of the ordinary who only robs the rich and has his hideout in Bandera, in the peaceful Somillo Indian tribe. Sheriff Franciscus is his greatest enemy, as he has pursued him relentlessly ever since Jed managed to escape from his prison by leaving behind numerous corpses. After initially reluctantly refusing to be accompanied by Sonny, Jed is forced to accept because of Sonny's stubbornness and the danger posed by Franciscus. After a few joint robberies, they get married, then rob the priest who married them. When Franciscus still manages to arrest the couple, they ask for amnesty and promise him a share of the loot. Franciscus sets fire to their love nest, but in the process loses his sight; Sonny and Jed manage to escape. At a banquet hosted by the noble Don García Moreno, Jed takes his wife hostage and takes her to Bandera. Sonny, jealous, then leads Sheriff Franciscus to Jed's hiding place. Jed is badly wounded and is being treated by Sonny. He must then follow Sonny in an almost servile manner - an inverted relationship from the beginning of the story.
text_b: The pretty Toinette lives with her aunt Julie, who runs a hotel. One day, Toinette is seduced by hotel guest Darius Kerbellan and almost driven to suicide. However, the painter Sillon saves them. Sillon introduces Toinette to a Parisian fashion designer and she gets a job as a model. In Paris, she meets on the street King Sandor of Astorra, who immediately falls in love with her. Sandor has political problems in his home country of Astorra. His state is nearly bankrupt and insurgents around General Padilla threaten his position of power. In Paris, he seeks financial support from the wealthy Cornelius Corbett to arm himself for the fight with Padilla. When Cornelius Corbett meets the young couple, he too falls in love with Toinette. The girl, however, wants nothing to do with him. Then billionaire Corbett makes an offer to Sandor. He would help him out financially, but Sandor would have to give him Toinette. Sandor refuses and travels with Toinette back to his home in Astorra. Corbett then begins funding General Padilla's coup plans. Padilla's coup attempt is successful. Sandor and Toinette are going to prison. The throne is lost to Sandor. However, Corbett is plagued by guilt, and he now allows the lovers to leave Astorra. For Toinette and Sandor, a new life is about to begin.

anchor_text: On 15 April 2019, a violent accidental fire broke out in the Cathedral of Notre-Dame in Paris. Men and women will do anything to save the building.
text_a: Jeanne Bécu de Vaubernier's irresistible ascent, between meeting houses and Versailles gold, adorable gray on the pavement of Paris became in a few years the last official favorite of the History of France, but whose fulgurant trajectory will end on the scaffold...
text_b: On 15 April 2019, a fire broke out in the cathedral of Notre-Dame in Paris. In the next few hours the fate of the cathedral will be decided.

anchor_text: It was November 2008 in Mumbai. The Sikh Arjun works as a concierge at the luxury hotel Taj Mahal Palace, and although the husband and father has already had a busy day, he goes to the hotel kitchen to ask the strict but kind-hearted chef Hemant Oberoi to earn a little more money, as his wife expects him to do so. Then, however, there is a series of terrorist attacks in Mumbai. In the luxury hotel, ordinary people of different nations, religions, and skin colors find themselves united in a bitter struggle for survival as terrorists begin to take hostages and set fires there as well. Besides businessman Vasili, wealthy Zahra and her American husband, David, along with a newborn baby and their Australian nanny, Sally, are among the guests who occupy the hotel's opulent suites. At a nearby cafe, Australian backpackers Eddie and Bree witness earlier gunfire and the escalation of attacks in the city.
text_a: In November 2008, the city of Bombay (Mumbai) was struck by a dozen terrorist attacks in crowded places. Among other things, armed men stormed the Taj Mahal Palace & Tower, deliberately killing, by bullets or grenades, any guests or staff they encountered, taking foreign guests hostage, and then setting fire to the hotel. The film follows, among other things, customers who try to escape and palace employees who try to get them to safety, such as the restaurant manager and an Indian waiter who will risk their lives to protect their customers. We're following a foreign tourist couple with a baby, accompanied by their nanny and a Russian client. A few local police officers try to stop the killers, unsuccessfully because they are less well armed. Special Forces finally arrive and neutralize 9 out of 10 of them.
text_b: A few years ago, a jinn, an Arab spirit, gave birth to a horribly disfigured child and hid him in a fishing village. When the villagers gave this way, the mother destroyed the village. Years later, the child grows up and doesn't know where he came from. Khalid and his wife, Salama, are going through a difficult time right now, as Salama lost their child together. Khalid is pushing for them to leave America and move to Ra's al-Khaimah to start over. They're moving into a newly opened hotel. But even the journey through the fog is mysterious. Strange things are happening in the hotel, a dark spirit appears again and again, first driving Salama crazy and then haunting Khalid. After a series of unexplained events, Salama confesses to her husband that she murdered their child because it was deformed and inhumane. Khalid, in a fit of rage, throws his wife out the window.

anchor_text: Malcolm, a young hip-hop geek from the '90s, lives in Inglewood, a hot neighborhood in Los Angeles. With his two friends Diggy and Jibs, they juggle music, high school and college interviews. An invitation to an underground party will lead Malcolm on an adventure that may well change him from "geek" to "cool", "dope".
text_a: Malcolm does everything he can to survive, from recording to interviewing, in a tough Los Angeles neighborhood full of gangsters and drug dealers, during his senior year, to trying to get into Harvard University. An invitation to an underground party will cause an adventure with unusual characters and bad choices.
text_b: In order to avoid giving his wife half of his fortune - and especially the shares of his company - a businessman in the process of divorce arranges his own kidnapping. At the same time, his wife who doubts that she will win the case has the same idea. Meanwhile, a disillusioned employee seeks revenge on her wealthy boss and plans to kidnap him as well. Also, a loser in need of money decides to bail out his finances with the boss's ransom. The trouble starts when all his parallels meet.

anchor_text: Los Angeles, South Central: A group of seven teenagers from Latino backgrounds are unimpressed by the hip-hop culture in their neighborhood. They listen to punk music, wear tight pants and skateboard. They try to avoid the brutality and violence of the various gangs around them. The young people decide to take the bus to Beverly Hills High School. There are the Nine Stairs, a real challenge for any skateboarder. The guys only know her from skateboarding videos. They meet some girls who find the "punks" from the ghetto South Central interesting. They get in trouble with the Beverly Hills police and the guys from the upscale neighborhoods, who obviously can't stand their girls having any contact with them. The South Central group evades the police and gets into a fight with the local youths. Slowly, the Latinos are fighting their way back to South Central, their turf.
text_a: The film shows five episodes of groups of people in New Orleans who are involved in criminal acts. There's a deadly showdown at a crossroads. A juvenile delinquent is shot dead in a store, two cars collide in front of the store, and a courier driver and her companion are killed. In the final episode, however, the story is told anew. The girl Few persuades the murderous gangsters not to cause any more pain, or karma might strike. The gangsters abandon their plan and all the protagonists survive the event.
text_b: Young Latino skaters live in the ghetto of South Central Los Angeles. One day they decide to go to Beverly Hills where they meet young bourgeois girls, rich white and the police. The group does not wish to conform to the hip-hop fashion of the southern city gangs whose violence and uncivility plague the surrounding neighborhoods. These Latino skaters mostly wear tight pants, listen to punk rock, and spend their time skating. The climax of the film comes when the group of young skaters are invited to a posh party in Beverly Hills. They realize that the racism towards them will never disappear, despite their incredible ability to adapt to this artificial and show business environment. The film also includes scenes of the police hunting down the group of young men and humiliating some of them, thus displaying notorious xenophobia and racism.

anchor_text: The young law student Armand Duval falls in love with Marguerite Gautier, known in Paris as the courtesan Camille. Armand abandons his studies and leaves his family to live with Marguerite, who abandons her friends, in seclusion outside the big city. The couple will soon run out of money. Armand wants his inheritance paid, while Marguerite wants to sell her jewelry. Armand's father learns of his son's situation and wants to protect the family's reputation. He convinces Marguerite to leave Armand and return to Paris to live as a courtesan. In a casino, Armand is reunited with Marguerite. Believing that she has left him for the wealthy Count de Varville, he denounces her in public. Marguerite now loses all her remaining friends. Suffering from dizziness, she dies alone and abandoned in her room. In her hands, she holds the only present Armand gave her: an edition of Manon Lescaut's novel.
text_a: On the occasion of her 80th birthday, Lady Lendale tells her life story to her biographer, Sir Percy. When she was young, she left her job as a laundress in Corsica to work in a brothel in Paris. There she meets the man of her life, Armand, a thief and an anarchist. They travel to Switzerland, where Armand finds himself involved in a plot to assassinate Prince Otto of Bavaria. Pregnant, Louise pretends to be a widowed countess in a hotel in Nice, where she attempts to rob Lord Lendale. Although he knows all about her, Lord Lendale is so eager to marry her that he offers to save Armand from the police on the condition that she marry him. She agrees. Later she joined Armand in Italy where she supported his activities with her husband's money. Then, tired of this existence, she returned to England to take up her role as Lady Lendale. In conclusion, she surprises Sir Percy by telling him that she still sees Armand and that he is the father of all her children, Lord Lendale being aware, since Armand is her chauffeur.
text_b: Armand, a young law student, is fascinated by Marguerite, a high-class courtesan, always surrounded by wealthy admirers. Marguerite rejects Armand's constant attentions, but eventually succumbs to his courtship. The two live happily through a season of love that will be very short. Marguerite leaves her lover with a letter: when Armand finds her and marries her, he becomes enraged because he believes that the woman has preferred her life as a luxurious socialite to their love. And when he meets her in society, he accuses her heavily in front of everyone. Marguerite has resumed her life as usual and the disease also resumes its course. Debts overwhelm her, and when the creditors send porters to seize her furniture and valuables, she asks them to leave her one precious thing, a book that once belonged to Armand. The beloved finally rushes to her bedside, but when he arrives, he finds her dead.

anchor_text: The melodrama tells the story of Esperanza and her husband José Luis Castro. The two have married against the will of their father, the landowner Francisco, who drives them from his estate. José Luis moves out to fight in the Mexican Revolution, only to find out that the bandits Ursulo and Rogelio Torres murdered his father. He wants revenge, but Ursulo has already died of typhus. Nevertheless, he hides the body and hangs the dead body. Rogelio Torres takes José Luis' wife and son hostage, so that the latter surrenders and exchanges his life for that of the hostages. He is murdered, but Esperanza lives to see her son grow up and join the Mexican military. In the final sequence, she holds her son in her arms, thus portraying the ideal of the new, reconciled society, and looks over the land that once belonged to her father and has now passed into the possession of the community.
text_a: An Italian immigrant, Antonio, known as Toni, found work in Martigues. He lives there first with Maria, but he falls in love with the beautiful Josepha, of Spanish origin, whose uncle is a successful small landowner. Josépha marries Albert, a Parisian who has mostly views on his uncle's fortune. In addition to his cynicism and greed, Albert turns out to be brutal with Josépha. When her uncle dies, to prevent Albert from getting his hands on the magot, Josépha plans to take it and go into exile in America with Gaby, another of her suitors. But she is surprised by Albert and, during their confrontation, she kills him with a gunshot. When Toni learns from Josepha the circumstances of Albert's death, he decides to disguise the murder as a suicide. Toni and Josépha carry the body into a pine forest to abandon it with the gun in hand, but they are caught in the act by the forest guard. To spare his beloved from prison, Toni blames himself for the murder. He'll be shot by a hunter who thinks he's a vigilante when he tries to escape. Josepha turned herself in to the police.
text_b: In a village in Bajío at the beginning of the twentieth century, José Luis (Pedro Armendariz), son of the landowner Don Francisco (Miguel Ángel Ferriz Sr.), secretly marries Esperanza (Dolores del Río), a beautiful and humble peasant woman. Displeased by the wedding and because his son has become a revolutionary, Don Francisco disinherits José Luis and throws him out of his house. After the triumph of the revolution, the couple lives happily until José Luis is forced to confront a pair of false revolutionaries who have kidnapped Esperanza and her young son.[2]

anchor_text: Jay and Silent Bob discover that Bluntman and Chronic, the comic book adaptation of their lives, is going to be adapted to film by Miramax. On the Internet, "people are drooling" - in Jay's words - already about themselves and the film. So Jay and Bob decide to go to Hollywood, to get the shoot canceled and get their badge back or failing that, to get back the copyright money that they're not being paid.
text_a: Jay and Silent Bob learn of the imminent release of a reboot of the film Bluntman & Chronic and decide to travel to Hollywood to stop the production at all costs. Along the way they suffer all sorts of mishaps: Jay learns he's probably the father and the inseparable couple has a close encounter with a strange version of the Ku Klux Klan.
text_b: Jay and Silent Bob discover that their old friend, Banky Edwards, has sold them out. It seems that he has gone to Hollywood to produce a film based on the comic book about the alter ego of each other, Bluntman & Chronic, starring two popular young Hollywood actors (Biggs and Van Der Beek) in the role of the couple. The real Jay and Silent Bob are stunned at the news, and then shattered to discover that they're being criticized on the Internet for selling their story. There's only one way to save their tarnished reputations: head to Hollywood to sabotage the production.

anchor_text: Rotterdam in the 1930s: Eight-year-old Pietje Bell is the naughtiest boy in the city, but he has a good heart. So he even makes it into his own series of stories with him as the headline hero in the newspaper. Together with his friends, he founds a youth gang, "The Black Hand", and distributes his loot among the needy. But then he learns of a theft plan and prevents it. In the end, he's celebrated as a hero of the city.
text_a: A child named Peter Bell is spreading terror in his hometown of Rotterdam. It's often on the front page of newspapers, which is no surprise to anyone. Accused of something he never did, he tries to prove his innocence and make up for his wrongdoing. Besides, he has a friend who betrayed him...
text_b: The plot of the film centers on the life of a young "bastard" (Jacob Katadreuffe), who lives with his mother (Joba Katadreuffe) and does not know the identity of his father (Dreverhaven). During the boy's altercations in the suburbs, he discovers that his father is the town tax collector and that he does not want to acknowledge his paternity. As the years go by, the relationship between the two becomes hostile.

anchor_text: It tells the story of the relationship between the painter Egon Schiele and five women who are important to him: these are, in addition to his younger sister Gerti, especially his beloved Wally Neuzil, his wife Edith Harms and his sister-in-law Adele as well as the variety dancer Moa Nahuimir. After the death of his father, the sixteen-year-old Egon, together with his twelve-year-old sister Gerti, travels to the sea to Trieste in 1906 to paint them there. Gerti becomes his first muse. In the following years, he earned a reputation as a provocative artist, his works were considered scandalous by Viennese society, and he was put on trial for allegedly molesting a thirteen-year-old girl. Despite all this, his patron and fatherly friend Gustav Klimt predicts a great future for him. Egon falls in love with Wally Neuzil, whom he immortalizes in the title painting Death and the Girl. In 1915, he marries his neighbor Edith Harms, who demands that Egon break up with Wally. Shortly before the end of World War I, Schiele fell ill with the Spanish flu in the autumn of 1918 and lay dying. As Gerti tries to save her brother's life, memories are awakened of other women who significantly influenced Schiele and his art.
text_a: At the beginning of the 20th century, Egon Schiele is among the most provocative and controversial artists in Vienna. His art is inspired by beautiful women and an era that is coming to an end. Two women in particular would really shape his life and artistic expression: his sister and first muse, Gerti, and 17-year-old Wally, probably Schiele's one true love, immortalized in his famous painting Woman and Death. As Schiele's paintings create scandal in Viennese society, forward-thinking collectors and already acclaimed artists like Gustav Klimt begin to recognize the exceptional value of the young man's provocative and tormented art. Schiele will choose to plunge into the pain of his existential discomfort, making it his stylistic figure, choosing to sacrifice love and life to his art.
text_b: The painter Claude Zoret feels emotionally connected to his model Eugène Michael, whom he has also adopted as a foster son. Michael is his artistic inspiration, and at the same time, he gives him everything that Michael needs. During a dinner at Zoret, besides Michael, the Duke of Monthieu, the friendly couple Adelskjold and the critic Switt are guests. Zoret tells them that he wants to paint a picture of Brutus' murder of Caesar; he looks at Michael. The Duke is showing his affection for Alice Adelskjold. The impoverished Princess Zanikov wants to have her portrait painted. Zoret can be persuaded, after all. Michael becomes infatuated with her and enters into a relationship with her. He finances the expensive lifestyle with the sale of paintings and other items of Zoret. This covers him against Switt, who advised the separation of Michael, and even buys back a picture given to Michael and offered for sale by the art dealer Leblanc. Michael's open admission that he's tired of serving as a role model doesn't change Zoret's unbroken affection for Michael. The Duke is killed in a duel by Adelskjold after the latter learns of the Duke's liaison with his wife. In an exhibition, Zoret presents his latest painting Job a man who has lost everything. As a "painter of pain", he is praised by those present. On his deathbed, Zoret leaves his entire estate to Michael. He does not think it necessary to appear there, and prefers to have fun with Princess Zanikov. Zoret dies with the words: "Now I can die in peace, for I have seen a great love".

anchor_text: Bridget Jones, in her mid-30's, believes she has finally found the man of her life. It's the attorney Mark Darcy, with whom she reconciled at the end of the first part after much embarrassment and misfortune. Bridget sees the chances of a lasting relationship with him diminishing, however, when she gets competition from Mark's colleague Rebecca. This one beats her in the bar association quiz and is also a better skier, as Bridget finds out when Rebecca shows up on her ski vacation with Mark. Jones' best friends Shazzer, Jude and Tom give her advice, which leads to her ending the relationship with Darcy due to Rebecca and the fact that he still hasn't proposed to Bridget. Because of her work for television, Bridget has to go to Thailand for a report with Daniel Cleaver, where he almost succeeds in seducing her again. Just as they are about to sleep together, a Thai stripper enters the room at Cleaver's request, whereupon Bridget leaves him for good. At the airport, the police still find cocaine in a gift from Shazzer's vacation acquaintance that Bridget had in her suitcase. She is arrested and taken to a Thai women's prison, where she teaches her fellow inmates songs by Madonna, while Mark Darcy alerts the British government to get Bridget out of prison. Back in England, she finds out that Rebecca is a lesbian and has fallen in love with her. After Darcy learns from Cleaver during a fight that he did not sleep with her, he makes her the long-awaited proposal of marriage.
text_a: Bridget Jones has finally found love with Mark Darcy. But her outspoken and clumsy nature leads her to commit oddities in Mark's somewhat cramped professional circle, especially since he has an absolutely gorgeous new collaborator, Rebecca, which plunges Bridget into anxiety. As a result of a new oddity, Bridget leaves Mark. She is forced by her employer to go to Thailand to shoot a show with her former love Daniel Cleaver. Bridget is about to give in to his advances when she discovers that he hasn't changed as he claims. At the airport, Bridget, a victim of a drug smuggler, is arrested in front of Daniel who does not intervene and is thrown into prison. It's Mark, very cold, coming to tell her that she's going to be released. Bridget, back in England, learns from her friends that Mark was not only a "messenger" as he claimed, but that he moved heaven and earth to free her. Bridget rushes home and bumps into Rebecca: thinking she is Mark's new companion, she is soon misled when Rebecca makes a fiery statement to her... and kisses her. After one final blunder, Bridget accepts Mark's proposal of marriage.
text_b: Lena, Carmen, Bridget and Tibby have known each other since they were kids. Their four mothers were attending a course for pregnant women. So the four heroines have been friends all their lives. They've never spent a summer apart before, but this summer's gonna be different. Lena goes to her family in Greece, where she meets Kostos, a charming young man who will disrupt her life. Carmen goes to see her father, unaware that he has arranged her marriage with a woman who has two children. Bridget goes to a soccer camp, a sport she is extremely good at, and falls in love with Eric...the coach, forbidden fruit, their relationship will lead her to confront old demons. Finally, Tibby will be left alone in Bethesda, home of the four teenage girls. While employed as a cashier, she meets Bailey, a 12-year-old girl with cancer, whose character upsets her. One night, Bailey's mother calls Tibby's mother. This one's upset: it's over, it's the end of Bailey... Anxious about splitting up for the holidays, the longtime friends find jeans that fit each other, even though they have very different physical appearances. They make a sort of pact. So they'll send each other the jeans, one at a time. That's what's gonna bring them together during this long vacation they're gonna spend apart...

anchor_text: This film focuses on the love life of Cleopatra, who first loved Julius Caesar, then his general, Mark Antony. She committed suicide after the conquest of Egypt by Octavian, the great-nephew and adopted son of Julius Caesar.
text_a: The queen of Egypt, using her charm, binds herself first to Julius Caesar and, after the latter's assassination due to a plot by the senate, to Mark Antony, thus causing the latter's clash with Octavian. The young Augustus will defeat the Egyptian army and conquer Egypt. Cleopatra and Mark Antony, to avoid falling into enemy hands, will die by suicide.
text_b: In the first century B.C.E., Julius Caesar led an army into Egypt in pursuit of his rival Pompey. Called to take sides in the rivalry between the Egyptian ruler and his younger sister, Cleopatra, he made her queen of Egypt.

anchor_text: A museum keeper in Boston, Roger, suddenly discovers that the entire collection will be moved to Copenhagen and is not resigned to the idea of having to give up forever the sight of "The Lonely Maiden". But he is not alone: his colleagues Charles and George are also obsessed with two works of art, a painting depicting a woman with cats and a sculpture of a Greek warrior. The three will then decide to put together a plan to steal the works before they are transferred to Denmark.
text_a: Ariane, Bruno and Patrice live together in love, fresh water and fun. For their summer holidays, they drive randomly on the roads of France according to their fancy. Their encounters are as unusual and surprising as their three-way relationship: a desperate man lying across the road who wants to end his life by reinventing the death of Christ, a lonely lover waiting in the middle of the road for his unknown lover, a woman dressed all in white mourning the death of her black husband.
text_b: The story centers on three museum security guards (Morgan Freeman, Christopher Walken and William H. Macy's) who develop a plan to steal pieces of art they have taken an interest in after the museum's new curator plans to transfer the material to another museum in Denmark. One of the guards (Walken) has a wife (Marcia Gay Harden) who is saving money for a trip to Florida the same day the heist will take place. The three guards are working to fix the problems before the museum hit.

anchor_text: Richard Hannay, back in the UK, rescues a nurse from an assassination attempt and discovers that her stroller contained only a... Turn it around. When he finds her, he finds himself involved in espionage. He learns that she is a spy working for England. She gives him the information she has about the enemy organization called the "39 Steps" who wants to steal the blueprints of a British missile by instructing her to contact the authorities while she has to travel to Scotland to follow the trail of the head of this organization. It only tells him that the head of this organization has one finger less on his hand. While he was making her tea, she was murdered in his home with one of his knives. Hannay, afraid of being accused of the crime, flees to Scotland to investigate the notorious organization himself, pursued by the police and threatened by the organization...
text_a: This is London. Young diplomat Richard Hannay witnesses an attack on a governess pushing a wheelchair in Hyde Park. Running to assist the woman, she discovers that the wheelchair is empty. The woman is actually a British intelligence agent and reveals to him that she is on the trail of a gang of spies who want to get hold of military secrets. This gang, called "The 39 Steps", is led by a mysterious man who is only known to have a severed phalanx on one finger. The woman is suddenly murdered, and Hannay fears he'll end up a suspect. To escape the police search and locate the leader of the gang, he travels to Scotland where, along with a local teacher, he is captured by the bandits who handcuff him along with the woman and load him into a car that must take them back to London. Taking advantage of a punctured tire, the man starts driving with only one arm (the other is tied to the teacher's arm) but an accident puts the car out of commission and forces the two to wander the woods of the Scottish Highlands. Taking refuge in an inn, the woman manages to free the wrists of the two from the handcuffs. In London, the spies are unmasked during a variety show, Hannay finally manages to prove his innocence.
text_b: Officer Jackie Morrison works in a Glasgow surveillance centre that covers the entire city and is designed to monitor, investigate and prevent crime. Jackie leads a seemingly unspectacular life, with a not-so-exciting daily routine. But one day, on one of her monitors, she discovers a man she seems to have known for some time. She inquires about him, and you learn that he's just been released from prison. But as a spectator, you don't really know what he's accused of or what his relationship is with Jackie. She now uses the technical abilities given to her by her work, instead of fighting crime more and more around Clyde Henderson - that's the name of the mysterious man - to observe and spy on his life; where he works, where he drinks at night and who his friends are. Even in her free time, she pursues him: she visits his neighborhood, his tribal bar, and tries to get into his circles. She succeeds in seducing Clyde into reporting to the police that he raped her. It's becoming clear that her only intention from the beginning was to put Clyde back in prison. Clyde's friend and former cellmate Stevie subsequently visits Jackie at her apartment and threatens her, telling her that Clyde once drove a car under the influence of drugs and killed her husband and daughter in an accident. Then she withdraws the charges against Clyde and calls him back for another hearing. He's now able to remember who she was, and he's talking to Jackie about her motive and about his accident that took the lives of her family.

anchor_text: Russian Countess Natasha works as a prostitute in Hong Kong after her family fled Communist Russia and became impoverished in China. One day, she and two friends accompany American diplomat and oil billionaire heir Ogden Mears to a party in Hong Kong. She then sneaks into the cabin of the drunken Mears to travel to America as a blind passenger and leave her old life behind. During the journey, Natasha makes a lot of noise, because Odgen and his friend Harvey have to hide them first from the occupation and later from the American Immigration Service. In addition, the diplomat falls in love with her, but he has a wife in Hawaii, whom he wants to divorce. After arriving in America, he has to decide whether he wants to stay with Natasha (which, however, would ruin his career), or want to return with his wife first, in order to maintain the appearance in public for the time being. He's going with Natasha.
text_a: Odgen Mears, appointed U.S. Ambassador to Saudi Arabia, returns home after traveling the world. He meets and then reluctantly welcomes Natasha, a Russian countess who enters the ship in Hong Kong as a stowaway to avoid being employed in prostitution and hides in the budding ambassador's closet, has no passport and is forced to stay in the cabin. He wants to get to the United States at all costs. As a plan to get her off the ship later, they decide to marry her off to Hudson, Odgen's valet. Hudson wants to take the opportunity to consummate the marriage, so that she, when they are about to reach the port of Honolulu and having spent the crossing avoiding him, jumps from deck and swims to dry land. Odgen's wife joins the cruise, and Odgen will have some explaining to do. A lawyer friend of Odgen's who helped prepare the marriage of convenience finds the Countess and informs her that the immigration department has accepted her as Hudson's wife. In the last scene, Odgen, who has left behind cruiser and wife, dances with the countess in a hotel cabaret.
text_b: Sir Stafford Nye's plane was forced to make a stopover in Frankfurt because of the fog covering his destination city of Geneva. Sir Stafford Nye has just returned from a long trip to Malaysia, where he attended a very boring diplomatic conference. While he was resting in the international transit lounge at the airport, a young woman approached him. She confided that she might be killed and begged him to help her. She wants the diplomat to give her his passport and his raincoat so she can impersonate him. As promised, upon his return to London, his passport was returned to him. However, before that, a man broke into her apartment looking for documents. Sir Stafford Nye attempts to locate the young girl by placing an advertisement in a newspaper. She answers him and so begins an adventure that will lead them to Bavaria, where Stafford is assigned the mission by the British secret service to put an end to a revolution that is preparing. At the end of the novel, Sir Stafford Nye marries the stranger from the airport with whom he has lived so many adventures.

anchor_text: Wealthy U.S. diplomat Ogden Mears is traveling by sea aboard a luxury steamship bound for the United States. Ogden is in the process of divorcing his wife, Martha, and is awaiting Washington's confirmation of his appointment as secretary of state. During a stopover in Hong Kong, the man casually meets the charming Countess Natasha Alexandroff, a Russian refugee of noble origin forced into exile for the revolutionary events of her motherland. Back on board and leaving Hong Kong, Ogden is surprised to discover the Countess hiding in his cabin closet. The woman explains that she wants to flee to the United States at any cost and, although she does not have a passport, in order to achieve her goal she is determined to exploit the situation to the detriment of the diplomat. Ogden fearing a scandal, to protect his own career and reputation, tries to arrange a fictitious marriage between his butler Hudson and Natasha, to satisfy the woman's request and thus allow him to become an American citizen, at the same time, to complicate the situation, he is given the news that another diplomat has been appointed foreign minister in his place. However, the situation takes an unexpected turn as Ogden, at first annoyed and irritated by the presence of the Countess in his cabin, in return, falls in love with the woman. Meanwhile, the ship docks in Hawaii where the diplomat is joined by his wife, who explains that a divorce would damage his career, at the same time Natasha, aware of the situation, secretly leaves the ship in tears. Tired of a conventional life and oppressed by Martha's obsessive presence, Ogden decides to abandon his political career and marriage, disembarks from the ship and joins the woman he loves to bind himself to her forever.
text_a: Sir Stafford Nye's plane was forced to make a stopover in Frankfurt because of the fog covering his destination city of Geneva. Sir Stafford Nye has just returned from a long trip to Malaysia, where he attended a very boring diplomatic conference. While he was resting in the international transit lounge at the airport, a young woman approached him. She confided that she might be killed and begged him to help her. She wants the diplomat to give her his passport and his raincoat so she can impersonate him. As promised, upon his return to London, his passport was returned to him. However, before that, a man broke into her apartment looking for documents. Sir Stafford Nye attempts to locate the young girl by placing an advertisement in a newspaper. She answers him and so begins an adventure that will lead them to Bavaria, where Stafford is assigned the mission by the British secret service to put an end to a revolution that is preparing. At the end of the novel, Sir Stafford Nye marries the stranger from the airport with whom he has lived so many adventures.
text_b: Odgen Mears, appointed U.S. Ambassador to Saudi Arabia, returns home after traveling the world. He meets and then reluctantly welcomes Natasha, a Russian countess who enters the ship in Hong Kong as a stowaway to avoid being employed in prostitution and hides in the budding ambassador's closet, has no passport and is forced to stay in the cabin. He wants to get to the United States at all costs. As a plan to get her off the ship later, they decide to marry her off to Hudson, Odgen's valet. Hudson wants to take the opportunity to consummate the marriage, so that she, when they are about to reach the port of Honolulu and having spent the crossing avoiding him, jumps from deck and swims to dry land. Odgen's wife joins the cruise, and Odgen will have some explaining to do. A lawyer friend of Odgen's who helped prepare the marriage of convenience finds the Countess and informs her that the immigration department has accepted her as Hudson's wife. In the last scene, Odgen, who has left behind cruiser and wife, dances with the countess in a hotel cabaret.

anchor_text: The nobility of Big Spring, a small town in Colorado, coveted the land of a young farmer, Stephen Kernay. To seize them, they accuse Stephen of murder, put him on trial, and hang him. Young Vincent, Stephen's brother, returns eight years later to take his revenge.
text_a: The mayors of Big Spring, a small town in Colorado, are unable to prevent the death sentence of young farmer Stephen Kerney. Young Vincent, Vincent's brother, returns after eight years to exact his revenge.
text_b: History tells of conflicts and battles between ethnic groups in the streets of Paris. In the past, Vincent belonged to these ethnic groups and is a former petty criminal. Now a police inspector in the vice squad, he is tasked by divisional commissioner Rigault to infiltrate these groups and become a werewolf as he was in the past. With the help of the crossbow, a prostitute and drug addict, Vincent opposes Falco, a racist and partisan police officer in the strongest way.

anchor_text: Accompanied only by his son Nicholas, British diplomat Harrington Brande, after his wife leaves him, takes on a new challenge in Spain, hoping to advance his career. But the post there, the supposed new chance at life in a new country, soon turns out to be a great disappointment for him. His career is stagnating. His son Nicholas, however, sees it all as a kind of adventure and quickly befriends the new gardener Jose. As Nicholas spends more and more time with Jose, the father is hit hard by the subjectively felt loss of his son's affection. He forbids Nicholas from seeing the gardener. As tensions mount and another servant accuses Jose of stealing, all involved reveal their true characters.
text_a: Nicolas leads the quiet, idyllic life of a little boy like any other. His parents love him dearly, and he and his band of extraordinary friends have a lot of fun and do all sorts of silly things until one day he thinks he understands that his mother is pregnant. The anxiety sets in, and Nicholas already imagines the situation ahead: a younger brother will take his place, and his parents will no longer care for him.
text_b: Harrington Brande is an English diplomat who moved to the Spanish coast with his son Nicholas after the end of his marriage. Rigid and extremely protective of his son, the man cannot accept the strong bond of friendship that is created between the latter and the gardener José. Complicating matters will be the accusation made by the perfidious butler García for a crime that José did not actually commit. Eventually, the consul will exonerate the gardener, regaining the boy's affection.

anchor_text: Around the 1920s. When the people of the small coastal town of Littlehampton receive scandalous and obscene letters, suspicion immediately falls on the fiery Rose Gooding, who could lose custody of her daughter. A group of women set out to solve the mystery.
text_a: Based on a true story, the residents of Littlehampton (Sussex, UK), in the 1920s, begin receiving obscene letters from an unknown source, leading a group of women to find the source.[2]
text_b: It's set in the 1930s. Rose has had numerous sexual experiences and has been abused in the past. She's employed by the Hilyer family as a domestic helper. Rose tries to seduce Hilyer, but he resists her. His wife, a scientist and feminist, takes care of the young woman. She takes care of Rose when she gets sick.

anchor_text: Junior Bonner is a rodeo rider whose heyday is behind him. His last stint on a stubborn cop has left him with a few injuries. Bonner travels home to Arizona to participate in the American Independence Day parade and rodeo. Upon arriving home, he finds that his parents' house has been leveled. This was instigated by his younger brother Curly, a land speculator who wants to build new homes on the site. His father Ace, a scoundrel and womanizer, and his down-to-earth and ailing mother Elvira have become estranged. Ace's wish is to emigrate to Australia to work as a sheep shearer or in a gold mine. He's trying to borrow Junior's money. Junior bribes rodeo manager Buck Roan so he can ride the bull "Sunshine" one more time. In return, Buck will receive half of the prize money. Junior manages to last the required eight seconds on the bull. He goes into a travel agency and buys his father a first-class one-way ticket to Australia.
text_a: Twenty-five years ago, the son of a wealthy cattle rancher, Lavery (Charles Bickford), was kidnapped. Now comes Choya (Alan Ladd), a cowboy who accidentally, but intentionally in reality because he is a con man sent by T. Jefferson Leffingwell (Robert Keith) to take over the Lavery estate, shows his boss a tattoo that proves he is their missing son. Hoping that they have found their kidnapped son, now an adult, they welcome him with open arms and Choya finds in this family what he has never known: appreciation, affection, especially as he falls in love with their daughter (Mona Freeman). This greatly complicates the situation as he must manage his feelings towards Ruth to play the role of the brother while undergoing the pressures of Leffingwell's wrath. Disgusted and perhaps overwhelmed by the roles he must play in a cattle drive in El Paso, he reveals his true identity to Ruth. The girl is furious, and her parents are devastated. To redeem himself for having caused so much pain, to deserve the reciprocal feelings of the one he loves, he decides to bring the real son back to his family. It's not easy because the kidnapped child has become a thirty-year-old man, Tonio, who lives in Mexico with someone quite formidable who is not determined to let him go. Finally Choya crosses the Rio Grande...
text_b: Junior JR Bonner (Steve McQueen) is a slightly marginal rodeo rider. He is sometimes injured, as this time by an aggressive bull named Sunshine. He travels across the U.S. from pageant to pageant, earning a living on the highs and lows. He returns to Prescott, his hometown in Arizona, for the Independence Day parade and rodeo. When he arrives, he finds his family's home razed by the men of his younger brother Curly, a real estate developer and entrepreneur, in order to build a caravan park. His good-for-nothing father and womanizer, Ace (Robert Preston), and his down-to-earth mother, Elvira (Ida Lupino), who has been suffering for a long time, are separated. Ace dreams of emigrating to Australia to raise sheep and search for gold but he fails to get funding from Junior, is broke, and refuses to ask Curly. After knocking out his arrogant brother with a punch, Junior bribes rodeo owner Buck Roan (Ben Johnson) to let him ride Sunshine again, promising him half the prize money. Buck thinks he's crazy but Junior finally manages to win by spending eight seconds on the bull. With his $950 bonus from the rodeo, Junior walks into a travel agent's office and buys his father and his dog a one-way first-class ticket to Australia.

anchor_text: At the headquarters of a New York magazine, infighting ensued between the aging and bitter editor Amanda Farrow and her young assistants. As she leaves office to marry a widower, it is the mistress of publisher Mike Rice, the ambitious secretary Caroline Bender, who is appointed in her place. But the life of a housewife does not suit Amanda and she decides to resume her activities...
text_a: The film is divided into 9 independent episodes, each with a different story, but with an absolutely female protagonist who gives the name to the episode itself. Each episode is shot with a single shot sequence of varying duration of 10-15 minutes.
text_b: Three women, Caroline, Gregg and April, leave the province and arrive in New York where they find accommodation together and go to work at a publishing house run by two executives, a woman and a man: she, Amanda Farrow, is a severe and rigid woman who ventes her displeasure on her subordinates; he, Mr. Shalimar, is a mature young man who does not fail to harass employees but with little success. Soon the three women will be able to make a name for themselves in the workplace, but because of their turbulent romances, they will not be as fortunate in their love lives.

anchor_text: Bank robber John Thunderbolt Doherty (adapted as Canardeur, played by Clint Eastwood), befriends Biche Pied (Lightfoot in the original version, played by Jeff Bridges), a young adventurer, who inadvertently saves his life. Together, they decide to retrieve a half-million-dollar stash that Thunderbolt's accomplice hid in an old school. But this one was destroyed. Thunderbolt and Lightfoot, joined by former associates of Thunderbolt's, plot the robbery of another bank. It is here that we discover why Thunderbolt ( Thunderbolt ) is so nicknamed: he uses a 20-millimeter cannon from the army's surplus to demolish the armored door of the safes.
text_a: John Doherty (Clint Eastwood), nicknamed Thunderbolt (in English thunderbolt means lightning or lightning), is a retired robber, whose sharp wit and nerves of steel have made him a master of his profession. He earned his nickname Thunderbolt for habitually resorting to the use of a 20 mm automatic cannon to destroy the walls of the safes of the banks he robbed. However, he is about to return to criminal activity with a new partner: Lightfoot (English: Agile Foot, played on screen by Jeff Bridges), a lively young man whose energy and exuberance offer the veteran a new perspective on life. Their target: the seemingly impenetrable Bank of Montana. After forming an uneasy alliance with Thunderbolt's two old cronies (the tough Red Leary, played by George Kennedy, and the naïve Eddie Goody, played by Geoffrey Lewis), the four will plot an incredible plan that will test their strength... and friendship.
text_b: Rescued in a shootout, gunman Alex Longmire vows to turn his life around. Coming to town, the only job he finds is as a deputy for Sheriff Jade Murphy, an honest man forced to juggle small farmers and a local gentleman. Murphy also has a beautiful daughter, and for various reasons, Longmire decides to stay and make the most of his experience with firearms.

anchor_text: On the sea route back to Baghdad, accompanied by his fiancée, Princess Parisa, Sinbad stops at the island of Colossa. He takes the opportunity to rescue Sokurah, a magician, from the clutches of a huge cyclops, who nevertheless manages to recover the magic lamp stolen by the sorcerer. To force Sinbad to return to the island of Colossa, Sokurah miniaturizes the princess. Only the shell of a Roc bird's egg can restore Parisa to her normal size. But the bird in question only lives on the cursed island. With the help of a crew of patibulaire sailors, Sinbad sets sail to return, but the adventure has only just begun.
text_a: The vile sorcerer Pendragon, banished from England, seeks to usurp the throne of Cornwall by having the king abdicate and marry his beautiful daughter, Princess Elaine. He has her kidnapped by one of his servants, a huge giant, but Jack, a humble farmer, manages to kill him and thus rescues the beautiful captive. But Pendragon did not give up his dark designs.
text_b: The adventure recounts Sinbad's perilous journey to the mysterious island of the Colossus. His goal is to break the spell cast by an evil wizard on his beloved princess, making her tiny. Before he can return her to her normal size, Simbad will have to fight off a host of mythical monsters such as, for example, the man-eating Cyclops, a skeleton wielding a sword, a ferocious two-headed bird named Roc, and a fire-breathing dragon.

anchor_text: The American Michael Mason makes his living as a pickpocket in Paris. One night, he steals a young woman's purse, which contains a bomb. When it explodes, killing four people, Michael becomes the focus of the terrorist investigation. CIA agent Sean Briar, stationed in Paris, tracks him down, takes him to a secret prison, and interrogates him. Michael demonstrates his dexterity to Briar, also obtaining a knife that allows him to escape. With the help of Michael's testimony, Briar tracks down Zoe, the woman in whose pocket the bomb was. Zoe's gonna testify to Briar that her partner instigated her and planted the bomb. When they arrive at his apartment, they find him dead, and a police badge exposes him as an undercover investigator. In fact, a corrupt group of police officers were behind the bombing, which they followed up with a seditious Internet campaign using the perpetrators to provoke mass demonstrations. However, this general chaos does not serve terrorist purposes, but as a distraction from a break-in at the National Bank. With the help of Michael and Zoe, Briar can finally stop the thieves from escaping with their loot.
text_a: I'm going to Paris. In the days leading up to the anniversary of the storming of the Bastille, the paths of Sean Briar, a CIA agent stationed in Paris, and Michael Mason, a hardened and skilled pickpocket, cross after the latter unwittingly steals a bag containing a bomb, which then explodes in a Paris square, killing four people. The attack is claimed by a mysterious criminal organization, which promises further attacks. Thus begins an escalation of violence and unrest throughout Paris: in all this atmosphere of tension, Sean and Michael will discover that in reality the unrest caused is nothing more than a diversion to allow a corrupt team of French RAID agents to carry out the coup of the century: robbing the Bank of France.
text_b: After losing her family in a plane crash, Stephanie Patrick fell into depression, drugs, and prostitution. One day, a journalist, Keith Proctor, proves to him that his loved ones died in a terrorist attack, disguised as a crash by the government. His source is a former MI6 agent named Boyd. Their first lead is the bomb maker, a man named Reza. Determined to avenge her family's death, Stephanie acquires a gun and manages to track him down. Yet, unable to kill him, she lets him escape. When she returns to Proctor's apartment, she finds the apartment ransacked and the reporter's body. She locates Boyd in Scotland and travels there, but he refuses to help her. Nevertheless, he reveals to her the reasons for the plane's explosion. A terrorist, known as U-17, shot down the plane to kill liberal Muslim reformer Abdul Kaif. The other victims are part of a "collateral damage" disguised as an accident. With Stephanie having nothing to lose, Boyd agrees to train her and assigns her a new identity, Petra Reuter, to cover her future mission. A few months later, after undergoing intensive training, Stephanie Patrick traveled to Madrid to join ex-CIA agent Marc Serra. The latter asks him to assassinate everyone connected to the plane explosion. However, Stephanie finely plots her revenge, financed by Abdul Kaif's mother. Especially as she slowly discovers that Serra is playing a double game with her.

anchor_text: In the documentary, Erich von Däniken explores the question of whether extraterrestrial life has landed on Earth in the past and left traces behind. To do so, he takes the viewer on a journey to various ancient structures and stone carvings around the world. Exhibits include pyramids in Egypt, rock paintings in the Sahara, stone sculptures such as the Moai on Easter Island, Aztec and Maya structures in Central and South America, and the Nazca Lines. The tenor of his explanations is that many buildings of past times could not have been created without the help of "strangers", since even in today's time with the most modern technology they would only be achieved with enormous effort. Images of these strangers had been captured by the ancient high cultures in many drawings, statues or frescoes and have been preserved until today. Däniken concludes that many of the gods of ancient cultures were actually these same aliens who once came from the depths of the universe.
text_a: In the documentary, which claims to be scientifically demanding, Erik von Däniken explores the question of the existence of extraterrestrial life forms with which our world has had contact in the past, leaving some traces for posterity. The film takes the viewer on a journey to various ancient buildings and works of art around the world (among the most famous are the pyramids of Egypt, the stone paintings of the Sahara, the stone sculptures of Easter Island, the Aztec pyramids of Central and South America, the Nazca plain). According to von Däniken's observations, no man in the past, with the technologies of his time, could have made the various objects, artifacts or monuments proposed in the documentary on his own, and therefore these realizations were possible only thanks to "external" aids that obviously had more advanced technologies.
text_b: A documentary filmed in 24 countries that narrates, in eminently visual, aural and musical terms, the evolution of the Earth and of Humanity, at the same time pointing out the ways in which the human being has been related to his environment. The apparent fragility of human life is contrasted with the grandeur of his works, while emphasizing the unequal relationship between man and nature. Amidst these contrasts, humanity's spirituality emerges as the most important element that distinguishes it from other species. In small fragments it shows religions, tribes, entire cultures, which makes it interesting; although some argue that it is not really a documentary, since it does not meet mainly the description requirement that these films handle, it is really one.

anchor_text: Young aspiring author Sal Paradise meets life-hungry Dean Moriarty shortly after his father's death. The two become friends, and shortly after, they set off together on a road trip to the west. Always with the next stage in mind, they both savor the highs of life to the fullest and set off in search of freedom and true love. Intoxicated by mere existence, they enjoy the infinite expanse of the countryside and their lives with drugs, sex, jazz and daring thoughts. During their road trip, they make new acquaintances but also visit old friends. These include Dean's lovers Marylou and Camille, the philosopher Carlo Marx, Old Bull Lee and his wife Jane. While Sal distances himself from Dean over time and increasingly considers a settled life, Dean also prefers the wild life over the course of the plot. After the marriage and subsequent divorce from Marylou, his ex-wife is his constant companion on trips throughout the United States. This infuriates his new wife Camille, especially after their child is born and Dean refuses to give up his easy life.
text_a: After the death of his father, Sal Paradise (Sam Riley), an aspiring New York writer, meets Dean Moriarty (Garrett Hedlund), a charming ex-convict, married to the very free and seductive Marylou (Kristen Stewart). Sal and Dean lock in instantly. Determined not to be trapped in a limited life, they sever their respective ties and head out on the road with Marylou. Thirsty for freedom, the three young men set out in search of the world, of other encounters and of themselves.[4] They are the portrait of the beat generation living the here and now during the 1940s. His search for "it" results in an energetic and accelerated journey with ups and downs throughout the United States.[5] The characters are based on real-life people, Jack Kerouac explained: "Due to objections from my early editors, I couldn't use each person's real names".[6]
text_b: In this classic love story set against the southern Riviera sun, the two French students Bertrand Griot and Dominique Vallon are the main characters. They both experience their love in a youthful, carefree way. One day, Dominique Bertrand, a law student, meets Luc Ferrand, her charming uncle, a handsome and married gentleman whose experience and skill impress her. The girl and the much older gentleman begin a short-lived but nevertheless very intense romance, which questions and confounds the entire previous relationship network of all parties involved. For the wealthy Luc, however, this short-lived romance was little more than a summer fling, a temporary love affair. All the more disappointed is the still somewhat naive Dominique, when after a short time it's all over again. After that, it seems that there are only losers: Luc has to explain himself to his equally noble and indulgent wife Françoise, and Dominique tries to save the relationship with the rebellious Bertrand.

anchor_text: In the aftermath of his father's death, Sal Paradise, an apprentice New York writer, meets Dean Moriarty, a young ex-taulard with devastating charm, in love with the very free and very attractive Marylou. Between Sal and Dean, the deal is immediate and merger. Determined not to let themselves be locked into a life that is too narrow, the two friends break off their ties and take to the road with Marylou. Thirsty for freedom, the three young men set out to meet the world, others and themselves.
text_a: Camille Bliss, a prisoner in a French prison, is chosen by Stanislav, a young professor of sociology, for the edition of his book: "Women criminals". Through several interviews, the professor delves into Camille's history in order to analyze her way of thinking and acting. Thus, it is Camille herself who is telling us her adventures from the most "tender" childhood to the present moment.[3]
text_b: A journey through post-war America to the rhythm of jazz music, in the continuous search for a new way of life, pure and mad. Dean Moriarty and Sal Paradise, fraternal friends, embark on the longest journey across the United States from east to west. Without money, without purpose, they roam the streets living off what they can find, meeting the most extravagant and crazy people. Between love, friendship and resentment, this experience is surprising and moving, rediscovering the beauty of simplicity and lightheartedness. Just wild and pure life.

anchor_text: The setting of the film is a night asylum where several failed existences (a prostitute, a convicted scholar, a fallen samurai, an alcoholic actor, a thief and a craftsman with his dying wife) find shelter. It is the story of destitute, exploited and humiliated people who all yearn for a better life, but cannot find in themselves the strength to change their lives. At the center of the plot is a friendly Buddhist priest, who also finds shelter in the Night Asylum. He tries to help the characters find themselves and lead them to a better life. But ignoring their physical and mental dependencies, he fails and only causes more harm.
text_a: A young Japanese businessman is arrested while on a business trip in the Philippines, accused of drug dealing and possession of one kilogram of heroin (a crime that carries the death penalty). Kohei is imprisoned in a prison called "Paradise". Although innocent, the man finds himself thrust into the harsh reality of Philippine prisons where money and friendships rule, where the corruption of the guards is the key to carrying on his business outside the penitentiary. In a short time, Kohei will understand this reality by becoming a "manoeuvre" of the boss Yoshida, also Japanese and looking for someone to trust. The events in 'The Paradise' will be mixed with the ever-living hope of the young man to be released through his wife and his lawyer. Just as the man is increasingly convinced that he has no hope of returning to everyday life, a blackout will put him in a position to escape in the company of some prisoners and the boss. Once outside the man will be forced to face the reality of life, in which, as in the penitentiary, only money matters.
text_b: In an unsanitary part of Edo, an inn is run by Rokubei and his wife. A thief, a former actor, a scrapper, an ex-samurai... All these people, living in misery, have dreams and hopes. One day, a mysterious pilgrim arrives, and encourages the inhabitants of the inn by lying to them, making them believe in miracles. The thief Sutakichi falls in love with Okayo, the owner's sister, and wishes to leave with her. But the owner will only allow her to do so if he agrees to kill her husband, and she will kill Rokubei herself and blame the murder on Sutakichi. The only one who can exonerate him is the pilgrim, who nevertheless fled...

It is the story of destitute, exploited and humiliated people who all yearn for a better life, but cannot find in themselves the strength to change their lives. But ignoring their physical and mental dependencies, he fails and only causes more harm.
