Once outside the man will be forced to face the reality of life, in which, as in the penitentiary, only money matters.
text_b: At the bottom of a chasm that serves as a landfill is a miserable public dormitory run by a cynical old loan shark and his scheming wife. Inside are a dozen vagrants: a big-hearted prostitute, an alcoholic actor, a fallen nobleman, a thief and the unemployed of every stripe, who dream, each in their own way, of an unlikely escape from the misery of the life in which they are imprisoned. The passage into the pilgrim-philosopher's dormitory seems to bring some hope.

anchor_text: The film begins in Strasbourg in 1968 when an Italian engineer requests an audience at the headquarters of the Council of Europe. This person is Giorgio Rosa, the creator of a steel platform that he built in international waters half a kilometer beyond the Italian territorial waters of Rimini, the Island of the Rose. He wants to live by his rules by founding a sovereign state, independent of the Italian state. A diverse group of fellow adventurers assist him in his quest: a German deserter, a 19-year-old pregnant girl, a shipwrecked man, an old friend who all want to change the world.
text_a: The film tells in an idealized way the story of the Italian anarchist engineer Giorgio Rosa, who at the end of the 60s decides to build a platform of 400 square meters in the Adriatic Sea, where to establish his own country, free from any rule of the government. Helped by his wife Gabriella and his friends, he will achieve his dream and thus found the Republic of the Island of Roses. He quickly caught the attention of the Italian authorities, who ordered the prince of the anarchists to submit to the laws of the country.[5][2][7][8]
text_b: The film is set in post-war Rome. The focus is on the Italian Fernando (Nando) Mericoni, who calls himself Santi Bailor and has a penchant for everything that comes from the USA, the films (his big hero is John Wayne), the sports (he wears a baseball cap and is infatuated with Joe DiMaggio), the food (ketchup, popcorn, chewing gum), the language. He claims to be able to speak English, but he produces nothing but gibberish. He set up a water dispenser in his room, like the one he saw at the American Embassy. His whole life is a laughable parody of the actual American way of life. The big goal, of course, is to finally go to America. Inspired by Henry Hathaway's film Fourteen Hours (1951), he goes up on the highest wall of the Colosseum, presents himself there to a curious crowd and threatens to jump if he is not granted a visa by the US Embassy.

anchor_text: Max Cartwright is the daughter of Amanda Cartwright, an actress who died in a car accident while they were together and whose roles in horror films made her one of cinema's most famous Scream Queens. But one night, at a memorial party in his mother's honor, the theater catches fire and Max and his friends find themselves mysteriously trapped in his mother's most famous horror film: Camp Bloodbath, from 1986. Together, they will have to face the stereotypical characters of the film, but most of all face the psychopathic killer, while looking for a way to get out of the film alive.
text_a: Max Cartwright, a senior in high school, travels with his friends to a public screening of Camp Bloodbath, a horror film starring his now-deceased mother Amanda. During the screening, a fire breaks out in the room and, to save themselves, they are victims of a strange accident that catapults them into the film; Max then finds his mother and together with his friends, facing the dangers predicted by the plot of the film, including a serial killer wielding a machete, will have to find a way to return safely to the real world.
text_b: Max Hunter returns for business in Germany to the castle of his ancestors, accompanied by his wife Mary. One night Mary is awakened by screams, she notices the absence of her husband in the marital bed and searches in the castle the origin of the screams. She ends up in an annex of the castle, which turns into a macabre museum that exhibits instruments of torture. Approaching a Nuremberg virgin, she noticed that blood was flowing, collected in a bucket. She opens the statue, discovers a corpse and loses consciousness. Then she meets some strange people, a bearded valet, a self-righteous housekeeper, and a strange stranger who says he's interested in the gardens of German castles. As her husband leaves on business, she takes a sleeping pill to calm her anxieties, she rejects the pills and follows her husband through the castle, seeing him disappear through a secret door, she looks for him without finding him in a basement where she hides from the scarred when she sees a character arrive dressed as the ancient wax executioner of the museum...

anchor_text: Emmanuelle runs a secret facility in a castle where the latest computer technology can be used to create a virtual reality that allows customers to fulfill their most secret sexual desires. Her friend Sophie, who in the past had been raped and deprived of her virginity by the drunk Carlos in a refrigerator and who as a result no longer had any sexual experience, wants to help her overcome the dramatic experience. The aim is to test a new method of manipulating the participants' memories in a sustainable way. So another event from the past that the two friends had experienced together with Frantz is re-experienced virtually, with the only difference that this time Sophie sleeps with the boy and not Emmanuelle, which makes Sophie find joy in love again. Also in virtual reality, Sophie is given the chance to take revenge on Carlos, as well as engage with the real Frantz, who is also there from time to time. Sophie and Frantz don't know they're dealing with another real person yet, and they're enthusiastic about each other. When they later realize that their partners are real, Emmanuelle brings the two newly in love together in the real world.
text_a: As a renowned cover girl, Sophie dreams of a different existence than the one she leads with Philippe, a photographer. When she discovers that he is interested in the rich American Barbara, she becomes furious with jealousy until she thinks about the crime of passion, and avenges the infidelity by messing with boys who like to have a threesome with her: Claude and Alain. One morning, she steals Philip's gun and tries to shoot him and Barbara. Alain finds it difficult to calm her, but he offers to play her lover to make Philippe jealous. The scarcity of their accumulated resources only allows Sophie and Alain to get a bed in a hotel storage room. There, Sophie's only thought is to destroy Philippe's idyll. But because Alain lives with her and sleeps on a mattress on the floor, he falls in love with her. Alain decides to speed things up, and demands an attitude from Philippe under the threat of Sophie's rifle. The next morning, an avalanche will convince Sophie that Alain is the man of her life and Philippe that Barbara can very well console him over Sophie.
text_b: Emmanuelle, now a mature woman, runs an experimental center dedicated to the achievement of virtual pleasure. All you have to do is put on a special suit and glasses to enter into your erotic fantasies or to relive past experiences. It is precisely in this latter direction that Sophie, Emmanuelle's dear and old friend, decides to use these new instruments to relive an experience of her adolescence, when she was in an institution run by nuns and during a sexual approach with a peer she was discovered and punished by a nun. Thanks to the help of Emmanuelle's center, Sophie will be able to relive that moment virtually but this time she will not be interrupted by the nun and will be able to have the comprehensive miss, thus overcoming her sexual block.

anchor_text: The turbulent Julian, who had been terrorizing Auggie, has now left the school. One day, her French grandmother came to visit her. She told him about her childhood as a Jewish girl in Nazi-occupied France during World War II. It will be hidden in the family of a classmate.
text_a: The story begins in Hungary, when the world is still divided in two by the Iron Curtain. The parents of a newborn baby girl are forced to leave her in Hungary when they flee to the United States. Five years later, during which the child is raised in a foster home, the family is reunited. But as she grows up, the little American by adoption decides to find her roots by returning to Hungary.
text_b: After being expelled from school following the events in Wonder, Julian is forced to attend a new institution. One day he receives a visit from his grandmother Sara from Paris and the elderly lady tells him about her childhood as a Jewish girl in collaborationist France during World War II, hoping to teach her grandson the importance of kindness.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of the Texas War of Independence in 1835 and 1836. The focus is on the defense of the fortified Alamo Mission Station, commanded by Officer William Barret Travis, only 26 years old. After a vote, the leader of the militia (volunteers fighting for Texas), James Bowie, is elevated to their commander. Travis remains in command of the Texas soldiers. Davy Crockett, a well-known folk hero and politician, is also in the Fort with his men. The 200 or so men are hoping for reinforcements against the many soldiers of Mexican General Santa Anna, but only 32 volunteers from Gonzales join them. Travis is even releasing his soldiers now, whether they want to fight and die or surrender, but none of them are going. After weeks of siege, Santa Anna has the fortress stormed, though he learns that a 12-pounder cannon was to arrive a day later, which could destroy the fortress walls. About 200 Texans are killed, but far more Mexican soldiers die. On his deathbed, Bowie shoots two more attackers before being killed himself. In the time gained by the defense of the Alamo, Texas General Sam Houston manages to assemble an army of 800 men, with which he initially retreats further and further from Santa Anna's remaining 2,500 men until the moment is right for him. He attacks Santa Anna's now divided army with the battle cry "Remember the Alamo" and is able to defeat them in a short battle. This victory over Santa Anna later became known as the Battle of San Jacinto. In the end, Sam Houston forces Santa Anna to withdraw the Mexican army from Texas.
text_a: 1836, Lieutenant Colonel William Barret Travis is tasked with guarding the Alamo to resist the onslaught of the Mexican army led by the dictator of Mexico, General Antonio López de Santa Anna. Arriving on the scene, he finds it difficult to get discipline from his men, who prefer Jim Bowie's authority to his own. Next up is Davy Crockett, the legendary American hero. Leading a group of 200 Texans, Travis resisted for about two weeks, waiting in vain for reinforcements from Samuel Houston; the fort thus ends up falling, at the cost of numerous losses by the Mexican army, with an attack on the verge of madness (in fact it would have been enough to wait until the next morning to use artillery and break through the fortifications). Later, drawing Santa Anna's divided and weakened forces eastward, General Samuel Houston (head of the Texan militia and volunteers) retreated, defeating the Mexican army in 18 minutes, giving Texas the shape and size it has today. Santa Anna was captured, but released in exchange for a promise never to attack Texas again with his armies.
text_b: Sam Houston is tasked with organizing the Texas Army to attack the Mexican Army led by General (and dictator of Mexico) Antonio López de Santa Anna. Houston desperately needs time to enlist soldiers and train them; the only way to give him that time is to defend the town of San Antonio de Bexar to the end by entrenching himself inside the mission, known as the Alamo. At the head of the small group of soldiers is Colonel Travis. Supporting these are the volunteers of Jim Bowie, a famous soldier who became a wealthy landowner in the area. Adding to the ranks from Tennessee is David Crockett, a popular hunter and pioneer, who was also elected to Congress, with about thirty men. Travis knows perfectly well that there is no hope against Santa Anna, but also that the Mexicans will have to waste time to seize the Alamo; Jim Bowie hopes to succeed with guerrilla actions to slow down the march of the Mexicans without having to stay in the mission; Crockett tries to mediate between the two. With even the last hope of receiving reinforcements gone, Travis decides to resist in spite of everything, and is also followed by Bowie first and then Crockett. Two days of battle and the death of the three protagonists follow. At the end of the battle, none of the combatants survive, but Captain Dickinson's wife, daughter, and an African-American child are rescued. They are joined by Smitty, a young follower of Crockett who had been sent to warn Houston and who returns in time to see the end of the battle.

anchor_text: 1836, Lieutenant Colonel William Barret Travis is tasked with guarding the Alamo to resist the onslaught of the Mexican army led by the dictator of Mexico, General Antonio López de Santa Anna. Arriving on the scene, he finds it difficult to get discipline from his men, who prefer Jim Bowie's authority to his own. Next up is Davy Crockett, the legendary American hero. Leading a group of 200 Texans, Travis resisted for about two weeks, waiting in vain for reinforcements from Samuel Houston; the fort thus ends up falling, at the cost of numerous losses by the Mexican army, with an attack on the verge of madness (in fact it would have been enough to wait until the next morning to use artillery and break through the fortifications). Later, drawing Santa Anna's divided and weakened forces eastward, General Samuel Houston (head of the Texan militia and volunteers) retreated, defeating the Mexican army in 18 minutes, giving Texas the shape and size it has today. Santa Anna was captured, but released in exchange for a promise never to attack Texas again with his armies.
text_a: In the spring of 1836, some two hundred Texians faced an overwhelmingly superior force. These two hundred brave men defended for thirteen days the fort of El Álamo, in their secessionist zeal, against the Mexican army commanded by General Antonio López de Santa Anna (Emilio Echevarría), considerably more nourished. This dramatic struggle has put into history men who believed blindly in the future of Texas as an independent state from Mexico and who only surrendered to the siege of Santa Anna when all resistance was already impossible. The heroes were commanded by three men: the young and reckless Colonel William Travis (Patrick Wilson), the violent and passionate James Bowie (Jason Patric) and the living legend portrayed by David Crockett (Billy Bob Thornton). Together they went down in history as the secessionist rebel cry of General Sam Houston (Dennis Quaid).
text_b: An adjutant of the Confederate army, Lloyd, has brought into his possession a shipment of gold with which General Sibley intended to purchase arms and ammunition. Lloyd and his buddies flee to the Mexican border. Sibley appoints three death row inmates - gentleman thug Chattanooga Jim, preacher Tim Dooley, and horse thief Fidel Ramírez - who can buy their lives by capturing Lloyds and recovering the gold. The task of the three soldiers is complicated by the intervention of the Mexican bandits around their leader El Primero, in whose hands the precious cargo falls. However, in cooperation with Lloyd, the three allies succeed in recovering the gold. In the ensuing gunfight, Lloyd is fatally wounded. When they meet with General Sibley, each of the three will receive a horse and their freedom. The next day, the exchange of the gold and weapons that the Mexicans are supplying will take place. Chattanooga, Dooley and Ramirez steal the car with the gold and get away.

anchor_text: Moscow bank employee Dmitry Dmitritch Gurow was married early to an older woman with whom he is bored, and with whom he has three middle-aged children. He takes every opportunity to travel alone. He doesn't feel comfortable in the company of men, but he has one love affair after another, all of which don't last long. On the beach promenade of Yalta, he observes a young woman with a lace that fascinates him. It's Anna Sergeyevna from Diederitz, the lady with the dog. Anna, from St. Petersburg, married the provincial town of S. at the age of twenty. Her husband can't accompany her to the clinic because he's unable to work. Anna despises her husband, she calls him a lackey. After a week of walking together, they become a couple in love. When Anna's husband falls ill, she must return home immediately, and Gurow also goes back to Moscow to his family. At the turn of the year, he pretends to his wife a business trip to Petersburg, but goes to the city S. The hotel concierge knows the address of the house of Diederitz. During a visit to the theater, he meets the surprised Anna by chance. She promises to visit him in Moscow. Anna lies to her husband, postpones women's suffering, goes to Moscow and gets off at the Hotel Slavic Bazaar. There she meets her lover. Chekhov writes: And only now, when his head was gray,... did he really love for the first time in his life... and they found it incomprehensible that he was married to another woman and she to another man.
text_a: During a summer stay in Yalta the Muscovite Dmitry Gurov meets the young and pretty Anna Sergeevna, with whom he begins a relationship. Both married, Gurov and Anna melancholyly spend the last days of vacation before returning to their families. Returning to the city and resuming work, Gurov seems to have almost forgotten his mistress, also because of the distance that separates them (she in fact lives in Saratov). As time passes, however, the memory of Anna resurfaces more and more vividly until it turns into a burning desire to see her again. In Saratov, where he went, Gurov meets Anna at the theater and she promises him that she will soon come to Moscow to visit him. The two lovers begin to meet secretly in the city's hotel rooms, but it becomes increasingly difficult for Anna to find an excuse to leave her husband. In what may be their last meeting, Gurov and Anna, although aware of the difficulties they will encounter on their way, promise to continue to meet and love each other.
text_b: Dmitri Gurov is a banker from Moscow, married and father of three children. Unhappy in his marriage, he begins to repeatedly cheat on his wife and, in addition, considers women to be an inferior race. While vacationing in Yalta, Gurov observes a woman walking her dog along the coast and attempts to seduce her. She learns that she, Anna Sergeyevna, is also on vacation, away from her husband. An affair between the two begins: they go together everywhere, in particular, to the municipality of Oreanda. Finally, everyone leaves for home. Back to his daily life, Gurov hopes to soon forget Anna, but realizes he can't. So, taking advantage of a business trip to St. Petersburg, he decides to visit Anna's village and meet her again. Once there, he asks the doorman at the hotel where he is staying for directions to the young woman's home. He tries to go looking for her, but the impression that she has already forgotten him makes him give up. He goes back to the hotel and takes a long, depressing nap. In the afternoon, Gurov remembers that in the village they were going to give a performance in the theater. He decides to go, hoping to see Anna and her husband, which eventually happens. Taking advantage of the fact that Anna's husband had gone away to smoke a cigarette, Gurov confronts her and she flees from him, but then confesses to him that she has not stopped thinking about it; she begs him to leave under the promise that she would visit him in Moscow. To make the trip, Anna explains to her husband that she will leave to see a doctor, an excuse that he believes but at the same time does not. Meanwhile, Gurov realizes that, for the first time in his life, he has fallen in love, and ponders how to proceed. The story ends with him and Anna together, making plans for their future.

anchor_text: Dmitry Gurov is a banker from Moscow, is married and has three children. He views women as inferior to men, and his marriage is not a happy one. One day, during a holiday in Yalta, he meets Anna Sergeevna in a restaurant who is having lunch in the company of her dog. Soon Dmitrij and Anna begin a relationship that leads the two protagonists to an involvement as deep as it is unexpected. Unexpected from Dmitrij, a masculinist who has had many relationships and is not prone to sentimentality; unexpected from Anna, who is a shy woman with a 'collegial' air, whose upbringing and culture should have discouraged her from living an extramarital relationship. An intense and tender love blossoms between them, and they begin to nurture feelings for each other that they have never felt before. If the situation is not surprising for Anna, who is young and beautiful and has had no romantic experiences before the "unhappy marriage"; it is surprising for Dmitrij, who finds himself facing for the first time a story that causes him to live his days in function of Anna. Anna experiences the story with great emotional involvement and with strong guilt complexes, unlike Dmitrij, but both realize that they cannot stay away, and this pushes them to look for a solution, a way out, for better or for worse to their story. But time passes and the solution does not appear. All they know is that the end of their love is still a long way off and the complicated part has just begun.
text_a: June 1946: Stalin asks Russian émigrés living in the West to return to their homeland, promising them passports and amnesty. Among them, in France, are Dr. Alexei Golovin, his French wife Marie and their son; they decide to return to the Soviet Union. But upon returning to Odessa, Alexei discovers that his fellow repatriates have ended up in a gulag. He is saved because he is a doctor; his wife Marie is in shock and declared a spy by the Russian authorities. Alexei soon has to adapt to playing the role of a happy returnee; Maria cannot accept this situation and befriends a seventeen-year-old who falls in love with her. When he manages to flee the country, Maria is imprisoned in a gulag. After Stalin's death, Marie is released, but her husband barely recognizes her. But Alexei has a plan to get her back to France.
text_b: Dmitri Gurov is a banker from Moscow, married and father of three children. Unhappy in his marriage, he begins to repeatedly cheat on his wife and, in addition, considers women to be an inferior race. While vacationing in Yalta, Gurov observes a woman walking her dog along the coast and attempts to seduce her. She learns that she, Anna Sergeyevna, is also on vacation, away from her husband. An affair between the two begins: they go together everywhere, in particular, to the municipality of Oreanda. Finally, everyone leaves for home. Back to his daily life, Gurov hopes to soon forget Anna, but realizes he can't. So, taking advantage of a business trip to St. Petersburg, he decides to visit Anna's village and meet her again. Once there, he asks the doorman at the hotel where he is staying for directions to the young woman's home. He tries to go looking for her, but the impression that she has already forgotten him makes him give up. He goes back to the hotel and takes a long, depressing nap. In the afternoon, Gurov remembers that in the village they were going to give a performance in the theater. He decides to go, hoping to see Anna and her husband, which eventually happens. Taking advantage of the fact that Anna's husband had gone away to smoke a cigarette, Gurov confronts her and she flees from him, but then confesses to him that she has not stopped thinking about it; she begs him to leave under the promise that she would visit him in Moscow. To make the trip, Anna explains to her husband that she will leave to see a doctor, an excuse that he believes but at the same time does not. Meanwhile, Gurov realizes that, for the first time in his life, he has fallen in love, and ponders how to proceed. The story ends with him and Anna together, making plans for their future.

anchor_text: In Free Solo, free climber Alex Honnold, without rope or other technical aids, conquers the 1,000-meter-high granite cliff El Capitan in Yosemite National Park. The film also shows the preparations of the daring enterprise. The film's title refers to Free Solo, the act of undertaking a climbing route alone without any technical aids or safety equipment.
text_a: Peter Garret is a young sports photographer haunted by the sacrifice of his father's life on a climb to save his life and that of his sister Annie. Annie is now an expert climber and part of a climbing team on K2, one of the eight-thousanders, but a weather forecast error causes a serious accident in which she is involved. Peter decides to risk his life along with other volunteers to rescue her.
text_b: The film follows climber Alex Honnold's solo ascent of the El Capitan rock formation in Yosemite Valley, on a 3,000-foot (975 m) vertical wall, and describes the background of the preparation for this ascent. This is the Freerider track (quote 7c+, 33 lengths).

anchor_text: The film is set in 12th century Arab Andalusia. The scholar Averroes (Arabic Ibn Ruschd) is the chief judge and advisor of Caliph al-Mansur. With his family and his students, he lives a life of wisdom, love and tolerance. The Caliph, distressed by the growing influence of Christians and radical Islamic sects, increasingly distanced himself from him, and eventually had his writings burned and Ibn Rushd exiled. Ibn Rushd's students, meanwhile, copied his books and rescued them abroad.
text_a: The film is about Averroes, a 12th-century Andalusian physician and philosopher known for his commentary on Aristotle. The protagonist, Averroes, (Latinization of the Arabic name أبو الوليد محمد بن أحمد بن محمد بن رشد ʾAbū l-Walīd Muḥammad ibn ʾAḥmad ibn Muḥammad ibn Rušd) was born in the Islamic Cordoba in 1126 and died in Marrakech (Morocco) in 1198. He was an eminent Andalusian philosopher and physician, teacher of philosophy and Islamic laws, mathematics, astronomy and medicine.
text_b: The film tells the story of Islam from the revelation of the Koran to the prophet Muhammad to the expulsion from Mecca to the reconquest of the same by the Muslims. Regarding the figure of the prophet Muhammad, the veto had been placed by the high offices of the Islamic religion, which prohibited its representation in any form; they even prohibited hearing its voice and even seeing only its shadow, so the scenes in which Muhammad is present often take place in subjective, with the protagonists speaking facing the camera.

anchor_text: The film is set in 12th century Arab Andalusia. The scholar Averroes (Arabic Ibn Ruschd) is the chief judge and advisor of Caliph al-Mansur. With his family and his students, he lives a life of wisdom, love and tolerance. The Caliph, distressed by the growing influence of Christians and radical Islamic sects, increasingly distanced himself from him, and eventually had his writings burned and Ibn Rushd exiled. Ibn Rushd's students, meanwhile, copied his books and rescued them abroad.
text_a: The film tells the story of Islam from the revelation of the Koran to the prophet Muhammad to the expulsion from Mecca to the reconquest of the same by the Muslims. Regarding the figure of the prophet Muhammad, the veto had been placed by the high offices of the Islamic religion, which prohibited its representation in any form; they even prohibited hearing its voice and even seeing only its shadow, so the scenes in which Muhammad is present often take place in subjective, with the protagonists speaking facing the camera.
text_b: In Cordoba, in the Arab Andalusia of 1195, the caliph Al-Mansūr tries to soften the fury of the integralists who have among their targets the philosopher and scientist Muhammad ibn Rushd (1126-1198), known in the West as Averroes, a famous commentator of Aristotle and the greatest exponent of that Arab-Hispanic culture that flourished in Andalusia between the 7th and 12th centuries in peaceful coexistence with Christian and Jewish culture. He is the centerpiece of a film that, in telling the stories and characters of 800 years ago, casts a shadow over the problems, the fanaticism and the bloody conflicts in the Arab world today.

anchor_text: A couple and their daughter are driving to the Great Motte for a vacation. On the highway, they are attacked by three bikers who end up causing an accident. During the ensuing fight, the man is knocked unconscious, and when he wakes up, he finds that his wife and daughter are dead. Faced with the inability of the gendarmerie and the judiciary to quickly find the culprit, the man decides to find the three bikers and avenge the rape and death of his wife and daughter.
text_a: Merchant Paul Varlin, his wife Helena and their daughter Patti are heading to the Grande Motte where they are going to spend their holidays. On the road, they are attacked by three motorists who end up causing an accident. The man is knocked unconscious and, upon awakening, discovers that his wife and daughter have been raped and murdered. Faced with the inability of the gendarmerie and the judiciary to quickly find the culprit, the man decides to find the three cyclists and avenge the rape and death of his wife and daughter.[3]
text_b: Paul Verdier is a modest chocolate salesman who hasn't found his ideal wife. Despite the fact that he was always in a bad mood and was a troublemaker at home and in business, Juliette found Paul to be a loving, loyal, hardworking, and ideal husband. Then, during his tours, chance will put him in a rather ambiguous situation: a young stranger will ask him to pretend to be her husband so that she can safely return to her native farm. The shy representative, surprised at first, finally agrees. At first, everything goes wonderfully, a great feast is held, the neighbors and the priest rush in. The next day after Paul sleeps alone in the barn the deception is discovered, but forgiveness comes quickly. Paul will always remember her kindness and the tenderness of Mary.

anchor_text: Paul Crewe (Burt Reynolds) steals a Citroen SM and eludes the police. He was caught and subsequently sent to prison. There, he organized a prisoners' American football team to face off against the guards.
text_a: Paul "Wrecking" Crewe is a professional American football player, sentenced to 18 months in the Georgia State Prison. There he meets the guard Rudolph Hazen, who entrusts him with the difficult task of leading an American football team made up of the prison inmates.
text_b: During a robbery, Paul Valery was abandoned by his accomplices and was arrested. After his conviction, he manages to escape, and the Professor Krüger who financed the bombing comes to his aid. After undergoing facial surgery, Paul is back on his feet and goes after his former accomplices, Mark and Hans Fischer, to recover the money.

anchor_text: Harry and his accomplice Roy break into the suburban New York mansion of entrepreneur Victor Feldman, which they plan to rob. They kidnap the businessman's eight-year-old son, Gary, for which they promise a ransom. It turns out that Gary was neglected by his parents and is being treated in a psychiatric hospital. Harry and Gary travel across the U.S. together; they become friends. Gary's condition is improving.
text_a: Harry and Roy are two brilliant little burglars who thought they found $250,000 in Victor Feldman's safe, but how surprised they are when they discover there's nothing in it. They planned to kidnap his son, Gary, in exchange for a ransom. But nothing is going to go as planned. The child who is a hypochondriac will prove to be very embarrassing and very disturbing to both acolytes.
text_b: Widowed factory worker Harry lives with his aspiring writer son. Fired from the company where he works, Harry is forced to confront his estranged young son, who tries to set his parent up with a neighbor, on a daily basis. # He resumed The film was shot on location in Florida, Broward County, Fort Lauderdale.

text_a: Widowed factory worker Harry lives with his aspiring writer son. Fired from the company where he works, Harry is forced to confront his estranged young son, who tries to set his parent up with a neighbor, on a daily basis. # He resumed The film was shot on location in Florida, Broward County, Fort Lauderdale.
text_b: Harry's goody two-shoes (Danny Aiello) and his quirky sidekick Roy (Joe Pantoliano) raid a businessman's mansion to embezzle the safe, which apparently holds half a million dollars. When they discover that it is empty, they change their plans and decide to take Gary (Alex Zuckerman), a young man in need of affection.

anchor_text: On October 13, 1972, a Uruguayan university rugby team, also accompanied by some family members, flying on the Fokker Fairchild FH-227D crashes on the Andes mountain range, in an attempt to reach Chile. The survivors, after several days of waiting for rescue, decide, in order not to starve, to eat the bodies of their dead comrades. The extreme efforts made by the initial 33 survivors, then reduced to 16, will be rewarded by the heroic enterprise of Fernando Parrado and Roberto Canessa, who manage to cross the Andes and reach Chile, where they ask for help.
text_a: After a plane crash that killed 52 people, the airline promotes an investigation which finds that the total responsibility for the incident is to be attributed to the drunken state of the pilot, Captain Jack Savage, who had been seen drunk before boarding the cabin. However, these conclusions do not convince the company's director of flight operations, Sam Mac Bane, who was well aware of the merits of the dead pilot, with whom he had been a fellow soldier during the second world war. That's why he starts his own investigation, and that's how, thanks to the help of the only survivor, the hostess Martha Webster, he manages to exonerate his friend and discover the real causes of the accident: a coffee that spilled on electrical wires had given a false alarm to Savage, who was forced to shut down a working engine.
text_b: The film tells the true story of the Uruguayan rugby team Old Christians Club de Carrasco, whose members were students of Colegio Stella Maris de Montevideo, and their friends and relatives. All of them were involved in a plane crash when their plane crashed in the Andes Mountains on October 13, 1972. Subsequently, the team must contend with freezing temperatures, snow avalanches, dehydration, starvation, the death of loved ones for 72 days until their rescue.

anchor_text: Castle rock, 1960. Twelve-year-old Gordon LaChance and his three best friends, Chris Chambers, Vern Tessio and Teddy Duchamp, are enjoying the last days of summer vacation, and learn (thanks to a surprise conversation by Vern between his older brother and a friend of his) that the body of a missing boy is found along the railroad tracks several dozen miles from town. So they decide to go looking for the body, making their respective parents, with whom they all have problems of various kinds, believe that they are all camping at Vern's. Following the railway tracks, they embark on a two-day journey, marked by several incidents, some comical and others more dangerous, at the end of which they will leave childhood for good. On the second day, they finally find the boy's body, but that's when Ace Merrill's gang of teenagers, including Chris and Vern's older brothers, arrive on the scene. They plan to claim the discovery of the body but Chris chases them away by threatening them with the revolver he stole from his father. The four boys nevertheless understand that they cannot bring the body back and return to Castle Rock. Soon after, Gordon and Chris begin to lose sight of Vern and Teddy. Vern, Teddy and Chris, in spite of the latter's best efforts to survive, all meet a tragic fate by dying young, while Gordon becomes a renowned writer.
text_a: Around 1905, ten-year-old Marcel is to prepare for the examination for high school. As a reward, he'll be staying at the cottage in his beloved hills of Provence. At the suggestion of the mother, the family also spends the weekends in the cottage. Marcel meets the girl Isabelle and falls in love with her. But Isabelle soon sets herself up to rule over him. Lili and Paul are watching the two and informing the parents. After the bitter disappointment of his first love, he turns again to his friend Lili. After a former student who is now a sewer guard illegally gives his father a key to cut their way to their vacation home and they are caught by a supervisor, his father is put in a precarious position. The mother collapses. But the canal keeper can solve the situation with two friends. After five years, the childhood idyll ends with the death of the mother. His childhood friend Lili is killed by a bullet in the war in 1917. His brother Paul, who later worked as a shepherd in the hills, died at the age of 30. When Marcel grows up and buys a castle for filming, he discovers that it is the castle that played an important role in his childhood. This is where they were caught by the supervisor.
text_b: The story takes place during the summer of 1960 in the fictional town of Castle Rock, Maine. After a boy from Chamberlain, Maine named Ray Brower goes missing and is presumed dead, Gordie Lachance and his three friends, Chris Chambers, Teddy Duchamp and Vern Tessio set out to find the body. During the course of their journey, the boys, who all come from dysfunctional families, have to face some of the harsh truths of growing up in a small industrial town that doesn't seem to offer much in the way of a future. Compared to previous works by Stephen King, The Body's narrative is complicated, as it is told in the first-person point of view of the now forty-something novelist Gordon Lachance. Most of the story is a straightforward retrospective of what happened, but commentaries, or entire chapters that relate to the present moment, are interspersed throughout the story. Although it is only at the age of 12 of the characters, the important stage of the story, Gordie's favorite pastime is writing and telling stories. Three times during the narrative, Gordie tells stories to his friends, and two stories are presented in the text as short stories by Gordon Lachance, complete with the attribution of the magazines in which they were published.[1]

anchor_text: Sixteen-year-old Franky Winter and his best friend Ballas Kohl attend the same high school and are both members of the school's swim team. Franky's with Priscilla, the prettiest girl in school. However, he is reluctant to have sex with her, partly because he notices that he is not sexually attracted to her, which the nice boy does not want to tell her. The two friends spend their days cycling around the city and bragging to their girlfriends. Ballas and his girlfriend Jess have had sex before. Priscilla's trying to move things along, and she wants to give Franky his first time for his 17th birthday, but it doesn't work out that way. On the night of Frankie's birthday, he has drunken oral sex in the dark with his best friend. It doesn't take long for Ballas to claim that Franky took advantage of him and seduced him for sex. The lie is spreading like wildfire at the school, and with it, rumors about Frankie's sexual orientation. He is bullied and beaten by members of his swim team. Franky's trying to get back in touch with his father, Ray. He had left his mother, Carly, for a man. While this seems fine to her, Franky had no contact with his father despite his mother's pleas to speak with him. Another important confidante will be Natasha, Bala's sister, whose sexual orientation is also rumored. After Franky learns that Natasha has been abused, which is why she can't have sex with him for the time being, he responds with understanding. Natasha's father, Nic, reminds Franky to treat his daughter with extreme caution because of what happened. Franky finds out that Ballas initiated things and his first sexual experience wasn't anything real. All the years of their close friendship suddenly seem to have been erased.
text_a: Franky Winter (Josh Wiggins) is about to celebrate his birthday at a special time for his family: his father Ray (Kyle MacLachlan) has started a new life with another man and does not forgive him for abandoning his mother Carly (Maria Bello), so he keeps him at a distance despite the parent's willingness to be present in his son's life. During the party with friends he tries to get closer to his fiancée Priscilla (Hailey Kittle), taking the opportunity of the absence of parents to lose his virginity, which his best friend Ballas Kohl (Darrenn Mann) put a lot of pressure on him. Franky is unsuccessful due to the sudden return of his mother, however, he decides to host Ballas at his home, as drunk as he is. At night, Ballas tries to perform oral sex on Franky, but in fear he runs away and for fear that his relationship with Jess (Kiandra Madeira) may end, he begins to circulate bad language about his friend at school. Franky finds himself fighting his friend's homophobia, starting to meet new friends including Mouse (Niamh Wilson) a girl who would like to change sex and Natasha (Taylor Hickson), Ballas' sister, and at the same time getting closer to his father.
text_b: Civil engineer Frank, married and father of two, encounters Molly Gilmore, who is married to a doctor, in a bookstore during a Christmas shopping trip. They accidentally swap their bags, and on Christmas Eve, when both families are unwrapping their gifts, they are surprised by the swapped books. After the holidays, Molly and Frank meet again by chance, as they use the same train connection to work. They immediately get along so well that they arrange their daily routine so that they meet more often. They may be strongly attracted to each other, but their moral compass forbids them from entering into a relationship. Molly's been going back and forth, reaching out to her supervisor, who happens to be her best friend. But she's always having problems with men, and she can't help Molly. Since no one dares to take the step of breaking away from their partnership, they eventually lose sight of each other again when Frank accepts the offer to go to Houston for six months to work. Their feelings for each other meanwhile have led to their partnerships becoming unsalvageable and to their separation from their spouses. Next Christmas, Frank will be back to sell his house. On a whim, he goes back to the bookstore where he met Molly a year ago, and it's a reunion. Unable to talk about their breakups, they almost miss each other again, until they meet again on the train and eventually find each other.

anchor_text: Ramira (Alexandra Jiménez) works in the house of Laura (Silvia Abril) as a housekeeper and babysitter of her children: Simeón and Ofendia (Óscar Lara and Laia Alda). Unfortunately, Ramira kills the boy because he saw the sun despite suffering from photodermatitis. The woman tries to cover up her crime and lies to her mother about her son's death.
text_a: Laura returns from Buenos Aires with her two children, but without her husband, to her native village in Spain, for the wedding of her sister Ana. There she meets all her family and Paco, a winemaker and former lover. But the party is cut short when Laura's teenage daughter, Irene, disappears during the wedding night and a ransom demand is made, shattering the family's unity and bringing up old grievances.
text_b: Ramira is in charge of taking care of Laura's children, Simeon and Ofendia. She accidentally murders Simeon, a photophobic child, by letting him out into the sunlight, so Ramira decides to lie about Simeon's disappearance. See also: List of Spanish film characters

anchor_text: In Los Angeles, a series of robberies are committed by two seemingly invincible outlaws. Mortis and Bigelow, two detectives assigned to the case, discover that the robbers they managed to shoot had already been dead for a week. After investigating the city's tripods, they discover a machine that revives the dead for a period of twelve hours. # Characteristics of the zombies Zombies are the dead brought back to life by a machine and retain the characteristics of the living (they talk, are intelligent, are aware of what they have become, etc.). However, their life-cycle is short; they decompose very quickly.
text_a: In Los Angeles, a series of robberies are being committed by criminals who seem invincible. Mortis and Bigelow, the two detectives in charge of the case, discover that one of the thieves has managed to kill someone who was supposed to have died a week earlier. After an investigation of the town's gambling houses, they discover a machine that resurrects the dead for a 12-hour period.
text_b: The evil Dr. Herbert West, with his colleague Dan Cain, after the disasters of their experiments work in a hospital in Peru, during the 1991 dictatorship. Using cadavers for their experiments, they discover a substance capable of bringing not only the dead back to life, but even the individual parts of a corpse. Back in Massachusetts, the two attempt to revive Cain's dead fiancée, whose heart has been preserved.

anchor_text: A gang of jewel thieves is terrorizing Beverly Hills. L.A.P.D.'s top detectives, Mortis and Bigelow, are assigned to the case. They quickly realize that the robbers are already dead. The two detectives follow a lead to a pharmaceutical company and discover that experiments are being conducted on the dead.
text_a: In Los Angeles, a series of robberies are being committed by criminals who seem invincible. Mortis and Bigelow, the two detectives in charge of the case, discover that one of the thieves has managed to kill someone who was supposed to have died a week earlier. After an investigation of the town's gambling houses, they discover a machine that resurrects the dead for a 12-hour period.
text_b: The Dr. Herbert West, with his colleague Dan Cain, after the disasters of their previous experiments at Arkham, go to work at a hospital in Peru, during the civil war in 1991. Using corpses for their experiments, they discover a substance capable of giving life, even to the individual dead parts of a corpse. Back in Massachusetts, the two attempt to bring Cain's dead bride back to life.

anchor_text: In 18th-century Spain, the cruel Marquis Siniestro humiliates a beggar at his wedding feast and has him thrown in jail. He was joined there years later by a deaf-mute servant girl, who wrongly rejected the Marquis' advances. Mad from captivity, man overpowers the poor and rapes. The young invalid one day manages to escape and dies giving birth to a son, Leon, who is adopted by an old professor, Alfredo Carido. But when the boy grows up, he turns into a werewolf and when the moon is full, he kills sheep and drinks their blood.
text_a: In 18th-century Spain, a deaf-mute young woman, the daughter of the jailer of a castle, is raped by an unknown prisoner. After wandering through the woods, she is picked up by Don Alfredo Carido, a professor who lives alone with his housekeeper Teresa. Taking care of the woman, Teresa realizes that she is pregnant, and that the baby will be born around Christmas. The child is actually born on December 25 and the mother dies in childbirth. He is baptized as Leon, and on the day of his baptism strange atmospheric phenomena occur around the house. Leon grows up with a good education, raised by Teresa and the professor, but he is marked by a terrible curse: when there is a full moon in the sky he turns into a horrible bloodthirsty creature.
text_b: The story begins with an antecedent: a couple with a beloved sick son in need of a bone marrow transplant decide to give birth to a third child, Henri, hoping that the latter will be compatible for organ donation. But even the newborn turns out to be incompatible with his brother who dies at only 7 years old. With the birth of the fourth child gradually the mourning for the loss of the first child seems to be reworked. Years later, the mother learns that she has been affected by the same illness as her son and searches for a donor among her now-adult children, all of whom have elaborate personal histories. The donor could be Henri himself who, however, having been removed from the family at the behest of his sister Elizabeth, unexpectedly returns to spend Christmas with the family in the city of origin, Roubaix, where he will have to decide whether to make himself available for a transplant that could save (but also kill) his mother.

anchor_text: The film depicts the fate of some of the last Berlin Jews, who are sent by rail to Auschwitz in April 1943 from track 17 of the Berlin-Grunewald station. The cynical occasion is Hitler's birthday, to whom they want to present a "Jew-free Berlin" as a gift. The journey will take six days, water and food are scarce, the situation on the train is unbearable. Some of the people crammed into cattle cars are trying to break out of the car. Among them are the Neumann couple, a young woman, Ruth Zilbermann and her boyfriend Albert Rosen. The Neumann's twelve-month-old son dies from the strains of transportation. In the course of the film, several other characters, previously introduced to the viewer, die in a dramatically staged way. In the end, only that Ruth Zilbermann manages to escape Auschwitz together with the Neumann's daughter at the last train station. Henry Neumann and his wife are separated from each other when unloading from the wagon in the Auschwitz concentration camp, Jakob Noschik is immediately shot. The last scene shows the escaped daughter of the Neumanns praying, looking up at the sky.
text_a: In August 1943, Gestapo officers conducted a final round of searches in Berlin to arrest the last remaining Jews. Among those arrested were famed boxer Henry Neumann, his wife Lea and daughter Nina, a young woman named Ruth Zilbermann, singer Jakob Noschik, Gabrielle Hellman, and Albert Rosen. A six-day journey by cattle train will take them to Auschwitz, where death awaits them. Conditions inside the train are horrendous, with many passengers dying en route. The Neumans attempt to escape the train by drilling a hole in the carriage, but on the day scheduled for their escape, only Nina and Ruth manage to escape, Lea being too scared to do so. The next day, the train arrives at its destination, and Noschik is shot dead on the spot. The Neumanns are separated from the rest of the passengers. During the night, Nina, who was recovered by resistance fighters, sings a prayer in the forest.
text_b: The film is set in 1933 in a German university town, Munich. Professor Viktor Roth is celebrating his birthday. The celebration is interrupted by the news that Adolf Hitler has seized power in Germany. Roth is Jewish and knows what the Nazi takeover could mean for his family. However, his step-sons Erich and Otto von Rohn are enthusiastic about the political change. Fritz Marberg, the lover of Roth's daughter Freya, also shares their enthusiasm. Only Martin Breitner, who is in love with Freya, shares the opinion of Viktor Roth. The city is changing fast. The Nazis exert violence and pressure on their opponents. Erich and Otto von Rohn leave the house of Professor Roth. Freya breaks the connection with Fritz Marberg and finds in Martin Breitner the partner for life. However, the young couple is soon separated again when Martin has to flee Germany because he helped a friend to leave Germany. Professor Roth loses his teaching position and ends up in a concentration camp. He's going to die there. Freya and her mother flee to Austria, where Martin Breitner awaits them. However, when Freya is detained at the border and not allowed to leave, Martin returns to Germany and tries to smuggle her across a mountain pass to Austria. On their perilous journey, they are tracked down by a group of Nazis just before the border. The group is headed by Fritz Marberg. Both of them, however, manage to escape across the border, with Freya being wounded in a gunfight and dying shortly afterwards.
