The next day, the train arrives at its destination, and Noschik is shot dead on the spot. The Neumanns are separated from the rest of the passengers. During the night, Nina, who was recovered by resistance fighters, sings a prayer in the forest.
text_a: The film is set in 1933 in a German university town, Munich. Professor Viktor Roth is celebrating his birthday. The celebration is interrupted by the news that Adolf Hitler has seized power in Germany. Roth is Jewish and knows what the Nazi takeover could mean for his family. However, his step-sons Erich and Otto von Rohn are enthusiastic about the political change. Fritz Marberg, the lover of Roth's daughter Freya, also shares their enthusiasm. Only Martin Breitner, who is in love with Freya, shares the opinion of Viktor Roth. The city is changing fast. The Nazis exert violence and pressure on their opponents. Erich and Otto von Rohn leave the house of Professor Roth. Freya breaks the connection with Fritz Marberg and finds in Martin Breitner the partner for life. However, the young couple is soon separated again when Martin has to flee Germany because he helped a friend to leave Germany. Professor Roth loses his teaching position and ends up in a concentration camp. He's going to die there. Freya and her mother flee to Austria, where Martin Breitner awaits them. However, when Freya is detained at the border and not allowed to leave, Martin returns to Germany and tries to smuggle her across a mountain pass to Austria. On their perilous journey, they are tracked down by a group of Nazis just before the border. The group is headed by Fritz Marberg. Both of them, however, manage to escape across the border, with Freya being wounded in a gunfight and dying shortly afterwards.
text_b: The Nazis want to exterminate the Jews of Berlin once and for all. More than 70,000 have already been deported from the city. In April 1943 a train leaves from Grunewald station with 688 Jews bound for Auschwitz. On a journey that will last six days in the midst of a struggle against heat, hunger and thirst. In their desperation, leaders such as Henry (Gedeon Burkhard), Albert (Roman Roth), Lea (Lale Yavas) and Ruth (Sibel Kekilli) will rise up in search of an effective escape, but time is running out and Auschwitz is getting closer and closer.

anchor_text: Captain Leblanc, an officer of the Foreign Legion, was put in charge of a group whose objective was to capture Ben Bled, an FLN leader. The first part of the operation was successful, but the return proved all the more difficult as the political situation changed due to the peace talks.
text_a: The captain of the French Foreign Legion, Leblanc, leads a squad of Legion paratroopers to capture an Algerian FLN guerrilla leader. On their way to the ward, they're accompanied by the prostitute Nora and an Arab boy. Their mission is crowned with success, but the helicopter that was supposed to bring the unit back is shot down, so the paratroopers have to fight their way back to the French lines.
text_b: War is the daily life of Pierre Raspeguy, a lieutenant colonel in the French army. At the head of a regiment of colonial paratroopers, he was tasked with finding the leader of the Algerian rebellion, a former officer of his team during the Battle of Dien Bien Phu.

anchor_text: An eruption is occurring on Mount Paektu. Scientists and military personnel from North and South Korea are working together to prevent this catastrophe, as more explosions may occur on the Korean Peninsula...
text_a: A Korean policeman, a member of the Imperial Japanese police force, must prevent a Korean independence movement, supported by Western elements and opposed to the country's modernization under the auspices of Japan, from committing a series of deadly attacks in Seoul.
text_b: Mount Paektu, an active volcano on the border between China and North Korea, suddenly erupts, causing strong earthquakes throughout the Korean peninsula. Relying on a theory developed by Professor Kang Bong-rae to prevent further disasters, Jeon Yoo-kyung organizes a special forces team to go to the volcano, led by Jo In-chang.

anchor_text: After a car accident, Victor, a young architect, wakes up in COMA, a dystopian, fragmented and unstable world in which the memories, dreams and nightmares of people in a coma are connected by a mysterious network of neurons. Having lost most of their own memories, Victor and his companions will have to develop new skills to survive and adapt to the chaos around them. In these incredible urban visions, where buildings, objects and landscapes defy the laws of physics, danger can arise at any moment. Pursued by the Reapers, wandering creatures who haunt the imprisoned spirits, they must discover who is manipulating these human psychoses, in order to awaken and find a way to their deliverance.
text_a: A talented architect suffers a terrible and mysterious accident. When he wakes up, he finds that the world is only partly like the real world. This world is based on the memory of the reality of people who are in a deep coma. The coma space is the same as human memory: unstable, fragmented and chaotic. This is a collection of memories, where the laws of physics are broken and rivers and glaciers and cities can fit into the same room. The main character will discover what laws exist in the coma, fighting for his life, to find love, as well as access to the real world.
text_b: Five men wake up in a dilapidated warehouse where they're held captive, battered and with no memory, none of them remembering how they got there, and most importantly, no memory of who they are. Warned that they are victims of a kidnapping for extortion, the five men try to remember the events and find a way to return to freedom. Between resentment and suspicion, the five men wage a psychological war to uncover the truth, because the truth is their only weapon for survival.

anchor_text: New York City police officer Johnny Blake is fed up with the city's businesses being taken over by gangsters. The newly appointed police chief, who wants to drain the criminal swamp that has covered all areas of urban life with the help of the public and the newspapers, (apparently) dismisses Blake his best man so that he can infiltrate the criminal organization of Al Kruger, who likes to take his old opponent with him. His local rival Fenner, however, does not trust him. As the police use Blake's information to bring down the syndicate more and more, Blake makes sure that Kruger doesn't do anything about it. When Fenner then murders Kruger, Blake manages to take control, which finally gives him access to the real bosses in the background. They include a bank manager, a corrupt politician, and another well-respected businessman. In a shootout between Fenner and Blake, the two hit each other, Fenner dies instantly, Blake still picks himself up, and finally succumbs to his gunshot wound at the end of the film after the arrest of the underworld boss.
text_a: When New York anti-crime crusader Ward Bryant is killed, suspicion immediately falls on the racket's boss Al Kruger. The murder prompts a grand jury investigation into criminal organizations like Kruger's. The investigation is being led by Captain Dan McLaren, a senior officer and friend of former police detective Johnny Blake now on patrol in the Bronx. At one time, Kruger invited Johnny to join his organization, but he chose to remain honest. Johnny expects to join McLaren's investigation, but the captain fires him for no apparent reason. His old friend Lee Morgan offers him a position in the lottery she runs, but he gratefully declines, preferring to accept Kruger's old offer. Under orders from his undercover partners, Kruger wants Johnny to eliminate weaknesses in the organization. Johnny's firing, however, is a setup, as the detective is working undercover for the police in order to find out who is behind the racket run by Kruger. "Bugs" Fenner, a Kruger man who killed Bryant on his own initiative, doesn't trust Johnny, and his suspicions are amplified by the police's continued victories in racketeering. When the other criminals also accuse Johnny of being an informant, he suggests starting a new racket based on Lee's lottery, achieving great success. Unbeknownst to Fenner, Lee believes Johnny betrayed her. After Fenner kills Kruger, Johnny takes over and meets the bosses, but Fenner learns he's working for the police. By deception, the gangster gets Lee to reveal Johnny's hiding place, and though she tries to warn her friend, Fenner and Johnny shoot each other. Fenner dies and Johnny is badly wounded, but he manages to frame the bosses before he dies himself.
text_b: Police detective Harry Collins is hunting down criminals in Phoenix. In his spare time, he likes to gamble, which is why he owes the Chicago bookie $32,000. Chicago proposes that Collins forgo the debt if the cop commits a murder on Joey Schneider for him. A friend of the cop, Mike Henshaw, offers to kill Chicago. Colleagues Collins, Henshaw, Fred Shuster and James Nutter decide to rob the money from the gangster Louie. After that, Collins wants to start a new life together with the bartender Leila, who is raising her daughter Veronica alone. There's tension between Collins and his accomplice during the execution. Shuster's wife, Katie, learns of the plan and reveals it to Clyde Webber, the chief of the four police officers. Collins is shot; he later catches Katie in bed with Clyde and calls the police there. Finally, already bleeding heavily, he meets Chicago at a diner in the desert and pays him back his debt. This one says they're even, but mentions that he slit Joey Schneider's throat anyway. Then Collins shoots him and his bodyguards and pays the landlord. Shortly after leaving the diner, he collapses at the wheel of his car.

anchor_text: In Chicago, Detective John Prudhomme, haunted by the accidental death of his son a year earlier, is sent with his partner Andrew Hollinsworth to the scene of a savage crime. The victim has had one arm amputated and has Roman numerals engraved on his body. Prudhomme immediately favors the serial killer lead. The following days proved him right, as other bodies were discovered, all mutilated and marked with Roman numerals. Hoping to put the serial killer out of harm's way as quickly as possible, Captain Whippley asks the two detectives to agree to collaborate with FBI profiler Agent Wingate.
text_a: In Chicago, Detective John Prudhomme investigates a gruesome murder in which the victim's arm has been amputated and his blood has been used to draw Roman numerals on the body and the phrase "He's coming" has been written on a mirror. When other crimes with the same characteristics follow the first, Prudhomme begins to intuit that everything is the work of a serial killer, whose intent is to reconstruct the body of Christ with the anatomical parts taken from the victims.
text_b: John Wayne Cleaver is a sociopathic young man with a strong passion for serial killers. His mother and aunt run a morgue, and so he has always had a direct relationship with death: this helps him to keep the "monster" in him in check, who can be satisfied with the corpses with which he comes into contact in this way, but to further curb his dark side he imposes strict rules on himself and consults a psychologist. When a serial killer begins to threaten his town, John begins to use his knowledge of the subject and his sociopathy to get into the killer's mind and try to stop him, however, this puts a strain on the defenses he has created to stop the monster in him. Also, as he investigates, John begins to realize how the serial killer may actually be a supernatural creature hiding behind the identity of someone very close to him.

anchor_text: In Chicago, Detective John Prudhomme, haunted by the accidental death of his son a year earlier, is sent with his partner Andrew Hollinsworth to the scene of a savage crime. The victim has had one arm amputated and has Roman numerals engraved on his body. Prudhomme immediately favors the serial killer lead. The following days proved him right, as other bodies were discovered, all mutilated and marked with Roman numerals. Hoping to put the serial killer out of harm's way as quickly as possible, Captain Whippley asks the two detectives to agree to collaborate with FBI profiler Agent Wingate.
text_a: John Prudhomme is a married detective whose partner is Andrew Hollinsworth. John is haunted by the death of his daughter a year ago, which has led him to distance himself from the church. One day he is assigned to investigate a murder in the victim's home. Once there, two details catch his attention: the man's dismembered arm appears, and a message written in blood on the window reads: "He will come". After two more murders in which the victims are missing body parts, he realizes that he is facing a serial killer who uses such parts to reconstruct the body of Jesus Christ before the arrival of Resurrection Sunday. That's why he's also been assisted ever since by federal agent Wingate. However the serial killer knows him and sees him as a threat to his endeavor after he almost manages to catch him during one of the murders. Thus a duel to the death, in which he has the advantage, begins.
text_b: John Wayne Cleaver is a sociopathic young man with a strong passion for serial killers. His mother and aunt run a morgue, and so he has always had a direct relationship with death: this helps him to keep the "monster" in him in check, who can be satisfied with the corpses with which he comes into contact in this way, but to further curb his dark side he imposes strict rules on himself and consults a psychologist. When a serial killer begins to threaten his town, John begins to use his knowledge of the subject and his sociopathy to get into the killer's mind and try to stop him, however, this puts a strain on the defenses he has created to stop the monster in him. Also, as he investigates, John begins to realize how the serial killer may actually be a supernatural creature hiding behind the identity of someone very close to him.

anchor_text: Ann Brooks is married to the much older but very wealthy Schyler Brooks. One day, she is lured into a compromising situation and blackmailed by her ex-husband Maurice, under the pretext that he would otherwise commit suicide, after Maurice reveals to her that they are not legally divorced. Ann flees to Havana under her birth name to escape the scandal, and on board the ship meets Neil Davis, a detective sent by her husband as a lookout after Ann. They both fall in love, and after Ann's first husband dies and her current husband agrees to a divorce, Neil and Ann can enjoy a future together.
text_a: Michael Schuyler is allegedly being blackmailed by a dancer who has in her possession incriminating letters about her affair with him. The press is all over Michael, who comes from a good family. Michael can pay to silence Binji Baker of the Tribune, but Stew Smith is incorruptible. Smith now gets in trouble with the young man's family. To add insult to injury, he also fell in love with Michael's sister, Anne. His colleague Gallagher won't give him a chance. And yet Anne and the rude Smith are married after only a short time. Smith also soon moves into the stately Schuyler home, with his wife trying to make him a complete gentleman. Smith enjoys the good life first. Yet, he cannot refrain from making cynical remarks that continually embarrass society. Smith is comforted by Gallagher, realizes that she is the one for him, and is willing to agree to divorce Anne.
text_b: Anne Brooks is blackmailed by her old dance partner Maurice; they married when she was young but later broke up. Anne then married the much older millionaire Schuyler Brooks just to hurt Maurice's feelings and bring him back to her. Anne reveals her secret to Schuyler's sister Portia, who devises a plan to get Maurice to leave the country. Once out of the country, Portia will use her influence to block Maurice's return. Anne's request for a vacation alone in Cuba sparks Schuyler's already searing jealousy. So he hires Detective Neil Davis to follow her and prove whether or not he's loyal to her. Neil fails to seduce Anne, then realizes he's falling in love with her.

anchor_text: A policeman, Pharaon De Winter, is investigating the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl in the countryside of northern France. The investigation is overlaid with the difficult relationship with the neighbor, Domino, engaged to his friend Joseph and with whom Pharaon is secretly in love. Ending enigmatic and surprising.
text_a: In late 1938, Francis Phelan returned to Albany. He had left his family twenty-two years earlier after accidentally causing the death of his youngest child. Since then, he's been hanging out with other tramps and alcoholics. Shortly after arriving, he meets his old friend Rudy, who has just been released from the hospital and is not expected to live much longer. Then he goes in search of Helen, a former singer, also homeless, who has been his companion and support in recent years.
text_b: In a town in northern France, Pharaon de Winter is a police lieutenant prone to depression, who lost his wife and baby in an accident three years earlier. He's been living alone with his mother ever since. Her neighbor, Domino, is a girl with a boyfriend, a bus driver named Jose. Pharaon likes Domino, whom she often invites along with her boyfriend to spend the evening together. The plot unfolds when he must investigate the murder and rape of an eleven-year-old girl, whose body was found in an abandoned field.

anchor_text: In a village in northern France, Pharaon de Winter is a slightly disabled police lieutenant who accidentally lost his wife and baby three years earlier. He's been living alone with his mother ever since. He lives next door to Domino, a girl whose boyfriend is a bus driver named Joseph. Pharaoh is attracted to Domino, who often invites her to spend the evening with her and her fiancé. The police lieutenant is brought in to investigate the murder and rape of an eleven-year-old girl, whose body was found in a field in the commune.
text_a: A policeman, Pharaon De Winter, is investigating the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl in the countryside of northern France. The investigation is overlaid with the difficult relationship with the neighbor, Domino, engaged to his friend Joseph and with whom Pharaon is secretly in love. Ending enigmatic and surprising.
text_b: Franck Adrien, imprisoned for the robbery of the savings bank of Aix in Provence, shares his cell with Jean-Louis Maurel, accused of rape of a minor. Maurel maintains his innocence and refuses to leave the cell, for fear of being raped by the other prisoners. When Jean-Louis is attacked by three men in the middle of the night, with the complicity of a guard, Franck intervenes at his own peril. The departure of Maurel, finally exonerated after his victim recanted, isolates Franck, especially since his former accomplice assaulted him during a concert given in prison. Assaulted by the men and having attacked Maurel, Franck manages to escape. So began a vast manhunt, led by Clare Linnaeus.

anchor_text: Keiko is a young, delusional single mother who lives alone with her four children - Akira, Kyoko, Shigeru, and Yuki. Between the ages of five and twelve, they come from four different fathers. In order to move into a larger apartment than the previous one, Keiko hid the existence of the three younger ones from the landlord. None of the children are in school. Akira, the eldest, handles the household chores and keeps the accounts with the help of his sister Kyoko while their mother works. And Keiko keeps coming home late and late, until one day she doesn't come home at all. Akira discovers that she has made a new life in Osaka with her new lover. The children are left to fend for themselves.
text_a: Yuki Kashima is born in prison; her mother dies in childbirth. In retrospect, we learn that the mother, her husband, and their young son were the victims of four money-grubbing criminals who murdered the father and child and raped the mother. One of the perpetrators fell in love with the mother and opened a tearoom with her. Later, the mother stabbed the man to death and was sentenced to life in prison. There, she sleeps with men at will to produce a child who can complete her revenge. The girl is raised by a former samurai who trains her to be a swordsman. Gradually, she tracks down the remaining criminals and kills them. In the end, she is severely wounded with a knife by the daughter of one of her victims, who pursued her to take revenge on her for the alleged crime, and sinks into the snow. When the sun rises the next morning, however, she survives and wakes up.
text_b: In Tokyo, 12-year-old Akira Fukushima moves in with his mother Keiko, an irresponsible young woman, in a small apartment in the capital. The boy's three younger brothers - Kyoko, Shigeru, and Yuki - also sneaked in. One day the mother of the four boys leaves to pursue the man she has fallen in love with leaving the eldest son a small amount of money that soon runs out. As time passes, their mother disappears, the bills are not paid, and there is no money to buy food. But Akira will not give up and will look for any way to help his family.

anchor_text: Hugo Wiederkehr deals with all kinds of customers as a conscientious bank employee. Among other things, with the industrialist and his beautiful daughter Christine, with whom he falls in love. But she remains unreached because she belongs to a different social class. In addition, he works as a craftsman and inventor, which also requires a lot of money. When his employer denies him a loan because his father is in prison for fraud, he quits disappointed. Hugo wants to find another way to raise the money he needs. As a former employee of the bank, he is familiar with the money transfers between Zurich and Geneva, which are carried out by plane. During a flight, he manages to carry 3 million francs unnoticed. The police are baffled. He changes his clothes and gets off at a luxury hotel in Geneva, where the wealthy upper class take notice of him. Without recognizing him, Meissen introduces him to his daughter Christine. At a nuclear conference, Hugo mistakes his briefcase for the briefcase of the Russian delegation filled with classified documents. A chase ensues, ending at a police station. The confusion will be cleared up. Christine wants to marry Hugo because of his courage. He sends the money back to his bank because he doesn't need it anymore.
text_a: In 1940, German-born engineer Kurt Muller, his American wife Sara, and their children Joshua, Babette, and Bodo cross the border from Mexico into the United States to visit Sara's brother David Farrelly and his mother Fanny in Washington D.C. For the past seventeen years, the Muller family has lived in Europe, where Kurt responded to the rise of Nazism by participating in anti-fascist activities. Sara tells her family that they are seeking refuge on American soil, but their mission is threatened by the presence of guest Teck de Brancovis, a Romanian Count who has been conspiring with the Germans in the country's capital. Teck searches the Müller's room and discovers in a locked suitcase a gun and money intended to finance secret operations in Germany. Shortly afterwards, the Müllers discover that their friend Max Freidech, a member of the resistance, has been arrested, and because he once rescued Kurt from the Gestapo, Kurt plans to return to Germany to help both Max and the people arrested with him. Aware that Kurt will be in great danger if the Nazis discover that he is returning to Germany, Teck demands $10,000 for his silence, but Kurt kills him. Realizing the dangers Kurt faces, Fanny and David agree to help him escape. Time passes, and when the Mullers stop having news of Kurt, Joshua announces that he plans to look for his father as soon as he turns eighteen. Though distressed by the possibility of losing her son and her husband, Sara decides to be brave at the time of Joshua's departure.
text_b: Hugo Wiederkehr is an employee of the bank and obeys many kinds of customers. Among them are the industrialist Meissen and his daughter Christine with whom he falls in love. But that doesn't matter to the pretty girl, because he's not from the same social class. In addition, he is active as a craftsman and inventor, which makes him appear to be a spendthrift. When his employer refuses a loan because his father is in prison for fraud, he appears disappointed. Hugo wants to get the money some other way. As a former employee of the bank, he knows the money is transported between Zurich and Geneva by plane. During the robbery, he manages to take 3 million francs unseen and unknown. The police are at a loss. He gets dressed and goes down to a luxury hotel in Geneva where the rich take him for one of their own. Without recognizing him, Meissen introduces him to his daughter. During a conference on atomic energy, Hugo mistook his briefcase full of money for the briefcase full of secret documents of the Russian delegation. There's a hunt that ends at the police station. The confusion is cleared up. Christine wants to marry Hugo for his courage. He sends the money to his bank because he doesn't need it anymore.

anchor_text: The true story of an armored truck robbery in New York City in 1982, considered at the time to be the largest in U.S. history. Chris Potamitis (Liam Hemsworth), who failed to show up to the local police academy, is given a job as a security guard with the Empire State Armored Truck Company. Chris makes the mistake of mentioning the company's lax security to his best friend Eddie (Michael Angarano). He is soon unwittingly drawn into an elaborate scheme to steal the huge piles of cash stored in that company, resulting in the largest cash heist in U.S. history, totaling $11 million. As the stakes continue to rise, Chris and Eddie must outwit James Ransone (Dwayne The Rock Johnson), the veteran NYPD detective who's on the trail, as well as the local crime chiefs who want to know who got a job on their turf to make sure the perpetrators face the consequences.
text_a: In 1983, New Yorker Chris Potamitis (Hemsworth) fails to get into the local police academy and applies for a security guard position at the Empire State Armored Truck Company. Chris makes the mistake of mentioning the company's poor security to his best friend Eddie (Angarano), and soon, unwittingly, becomes involved in an elaborate plan to steal the plentiful piles of cash stored there, resulting in the largest cash heist ($11 million) to that point in U.S. history. As the stakes rise, Chris and Eddie must outwit James Ransome (Johnson), the veteran NYPD detective who is on their trail, as well as local crime bosses who want to know who carried out the robbery on their turf to make sure the perpetrators suffer the consequences.
text_b: In Las Vegas, James Henry, a decorated soldier just back from the Korean War, is falsely accused of killing a bargirl he'd been seen with earlier. He escapes police custody, steals a police car and drives to California. He comes across a broken-down car with two women on board. He abandons the police car downstream and, pretending to be a hitchhiker, offers the women a helping hand. When the breakdown was repaired, they took Henry into their vehicle. The police are after him...

anchor_text: Doug Harris is a young man with no social life at all. The best thing that ever happened to him was meeting Gretchen Palmer, who he's about to marry. But Doug has a huge problem, he has no family and no friends and so he has no witnesses to his wedding. He's totally freaked out, because he only has ten days left to find a witness and seven friends. On the advice of the wedding planner, he decides to call on Jimmy Callahan, the owner of a small company, which is responsible for providing fake environments (witnesses, families, friends or colleagues...) to the future bride and groom.
text_a: Doug Harris (Josh Gad), a lawyer in charge of his own firm but having difficulty relating to others, is finally going to marry the girl of his dreams: Gretchen Palmer (Kaley Cuoco). The problem arises when, two weeks before the big wedding, Doug finds himself desperate because he has no friends who can be his best man and fears that, if he tells his future wife, she will leave him. This leads him to Jimmy Callahan (Kevin Hart), a man dedicated to offering his services as a godfather, through a farce studied in detail, for those who need it (and for a modest price). The two set off, along with six other characters who will form a group of friends, to play their roles and recreate revelries and adventures that have never happened.
text_b: Doug Riley and his wife Lois have become estranged from each other since the death of their daughter and Lois remains unable to leave her home. While on a business trip, Doug visits a strip club where he meets Mallory (Kristen Stewart), a young prostitute who reminds him of his own daughter. He offers to pay her to move in with him, explaining that he does not want to have sex with her. Mallory is suspicious, but she agrees. Doug soon tries to put the girl's life in order. When his wife Lois joins him after overcoming her fears of leaving home, she understands what he is trying to do. The couple decided to take care of Mallory and start a family. But the girl's not gonna make it any easier for the Rileys.

anchor_text: Doug is two weeks away from marrying the lovely Gretchen, but he's still missing a best man. The search for a suitable candidate is extremely difficult, as Doug is not very popular and has few friends. By chance, he ends up with Jimmy, who uses his agency to provide professional wedding witnesses. Since he promised his bride and her family seven witnesses, he must take what he can get. At the wedding, the long-standing friendship between the men is now to be played for the guests, but the deception soon threatens to fly.

anchor_text: In 1980, as the military dictatorship reaches its peak, the head of the KCIA's foreign intelligence service, Park Pyong-ho, and the head of the domestic intelligence service, Kim Jung-do, are tasked with locating the North Korean spy known as Donglim, who is deeply integrated into their agency. The two units are tasked with investigating each other when the spy leaks highly classified information that threatens national security. Knowing themselves to be the suspects, they should not track down the traitor, Pyong-ho and Jung-do slowly reveal the truth and they realize that someone is planning to assassinate the South Korean president.
text_a: After a senior North Korean official has requested asylum in the Republic of Korea, the head of the Foreign Section of the KCIA, Park Pyong-ho (Lee Jung-jae), and the head of the National Section, Kim Jung-do (Jung Woo-sung), are tasked with uncovering a North Korean spy, known as Donglim, who is deeply entrenched in their agency. When the spy starts leaking top-secret information that could jeopardize national security, the two sections are assigned to investigate each other. In this tense situation where if they can't find the mole, they may be blamed themselves, Pyong-ho and Jung-do slowly begin to uncover the truth. In the end, they must deal with an unthinkable plot to assassinate the president of South Korea.[1]
text_b: North Korean agent Pyo Jong-seon is negotiating the purchase of weapons with a group of Arab terrorists, when the Israeli Mossad raids the hotel room where the negotiations were taking place. Meanwhile, a South Korean agent is on the trail of Pyo Jong-seong, who in addition to trying to escape capture must deal with suspicions that inside the North Korean embassy, where his wife works as a translator, there is a double agent.

anchor_text: In 1980, as the military dictatorship reaches its peak, the head of the KCIA's foreign intelligence service, Park Pyong-ho, and the head of the domestic intelligence service, Kim Jung-do, are tasked with locating the North Korean spy known as Donglim, who is deeply integrated into their agency. The two units are tasked with investigating each other when the spy leaks highly classified information that threatens national security. Knowing themselves to be the suspects, they should not track down the traitor, Pyong-ho and Jung-do slowly reveal the truth and they realize that someone is planning to assassinate the South Korean president.
text_a: North Korean agent Pyo Jong-seon is negotiating the purchase of weapons with a group of Arab terrorists, when the Israeli Mossad raids the hotel room where the negotiations were taking place. Meanwhile, a South Korean agent is on the trail of Pyo Jong-seong, who in addition to trying to escape capture must deal with suspicions that inside the North Korean embassy, where his wife works as a translator, there is a double agent.
text_b: In 1980, President Park was assassinated and the military returned to power. North Korea wants to take over South Korea by sending a spy. Park Pyeong-Ho (Lee Jung-jae) and Kim Jung-Do (Jung Woo-sung), two senior members of the National Intelligence Service (NIS) are assigned to track down this spy. The more the two agents investigate, the more secrets they'll uncover that threaten to upend the country's history...

anchor_text: Joe Pendleton is the quarterback for the Los Angeles Rams football team that's trying to win the Super Bowl. He dies in a car accident. In heaven, he's told that his death was a mistake and that he should return to earth. Now that his body has been cremated, he is to return in the body of recently murdered millionaire Leo Farnsworth. Pendleton in Farnsworth's body buys the Los Angeles Rams upon his return, astonishing his wife Julia and his secretary Tony Abbott who are a couple in love and murdered the millionaire. He wants it to give him the power to play quarterback in his new body. It's only after much persuasion that Coach Max Corkle allows this deployment. Pendleton falls in love with environmental activist Betty Logan, who is protesting some of Farnsworth's industrial projects. He is murdered by Abbott again, whereupon Pendleton assumes the body of an athlete; his previous experiences are erased from his memory.
text_a: Joe Pendleton is the quarterback of the Los Angeles Rams preparing for the Super Bowl, but he dies in a traffic accident. After verification with St. Peter, his death was not expected for several years. He is sent back to Earth, but with his body cremated, Joe assumes the identity of tycoon Leo Farnsworth, murdered by his unfaithful wife. Now wealthy, Joe (Leo) decides to take control of his former team's destiny, not without convincing his coach of his double identity. He buys the Rams and offers to take them to the playoffs. He also plans to marry Betty, an environmental activist. Just when everything seems to be going well for him, a messenger from Heaven appears to him...
text_b: U.S. Navy Lieutenant Tuck Pendelton volunteers for a scientific experiment that involves miniaturizing him into a capsule that will then be injected into the body of a rabbit. However, industrial spies break into the lab to steal the chip that enables miniaturization. During the robbery, the chief scientist may escape with the syringe containing the capsule. However, those spies manage to kill him, but not before he can inject it into a depressed, hypochondriac supermarket employee named Jack Putter. From there, once they both understand the situation, they will be forced to collaborate with each other to get Tuck out of Jack's body and save their lives from those industrial spies who are now after them for a second chip that has the capsule and that is also necessary for miniaturization.

When he was traveling on the road with his bicycle he enters a tunnel and suffers an accident with a truck. An angel snatches the soul from the body before he can certify Pendleton's death. As a result of the error, Pendleton reincarnates into different people until he finds his new physical body.

anchor_text: A Russian double agent, who has been posing as a British spy for 18 years, wants to hang up, but his superiors won't let him. His task is complicated when the British secret service gives him the order to eliminate one of his compatriots and friend...
text_a: In London, during the Cold War, British intelligence assigns Agent Eberlin the mission to travel to West Berlin, along with his colleague Gatiss, to kill Krasnevin, a dangerous Russian agent. But Eberlin, who keeps meeting the beautiful Caroline by chance, is actually Krasnevin himself, and is terrified that his true identity may be discovered.
text_b: In 1945, a Soviet officer, Mischa, discovers Anna, a little girl in a Berlin basement and helps her out. In 1952, Mischa returned to the German city as an engineer and decided to find Anna.

anchor_text: The rusty cargo ship Kedma reaches the coast of the British Mandate of Palestine in May 1948, shortly before the declaration of independence of the State of Israel. On board are several hundred Jews from various European countries who survived the Holocaust and are now coming to Palestine as illegal immigrants. British soldiers are trying to stop them from entering the country. The film shows the fate of a group of immigrants who managed to escape the soldiers. In a makeshift camp run by the Jewish underground organization Palmach, they are armed and take part in the battles of the civil war. In the monologues of an Arab farmer and a Jewish refugee who has suffered much in Europe, Gitai expresses the positions of both sides.
text_a: The film takes place entirely inside a tank, while what happens outside is always seen through the lens of the muzzle, accompanied by the noise of the turret engines. The dramatic war action in the outside world thus becomes almost virtual for the four Israeli soldiers in the crew. The relationship develops in a dramatic way, with dynamics fueled by the frequent opening of the gate from which they descend or ascend: a superior officer making the iron fist of discipline felt, the corpse of an Israeli soldier waiting for the helicopter to evacuate him, a Syrian prisoner of war who will remain chained in the wagon, a Phalangist who presents himself ambiguously as an ally of the Israelis. The characters of soldiers who are fundamentally incompetent, the indecisive foreman, the pointer still too focused on his childhood to be able to shoot, the servant with the frank but undisciplined humanity, the driver who just wants to go home, all while the four soldiers find themselves in increasingly dramatic situations. To counter, off the tank, Officer Jamil, a soldier for all intents and purposes, brave but also ruthless, as when he agrees a code name to give the order to use phosphorus bombs, just banned by the convention on certain conventional weapons.
text_b: The film is a historical tragedy, set during the beginning of the 1948 Arab-Israeli War. It describes the vicissitudes of a group of refugees, who flee Europe to escape the persecution of Jews with the help of the Palmach. Once in Palestine, they must escape the British soldiers. Having escaped, they are immediately drawn into the war against the Arab forces. The film focuses on two long monologues. The first is that of an Arab peasant, who vows to fight against the Jews forever; the second is that of a refugee, who suffers from the seemingly endless persecution suffered by the Jewish people.

anchor_text: A group of teenagers go on an outdoor excursion with the intention of spending a weekend in the woods. The first night, after a booze-fueled party, Jeremy accidentally cuts off his penis and is flown to the hospital by helicopter. The boys, however, discover that their friend's penis has remained in their freezer and must do everything they can to bring it to the hospital in good condition.
text_a: A respected businessman with a dark past seeks revenge after his daughter is kidnapped by men who want to settle old debts. To his friends and family, Paul Maguire (Nicolas Cage) is a family man and a hard worker. But what they don't know is that Paul has a very different past, where life was cheap and debts were paid in blood.
text_b: A group of friends set off on an outdoor trip with the intention of spending a weekend in the woods. During the first night, after a booze party, Jeremy accidentally cuts off his penis and is transported to the hospital by helicopter. The boys, however, discover that their friend's penis has been left inside their refrigerator and so they must do everything they can to get him to the hospital in good condition.

anchor_text: At the beginning of the 20th century, old Henri Fortin, father of young Henri, is accused. He is said to have killed the Comte de Villeneuve, for whom he worked as a chauffeur. During the trial, his wife takes a job at a tavern on the beach in Normandy. Wrongfully convicted, he tries to escape from prison with other prisoners and dies in the process. When she receives the news of her husband's death, his wife kills herself. So little Henri has to grow up an orphan.
text_a: In 1386, in Normandy, the knight Jean de Carrouges, returning from a trip to Paris, found his wife, Marguerite de Thibouville. She accuses the squire Jacques le Gris, an old friend of the knight, of having raped her. The Grey says he's innocent. The case reaches to the highest spheres of power, with King Charles VI having to decide whether there will be a trial by combat, as the knight wished. This duel is supposed to determine the truth. If her husband is defeated, Marguerite de Thibouville will be burned alive for false accusation. Ruined, John of Carrouges had little support, while James the Grey could count on the support of the powerful Count Peter II of Alençon.
text_b: Early 20th century Henri Fortin, falsely accused of murdering his boss, the Comte de Villeneuve, is sentenced to the gallows. His wife Catherine named her son Henri, formerly called Leopold. She took refuge with their son Henri in the region of Arromanches, with an innkeeper who had the flu. As a result of a failed escape, Henry Sr. dies. As an adult, Henry Jr. became a middleweight boxing champion. In 1940, he became a migrant, helping André Ziman, his wife Élise and their daughter Salomé to flee Nazi persecution. During their journey to the Jura, he is told the story of Jean Valjean, a nickname given to him in tribute to his great physical strength.

anchor_text: Muskaan and her brother Vishal have been living with their uncle Thakur Ajay Singh since the death of their parents. She gets one marriage proposal after another, but Vishal rejects all the suitors because he wishes to see a strong man at her side. When Muskaan decides to go to college in Mumbai, Vishal grants her wish with a heavy heart, just because he promised it to her parents. In college, Muskaan meets Suraj Khanna, whose petrified father doesn't particularly love him and whose stepmother has made him leave his home. They fall in love with each other, but when Vichal shows up at the college, Suraj considers him a rival, and their acquaintance begins with a fight. Now Suraj must convince the stubborn brother that he is the right man for Muskaan.
text_a: Bittoo Sharma (Ranveer Singh) is intelligent and fun loving. He sneaks into a wedding for the free food, and Shruti Kakkar (Anushka Sharma) a smart and quirky girl who is helping the wedding coordinator, confronts him. Bittoo pretends that he is part of the film crew and makes a video of Shruti's performance when he sees her dancing at the wedding. The next day, she tries to impress Shruti with a compiled DVD of her dancing at the wedding. Shruti is not interested in flirting, and reveals that her main interest is starting her own wedding planner business. Bittoo is under pressure from her parents to return to the village and work in the family's sugar cane fields, and Shruti is convinced to get married as soon as possible. After performing the exams, Shruti makes a deal with her parents that she has five years to get her business up and running before they arrange a marriage for her. When Bittoo's father tries to take him back to his village, Bittoo finds that he cannot return because he is starting a wedding planning business. He goes to Shruti with this idea, but she refuses because she is worried that a romantic complication will arise between the two of them. She tells Bittoo that the number one business empire is not to let love get in the way.
text_b: Following the death of their parents, Muskaan (Kajol) and his brother Vishal (Arbaaz Khan) were raised in the countryside by their uncle and aunt. Muskaan is now a charming young girl who wants to study in Mumbai. Despite his fears of seeing his sister lost in this big city, his brother agrees and accompanies him to his university where he meets the president (Tiku Talsania) to ensure the seriousness of the institution and Muskaan's welfare. She receives many marriage proposals from good families but Vishal rejects them all one after another. He makes each contender undergo a test: the turn of his property on horseback but which turns to drama every time... Only the one who passes the test will be able to marry Muskaan. When the latter falls in love with Suraj Khanna (Salman Khan) met at the university, she introduces him to her family and Vishaal subjects him to his test but Suraj fails. Vishaal then wants Muskaan to marry Thakur Vijay Singh's brother (Aashif Sheikh) without knowing that Thakur Vijay Singh (Nirmal Pandey) only wishes this union in order to take revenge on Muskaan's family for a dispute that the two families had long ago...

anchor_text: One evening at home in Marseille, Frank Martin, a renowned conveyor, sees his living room clogged by the car of his colleague Malcolm who replaces him for a contract. But when he came to him for help, he died. Subsequently, Frank is forced by his employer to carry two bags and accompany a young Ukrainian woman from Marseille to Odessa, under duress (an explosive bracelet is attached to his wrist). If Frank doesn't know anything about the identity of the girl and the contents of the bags, he is certain to be involved in a high-risk trip that he will gradually discover the stakes and try to stay alive, while in Odessa they negotiate to force a high-ranking official to let a cargo ship with extremely toxic illegal waste dock at the port...
text_a: Frank Martin is back in France but he's done with his illegal delivery business. Unfortunately, this time he is forced by Johnson to make a delivery, under penalty of death by explosive bracelet. If he moves 80 feet from the car, the bracelet explodes. During the journey he discovers that the object of the package is Valentina, the daughter of a Ukrainian minister, kidnapped to force her father to sign an agreement for a traffic of ships loaded with toxic waste. When he reaches his destination, he is stopped by Johnson's men who take the girl and unsuccessfully attempt to kill him. So Frank goes after the gang of criminals who took Valentina hostage.
text_b: Frank Martin, ex-Special Forces, lends his services as an expert driver to anyone who pays him. His main personal rules are not to get personally involved in the affairs in which he is involved, purely instrumentally. Nevertheless, when Frank breaks rule number 3 during a transport and discovers a young woman bound in his trunk, he will have to pay the price for the transgression by fleeing not only from the police but also from ruthless gangsters who have made him their target. Because now he knows too much. To make matters worse, the bound woman, Lai Kwai, whom he rescued, is going to implicate him to bring down the gangster network that deals in smuggling illegals...

Along the way, with the help of Inspector Tarconi, Frank has to face armed people to do his job, Vasilev's agents trying to intercept him, and his companion's lack of cooperation. Despite Valentina's cynical behavior and Frank's reluctance to get involved, the two fall in love, escaping one dangerous situation after another. Statham again drives an armored Audi A8 W12 in the film. Frank's skills in hand-to-hand combat are outstanding and his Karate Do techniques that he learned in military strategic training are striking.

anchor_text: Young journalist Monique Grant is hired under the guise of a charity event to conduct an interview with retired Hollywood icon Evelyn Hugo. When she is invited to Evelyn Hugo's house, she learns that Hugo is not really interested in such an interview, but wants Grant to write a biography about her that will allow a completely new perspective on her.
text_a: Hollywood star Evelyn Hugo, an icon of film history, who has reached the age of 79, tells reporter Monique Grant all the details of her rise in the world of film, her relationship with Celia St. James, and her seven husbands.
text_b: In the late 1960s, The Wizard of Oz star Judy Garland was preparing to give a series of concerts in London. But her voice has weakened over time, and Judy suffers, traumatized by a life devoted entirely to Hollywood and her career. To recover financially, she has to regretfully leave her children in the United States with her former husband, with whom she has strained relations. In London, she's on the edge, and her assistant Rosalyn has to deal with her temper and unpredictable outbursts.

anchor_text: On the advice of her brother-in-law, Marlene von Steinitz, a young countess and poor widow, must marry the limited-intelligence but extremely wealthy Imperial Count Adolar von Warowingen, as it would rid her of all her worries at once. She asks Count Steinitz for a delay of eight days and goes to the city to make a decision and, incidentally, to enjoy her free life again to the fullest. At an artists' festival there, she meets the painter Frank Merwin, ensures he is drugged and has the handsome young man brought to her castle. When he wakes up in the middle of the night, we treat him like a celebrity. Marlene, wearing a mask, pretends to be his wife to test him and see if he is good as a future husband. Although Merwin does not pass this test very well and is sent back to his poor little room, the countess chooses him. Count Adolar is rejected by Marlene. Marlene and Frank celebrating their engagement in the city.
text_a: Countess Marlene, widowed and impoverished, is advised by her brother-in-law to marry Count Adolar von Warowingen, who, although not sane, is nevertheless very rich. She then asks to be left free for another eight days which she uses to savor the freedom she has left. He travels to the city and, in the artists' quarter, meets the painter Frank Merwin. Marlene wants to test him and, after stunning him, takes him to the castle where she pretends to have married him. Everyone treats the man like a celebrity and the test doesn't turn out to be a positive test. Marlene, however, decides to marry Frank, rather than the idiot chosen for her by the family.
text_b: The plot takes place in Paris in the 1930s in a loose-jazzy Viennese operetta version: Marcel, who works as a shop window decorator, surprisingly receives a ticket for the theater from a stranger for him. To dress appropriately, he 'borrows' a coat from his employer's stock at his own discretion. At the theater, he meets Madeleine,* a chocolate saleswoman, who immediately falls in love with the elegantly dressed and extremely charming young man. To be his equal, she confides in her friend Lily and the revue star Yvonne, who, with the help of the clothes from the theater fund, transform Madeleine into a lady of society. Marcel, in turn, becomes aware of the lovely young lady and courts her. So it is that after the show is over, the two of them go to a dance hall together. A found business card leads to Marcel being mistaken for Baron de Monteuil. That's why he has to give the baron's address on the card on the way home in the taxi. He takes Madeleine to the villa where, fortunately, no one else lives, and the young couple spends happy hours there. The next morning, however, disaster looms as Baron and Baroness Monteuil, who had been on a journey, return to their home. The baroness, entering her bedroom, encounters Madeleine, who is still in bed. She now believes her husband is having an affair. Marcel sees only one way to clarify the matter. He tells us what the connections really are. Madeleine's reaction, which Marcel did not necessarily expect, makes him happy, because it lets him know that she prefers a husband from her own circles to a nobleman. * in the French version Simone; ** in the French version Colette and Mariette

anchor_text: Psychologist Richard Clayton has a great life: he's about to get married, he drives an expensive car, and his new life coach is on his way to becoming a best-seller. When he finds out he's adopted, he goes to great lengths to get to know his birth parents. While searching for them, he meets Agnes and Frank Menure, who turn his life upside down.
text_a: The young Gloria accidentally meets the rich Francisco Galván at the mass. He falls in love with her and convinces her to marry him. However, she soon discovers, on their honeymoon, that he is afflicted with pathological jealousy and paranoia. So he sticks a needle in the lock in case the roommate spies on them. But it's only the beginning, and Gloria's life is about to become an ordeal.
text_b: At the age of thirty-four, psychologist Richard Clayton (Ron Livingston) is told by his parents that he was adopted. He then sets out to find out who his biological parents are, but disaster strikes when it turns out that his parents, Frank (Danny DeVito) and Agnes Menure (Kathy Bates), are a total disaster, earn their living as holidaymakers, and also go to live at his house and spend part of their life making him go through embarrassing moments.

Shocked by his new past, he set out on a quest to discover the identity of his biological parents. He bit his fingers because his parents were opposed to his values and way of life. Her invitation to spend a few days with her adoptive family could completely change her life and that of her loved ones.

anchor_text: In 1960, a small group of Mossad agents led by Peter Malkin kidnaps former German SS leader and National Socialist Adolf Eichmann, who is hiding under a false name in Argentina.
text_a: Most of the action takes place on a train crossing Germany. On board, among others, is a pacifist German professor who is kidnapped by a gang of neo-Nazis. But a young American, in love with the professor's secretary, manages to free him.
text_b: Fifteen years after the end of World War II, in 1960, a team of Israeli secret agents tracks down Nazi Adolf Eichmann, a high-ranking official of the Third Reich, a Holocaust logistician, on the run, in Argentina where he is hiding under the false identity of Ricardo Klement.

anchor_text: A family man incurs the wrath of his wife and son as he forces them to leave Chicago to move to a small town in Oregon, where he must participate in the development of a real estate project in the middle of the forest, supposedly green. Dan's troubles are not over, however, for the animals of the forest have no intention of allowing this construction project to destroy their environment and will do anything to make Dan's life impossible. Then a merciless war will break out between Dan and the hairy defenders of wildlife, led by a cunning raccoon.
text_a: In 1916, Bill, a foundry worker, his girlfriend Abby, and his sister Linda fled Chicago for the Texas harvest. Seeing this as an opportunity to escape poverty, Bill urges Abby to give in to the advances of a wealthy farmer, who they know has an incurable disease. But Abby ends up falling in love with the farmer. Bill understands, though.
text_b: Chicago entrepreneur Dan Sanders forces his family, consisting of his wife Tammy and teenage son Tyler, to move for a year to the remote town of Rocky Springs, Oregon, to clear the forest and create a metropolis, according to the plan of his insidious boss, Neal Lyman, the first man "environmentalist against the environment", together with his company "Lyman Enterprise". To prevent this, the animals of the forest, unable to confront Neal with words, unite and make life hellish for his deputy and organizer Dan, combining cruel jokes: his family does not believe him, convinced that he is stressed and needs a psychologist. When his son Tyler becomes engaged to Amber, a genuinely environmentally conscious local girl, Dan understands the animals' point of view and, after the latest disagreements, joins forces with them to prevent Lyman's project from being realized.

anchor_text: The plot of the story is a joint boat trip of three young men at the end of the 19th century: Jerome, the narrator, plans to rent a rowboat with his two friends George and Harris and sail the Thames between Kingston and Oxford for about two weeks. The characters have real-life role models, but the dog Montmorency who accompanies the three is fictitious. But he has, as Jerome put it, "a lot in common with me". Jerome uses an associative narrative style. He uses individual events of the preparations for the trip and the trip itself to tell in detail various stories and anecdotes that have little or nothing to do with the trip. The journey therefore forms only the frame action and, as far as the scope of the overall work is concerned, it clearly takes a back seat to the sprinkled anecdotes. Originally, the book was intended as a serious travel guide, with stories about the history of places along the route, but the humorous descriptions ultimately prevailed. A later, but not so successful sequel, titled Three Men on the Bummel, is about a bicycle tour through Germany.
text_a: Upstream of the River Thames, the three friends Jerome ("J". voice narrator), Harris and George, accompanied by the dog Montmorency, travel for days on their boat, parading along the English countryside where they live unexpected adventures. The journey is marked by a series of comic gags about the joys and sorrows of life on the boat, combined with humorous ramblings that constitute stories in their own right, just think of the famous story of Uncle Podger struggling with a painting to hang. The whole is spiced with the unmistakable style of English humour, but also with realistic descriptions of the regions crossed by the three friends and with brief philosophical notations for the uninitiated.
text_b: It is an adventure story aimed at the young Austrian and Italian audience of the 18th century. It tells the adventures of a teenager of Italian origin, named Lukas Grassi, orphan of parents, who lives poorly in Vienna and who longs to return to his native country. His greatest desire is to study art in Florence. By magic, his wish is granted, in exchange for every two days he becomes the dog of Archduke Ludwig, named Kambyses. In this way, alternating his human and canine condition, he travels from Vienna to Florence along with the Archduke's troops. It's the only book by Salten in which supernatural events occur, showing a noticeable E.T.A. influence. Hoffmann is married.[1]

anchor_text: In the suburbs of Paris, young Dounia thirsts for power and success. Supported by her best friend Maimouna, she quits the vocational high school and offers her services to Rebecca, a respected dealer whose respect she gradually gains. His unexpected encounter with Djigui, a disturbing dancer of sensuality, will shake him, but it is difficult to get out of Rebecca's grip.
text_a: The story begins with four women who are great rivals in a martial arts competition that can only be accessed by invitation. These women will have to come together to fight as a team against a totally alien force. Helena Douglas is a great extreme sports athlete who because of her "horrible" past is forced to hide in a distant palace in Southeast Asia, which is where the Dead Or Alive tournament takes place. Princess Kasumi (Devon Aoki), is an Asian wrestler who was trained by martial arts masters. All of them will join forces to fight against the other rivals and will have to do their utmost to get the prize of 10 million dollars.
text_b: Two teenage friends from the suburbs of Paris, both very smart, are determined to get rich or die trying. His plan is to follow in the footsteps of a famous drug dealer from the neighborhood, a place marked by drug trafficking and Islamism. Dounia has a thirst for power and success. Helped by Maimuna, her best friend, she decides to follow in the footsteps of Rebecca, a respected woman even though she is involved in trafficking. But her encounter with Djiui, a young dancer, will open the possibility of glimpsing a new kind of life in her future.

anchor_text: Paris, early morning, in the deep light of a sex club, the bodies of Théo and Hugo meet. One look, and all they want is each other. After the sex, they leave the club together, just running off, talking about the beauty of penises, the intoxication of the senses and the great love.
text_a: Elias is a young illegal immigrant who arrived on the beach of Eden, a Greek resort for the wealthy. Escaping to avoid arrest, he tries to reach Paris, and in that odyssey he encounters characters and situations of all kinds.
text_b: Two men meet in a libertine club, and it's a love affair: they get to know each other in the early hours of the morning, they admit to each other that one is HIV positive and the other doesn't wear a condom.
