anchor_text: Taeko lives with her husband Jiro and son Keita in an apartment building near her in-laws. Keita is the local champion of the Othello game. At his stepfather's father's birthday party, the child accidentally dies. Keita's biological father then resurfaced. He and the husband's ex-fiancée create chaos within the couple.
text_a: The tension of the typical Japanese family circle between modernity and tradition, symbolized here by the three generations living together in the same house. Noriko, the heroine, is a modern Japanese typist who does not plan to marry at the age of twenty-eight. However, under pressure from family members, she resigns, but her decision to marry her doctor brother's poor assistant instead of her family's favorite businessman causes the family to break up for good.
text_b: Taeko is a devoted mother who lives a happy life with her husband and young son Keita. When, following the accidental death of the child, Keita's biological father unexpectedly returns to her life, deaf, sick and homeless, Taeko decides to take care of him.

anchor_text: Frankie, an aimless teenager living on the outskirts of Brooklyn, is having a miserable summer. First his father dies, and then his mother pushes him to finally find a girlfriend. Frankie tries to escape the desolation of his family life by flirting with older men online. When chatting and webcamming are not enough for him, he seeks contact with men while cruising on the beach and at the same time starts a relationship with a young woman. Under the influence of three friends with whom he regularly takes drugs, he finds it increasingly difficult to keep his double life a secret.
text_a: In the opening scene, Frankie, the film's protagonist, is beaten by a small group of men. Whether he survives remains to be seen. The rest of the film is a flashback and can be roughly divided into three parts. Frankie's troubled childhood, his time as a skinhead, and a time of change in which he tries to believe in hope and love. He enters into a relationship with Helen, who studies art and works in a record store. When the differences between them become too obvious, she leaves him. He then decides to join Alcoholics Anonymous or a similar group. He also joins a theater group, where he meets Mary, with whom he begins a relationship. However, he turns away from her when he - also due to his past - succumbs to the delusion that she had an affair. On the verge of a relapse, he stops drinking and decides to apologize to Mary. However, on the way to meet her, he is ambushed and beaten by his former friends.
text_b: Frankie is a confused teenager living in a Brooklyn neighborhood with his sick father, mother, and younger sister. Frankie spends her summers in the company of her promiscuous friends and chatting with mature men on the Internet, with whom she consumes casual and secret relationships. The meeting with a girl, the association with a group of boys, the death of his father and the hidden homosexuality mark his sad life between drugs and self-shame.

anchor_text: The old Duke of Chavres has taken an interest in the young, passionate Jessie Cordier and would like to make her his mistress. But she's not willing. She's more interested in the composer, Max Brignon. They're both getting married. When one day the Duke's son, the elegant Marquis Serge de Chavres, appears on the scene and rattles around Jessie, the young wife suspects Max of having been unfaithful to him, her husband. However, Jessie can assure you with all her heart that nothing of the sort has happened. However, when the old duke harasses Jessie again, Max gradually goes crazy: he starts drinking and eventually commits suicide. The bereaved widow is left alone and heartbroken.
text_a: In this classic love story set against the southern Riviera sun, the two French students Bertrand Griot and Dominique Vallon are the main characters. They both experience their love in a youthful, carefree way. One day, Dominique Bertrand, a law student, meets Luc Ferrand, her charming uncle, a handsome and married gentleman whose experience and skill impress her. The girl and the much older gentleman begin a short-lived but nevertheless very intense romance, which questions and confounds the entire previous relationship network of all parties involved. For the wealthy Luc, however, this short-lived romance was little more than a summer fling, a temporary love affair. All the more disappointed is the still somewhat naive Dominique, when after a short time it's all over again. After that, it seems that there are only losers: Luc has to explain himself to his equally noble and indulgent wife Françoise, and Dominique tries to save the relationship with the rebellious Bertrand.
text_b: The old Duke of Chavres is eyeing the passionate young Jessie Cordier and would like to make her his mistress. But she's not consenting, but rather interested in the composer Max Brignon. They're both getting married. When one day the Duke's son, the elegant Marquis Serge de Chavres, appears and mocks Jessie, the young bride suspects Max of cheating on her husband. However, Jessie can honestly say that nothing has happened in this regard. When the old duke presses Jessie again, Max slowly goes mad: he starts drinking and ends up committing suicide. The tragic widow is left alone and heartbroken.

anchor_text: The British Secret Service sends one of their men, Quiller, to West Berlin to investigate the murder of two of their agents by a neo-Nazi organization led by "Oktober", who poses as a German aristocrat. Quiller, accustomed to working alone, seduces the man responsible for protecting him and contacts a beautiful young woman, Inge, a teacher and colleague of one of the members of the organization who, after being arrested, hanged himself. She gives him some information. Quiller is kidnapped, beaten, interrogated about the British Secret Service, then found in a pitiful state on the edge of a canal. His boss wants to know where the neo-Nazi headquarters is. Quiller spends a night with Inge, who says she knows someone who can give him information. After this encounter Quiller and Inge find themselves in front of a strange building, half in ruins. He leaves to explore, leaving Inge in the car with a number to call in case he doesn't come back soon. Quiller is once again in October's hands and, in the same room, Inge is a prisoner. Oktober offers a deal: information about the British secret service in exchange for his life and that of Inge. Quiller has the night to think. He wanders around Berlin looking for a phone to warn his superiors but everywhere he meets the men of October. He finally finds a subterfuge, the whole group is arrested, Inge is not among them. Quiller sees her again later, in his high school, he realizes that she was October's accomplice, had lured him into a trap but he lets her go free and goes back to London.
text_a: In a section of British intelligence, there are a few elderly men who feel frustrated that they have been relegated to sidelines. The Cold War is experiencing a period of decreased conflict intensity; peaceful coexistence implies only limited routine activity. World War II, the time of V2, was its great epoch. They pathetically proclaim that they risked their lives (but preferably the lives of others) to give 50 million British citizens a better night's sleep. Now they're looking for a way to reaffirm themselves. They happened to come across photographs of missile units in the GDR, the stationing of which may be contrary to international agreements. A young Pole who fled to the West for reasons of conscience is hired by the secret service to obtain more pictures on the spot. Although the prospects of success are rather doubtful and the life of the Pole is in danger, the elderly men are primarily concerned with being able to indulge in their dreams. After a thorough preparation, the Polish agent succeeds in entering the GDR. He has to kill some people to get to his goal. The strategic value of the operation is zero. Perforated by machine-gun bullets, the young man dies, and his East German girlfriend suffers the same fate.
text_b: Agent Quiller is sent to Berlin on a mission to uncover a mysterious neo-Nazi organization that ruthlessly eliminates opponents who oppose his rise to power, Quiller learns of the disappearance of the agent who preceded him on the same mission, but decides to act alone and unarmed. On the contrary, he is immediately spotted by those he has to fight and is deliberately kidnapped and drugged in order to gain access to the headquarters of the organization he is investigating. His kidnappers, however, leave him alive and, having been unable to discover anything concrete in his delirious words, follow him in the hope of identifying through his ignorant collaboration the agency that is fighting them. Warned by his direct superior not to be drawn into the game of the opponents, Quiller through Inge, a young teacher with whom he makes friends, identifies the hiding place of the neo-Nazis and signals it in time to his colleagues. But when the mission is actually accomplished, Quiller uncovers some dark points about Inge, who he believed was not part of the criminal organization. Quiller then leaves sadly, convinced that he has only cut off one of the heads of the dangerous neo-Nazi hydra.

anchor_text: Fourteen-year-old Mouse is desperate to be accepted into the Midnight Clique, a notorious Baltimore motocross gang that included his older brother. He is taken under his wing by their boss Blax, but on the other hand, Detective Rivers talks him into his conscience, causing Mouse to be quickly torn back and forth between two mentors.
text_a: Fourteen-year-old Mouse desperately tries to join the Midnight Clique, a notorious youth motorcycle gang that dominates the streets of Baltimore in the summer with their reckless stunts, but is thwarted by his mother due to the death of his older brother in a similar accident a few years earlier.
text_b: Jérôme Crainquebille is a poor street vendor of vegetables in Paris. Accused of insulting a police officer, he finds himself a victim of the machinations of a Kafkaesque justice system. When he finally gets out of prison, his middle-class clients avoid him. On the verge of committing suicide out of desperation he is saved by a small screaming orphan, La Souris, even poorer than him.

anchor_text: Lord Brummell, an elegant and aristocratic dandy, is a friend of the Prince of Wales (the future King of England George IV). Envied by all, after years of partying and fun, Brummel will quarrel with the prince, falling into disgrace. Out of pride, he'll leave London and go abroad. He'll never return to his homeland, and he'll die poor in France.
text_a: In 1796, Captain George Brummell of the 10th Royal Hussars offends the Prince of Wales with his blunt frankness and is dismissed from the army, but his sharp wit, good taste and sharp tongue lead him to become the Prince's personal adviser. When a conspiracy against George III is uncovered, he will have to abandon his pleasant life and act accordingly.
text_b: Lillie's married to Uncle John Sterling. The two sleep in separate beds, and John is usually distant from his young wife. On a ship crossing, they meet the Javanese Prince de Gace. Lillie is as sexually attracted as she is humanly repulsed by the prince who whips his servants and yet has perfect manners. In Java, the prince bids fiercely for Lillie, and during a tiger hunt, she almost becomes weak. But in the end, she stays with her husband.

anchor_text: In early 19th-century London, George, aka Beau Brummell, a young commoner turned soldier, devotes all his energy to outdoing his worldly rivals with elegance and wit. He thus manages to win the dazzled friendship of the Regent, the Prince of Wales, the obese, futile, and despotic offspring of the mad King George III. Now the favourite of the court, he also wonders, between two Cornish tie choices, whether to marry his mistress, the ominous Lady Patricia...
text_a: In 1796, Captain George Brummell of the 10th Royal Hussars offends the Prince of Wales with his blunt frankness and is dismissed from the army, but his sharp wit, good taste and sharp tongue lead him to become the Prince's personal adviser. When a conspiracy against George III is uncovered, he will have to abandon his pleasant life and act accordingly.
text_b: British Army officer George Bryan Brummell decides to remain a bachelor after his beloved, Lady Margery, is forced to marry another. Brummell lives with great expenditure of means: elegant and aristocratic dandy, arbiter of English fashion and society, he is a close friend of the Prince of Wales (the future king of England). Envied by all, after years of parties and amusements, his insolent and indiscreet character will lead him to quarrel with the prince, which will decree his fall from grace. Out of pride, he'll leave London and go abroad. He will never return to his homeland and will die in poverty in France, covered in debts. The only one who'll be with him to the end will be his valet Mortimer.

anchor_text: Danilo, the nephew of the cult king André Napoleon Renard, is to take care of the company's branch in Paris. But Danilo prefers to spend his time and his uncle's money on the dancer Valencienne and her cabaret Maxim. Camillo, Valencienne's very jealous husband, is not at all pleased with the young Lebanese man's interest in his wife. In order to get the playboy to live a more solid life, the serious uncle hatches a clever plan. He fakes a marriage to his secretary Hanna and then fakes his own death during their honeymoon in the South Seas. His "widow" and only child then travels to Paris and turns the faucet on the easy-going Danilo. Suddenly without money, Danilo is forced to look for work for the first time in his life. However, whenever he finds a job, Hanna makes sure that he quickly loses that job. Despite all the difficulties, Danilo's good heart emerges and he slowly begins to fall in love with Hanna, even though she is now wasting his uncle's money senselessly. Hanna, on the other hand, falls in love with Danilo. When Danilo accidentally learns that his uncle is not dead and never married Hanna, he sets a trap for the two of them. In Paris, he has the uncle reappeared together with Hanna, arrested by fake policemen as impostors. Shortly after the "arrest", Danilo clarifies everything and announces his upcoming marriage to Hanna.
text_a: Danilo, the nephew of champagne king André Napoléon Renard, will be in charge of the Paris branch. But Danilo prefers to spend his time and his uncle's money with the Valencian dancer and the cabaret Maxim. Camillo, the very jealous husband of Valencienne, is not at all pleased with the young man's interest in his wife. To get the playboy to adopt a more serene lifestyle, the uncle has a clever plan. He pretends to marry his secretary Hannah and soon fakes his death on their honeymoon in the South Seas. His widow and universal heiress travels to Paris and discusses money with Danilo. Now that Danilo has suddenly run out of money, he has to look for work for the first time in his life. But every time he finds a job, Hanna makes sure he loses it quickly. Despite all the difficulties, Danilo's good fund appears and slowly, he begins to fall in love with Hanna, although she now wastes her uncle's money meaninglessly. Hanna falls in love with Danilo. When Danilo accidentally learns that his uncle is not dead and that he never married Hanna, he sets a trap for them. In Paris, he has the resurrected uncle and Hanna arrested by fake policemen as impostors. Shortly after the "arrest", Danilo clarifies the situation and announces his upcoming marriage to Hanna.
text_b: Jack is a ten-year-old boy who lives with his mother (happy and affectionate, but irresponsible) and his younger brother, having to take care of him and the house. One day, little Manuel burns himself in the bathroom, and Jack is committed to a social center. Your roommate will be an introverted boy with a passion for ornithology. Jack asks him to lend him his binoculars, and he does so with reservations, as his late father forbade him to leave them to anyone. Jack suffers harassment from Danilo - an older companion - without caregivers worrying too much about the visible marks Jack presents on the body occasionally. Vacation comes and Jack packs his bags to go home, but he gets a call from his mother telling him he'll be a couple of days late. Angry and disappointed he returns to his room, where he finds that his partner has left him as a gift before leaving the binoculars on the bed. Jack goes to a swamp to use them, but there he meets Danilo, who throws the binoculars into the water, and Jack hits him with a stick and knocks him unconscious. Overwhelmed by the weight of his guilt, he decides to run home. But when he arrives, his mother isn't there. Jack decides to go find his brother, whose mother has left him at a friend's house, and wait for his mother to return. But he doesn't have the keys and he can't get in the house. So they spend their days, looking for their mother, sleeping around and taking care of Manuel as they can, in the midst of a hostile world not without dangers, in which no one seems to care about the fate of the little ones.

anchor_text: Tess, Dawn and Kara are barely getting by with their little jobs in Las Vegas. But their lives are turned upside down when they agree to work for a local gangster named Mel. With their lives at stake, they must contend with a psychotic hit man, a drug dealer, and a depressed cook.
text_a: Four close friends live in Los Angeles, California. They're all poor and they've suffered a lot. Finally one day they decide to plan an execute a bank robbery. They decide to do so for different reasons, although all four want the best for themselves and their families.
text_b: Tess, Dawn and Kara are three girls working jobs with no future and barely enough to live on in Las Vegas. Their lives change dramatically when Tess meets an interesting stranger named Mel. When Mel offers the girls a chance at a better life through crime they accept the offer to be thrust into a life-or-death situation involving a psychopathic contract killer, a truck driver and a delusional cook.

anchor_text: Tess, Dawn and Kara are barely getting by with their little jobs in Las Vegas. But their lives are turned upside down when they agree to work for a local gangster named Mel. With their lives at stake, they must contend with a psychotic hit man, a drug dealer, and a depressed cook.
text_a: Four close friends live in Los Angeles, California. They're all poor and they've suffered a lot. Finally one day they decide to plan an execute a bank robbery. They decide to do so for different reasons, although all four want the best for themselves and their families.
text_b: Tes, Kara and Dawn are three criminals hired by Mel for a seemingly simple job that's going to pay the girls a lot of money. But by the time they realize they've been conned, it'll be too late to back down and they'll have to fight for their lives.

anchor_text: Larkin Moffatt (Edmond O'Brien) is testing ore to determine the silver content. Suspected of having sold information to bandits, his friend and colleague Richard Arlen Charles Storrs had him fired for dishonesty, ensured that he never found another job and, in the process, married his girlfriend. Hiding in Silver City, he meets Catherine Surrency (Yvonne De Carlo): with her father she has to exploit a silver mine whose lease expires twelve days later...
text_a: A miner rents a mine from Jarboe, an unscrupulous man, and turns to Moffat for a survey of the deposit. Moffat, who after a stormy past has decided to lead a scrupulous and honest life, reveals to the man that this is a very rich silver lining. The lease is about to expire and the miner tries to speed up the work to make the most of the vein of precious metal, but Jarboe, having learned of the discovery, tries with some partners to force the miner and his daughter to evacuate. When Moffat takes the side of the miner and the girl, the wrath of the opponents is unleashed against him, but after a series of attacks and attacks, they have the worst: Moffat can complete the work for the miner and marry his daughter.
text_b: Miners' salary money has been stolen from an inn safe, and Sheriff Jake is suspicious of Carruthers, a young cowboy found at the scene moments after the robbery. Carruthers is instead a federal marshal sent by the government to suppress acts of banditry occurring in the area. He managed to discover that the perpetrator of the robbery is part of a large gang, secretly led by Malgrove, one of the most important citizens. Malgrove has learned of the existence of rich gold veins in the area and wants to force the inhabitants of the area to leave, intercepting food deliveries from the city. After a series of shootings and endless horseback chases across the West, Sheriff Carruthers is able to identify all the culprits and bring them to justice.

anchor_text: Charlotte Castang is a 13-year-old working-class girl growing up without a mother in the French provinces. Her widowed father works as a plumber and is overburdened with raising his daughter, while the household is run by housekeeper Léone. Charlotte's only friend is the somewhat sickly Lulu, whose affection is annoying to Charlotte. She lives in the day and dreams of breaking out of her narrow world. One day, she meets Clara Baumann, a pianist who travels the world and is hailed as a child prodigy. She begins to project her dreams of glamour and the "big world" onto Clara. She recklessly proposes to accompany her on her concert tours. When the somewhat naive dream is finally shattered and Charlotte is rejected by Clara, she turns back to her surroundings. She finds that love, affection, and admiration can be found in the circle of her friends and family.
text_a: Thirteen-year-old Charlotte, grumpy and always dissatisfied with everything, lives with her father Antoine and brother Jacky in a provincial French commune. Antoine hired Léone, a colf, to take care of her daughter. On the last day of school before the summer holidays, while watching a concert on television, Charlotte falls in love with a famous pianist, her peer Clara Bauman, who is about to perform in her hometown. She tries everything to meet her, but once she succeeds in her "enterprise", Charlotte discovers that Clara is nothing more than a proud, spoiled and spiteful girl; she herself will end up a victim of the pianist's evil joke.
text_b: The year is 1888. Lulù, a former florist, wants to make a career in the world of variety, and that's why she's Dr. Schön's lover. However, he, aware of her numerous affairs, decides to leave her to marry Charlotte Marie Adelaide. Alwa, son of Schön and in love with Lulù, writing for a show. During the premiere, Dr. Schön is surprised by Charlotte in Lulu's arms and, because of this, decides to marry the latter. However, during the wedding party, Schön, jealous of the presence of the old Schigolch (whom Lulù indicates as his father) in the wedding bedroom, grabs the gun and, in the ensuing dispute with Lulù, is accidentally killed by her. Prosecuted, Lulu is found guilty, but Alwa - in league with Countess Anna Geschwitz, also attracted to Lulu - creates a diversion and flees with her. On the train, however, the two are recognized and blackmailed by the Marquis Casti-Piani, who takes them to a clandestine party on board a boat in France, holding them hostage and threatening to hand them over to the police. Casti-Piani wants to sell Lulu to an Egyptian, prompting Alwa, Schilgoch and Lulu to flee again, taking refuge in London. Reduced to poverty, Lulu decides to prostitute herself to feed herself and her friends, ending up a victim of Jack the Ripper.

anchor_text: Forty-year-old Billy Chapel is a Detroit Tigers baseball player whose pitching career is coming to an end. He meets his girlfriend Jane Aubrey in New York City before the final game of the season, who tells him she's got a new job in London. Chapel is distracted during the game; in a first flashback, he is shown meeting Jane five years earlier. She had a car breakdown, he helped her and invited her to his team's game. The next flashback shows Davis Birch, a teammate of Chapel's, moving to another team in New York. Chapel's helping his family move. Some further flashbacks show the development of the relationship with Jane as well as a meeting Chapel had with Jane's daughter Heather who wants to study. During the course of the game, Jane packs her things to leave for London. That Chapel, with these thoughts running through his head, is on the verge of a perfect game, he does not realize until his last inning, and although he actually finishes this extraordinary performance, Chapel returns to his hotel after the game devastated. The next morning, he drives to the airport, where he meets Jane, who has been watching the game on television at the airport and has deliberately missed her flight to London so that she can watch the end of the game. The movie ends with the happy ending of the two of them reconciling.
text_a: Billy Chapel, baseball champion of the Detroit Tigers at the end of his career, faces the last game before retirement, following an injury to a hand; the old owner has sold the team and the following year will be sold to the San Francisco Giants. After 19 years with the Tigers, Bill decides to call it quits after the last game of the season against the New York Yankees. His intense focus on baseball, however, has strained relations with his girlfriend, Jane Aubrey, who tells him he must leave after accepting a job offer in London. As the woman waits for the plane, she watches the game on television while Billy begins to think back to the past five years, since he met Jane on a highway. Lost in his thoughts, Billy begins to lose sight of the game and blames a pain in one shoulder that causes him to drop the ball twice out of the strike zone and being put on the bench and here, he keeps thinking about his deceased father and Jane. After finishing the perfect game, Billy sits alone in his hotel room realizing that his life as a gambler is over. Despite his extraordinary achievement, Billy weeps not only for the loss of baseball but also for Jane. The next morning, Billy goes to the airport to get information about a flight to London. Here, he finds Jane confessing to him that she missed her flight the night before to see the perfect match after which they embrace and reconcile while waiting together for the plane.
text_b: The film chronicles the life of Billy Hope (Jake Gyllenhaal), a boxer who has managed to reach the pinnacle of his career. Undefeated in dozens of fights, he holds the title of world champion in the light heavyweight division. In addition to athletic success, Hope has his loving and understanding wife Maureen (Rachel McAdams) and daughter Leila (Oona Laurence). But his life changes drastically after being intimidated by the pugilist Miguel Magic Escobar (Miguel Gomez), and unleashing a fact that will leave him sunk, physically and financially. Although Hope has fallen out of favor, the boxer does not give up and goes to Titus Tick Wills (Forest Whitaker), a coach who, retired from his work at a professional level, is dedicated to training amateur boxers. As Hope struggles to find redemption, she realizes that if she wants her life back, she will have to face her past again and regain her faith in herself.

anchor_text: Nick Barrow carefully plans robberies that he then resells to the highest bidder. Alejandro Vasquez buys one from him, realizes it but is murdered once in possession of the money. Nick and his daughter then find themselves pursued by Alejandro's father, who is a godfather to the mafia, hit men and rogue cops.
text_a: Nick Barrow, a thief wanted across the city by mob bosses, contract killers and corrupt cops, decides before his life is over to write an insurance policy in the name of his estranged daughter Cate. For the policy to be active, however, it takes 21 days, during which time Nick will have to do whatever it takes to stay alive.
text_b: Nick Prescott, a former Special Forces soldier, returns to his hometown in Texas: shortly after, his father is murdered. Prescott discovers that the killers are part of a criminal organization that is attempting to take control of the city.

anchor_text: Lonnie Lucky Man Johnson is a very famous drag racing driver. He's part of a team sponsored by Fast Company and managed by a corrupt man named Phil Adamson. Lonnie also became the mentor of a young pilot, Billy Crocker, nicknamed "Billy the Kid". Lonnie then comes into conflict with Phil Adamson. The latter teamed up with Lonnie's rival, Gary Black, nicknamed The Blacksmith ( the blacksmith ).
text_a: Deeply scarred by his prison experience, the ex-convict, after serving his sentence, gets out of prison and finds work as a truck driver in a transport company, where drivers risk their lives daily, forced to engage in high-speed routes to be paid based on deliveries. He reluctantly accepts the violent bullying of the boss, until he challenges him in a mad race aboard two beasts, where the boss takes his life.
text_b: The story is set in the world of drag racing, car acceleration races, in which FastCo, a company that produces engine oil, participates with a team. The team's top driver is Lonnie Johnson, a true pioneer of these races and, thanks to his popularity, has brought great fortune to Phil, team manager on behalf of FastCo. After a breakup between Phil and Lonnie, the driver leaves the team and is replaced by his rival Gary Black. Lonnie decides to create an independent team by continuing to participate in these races, having the talented Bill race alongside him.

text_a: Lonnie "Lucky Man" Johnson is a famous drag racing driver. Because of his expertise he has managed to become the star runner of his sponsor, Fast Company, a company run by Phil Adamson a man of dubious reputation. In addition Johnson has become the mentor of a promising young racer Billy "The Kid" Brooker. When Adamson makes a deal with Johnson's rival, Gary "The Blacksmith" Black, the car he was racing in is stolen. When Johnson and Brooker find out, they decide to recover it with the intention of participating with it, but independently and without any link with their previous company, in the next competition. This decision infuriates Adamson who will try by any means to impede the plans of his former star.
text_b: Deeply scarred by his prison experience, the ex-convict, after serving his sentence, gets out of prison and finds work as a truck driver in a transport company, where drivers risk their lives daily, forced to engage in high-speed routes to be paid based on deliveries. He reluctantly accepts the violent bullying of the boss, until he challenges him in a mad race aboard two beasts, where the boss takes his life.

anchor_text: Raj Singh Puri is very good friends with L. K. Malhotra and his younger brother J. K. Malhotra. The brothers run the big company Malhotra Empire and Raj is their employee. After Raj's wife Shalini was run over, he promises her on his deathbed to let his three daughters Avantika, Sania and Isha marry whoever they want. When they return home from London, the eldest daughter, Avantika, marries. The marriage was arranged. When the second daughter wants to marry her lover, the father reluctantly agrees. But soon there are the first difficulties. Sania wants to file for divorce because she doesn't get along with her in-laws at all. That's why Raj and his youngest daughter Isha are against love and relationships. But this changes for Isha when the heir to the Malhotra empire, her childhood friend Ronit, courts her. Raj, on the other hand, had already arranged a marriage for him with the stony rich Monishka. When he hears of Ronit's marriage plans with Isha, Raj is very shocked and wants to prevent the wedding. Ronit now has to prove to Raj that Isha is the one for him. So Ronit arranges a meeting with Monishka and Raj. In this meeting, Raj will ask Monishka three questions. When Raj hears their answers, he realizes that Monishka is the wrong woman for Ronit. Raj asks the Malhotras to break off the engagement. But J.K. Malhotra doesn't even think about it and insults Raj. Until Ronit's mother and uncle bring J.K. to his senses, nothing will stand in the way of Isha and Ronit's marriage.
text_a: Raj Mathur is a truck driver and has a small business. Priya is an educated, successful woman who works as a fashion designer. They both meet in an accident. Priya is initially not impressed by Raj and complains at a wedding about the rude truck driver who is to blame for her showing up so late. Raj overhears the conversation and introduces himself to her. They get along well, and Priya gives him her number. Raj immediately falls in love and wants to meet Priya. However, he loses her phone number and desperately searches for her. When he finds Priya again, he learns that she wants to get engaged and travel to Greece soon. Raj doesn't think twice and flies with her to Athens. But the plane has to make an emergency landing, and it's running late. During this time, Priya also falls in love with Raj. She breaks off the planned engagement with Sameer and marries Raj with the consent of her parents. On the first wedding day, the difficulties and quarrels in their relationship begin, which go so far that the couple separates after a violent argument broken by Raj from the fence. Priya is upset and wants to fly back to her Greek homeland. Raj tries to stop them, but to no avail. The only thing that can stop Priya at the last second is Raj's talisman, which was a symbol of her love. Raj gets him on the plane and actually Priya gets off the plane and surprises him at home. Their dramatic relationship continues.
text_b: Raj Singh Puri (Jackie Shroff) is the best friend of L.K. Malhotra (Anang Desai) who is J.K.'s younger brother. Malhotra is Amrish Puri. Both brothers are prominent businessmen and Raj is their employee. Following the death of his wife Shalini (Rati Agnihotri), Raj is left alone with his three daughters, Avantika (Avni Vasa), Sania (Himani Rawat) and Isha (Kareena Kapoor). While the first's marriage was arranged, the second married the one she chose. But the second wife asked for a divorce. All this has caused the despair of Raj and his youngest daughter Isha who are firmly against love and human relationships. But Isha changes her mind when she meets and falls in love with the heir to the Malhotra empire, Ronit (Hrithik Roshan), son of L.K. Malhotra, what are you doing? When they ask for Raj's consent, he refuses and Isha stops seeing Ronit. Raj then arranges the marriage of the latter with Monishka Rai (Kiran Rathod), daughter of another businessman.

anchor_text: The three ex-students Johann, Max and Mao are doing more badly than well in Graz. Johann works at the post office and Max is still trying to realize his revolutionary advertising ideas. Mao occasionally acts as a drug dealer and thus meets the pimp Schorsch, who advises her to make a porn film. Johann and Max are quickly persuaded and through a casting they also find the female leads Mara and Martha. But soon it turns out that not everything is so easy and sex in front of the camera is really hard work.
text_a: Johann, Max and Mao, three former students, vegetate in Graz. Johann works at La Poste, Max tries to impose his revolutionary ideas in advertising. Mao sometimes deals drugs for Schorsch who introduces him to a pimp, who advises him to shoot in a porn movie. Johann and Max quickly agreed and hired Mara and Martha for the lead roles after a casting. But soon it turns out that everything is not so light and that sex in front of the camera is not such an easy job.
text_b: It is a documentary about teenagers and young men in Prague who prostitute themselves both in the city and in other European cities. The director interviews a group of men between the ages of 14 and 20 to find out more about their lives and the reasons behind their choices. The film explores their hopes and fears, what they hope to do in the future and the possibility of being infected with HIV. The film also features comments from a man who organizes meetings with tourists in Prague's central station. The dialogue is in Czech with English and English subtitles.

anchor_text: Al McCord leaves to go to his favorite restaurant, when he meets an attractive young woman (Ellie) who is looking for a ride to the Mojave Desert, where her mother lives. The events will lead Ellie to fall in love with Al, while Al will fall in love with his mother Julie, despite the bulky presence of his partner.
text_a: Al McCord (Danny Aiello), a man in the midst of a midlife crisis, is dining at his favorite restaurant when he meets an attractive young woman (Angelina Jolie) who is looking for someone to take her to a town in the Mojave Desert, where her mother (Anne Archer) lives.
text_b: When his wife is kidnapped, Brent Magna must follow the kidnapper's instructions to save her. During his run through Sofia, he meets a young woman who will help him in his quest, this teenage girl is a delinquent.

anchor_text: Victoria Spick is a young lawyer in the midst of a turmoil. While attending a wedding, a young woman is stabbed and one of her friends (the woman's companion) is accused. Although embarrassed at the thought of defending one of her acquaintances, she eventually accepted the case. At the same time, overwhelmed with managing her day-to-day, she hires Sam, a former drug dealer she got out of the business, as an au pair to look after her two young daughters. She also discovers that her ex, who aspires to be a writer, has posted an inspired account of their story on the Internet, overwhelming her with all sorts of ills. Despite being dynamic, Victoria is on the brink of crisis, both professionally and romantically.
text_a: Victoria Spick is a young lawyer. While attending a wedding, a young woman is stabbed. And it is precisely one of his friends, the woman's companion, who is accused. Although she resents the idea of defending someone involved in this kind of gender-based violence, she eventually accepts the assignment. At the same time, overwhelmed by the stress of everyday life, she hires Sam as an au pair to take care of her two young daughters. In addition, she discovers that her ex-husband, who aspires to be a writer, has published on the Internet a story inspired by her true story. Despite her dynamism, Victoria is on the verge of a crisis, both professional and sentimental.
text_b: Erica Bain is a radio host in New York. She's engaged to David. One night, while they were taking a lovers' stroll through Central Park, they were violently attacked. David died of his wounds. After a long convalescence, Erica develops an aggressive psychosis and illegally acquires a gun. Witnessing a bloody robbery in a supermarket, and overcome with terror, she responds viscerally by shooting the murderer. Shortly thereafter, when two thugs burst into a subway train and terrorized the passengers before attacking her, she killed them and ran away. Detective Mercer, assigned to investigate this series of murders, is also one of Erica's most loyal listeners to the urban chronicles. He consults her because he thinks that the investigations she's conducting in the city can put him on the trail of the New York vigilante. Meanwhile, caught in an offensive spiral, Erica sets out to find David's killers.

anchor_text: Victoria is a criminal lawyer who has to cope with many daily dramas: from the decline of sexual desire, compensated by casual and fleeting encounters, to the assaults of an ex-husband who tries to exploit, even economically, the gritty aspects of their past relationship. There is no lack of guilt towards the two young daughters, neglected in the search for a strenuous professional affirmation. He agrees to defend his friend, Vincent, relying on two unlikely witnesses, a chimpanzee and a Dalmatian dog, wins and stumbles into new drama.
text_a: Erica Bain is a radio host in New York. She's engaged to David. One night, while they were taking a lovers' stroll through Central Park, they were violently attacked. David died of his wounds. After a long convalescence, Erica develops an aggressive psychosis and illegally acquires a gun. Witnessing a bloody robbery in a supermarket, and overcome with terror, she responds viscerally by shooting the murderer. Shortly thereafter, when two thugs burst into a subway train and terrorized the passengers before attacking her, she killed them and ran away. Detective Mercer, assigned to investigate this series of murders, is also one of Erica's most loyal listeners to the urban chronicles. He consults her because he thinks that the investigations she's conducting in the city can put him on the trail of the New York vigilante. Meanwhile, caught in an offensive spiral, Erica sets out to find David's killers.
text_b: Victoria Spick is a young lawyer. While attending a wedding, a young woman is stabbed. And it is precisely one of his friends, the woman's companion, who is accused. Although she resents the idea of defending someone involved in this kind of gender-based violence, she eventually accepts the assignment. At the same time, overwhelmed by the stress of everyday life, she hires Sam as an au pair to take care of her two young daughters. In addition, she discovers that her ex-husband, who aspires to be a writer, has published on the Internet a story inspired by her true story. Despite her dynamism, Victoria is on the verge of a crisis, both professional and sentimental.

anchor_text: In Crime, Captain Malinowski is often faced with difficult cases. Then one day, his daughter is raped and murdered, and his life is turned upside down. Seeing his distress, his colleagues conduct the investigation very quickly. One man, Daniel Eckmann, was quickly arrested and convicted. But he insists he's innocent and decides to write a letter to the victim's father, Malinowski. The latter will then seem to doubt the hasty conclusions of the investigation and conduct a counter-investigation in solitary, hiding from his wife. The testimony of a woman in love with the criminal in prison, during the criminal's retrial, finally acquits him. He finds Captain Malinowski at home... At the end of the film he says that fathers who swear to kill the murderers of their children after 20 years in prison don't do it; he does. His superior, full of doubts, comes to find him at the place where his daughter is buried and asks him how he will now continue his life...
text_a: In the criminal brigade, Captain Malinowski often has to deal with difficult situations. One day, her daughter is raped and then murdered, and her life is shaken and seems to come to a halt. At the sight of this great misfortune, his colleagues quickly take charge of the investigation. A man, Daniel Eckman, is soon arrested and later convicted, though he claims to be innocent. Given the events, the convict writes a letter to the victim's father, Malinowski, who then begins to doubt the first and hasty conclusions of the investigation, and consequently will lead a counter-investigation alone.
text_b: The film is about Artur Ludvik, aka Gerard, Deputy Foreign Minister of Czechoslovakia. He realizes he's being watched and shadowed. One day he is arrested by an organization that describes itself as over the ruling party. He's locked up in solitary confinement for months without ever learning the reasons. Using brainwashing methods, including sleep deprivation and forcing him to go up and down constantly, he is slowly forced to confess to imaginary crimes, including treason. He must repeat these confessions in a show trial and is sentenced to life imprisonment. With the onset of de-Stalinization, he was released and rehabilitated a few years later. Later, he meets his now-disgraced tormentor, who tries to embellish his former role. He emigrates with his family in the 1960s - his wife is French - to France and tells the French Communists, whom he still knows from the Résistance, his experiences. Together with his wife, he is writing a book about it and wants to publish it in 1968 in the course of the Prague Spring in Czechoslovakia. When he arrives in Prague, he witnesses the tanks of the Warsaw Pact rolling through the streets of Prague, and is completely disillusioned.

anchor_text: Claude Lantier is the son of Gervaise Macquart and Auguste Lantier (see L'Assommoir, novel where we learn that he was brought at the age of eight to Plassans by an old gentleman seduced by the quality of his drawings). He also appears in Le Ventre de Paris. Here he is the childhood friend of novelist Pierre Sandoz, a character in which Zola put much of himself. With Sandoz and other painters or sculptors, Claude fought to impose a new form of painting, far removed from the neo-classical canons favored by official exhibitions. If some of them eventually succeed in establishing themselves, Lantier goes from failure to failure, remaining misunderstood by the public and often by his own friends. The novel is also a story of love and friendship. Claude Lantier met a young woman named Christine on the porch of his apartment building on a rainy night, with whom he would share his life and his failures. They settled in the countryside, where Claude first found relief. They have a child, but he's hydrocephalus, and he'll die when he's twelve. In the meantime, the couple moved back to Paris, where Claude found both his friends and his sense of failure. Eventually he separated from his wife to spend his time in a large shed where he undertook a gigantic work, a canvas that he will leave unfinished and before which he will hang himself.
text_a: Claude Lantier, son of Gervaise Macquart, together with other painters and sculptors, struggles to impose a new form of art, far from the neoclassical canons that at the time receive the favor of gallery owners and large official exhibitions. Although some of his comrades eventually prevail, Lantier goes from defeat to defeat, remaining misunderstood by the public and often even by his own friends. The novel also weaves together stories of love and friendship. On a rainy night, Claude Lantier meets a young woman, Christine, who is destined to become his life partner. The couple move to the countryside and have a son who, however, suffers from hydrocephalus and dies at the age of twelve. Meanwhile, the couple has returned to live in Paris, where Claude rediscovers his friends but also the clear sense of his defeat. He ends up separating from his partner and spending most of his time in a large shed where he began to paint a gigantic work, Plein Air, which he will leave unfinished by hanging himself in front of it.
text_b: The protagonist of the Ventre de Paris, Florent, is a young man at the time identified as an opponent of the government of Napoleon III and for this reason deported to the Caienna at the penitentiary of Devil's Island. Seven years later, Florent, escaped from prison, returns to Paris and finds his half-brother Quenu, whom he had raised by giving up his studies. Quenu has meanwhile created a good economic position: he has a sausage shop in the Halles area, the large Parisian food market, and a wife, Lisa Macquart (Antoine's daughter), beautiful and envied. Florent finds himself caught between the meanness and intrigue that characterize the environment of small shopkeepers. In his naïve zeal, he thinks he can further his republican ideals by plotting against the government. It will be his sister-in-law Lisa who denounces him, resulting in his arrest and deportation. Among the characters of the novel is also the painter Claude Lantier, son of the sister of Lisa Macquart (Gervaise Macquart, protagonist of L'Assommoir), who will be the protagonist of the novel L'Opera fourteenth novel of the cycle de I Rougon-Macquart.

anchor_text: After being accidentally left for dead by his friends, Robert Griffin escapes from the mental hospital where he was confined in Africa. Back in England, he has only one thing on his mind: revenge. Then he meets a scientist, who wants to test his invisibility formula...
text_a: Sir Geoffrey Radcliffe is sentenced to death for the murder of his brother, a crime he did not commit. Dr. Frank Griffin, the brother of the original Invisible Man, injects him with the invisibility drug in his cell. Radcliffe disappears from his cell and sets out to find the real culprit behind his brother's death.
text_b: Dr. Drury, a renowned scientist, is testing an invisibility serum on a poor escaped convict. The experiment succeeds but the thief escapes the scientist's control and, in the city, takes revenge on those who betrayed him.

anchor_text: In 1868, in the Arizona Territory, Cochise was on the warpath. Captain Coburn was charged with driving the settlers to Apache Wells so that he could better protect them from Indian attacks. That would require a load of 40 repeating rifles to arrive. Coburn is ordered to meet that shipment, but he's going to be thwarted by one of his men, Corporal Bodine, who wants to resell the guns to the Indians...
text_a: A group of Mormons, led by Elder Wiggs (Ward Bond), set off for the San Juan River territory. To accomplish their task, they hire two horse dealers: Travis Blue (Ben Johnson) and Sandy (Harry Carey Jr.), who will be in charge of guiding the caravan. Along the way they are joined by an itinerant healer, along with his assistants Prudence Perkins (Kathleen O'Malley) and Denver (Joanne Dru). However, the expedition will be interrupted by the arrival of a gang of criminals.
text_b: This is the Arizona Territory, 1868. The Apaches, led by Cochise, are on the warpath. To try and keep up with them, you have to go pick up 40 new repeating rifles. In the end, the mission will be entrusted to Captain Coburn, who will succeed in carrying it out. One of the recruits and her date, Mike, is still in the field.

anchor_text: Marybeth has managed to escape the clutches of Victor Crowley. She learns the truth about her curse, and returns to the haunted swamp of New Orleans to avenge her family and kill Victor Crowley once and for all.
text_a: After managing to escape Victor Crowley (Kane Hodder), Marybeth (Danielle Harris) now knows the truth about his curse, and heads to the haunted swamp of New Orleans to seek revenge for her family and kill him once and for all.[1]
text_b: Three couples are invited to spend the night in the villa of a wealthy industrialist. Not far from the mansion, a mad caveman wakes up a legion of hungry undead. The creatures from beyond the grave attack the guests as well as the owner, all trapped in the residence under siege by the zombies. A nightmare is about to begin...

anchor_text: The multinational Chaank Armament Corporation produces deadly weapons in the very near future. For the multinational collaborates a mad scientist, Jack Dante, devourer of cartoons and porn magazines, hidden in a bunker where he lives and where he is designing the experiment WarBeast, where he is building a war machine completely of steel and indestructible. As the experiment succeeds the scientist works to enslave the top of society to his will by threatening them with his invention with which he kills anyone who tries to oppose him. Dr. Hayden Cale, with whom Dante is in love, investigates the murders of the scientist's enemies, who is aware of her investigation but lets her do it. Meanwhile, a small task force of three environmental activists break into the corporation's headquarters to investigate its activities, only to discover the existence of the death machine. In their attempt to destroy it, they are aided by Dr. Cale, and together they must try to escape the onslaught of Dante's automaton, which wants to kill them to hide the truth.
text_a: In an effort to reduce the high crime rate in Johannesburg, South African police decide to buy armored robots from the arms manufacturer Tetravaal, which successfully reduce crime. Its inventor, Deon Wilson (Dev Patel) creates at his home a prototype of artificial intelligence, which imitates the human mind to the point of feeling emotions and thinking for itself. However, Tetravaal's director, Michelle Bradley (Sigourney Weaver), denies him permission to test it on a police robot. Deon steals a damaged robot before it's destroyed, taking it in his van. Along the way he is kidnapped by a group of gangsters, Ninja (Watkin Tudor Jones), Yolandi (Yolandi Visser) and Amerika (José Pablo Cantillo), and forced to reprogram the robot police for the purpose of employing it for their benefit. Deon takes the opportunity to install his new software on the damaged robot and gives it the name Chappie.[6][7] Ninja gang only has a few days to pay a debt to Hippo, a powerful gangster. Ninja trains Chappie in the use of weapons and Yolandi manages to convince him to turn to crime. In this way, the gang uses the robot to rob a cash truck. Vincent (Hugh Jackman), who has investigated Chappie after following Deon into hiding, will also attempt to use him for his own purposes.[6][7]
text_b: A new director has been appointed to the Chaank Weapons Factory, which has a highly advanced and futuristic technology. Tough, efficient and intelligent, she is Hayden Cale, a woman who must investigate the strange circumstances of her predecessor's death in office. For this he will face a psychotic (Jack Dante) with a special ingenuity to create and design murderous weapons, and who also has a thirst for power without limit. The duel between these characters transforms into a technological nightmare, into an uncontrollable desire for destruction, into a desperate hunt that brings the fates of five beings together to confront Warbeast, the diabolical killing machine.

anchor_text: Charles Monroe King was a first sergeant in the U.S. Army and a veteran of the Iraq War. Feeling in danger, he decides to write a diary for his son Jordan, who was born after he left Iraq. In it, he shares his fears, his doubts, and prepares him for life without him.
text_a: Based on a true story, while First Sergeant Charles Monroe King is deployed to Iraq, he keeps a diary of love and advice for his young son. Sharing this diary with his son, his fiancée, Dana Canedy reflects on his unlikely but powerful romantic relationship with King.[2]
text_b: Brandon King is a hero of the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. Upon returning to Texas, he tries to reconnect with his old life, reconnect with his family and his best friend, Steve Shriver, who was with him in Iraq. On the day his contract ended, he learned that the military had unilaterally extended his contract, and that he had to return to Iraq. He refuses.

anchor_text: Yolanda's boyfriend, a singer and drug addict, died of an overdose (and strychnine). The police are looking for her and she decides to hide in a convent. But a nun's life isn't just about praying and loving the Lord. Drugs, conspiracies, secrets, the tiger, they all go together.
text_a: Yolanda is a singer who performs in the main night clubs of Madrid. One night, the boyfriend's body is found, dead from an overdose. For fear of being accused by the police, the girl decides to take refuge in a convent. Humiliated Redemptorist nuns lead a life of excess, writing obscene novels, raising beasts, injecting themselves with heroin, and even dealing it.
text_b: A Hindu sage with knowledge of African voodoo and Indian magic named Krisna (Jacinto Molina) has as a collaborator his lover, Kala (Mirta Miller). Their mission is to resurrect dead women for use in murders.

anchor_text: High school student Alex Truelove and Claire have been best friends since childhood. Eventually, though, more develops between the two, and they kiss and date. For the first time, they plan to book a hotel room. But then Alex meets gay teenager Elliot at a party. Alex becomes friends with Elliot, and the two of them start seeing each other more and more. Elliot, who is obviously in love with Alex, causes Alex to question his own sexuality and confess it to his friend Del. This one, however, attributes this to the fear of the first time with Claire. One day when Alex meets Elliot again, he kisses him, but immediately regrets it and storms away. Just before Alex and Claire experience their first time in the hotel room, Alex confesses that he has feelings for someone else and Claire throws him out. Alex avoids Elliot and has drunken sex at a party with a girl he just met there. Claire catches him doing it and runs away, Alex follows her and falls into a pool. Claire later finds him outside the pool and Alex confesses to her that he's gay. The two of them decide to go to the prom together anyway. At the prom, it turns out that Claire invited Elliot to accompany Alex to the prom because neither of them would make the first move. At first, Alex is still worried that everyone is watching him, but eventually the affection for Elliot is so strong that he just kisses him. The film ends with Alex and Claire creating a coming-out video, which is overlaid with coming-out videos from many other real people.
text_a: Eighteen-year-old Alex Owens works as a welder by day and as a dancer in a nightclub, cultivating the dream of entering the Pittsburgh Dance Academy. He trains relentlessly, but when he goes to school for an audition, he is overcome by discouragement and gives up. One night at the club where he performs, he meets Nick Hurley, his boss at the factory. The two of them like each other, but Alex doesn't think it's right to start a relationship with her boss. In the days that follow, an ambiguous character, Carlos, tries to convince Alex and his friend Jeanie to work at his strip club. The girls refuse, but only Nick's intervention will dissuade Carlos, who has now moved on to threats. So Alex starts dating Nick, and soon the two find themselves entwined in a passionate love affair. Meanwhile, Alex learns that she has been accepted to an audition for the Academy of Dance; but this is thanks to a recommendation from Nick. There's no way he's going to audition. Although emotion makes her miss the first step, she bravely starts again and throws herself into her compelling choreography: a perfect combination of classical, modern and breakdancing, which allows her to achieve admission.
text_b: High school student Alex Truelove has long been Claire's best friend, but after discovering that her mother has cancer tests, they kiss and begin dating. They're planning on booking a hotel room. At a party, Alex meets Elliot, an openly gay teenager, and also begins to have feelings for him, which leads him to question his sexuality. On the night of the first time they have sex, Alex admits to Claire that he has feelings for someone else and then she dumps him. Avoiding Elliot, Alex goes to a party where he sleeps drunk with a girl he just met. Claire discovers them, and Alex chases her into the night. He falls into a pool where suppressed memories of his childhood return to him. Claire finds him outside the pool, at which point she tells him he's gay. They decide to go to the dance together anyway. At the prom, Claire reveals that she invited Elliot to be Alex's actual date, knowing that neither of them would actually take the plunge without her "passing the baton". While Alex worries about being stared at, she decides her affection for Elliot is stronger and kisses him. The story ends with Alex creating a video with Claire detailing her coming out, which overlaps with videos from the real lives of many other people.

anchor_text: Said, Sarah and Yanis are besieged in their sawmill by Adama's gang who came to retrieve drugs stolen from the seals of a gendarmerie and hidden in a car.
text_a: A father is in the process of selling his sawmill so that he can get the money to give to his daughter. When one of the employees hides a load of drugs inside the sawmill, he'll find himself involved in a big fight.
text_b: Two hit men arrive in San Francisco charged with recovering three packages of heroin smuggled into the luggage of unsuspecting passengers. They're being chased by a couple of cops.

anchor_text: In 2044 Earth is an overpopulated and polluted planet, where most individuals live in poverty and energy sources are almost completely depleted. The only entertainment for ordinary people is OASIS (an acronym for Ontologically Anthropocentric Sensory Immersive Simulation), a virtual world created by programmer James Halliday that can be accessed for free, thanks to a simple viewfinder and a pair of haptic gloves and where you can perform any activity, from school to sports. Real life is subordinate to the virtual one: in fact in case of virtual death all the advances, the skins, the virtual money and the fame are lost. Upon Halliday's death, it is revealed that he has bequeathed his virtual world - worth billions of dollars - to the first person to successfully solve a series of puzzles and mind games scattered throughout the OASIS universe. Among them is Wade Owen Watts, an eighteen-year-old overweight nerd with a passion for video games, role-playing games and 1980s culture in general, who lives with his aunt in Oklahoma City in the "cataste", a poverty-stricken district made up of containers and mobile homes stacked on top of each other. Wade has an advantage over many Gunters: he grew up in the Halliday myth, knows everything about Halliday's life and personal tastes, and this could represent a considerable advantage for solving intricate puzzles. But the opportunity to get their hands on OASIS has also attracted the IOI (Innovative Online Industries), a powerful and unbiased multinational willing to do anything to win the challenge.
text_a: In the year 2044[1][2] the world is a disaster. Energy sources are practically exhausted, there is overpopulation and the price of fuel is skyrocketing. In the midst of a huge worldwide economic depression most people survive as they can. However, a virtual reality video game called OASIS provides the escape route that people need. People spend more time playing the game than they do in real life. The game offers every conceivable possibility and anything is possible, both playing and working. The creator of OASIS is James Halliday, a huge fan of the 1980s as well as a fantastic video game programmer who, along with his partner Ogden Morrow, has amassed an immense fortune thanks to his creation. After his death, it is announced in a video that the game contains an easter egg, and that whoever finds it will inherit his entire fortune and total control of GSS, the company that owns OASIS. Wade Watts is a young orphan living with his aunt on the outskirts of Columbus. Wade secretly attends the OASIS public education system, and in his spare time, under the alias Parzival, he devotes himself to trying to unravel Halliday's secret. Many other OASIS users - including Hache, Wade's best friend - are dedicated to the same task, and are popularly known as "gunters" (from egg hunter). Also looking for Halliday's Easter egg is the powerful IOI (of Innovative Online Industries), a company providing connection to OASIS that wants to take control of GSS in order to charge for access to it. In the midst of this struggle for control of OASIS, Wade will be the first to find the first of the clues that lead to the resolution of Halliday's riddle.
text_b: The work is about the operator of a simulated metropolitan area used for market research purposes. The gigantic computer of the so-called TEAG (Test AG) is making this possible. In its stores are thousands of inhabitants whose surroundings have been designed down to the smallest detail. The simulation is so good that the inhabitants have a consciousness of their own, but are unaware that they exist only as software in a computer. Over time, the protagonist Douglas Hall, the technical director of the facility, realizes more and more that his world is not real either, but also exists only as a simulation in a higher reality. With the help of Jinx, an administrator of the facility there who has "ascended" from this higher reality, he finally manages not only to save his own simulated world from destruction by the "Great Simulator" of the higher world, but at the same time to transfer his own mind into his body, which was perfectly mimicked externally, and thus hurl the Great Simulator to the simulated level of the computer, where his body is destroyed in the course of a popular uprising against the political-economic abuse of the TEAG. The carefully told love story between Jinx and Douglas forms the emotional backdrop of the story.

anchor_text: In the United States, in 1862, during the American Civil War, after the loss of Fort Holman, abandoned by the Union army without fighting, Colonel Pembroke is expelled from the army on charges of treason. Subsequently, Pembroke is accused of theft and arrested by a Northern detachment, along with a low-caliber bandit, Eli Sampson. The two men are being taken to a northern fort. Here, Pembroke recruits, after an agreement with Major Charles Ballard, commander of the fort, a group of seven men, condemned to death, to follow him in the recapture of the fort. Only after a series of misadventures, the group will be able to enter the fort, thanks also to the help of Sampson, who will infiltrate the ranks of the southerners, and will be able to take control of it. In the end, Pembroke and Sampson will be the only survivors; Pembroke will also take the satisfaction of taking revenge on the fort's commander, Major Ward who had earlier blackmailed Colonel Pembroke by trading his son's life for the abandonment of Fort Holman, resulting in Pembroke's dishonorable discharge.
text_a: An adjutant of the Confederate army, Lloyd, has brought into his possession a shipment of gold with which General Sibley intended to purchase arms and ammunition. Lloyd and his buddies flee to the Mexican border. Sibley appoints three death row inmates - gentleman thug Chattanooga Jim, preacher Tim Dooley, and horse thief Fidel Ramírez - who can buy their lives by capturing Lloyds and recovering the gold. The task of the three soldiers is complicated by the intervention of the Mexican bandits around their leader El Primero, in whose hands the precious cargo falls. However, in cooperation with Lloyd, the three allies succeed in recovering the gold. In the ensuing gunfight, Lloyd is fatally wounded. When they meet with General Sibley, each of the three will receive a horse and their freedom. The next day, the exchange of the gold and weapons that the Mexicans are supplying will take place. Chattanooga, Dooley and Ramirez steal the car with the gold and get away.
text_b: During the Civil War, the Union army loses Fort Holman, abandoned by the troops before they had even engaged in combat. Now the fort is in the hands of the Confederates. Colonel Pembroke, commander of the fort, is charged with treason for this act. So he sets out on a suicide mission: to storm the enemy's stronghold in order not to be convicted of the crime and get free, even though he would not be able to return to the army. For this, he manages to convince 7 prisoners sentenced to death to follow him in exchange for freedom, with the additional argument of the existence of gold hidden under the fort and with the promise of giving them part of the gold in exchange for their help in taking the fort. At the end of the plot, the colonel's true motives are revealed. It turns out that there is no gold and that the current Confederate chief of the fort, Major Ward, had Pembroke's son in his hands and that he blackmailed Pembroke into leaving the fort, using his son to do so only to still kill him afterwards. Therefore, in revenge, he has organized the assault on the fort with the condemned. The Confederates are defeated and the fort is taken. Only Pembroke, Eli, one of the condemned prisoners who later learns the truth and supports him, and Ward survive the massacre. During the event Pembroke manages to eventually take Ward prisoner, but later, in revenge for the murder of his son, Pembroke kills him with his own sword when Ward demands Pembroke's rights as a prisoner of war for surrendering to him. Then the Confederate flag from the fort is taken down and they both leave the place as free men.

anchor_text: In 1919, Count Volsky, an old Russian nobleman now penniless, brought to the publishing house headed by Nadena Kalenin, who took over his father's estate, a manuscript in exchange for a few rubles. The latter, having once known Volsky and despite the repugnance that the character inspires in her, agrees to take it from him. When she's alone, she hesitates to look through it. But the name of Fedor Petroff, former provincial investigative judge, who seems to be the author of the text, attracts his attention. She had been engaged to be married to him for seven years. Little by little, as the pages turn, she discovers the horrifying truth about her ex-fiancé's life...
text_a: The Russian Count Volsky, deprived by the incipient Bolshevik revolution not only of his noble title but also of a large part of his wealth, offers his young acquaintance Nadena Kalenin (director of a publishing house) a manuscript written by Fedor Mikhailovich Petroff, Kalenin's ex-fiancé, with the hope that it can be published. The woman learns from the text that Petroff had claimed responsibility for the murder of Olga Urbenin. Costei, in addition to being the person for whose love Petroff had left Kalenin (as evidenced by the manuscript), had been, some time earlier, before the revolution, contested between several lovers (including Volsky himself). An innocent man had been convicted of the crime. Kalenin, to finally do justice to the matter, would like to present Petroff's confession to the police, but fails to do so, because she is still in love with the murderer. It will be Petroff himself, in the grip of remorse, who will send to the authorities, between hesitation and reconsideration, the manuscript that accuses him.
text_b: Suspecting that the murderer of Vladimir, her fiancé found dead in the country estate, may be the nihilist Loris Ipanoff, Princess Fedora tries to track him down. While searching for it, she meets a man she ends up falling in love with. She discovers, however, that this is indeed Loris but her love for him now leads her to believe him innocent. But when Loris confesses to the murder, Fedora reports it to Vladimir's father. The princess will regret her action, discovering that Vladimir was a scoundrel who had stalked and raped Loris' sister and that Loris had killed him in self-defense. Loris' family must now serve a sentence in Siberia: his brother, trying to resist arrest, is killed. Abandoned by Loris, Fedora poisoned herself. The man, remorseful, returns to her in time to watch her die in his arms.

anchor_text: Guled and Nasra live with their son Mahad on the outskirts of Djibouti. Guled works as a gravedigger to make ends meet, but it is not easy to keep the little family afloat. Now Nasra also urgently needs an expensive operation to treat a chronic kidney disease.
text_a: Guled (Omar Abdi) is a gravedigger. He lives with his wife Nasra (Yasmin Warsame) and their son Maha in the poor neighborhoods of Djibouti. But Nasra is suffering from a serious illness and needs to undergo emergency surgery. How do we get the money?
text_b: Cáit is a nine-year-old girl who lives with her poor, neglectful parents in rural Ireland. In the summer of 1981, with Cáit's mother pregnant again, her parents decide to send their quiet daughter to live with her middle-aged distant cousin Eibhlín Cinnsealach and her husband Seán.

anchor_text: A group of outlaws around Billy Gunn steals a sizable war chest from the Army, killing several soldiers in the process. Captain Clive Norton will be tasked with apprehending the criminals and returning the money. Norton insists on having Chaleco, an old companion of Gunn's, accompany him, who is actually sentenced to death. He gladly seizes the opportunity not only to escape the snare but also to settle a personal score. As they search and pursue together, the two men learn to respect each other and work together. When they encounter a group of Mexicans surrounding the leader Cordero, they must also defend his interests. Eventually, they both succeed in reaching their goals.
text_a: A group of outlaws led by Billy Gunn steals a considerable military treasure and kills several soldiers. Captain Clive Norton is in charge of arresting the criminals and returning the money. Norton insists that death row inmate Chaleco, a former associate of Gunn's, accompany him. He gladly accepts the offer, not only to avoid the hanging, but also to settle a personal account. In the course of pursuing and searching for clues together, the two men learn to respect each other and work together. When they encounter a group of Mexicans around Chief Cordero, they must also defend themselves against the latter's interests. Eventually, they succeed in reaching their goals.
text_b: A group of outlaws led by the fierce Chief Jarrett undertake a sophisticated bank robbery in Middletown. While the sheriff and a few men pursue the gang, the mastermind of the plot, David, seeks refuge in Poorlands, taking the loot with him. After a while, the other members of the group arrive to distribute the money. Sheriff Martin there is trying to master the violence that the outlaws bring, which he must pay with his life. His brother Billy "Rum" Cooney now sets out to avenge his death. He allies himself with David, who wants the spoils for himself. Little by little, they're decimating the bandits. In this way, Billy's revenge succeeds; David is also on the verge of success when the money hidden in a statue of Saint Abelard is blown up with Jarrett in a powerful explosion.

anchor_text: In Richmond at a time reminiscent of the 1950's Singer Ellen Aim (Diane Lane) is kidnapped by the Bombers, a motorcycle gang led by the ruthless Raven Shaddocks (Willem Dafoe). Ellen's fate now rests on unlikely heroes: a soldier named Tom Cody (Michael Paré) and his sidekick, the brawler McCoy (Amy Madigan). They are joined by Billy Fish (Rick Moranis), Ellen's manager. The trio is thrust into a world of relentless pursuit and killing.
text_a: A timeless rock fable in the midst of a landscape of debauchery and rock & roll. The gang of criminals called The Bombers, led by the dangerous Raven Shaddock (Willem Dafoe), kidnaps the beautiful fashion singer Ellen Aim (Diane Lane). The hope of being rescued from her kidnapper rests on atypical heroes: fortune hunter Tom Coddy (Michael Paré), who was Ellen's old boyfriend, and her assistant and ex-soldier McCoy (Amy Madigan), who can fix anything on wheels. Both will have the help of the singer's manager, Billy Fish (Rick Moranis). All together you will enter a world of dangerous streets, fast cars and murderers everywhere.
text_b: Molly Gunn is a wealthy, spoiled heiress who, as the daughter of a now-deceased popular rock singer, lives on rent and leads a life of shopping, partying and luxury. However, when the young woman's accountant runs away with her father's wealth, Molly finds herself in trouble, unable to even afford the rent. Forced by circumstances, Molly decides to work as a babysitter for little Lorraine Schleine called "Ray". The child, neglected by her mother, a prominent record executive, is spoiled and annoying and goes to great lengths to make her new nanny's life a living hell. Molly will realize, by seeing herself in Ray, how empty and shallow her life is. The friendship that will slowly begin to mature between the two girls, will seem to be able to give meaning to Molly's life.
