Ellen's fate now rests on unlikely heroes: a soldier named Tom Cody (Michael Paré) and his sidekick, the brawler McCoy (Amy Madigan). They are joined by Billy Fish (Rick Moranis), Ellen's manager. The trio is thrust into a world of relentless pursuit and killing.
text_a: Molly Gunn is a wealthy, spoiled heiress who, as the daughter of a now-deceased popular rock singer, lives on rent and leads a life of shopping, partying and luxury. However, when the young woman's accountant runs away with her father's wealth, Molly finds herself in trouble, unable to even afford the rent. Forced by circumstances, Molly decides to work as a babysitter for little Lorraine Schleine called "Ray". The child, neglected by her mother, a prominent record executive, is spoiled and annoying and goes to great lengths to make her new nanny's life a living hell. Molly will realize, by seeing herself in Ray, how empty and shallow her life is. The friendship that will slowly begin to mature between the two girls, will seem to be able to give meaning to Molly's life.
text_b: Ellen Aim, leader of a band called Ellen Aim and the Attackers has just returned to Richmond for a concert, but while doing so Raven Shaddock - leader of a motorcycle gang called the Bombers - breaks into the rally and kidnaps her. Reva Cody, who witnessed the incident, asks her brother Tom, a former soldier and formerly related to Ellen, to work to save her.

anchor_text: Pierre, a professional dancer at the Lido, tells his sister Élise, a social worker, what he has just learned: his heart is seriously ill, and he is doomed in the short term if he does not receive a heart transplant in time. Elise finds herself single and moves in with her three children in Pierre's apartment, thus resolving her concern about accommodation and wanting to take care of her sick brother. No longer able to dance and having lost his taste for life, Pierre, when not looking after Élise's children, now spends his days observing anonymous Parisians from his balcony or meeting them on his rare outings. Thus, under his watchful eye, several protagonists will cross paths: an undocumented African who has embarked on a long journey to reach Paris; itinerant traders working in the neighborhood market and procuring supplies in Rungis; Roland, a professor at the Sorbonne, fell in love with one of his students and with whom he manages laboriously to start a relationship; the latter, Letitia, whose apartment window is right in front of Pierre's house and with whom he himself is tempted to get acquainted; Philippe, Roland's brother, who is an architect; a model baker from the neighborhood, irascible and endowed with prejudice, and his young Magébine intern; a woman who lives in a carefree way... Everybody has their own story, their own doubts, their own spleen, their own little troubles in life. But they all have in common the same setting, that of Paris, the city of light...
text_a: Pierre, a professional dancer, is forced to make drastic changes in his life because of a serious heart problem. While waiting for a life-saving transplant, his sister Elise moves into his home with her three children to care for him. Meanwhile in Paris, as Pierre watches from his balcony, stories of other characters unfold. Roland, a history professor, falls in love with Laetitia, a student of his who begins courting him via text message. He then agrees against his principles to make an educational series for television because of the lavish compensation offered. During a recording, however, he has a panic attack and ends up with the psychoanalyst, so he seeks comfort in his brother Philippe, an architect about to become a father, who initially needed comfort for the death of his elderly father. Roland has an affair with Laetitia who then, in spite of Pierre who tries a strange approach with her through her sister, gets back together with her ex-boyfriend. Khadija, a young woman of North African origin, finds work in a bakery run by an extremely demanding woman who is forced to review her own prejudices. Benoit, called by his brother, leaves Cameroon and crosses all of Africa and the Mediterranean in the hope of finding a job in Paris where he also counts on the support of Marjolaine, a model he had met by chance. Then there is Jean, a fruit vendor who is deeply shaken by the sudden death of his ex-wife. After an easy adventure that he says no to, he finds love with Elise who at 40 years old and after a long affair ended badly seemed to have closed the possibility of falling in love again, and instead finds the joy of life right after assisting his sick brother.
text_b: Pierre lives in Paris. He's a widower and an old man. He's mourning the loss of his wife, and he's trying to avoid change. To bring Pierre back to life, his daughter Sylvie hires her daughter Juliette's boyfriend, the unsuccessful writer Alex. He's supposed to introduce Pierre to the Internet. This proceeds slowly until he meets a dating site. There he becomes an anonymous seducer with romantic lyrics. He's dating the Belgian Flora. Since he's too old for her, he sends a picture of Alex and makes a date. Since Alex needs money, he'll go along with it. Pierre and Alex are going to Brussels. Flora falls in love with Alex and spends the night with him. She wants to visit him in Paris, and Pierre offers her his apartment as accommodation, where Alex also lives. When Sylvie appears with her daughter, Pierre introduces her as Alex's sister. As Pierre intended, the two of them think Flora is his girlfriend, and it sounds like they're having sex. At the train station, Flora discovers on an advertising poster the photo of the woman who is supposed to be Alex's deceased wife, and she makes contact with the men. They meet in front of Flora's apartment in Brussels. She explains that she loves Pierre because of his poetic streak. Flora and Alex become a couple, and Pierre also finds a new love.

anchor_text: Pierre, a young dancer, is told by his doctor that he has a serious heart defect and that a heart transplant may be the only way to save his life. While he waits for an organ to be donated, his sister, Elise, a single-parent social worker, moves into his apartment with her three children to support him and ease his loneliness. The main pastime of the weakened and also slightly depressed Pierre is to observe the neighbors and passers-by from the balcony of his apartment and make up stories to them. In a style reminiscent of Robert Altman's Short Cuts, but also drawing on the narrative tradition of Honoré de Balzac, the film follows these stories by attaching itself episodically to individual characters and following their daily routines over a period of time. The narrative threads occasionally overlap, some even lead seamlessly into each other, but overall the perspective is a tribute to the small and large drama of the everyday lives of very different people and of course the city in which these lives take place: Paris. It's a film about love, family bonds, loneliness and compassion that brings the fates of the characters together in unexpected ways.

anchor_text: It's the year 2069. On his deathbed, His Royal Highness Alfredo, King without a crown, recalls his youth, when he wanted to become a firefighter. The meeting with his instructor Afonso ignited a passionate love and the common will of the two young men to change the social status quo.
text_a: Toni is an Italian immigrant who arrived in a rural part of France. There he will stay in a house where he will meet Marie with whom he will fall in love. Years later, Toni meets Josefa, a Spanish immigrant who has arrived in the region, which causes him to change jobs in the quarry and complicate his relationship with Marie.
text_b: Alfredo is dying. The latter is bathed in memories of his youth: he meets Afonso, an instructor in the fire brigade, who has fallen under his spell. Thanks to their meeting, everything had changed, his life, his love,...

anchor_text: The story is set in Laalgunj, a small village in Uttar Pradesh where Chulbul Pandey, nicknamed "Robinhood" Pandey (Salman Khan), a tough and fearless police officer, maintains order using unorthodox and even illegal methods. Chhedi Singh (Sonu Sood), a local godfather who kills anyone who gets in his way, exacerbates and exploits the rivalry between Chulbul and his half-brother, Makhanchan Pandey (Arbaaz Khan), to triumph over the policeman. During his interventions, Chulbul falls in love with a beautiful young woman, Rajjo (Sonakshi Sinha), which causes him to reconsider his ways.
text_a: In the Bombay mafia, Maqbool (Irrfan Khan) is one of the most loyal men to the leader, Abbaji (Pankaj Kapur). However, this loyalty hides a love of power but also a love for its mistress, Nimmi (Tabu). The latter begging him to take power from the mafia and thus, also be free to have a relationship with her, he will plot and succeed the murder of Abaji with the help of his secret lover. However, remorse will soon follow, as will a slow path to madness.
text_b: In Laalgunj, a small village in Uttar Pradesh, Chulbul Pandey, nicknamed "Robin Hood" Pandey, a strong and courageous policeman, maintains order using unorthodox, as well as often illegal, methods. Chheda Singh, the local mafia boss, who kills anyone who gets in his way, escalates the rivalry between Chulbul and his half-brother, Makhanchan Pandey so that he can take advantage of the situation and get rid of the cop. However, Chulbul will also have to deal with the young and beautiful Rajjo who will force the policeman to reconsider his attitude, and who will make Chulbul fall in love with himself.

anchor_text: Thousands of cats have lived on the streets of Istanbul for many centuries. Kedi chronicles the lives of seven of these cats (Sarı, Bengü, Psychopath, Deniz, Aslan Parçası, Duman and Gamsız) and observes and interviews people who care for the strays. In doing so, the film develops a portrait of the city and its inhabitants.
text_a: Thousands of cats live in Istanbul, the largest city in Turkey. Some are wild and take care of themselves, while others are more domesticated and people take care of them. Kedi studies these cats and interviews the people who interact with them. The documentary is particularly interested in eight cats: Corny (the tramp), Soffietto (the gentleman), Eneri (the lover), Jackina (the huntress), Emmino (the salamander player), Flashina (the crazy one), Calime (the obesus) and Kicca (the saint).
text_b: Over the course of a single night, a tribe of cats called the Jellicles take what is known as "the Jellicle choice" and decide which cat will ascend to the Heaviside layer and return to a new life.

anchor_text: The film depicts the life of African-American radio host Ralph Waldo Greene from 1966 to 1984. Ex-con Greene works for WOL-AM in Washington, D.C. His girlfriend Vernell Watson is supporting him. After numerous listeners call, the station owner E.G. Sonderling gives him better airtime. In the aftermath of the assassination of Martin Luther King, Greene appeals to the angry masses to come to their senses. Later, he has his own TV show.
text_a: An African-American captain is put in charge of investigating the murder of a black sergeant, a World War I veteran, near a military base in Louisiana near the end of World War II. Investigating the past of an apparently "ghost" man, the officer is forced to suffer the prejudices and resentments that the population and his fellow soldiers show him, especially because he is the first black man to be seen in his position and rank.
text_b: It is the story of a very popular 1960s Washington DC disc jockey, Ralph Petey Greene, an African-American and former prisoner who became an iconic civil rights figure in the 1960s through his radio programs that dealt primarily with racism, poverty, drugs, and other problems of American society. The film covers the period from 1966, the year of Petey's professional entry at WOL (a local radio station in Washington), to 1984, the year of his death (Petey died of liver cancer on January 10, 1984).

anchor_text: In the 1940s, in a remote village in Spain, after the civil war, two sisters watch a film screened in the village: Frankenstein (James Whale, 1931). Ana, the younger sister, is so impressed by the projection that she believes the monster really exists.
text_a: Baron Frankenstein, once again in exile, moves into a family boarding house, run by the beautiful Anna, with Karl, his assistant. There he undertook a dangerous cervical transplant.
text_b: In a small town in Castile, in the mid-1940s, Isabel (Isabel Tellería) and Ana (Ana Torrent), two sisters aged eight and six respectively, watch the film Frankenstein on a Sunday. The sight of the film makes such an impression on the little girl that she keeps asking questions to her older sister, who assures her that the monster is alive and hiding near the village.

anchor_text: A ronin arrives in a village where the Ushi-Tora and the Seibei, the thuggish gangs of two rival gambling enterprises, are fighting for supremacy. He takes up quarters in an inn and observes the situation. Eventually, he devises a plan to pit the gangs against each other, with the goal of them extinguishing each other. He kills three members of the Ushi-Tora in an open fight and then offers his services to the Seibei. In the course of the film, he then switches sides several times, as both gangs are convinced of his strength and want to use him to achieve victory over the rivals. Only the brother of the Ushi-Tora leader, Unosuke, who returns from a journey in the course of the film, is suspicious of the ronin. He also brought back a revolver from his travels, giving him and his gang an advantage over rivals armed only with swords. The Ushi-Tora can capture the Ronin and defeat the Seibei. In the course of this battle, however, the Rōnin is able to free himself and finally defeat Unosuke and the Ushi-Tora in the final battle.
text_a: In a plane crash over the Japanese mountains, the sole survivor - a newborn named Andrew - is rescued by Tatsuya Sanga, a samurai master who adopts him and raises him alongside his son Kenjiro, training them at an early age in the path of the warrior. The two minors demonstrate an exceptional talent for martial arts, but within a few years Andrew manages to surpass his half-brother's fighting skills. The jealousy engendered in Kenjiro motivates him to join a Yakuza clan, taking the oath of the mafia and reneging on the moral values of the samurai code. He finally abandons his father's dōjō, not without first vowing revenge against his brother. Ten years later, Andrew becomes a successful reporter in Los Angeles, working for a prestigious publishing house. He, along with a beautiful photographer, embark on a trip to Turkey to investigate and unmask an opium smuggling operation between that country and North America. However, on the verge of fulfilling that goal, they are discovered by the cartel bosses, who kidnap the woman, forcing Andrew to fight in a millionaire but deadly underground wrestling tournament, whose ultimate champion is the deadly Kenjiro. Both brothers will have to push their skills to the limit, in a fight where only one will remain alive.
text_b: The year is 1860. The rise of a middle class in Japan ended the power that was concentrated in the Tokugawa shogunate. A rōnin calling himself Sanjurō (Toshirō Mifune), once a samurai of royalty, is now masterless, and has no master but his own will to survive and no other resource but his wit and his sword. With a hawkish spirit, and the soul of a life-seeking adventurer, the mercenary arrives in a small town and looks for an opportunity to offer his services. There he learns that the city is dominated by two opposing sides: Ushi-Tora's and Seibei's. They're both fighting for power. Sanjurō feels contempt for both sides of malefactors but, after showing his skills in combat, he will deceive both sides by offering to hire him. Making both sides believe that he will side with the highest bidder will seek, out of greed, to eliminate both clans from the city.

anchor_text: The film follows William Gates and Arthur Agee, two African American teenagers recruited by a scout from St. Joseph High School in Westchester, Illinois, a predominantly white school with an exceptional basketball program, whose alumni include NBA great Isiah Thomas. Agee and Gates both come from poor African-American neighborhoods in Chicago, Illinois. Gates lived in Cabrini-Green while Agee and his family reside in West Garfield Park. By traveling 90 minutes to school, enduring long and difficult workouts, and having to acclimate to a foreign social environment, Gates and Agee struggle to improve their athletic skills in a highly competitive job market. Throughout their schooling, their families celebrate their successes and support each other during times of economic hardship caused by the school change. The film raises a number of issues concerning racial, social, and economic issues in the United States in the 1990s.
text_a: The documentary tells the story of William Gates and Arthur Agee, two African-American teenagers who are recruited by a scout to play basketball at St. Louis University. Joseph High School Joseph) in Westchester, Illinois, a predominantly white school with a renowned basketball program, whose graduates include NBA player Isiah Thomas. Agee and Gates live in poor African-American neighborhoods in Chicago. Gates resides in the Cabrini-Green and Agee project in West Garfield Park. Both of them travel 90 minutes every day to their new school and have to endure long and difficult training, in addition to acclimating to a different social environment from the one they came from. The two struggle to improve their skills to excel in a highly competitive market, while their families celebrate their successes and support each other during the economic hardships caused by the school change.
text_b: At the beginning of the film, Ken Carter takes the job of basketball coach at his old high school in Richmond. At the beginning of his training, the boys are unfriendly and have no respect for their new coach. They had only four wins in the previous season, but 22 losses. They are then introduced to the new guidelines that Carter has devised for them; in order to be allowed to play, they must sit in the front row of the classroom and attain at least a certain set by him grade point average. They must also wear a tie on game days. But under Carter's leadership, the boys suddenly win again, and the team is reinforced by Carter's son Damien, who first went to a private school, but joins Richmond High School against his father's wishes to play basketball under his father. Although the boys are now winning games, Carter finds that their academic performance has not improved at all, and they continue to make fun of the other teams. After receiving his students' performance evaluations, he becomes very upset and immediately locks the gym. The teachers and parents are against it, and at a meeting it is decided that the hall will be reopened. That's when Carter resigns. But when he enters the hall again, he sees that all his players have sat down in the hall and are studying for school. The final game is against St. Francis, which they lose 70-68 in the closing seconds. With the right attitude, they enter the cabins. There, however, Carter explains that they would still have gained a great deal, namely, the opportunity to start life with a good education. The trailer shows what has become of the individual crew members. Six went to college, five on athletic scholarships.

anchor_text: Carmen, a young Mexican girl, after the death of her mother, tries to cross the border and encounters an American patrol. Aidan, a young ex-marine who is a border patrolman, saves her life by killing one of his own. Never bound by that tragic night and now pursued by law enforcement, they journey together to the City of Angels, confronting their demons...
text_a: To escape a drug cartel after the murder of her mother, young Carmen leaves Mexico and heads north to California. At the border, he meets vigilante Aidan, a veteran desperate for money for his family. The two fall in love and travel together to Los Angeles to seek help from Masilda, Carmen's mother's best friend who runs the nightclub "La Sombra".
text_b: General Gutierrez, the evil governor of Mexico, taxes the people and terrorizes them with his thugs. The young Ramon Martiney, discovers that the murderer of his father for self-interest is the same Gutierrez, takes the name of Zorro to the side of the conspirators, and manages to bring peace and freedom to the country. Finally he regains possession of his father's name and wealth.

anchor_text: The film is based on a true story. In an attempt to cover up the violent deaths of her husband and son, Dr. Laura Bowman travels to Myanmar in 1988, where demonstrations for more democracy are taking place. Instead of leaving Myanmar, as the country's government encourages visitors to do, she accompanies Professor U Aung Ko on his journey through the country. She's committed to the people of this country. Her own life is worth nothing to her, as she herself says, "if only she would die to be with them again". When a soldier tries to rape Laura, who is looking for medicine for the sick professor, she shoots him. Later, she and the professor arrive in the capital, where they try to reach the American Embassy. They're looking for the professor and they're looking for her. They're on the run. In the streets, they witness massacres of protesting students. Some of the young men take them to the rebel camp near the state border. When Laura is asked by a doctor there how long she can stay and help, she replies, "As long as I'm needed".
text_a: Young mother Aung San Suu Kyi (Suu) lives in London with her husband, Michael Aris and children, Kim and Alexander. A sudden phone call informs her that her mother has had a heart attack and has been admitted to a hospital in Myanmar, the country where Suu lived as a child before her father's death. At the time, Burma was going through a series of student uprisings that would turn into actual mass killings by the military dictatorship. Aung San Suu Kyi will fight for peace and for the development of democracy in her country. Once in the country, she will be placed under house arrest and her supporters imprisoned. Once she has won the first democratic elections, she will be freed, along with her comrades, although kept under strict and continuous control. In the meantime, the family, who resided in London, will be prohibited from attempting to access the Burmese border. After 6 years, in March 1999, her husband was diagnosed with a cancer that will force him to go to the hospital where he will die shortly after, exactly on his 53rd birthday. Aung San Suu Kyi, thanks to her courage and strength, continues the non-violent struggle in honor of her husband, and in 2010 she will finally be released.
text_b: Dr. Laura Bowman is the victim of a robbery in which her husband and son are killed. After a brief period of rehabilitation, her sister Andy convinces her to take a trip to Myanmar, formerly Burma. Here Laura witnesses a popular demonstration against the ruling military regime: the courageous opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi unarmed challenges the soldiers deployed against the people, and in her the protagonist sees the ability to fight violence with peace. He later meets U Aung Ko, an elderly Burmese man who works as a taxi driver to survive. With him she makes a long journey through the country, discovering that the elder is a famous university professor, removed from his profession by the regime that accuses him of being a spiritual leader of the student movement; Laura meets her former students who are part of the anti-government movement: among the boys lives Sein Htoo who, in an attempt to make her return to America, is killed by a soldier, while Professor U Aung Ko remains injured. She decides not to return home but to stay to help the elderly teacher, facing extremely dangerous situations and endangering her own life. Once in the capital, Rangoon, she seeks refuge at the U.S. Embassy, but fails to have an audience, instead being chased by the local police, who consider her a collaborator of the rioters. She finds refuge with Professor U Aung Ko on a truck that transports refugees to the border with Thailand, which she manages to bag with her friends, and then volunteers at a field hospital set up to help refugees.

anchor_text: It is a comedy-drama telling the story of a young baseball player (Billy Wyatt) whose athletic qualities lead him to high-level competition. His father's death will put an end to his plans. Some 15 years later, her mother tells her that her childhood best friend committed suicide. So Billy goes back in time to find the people he loved.
text_a: Adam Sandler plays the role of a mentally handicapped boy struggling with American football, first as a water boy for a college team (hence the title "Waterboy") and then as a talented player. At first his teammates treat him badly but when Bobby starts With his great talent and his ability to lead the team to victory, things change.
text_b: Former baseball player Billy Wyatt is now a grown man, but a phone call brings back memories of when he was little more than a promising teenager in love with Katie Chandler, a rebellious girl with a tragic destiny.

anchor_text: Ten years after the events of the film Clash of the Titans, Perseus has become a simple fisherman again and raises his ten-year-old son, Helios, alone after the death of his wife Io some time earlier. Meanwhile, the humans' lack of devotion weakens the gods of Olympus, who are about to lose control of the monstrous Titans, especially their leader, Cronus, defeated and locked in Tartarus by Zeus, Hades, and Poseidon in the distant past. Resuming his plots against Zeus, Hades makes a pact with the god of war, Ares, and Cronus himself. The three manage to capture Zeus and imprison him in the underworld. The unleashed Titans are wreaking havoc on the world. Perseus is then forced out of his retreat; with the help of Andromeda, Poseidon's son Agénor, and the god Hephaestus, he sets out to free Zeus and save mankind.
text_a: Hercules' foster parents are murdered, he sets off for Troy himself. There he falls in love with Cassiopeia. Since the godfather Zeus has promised her to King Minos, he is enraged and prevents the faithful togetherness by hurling Hercules into the sea. He then has to deal with the sorceress Circe, a hydra, and the underworld before finally reaching Crete. There, he challenges Minos, who, as he has found out, is also responsible for the death of his parents, to a duel and, after its successful outcome, can save Cassiopeia from the clutches of the Phoenix with the help of a magic sword. After all, all of Crete is burning.
text_b: A decade after his heroic victory over the monstrous Ceto, Perseus (Sam Worthington) - the demigod son of Zeus - attempts to lead a quieter life as a fisherman in a village and as a widowed father with his 10-year-old son, Helios. Meanwhile, a bitter struggle for supremacy unfolds between gods and titans. Dangerously weakened by mankind's lack of devotion, the gods lose control of the imprisoned titans and their fierce leader, Cronus, father of the long-ruling brothers Zeus (Liam Neeson), Hades (Ralph Fiennes), and Poseidon (Danny Huston). The triumvirate had overthrown its powerful father long ago, leaving him to rot in the gloomy abyss of Tartarus, a dungeon located in the depths of hell. Perseus cannot ignore his true calling when Hades, along with Zeus' pious son Ares (Edgar Ramirez), switch allegiances and make a deal with Cronus to capture Zeus. The Titans' strength increases as Zeus' divine powers diminish, unleashing hell on earth. Enlisting the help of the warrior queen Andromeda (Rosamund Pike), Poseidon's demigod son Agenor (Toby Kebbell), and the fallen god Hephaestus (Bill Nighy), Perseus bravely embarks on a perilous quest through the underworld to rescue Zeus, overthrow the Titans, and save humanity.

anchor_text: A gang of outlaws terrorizes the landowners of Blakestone Hill: those who refuse to sign the deed of sale are killed, those who do not are forced to do so. Fred Danton and Johnny, two bounty hunters enticed by the prizes at stake, decide to take on the outlaws. Pretty soon, they're spotting the Rodgers, like the ones running the land racket.
text_a: A gang of outlaws is terrorizing the landowners of Blakestone Hill: some who refuse to sign the deed are killed, and others are forced to sign the deed. Fred Danton and Johnny, two bounty killers lured by the showy bounties set to capture or kill outlaws, jump into action and identify the Rodgers, one of Blakestone-Hill's mayors, the mysterious head of the land racket.
text_b: Sam Clayton and his gang of thugs are terrorizing the small town. When Father John, the town's benefactor, is murdered, Johnny, his protégé, decides to turn to Lewis, a retired hitman and brother of the deceased. Together, the two men devise a plan to thwart Clayton and his men...

anchor_text: A mysterious group of outlaws terrorizes the ranchers of Blakestone Hill, and anyone who refuses to sell their land to them is found brutally murdered. The two cunning bounty hunters Johnny and Fred are hired to capture or kill the gang members. Soon they recognize in businessman Rodgers the mastermind behind the gang, whose members are able to take out Johnny and Fred through a series of clever maneuvers. When Rodgers learns that Johnny is the son of the widow Reed, on whose behalf he once shot his father, additional emotions are unleashed. He's the father of the man who's hunting him. The widow, Rodgers, their son, and Fred can work together to pacify the area.
text_a: A mysterious gang of gunmen is terrorizing the people of Blagstone. By not accepting the demands of the bandits and refusing to sign the documents of cession of their lands, the atrocious revenge of the bandits begins. Johnny (Mark Damon) and Fred (Antonio de Teffé), two young bounty hunters seduced by the actions and escapes of the gunmen, are immediately thrown into action and risk by recognizing Rodgers (Luis Induni), one of the important men of Blagstone, as the mysterious organizer of the criminal enterprise. Eliminating the entire gang with a series of ploys, Fred and Johnny are about to succumb at the hands of the sole survivor, Rodgers. They survive by saving their lives almost providentially by the wife of Rodgers himself, who has discovered that she is the mother of one of the young men, Johnny, whom he abandoned as a baby. Or at least, that's what she thinks...
text_b: A gang of criminals, led by Sam Clayton, rule the small town of Juno City, Texas. The sheriff and the local judge suffer the indignities of the bandits without intervening. The only one who opposes the outlaws is Father John, who is killed for it. Johnny, having lost his voice due to the trauma of his father John's death, travels to Mexico, to Pueblo Quemada, to seek help from Lewis, brother of the murdered priest and former gunfighter known as Diamante Lobo. The man, taking advantage of the resemblance to his brother, returns with the boy to Juno City and puts on the priest's clothes. The bandits, terrified and convinced that the priest has returned to life, end up killing each other. Eventually Lewis finds himself up against Sam Clayton, who turns out to be Johnny's father, and is forced to kill him in front of the boy and his mother.

anchor_text: On the eve of a rigged fight, boxer Charlie Davis is haunted by the memory of a man named "Ben". Troubled, he goes to his mother's house, who rejects him, and then visits a cabaret singer. In the locker room, still in shock, he tries to regain his composure and reconsiders his career.
text_a: Todd Howard is Scott Howard's cousin. He received an athletic scholarship to Hamilton University, where Coach Finestock hopes that he, too, will have the family genes and be able to turn into a werewolf like his cousin in order to use that power to shape the boxing team into a winning team.
text_b: The day after a botched fight, boxer Charlie Davis is assaulted by memories of a certain "Ben". Embarrassed, he goes after his mother and they visit a cabaret singer. In the lobby, still in a state of shock, he tries to regain his courage and reviews his career.[4]

anchor_text: The painter Francisco de Goya gained prestige and wealth through his talent and creative power. His clients come from the most important houses of Madrid and so he gradually arrives at the court of Charles IV. In passionate love, he is attracted to the Duchess of Alba and at the same time hates the decadent aristocrat in her. He believes in the king and the church, enjoys his position at court. His colleague and friend Esteve shows him the contradictions of this closed world and leads him to the simple people of the country. In a tavern in Madrid, he meets the singer Maria Rosario; later he has to witness how she is condemned by the Inquisition. He is deeply shaken by the song that Mary must sing as proof of her guilt. The more he penetrates into the life of the people, from which he draws motives for his art, the greater becomes his inner anguish in the face of the conditions in the country. His relationship with Alba grows increasingly self-destructive, and he suffers a hearing loss. He turns his back on the court and travels completely deaf to his home village in Aragon to his mother. With the help of his companion Esteve, he finds his way back to his work, which leads him into an inner deviation from the values of society and the church. He is plagued by demons, which he recognizes in their actions and their social influence, and which flow into his work. He himself falls into the clutches of the Inquisition; the Grand Inquisitor begs him to renounce this thought. But Goya is firmly convinced that the misery and horror are caused by the circumstances themselves, and remains convinced of the truth of his paintings. He chooses exile.
text_a: The story is set in the early 19th century at the court of King Charles IV of Spain. The Duchess of Alba befriends the painter Francisco de Goya as well as the politician Manuel de Godoy, who prefers the company of Pepita Tudó. Godoy wants Goya to paint a picture of Pepita Tudós, and hopes to have an affair with the woman. Queen Maria Luise of Bourbon-Parma insists on a marriage between Godoy and the Countess María Teresa de Borbón y Vallabriga, the Countess of Chinchón. The Duchess of Alba dies after a feast, after which rumors spread in the court, accusing several personalities of the murder. Suspects include the queen, Godoy's wife, and his brother-in-law. In the end, it turns out that the brother-in-law Willibald committed the murder in order to assume the throne, which in his opinion was righteous. The possibility of suicide by poisoning is also being considered. The painter paints a picture of Tudós as well as the Duchess.
text_b: The painter Francisco de Goya achieved fame and prosperity through his talent and creative force. His patrons were from the biggest houses in Madrid and this is how he gradually reached the royal court of Charles IV. Attracted by a passionate love for the Duchess of Alba, he at the same time hates the decadent aristocracy she represents. He believes in the king and the church and enjoys his position at court. His colleague and friend Esteve shows him the contradictions of this closed world and takes him to meet the simple people of the country. In a Madrid tavern, he met the singer Maria Rosario; later, he had to witness her condemnation by the Inquisition. He is deeply disturbed by the song that Maria has to perform as proof of her guilt. The more he immersed himself in the life of the people and drew from them motives for his art, the more his inner pain increased in the face of the situation in the country. His relationship with the Duchess of Alba becomes self-destructive and he falls victim to deafness. He turns his back on the court and goes, completely deaf, to his native village in Aragon, to his mother's house. With the help of his partner Esteve, he regains his job, which leads him to an inner rejection of the values of society and the church. He is tormented by demons whom he recognizes in their actions and social influence and who are thus integrated into his work. He himself falls into the clutches of the Inquisition; the Grand Inquisitor conjures him to renounce these thoughts. But Goya is rooted in the belief that misery and horror come from the circumstances themselves, and remains convinced of the truth of his paintings. He chooses exile.

anchor_text: The story is based on the life of Parinya Charoenphol, aka Nong Toom, a Muay Thai katoi boxer. The film narrates the childhood of Nong Toom, a sensitive child, who likes to play with girls and for this suffers pain from some of his classmates. As a teenager, still considered a man, he accepts the challenge of a boxing match, which shows him that the will is stronger than the uncertainties of life. She decides to train seriously and, after many difficulties, progresses to become a champion, all without renouncing her gender identity that allows her to stand out from her opponents to her advantage. But her deepest desire is to transition, which she does at the peak of her career.
text_a: Since childhood Parinya Charoenphol, known as Nong Toom, has felt a woman trapped in a man's body. After a period of uncertainty, she decides that the way to gain respect and get the money needed to change sex is to master the most masculine and lethal sport: Muay Thai. With tenacity he manages, starting from his childhood in the Lanna Muay Thai Boxing and going into adolescence as a traveling monk, to become one of the most powerful fighters of this Thai sports discipline against his rivals in Thailand and Japan. But their fights sometimes touch the Dante show.
text_b: The film tells the story of young Ting from the village of Nong Pradu, a peaceful village nestled in a forested valley in Thailand. He's a fan of Muay Thai and secretly trains in it. The villagers entrusted him with the task of recovering the head of the Buddha statue (Ong-Bak) that had been stolen from them. Ting goes to the furnace in Bangkok, discovers the mafia responsible for the theft of Ong-Bak's head and confronts them. He would also have to defend himself against the city's thugs and other opposers. Action, stunts and chase races will now guide his quest.

anchor_text: On Halloween night, Stan, after finishing his shift at a video store, leaves with his friends for a party. On their journey, they first encounter a homicidal maniac known as "The Butcher" who is thrown out of the car, and then two strange hippies at a gas station. Arriving in a deserted California town, the guard at the entrance informs them that the town is cursed, having been destroyed by a fire. The city before the fire was a major horror movie center, the guard even warns them to leave before midnight. The group enters the city and heads to a bar where they meet Kay, the bar's owner in disguise. After midnight, the friends find themselves up against the six most famous monsters in horror: Freddy Krueger, Jason Voorhees, Michael Myers, Chucky the Killer Doll, Pinhead and Leatherface. Taking refuge in a church, a clergyman tells Stan of a prophecy concerning him, in fact according to it he will defeat the monsters and remove the curse from the city. Eventually the group finds themselves fighting against the group of six monsters in a singing competition judged by Kay; defeating them, the boys free the city from the curse.
text_a: The story begins when a boy named Stan, who works at a video club, along with his friends, gets lost while going to a costume party. Along the way, in a mysterious town, he realizes he's being chased by the most famous movie monsters. His duty, bequeathed by his grandfather Van Helsing, is to capture and destroy monsters, in addition to singing karaoke... The film will treat his character in the form of a parody that mixes horror with humor and in which a large number of characters of the genre will meet, such as Freddy Krueger (A Nightmare on Elm Street), Pinhead (Hellraiser), Chucky (Diabolical Doll), Jason (Friday the 13th) Michael Myers (Halloween) and Leatherface (The Texas Massacre).
text_b: Stan, a young and charming fairground impostor, learns the secrets of the trade from Zeena, a companion of an old magician, and uses it to make a living with fake experiments in spiritualism. In his performances he is helped by the sweet Molly, who is in love with him. Fatal for him will be his encounter with Lilith Ritter, an unscrupulous psychologist, who makes a deal with him to defraud his wealthy clients. Publicly exposed by Molly, the man will have to put an end to his deception, while the icy Lilith, revealing a criminal nature, will not hesitate to get rid of him. Caught in a self-destructive spiral and plagued by guilt, Stan must return to the circus where, forced to perform as a clown phenomenon, he will be tormented by terrifying nightmares until he loses his mind. Still in love with him, Molly rushes to his aid and, saving him from the worst, decides to take care of him.

anchor_text: Stan Helsing works at a video store. On Halloween night, before she left work, her boss ordered her to drop off some videos at the landlord's mother's house. While driving with friends to a party, he convinces them to take him to Linwood first, where he will drop off the videos. Meanwhile, a group of horror movie villains, including Freddy, try to track him down. Stan is accompanied by Nadine, his ex-girlfriend, Teddy, his friend, and Mia, a friend of Teddy's. Stan is often mistaken for Van Helsing, but he corrects people about that. They stop to fill up, but the gas station attendant cheats them out by siphoning the gas instead of filling it. Their car breaks down near a private residential area called Stormy Night Estates. They decide to deliver the videos on foot. The group of bad guys finds them and is about to attack them, but the locals who witness the scene oppose it and offer to settle this confrontation with karaoke. The bad guys lose and still decide to attack Stan. Freddy rips off Stan's top with his claws, revealing a mark on his torso: the initials VH. Stan Helsing turns out to be Stan Van Helsing, who is a descendant of Abraham Van Helsing, the famous vampire hunter. Stan begins to fight the bad guys, inspiring his friends to help him. The monsters are defeated. The group of friends leave in a taxi, Stan gets back together with Nadine.

anchor_text: Molly, a teenage amateur detective, is hired by the FBI to protect a girl whose father is to testify in a trial. So she is sent undercover to the southern United States to integrate into the university and the girl's brotherhood. Missed boy because of her upbringing, Molly is going to have a hard time going unnoticed and her mission is not going to be easy.
text_a: Molly Hartley (Haley Bennett), is a young student who tries to make a new life after being attempted murdered by her own mother, who was taken to a psychiatric hospital. A somewhat nervous Molly enters a new school; everything seems to be going well until she begins experiencing horrible visions and hearing voices that portend something bad as her 18th birthday approaches. Is this young woman experiencing supernatural events, or did she simply inherit her mother's psychosis?
text_b: Molly is a teenage private investigator who works to pay off her father's debts. Her abilities to photograph unfaithful husbands catch the attention of FBI Agent Armon, who decides to hire her to go undercover to a college sorority and protect the daughter of a man who is going to testify against some top mobsters. In order to go unnoticed, Molly has to leave behind her vulgar and misaligned image to become a sophisticated girl.[4]

anchor_text: Juni Cortez, has resigned. Feeling betrayed, he now refuses to work for the OSS. Yet, when the boy learns that his sister, Carmen, is in danger, he does not hesitate. For trying to unlock the diabolical secret of a video game that captures the minds of the losers, the girl has remained a prisoner. This game-event, aptly named Game Over, will go on sale in a few hours. Behind this cunning and fearsome weapon lies the Toymaster, a brilliant creator who is about to take control of the brains of every child on the planet. For Juni, there's not a second to lose. In order to save his sister and the future of the world, he has to win the game, and he can only do that from the inside.
text_a: The secret agent, Juni Cohete Rebelde Cortez, is retired from the OSS and longs for a quiet life. But he is contacted by his former boss warning him that his sister, Carmen, has been lost in a 3-D video game invented by the villainous Toymaker (Sylvester Stallone) who plans to control all the world's youth through the video game. This causes Juni to return to the OSS to carry out the most exciting mission and adventure of her life: saving her sister on a journey into virtual reality where amazing computer graphics and strange creatures come dangerously alive. As Juni and other beta-testers face increasingly difficult challenges in the game's various levels, the spies must use their good humor, high-tech gadgets, and family ties to stop a power-hungry villain.
text_b: Carmen and Juni have achieved level 2 as OSS agents, and are about to face their first solo mission, or so they believe. However, this time it's going to take the whole family - and later some more - to save the world from a mysterious volcanic island inhabited by a mad scientist and his imaginative zoo populated by incredible creatures; an island where none of their gadgets will do, so they can only rely on their super agent skills and family.

anchor_text: Shane O'Shea lives in the suburbs of New York City in 1979. One day he drives to Manhattan, where he discovers for himself the then-fashionable Studio 54. He gets a part-time job as a bartender and meets many celebrities, including Andy Warhol. O'Shea becomes popular, they call him Shane 54. He is particularly close friends with colleagues Anita and Greg Randazzo. Turns out Greg is stealing from his employer, Steve Rubell. OShea falls in love with Julie Black, but she disappoints him. The studio is targeted by tax investigators after Rubell insinuates in a television interview that he is engaged in tax evasion. One day, Anita is singing on stage, and during her performance, an elderly visitor collapses and dies. At the same time, authorities are searching the studio's offices. Rubell is arrested and sentenced to 18 months in prison. Black offers friendship to O'Shea. The studio changes hands; the off-voice OSheas says the scene has become boring. The new owner allows Rubell to throw a party in the studios after he's fired.
text_a: Brian Flanagan, a young and ambitious job seeker, after leaving the army and trying his luck in finance and advertising, accepts a job as a bartender in a small New York bar. Introduced to the trade by colleague Doug, the two make their fortune, after Brian has invented a new cocktail, becoming the two most famous acrobatic bartenders in Manhattan with the prospect of opening a chain of clubs with the name "Cocktails & Dreams". Because of a woman, however, the two friends take different paths, and Brian decides to continue working as a bartender in Jamaica, where he falls in love with Jordan, a waitress on vacation with friends. But because of a bet with his newfound friend Doug, who's married to a beautiful blonde millionaire and also on vacation in Jamaica, Brian gets dumped by Jordan. The man will return to New York as the companion of a prominent middle-aged businesswoman in hopes of finding a job as a manager. But that's not his world and so he goes looking for Jordan and finds her in the bar where the girl had entrusted him to work. He also finds his friend Doug who, despite everything he had told him, is bankrupt and has been betrayed by his wife, who also makes advances to Brian, which is why Doug will commit suicide shortly after leaving him a letter. Brian finally realizes that his future is with Jordan, who turns out to be not just a waitress but a girl from a wealthy family. And that she's pregnant. So Brian takes responsibility and decides to invest his life savings to upgrade his uncle's bar, which he calls "Cocktails & Dreams".
text_b: Shane O'Shea is a young man from Jersey City who in 1979 gets a job as a bartender at the Studio 54 nightclub. O'Shea leaves his life in New Jersey behind and moves to New York where he befriends two co-workers, the married couple Anita and Greg Randazzo, with whom he shares an apartment. Also, Shane is in a relationship with actress Julie Black. As Shane plunges into the hectic nightlife of Studio 54, his life spirals down the same way the club will. Following the death of Disco Dottie during Anita's debut performance at the 1980 New Year's Eve party, Shane confronts Steve Rubell and is fired. At the same time, the FBI breaks into the club and arrests Rubell for tax evasion. Shane and Julie break up, but decide to remain friends. Years later Shane, Greg and Anita meet again at the reopening of Studio 54, where Rubell throws a party after being released from prison.

text_b: It's 1979, and Shane is a young boy from New Jersey who lives with his two sisters and their father. He works as a gas station attendant, dreaming of moving to New York City, only three miles away, yet so incredibly far away for him. One night he decides to go with two friends to the famous nightclub "Studio54", where, however, the owner of the club, Steve Rubell, decides to let only him in. The club has a very select clientele and drugs and sex circulate freely inside. Through acquaintances he manages to get an interview with Steve and is then hired, but relations with the father become increasingly harsh as he does not approve of his son's choices. Shane meets Anita and Greg, a married couple who work at Studio54 and who become his new family by hosting him in their home. Meanwhile, Shane meets celebrities and improves his financial situation by becoming one of the "54" bartenders, described by Steve Rubell as "the cream of New York bartenders". Through the events of the protagonist we are shown the life inside the legendary local, a dream for many but a reality for few. The club is booming, is frequented by more and more VIPs and its fame increases day by day, but Steve Rubell arouses the suspicion of the tax police who investigate the income of the club and the drug trade inside.

anchor_text: New York advertising executive Howard Inlet is in a deep depression after losing his infant daughter. This makes his life seem more and more unbearable. He writes passionate letters to death, time and love. Concerned, his friends and coworkers try to rebuild him with an unusual "therapy". Three people take on the acting roles of death, time, and love, directly confronting and responding to Howard.
text_a: A young bourgeois woman raised in the strict morality of the Victorian era, Beatrix Potter will know everything: from the anguish of creation to success as brilliant as it was unexpected, from submission to bourgeois family morality to the freedom of great love. Separated by illness and the death of her fiancé, Beatrix overcomes all the trials under the amused and complicit eye of her characters, breaks free from her parents to live in the countryside and there she meets a notary clerk, son of a peasant, also in love with nature and marries him.
text_b: Howard Inlet (Will Smith), a New York advertising executive, experiences tragedy and falls into a deep depression when his six-year-old daughter dies. His colleagues (Edward Norton, Kate Winslet and Michael Peña), fearing for his health and for the future of their company, hatched a most unexpected plot to force him to face his suffering. Since Howard writes therapeutic and symbolic letters to Time (Jacob Latimore), Death (Helen Mirren), and Love (Keira Knightley), his three colleagues decide to employ three actors to be the embodiment of these three symbols.

anchor_text: Paul Blart travels to Las Vegas with his daughter Maya to attend a security fair and relax after six years of hard work as a department store security guard. At the same time, he wants to use the opportunity to spend as much time as possible with his daughter before she leaves home for college. However, Maya soon has eyes only for the charming hotel employee Lane, while Paul, because of his job as a department store cop, has to put up with all kinds of pricks from the casino security people. But when a group of criminals led by the ruthless Vincent plan to rob several hotels and casinos in Las Vegas, Paul's big day has come.
text_a: It's been six years since Paul Blart became famous for his daring rescue at the West Orange Pavilion. Since then, this watchman has been in charge of the security of the shopping centers. Now it's time to take a well-deserved vacation, so he's decided to take a trip to Las Vegas with his daughter, who's about to leave for college. However, when Blart uncovers a criminal threat against his hotel, he must respond to the call of duty.[1]
text_b: Paul is a former big time gangster from New Orleans. He's cleaned up and lives in Mississippi, where he runs a small garage. One day, he receives a letter from the university where his daughter, Beth, is studying. Learning that she has not attended classes for several months, he tries to contact her and later learns that his daughter has become a drug addict and has run away to New Orleans with a small dealer. With the help of Angela, an inveterate partygoer and friend of his daughter, he is forced to return home and resume duty to find Beth. But when Omar, the town's boss, learns of his arrival, he decides to intervene in order to exact his revenge.

anchor_text: After divorcing his wife after only six days of marriage and losing his mother to a milk truck that hit her while she was picking up the newspaper, it seems that Paul Blart's life is destined to fall apart. But when he receives an invitation to Las Vegas to attend the annual security officers' convention, Paul realizes that fate has something in store for him...
text_a: It's been six years since Paul Blart became famous for his daring rescue at the West Orange Pavilion. Since then, this watchman has been in charge of the security of the shopping centers. Now it's time to take a well-deserved vacation, so he's decided to take a trip to Las Vegas with his daughter, who's about to leave for college. However, when Blart uncovers a criminal threat against his hotel, he must respond to the call of duty.[1]
text_b: The two brothers, Chuck and Wes have a plan on how to rob Caesar's Palace in Las Vegas and they join a biker gang and while the others are drinking and partying out of town, they change their clothes and go to the casino to do the robbery. Of course, the police aren't looking for two well-dressed criminals among the Hell's Angels.

anchor_text: Through an affair with the daughter of industrialist Jean-Baptiste Liégard, François Leclercq gains access to "fine society" and a position in Liégard's company. Later, Leclercq runs a nightclub together with Raphaël Di Massa. One day he finds out that drugs are being sold there behind his back. He explains to Di Massa that he wants nothing to do with it. Shortly afterwards, Karine, an employee of the nightclub, and the footballer Cojac are shot with Leclercq's pistol. Leclercq is sentenced to ten years imprisonment for these murders. Released from prison after seven years, Leclercq sets out to find the men behind the murders. He finds out that Liégard is behind the drug trade and Di Massa's boss, and pits the two against each other. Liégard reveals Di Massa's whereabouts to Leclercq, who then has Liégard shot by an assassin.
text_a: Kyle LeBlanc (Van Damme) is an American who works in Russia. When he hears his wife being attacked on the phone, Kyle rushes home to find it's already too late. The man who killed your wife is found not guilty for lack of evidence and bribery of the judge. So Kyle takes the law into his own hands, kills the man to avenge his wife's death. He is sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. The Russian prison he is sent to is quite violent, and the warden enjoys organizing violent hand-to-hand fights where pockets are filled with gambling money. Kyle starts to buckle under the pressure and lose his sanity, so he's forced into these fights. The death of his prison buddy makes him think that's what he's fighting for. With that, Kyle knows he must now fight another battle: the fight for inner peace. It's the only way he can become the man he once was.
text_b: After serving seven years in prison for the murder of a certain Cajac and his mistress Karine, of which he is innocent, Francois Leclerc returns to Cournai, a small capital of the French textile industry. Having been the lover of Gilberte, the daughter of the powerful Jean-Baptiste Liégard, Leclerc had quickly risen from poverty to wealth, and now he wants to find out at any cost who framed him earlier, making him unjustly arrested. Resolved and determined to succeed in his goal, and helped by an old friend and Gilberte herself, Francois manages, patiently revisiting his past, to discover the truth about his case: that he was, that is, a victim of Liégard, an unsuspecting drug trafficker. So, without getting his hands dirty, he gets Liégard's "friends" to do justice for him.

anchor_text: After being exposed to a bizarre mixture of various household cleaning chemicals, Pat Kramer begins to shrink. It astonishes scientists, causes him a great deal of anxiety in his daily life, stirs the heart of America but most of all attracts the attention of a group of ill-intentioned people. They plan to capture Pat in order to unravel the mystery of her shrinking so they can use her on other people.
text_a: Off the coast of South Korea, a giant ape escapes from its captive ship. After a fight with a shark, he flees to Seoul, and falls in love with a beautiful blonde actress, torn between horror and compassion for the primate...
text_b: After being exposed to a strange combination of chemicals contained in household products, Pat Kramer begins to shrink. He's getting smaller and smaller, to the point where he has to use his daughter's dollhouse to live. Scientists are amazed and want to study it and citizens are moved and want to help. Pat catches the attention of a group of people who want to take over the world: their intention is to abduct her to study her so they can shrink other people.

anchor_text: After being exposed to a bizarre mixture of various household cleaning chemicals, Pat Kramer begins to shrink. It astonishes scientists, causes him a great deal of anxiety in his daily life, stirs the heart of America but most of all attracts the attention of a group of ill-intentioned people. They plan to capture Pat in order to unravel the mystery of her shrinking so they can use her on other people.
text_a: Washing dishes for a housewife can be very dangerous: the use of detergent will cause housewife Pat Kramer to shrink to the size of a coffee cup. But the trouble doesn't end there, as a group of criminals are determined to steal his secret in order to dominate the world.
text_b: Off the coast of South Korea, a giant ape escapes from its captive ship. After a fight with a shark, he flees to Seoul, and falls in love with a beautiful blonde actress, torn between horror and compassion for the primate...

anchor_text: During the Napoleonic Wars in Spain, two enemy officers find an old manuscript in a hut and forget about the war and the soldiers fighting outside the hut after reading the richly illustrated manuscript. Now the film tells the story of the young Walloon officer Alfons van Worden, who in 1739 rides through the wild and inaccessible Sierra Morena. He meets two Moorish princesses who pretend to be his relatives. After a hot night, he wakes up under a gallows next to two hanged men. He thinks he's been seduced by demons. From now on, he wakes up repeatedly under the gallows or in the dungeons of the Inquisition, the story takes on increasingly delusional traits. Van Worden encounters many people who tell him their life stories, which intertwine in many ways and branch out further and further and go funny or creepy.
text_a: The film is set in 1492 in Spain during the time of the Inquisition under the power of the great Inquisitor of Castile and Aragon named Torquemada,[3] who tortures and murders in the name of religion. Maria and Antonio, two young married bakers, find themselves involved in an execution of a heretic called Auto De Fe.[4] Seeing themselves in this situation Maria tries to intervene in the face of the cruelty she is witnessing but this only leads to attracting the attention of Torquemada who upon seeing her begins to have erotic dreams with her seeing her as the Virgin Mary and decides to arrest her accusing her of being a witch.[2] Being in prison Torquemada demands that her subjects torture her until she confesses to being a witch and in addition to this cut out her tongue. During her captivity Maria befriends Esmeralda (Frances Bay), who has already confessed to being a witch, and together they try to protect themselves from Torquemada's violence. While all this is happening Antonio seeks a way to enter the castle to save his wife from death but in one of the attempts the young man is arrested and Torquemada decides to turn all her cruelty against him.
text_b: At the beginning of the 19th century, in Spain, during the Napoleonic Wars, a French officer and a Spanish partisan took refuge in an inn and discovered an old manuscript that, curiously, tells the story of Alfonse van Worden, captain of the Walloon Guard and the officer's grandfather. The story begins when Alfonso is looking for a shortcut to cross the fearsome mountain range of the Sierra Morena. Here is the texture of the manuscript: One evening, Alfonso dines at an inn, the Venta Quemada, which is said to be haunted. Asleep, he is awakened by a Moorish servant who takes him to a cellar of the inn, and introduces him to two Moorish princesses, the sisters Emina and Zibelda. They call him cousin and do everything to seduce him, but after a night of love, he wakes up alone, in the early morning, in the middle of a pile of skulls under a gibbet where two hanged rotting. The next day, after a hermit and a half-Gypsy, half-peasant goat herder each told him a story, he wakes up again under the same gibbet... Soon after, narrowly escaping the Inquisition, he meets a cabalist and hears new stories intertwined like Russian dolls... Back in Venta Quemada, the two women await him with astonishing news... In every story of stories The narrative seems to feed on itself, if one can analyze the manuscript, a plot emerges in filigree:

anchor_text: After six years, Hanson is released from prison and finds himself in a world of responsibility and freedom. Trying to reconcile with his past, he begins a journey to Louisiana to reunite with his ex-wife May. During the journey, the man meets the teenagers Martine, a 15-year-old runaway, and Gordy, an outcast trying to fit in.
text_a: Nora, a 43-year-old fast food waitress who lives alone after the tragic death of her son, has an affair with Max, a wealthy 27-year-old recently widowed man. The differences in age and class make themselves felt, but the happy ending will come.
text_b: Brett, an ex-convict, goes on a car adventure with two young strangers: Martine, a girl looking for love; and Gordi, an insecure young man. Brett will establish a strong relationship with both of them, gradually revealing to them the reasons for his imprisonment.

anchor_text: Richard, Duke of Gloucester, does not accept that his eldest son, the dying King Edward IV, chooses one of his nephews as his successor. So he murders his younger son, the Duke of Clarence, whom the dying king has appointed as his protector. From then on, Richard plunges into a murderous machination in order to cement his position on the throne but is haunted by the ghosts of his victims who prophesy the sudden death of the pretender to the crown.
text_a: Richard, Duke of Gloucester, brother of King Edward IV and in the line of succession to the throne, is determined to obtain the crown by any means, including dangerous shortcuts. First, he kills his brother Clarence, just appointed by the dying king as protector of his two sons. The king, on his deathbed, unaware of Richard's treachery, appoints him protector of his heirs. Richard continues to sow death to get to the throne and is haunted by the ghosts of those he has killed or had killed. His reign of terror comes to an end, however, when he is defeated at the Battle of Bosworth Field.
text_b: Richard is Duke of Gloucester and his brother is heir to the throne of England. But Richard wants to be king himself and begins to eliminate his rivals with the help of his sinister executioner's murder. John Wyatt, Lady Alice's lover, is exiled to France and kills the feeble King Henry as well as the Duke of Clarence. Whenever a competitor is eliminated, Richard removes a figure from a dollhouse to represent the royal court. His brother Edward is now king, unaware of Richard's activities, so on his deathbed he appoints him protector of his sons. Only Henry Tudor stands in his way as John Wyatt returns and steals royal coffins. Wyatt is captured, but manages to escape and return to Henry. He's coming back to end the reign of terror.

anchor_text: When con man Stan Minton finds out he's about to go to prison, he hires a martial arts guru to teach him self-defense techniques. Once in prison, he also uses what he has learned from the "master" to help other inmates and to prevent them from hurting one another. As a result, over time he gains the trust of the other prisoners and becomes their leader. He succeeds in establishing a harmonious atmosphere in the prison, but this is soon ruined by the director of the prison, who intends to profit from the wars between the prisoners, since a climate of violence inside the prison and the death of some prisoners could lead to the closure of the prison, thus allowing the director to sell the facility. Stan initially decides to help him in exchange for an early release for good behavior. But on the day of the hearing, Stan decides to go back to prison to save the lives of his fellow inmates.
text_a: Stan Minton (Rob Schneider) is a fraudulent real estate agent who panics when he learns he's going to jail for fraud. Fear of being raped leads him to hire the mysterious guru known as The Master (David Carradine), who will help him become an expert in martial arts. After being incarcerated, Stan uses his newfound ability in martial arts to intimidate his fellow prisoners. He earns their respect and eventually becomes their leader, bringing peace and harmony to the prison yard. But the corrupt warden wants to urbanize the area where the prison is located, so he asks Stan to turn the courtyard into a war zone thus creating a reason to close the prison.
text_b: In Paris, Commissioner Stan Jalard (Jean-Paul Belmondo) and Inspector Simon Lecache (Michel Creton) are considering leaving the police to lead a more peaceful life in the West Indies. At a nightclub, on a routine visit, they spot Schneider, a formidable thug and public enemy number one. As Simon was about to be apprehended, he was killed at gunpoint by the bandits. Stan then decides to stay on the force to avenge Simon. As the godfather of Christian, Simon's son, who sometimes lives in a boarding house, sometimes at home, he takes care of his education. Two years later, when Stan became head of the Central Crime Control Bureau (OCRB), he was informed that Schneider had resurfaced in the capital. A long chase begins.

anchor_text: Real estate agent Stan is sentenced to three years in prison for fraud. He's got six months until he has to serve his sentence. Afraid of being raped in prison, he tries to learn karate. In the process, he meets "The Master", who teaches him various martial arts. Arriving at the prison, Stan takes on the most dangerous inmate, beating him to a pulp and earning the respect of the other inmates. The corrupt prison warden Gasque promises Stan that he will be released after just one year if he helps Gasque with his plans to close the prison to build a luxury housing complex on the site with the help of Korean mafiosi. Stan, who has assumed the regency among the prisoners and ensured that the fighting between the rival gangs has stopped, agrees to the proposal. When Stan learns that Gasque intends to incite a prisoner uprising to be bloody suppressed in order to achieve the closure of the prison, he changes his mind and makes Gasque's plan fail. Although he has to serve the full three years, he has ensured that the prison remains peaceful and that Gasque himself is a prisoner.
text_a: In Paris, Commissioner Stan Jalard (Jean-Paul Belmondo) and Inspector Simon Lecache (Michel Creton) are considering leaving the police to lead a more peaceful life in the West Indies. anchor_text: The Baker family lived in Midland before moving to Chicago because of Mr. Baker, who found his dream job there: coaching a football team. But there's something special about this family. It has 14 members including 12 children: Nora (22 years old), Charlie (17 years old), Lorraine (16 years old), Henry (12 years old), Sarah (11 years old), Jake (10 years old), Mark FedEx (9 years old), the twins Kim and Jessica (8 years old), Mike (6 years old) and the twins Kyle and Nigel (5 years old), as well as their parents Tom and Kate. Upon arriving in Chicago, Mrs. Baker was offered the opportunity to promote her book Cheaper by the Dozen, which would take 14 days. Will Mr. Baker be able to manage two teams while caring for his family during his wife's absence?
text_a: The Baker family consists of father Tom, mother Kate, and their twelve children, who live in a house in Midland, Indiana. The children are: Nora, a 22-year-old job seeker who lives with her boyfriend Hank; Charlie and Lorraine, high school students; Henry, a boy who plays the clarinet who is very fond of his brother Charlie; Sarah, the leader of the troubles; Jake, a boy who loves skateboarding; Mark, a very shy child whose only friend is the frog Beans; the biovular twins Kim and Jessica; Mike; the monozygotic twins Nigel and Kyle. When Tom Baker achieves his dream of coaching a football team, he moves his family from the countryside to Evanston, Illinois. The children are very sad and sorry about this move. His wife, Kate, succeeds in achieving her dream of publishing a book, called Cheaper by the Dozen, about the lives of her 12 children. So to launch her new book, she has to go on a promotional trip, leaving Tom alone with the kids for two weeks. There's a lot of trouble around the corner at this point.
text_b: Kate Brady is a strictly religious girl from Ireland. She works as a grocery saleswoman and lives with her girlfriend in a sublet in Dublin. She is only moderately endowed with outward qualities, so it is not easy for her to get a husband, although she would like one for her life. One day, however, she still finds an opportunity to fall in love with a stately specimen of a man. Eugene Gaillard is the Chosen One. Although he writes books, he shows himself (later) to be by no means an extraordinary man. Because she took the initiative, he follows her; then Kate hesitates again. For a time, they are happy together. However, he is much older than the girl and is married, but lives separately from his wife in a feudal country house. Soon what was naturally to be expected occurs: separation, separation. Still on the ship that is supposed to take Kate to London, she hopes that her lover will find her again. In vain.

anchor_text: Tom and Kate Baker decided 23 years ago that they wanted to have eight children. Eventually, there were twelve. The extended family lives in the country, where Tom coaches the local college football team. Kate quit her job as a sports reporter. One day, Tom gets the dream offer to coach the Stallions. That's why the family has to go to Chicago, which bothers the kids. Nevertheless, Tom and Kate eventually manage to move in with them. But Kate has to go on a promotional tour for her new book (which is called "A Dozen Cheaper") all over the states. Tom is alone with his children for the first time. The situation is becoming more and more acute. Nevertheless, Tom manages to achieve success with his football team and keeps telling Kate on the phone that everything is under control. The children, however, do not see it that way, and the home is in chaos. Eventually, alarmed by the children, Kate cancels her trip. When another child runs away, Tom makes his decision and ends his career as a football coach for the Stallions. Kate's book is on the bestseller list for twelve months, Tom rejects twelve job offers until he gets one that allows him to spend more time with the family, and they celebrate as once again as part of the possible harmoniously united family together Christmas.
