His wife, Kate, succeeds in achieving her dream of publishing a book, called Cheaper by the Dozen, about the lives of her 12 children. So to launch her new book, she has to go on a promotional trip, leaving Tom alone with the kids for two weeks. There's a lot of trouble around the corner at this point.

anchor_text: A young photographer from Australia named Clare is spending her holidays in Berlin. She falls under the spell of a certain Andi, an English teacher at a sports school. He invites her to his home, and after a night of love, the young woman will later understand that the young man is determined not to let her leave his home.
text_a: Clare, a young photographer, travels to Germany, where she meets an attractive young man, Andi. Waking up after a night of passion, Clare realizes that Andi has locked her in the apartment and will never let her go again.
text_b: The novel paints a picture of Germany in 1923, a year of hyperinflation, ten years before the Nazis came to power. The first volume takes place in Berlin, where the hero is the symbol of this time; the second volume subtitled The Burning Campaign shows a peasant revolt in East Prussia.

anchor_text: A young photographer from Australia named Clare is spending her holidays in Berlin. She falls under the spell of a certain Andi, an English teacher at a sports school. He invites her to his home, and after a night of love, the young woman will later understand that the young man is determined not to let her leave his home.
text_a: The novel paints a picture of Germany in 1923, a year of hyperinflation, ten years before the Nazis came to power. The first volume takes place in Berlin, where the hero is the symbol of this time; the second volume subtitled The Burning Campaign shows a peasant revolt in East Prussia.
text_b: Clare Havel (Teresa Palmer), a young Australian journalist travels to Germany to tour and photograph the country's architecture. In Berlin, she meets Andi Werner (Max Riemelt), a very attractive man with whom she spends a night of love. The next morning, Clare discovers that Andi has locked her in his apartment with the excuse that he forgot to leave her a copy of the key: but in reality he plots to keep her kidnapped with sinister plans and without the possibility of letting her go so easily.

anchor_text: Aura Petrescu, who suffers from an eating disorder, breaks out of a sanatorium. After escaping from the asylum, she is stopped by David Parsons when she tries to take her own life. David promises Aura that he will support her. Shortly thereafter, she is arrested by the police and returned to her parents. In her parents' house, a séance is held one evening under the direction of Aura's medially gifted mother, Adriana Petrescu. During this séance, Adriana apparently makes contact with the spirit of a murder victim and runs out of the house as if in a trance, followed by her husband. Aura watches her parents as they leave the house and follows them into a forest. There, she sees a murderer holding the heads of her parents in his hands and flees. This killer has already killed several people by decapitation, and now it seems that Aura is also being hunted by him. After some time, Aura's therapist is shot by the police, as the severed heads of the murder victims are found in his trunk. Subsequently, Aura vanishes without a trace, and David frantically tries to find her. When he sees a passerby wearing a bracelet like Aura, he follows him to his apartment. There he will be crushed. It turns out that Aura's mother is the killer. She only faked her own murder so she could continue her revenge unmolested. Many years ago, when she gave birth to her first child, there was a blackout and an explosion, and the doctor freaked out and cut the baby's head off with a scalpel. Just before the mother can attack David again in the basement, a neighbor boy puts the decapitation device used in the first murders around her neck and decapitates her.
text_a: Aura (Asia Argento), a young woman suffering from anorexia, escapes from the psychiatric hospital where she is being treated and meets a young man (Christopher Rydell), who helps her to hide so that she will not be hospitalized again, but her efforts are in vain as the girl is found and taken back to the hospital. When Aura returns to the hospital, a sequence of murders of people inside the nursing home begins to unfold. All the murders are done by decapitating the victims with a handmade club and making their heads disappear, and among the victims is Aura's own father. That's where Aura and David team up to find the killer. Eventually, they find that the killer is a woman, who turns out to be Aura's mother. All this, leads to remember how it was that Aura's mother would have become a murderer and explain that Dr. Lloyd (Brad Dourif) was supposed to care for and deliver Nicholas, Aura's brother, to his mother, but an electrical storm and a power outage prove fatal to the baby as his head is cut off while being delivered to his mother. The head nurse and the doctor, against Aura's will, give her an electroshock in order to erase everything from her mind.[1]
text_b: Amid an aura of horror tale, pregnant with supernatural elements, unfolds the story of Allan Gray, a young man absorbed in the study of demonology and vampire traditions. His interest in the extravagant ideas of past centuries turned him into a dreamer and a fantasizer, lost in the borderline between the real and the supernatural. One day, wandering aimlessly late at night, he arrives at an isolated inn near a river in the village of Courtempierre. Little by little he discovers that strange things are happening in the village: murders, sudden and unexplained illnesses... plus the presence of strange creatures. Allan is required to help one of the daughters of Bernard, the castle's owner. After a blood transfusion to save the young woman, a debilitated Allan begins to suffer hallucinations, seeing himself being buried alive. Recovered, he discovers that it is all due to the presence in the village of an old vampire witch, who is stabbed in the chest while sleeping in her graveyard tomb.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of Yong-hoo, a martial arts champion who gains divine powers to fight against a powerful force of evil. After a tragic childhood, which resulted in the death of his parents, Yong-hoo harbored a deep resentment towards the Almighty. After a fight in the United States, he begins to have stigmata that force him to seek the help of the priest Ahn. The priest, who is an exorcist, sees potential in Yong-hoo when he discovers his power. The two become partners and fight side by side.
text_a: The film tells the story of Yong-hoo, a martial arts champion who gains divine powers to fight a powerful evil force, after he develops unexplained wounds on the palms of his hands called stigmata. Although he shows himself to be strong, Yong-hoo actually suffers emotional scars from losing his father in a tragic accident at a young age. In search of answers, Yong-hoo meets Father Ahn, a Vatican exorcist, and they end up joining forces in their fight against the powerful evil forces wreaking havoc on the human world. On the other hand, Ji Shin is a mysterious young man with the ability to see the weaknesses of others, use and exploit them to his advantage and cause evil. With Ahn's father at his side, Yong-hoo will use his newfound power to confront Ji Shin.[4]
text_b: A mentally handicapped man, Yong Gu, lives with his 6-year-old daughter Ye-seung. Unfortunately, he finds himself accused of a murder he didn't commit simply because he was at the scene. Convicted, he is locked in a cell with five other prisoners who eventually help him bring his little daughter to prison.

anchor_text: When he was a child, Yong-Hoo (Park Seo-joon) lost his father in an accident and remained suspicious of those around him and of the Christian faith. As a martial arts champion, he achieved everything he ever dreamed of until mysterious wounds appeared in the palm of his hand. Seeking the help of priest An (Ahn Sung-ki), who is also an exorcist, they both find themselves fighting a mysterious evil force.
text_a: A mentally handicapped man, Yong Gu, lives with his 6-year-old daughter Ye-seung. Unfortunately, he finds himself accused of a murder he didn't commit simply because he was at the scene. Convicted, he is locked in a cell with five other prisoners who eventually help him bring his little daughter to prison.
text_b: The film tells the story of Yong-hoo, a martial arts champion who gains divine powers to fight against a powerful force of evil. After a tragic childhood, which resulted in the death of his parents, Yong-hoo harbored a deep resentment towards the Almighty. After a fight in the United States, he begins to have stigmata that force him to seek the help of the priest Ahn. The priest, who is an exorcist, sees potential in Yong-hoo when he discovers his power. The two become partners and fight side by side.

anchor_text: In Great Depression-ravaged Yorkshire, workman Sam Carraclough loses his job. Struggling to support his family, he is forced to sell his dog, Lassie, a beautiful female Rough Collie that his son Joe is very fond of. The new "owner" - the wealthy Duke of Rudling - keeps Lassie in a large cage in the park of his residence, not far from Carraclough's house. From here, Lassie manages to "easily" escape twice (once by digging a hole under the cage and once by climbing over it) and return to her beloved master. With the excuse of a dog beauty contest, but mainly to prevent a new escape, the Duke decides to transfer Lessie to his estate in Scotland, several hundred miles away from Yorkshire. Here, however, little Priscilla, the Duke's niece, opposed to the cruelty with which Aldo - the servant assigned to guard the dogs - treats Lassie and realizes the dog's unhappiness, favours its final escape. The animal then faces the long journey back to Yorkshire, overcoming threats and dangers (from nature and man), but also encountering help and affection along the way. Finally, when Joe seems to have given up all hope of seeing his beloved dog again, an injured and exhausted Lassie reappears in the schoolyard where she used to wait for him to walk him home. At which point the Duke and his niece pretend not to recognize in that debilitated animal the beautiful collie previously purchased and instead offer Mr Carraclough a job on their estate, just as a guardian of the dogs.
text_a: Frank Nolan and Martin rob a bank and make a fortune; Frank is injured and left helpless by Nolan, who takes the money. Frank can still shoot him in the back. Many years later, Martin controls a sizable tract of land and is confined to a wheelchair. With numerous men, he extorted protection money and rents from the surrounding farmers and brutally crushed any form of resistance. Only Sarah, a young widow, remains steadfast. One day, a stranger named Lassiter joins her, having met Sarah's brother. Lassiter scares Martin with his knowledge of his former buddy Frank, who is still alive and out for revenge. In exchange for a reward, he'd calm him down. Martin sends his son and his best man to confront Frank, but they fail. Lassiter still has a letter in the back of his hand containing Frank's whereabouts. Martin is now entering the trade. In this cat-and-mouse game, only Lassiter has the upper hand. He arranges for the two former friends to eliminate each other and for him to collect the loot himself.
text_b: Set in the depression era of Yorkshire, England, Mr. and Mrs. Carraclough are going through hard times and are forced to sell their collie, Lassie, to the rich Duke of Rudling, who has always admired her. Young Joe Carraclough is heartbroken by the loss of his partner. However, Lassie will want nothing to do with the Duke and finds ways to escape his kennels and return to Joe. The Duke finally takes Lassie to his home hundreds of miles away in Scotland. There, his granddaughter Priscilla senses the dog's unhappiness and arranges his escape. Lassie then sets off on a long journey home to Yorkshire. He faces many dangers along the way - dog-hunters and a violent storm - but he also meets kind people who offer help and comfort. In the end, when Joe has given up hope of ever seeing his dog again, the tired Lassie returns to her favorite resting place in the schoolyard at home. There, Lassie is happily reunited with the boy she loves.

anchor_text: Lassie, a collie, is the best friend of young Joe, who lives in Yorkshire, England. Every day Lassie picks the boy up from school at four o'clock in the afternoon. With the family in dire straits, Joe's parents sell Lassie one day to the Duke of Rudling, whose granddaughter Priscilla is delighted with the collie. But Lassie runs away and returns to the family, only to be taken back by an assistant of the Duke. When Lassie runs off with the Duke again and Joe tries to run away with her, his father brings the dog back one more time. Joe's mom's happy that Lassie's out of the house. One day, while walking with the Duke's canine guard, Lassie breaks out again; a protracted escape through wind and weather begins, during which she almost becomes a victim of hunters who mistake her for a wild dog. When the exhausted Lassie reaches a farm, she is picked up by the farmers. When the farmer's wife notices that Lassie gets restless every day at four o'clock, the couple lets Lassie move. Lassie meets the gypsy Rowlie, who takes her on a journey where she one day defends him from thieves. During her subsequent journey, she manages to escape two dogcatchers. Lassie returns home from the long journey exhausted. The family is still suffering. However, Joe's mother is now relieved that Lassie is back because her son Joe was very sad without her. The Duke will be visiting the family. He offers Joe's father a job guarding his dogs. When he sees Lassie, he pretends not to recognize her. At four o'clock, despite her exhaustion, Lassie leaves to pick up her friend Joe from school as usual. Joe and Priscilla become friends and spend their time together with Lassie and their offspring.

anchor_text: In the vicinity of Weimar, Edward and Charlotte, an aristocratic couple united after a first marriage, invite a childhood friend of Edward named the Captain, and then, despite Charlotte's ominous premonition, Charlotte's beautiful and orphaned niece, Odile, joins them. The description of a scientific experiment, that of "elective affinities", in chapter four of the first part, already allows foreshadowing the continuation of the narrative in which Edward's increasingly exclusive attachment to Odile will have disastrous consequences.
text_a: In this novel, Goethe questions the very foundations of marriage. Four people who spend an isolated season in a rural mansion are drawn to each other in ways that threaten established and expected relationships. The force of this attraction is imperative and mysterious, like the natural powers - from which Goethe borrows the title - that push certain minerals to join and others to separate. Reflections on morality, self-control, and the unhealthy alienation caused by the difficulty of facing one's own passions make The Elective Affinities a highly topical work.
text_b: The novel depicts family life in a Chinese village before World War I. The story begins with Wang Lung's marriage to the young O-Len. Despite the hardships, the couple managed to accumulate a sizable fortune through unremitting hard work. The novel ends as Wang Lung, now an old man, learns that his sons intend to sell their land.

anchor_text: 15 years ago, Ben Walker decided to leave his college sweetheart Wendy and ultimately renounce his faith to pursue a lucrative career as a businessman. Instead of a doctorate in theology (DTh) and a minor in Biblical archaeology, he graduated from Harvard Business School at the top of his class. In the process, Ben has become the head of his own firm and a financial genius. Just as he had proposed to his beautiful but materialistic fiancée and bought an expensive car, his brand-new car broke down on the way to the airport. Like an angel, the rude mechanic appears, telling him to look at what his life would have been like if he had heeded God's call 15 years earlier. Suddenly, Ben finds himself in a different reality, where he is married to Wendy and has two daughters: Kimberly, a rebellious teenager, and her little sister Megan. It is Sunday morning, when his family is getting ready for church and he is to give his inaugural sermon as the new pastor of the congregation. The mechanic "angel" explains to Ben that God is giving him this experience to show him what would have happened if he had followed the other call. If Ben wants to return to his old life, he must first learn the value of family and faith and perhaps rediscover the love of his life.
text_a: Ben Walker decides to leave his college girlfriend Wendy and his faith to pursue a fruitful job opportunity, graduating with honors from Harvard Business School instead of earning a Ph.D. in biblical architecture from Moody Bible Institute. Fifteen years later, Ben has become a financial wizard at the head of his company, and he's about to marry a pretty but materialistic girl. When his car breaks down on the way to the airport, a grumpy mechanic shows up in front of him, and tells him that he needs to see what his life would have been like if he had followed God's call. Suddenly, Ben finds himself in an alternate reality married to Wendy and with two daughters - the rebellious teen Kimberly and her younger sister, Megan - who are preparing for Sunday morning Mass, where he has been assigned to preach for the first time as their new pastor. The mechanic explains that this is an experience that God has allowed him to show Ben what would happen if he accepted his other call, the great "what if"... If Ben wants to go back to his old life, he must first learn to appreciate the value of faith and family, then understand the true value of life.
text_b: Ben Kalmen was a successful car dealer in New York. When he learns that there are irregularities in his heart and his doctor wants to do more tests, his world changes. He's never had any further tests done because he's afraid of the results. He begins to cheat on his wife Nancy, who eventually divorces him. Afraid of his age, he takes refuge in one-night stands with women much younger than himself. In addition, he begins to defraud fellow dealers and customers, for which he is eventually convicted and loses his dealer's license. The only way he gets out of jail is by paying a hefty fine. As he is about to regain his dealer's license, he is asked to accompany Allyson, the daughter of his current girlfriend Jordan Karsch, to the university interview as Leumund. On this trip, he ends up in bed with Allyson. When Allyson briefly informs her mother, Jordan throws Ben out of the house and ensures that he does not get his dealer's license back and thus ends up financially. Ben's refusal to change his lifestyle, his refusal to accept his age, and his pursuit of young women have resulted in his own daughter turning her back on him. Now that he's lost everything, he starts waiting tables at his college roommate Jimmy's restaurant. As a warning to stay away from Allyson in the future, he is beaten up on her father's orders. His ex-wife Nancy finally presents Ben with the choice of accepting his old age or continuing to pursue youth. His decision leaves the film open.

text_b: Fifteen years ago, Ben Walker (Kevin Sorbo) decided to leave his college girlfriend, Wendy (Kristy Swanson) and ultimately his faith, in order to pursue a lucrative business opportunity. Thus, he graduated first in his class from Harvard Business School instead of receiving a doctorate in Theology with a specialization in Biblical Archaeology. In the present, Ben has become a financial wizard as the head of his company, and has just proposed marriage to a lovely, materialistic bride. When his car breaks down on the way to the airport, he is visited by a brusque but angelic mechanic (John Ratzenberger), who tells him that he has to see what his life would have been like had he followed God's call. Suddenly, Ben finds himself in an alternate reality. In it, he is married to Wendy, and has two daughters: a rebellious teen named Kimberly (Debby Ryan), and Kimberly's younger sister, Megan (played by debutante Taylor Groothuis), and is preparing for church on Sunday morning, where he is scheduled to give his first sermon as a new pastor. The mechanic explains that this is an experiment that God has set in motion to show Ben what would have happened if he had accepted his other profession, the great What would have happened if...? If Ben wants to get back to his old life, the first thing he has to do is learn to appreciate the value of faith and family, and maybe rediscover the love of his life. In the tradition of it's a wonderful life and family man, What If... tells the story of a man whose idea of what he's missing reminds him of what he really wants.

anchor_text: The Quaternus magne expeditionis (Book of the Great Expedition) was created in 11501152 in a first edition under Roger II, then revised under William II in the years 1167/1168 and provided with an augmentum (addition). The purpose of the revision was not to supplement documents, although in Abruzzo and parts of Apulia quaterniones curie are occasionally cited as the basis of the study. Rather, the aim was to compile the documentation for an extended range which could be offered as and when required. It was a precautionary measure, no specific reason can be identified. Where the economic circumstances of the individual premium contributor permitted it, a doubling of the planned bid should be requested per augmento. In addition to the combat cavalrymen, servientes are also registered, usually in a ratio of 2:1. Churches were exempt from military service per se, but as part of the preparations for the great campaign, a participation pro auxilio magne expeditionis was planned. The list of 1175 (No. 12631372) has no title of its own in the manuscript. The hierarchical list for the Capitanate (Pheudatarii iusticiaratus Capitanatae) is divided into secular (No. 13731427) and clerical (No. 14281442). For our knowledge of the Norman and Stave nobility in the mainland part of the kingdom, the catalogue is an important source that has no parallel in the European empires of the 12th century. Similarities exist with the Domesday Book, which originated in Norman England, but has a different purpose.
text_a: The work begins with a concise summary of the geography of the world and then gives a list of geographical data on the Iberian Peninsula and its provinces, cities and rivers and an overview of the chronology of world history since Adam, the progenitor of mankind. It then covers the history of the Roman Empire from Romulus to the Byzantine emperor Tiberius II (698-705). It follows the history of the Visigoths, beginning with King Athanarich, and in particular of the Visigothic Kingdom on the Iberian Peninsula until King Roderich (710711), with whom the empire went down. The conclusion is the history of the Kingdom of Asturias from Pelayo (718737) to the present day of the author. As the time of the chronicler approaches, the account becomes more and more detailed. At the same time, the style changes; the older history is summarized with dry words, the deeds of Alfonso III are described in detail and vividly by the chronicler as a knowledgeable contemporary. He glorifies this ruler, whose military success he calls "holy victory", thus avoiding the later customary interpretation of the fight against the Muslims as a holy war.
text_b: The Catalogus baronum is the collective name (not original, but used in modern times) of three texts found in the Angevin registers (n. 242 from 1322, pp. 13-63) which contain feudal data on the Principality of Capua and the Duchy of Apulia. Most of it consists of the Quaternus magne expeditionis (no. 1-1262), begun during the reign of Roger II, in the years 1150-1152, and revised in the period 1167-1168. According to historian Evelyn Jamison, it was prepared for military defense (magna expeditio) by the Greek-German alliance. The entries are in geographical order and begin with the Terra di Bari indicating whether the fief was assigned directly by the King or if it was of a valvassore, the name of the feudal lord, the name of the fief, the valuation in units of soldiers (milites) that it can provide and the total yield cum augmento. During the revision of 1167-1168, which concerned mainly the Abruzzi, but also partly Apulia, quaterniones curie were used. The second part (1263-1372) is another Norman register, drawn up around 1175, containing the knights of Aquinas, Arce and Sora. The third part is from the Swabian period (circa 1239-1240) and contains the secular feudal lords (circa 1373-1427) and ecclesiastical lords (circa 1428-1442) of the Captaincy. The text in the Angevin register is taken from the Swabian copy. The Angioino manuscript was destroyed in 1943 along with most of the documents of the State Archive of Naples, transported to the historic Villa Montesano in an attempt to preserve them, but fortunately there are photographs that reproduce it in its entirety taken before the fire intentionally set by the German occupying forces on the morning of 30 September 1943.

anchor_text: Journalist couple Jenny and John Grogan from Michigan decide to leave the cold winters behind and head south to Palm Beach. There they buy a house and soon face the question of whether they are ready to have children. Since John, in particular, is not quite sure, they decide to "try out" parenting and family life with a dog. This leads to the purchase of a Labrador retriever puppy, which John names in honor of Bob Marley Marley. He becomes a beloved member of the family, causing a great deal of chaos. Even the animal trainer is desperate and throws Marley out of the course. The film follows the couple's subsequent years: marital disputes are overcome, children are born and Marley accompanies the family as a faithful friend. John takes a job near Philadelphia. But the family of five is slowly coming to terms with the fact that Marley has grown old and weak. The parents are preparing the children for the fact that Marley won't live much longer. In Philadelphia, John is reunited with his former friend and colleague Sebastian, who has chosen a different path in life. He made a career as a journalist and leads an independent and varied life without a constant girlfriend with constantly changing partners. They assure each other of their mutual respect and then go their separate ways. Marley has repeated stomach problems and the vet advises John to put him to sleep no matter what he does. Marley is buried in the garden and John remembers the years together and the animal's unconditional love for him.
text_a: Bob Saginowski (Tom Hardy) is a bartender at a neighborhood bar in Brooklyn. Marvin Stipler (James Gandolfini) ceded ownership of the bar years earlier to Chechen mobsters and now manages it with Bob. On his way home, Bob finds a battered pit bull pup abandoned in a trash can. While rescuing him, she meets Nadia (Noomi Rapace) and Bob leaves the dog in her care until she can decide whether to adopt him. When two masked gunmen rob the bar, Marv is upset because Bob told Detective Investigator Torres (John Ortiz) that one of the gunmen was wearing a broken watch. Torres has seen Bob before at the church that they've both been attending regularly for some time. The Chechen thug Chovka (Michael Aronov) then threatens Marv and Bob and tells them that they must compensate for the stolen money. Later, Marv meets one of the perpetrators, Fitz (James Frecheville), and reveals to him that he orchestrated the robbery. Bob decides to keep the dog and calls him Rocco, while being joined by Nadia, who agrees to take care of the dog whenever Bob attends the bar.
text_b: During their wedding night, the newlyweds, John (Owen Wilson) and Jenny Grogan (Jennifer Aniston), decide to leave Michigan with the goal of starting a new life as a married couple in sunny West Palm Beach, Florida. There, they both get jobs at rival newspapers, buy their first house, and make their way into their new careers, all the while facing new challenges as a couple, including taking the step of having a child. Uncertain about his ability to be a father, John asks his friend Sebastian (Eric Dane) for advice and he suggests that he give his wife a pet. Convinced of his friend's good idea, John adopts Marley, a five-pound Labrador. The problems come when Marley grows up and becomes a 100 pound adult dog but without any training and certainly with the same energy as any puppy. The problem is such that John will have to seriously consider changing the dog's attitude, which will bring him one problem after another.

anchor_text: Sasha Tran and Marcus Kim, both of Asian immigrant background, have known each other since their earliest childhoods in San Francisco, but after a brief teenage adventure, they lost touch. Since then, 16 years have passed, and the two have grown up in completely different circumstances: Sasha, who had learned to cook with Marcus' mother, has now become a star chef. Marcus' life, on the other hand, has been almost "frozen" since his mother's early death: he still lives with his father and works in his air conditioning service. He's also in a band, but it's not a hit. Marcus meets Sasha again when she returns to San Francisco to open a restaurant. Once again, the two are drawn to each other, but their completely different circumstances make a lasting relationship impossible at first. It is only when Marcus is able to overcome his fears triggered by the loss of his mother that the two are reunited.
text_a: Two childhood friends from San Francisco who haven't been in touch since a brief teenage fling ended badly. In the 16 years since then, they have grown into very different circumstances: Sasha is a famous chef, while Marcus still lives in his childhood bedroom and works for his father's air conditioning service, his life almost frozen since his mother's untimely death. Sasha and Marcus reconnect when Sasha returns to San Francisco to open a restaurant and the romantic chemistry of their teenage years continues, but Marcus' fears and Sasha's demanding career challenge their relationship.
text_b: Sasha is a former medical student and taxi driver in Berlin. When he learns from his girlfriend Lina that she is pregnant with his child, his life is thrown into disarray and he makes an accident. At the hospital, he meets 87-year-old Ella Friday, who is about to have an eye operation. As Sascha learns about her medical background and the unnecessary surgery would be too much of a risk, he abducts her from the hospital. At home, Mrs. Ella, as Sasha calls her, learns that Lina and Sasha have separated. Because the police are looking for Sasha for the kidnapping, he wants to take her back to the hospital, but Mrs. Ella refuses. Instead, they drive with Sasha's roommate Klaus to an abandoned hospital in the Berlin area. Here, after World War II, Mrs. Ella had an affair with an American soldier named Jason. Sasha finds out that Jason last lived in Paris and wants to arrange a reunion. The three of them decide to go to Paris. This is where they're gonna hear that Jason moved out. At a nursing home, they learn their new address on the Breton coast. Shortly after Paris, Klaus' car breaks down. During the repair time, Klaus takes Mrs. Ella for a spin on a motorcycle. This leads to a quarrel between Klaus and Sascha, which is soon settled. Meanwhile, Mrs. Ella has gone on to Jason's residence. There she meets Anna, Jason's daughter, who tells her that Jason died the previous year. After a short period of mourning, Ella returns to Berlin with Sascha and Klaus. As soon as she arrives, Ella thanks Sascha for the good time and dies. He's finally reconciled with Lina.

anchor_text: When Sasha (Ali Wong), now an internationally renowned chef, moves to San Francisco to open a new restaurant, he runs into his old friend Marcus (Randall Park), a musician still dependent on his father. Despite their very different lifestyles, the attraction between them never seems to fade. When the talented chef's fiancé dumps her shortly before their wedding, a new opportunity opens up for the two. Will Sasha and Marcus be able to overcome their past and give each other a second chance at love?
text_a: In the middle of the summer, 30-year-old Sasha died suddenly. While they barely know each other, her partner Lawrence and his sister Zoe become close. They share as much as they can the pain and the weight of absence, between Berlin, Paris and New York. Three summers, three cities, the time of their return to the light, carried by the memory of the one they loved.
text_b: Two childhood friends from San Francisco who haven't been in touch since a brief teenage fling ended badly. In the 16 years since then, they have grown into very different circumstances: Sasha is a famous chef, while Marcus still lives in his childhood bedroom and works for his father's air conditioning service, his life almost frozen since his mother's untimely death. Sasha and Marcus reconnect when Sasha returns to San Francisco to open a restaurant and the romantic chemistry of their teenage years continues, but Marcus' fears and Sasha's demanding career challenge their relationship.

anchor_text: A night watchman flees in panic from the morgue where he works and is accidentally run over. The police are called in to conduct the investigation due to some inconsistencies that are detected, and Inspector Jaime Peña soon discovers that the body of a woman, Mayka Villaverde Freire, recently deceased, has disappeared. The inspector then decides to turn to the widower and over the course of the evening discovers the truth behind the woman's death and solves the mystery of the stolen body.
text_a: The body of a woman (Belén Rueda), died that same afternoon due to a heart attack, mysteriously disappears from the morgue without leaving any trace. Inspector Jaime Peña (José Coronado) investigates the strange occurrence with the help of Álex Ulloa (Hugo Silva), the widower of the missing woman.
text_b: Alice, a 12-year-old girl, arrives at a police station to report a murder committed by her mother, the very wealthy and dangerous Eva Kristensen. The police gave little credence to her words, and Alice was forced to flee. Along the way, she meets Hugo, a mysterious man who takes her under his wing and agrees to accompany her to Portugal where she wants to find her father, officially dead. In the guerrilla war in Eastern Europe, Hugo became a master of warfare, but he was still traumatized by the death of an innocent child. However, Eva's men are after them, as well as Anita, a young policewoman convinced that Alice was telling the truth. A sensitive killer who saves a little girl's life.

anchor_text: Harry Caul, a man of principle and a tacit misanthropist, is a great specialist in spinning. He's on a mission to follow a couple and record their conversations. After completing his mission, he discovers by repeatedly listening to his recordings, that the couple is in danger of death. Recalling a previous mission in which a family was killed, he is caught in a kind of moral dilemma, which will haunt him until the end...
text_a: The film tells the story of Tom Powers and Matt Doyle, two young men in the Chicago underworld who engage in mischief. With the arrival of prohibition, both take the opportunity to enter the business of smuggling alcohol and gain fame and money, but not without having to face the police and rival gangs. Tom seeks to keep in touch with his brother Mike and his mother, the only person he truly loves.
text_b: It tells the story of a professional spy (Gene Hackman) who works for both private and public clients. So far, he's never considered what could happen with all the information he gets from people, but when arranging a wiretap for a client that takes place in a square and right at the beginning of the film, he'll be tempted to listen to what the two people he's been assigned to spy on are saying. Soon the situation will haunt him and he will want to know more by becoming the hunted hunter.

anchor_text: Argentina in the 1920s. Strictly guarded by her mother and a governess, Ana and her older sisters grow up. Their father is a wealthy and influential landowner. In order to suppress Ana's awakening erotic sensations and keep her "pure", the sensitive girl is not only isolated from the sisters, but also largely shielded from the environment. Nevertheless, the mother, who imposes her prudish and bigoted religious prejudices on the children with great intransigence, cannot prevent Ana from meeting by chance with the young hot-blooded politician Pablo Aguirre and secretly falling in love with this man. In their eyes, he becomes a hero when he stands up for freedom of speech in the House of Representatives. This calls Paul's political opponents to action. A heated argument ensues, during which Pablo publicly slaps Congressman Esquirel in the face, who then challenges him to a duel. Ana's father, who is friendly to Pablo, provides the garden of his estate for the duel. The night before the duel, Ana goes to Pablo's guest room and gives herself to him, voluntarily or involuntarily. Pablo survives the duel, but Ana now begins to hate him so much that she wishes him dead. Years pass; the mother dies, the sisters marry. Ana regularly encounters her former lover, who ruined her life, but whose image haunts her constantly. A helpless gesture, a silent, embarrassed look, are the only, tentative hints of a flirtatious-romantic, but short-lived bond that no one will ever know about because it was illicit, that is, "sinful".
text_a: The story centers on Ana Castro, a fourteen-year-old teenager who lives with a very religious mother and a father el Dr. Castro was interested in politics and government. Ana also lives with her sisters: Julieta (who openly disobeys her mother's religious teachings) and Isabel, always absent and sometimes very strict and vain. Ana begins to experience new changes in her life, which will leave her totally confused. During the summer holidays her cousin Vicenta comes to visit the family and always intends to annoy Ana with the boys, and they even talk about sex and matters that she is not used to hearing. As the days go by, Ana begins to be disturbed by her mother's religious obsessions, thoughts about sex, and the image of hell and the apocalypse instilled in her by her nanny. But his life will change when he meets the deputy Pablo Aguirre, a womanizer who killed several opponents in the duels. Aguirre challenges to a gun duel the deputy Esquivel, an opponent who accuses him of fraudulent. The duel is going to take place at Ana's house, where thinking it may be her last dance she invites her to dance at a party. On the night of the duel, Ana approaches Aguirre's room to wish him luck and gives him her rosary. There, Aguirre ends up raping her and Ana heads to her room. Then, she wishes him dead in the duel but Aguirre survives and becomes friends with his father. Years go by, Aguirre still goes to Ana's house to visit her father and they stay for hours talking. The rape is never revealed and Ana ends up sadly wandering the streets. She mentions that she sees Aguirre everywhere and even appears at the most critical moments.
text_b: Now a woman, Ana remembers her childhood twenty years earlier. She is bound by a deep affection for her mother, who died of an incurable disease, while she hated her father - a Francoist officer unfaithful to his poor wife - to the point of wanting to poison him (but the potion used was an innocuous powder). The man did die, but of a heart attack, during a love affair with a friend of the house. An aunt, Paolina, then began to care for the orphan and her two younger sisters, but she did not know how to win their affection. Ana, whose mother constantly returned to her dreams, believed - convinced of her own magical powers - that she could rid herself of her as well, in the same way she had used with her father. Of course, nothing happened to Paolina either. Finally, with the reopening of schools, the saddest summer of Anna's childhood came to an end.

anchor_text: Single mother Millie lives in South Central Los Angeles in 1992 and has a heart for vagrants. Although she already has eight children, all of whom live in her apartment, she will soon be taking in another child. The only white person in the predominantly African-American, Latino, and Korean neighborhood is her neighbor Obie. As it seems, the two should never become allies, but when the four officers who brutally beat African-American Rodney King during an arrest a year earlier are acquitted, which was completely disproportionate, and riots begin in the city, they are forced to work together to protect millions of children.
text_a: Millie Dunbar is a single mother with eight children in foster care in South Los Angeles. His neighbor Obie is the only white man in the neighborhood in a neighborhood populated by African-Americans, Latinos and Koreans, grumpy and a drinker. But it is he who comes to the rescue of Millie, his next-door neighbor. In fact, Millie, a single mother of eight children, many of whom were taken into foster care to snatch them from the street, is first unjustly arrested and then, once released, is forced to return to the eye of the cyclone in search of three of her children, who have recklessly left home. Together they form an unlikely pair trying to contact her children, to get them to safety during the three days of the Los Angeles riots that erupted over Rodney King's beating. The story beyond the historicization imposed by the clothes, the huge use of vintage television material and the events themselves (above all the murder by a Korean trader of a black girl accused of stealing a fruit juice), is sadly current.
text_b: Los Angeles in the '50s. A serial killer is kidnapping and killing little girls. The city is traumatized by these many unsolved murders. The police are unable to find the murderer. In the face of mass hysteria sweeping California, the mob decides to investigate to neutralize him before he kills again as federal authorities multiply raids and searches in the city. A race against time begins between the Mafia and the police, both in pursuit of the elusive child killer. A blind balloon salesman says that he can recognize him because he regularly hears the whistling of a man approaching the little girls. The murderer's name is Martin H. Harrow and he's being intercepted by the underworld.

text_b: The film is set in the city of Los Angeles in 1992, where riots occur following the acquittal of several police officers involved in the beating of black American taxi driver Rodney King. Olli (played by Daniel Craig), one of the few white residents living in South Central, helps his neighbor and divorced mother (Halle Berry) find and protect her children in a city filled with chaos and violence. Kings is based on real events and centers its story around what happened for six days in the city of Los Angeles in the year 1992. A group of rioters sparked a citywide spiral of violence in order to show their disapproval of the acquittal of several police officers involved in the brutal assault of black American taxi driver Rodney King. The tragedy left in its wake dozens of dead and around 2000 injured. Recording began in December 2016 and continued through February 2017.

anchor_text: 1536: Immediately after the execution of his second wife, Anne Boleyn, King Henry VIII marries Jane Seymour, but she dies eighteen months later giving birth to the heir to the throne. The monarch then remarries a German princess, Anne of Clèves (played by Laughton's real wife Elsa Lanchester). This marriage is never consummated and the two spouses divorce by mutual consent. After the divorce, Henry VIII marries the beautiful and ambitious Lady Catherine Howard. The girl, who had given up the love of her life in favor of the crown, after the wedding, falls in love and betrays the King with Thomas Culpeper. The infidelity is discovered by Henry's courtiers and the couple is executed. 1543: The King, now saddened and aged, decides to console himself by marrying one last time to the judicious and maternal Catherine Parr, who will outlive her husband.
text_a: In May 1536, just after the execution of his second wife, Anne Boleyn (Merle Oberon), Henry VIII (Charles Laughton) marries his new lady, Joan Seymour (Wendy Barrie). She dies after a year and a half, so the king sets his sights on the German princess Anne of Cleves (played in the film by Elsa Lanchester, Laughton's wife in real life). The marriage between Henry and Anne will end in divorce, after which the monarch will marry again, this time to the beautiful and ambitious Lady Katherine Howard (Binnie Barnes). His new wife puts her feelings on the back burner to satisfy her ambition, but falls in love with Thomas Culpeper (Robert Donat), Henry's courtier. His lie and treachery will eventually cause the ruin of the couple of lovers, who will be executed by their king.
text_b: In 1558, Elizabeth I became Queen of England. Mrs. Ashley, his housekeeper, and Mr. Parry, his steward, remember the joys and sorrows of his youth... In 1536, Henry VIII had his wife, Anne Boleyn, executed and removed his daughter, Elizabeth, nicknamed Bess, from the royal court. After three marriages, Henry married Catherine Parr, the new queen, from whom Bess, now back in favor, found sympathy and support. With a strong personality, Bess asserted herself and surprised her father. She falls in love with Thomas Seymour, admiral of the English fleet and confidant of the king. The aged King Henry VIII died and was succeeded by his young son Edward under a regency council headed by Thomas' brother Ned Seymour. But Ned Seymour and his wife are intriguing. Bess, who hopes to become queen one day to reorganize and assert the power of England, violently opposes with Thomas the despotism of Ned Seymour. She later discovered the affair of Thomas and Catherine Parr, but despite this, she favoured their marriage. Thomas will prove to Bess that he loves her as much as Catherine. Very devoted to his wife, he devoted himself entirely to her, but she died shortly thereafter in childbirth. Later, Bess sees Thomas again, still in love with her, but Ned Seymour has his brother sentenced to death for treason. Despite Bess's determination to save Thomas, he will be executed. The future queen of England, hardened by trials, will be ready, when the time comes, to challenge and face her destiny.

anchor_text: Sharp is a Texas Ranger who has dedicated his life to the pursuit of crime and the defense of justice. Accustomed to all dangers, a specialist in high risk, he is now in search of a vital informant, whom he plans to uncover with the help of an ex-con turned priest, Percy Stevens. When this key informant is shot, the case gets complicated. Sharp now has to protect the only witnesses to the murder... five University of Texas cheerleaders! In order for his cover to be credible, Sharp has to move in with these five uncontrollable, rampant young women.
text_a: Jack Benteen, a member of the Texas Ranger Division, fights drug trafficking and illegal immigration in a small Texas town on the border with Mexico. And the leader of the traffickers, Cash Bailey, is a childhood friend of the Ranger and the former lover of his wife, Sarita Cisneros, of Mexican descent. To complicate matters, a commando team of Vietnam War veterans, led by Paul Hackett, is sent by the CIA to kill Bailey...
text_b: When a key informant against a notorious crime boss is murdered, the only witnesses to his murder are five cheerleaders from the University of Texas: Barb, Anne, Evie, Heather and Teresa. With the girls' lives in danger until the authorities discover the killer, headstrong Texas Ranger Roland Sharp (Tommy Lee Jones) has to go undercover and move with them to a secret safe house, as the mysterious killer is still at large. However, in order to effectively protect the teenage quintet of eyewitnesses, Sharp must first establish the basic rules for a harmonious and, above all, safe coexistence.

anchor_text: The film follows the misadventures of Texas Ranger Roland Sharp, who is assigned to protect five cheerleaders who witnessed the murder of a drug dealer. At first serious and detached, he will gradually come to appreciate girls, both as people and as cheerleaders. In the meantime, he meets the English literature professor of one of the girls, with whom he falls in love; he invites her to dinner at the house of the five girls (who on audio link will give him advice on how to behave) and ends up marrying her.
text_a: When a key informant against a notorious crime boss is murdered, the only witnesses to his murder are five cheerleaders from the University of Texas: Barb, Anne, Evie, Heather and Teresa. With the girls' lives in danger until the authorities discover the killer, headstrong Texas Ranger Roland Sharp (Tommy Lee Jones) has to go undercover and move with them to a secret safe house, as the mysterious killer is still at large.
