To complicate matters, a commando team of Vietnam War veterans, led by Paul Hackett, is sent by the CIA to kill Bailey...

anchor_text: Jackson Pollock, a bitter American painter, meets the young artist Lee Krasner at an exhibition in 1941. The young woman is interested in the painter and his works. They're going to be a couple. When Lee notices the outstanding talent that Jackson possesses, she neglects her own career for him to support him. Peggy Guggenheim agrees to exhibit Pollock's work at her Art of This Century gallery in Manhattan. The exhibition is a huge success. Pollock's name is on everyone's lips. Life magazine even calls him one of the most important artists in the world. But Pollock's fame soon goes to his head. Not averse to alcohol before, he now drinks to excess. His affairs with women - including Peggy Guggenheim - destroy his relationship with Lee.
text_a: Young Ken begins working with renowned abstract expressionist Mark Rothko as the artist creates the works commissioned by New York's prestigious Four Seasons. While working with the master, Ken learns all about his worldview and his poetics, but also more human traits, such as his arrival in America as a child and the controversial relationship with Pollock and Pop Art. At the end of the opera, Rothko, extremely protective of his works, compensates the Four Seasons by not considering it suitable for his work and dismisses Ken so that the boy can embark on his own career as an artist.
text_b: The film chronicles the life of Jackson Pollock (Ed Harris), an American painter with a complex personality and addicted to alcohol, from his search for success in the art world in the 1940s, to his death in 1956. Alongside him appear on the screen both his family, as relevant names of the American culture of the post-war: the painter Lee Krasner (Marcia Gay Harden), with whom he ends up marrying; the painter of Dutch origin Willem de Kooning (Val Kilmer); his patrons Peggy Guggenheim (Amy Madigan), etc. Pollock goes from being a complete stranger, to becoming the most relevant American artist of his time, in just a few short years.

anchor_text: The parable is about a man from the country trying to get into the "law". The man learns from a doorkeeper standing in front of him that it is possible, but not at the present time. He waits for the doorkeeper to let him in, "days and years", all his life. He's trying to bribe the doorman. He even asks the fleas in the doorkeeper's fur collar, after years of studying it, to help him. But it's all for nothing. Just before the farmer dies, he asks the doorkeeper why in all these years no one has asked to be let in but him. The doorkeeper replies that this entrance was meant only for him. He's going to close it now.
text_a: The shooter, Montgomery "Monty" Kessler' (Brendan Fraser), is a bright Harvard student who is convinced he will graduate Cum Laude (with honors). To do this he must submit his doctoral thesis, an 88-page work that is practically finished already in the first minutes of the film Wazaaa. But after his hard drive breaks down in the middle of the night, he decides to go out with the only printed copy of his work he has, with the goal of photocopying it or duplicating it somehow. Nerves and bad luck cause his doctoral thesis to fall into the hands of an irascible vagrant who decides to warm up by burning the pages of his work one by one. Faced with Monty's pleas, homeless Simon Wilder (Joe Pesci) proposes an exchange: Simon will give him a page for every request Monty grants him. The obvious risk of the whole shebang falling immediately into the fire convinces him. At first his roommates Everett (Patrick Dempsey), Jeff (Josh Hamilton) and Courtney (Moira Kelly) do not support him, but as the relationship deepens, the fact of recovering or not the thesis passes to a second place.
text_b: A sentry stands before the Law; a man from the countryside comes to her one day and asks permission to enter. The sentry tells him that it is possible, but not now, and frightens him by telling him of the many obstacles that await him. The man decides to wait for years. Finally, as the man was dying, he asked why no one else had tried to enter. The guard replied, "This entrance was only for you, now I'm going out and closing the door".

anchor_text: Melvin Junko is a pool attendant in Tromaville, New Jersey, and the pain killer for the gang of Bozo, a psychopath whose hobby is crushing children with his car. Caught in an ambush intended to publicly humiliate him, Melvin escapes, defends himself, and falls into a radioactive barrel being transported by a truck stopped at the scene. It turns him into a hideous, overpowered monster. He'll become a vigilante known as the Toxic Avenger. But the town's mayor, the godfather of a local mafia, has a very negative view of this superhero's arrival.
text_a: The Cusacks are an upright, honest couple from a poor neighborhood in Brooklyn. They have two children, little Alice and her older brother Frank. The latter, tired of the poverty of his family, joins the Dukes gang, apparently good kids from the suburbs, but who lend a hand to Gaggsy Steens, a patented racketeer. Frank, on the wrong track, is one day involved in a serious crime...
text_b: A town called Tromaville located near New Jersey as this city is seen in the background, has the dubious honor of being the largest waste management capital, toxic waste and evildoers. In that city there is a gym, where the protagonist works as a maintenance employee, a somewhat introverted and stupid young boy, also known as Melvin (Mitchell Cohen). Everything goes more or less normally, since the mayor is corrupt, there is a gang of young people who are engaged in running over children and women. The protagonist is mocked by a group of gym nuts, who make a joke of bad taste and for his shame, runs out of a corridor towards a window and falls into a barrel of acid with toxic pestilence in which he deforms acquiring powers, as well as powers to save the city, with which he will take revenge on those who caused his misfortune and in passing will clean the city of racists and bad people.

anchor_text: Jeff and Jane Blue are a happily married couple vacationing in New Orleans with their baby girl. In reality, the spouses are former secret agents who now wish to lead a quiet life; however, they are soon called back to work by the CIA, on the trail of Paulina Novacek, a Czechoslovak criminal who has come into possession of a powerful explosive. In the course of the hilarious adventures to the rhythm of blues they will be flanked by a bizarre pair of cops, but they will have to face the men of Novacek and an unusual four-dollar criminal named Muerte (or Marta, as the Blue call him).
text_a: Jefferson and Jane Blue (Dennis Quaid and Kathleen Turner) have just become parents to a beautiful baby girl, but they are also two secret agents, who met on a dangerous mission. While enjoying their vacation in New Orleans a Czech terrorist gang threatens the national security of the United States. They intended to devote more time to their families and their children, but just at this moment they are assigned a new and important mission, on which world peace and the future of their country will depend. This will not be an easy task for them, since they will have to carry out the work with their daughter present during the entire operation, since they are not willing to be separated from her for a long time.
text_b: Bill Reimers (Emilio Estevez) and Chris Lecce (Richard Dreyfuss), will have to dive into another complicated mission. On this occasion, these two Seattle police officers will have to locate a woman who is a witness to the crimes committed by Lu Delano, a Mafia boss. The woman is missing and you will have to find her as soon as possible, although you will not be alone in this endeavor. The young and inexperienced Gina Garrett (Rosie O'Donnell) will be sent by the prosecutor to help the cops. The three, accompanied by Archie, Gina's dog, will move to a luxurious urbanization to pose as a quiet family and thus catch the dangerous mafioso who is about to arrive.[2]

anchor_text: Married secret agents Jane Blue and Jefferson Blue are taking the baby on a vacation to New Orleans. There they are harassed by the unsuccessful killer Muerte, whom Jefferson derisively addresses as Morty. Soon Officer Halsey becomes aware of the two, only after some time does he realize that they are secret agents. An informant is put under pressure by the couple by being threatened with torture; the baby's cries from the next room are presented to the man as the result of previous torture. In a showdown, Jane and Jefferson fight against Czech arms dealer Paulina Novacek, who manages to escape in a helicopter. There she is arrested by the Czech security guards. Jane's and Jefferson's job was to lure Novacek aboard a Czech helicopter, where she could be arrested, legally on Czech soil. In the final scene, Jane and Jefferson leave for a yacht trip; Halsey is shocked to discover that the couple is heading to Cuba.
text_a: A key witness against the Mafia is threatened with her life, and an attempt on her life almost failed. Her bodyguards and her husband were killed. She's going under. The police believe she's hiding in a remote country house. Police officers Chris Lecce and Bill Reimers have come under fire recently. Lecce, Reimers and District Attorney Gina Garrett are guarding a country house where Brian O'Hara and his wife Pam live. Lecce and Garrett play husband and wife, Reimers plays Lecce's son from a previous marriage. Also with him is Gina's Rottweiler Archie. Chris himself has a relationship with Maria, and that relationship is about to fall apart. During the surveillance, things go wrong: When Chris sneaks into the stranger's house to plant bugs, he is crushed because Brian O'Hara thinks he is a contract killer. When the real hit men show up, it's a showdown where the cops manage to kill them. At the end of the film, Maria Chris proposes.
text_b: Jeff and Jane Blue are a happily married couple vacationing in New Orleans with their baby girl. In reality, the spouses are former secret agents who now wish to lead a quiet life; however, they are soon called back to work by the CIA, on the trail of Paulina Novacek, a Czechoslovak criminal who has come into possession of a powerful explosive. In the course of the hilarious adventures to the rhythm of blues they will be flanked by a bizarre pair of cops, but they will have to face the men of Novacek and an unusual four-dollar criminal named Muerte (or Marta, as the Blue call him).

anchor_text: Dr. Ferguson and his wife, Helen, were on vacation in a Latin American country when they were dragged by the military to the Presidential Palace. The head of state, Raoul Farrago, is a dictator. He's on short-term disability from a brain tumor, unless Ferguson attempts a last-chance surgery. The surgeon hesitates, but his sense of duty compels him to accept. That's when opponents of the regime abduct Helen and inform Ferguson that she will be executed if the President survives this operation...
text_a: Helen Ferguson is a young woman abandoned by her lover. Pregnant, she met on the train to California, a young married couple Hugh Harkness and his wife Patrice. The two women sympathize on the train, but an accident occurs that kills the Harkness couple. Helen Ferguson survives, but at the hospital where she is being treated, she is mistaken for Patrice Harkness by the family of the deceased husband. She then assumes the role of the deceased wife, and enters into a relationship with Bill Harkness, Hugh's brother. But her former lover finds her threatening to reveal her true identity.
text_b: Surgeon Eugene Ferguson is held hostage by a group of soldiers in the midst of an insurrectionary movement against dictator Ferrago. The man eventually manages to free himself, but is warned that the politician is gravely afflicted with a brain tumor and needs immediate surgery. Meanwhile, the rebel Gonzales kidnaps Ferguson's wife, threatening to kill her if her husband performs the operation.

anchor_text: Dan is a failed writer who works in the obituary section of a newspaper. He witnesses an accident involving a stranger being run over by a taxi, Alice. From that moment on, they fall madly in love. Dan is writing a book in which he tells the story of Alice's life. After a photo shoot for this new book, he falls in love with Anna, the photographer. Unfortunately for Dan, Alice soon finds out and Anna refuses to see him again. Playfully, Dan arranges a date between Larry, a man he met through the Internet, and Anna, in an attempt to make her uncomfortable. It's a long shot because Larry and Anna are hooking up. The problem is that Dan and Anna love each other. From then on, the film becomes a crossover between the two couples where feelings, hatred and love coexist.
text_a: Anna M. is a lonely girl who lives in seclusion with her mother and her dog. As a book restorer in the French National Library, she is popular with her colleagues, especially the young Éléonore is very fond of her. But Anna longs for love, and in her desperation, she throws herself in front of a car. Injured, she is taken to a hospital, where Dr. Zanevsky treats her lovingly. Anna falls in love with the handsome doctor, but he doesn't notice. Every little attention he gives her is only proof of his love for Anna and increases her unrealistic desire for the happily married man. She gets more and more involved in the lives of Dr. Zanevsky and his wife, to the point of obsessive stalking, which eventually leads her to the psychiatric ward. There, it is determined that Anna is pregnant. With Albert, a security guard, she had a lovemaking night in a hotel where she had previously, in her delusion, thought she had an appointment with Dr. Zanevsky. Her colleague Éléonore is now the only person who still supports her. Together they start a new life and want to be loving parents to Anna's little daughter.
text_b: Dan, an aspiring obituary writer, becomes engaged to Alice, a young American stripper seeking her fortune in London. But then he meets the photographer Anna, falls in love with her and is willing to do anything to have her. Chatting under false identities with a dermatologist and pretending to be Anna, Dan pushes Dr. Larry into the arms of the unknown woman. The two marry, but the writer does not give up: he causes the separation between Larry and Anna, but ends up being cornered by the dermatologist, who also allows himself a night at the night club with Alice. Alice eventually leaves Dan because he forces her to confess her infidelity to Larry, who returns with Anna. Alice's real name is Jane Rachel Jones, as revealed when she shows her passport to customs on her return to the United States of America. She has always hidden her true identity from Dan, revealing it only to Larry, who does not believe her, when he meets her at the nightclub. The identity of Alice Ayres is revealed at the end of the film, when, in the cemetery where Jane had introduced herself as Alice Ayres, Dan stares at the memorial stone of the real Alice Ayres.

anchor_text: Interpol Inspector Keaton is on an official mission to Istanbul to disrupt the operations of a drug lord and his accomplices. In order to get rid of him, several unsuccessful attacks are carried out; finally, the now-peaceful merchant living with his family, Genghis, is sent to shoot Keaton. When Genghis refuses, his son is killed, his wife is kidnapped and raped. Genghis finally agrees, but then he teams up with Keaton and the policewoman Jasmine and kills the gang.
text_a: Interpol detective Keaton, on assignment in Istanbul, manages with a few good shots to disrupt the business of a drug lord and his accomplices. To get rid of him, they force the ball-trap champion Genghis to shoot the inspector. When he refused, the bandits first killed his mother and then kidnapped and raped his wife. After briefing Keaton, Genghis pretends to do what the thugs want him to do. The shooting champion thus contributes to the final defeat of the band, which is stopped in an ambush.
text_b: A New York City police inspector takes his wife on a trip across Europe. On their flight, they meet a mysterious man who invites them to a small, family-friendly dinner party on an elderly billionaire's yacht. When the rich man is murdered, they become the prime suspects of Interpol agent Laurent Delacroix.

anchor_text: Interpol Inspector Keaton is on an official mission to Istanbul to disrupt the operations of a drug lord and his accomplices. In order to get rid of him, several unsuccessful attacks are carried out; finally, the now-peaceful merchant living with his family, Genghis, is sent to shoot Keaton. When Genghis refuses, his son is killed, his wife is kidnapped and raped. Genghis finally agrees, but then he teams up with Keaton and the policewoman Jasmine and kills the gang.
text_a: A New York City police inspector takes his wife on a trip across Europe. On their flight, they meet a mysterious man who invites them to a small, family-friendly dinner party on an elderly billionaire's yacht. When the rich man is murdered, they become the prime suspects of Interpol agent Laurent Delacroix.
text_b: Inspector Keaton of Interpol, on assignment in Istanbul, manages with a few right moves to disrupt the business of an unsuspecting drug lord and his accomplices. To get rid of him, they force, first by killing his mother, and then, by kidnapping and raping his wife, the saucer-throwing champion Genghis to shoot the inspector. According to Keaton, Genghis pretends to do what the bad guys want him to do. In this way, the champion contributes to the final defeat of the gang who, during an ambush, is arrested.

anchor_text: After divorcing Baron Casimir Dudevant, with whom she had two children, and then living with various lovers, including Alfred de Musset, Michel de Bourges, or Félicien Mallefille, the famous 19th-century novelist George Sand had an impromptu crush on the music of the young virtuoso pianist Frédéric Chopin, which she heard for the first time during an evening in 1836 in the fashionable Parisian salon of her friend the Countess Marie d'Agoult (who shared her life with Franz Liszt, a great friend of Chopin). George Sand then falls madly in love with Chopin (the great love of his life) before meeting and courting him a little later during a stay with their mutual friends Marie d'Agoult, Franz Liszt, and Eugène Delacroix, at the patron Duchesse d'Antan in Angers (fictional place and character). Following various twists and intrigues of Marie d'Agoult, Chopin in turn falls madly in love with Georges Sand, with whom he then begins a love life for 9 years of his life, until the dawn of his early disappearance from the consequences of his tuberculosis, in 1849, at the age of 39. The whole background of many works of Chopin, including in particular his romantic masterpiece Fantaisie-Impromptu de Chopin of 1834, at once joyful, lyrical, inflamed, romantic, and nostalgic, in the image of the love relationship of the famous couple,,.
text_a: The well-known music teacher, Professor Joseph Elsner, arranges for his pupil, the young Polish composer Frédéric Chopin, to give a concert in Paris. For lack of money, the Chopin family cannot afford the trip and it is another eleven years before Frédéric makes the leap to France and to fame and recognition. Franz Liszt introduced Chopin to the writer George Sand. They both fall in love. The composer's fragile health prompts George Sand to organise an extended stay in Mallorca. The mild climate is good for Chopin, and he composes some of his finest pieces on the island. After their return to France, there is a dispute between Professor Elsner and George Sand over the further course of Chopin's life. Elsner believes that a genius like Chopin should serve the whole world and not be tied to a single person. Chopin is convinced and together with Elsner he goes on a concert tour across Europe. His failing health eventually takes its toll, and Frédéric Chopin collapses dead over his piano while working on a composition.
text_b: Recently divorced from Baron Casimir Dudevant, the well-known French writer and feminist Amantine-Lucile-Aurore Dupin, better known by the pseudonym George Sand, meets in Paris the Polish pianist Fryderyk Chopin. She falls in love with him right away, even before she's seen him, hearing him play some of his compositions. The meeting takes place fortuitously in the villa of the Duchess D'Antan, where several artists have been invited, including a friend of George Sand and companion of Franz Liszt, the painter Eugène Delacroix and the writer Alfred de Musset. Between various adventures, including an attempt to sabotage George's friend towards him, Sand manages once back in Paris to break into the composer's afflicted heart. She teaches him to be freer and he decides to duel Felicien Mallefille (guardian of her children and ex-lover not resigned) to protect the new love; during the duel Chopin loses his senses and it will be the Sand to hit the ex-lover in his place, giving the composer the merits.

anchor_text: John Dolittle can talk to animals when he was a kid. His father forces him to suppress that ability. When Dolittle grows up and works as a doctor in San Francisco, he regains this skill. The animals come to be cared for by Dr. Dolittle. His wife Lisa thinks he's crazy. When Dr. Dolittle operates on a tiger in front of an audience, he gets a grant from a corporation.
text_a: John Dolittle, a well-known physician, lives happily with his family in San Francisco. One day, he discovers that he can understand animals and communicate with them. This curious phenomenon upends his life, for the animals are so demanding that they take up all his leisure time. His wife Lisa and her colleagues begin to regard John Dolittle as a madman.
text_b: Maya Dolittle (Kyla Pratt) wants to be a normal teenager, but has a small problem: she has inherited from her father her unusual ability to talk to animals. This gift causes him innumerable difficulties with his parents and is driving his friends crazy. During a summer vacation at a ranch for apprentice cowboys, Maya does her best to hide her unique talent and go unnoticed, but when her friends are in danger, Maya naturally comes to their rescue in that peculiar Dolittle way: enlisting the help of the ranch's scandalous talking animals.

anchor_text: In 1928, after ten years as a journalist, Marjorie turned her back on financial security and her marriage, and moved to Cross Creek, Florida. Here he goes to live in a dilapidated cottage surrounded by an orange grove. The knowledge of the neighbours, simple people, and the contact with the wild nature will give her the inspiration for her first novel.
text_a: In 1928 Marjorie Kinnan Rawlings, a city girl who is unsuccessful as a writer, decides to move to Cross Creek in Florida after her last manuscript was rejected and buys an orange plantation there, which turns out to be in bad shape. Still, he begins to do everything he can to make it through. To do this, you will hire and receive help from several residents. Meanwhile Rawlings tries to find, again, the possibility of succeeding as a writer and gets in touch with Max Perkins, who offers advice on the matter.
text_b: This is Los Angeles, 1948. Fired from his job at Champion Aircraft, veteran Easy Rawlins needs money. Joppy, the owner of the bar he frequented, put him in touch with Albright Dewitt, who offered him money to find a politician's fiancée. Easy spends the evening with a couple, drives them home, and uses the drunken husband's slumber to make love to the wife, Coretta. The next day, Rawlins resumed his investigation, but the police arrested him for the murder of Coretta...

anchor_text: In the USSR in 1983, still in the midst of the Cold War. A neuropsychologist with controversial methods, Tatiana Klimova is recruited by the military to follow the case of Konstantin Veshniakov, a lone cosmonaut who survived a crash of his spacecraft and has since been living with a foreign organism in his body that has completely colonized him and that could be dangerous to the human species, but could also prove to be a potential weapon.
text_a: Twenty-five years after its disappearance, the cargo spacecraft suddenly reappears and threatens to collide with the station Comstat 5, orbiting Mars. The crew of the station then makes every effort to try to avoid the crash and understand the reason for the disappearance of the Siberia, whose entire crew perished...
text_b: In 1983, two Russian cosmonauts return to Earth. Landing on a remote steppe in Soviet Kazakhstan, only one of them is found alive. However, their strange behavior and abnormal physical strength make the members of a military facility think that it has not arrived on earth alone, and that it holds within itself a being from another planet. To investigate this phenomenon, Colonel Semiradov enlists the services of Tatyana Yuryevna Klimova, a young psychiatrist renowned for her unconventional but effective methods.

anchor_text: Miyamoto is looking for the man responsible for the death of one of his childhood friends. He finds Mizoguchi, a notorious yakuza known to be an expert in human organ trafficking. When Milly literally falls from the sky, her arrival in Miyamoto's little world delays her revenge a bit. He learns that Milly is sent from the future, that her world is 80 years older than Miyamoto's, and that she is there to prevent a war between humans and an alien race headed by the Daggra.
text_a: To fend off the alien invasion that threatens Earth, young soldier Milly faces a journey back in time from 2084 to 2002. Arriving in Tokyo, Milly will be thwarted by Mafia boss Mizoguchi, who is determined to exploit the aliens for his own nefarious ends. The soldier will be joined by professional hitman Miyamoto.
text_b: Mizuki is about to commit murder. His action is however anticipated by Shuichi, who kills a group of yakuza in cold blood. Fleeing with money from his job, Mizuki travels to the island where he spent his childhood in an orphanage. There he meets Shuichi again, and discovers that he is his childhood best friend. The two meet another friend, Kōhei, now married and expecting a child, and reconstruct the fragments of the past. The two are, however, pursued by a group of yakuza killers. Mizuki then proposes to Shuichi a strange plan: to carry out a series of murders, the proceeds of which will be used to send medicine to children in the Third World. The two carry out this series of murders and suddenly on their shoulders sprout a pair of wings, similar to those of angels. The two are killed, but a new life is born: it's Kōhei's son.

anchor_text: The Basque priest Angel, after a long study of the Bible, determines the time for the arrival of the Antichrist. At Christmas time, he travels to Madrid to come into contact with the Antichrist by committing many sins. He meets Jose Maria, a death metal fan working in the music store, and ends up in his mother's boarding house. Together, they seek out Dr. Cavan, the host of an occult TV show. Together they go hunting in Christmastime Madrid to prevent the rebirth of the Antichrist.
text_a: In 1987, Father Gabriele Amorth is the chief exorcist of the Vatican. He is personally commissioned by the pope to help a possessed boy in Spain, since the local priest does not know much about exorcism. In the course of his investigations, Father Amorth finds himself on the trail of the demon Asmodeus, who seems to have had a previous connection to the Vatican.
text_b: The priest Ángel Beriartúa deciphered the Apocalypse of John and was able to determine the date of the Antichrist's birth. According to this message, the Antichrist will be born on December 25, 1995, in Madrid, where a wave of vandalism and crime begins. On the other hand, he does not know where he will be born. Convinced that this satanic birth must be stopped, the priest joins forces with a death metal fan, José Maria (Santiago Segura), to try, by all means, to find where the event will take place. So he will do everything he can to find out, trying to get the Devil's favor. In a hectic Madrid, he will try to get the collaboration of "Professor Cavan", a charlatan star of a TV show.

anchor_text: Upon the death of their uncle, two rival brothers, the Mulligans, must live together for six months in order to claim his inheritance. The first, Monty, is a gambler and an innovator while the second, Ted, is a loudmouth and a loner. Despite their opposite personalities, they're both broke. While waiting to receive the money from the will, they embark on criminal adventures to reconcile and enrich themselves. They kidnap a banker's daughter for ransom but the banker is very happy to never see her again... They attempt to storm a cargo of gold but encounter a band of looters who also want to steal it. The encounter goes wrong and Ted is hit by a bullet, falls into the water and is dragged by the current. After wiping out the rival gang, Monty is celebrated across the country for defending the cargo. As a reward, he can marry any girl, but at his wedding, he is rescued by his brother Ted, who is still alive, and they leave together.
text_a: On Grandpa Ted's death, Monty Mulligan has a chance to inherit a large fortune, but only if they live together for at least six months. Although the two have very different habits and characters they are united by the fact that they are squatters. Waiting to receive the inheritance, they embark on criminal adventures with unexpected outcomes: first they kidnap a banker's daughter in order to demand a ransom but he is very happy not to see his daughter again. They attempt an assault on a gold shipment but encounter another gang with the same objective. Ted is hit, falls into the water and is swept away by the current while Monty, after annihilating the rival gang, is welcomed into town as the defender of the gold cargo and as a reward he can marry the girl from whom he too would like to escape. Fortunately, Ted reappears, saving him from a dangerous marriage.
text_b: Monty Walsh and his friend Chet, two cowboys in the Wild West, see their job prospects diminish the day Cal Brennan's ranch, where they've worked for years, is sold to some industrialists. After trying in vain to do some menial work so as not to abandon the horses and the life that is more congenial to them, the two friends separate. Monty gives himself to a life of vagabond, despite the exhortations of Martine, a prostitute with whom he is in love, to dedicate himself to a new activity in the village; Chet, on the other hand, after meeting the widow of a shopkeeper marries her and dedicates himself to trade. Another cowboy friend of the two, Shorty, unable to adapt to the new times, becomes a bandit and during a robbery kills Chet in his shop. Monty Walsh, upon hearing the news, has no choice but to avenge his friend: so after chasing the murderer, he kills him in a duel. With his revenge, he resumes his aimless wandering.

anchor_text: The two unequal brothers, over-indebted gambler and daredevil Monty and farmer Ted, dislike each other and have successfully avoided each other until the death of their wealthy uncle brings them together. In order to enjoy the inheritance, however, the two must meet one condition: live together peacefully for six months. However, their coexistence is far from peaceful. As soon as Monty arrives at Ted's farm, it is raided by horse thieves led by the infamous bandit Jim. The whole farm will go up in flames. The two brothers, now penniless, try to survive by deception until they receive their inheritance. But both the kidnapping of a spoiled banker's daughter and the robberies of a stagecoach and a money train fail, each time resulting in savage fights. One good thing about failures, though, is that they weld the Monty and Ted quarrels together.

anchor_text: Jack and Sally announce to their friends Gabe and Judy that they're splitting up. The news comes as a shock and challenges the beliefs of this quartet of New York intellectuals and their friends. What follows is a love-crusade between the various protagonists of the story.
text_a: Two pairs of lovers star in a comedy of errors set in New York City, in which Maggie (Ryan) and astronomer Sam (Broderick), team up and obsessively try various dirty and unethical tricks to undo the relationship between their respective ex-partners, Antoine (Tchéky Karyo) and Linda (Preston), when they fall in love. Sam just wants to get back with his ex-girlfriend, Maggie wants revenge on her ex-fiancé.
text_b: Jack and Sally are going through a divorce and they ask a couple of friends, Gabe and Judy, for help. After experiencing extramarital affairs, their divorce plan comes back into play, but paradoxically at the end of the film it is Gabe and Judy who decide to separate.

anchor_text: It's in L.A. A dangerous criminal, Dave Purvis, is the mastermind behind many robberies. Always discreet, the police don't even know he exists. He organized the robbery of an armored truck during a baseball game. Police intervened more quickly than expected, gunshots were exchanged, wounding one of the robbers and killing one of the police officers. This murder puts Detective Cordell on the band's trail.
text_a: It's in L.A. A dangerous criminal, Dave Purvis, is the mastermind behind many robberies. Always discreet, the police don't even know he exists. He organizes the robbery of an armored truck during a baseball game, hiring some criminals. Police move in faster than expected, gunshots are exchanged, wounding one of the robbers and killing one of the policemen. This murder puts Detective Cordell on the trail of the gang.
text_b: Nick Pulovski is a veteran LAPD officer with unorthodox methods. While trying to capture sports car thieves, his teammate, Powell, is shot and killed by their leader, a man named Strom. David Ackerman, the son of a very wealthy businessman, has just joined the police force. He is trying to protect the innocent and redeem himself for not being able to save his brother, who died because of him in their childhood. Chosen to replace Powell and team up with Pulovski, Ackerman tries to help him capture Strom and his gang but Nick's unpleasant nature makes their duo explosive.

anchor_text: The unscrupulous gangster Jack Carter is collecting unpaid debts for Las Vegas pawn shops. This job fits his profile: cold, relentless and precise. After the death of his brother Richard in a car accident, Jack leaves Las Vegas to return to Seattle. Apparently, he's the only one who believes his death was staged. Even Richard's wife Gloria, his daughter Doreen, his co-workers and the police don't see the point of continuing the investigation. Jack, on the other hand, is determined to find out the truth and avenge his brother's death.
text_a: Jack Carter, a hitman hired by a gangster, returns to his hometown after many years having learned of the death of his brother, a married man with a young daughter, officially due to a car accident. Carter doubts from the beginning that this could be a real accident and begins a personal investigation, focusing mainly on the place where his brother worked. Here he comes into contact with a girl who had an affair with his brother, with the manager of the club and with his old acquaintance, Cyrus Paice, involved in a pornography business and whom Carter suspects to be linked to his brother's death.
text_b: Billy Carter lives with his parents on the family farm. When a dispute over land between his father and wealthy neighbor Jon Pryce causes the latter to send thugs to underscore his demands, Billy is sent into town to get the sheriff's help. However, he hesitates; on his return, Billy finds his father murdered and his mother injured, the house burned to the ground. Against the sheriff's opinion that it was an accident, Billy sets out to find those responsible. He finds her in the person of the Black Jack hired by Price and his gang. Carter manages to take out the members one by one until he can face the villain in a victorious duel.

anchor_text: Jack Carter, a member of the Mafia, lives in Las Vegas. When his brother dies in a car accident, Carter travels by train to Seattle to attend the funeral, but also to investigate the true circumstances surrounding his brother's death. In Seattle, he contacts Gloria, his dead brother's wife, but Carter accuses her of being complicit in his brother's death because he hadn't taken care of his brother lately. Carter quickly begins his investigations in the community, and he doesn't go overboard. After a short time, it becomes clear to Carter that his brother has been murdered. His quest for revenge parallels his investigations, which lead him from an ominous CD-ROM to a blackmail and rape case, to the end of the story of his brother's murder. All the while, Carter tries to take care of the kidnapped Gloria and her daughter Doreen, which is initially rather unsuccessful, but eventually takes on conciliatory forms. At the end of the film, Carter is alive and free.

anchor_text: After the death of Philip II, King of Navarre, his two granddaughters, the princesses Malva and Isabella, compete for his succession. The cruel and ambitious Malva fears that a will designates Isabella as heir to the throne, and wants it stolen in order to replace it with a paper in her favor. With the implementation of this idea, she entrusts Maciste, who, however, has to deal with Zorro, whom Isabella has called because she suspected of her cousin's machinations. After several adventures, in which luck swings back and forth, Maciste succeeds in carrying out his mission. However, when he realizes that he has been misused for evil purposes, he refuses to hand over the document and turns against Malva; instead of her, Isabella ascends the throne with her lover, who is none other than Zorro. Maciste is riding towards new adventures.
text_a: The action takes place in Spain, a few days after the death of Philip II, king of Navarre, who has had no son but two daughters, the princesses Isabel and Malva. There's a great rivalry between the two. Malva is cruel, ambitious and unscrupulous. In the certainty that the chosen one will be Isabel, he tries to seize the will to replace it with another that is favorable to him and therefore hires Maciste with the task of stealing the document. At the same time, Elizabeth, suspecting some dirty maneuver, hires the Fox for the same purpose. After several confrontations between Zorro and Maciste, the will reaches the hands of the latter, who despite everything realizes Malva's bad intentions.
text_b: A sheik kidnaps a girl named Isabella who he's deeply in love with. Maciste is contacted by Isabella's fiancé, a young Spanish officer named Antonio, to help him in the enterprise of freeing his beloved. Disembarking in Africa, Maciste finds Isabella, who refuses to run away, saying that she wants to marry the perfidious sheik. Maciste is not convinced and manages to infiltrate the secrets of the sheikh's palace discovering that Isabella's father, the Duke of Malaga, is being held hostage to force the girl to marry. After a series of enterprises and adventures, Maciste persuades the Sheik to be more lenient. Back in Malaga, the Duke resumes his post, while Antonio and Isabella can finally marry, Maciste leaves again to undertake new ventures and adventures.

anchor_text: At the death of Philip IV, King of Nogara, the two heirs of the deceased are the two nieces: Princess Malva and Princess Isabella. The two are very different, Isabella is wise and shrewd while Malva is cruel and very ambitious. They both know that before dying the sovereign almost certainly chose Isabella, and for this reason Malva bribes Maciste to try to get his hands on a chest containing the king's will, to replace it with one favorable to her. Isabella senses the maneuver and hires Zorro with the task of getting her hands on the casket before her rival. At the end of a hard battle between the two heroes, the coffin remains in the hands of Maciste, who, however, intuits the sordid plans of Princess Malva and agrees to deliver it to Zorro. In the end, Zorro and the queen declare their love for each other, while Maciste, as always, sets off on other adventures, also having found a companion in the queen's former chambermaid.
text_a: Around the 16th/17th century: The soldiers of the Duke of Málaga grind the Holy Obelisk of Melida before being defeated by the people of Sheikh Abd El Kader. The sheikh then has the Duke's beautiful daughter, Isabelle, captured in Spain and imprisoned to marry her and blackmail the Kingdom of Malaga from the now also imprisoned father. Isabella's fiancé, the young officer Antonio, calls Maciste for help, with whom he travels to Melida, breaks into the prison and as a result has to overcome numerous dangerous situations. They are also captured; Maciste has to rebuild the obelisk alone and is able to escape with Antonio. After numerous further battles, the liberation succeeds, the punishment of evil, the return and the marriage of the two young people.

anchor_text: Private investigator Jack Singer swore to his dying mother that he would never marry. A few years later, he's still engaged to Betsy. Shortly before the wedding, he agrees to let his fiancée spend a weekend with rich Tommy Korman to pay off Jack's gambling debts. Tommy Korman is trying to win Betsy over and marry her. He and Betsy travel to Hawaii, where Tommy tries to impress the woman. Jack happens to see a television show where Betsy and Tommy watch a volcanic eruption and embrace. He wants Betsy back, so he follows them to Hawaii. There, a taxi driver paid by Korman leads him on the wrong track. Betsy agrees to marry Tommy, and the wedding is to take place in Las Vegas. In order to reach Betsy, Jack joins a group of Elvis Presley's doppelgangers. The Presleys parachute over Las Vegas and land in the city. Jack is winning Betsy back.
text_a: On their vacation, Jack and Ben want to meet girls in a luxury hotel in Florida. Jack approaches Dana, who has checked in with her wealthy grandmother and cousin. Meanwhile, Ben tries to land with Patti, the waitress. The hotel, which forbids private contact between staff and holidaymakers, complicates the situation. The house detective gets in the way of the two extra, but is looking for the jewel thief Maestro. Maestro, who also checked into the hotel with his wife Bobbie Sue, is after Dana's grandmother's necklace. So he tries to win her heart by disguising himself as a Swiss businessman. Because Jack can't get it on with his chosen lady right away, even though he leaves his friend Ben alone in a hotel room with Dana's cousin Shirley, he tries to pick up other ladies. But he is not successful with them either, because they are constantly disturbed or pursued by the detective. At the end of their vacation, Jack and Ben realize they've fallen in love with Dana and Patti. Before leaving, Maestro Dana's grandmother can snatch the necklace and lock it in a closet. On the run, while his wife is waiting for him in the taxi, he throws himself at Ben, who has disguised himself as a widow from the jewel thief. When the cover is revealed, the situation threatens to escalate, but the grandmother, meanwhile freed, can eliminate the perpetrator with her karate arts and take her chain. At the end of the movie, Jack and Dana and Ben and Patti are a couple.
text_b: Jack Singer, a detective by profession, has been engaged to Betsy Nolan for a long time. Although she insists on getting married, he delays. He proposes to the girl to go to Las Vegas where, being a very good poker player, he expects to win. In Vegas, he meets Tommy Corman, a very skilled gambler. Singer loses a huge sum of money, and the adventurer offers him a deal: if Betsy, in whom he sees his late wife, will spend the weekend with him, the debt will be canceled. Betsy agrees to fly with Tommy to an island in Hawaii, where he owns a beautiful mansion. As Betsy begins to give in (Corman has now started talking about marriage, giving her a ring), Tommy is warned that Jack is coming to take Betsy back. Corman tries to stop him, and while the young man manages to hurry, he lets the girl believe that the boyfriend has sold her for a few coins. Humiliated, Betsy is willing to marry Corman. Corman has Singer arrested by the police, but a distant friend with $500 bails him out. In order to get to Las Vegas (there are no flights for a few hours), the detective boards a private plane, on which a group of parachuting artists, the Flying Elvis Presley, travels, and participates in their show, jumping into downtown Las Vegas, where he finds Betsy. She, having learned of Tommy's deception, decides to marry her eternal fiancé.

anchor_text: Lara Jean and Peter are a couple now. They also have a real first date, and things seem to be going well, except for the arrival of a letter, the only one Lara Jean hadn't gotten back. John Ambrose is sweet and kind and caring, but most of all, he's back in town. Lara Jean and John Ambrose are destined to meet at a nursing home, where they do their volunteer work for the school. Peter and John Ambrose don't know they're competing for Lara Jean's heart, and she seems uncertain about her choice. But the snow will make everything easier, including Lara Jean's choice.
text_a: In the aftermath of World War II, Paul Sutton, a young demobilized soldier turned chocolate salesman, meets Victoria, a young pregnant woman abandoned by her father. She is about to reunite with her family, Mexican-American winemakers settled in California, dominated by a hostile father, unaware of the young woman's new situation. Paul, who was engaged to another woman, agreed to pretend to be Victoria's husband. Welcomed by the family members, except the father, he decides to stay until the grapes are harvested. In time, his desire for Victoria will be revealed...
text_b: The movie starts right where the last one left off. After cementing their relationship and becoming boyfriends, Lara Jean (Lana Condor) begins her relationship with Peter Kavinsky (Noah Centineo), living her first courtship. There he lives some typical situations of a relationship, such as a first kiss and the first Valentine's Day, among others, and continues to have the trust of Kitty (Anna Cathcart), Margot (Janel Parrish) or Chris (Madeleine Arthur). However, problems begin when she meets a former recipient of a letter from her, John Ambrose (Jordan Fisher), and a new feeling of love blossoms. This problem is mixed with the insecurity before his relationship with Peter because, especially, of his previous relationship with Gen (Emilija Baranac). This set of problems led to her having to rely more on herself and whether she could love two boys at the same time.[4]

anchor_text: Robbie is a six-year-old orphan living in London with foster parents who are both indifferent and abusive. Having accidentally set a small fire in the house, he fears that he will be severely punished and beaten by his adoptive father, as he has been in the past. He fled into the streets and took refuge in the basement of a ruined building, a remnant of the war. There, he meets Chris Lloyd, a young sailor, who is himself on the run because he has just, in the heat of the action, killed his wife's employer, with whom Lloyd had discovered they were having an affair. As Robbie becomes attached to Lloyd, Lloyd repeatedly tries to get rid of the boy but as carefully as possible. With much hesitation, Lloyd decides to use the orphan to retrieve some much-needed money from his apartment. Eventually, Lloyd feels compelled to bring Robbie along. At the beginning of their escape, Lloyd views the young boy with disdain and as a burden, while Robbie is suspicious and distrustful of the young man. As their journey progresses, however, this unusual couple gradually develops a strong bond of friendship, trust and common cause. Both feel that they have "broken the bridge" and have nothing left to lose. As they travel north towards Scotland with the police on their tail in a somewhat disconcerting pursuit, the duo finally reach a small Scottish fishing port, where Lloyd steals a boat and sets sail for Ireland. During the trip, Robbie falls seriously ill and needs to see a doctor. Lloyd then decides to return to Scotland to find treatment for the child, knowing full well that the police are waiting for him.
text_a: The story takes place in 1985. Robbie Hart once ambitious is now a singer who sings at weddings and bar mitzvahs. At one of these parties, he meets Julia. Robbie himself is on the verge of his own marriage to Linda, but she leaves him on the day of the wedding. After this incident, Robbie is depressed, which also affects his work. The wedding parties at which he sings become a nightmare for the newlyweds as Robbie takes the opportunity to vent his frustration publicly. At one of the weddings, he gives a cynical speech, after which the celebration ends in a brawl. Julia comforts Robbie and tries to find him a partner. But then the two fall in love, even though Julia is torn between her fiance Glenn, a macho man, and the shy wedding singer Robbie. Julia's going to Las Vegas to marry her boyfriend, Glenn. Robbie finds out that Glenn has already cheated on Julia several times and has no intention of not doing so after the wedding. Julia is unsure of her feelings, but decides to marry Glenn in Las Vegas. Robbie flies the same plane in first class where he meets Billy Idol and tells him his story. Through a flight attendant who complains about a rowdy passenger, he learns that Glenn and Julia are on the same plane. Billy Idol helps Robbie, who now has the cabin crew and some passengers on his side. Robbie sings a song about his love for Julia over the speakers. When Glenn finds out that Robbie is singing for Julia and then discovers him, he wants to throw himself at him, but is prevented from doing so by a steward who meets him with his drink cart and is overwhelmed by Billy Idol and a rocker and his people. Touched, Julia accepts Robbie's proposal of marriage and they kiss deeply. The film ends with the wedding of the two.
text_b: Chris Lloyd is on the run on the streets of London because he is wanted for the murder of his wife's lover; little Robbie Campbell, not yet seven years old, is on the run because he is convinced (and is mistaken) that he has set fire to his own house, and fears the corporal punishments that his adoptive father uses to inflict on him. The two meet in the basement of an abandoned building in the city. In the course of their common escape across the countryside of Britain, while Chris develops affection for the child who was initially seen as a kind of impediment, Robbie encounters in the murderer an adult male figure of reference, which could not be said of the abusive adoptive father. After several pilgrimages, Chris - in a Scottish village - seizes a fishing boat, and, together with the child, moves away from the coast in an attempt to get out of territorial waters and at least momentarily to safety; once on board, however, he realizes that Robbie is quite seriously ill. He then turns the boat 180 degrees and goes back, aware that the police will be waiting for him there, but also that Robbie can be rescued.

anchor_text: Robbie, a six-year-old Scottish orphan living in London with strict foster parents, has started a small fire in his apartment while playing with forbidden matches and is running away for fear of punishment. In the basement of a bombed-out house, he meets Chris Lloyd, who is standing next to the body of a man (as the viewer later learns, this was the boss and lover of Lloyd's wife and was probably killed by Lloyd out of jealousy). Lloyd decides to take the boy, now the only witness to the murder, with him, especially since Robbie doesn't want to go home. As the two flee from the police to Scotland, trust and friendship grow between them. Robbie receives more affection from Lloyd than from his adoptive parents, while Lloyd, on the other hand, instinctively feels responsible for the boy, especially since it turns out that Robbie was physically abused during the first night in a boarding house. On the other hand, Lloyd is now also wanted as a child abductor, so Robbie's company becomes dangerous to him, but he is determined to stay with Lloyd. They make their way to Lloyd's brother, Jack, on foot; to avoid being seen, they have to spend the night outdoors with little to eat, which robs Robbie of his strength. But even with Jack, they can't stay because of his reputation. As a last resort, Lloyd steals a fishing boat in the harbor. When they are out on the open sea and no longer being pursued, Lloyd notices one morning that Robbie has a high fever, can no longer speak, and urgently needs medical attention. To save the boy, he turns around and abandons the already successful escape. The film ends as they return to the harbor and Lloyd carries the boy in his arms through the expectant crowd.

anchor_text: In the year 2055: A man named Eckels enters the company Zeit-Safari GmbH (originally: Time Safari, Inc.) and is fascinated by the idea of traveling to the past and hunting animals. After paying, he travels with a group led by Mr. Travis to prehistoric times because Eckels wants to take down a real dinosaur. Mr. Travis instructs Eckels that he must never leave the path of the time machine and touch anything, or the past will be altered and so will the future. But when Eckels sees a real Tyrannosaurus rex, he gets scared and panics, making a serious mistake and stepping on a butterfly. This event changes many things in the present, to which they return to find a joyless, colorless and totalitarian world.
text_a: In the year 2055, time safaris are organized for hunters seeking thrills out of time and ordinary. One expedition ends badly, however, when a frightened hunter escapes from the anti-gravity metal platform by walking a few steps on the earth of millions of years ago. This seemingly insignificant event causes radical changes in the future, such as the change of the newly elected president or the language: the presentation plaque of the time travel company changes and with it also the English spoken by a partner of the company. So the hunter checks under the sole of his shoe, and there he finds a beautiful, dead, prehistoric butterfly. For one small death, a domino-shaped chain of events changed the future forever.
text_b: Charles Hatton runs a travel agency like no other. It allows you to jump back in time and go kill an Allosaurus 65 million years ago and come back with a video. Despite the opposition of a small part of the population who sees a certain risk in these jumps, the hunters accompany the tourists of time to make and repeat the scenario of the Allosaurus... But during a jump, the guns fail, and one of the customers, panicked, accidentally crushes a butterfly. The whole course of evolution is then changed, and the new present catches up with the present, gradually changing the fauna and flora back to where they should be without the butterfly. In order to correct the time error, a hunter and a scientist attempt to go back to the time of the time jump to prevent the error from occurring.

anchor_text: The film is inspired by the true story of a 14-year-old American boy who suddenly took his own life after severe cases of bullying in school and on the Internet. In addition to this main story, there are side stories of other young homosexuals, lesbians and transsexuals who are victims of homophobic attacks or driven to suicide in different parts of the world. On May 17, 2017, sixty years after the death of the protagonist, a group of artists will gather for a commemorative gathering that will last all day during the International Day against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia.
text_a: 54 schoolgirls commit suicide together by throwing themselves on the train tracks after an apparent deadly pact. This event, which has left researchers stunned, is just the beginning of what appears to be a series of suicides across Japan. Detective Kuroda and his team will be in charge of carrying out the investigation and will try to find an answer, but figuring out the motive of the mass suicides that are happening all over the country will not be an easy task for the investigators.
text_b: The film is inspired by the true story of a fourteen-year-old American boy who committed suicide following a dramatic sequence of severe bullying at school and cyberbullying he was subjected to on social media. This story is linked to that of other gay, lesbian and trans teenagers who have been victims of homophobic attacks, killed or driven to suicide in different parts of the world. On May 17, 2071, sixty years after the death of the young protagonist on the World Day Against Homophobia, a group of artists find themselves united in a commemorative performance that will run throughout the day.
