text_a: The film is inspired by the true story of a 14-year-old American boy who committed suicide following a dramatic sequence of serious acts related to school homophobic bullying and cyberbullying. This story is linked to those of other young gay, lesbian and trans people who have been victims of homophobic attacks, killed or driven to suicide in different parts of the world. On 17 May 2017, sixty years after the death of the young man, during the International Day against Homophobia, Biphobia and Transphobia, a group of artists united in a commemorative performance that will run throughout the day.
text_b: 54 schoolgirls commit suicide together by throwing themselves on the train tracks after an apparent deadly pact. This event, which has left researchers stunned, is just the beginning of what appears to be a series of suicides across Japan. Detective Kuroda and his team will be in charge of carrying out the investigation and will try to find an answer, but figuring out the motive of the mass suicides that are happening all over the country will not be an easy task for the investigators.

anchor_text: Reporter Mila bets her publisher Yasia that she will get the first interview with the public-shy director of the Pink Prison. If she wins, she gets exclusive publishing rights. If she loses, she has to invite the publisher to a weekend in Paris. Failing to pass the interview through official channels, she breaks into the prison one night. On the way to Director Sam, who turns out to be the dominant Director Samantha, she experiences various erotic adventures, including watching two men engage in same-sex anal intercourse.
text_a: Katja Kean plays a photographer and journalist, Mila, who enters a prison to get an interview with a mysterious guard and win a bet with her publisher. Various encounters with prisoners and staff members, including the head of the prison (Mr. Marcus) are gradually bringing her closer to her target, but not everything is as it seems.
text_b: The recently divorced writer Michael is working on his new novel in a hotel in Paris, where he meets his new lover Anna. In New York, a young mother named Julia is embroiled in a legal battle over custody of her young son. This was sued by the child's father after her child almost had a fatal accident because of her inattention. Scott, an American businessman, is in Rome on business. In a bar he meets the attractive Monika, who pretends to need money to be able to redeem her missing daughter from criminals. The fates of all involved are joined together in the course of the plot of the film.

anchor_text: The young cook Nikki Angioli has to return to her old home, Little Italy. Her parents own a thriving pizzeria there. On her visit, Nikki meets Leo, a friend from her childhood. The two quickly realize that they still have feelings for each other, but their two families are torn apart, putting their love in jeopardy. Nikki and Leo secretly hide their love, unaware that they're not the only family members who keep a secret.
text_a: A chef in a fine restaurant in Hamburg, Martha is passionate about her work, devoting her few free moments to analysis sessions. His obsessive life is turned upside down by the death of his sister. She has to take her eight-year-old niece, Lina, home. And with the arrival of a new sous-chef at the restaurant, the exuberant Italian Mario, the changes continue...
text_b: Nicoletta "Nikki" Angioli and Leo Campoli both grew up in Toronto's Little Italy, where their families ran a pizzeria together; later their parents had argued and each founded their own pizzeria, starting to sabotage each other. Nicoletta and Leo develop a relationship, which they try to keep secret from their parents.

anchor_text: Aaron Kurlander lives with his family in a dilapidated St. Louis hotel during the Great Depression. To hide his real life, Aaron tells lies about himself and his family at school. When his mother is hospitalized and his father is away on business, the 12-year-old is suddenly left on his own.
text_a: Set in the era of the Great Depression, the film follows the story of a boy who fights alone for survival in a St. Louis hotel after his mother is locked up in a sanitarium. His father, a German immigrant and traveling salesman who works for the Hamilton Watch Company, is away on long journeys from which the boy cannot be certain he will return.
text_b: When Moritz was 15, his parents ran out of money and were about to leave their beautiful house in Hamburg. He's very close to his grandmother, but his parents sent him to a nursing home.

anchor_text: During the Great Depression in 1933, a family of German origin, the Kurlanders, tried to lead a normal life in a passing hotel in St. Louis. Young Aaron is left on his own: his mother enters a sanatorium to treat her tuberculosis, and his younger brother Sullivan goes to live with an uncle to save money. Eric, the father, survives on various odd jobs. He landed a coveted position as a salesman for the Hamilton Watch Company but in other states. He assured his son Aaron that he would remain there to nurture and raise him. But Aaron soon realized that he had really been deceived. The young boy invents a life with his classmates and finds little tricks, thanks in part to Lester, to survive. He makes new friends, such as Ella, the epileptic neighbor, and Billy Thompson, a classmate from a wealthy family. Aaron, however, earns the wrath of Officer Burns.
text_a: During a search, Officer Frank Williams and his colleague discovered in the house in question a woman with no eyes. One's arm is violently cut off, while the other is killed with an axe. Williams has just enough time to put a bullet in the killer's head before he's hospitalized. Three years after the events, Williams and his colleague, Hannah, organize a project with several offenders, with the aim of reducing their sentences. The job seems simple: restore the Blackwell Hotel. But what they don't know is that the man who wounded Williams three years ago made his home in the walls of the hotel. When one member of the group goes missing, all the others go looking for him. And one by one, they all start to disappear...
text_b: After witnessing his father's departure by accepting a job Aaron is left alone in a motel room with his father's promise to return and having left two watches, one for his stay at the motel and the other to receive a free dinner. After completing his primary studies with honors, he risks losing his home even more during the great depression caused by the arrest of his Jewish friend Lester, who appreciates him. After receiving a threat from the bank, he clings to the room refusing to leave it to avoid his eviction, unwittingly witnessing the suicide of his neighbor. Hunger and basic necessities soon cause him to succumb, but the arrival of his younger brother restores his strength. Suddenly their father arrives to take them to a new home but Aaron refuses to accompany him so as not to allow the bank to take his things. Finally with much ingenuity she manages to get her things out of the motel with the help of her brother and they go to their new home to wait for the return of their parents.

anchor_text: Jeff McCloud, a rodeo celebrity crippled by a wound that takes time to heal, comes to visit the house where he was born and meets Wes and Louise Merritt, a young couple who came to visit the same house in order to buy it. Wes asks McCloud to introduce him to the rodeo. McCloud manages to make him a champion, which creates tension with Louise, worried about the risks her husband is running. With Wes Merritt's rapid rise to fame increasingly worrying Louise, she begs Jeff to persuade her to stop, and it is then that he tells her that his only motivation for teaching her his art was to be near her, as he is in love with Louise. But she refuses his love. McCloud accedes to Louise's request and takes her to a party, which causes an argument with Wes that escalates into a fight, with Jeff knocking the young man out. After that, McCloud decided to do the rodeo again, despite his lack of preparation. At first everything goes well, but at the end of the test on a mustang he falls from the horse, one of his feet getting caught in a stirrup, he is dragged on the track. This accident causes the death of Jeff McCloud and a realization of Wes Merritt, who decides to stop the rodeo to realize his first project, take a farm and live there quietly with his wife.
text_a: Jeff McCloud, a rodeo star who was injured in an accident, visits his former home, where he meets Wes and Louise Merritt, a young couple intent on buying it. Wes, also in the rodeo world, asks McCloud to help him improve his discipline. McCloud manages to make him a champion; but this creates tension with Louise, worried about the risks her husband is taking. Wes's rapid rise to fame worries Louise more and more, and she begs Jeff to talk him out of it. That's when he declares that his only motivation for teaching Wes his art was to be close to her, since he's in love with her. Louise, however, rejects him. McCloud agrees to Louise's request and takes her to a party, which causes an argument with Wes that escalates into a fight, in which Jeff knocks the young man out of the fight. Later, despite not training for a long time, McCloud decides to return to competing in rodeos. At first all goes well, but at the end of a performance on a horse, he falls; one foot, however, gets caught in a brace, being dragged with the animal's great power along the entire track. The accident causes the death of Jeff and the awareness of Wes, who decides to stop with the rodeo to realize his first project, take a farm and live there in peace with his wife.
text_b: Cowboy Tex Masters and his sidekick Lucky are searching all Texas rodeos for the man who killed Tex's brother. En route to Middleton, they meet young federal agent Carmen Serano, who has been assigned to solve a series of mysterious deaths of several local rodeo winners. Suspect is the gang of a man named Barker. In her undercover investigation, Carmen sneaks into Barker's saloon as a dancer. After Tex and Lucky take on some gang members, the Middleton Sheriff hires them to help him solve the murders. Once again, Tex and Carmen's paths cross, and they finally get closer. At the rodeo, Tex wins all the competitions, which is why Barker orders Carmen to engage Tex in a fight with another rodeo rider named Squint. Although Carmen warns Tex instead, he still goes to the saloon to see her dance. There, he gets into a fight with Squint, from which he emerges victorious, saving Lucky's life. Pinto, a follower of Barker, attempts to kill Tex by attaching a poisoned needle to the reins of Tex's horse. Tex and Lucky, however, catch Pinto in the act just in time. He manages to escape, robs a bank with his men, and makes off with the loot and a wagonload of dynamite. At the next rodeo, Tex manages to overpower the gang and save other riders from being killed in an explosion. As it turns out, Barker and his gang were actually responsible for the murders of the rodeo riders, which included Tex's brother. Tex gives the sheriff the poisoned needle as evidence. Eventually, he learns that Carmen is a secret agent. As a result, Carmen gives up her agency to build a ranch with Tex.

anchor_text: After wondering for a long time why he is feared by humans, Death decides to go in human form in search of the answer. He's spending three days as Prince Sirki as Duke Lambert's guest. Some of the female guests are interested in him, but ultimately shy away from him. Only Grazia falls in love with him, though she learns of his true nature. Even death is falling in love with Grazia. When he learns of this, the Duke, who is also the father of Grazia's fiancé Corrado, asks him to give her up and leave her with the mortals. Death must decide whether to give in to his love for Grazia or renounce it and leave her among the living.
text_a: Next to the body of his young wife, who died by suicide, the husband relives the couple's history: she, a poor girl, agrees after many insistences to marry him, but the two remain two strangers. The man, who runs a pawn shop, gives great importance to money and material goods, she shows sensitivity to the arts, music, theater, literature. At the Louvre he confides to his wife that he sees only an instrument of pleasure in the exposed Venuses, while she remains amazed by their perfection; he delights in enigmatics while she listens to symphonic music. He lives in a conformist atmosphere, while she refuses to conform to convention. Hence the slow degeneration of relations between the two, the man increasingly desperate and possessive towards the woman to whom he can neither give nor receive love, she who, wounded by her jealousy, locks herself in silence and fasting until consummation-suicide.
text_b: After a few years spent vainly wondering why people are afraid of her, Death decides to personify herself in a man so that she can learn about their world and satisfy the questions about life that have long oppressed her. Events, however, upset his plans and he falls in love with the young and attractive Gracie, who, knowing his secret, proves not to be afraid of the love that unites them. At the now human death, the Duke Lambert, Grazia's father, is presented, who once the man's identity is known begs him to return to his form if he really cares about the relationship with his daughter. Meanwhile Death completely forgets his functions as a human and the life cycle runs without stopping, blocking deaths all over the world, and man is tormented by the question of returning to remain alone in eternity leaving the world of feelings and, above all, Grace.

anchor_text: For five years, the bassist Jérôme played his solo on stage every night in a hit piece. After the last day of the performance, at a farewell party, he wants to give it one more try, but instead of the instrument, he finds a corpse in the contrabass box, the thug Pommes-Chips, which Jérôme's slightly crazy cousin Jacques has stabbed in self-defense. At first, the dead man causes great trouble, but then Jérôme's uncle, a caucasian museum preparator, quickly transforms him into a skeleton for the medical faculty. Unfortunately, the joy is short-lived, because in the jacket of "French fries" was the ticket of a friend who brought a huge quota. While the gang suddenly makes hypocritical visits to museums, Jérôme has already taken the ticket. And while the desperate Oberganove returns to counterfeit artworks and ends up in the Kittchen, the musician collects the coins and drives with a light girl from Danen.
text_a: Jo Arengeot and Pommes-chips just got out of prison. Jo's asking Pommes-chips to play third base for him. Shortly after, Chips finds Jack, a rival in love, and pursues him to kill him. Eventually, Jack accidentally kills him and hides his body in his cousin Jerome's bass case. The two men discussed the best way to dispose of the body. Meanwhile, Jo discovers he's won in the third round. But the winning ticket is still in the dead potato chip jacket, and Jo's gonna do everything she can to try and get it back. In the background of the beginning of the film a play entitled La Lune dans la bière is played for the fifth year at the Paris theatre. The play seems to be a wacky parody of both Prince Igor and War and Peace.
text_b: François Trémin is the manager of a factory that's about to close. The staff learns this when François arrives with his wife Céline and their son Léo. Léo is barely two years old and is taken into the car by Céline in front of the angry staff. The employee Bruno breaks the car window and kidnaps Léo to the horror of the parents, but also to the staff, who see their position weakened by this. The police arrive, and Bruno flees into the woods with Leo in his arms. After falling down a slope, Bruno initially wants to go on without Léo, but returns to the crying child. Both eat blackberries from the bushes, Léo gives Bruno berries and inspects his finger stump, which Bruno actually hides under a cuff. Bruno slowly returns to the factory, Leo falls asleep in his arms. The situation has escalated outside the factory, with clashes between workers and police. François and Céline see Bruno, who lets Léo walk to them on his own. Cops start hitting their shields. When Léo arrives at his parents' home, the noise is deafening, and Léo looks around at Bruno for a moment.

anchor_text: The film traces the lives of French painter Pierre Bonnard (1867-1947) and his wife Marthé de Méligny (1869-1942) over five decades. The artist, known in his home country as the "Painter of Happiness", portrayed his wife, a self-styled aristocrat, in more than a third of his paintings. He is considered one of the greatest French painters of the 20th century and devoted himself in his works to impressionism and abstraction. Bonnard used vibrant colors and depicted scenes of everyday life. Together with other artists, he founded the group Nabis in 1888. The artist cheated on Martha, but kept returning to her until he married her. Later in the film, Marthe goes mad. After her death in 1942, Pierre left her bedroom untouched.
text_a: The film traces over five decades the life of the French painter Pierre Bonnard (1867-1947) and his wife Marthe de Méligny (1869-1942). Known in his native country as the 'painter of happiness', he painted the portrait of his wife, a self-proclaimed aristocrat, in more than a third of his paintings. Considered one of the greatest French painters of the 20th century, he devoted himself to impressionism and abstraction in his works. To do this, Bonnard used bright colors and depicted scenes from everyday life. With other artists, he founded the Nabi group in 1888. The artist deceived Martha, but always returned to her until he married her. Later, Marthe goes mad in the film. After his death in 1942, Pierre left his bedroom untouched.
text_b: The famous Impressionist painter Pierre-Auguste Renoir lives in seclusion during the First World War on an estate in a light-flooded landscape near Cagnes-sur-Mer on the Côte d'Azur. He's burdened by his arthritis and the death of his wife. A new model, the young, carefree Andrée awakens in him new courage and new creativity. One day, Renoir's son Jean, seriously wounded at the front and on leave to recuperate, returns to the country estate. He too is awakened by André's presence and begins to emerge from the shadow of his famous father. He shares Andrée's passion for cinema and wants to join her in film production after the war. The film tells in snapshots with opulent and melancholy images the relationship of the young Andrée to the old painter at the end of his career and to his son, who is still in self-discovery just before the beginning of his career.

anchor_text: Johnny Saxby (Josh O'Connor) lives on the family farm in Yorkshire with his father Martin (Ian Hart), reduced by a stroke, and his grandmother Deirdre (Gemma Jones). Her daily life is marked by hard work with animals, solitary escapades to the bar in the nearby town, and brief, cold conversations with young men she does not know. As lambing season approaches and the patriarch's health declines, the only candidate to apply to work on the farm is a seasonal Romanian, Gheorghe Ionescu (Alec Secăreanu). Their relationship, which began as a struggle, eventually turns sour, and new, unexpected feelings begin to emerge.
text_a: Johnny Saxby is a 25-year-old who lives in Yorkshire and works as a sheep farmer. He sacrificed his studies and a secure job in the city to run the family farm after his father Martin became an invalid. Johnny leads a hard, self-sacrificing life, indulging only in beer and occasional sex. As the breeding season approaches, the father decides to hire Georghe, a Romanian immigrant, to help his hard-working son. Johnny initially resents Georghe, but soon the newcomer proves to be a valuable help and a strong bond is formed between the two, sexual and sentimental. A feeling that marks a turning point in Johnny's life.
text_b: Johnny, a journalist engaged in a collection of interviews focused on the mental states of children and adolescents, embarks on a journey across the country with his young nephew, Jesse, aged 9, entrusted to him by his sister. The latter has to deal with the problems posed by the father of the child who has a psychiatric disorder. Jesse and Johnny barely know each other, and the young boy has a complex personality that needs to be dealt with. Gradually, the current will pass between them.

anchor_text: Severely burned after a motorcycle accident in the Quebec countryside, Rose underwent emergency surgery at a nearby private clinic, Keloïd. Specializing in cosmetic surgery, this facility is about to experiment with new skin grafting techniques, and Rose will serve as a guinea pig. Healthy skin is taken, neutralized by a special process, and then grafted to repair damaged tissue. After a month in a coma, the young woman suddenly awakens. His metabolism changed and a new orifice appeared under his armpit. It can no longer digest food and must feed directly on blood, which it pumps through a retractable dart housed in the new orifice. The victim is then infected and develops symptoms similar to those of rabies. Driven by impulses of extreme violence, she must in turn seek blood. The epidemic spreads rapidly, martial law is declared.
text_a: Dr. Herbert West and Dr. Dan Cain have developed a serum with which they can resurrect more than just the dead. It allows them to create new, bizarre life forms out of all possible and impossible body parts (such as an eyeball with three human fingers as legs). As they regularly steal body parts from the hospital's observation room in order to assemble their "dream wife", a police officer comes on their trail, but this is the least of their problems, because the severed, reanimated head of Dr. Carl Hill wants to take revenge for what the two scientists once did to him and pay them back with the help of a zombie army.
text_b: Rose is a young woman who suffers a motorcycle accident with her boyfriend Hart and is admitted to the Keloid Clinic, a center specializing in cosmetic surgery, where she undergoes revolutionary treatments. However the result of the grafting of new neutral tissue into Rose is fatal: she wakes up with a fissure under her armpit, from which a phallic appendix emerges, and simultaneously begins to experience an unbearable thirst for human blood. Rose will satisfy her thanks to her new appendix with which she penetrates, to extract blood, from the bodies of her victims. They then fall prey to an uncontrollable, murderous rage that slowly spreads through the city like a savage plague of terror and violence.

As they talk in the evening about their past relationships and learn things about each other that they didn't know, their love is put to the test.
text_a: A filmmaker (John David Washington) returns home with his girlfriend (Zendaya) after celebrating the release of a film he hopes will likely be a critical and financial success. anchor_text: This is Zurich, 1938. Willie, a German cabaret singer, became romantically involved with Robert Mendelsson, a Swiss Jew who was a member of a secret organization helping Jews to escape Nazi Germany. Robert's father, David, sees this relationship in a bad light and tries to end it. Willie comes to the aid of their organization, but because of David's maneuvers, she finds herself stranded in Germany at the end of her first assignment. Separated by events, Willie and Robert took seemingly opposite paths during World War II. He will help the German Jews to flee their country, she will become a great star of Hitler's Germany thanks to the song Lili Marleen. But Willie would not hesitate to support Robert and his family in their fight against Nazism, risking his life.
text_a: Set in Zurich during the Third Reich in 1938, the film tells the story of an impossible love between Willie, a German singer, played by Hanna Schygulla, and the Swiss composer of Jewish origin Robert Mendelsson, played by Giancarlo Giannini, who actively seeks to help a group of German Jews. The outbreak of World War II will separate them. The singer, back in Germany, becomes famous thanks to the song Lili Marleen. At the end of the war he goes to Zurich, where he finds his beloved Robert married and leaves again.
text_b: Vienna in 1900. Friedrich Mitterer, famous actor of the Burgtheater, sees Leni Schindler, the daughter of the master tailor, praying in the church and falls in love with her. But she loves the young unknown actor Josef Rainer who lives with her parents. When Leni discovers an invitation card for Mitterer to a reception at Baroness Seebach's, she gives it to Rainer who comes in Mitterer's place. He is noticed and gets a small role at the Burgtheater. When the Baroness's jealous husband realizes that his wife has fallen in love with Rainer, there is a scandal as a result of which Rainer is no longer welcome at the Burgtheater. As he is about to commit suicide, he is saved by Mitterer who knows about the relationship between him and Leni.

anchor_text: In 1648, Captain Sylvester switched sides during the Civil War for his own interests. He decides to fight alongside the royalists against the ruling power...
text_a: Favourite of King Charles II, the Duke of Brampton eliminates the rich lords of the kingdom, one by one, in order to seize their fortunes. But the little black book in which he records his misdeeds falls into the hands of Michael Dermott, a former officer turned outlaw...
text_b: During the English Civil War, King Charles I is captured by the men of the treacherous Colonel Judd and Captain Sylvester. A group of men loyal to the sovereign attempt to free him with the help of Claire, the daughter of Judd himself.

anchor_text: In New York City, Joe Gower works as a librarian and leads a simple, carefree life. One day, he gets a visit from his friend Abe, a police officer. He asks her to fill in for him at an audition for the annual police charity ballet. After passing all the exams, Joe finds himself leading a double life to Abe's dismay. Things get even more complicated when Joe is taken hostage by two gangsters during a bank robbery...
text_a: Joe Gower's job is to go from shelf to shelf reaching for books. A friend of his, a police officer, is chosen to participate in a charity ball. Gower agrees to take his place on the show by posing as a cop. In the show, he falls in love with a girl and gets into several fights with other cops and criminals.[1]
text_b: A dying man finds that horses, blondes, and the mob can end his life faster than cancer. Joe (Billy Connolly) is an ordinary guy who runs a flower shop and has never had much luck. Joe's prospects seem bleak when he is diagnosed with a brain tumor, but the horizon lights up somewhat when he wins the big prize at the races. However, Hush (Sharon Stone), an exotic dancer who is heavily in debt to a gang of thugs, steals his newly acquired fortune to repay them. Joe gets his money back by explaining to the gangsters what happened, but only because they mistake him for a union baron they've heard of but never met. When the bandits discover that Joe is a florist and not a career criminal, they find the situation anything but amusing.

anchor_text: Joe Gower, a librarian employed by the New York Public Library, is unhappy with his life. His boss is harassing him, his girlfriend is leaving him. Police officer Abe Washington, a friend of Gower's, asks him to fill in for him during a rehearsal for a dance show organized by the police for charity. Gower agrees, and during the rehearsal, he meets police officer Rachel Wareham. He poses as a police officer and helps Wareham pursue a criminal in which they both happen to be involved. Gower takes part in further rehearsals to get to know Wareham better. He's gonna be the solo dancer the show is supposed to be built around. Meanwhile, his friend warns him of the consequences of falsely impersonating a police officer. Gower gets into a bank robbery in a police uniform. The police are surrounding the building. Gower distracts the robber with a few dance moves and overwhelms him. In the last scene, you see him in the show.
text_a: A dying man discovers that horses, blondes and the mob can end his life faster than cancer. Joe (Billy Connolly) is a normal guy who runs a flower shop and never had much luck. Things look bleak for Joe when he's diagnosed with a brain tumor, but a little bit of good luck appears on the horizon when he wins a big prize at the racetrack. text_b: Joe Gower is a quiet librarian, eccentric in character, and good friend of police officer Abe Washington; when the police department puts on a musical show with real officers, Abe, who wants nothing to do with it, convinces the recalcitrant Joe to impersonate him at the audition and get himself kicked out; whereas Joe meets police officer Rachel Rareham at the tryouts and becomes infatuated with her. So he decides to persist and be admitted. Abe does not see it well, but Joe manages to convince him to let him continue; in order not to arouse suspicion, he pretends to be a policeman even outside the rehearsals, in order to get close to Rachel; but in his way is Rachel's ex, Pete Peterson, also a policeman; a long series of gags and misunderstandings bring the story to the end. Joe at one point confesses the truth to Rachel, who remains wounded; Joe contemplates quitting the show, but during a commission in the bank is taken hostage with the other customers by two clumsy robbers; his fake name leaks and Abe ends up in trouble, and must go to the bank hoping to save the salvageable (on horseback, as a punishment decided by his superior); but Joe manages to distract and disarm the robbers with a dance step. Eventually, Joe and Rachel attend the successful show, and they reconcile.

anchor_text: The film is set in 1933, during the Great Depression, and is about hobos, migrant workers who forbiddenly travel on freight trains through the western United States. One day, the experienced hobo Ass No. 1 manages to ride a piece on freight train No. 19, the so-called 19er. Its leader is the ambitious Shack, who tries with all his might to keep Hobos away from his train and does not shy away from the sadistic use of force. Also on board is the young, inexperienced, but all the more pompous Cigaret, who, however, is discovered by Shack and locked in the car with Ace No. 1 to his displeasure. But ace number one finds a way out, and from then on Cigarette clings to him and talks all the time about becoming an equally successful hobo. With other hobos and railroad workers, Ace No. 1 bets that he will be the first to make it all the way to Portland on Shack's 19er. Due to Cigarette's carelessness in boarding the oncoming train, they are both spotted early by Shack. With all his might, he now tries to get them off the train. In the final fight on an open freight car, he manages to injure ace number one, Shack, and knock him off the train. After that, Cigarette bragged as if he'd been equally instrumental in defeating Shack. Now ace number one has finally had enough of the nerve saw and throws him off the train too on the grounds that he doesn't have enough class for a good hobo.
text_a: Hud Dixon, a gunfighter who wears a metal mesh vest, arrives in Blackstone. In that city his brother Charlie was lynched, falsely accused of being the author of a bank robbery. His purpose is to find the real person responsible for the theft and recover the loot from the robbery that mysteriously disappeared. After escaping an ambush, he kills Boot Johnson, a man suspected of being one of his brother's assassins, with his bare hands. Before he's arrested, he leaves after getting rid of Sheriff Gideon's surveillance. The latter, however, does not delay. She chases him and ends up joining him in his search for the truth. From El Diablo, a one-armed Mexican, a bit of an outlaw and a bit of a revolutionary, they learn crucial information. Little by little, Hud discovers that his brother's robbery and murder stem from an attempt by Virginia Pollicut, the owner of the bank, to seize the money deposited in its vaults. When El Diablo captures the woman and tries to get his hands on the loot by killing Sheriff Gideon, Hud isn't looking. He confronts the Mexican and his gang and after eliminating them he sets fire to the money that was the cause of so much bloodshed. Wounded, he still finds the strength to put on the run a group of young thieves who tried to take over Blackstone by taking advantage of the lack of authority, then gets in the saddle and leaves.
text_b: The film is set in 1933, during the Great Depression, and is about hoboes, migrant workers who travel illegally on freight trains across the western United States. One day, the experienced drifter "Axis Number 1" manages to get a ticket on freight train number 19, the so-called "19er". The foreman is the ambitious Shack, who tries to keep the vagrants away from his train with all his might and does not shy away from sadistic violence. Also on board is young Cigaret, less experienced but more brash, who is discovered by Shack and locked in the car with Ace number 1, to his great disappointment. The latter finds a way out, however, and from that moment Cigarette becomes attached to him and constantly aims to be just as successful. With other tramps and railroaders, ace number one is betting he'll be the first to drive shack number 19 all the way to portland. But, due to Cigarette's carelessness in boarding the oncoming train, both of them are discovered by Shack, who does everything to throw them off the train. In the final fight on an open freight car, Ace number 1 manages to wound Shack and push him off the train. Afterwards, Cigarette boasts as if he also helped defeat Shack, but the old tramp has had enough of him and throws him off the train, claiming he doesn't have enough class to be a good hobo.

anchor_text: In the fictional small town of Ranjaar in Gujarat, the Rajadi and Sanera families have clashed violently since time immemorial, the slightest incident being used as an excuse to start a fight. When on the occasion of the Holi festival, Ram Rajadi sneaks into the Sanera house, he meets Leela and immediately falls in love with her. The girl responds to his feelings and the lovers elope to get married. But they are quickly caught up by their respective families who want to bring them back to an attitude more in line with the honor of their clan. However, the young people are stubborn and this "forbidden love" brings the tensions to a climax.
text_a: Set in Gujarati territory between shootings, vendettas and extraordinary musical atmospheres. During the Holi festival, Ram, a Rajadi, enters the Sanera's house and flirts with Leela while her mother Dhankor Baa, the chief cashier, tries to arrange her daughter's engagement to an NRI. Leela's older brother, Khanji, realizes Ram's intrusion and sends the police to arrest him which doesn't happen as Ram offers him some of his films at red lights. Eventually Ram and Leela fall in love but things get complicated when Khanji accidentally kills Ram's brother Meghji and Ram kills Khanji. Ram and Leela decide to run away together. They marry in secret but before they can consummate the marriage Ram's friends lure him in with deception and get him drunk and inform his family of Leela's location. The next day Leela is forcibly taken back to her cousin, Bhavani. Ram's family takes this in a positive way, having damaged Leela's reputation and appoint him as the head of the Rajadis.
text_b: Raj (Salman Khan), a singer, exercises his talents on the beaches of Goa. An orphan, he believes in signs. One day, he finds a Mumbai license plate in the sand. He deduced that destiny was calling him to this city to fulfill his dream of becoming a pop star. Initial auditions are promising, but the head of the production company dies on the day Raj is due to hand over his recordings. Feeling that he has taken the wrong road, Raj prepares to leave Mumbai. But just as he's about to give up, he witnesses a car accident. The singer rushes in and manages to save the life of a young woman trapped in the car body just before a train hits her. Raj accompanies the victim to the hospital, signs the admission papers. He is believed to be the husband of the victim, Pooja (Rani Mukerji). This one goes into a coma. To his surprise, Raj discovers that the woman whose life he saved is the daughter of a famous film producer. The imposture becomes interesting to the aspiring singer. But then Pooja's best friend Jahnvi (Preity Zinta) arrives, and it's love at first sight between her and Raj.

anchor_text: For centuries, the families of the confident Ram and the fearless Leela have been enemies. Despite the odds, they are drawn to each other and fall in love. Naturally, this causes turmoil in both families, and instead of seeing the union of the children as a way to resolve their conflicts, the parents forbid the lovers to meet again. The film depicts the course of the love story against the backdrop of these difficulties.
text_a: Sapna is an orphan, raised by her three caring but eccentric uncles. The priest Prabhu, the fitness fanatic Bhola and the always dancing Vicky. Everyone has their own lifestyle, which they also want to impose on Sapna, and a different idea of the man Sapna is supposed to marry. Sapna can't stand living at home anymore, so she runs away to live the way she wants, at least for a while. She wants to take a trip to Europe. Seth Oberoi would like to make Sapna his daughter-in-law, but Sapna's uncles have rejected his proposal without ever meeting his son Raja. To fulfill his father's wish, however, Raja promises him that he will succeed in marrying Sapna, and follows her unrecognized to Europe, where the two meet and fall in love.
text_b: In the fictional small town of Ranjaar in Gujarat, the Rajadi and Sanera families have clashed violently since time immemorial, the slightest incident being used as an excuse to start a fight. When on the occasion of the Holi festival, Ram Rajadi sneaks into the Sanera house, he meets Leela and immediately falls in love with her. The girl responds to his feelings and the lovers elope to get married. But they are quickly caught up by their respective families who want to bring them back to an attitude more in line with the honor of their clan. However, the young people are stubborn and this "forbidden love" brings the tensions to a climax.

anchor_text: An American teenager, Jason Tripitikas (Michael Angarano), is a fan of Hong Kong films and the classics of Kung fu. If he knows all about kung fu, he doesn't practice it. One night, assaulted by a gang of thugs, he had the door of the Chinatown store where he buys his movies opened. The old Chinese merchant is shot by the gang leader. Before Jason escapes, threatened in turn by the thug, the old man assigns him the task of returning a richly carved iron staff (bô) to its owner. Arriving on the roof of the building, he suddenly feels dragged by the gun; propelled into the void, he finds himself in another place, another time. Jason then finds himself in a village that's being attacked by warriors. It is Lu Yan (Jackie Chan) who rescues him and reveals that he holds a legendary weapon belonging to the Monkey King (Jet Li), who was betrayed by a warlord...
text_a: In the southern United States, two prisoners, Black Noah Cullen (Sidney Poitier) and White John Jackson (Tony Curtis), are transported by cell van to their new place of detention. The vehicle has an accident and the two prisoners take the opportunity to escape. While the county sheriff (Theodore Bikel) organizes the pursuit, Cullen and Jackson, despite the racist hatred they have for each other, have no choice but to cooperate if they want to escape their pursuers as they are unable to break the chain that binds them. They try to steal food in a village but are surprised by the villagers who try to catch them and hang them. Luckily, one of them (Lon Chaney Jr.) gives them a way to escape. They end up stranded on a farm where a woman (Cara Williams), abandoned by her husband, lives with her son (Kevin Coughlin). It gives them the means to cut the chain. She and Jackson became friends. However, when the latter learns that she has sent Cullen to certain death by showing him a path leading straight to a swamp, he leaves her and runs to warn him. Unable to catch a moving train that could have taken them out of the state, the two escapees were eventually caught by their pursuers.
text_b: In Boston lives Jason Tripitikas, a clumsy boy obsessed with martial arts, kung fu movies and Chinese culture. He often goes to Old Hop's store to buy movies; as he wanders around the store he is lured by an antique cane. Hop tells the boy that the staff was left in the custody of his grandfather by a mysterious stranger, who never returned to retrieve it. On his way home, Jason is forced by a group of petty thieves to return to the store to rob it. While the boys are destroying everything, Hop asks Jason to run away with the stick. In the race, Jason falls off a roof, faints from the fall, and wakes up in ancient China. He soon meets the immortal Lu Yan, who tells him the story of the Monkey King, who was tricked by the Jade Captain and turned to stone by him. Subsequently, the Captain turned the realm into a reign of fear and violence. Legend has it that a traveler will bring the sacred staff back to the realm and bring peace.

anchor_text: This film tells the story of director James Whale's final days in Hollywood (in 1957) and his final suicide. He evokes his works including the famous Frankenstein, his participation in the First World War, his love of painting, his physical degradation, through the eyes of a gardener, Clay Boone, whom he has just hired and with whom he has fallen in love.
text_a: An old director, J.J. Hannaford, a cross between John Ford and Ernest Hemingway returns to Hollywood after several years of exile in Europe. He's finishing a new film with which he wants to challenge, on the ground, the entire spectrum of young American cinema, from melancholic moviegoers to Andy Warhol. A party is held in his honor at his ranch by all the Hollywood moguls on the occasion of his seventieth birthday. This evening will end with the death of the director at the wheel of the sports car he wanted to give to his favorite actor, without knowing if it is an accident or suicide.
text_b: The film chronicles director James Whale's final days in Hollywood in 1957 until his suicide. He also evokes his creations, such as the famous Frankenstein, his participation in the First World War, his interest in painting, his old age, through the eyes of Clayton Boone, his new gardener with whom he fell in love.

anchor_text: The film chronicles director James Whale's final days in Hollywood in 1957 until his suicide. He also evokes his creations, such as the famous Frankenstein, his participation in the First World War, his interest in painting, his old age, through the eyes of Clayton Boone, his new gardener with whom he fell in love.
text_a: It's the last day of Jake Hannaford, an old Hollywood director who was killed in a car accident on his 70th birthday, narrated by an elderly Brooks Otterlake who had been Hannaford's protégé. Shortly before his death, Hannaford was attempting to revive his declining career by making a flashy film loaded with gratuitous sex and violence, with mixed results. During Hannaford's birthday party, this unfinished film, The Other Side of the Wind, is shown.
text_b: In 1957, young Clayton Boone starts working as a gardener at the home of an old retired film director in Los Angeles. The old man turns out to be James Whale, creator of legendary horror titles, whose doctors have predicted short life expectancy. Little by little, she begins a game of seduction with the young Boone making him the confidant of her memories.

anchor_text: The film begins at the armistice of 8 September 1943 and ends at the time of the uprising of Naples, twenty days later. Second Lieutenant Innocenzi and his detachment found their barracks abandoned and were astonished to find that the German troops now regarded them as enemies. Innocenzi seeks to preserve a semblance of authority, but his soldiers keep him company, except for one, Ceccarelli. Disguised as civilians, with the help of peasants, the two men will have to go through rocky and yet terribly tragic situations. In Naples, Private Ceccarelli was shot by a German soldier while trying to escape, he died shortly afterwards in Innocenzi's arms. He then joined a group of resistance fighters.
text_a: The film describes the popular revolt that broke out in Naples spontaneously following the shooting of some Italian sailors on 28 September 1943 and that in four days defeated and fled the German troops from the city before the arrival of the Allies. The film is choral and mixes individual episodes and popular characters protagonists of the revolt. From the boys who escaped from the reformatory to join the insurrection to the little Gennarino Capuozzo who dies with a bomb in his hand ready to throw it on the Nazi tanks to many other characters, among whom Adolfo Pansini should be remembered.
text_b: On September 8, 1943, Italy surrenders and signs an armistice with the Allies. Amidst the general bewilderment and confusion, part of the Italian army disperses and the soldiers begin to return home. Alberto Innocenzi (Sordi), Subteniente de Complemento, tries to keep his soldiers together and find some command to report to. Given the widespread chaos, he ends up surrendering and decides to flee with Sergeant Fornaciari and engineer Ceccarelli.[1]

anchor_text: Two Chinese immigrants, Thomas and David, run a food truck in Barcelona. When the two meet the pickpocket Sylvia, they immediately fall in love with her and of course also help her when she is pursued by a creepy man. It turns out that this private investigator is tasked with finding Sylvia, as she is the illegitimate daughter of a Spanish nobleman who has just died and left her an inheritance worth millions. The only catch is that Sylvia has to claim her inheritance within two weeks. But since Sylvia's evil uncle is also after the money, he hires some villains to kidnap Sylvia and keep her imprisoned in a castle until the deadline expires. When Thomas and David learn of this, they team up with private investigator Moby and break into the castle to free Sylvia. Although David is captured, they manage to join forces and defeat the villains.
text_a: Moby's private investigator business isn't doing very well. Fortunately, he is hired to track down Sylvia, a young heiress who has recently disappeared. What he doesn't know is that the young woman's uncle is also trying to locate her... but only to get rid of her and seize the inheritance! Fate will place on Moby's road two friendly adventurers, Thomas and David, who survive thanks to their business of Chinese fast food. Together, they find Sylvia and try to rescue her from the clutches of her pursuers...
text_b: Crazy/Beautiful tells the love story between Nicole Oakley (Kirsten Dunst) and Carlos Núñez (Jay Hernandez) and is based on a true story. Carlos is a hardworking student who does everything to succeed, which is not the case with Nicole; she is the daughter of a prominent politician; she plunges into alcohol, but when she meets Carlos, Nicole changes completely and becomes madly in love; Nicole's tragic destiny surfaces when Carlos has to leave her to pass the entrance exam to a prominent naval school...

text_a: Tomás (Jackie Chan) and David (Biao Yuen) are cousins who run a fast food outlet in Barcelona. The food is delivered by Tomás, who moves around the square on a skateboard. After a clash with bikers, they go about their business as if nothing had happened. They visit David's father, who is in a mental institution, and they meet Sylvia, the daughter of David's father's friend. Tom encourages David to ask her out, cowering and making the excuse that she would have turned him down anyway. Late at night, back at the food stall, Tom runs into Sylvia, who pretends to be a prostitute, being in fact a pickpocket, robbing a man in his room and fleeing to the food stall. Tom and David have fallen in love with her. After allowing her to stay in their apartment that night, they wake up to discover that Sylvia has stolen their money. The next day, they run into Moby, a babbling private investigator, who is also stalking Sylvia. Later they discover that she is the heiress of a large sum, which she tries to steal from a criminal gang. When she is kidnapped, Thomas, David, and Moby band together to save her, infiltrating the villain's castle, defeating them all in martial arts combat. The final scene of the film shows David, Tomás and Sylvia reunited. Sylvia asks them for a summer job, and Moby asks them if they want to work as private detectives with him, something they decline.
text_b: Crazy/Beautiful tells the love story between Nicole Oakley (Kirsten Dunst) and Carlos Núñez (Jay Hernandez) and is based on a true story. anchor_text: The setting is an exclusive ski resort in Switzerland, the time is the present. Barbara, a wealthy American, is married to lawyer Mark Sawyer. Both are elderly, and while Mark is going through a midlife crisis, she suffers from the fading of her beauty and travels to Switzerland, where she undergoes extensive cosmetic surgery without his knowledge. It succeeds, and while Barbara is waiting for Mark, she begins an affair with the young playboy Erich. Her hope that the surgery could save her marriage is first challenged by Kate, Barbara's daughter, who does not believe that this marriage can be repaired. Then Mark arrives from Detroit, explaining that he wants to divorce her to marry a younger woman. Barbara has to come to terms with the fact that she and Mark have grown apart and have to go their separate ways.
text_a: Barbara (Elizabeth Taylor), a mature woman with a marriage in crisis, decides to have cosmetic surgery to look younger and win back her husband Mark (Henry Fonda). After the operation, which has been a success, Barbara waits for her husband at an alpine station to give him a surprise, but before meeting him again, she meets an attractive young man who seduces her. She succumbs to his charms taking advantage of his rejuvenation. When she meets Mark again, he tells her that he has met a younger girl.
text_b: The story takes place in Kansas City in the 1930s and 1940s. The children of lawyer Walter Bridge - Carolyn, Douglas and Ruth - reject their parents' conservative values. One day, Walter ignores a tornado warning, leaving his wife, India, to stay with him while the other humans seek shelter. A little later, it is criticized that India follows her husband unconditionally. Ruth travels to New York City to pursue an acting career. She works there as an assistant in a newsroom and as a waitress. Carolyn marries a man who tells her father that he and Carolyn don't need permission to get married. Douglas is in college, but he wants to volunteer for the Air Force. Meanwhile, Carolyn is abused by her husband Gil Davis and returns to her parents. Ruth moves in with a man. She later travels to Paris, where she engages in short-lived affairs with artists. Grace Barron, a friend of India's, commits suicide.

anchor_text: Terrorists are taking over the Eiffel Tower. They're threatening to detonate a hydrogen bomb. Superman steps in and disposes of the bomb in space. Unnoticed, the explosion frees the three Kryptonian criminals General Zod, Ursa, and Non, who were previously trapped in the Phantom Zone. On Earth, Lois Lane has discovered that Clark Kent and Superman are one and the same person. Despite successful evasions, Clark finally confesses his secret identity to her. The two withdraw into the fortress of solitude and become a loving couple. Clark's giving up his superpowers for Lois. Meanwhile, the three Kryptonian criminals reach Earth. You have the same superpowers as Superman. This makes it easy for them to take over the world because the only one who could stop them has given up his superpowers. Lex Luthor, meanwhile, escapes from prison and discovers Superman's Fortress of Solitude, where he learns, among other things, that Superman's biological father, Jor-El, arranged the incarceration of the major criminals. He offers himself to General Zod by exposing Superman as Jor-El's son and Lois as Superman's girlfriend. Now Clark realizes that he has to put his responsibility above his private love life. He'll find a way to get his superpowers back. It's a showdown between Superman and the three supervillains. In his fortress of solitude, he confronts the criminals. He robs them of their superpowers so he can defeat them. In the end, he even manages to make Lois forget his secret identity, as the knowledge was hard for her to bear.
text_a: The film opens with Superman trying to save Lois Lane in France, where terrorists have planted a bomb in one of the Eiffel Tower's elevators. Superman, after saving Lois, takes the elevator into space. Throwing it away, unwittingly, causes the phantom zone where the Kryptonian villains, led by General Zod, were imprisoned, to be broken. These, when released, head for Earth. Meanwhile, Lex Luthor, with the help of his henchman Otis, escapes from prison and together with his friend Miss Teschmacher reaches the North Pole, more precisely the Fortress of Solitude where he obtains data on the three Kryptonian villains, which explains the additional signals in the alpha-wave radar he created to detect Superman. Meanwhile, Lois and Clark are sent to Niagara Falls. When a child falls from a gazebo and Superman rescues him, Lois notices that each time the Man of Steel appears, Clark disappears and tries to bring down Superman, who in the hotel room, after stumbling and falling into a chimney without being burned, reveals his secret to her. They both fly to the Fortress where Kal-El renounces his powers because of his love for Lois. Meanwhile, Zod and his minions have taken over Earth and are teaming up with Lex Luthor to take on Superman. General Zod, upon learning that Superman is the son of Jor-El, will seek revenge on his jailer. Realizing what happened, Superman returns to the Fortress where he regains his powers and returns to Metropolis where he battles Zod, Ursa, and Non. After feigning defeat, he takes them to the pole where he uses Kryptonian technology to strip them of their powers and defeat them. Imprison Luthor and erase Lois Lane's memory so she won't remember the identity of Clark Kent.
text_b: Reporters Clark Kent and Lois Lane are sent to the small town of Silsby to document the opening of the world's deepest oil well. During the excavation, however, the drill penetrates the city of the mole men, a race of small, hairy humanoids who live deep underground. During the night two mole men, climbing up the drilling hole, emerge to the surface, frightening the townspeople. Soon an angry mob, led by the violent Luke Benson, begins hunting down the "monsters" to kill them and Superman is the only one able to calm the situation by stopping Benson and the other people. One mole man is injured in the clashes and taken to the hospital for surgery, while the other disappears into the drilling hole. The townspeople, again led by Benson, storm the hospital in an attempt to capture the wounded mole man and Superman is forced to stand guard outside to stop them. Meanwhile, the mole men come out of the hole to retrieve their mate. They're armed with a laser, and they don't hesitate to use it when Benson tries to attack them. Superman, however, stands between the mole men and Benson and saves his life, though, as he later tells him, maybe he didn't deserve it. After recovering their wounded comrade, the mole men return to the underground, blowing the hole that led to the surface, so as not to be disturbed anymore.

anchor_text: A young girl is found hanging in her room. Everything indicates that she committed suicide, but the autopsy results speak a different language. The film shows the intrusion of violence into the supposed safety of one's own habitat. Suddenly, life becomes an uncertain journey through time and space.
text_a: Detective David Callahan is a rogue cop with a history of drug trafficking. One day, he gets shot in a fight that ends badly and he's left for dead. But a good Samaritan saves his life and disappears. Some time later, as Callahan decides to become a good cop back on track, the man who saved him comes back into his life and asks him to investigate the murders of young strippers. Looking for redemption, Callahan agrees to go after the killer.
text_b: This is Bridgeport, January 17, 2008. A young girl was found hanging in her room. All indications are that she committed suicide, but the results of the autopsy reveal other evidence. Ten years later, the director examines the past causes as well as the future consequences of this unsolved crime. Like an imaginary biography, the film explores the relationship between the safety of the habitable place and the violence that can endanger it, like a fluid journey through time and space.

anchor_text: A young couple, Patrick and Louise, had their nine-year-old daughter, Alice, tragically killed by a rabid dog. To mourn, they moved from town to Wake Wood, a quiet little village. They soon discover that the place is strange and that rituals are being prepared to bring the dead back to life. Arthur, the mayor informs them that they can see their daughter again but she can't stay for more than three days. At first, they're reluctant to do this horrible thing, but then they change their minds, too eaten up with grief.
text_a: The parents of a girl who was killed by a wild dog are given a chance to spend three days with their deceased daughter. A young couple arrives in a village called "Wake Wood", the woman works in a pharmacy and the husband is a veterinarian. they realize that there is a sect with Celtic rites where they can bring a loved one to life for a period of time of 3 days... time that according to them remains the vital energy, there are several rules... they can not go outside the boundaries of the village and the person they bring must not be more than a year of death, the end leaves the viewer the question and unpublished conclusions.
text_b: Jan, a wealthy and in love young man whose life is running successfully, decides to renew the bonds with his family, which he has abandoned some many years ago. So, together with his wife Maria, he returns to his hometown and more specifically to the pension run by his mother and sister. Not knowing how to reveal his true identity, Jan stays at the hostel, waiting for the opportune moment for his statement. Yet a cruel fate awaits him. His mother and sister Martha have a habit of murdering travelers who spend the night at the hostel to rob them and thus obtain the necessary resources to escape from those sad wastelands and find sunnier lands. Jan, by not revealing his identity, will suffer the same fate as the rest of the travelers.

anchor_text: After the death of his loving wife, Aidan Breslin became a violent and bitter cop, growing increasingly distant from his two young sons, Alex and Sean. As he investigates a series of particularly violent murders, he discovers a possible and traumatic connection between these deaths and that he himself has a connection to the killer. So now Aidan is going to do everything he can to figure out what ties him to the murders and trace the trail back to the killer, who seems to be inspired by the biblical figure of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse. The war, the famine, the plague... and the death...
text_a: Owen Casserly, a forensic accountant in Florida, reports on television the murder of his boss by Black Ops mercenaries. Knowing that he, too, was in danger, he hurried off with his son Connor. They then headed to Park County, Montana, where Owen's brother-in-law, Ethan Sawyer, was a deputy sheriff. But they don't reach their destination. On a road through the woods, Owen is shot dead by the same two killers responsible for the murder. Connor then watches the whole scene. Before he died, his father told him to follow the river that would lead him to a city. Not wanting to leave any witnesses, the two killers went after him. The teenager then turns to Hannah Faber for help. Posted in a fire tower, the latter is a paratrooper firefighter and remains traumatized after a terrible accident during a previous intervention. Through a burning forest, Connor and Hannah will try to find a way out.
text_b: Detective Aidan Breslin is investigating a dangerous and mysterious serial killer who is terrorizing New York City. Since the killer signs the locations of his murders with the inscription as and see, quote from Revelation 6, 1.3.5.7, Breslin comes to the deduction that he, to kill, is inspired by the legend of the 4 Horsemen of the Apocalypse. With the intent of anticipating the killer's moves, the detective will find a link between the victims that to his utter amazement will involve him.

anchor_text: Piglet Wilbur is born on the farm, the smallest and weakest of his litter, and the farmer wants to kill him, but little Fern Arable can stop him. She takes Wilbur in and raises him until he's too difficult to keep and has to return to the Sugar Man's farm. Wilbur threatens the slaughterhouse again for the upcoming Christmas, but there he befriends a spider named Charlotte, who lives above his stable. With her and the other animals, he devises a plan to prevent the slaughter. Charlotte astonishes the Zuckermanns with messages that she weaves into their networks, and Wilbur becomes the winner of the competition of an agricultural show after some adventures. In the end, Wilbur is spared, which also pleases Fern, but at the same time he has to say goodbye to Charlotte, because the spider has laid eggs during the competition and is now dying. Sadly, but with the realization that nothing lasts forever, Wilbur returns to the farm with the egg sack. Later, Charlotte's children hatch and move out into the world. Only three of them stay behind on the Zuckerman farm and make friends with Wilbur.
text_a: The film begins during springtime on a farm in the U.S. state of Maine. When Fern Arable (Dakota Fanning) realizes that her father plans to kill a piglet for being too small, she successfully convinces him not to do it. He gives the little cub to Fern, who names him Wilfrido and raises him as her pet. But when the little pig grows up, Fern is forced to take him to her Uncle Zuckerman's farm, where he will most likely be prepared for Christmas dinner. Charlotte is a spider who lives above the stable where Wilfrido is; they become friends and she decides to help him so that he is not killed. With the help of the other stable animals Ike the horse, Samuel the sheep, Gussy and Golly the geese, Betsy and Bitsy the cows and Emilio the rat, Charlotte tries to convince the family that Wilfrido is a special animal by writing messages on his wire.
text_b: The film is set in Glasgow, Scotland. The self-centered Wilbur tries to kill himself, but is repeatedly saved by his brother Harbour. After another suicide attempt, Wilbur is banned from his therapy group due to his provocative behavior, he has to leave his apartment and move in with his brother. This one tries to carry on her late father's chaotic and dilapidated bookstore, but the business is doing rather poorly. There, Harbour meets a single mother named Alice. She works as a cleaner at the hospital and sells Harbour the leftover books as a side dish. One day, she saves Wilbur when he tries to hang himself in the store. Harbour and Alice fall in love and marry, but even on their wedding night, Wilbur makes another suicide attempt. Shortly afterwards, Harbour collapsed and was admitted to hospital, where she was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer. While he is in the hospital undergoing chemotherapy and waiting to die, Wilbur and Alice fall in love. After a last Christmas dinner together, Harbour commits suicide in the hospital, while for Wilbur himself, suicide is no longer an issue.

anchor_text: In 1988, young American prodigy Randy Daytona competed in the semi-finals of table tennis at the Seoul Olympics, but he lost to German competitor Karl Wolfschtagg. His father, having bet on his son's victory, cannot repay his debt and is killed by agents of a triad in the pay of the mysterious Feng. 19 years later, Randy retired from competition and did ping-pong tricks in a casino in Reno. He is contacted by an FBI agent who wants him to participate in a big tournament organized by Feng, hoping to arrest Feng.
text_a: Eleven-year-old Randy Daytona is a member of the Olympic table tennis (ping pong) team representing the United States at the 1988 Summer Olympics. Unfortunately, Randy loses the final and his father is unable to pay off a debt, accrued by betting on the victory of Randy's team and is killed. Nineteen years later, Randy returns to the ping-pong courts to help the FBI catch an arms dealer.
text_b: After defeating Tong Po, Kurt returns to the United States to continue his life with his wife. But two fake cops kidnap him and take him to Thailand, where he is imprisoned and forced to fight the great Mongkut. At first, Kurt refuses, then his wife is kidnapped by millionaire Thomas Moore to force him to fight Mongkut. In prison, Kurt finds unexpected allies who help him prepare for his death match against the giant.

anchor_text: Lucy is a former child star, and she's making a pilgrimage to her guru, Elon Bello, to find enlightenment. The quiet retreat is located in a beautiful mountain resort, where the parking lot is packed with Tesla cars. Before Lucy turns off her phone, she contacts her daughter Dylan, who works as a stuntwoman and is currently training for a dangerous fight scene.
text_a: Acupuncturist Janet and her 11-year-old daughter, Lacy, live in a house in the woods in rural western Massachusetts. It's the summer before the red-haired girl is sent to the sixth grade. anchor_text: Ben Healy moves his adopted son, Junior, from Cold River to Mortville. Numerous divorced women are interested in the single man, however, Junior sabotages their attempts to approach. At all costs, he wants to prevent a new woman from coming between him and his father and jeopardizing the intact father-son relationship. Junior and his classmate Trixie Young, who dislike each other, occasionally compete to see who is causing more trouble. Ben takes a liking to Trixie's mother Annie, the school nurse, who is also a single parent, but does not want to have a relationship with a man because of her difficult daughter. As it turned out later, Trixie, like Junior with his father, had taught potential lovers to fear. The wealthy Lawanda Dunmore finally manages to win Ben's heart. In Ben's absence, she openly expresses her dislike for Junior and threatens to send him abroad as his new stepmother. Trixie and Junior end their feud and do their best to prevent the impending wedding at the last minute and bring their parents together.
text_a: Junior and his dad just moved to Mortville, the city with the highest rate of singles. Junior must play out all of his malice and twisted moves in order to protect his father from the clutches of a wealthy, child-hating banker. Since arriving in Mortville, Junior has started wreaking havoc again: causing a neighbor's gas grill to explode, humiliating his babysitter with a camera and projector, covering a school bully with duct tape, giving misery to his grandfather and his dog Nippy, replacing lemonade with urine, smuggling a ride at a carnival party, ruining a family dinner with insects, and preventing a wedding with the help of the town's other terror, Trixie. After a series of catastrophes, the two monsters have only one idea in mind: to reunite their parents.
text_b: Ben Healy, an employee of his father's company "Big Ben" Healy, and his wife Flo adopt the seven-year-old junior. This one quickly turns out to be a devil's roast who's been returned to the orphanage several times. He's also shaking up the Healys household. When he ruins a neighbor's kid's birthday party, Ben decides with a heavy heart to send him back to the orphanage. But Junior, who now realizes that at least Ben means well with him, foils a plot by Flo and her brother against Big Ben's fortune. Ben breaks up with Flo and realizes that Junior has good qualities too. He finally decides to raise him on his own.

anchor_text: Jack Foster, a former CIA agent, is planning a trip to his wife's home country of Romania to mark the fifth anniversary of her death. Grandpa George and daughter Amanda flew to Bucharest ahead of us. What Jack doesn't know is that his father-in-law is using him as a courier for a deadly virus stolen from the CIA's top-secret MK-Ultra program. At the airport, Amanda is kidnapped and her grandfather is blown up in a car. Jack can untangle a murderous spy ring and get his daughter back.
text_a: Jack Foster, a former CIA agent, goes on vacation to Romania with his daughter Amanda to visit George, the father of his late wife. As soon as they arrive in Bucharest, George is the target of a car bomb, while Amanda is kidnapped before Jack's eyes. In search of his daughter, Jack soon finds himself embroiled in a spy case involving corrupt agents, who covet a bacteriological weapon.
text_b: The unscrupulous gangster Jack Carter is collecting unpaid debts for Las Vegas pawn shops. This job fits his profile: cold, relentless and precise. After the death of his brother Richard in a car accident, Jack leaves Las Vegas to return to Seattle. Apparently, he's the only one who believes his death was staged. Even Richard's wife Gloria, his daughter Doreen, his co-workers and the police don't see the point of continuing the investigation. Jack, on the other hand, is determined to find out the truth and avenge his brother's death.

anchor_text: Jack Foster is a former CIA agent whose daughter is kidnapped. In his quest to track down the kidnappers, he'll discover he's at the center of a national conspiracy.

anchor_text: British actor Joe Scott works in the United States. His career has been in crisis since he turned 40. Except for his housekeeper, Ophelia Franklin, he has no one close to him. Before travelling to an English town for his friend's funeral, Scot recalls his youth, which is shown as a flashback.
text_a: Joe Scott is a Hollywood star in decline who lives only through women and drugs. When he learns of his former best friend's death, memories come flooding back: his childhood in England, his first love... and the tragedy that drove him away from his family.
text_b: A courageous teenager rebels against his father's rules, joins the Arrow, a ship leaving the port of San Francisco, and sets sail for unforgettable adventures on the seas.

anchor_text: An American developer wants to redevelop the harbour district of Newcastle. The owner of the Key Club, a jazz club, resists him.
text_a: Brendan works as a doorman at a jazz club, but the business is in jeopardy due to pressure from an American mobster, who intends to buy the place.
text_b: During the performance of a bad play called The Rooster, a spectator named Yannick stands up and interrupts the show. With his imagination and his gun, he's determined to take back control of the evening.

anchor_text: After a plane crash, a young boy wakes up hanging from a parachute hanging from a tree in the middle of the wilderness. He then discovers an unknown and exotic land, but is hunted by a frightening giant humanoid. Accompanied by a tiny yellow bird, he sets off in search of a way home.
text_a: The film shows the annual migration of migratory birds. Most of the footage shows the birds in flight, with the camera carried by means of an ultralight aircraft. The film relies entirely on the fascination of the images, scientific explanations are not in the foreground. The narrator rarely speaks to the viewer. The path of the animals begins at a small European lake, with a boy wandering through the countryside, and ends a year later with the same motif.

anchor_text: The outsider and casual worker Poet works at a remote gas station. One day, he receives a visit from the rock band Hells Angels, led by Buddy. Poet joins the gang and quickly adapts to the harsh customs. In the process, he attracts the attention of Shil, who is, however, the bride of the gang leader. A deadly conflict ensues.
text_a: A man wakes up on a bench in New York's Central Park with no idea who he is or why he's there. In his pocket he finds a piece of paper with a phone number that leads him to a woman, Gloria, who tries to help him. During his search, he meets three women, each of whom reminds him of a person named Grace. Who is this woman?
text_b: Poet, a young gas station attendant decides to join a gang of motorcyclists, who drive their motorcycles past the gas station where Poet works. But their leader hides a secret: he enjoys mistreating women, proving to be violent and moody. Poet clashes with him, causing his death.

When a motorcycle gang comes roaring through town, Poet intrigued, follows them and helps the Hell's Angels in a bar fight. Buddy (Adam Roarke) one of the leaders, invites Poet to join them. Soon Poet will walk with them and live their violent and licentious lifestyle. But Poet begins to tire of this uprooted life and Buddy confronts him when he finds out they're both interested in the same woman.

anchor_text: New York demolition contractor Stewart McBain runs his own real estate company. He knows only one purpose in life - to make money so that his three children will have everything they need. The spoiled offspring trio enjoy a comfortable life in abundance and make no attempt to change that. Public outcry is preventing Stewart McBain from demolishing the architecturally valuable Dutch House in Brooklyn to make way for new construction. His adult children Daphne, Chloe, and Jimmy mock his appearance on television, which Stewart is fed up with and banishes his children from the house so they can learn to stand on their own two feet. They move into the Dutch House, which they inherit from their father. For lack of money, they rented some apartments in the dilapidated house. Tenants include fashion designer Lionel and magician Shitty. Chloe prepares a calendar for an insurance company; some of her roommates act as models. Meanwhile, Stewart's company goes bankrupt and he loses his house. His kids are taking him to the Dutch House. In the end, the family is reunited and wealthy again.
text_a: Eight-year-old Jenny has been caught in the crossfire of her parents' separation. Her mother, Maggie, is a lawyer. Her father, Jeff, is an artist. Meanwhile, as a child of divorce, Jenny is used to being on her own more often. She spends most of her time with a strange doll named Grisly Kin, based on her father's work. When her mother is killed in a car accident, the girl's world collapses. Her father brings her to Brooklyn and wants custody of Jenny. The new nanny Samantha is to serve as the female point of reference in her life, a difficult situation for Jenny. To make matters worse, her doll develops a creepy life of its own, one that only Jenny seems to be able to see.
text_b: Stewart McBain is a self-taught, successful contractor specializing in building demolition. For the future construction of a skyscraper, he has to destroy a block of houses in Brooklyn, including the Dutch House. Protesters opposed the destruction, including one of his daughters. Back home, the domineering father is appalled to see his three children, Daphne, Chloe, and Jimmy, do nothing with their lives. More like artists, they're not interested in business. Stewart McBain then decided to evict them from the family home by giving them $750 each. Daphne, Chloe and Jimmy then take refuge in the Dutch House and try to organize their new life. As Stewart McBain struggles with his career, his children will demonstrate their abilities to him.

text_a: Stewart McBain is a successful demolition expert who blows up buildings for a living. In the middle of one of these projects, a group of protesters stop the last building on a lot to be demolished, the Dutch House. When McBain appears on television to reject the protest, he is ridiculed. Returning home, his three children Daphne, Chloe and Jimmy, ridicule him. Partly because of reproaches, and partly because she wants to teach her children humility and the value of money, she throws them out of the house. After he gives them $750 each, he sends them to the Dutch House to live their lives. The house, however, is dilapidated and on the verge of collapse. To finance their lives, McBain's three sons are employed by their tenants. Among them are stylist Lionel, homeless conjurer Shitty, stockbroker Tom and aspiring occultist Shirley. Chloe's in charge of finishing a calendar for an insurance company. Lionel has completed his project for a fashion show. Chloe uses her roommate in the calendar and Lionel ends up using some of their models for his show. The story is told against the backdrop of McBain's stock market crash leading to his downfall. He desperately tries to avoid a hostile escalation of demolition and his company fails. He loses his house and becomes needy. Basically, his children take him in and he starts to see the world in a completely different light. Towards the end, McBain's children, their relatives and their friends are all evicted from the house. They come up with the idea of blowing up the building to avoid detection and to get everyone back to their previous standard of living. Throughout the film there are numerous romantic misunderstandings that are linked together at the end.
