anchor_text: Summer 1993: Arthur (Vincent Lacoste), a student in Rennes, meets Jacques (Pierre Deladonchamps), a writer who lives in Paris with his son. They like each other, they love each other and, for Jacques, you have to take advantage of their love before you die of AIDS.
text_a: Jacques Laurent, a forgotten and aging director of pornographic films, is forced to resume his work as a result of financial difficulties. He is reunited with his son Joseph, with whom he had a falling out a few years earlier when Joseph discovered what his father's films really were.
text_b: Jacques is a writer who lives in Paris. He's not even 40 yet, and he thinks the best is yet to come. Arthur is a student living in French Brittany. Lee, smile a lot and refuse to accept that there is something impossible in life. Jacques and Arthur like each other and live as if they were in a romantic dream or a sad story.

anchor_text: The film follows the career and rise of Stephen Fermoyle, a young American of humble origins. Convinced of his vocation, Fermoyle was ordained a priest in 1917. Fermoyle feels so close to the Catholic Church that his dogmatism prevents his sister Mona from marrying a Jew. Years later, when Mona falls seriously ill during her pregnancy, Stephen decides that the life of the unborn child comes first: Mona dies in agony. Tormented by guilt, Stephen leaves his priesthood and works as a language teacher in Vienna. Here he falls in love with the beautiful student Annemarie. But Stephen's calling to the priesthood ultimately proves to be stronger. Innerly matured, he entered the service of the Vatican until he returned to Vienna in 1938 in a papal mission. Stephen is supposed to help prevent National Socialist terror. But politics turns out to be much more powerful and Stephen has to stand by while Annemarie is held captive by the Gestapo. In 1939, just before the outbreak of World War II, Stephen was made a cardinal.
text_a: Catholic priest Andrews is caught at the scene of a murder with the victim's blood on him. The police will arrest him. Another priest, Daniel Clemens, visits Andrews in custody and learns that Andrews knows the identity of the killer. This cannot be revealed because Andrews feels bound by the secrecy of the confessional. A short time later, Andrews is found hanging in his cell. Clemens wants to prove Andrews was innocent. He investigates the priest's former parish, defying the will of the church hierarchy. Journalist Madeline Finney, who he dated before he became a priest, is helping him. They discover a group of homosexual Catholics who have nothing to do with the crime. Clemens comes into conflict with a woman who secretly records the confessions of her foster children; she slanders him to his superior. Clemens, who collects donations for the church organization, is assigned to visit a donor in another city. His superior tells him that he tolerates his insubordination only because Clemens is a capable fund-raiser. Since Clement refuses to travel, he is suspended as a priest; he is to leave the tabernacle. Nevertheless, he continues to search for the murderer and finds him. The perpetrator commits suicide after the alerted police appear. The film ends with the reinstated Clemens celebrating a Mass in memory of Andrews.
text_b: In 1917 Stephen Fermoyle, the son of a Boston streetcar driver, after studying in Rome became a priest; then he returned to Boston as a parish vicar. Here he sends a manuscript to his cardinal archbishop who, however, finds him ambitious and sends him to a small poor parish, where he finds the parish priest very sick. On the death of the parish priest, the archbishop hires him as secretary and takes him to Rome for the conclave as his assistant. Meanwhile in Boston she is faced with the choice of choosing the spiritual path or the human path when her pregnant sister is due to give birth and the lives of mother and daughter are in danger. He decides to save the unborn child. While in Rome he reveals to the cardinal his priestly crisis and then a period of priestly suspension is decided that he will spend in Vienna. There he becomes a language teacher, and almost falls in love with a student; nevertheless at this point he decides to follow his vocation. Returning to Rome, he was promoted to the Vatican Secretariat of State, and was entrusted with various duties, which he carried out successfully. Consecrated bishop, he witnesses the entry of the Nazis into Austria and finds the young student, arrested by the Gestapo. Returning again to Rome, he was named cardinal and archbishop of Boston.

anchor_text: The action takes place in June 1942, with the British 8th Army in retreat, after the fall of Tobruk. During the escape, the English corporal John Bramble finds refuge in a hotel, which, however, is soon filled with German officers, including Field Marshal Rommel. The English corporal manages to pass himself off as a German spy, but his real purpose is to discover the location of the Afrika Korps fuel depots. Between switches of identity, lies and threats, he succeeds in his purpose thanks to a geographical map rigged: he can thus help the allies to stop Rommel's tanks.
text_a: English soldier John Bramble (Franchot Tone), serving in a tank and surviving a battle against the Afrika Korps in the Sahara, flees north across the desert. There he finds the Empress of Britain Hotel and its owner Farid (Akim Tamiroff). The only employee of the hotel is the Frenchwoman Mouche (Anne Baxter) after the cook fled and the receptionist was killed. The hotel is going to serve as Rommel's headquarters, and Bramble decides to go into hiding by taking the identity of the Davos receptionist to save himself.
text_b: As the Afrika Korps, under the command of Field Marshal Rommel, marched towards Cairo, the British forces, unable to counter its advance, chose to resort to deception. They imagine disguising German Jews living in Palestine as enemies and sending them to the citadel of Tobruk where Rommel has stored the fuel reserves of his army. To reach Tobruk, the commando must travel five hundred miles through the desert. This is the beginning of an unusual journey, where pseudo-Nazis, in cars stolen from the Germans, cross the enemy lines escorting unarmed British supposed to be their prisoners. During the journey, the group maneuvered in such a way that a column of German and Italian tanks, believing each other to be attacked by the British, engaged in battle. The commandos are using this as an opportunity to get through without being spotted...

anchor_text: After years of research, Dr. Peter Elliot has managed to teach gorilla lady Amy to speak using a form of sign language and a computerized evaluation process. However, the animal suffers from stress symptoms that cannot be alleviated by occupational therapy (playing, painting). So Peter and his assistant Richard decide to take the monkey back to its home in the Congo. Transportation costs are immediately covered by the shady businessman Herkermer Homolka and communications scientist Dr. Karen Ross. During the trip, the real motives for this generosity are revealed: Herkermer has recognized a symbol on one of Amy's paintings that he associates with a legendary diamond mine of King Solomon. Karen's motives are similar, but she is also searching for missing colleagues who have been in the area before. Led by Captain Munro, they find the diamond mines in the Congolese jungle, in the lost city of Zinj. The question of why the mines were eventually abandoned is quickly settled: the builders bred a particularly intelligent and aggressive breed of gorilla for their defense. So aggressive that over time, the animals have driven out the people they were meant to protect. What follows is a spectacular battle between humans and gorillas, the former barely able to decide for themselves thanks to a volcano erupting at the last moment. Eventually, Amy joins another group of gorillas, and a small band of survivors makes their way home.
text_a: In Africa in 1885, gold prospector Patrick O'Brien and his daughter Kathleen discovered a diamond that turned out to be nothing more than a pebble. They decide to go to the coast because they can look for ivory there. At the same time, they meet an adventurer named Allan Quatermain who is going in convoy to look for white hunters for an African safari. Thanks to Kathleen, he agrees to take them with him. While camping, Kathleen spotted a wagon on the other side of the river. Once there, they discover two people, Umbopa the African and Sylvestra who dies moments after revealing to them the existence of a map showing King Solomon's diamond mine. O'Brien finds the card and announces to his daughter that he is going to get the diamonds, he makes a copy of it before returning it to Quatermain. That same evening, he told her that he would go alone to look for them and asked her to look after his daughter. Quatermain, unable to dissuade him from leaving, agrees to protect Kathleen.
text_b: In East Africa, researchers working for satellite company TraviCom are attacked by mysterious attackers after discovering diamonds in the ruins of a lost city. Director Travis then sends his best man, Karen Ross, to retrieve the stones. At the same time, Professor Peter Elliot is conducting research on Amy, a female gorilla who communicates through sign language. Thanks to modern technology, the animal's gestures are translated by a vocal apparatus, allowing it to converse with humans. But for some time now, Amy's been wanting to get back to nature. Peter and his friend Richard decide to take her back to her home region in the heart of the Virunga Mountains. A Romanian philanthropist, Herkermer Homolka, offers to finance and accompany them. In reality, he wants to take advantage of the trip to find the legendary city of Zinj, home to a diamond mine of extraordinary purity. Karen Ross joins them by paying for fuel at the airport. Arriving in Africa, they pass through the civil war thanks to their guide Monroe, also known as an arms dealer. Parachuting through the jungle, they will have to face many dangers - snakes, leeches, hippos, torrential rains and indigenous people. To finally discover the mines of Zinj, guarded by a new species of killer gorilla. These animals were once bred by the people of the city to protect the diamonds, forming an elite, incorruptible guard. After a bloody battle, Monroe, Karen and Peter, the only survivors, will have to face the ultimate danger: a spectacular volcanic eruption that engulfs the city forever. The group rejoins civilization in a hot air balloon, while Amy remains in the jungle with a group of mountain gorillas...

anchor_text: Carl Behrend and Pauli Arndt just got married. He's the son of a businessman, she's the daughter of a teacher. In 1917, as World War I raged in Europe, Carl was called to the front, and life became difficult for those who remained behind. Pauli feels helpless, especially since her father has been dismissed for his pacifist views...
text_a: The film is set in and around Berlin during the period of inflation following World War I. The film focuses on a Polish refugee family that tirelessly fights against all the adversities of the times: you see the family members at work, standing in line for a pound of meat for 12 trillion marks, growing potatoes, eating the meagre rations and finally building houses to counteract the cramped housing conditions. And in the meantime, the loving couple, consisting of the one son and the foster daughter, happily walks there again and again.
text_b: In Austria, Carl Behrend marries Pauli Arndt, the daughter of a pacifist professor, and when World War I breaks out, the young man is drafted, leaving for the war. Pauli's father is fired for his anti-war views and the family goes hungry. Despite Pauli's attempts to save his child by turning to prostitution, the child dies of starvation. The news comes that Carl is also dead but, at the end of the war, the man returns from the war while Professor Arndt is reinstated in his job. The unfortunate family begins to live again, reunited.

anchor_text: Constance, an unemployed real estate agent in Paris, returns to her hometown to try to get a job at the agency where she started her career. Although Constance is supported by a former colleague, a young competitor, Audrey, is given the position to fill in her place. From then on, considering herself unfairly treated and deserving of the job, Constance will do anything to dismiss Audrey and get her job back.
text_a: After being fired from her job, Constance faces financial problems. With no other choice, and upon learning that the small real estate agency where she started her career has a vacancy, she decides to call her former boss. To her surprise, he tells her that he has already found someone, but she is convinced that she will finally get the job because of her experience.
text_b: Two clumsy and simple-minded American friends, Audrey and Morgan, find themselves embroiled in an international intrigue after Audrey discovers that her ex-husband was a CIA agent who, before dying, asks her to bring to Vienna an object contested by secret services and terrorist movements around the world. Morgan's experiences as a gymnast and Audrey's practice of video games, in addition to a brazen fortune, allow the girls to cross the whole of Europe almost unscathed and complete the mission, not without cruelly slaughtering (and often unknowingly) the dozens of militarily trained and armed spies to the teeth who try uselessly to hinder them.

anchor_text: Rick, a young worker, and Lissa, a veterinary assistant, are in love. It's just that they can't stand living in the depressing atmosphere of their town, Tropico, and they don't know how to leave. Looking for money, Nick is involved in a robbery. But what appears to be a simple crime soon turns into a disaster.
text_a: Nick Wild is a former Marine. He's reverted to the close protection of wealthy clients. He's a debt-ridden gambler who plans to leave Las Vegas for a better life. But when his ex-girlfriend Holly is found beaten and left for dead, he agrees to help her. He discovers the culprit is powerful mobster Danny DeMarco.
text_b: A young man named Nick has just gotten himself into a huge mess: A cocaine dealer demands that he pay off a debt - a robbery, really - worth $15,000 before the weekend. Looking for a way to pay, Nick and his girlfriend encounter an old friend, who soon becomes a pawn and accomplice in a sordid scheme.

anchor_text: In 1979 Paris, gay pornographic film producer Anne is abandoned by her partner and editor Lois. To win her back, he decides to finance a much more ambitious film, with the help of his trusted assistant, the exuberant Archibald. During the filming, however, a mysterious assassin kills one of the actors. However, Anne decides to continue filming. After a very short time another actor of the cast is brutally killed and the atmosphere among the actors is of deep concern. Anne takes the opportunity to adapt the content of the film to the new climate of the set, while the killer continues to sow terror.
text_a: Paris in the late 1990's. On leaving her home, Anne, a young actress, meets Jean, the brother of her friend, Georges. The latter, a photojournalist, is often absent. His younger son had no desire to take over the family farm and came, on a whim, to the capital. Jean throws a used wrapper into the hands of a beggar sitting on the corner of the street. While the woman is puzzled by this gesture, a passerby, Amadou, calls the young man to apologize. The discussion quickly escalated to the point where the police intervened.
text_b: Paris, the summer of 1979. Anne, a producer of cheap gay porn, is a violent woman, a victim of alcohol and her own demons. When Lois, her publisher and partner, abandons her after years of relationship, Anne is devastated. Desperate and determined to win her back, she makes a much more ambitious film. But a mysterious masked killer is lurking in his surroundings and this will alter his life.

anchor_text: Paris in the '79s. The porn producer Anne Parèze has not yet put an end to her relationship with the film editor Loïs, and hopes that they will get back together. To win her back, she decides to make her most ambitious film yet. Gay porn is supposed to be so bad that Lois has no choice but to go back to her. She is supported in her endeavor by her extravagant best friend Archibald. A masked killer seems to be targeting the cast of your low-budget production exclusively. During intercourse, he kills them with a black dildo in which a spring knife is hidden. The man starts with Anne's newest star. Since the police don't want to help Anne, she decides to take matters into her own hands. In Paris at night, Anne goes looking in the clubs.
text_a: In Paris, at the end of the 1970s, Anne Parèze was a producer of homosexual pornographic films. But to regain the favor of her partner of 10 years, her younger sister and editor Lois, Anne will decide to change the register by embarking on the financing of a much more ambitious film that will be directed by her long-time friend, the flamboyant Archibald. Her plans, however, are disrupted by a masked serial killer who first attacks the young Karl, an actor who shot a lot for Anne before attacking most of the film's protagonists. Anne is then drawn into a strange investigation that will turn her life upside down.
text_b: Paris in the late 1990's. On leaving her home, Anne, a young actress, meets Jean, the brother of her friend, Georges. The latter, a photojournalist, is often absent. His younger son had no desire to take over the family farm and came, on a whim, to the capital. Jean throws a used wrapper into the hands of a beggar sitting on the corner of the street. While the woman is puzzled by this gesture, a passerby, Amadou, calls the young man to apologize. The discussion quickly escalated to the point where the police intervened.

anchor_text: Raj, a young man, is charged with the attempted murder of Judge Raghunath. He is defended by the young lawyer Rita, who asks Raghunath about his past: 24 years ago, Raghunath disowned his wife. At that time, she was kidnapped by the gang leader Jagga for revenge, because Jagga of Raghunath was once put behind bars and thus branded as a rapist. And this only for one reason: Jagga's ancestors were gangsters and in Raghunath's eyes a son of a good man is also a good man as well as a child of a villain will always remain a bad man and this made Jagga guilty. Jagga released Leela again, but when it turned out that Leela was pregnant, the rumor spread that the child was not Raghunath's. Even Raghunath was soon convinced that Jagga had raped his wife. Now, when Leela was rejected by her husband, she gave birth to her son Raj in the gutter. Jagga made Raj a thief because Raj trusted him as a father figure. But this becomes his undoing, as he loses his childhood friend Rita, is thrown out of school, and ends up in juvenile detention. After being freed as a young man, Raj reconnects with the now-adult Rita, who has been living with Raghunath since her father's death. Also, Raj realizes that Jagga is responsible for all of his mother's suffering and kills him as a result. Only in the attempt to murder his own father Raghunath does he fail. Raj goes to jail for his crime, and Rita promises to wait for him.
text_a: Judge Raghunath, mistakenly believing that his wife is expecting a child from the outlaw Jagga, hunts the house. The woman is forced to raise the child in conditions of poverty and social discomfort that lead him to take the path of crime. Raj's encounter with Rita, a young lawyer, puts him in front of the chance to change his life and rehabilitate himself. The main theme of the film revolves around the dilemma of whether being evil is a hereditary trait or the result of a series of injustices and exclusions by society. There are many elements that made this film one of the classics of Hindi-language cinema of the 1950s, first of all the theme that challenges a certain type of mentality then very widespread, according to which children inherit the good or bad propensities of the fathers. Another element is the success achieved abroad, particularly in the Soviet Union. In some countries the song Awaara hoon ("I am a vagabond"), has become a kind of unofficial Indian national anthem. There were innovations in scenography, very sumptuous and elaborate, which helped influence the rich settings of many later Hindi films. In this film came to life the character of the tramp, inspired by Charlot, who finds full development in the film Shree 420 and who would be another recurring element in some films of Raj Kapoor.
text_b: Veera, a young and very wealthy heiress, is accidentally kidnapped in Delhi a few days after her wedding. Faced with the problem posed by his capture and the notoriety of his family, his kidnapper Mahabir is excluded from the gang and tasked with solving the problem. He's considering asking for a ransom or abandoning her in a country brothel. With the help of an accomplice, they fled first to Rajasthan. Veera, at first very hurt by her abduction, eventually finds in it some of her aspirations to flee her environment. Especially since Mahabir, under a rough exterior, reveals some flaws in his personal life. In search of a solution to the situation, and to escape the pursuit of which they are the object, he decides to head for Kashmir, while the road brings him closer to Veera, in an almost hopeless relationship. Arriving in the mountains, Veera discovers the environment, and the house, of her dreams. But the law enforcement officers show up and shoot Mahabir. Once reunited with her family, Veera will not be able to bring herself to fit into the social mold.

anchor_text: Jim Slade is a soldier and one of Jehovah's Witnesses, so he refuses to take up arms. He was sentenced to two years of hard labor. When he returns to his hometown of Tucson after being pardoned and dishonorably discharged, he finds his parents murdered. Thanks to his friend Cassidy, he learns the identities of the four bandits responsible: Jeff Logan, the Butcher brothers, and the Texan Corbett. Jim quickly learns to use weapons and goes after criminals. Corbett tells him that Slade has moved to Gulberstone, where he works with the lay preacher Douglas. Corbett himself prepares an attack on the town's bank, which is waiting for monthly funds, and for this reason has prepared ambushes on every road leading to the bank; Slade nevertheless manages to get out of town and organize the fight against the bandits, while in a nearby asylum for the insane, some inmates escape following a catastrophic fire and now bring further misfortune to the town. The Texan, the only survivor of the gang, reveals to Slade that Douglas, previously unsuspected, is the financier of the gangsters. Jim manages to catch the false priest in a cemetery, where one of the crazies has also cut himself, and defeats him in a duel.
text_a: A gang of criminals, led by Sam Clayton, rule the small town of Juno City, Texas. The sheriff and the local judge suffer the indignities of the bandits without intervening. The only one who opposes the outlaws is Father John, who is killed for it. Johnny, having lost his voice due to the trauma of his father John's death, travels to Mexico, to Pueblo Quemada, to seek help from Lewis, brother of the murdered priest and former gunfighter known as Diamante Lobo. The man, taking advantage of the resemblance to his brother, returns with the boy to Juno City and puts on the priest's clothes. The bandits, terrified and convinced that the priest has returned to life, end up killing each other. Eventually Lewis finds himself up against Sam Clayton, who turns out to be Johnny's father, and is forced to kill him in front of the boy and his mother.
text_b: Jim Slade (Peter Lee Lawrence), a Northern soldier, is sentenced to hard labor for refusing to fight at the front. Once the civil war is over, Jim is pardoned, and upon returning to his farm finds that his entire family has been murdered. This situation leaves him distraught and vows to put an end to the culprits. One by one he kills them, but he still has to pay off the debt with the head of the gang, Corbett (Piero Lulli). In his search, he comes to Galverston, a town threatened by a gang of outlaws who want to rob the bank. Corbett happens to be the boss. However in a showdown to the death at the hands of Slade himself, Corbett confesses to him that there was another man who ended his family, and his name is Douglas (John Ireland). Finding this one, they face each other in a mythical duel in a graveyard.

anchor_text: The film deals with the rivalry between mobsters Frank Costello and Vito Genovese of the American Cosa Nostra from New York City. During the 1950s, power-hungry Vito tries by all means to take over the position of his boss Frank, who is currently the head of the powerful New York Luciano family, but one day will be known everywhere as the Genovese family.
text_a: The film traces the fragmented biography of the mythical figure of the Italian-American mafia, Salvatore Lucania, aka Lucky Luciano. After the Castellammarese War and the elimination of about forty opponents, he became the undisputed capo di tutti capi of the Commission in 1931. He was deported from the United States in 1946 after his release from prison. From Naples, Italy, he set up a huge worldwide heroin trade. The film ends with his death at Naples airport in 1962.
text_b: In the 1950s, Vito Genovese and Frank Costello are two leaders of the Italian-American Mafia in New York, whose rivalry reaches a climax in 1957 when the former orders the assassination of the latter. But the killer Vincent Gigante only manages to wound Costello. The latter will then prefer to withdraw from its activities. Vito Genovese will then take control of the Luciano family which will become the Genovese family.

anchor_text: The film deals with the rivalry between mobsters Frank Costello and Vito Genovese of the American Cosa Nostra from New York City. During the 1950s, power-hungry Vito tries by all means to take over the position of his boss Frank, who is currently the head of the powerful New York Luciano family, but one day will be known everywhere as the Genovese family.
text_a: Vito Genovese and Frank Costello are rival Italian-American mob bosses, and Genovese orders a coup against Costello. Costello survives but is injured in the attempt and eventually decides to retire from the Mafia.[1]
text_b: The film traces the fragmented biography of the mythical figure of the Italian-American mafia, Salvatore Lucania, aka Lucky Luciano. After the Castellammarese War and the elimination of about forty opponents, he became the undisputed capo di tutti capi of the Commission in 1931. He was deported from the United States in 1946 after his release from prison. From Naples, Italy, he set up a huge worldwide heroin trade. The film ends with his death at Naples airport in 1962.

anchor_text: During World War II, seven people from different backgrounds are trapped in a cave that serves as a warehouse. Despite their differences and contrasts, they must live together and join forces to find a way out into the open.
text_a: During an underground exploration in the Esa'ala Cave in Papua New Guinea, a group of five people are trapped in a cave during a tropical cyclone. With the water rising and the air depleting, their only hope is to travel through unexplored underwater caves following the course of the river that flows into the ocean...
text_b: World War II, 7 people from different backgrounds are trapped in a cave used as a warehouse. Despite their differences and contrasts, they will have to live together and form alliances in order to find a way out into the open air.

anchor_text: In the state of Florida, a gang of teenagers do whatever they can between surfing, drugs, and sex. After years of constant humiliation and abuse at the hands of his best friend Bobby Kent, young surfer Marty Puccio decides to end his life. With the support of his group of friends, all equally victims of Kent's tyranny, he will carry out his assassination. The place is chosen from the start: a symbolic swamp where the band spent its best moments.
text_a: Marty Puccio (Renfro) and his girlfriend Lisa (Miner) plan the murder of their friend Bobby Kent (Stahl) as revenge for the continuous abuse and mistreatment he causes them, especially Marty. They are joined by other friends, such as Donny (Pitt), Heather (Garner), and Ali (Phillips), who helps them by making Bobby believe that he wants to have a relationship with him. For the crime they have the help of Derek Kaufman (Fitzpatrick), a member of a small gang and who thinks he knows how to carry out the plan.
text_b: While riding his bicycle to school, Jason Shepherd crashes into the luxurious limousine of movie producer Marty Wolf. The driver stops the vehicle to check if Jason is injured. Seeing that he had nothing but his bike in a dirty state, he drove it to his school. But as he gets out of the car, he forgets a paper he's just written. Marty appropriates it and decides to adapt it, without Jason's consent, for the big screen. The latter learns of this information on television and then goes to Hollywood with his friend Kaylee to solve the case. Upon seeing the teenager enter his office, Marty calls his security guards, who promptly kick him out. But Jason's not gonna fall for it. He's a lot smarter than he looks.

anchor_text: The film centers on the ambiguous and destructive relationship of childhood friends Bobby and Marty, the latter perpetually bullied and humiliated by Bobby who exercises total control over him; together they spend their days pulling pranks and vandalism, abusing drugs and financing their crimes with amateur porn films and strippers in gay clubs. But when Marty falls in love with Lisa something breaks and Bobby loses his sadistic control over Marty and the change of rules within the group leads to murder. Everyone will then accuse the one who spoke, so they go to court where everyone threatens each other.
text_a: Marty Puccio (Renfro) and his girlfriend Lisa (Miner) plan the murder of their friend Bobby Kent (Stahl) as revenge for the continuous abuse and mistreatment he causes them, especially Marty. They are joined by other friends, such as Donny (Pitt), Heather (Garner), and Ali (Phillips), who helps them by making Bobby believe that he wants to have a relationship with him. For the crime they have the help of Derek Kaufman (Fitzpatrick), a member of a small gang and who thinks he knows how to carry out the plan.
text_b: The film tells the story of Jason Shepherd, a boy who tells a lot of lies to everyone, his family and even at school. As punishment, he is assigned a homework assignment on anything, and Jason slowly becomes passionate about writing a very long story. The next day he's about to be late for school but asks for a ride to the well-known producer Marty Wolf who agrees to accompany him. Jason forgets the theme in the car and Marty reads it and realizes that the theme is a fantastic story that could become a Hollywood movie, so he steals it by denying Jason credit. The boy will do anything to convince his parents that he's the author of this story, that it's about to become a Hollywood movie. Jason, along with his best friend Kaylee, then lands in Hollywood, to get Marty Wolf to confess that, in fact, he is the author of the story. However, the producer tries to get rid of the boy, but Jason does not give up and together with the people who are snubbed and ridiculed by Wolf will do everything to make him pay.

anchor_text: In the year 2525, humanity has colonized numerous other planets and is engaged in a civil war. Cadet Thomas Lasky, along with other cadets, attends the Corbulo Military Academy, named after Gnaeus Domitius Corbulo, on another planet, to be trained as an officer. His brother had already died as a soldier in the Civil War and Lasky himself sees little point in this war, which also affects his achievements at the Academy. He also has strong feelings for Chyler Silva. The day before Lasky could leave the academy due to health problems, the academy is attacked. To everyone's surprise, it turns out that the attackers are not human rebels, but the Alliance, a religious alliance of various extraterrestrial species whose existence was previously kept secret from the masses. The Alliance is waging a holy war against humanity with the goal of exterminating it. The entire academy is overrun by the Alliance, and only a few of the cadets, including Lasky and Silva, can hide. These are rescued by the Master Chief, a super soldier, and taken to an evacuation point. On the way there, Silva dies in a fight. On their way off the planet, the cadets watch as the Alliance begins to bombard the planet's surface with their spacecraft.
text_a: As Commander Thomas Lasky listens to Cortana's distress call, he recalls the circumstances of his first encounter with Major Spartan 117. On the planet Circunius-IV, at the Corbulo Military Academy of the United Nations Space Command (UNSC), which trains Marines and ODSTs (Helljumpers in English), a group of young cadets from good families are training to take over the war against the Insurgents from the Outer Colonies. Among them, Thomas Lasky must face his doubts about this war and his own abilities, as he is pressed for a great military career. As Lasky gradually asserted himself, his world was suddenly set on fire and blood by the intrusion of a new threat: the Covenants, a terrifying alien alliance determined to exterminate humanity...
text_b: Professor Planetarius is building a spacecraft, the Excelsior, destined to reach the planet Mars. It is the son of the professor, Captain Avanti Planetaros who commands the expedition. Taking off from Copenhagen, the spacecraft, after a long journey complicated by a case of alcohol abuse and a mutiny of the crew, arrives on the Red Planet. The atmosphere of Mars is breathable, so astronauts can travel without a spacesuit outside the spacecraft. Afraid of encountering aggressive Martians, the crew is heavily armed, but is greeted by pacifist, vegetarian and anti-alcoholic Martians. Avanti wants to show the Martians how animals are hunted on Earth and killed, a bird with a gun. Confusion ensues as one of the crew members throws a large one that injures one of the Martians. The earthlings are controlled and invited to judge for themselves. Avanti recognizes his mistake and falls madly in love with Marya, the beautiful blonde daughter of the high priest of Mars. Her love is reciprocated and they both decide to get married and return with the crew to Earth to spread the Martians' message of peace.

anchor_text: In the near future, Frank Warren - a safecracker - is arrested following a botched robbery. He's being double-crossed and betrayed by his two accomplices, Sam and Noelle. Sentenced to 12 years in prison, he was immediately incarcerated at Camp Holliday, a revolutionary prison where inmates are presented with a brand-new system designed to discourage prisoners from attempting to escape. This system works in pairs: each person is electronically connected to another member of the prison without them knowing who is connected to whom. Due to incongruous circumstances, Warren manages to find his twin and escapes in the company of the latter, Tracy Riggs. It's time for him to get his hands on his magot again, while trying to escape from Sam and Noelle.
text_a: Life in prison Frank Perry now lives with resignation, knowing that he has to serve his sentence day by day, but when an unexpected letter informs him of the serious health condition of his beloved daughter, Frank decides that he must find her at all costs, so he contemplates escaping from prison. But the not-so-young Frank is unable to organize the escape on his own, so he assembles a diverse group of inmates. During the preparation of the escape plan, young James Lacey arrives at the prison and becomes Frank's new cellmate. The young man soon becomes the victim of the special attention of the drug addict Tony, Rizza's brother, the undisputed leader of that prison arm. The film unfolds through two parallel narratives, recounting the events leading up to the escape and the escape itself.
text_b: After stealing diamonds in a robbery, diamond thief Frank Warren is betrayed by his best friend Sam and his girlfriend Noelle, who reports him to the authorities. Frank is sentenced to serve 12 years in Camp Holliday, an experimental prison where each inmate is given an electronic collar containing an explosive device that is electronically connected to another inmate. If a prisoner attempts to escape from Camp Holliday and is separated from the companion to whom it is connected by more than 100 meters, both collars will explode. Frank learns that the inmate he is connected to is Tracy Riggs, who convinces him to run away with her. Tracy and Frank both manage to escape. On the run from the authorities, the two are pursued by Sam and Noelle, former members of Warren's gang, believing that Frank will lead them to the diamonds he has hidden.

anchor_text: Frank Warren participates in a robbery in which diamonds worth several million dollars are looted. His accomplices Sam and Noelle betray him, Frank goes to jail. First, though, he manages to bring the prey to safety. In prison, every inmate wears an electronic collar. The straps are paired electronically; if a prisoner is more than 100 yards away from his partner, both straps explode, killing both carriers. It's kept secret who's paired with who. Sam and Noelle want to get their hands on the diamonds, and they're working with the warden, Warden Holliday. Prisoner Tracy Riggs accepts Holliday's offer to help them. She was wrongfully convicted and sees this as an opportunity to escape a long-term prison sentence. Holliday facilitates Warren and Riggs, whose collars are coupled, escape from prison together. In the showdown, Warren outwits Holliday and Noelle. He gives Holliday one of two suitcases containing money from the sale of the captured diamonds, as well as explosives from one of the now-damaged necklaces. He gives the second part of the explosive to his former partner, along with the car keys, hidden in a keychain. When she finally triggers a remote trigger in the belief that it will kill Warren and Riggs, she detonates Holliday, aboard a helicopter, and blows herself up. Warren and Riggs remains the second suitcase with well over $12 million in contents. They decide to stay together.

anchor_text: Cesare Guzmán is the owner of a factory. His wife is killed by bandits. He decides to take revenge, with the help of his former classmates.
text_a: A young and courageous postman from Cádiz (José Suárez) manages to unmask after countless misadventures a gang of outlaws commanded by a high official (Barta Barry).
text_b: Cesare Guzmán, a farm owner whose wife is killed by bandits, decides to take revenge with the help of his two companions.

anchor_text: In Bertolt Brecht's one-act play "The Petty-bourgeois Wedding", the playful idyll of the petty bourgeoisie is comically put on display. The father of the bride persists in trying to tell different anecdotes from the past. The bride proudly tells the guests about her groom's homemade furniture, as she does not want her father to tell embarrassing and inappropriate anecdotes. More and more guests are leaving the wedding as the whole celebration is spiraling out of control. The wedding dinner is ruined and the furniture is destroyed. It starts with a broken table leg, goes through a ruined couch, to a finally collapsing bed in which the couple find themselves at the end of the ceremony. Brecht built into the play a short dialogue in which the protagonists exchange disparaging remarks about his own play Baal, apparently with the cunning intention of arousing the audience's interest in his drama. The wedding, which should be the most beautiful day in the life of a bride, becomes a farce, destroying not only all the furniture, but also the good reputation of the bride and groom, especially that of the pregnant bride, and the seemingly healthy world of the upper middle class.
text_a: The bride's father is constantly trying to tell different stories, the bride is proud that the groom made all the furniture, the groom is jealous of his friend. The woman full of malice who seeks and rejoices in the prejudice of others (the pregnancy of the bride, the disaster of the furniture (which is broken throughout the work), the unhappy husband... a general conflict that ends in the greatest of disasters. The newlyweds' discussion and reconciliation laughing at the sloppiness of the celebration that ends with the collapse of the bed. In the play there is a brief dialogue, with derogatory comments, about another Brecht play, Baal, apparently with the intention of arousing the viewer's interest in it.
text_b: Wedding morning on a Swedish farm. The daughter Hildur marries the widowed butcher Westlund, but is expecting a child from the day laborer Martin. Hildur's sister Irma struggles with her fate, which forces her to live with an illegitimate son on the seedy farm. Your brother Rudolf is no more than the servant Soren. The father lives with a mental illness on the top floor of the house, the mother expects every day that she will have to move to the poorhouse. Westlund gets drunk with his rival Simon, who also desires the bride. His daughter Siri, a playful girl, longs for a gentle, understanding husband. No one's happy on this wedding morning. At the wedding reception, alcohol is eagerly consumed. Burlesque pranks alternate with cynical arguments and self-incrimination, which are repeatedly washed away with alcohol. It's not until the announcement of Martin's death that the slightly macabre celebration comes to an end. While Westlund lies drunk on the kitchen floor, Simon climbs into bed with the bride.

anchor_text: Georges Fauré is a French musician who is offered a job in the United States and Brontë Parrish is a horticulturist. Initially the two marry out of sheer convenience, as Georges needs the green card to be able to start his work, while Brontë desires an apartment (with a greenhouse) that can only be rented to married couples. Moving into their new home, the two begin to act like a married couple in order to deceive immigration inspectors, who are investigating the legitimacy of their marriage. So, from fiction to reality, they begin to fall in love, until the final twist.
text_a: French citizen Georges Fauré (Gérard Depardieu) has been offered a job in the United States, which means for him to start a new life. However, you need a 'green card', a document that allows a foreigner to live and work in the country. The quickest way to get it is by marrying an American citizen. On the other hand, Brönte Parrish (Andie MacDowell) is excited about renting an apartment, but it's only available to a married couple. The marriage of convenience is served.
text_b: Georges Laurent, a journalist, hosts a literary program on a television channel. He lives peacefully in a house in Paris. That peace was shattered the day he and his wife, Anne, received their first anonymous video tape in their home. Their house is filmed in a fixed frame from the street opposite, the Rue des Iris; their family is observed in an anonymous and deliberately disturbing way. Other tapes and bloody drawings were sent to them. One video showed the farm where George spent his childhood, another showed a building in Romainville and the corridor leading to an apartment. Since the police do not want to investigate until Georges and his family are attacked, he decides to find out who sends him tapes intended to impress, in the absence of any claim.

anchor_text: Brontë (Andie MacDowell) is a young New Yorker with a passion for ecology, herbalism and plants. She dreams of renting a penthouse with a beautiful winter garden overlooking Manhattan. However, the building trustee only agrees to rent the apartment to a married couple. She then appeals to her friend Anton so that she can contract a white marriage with a friend of his, Georges Fauré (Gérard Depardieu). The latter is a Frenchman who has just immigrated to New York and needs to marry an American citizen in order to obtain a work permit, a green card. So the wedding is celebrated and Brontë gets the apartment. But soon the immigration services are conducting an investigation to ascertain the sincerity of the marriage. The two "newlyweds", who are opposite in every way, will have to live together for a few days and prepare together for the interview of sincerity of the immigration inspectors.
text_a: Georges Fauré is a French musician who is offered a job in the United States and Brontë Parrish is a horticulturist. Initially the two marry out of sheer convenience, as Georges needs the green card to be able to start his work, while Brontë desires an apartment (with a greenhouse) that can only be rented to married couples. anchor_text: Countess Elisabeth discovers through an accident that her servant's blood can bring her eternal youth. She orders that the young woman be murdered, and drained of her blood so that she can soak in it. Now the countess has found the face of her twenties. But this bloody act will not last forever: he will have to murder more innocent victims to continue appearing in his youthful and beautiful form... This film is inspired by the Hungarian Countess Erzsebet Báthory (1560-1614), accused of murdering young girls.
text_a: Countess Zaleska (Gloria Holden) steals her father's corpse, performs an exorcism, and incinerates him in order to rid herself of his influence. But he can't make his vampire tendencies go away. Van Helsing (Edward Van Sloan), meanwhile, is accused of the death of Count Dracula and seeks help from his friend Jeffrey (Otto Kuger), who was his student. Everything will be complicated when Jeffrey meets the Countess at a party and she becomes possessed by him.
text_b: In 18th century Hungary, Countess Elisabeth Nádasdy (Ingrid Pitt) discovers that her youthful appearance and libido can be temporarily restored by bathing in the blood of young women. She and her lover, Captain Dobi (Nigel Green), enlist to help with the kidnapping and murder of countless women, while she carried out other sexual conquests with Lieutenant Imre Toth (Sandor Elès). To cover up his crimes, he uses his rejuvenated state to take on the identity of his own daughter; a plan that spirals out of control when his daughter, Ilona (Lesley-Anne Down), returns home.

anchor_text: The film is inspired by the historical character of Erzsébet Báthory and draws inspiration from the novel biography Erzsébet Báthory the Bloody Countess, published in 1962 by Valentine Penrose; it has nothing to do with Dracula, despite the original title is Countess Dracula, a metaphor used to indicate the bloody sadism of Countess Bathory (who was not related to Vlad III of Wallachia, a character who inspired the myth of Dracula, as of Hungarian descent and not Romanian like the latter). Countess Elisabeth, a middle-aged widow, had been a beautiful woman and refused to grow old. One day, in a fit of rage, hitting a waitress, the girl's blood splashes on his face making it young, soft and silky. Since then, the bloodthirsty countess has been posing as her own daughter but constantly needs the blood of virgin women to maintain her youthful appearance.
text_a: One night in 1876. Count Dracula comes to a girls' boarding school and takes Barbara, a young Austrian countess, to his castle. The mob arrives and neutralizes Dracula but it's too late, the girl is dead. We'll bury him in a sealed casket. A hundred years later, the coffin is unearthed during excavation work by an excavator, the seal is broken. However, before the driver informs his superiors, he is kidnapped by petty crooks and sold back to an antique dealer. In the middle of the night, Barbara wakes up and stumbles upon the antique dealer. As soon as she sucks his blood, she becomes a full-grown woman. Barbara quickly adapts to the contemporary world and finds a job in thanatopraxia in Vienna, which allows her to feed on blood. But during the carnival, she assaults her manager's mistress and gets fired. Now looking for blood, she roams the streets and gets arrested by the police. It is entrusted to the Commissioner and an inspector subordinate to him. The commissioner falls in love with Barbara as soon as they meet. After a first intimate date, he soon discovers his true nature and a fight ensues. When the sun rises, she runs for refuge to a secret room in her apartment where she puts her coffin. The Commissioner follows her unconsciously and is locked in the closing coffin. When the detective, who has discovered the identity of the vampire, rushes into the apartment and opens the coffin, he remarks helplessly: "Now we will probably never know if we should bother!"
text_b: In 18th century Hungary, Countess Elisabeth Nádasdy (Ingrid Pitt) discovers that her youthful appearance and libido can be temporarily restored by bathing in the blood of young women. She and her lover, Captain Dobi (Nigel Green), enlist to help with the kidnapping and murder of countless women, while she carried out other sexual conquests with Lieutenant Imre Toth (Sandor Elès). To cover up his crimes, he uses his rejuvenated state to take on the identity of his own daughter; a plan that spirals out of control when his daughter, Ilona (Lesley-Anne Down), returns home.

anchor_text: Bread and Chocolate tells the story of an Italian migrant in Switzerland, which is, according to the imagination of the emigrants, a rich and hospitable country, where it is possible to make a fortune because there is plenty of work for everyone. But the reality is different: immigrants find work but it is almost all hard work where competition is fierce among the different nationalities. Nino Garofalo has a decent job as a waiter with a fixed-term contract. Denounced after being caught urinating in the open, he loses his residence permit and is forced to live in hiding. He is accommodated first by Elena, a Greek woman, and then meets a compatriot, an industrialist who is a refugee in Switzerland because of tax problems. He cares for him but, on the verge of bankruptcy, he spends his last savings and commits suicide. All that's left for Nino is to join a group of illegal Italians who are sleeping in a chicken coop with the chickens they have to kill and pluck to survive. Amazed by the sight of a group of clean, blonde Swiss youths, he decided to dye his hair and get out of this foolish state by blending in with them. Arriving at a bar where a football match between the Swedish team and the Squadra Azzurra, during the 1970 World Cup, was being broadcast on TV, he was nevertheless noticed for his patriotism and was arrested as a stowaway by the police who ordered him to leave the country. Nino gets on the train, but once he's in the middle of his countrymen, he changes his mind, triggers an alarm, and descends from the carriage into a tunnel.
text_a: The story is about a candy factory salesman with a stable, albeit boring, family life in a large city in northern Italy. While traveling on a train bound for southern Italy on company business, a young woman's voice surprises him as she is ejected from the train by the ticket inspector. The agent takes pity on her and helps her stay on the train. She just got dumped by her boyfriend when she got pregnant and now she's going back to the family farm. The young woman begs the agent to come home with her and pretend to be her husband, terrified of the reaction her parents might have upon discovering that she will be a single mother. The deception only has to last a couple of days, after which she can go back to her normal life and work and she can claim to have been abandoned. The agent decides that taking a couple of days off work is a small price to pay to save the girl's honor for the rest of her life and gets off the train with her. Upon arriving at the farm, the agent struggles to maintain the lie, but in an impassioned speech he convinces the girl's father to let her stay at home. Afterwards, he returns to his wife and family without mentioning the incident.
text_b: Like many other southern Europeans in the 1960s, Nino Garofalo is a "gastarbeiter", an Italian immigrant who works as a waiter in Switzerland. Absurdly, he loses his work permit when he is caught urinating on the street, so he begins to lead a clandestine life in Switzerland. At first, he is supported by Elena, a Greek political refugee. Then he befriends an Italian industrialist, moved to Switzerland for financial problems. But the businessman commits suicide and squanders his meager savings. Nino is forced to find refuge with a group of Neapolitans who live in a kind of chicken coop. After seeing a group of young Swiss blondes bathing in the river, she decides to dye her hair and go to a local. But in a bar, while broadcasting a match of the national football team of Italy Nino is discovered when Fabio Capello scores a goal. Surprisingly, he is arrested and taken to a station for deportation. There he is reunited with Elena, who wants to give him a renewed leave, but he refuses. She boarded a train and found herself in a cabin full of returning Italian "gastarbeiter". In the middle of the "sun" and "sea" songs, he looks thoughtful again. Get off at the first stop: a life as an illegal immigrant is better than a life of misery.

anchor_text: Big Bob Carter, former police commissioner of Cleveland, and his wife Ethel are taking their family to California to celebrate their silver wedding. Accompanied by Lynn, Brenda and Bobby, their children, Lynn's husband Doug, their own baby and the two dogs Beauty and Beast, they decide to take a detour through the New Mexico desert. Guided by the strange guard of a gas station, they take an unlisted route. Their tires explode, and the family is stranded in the middle of the desert. They are then hunted by a family of cannibalistic mutants and disfigured victims of nuclear radiation.
text_a: The film revolves around the Carters, a middle-class family from Ohio whose vacation to San Diego, accompanying their parents on their silver wedding, is cut short when their caravan suffers a freak accident in the New Mexico desert. Cut off from the world, they'll soon realize they're not alone: the hills are inhabited by a race of bloodthirsty mutants born of the army's continuous nuclear tests, such as Grunts and Slott, who will not hesitate to annihilate the Carters in the most frightening ways. In 1977, Wes Craven was inspired by the story of the Scottish family Sawney Beane who, according to legend, murdered travelers and then devoured them in the 17th century, until King James I of England sent troops to their lair and annihilated them in the same way they killed their innocent victims.
text_b: After the events of the first film, the site of the massacre in Sector 16 was placed under electronic surveillance by a small group of scientists and military personnel. At the same time, a group of young National Guard recruits are in training. To get to their firing range, they have to go through Sector 16 bypassing the scientists' camp. But when they arrive, the camp is empty. Intrigued by a radio signal coming from the surrounding hills, the young soldiers decide to leave to find the source of the signal. What they don't know is that the hills are inhabited by a tribe of cannibalistic, disfigured mutants who wait for their prey to feed and breed. Hell may begin again...

anchor_text: Jim, known as Il primo, is a gunfighter who has decided to give up the business for good. But when a gang of thugs comes to town, he gets carried away, decides to go undercover.
text_a: Jim, aka The First, is a gunfighter who has decided to give up his business for good. But when a gang of criminals arrives in town, he changes his mind and decides to go undercover.
text_b: In the midst of an exchange of gunfire, Sheriff Jim kills his father: he throws his star and pistol to the thorns, which reassures the local bandits. When he decides to punish them, it's a disaster for all of them.

anchor_text: Connor Mead is a people photographer. Firmly convinced that love is a utopia, the food of the weak, he has no equal to chain the conquests of women and thus satisfy his ego. The only woman he ever truly loved was his first love Jenny, a childhood friend who seems to resist his attempt to sabotage her brother's marriage. One night, Connor is visited by the ghost of his Uncle Wayne. The latter announces to him that three ghosts will come to see him.
text_a: Connor Mead (Matthew McConaughey), a hopeless bachelor and womanizer, helps his brother Paul (Breckin Meyer) with his wedding preparations; however, he is visited by the ghost of his late uncle Wayne (Michael Douglas), who makes him see that his attitude in his relationships with the opposite sex must be reassessed. To do this, he will be visited by three ghosts who will take him through his romantic past, present and future and he will realize that the love of his life is Jenny (Jennifer Garner), his childhood friend.
text_b: Conor is a young boy who has a difficult life: his mother is dying of cancer, he is bullied at school, and he has a bad relationship with his grandmother and father. The only thing that makes him happy is drawing. One night, Conor is visited by an enormous tree-like humanoid being, who comes to tell him three stories and in return gets one from Conor, related to his "truth". Thus begins a bond that will help the child to overcome his unhappiness.

anchor_text: Spoiled 18-year-old college student Clay is flying home from his college in New Hampshire to visit his parents in California for the holidays. At the airport, his ex-girlfriend Blair picks him up and takes him home. It's hard for Clay to go back to his old life. Many of his friends have become estranged from him, and he himself is constantly demotivated and discouraged. Almost every day he attends parties of friends or goes to clubs, where he also takes a lot of drugs and has promiscuous sex with women as well as men. His relationship with Blair keeps deteriorating while he still has feelings for her. His mother is permanently under the influence of psychotropic drugs. His friend Julian, meanwhile, is addicted to cocaine and prostitutes himself for the pimp Finn, to whom he is indebted. At a party, a friend of Clay's brings a snuff film, but it doesn't bother anyone except Clay and Blair. Another time, Clay visits one of his friends and finds a 12-year-old girl tied to a bed being raped by the men, again being the only one present to express moral concerns. A few days later, he hears on the radio that the body of a young girl has been found in Los Angeles. After losing touch with his old life, Clay decides to return to New Hampshire. Whether he'll ever come back to Los Angeles remains to be seen.
text_a: Minnesota Clay is serving a lengthy sentence in a penitentiary for the murder of two outlaws who challenged him to a duel. He maintains his innocence - in vain - on the basis of self-defence. Taking the camp doctor hostage, he is treated for a serious eye problem, manages to escape from the camp and return to his hometown where he left the grave of his wife who died in childbirth. His daughter Nancy was raised by his best friend Jonathan and both are exploited by Clay's jealous rival Fox. This one stormed into town to the dismay of the bandit Ortiz's Mexicans. Clay will clear the city of all these people, but blindness is lurking for him making things more difficult. The task is further complicated because he does not want to reveal to Nancy that he is her father.
text_b: The novel is a slice of life for Clay, a young, wealthy student at Camden University in New Hampshire who returns to his hometown of Los Angeles for winter vacation. He spends most of his vacation going to parties filled with alcohol, drugs and sex, wondering about his relationship with his girlfriend, Blair, no longer being very sure of his feelings for her. In addition, he has a few one-night stands with other people, men or women. Throughout the book, strange events occur and Clay becomes increasingly uncomfortable with the people around him: family, longtime friends... The decadence, violence, and meaninglessness of California's upper-class lifestyle gradually dawns on him, without explanation. He tries to find out the reasons for the absence of contact with his friend Julian, which leads him to discover that he has become a prostitute to satisfy his drug addiction. He also surprises his friends by watching a snuff movie, then later goes to observe a decomposing corpse in an alley, or discovers that an acquaintance has a 12-year-old girl as a sex slave in his home, tied naked to a bed and kept in a state of stupor. The various scenes of the book are interspersed with memories and introspective passages, written in italics. Clay recalls in these passages the last days he shared with his grandmother dying of cancer and a few moments shared with Blair, his girlfriend.

anchor_text: Alleluia and Sartana steal some horses from a gang leader named Lupo. His men are in pursuit. Sartana is captured, but Alleluia escapes. He disguises himself as a preacher to get his accomplice to escape. They are, because of the disguise, forced by a young woman to contribute to her plan to build a church. Lupo, however, unmasks them and attacks them with the Scottish brothers McGregor and two Englishmen. Sartana and Alleluia manage to defeat everyone and continue their adventures elsewhere.
text_a: Alleluia and Sartana steal some horses owned by a thug nicknamed Wolf. His men chase them, the second is captured but the first manages to save him. They disguise themselves at that point as priests, and to help a young woman they continue to pretend to be men of the church. They continue in the fiction until Lupo finds them and with the help of the Scottish brothers McGregor and two Englishmen confront them. After the victory, the two run away.
text_b: Set in 1912 Mexico, an Arizona sheriff named Lyedecker (Jim Brown) travels to a remote village looking for Yaqui Joe (Burt Reynolds), a mixed-race bank robber, who stole $6,000 to buy rifles from his people who are being repressed by the government. Lyedecker is not concerned with Yaqui Joe's cause, and all he cares about is getting the money returned to a Phoenix bank within his jurisdiction. Eventually they become allies and fight for the Indians.

anchor_text: The two heroes Alleluja and Sartana (mutated into Salto and Mortale in the German version) go rogue through the West and get on the trail of a land baron (in the German version with Saxon accent), who tries to drive good farmers and honest citizens from profitable land by making ghosts appear and staging hauntings. They succeed in defeating the not-so-gifted bandits with tricks and skill they are provided with pretty daughters in return.
text_a: Alleluia and Sartana steal some horses from a gang leader named Lupo. His men are in pursuit. Sartana is captured, but Alleluia escapes. He disguises himself as a preacher to get his accomplice to escape. They are, because of the disguise, forced by a young woman to contribute to her plan to build a church. Lupo, however, unmasks them and attacks them with the Scottish brothers McGregor and two Englishmen. Sartana and Alleluia manage to defeat everyone and continue their adventures elsewhere.
text_b: Set in 1912 Mexico, an Arizona sheriff named Lyedecker (Jim Brown) travels to a remote village looking for Yaqui Joe (Burt Reynolds), a mixed-race bank robber, who stole $6,000 to buy rifles from his people who are being repressed by the government. Lyedecker is not concerned with Yaqui Joe's cause, and all he cares about is getting the money returned to a Phoenix bank within his jurisdiction. Eventually they become allies and fight for the Indians.

anchor_text: Academic expert in criminal psychiatry, Dr. Jack Gramm is also a consultant to the FBI. He has built his remarkable reputation on his infallible assessments of individuals and risk factors. It's thanks to him that serial killer Jon Forster was arrested and is about to be executed. Yet the same murders are being committed again. Jack is convinced that he saw right and that he is a copycat carrying on Forster's work. When Jack finds himself directly threatened with death, he is himself condemned to prove his theories correct because otherwise, in 88 minutes, whoever is tracking him will kill him.
text_a: The Dr. Jack Gramm (Al Pacino), a college professor who spends his time teaching and working as a forensic psychiatrist for the FBI, receives a death threat with a cell phone call advising him that he has only 88 minutes to live, in a case possibly related to a murderer he helped convict in the past. He will have to use all his skill to find out who is threatening him in the following hours of the day and in this way save his life, and at the same time solve the case to punish the guilty before justice. Among the possible suspects are a disgruntled college student, a rejected former lover, and a serial killer identified by Dr. Seuss himself. Jack Gramm had you incarcerated years ago.
text_b: The film centers on convicted murderer Michael Perry, who is facing the death penalty he was executed on July 1, 2010 and his accomplice Jason Burkett, who is serving a life sentence. Herzog reconstructs the murders that led to the convictions, shows the accusations and justifications of the perpetrators; he describes their life stories and environments in interviews with their families and partners, but also those of the victims and their survivors. Conversations with police officers and a prison chaplain paint a picture of the U.S. justice system and the realities of the death penalty.

anchor_text: Two truckers, one young and one older, more experienced, visit a roadside ramen stand. Business is not going too well, and after getting into a brawl, the two decide to help the owner, Tampopo, turn the shop into a model in the art of noodle soup. The samurai film genre is being perverted. In the further course of the film, other genres are also filtered, such as the mafia film, love films and Western the latter simply because the elderly truck driver constantly wears a cowboy hat, like Clint Eastwood also when bathing. This mixture of Persian fragments and film quotations makes the film an early statement of postmodern cinema. Tampopo is interspersed with subplots that have nothing to do with the main story and whose basic theme is always food, often in direct connection with sex. In one scene, a yakuza and his mistress are making love in a hotel room using food, including the yakuza preparing "dancing shrimps" (Odori ebi) on the woman's body. Other scenes revolve around a young businessman who exposes his older colleagues while ordering at a French restaurant, a housewife who rises from her deathbed to prepare a last meal for her family, and a group of women who are being taught how to eat spaghetti in the Western way but are dissuaded from doing so by a European who sips the noodles in the Japanese way in the background.
text_a: The beginning of the film shows the two lead actors squatting, counting from 1 to 4. This is followed by a series of rapidly edited video-like sequences showing violent images of murder, sodomy and drugs: a woman falls from a balcony, an elderly man inhales a long strip of cocaine and is subsequently killed by a gunshot from the roof of his car, a man eats a series of exaggerated plates of spaghetti and is eventually killed with a gunshot to the stomach, from which blood mixed with spaghetti escapes, a gay couple is consuming sexual intercourse: when the first is killed with a knife to the throat the second welcomes his blood on his face, as if it were ejaculation, some girls are beaten in the street by a group of policemen, with no one intervening. The whole thing is punctuated by a wild lap dance performed by a young girl. All these murders were carried out by the gang headed by Ryuchi, a Chinese who landed in Japan to seize power. The yakuza, to defend their territories, are allied with the Chinese triad. Also on the trail of Ryuchi's gang is Detective Jojima, who has a bad relationship with his wife and has a sick daughter who despises him. Ryuchi, on the other hand, is unable to have children and with the money earned from the robberies allows his younger brother to study in the United States, who, once he learns of his brother's role as a killer, no longer accepts the money. Since the police chief is against using violence to stop the murders, Jojima is forced to ask for help from some local thugs to stop Ryuchi's gang. Jojima thus begins the hunt for Ryuchi, until a final confrontation that is resolved in an apocalyptic way, with a real end of the world.
text_b: Tampopo begins with two Japanese truckers who are driving in shifts for some time, the more experienced truck driver Goro and his young assistant Gun (the actors are Tsutomu Yamazaki and Ken Watanabe respectively), decide to stop and take in a typical Japanese establishment a noodle soup (specifically some ramen noodles), the music of the beginning is the Symphony No. 1 of Mahler. The place does not look good to them, but they are hungry and despite everything they decide to enter, and in the local they are involved in a fight with local gangsters for trying to stop the harassment to which the young woman who runs the Tampopo business is subjected ("Lion's Tooth", role played by actress Nobuko Miyamoto). Once the gangsters are expelled, they decide to help her cook the noodles by introducing her to the "art of making noodle soup". One of the peculiarities of the film is that the main plot is interrupted with a kind of gastronomic vignettes that introduce the viewer to the world of Japanese cuisine (sometimes comparing it to Western tastes), one of the stories involves a yakuza gangster dressed in white suit (Koji Yakusho) and his companion (Fukumi Kuroda) who live various erotic-gastronomic scenes. Some scenes have a great cultural impact such as Japanese women dressed in Western style eating spaghetti (a Western dish) in Eastern style (making noise with their mouths, not being gaijin), a group of executives ordering the menu in French in a French restaurant in Tokyo, etc.

anchor_text: Goro and Gun, two gourmet truck drivers, stop in a city intending to eat ramen, a Japanese culinary specialty. Their arrival at the inn of Tampopo, a widow with a dependent child, is, however, greeted badly by some mischievous people; the fight almost inevitably breaks out. The next day Goro wakes up in Tampopo's house; the woman, a mediocre cook, asks him to help her raise the fortunes of her business, which has been in decline since her husband died. Moved by compassion, Goro decides to help her, enlisting the help of an old tramp who knows ramen, a cook, and Pisuken, a decorator. In a short time and with a formidable team game, the business begins to flourish; Tampopo, thanks to the advice of his rescuers, manages to steal the secrets to cooking a great ramen and becomes a proven cook. Customers are beginning to arrive in droves. Successfully completing the mission, Goro sets off again. The main plot of the film is punctuated by short satirical sketches, which depict singular, often funny situations, all with food as the dominant element of the action.
