text_b: A soccer player, Joseph Bloch (goalkeeper), is sent off after a violent altercation with the referee. He wanders aimlessly through Vienna and spends the night with Gloria, a girl, recently known, employed in a cinema. When she tries to seduce him, he suffocates her to death for no apparent reason. He removes his fingerprints from the apartment and resumes wandering the city aimlessly. By bus she reaches a border village in Burgenland where a friend from the past runs an inn. He learns from newspaper accounts of the investigation that he has left traces of himself with a trail of American coins dropped from a perforated pocket of his jacket. A lot of cops on the streets are looking for a missing mute child. Then he no longer tries to hide his tracks and deliberately provides clues that might attract the attention of witnesses - for example, he starts a fight and orders his landlady to mend the hole in his pocket. At the end of the movie, he watches a soccer game and explains to his neighbor how much the goalkeeper and the shooter have to focus on each other. The film ends without the viewer knowing if Bloch will be arrested.

anchor_text: The Brazilian industrialist Mr. Ardonian longs for world domination. He plans to launch a rocket into space whose rays will sterilize every man. Then he wants to repopulate the earth with the beautiful women he kidnapped earlier. CIA agent Kelly and his British colleague Susan are trying to foil the plan. They get help from Susan's chauffeur, who drives a Rolls-Royce full of technical tricks.
text_a: A Brazilian industrialist plans to exterminate humanity with the use of satellites, and then repopulate it personally with beautiful women whom he has kidnapped and kept in a suspended state of life. A CIA agent and an aristocratic MI6 agent with their chauffeur driving a Rolls-Royce full of spy gadgets try to stop the industrialist.
text_b: During World War II in Istanbul, an American weapons engineer narrowly escapes being murdered in a cabaret by Nazi agents. The local secret service sends him out of the country via the Black Sea, but the discreet ship he uses is filled with stowaways, and the engineer doesn't know who to trust anymore.

anchor_text: Santiago Pope Garcia has been fighting drug trafficking for years by advising local police in South America. One day, one of his "indices", Yovanna, reveals to him where a drug dealer, Gabriel Martin Lorea, and his millions of dollars are hiding. He is holed up in a house on the triple border, a geographical point where Colombia, Brazil, and Peru meet. Lorea turned her house into a bunker and a giant safe to protect the proceeds of her trafficking. Santiago returned to the United States to offer four of his former brothers-in-arms to help him locate Lorea's home on the Brazilian side of the border, so that the military could then intervene. The five friends make up their minds and enter the country with fake passports. After reconnoitering the house and its surroundings, Santiago proposes to his friends not to inform the military (at the risk of a leak alerting Lorea) and to solve the problem themselves by assassinating the drug lord and taking his money, before fleeing by helicopter to reach the Pacific Ocean. This private military operation, though carefully planned, is going to get complicated and put a strain on the friendship between the five veterans.
text_a: Five professional Special Forces veterans meet in a sparsely populated area of South America where three borders converge. For the first time in their prestigious careers, these forgotten heroes embark on a dangerous mission of their own for the benefit of themselves, not their country, the United States, taking on a powerful drug cartel and using the support of an infiltrated mole. At first the operation goes according to plan; but greed and ambition can more than professionalism and suddenly everything takes an unexpected turn when they try to escape to the Andes Mountains. Then their skills, their loyalties and their morale will be pushed to a breaking point in an epic battle for survival in the Andes. Being close to the Andes, it is presumed that the triple border area where the story takes place is on the border of Colombia, Brazil and Peru, due to the Colombian accent among some actors who play the narcos. For this reason it should not be believed that the film takes place in "the other triple border" that is between Brazil, Paraguay and Argentina.[3]
text_b: The Spanish missionary Javier Mendoza (his original nickname was Balarrasa, which means as much as Hallodri or Luftikus) is surprised in Alaska by a heavy snowstorm. In the face of death, he recapitulates his life once more. He was an officer in the Spanish Civil War, where he was indirectly responsible for the death of a comrade, whom he defeated in a card game and who therefore had to take over a guard duty, during which he was killed. This experience causes Mendoza to radically change his life. He's entering the seminary in Salamanca. Towards the end of his time at the seminary, he is persuaded to return to his old life for a few days and visit his family. Like himself, after the death of his mother, all the members of the family have left: his father plays cards for money he does not have, his brother Fernando works with the foreign currency smuggler Mario Santos, who is also the lover of his sister Lina, and the younger sister Mayte has left her friend Octavio for a tennis player. Mendoza tries to convince his family of the need for a positive lifestyle and succeeds, except for Lina, who dies in a car accident while fleeing with her lover. Eventually, Mendoza returns to the seminary and is ordained a priest. With Empty Hands is a typical example of the "mixture between military and religious propaganda cinema" in Francoist Spain in the 1950s.

anchor_text: The well-known music teacher, Professor Joseph Elsner, arranges for his pupil, the young Polish composer Frédéric Chopin, to give a concert in Paris. For lack of money, the Chopin family cannot afford the trip and it is another eleven years before Frédéric makes the leap to France and to fame and recognition. Franz Liszt introduced Chopin to the writer George Sand. They both fall in love. The composer's fragile health prompts George Sand to organise an extended stay in Mallorca. The mild climate is good for Chopin, and he composes some of his finest pieces on the island. After their return to France, there is a dispute between Professor Elsner and George Sand over the further course of Chopin's life. Elsner believes that a genius like Chopin should serve the whole world and not be tied to a single person. Chopin is convinced and together with Elsner he goes on a concert tour across Europe. His failing health eventually takes its toll, and Frédéric Chopin collapses dead over his piano while working on a composition.
text_a: In the film, Vidor deals in a romantic way with Chopin's patriotism. It was produced during World War II. Chopin, played by Cornel Wilde, is presented to the audience as a child prodigy playing a Mozart piece, but as soon as he starts playing the piano he can see through the window how the Russian authorities are arresting Poles. The film also shows Chopin attending secret meetings to save Poland. Vidor shows patriotism as the most important reason why Chopin plays the piano. He goes to a secret meeting instead of showing up on time for the concert he was supposed to give for a count and his distinguished guests. Moreover, when he finally plays at the concert, he interrupts his performance when he sees the Russian Governor of Poland enter. He stands up and announces, "I don't play for the Tsar's executioners". He storms out of the room. Before leaving Poland for Paris, Chopin takes Polish land with his hands. The first of Chopin's works to be heard in Paris is his Heroic Polonaise, a song dedicated to Poland. The appearance of George Sand, played by Merle Oberon, alters Chopin's life. Vidor portrays Sand as a negative figure in Chopin's life. She seduces him and makes him forget Poland. At the end of the film, he offers a passionate concert tour of Europe, despite his frail health. At one of the concerts, Chopin begins to bleed.
text_b: After divorcing Baron Casimir Dudevant, with whom she had two children, and then living with various lovers, including Alfred de Musset, Michel de Bourges, or Félicien Mallefille, the famous 19th-century novelist George Sand had an impromptu crush on the music of the young virtuoso pianist Frédéric Chopin, which she heard for the first time during an evening in 1836 in the fashionable Parisian salon of her friend the Countess Marie d'Agoult (who shared her life with Franz Liszt, a great friend of Chopin). George Sand then falls madly in love with Chopin (the great love of his life) before meeting and courting him a little later during a stay with their mutual friends Marie d'Agoult, Franz Liszt, and Eugène Delacroix, at the patron Duchesse d'Antan in Angers (fictional place and character). Following various twists and intrigues of Marie d'Agoult, Chopin in turn falls madly in love with Georges Sand, with whom he then begins a love life for 9 years of his life, until the dawn of his early disappearance from the consequences of his tuberculosis, in 1849, at the age of 39. The whole background of many works of Chopin, including in particular his romantic masterpiece Fantaisie-Impromptu de Chopin of 1834, at once joyful, lyrical, inflamed, romantic, and nostalgic, in the image of the love relationship of the famous couple,,.

anchor_text: Newspaper reporter Dick Stallings meets an American woman named Linda Terry in London. They travel together across Europe, interviewing politicians and other celebrities. Meanwhile, the brothers Phineas and Don Merrill attempt to kidnap the beautiful Linda.
text_a: The life of pornographic actress Linda Lovelace, star of Deep Throat, her tumultuous relationship with her husband and manager Chuck Traynor, then her emancipation and commitment to pornography.
text_b: Dick Stallings, a young news cameraman, falls in love with a young, wealthy American woman, Linda Terry, who is engaged to a man she doesn't want to marry. They flee across Europe via London, Paris and Rome, interviewing politicians and high-profile celebrities on their way, while being chased by a gangster who seeks to kidnap Linda...

anchor_text: Tommy Basilio is a regular at the Trees Lounge bar, who just lost both his job as a mechanic and his girlfriend. He spends his time drinking and lamenting his fate when he meets Debbie, a 17-year-old niece of a friend.
text_a: The picaresque adventures of a very attractive young man who is successively courted by very beautiful and very rich women: an institute director, a fashion designer, a movie star, a rally driver, a doctor in nuclear physics and even the director of a helicopter factory...
text_b: Tommy Basilio, a 30-year-old Italian-American semi-alcoholic, after losing his job as a mechanic and his girlfriend of eight years, spends most of his days at the Trees Lounge bar. Around him, various friends and outcasts, and the daughter of one of his friends, who Tommy has a crush on.

anchor_text: The beloved Chief Engineer Bonicke is forced to resign from his beloved job in the Flying Corps after an injury. After an argument between the two Sergeants Fritz and Robert, Fritz persuades his comrade Robert to a dangerous flight maneuver, which leads to an emergency landing, whereupon the superior Mithoff imposes a flight ban on the two. Bonicke, however, ensures that the two are allowed to fly again. Fritz and Robert are about to make another emergency landing over the North Sea. Bonicke sets out with his old machine, a Fokker Dr.I with registration number D III 88, to find the two and manages to steer a ship to the crash site, but dies in the process. The 1941 film Battle Squadron Lützow, partly with the same actors, is considered a continuation of the theme.
text_a: Ellissen, a bold and courageous pilot, supports his old friend Droste's project: an ocean platform for transatlantic flights. Heiress Claire Lennartz is also joining the venture, funding the project. Two years later, the construction of the platform is almost complete but a series of incidents suggests that someone is trying to boycott the entire project. Ellissenn, who had left Droste and Claire for his job as a tester, when he returns, sees that the woman - with whom he is in love - is engaged to his friend. As the entire crew of the rig works to save it from sinking, the love triangle between the pilot, the engineer, and the heiress ignites tension between the three friends.
text_b: Bonicke has to leave his post at the air base after an injury. After an argument between the two corporals Fritz and Robert, Fritz persuades his comrade Robert to perform a dangerous flight maneuver, which leads to an emergency landing. Mithoff's imposing a no-fly zone on both of them. However, Bonicke ensures that the two are allowed to fly again. Fritz and Robert make another emergency landing over the North Sea. Bonicke searches for the two airmen with his old machine, a Fokker Dr.I with registration number D III 88, and manages to guide a ship to the scene of the accident, but he suffers a heart attack and falls into the sea with his machine.

anchor_text: The two reporters Michael Anthony and Barnabas Barney Pells are friends in life and professional rivals. Mike is writing a story about the upcoming wedding of millionaire Sally Parker to a European prince. Barney has the uninteresting task of conducting an interview with Baron Otto Spanderman. Sally's wedding is falling apart and Mike is hanging on to Sally's fugitive heels. When Sally confronts her stalker, the two instantly fall in love. The two flee the wedding party in an airplane across Europe, with Barney in tow. Mike hasn't told anyone he's a reporter. When the truth comes out, Sally is very disappointed and turns to Barney. All sorts of entanglements and misunderstandings later, Sally is kidnapped by Baron Spanderman, only to be freed by Mike in the end. They both fall into each other's arms and get married.
text_a: Detective Hercule Poirot and his friend, Colonel John Race, are on vacation in Egypt. But their vacation will be short-lived: as soon as they embark on a Nile cruise, one of the passengers, wealthy heiress Linnet Ridgeway, is murdered in the night. Colonel Race takes charge of the investigation with Poirot at his side. And the case has only just begun, as Race and Poirot soon discover that each of the other passengers would have had a good reason to commit this murder...
text_b: An adventure comedy, the film focuses on the adventures of a rich and famous young woman who runs away from her own marriage and a reporter. The action begins in London where two reporters fight: one (Clark Gable) goes to cover the wedding of a celebrity, the other (Franchot Tone), the test flight of a baron, a fan of aviation. Nothing's going as planned. The young woman (Joan Crawford) runs away before her wedding, renouncing a fiancé who is too interested in her money. Clark Gable then helps her and flees with her aboard the plane stolen from the baron. The plane landed in France but in the meantime they discovered spy documents: they now know that the Baron and his wife will pursue them. Clark Gable takes Joan Crawford on a wild ride, all the while hiding his identity from her. She hates reporters and intends to provide her newspaper with a fantastic soap opera about their adventures.

anchor_text: In the small community of Canton Bluff, a young man named Ian moves in. To support himself, he does odd jobs, mostly for the local church. One day, however, Mr. Harker discovers that Ian is a werewolf and forces him to work for him as a freak show for his circus.
text_a: Ian Lafferty is unlucky: his younger brother is more popular than him, he is mocked by his older brother and has a humiliating job in a doughnut shop. But his biggest problem is that he's going back to college a chip off the old block. Not getting anywhere with the girl of his dreams, Ian turns to the Internet to meet girls. He quickly stumbles upon a gorgeous, flamboyant blonde in a hurry to get her act together. Ian has to travel 500 miles to finish the deal.
text_b: Ian, a lonely young man, lives in the small town of Canton Bluff, where he works as a church repairman. After checking out that he's a werewolf, R.B. Harker captures Ian and forces him to work on his traveling freak show. Harker himself carries another monstrous secret, revealing that he is a vampire.

anchor_text: Ian is a sympathetic but lonely hobo and he agrees to make repairs to the local church in a barren, rural town in Bluff Township. Ian avoids all human contact and seems unusually suspicious of the approaching full moon. When R.B. Harker, owner of a traveling carnival, realizes that Ian is a real werewolf, he captures the young man and forces him to work for him. Ian becomes one of Harker's attractions, just like the other "curiosities" of man. To make matters more complicated, Harker has a monstrous secret of his own.
text_a: Ian, a friendly but lonely drifter, agrees to do repairs at the local church in an arid, rural town in Bluff County. Avoiding all human contact, Ian seems unusually wary of the approaching full moon. Aware of the fact that Ian is a real werewolf, R.B. Harker, the owner of a traveling carnival, captures the young man and forces him to work for him. Ian thus becomes one of Harker's attractions, along with the other "bizarrieties" of man. To complicate matters further, Harker is revealed to have a monstrous secret of his own.
text_b: Ian Lafferty is unlucky: his younger brother is more popular than him, he is mocked by his older brother and has a humiliating job in a doughnut shop. But his biggest problem is that he's going back to college a chip off the old block. Not getting anywhere with the girl of his dreams, Ian turns to the Internet to meet girls. He quickly stumbles upon a gorgeous, flamboyant blonde in a hurry to get her act together. Ian has to travel 500 miles to finish the deal.

anchor_text: London during the Blitz. A man, released from a mental asylum for helping his wife die without suffering, enters a carnival party, accidentally utters a phrase that is a code for Nazi spies, and wins a cake with a microfilm hidden inside. Hunted by the Nazis whose network he's about to unravel, he enlists the help of a young Austrian refugee and struggles to convince Scotland Yard of his innocence.
text_a: Dr. Malic owns and operates a psychiatric clinic. Colonel Howard of the U.S. Army asks him, in exchange for a large sum of money, to hide his secret agent Alex in his institute for a few days. Soon after Alex's arrival, the clinic is kept under close surveillance by other spies and counter-spies so much so that you can't figure out the true identities of the people and what forces they work for.
text_b: London, during the Blitz. Stephen Neale (Ray Milland), fresh out of a mental asylum, arrives at a fair where he wins a cake, inside of which is a microfilm. From that moment on he finds himself embroiled in an international network of spies and pursued by foreign agents after inadvertently receiving something they want.

anchor_text: Former high-end prostitute Wanda Brandt has found a more lucrative path to wealth by setting up shell companies to take over some of the players on Wall Street. It achieves its goal by seducing married shareholders of large investment firms and then blackmailing them for their shares. When the company's CEO notices that the company's value is falling, he hires Detective Lou Perrini to find out what's going on. So Wanda enlists the help of her former company's secretary and a stockbroker to achieve her goal.
text_a: Wanda Goronski, an apathetic young woman living in rural Pennsylvania, one day decides to abandon her husband and children and finds herself passively living a series of misadventures. Abandoned by a man she knew the night before and robbed at a movie theater, she accidentally meets criminal Norman Dennis with whom she establishes a complicated relationship and becomes involved in a robbery that ends with Dennis' death. After escaping a sexual assault, Wanda takes refuge in the woods and finds hospitality in a house.
text_b: The beautiful and enterprising Wanda Brandt, an engineer in a buyout firm, plots to take control of a Wall Street investment firm by sexually blackmailing shareholders out of their holdings. Things get complicated for Wanda when the company hires an investigator to determine the cause of the stock's instability.

anchor_text: Joko/Rocco, Mendoza, Richie and Domingo ambush a couple of bandits who have a wagonload of gold in their possession. But Domingo is a traitor, and Mendoza disappears into the corridors of an underground mine when he's supposed to cover the retreat of the other two. Richie manages to escape with the gold, but is stopped by five bandits. His silence about the planned meeting with Rocco costs him his life: he is torn apart by their horses. Rocco then sets out to avenge his friend's death and can first kill four of them, on whose bodies he throws a bloody piece of string. The last of them reveals to Rocco that the fifth wanted man is none other than Mendoza, who is actually the leader and planned the course of the robbery from the beginning. Rocco searches for Mendoza in his cave system and is finally able to confront and shoot him in a duel. The gold goes to Pinkerton agent Lester.
text_a: Joko, Richie, Domingo and Mendoza are plotting to steal gold from bandits. Domingo, however, betrays them by revealing the attempted robbery to the bandits. Joko, Ricky and Mendoza are discovered during the robbery: Mendoza dies while trying to cover up his friends' escape. Ricky escapes with the gold but is captured by five bandits and tortured in an attempt to reveal where he was supposed to meet Joko. When the bandits realize Ricky won't talk, they dismember him by tying his limbs to horses with ropes. Joko thus begins his own personal war to avenge his dead comrades and kills first Domingo, the traitor, and subsequently goes in search of the five assassins who killed Ricky: to each bandit, before killing him, Joko throws a bloody piece of rope collected from those used to dismember Ricky. Joko, however, knows only four of the five bandits: the discovery of the identity of the last one will be a bitter surprise for him. During the hunt for the killers, Joko will also have to understand the role of a mysterious detective who follows him like a shadow in all his movements.
text_b: Django (originally Johnny Hamlet) returns home after two years in the Confederate Army to find his father dead. His mother is married to his uncle, Claudio Hamilton, who now owns the family ranch and gold treasure. In addition, Django's former bride was forced to marry one of his favorites. It is said that Django's father was killed by the bandit Santana, who had to pay with his life. However, evidence is mounting that Santana is alive and that Django's father was murdered by his own brother. Django is bitterly bent on revenge. A silver earring of the showgirl Eugenia, with whom he enters into a relationship, leads him to the track of the supposedly dead bandit. In the process, he discovers the complicity between Santana and Claudio Hamilton. He sows discord between the two by telling Santana about the family gold, which his uncle has withheld from him. Eugenia and Django's mother are framed as co-conspirators. In the dramatic final battle, Django kills his uncle when he tries to escape with the gold. In one of the final scenes, Django crushes the bags of gold dust, which is then blown away by the wind.

anchor_text: The film opens with a scene where Ricky, one of the men who participated in a robbery, is tortured by five of his men in order to make him confess where he is supposed to meet Django. He is tied with ropes to the horses of men, and when he refuses to speak, they drive him away. Django arrives too late at the scene picks up a piece of each rope and vows to avenge his friend. Django first finds Domingo, gets him to talk and gets three names, the fourth is unknown. It is then learned that the gang led by Mendoza robbed the safe of a bank, but that Mendoza died in the collapse of the building. Django in his quest for revenge will successively kill a professional card cheater who has just sold his girlfriend Jane to a supporter, he then kills the supporter and befriends the young woman, then despite the fact that he is injured, he will defeat in a duel another man and try to make him confess before he dies, the identity of the last comparse, but in vain, it is then that he discovers hanging on his neck the medallion of Mendoza. Django then realizes that he has faked his own death and will hunt him down in the galleries of a sulfur mine. A marshal on the trail of the stolen treasure will help him get out and let Django and Jane go free.

anchor_text: After 16 years of marriage, Barbara and Richard Harmon live in luxury. Their house in Los Angeles is lavishly furnished, they have an expensive car and two well-behaved sons. Despite the material comforts, Barbara suffers from a lack of communication with her husband. She seeks marriage counseling, which soon leads to an argument with Richard. The couple wants to divorce. The judge sided with Barbara and gave Richard a very low cut of just under $90 a week. When Richard spends a weekend with his two sons, he meets Nelson Downes. Nelson is also divorced and suffers from horrendous child support. So he goes to great lengths to find a new husband for his ex-wife, Nancy. Nelson meets with Nancy, who finds Richard sympathetic. She agrees to marry Richard on the condition that Barbara also finds a new husband. In Big Al Yearling, a successful car dealer, they think they've found the right candidate. In fact, Barbara is responding to the seller's advertisement. The night before the Harmons' divorce becomes final, everyone involved, including Nelson's new fiancée, the pregnant Eunice, meets at a nightclub. Barbara allows the hypnotist Pat Collins to incorporate her into his show. After a striptease entrance, Collins asks her to go into the audience and kiss the man she really loves. Barbara goes to Richard and gives him a hearty kiss. The divorce is over. Barbara and Richard go home, while Nelson doesn't want to give up and tries to get Nancy interested in Big Al.
text_a: Barbara is a young American aspiring actress who, on a trip to a small town in northern England, has a brief affair with Richard, a funeral director, who writes obituaries for the local newspaper. Richard's real dream is actually to break into the movie business, so instead of marrying a rich man's daughter and working with his father in their funeral home business, he goes to Los Angeles in pursuit of Barbara with whom he has fallen hopelessly in love. Shortly after Barbara returns to Los Angeles, where she earns a living as a waitress in a Japanese restaurant, she finds herself confronted by Richard, who has followed her to earn a place on the Hollywood film scene. Richard will have to use all his weapons to win over another suitor, the handsome director Patterson who has promised Barbara to make her a star, and in this he will also be helped by his new friend Moss.
text_b: After 17 years of marriage, a wealthy Los Angeles couple, Richard Harmon and his wife, Barbara, fight constantly, even though they seem to have everything. When they find that they can no longer communicate, even to argue, they make an effort to save their relationship through specialized counseling. But after emptying their joint bank accounts (at the urging of friends), they ask for a divorce. Richard finds himself living in a small apartment and trying to survive on $87 a week. His income has been drastically reduced by the alimony he has to pay his wife. Richard meets Nelson Downes, also recently divorced, who introduces him to his ex-wife Nancy. Nelson wants to get Nancy married so he can get out of alimony and marry his pregnant fiancée. Nancy wants to get married, too, because she's alone. Since Richard cannot afford to remarry at the moment, Nelson and Nancy plot to have Barbara meet Big Al Yearling, a car dealer and millionaire. Barbara begins a relationship with Big Al, but the night before the divorce from Harmon becomes final, all the main characters meet to celebrate the success of their plans. In a nightclub, a hypnotist draws Barbara from the audience and puts her into a trance. After inducing her to perform in a fake strip club, he instructs Barbara to kiss her true love and Barbara passionately kisses Richard; in this way their marital problems are solved. Nelson, not at all discouraged, immediately tries to convince Nancy to take an interest in Big Al, while Richard and Barbara go back to fighting as before.

anchor_text: In 1937, a student named Richard Samuels (Zac Efron) walks through the streets of New York City and discovers the Mercury Theatre, a theater that will soon open its doors, and meets its founder, a man named Orson Welles. He would then get a small role in the play Julius Caesar which propelled Orson Welles to stardom.
text_a: The story takes place in New York City in the year 1937. Richard Samuels, 17, (played by Zac Efron) meets theater director Orson Welles (Christian McKay), who convinces him to play the role of Lucius in the first Broadway production of Shakespeare's Julius Caesar at the Mercury Theatre.[3][4]
text_b: The film tells the story of a young man, Luc, between his friendship with his father, his studies in Paris and his love life, taken from three stories, with Djemila, Geneviève and finally with Betsy.

He would then get a small role in the play Julius Caesar which propelled Orson Welles to stardom.
text_a: The film centers on the story of a young student who is accidentally spotted one day by Orson Welles outside the Mercury Theatre. Welles is so impressed with this young man that he gives him a small part in Julius Caesar, his next theatrical production. An encounter, with the famous and immortal director, that will lead the boy to learn many things about life and love.
text_b: The film tells the story of a young man, Luc, between his friendship with his father, his studies in Paris and his love life, taken from three stories, with Djemila, Geneviève and finally with Betsy.

anchor_text: Crocodile, thief, swindler, violent, lives with his father and a young boy by a river, under a bridge. He makes money by going to the bottom of the water to retrieve the wallets of people who have thrown themselves into the river. Until the day he saves the life of a woman who wanted to kill herself. She begins to live with them, and a relationship develops between them that can only exist between people who are adrift, navigating between violence and affection.
text_a: Crocodile is a homeless boy who, from under the bridges of the Han River, waits for suicides to rummage through their clothes and steal valuables; together with him live a child and an elderly man. One day, Crocodile meets Hyun-jung, a woman chased away by her man, and saves her from attempted suicide to make her his slave. Hyun-jung is repeatedly raped by Crocodile, who slowly falls in love with her.
text_b: Pamela Andrews is a humble and honest 15-year-old girl who works as a maid for Mrs. B., a wealthy landlady. After her mother's death, the son of the house tries to seduce her, but she resists. He forces her to live with a mean woman who beats her and tries to rape her again. Touched by her secret diary, he eventually marries her. In the second part of the novel, Pamela tries to adapt to bourgeois society and establish a successful relationship with B.

anchor_text: Don't Swallow depicts the daily life of a family torn apart by violence, alcohol and drugs, without ever falling into misery. Raymond's family - his wife Val and his brother-in-law Billy - live in a working-class area of London. Billy is drugged and Raymond kicks him out of his home, forcing him to live outside, hoping for help from his mother Janet and his grandmother Kath. Raymond, the father of a little girl, is often drunk and occasionally violent, including with his pregnant wife.
text_a: In a working-class neighbourhood of London live Raymond, his wife Valerie, his brother Billy, his mother Janet and his grandmother Kathy. The family is dysfunctional, due largely to Raymond's temper and violent nature. Billy's a drug addict, and Raymond kicks him out of his house. Billy, with nowhere to go, starts hanging out with his friends, also addicted to heroin.
text_b: Don Birman is a dipsomaniacal writer who cannot overcome his alcoholic outbursts because of which he has become a ruined man physically, morally and economically, totally devoid of personality. To get money to keep him drinking, he is capable of committing any action, even stealing from his friends, not excluding Helen St. James, a beautiful woman who, in love with him, tries to regenerate him. Don steals Helen's fur coat and takes it to a pawn shop. With the money he gets, he buys a revolver. Despite this unfortunate incident, Helen continues to help and encourage him to write a novel recounting his personal tragedy.

anchor_text: It's set in the Ming Dynasty. Wei Yangsheng falls in love with and marries Tie Yuxiang, the daughter of a Daoist priest. But he can't satisfy his wife sexually. In the "Pavilion of Supreme Bliss" of the Prince of Ning, Wei Yangsheng seeks help from the Master of Bliss, a transgender. He undergoes a penile transplant and receives the genitals of a donkey.
text_a: The subject of this popular Chinese love story is the love of young exam candidate Zhang Junrui (張君瑞) and the daughter of a state minister, Cui Yingying (). Zhang Junrui and Cui Yingying fall in love with each other, with Hongniang serving as a go-between. But their relationship seems doomed until they can get married. The play was translated into German by Vincenz Hundhausen: Das Westzimmer: Ein chinesisches Singspiel from the 13th century. In there. Afterword by D. Chin. Primary texts d. Wang She-Fu and others Guan Han-Ching is here.
text_b: Wei Yangsheng, a young scholar of the Ming Dynasty era, is convinced that everyone should pursue their own sexual pleasure as long as time permits. After meeting Tie Yuxiang, the daughter of a Taoist priest, he falls in love with her and marries her. The girl, however, is unable to satisfy Wei's sexual needs, who one day is thus accompanied by his friend Lin to the Tower of Rarities, where the prince of Ning resides. Having gained the prince's trust, Wei will begin to participate assiduously in the orgies held in the tower.

anchor_text: During the Ming Dynasty, Wei Yangsheng is a young scholar who wants to use his youth to seek ultimate sexual pleasure. Accompanying a friend to see a young girl whose father has engaged their marriage, he meets Tie Yuxiang whom he, like her, immediately falls in love with. Much to the chagrin of her previously engaged friend, the marriage is announced. However, their sexual development is not guaranteed! Wei Yangsheng then begins a quest for all pleasures that leads him to meet the Prince of Ning, a dissolute despot through whom he will go to the extreme limits of physical love. Despite this unbridled race, his love for Tie Yuxiang never leaves him. All this sounds too good... but it's too late for Wei Yangsheng to discover that he has thrown himself into a Machiavellian trap...
text_a: Wei Yangsheng, a young scholar of the Ming Dynasty era, is convinced that everyone should pursue their own sexual pleasure as long as time permits. After meeting Tie Yuxiang, the daughter of a Taoist priest, he falls in love with her and marries her. Having gained the prince's trust, Wei will begin to participate assiduously in the orgies held in the tower.
text_b: Young scholar Zhang travels to the capital to take the imperial exams. He stops at a monastery on the way, where he meets the young Cui Yingying, accompanied by her mother, and fatherless. The monastery is attacked by bandits, their leader wanting to get his hands on the girl. Yingying's mother promises her daughter's hand to whoever saves them. Zhang then calls on one of his friends, a general, who repels the bandits. The mother who had previously promised her daughter to another, refuses to honor her promise. Hongniang, Yingying's servant, arranges for the lovers to meet in secret. The mother learns of the appointment and agrees to the marriage, provided Zhang passes the exams. This one passes them and is received, as of right, first. The first fiancé then tries to make it look like Zhang got married in the capital. When his lie is exposed, he kills himself. Therefore, there is nothing to prevent Zhang and Cui Yingying from marrying.

anchor_text: Alex Claremont-Diaz is the son of the first female president of the United States. As his mother prepares for the upcoming election, Alex causes an accident with Prince Henry of England at the wedding of the latter's brother. In order to prevent this accident from distracting his mother's re-election campaign, the two boys, who have always been rivals, are forced to pretend to be friends. Nevertheless, the two young men gradually fall in love with each other as they spend more time together and more time in each other's company.
text_a: Alex Claremont-Diaz is the son of Ellen Claremont, the first woman president of the United States, who is running for re-election. During her visit to the United Kingdom for a royal wedding, she has a physical altercation with Henry, a British prince. The incident is photographed and heavily publicized and both sides force Alex and Henry to pretend to be friends to prevent it from turning into a full-blown diplomatic and media crisis that would distract attention from Alex's mother's electoral candidacy. The two become close over time and develop a relationship of friends with benefits, eventually falling in love.
text_b: Contrary to what the title might suggest, this isn't about the first female president of the United States, but about all sorts of political machinations surrounding the nomination of a presidential candidate. The focus is on Lucy Chase-Wayne, the granddaughter of a former U.S. president who is married to the current U.S. Secretary of State, Stephen Wayne. Lucy is desperate to have her husband put forward as a candidate for the next presidential election. Her main rival is Irene Hibbard, who is married to a corrupt Supreme Court justice. She wants to divorce him and marry her lover, Senator Gordon Keane, whom she sees as the best candidate for the highest office in the state. In return, Lucy organizes a campaign that not only gives Wayne the coveted nomination, but also clears the judiciary of corruption and nepotism.

anchor_text: Jack Andrus was once a Hollywood star. When his fame faded, he turned to alcohol. His marriage failed. After a serious car accident and a nervous breakdown, he spent three years in a sanatorium in New England. Finally, he receives an offer for a small role in a costume film by Maurice Kruger. Kruger was once the director to whom Jack owed his great career. Filming is taking place at Rome's Cinecittà film studio. But Maurice Kruger's best days as a director are behind him. In Italy, he wants to revive his career. In Rome, Jack meets his estranged wife Carlotta and learns that his role has already been cast. The entire production is in a lot of trouble, and Jack has been asked to oversee the film's synchronization. Jack feels like a loser again and consoles himself with the beautiful Italian Veronica. However, Veronica is in love with the film's lead actor, Davie Drew. One night, director Kruger has a heart attack and goes to the hospital. Jack promises Kruger's wife Clara that he will complete the production for his former mentor. He successfully throws himself into his work and makes a fatherly effort to care for Veronica and Davie. When Kruger learns of Jack's progress, he attacks him and accuses him of trying to steal his film. Jack then leaves the production and returns to his ex-wife Carlotta. The night ends in drunk driving and a wild car ride. Eventually, Jack realizes that he's responsible for his own life. He says goodbye to Veronica and Davie and flies to Hollywood.
text_a: Jack Andrews is an actor in crisis. He tried to commit suicide in his car and can no longer make a successful film. Director Maurice Kruger, his mentor and friend, sends him a letter from the alcoholism clinic he is now in calling him to Rome where he is shooting a new film in Cinecittà. Jack agrees to direct the dubbing but in the same hotel where he is staying, his ex-wife, the charming Carlotta, arrives, whom he is still obsessed with and cannot do a good job. He enlists the help of Veronica, a Roman girl in love with the young actor Davie Drew, the protagonist of the film. The work barely takes off and the producer kicks and pushes when suddenly Kruger has an illness that forces him to go to the hospital. The director confides to Jack that he too is on the verge of bankruptcy and desperately needs the film to be finished and to be a success and is worried that the producer will call an incompetent director to finish the job. So Jack volunteers to replace him without being in the headlines and it turns out he's really good at handling capricious actresses and bad boys as well as packing a good movie. Jack's remarkable exploits soon reach the ears of Kruger, whose jealous wife, Claire, tries to discredit the new director. Andrews, disheartened by the reversal of what he thought was a good friend, falls back into the arms of Carlotta who, however, again betrays him brazenly. In the throes of a crisis and alcohol, Jack climbs into the car with her, attempting suicide again, but fortunately realizes that she has proven that she can work without Kruger and live without Carlotta, and that she can do it.
text_b: Jack Carter is a cold, methodical killer working for the London underworld. When he learns of his brother Frank's death, he returns to Newcastle where Frank lived, and where he himself grew up. He soon realizes that, despite appearances, this death is not accidental as the police report concluded. He conducts his own investigation, which interferes with the activities of Kinnear and Brumby, two rival bridges controlling various traffic behind a respectable facade. Carter discovers that his niece Doreen has been abused in an amateur pornographic video and that it was Margaret, the companion of Doreen's father, who played the role of the batterer. As he threatened to expose the involvement of community leaders in the making of the film, Frank Carter was killed by Eric Paice, Kinnear's chauffeur. Carter avenges his brother by eliminating the links one by one, whether they took an active part or let go. When he finally finishes Peace, he is shot by a hitman thrown at him by Kinnear.

anchor_text: The once flying Ramper, stranded on Greenland, was forgotten by civilization there. In about a decade and a half of being a hermit in the polar ice, without the slightest contact with fellow humans, Ramper has gradually become civilized, becoming "animal man". His return to civilization is dramatic. Unfortunate whalers catch him and smuggle him to a rumble pit, where they try to market Ramper as a creature from another star, as a freak and circus sensation. Admired and gifted by the visitors, Ramper, this temperamental primate, who can only be tamed a little by the loving treatment of the daughter of the rummage booth owner, is finally deprived of his last remaining dignity, human dignity. His fate seems to be turning for the better when a scientist takes him in. In his sanatorium, the doctor is trying to restore Ramper to his former life. However, Ramper, the "Animal Man", can no longer find his way in this life, and so he returns, bent but not broken, on a whaling boat from the supposed civilization to his old habitat, the uncivilized polar desert of Greenland.
text_a: Arctic explorer Ramper sets off with his pilot Ipling for the pole, but the plane crashes somewhere on the Greenland ice sheet. Every attempt at research is in vain, and, while Ipling dies after three years of hardship, even fifteen years of very hard and solitary life will have been spent, without any contact with humans, when the explorer is picked up by the crew of a whaling ship that was momentarily trapped by the ice of the coastal shoal. Rampling is profoundly debilitated mentally, has lost the use of speech, and appears almost not to belong to the human race. In fact, when he returns to "civilization" he finds himself acting as a circus attraction as a kind of ape-man. Only the intervention of the self-styled psychiatrist, prof. Barbazin - who rescues him and takes him to his clinic - manages to make him regain, gradually and partially, his memories and the use of speech. When Ramper finally begins to associate with society again, he encounters addiction, gambling, senseless toil, greed, crime. So he gets on a boat and goes back to Greenland.
text_b: During an Arctic expedition, anthropologist Stanley Shephard discovers a hibernated Neanderthal man. Transported to a hospital laboratory, it is discovered that the low temperature has preserved the man alive, who is extracted from the ice to be subjected to a series of experiments. The man is given the name Charlie, but soon the same science that resurrected him decides to sacrifice him again in the name of research. Scientists are soon divided on two distinct fronts, divided by questions of an ethical nature: some want to make contact, perhaps with the aim of educating him to a new life, others want to return him to an environment as similar as possible to the one in which he lived. Although the Iceman responds to the induced stimuli, both attempts fail.

anchor_text: Jean Louis is a 33-year-old engineer who, after working for a decade in America, returns to France, to Clermont-Ferrand, where he finds a job at Michelin. One morning at mass, he sees Françoise, a 22-year-old student, and falls in love with her. He follows her in his car as he cycles away, but loses sight of her. One evening, at a bar, he meets an old schoolmate, Vidal, a philosophy professor, who invites him to the home of a friend, Maud, a divorced pediatrician. The three of them have dinner, talk about Pascal and Catholicism. The two criticize him for his conservatism, especially when it comes to love. Meanwhile, it's starting to snow outside. Vidal says goodbye and Maud proposes to Jean Louis, knowing that he lives far away and that the route by car on the icy road is risky, to stay for the night. Continuing to chat confidentially, she tells him about her divorce: her husband loved a student and she had a lover, who on a snowy day, had died in a car accident. Jean-Louis is torn between his attraction to Maud, his awareness of her availability, and his moral scruples. Leaving the pediatrician's house the next morning, he accidentally meets Françoise. They get to know each other and make an appointment for the next day at Mass. Convinced that she is the woman of his life, he declares his intentions to her and Françoise confesses to having had a married lover. Jean-Louis reiterates his love for you. Five years later the couple has a son and is on vacation at the sea. By sheer chance, they meet Maud. She and Françoise already know each other: the man Françoise had been with was Maud's ex-husband. Jean-Louis is not responding. Together they take their son for a bath.
text_a: Marie and Jean are a middle-aged couple who have been married for 25 years. He's French and she's English, they live in Paris, they have no children. One day, during a vacation at the sea, Marie falls asleep on the beach while Jean goes to take a bath. The man, however, vanishes into thin air. Back in town, Marie tries to start a new life, while the police investigate her husband's disappearance. On the other hand, however, she does not accept the disappearance or death of her husband and begins to lead a life that oscillates between fantasy and reality. She is prone to mood swings, bouts of fatigue and hilarity. Her mother-in-law reproaches her for not being a good wife, for which Jean would not have died, but simply ran away to build a new life for herself. Marie, by the way, has had a lover since her marriage. With considerable delay, the woman returns to the place of her husband's disappearance: in fact, the authorities have been expecting for quite some time that the body of a man drowned on the beach of the couple's vacation will be recognized. Upon arriving at the scene, the protagonist refuses to admit that it is her husband's body, despite the fact that several tests performed by the responsible confirm that it is him. Marie simply states that the watch found on the corpse's wrist is not Jean's. In this way, the protagonist avoids confronting the death of her husband and resumes to vacillate between truth and illusion.
text_b: In the city of Clermont-Ferrand, a few days before Christmas, Jean-Louis (Jean-Louis Trintignant), a young engineer and devout Catholic who has just returned from abroad, notices during a mass a blonde woman, and decides at that moment that she will become his wife. He then meets by chance Vidal (Antoine Vitez), an old communist friend, who invites him to a Christmas Eve dinner at the home of his divorced friend, Maud (Françoise Fabian). The night passes between long conversations about marriage, morality, religion or Blaise Pascal. First between the three of you, and then between Jean-Louis and Maud, joining in equal parts sincerity and seduction, but by the end of the night, the platonic barrier will not have been crossed. The next day, the engineer addresses the blonde woman, Françoise (Marie-Christine Barrault). Shortly thereafter, he proposes to her, but she resists, having just ended a turbulent relationship with a married man. Five years later, the couple arrives on a beach with their young son at the exact moment Maud leaves. They cross paths and have a small talk before some things are clear.

He accidentally meets Vidal, an old friend, a communist, who invites him to a Christmas Eve dinner at a divorced friend, Maud. text_b: Like every year, Marie and Jean spend their holidays on the coast. Suddenly, Jean has disappeared on the beach, and a search launched immediately has been unsuccessful. Marie clings to the idea that Jean is still alive. In Paris, Marie's day-to-day life begins again in her profession as a lecturer. Mary's best friend introduces her to a new man: the publisher Vincent. Even though she's committed to a relationship with him, she can't come to terms with Jean's death. When the police report to her because a body has been found, she doesn't react. By chance, she is confronted with the fact that Jean was severely depressed and taking medication. His mother shares her assessment that Jean, bored with his life and his marriage, used this route to start his life anew. Only now does Marie decide to go to the place of disappearance. And although the body has now been unambiguously identified as her husband - one detail being the exact wristwatch described by Marie - she now claims to the police that the body is not her husband and the watch shown is not her husband's. So she can maintain her illusion.

anchor_text: A judge, accompanied by his bodyguard of four young Carabinieri, tries to bring order to a Sicilian town. Corrupt local politicians, working with the Mafia, will stop at nothing to prevent their crimes from being exposed.
text_a: Lucania of 1850 is under the rule of Ferdinand II of Bourbon. Rocco Gravina leads a band of bandits operating near Lagonegro. One day Rocco robs a stagecoach and, after robbing the passengers, kills a young woman, Dina Franco. Her brother Antonio, hearing what had happened, sets off for the country, determined to find the killers and kill them.
text_b: The film shows the difficulties of an honest and courageous judge and his close-knit guard of four men, as he tries to "clean up" a town in Sicily. The corrupt local politicians, working hand in hand with the mafia, will not stop until they prevent the disclosure of their rackets.

anchor_text: Hortense Laborie is a renowned cook who lives in the Périgord. To her great surprise, the President of the Republic appointed her to be in charge of his personal meals at the Élysée Palace. Despite the jealousy of the chefs of the central kitchen, Hortense stands out with her temperament. The authenticity of his cuisine quickly seduces the president, but behind the scenes of power, there are many obstacles. The sequences at the Elysée alternate with those in Antarctica, on the Alfred-Faure base of the Crozet Islands, where Hortense distributes his cuisine to the members of the base.
text_a: Hortense Laborie is a renowned chef who lives in Périgord. To her great surprise, the President of the French Republic puts her in charge of his personal kitchen at the Elysée. Despite the jealousy of the chefs who work in the central kitchen of the Palace, Hortense manages to prevail thanks to her strong character and her temper. The genuineness of your cooking will seduce the president in no time, but what happens behind the scenes in the halls of power will create many obstacles...
text_b: Lucy Hill (Renée Zellweger) is an ambitious, up-and-coming executive living in Miami, delighted with her lifestyle in the bustling city. He loves his shoes, he loves his cars, and he loves climbing the corporate ladder. When she is suddenly offered a temporary position far from her hometown, in a small rural town in Minnesota, to restructure a factory, Lucy immediately accepts, knowing that a big promotion is not far off. But what begins as a simple job ends up being a life-changing experience, as Lucy finds a greater meaning to her existence and also, unexpectedly, the man of her dreams (Harry Connick Jr.).

anchor_text: Isabel travels to Paris to be at the side of her sister Roxeanne, pregnant and abandoned by her husband, Charles-Henri de Persand, a member of the French upper middle class. Isabel enjoys the evocative Parisian life and meets the charming Edgar Cosset, uncle of her sister's ex-husband. There will be no shortage of resentments, tensions and family quarrels because of the impending divorce but also because of the cultural differences that divide Americans and French.
text_a: Paris-based psychologist Anna examines the fate of the Mexican writer Antonieta Rivas Mercado, who lived at the beginning of the 20th century and shot herself in the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris in 1931. As part of her research, Anna decides to travel to Mexico and interview people who know Antonieta Rivas Mercado. In this way, Anna gets to know the time of the Mexican Revolution and the modernization of Mexico according to the European model. Antonieta, who comes from a respected family of the upper class, first marries, but then falls in love with the painter Manuel Rodríguez Lozano, who rejects Antonieta because of his homosexuality. Later, she had a relationship with the intellectual José Vasconcelos, with whom she went into exile in Paris.
text_b: Elizabeth Walker travels to Paris to accompany her half-sister Roxeanna, who is pregnant, and, upon arrival, discovers that Roxeanna has been abandoned by her husband, Charles-Henry de Persand, and they are going to divorce. Once in Paris, Isabelle falls in love with a French diplomat who happens to be the uncle of Roxeanna's future ex-husband. This causes a scandal and further complicates the relationship between the two families, until a crime of passion leaves everyone shocked.

anchor_text: A couple, the Maynards, are grieving over the sudden death of their infant daughter. Jack's husband is committed to a mental institution, while his wife, Lilly, stays home and is left alone with her own feelings. Lilly's trying to pull herself together until Jack gets back from the asylum. She works in a grocery store and takes care of her family's possessions. As if Lily's problems weren't bad enough, a star whose nest is in a tree in her backyard begins to harass and attack her, and she is obsessed with stopping him. Besides, she drives two hours every week to see Jack and wonders if he really wants to come home and what his life would be like if he did. Since she prioritizes Jack's grief over coping with her own, a counselor at Jack's hospital suggests that Lilly see someone for her own mental health before he comes home. This leads her to the practice of Larry Fine, a quirky psychiatrist who pretends to be a veterinarian. The two form a unique and unlikely bond as they help each other recognize and cope with their problems. He's helping her with the star, while also incorporating ways to deal with her grief into the conversations. Lilly begins to bond with the bird, and when she accidentally hits it with a rock and nearly kills it, she turns to Larry to help her resuscitate the bird. You and the bird start to tolerate each other instead of mistreating each other. Jack and Lilly end up back in their house and face the world together.
text_a: Jack is a 30-year-old advertising executive living in New York City. He's considered successful, but he's bored. He meets Jill and is fascinated by her from the first encounter. They get closer, and it's not long before Jill moves into Jack's comfortable bachelor pad. They quickly notice fundamental differences in their lifestyles. While Jack leads a life pampered by success, Jill is the spontaneous and simple soul who seeks daily happiness. She's an animal rights activist, she drinks Fairtrade coffee, and she's really messing with Jack's orderly life. To make their life work, Jack and Jill make a list of rules they want to live by. At the top of their manifesto is honesty. But Jill also wants her life to be a fairy tale and to be able to stop time. Soon, Jack notices that his girlfriend occasionally disappears without explanation. He investigates and discovers that Jill is seriously ill (cystic fibrosis). He angrily accuses her of dishonesty and ends the relationship. But it doesn't take long for him to realize how much he misses her. It's also messed up his professional life, but it's also inspired him. His approach to advertising has changed since Jill's appearance, and suddenly it doesn't satisfy him anymore. Jack starts looking for her and learns from Jill's friend Lucy that Jill allegedly left New York and took a bus to Los Angeles. He's quitting his job to follow Jill. Unexpectedly, however, she suddenly appears before him. Jill had noticed how much Jack had changed and had inadvertently added one rule to her list: Always be willing to admit your mistakes. They both apologize to each other and they reconcile.
text_b: Lilly and Jack are a busy couple dealing with the untimely disappearance of their daughter. Jack finds himself locked away in a mental institution, while Lilly finds herself at home alone, struggling to deal with her feelings. Lilly's trying to hold on tight for Jack's return from the facility. Employed in a grocery store, she works to maintain her family's property. As if Lilly's problems weren't bad enough, a stork whose nest is in a tree in her backyard starts molesting and attacking her and she becomes obsessed with stopping it. Prioritizing Jack's pain over managing her own, a counselor at Jack's hospital suggests Lilly see someone for her mental health before she goes home. This brings you to the office of Larry Fine, an eccentric psychiatrist turned veterinarian. They form a unique and unlikely bond as they help each other recognize and deal with their problems.

anchor_text: Làn fhìrinn na sgeòil (Scottish Gaelic pronunciation: [lɣ̪aːn iːɾjɪɲ nə s̪kjɔːl], The truth lies in the story): When a young man named Angus visits his dying grandfather in the hospital in Glasgow, he cannot hide from him his constant search for the truth since his childhood the truth about his parents' death and the truth about his grandfather's ancient, unbelievable, terrifying stories. Stories that reflect the full range of Gaelic culture in Scotland's Gaelic Highlands. Stories of poisoned lovers, bloody revenge and sorcerers, watermen and water horses (Each Uisge) and Spanish gold. Angus' grandfather kidnaps the boy into the Celtic culture of Scotland. One last time, he leads him to one of Scotland's most treacherous mountains, the Inaccessible Pinnacle on the Isle of Skye. Angus finds there a truth he never thought he'd find.
text_a: When a young man, Angus, visits his dying grandfather in the hospital, he can't bear to ignore that as a child he sought the truth about his parents' deaths and the veracity of his old grandfather's incredible and frightening stories. Tales of poisoned lovers, bloody revenge, water-horses and Spanish gold are from all parts of the Gàidhealtachd and its history. For the last time, his grandfather interrupts Angus' life, leading him to one of Scotland's most dangerous mountains, the inaccessible Pinnacle on the Isle of Skye, and to an ancient truth he never expected to find.
text_b: Scotland in the 18th century. The narrator explains that in 1745, Bonnie Prince Charlie returned to Scotland and asked for help in regaining the throne from King George II of the House of Hanover and restoring the House of Stuart there. Jamie Durrisdeer is the heir to the Ballantrae estate. Loyal to the Stuarts, a lover of brawls and gallant adventures, Jamie joined the Scottish rebel troops and took part in the battles against the English. But, victorious, they soon occupy the land. Wanted by the new authorities, Jamie is denounced by Jessie, a jealous mistress. He narrowly escaped his pursuers and, wounded, had to hide in the Maquis. However, more than physical pain, Jamie suffers from the thought that it was his brother, Henry, who sold him to the English to take the estate from him and his fiancée Allison... He manages to escape with the help of his friend Burke, an Irish colonel. After various adventures with pirates, they captured a Spanish galleon and returned to Scotland wealthy. At Ballantrae, they find the English and are arrested. With the help of Henry and Jessie, who has confessed her treason, they manage to escape. Jamie gives his brother part of the Spanish jewels, leaves him the estate and returns to the Americas with Colonel Burke and Allison.

text_a: Làn fhìrinn na sgeòil [̪aːn iːɾjɪɲ nə s̪g̊jɔːl] is Spanish for "the truth is out there". The truth is in the story. When a young man, Aonghas, visits his dying grandfather in the hospital, he can't suppress the memories of his childhood quest - searching for the truth behind his parents' deaths, and for the truth behind the ancient incredible stories told by his grandfather. Stories straight from Gaelic legends of poisoned lovers, bloody revenge, horses haunting the lochs and Spanish gold. His grandfather will turn Aonghas' life upside down, taking him one last time to one of Scotland's most dangerous mountains, the Unreachable Pinnacle on the Isle of Skye, and leading him to a truth he never expected.
text_b: Scotland in the 18th century. The narrator explains that in 1745, Bonnie Prince Charlie returned to Scotland and asked for help in regaining the throne from King George II of the House of Hanover and restoring the House of Stuart there. Jamie Durrisdeer is the heir to the Ballantrae estate. anchor_text: The 7th of December, 1941. Mrs. Hadley, a wealthy member of Washington's finest society, celebrates her birthday with all the pomp and circumstance she's used to. Surrounded by her closest friends and her two children, Theodore and Patricia, Mrs. Hadley especially enjoys the attentions of her old admirer, Elliott Fulton. Fulton, who holds a position of responsibility in the War Department, accuses Mrs. Hadley of living in an ivory tower of ignorance. Just as the hostess is about to disarm, the shocked society hears of the attack on Pearl Harbor. Everyone except Mrs. Hadley is deeply moved. The world has changed since then, but Mrs. Hadley insists that the war must not interfere with the course of her comfortable life. When Theodore is drafted, Mrs. Hadley tries everything to get him out of the army, but her intervention with Elliott Fulton is in vain. Her selfishness and strict refusal to accept any limitations eventually isolates Mrs. Hadley from her family and friends. It wasn't until Theodore died a hero's death in the Pacific and President Franklin D. Roosevelt personally offered her condolences that Mrs. Hadley was prepared to make the necessary concessions on the home front. She accepts Elliott's proposal of marriage and is exemplary in the volunteer corps.
text_a: Mrs. Hadley, a wealthy member of Washington's finest society, celebrates her birthday with pomp and circumstance. Surrounded by her closest friends and her two children Theodore and Patricia, she particularly appreciated the attentions of her longtime admirer Elliot Fulton. This man, who occupies a post in the War Department, accuses Mrs. Hadley of living in an ivory tower of ignorance. At the news of the attack on Pearl Harbor Mrs. Hadley is not as moved as her entourage because she believes that the war does not have to affect the course of her comfortable life. When her son Theodore is conscripted, Mrs. Hadley does everything in her power to get him out of the army, but her intervention with Elliot Fulton is in vain. Her selfishness and strict refusal to accept wartime restrictions eventually alienated Mrs. Hadley from her family and friends. It wasn't until Theodore was killed as a hero in the Pacific and President Franklin D. Roosevelt offered his personal condolences that Mrs. Haldley agreed to contribute to the war effort...
text_b: Woodrow Lafayette Pershing Wilson lives in a small town. His father was a hero in World War I. The Marine Corps rejected Woodrow because of his hay fever. In order not to disappoint his mother, he pretends to be shipped overseas to fight in World War II. He sneaks off to San Diego to work in a shipyard. In a bar, he meets a couple of Marines with their superior, Master Gunnery Sergeant Heppelfinger, who have returned from the Battle of Guadalcanal. After a few drinks, the soldiers decide to take the young man home so that his mother does not have to worry. To Woodrow's amazement, they hand him their medals and awards. Unfortunately, word of Woodrow's return is spreading in the city. The whole town is coming together to give a proper welcome to the returning war hero. Woodrow is expected to run against the incumbent, Mr. Noble, in the upcoming mayoral election. To complicate matters, Woodrow wrote a letter to his girlfriend Libby telling her not to wait for him. Now Libby is dating the mayor's son, Forrest Noble. Woodrow won't be able to live with the lie for much longer. At an election event, he confesses that he wasn't in the war. He's going home to pack his things and get out of town. Libby's leaving the mayor's son and she wants to go with Woodrow. Meanwhile, Heppelfinger, the sergeant, is about to praise Woodrow as a brave man who hates the lie. The fact that Woodrow has confessed to the circumstances gives the townspeople the conviction that Woodrow is the right man for the mayor's office.

anchor_text: The Mughal ruler couple Jalal-ud-din Mohammad Akbar and his Rajput wife Jodha Bai have a son, Salim, who, however, devotes himself more to pleasures than to the duties of a heir to the throne. After the successful educational measure of growing up outside the palace, Salim returns to the court, but to the displeasure of Akbar, torn between the duties of a mogul and the feelings of a father, falls in love with the beautiful slave and dancer Anarkali and even wants to marry her. Akbar throws Anarkali in jail, but that doesn't break her love for Salim. He even goes to war against his son Salim without being able to kill him in the end. He pronounces the death penalty against him, but in doing so he only incites his subjects to revolt. Anarkali is to die eventually. She accepts her death to save Salim, but before the execution of the death sentence against her, as her last wish, she wishes to become Salim's queen. Akbar agrees without changing the outlook of death. Anarkali is being walled up alive. At the last moment, you allow Akbar to leave the country and live.
text_a: Nargis (Meena Kumari), a courtesan repudiated by her husband's family, Shahab-ud-din, dies after giving birth to her daughter Sahibjaan (Meena Kumari). This one, entrusted to Nargis' sister, grows up in a brothel, becoming a beautiful dancer and singer. For example, Shahab-ud-din's nephew, Salim Ahmed Khan (Raaj Kumar), falls in love with Sahibjaan. So he persuades her to run away with him and marry him. But not wanting to tarnish the reputation of the man she loves, she refuses and returns to her former job. Salim eventually marries another woman and, invited to his wedding, Sahibjaan agrees to dance for him. The secret of his sonship is revealed during the festivities.
text_b: The story is set in the 16th century in the Mughal Empire. Sent to the battlefield to strengthen his character, Crown Prince Salim returns to the imperial palace after fourteen years of victorious wars. Received with pride by his father, Emperor Akbar, and with love by his mother, Queen Jodhaa Bai, he rejoices in the sweetness of the court. There he falls madly in love with the dancer Anarkali whom he wants to make his wife. Concerned about the interests of the empire, Akbar violently rejects this misalliance, imprisons the young woman and condemns her to death. The prince then opposes his father, challenging his right to control his feelings as he does the country. He breaks Anarkali out, hides her and rebels, dragging part of the army with him. The confrontation ends in Akbar's victory, who decides to have his only son executed. Anarkali then surrenders to the emperor to save the man she loves.

anchor_text: Prince of Basra, Harun travels to Baghdad to discover and punish his father's killers. Along the way, he discovers a magnificent sword with magical powers. But the precious blade is soon stolen from him by the grand vizier and his son, Hadi. While the caliph is assassinated by the two renegades, a tournament is organized, whose stake is the hand of the delightful princess Khairuzan, the caliph's daughter. The captain manages, by treachery, to triumph over his only opponent, Harun...
text_a: The young Harum, after falling in love with the beautiful Khainzan, daughter of the Caliph of the country, kidnaps her to escape the wrath of his father, who had beaten him with a ruse in a duel. The two, pursued by soldiers and the Caliph, take refuge in a strange castle. The young man manages to seize a magic sword with which he defeats the pursuers, marries the beautiful beloved and becomes the new Caliph of the country.
text_b: An evil wizard offers his daughter's hand to a prince if he can complete a dangerous mission to retrieve a magical rose. Aided by a boy and a magic carpet, Prince Hasan must overcome genies, fire monsters, and treacherous swamps to reach the goal and claim the princess's hand.

anchor_text: Upon his return from the war, Mexican General Juan Carrasco is killed by Tomas, an adventurer who in his absence has won the love of Carrasco's wife Anna. Sebastian and Isabelle, Carrasco's children, become eyewitnesses to the crime and are able to escape with the help of a nanny. Fifteen years later, the siblings decide to return to their birthplace and avenge their father's death. With the help of Rafael, a friend since childhood, they are able to escape several attacks by bandits in the service of their evil mother and her notorious lover Tomas. When Tomas sees death approaching under Sebastian's attacks, he kills Anna, who can reveal beforehand that she is not the biological mother of the siblings, but an employee of General Carrasco. Isabelle and Sebastian share their triumph with their confidant Rafael, leaving behind the burning estate on their way to a new future.
text_a: Returning home after a long war, Mexican General Carrasco is murdered by Thomas, an adventurer who, taking advantage of his absence, has stolen the love of his wife Anna. Carrasco's two young sons, Sebastian and Isabelle, after witnessing their parent's murder, manage to escape with the help of a faithful nanny. Fifteen years later, the two brothers, now adults, decide to return to their birthplace to take revenge on their treacherous mother and her lover. Aided by Raphael, a gunfighter with whom they are linked by an old friendship, Sebastian and Isabelle, after escaping a series of attacks by Thomas' men, come to the presence of Anna and her mature lover. Blinded by terror at the approach of his end, Thomas kills Anna and, before falling under Sebastian's gunshots, reveals to the young man that the woman was not his mother: both Sebastian and Isabelle are in fact the children of a servant and General Carrasco. With their revenge complete, Sebastian and Isabelle set off with their trusty Raphael, leaving behind their old home in flames.
text_b: The beautiful young Rosario, from a modest background, is married to Don Carlos Montero, a wealthy antique dealer. When the father learns that his son, Carlitos, has just committed a theft, he punishes him severely. Locked in his room, the boy makes a run for it. He is soon picked up by an adult, engineer Julio Mistral, who takes him back to the fold. Julio then becomes Rosario's lover. Their union produces a son, Miguel, whose mother conceals his identity from the rest of the family. Don Carlos suffered a heart attack. Rosario interprets this event as a sign of fate and therefore refuses to accompany Julio. Years later, Miguel and Carlitos became enemies after Julio, who died in a distant land, bequeathed his property to his legitimate son. Overcome by jealousy and suspicion, Carlitos finally learns the truth. After the death of the antique dealer, Rosario tells her sons that she lived a life without love to ensure their happiness. Finally reconciled, Miguel and Carlitos follow, separately, the course of a new life while their mother is now alone...

text_a: Pursued by a gang of killers, Rafael escapes and takes refuge in a hut where he is taken in by Sebastian: unwillingly, he has just found his childhood friend after fifteen years. Rafael was also the unhappy lover of Isabel, Sebastian's sister. It is then learned that when General Juan Carrasco returned victorious to his hacienda, his wife Anna had him killed by her lover Tomas and that all the people present had been killed. The children escaped, but Rafael was emasculated, while Sebastian escaped with "the witch" (actually the governess). As for Isabel, Anna and Tomas married her off to a shopkeeper in order to keep her under control. So Sebastian decides to avenge his father with the help of Raphael...
