anchor_text: The sculptor David Fowler has numerous affairs. He thinks he's possessed and seeks help from the psychiatrist Marianna. He later dies in an accident. Marianna tells the story of Fowler's life in flashbacks. He visits one of his lovers at a gym, where he ducks to avoid being recognized. But that is precisely what attracts attention. Fowler meets Louise Carr, the wife of an oil millionaire, on the verge of opening an art exhibition in Texas. Loving the risk, she drives Fowler to her apartment in the adjacent high-rise and seduces him. Her jealous husband finds his wife and Fowler admiring the view through two windows a few feet apart. Another time, the husband almost caught Fowler hiding in a closet. There he smears himself with glue, the Carrs' little dog sticks to Fowler's hand. So he drives home with the dog dangling from his hand.
text_a: David Fowler is a sculptor who loves women. A fixation of his that leads him to look for the reasons in psychoanalysis. The beautiful analyst Marianna listens to him with professional patience and interest: David is full of problems and anxieties, up to a temporary impotence. On the ritual sofa, David enumerates to Marianna his successes, but also his dissatisfaction, reliving childhood and first approaches with the female world. At the end of the excursion, David realizes that he desires Marianna and Marianna realizes that she loves her client. David stops the analysis to live a few happy months with his new lover. A period of crisis ensues between the two who decide to separate. David resumes his life of women and sculpture, but a car accident ends his life.
text_b: The film presents a realistic portrait of a sculptor immersed in everyday life. The viewer follows the few days that separate Lizzie from her next exhibition. He attends, through quick shots, to the setbacks and distractions that disrupt his creative days. Lizzie picks up an injured pigeon that her neighbor, and owner, no longer wants; her water heater breaks down; her family is dysfunctional. The evolution of the situation raises the question of the extent to which the figure of the animal is a duplicate of the artist.

anchor_text: An elderly man and his estranged son search for treasure and try to improve their relationship. Harry Agensky is a former world heavyweight boxing champion who lives in Canada with his son Moses. Harry's other son, Lance, realizes that his father never cared about his dreams and ambitions, which is why he doesn't have a good relationship with his father now. Lance's relationship with his son Michael isn't much better, though. Lonely since his wife's death and frail from a stroke, Harry wants to retire to a ranch in northern Canada, but he can't afford it. Lance invites Harry to go skiing with Michael, and Harry accepts the invitation. Harry claims he threw a fight years ago and was supposed to get paid in diamonds hidden somewhere in Las Vegas. If he found the gems, he'd be able to buy the ranch. Lance has his doubts, but he's helping Harry and the three of them hope to find the diamonds. On the way there, the men visit Madame Sin-Dee's brothel after Harry convinces the others. The three of them find the diamonds, and Harry and Lance reconcile.
text_a: Stroke-stricken, widowed former boxer Harry Agensky exercises every day to climb the slope of his partial facial paralysis. Visited by his son and grandson, he shares his anguish at the prospect of ending up in the hospice, and tells one of his legends, a fight where he was paid by a diamond thief, which diamonds were hidden in a very specific place. Harry, his son and grandson decide to go in search of the so-called diamonds: a journey between men that leads them to the casino and a luxury brothel, where the grandson, still a minor, is supposed to have his first experience with a blonde with provocative language, Sugar. It is precisely in this place of perdition that we find the trace of diamonds, which will allow everyone to start again for a new, more peaceful future.
text_b: After being released from prison, where he had served six months for stealing a car, young Floyd Gambino and older Joe rob a jewelry store. The coup succeeds, but a policeman dies in the process. On the run, the two separate and make an appointment to meet at the Canadian border. Because of a small accident, Floyd, who is carrying the loot worth half a million dollars, has to take a room in a motel. Its owner, Fred Norton, and his wife, Michelle, discover their guest's true identity and prevent his discovery. After one night of lovemaking with Michelle, the diamonds have disappeared and are nowhere to be found. Meanwhile, Joe's on his way to the motel, too, because he senses a betrayal. Floyd and Michelle leave the motel separately; while he leaves in a car, Michelle takes the bus. Joe records the pursuit of the bus and kills Michelle, but can't find the gems on her. Then he sets out on a quest with Floyd, who in turn kills him. Meanwhile, Michelle's niece is celebrating the perfect crime with her fiancé at the motel. But now Floyd knows what's been played, and he puts an end to the fun.

anchor_text: After the death of his parents, a man, now responsible for his young nephew and niece, hires a young governess to live with them in a remote country house in Maine. Nanny Kate wants to make a fresh start and is assigned to look after the two behaviourally challenged children, but it doesn't take long before she discovers that both the children and the house are hiding dark secrets.
text_a: Kate Mandell is hired as a governess to two orphans, Miles and Flora. The young woman is soon convinced that the mansion in which they live is haunted. She soon discovers that the children and the house have dark secrets and that things are not what they seem.
text_b: Kate is a fine-dining chef at one of the finest restaurants in Manhattan, New York. She focuses all of her life and energy on her work and her very high ambitions to be the best in her field. Her younger sister (the mother of nine-year-old Zoé) is tragically killed in a car accident while visiting her father. Kate then takes care of her niece and realizes that she is unable to raise her properly on her own. The sympathetic and charismatic chef Nick, a lover of Italian cuisine and a great fan of operas by Giuseppe Verdi and Giacomo Puccini, then enters their lives and introduces them to the "taste of life" in his own way...

anchor_text: The heroine is Hélène Mouret, daughter of Ursula Macquart and the hatter Mouret. At the age of seventeen, she married a man named Grandjean with whom she had a daughter, Jeanne, who became ill and suffered from regular seizures. The family moved to Paris, where Grandjean died shortly after his arrival. Widowed by a man she never truly loved, Hélène is seized by a violent passion for Dr. Deberle, her neighbor, who rescued her during one of her daughter's seizures. But the latter has an equally violent passion for her mother: she cannot bear to see her smile at other children or other men. On the day Hélène gave herself to Deberle, Jeanne, who had foreseen everything, went to her window in the rain and contracted what was then called a galloping phthisis (tuberculosis), from which she died three weeks later. Later, Hélène married a man named Rambaud, with whom she went to live in Marseille. She will return anyway, at the end of the novel, to Paris, where she will stay only a few hours.
text_a: Hélène has a relationship with Jean, who she is obsessed with. When she learns through a friend that Jean's love for her has cooled, he is bored with her, she breaks up with him ostensibly for good. Secretly, however, she is intent on revenge and tries to trap him: she offers money to Agnès, an impoverished dancer who has to earn her living as a prostitute, to seduce Jean. She dresses her, provides her with a nice apartment and money, and then introduces her to Jean. Hélène's goal is to compromise Jean by marrying a prostitute. Jean falls in love with the young woman, but knows nothing of her past and wants to marry her. It is only during the wedding with Agnès that Jean and Hélène's guests learn the truth about the bride's background. Jean leaves the wedding party, the guests are shocked, and Hélène triumphs. Agnès is knocked unconscious. During his wanderings, Jean becomes aware of his love for Agnès and he returns to her.
text_b: The story takes place in 1854-1855. At the beginning of the novel, Hélène has been a widow for 18 months and lives in a villa on the outskirts of Paris with her sick daughter Jeanne, who is very close to her mother. In order to spare the child any disturbance Hélène leads an isolated life sacrificing serenely her beauty and her youth; limiting herself to hosting the priest and his brother, Mr. Rambaud, for dinner every Tuesday night. One night, the little girl is sick and frightened, so she calls her neighbor, Dr. Henri Deberle, to come and treat her. That same week, now cured, Hélène goes to thank Dr. Henri Deberle and befriends his wife Juliette and her circle of friends, including Mr. Malignon, a handsome and wealthy man, particularly well regarded in the female society. Deberle falls in love with Hélène who at first tries to resist the passion and refuses any encounter but a fortuitous chance wants the two to meet. Meanwhile, Juliette entertains a love affair with Malignon, promptly interrupted by Helene's intervention. Jeanne, always very weak, always very possessive in relation to her mother, noticed the attention that the same reserve to Henri develops an irrepressible jealousy, so much so that one evening, waiting for the return of the mother (exit to avoid the compromise of Juliette with Malignon) is exposed for a long time to the cold and, sick, dies of thirst months later. Hélène, struck by remorse, renounces her love for Deberle and, subsequently, begins a new life with Mr. Rambaud.

anchor_text: The first-person narrative, to an unspecified interlocutor, tells of a young Justine, of noble lineage and suddenly become an orphan who, complicit in her blind devotion to Christianity, intends to live honestly following a strict Catholic morality. All the girl's efforts are in vain: kidnappings, rapes and false accusations are just a part of a painful life for fifteen years. Justine will passively suffer all kinds of violence from increasingly perverse characters who will involve her in orgies, murders and torture. Each of them, by poetic license of the author, will grant you an exemplary discourse on the motivation of the cruel nature, proper and of the human being. The girl punctually and fortunately manages to escape certain death. In the finale, Justine is about to be executed for a murder she did not commit but is saved on the gallows by her sister Juliette who, unlike her, has indulged in vices and perversions throughout her life, making a fortune and even acquiring a noble title. The novel has its own epilogue where Justine's story ends. The interlocutor turns out to be his sister Juliette, with whom he has found refuge. Shortly afterwards, however, looking out of a window, the protagonist is killed by a bolt of lightning. Juliette watches his corpse in shock, wondering about their diametrically opposed life choices.
text_a: Justine is a teenager who becomes an orphan after her parents pass away, she turns to all strata of society to get a decent job and preserve his virtue, and in every case, instead of receiving help, what he finds is incitement to vice. Justine symbolizes the virtue that with a poor intelligence must face the dangers of vice and, contrary to what might be expected, instead of being rewarded for maintaining her virtue, what she receives is all kinds of grievances. On the contrary, the libertines who abuse it are rewarded. Anti-heroes, so to speak, often emphasize their philosophical beliefs, rooted in atheism, and sadism. Sade pours into this work his misanthropy, describes how in the society in which he had to live practicing virtue always turns out to be onerous, while whoever practices vice finds a society complicit and corrupt, which allows him to acquire advantage.
text_b: Diderot tells the story of Suzanne Simonin, but does not make any inroads and does not deviate in the sequence of events. Denis Diderot had a sister, Angélique Diderot (17201749), who had joined an Ursuline order and died there at a young age in a state of mental confusion. Perhaps this event inspired his work. The nun Suzanne Simonin tells the story of her life in letters. She is forced by her parents to become a nun against her will, as she lacks the necessary financial means for a proper marriage, since she was an illegitimate child of her mother's former lover. She hates the monastic life, although she initially enters into a relationship with an understanding superior, her love of freedom remains unbroken. Thus, under a new, fanatical and cruel abbess, she becomes the target of reprisals and harassment by her and fellow sisters. In three monasteries, she is confronted with the power of convention and money, as well as hypocrisy and religious fanaticism.

anchor_text: In a violent Mexican village live the Anguiano and Menchaca families, enemies for generations. There is an agreement between them that anyone from the other family who kills a member of his own clan will not be held accountable if he succeeds in crossing the river and will not return to the village in the future. Only two young men are left from both families and the whole village is waiting for the final and decisive showdown. On one side is Felipe Anguiano, a polio sufferer who grew up outside the village in a big city, studied medicine, and is a practicing physician. On the other side is Rómulo Menchaca, who has never left the village and is just waiting for his counterpart to return to the village to avenge his blood. In fact, one day the doctor returns to the village because he is expected to do so. Regardless of the opinion of the rest of the villagers, he persuades his opponent to make peace in public and put an end to the old family feud once and for all.
text_a: In the coastal village of Santa Bibiana, the slightest misunderstanding can escalate into a deadly shootout. Between the Anguiano and Menchaca families, the torch burns. Gerardo Anguiano, an idealistic doctor, chose to set up his practice in the city. Hospitalized in a steel lung, he was visited by Rómulo Menchaca. Gerardo's father once murdered Rómulo's father, and Rómulo demands reparation. Gerardo, refusing this alternative, is then slapped by Rómulo. Flashback: explains the origin of this incessant war that tears the two families apart. Mercedes' fiancé, Felipe Anguiano, was forced to kill Filogonio Menchaca and flee across the river into the desert. Surprised by a Menchaca, Felipe is, in turn, shot. That's the end of the flashback. Returning to the village, Gerardo manages to subdue Rómulo without using weapons. The cycle of violence is finally broken and the two men end up fraternizing. Convinced, Rómulo settled in the city to live in peace.
text_b: Tokyo at the end of World War II. Gangster Matsunaga, who was injured while "working", goes to Dr. Sanada for treatment. He thus discovers that he has tuberculosis but, too proud to admit it, refuses the doctor's help, who intends to cure him. The doctor, however, does not give up and, despite the man's brutal reactions, succeeds, after persistent attempts, in convincing him to seek treatment. Meanwhile, the doctor's young assistant tries to hide from Okada, her violent husband just released from prison (also a member of Matsunaga's gang), who wants her back. The yakuza boss, hearing of Matsunaga's serious illness, decides to liquidate him and replace him with Okada. At that point Matsunaga, disappointed and furious, confronts Okada to eliminate him but, also causes the disease that devours him, during the fight he takes the worst and is killed.

text_b: In one Mexican country there is an exasperated law of honor that does not stop at anything as for example when Felipe (Miguel Torruco) is slapped and challenged by Romulo (Jaime Fernández) while he is hospitalized and totally inserted into a breathing machine that leaves him outside visible only his face. That's because Felipe's father had just killed Romulo's father. In this village, everyone has a gun, even the priest. A deep river divides the village from the cemetery, and when a person is murdered, society allows the murderer to preserve his life in solitude after crossing the river. The end of the story is the breaking of this cruel law that existed almost to give a perverse satisfaction to the inhabitants of that country.

anchor_text: Jack Thomas and Joe Jarrett, two passengers traveling in a stagecoach, manage to fend off the bandit Matson's gang of thieves, then quarrel over the $100,000 contained in the vehicle's safes. Jack and Joe will take turns as friends and enemies accompanied by two beautiful women, Elya and Maxime.
text_a: Jarrett stops a stagecoach carrying a hundred thousand dollars belonging to a bank in the city of Galverston. Jarrett has the audacity to go to Galverston and deposit them into an account in his name at the same bank they belonged to. The bank is owned by Zacarias Thomas, who controls the entire city and sees his monopoly threatened with Jarrett's arrival.
text_b: Nick, a pizza delivery boy, finds himself involved in a rocky and tragicomic adventure when he is kidnapped by two armed criminals and forced to rob a bank with an explosive device strapped to his chest.

anchor_text: England in 1988: Jean works as a physical education teacher in a school. She is the target of homophobic accusations after the Conservative parliamentary majority under Margaret Thatcher banned the promotion of homosexuality by local authorities (see Clause 28). Lesbian and gay people are stereotyped as pedophiles who would recruit children for their "divergent" lifestyle. Athletic trainers like Jean are the main targets of such accusations. As a result, she is forced to lead a double life. During the week, she cultivates the image of the esteemed employee. On weekends, she and her friend Viv make secret trips to the Newcastle gay scene. One day, Jean meets a student of hers in a lesbian bar. As a result, she is forced to make extreme efforts to save not only her job but also her mental health.
text_a: In 1988, Jean was a young sports teacher at a college in Newcastle. Reserved and secretive, she speaks little to her colleagues and neighbors, but regularly joins her friends, as well as Viv, her partner, in a lesbian bar. While Margaret Thatcher's government was in the process of enacting a law banning the promotion of homosexuality (known as Section 28), she was forced to hide her private life from her colleagues. One evening, in the bar where Jean is accustomed to, she meets Lois, a new student from her high school. Fear of being ostracized and losing his job causes John to lose his footing.
text_b: The Commission's proposals are as follows: Jean is 30 years old, he's chief operating officer, recognized, talented, curious about everything and HIV-positive. In Jean's huge harem, there is Samy, half-brother, half-lover, whose drift to the fascist extreme right he does not accept. During a casting for a commercial, he meets Laura, young, beautiful, alive. A passion arises between them. Jean's bisexuality and the fact that he has other affairs upsets the young woman. She also learns that John has been infected with AIDS and that she, too, may be nearing the end of her life.

anchor_text: Thirteen-year-old James "Ray" Steam hopes to become as successful as his father and grandfather. So he builds his own steam-powered means of transportation. One day, he receives the "Steamball" in the mail from his grandfather Lloyd. The ball uses compressed fluids as a novel energy source. When mysterious gentlemen who have been looking at the steam ball suddenly appear, Ray flees with his grandfather. He is rescued by inventor Robert Stephenson, but is eventually taken with the steam ball by the men to the O'Hara family. There, Ray meets his father Eddie, who works for O'Hara, and also meets Scarlett O'Hara, the family's spoiled daughter. O'Hara wants to use Eddie's inventions for the weapons they sell. Ray refuses, choosing instead to work with Stephenson. At the World's Fair, where OHara wants to show off their weapons, it comes to a battle between the opponents. Eventually, Ray and Lloyd will be able to save London from greater destruction at the arms show.
text_a: 1866, alternate Victorian England. On the eve of the first World's Fair, Ray Steam, the son of a Manchester family of scientists, spends his days and nights devising new inventions. Ray eagerly awaits the return of his father and grandfather, who have left for America to continue their research. One day the boy receives a mysterious metal ball sent to him by his grandfather. But the Ohara Foundation wants to get their hands on the orb, so they kidnap Ray and take him to London, to the Ohara Foundation pavilion. Here the boy discovers the powers of the sphere: it is a container of compressed high-density steam, which can generate energy of incredible proportions. Ray will do everything in his power to prevent the orb from being used for evil.
text_b: Eleven-year-old Timmy is returned to Ray, his father, after Ray is released from prison after four years. The young man would like to spend some time with his father, but things do not go as he hopes: in fact, Ray and two of his accomplices, also former prisoners, decide to illegally seize some precious coins of inestimable value of state property. The robbery succeeds, but young Timmy will figure out how to blow up the plans: he will hide the loot, in exchange Ray will have to behave like an exemplary father, if this does not happen Timmy will denounce him and his two accomplices.

anchor_text: In 19th-century England, Ray Steam is the son of Edward and grandson of Lloyd, two scientists who invented the steam engine. They did not appear again for several months after their departure for America, when a package containing a metal ball (the steamball) arrived at the home of Ray and his mother. But two men from the O'Hara Foundation, who are funding the two fathers' research, want to get their hands on it. Ray escapes and is rescued by scientist Stevenson, a fervent patriot. Meanwhile, London is preparing to open the World's Fair, where steam engines will be featured. The O'Hara Foundation has prepared a pavilion and is sending the founder's daughter, young Scarlett, there. She's accompanied by Simon, who's in charge of selling the foundation's technology.
text_a: After a long time without seeing each other, little Timmy (Macaulay Culkin) shows up at his father's (Ted Danson) house with the intention of spending a season with him. Unfortunately, your father has other plans. Caught in a quarrel with two clumsy collaborators (Gailard Sartain) and (Saul Rubinek), Ray has promised himself that it will be the last blow, the one that allows him to earn enough money to set up a bakery and earn an honest living.
text_b: 1866, alternate Victorian England. On the eve of the first World's Fair, Ray Steam, the son of a Manchester family of scientists, spends his days and nights devising new inventions. Ray eagerly awaits the return of his father and grandfather, who have left for America to continue their research. One day the boy receives a mysterious metal ball sent to him by his grandfather. But the Ohara Foundation wants to get their hands on the orb, so they kidnap Ray and take him to London, to the Ohara Foundation pavilion. Here the boy discovers the powers of the sphere: it is a container of compressed high-density steam, which can generate energy of incredible proportions. Ray will do everything in his power to prevent the orb from being used for evil.

anchor_text: Agata, a bookseller endowed with an inner energy that makes light bulbs and computers or even traffic lights burn through, falls in love with a much younger man. Her supposed brother Gustavo, a well-placed architect, suddenly finds out he's been adopted. In the person of Romeo, her real brother, the family now receives an addition of a new member. Romeo, a fashion representative who is also privately behind every skirt, but loves his paralyzed wife above all else, brings a lot of momentum into the lives of the two "siblings" with his entrepreneurial spirit. Gustavo separates from his wife, who as a marriage counselor on television can save any marriage, but destroys her own in real life. He neglects his architectural office and enjoys working with Romeo to plan an excursion paradise around a trout pond. Agata is the centerpiece of this cheerful, colorful summer comedy that also touches on serious issues.
text_a: Sister Consuelo is the Mother Superior of a convent in Catalonia in the early 1950s. In order to convert the monastery's hospital into a nursing home, she begins to raise money. In search of financial support, she meets the wealthy landowner Don Augusto Aixelá. Aixelá shows interest in the project, which is why they see each other often in the aftermath. Sister Consuelo, who is increasingly drawn to Aixelá, learns not only details of his romantic past, but also that he supported Franco in the Spanish Civil War. As floods ravage the region and parts of the convent are submerged, Sister Consuelo struggles with her feelings. In a letter to her superiors, she wants to report with guilt about her love for Aixelá and ask for a transfer to another monastery. However, she does not send the letter. Despite her remorse and against her vows, she engages in a brief romance with Aixelá. What love means to her, however, is nothing more to him than another of his numerous amorous adventures. Back at the convent, she prays to Mary and decides to leave the convent secretly. On her way through the forest, she encounters bandits, whose leader Balaguer was seriously wounded in a gun battle with soldiers. In his hiding place, she tends to his wound while he tells her about his life. When soldiers discover the hiding place, Balaguer and his men are shot. Sister Consuelo returns to the convent, where she once again cares for her duties as a nun on her own. However, many years later, as she lies dying, her last thoughts are not about the many good deeds she did during her life, but about the time she spent with Aixelá, through whom she came to know love.
text_b: On the verge of death Romeo's mother, a casual clothing merchant in the vicinity of Comacchio, reveals to him that in his youth he had sold his firstborn son to a wealthy Genoese family many years before. In the hope of making his son see his mother for the last time, Romeo goes to Liguria, in order to find his brother; it is the architect Gustavo Torregiani who, once he came to know the truth about him, goes to Emilia to find his roots, questioning his whole life, his work (he is intent on designing a velodrome in Denmark), the relations with his wife Ines, a psychologist and television personality, and with his older sister Agata, an independent and cultured woman, owner of a bookstore in the Ligurian capital, in the area of San Lorenzo. In love with young Nico, Agata, who flashes light bulbs and all electrical devices in her path when she is seized by strong emotions, reaches her brother when she believes that the suitor has betrayed her. The isolated house in Cicognara, a fictional town in the Lidi of Comacchio, becomes a meeting point between the different characters, who will intertwine their stories and their dreams, plan to build a large nursery and remain united even after the sudden death of Romeo.

anchor_text: Ogami Itto is the former shogun's executioner now fallen from grace at the instigation of the Yagyū clan and become rōnin. He travels the country with his three-year-old son, Daigoro, in a stroller.
text_a: A group of travelers are staying in an inn during a flood of a small river. The ronin Ihei Misawa wanders through Japan with his wife Tayo in search of a new master. He too must seek shelter in the inn. There he is warmly welcomed and can even settle a dispute. He meets Lord Nagai, who becomes his new master.
text_b: Ogami Itto is the former shogun's executioner who fell from grace at the hands of the Yagyu clan who became rōnin. He wanders about with his three-year-old son, Daigoro, whom he carries in a stroller.

anchor_text: Professor Girasole, interviewed on a television programme, appeals to scholars of all nations to help fight world hunger. His Spanish colleague, Antémar Zallaméa, sends him a package containing a blue orange, a citrus variety that would be able to grow even in the desert, so Sunflower leaves to reach Zallaméa in Spain, but they are both kidnapped. With the help of a group of young boys, Tintin and Captain Haddock free the two scientists, who were kidnapped by an emir who wanted to appropriate the discovery.
text_a: A British scientist escapes from a research facility with an atomic bomb. In a letter to the British Prime Minister, he threatened to demolish central London if the government did not announce the end of research in the area within a week. Special agents from Scotland Yard attempt to hunt down and arrest the mad scientist, with the help of the latter's assistant.
text_b: Professor Tornasol has just published a book on world hunger and appeals to the world's scientists to help fight it. His Spanish colleague, Professor Zalamea, sends him a package containing the prototype of a blue orange, a variety that could grow on desert soil. At night, the orange is stolen from the castle of Moulinsart. The next day, Tornasol, Tintin and Captain Haddock travel to Valencia (Spain) and stay at the home of Professor Zalamea, but the scientist is kidnapped, and shortly after Tornasol is also kidnapped, with the purpose that he help with his knowledge to Zalamea in his discovery, for the benefit of the kidnappers. With the help of a group of children, Tintin and Haddock must free the two scholars, kidnapped by an emir who wants to appropriate the discovery.

anchor_text: Professor Sunshine has just published a book on world hunger and is appealing to the world's scientists to help him fight it. His Spanish colleague, Anténor Zallaméa, sent him a package containing a blue orange, a variety that could grow in desert soil. In the night, the orange is stolen from the castle of Moulinsart. The next day, Sunshine, Tintin and Captain Haddock leave for Valencia in Spain to find Zallaméa, but the scientist has been kidnapped, and soon after Sunshine is kidnapped as well. With the help of a gang of children, Tintin and Haddock free the two scholars, kidnapped by an emir who wants to appropriate the discovery.
text_a: Professor Girasole, interviewed on a television programme, appeals to scholars of all nations to help fight world hunger. His Spanish colleague, Antémar Zallaméa, sends him a package containing a blue orange, a citrus variety that would be able to grow even in the desert, so Sunflower leaves to reach Zallaméa in Spain, but they are both kidnapped. With the help of a group of young boys, Tintin and Captain Haddock free the two scientists, who were kidnapped by an emir who wanted to appropriate the discovery.
text_b: A British scientist escapes from a research facility with an atomic bomb. In a letter to the British Prime Minister, he threatened to demolish central London if the government did not announce the end of research in the area within a week. Special agents from Scotland Yard attempt to hunt down and arrest the mad scientist, with the help of the latter's assistant.

anchor_text: When Sidney Young, a columnist for an obscure English newspaper, is hired by Sharps, New York's famous hype magazine, he rejoices, convinced that his place in Manhattan's elite nightlife is assured. But as Alison, his handsome superior, makes him understand, his unashamed side isn't to everyone's taste. Sidney starts to accumulate the crap, and he sets out to seduce Sophie Maes, the bimbo of the moment...
text_a: Jake Davis (Russell Crowe) is a Pulitzer Prize winning novelist. His life is not at all easy as he has to deal with the education of his 5-year-old daughter after the tragic death of his wife in a car accident. Katie's upbringing in New York is not easy. Jake is mentally unstable because of his nervous breakdowns. Twenty years later, Katie (Amanda Seyfried), becomes a Social Worker who copes with the consequences of her difficult childhood by attending to children with psychological problems.
text_b: British journalist Sidney Young (Simon Pegg) is faced with the opportunity of a lifetime: working for New York's most prestigious magazine. The journalist, who will be involved in unfortunate situations in New York's high society, will then have to deal with fellow journalist Alison (Kirsten Dunst) and budding actress Sophie (Megan Fox).

anchor_text: War correspondent Sally Tyler works in Beirut during the Cold War. There she meets Leo Cauffield, who works for British intelligence. She falls in love and marries him. One day, Leo disappears without a trace. Soon it turns out that Cauffield was a double agent working for the KGB. Sally's following him to Moscow.
text_a: Leo and Lily, two con artists, are partners in business and in life. Lily wants to get out of crime and move to a nice place. Leo agrees and estimates that within a week, they should have enough to leave for good. But not everything goes as planned.
text_b: Leo Cauffield, war correspondent, former British spy and husband of Sally Tyler, an American woman, disappears without explanation one day. His wife Sally does all the research she can to find him and discovers that her husband is a double agent working for the KGB... This film is inspired by the story of Eleanor Brewer who, in 1959, married Kim Philby, a double or even triple agent, who infiltrated the British secret services on behalf of the KGB.

anchor_text: Johann Strauss, son of the famous waltz composer of the same name, works in a bank at the beginning of his illustrious career. One day he'll get fired for thinking too much about the music and too little about the customers at the counter. His friend Poldi Vogelhuber, the lovely daughter of the bank director, supports Johann in his ambition to become a musician. The road to success is rocky, but with Poldi, now as his wife, at his side, Johann Strauss's fame grows. But it wasn't until soprano Carla Donner brought the melodies to the masses. Soon Johann will not be able to resist Carla's charms. Poldi sits alone at home, bearing her fate as a betrayed wife with stoic composure. She is so self-sacrificing that she wants to agree to a divorce just to make John happy. Carla is not willing to build her own happiness on the misfortune of another and refuses to continue the relationship. Johann and Poldi will be happy again.
text_a: The highly fictional story sees "Schani" fired from his job at a bank. He assembles a group of unemployed musicians who give a performance at Dommayer's café. The audience is minimal, but when two opera singers, Carla Donner (Miliza Korjus) and Fritz Schiller (George Houston), visit while their carriage is being repaired, the music attracts a wider audience. Strauss becomes embroiled in a student protest; he and Carla Donner evade arrest and escape to the Vienna Woods, where he is inspired to compose the waltz "Tales from the Vienna Woods". Carla asks Strauss for some music to sing at an aristocratic evening, and that leads to the composer receiving a publishing contract. He's on his way and now he can marry Poldi Vogelhuber, his girlfriend. But the closeness of Strauss and Carla Donner, during the operetta rehearsals, attracts comments, especially from Count Hohenfried, Donner's admirer. Poldi remains loyal to Strauss and the marriage is long. He is received by Kaiser Franz Joseph I of Austria (whom he unknowingly insulted in the wake of the student protests), and the two stand before crowds on the balcony of Schönbrunn.
text_b: This is Vienna, 1906. Baroness Lena Eggersdorf has been having an affair with the young and attractive Franz Lobheimer for a year. This one wants to break up but doesn't know how. One evening, his best friend and confidant, Lieutenant Theo Kaiser, drove him to Grinzing and introduced him to two charming young women: one dark and mischievous, named Mizzie and the other blonde and secretive, Christine. A flirtation develops between Mizzie and Theo while Christine and Franz, intimidated, remain face to face without daring to look at each other. On escorting Christine home, Franz learns that she is the daughter of an opera musician, Mr. Weiring, and that she is almost engaged to a bland and ridiculous composer. # Synopsis from The film paints a picture of Viennese society before World War I. Lieutenant Franz Lobheimer is having an affair with Baroness Eggersdorf. Through his friend Theo Kaiser, he meets Christine, the daughter of a chamber musician. The two young people fall in love. But Franz's past catches up with him: the baroness's husband discovers evidence of their adulterous relationship and demands reparation. He demands that the matter be settled by a duel. At the risk of losing his position, Theo tries to convince the military hierarchy to intervene in the case, to no avail. In the duel, Franz was mortally wounded. Disgusted by the codes of honor that cost his friend his life, Theo left the army and returned to civilian life. When he and his girlfriend, Mizzi, announce to Christine the death of her beloved, Christine, in despair, throws herself out the window.

anchor_text: In Chicago, a young man must face the trials of marriage and married life, between the desire to keep his boyish life, the love he has for his new wife, and their respective parents' rush to have grandchildren.
text_a: Jo is a woman who has been married several times. It seems that with Jake, her last husband, she has finally found the stability she needed; but when she discovers that Jake is unfaithful to her and, in addition, she is pregnant, everything will fall apart.
text_b: Jake and Kristy Bridges are a young newlywed couple moving to the suburbs of Chicago. He's an aspiring advertising novelist, she's a research analyst. After the excitement of the honeymoon begins the problems of everyday life, and especially those related to a fatherhood that Jake is not ready to face.

anchor_text: Jake and Kristy Bridges are a young newlywed couple moving to the suburbs of Chicago. He's an aspiring advertising novelist, she's a research analyst. After the excitement of the honeymoon begins the problems of everyday life, and especially those related to a fatherhood that Jake is not ready to face.
text_a: Jake and Kristy Briggs are newlyweds. Being young, they may be somewhat prepared for the full reality of marriage and everything it (and their parents) expects of them. He will abandon his dream of becoming a writer, while she works as a telephone operator and devotes herself to the home. The film comically portrays a full-blown marital crisis, with its misunderstandings, frustrations and anger.
text_b: Jo is a woman who has been married several times. It seems that with Jake, her last husband, she has finally found the stability she needed; but when she discovers that Jake is unfaithful to her and, in addition, she is pregnant, everything will fall apart.

anchor_text: During the Cold War in the West, Josef Popov and Ivan Federenko are two aging Soviet spies who gradually want to retire. In order to secure their retirement, they plan the next big thing: they want to acquire a microfilm that they can sell for a profit. But they are not the only ones who are after this important secret material. Emily Hopkins, a leading Salvation Army soldier on the Lord's errand, has long since seized the film and plans to make the most earthly use of it: she wants to sell the coveted recording to the Chinese intelligence service, which is also very interested. All sorts of complications arise for the three elderly gentlemen, especially since the British and Americans, as well as two seductive young ladies, Ludmila and Miss Ly, are also involved.
text_a: During the Cold War, in the West. Josef Popov and Ivan Federenko are two aging Soviet spies who wish to gradually retire. In order to secure their old age, they plan the very big move shortly before their retirement: they want to acquire a microfilm that they want to sell for a profit. But they're not alone in pursuing this important intelligence material. Emily Hopkins, who is a high-ranking representative of the Salvation Army on a mission for the Lord, finds herself in possession of the film and plans to give it to the highest bidder as the Chinese secret services are very interested. This leads to all sorts of entanglements in which the elderly gentlemen become entangled, especially since the British and the Americans as well as two attractive young women, Ludmila and Miss Ly, took turns.
text_b: Two clumsy and simple-minded American friends, Audrey and Morgan, find themselves embroiled in an international intrigue after Audrey discovers that her ex-husband was a CIA agent who, before dying, asks her to bring to Vienna an object contested by secret services and terrorist movements around the world. Morgan's experiences as a gymnast and Audrey's practice of video games, in addition to a brazen fortune, allow the girls to cross the whole of Europe almost unscathed and complete the mission, not without cruelly slaughtering (and often unknowingly) the dozens of militarily trained and armed spies to the teeth who try uselessly to hinder them.

anchor_text: Fourteen-year-old Nicole is on vacation with her father. She falls in love with a 17-year-old boy, Ben, whom her father thinks is way too old for her. Tired of being treated like a child when she is at her father's side, Nicole invents a whole story in order to spice up her journey: her father is actually her lover, who saved her from a life of debauchery a few years earlier - which will give rise to humorous situations, as Nicole's father is unaware of the reputation his daughter is making him.
text_a: André Arnel, a Frenchman divorced from his wife, takes his daughter Nicole on vacation to the Bahamas. She's desperate to impress a local boy, Ben. He makes up increasingly ridiculous stories, starting with André being his lover and leading to some bizarre assumptions by the rest of the community. André is desperate to make Nicole happy (especially since she is increasingly upset about his relationship with his girlfriend Isobel) and so he plays with their crazy games, and the stories they invent become increasingly bizarre.
text_b: André Ripois, a Frenchman living in London, is a real dongiovanni. His wife Catherine, tired of the constant betrayals, leaves him to go to Edinburgh, where she wants to get a divorce. He, infatuated with Patricia, a friend of his wife, tries in every way to seduce the girl, going to extremes. Pretending to want to commit suicide, he jumps out of the window but falls badly and is paralyzed. The wife, believing the same as Patrizia that André wanted to commit suicide because of the divorce, decides to return to him: André becomes, against his will, a prisoner for life of his wife.

anchor_text: An apocalyptic earthquake is devastating the coast of California. Eighty thousand people are dead, and the cities are in anarchy. A gang of surfing neo-Nazis, consisting of the leader Adolf, his girlfriend Eva and Mengele, wants to take advantage of the situation to bring the area under their control. Several rival groups, such as the Samurai Surfers, are eliminated by the brutal march after a brief counterattack. One day, the surf Nazis run into the black Leroy Washington and kill him after an argument. When Leroy's mother learns of her son's death, she gathers an arsenal of weapons and launches a bloody and successful campaign of revenge.
text_a: Separately, two men on the beach watch as young Francesco Villaverde apparently suffocates Mrs. Simmons after a conversation. The next day, her husband assigns Detective Bob Martin to retrieve an expensive necklace from the deceased. With the support of a colleague and his girlfriend, Martin now deals with the circumstances of the wealthy young man who lives with his mentally ill wife in a luxurious mansion. At the same time, one of the witnesses tries to blackmail Locatelli, Villaverde, as they have debts with the Mafia, which sends two agents with Sophia Ferretti and the "Commodore". Villaverde attempts to strangle Sophia at a meeting; it fails but she is murdered by Locatelli. This is eliminated by the Commodore, who in turn is mortally wounded by Bob Martin. He reveals that Locatelli was responsible for both the murders and the theft. Classified as a danger to the community, Francesco takes his own life.
text_b: An earthquake leaves the coast of California in ruins and reduces the beaches to a state of chaos. A group of neo-Nazis led by Adolf (Brenner), the self-proclaimed "Führer of the New Beaches", take advantage of the resulting chaos to fight off several rival surf gangs for control of the beaches. Meanwhile, Nazi surfers kill an African-American oil worker named Leroy (Harden) while jogging on the beach. Leroy's mother, "Mama" Washington (Neely), devastated by the loss of her son, vows revenge. After arming himself with a gun and grenades, he leaves his retirement home to take revenge on the Nazi surfers.

anchor_text: An apocalyptic earthquake is devastating the coast of California. Eighty thousand people are dead, and the cities are in anarchy. A gang of surfing neo-Nazis, consisting of the leader Adolf, his girlfriend Eva and Mengele, wants to take advantage of the situation to bring the area under their control. One day, the surf Nazis run into the black Leroy Washington and kill him after an argument. text_a: After a violent earthquake in Los Angeles, the city has fallen into chaos and youth gangs rule undisturbed. The most dangerous gang is the "Surf Nazis", a group of neo-Nazi surfers who rule the beaches, led by Adolf (Barry Brenner), the self-proclaimed beach Führer. Adolf is ruthless, wears a black leather jacket and never leaves his long knife. His girlfriend is Eva (Dawn Wildsmith), who is also the gang's cook and doctor. Adolf's right-hand man is Mengele (Michael Sonye), a bloodthirsty killer who invents all of the group's weapons. Hook (Joel Hile) is one of the most dangerous Nazi surfers. He has a hook for his right arm, which was severed by a shark. Finally there is Smeg (Tom Shell), the youngest of the group, who spends all his free time with the Nazi surfers. One day, a young African-American man named Leroy (Robert Harden) is killed by Nazi surfers. This is the beginning of their end: in fact, Leroy's mother, Eleanor 'Mama' Washington (Gail Neely), obese, arms herself with rifles and grenades and starts her revenge, exterminating the whole group.
text_b: Separately, two men on the beach watch as young Francesco Villaverde apparently suffocates Mrs. Simmons after a conversation. The next day, her husband assigns Detective Bob Martin to retrieve an expensive necklace from the deceased. With the support of a colleague and his girlfriend, Martin now deals with the circumstances of the wealthy young man who lives with his mentally ill wife in a luxurious mansion. At the same time, one of the witnesses tries to blackmail Locatelli, Villaverde, as they have debts with the Mafia, which sends two agents with Sophia Ferretti and the "Commodore". Villaverde attempts to strangle Sophia at a meeting; it fails but she is murdered by Locatelli. This is eliminated by the Commodore, who in turn is mortally wounded by Bob Martin. He reveals that Locatelli was responsible for both the murders and the theft. Classified as a danger to the community, Francesco takes his own life.

anchor_text: Buzz Rickson is stationed in England during World War II as a pilot of a Boeing B-17, a so-called Flying Fortress. The egotist is unpopular with his team. He's a risk-taker and he loves to fight, and he's good at it. His co-pilot, Lieutenant Boland, is the only one Rickson can handle. When young Daphne falls in love with Boland instead of Rickson, there is a conflict between the two. Rickson completely freaks out and tries to rape Daphne. That's the end of our friendship with Boland. Although Rickson enjoys his "victory" at first, on the next mission he lacks the other self-confidence. Rickson is plagued by doubts as to whether he can really afford anything. When Rickson is badly injured on a mission, Boland proposes to abort the mission and abandon the machine by jumping off. But Rickson wants to prove himself and get the machine back to base safely. He's dropping the crew over the English Channel, essentially forcing Boland to do it. Rickson doesn't make it, his plane crashes on the cliffs of Dover.
text_a: A failed emergency landing in the African jungle kills Richard Lancing, his wife and the pilot. The only survivor is Lancing's son, who is found and "adopted" by Tarzan and Jane. They call the boy Boy. Five years later, Sir Thomas Lancing sets out to find his relatives. The safari under his command finds the plane wreckage, prompting Sir Thomas to continue the search. His cousin Austin, who would benefit from Richard's proven death, wants to thwart this. Austin and Thomas meet Tarzan and Jane, who explain that there were no survivors from the crash. Thomas is not convinced by the family resemblance of Boys with the Lancings. Austin's wife proposes to employ the boy as a servant in order to control the expected inheritance. Thomas agrees to the plan. Austin asks him to convince Jane to hand over the boy. Tarzan is against the agreement, but is betrayed by Jane, who lures him into a cave. Jane leads the safari through the jungle until she is alerted to Austin's real plan by Thomas, who has grown scrupulous. Before Thomas can get Tarzan to help him, Austin kills him. When the safari is captured by cannibals, Jane risks her life to allow Boy to escape. Boy returns and frees Tarzan from the cave. With a herd of elephants, Tarzan attacks the cannibal village. He saves the badly wounded Jane and forgives her.
text_b: World War II: A group of American pilots are transferred to England as a base for air raids over Germany. In the squadron, the character differences of the components emerge: the lover of flight and war as reckless as he is allergic to military discipline, the lighter to the regulations who performs his duty only as required by his country, the one who carries out his duties with tranquility thinking of the family at home, the young and inexperienced boy who cannot contain fear, etc. Over time, forced cohabitation will inevitably lead to conflicts that shift the battle from the military to the personal.

anchor_text: In the final days of World War II, an American soldier and a Japanese soldier were stranded separately on an uninhabited island in the Pacific. As members of two warring factions, they are opponents, and the images of enemies they have in their minds make life and survival on the island difficult for them. They steal each other's scarce supplies and take each other prisoner. In time, however, they realize that only by helping each other can they survive together. They decide to build a raft together to escape the island. In the end, however, both are killed by a bomb blast.
text_a: During World War II in the Pacific Ocean, an American pilot is shot down and stranded on an island. This seemingly deserted island is already occupied by a Japanese sailor. Each will try to take possession of the island. Eventually, they will build a raft together and reach a ruined camp. But pictures of tortured soldiers and more generally their cultural differences will challenge their mutual cooperation.
text_b: During World War II, after the Battle of Midway and before the end of the war in the Pacific. A lost island in the Solomon Islands is the scene of a clash between a small battalion of Japanese soldiers who survived an amphibious operation and a group of U.S. Marines who survived the crash of the C-47 that was carrying them. Sometimes enemies, sometimes friends, the two groups of men gradually learn to live in harmony until reality calls them to order: honor, survival or death.

anchor_text: In the final days of World War II, an American soldier and a Japanese soldier were stranded separately on an uninhabited island in the Pacific. As members of two warring factions, they are opponents, and the images of enemies they have in their minds make life and survival on the island difficult for them. They steal each other's scarce supplies and take each other prisoner. In time, however, they realize that only by helping each other can they survive together. They decide to build a raft together to escape the island. In the end, however, both are killed by a bomb blast.
text_a: A Japanese soldier (Toshirō Mifune) who has been shipwrecked on a deserted Pacific island one day watches as another castaway arrives, this time a USAF officer (Lee Marvin). After discovering each other, both men begin an arduous personal confrontation trying to steal the resources of survival, then fall prisoner (alternately and successively) of the adversary and suffer slavery and mistreatment of this. However, the loneliness of the island eventually imposes a symbiotic relationship of collaboration between the two, in order to build a raft that will allow them to return to civilization. From that moment on, the two men's antagonism gradually turns into camaraderie, and eventually friendship, until they arrive on an island that was recently occupied and abandoned.
text_b: During World War II, after the Battle of Midway and before the end of the war in the Pacific. A lost island in the Solomon Islands is the scene of a clash between a small battalion of Japanese soldiers who survived an amphibious operation and a group of U.S. Marines who survived the crash of the C-47 that was carrying them. Sometimes enemies, sometimes friends, the two groups of men gradually learn to live in harmony until reality calls them to order: honor, survival or death.

anchor_text: Soichi Negishi is a shy and gentle young man who dreams of a career as a Swedish pop musician. However, his dreams did not come true as he should have, so he eventually became the lead singer and guitarist of a death metal band called "Detroit Metal City". In stage costume, he is Johannes Krauser II. Rumor has it that he is a terror demon from hell, that he killed and raped his parents, and that he exercises his giant death penis (Death Penis) without restraint (he is accused of many rapes), he can also say the word "rape" ten times in a second. Negishi despises DMC and everything it stands for, but he can't shake it because with him, he knows fame, unlike when he tries to demonstrate his talent for pop.
text_a: Soichi Negishi is a shy and kind man who dreams of a career as a Swedish pop singer. Dreams don't pay the bills, so he ended up as the lead singer and guitarist for a death metal band called "Detroit Metal City". In the stadium is Johannes Krauser II, rumored to be a demon from hell and a terrorist-like, who has killed and raped his parents, etc, etc Negishi despises everything DMC stands for, but also helps him, he is a fabulous artist and can't walk away from that. As a star of DMC Negishi tries to satisfy both worlds, that of his true tastes and passions in which he also tries to conquer the girl he likes and the world of Death metal. As a curiosity, Negishi has a poster of Amélie (French film Le Fabuleux Destin d'Amélie Poulain) in his room.
text_b: Yamamoto, a yakuza from Tokyo, refuses to submit to the rival clan that had his leader killed. With a price on his head, he left Japan and joined his half-brother Ken in the United States. So he arrives in Los Angeles, not speaking a word of English, and discovers a society he doesn't know. But he soon finds his half-brother, who's become a small-time dealer. During an altercation between his half-brother and the dealer, Yamamoto intervenes and beats up the dealer. In this way, he gradually formed his own clan by applying the methods of the yakuza.

anchor_text: Soichi Negishi is a shy and gentle young man who dreams of a career as a Swedish pop musician. However, his dreams did not come true as he should have, so he eventually became the lead singer and guitarist of a death metal band called "Detroit Metal City".
