Negishi despises DMC and everything it stands for, but he can't shake it because with him, he knows fame, unlike when he tries to demonstrate his talent for pop.
text_a: Soichi Negishi abandons his life on the family farm in Ōita Prefecture, to move to the big city of Tokyo. Destiny wants him to become nothing less than the lead vocalist and guitarist of the death metal band "Detroit Metal City", a rapidly rising group in the city that has numerous legions of fans. However, his profession as a singer has to clash with his true ego, shy, introverted and awkward, as well as decidedly more passionate about Swedish pop music than death metal, of which he is even considered Emperor, under the pseudonym of Johannes Krauser II. So many situations of various types are articulated that they see on the field the contest of the two contrasting faces of Soichi's life, on the one hand a simple boy like many, on the other the king of extreme metal.
text_b: Yamamoto, a yakuza from Tokyo, refuses to submit to the rival clan that had his leader killed. With a price on his head, he left Japan and joined his half-brother Ken in the United States. So he arrives in Los Angeles, not speaking a word of English, and discovers a society he doesn't know. But he soon finds his half-brother, who's become a small-time dealer. During an altercation between his half-brother and the dealer, Yamamoto intervenes and beats up the dealer. In this way, he gradually formed his own clan by applying the methods of the yakuza.

anchor_text: In 1944, during World War II, Lieutenant Colonel Clemens Forell boarded a train to fight on the front in the Soviet Union. He bids farewell to his wife Kathrin and his daughter in the hope of returning home for Christmas. But as a Soviet prisoner of war, Forell is sentenced to 25 years of hard labor in an East Siberian lead mine at Cape Deschnjow. After a first escape attempt, he is beaten half to death by his comrades in a spikebreaking race because they have been punished for his escape attempt with deprivation of food. Forell meets the German doctor Heinz Stauffer, who prepares himself for an escape, but knows that he has not long to live. So Dr. Stauffer Forell bequeaths his equipment and informs him of a possible escape route. When the Soviet commander Kamenev notices Forell's second escape, he finds Stauffer dead in his room. Kamenev pursues Forell through the Soviet Union, but he escapes several times. When Forell, with the help of the Armenian Jew Igor, whom he meets in Kasalinsk, is able to obtain a passport and crosses the border bridge, Forell and Kamenev meet in a "Western way". Kamenev lets trout pass with the words "I defeated you!" In Tehran, Forell is arrested in December 1952 on suspicion of being a Soviet spy, but his uncle Erich Baudrexel, who has been working in Turkey for several years, is able to identify him. Finally, on Christmas Eve, Forell returns home to his family.
text_a: Clemens Forell (Benhard Bettermann), a lieutenant in the Wehrmacht, is taken captive by the Soviet Army at the end of World War II and is sent along with tens of thousands of prisoners to a Gulag in Siberia in winter through the steppes on a hellish trek. Before being sent to the front he had promised his daughter and wife to come back alive. Along the way, several prisoners are executed, frozen, or killed in accidents. Arriving at the Gulag labor camp, he will be subjected to the brutal treatment reserved for millions of political dissidents and German prisoners of war whose purpose is to exterminate them for forced labor in a coal mine. At the camp they are met by Lieutenant Colonel Kamenev, a Hero of the Soviet Union who attempts to subdue Forell's will and competes with him to bend him. Forell just wants to escape even though the Gulag is in the middle of the steppe and it's winter. A first escape attempt is unsuccessful, but a second attempt, aided by a German doctor in charge of the prisoners, is successful, initiating the protagonist an epic trek through hostile territory through the wild Siberian landscapes, always with the idea of returning home and fulfilling the promise he made to his wife and young daughter before leaving for the front: to return home soon. During their escape, the SA is aided by women, gypsies, Eskimos and a Jew to the happy end. The real name was not revealed until much later:[1] Cornelius Rost.[2]
text_b: The Russian Sergeant Grisha Paprotkin is a prisoner of war of the German army and is housed in a camp in Poland. One winter night in 1917, he managed to escape. He's visiting his wife, Babka, and his newborn child. For a while, the little family lives unmolested in Poland. But Grisha is filled with the desire to return to Russia. Babka helps him with a dead soldier's uniform. Grisha is captured by the Germans again. The commander of the Eastern Front, General Schieffenzahn, sees the prisoner as a spy and sentences him to death. Grisha is obsessed with his new identity. Nevertheless, his lawyer Posnanski and his nephew Winfried are trying to overturn the execution order. Babka appears as a peddler at the camp and plans Grisha's escape. But Grisha doesn't want to run away, he wants to be rehabilitated. He seeks help from General von Lychow, who, after a fierce debate, sends the order to suspend the execution. However, the telegram does not arrive because a storm hits the telegraph pole. Grisha collapses and is led to execution.

anchor_text: With the accidental death of one of his drivers, team boss Kazarian is forced to look around for a new teammate for his NASCAR driver Mike Marsh. He can win the ambitious Ned Arp for his team. After a short time, however, he must realize that Arp is not a team player, but an unscrupulous and ice-cold type. Arp is going after Kazarian's sister, which threatens to further escalate tensions on the team. The tide will not settle until Marsh and Arp take their first victories in some major races. When renowned racing driver and longtime rival of Marsh, Dan McCall, arrives from Paris with his lover Gabrielle, the rivalry between the two men flares up again. McCall becomes increasingly interested in other women, and the beautiful Gabrielle turns to the wayward Marsh.
text_a: The racing team run by Pat Kazarian includes two drivers, Mike Marsh and Jim Loomis, but an accident at Daytona results in Jim's death. His girlfriend, Holly, arrives too late for the race and feels guilty about not being there. Young rider Ned Arp joins the team and also tries out Kazarian's sister, Julie. A third pilot, Dan McCall, arrives from France and brings his girlfriend Gabrielle with him, but soon develops an interest in Holly. Arp is badly injured in an accident and loses a hand. Mike, meanwhile, doesn't care about Dan's business with women and tries to take him out during a race, but Dan survives. He and Holly are engaged, but Mike is comforted by Gabrielle.
text_b: One evening, Jan, a successful lawyer and womanizer, is forced to watch through the window of his expensive apartment as a parked car scratches his red convertible. Instead, he meets the driver, the equally successful publisher Katrin. Despite initial mutual accusations, the two soon become a couple. With them, as well as with Jan's brother Rüdiger and his pregnant girlfriend Melanie, various theses on the behavior of men and women are played through in the episode. Relationship challenges arise from differences in communication styles, thinking and feeling, and the occurrence of alternative sexual partners. Jan instinctively hires the smug blonde Angie as his secretary; Katrin is impressed by the world adventurer Jonathan Armbruster. Jan's near fling with the secretary is prevented by the appearance of her jealous ex Sven. However, his confession to Katrin has the consequence that she gets involved with arm-twisting. John's attempts to win back Catherine's affection are initially unsuccessful. But in the end, Katrin chooses him.

anchor_text: Twelve-year-old half-wit Jack Sawyer is desperate: his mother, actress and B-movie "queen" of the 1960s, has cancer and is already dying. On the run from Morgan Sloat, the former business partner of his father who was killed in a hunting accident years ago, the two have taken up residence in an old, dilapidated hotel on the East Coast. At a nearby amusement park, Jack soon encounters the mysterious Speedy Parker, who tells him about the even more mysterious territories, a mystical world beyond our known reality, where magic is as natural as technology in our world. Jack has known the Territories since his earliest childhood: they are the land of his constant daydreams. Through Speedy's enlightenment and a bottle of mysterious potion, he develops the ability to switch between the territories at will with the help of the flipper. His long-dead twin, he now learns, was Jason DeLoessian, the son of the Queen of the Territories, Laura DeLoessian. She suffers just like Jack's mother from a deadly disease and the entire magical world is threatened by a malevolent enemy who turns out to be none other than the doppelganger of Jack's evil Uncle Morgan Sloat who hunts him and his mother in the normal world. Jack's task now is to travel to the western territories in search of the magical talisman that he can use to heal his mother and the queen. When he receives help from an unexpected source, he is accompanied for a time by a friendly and loyal werewolf. But Sloat and his minions have long noticed that someone is trying to thwart their sinister plans, and before Jack knows what's happening to him, he's drawn into a conflict that began long before he was born and involves nothing less than the fate of the two worlds in which he lives.
text_a: Twelve-year-old Jack Sawyer (one of the first great references of this play is in the last name of its protagonist), knows that his mother is dying. Or at least he strongly suspects it. However, his mother, a wry, sharp-tongued former actress, seems to be running away from someone in particular, someone very close, as she and her son waste their days in a desolate coastal hotel. Suddenly, Sawyer finds out some very disturbing things about his family history, his business and himself, and must set off across the country from coast to coast to find a cure for his mother's illness. The adventure alone would be enormous for a 12-year-old boy if it were not for the fact that his journey does not begin in the same world he lives in, and that there are things out there willing to stop him, and because his mission (commissioned by an old black bluesman who takes care of an amusement park in the off-season) affects the fate of more than one world and of more than one dying mother, kingdoms, domains and universes. On the way to the black hotel where the fabulous talisman, axis of worlds, is kept, Jack will find out even more things, most of them unpleasant, about the nature of his quest, the powers that oppose him and about other dying mothers on whom he depends so much. There's still room for wonder and magic in Jack's journey, as well as for friendship and camaraderie. However, the closer Jack gets to the heart of the matter, the more twisted, nightmarish and surreal the worlds he is able to see become, and the more terrible the opposition.
text_b: Jack and Bette Tremont (Jack Lemmon and Olympia Dukakis) are a quiet retired couple where the housewife handles the couple's daily life with an iron fist. Until the day Bette was hospitalized for a heart attack. Overnight, the quiet Jack Tremont finds himself alone at home for the first time in his life, master of his own destiny. Of course, such freedom may at first make him a little wary. Therefore, he decides to call on his children John (Ted Danson) and Annie (Kathy Baker) to give him a hand in the gestures of daily life. John is a successful businessman, always up and down in his career, and it is in these circumstances that he becomes closer (more than he has ever been) to his father: he teaches his father to become more independent and self-reliant, more in his image, and he gradually realizes in the process, that he himself is estranged from his own son, Billy (Ethan Hawke). So it's through learning about his relationship with his father that he discovers his relationship with his son...

anchor_text: The film tells the story of Léo Lauzon, a 14-year-old boy who lives with his family in poor conditions in a house in Montreal. Some members of the family are mentally ill and the boy uses his imagination to escape from real life and be free from fear. He believes that his beloved mother was not impregnated by his father, but by an imported tomato that accidentally entered her vagina, on which his Italian father had ejaculated in the face of the charms of a well-built young Italian woman. From now on, his name is Léolo. The boys around him are not a good environment for him either: when drunk, they have sex with a female cat. Léolo's sexuality is awakened as he dreams of the slightly older Italian girl next door, Bianca. In the end, the disease that has spread to his family catches up with him and sends him to the psychiatric ward. There, for example, he is "treated" with ice baths that are supposed to cure his illness. A key role in the film is played by the book L'avalée des avalés by Réjean Ducharme. The book was first translated into German in 2012 (Von Verschlungenen verschlungen, Traversion 2012).
text_a: A boy named Léo Lauzon (Maxime Collin), trapped in a shattering reality, tries to prevent the environment from consuming him, allowing himself to be dragged into the placid world of dreams and words. In this context he imagines that he is the son of a fertilized Italian tomato and calls himself Léolo Lozone. In the midst of a family socioeconomically and genetically destined for madness, Léo (Léolo) is the only one who is saved from it. "Because I'm dreaming, I'm not dreaming", he repeated. The oppressive and deranged reality contrasts with the child's dreams and inventions, poetic and suggestive. In addition to Léo's family, other characters mark his existence: Bianca, the Sicilian neighbor with whom he is in love, or the Tamer of Words, who collects verses and phrases to save them from destruction. Léolo's screenplay is a free adaptation of the novel L'Avalée des avalés (published in 2009 as El valle de los avasallados), the first published work by Quebec writer Réjean Ducharme.
text_b: Leo likes to hang out and watch movies. He sees all of this in jeopardy when his girlfriend Louise reveals to him that she is pregnant. Her brother Louis, on the other hand, a racist thug, is overjoyed at the prospect of becoming an uncle. Leo's friend Lenny, on the other hand, who can recite his favorite B-horror comics like "The Texas Chainsaw Massacre", "Maniac", or "Andy Warhol's Frankenstein", is fresh off the boat. His dream wife, Lea, works nearby, and although she's more interested in books than movies, the movie-crazed maniac manages to make short dialogues. Leo, meanwhile, is struggling to cope with the prospect of fatherhood, and the relationship is falling apart. Leo gets more and more violent, until eventually the situation escalates. He kicks Louise's abdomen so hard that she loses her child. Louis seeks revenge and infects Leo with HIV through the blood of a heroin addict. Completely out of control, Leo now transmits the virus to Louis by shooting him and allowing his blood to flow into him, after which he shoots himself. By the end of the film, Lenny manages to overcome his shyness. He dates Lea and manages to have deeper conversations.

anchor_text: Johnnie is the mechanic ( the mechanic ) of a locomotive called the General, which is owned by the Western and Atlantic Railroad, a railroad company in the eastern United States. When the Civil War broke out, Johnnie had two loves: his train and Annabelle Lee. As he cannot enlist in the army, Annabelle refuses his love after his brother explains to him by misunderstanding that he did not even see Johnnie in the queue to enlist. However, when the General is stolen by Union spies, with Anabelle on board, Johnnie does not hesitate to go after his train and his fiancée. After infiltrating the Union Army, Johnnie rescues Annabelle through disguise after accidentally overhearing the secret plans of generals. She and he fled by train, pursued by the enemy army. After many adventures and defeating the Union by aiding the Confederates, Johnnie was promoted to lieutenant and reunited with his fiancée.
text_a: The film depicts the story of Johnnie Gray (Buster Keaton), a Western & Atlantic Railroad engineer who has two passions in his life, his locomotive The General and his girlfriend Annabelle Lee (Marion Mack). When war breaks out, Annabelle asks Johnnie to enlist in the Confederate army to fight against the Union. Despite his efforts to enroll, he is not admitted because he is considered more useful in his profession as a machinist than as a soldier. Annabelle Lee believes her denial of admission to the army is due to her cowardice and decides not to speak to Johnnie anymore. A year later, Annabelle has to travel because her father is injured, and she does so in Johnnie's locomotive, to whom she still does not speak. Midway through the tour a group of Northerners, attempting to link up with the Union Army in Chattanooga, decide to go ahead with a plan that includes the robbery of La General. When they carry out their escape they discover that Annabelle remains in the locomotive and take her as a prisoner. Johnnie will try to recover his locomotive himself and at the same time his beloved, although from the beginning he does not know...
text_b: Detective Brown is to accompany the widow of a gang boss on the train ride from Chicago to Los Angeles. It's for the protection of Mrs. Neall, who's about to testify in court. Brown is accompanied by his partner Forbes, who is killed by gangsters immediately after meeting with the widow. At the train station, Brown recognizes that the gangster Kemp is following him. On board the train, Brown makes the acquaintance of the attractive Ann Sinclair and her inquisitive son Tommy. Kemp sees Brown with his acquaintance and concludes that Ann is his target. Kemp's trying to bribe Brown. It'll let Brown know Kemp was wrong. He hands Kemp over to overweight fellow passenger Jennings and warns Ann. But she surprises him by telling him she's the real Mrs. Neall. The woman Brown has so far mistaken for Mrs. Neall is actually Sarah Meggs and serves as a decoy for potential pursuers. Kemp's partner, Densel, who killed Forbes, can take down Jennings and free Kemp. They storm Brown's compartment and kill Sarah Meggs. Then Densel is looking for Mrs. Neall. He locks himself in her compartment with her. Brown can shoot Densel. To do this, he shoots through the door when he sees Denzel's reflection in a second suit at a train station. Brown kills Densel as he enters the compartment. Kemp manages to jump from the halting train, but is quickly arrested.

anchor_text: At the end of the 19th century, in Canada. The gold rush is in full swing, and hundreds of prospectors need sled dogs to get to the snowy sites of the Klondike. One of these dogs was Buck, a dog stolen from a wealthy owner and sold to John Thornton, who was commissioned by the government to deliver mail to these inhospitable areas. Together, they will face a thousand dangers, challenge a particularly hostile nature, be attacked by wolves, fight Indians, and become the best friends in the world.
text_a: John Thornton is a gold prospector who participates in the Klondike Gold Rush in 1897. Thornton adapts to life in the untamed regions of the Yukon and will have to fight against cruel and ruthless rivals who try to rob him. With him is only his trusty dog Buck, also characterized by a sad and peculiar history, with whom he forms a deep and sincere friendship that goes beyond that one could have with a human being. Thornton will be killed by the Yeehat Indians, at which point Buck will be able to kill the killer himself. After avenging his great friend, Buck will arrive on the banks of the White River, where he will die in peace.
text_b: Gold fever is a contagion that's sweeping across the United States from west to east. From Dawson City, Berna also leaves with her old blind grandfather, to go to the Klondike to open a place of refreshment for the miners. During the hard journey, the grandfather dies and Berna strengthens her relationship with Larry, an adventurous young man who also went in search of gold. Together with Lars, Jim and Samuel, Larry finds a vein of gold and, while the other comrades go to town to search the mine, he stays on guard with Samuel who, however, betrays him, leaving him to starve to death. Larry manages to escape, while Samuel ends up being attacked by wolves that tear him apart. Back in town, Larry finds Berna who, forced by Locust, has become a lost woman. After a deadly encounter with Locust, Larry stays with Berna, trying to put their lives back together, along with Lars and Jim.

anchor_text: Twelve years later, Dante Hicks and his friend Randal Graves are still working at the Quick Stop, a grocery store owned by Leonardo in New Jersey. But one morning as Dante opens the metal shutter, he discovers the grocery store is on fire. Randal forgot to turn off the coffee machine again... A few months later, Dante and Randal are now employees of Mooby's, a fast-food restaurant run by Becky Scott. Jay and Silent Bob, who were always squatting in front of the Quick Stop, have followed them and are now squatting in front of the restaurant. Randal keeps arguing with another Mooby's employee, young Elias. But Dante announces to Becky and Randal that he is leaving them to follow his future wife, Emma, to Florida, where her father offers him a home and a job. Randal, saddened by the departure of his best friend, throws a party in his honor and wants to rehabilitate an expression, which, according to him, is not racist. Becky, after a dance lesson for Dante, learns that he is in love with her. Moreover, she announces to him that she is pregnant with his child. Dante is torn between leaving with Emma and starting a new life or staying with Becky, whom he's been in love with for a while.
text_a: Burned the Quick Stop, the old store where Randal and Dante worked for a decade, the two now earn their salary from Mooby, a fast food run by the beautiful Becky, who has with Dante a friendship always in the process of blossoming into something deeper; Dante, however, is about to marry Emma, with whom he plans to move to Florida shortly after the wedding. Along with Dante and Randall is 19-year-old Elias, obsessed with Transformers and The Lord of the Rings, which makes him a victim of Randall's insults. To complete this little microcosm, outside the store, as in the old days of Quick Stop, dealers Jay and Silent Bob sell amazing drugs. What should be Dante's last day of work at Mooby ends up revolutionizing each of their lives. Dante himself discovers that Becky is expecting a child from him, conceived during a fleeting affair some time earlier. Inevitable misunderstandings lead to a rift between Emma and Dante, as well as Dante's arrest along with Randall, Jay, Silent Bob, and Elias. During the night in prison, put with their backs to the wall and forced to reflect on their shabby lives, the two friends finally have some clarity about their dreams and aspirations.
text_b: Dante is 22 years old and works at the Quick Stop. He's accompanied by his friend Randal from the video store next door. Dante's morning begins badly, he is awakened after a short sleep and has to come to work on his day off. In the quick stop, the roller shutters won't open because the locks were glued together with chewing gum. To add insult to injury, all sorts of strange customers come into the store: militant non-smokers, drug dealer Jay and Silent Bob, a deranged school counselor in search of the perfect egg. In the course of the film, Dante learns that his girlfriend Veronica has already had oral sex with 37 men and that his old love Caitlin wants to marry, in addition, an old school friend has died of an embolism while swimming. Randal is proving to him that things can get even worse in his quiet life. Thanks to him, he is constantly in contact with angry customers who come directly from the video store and take out their bad mood on him. Randal screws up the funeral of his dead high school sweetheart and gives Dante a $500 fine for selling cigarettes to a four-year-old. Despite all the other unpleasant surprises of the day, the two finally understand each other splendidly and philosophize about life, death, sex and Star Wars.

anchor_text: Jim Halsey and Grace Andrews are a young couple traveling across New Mexico in an Oldsmobile 442 to Lake Havasu. On the way, they meet a strange hitchhiker and decide to help him. Little do they know they're dealing with a dangerous killer named John Ryder. The couple manages to escape, and John takes revenge by committing a series of murders that the young couple is blamed for.
text_a: Fifteen years after surviving the tragic events of Hitcher, Jim Halsey has now become a police officer. However, he is currently suspended for being too violent during an interrogation. He decides to visit his former mentor, Captain Esteridge, who lives in Texas. His girlfriend, Maggie, accompanies him on this road trip during which they decide to take a struggling hitchhiker.
text_b: This horror film is about a couple who go on a road trip and on the road they meet a man with the car seemingly broken down, but they pass by. Later, at a gas station, they find him and the boy apologizes for passing by, and they decide to take him to his destination. The man turns out to be a ruthless killer who won't stop chasing the couple throughout the film.

anchor_text: Kim Gyeong-Su, a young and undisciplined Beta student, is expelled from his high school for the ninth time for using his paranormal powers against a teacher. Determined not to use them anymore, he finds himself at Volcano High School, the last chance high school where many students possess the same extrasensory faculties. When he arrives, he finds a chaotic atmosphere, gang wars between students and even between teachers, and he is determined to remain neutral. The origin of these conflicts stems from a persistent rumor that a gifted student or teacher could decipher a mysterious manuscript and be endowed with infinite powers. Teased by some of the students, and despite all his good intentions, Gyeong-Su is forced to face his enemies.
text_a: Determined to do his best in school, young Kim Kyung-soo, a high school student who has already been expelled from nine schools, decides to enroll in the prestigious Volcano High School. Here he finds himself caught in the middle of a war between students and professors who, in an absolutely anarchic and disrespectful way, fight each other for the conquest of the Master's Manuscript, a book of inestimable value hidden in the school through which it is possible to obtain absolute power. The prerogative of those attending the institute is the enormous skill in the use of martial arts and the possession of strange powers, powers that Kyung-Soo is also endowed with. Initially intending to go straight, when the vice-principal takes over the school along with five other powerful professors, Kim Kyung-soo is forced to use his extraordinary skills and step into the field...
text_b: In the near future, Andy Norris, a young music teacher, is appointed as a substitute teacher at Abraham Lincoln High School. Soon he came across Peter Stegman, a teenage gang leader. Norris first tries to tame the violence of teenagers. The suicide of a high school student under the influence of drugs especially shocked him. Norris knows that the drugs were sold by Peter Stegman, but the only teenage witness, Arthur, refuses to talk. Andy Norris soon came under pressure and threats from the turbulent teenagers, especially after he refused Stegman into his orchestra despite his qualities as a musician. Car wrecks, threats, etc., the relationship between the teacher and his student continues to escalate to the point of no return.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of Kim Kyeung-su, who has already been expelled from nine different schools. Reasons for this included vandalism and the beating of a school principal. Kim arrives at Volcano High and is caught in the middle of a feud between students and teachers who all want to get their hands on the Secret Scripture. When the director is poisoned, Song Hak-rim, the school's best fighter, is locked away, and the Kendō team (whose leader Kim has fallen in love with) asks for his help, but he refuses. The assistant principal later hires a few new "teachers" who deal with any violation of the rules with great violence. When the leader, weightlifter Jang Ryang, is tortured by these teachers, it's enough for Kim and he stops hiding and duels with the teachers. In the end, he exposes the deputy director who claimed to possess the secret writing by destroying the box in which it is supposed to be.
text_a: Determined to do his best in school, young Kim Kyung-soo, a high school student who has already been expelled from nine schools, decides to enroll in the prestigious Volcano High School. Here he finds himself caught in the middle of a war between students and professors who, in an absolutely anarchic and disrespectful way, fight each other for the conquest of the Master's Manuscript, a book of inestimable value hidden in the school through which it is possible to obtain absolute power. The prerogative of those attending the institute is the enormous skill in the use of martial arts and the possession of strange powers, powers that Kyung-Soo is also endowed with. Initially intending to go straight, when the vice-principal takes over the school along with five other powerful professors, Kim Kyung-soo is forced to use his extraordinary skills and step into the field...
text_b: Peter Stegman, the leader of a gang of punks, and its members terrorize students and teachers and engage in criminal activities such as drug trafficking and prostitution with impunity. When a student climbs a flagpole and falls to his death after ingesting cocaine, Norris decides to stop the march. He is targeted by the youths and the events reverse: his colleague Terry is killed in an assassination attempt and his wife is raped. Norris decides to take his revenge. There will be a final showdown. Norris kills the youthful gang members one by one. On the roof of the school building, Norris and Stegman face off in the showdown of the film. Stegman is severely beaten by Norris, falls through the roof of the building, and dies in the process. The film ends with Norris and his wife collapsing from exhaustion. Norris will not be convicted for lack of evidence.

anchor_text: At the sight of one of her old dresses, an unhappy young woman on the verge of divorce recalls her first love... This is New York, 1939. As a student, Eloise meets Walt at a student party. A few days later, Walt asks her out. For him, it's just an opportunity to have a good time. When Eloise finds out, she lets him know she's a serious girl. Walt keeps seeing her, and they end up falling in love. War breaks out and Walt is drafted. Before he leaves for the other side, he asks Eloise to spend the night with him. At first hesitant, she finally agrees. When she finds out she's pregnant, she decides to hide her condition from Walt because she wants him to propose to her out of love and not to legitimize the child...
text_a: The film deals with the fates of three Holocaust survivors. During World War II, Jewish author Jakob Bronski protected English-speaking children Melanie and Christopher in a concentration camp in the French town of Drancy. They parted ways when Jakob came to Auschwitz in her place. Some forty years later, in 1985, Melanie is the wife of David Winters. They are the parents of now grown-up Benjamin, who in turn has a young son named Timmy. The Winters' marriage is strained by David's infidelity. Christopher, Melanie's childhood friend and fellow sufferer, and Jakob visit Melanie at her home in rural Quebec. Christopher suffers from the fact that he survived the Nazi atrocities. Melanie feels that she is a witness to the events of that time, which must not be forgotten. David's jealousy is aroused when a love affair develops. On a summer evening, the characters in black and white flashbacks try to overcome the tormenting memories of the past and their psychological wounds and find a way to a better future.
text_b: Seeing one of her old dresses, a young but unhappy woman, who is on the verge of divorce, remembers her first love. Then the story is told through a series of flashbacks. In 1939, in New York City, student Eloise Winters (played by Susan Hayward) meets Walt Dreiser (played by Dana Andrews) at a student party. A few days later, Walt asks her out. To him, it's just a chance to have a good time. When Eloise realizes, she lets him know she's looking for a permanent relationship. Walt continues to pursue her, and eventually they both end up falling in love. World War II breaks out and Walt joins the U.S. Army. Before traveling abroad, he asks Eloise to spend a night with him. At first he hesitates but finally accepts the proposal. Upon realizing that she is pregnant, she decides to hide her condition from Walt because she wants him to marry her only for love and not to legitimize the child.

Melanie feels that she is a witness to the events of that time, which must not be forgotten. David's jealousy is aroused when a love affair develops. On a summer evening, the characters in black and white flashbacks try to overcome the tormenting memories of the past and their psychological wounds and find a way to a better future.

anchor_text: British Lieutenant Goodbody, inept and naive but idealistic, leads his troops through a series of misadventures in North Africa and Europe during World War II. Such ineptitude coupled with his reckless recklessness will lead to the death of almost all the men in his unit.
text_a: The story is set in World War II. It tells the story of Lieutenant Earnest Goodbody (Michel Crawford), a useless military man who leads a troop of useless soldiers. Goodbody and his men are ordered to build a cricket pitch 100 miles from enemy lines. This one accepts without thinking that he might endanger the lives of his companions.
text_b: It was the custom of the section chiefs of the Third Company to spend the hours before dusk in society. In the shelter, Lieutenant Sturm reads to his comrades excerpts from a series of short stories he is composing. A heavy bombardment interrupts the reading, then it's the inspection of the sentries, an attack by the English...

anchor_text: A plane carrying the participants of a beauty pageant is forced to make an emergency landing on a deserted island. Captain Maximus Powers (Roberts) and co-pilot Mike Saunders (Schlatter) must take care of their passengers, avoiding the dangers of a "Jurassic Pork" (a giant prehistoric pig) and a group of apes, similar to those in Planet of the Apes, who are searching for Noah's Ark. An R2-D2-like droid projects the image of "Agent MJ" (Jackson), who has been assigned by Pope Wojtyla and the Vatican to give a mission to Saunders for secret purposes.
text_a: Twenty-five years after the last nuclear war, the dark Lord Grail has escaped from the prison satellite and is once again seeking to wrest control of the galaxy from his brother. He is to be assisted by Dr. Kraspin, who has developed the substance Kappatron, which can transform any human into a willless and docile humanoid. On Lord Graal's planet, the cargo pilot Golob is transformed into such a humanoid in a first experiment and immediately assigned to kill the scientist Barbara Gibson on the planet Metropolis (formerly called Earth), who once helped develop the Kappatron and is therefore able to neutralize it. With the help of the daring soldier Nick, the attack can be prevented and under the influence of the boy Tom Tom, gifted in the magical powers, Golob is freed from the influence of Lord Graal despite his transformation. Together, they set out to save the galaxy from the evil lord and to turn Golob back into a human.
text_b: The plane carrying the contestants of the Miss Galaxy beauty pageant crashes in the Pacific Ocean on its way to Japan. All of the surviving misses, as well as the pilot, Maximus Power (Eric Roberts), and the co-pilot, Mike Sanders (Charlie Schlatter), arrive on a deserted island. Here the castaways will make an incredible discovery: they will find Noah's Ark. The Ark is protected by Jurassic Pig (a giant prehistoric pig) and Noah has been kidnapped by monkeys identical to those on the planet of apes.

anchor_text: At the beginning of the film, a plane carrying participants in a beauty pageant crashes on a deserted island. Captain Maximus Powers (Eric Roberts) and co-pilot Mike Saunders (Charlie Schlatter) must care for their passengers while avoiding the dangerous Jurassic Pork (giant prehistoric pigs) and a group of great apes busy building Noah's Ark. Then Agent MJ (Michael Jackson) arrives, having been sent by the Vatican to manipulate them for its benefit.
text_a: The plane carrying the contestants of the Miss Galaxy beauty pageant crashes in the Pacific Ocean on its way to Japan. All of the surviving misses, as well as the pilot, Maximus Power (Eric Roberts), and the co-pilot, Mike Sanders (Charlie Schlatter), arrive on a deserted island. Here the castaways will make an incredible discovery: they will find Noah's Ark. The Ark is protected by Jurassic Pig (a giant prehistoric pig) and Noah has been kidnapped by monkeys identical to those on the planet of apes.
text_b: In the near future, a military family decides to take part in a revolutionary experiment of genetic evolution and space exploration that tries to accelerate the genetic development of the human being with a goal: to be able to relocate humanity on another planet and thus avoid its extinction. A story very similar to the book Homo Plus by Frederik Pohl, winner of the Nebula award in 1976.

anchor_text: Pinky and Atul, aka Bunty, fell in love in college and now want to spend the rest of their lives together. But their parents have very different plans for the two: Pinky's father has already chosen an honest policeman Arjun as a dignified son-in-law. Bunty's father also wants Shobha, the daughter of a family friend, to be his daughter-in-law. Pinky and Bunty can't refuse the arranged marriages, since Bunty would be disinherited and Pinky expelled. There is no other way but to agree to the marriage with the respective partners, however, they want to annoy Arjun and Shobha so much that they want to get divorced voluntarily. This turns out to be more difficult than expected, because Arjun and Shobha are very understanding and shower her with their love. But at some point, they too are overwhelmed by the situation and really want to get a divorce. Everything changes for Pinky when Arjun saves her life and puts his own at risk. Now she understands what she feels deep in her heart for Arjun. Bunty also falls apologetically into Shobha's arms.
text_a: Five storylines form the backbone of Monsoon Wedding, the core of which is Aditi's wedding, to which relatives of the Punjabi family Verma arrive from all over the world. After ending her affair with the married television producer Vikram, who is also her boss, Aditi agrees to marry Hemant, an Indian living in Houston, Texas. However, she does not want to give up on Vikram and decides to meet him again. For Aditi's parents, Lalit and Pimmi, who have a typical arranged marriage, the nerve-racking wedding preparations will once again prove how much they can rely on each other. P.K. Dubey, who has made a name for himself through his successful catering business in New Delhi, is also organizing Aditi's wedding. There he meets Alice, the employee of the Vermas, as she is secretly trying on Aditi's wedding jewelry and falls head over heels in love with her. Aditi's cousin Ayesha is not quite what Rahul, who arrived for the wedding from Sydney, where he studies, imagined himself with an Indian woman. Sexy and confident, she teaches him about modern India. Ria, also an unmarried cousin of Aditis, is a writer and observes the wedding rush more distantly. But she carries around a dark secret that she eventually reveals. Thus the situation escalates until the wedding day, when finally the liberating monsoon begins, cooling the heated spirits and restoring family peace.
text_b: Pinky (Kajol), a charming young girl, and Bunty (Atul Agnihotri), a young man full of life, are two carefree students. As soon as they meet, they fall madly in love and plan to marry. But their families do not approve of them: indeed, Bunty's marriage was arranged with Shobha (Ayesha Jhulka), the daughter of her parents' best friend and similarly, Pinky was promised by her father (Kulbhushan Kharbanda) to Arjun (Jackie Shroff), a trustworthy police officer. Pinky and Bunty decide to elope to get married and go to an aunt (Aruna Irani) for her advice. She realizes that the feelings of the two young people are not very sincere and thinks that their marriage would be a mistake. So she pretends to approve of them but guides them in the same sense as their families: she suggests that they marry according to the vows of their parents but lets them believe that they can divorce in order to be reunited. Pinky and Bunty take his advice and then go to great lengths to make their married lives hell. Their comedy seems to be working until they discover that Shobha and Arjun are secretly meeting...

anchor_text: After a romantic summer with her boyfriend Noah and her best friend Lee, she's about to start her senior year of high school in Los Angeles. Soon, Noah's absence impacts her as she realizes at the same time as him to a new life in Boston, where he studies.
text_a: Elle finds herself facing the problems of a long-distance relationship, as doubts and uncertainties pervade her mind. A new school year begins, the last year for the girl and Lee, her best friend; the impending enrollment at the university will lead Elle to make an important decision. There are new characters, like Marco and Chloe, who will question the relationship between Elle and Noah.
text_b: Born on the same day in the same hospital, Elle Evans and Lee Flynn have been best friends all their lives. To protect their friendship, they have established rules to follow, including one that forbids Elle from seeing Lee's brother, Noah, or their friendship will end. However, Elle falls in love with Noah, known at school to be a heartthrob. At this point, Elle has to make a choice: friendship or love.

anchor_text: In a small spa town in Norway at the end of the 19th century, Dr. Stockman receives from his laboratory the results of his tests, which show that the water is polluted. Eager to alert the public to the need for much-needed renovation work, he encounters opposition from his brother Peter, the town's mayor, who fears the disastrous economic consequences. Gradually, the entire population unites against the doctor and his family, who are considered enemies of the people.
text_a: Lars von Trier and Niels Vørsel play themselves in the act of delivering a script to a producer. The film alternates moments of reality with moments of watching the film the two delivered in which von Trier plays Dr. Mesmer who is searching for a cure for a modern epidemic.
text_b: In a small spa town in Norway, at the end of the 19th century, Dr. Stockman receives from the laboratory the results of the research he has directed, which testify to the pollution of the waters. Eager to alert the public to undertake the essential improvement work, he clashes with the opposition of his brother Peter, mayor of the city, who fears disastrous economic consequences. Slowly, the entire population unites against the doctor and his family, who are regarded as enemies of the people.[1]

anchor_text: A young gunfighter, Steve McGowan, allied with a Mexican bandit, León Pompero, decides to avenge the death of his father, caused by Miller, the local official. Miller, aware of the danger, hires a gang of criminals, Sabata and his cronies, to remove Steve and León.
text_a: It's from 1875. The intransigent Rodock, a horse breeder, does not hesitate to lynch any thief caught on his land. But one day, when he was surrounded by a gang of thugs, the only thing that saved him was the arrival of Steve Miller. In gratitude, he hired her to work on his ranch...
text_b: A gunfighter named Steve McGowan, along with a Mexican bandit friend of his, Leon Pompero, decides to avenge the death of his father at the hands of Miller, the local cacique. Miller, aware of the danger he is in, hires a gang of criminals, Sábata and his cronies, to get rid of Steve and Leon. Fate will unite Steve and Leon, as they both decide to defeat the bloodthirsty gunman so they can see their goals fulfilled.
