text_a: A young gunfighter named Steve McGowan, along with his friend, Mexican bandit León Pompero, decides to avenge his father's death at the hands of Miller, the local chief. Miller, aware of the danger he is in, hires a gang of criminals, Sabata and his cronies, to free him from Steve and León. Fate will unite them, as they both decide to defeat the gunman to achieve their goals.
text_b: It's from 1875. The intransigent Rodock, a horse breeder, does not hesitate to lynch any thief caught on his land. But one day, when he was surrounded by a gang of thugs, the only thing that saved him was the arrival of Steve Miller. In gratitude, he hired her to work on his ranch...

anchor_text: A couple of friends, Hugo and Braulio, who are highly educated, are tired of looking for work in Spain. After watching the show Españoles por el mundo, they decide to try their luck in Germany, then described as the paradise of employment, but they will soon realize that the reality is much more difficult...
text_a: Two friends of German nationality, during the First World War, emigrated to the United States. One is fully integrated while the other tries to remain loyal to a culture and a country that at this time is increasingly rejected. His stubbornness and blind loyalty will be the cause of the tragedy.
text_b: Hugo and Braulio, two young people with university education, tired of not finding work or a future in Spain, decide to emigrate to Germany following the siren songs of a Spanish-style television program around the world. But they'll soon discover that being superfluous in one place doesn't mean being necessary in another and that pursuing the German dream can be quite a nightmare.

anchor_text: On his release from prison, Joe Dennis, a repentant gangster, is hired by a department store whose manager recruits some of his staff from former inmates to help them reintegrate professionally and morally. Joe marries a saleswoman, Helen Roberts, who for fear of disappointing him conceals from him that she herself is on parole, which according to U.S. law at the time is grounds for marriage prohibition. That's why she's doing everything she can to keep this union a secret. When Joe discovers the truth, he returns to his former accomplices, with whom he had broken up, and prepares to rob the store. But Helen manages to convince the gang to abandon her project, explaining, chalk in hand on the blackboard, that crime does not pay. A child will soon seal Helen and Joe's love, in the presence of all their friends gathered at the maternity ward...
text_a: Just out of prison, Joe Dennis, a gangster, is hired at a department store. The owner, Mr Morris, has taken the commendable initiative of employing ex-convicts in order to offer them an opportunity for social and economic reintegration. After becoming a model employee and completing his probation, he decides to marry Helen Roberts, a clerk who does not reveal to him that she is also an ex-convict. She fears disappointing him, and American law at the time forbade parolees from marrying. When Joe discovers the truth, he returns to his former gang associates and plots a robbery against the warehouse. Helen manages to convince the gang to give up their criminal plan. Helen and Joe are expecting a baby and when she is admitted to the maternity clinic, the repentant criminals, dressed in elegant clothes, come to visit her and celebrate the happy event.
text_b: A dying man discovers that horses, blondes and the mob can end his life faster than cancer. Joe (Billy Connolly) is a normal guy who runs a flower shop and never had much luck. Things look bleak for Joe when he's diagnosed with a brain tumor, but a little bit of good luck appears on the horizon when he wins a big prize at the racetrack. However, Hush (Sharon Stone), an exotic dancer who has been deeply in debt to a gang of thugs, steals Joe's newly earned fortune to pay them off. Joe gets his money back by explaining to the gangsters what happened, but only because they mistake him for a union boss they've heard of but never met. When the mobsters discover that Joe is a florist and not a criminal, they find the situation unfunny.

anchor_text: Josie Alibrandi is nearing her high school graduation and has a difficult relationship with her single mother Christina. She is also at odds with her Italian-born grandmother Katia, who often criticizes Josie's absent father Michael. Josie's classmate Carly Bishop harasses her because of her ancestry, calling her a "bastard". Then Josie goes berserk and breaks her nose with a book. Josie's father, Michael, who has recently reappeared, is helping to settle the matter. After that, he befriends Josie and teaches her how to drive. In love, she is initially less fortunate, as John Barton, with whom she is in love, commits suicide. Josie finds a new boyfriend, Jacob Coote, but she doesn't want to have sex with him. Grandmother Katia reveals to Josie that Christina's real father is not an Italian but an Anglo-Saxon. But for the sake of the family, Katia did not divorce her Italian husband. Josie convinces her grandmother to tell Christina, who later says she already suspected. The film ends with a party attended by Josie, Jacob, Christina, Michael and Katia, among others.
text_a: Sydney, in the last decade of the 20th century. Josie Alibrandi is a seventeen-year-old Australian teenager of Italian descent. Josie lives with her mother Christina and her grandmother Katia. Josie's mother, Christina, is unmarried: she became pregnant with Josie at the age of 17, raised the child against her father's wishes and without the help of Josie's parent who has left Sydney and is unaware that he has become a father. Josie feels Australian and shows intolerance for the Italian traditions observed by relatives, especially by grandmother Katia. Josie is in her final year of high school at an exclusive Catholic school in Sydney, which she attends on a scholarship. The social divisions among the students are very strong: Josie mostly frequents two friends who are also of Italian origin, while she hardly endures the racist attitude of Carly Bishop, a snobby schoolmate who belongs to a wealthy family. Josie has to face and overcome serious problems with her peers (for example, the suicide of a friend) and with her family. He knows his father, with whom he establishes an excellent relationship, and finally calmly accepts the events and Italian traditions of his family.
text_b: Josie Geller is an intelligent but insecure editor for the Chicago Sun-Times. One day, during a meeting, her boss Rigford asks her to infiltrate a high school pretending to be a 17-year-old student in order to document the lives and ways of the new generations. So Josie's back to being the shy girl she was in high school. He has an unfortunate run-in with Kirsten, Gibby and Guy Perkins, the most popular kids in school. He also meets a girl named Aldys, whom he befriends. Josie is immediately noticed for her intelligence by literature professor Sam Coulson, thus becoming the best in the class. Josie falls in love with the young and attractive teacher who is already engaged. Her editor-in-chief Gus Strauss, meanwhile, orders her to befriend the popular boys at school, whom she would rather avoid, and to wear a hidden camera to record what happens during the day, asking her to find a "scoop". Josie confides in her brother Rob who urges her to let go of her old identity and start anew. To help her, Rob enrolls himself in high school and becomes the popular student he once was. Thanks to her brother's charisma, Josie gains instant fame and popularity: Guy invites her to the student ball and the three girls who initially snubbed her make her join their group. Josie's boss, meanwhile, has found his scoop: he asks his envoy to document the relationship that is developing between her and Professor Coulson in order to denounce the teacher's unethical behavior towards an underage student. Josie struggles: she knows that this would be unfair to the young professor, who ultimately shows her only sympathy and attention, but her career is at stake and she must make a difficult decision quickly.

anchor_text: S. Quentin Quale (Groucho), a self-assured swindler, is on his way to the Wild West where he hopes to make a fortune. In the hall of the departure station, he meets the two Panello brothers (Harpo and Chico) who are even less honest than him since they manage to extort all his money, which also allows them to travel to these lands full of promise of fortune. Once there, they sympathize with the old Dan Wilson to whom they lend a little money: as a pledge, he gives them the deed of ownership of a land that is apparently not worth much. But old Wilson doesn't know that the railroad will soon pass through this land, which increases its value tenfold in the eyes of the railroad company.
text_a: JR, a nineteen-year-old petty criminal, is sent for the first time to one of Australia's harshest penitentiaries for six months. There he discovers the clans that run this prison and the sexual abuse of the weak. Through his knowledge of chess, he made the acquaintance of Brendan Lynch, a former public enemy No. 1 Australian who plays chess by correspondence. The latter, a specialist in robberies and sentenced several times to life imprisonment, noticed the young man for his lack of experience but also for his strategic mind. Helping out his cellmate who is being harassed by inmates, JR is approached by Brendan who offers him his protection. In exchange, when he gets out of prison, he's supposed to break him out with two other accomplices. Upon his release, JR meets the beautiful Tasha with whom he falls in love. She connects him to Sam, the brains of Brendan's gang, and provides him with the escape plan. As Brendan organizes a mutiny, JR hijacks a helicopter that allows Brendan and his two men to escape. Once outside, Brendan orchestrates a large-scale robbery, the theft of a stockpile of gold from a gold mine. As a reward, JR is invited to join his gang. But the case goes bad.
text_b: St. Quentin Quale is heading west in search of fortune. At the station he meets Joseph and Rusty Panello, two simple but very resourceful brothers who, wanting to also go west to become gold prospectors, steal all the money in poor Quale's possession with the one-dollar scam. Once they reach the west, the two strike up a friendship with old Dan Wilson whose property, Dead Man's Gulch, is completely devoid of the precious yellow metal. Joseph and Rusty buy the property for ten dollars. Neither the old man nor the two are aware of the fact that meanwhile Terry Turner, i.e. the fiancé of Dan's daughter (son of a long-time rival of the old man) is selling the land to the railways to finally make a profit from that unproductive land and thus win Dan's favor and finally marry his beautiful daughter. However, the already complicated series of misunderstandings is made worse by the evil Mr. Beecher, who wants to prevent the profitable agreement with the railways. It will obviously be up to Quale, Joseph and Rusty to join forces to save the day and allow the two young people to marry.

anchor_text: The film focuses on the stories of Vann Nath and Chum Mey, survivors of internment in Phnom Penh's S-21 prison, set up on the site of a former school. The testimonies of Nath and Mey are contrasted with the accounts of some of the former prison guards: guards, sentries, a doctor, a photographer, many of whom served in the prison at a very young age, under the threat of being otherwise punished, killed or forcibly enlisted in the army. Much of the filming of the documentary took place in the prison itself, which became the home of the Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum after 1980. Former guards are asked to describe the functioning of the prison, the daily routine of the duties of surveillance and detention, the procedures followed in interrogations, the methods of torture used to extract information. The highlight of the film is a direct encounter between Van Nath, who survived the internment, and some of his former executioners, organized in an attempt to understand the meaning of that experience, the individual responsibilities of those who were involved in the crimes committed in prison, the psychological and moral consequences that the internment produced in the lives of the victims and the former executioners.
text_a: The film evokes the September 30, 1965 movement in Indonesia and the subsequent massacres, which caused the deaths of 500,000 to one million people. The perpetrators of these massacres, in particular Anwar Congo and Adi Zulkadry, thugs (preman) to whom the army entrusted the executions, were given the floor. Anwar Congo alone murdered about 1,000 people, usually by strangling them with a wire. He is now one of the founders of the Indonesian far-right paramilitary group Pemuda Pancasila, and has never been apprehended for his crimes. When invited by the director, Anwar and his friends willingly reenact the killings for the cameras, where they evoke their memories and feelings. The scenes are recreated in the style of their favorite movies: gangster movies, westerns and musicals. Some of Anwar's friends admit in front of the camera that they did wrong. Others are more concerned about the impact of the film on their public image.
text_b: The film, shot entirely with real characters, begins at the home of one of the former S-21 guards who is invited to Phnom Penh to speak openly about the experience in that former torture center. The old guard, like others, is housed in what is now the Genocide Museum. Also in attendance is another of the few survivors who, however, shows emotion. The former guards, confronted by Master Vann Nath, another of the survivors, describe one by one the details of daily life in the prison, the forms of torture, the details of the interrogations that led to false and ridiculous accusations, the abuses of which the women were victims, the cold blood of the executioners, the distribution of space. Master Vann Nath, as the only authoritative voice of the more than 17,000 human beings who passed through and never left that place alive, demands an explanation that will never have a satisfactory answer. The guards were boys and they were themselves terrified and "indoctrinated" by a paranoid system that saw enemies everywhere.

anchor_text: The novel consists of three parts. "The Spectacle" features a discussion among sitting intellectuals about the "law of murder" and, according to them, it seems that society, with its laws, does nothing but moderate and address this primitive need. The first part of the narrative that follows, entitled "On Mission", is a grotesque caricature of the political environments of Belle Époque France. The second part, "The Garden of Torments", is the account of the journey of two Europeans, the sadistic and hysterical Englishwoman Clara and the anonymous French narrator, in the middle of the torture garden of the penal bath in Canton, an infernal and heavenly garden, where the art of torture is practiced according to ancient Chinese practices. Clara approves and shares these practices and derives sexual pleasure from them.
text_a: The novel has three parts: in the first part, "Frontispicio", during a meeting of positivist intellectuals, at the home of an academic and anti-Dreyfusist writer, he speaks with complete freedom about crime, as a natural instinct. The second part, "On Mission", is the political story of the narrator, a corrupt man, sent as an embryologist on a "scientific" mission to Ceylon, and his encounter with a sadistic and hysterical Englishwoman, Clara. In the third part, "The Garden of the Tortured" stricto sensu, the anonymous narrator and his lover Clara, visit the Chinese prison of Canton, where the tortured are, and the spectacle of horrors leads Clara into an erotic ecstasy.
text_b: The novel is about a hysterical priest who, torn between his 'demands' for lust and his pursuit of heaven, finds himself in constant rebellion against the Catholic Church. As the setting for the action, Mirbeau chose a small village in the Norman hill country of Le Perche, a place reminiscent of Rémalard, where he had spent his youth: everyone is constantly exposed to everyone's gaze and the needs of the body and the mind are suppressed. Suddenly, the priest (Abbé Jules), so the beginning of the novel, returns to this place of his childhood after years of absence, which causes a scandal. The story of the last years of his life there is not told continuously, but two long flashbacks of his previous life are inserted, containing an ominous gap of six years. This gap about the time the abbé had spent in Paris leaves the villagers puzzled, and the reader also learns nothing about them, so that his curiosity also remains unsatisfied until the end. The conclusion, in the form of an experimental posthumous joke by Jules, is the opening of his will: In it, he bequeaths all his property and his entire fortune to the priest of his diocese who is the first to give up his clerical status! Then, as a final autodafé, his oft-mentioned mysterious suitcase is burned "on a pyre", revealing that it was filled with pornographic writings and obscene pictorial representations, and its owner's sexual frustrations come to light as a symbol of the imperfectly repressed unconscious.

anchor_text: 17th-century Netherlands: Hajo, Rolf and Padde have only one dream - to go to sea! When Captain Bontekoe, a ship of the Dutch East India Company, calls at their port and wants to hire sailors for the voyage to the East Indies, the friends do not hesitate for a second. But the challenges of the arduous journey far exceed their wildest expectations. After a dramatic shipwreck and an attack by the natives of Sumatra, a fight for survival begins.
text_a: Dutch Captain Laurent Van Horn is stranded on the rocks of Cartagena de Indias which belongs to the Kingdom of Spain. Viceroy Don Juan Alvarado imprisons Dutch immigrants trying to reach South Carolina. After his escape, the Dutch captain will not cease to take revenge on the viceroy; until he captures his fiancée Francisca and marries her. A passion develops between Laurent Van Horn and Dona Francisca... and he takes her to Turtle Island. But pirate Anne Bonny is also in love with Laurent Van Horn.
text_b: The story is taken from the eponymous children's book by Johan Francius published in 1923 itself inspired by the shipwreck of Willem IJsbrantsz. Thank you. Hajo, a 14-year-old boy, dreams of sailing the oceans like his late father. When Captain Bontekoe recruits sailors for his next expedition to India, Hajo knows this is the chance of a lifetime, but he has no idea of the hectic adventures he will experience on this expedition.

anchor_text: The inmates in the maximum-security section of the Maze Prison want to be recognized as political prisoners. In protest, they refuse to wear prison clothes, smear the walls of their cells with excrement, and refuse to wash. Prison guards and special police forces regularly abuse inmates. One of the guards, Ray Lohan, is then shot by an assassin. In a conversation with Father Moran, Bobby Sands announces that he and the other prisoners will go on a hunger strike to enforce their demands. Moran calls this suicide and tries to dissuade Sands from the idea, but Sands replies that it is murder, as her legitimate demands are not being heeded. Sands is determined to sacrifice his life for his political goals if necessary. After nine weeks, he dies of malnutrition. In his final moments, he encounters himself as a child. His parents are still visiting him. His death is symbolized by images of flying ravens. The film ends with guards carrying Sand's body away on a stretcher.
text_a: After a bomb attack on a military transport, two IRA activists Gerard Quigley and Frank Higgins are arrested. They meet Bobby Sands in prison. Sands' activities have almost earned him the reputation of a fighting legend. Quigley and Higgins can barely escape Sands' appeal and are fascinated by his tales. All three are rising more and more in the minds of the people, entering the history of the underground movement as martyrs. Due to the poor conditions of detention, they demand that they be treated as prisoners of war and not as criminals. When the British government refuses, they later go on hunger strike with other prisoners to make their demands known. As the health of the men in prison continues to deteriorate and the resulting confrontation between the IRA and the British government escalates, the mothers of Gerard and Frank fight for the lives of their sons with all possible means. But even interventions from political circles cannot improve the situation. When Bobby Sands dies after 66 days, Kathleen Quigley begins to question whether the political goals are worth the many human sacrifices. When she receives the news of Frank Higgins' death, those doubts become certainty.
text_b: In the 1970s, prisoners of the IRA and INLA led a sanitation strike at Maze Prison in Northern Ireland. This action is aimed at getting the British Government to recognise the political status of their detention. The power struggle intensified when Bobby Sands, the leader of the movement, decided to go on a hunger strike in 1981. The film unfolds in three acts. The first describes the conditions of detention and the struggles of the inmates during the sanitation strike, among whom Bobby Sands is only an anonymous figure. It starts with a camera following one of the guards from his home to the prison. The second is in two shots, one 17 minutes long and the other just over 20 minutes long, showing and hearing a verbal exchange between Bobby Sands and the prison's Catholic chaplain, a moderate member of the IRA, who is trying to dissuade him from starting his hunger strike. The third shows the last days and the agony of the young prisoner, the first of the group of prisoners to go on hunger strike.

anchor_text: Sheriff Robert Rogers accidentally kills the son of a gunfighter, Dan Murphy. This one takes revenge by kidnapping the sheriff's son. He is then forced by circumstances to return to his former life and return the child, before facing off in a duel with the sheriff.
text_a: Sheriff Robert accidentally kills the son of one of the city's gunmen. Given the size of the scandal, the gunman will attempt to exact revenge by kidnapping the sheriff's son.
text_b: The Dakota family is chased into the middle of a village by Gary Stevens and his henchmen. Before a sheriff indifferent to their plight, Bill Dakota and his wife are murdered. After raising their son, his brother Ken went to Mexico to get revenge. He discovers that Bill's gold mine is coveted by a wealthy landowner, Christopher Malone.

anchor_text: Kemal, a 30-year-old, well-educated son of a manufacturer, and his girlfriend Sibel plan to get engaged soon. Kemal accidentally meets poor 18-year-old relative Füsun when he went to buy Sibel a handbag. Emotionally touched by Füsun, he begins to meet with her and help her prepare for the university exam. These meetings end when Kemal and Sibel get engaged. Kemal can no longer reach Füsun and therefore becomes depressed and unhappy. He separates from Sibel and spends his days in the apartment where Füsun and he made love. After his father's death, Kemal receives a message from Füsun and learns where she lives. He visits her and sees that she is married. Her husband Feridun is a filmmaker who has loved Füsun since childhood. He needs financial support for his film, and that's why Füsun invites Kemal. They meet often, go to the movies together, but Füsun is formal. Kemal fears that one day he will lose Füsun, and secretly collects her personal belongings. Fushun's husband falls in love with the lead actress of his film. The film is successful, but the marriage of Feridun and Füsun breaks down. After the death of Füsun's father, Kemal and Füsun live together and plan to go to Paris with Füsun's mother. In a car accident in Edirne, Füsun dies and Kemal is seriously injured. When he gets better, he decides to use Füsun's artifacts that he has collected all this time to open a museum in that place where Füsun and her family used to live. So he offers the writer Orhan Pamuk to write a catalog for the museum, and Pamuk writes the book.
text_a: A seemingly ordinary story: Kemal, the young heir of a family of industrialists from Istanbul, was to marry Sibel, the daughter of a Turkish diplomat, a marriage that suited both families very well. But the Evil One who twists the fates and mixes the cards causes him to enter a shop to buy a bag and remains fascinated by Füsun, the beautiful saleswoman, who is also a distant cousin. It's love at first sight, but this disliked Kemal wants it all: the respectability of marriage with Sibel and the passionate love with Füsun his mistress. This is the case in societies stifled by convenience, and Pamuk knows how to expose the flaws of Turkish society. Füsun disagrees and she disappears after the engagement of Kemal and Sibel. Kemal goes in search of it through many initiatory adventures, fiery reunions, infidelities, rumors, in the meanders of the Golden Horn, Beyoglu and Nisantasi. But for him, it is too late and he consoles himself by becoming a cleptomaniac and fetishist, gathering as in a museum all the objects reminding him of Füsun that he can find, the museum of innocence disseminating a whole genealogy of which he will ask a certain Orhan Pamuk, a character in the novel, to specify them.
text_b: In a country house near the coast, surrounded by a lot of nature, several people associated in different ways with the famous film director Keve gather: his wife Ann-Marie, his estranged daughter Nina, and the model Inger, with whom he used to have a relationship. Keve has doubts about his creativity and is struggling to get his new film project off the ground. When he visits a fugitive acquaintance, he meets his beautiful wife, Eva-Britt. The two fall in love at first sight and meet regularly in an apartment for sex. Ann-Marie feels inadequate in her marriage to Keve. After some time, Eva-Britt leaves her husband and ends the relationship with Keve. From London arrives the actor Benito, who is to take over a role in Keve's film. While Keve boards kites with other attendees on the seashore, Ann-Marie engages in an intimate conversation about love and life while cooking together. Her hopes of an affair with Ben are dashed because Ben is hooking up with Inger. Because Keve can't explain his role in the film, Ben leaves. Inger kills herself with pills.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of the unfulfilled love of the poet Anton Chekhov to the young teacher Lika against the backdrop of the premiere and failure of Chekhov's play The Seagull (1895).
text_a: Anton Chekhov recalls the passion he felt, before his marriage to the actress Olga Knipper, for the singer Lika Mizinova who later inspired him the character of Nina in his play The Seagull.
text_b: Isabelle Huppert plays Frédérique, a young woman from the countryside. She is enormously attracted to wealthy and worldly men, although she ultimately refuses to have sex with them. Her experiences have taken her from France to Japan.

anchor_text: The book is set during the gold rush years and chronicles the journey of two Canadian cousins, Summy Skim and Ben Raddle, who inherit a concession on the banks of the Klondike. The plot focuses on the description of the protagonists' journey and the difficulties that the duo faces in order to seek fortune in inhospitable lands. Their encounter with two women (one, Jane Edgerdon, brave and determined to become a gold hunter; the other, Edith, shy and introverted) will lead them to many adventures and make a wonderful discovery: the golden volcano.
text_a: The story begins in 1893 during the so-called Gold Rush in the Yukon Territory, Canada. Among his colleagues, the protagonist Elam Harnish, nicknamed Burning Daylight, is considered the most successful gold prospector, who succeeds in an outstanding gold discovery in the course of the plot. Elam Harnish becomes a mine owner and grows up to be a businessman who eventually goes to the New York Stock Exchange, where he is confronted with a contrary attitude to life compared to his previous environment: contempt for human beings and cruelty. Eventually, he turns his back on this world alongside his former secretary and moves to a ranch with her.
text_b: Cousins Ben Raddle and Summy Skim live in Montreal, Canada, and travel to the Yukon to learn of the last will and testament of their late uncle Josias Lacoste. Once in Dawson City, an earthquake buries their hopes. Saddened, the two cousins discover - thanks to the Frenchman Jacques Laurier - the existence of a gold mine that, by chance, is in the crater of a volcano.

anchor_text: The handsome Adrien was promised a dancing career, until a terrible motorcycle accident. Today, it is maintained by Martha, a former film star living on the Côte d'Azur. Everything will change when Adrian meets the beautiful and young Margot. The latter thrives on fraud and manipulation. They will then team up to pull off a diabolical scam through a sentimental charade. For their plan, Adrien and Margot need the perfect pigeon. So they target Simon, a real estate agent.
text_a: Adrien has been a gigolo on the Côte d'Azur ever since a motorcycle accident cut short his promising dancing career. While living off his last client, Martha, a former movie star, he meets and falls in love with the beautiful Margot, who lives off scams and gimmicks. The two join forces to set up a scam against the moneyed Simon, a real estate agent on vacation.
text_b: Adrienne, a gypsy girl who performs in a traveling amusement park, is unable to find true love, until she meets Prince Maurice. They fall in love, but must separate when, for diplomatic reasons, the prince must become engaged to the wealthy wife of an influential duke. Adrienne later becomes a popular stage actress and meets the prince again, just as she is acting in a play that recalls the sad story of her previous relationship with the prince. Maurice is fighting to regain the throne, usurped by a violent dictator. With Adrienne's help, he avoids an assassination attempt, regains his throne, and is once again king.

anchor_text: The attractive Adrien worked as a dancer until his career was cut short by a motorcycle accident. Since then, he has spent his youth idling on the Côte d'Azur, assisted by former film actress Martha. One day, an encounter with Margot changes his life. The equally attractive young woman finances her livelihood through fraud and amorous manipulation. Both Adrien and Margot dream of a better life. Together, they begin to devise a diabolical plan to achieve their goal.
text_a: Adrienne, a young Bohemian woman, takes to the stage during a carnival party. She falls in love with Prince Maurice, but they have to separate because he is called to a very influential duchess. A few years later, Adrienne becomes a famous theater actress and she reunites with the prince. In a providential way, she plays in a play that recalls her first affair with Maurice of Saxony. He is fighting to regain his throne, which was usurped by a dictator. With Adrienne's help, he's going to kill him.
text_b: The handsome Adrien was promised a dancing career, until a terrible motorcycle accident. Today, it is maintained by Martha, a former film star living on the Côte d'Azur. Everything will change when Adrian meets the beautiful and young Margot. The latter thrives on fraud and manipulation. They will then team up to pull off a diabolical scam through a sentimental charade. For their plan, Adrien and Margot need the perfect pigeon. So they target Simon, a real estate agent.

anchor_text: John Finnegan and his crew make their money as mercenaries and are hired by a group of criminals to commit insurance fraud. So they take their small ship to the cruise ship Argonautica, which is about to be sunk. When they board the ship, however, they quickly realize that everything is different from what they expected. You'll find numerous traces of blood and the passengers and crew are missing. After finding the thief Trillian St. James alive, they gain access to the vault, where they meet the ship's owner Simon Canton and Captain Atherton. Gradually, it turns out that the passengers and crew have fallen prey to a giant deep-sea monster with tentacles, which has devoured them alive and sucked out their bodily fluids. The bones are spit out again. After the group is decimated and the plan of insurance fraud fails, the remaining survivors decide to flee with Finnegan's small ship. However, since this is no longer controllable, it is set by autopilot to ram the Argonautica and explode the cruise ship with the missiles it carries. Ship owner Canton had previously tried to mislead the others and separated himself from the group, so he was unaware of the plan and therefore tried to escape with the small ship. The explosion kills the giant octopus as well. John and Trillian appear to be the only survivors. They can barely escape on a jet ski and get stranded on a deserted island. Shortly thereafter, John's ship's mechanic, Joey, also emerges from the sea. At this moment, sounds suggest the island is inhabited by a monster.
text_a: Captain Freytag is a man as monosyllabic as he is uncompromising and true to his principles, doing his duty conscientiously on a fire ship in the North Sea. To show his son Fred his work on the spot, he took him aboard the ship this time. Shortly thereafter, three shipwrecked sailors are spotted floating on a boat in the murky North Sea. Freytag's bringing the men aboard with a lifeboat. Soon he must realize that this was a serious mistake, because it is about three unscrupulous bank robbers who saved themselves with their prey while fleeing from the police. Their leader, a Dr. Caspary, is a cold-blooded criminal with manners who knows exactly what he wants. He's asking the captain for help. First, the leaky boat is to be repaired, and then Freytag is to pull out his own boat, which, however, he desperately needs to get to shore on his own. Finally, the gangsters demand to change the position of his fire ship so that the pursuers can not identify them. Captain Freytag remains principled to the point of stubbornness, he does not fear death and does not want to endanger other ships by changing the position of the fire ship. When Caspary begins to threaten the lives of the hostage-taken crew, the men on the fire ship are determined to take action and overpower the gangsters. Freytag's deepest convictions are against violence, even at the risk of his own son thinking he's a coward. The situation on board escalates as Caspary uses bribery and threats to win over parts of the crew. Eventually, a shootout ensues in which Freytag must pay for his loyalty with his life. Before they can escape, the gangsters are overpowered and arrested.
text_b: John Finnegan (Treat Williams) is hired to lead a group of mercenaries led by Hanover (Wes Studi) to a spot in the middle of the ocean. Meanwhile, on the luxury cruise ship Argonautica, white-gloved thief Trillian (Famke Janssen) is locked by the captain in the cold room, which is lucky for her as soon after the ship is attacked by creatures that emerge from the depths. The mercenaries, who were also planning to rob the cruise ship, get on board and find it all stained with blood and no trace of the passengers, which seems a bit strange to them. Exploring the ship they find some survivors, in a state of panic: not for nothing, because some strange giant carnivorous tentacles have finished off the crew and are now killing all the mercenaries one by one. Finnegan manages to survive and save the thief by escaping on a jet ski after discovering that the tentacles are part of a gigantic creature that has taken up residence inside the cruise ship. Finnegan uses the torpedoes the mercenaries had brought to blow up the cruiser and kill the giant monster. In a strange and ambiguous epilogue, Finnegan and Trillian arrive on a jungle island where they soon hear dinosaur-like roars and the sound of something huge moving through the trees.

anchor_text: Jacob returns from his trip to Amityville and brings back a mysterious clock for his entire family. But since that gift arrived in the house, unexplained phenomena have surfaced. Jacob becomes aggressive and very sick and Lisa, the young girl, gradually turns into a sex bomb ready for anything. Between Rusty, the family's teenager, who is a firm believer in paranormal activity, and Andrea, Jacob's ex-girlfriend, who is trying to figure out what's going on, everything is falling apart and their lives are slowly turning into a living hell.
text_a: 16-year-old Andi and her brother Bruce are two young orphans with an unbridled passion for dogs. But when the new guardians decide to forbid them from keeping a puppy in the house, the two take over an old abandoned hotel and turn it into a dog hotel, welcoming all the strays in the area. Can Andi and Bruce keep their business going undetected? A tender and funny comedy about friendship, teamwork, helping others and brotherhood.
text_b: Jacob Sterling, a resident of New York, buys a watch considering it to be a rare and unique antique. With that, he returns to New York triggering a series of paranormal events that put him and his family at risk. After analyzing the possible causes of these events, he discovers that the watch belonged to the infamous Amityville house. Now he'll be forced to fight the demons stored in the clock's energy before it's too late.

anchor_text: Masaru Daisato, who lives near the subsistence level, has the ability to transform himself to about a hundred times his body size by means of high-voltage shocks. This ability is exploited by the Japanese Ministry of Defense to use it as a weapon against giant monsters that appear sporadically in Japanese cities. The fights are shown on television, which has given him a certain notoriety. However, the aspect of safety is completely ignored by his environment the only thing that counts is the entertainment value of his actions against the monsters, which is estimated to be very low. However, people do not perceive his actions as heroic, but as an embarrassing nuisance and disturbance. As the film progresses, many small embarrassing details of his life are revealed: he is not divorced, but lives separately from his wife and daughter. He would have liked to have used this as an 'inheritor' of his abilities. This is unacceptable to his wife and is the reason for the separation. He also has a manager who lets him walk around in a fight with billboards painted on his body, cheating him out of his sponsored earnings.
text_a: This is Japan, Tokyo. Masaki is a simple boy, not too bright, with a passion for baseball, the sport that his team plays without too much luck on the suburban fields; he works as a gas station attendant, in which during a shift he rebels against the demands of a yakuza member and attracting the ire and attention of the organization on the establishment. In an attempt to repair the damage, he asks for help from the team's coach (a former affiliate), who will however fail in his attempt at mediation and will be punished by his former Yakuza comrades. In search of weapons to avenge him, Masaki and a friend head to Okinawa where they meet Uehara, a psychopathic and violent boss who takes to heart their absurd enterprise of ragtag heroes; at the same time, however, Uehara himself finds himself having to sustain a (personal) war against another cell of the organization, which will lead him to a clash where he will also lose his life. Determined to exact revenge, reinvigorated by Uehara's madness, Masaki launches into a last, desperate gesture (after the failed attempts and the disappointment of his friends) in the company of a girl who will silently follow him in his final decision.
text_b: Big Man Japan begins as a documentary film about Masaru Daisatō (Hitoshi Matsumoto), a Japanese outcast hated by his neighbors, who is regularly called upon by the government to fight giant monsters that appear in the archipelago. To do this, he goes to a power plant where he transforms himself into a giant. His grandfather, now in a retirement home, had held the post, but since that time his position has lost all prestige: the television audiences of his exploits are deplorable and his press attaché forces him to finance himself through advertisements painted on his body. A series of fiascos against particularly grotesque monsters ends up discrediting him (a kind of baby, a pair of monsters who only think about mating, etc.). While the beginning of the film is a mixture of live-action footage and synthetic imagery, its final minutes cap off the character's downfall by being shot in a cardboard backdrop and costumes reminiscent of the 1960s television series Ultraman: a family of American superheroes intervenes to beat the giant baby under the eyes of Big Man Japan lurking behind a building.

anchor_text: In 1995, South Africa hosted the Rugby World Cup. Nelson Mandela begins his term as president of the country. Contrary to the opinion of his supporters, he sensed in the sporting event the opportunity to create a sense of national unity behind the Springboks team, a symbol for several decades of white South Africans, their domination and apartheid (1948-1991): One team, one country ( One team, one country ). Can a sport affect a man's state of mind? It is Nelson Mandela's political and human gamble, after 27 years of imprisonment, against the mutual fear of the communities of South Africa that creates a climate of tension. It's about keeping the rainbow nation alive. Mandela, as a pacifist, tries to change things, mindsets; you have to know how to change yourself to try to change the rest, which is what the film tries to portray by highlighting the necessary struggle for forgiveness. A president and a team captain, Francois Pienaar, each from communities that hated each other, two leaders for one cause: unity. Will the mediocre Springboks team help South Africa's reconciliation? This nation thirsts for greatness that is the hope. Mandela gave the captain of the Springboks the poem Invictus by William Ernest Henley, which reads in part: I'm the master of my fate, I'm the captain of my soul. ( I'm the master of my fate, I'm the captain of my soul. )
text_a: After the failure of the Netherlands to qualify for the 2002 World Cup, Kramer decided to "elaborate" the defeat of his national team by organizing a football match between the two national teams that occupy the last two places in the FIFA ranking. The two nationalities in question could not be more different: one is Bhutan, a small country perched high in the Himalayas; the other is Montserrat, a small island in the Caribbean. The initiative is greeted with enormous enthusiasm in both countries, as news travels around the world. The "other final" was played on the same day as the World Cup final (30 June 2002) between Brazil and Germany, in front of some 25,000 spectators. Also on the podium are the Crown Prince of Bhutan Dasho Jigyel Ugyen Wangchuk and the entire Himalayan government. The national team of Montserrat blames the considerable climate change (it plays at about 2250 meters above sea level) and a problem of food poisoning for seven of its members. The Caribbean defends well, but Bhutan shows considerable superiority. The match, refereed by Englishman Bennett, ended 4-0 for the Himalayan national team. At the end of the match, both national teams are awarded a special trophy: a cup divided in two, half of which will be held by both contenders. # Results of the race
text_b: After the fall of the apartheid regime and the installation of Nelson Mandela as president, South Africa is going through a very delicate historical period. As soon as he took office, Mandela, the nation's first black head of state, set out to pacify the South African people, still divided by hatred between the black majority and the white minority. Symbol of this split are the Springboks, the national rugby team, a symbol of white Afrikaner pride and therefore detested by blacks, which just after the fall of the apartheid regime is readmitted to international competitions after an exclusion of about a decade. In view of the 1995 World Cup, hosted by South Africa, Mandela is interested in the fortunes of the team, with the hope that a possible victory will help strengthen national pride and the spirit of unity of the country. In particular, he comes into contact with Captain François Pienaar, making him understand the political importance of the upcoming sports competition. This meeting between Pienaar and Mandela, which leads to a real friendship, begins a series of events that strengthen the morale of the Springboks (reduced by a long period of defeats) and lead them on a lucky path in the World Cup, until an unexpected victory in the final against the fearsome All Blacks. The success of the national team becomes a symbol of the rapprochement of the black population to the white population and the progress of the integration process.

anchor_text: After eleven years in prison for manslaughter, former GDR citizen Martin Schulz is released from prison in 2000. In the meanwhile reunified Germany, which he knows only from television, he finds it difficult at first and his family has also become foreign to him. Manuela, his wife, is now in a relationship with Wolfgang, their 11-year-old son Rokko has not seen Martin for years at his own request. His desire to spend as much time as possible with his family is met with resistance by Wolfgang, who is skeptical and dismissive of the strange ex-convict. Meanwhile, Martin also reunites with some of his old friends, including his former locksmith colleague Enrique, who now works as a taxi driver. Martin decides to take up the same profession and, with Manuela's help, learns for the entrance examination. By the way, he's taking a job at his former cellmate Victor's sex shop. With the money he earns there, he wants to go on vacation with his son. He secretly dreams of winning Manuela back. At first, everything seems to be going well, but then Martin learns that because of his criminal record, he is not allowed to take the taxi driver's exam. His dream is shattered, Martin is devastated. Shortly thereafter, he is arrested for Victor's illegal trade in child pornography. At the last moment, the fugitive Victor turns himself in to the police to exonerate his friend. Martin feels like he's in a time machine.
text_a: Martin works as a bus driver for a transportation company in Madrid. He's frustrated and wants to commit suicide. When he wants to throw himself off a bridge, Salva speaks to him and advises him to let go for a little while and borrow a large sum of money from the mafia, for which Martin could live as a millionaire for a week. Martin accepts the advice, and Salva acts as his accountant. At first, everything goes well, until Martin meets Lola, a Mexican waitress at a party, and falls in love with her. Martin finds new courage and abandons his suicidal intentions. However, since he cannot repay the sum demanded, he becomes increasingly paranoid and flees from the alleged mafiosi. Meanwhile, Lola also falls in love with Martin, after initially only wanting to hook up with him to rob him. She had intended to care for her sick sister, who had been badly injured in a suicide attempt. Salva tells Martin of Lola's deceitful intentions and provides him with an opportunity to escape the Mafia abroad. But instead of running, Martin decides to confront Lola. She frankly admits that she wanted to make Martin lose his money, he then leaves the apartment disappointed. Becoming suspicious of being watched, he secretly follows Salva and realizes that Salva is also a Mafioso. He wanted to use Martin to launder a larger amount of money: he had to make sure that Martin only spent the money provided to him in Mafia-controlled shops. So the money was laundered inconspicuously. Martin returns to Lola, and they decide to start over.
text_b: In the year 2001 Martin is released from a penitentiary in Brandenburg. He was institutionalized for 11 years. In 1989 a court had convicted him of murder. Then the sentence was reduced to 11 years for manslaughter. Martin knew only the GDR and suddenly he is faced with a reunified Berlin. He stays in a hotel, looks for work, but to no avail. He visits Manuela, his wife and mother of the son he's never known. His wife's new partner looks at him suspiciously. Martin reunites with his old teammates. One of them offers him an hourly job at a sex shop. There he meets a stripper, a nice girl from the Balkans, who studies and works temporarily in this sector. Martin also reunites with an old friend, Enrique, who is a taxi driver. He loves the idea of driving a taxi, wants to get the necessary permit and study for the exam. But during the same examination he learns that because of his criminal record he is prohibited from obtaining this permit. Desperate, he goes to the sex shop and wants to get drunk. Soon the police arrive at the business: it seems that drugs were sold or at least it was a place where drugs passed from one hand to another. Martin runs away and takes refuge in Manuela's house. There he is arrested because Manuela's boyfriend reported him to the police. But Manuela and Rocco support him. The reintegration worker also promises to help. He is eventually released.[4]

text_b: Martin Schulz is released after 11 years in prison. A citizen of the former GDR, he witnessed the fall of the Berlin Wall and ten years of reunification. through his television, the only link to the outside world from his cell. Upon his release from prison, he was given what he had in his possession at the time of his arrest: his identity card, his East German driver's license, and his wallet with bills bearing the portrait of Karl Marx. The ex-convict tries to find his bearings in a completely transformed Berlin. In the face of a hostile world Martin Schulz remains optimistic. He decided to try to reconnect with his wife, Manuela. who was pregnant with their son, Rokko, when Martin was arrested for murder. Now she's rebuilt her life with Wolfgang, a teacher. He also reunites with one of his old friends, Peter, an outcast from society whom Martin saves from suicide. They both started looking for jobs. However, their efforts are in vain. Eventually Martin agrees to work in the sex shop run by Victor, a prisoner he knew behind bars. His desire to reintegrate into a reunified Germany would encounter many obstacles. But thanks to his friends, he will find his place in society and hope for a better future.

anchor_text: Wilhelm Meister is a young merchant who believes he has a vocation as a dramatic actor, a vocation that awakened in him the day his parents acquired a puppet theatre. He joins a traveling troupe and discovers the world, love, societies. For him, literature and theatre can change society because they allow us to approach reality and the ideal, which are two inseparable poles.
text_a: Wilhelm Meister is a young lover of theatre and dramaturgy and his vocation is to become an actor and director of plays. So, with the help of his family, he buys a theater where he exhibits puppet shows to the public, soon becoming very famous. All this Wilhelm does to perfectly reach the essence of truth and fiction and try to make them unite with each other.
text_b: The film is inspired by the apprenticeship novel written by Goethe: The Apprenticeship Years of Wilhelm Meister. Living under the sway of an oppressive mother, young Wilhelm decided to go to Bonn with the desire to become a writer. On the train, he meets a former Olympic athlete and his girlfriend. An actress and a young man join them. From now on, nothing will go as planned for Wilhelm... Six days in the life of a young man, six days to decide his future and face learning reality.

anchor_text: Berlin in the early 1950s: The young Polish woman Pauline Karka comes to the city to go to West Germany. She's pregnant and homeless. When Pauline meets Anna John, the owner of the laundry, Pauline has hope. Anna John is childless and makes a deal with Pauline. She'll take care of the girl until she gives birth and adopt the child as her own. The child is born and Pauline comes, before going to West Germany, back to Mrs. John once more to say goodbye to the child. Anna John, however, denies her a last look at the child. Pauline wants to kidnap the child in her distress, but accidentally takes the sick child of the neighbor Mrs. Knobbe. Mrs. John orders her brother Bruno to find Pauline and kill her. During the assassination attempt, Pauline kills Bruno with a stone. After her brother's death, Mrs. John turns herself in to the police and confesses her guilt.
text_a: This is Berlin, 1954. On New Year's Eve, a young woman with amnesia hiding a doll and a bloodied man's passport in her old coat is recovered by soldiers at a checkpoint outside the British zone. Placed in the presence of people she knew, she will regain her memory and a woman, Anna, will reveal her story. Pauline, the young amnesiac, undocumented foreigner, seduced and then abandoned by her German lover, tries in vain to find him. She's pregnant and she doesn't want that baby anymore. Desperate and penniless, she meets Anna, an emotional woman who, after another miscarriage, tricks her husband into believing that her current pregnancy is going well. Anna offers to take the baby back and pass it off as hers. But Pauline is beginning to hesitate.
text_b: In Berlin at the beginning of the 20th century, the five members of a singing group live in squalor in a dilapidated building. When the building's manager is found murdered, Peter and Paul, two members, are suspected of the murder. When they return from a concert, the police are waiting to arrest and interrogate them. As Paul is more involved in the investigation, he is immediately arrested. Peter knows Paul's innocence and cannot allow the arrest, also because he knows how much Mary, a mutual acquaintance, has Paul in her heart. Convinced of Paul's innocence and optimistic, Peter blames himself for the murder. His friend Paul is disqualified, Peter goes to prison. Meanwhile, the other four singers have an engagement where they show off their beatboxing skills. Shortly thereafter, they made a fortune and moved out of their dilapidated apartment building. In prison, Peter waits to seize the moment to launch an escape attempt. He succeeds and meets Marie who is in charge of the singing group. Meanwhile, the police continued their investigation, doubting Peter's confession, and arrested the killer.

anchor_text: The story takes place in São Paulo during the 1970 FIFA World Cup in Mexico. Mauro, 12 years old, is left by his parents, gone on vacation (actually, fleeing the dictatorship that was then raging in Brazil), with his paternal grandfather, a barber in the Jewish quarter of Bom Retiro. But he's not at the rendezvous, and Mauro is being hosted by a high-ranking black community. It is the summer of all emotions, between joys (the magnificent and triumphant national team led by a Pelé to the top, the first teenage discoveries) and deaf anguish related to the strange disappearance of the parents.
text_a: An elderly couple invited their children and grandchildren to their home for Christmas. Everything goes smoothly, respecting tradition, until they ask which of their two sons and two daughters wants to take them home now that they are old. In exchange, they offer to give up their pension and their house as an inheritance, but none of the children are willing to give up their comfort.
text_b: Mauro is a boy with a strong passion for football who, during the summer of 1970, is left at the home of his Jewish grandfather in São Paulo by his parents. The only thing he is told is that the two will go on an indefinite vacation and will try to return before the end of that year's World Cup. Mauro knocks on his grandfather's door, but Shlomo, a grumpy, reclusive Jew who lives in the apartment next door, informs him that the man has just died. Shlomo is forced by the Jewish community to take care of the little Mauro, although initially the coexistence of the two is more difficult than expected. Fortunately, Mauro makes friends with the children in the neighborhood where he lives and gradually begins to love Shlomo in his own way, who, moved by compassion, begins to investigate the disappearance of the child's parents, until he finds himself in the middle of a police raid against a student club to which he had turned and everything seems to fall apart...

anchor_text: Because of his kindness and humanity, the doctor enjoys great popularity among his patients, the medical faculty, and the student body. Only his colleague Prof. Speiter doesn't want him to succeed. When his patient Maria Violetta wants to commit suicide because of an out-of-wedlock pregnancy, Prätorius takes in the young woman. As he carefully prepares her father for the news, this Praetorius considers him a more than welcome admirer of his daughter. As a deep affection develops between the doctor and his patient, the two eventually marry. The private happiness of the two fuels the envy of Prof. Hurry up. Through Prätorius' mysterious factum, Shunderson finally believes he can uncover dark dots from the medicine man's past. Yet, in a court of honor, Praetorius manages to refute all accusations with wit and astonishing revelations. In Goetz's staging, the tragicomic and melancholy moments of the play are particularly emphasized. It emphasises humanist values and takes a critical view of the problems of abortion and the death penalty. In addition, Praetorius is as persistent as he is vain in his efforts to investigate the germ of human stupidity, which he believes to be the cause of envy, hatred and war.
text_a: Dramatis personae are Theseus, his wife Phaidra, their children Hippolytus and Strophe, a priest and a physician. The play is divided into eight scenes, and it takes place at the royal palace of Theseus. Theseus is at war. Hippolytus is the TV-addicted, food- and sex-addicted stepson of Phaedra, who is in love with him. On his birthday, she offers him her sexual devotion, which he reluctantly accepts. At the same time, he hurts her by mentioning the fact that he also had sex with Strophe, his stepsister. Phaedra commits suicide. In her suicide note, she accuses Hippolytus of rape. He is then taken into custody and is to be executed. A doctor and a priest seek to heal his physical and mental soul, with the priest also falling for the attractive Hippolytus and having oral sex with him. On the day of the execution, Theseus returns to the city, vowing to avenge his son, rapes his own daughter Strophe, who has disguised herself as him. Theseus will slit her throat. After identifying his victim as Strophe and seeing Hippolytus dying, he slits his own throat as well. Hippolytus is lynched by the mob awaiting his execution and, for the first time in his life, experiences the change he has always longed for.
text_b: Dr. Praetorius enjoys great popularity because of his kindness and generosity to his patients, colleagues, and students at the medical school. Only his colleague, prof. Speiter is a little jealous. When his patient Maria Violetta wants to commit suicide because of an extra-marital pregnancy, the doctor decides to take care of her. Since he wants to prepare her father for the news, the doctor seems more than sympathetic to his daughter. A deep affection develops between the doctor and his patient who decide to marry. Their happiness fuels the jealousy of Speiter. Thanks to Shunderson, who knows Praetorius, he believes he can reveal the doctor's dark past. But before the court, Praetorius manages with humor and astonishing revelations to dismiss all blame.

anchor_text: Dana Stribling is tracking Brendan O'Malley, who murdered Dana's brother-in-law, to Mexico. He finds O'Malley, who works for the hard-drinking cattle baron John Breckenridge. Breckenridge wants to drive a herd of cattle across the Texas border into the town of Crazy Horse. O'Malley unsuccessfully attempts to continue an affair that began 17 years ago with Breckenridge's wife, Belle. In order not to lose track of O'Malley, Stribling is hired as a cattle driver. He warns O'Malley that there will be a duel once the border is crossed. Shortly after the trek starts, Breckenridge is killed in a saloon brawl. Stribling and O'Malley take the lead. Even though they are enemies, their admiration for each other grows. As the days go by, Stribling and Belle fall in love. Meanwhile, Belle's 16-year-old daughter, Missy O'Malley, is following in her footsteps. The Rio Grande, the border river to Texas, is reached. Belle wants to throw a party. Missy's wearing one of her mother's dresses, which reminds O'Malley of the affair with Belle. He enthusiastically yields to the young woman's advances. However, Belle explains to him in Crazy Horse that Missy is his daughter, giving him a shock. He deliberately confronts Stribling in the upcoming duel with his unloaded derringer and lets himself be shot by him.
text_a: Brendan 'Bren' O'Malley, convicted of murder, fled to Mexico. He decides to visit a woman he loved sixteen years ago, Belle. He learns that she is married to John Breckenridge, currently absent; and that she has a sixteen-year-old daughter, Missy, to whom he is immediately attracted. Back at the ranch, John Breckenridge is looking for men to drive his herd all the way to Texas. O'Malley agrees, against one-fifth of this herd. Turns out he's being chased by Sheriff Dana Stribling who joins him at the Breckenridge Ranch. O'Malley offers to take him back to his old job as a cowboy and help him lead the animals to the town where he himself is wanted for the murder of his brother-in-law. Stribling agrees, hoping to deliver O'Malley to justice as soon as they pass through his jurisdiction. During the voyage, Breckenridge, who was also a drunk and a coward, was killed in a brawl; Stribling and Belle, increasingly attracted to each other, planned to marry. Meanwhile, O'Malley and Missy fall in love with each other. This increases the tension between the outlaw and the sheriff. The day before their planned confrontation, Belle reveals to O'Malley that Missy is his daughter. Faced with his impossible love, O'Malley confronts the sheriff with an empty revolver barrel, and dies.
text_b: Cowboy Tex Masters and his sidekick Lucky are searching all Texas rodeos for the man who killed Tex's brother. En route to Middleton, they meet young federal agent Carmen Serano, who has been assigned to solve a series of mysterious deaths of several local rodeo winners. Suspect is the gang of a man named Barker. In her undercover investigation, Carmen sneaks into Barker's saloon as a dancer. After Tex and Lucky take on some gang members, the Middleton Sheriff hires them to help him solve the murders. Once again, Tex and Carmen's paths cross, and they finally get closer. At the rodeo, Tex wins all the competitions, which is why Barker orders Carmen to engage Tex in a fight with another rodeo rider named Squint. Although Carmen warns Tex instead, he still goes to the saloon to see her dance. There, he gets into a fight with Squint, from which he emerges victorious, saving Lucky's life. Pinto, a follower of Barker, attempts to kill Tex by attaching a poisoned needle to the reins of Tex's horse. Tex and Lucky, however, catch Pinto in the act just in time. He manages to escape, robs a bank with his men, and makes off with the loot and a wagonload of dynamite. At the next rodeo, Tex manages to overpower the gang and save other riders from being killed in an explosion. As it turns out, Barker and his gang were actually responsible for the murders of the rodeo riders, which included Tex's brother. Tex gives the sheriff the poisoned needle as evidence. Eventually, he learns that Carmen is a secret agent. As a result, Carmen gives up her agency to build a ranch with Tex.

anchor_text: Brendan 'Bren' O'Malley, convicted of murder, fled to Mexico. He decides to visit a woman he loved sixteen years ago, Belle. He learns that she is married to John Breckenridge, currently absent; and that she has a sixteen-year-old daughter, Missy, to whom he is immediately attracted. Back at the ranch, John Breckenridge is looking for men to drive his herd all the way to Texas. O'Malley agrees, against one-fifth of this herd. Turns out he's being chased by Sheriff Dana Stribling who joins him at the Breckenridge Ranch. O'Malley offers to take him back to his old job as a cowboy and help him lead the animals to the town where he himself is wanted for the murder of his brother-in-law. Stribling agrees, hoping to deliver O'Malley to justice as soon as they pass through his jurisdiction. During the voyage, Breckenridge, who was also a drunk and a coward, was killed in a brawl; Stribling and Belle, increasingly attracted to each other, planned to marry. Meanwhile, O'Malley and Missy fall in love with each other. This increases the tension between the outlaw and the sheriff. The day before their planned confrontation, Belle reveals to O'Malley that Missy is his daughter. Faced with his impossible love, O'Malley confronts the sheriff with an empty revolver barrel, and dies.
text_a: Brendan 'Bren' O'Malley (Kirk Douglas), a gunman on the run from justice for committing murder, is pursued by Sheriff Dana Stribling (Rock Hudson). On his flight to Mexico, O'Malley stops at the ranch where Belle (Dorothy Malone) lives, with whom years ago he had a romantic relationship. Belle lives with her husband, John Breckenridge (Joseph Cotten) and their sixteen-year-old daughter Melissa ('Missy', Carol Lynley), who quickly falls in love with the gunman. After O'Malley, Stribling arrives at the ranch, where he informs O'Malley your intention to detain him so that he can be tried and convicted in Texas for the murder committed by him. Stribling, in turn, falls in love with Belle, while Breckenridge offers to hire both of them to help him drive his cattle to Texas itself and sell them. During the transfer, in which O'Malley and Stribling maintain a constant dialectical duel, Breckenridge dies in a quarrel and various events occur. Despite this, the cattle are finally delivered in Texas, and O'Malley and Stribling face off in a final duel.

At the rodeo, Tex wins all the competitions, which is why Barker orders Carmen to engage Tex in a fight with another rodeo rider named Squint. Although Carmen warns Tex instead, he still goes to the saloon to see her dance. There, he gets into a fight with Squint, from which he emerges victorious, saving Lucky's life. anchor_text: The film is a fantastic reenactment of real events that took place about two centuries before the birth of Christ when the people of Israel had to fight against the Hellenistic rulers led by Judas Maccabeus and his brothers.
text_a: In about 1210, a young shepherd, James of Cloys, preached a new crusade. Hundreds of young people leave their villages on hearing the call and go out on the roads to free the tomb of Christ. A traveling monk hears confession from some children along the way.
text_b: A century and a half before the birth of Jesus, the people of Israel led by Judas Maccabeus and his brothers fight against the Syrian invaders.

anchor_text: In 1988, Peru is at the height of a political crisis. When Georgina, a young Quechua woman from the Andes who sells potatoes on the border with Lima, goes to a fake maternity hospital. As soon as she gives birth, the child is taken from her. In her distress, she turns to a major newspaper, where she reports the strange disappearance of her newborn daughter. The journalist Pedro Campos is assigned to the case and begins to investigate. His investigations lead him to a government official and the discovery of a state secret.
text_a: Buenos Aires in the early 1980s. The wife of a wealthy official of the ruling military regime in Argentina, Alicia, a high school history teacher, has a doubt: after listening to the confession of her best friend Ana, who tells her that she suffered sexual violence during a period of detention for her political ideas, she begins to speculate that her illegally adopted daughter Gaby may have been born to desaparecidos. During his search for news regarding his adopted daughter, he meets a lady, Sara, who is part of the grandmothers of Plaza de Mayo, also looking for a granddaughter born during the detention of her daughter, then died, and given in foster care to people close to the military junta. Alicia begins a painful journey to discover the truth.
text_b: Peru, 1988, under the presidency of Alan García, in the midst of armed conflict (1980-2000) with the Shining Path movement, a young Quechua woman representing an entire marginalized indigenous population, is pregnant with her first child. Promised free, she gives birth in a private clinic, where the child is immediately taken from her. With no response from the police or the judiciary, the couple turned to a gay journalist who agreed to investigate.

anchor_text: Rollo Treadway is an idle, carefree, lazy young billionaire who's never had to work. Due to a strange combination of circumstances, he is abandoned on a cruise ship adrift in the Pacific Ocean, the Navigator, accompanied by Patsy, the girl he would like to marry. Will our two young men manage to survive, alone on a boat and without the help of servants?
text_a: The film takes place during World War II. Tugboats were sent on missions in dangerous waters to rescue damaged Allied ships. An American named David Ross is entrusted by the captain of the first tugboat with the key to the apartment where the attractive Stella lives. If any misfortune should befall the real owner of the key, a member of the tugboat's crew, Ross could use it.
text_b: A shipowner, whose daughter is in love with Rollo Treadway (Buster Keaton), sells one of his ships to a country to use it in the war with another country; however, the agents of the enemy country have the mission to leave the ship adrift so that it sinks and cannot be used against their country. Unaware of the ship's fate, Rollo Treadway and the girl who spurned his proposal of marriage, Betsy O'Brien (Kathryn McGuire), embark on the abandoned ship adrift in the Pacific Ocean.

anchor_text: The pretty Toinette lives with her aunt Julie, who runs a hotel. One day, Toinette is seduced by hotel guest Darius Kerbellan and almost driven to suicide. However, the painter Sillon saves them. Sillon introduces Toinette to a Parisian fashion designer and she gets a job as a model. In Paris, she meets on the street King Sandor of Astorra, who immediately falls in love with her. Sandor has political problems in his home country of Astorra. His state is nearly bankrupt and insurgents around General Padilla threaten his position of power. In Paris, he seeks financial support from the wealthy Cornelius Corbett to arm himself for the fight with Padilla. When Cornelius Corbett meets the young couple, he too falls in love with Toinette. The girl, however, wants nothing to do with him. Then billionaire Corbett makes an offer to Sandor. He would help him out financially, but Sandor would have to give him Toinette. Sandor refuses and travels with Toinette back to his home in Astorra. Corbett then begins funding General Padilla's coup plans. Padilla's coup attempt is successful. Sandor and Toinette are going to prison. The throne is lost to Sandor. However, Corbett is plagued by guilt, and he now allows the lovers to leave Astorra. For Toinette and Sandor, a new life is about to begin.
text_a: Pretty Sonny dreams of escaping her dreary routine and living the life of an outlaw. When she meets the dreaded bandit Jed, she sees her chance coming. Jed is an unusual outlaw who only robs the rich and hides out in Bandera with the peaceful Somillo tribe. Sheriff Franciscus is his worst enemy, because he's been after him relentlessly ever since Jed escaped from his prison, leaving behind a trail of corpses. After initially bluntly rejecting Sonny's accompaniment, Jed finds himself forced to accept her due to Sonny's stubbornness and the threat posed by Franciscus. After several joint robberies, the two marry and then rob the priest. When Franciscus manages to stop the pair, they demand amnesty and promise him a share of the spoils. Franciscus sets their love nest on fire, but blinds himself in the process; Sonny and Jed escape. At a banquet hosted by the noble Don García Moreno, Jed takes his wife hostage and brings her to Bandera. Jealous Sonny then leads Sheriff Franciscus to Jed's hideout. Jed is badly wounded and is being nursed back to health by Sonny. Almost slavishly, he now has to follow Sonny reverse ratio compared to the beginning.
text_b: The pretty Toinette lives with her aunt Julie, who runs a hotel. One day, Toinette is seduced by a hotel guest, Darius Kerbellan, who almost drives her to suicide. However, she is saved by the painter Sillon, who introduces Toinette to a Parisian couturier and she gets a job as a model. In Paris, she meets on the street King Sandor of Astor, who immediately falls in love with her. Sandor has political problems in his home country, as his state is nearly bankrupt and insurgents led by General Padilla threaten his position in power. The king unsuccessfully sought financial support from the wealthy Cornelius Corbett in order to arm himself for the fight against Padilla. When Cornelius Corbett meets the young couple, he also falls in love with Toinette. The girl, however, wants nothing to do with him. The billionaire then makes an offer to Sandor. He would help him financially to get out of trouble but Sandor must leave him Toinette. Sandor refuses and returns to his homeland with Toinette. Corbett then began financing General Padilla's planned coup. Padilla's coup attempt succeeds and Sandor and Toinette are imprisoned. The throne is lost to Sandor but Corbett, consumed with remorse, allows the lovers to leave Astorra. A new life is beginning for Toinette and Sandor.

anchor_text: After losing her husband in a car accident, Edna Mae Macaulay discovers she has the power to heal. Some people in the small town she's retreated to accept this strange power while others are wary...
text_a: It's the story of a woman who survives her husband in a car accident and from that moment on discovers that she has acquired the power to heal others.
text_b: After the 1929 stock market crash in Texas, U.S.A., a widow, Edna Spalding, fought to preserve her farm, her family. While others advised her to sell her property, Edna, a young widow with two children, struggled to keep her property. She'll get help from a blind man and a black sharecropper.

anchor_text: Pepi is growing marijuana on his balcony. A policeman who lives across the street notices this and comes to her house to reprimand her. His eyes are so lustful that she lifts his robe and offers to make love to him in exchange for his silence. The policeman agrees. However, she tells him that he must go through behind, not wanting to lose her virginity, which she wanted to coin for 60,000 pesetas (about 360 euros), as we will learn later. The policeman refuses and penetrates her sex. He realized when he heard her screams that Pepi was telling the truth. She then decides to avenge this rape with the help of Bom and his group of musicians, but also that of Luci, the masochistic but abandoned wife of the policeman who treats her like her mother.
text_a: Pepi is a middle-class girl from post-Franco Madrid who lives alone and is supported by her family. Discovered by her harasser, a cop, for marijuana plants grown on the terrace, she offers herself sexually in exchange for silence. For the economic damage suffered, as she intended to sell her virginity, Pepi plans to take revenge but the beating of the policeman does not go as planned, as the man's twin brother is the one who pays for it. She then fraternizes incognito with the policeman's wife, Luci, a frustrated housewife originally from Murcia, introducing her to Bom, a transgressive sixteen-year-old rocket girl with whom she begins a homosexual relationship. Light for the young woman abandons the marital roof, as she can satisfy her own instinct of submission, which the jealous husband denies her. After a parenthesis of grotesque situations that affect the characters, the policeman reaches the consort by beating her hard: having found the virile bitterness he so desired, Luci returns to her husband and abandons Pepi and Bom, now ready to start a new life together.
text_b: The plot begins with the death of Bernarda's second husband, Antonio María Benavides. Family tradition requires that after the funeral mass, the widow commit her five daughters to eight years of mourning. For the young women, this means complete isolation from the outside world. Thus begins the central conflict of the drama. Angustias is the only daughter from her first marriage and inherited a fortune from her father. Because of this, and because she is the eldest of the daughters, she has permission to become engaged to Pepe el Romano. Adela, at 20 the youngest, rebels against her mother's strict order and also falls in love with Pepe el Romano, who returns her love. Nevertheless, for financial reasons, he decides to marry Angustias. Pepe el Romano meets at night not only with his future wife Angustias, but also secretly with Adela. Martirio, her jealous sister, denounces Adela in front of her mother. Wishing to restore order to the house, Bernarda shoots Pepe el Romano with a rifle in the courtyard. When Adela hears the shot, she mistakenly believes her lover is dead. As a result, Adela took her own life.

She wants to have him beaten up by friends, the Bomitoni, a punk rock band, but it doesn't hit the rapist, but his twin brother. Bom is Pepis' girlfriend and the lead singer of the Bomitoni. Together with her, she still befriends Luci, the police officer's masochistic wife, out of vengeance. Luci leaves her husband and becomes Bom's submissive sidekick. The three enjoy the wild parties and concerts of Madrid's underground Movida life, where Pedro Almodóvar personally organizes an erection contest. Luci, despite being constantly abused by her sadistic husband, returns to him. That leaves Pepi and Bom, the girls from the gang. Hand in hand and full of ideas, they plan their future.
