anchor_text: The story picks up where the last installment left off: Chev Chelios falls out of the helicopter and crashes into a car. Miraculously, he's still alive, but as soon as he opens his eyes, he's kidnapped by the Chinese. They take him to a clinic to harvest most of his organs. They start by removing his heart, which they replace with an artificial heart powered by a small external battery that indicates the level of charge. Shortly after this operation, he manages to escape and learns that Johnny Wang, a Japanese thug, holds his biological heart. Then a race against time begins because his artificial heart, which must be regularly recharged with electricity, has a limited lifespan.
text_a: Hitman Chev Chelios learns that his rival Verona has injected him with a poison that blocks the adrenaline receptors. To survive, Chev needs to be in constant motion, because if his adrenaline levels drop too low, his heart could stop. From then on, Chelios races against the clock to find the antidote and embarks on the craziest schemes to stay alive. He'll drink more or less safe cocktails in order to keep his frenzied pace and his adrenaline levels high. When he can't swallow all kinds of exciting products, we'll be able to see him running around, stealing a cop's motorcycle, speeding through the streets of Los Angeles, going nuts in an elevator or having sex in the middle of the street in Chinatown, etc.
text_b: Immediately after being temporarily dead, Chev Chelios is taken to a health facility by Chinese operators, who, trying to bring him back to life, transplant him an artificial heart that must be continuously recharged to avoid death, using electronic devices. Before he awoke from his eternal slumber, the same Asian criminal organization that previously injected him with the poison, transplanted his heart into the boss Poon Dong, who needed it. In the mad rush that sees him chasing his enemies to retrieve his heart, with the help of Dr. Miles, Chev must constantly attach himself to electronic devices of all kinds to recharge his new apparatus. Eventually, with the help of Dr. Miles, he gets his heart back.

anchor_text: Tessa Golding is a young orphan hired as an assistant by her stepmother, Divine, and her sisters-in-law, Olympia and Athena, to accompany them to the Royal Lagoons Resort for an audition. The sisters-in-law want to audition for a musical adaptation of Cinderella alongside Reed West, a famous singer. Tessa befriends a make-up artist, Georgia, who persuades her to audition for the role of Cinderella by wearing makeup to avoid being recognized by her mother-in-law and sisters-in-law. So Tessa becomes Bella Snow. But when she gets the part, she's forced to keep that cover so she can continue to live her passion. But realizing her dream in secret is going to prove to be harder than she thought.
text_a: Tessa Golding (Sofia Carson), is forced to accompany her stepmother (Jennifer Tilly) and stepsisters (Amy Louise Wilson, Jazzara Jaslyn) to a Cinderella competition to work as her assistant. In this competition, Tessa realizes that she has everything she needs to be the next Cinderella and, although she initially hesitated, she decides it's time to follow her heart and make her dreams come true, and she shows up at the auditions.
text_b: Ali Rose, a young woman with an incredible voice, quits her job at a small-town bar after her boss refuses to pay her. She decides to pursue her dreams in Los Angeles. After several failed auditions, she accidentally stumbles upon a cabaret bar, the Burlesque Lounge, where she discovers Tess, the owner, in the middle of a performance. Falling under the spell of the place, she tries to convince Tess to hire her but she refuses. Stubborn, Ali then begins to serve customers, prompting Tess to hire her but only as a waitress. But when one of the dancers gets pregnant, a spot opens up in the troupe. Ali decides to give up everything for Tess to give him a chance. She then joins the troupe and finally begins to exploit her talent, which earns her an enemy, Nikki, one of the main dancers. Nikki, wanting to trap her, switches off the sonic whilst she dances. After a moment of hesitation, Ali then begins to sing in front of Tess, the team and the audience are astonished and receive an ovation. Meanwhile, Ali grows closer to Jack, the bartender, who is engaged to be married. For her part, Tess is dealing with the cabaret's loss of momentum and mounting bills, however, Ali's spectacular talent could help her climb the ladder.

anchor_text: It's 1982. Will Proudfoot is a young boy raised in a very strict religious community: he is not allowed to watch television or go to the movies. But one day, his friend Lee Carter shows him a pirated version of the movie Rambo. Captivated by the film, Will and Lee decide to make their own remake of the adventures of John Rambo. Their version is a big hit with their classmates. But that was without Didier Revol, a French correspondent whose arrival is causing trouble...
text_a: After picking up a cell phone during a student brawl, a New Orleans bartender named Will begins receiving disturbing text messages and calls. The stored photos are ultra-violent and bloody. While his girlfriend Carrie loses herself physically and mentally while investigating the contents, Will refuses to get involved with her in her research. However, their investigation leads them to a group of students who have discovered a strange book explaining how to create monstrous creatures out of wounds...
text_b: It's 1982. Growing up in a strict religious community, little Will has no right to watch TV or go to the movies. His daily life is disrupted the day his friend Lee Carter shows him a pirated copy of Rambo. Letting their imaginations run wild, Will and Lee decide to make a film inspired by the adventures of the famous character played by Sylvester Stallone. The "remake" is a sensation among the classmates of the two boys, but the arrival of Didier Revol, a French correspondent, will create quite a few problems in the classroom.

anchor_text: Madame Anaïs de Beauperthuis is meeting with Lieutenant Tavernier for a secret tête-à-tête. Fadinard happens to be passing by in his carriage on his way to his wedding. At a little rest, the horse eats the lady's fashionable straw hat. In order not to embarrass Madame de Beauperthuis in front of her husband, Fadinard promises to get an identical hat as a replacement. Fearing that Fadinard could not fulfill his promise, Madame de Beauperthuis now accompanies Fadinard to his bride. This leads to countless entanglements and misunderstandings: Lieutenant Tavernier threatens Fadinard with a duel if a suitable hat is not found, and Helene his bride is jealous because of the constant presence of Madame de Beauperthuis.
text_a: While Fadinard is about to marry Helen, his horse eats the hat of Anaïs, a walker. However, she is in gallant company with Emile, a soldier, and cannot reappear without her hat in front of her husband, for fear of arousing his suspicions. So she demands that Fadinard bring her an identical hat. While going to the town hall followed by Helen, her future father-in-law and his guests, Fadinard tries, without them suspecting anything, to find the famous hat...
text_b: Michel (René Lefèvre) was a penniless painter who lived in the attics of a Paris apartment building with Prosper (Jean-Louis Allibert), a sculptor, and Béatrice (Annabella), a star dancer. Although engaged to Beatrice, who is herself courted by Prosper, Michel flirts with a beautiful American, Wanda. Pursued by his creditors, cornered on the ground floor by all the shopkeepers in the neighborhood, he learns that his lottery ticket has won one million florins. Unfortunately, in the confusion of the chase, a mafia boss named Father La Tulipe (Paul Ollivier), officially an honest pickpocket, broke into Beatrice's apartment and to escape the police borrowed Michel's old jacket. La Tulipe quickly sold the jacket to a lyric artist, Sopranelli, who was leaving for America and looking for an outfit for his role in The Bohemians. Turns out the winning ticket was in his jacket pocket...

anchor_text: Every year, 34-year-old New Yorker Lenny is allowed to invite his two sons, Sage, 9, and Frey, 7, to his home for two weeks. These two weeks, granted to him after the divorce in the custody agreement, must be enough for the next few months to pass on to his sons fond memories of their father together. Born Chaos Lenny makes an honest effort to be a good father to Sage and Frey. But his efforts get bogged down by his work commitments. Nevertheless, he manages to establish a connection with his sons: with a short trip to the countryside, women who serve as surrogate mothers, magical blankets and Lenny's anarchic parenting model. This, above all, puts him to the test: Father or friend?
text_a: Sportswriter Lenny and his wife, Amanda, adopt young Max, who grows up to be a smart, witty, and resourceful child. One day, Lenny can no longer control his curiosity and sets out to find the boy's birth mother. This one is the prostitute Linda, who also does not correspond to Lenny's idea in any other way - especially in terms of her intellect. Nevertheless, they become friends, and Lenny tries to put them back on the path of virtue. He buys her off her pimp for basketball cards and tries to set her up with a decent guy. When his marriage is in crisis, Lenny sleeps with Linda, and the two have a daughter, which he never learns, just as Linda never learns that Lenny's son Max is her biological child.
text_b: In New York City, Lenny, an imaginative and unpredictable father, gets his two boys, Sage and Frey, aged 7 and 9, back for a fortnight. He's a projectionist and has a relationship with a young woman who visits him from time to time. Raised in an atmosphere of permissiveness and everyday foolishness, both children are very inventive when it comes to jokes and nonsense of all kinds. Like the various adventures and characters involved, the film is made up of uncoordinated and unpredictable sequences filmed with a shoulder-mounted camera.

anchor_text: Lorna Green is a dominatrix who practices her BDSM art on the stage of a nightclub. Her manager Bill Malway, who discovered her in Lisbon when she broke into his locked room, is having an affair with her. But he doesn't know anything about this enigmatic woman's past. As a bisexual, she suffers from nightmares in which she kills her romantic partners, both male and female. She seems to be under the influence of a strange man who drives her to commit crimes. Is he the instigator of real murders committed by Lorna in a trance? Could it be Satan, who came to Earth to watch over Lorna, the Succubus from Hell? The bodies pile up as Lorna loses her sense of reality and dreams. After murdering Bill and completing her mission of death, Lorna returns to the castle of her master, Lucifer...
text_a: A gangster's body is found lifeless by the police. The investigation leads to a socialite who has since escaped. Even the press investigates her, discovering that she's become rich and famous out of the blue. Edda, that's her name, went to her parents' house and here she recalls her past, from the failure of her marriage to the latest events. Edda was married to Roy, and they had a son, Tommy. But after the death of her son in a tragic bicycle accident, she and her husband separate. Since then Edda accepts any job to provide for herself, becoming first a saleswoman and then, taking advantage of her beauty, a model and an escort in the evenings. You wouldn't happen to know Martin, an honest, low-wage employee. Edda helps him find another job and a career, but both end up getting more and more entangled in a vicious circle where social position and wealth are pursued without any moral scruples.
text_b: Lorna Green is a young and exuberant woman who works as a dancer in one of the most fashionable nightclubs in Lisbon: the Nov Club. Green's role is to participate in a show of sadomasochistic tinge that culminates in a simulated murder. Her performances are a great success with the audience but, little by little, Lorna begins to let herself be mentally influenced by her character and begins to lose the contact between reality and fiction. Her representative, Bill Mulligan, will try to help her, unaware that searching for the causes of Lorna's behavior will become a nightmare from which no one will be able to escape.

anchor_text: My friends Noah and Tech are talented basketball players. Noah wants to be a doctor on an athletic scholarship. When he commits a felony, Tech takes the fall to make Noah's scholarship look safer. Unlike Noah's future career aspirations, Tech just wants to catch up on his high school graduation and then earn his money playing basketball. During their many games, when scout Vaughn notices Noah and wants to sign him to a professional club, Noah reconsiders his future. As the duo's team wins more often on the road against other teams, a match is organized against their biggest rival. This is the jewel. The Jewels have been undefeated for a long time, and the players have become arrogant from their winning streak. Noah, Tech and their friends travel to Los Angeles, and with hard training, they pass the game.
text_a: Noah Cruise (Wesley Jonathan), a talented basketball player, receives a scholarship from UCLA after the death of his mother. Despite his athletic abilities, he decided to use his scholarship to become a doctor. He then has to face his agent Vaughn who wants him to become a professional in the NBA. Tech, his best friend, wants to graduate and beat his opponent, Jewelz, in a streetball tournament. Their lives will change when they fall in love with Vanessa and Eboni and decide to move to Los Angeles.
text_b: Marco has numerous professional and personal problems. After being fired as coach of a basketball team, he causes a traffic accident, which is punishable by a large fine. Marco has to choose between 18 months in prison or 90 days of community service. He opts for social work, in which he is asked to train the basketball team 'Los Amigos', which is made up of athletes with various disabilities. At first, Marco takes the situation negatively, but as time passes, he realizes that there is much more he can learn from these people.

anchor_text: When Mae Holland gets hired by the Circle, she doesn't look back. Based on a campus in California, Circle, an Internet service provider, connects personal emails, social networks, consumer purchases and banking transactions to a universal operating system, creating a new hyper-digital era of civility and transparency. As the young woman walks around the circular headquarters, the open spaces, the huge glass cafeterias, the comfortable dormitories for those who stay to work in the evening (in La Colonie), the modernity of the premises and the intense activity delight her. There's all-night parties, famous musicians play on the lawn, sports activities, clubs and brunches are offered, and there's even an aquarium containing rare fish reported by the CEO. Mae can't believe her chance to work for the most influential company there is - even if the campus absorbs her completely (several screens, several connected bracelets), distancing her more and more from her loved ones, even if she exposes herself to the eyes of the world by participating in the Circle's latest project, a technological advance as considerable as it is disturbing, total and live transparency.
text_a: In the near future, the internet company The Circle creates a social network that connects as many users as possible, pursuing the idea of total transparency, a prerequisite for a safer and healthier world. This goal is achieved through a series of projects and experiments, some of which end tragically. The protagonist of the story, narrated in the third person, is Mae Holland. Hired by the company thanks to her friend Annie, she will soon become one of its most convinced and popular members. As her prominence grows and the number of users who follow her increases, Mae finds it increasingly difficult to relate to family and friends who do not share the direction taken by The Circle, and she moves away from it to relate only to members of society. She voices some of the social networking slogans used in the book: "Secrets are lies", "Sharing is caring", and "Privacy is theft".
text_b: In the near future, in the United States, young Mae Holland leads an ordinary life; she is a secretary in a small company, still lives with her parents Bonnie and Vinnie, has no boyfriend and rejects the advances of her friend Mercer. The only solitary activity she offers herself to decompress and recharge is kayaking. One day, her friend Annie Allerton helps her get a job at her company, The Circle, the largest technology services group in the world. Among other things, this is a wonderful opportunity to finally get his father, who suffers from multiple sclerosis, into proper care. As her involvement in the company continues to grow, her bosses Eamon Bailey and Tom Stenton encourage her to participate in a groundbreaking experiment whose goal is to be continuously filmed by a connected mini-camera SeeChange. Now, Mae's choices impact the lives of her loved ones, her friends, and all of humanity, pushing the boundaries of privacy, ethics, and individual freedoms.

anchor_text: In a former mining town in northern England now in decline, "TJ", the owner of the Old Oak, the town's last remaining pub, is struggling more and more to keep open what is now one of the few remaining public spaces for residents. As tension mounts between the locals and the newly arrived Syrian refugees in town, TJ befriends one of them, Yari.
text_a: The Old Oak is the last remaining pub in a former mining village in County Durham, northeast England. Because the mines are closing, people are leaving the country. So, the houses here are cheap and empty, which is why the Syrian refugees are staying there. One of them, the young Yara, befriends her landlord, TJ Ballantyne.[1][2]
text_b: A 210-year-old, 17-metre-high oak tree in the Sologne forest, 10 km from Château de Chambord, is home to a very large number of species: squirrels, geese, mules, ants, etc. This biodiversity is explored for several seasons, without commentary or voice-over. The tree is presented as a multi-storey building.

anchor_text: The Old Oak is the last remaining pub in a former mining village in County Durham, North East England. Because the mines are closed, people are leaving the country. The houses here are therefore cheap and empty, which is why Syrian refugees are being housed in the mining town in 2016. One of them, young Yara, befriends her landlord, TJ Ballantyne, the owner of the "Old Oak", who has made his space available to them as a refuge. Together, they are trying to revitalize the local community by setting up a soup kitchen that feeds people regardless of their background.
text_a: In a former mining town in northern England now in decline, "TJ", the owner of the Old Oak, the town's last remaining pub, is struggling more and more to keep open what is now one of the few remaining public spaces for residents. As tension mounts between the locals and the newly arrived Syrian refugees in town, TJ befriends one of them, Yari.
text_b: The documentary is set in a 210-year-old, 17-metre-high stilt pond in the forest of Sologne. It accompanies the tree and its animal inhabitants for one calendar year. The oak tree is home to squirrels, squirrels, squirrels or chestnut borers, wood mice, woodpeckers, beetles, gophers, robins, cabbages and bluebirds; the surrounding area is home to wild boar, nutria, deer, deer, badger, fox, water frog, heron and hawk. The events are not commented on, but partly accompanied by music.

anchor_text: In 14th century Korea, rebels fight against their king and the monarchy. At a camp in the mountains, Inde, the leader of the rebels, and his comrades are captured. The blacksmith Takse died in captivity. Before he dies, he forms the little figure of Pulgasari out of rice, which is given to his daughter. Pulgasari comes to life through contact with blood. Pulgasari feeds on iron. After each meal, Pulgasari continues to grow into a giant metal-eating monster.
text_a: During the Goryeo period, a king rules the country with an iron fist and reduces the peasant population to misery and famine. An old blacksmith carves a monster out of grains of rice. When it comes into contact with the blood of the blacksmith's daughter, the figurine comes to life and transforms into a giant metal monster called Pulgasari. The king learns that a revolt is taking place and decides to crush it. However, Pulgasari fought alongside the peasant army to overthrow the monarchy.
text_b: The film tells the story of young Ting from the village of Nong Pradu, a peaceful village nestled in a forested valley in Thailand. He's a fan of Muay Thai and secretly trains in it. The villagers entrusted him with the task of recovering the head of the Buddha statue (Ong-Bak) that had been stolen from them. Ting goes to the furnace in Bangkok, discovers the mafia responsible for the theft of Ong-Bak's head and confronts them. He would also have to defend himself against the city's thugs and other opposers. Action, stunts and chase races will now guide his quest.

anchor_text: In 1830, veteran detective Augustus Landor is assigned to investigate a series of murders at the West Point Military Academy. He will be assisted in his task by a young cadet of the academy, the future famous writer Edgar Allan Poe.
text_a: In 1830, veteran detective Augustus Landor must investigate a series of mysterious murders at the United States Military Academy at West Point, New York. He is aided by Edgar Allan Poe, a young cadet at the academy.[1][2]
text_b: Private Franklin Bean gets drunk and deserted after his father's death. As punishment, he is transferred to a military prison populated only by black inmates, and Bean joins forces with fellow inmates and rebels against the prison's fanatical warden, Sergeant Major Otis McKinney.

anchor_text: New York in the year 1980. There are no more cars, only planes. People wear numbers instead of names. Food is taken only by means of pills. Love is regulated, there are only state-arranged marriages. When a woman has more than one lover, the government steps in and performs an exorcism. This time, the young pilot J-21 must outwit the wealthy MT-3 in order to marry his girlfriend LN-18. He's got four months to clean up his image. During those three months, MT-3 is considered the fiancée. Scientist Z-4 gives J-21 the opportunity to launch a rocket to Mars. J-21 and his friend RT-42 meet Single 0, who was killed by a lightning strike in the 1930s and has now been revived by scientists. The three men become friends. Unable to cope with life in the future, Single 0 hides aboard the Mars rocket. J-21 is to be escorted by RT-42 of all things. All Martians are twins, with one part good and the other evil. The good ones are called Loo-Loo (woman) and Loko (man), the bad ones Boo-Boo (woman) and Boko (man). Since the three Earthlings don't know if they're dealing with the good or the bad side of the twins, incidents happen all the time. Single 0, RT-42 and J-21 can escape with the rocket back to Earth. They're going to see if J-21 is worthy of marrying LN-18. As proof that they were on Mars, Single 0 presents the authorities with a captured Boko. There's nothing stopping the wedding now. In addition, Single 0 meets his aged son Axel.
text_a: New York, a 1980 from the future. Single 0, a man who was electrocuted in the year 1930 while playing golf, is resurrected by a group of doctors and scientists. The city has changed a lot: road traffic has become aerial, with considerable traffic of flying vehicles. The inhabitants don't have a name, they have a number. Marriages are now arranged by the government and children are born artificially. Single 0 comes to meet LN-18, a beautiful brunette courted by two different men, J-21 and MT-30. Although the authorities have already chosen MT-30 as the girl's future husband, she loves J-21 and is reciprocated. Single 0 then accompanies J-21 aboard a spaceship on a journey to the planet Mars; there the population is entirely composed of beings with dual personalities, one good and the other bad. The main characters meet a beautiful Martian princess. The troubled adventure provides the two, upon their return, with the fame of heroes, so J-21 is finally granted permission to marry the beloved.
text_b: J.R. (Harvey Keitel) is a typical young Italian-American Catholic on the streets of New York City. Even as an adult, he stays close to home with a basic group of friends with whom he drinks and has fun. He gets involved with a local girl he meets on the Staten Island Ferry and decides he wants to get married and settle down. As their relationship deepens, he rejects her offer to have sex because he believes she is a virgin and wants to wait rather than "consent" to her.[1] One day, his girlfriend tells him that she was once raped by an ex-boyfriend. This crushes J.R., who rejects her and tries to return to his old life of drinking with his friends. However, after a particularly wild party with friends, he realizes he still loves her and returns to his apartment early one morning. He awkwardly tells her that he forgives her and says that he "will marry her anyway".[1] Upon hearing this, she tells him that the marriage would never work if his past weighs so heavily on her. J.R. gets furious and calls her a "whore", but quickly backtracks and says he's confused by the whole situation. She tells him to go home and he returns to the Catholic church, but finds no comfort.

anchor_text: In a German state, 1780. The witch-hunter Belshazzar of Ross makes the people feel his hard hand; he rules according to lust and sudden whim, exploiting his position to enrich himself and at the same time to make young women sexually submissive to him. He had his opponents thrown into dungeons and later burned at the stake after negotiations and confessions extorted under torture and falsified evidence. When the highly respected Alexander of Salmenau, who had Ross declared a master of witches, his wife Elisabeth declared a witch, and their son excommunicated, also ends up in this way, a jailer helps the prisoners to escape. Elisabeth succeeds in rousing the people against the increasingly brazen burnings and atrocities committed by Ross. Eventually, his residence is stormed, his people are killed, and he himself is killed.
text_a: In a German state in 1780, the witch hunter Balthasar von Ross wielded an iron grip over the people, ruling according to their whims and caprices, using his position to enrich himself and, incidentally, to have sexual relations with several young women. He had his opponents thrown in jail and then burned at the stake after mock negotiations, forced confessions under torture, and falsified evidence. When the well-respected Alexander of Salmenau, whom Ross had declared a witch, his witch wife Elisabeth, and their excommunicated son also end up in the same way, a jailer helps the prisoners escape. Elizabeth succeeds in stirring up the people against the increasingly audacious wood burning and evil deeds committed by Ross. Eventually, his residence was stormed, and he and his men were killed.
text_b: Former witch hunter Abner Lundberg is forced to take up the fight again when the centuries-old witch Elisabeth Dexter returns. Because of a family curse, it's still looking for victims. But this time, Lundberg is backed by young ghost hunter Edward Carnby, who is also using a magic dagger to hunt down the witch. When he injures himself with the weapon, falls into a coma-like state and has visions, he is picked up by a troop of PSI mercenaries who are also hunting the witch. Eventually, they manage to locate the witch's secret laboratory in New York's Central Park and engage her in a final battle.

anchor_text: Messina is a small town in the United States. The residents are crazy about American football and support the local high school football team, the Messina Spartans, which was particularly successful under coach Eddie Rake. Rake was the Spartans' head coach for 34 years, winning thirteen championships with them and coaching 714 players. One of them was All-American Neely Crenshaw, who had a love-hate relationship with his coach. Crenshaw played quarterback for the Spartans for three years and then went to Georgia Tech, where he played for their football team. After a hard hit, the player's career is suddenly over and he wanders the world. When he learns that Rake is dying, he returns to the small town. There he meets old teammates and more Spartans. They tell each other stories and reminisce. He also meets his childhood sweetheart, whom he left for a quickie. He confesses that he still loves her, but the happily married mother of two turns him down. Eventually Rake dies and Crenshaw gives a eulogy at his funeral.
text_a: Chester Lee (Rodney Dangerfield) is desperate for a promotion and some respect from his boss. To impress her, he proclaims to have been a good football (soccer) player in his youth and so finds himself leading a team called the Ladybugs with his assistant Julie (Jackie Harry). The team, sponsored by his company, had largely dominated the previous seasons. Unfortunately, only one player is back for the new season. The players on the new team, which includes the boss's daughter, Kimberly, are at a loss, making a terrible start to the season, and the boss is less than impressed. In his personal life, Chester is engaged to Bess (Ilene Graff), who has a son, Matthew (Jonathan Brandis), from a previous marriage. Matthew happens to be a great athlete, but he's got bad grades that get him kicked off the football team. Chester invites Matthew to watch the Ladybugs practice and get some advice. Matthew has had a crush on Kimberly since high school, which partly inspires Chester to persuade him to dress like a girl and play for the team as "Martha"...
text_b: Eddie Rake was the legendary coach of the Spartans, the high school football team from the fictional town of Messina, deep in the American provinces. Rake was an extremely demanding coach, subjecting his boys to grueling and exhausting workouts, never rewarding them with praise or applause. Now old and sick, the coach is dying. The news spread and reached his former student-players, who gather every night on the bleachers of the stadium to relive the moments of glory of a team that had won many championships and had ignited an enormous passion in the inhabitants of Messina. Even Neely Crenshaw, the unfulfilled promise of those Spartans, returns to his hometown, where he'll find his former teammates, the old stadium he played in, a girl he left too lightly, but most of all, he'll find the answers to the questions he's always asked, getting to see the much-hated former coach from a completely different perspective.

anchor_text: Marie Colvin is a journalist sent to Sri Lanka where, due to an explosion, she loses sight in one eye. Nevertheless, he continues to travel to war zones to witness the horrors.
text_a: Mary is a writer who has an illusive relationship with her publisher, only to find out he's married. Her faithful lover, on the other hand, meets the publisher's wife and brings the two women together.
text_b: The story of war journalist Marie Colvin, who managed to get deep into the winter of 2012 in Syria to cover the civil war. Colvin and photographer Rémi Ochlik were killed by an improvised explosive device, as the official media fled the Syrian army's bombardment.

anchor_text: Antoine Doinel and Christine Darbon the couple of Stolen Kisses have married and are struggling with married life. The everyday life of its protagonists makes the film a masterpiece of light and poignant everyday comedy. The couple lives in a large rented house with a backyard. Christine gives violin lessons to untalented children in the living room, while Antoine paints flowers in the yard. Sex life appears as if two children or siblings were playing in bed, reading or joking. It is only when Christine complies with her husband's request to put on the glasses that it appears as a fetish. The film accompanies the couple to Christine's parents and in numerous scenes of everyday life. Antoine's affair with a Japanese woman leads to a breakup, but after a brief separation, they reconcile with the bitter end of being a normal married couple. The climax is the scene in which Christine learns of her husband's adultery through the opening of tulips and awaits him at home: as a geisha, dressed and made up, this image of guilt seems like a triumph. Then a tear will roll down your cheek.
text_a: Antoine and Christine Doinel, a young married couple awaiting the birth of their first child, live a modest but quiet existence: she gives violin lessons, he prepares the artificial colors of flowers destined for a neighboring building. Trying to integrate into the machinery of social and economic progress, Antoine gets a job in the industry of the American Max. Christine, meanwhile, completes the maternity and gives birth to a child who wants to call Ghislain but which her husband registers in the registry under the name of Alphonse. Life for the two spouses undergoes some changes: Antoine is surprised by the exotic charm of Kyoko, a client of Max, whose lover he becomes. Through a trivial incident, Christine discovers Antoine's affair and violently attacks him, forcing him to move into a hotel. But the encounters with Kyoko have gradually lost their initial charm and have forcefully reawakened in Antoine the image of the sweet Christine who, in turn, no matter how embittered has not ceased to love her husband. Overcoming their final hesitations, Antoine and Christine finally reconcile and resume their existence together. A year later, they're back together.
text_b: The marriage of Antoine and Christine Doinel, the couple of robbed kisses and table and bed, fails after Antoine cheats on his wife with her friend Liliane. Antoine had previously suspected Christine of having a lesbian relationship with Liliane. Meanwhile, he's in love with Sabine, a record saleswoman. Antoine and Christine are the first couple to separate under the new divorce law, which requires mutual consent. Son Alphonse is staying with Christine. When Antoine takes the boy to the station, he sees his platonic childhood sweetheart Colette and jumps on the train to join her. The two argue over the untruths in Antoine's autobiographical novel "Love Salad". Antoine pulls the emergency brake and flees. He meets his dead mother's ex-lover and accompanies him to the cemetery. Shortly afterwards, Christine is asked to mediate between Antoine and his new flame Sabine. She does not meet Sabine, but Colette, who is in love with Sabine's brother, the bookseller Xavier. The two women talk about Antoine, who in the meantime rushes into Sabine's arms and tells her a story that moves Sabine so much that she wants to at least try to stay with him.

anchor_text: The two children of the Ubriacco family have grown up and speak normally, enough to ask for a dog for Christmas. They'll have two: Rocks, a rescued hornbill from the pound, and Daphne, a refined poodle owned by James' entrepreneurial boss, Samantha. Rocks and Daphne, who are opposites in every way, give us their heated comments.
text_a: Mickey and Julie are all grown up now, and they speak with their own voices. Her parents, Mollie and James are still together raising their children. One day, Mollie loses her job and spends the day at home taking care of her children, while James works as a private pilot for Samantha, a beautiful executive, who submits him to relentless harassment. Additionally, two new members have been added to the family: Rocks, a stray dog that was about to be euthanized, and Daphne, a spoiled poodle owned by Samantha.
text_b: Sam and Charlotte, two elderly married couples, have decided to separate, but are waiting to tell their children and relatives until after Christmas, so they can have one last meal together. In reality, however, there are many secrets hidden by all members of the extravagant Cooper family.

anchor_text: James Ubriacco is hired as a pilot by Samantha DBonne, the handsome president of Majique. On the same day, his wife, Mollie, receives her notice. After numerous unsuccessful applications, she works temporarily in a department store as Santa's elf. Son Mikey has been wanting a dog for a long time and having learned that Santa Claus is not real during a visit to his mother's department store, James wants to cheer him up by giving him a dog. Mikey calls this one Rocks. Shortly after, they meet Samantha, who also knows of the children's desire for a dog, and since she can no longer take care of her dog Daphne due to the numerous business trips, she entrusts her to the Ubriacco family. Now the family has two dogs. At Christmas, Samantha convinces James to accompany her on a business trip under the guise of a vacation. In fact, she intends to seduce him. James promises his family to be home in time for Christmas Eve, but Samantha prevents this. So Mollie decides to take the kids and the dogs to see him. In the woods, she goes off the road and gets stuck. Luckily, they are spotted by park rangers and taken to a ranger station, but Rocks escapes into the wilderness. When James sees Samantha's play, he's upset and wants to go home right away. Samantha threatens to fire him, but James doesn't care, so he leaves. In the forest, he is confronted by a pack of wolves, but Rocks comes to his aid and is slightly injured in the fight against the lead wolf. Eventually, the wolves escape when one of them bites James's beeper, which is then activated and the resulting noise hurts the wolves' ears. Shortly thereafter, he meets Mollie and the children, and they can still celebrate Christmas together.
text_a: Family Dog Rag tells the story of "his family" from a dog's point of view: The marriage between Charlotte and Sam Cooper is not going well. After the kids are out of the house, there's little that connects the two of them. But the Coopers aren't the only ones who have their problems with each other. Now that Christmas is just around the corner, other members of the family want to keep the difficulties they are facing a secret from others so as not to tarnish the Christmas spirit. So Hank, the Coopers' son, has just been fired and he's hiding his unemployment because he doesn't want to be seen as a "loser" after his divorce. Eleanor, his sister, is annoyed at being asked every year why she's still single, so she brings Joe, a soldier she met by chance at the airport, to introduce him as her boyfriend. Charlotte's father, Bucky, would rather spend Christmas at his family's pub and has brought along a young waitress, Ruby, who doesn't know what to do with her life. Emma, Charlotte's sister, suffers from inferiority complexes towards Charlotte and is caught shoplifting. But on Christmas Eve, the family's secrets are revealed.
text_b: The Ubriacco family is going through a period of ups and downs as James gets a job as a private jet pilot for Samantha, a big shot in finance. Molly, on the other hand, lost her job as a tax collector and now works as Santa's elf helper in a department store. Mikey and Julie, on the other hand, are all grown up: he's about eight years old, she's six. Mikey is traumatized because he has discovered, to his great surprise, that Santa Claus does not exist while Julie is fascinated by the giant basketball players because she is convinced that they can fly. But two more tenants arrive in the house, two dogs: Scag, a stray who enjoys life as much as he can, and Delilah, a purebred puppy. Initially reluctant to interact, the two animals find themselves drawn to each other. Molly, seeing that James has been away for many days, suspects that there is some tenderness between him and Samantha, so she decides to go look for her husband in the high mountains, in the middle of the snow, where the two are trapped, and will also be attacked by wolves. But suddenly, the sun comes out and the family can celebrate Christmas in peace and quiet.

anchor_text: Director Gordon kicks the talented ice dancer Eva out of his ice show because her audience success is greater than that of his wife, the jealous star Alida. Even colleagues who work for Eva will be fired. Radio reporter Thomas Haller tries to help Eva and her colleagues with an outdoor program to other appearance possibilities. During the preparations, however, a cloudburst causes the lake's ice sheet to melt. Only when the entire ensemble of the revue organizer goes on strike to help their colleagues can the new revue spring on the ice. This one will be a resounding success, and Thomas can win Eva's heart.
text_a: Magazine editor Gordon fires talented ice dancer Eva from his ice magazine because her success is greater than that of his wife, the jealous star Alida. The colleagues who are defending Eva are also fired. Radio journalist Thomas Haller tries to give Eva and her colleagues other opportunities to perform with an outdoor show. However, a rainstorm caused the lake's ice cover to melt during the preparations. When the magazine's entire staff went on strike to help their colleagues, the new magazine Le Printemps sur la glace began. It is a great success and Thomas wins Eva's heart.
text_b: Hermann König, the owner of a skating rink, wants his niece Inge to become a skater, but she has other ideas: she really wants to be a singer in magazines. A confusion gives Inge the chance to get a leading role in the revue on which the existence of the theater will depend. However, the theatre manager and the indebted owner agreed by contract that the theatre would continue to exist only if the revue, starring the owner's unmusical and no less unreasonable wife, was a flop. However, due to Inge's confusion and commitment, the review has the best chance of being a success. After a scandal at the premiere and calls for more audience, the owner kicks the team out of the theater. The stage designer and ice hockey player Hans Haller, who fell madly in love with Inge, has the idea of turning the show into an ice skating revue without the knowledge of the skating rink owner.

anchor_text: Brigitte's sister Ginger is dead, and Brigitte herself is infected with lycanthropy. Only the regular injection of an extract of the wolfsbane plant prevents its transformation into a ravening beast. Ginger appears to Brigitte in a vision and tells her that the injections will only stop her transformation, but will not cure her. So Brigitte takes a shot with a lethal dose of Iron Hat. At that moment, she notices that she is being pursued by a male werewolf and tries to escape, but collapses with a toxic shock. When she wakes up, she's in a closed facility, where she's being put into rehab. Brigitte is gradually changing, which is noticed only by Ghost, a young girl with a flourishing imagination and a predilection for horror comics. Tyler, a nurse at the clinic, is now in possession of Brigitte's Iron Hat extract, but only wants to give it to her in exchange for sexual favors, which Brigitte refuses. The other male werewolf is still after Brigitte because he wants to mate with her, and has followed her to the clinic and is stalking her. The only way to escape him is to run. Together with Ghost, she escapes from the asylum, but soon Brigitte needs some of the extract again, and they decide to ask Tyler for help. It also helps, but this time Brigitte reacts differently to the extract. Her body's rejecting it, the transformation's already too far along. With Ghost's help, Brigitte is finally able to kill the werewolf that has been pursuing her, but her own transformation is almost complete by this point. In the end, Ghost locks Brigitte in the basement to turn her into her personal werewolf and gradually prey on all of her enemies.
text_a: At the end of Ginger Snaps, Brigitte had voluntarily infected herself with Ginger's blood in an effort to calm her down, convinced that the werewolf-killing aconite antidote she had made with Sam would cure her. Years later, Brigitte lives on the run, haunted by the apparitions of her dead sister and regularly injecting herself with the antidote. The aconite hadn't cured her. It only slowed down the transformation. Brigitte is still doomed. She's also being chased by a male werewolf who clearly wants to mate with her. One night, she injected herself with too much aconite and collapsed in front of a librarian. The latter calls for help, and Brigitte finds herself locked in a psychiatric hospital, her antidote confiscated. At the hospital, she is confronted by Alice, the headmistress who is convinced Brigitte is simply a drug addict, Tyler, an unscrupulous nurse who offers her a dose of aconite if she sleeps with him, and Ghost, a strange little girl who is the only person who believes her. Between her inevitable metamorphosis and the second werewolf still chasing her, Brigitte must flee the hospital at all costs.
text_b: 1815, in a forest in Canada. Two sisters are wandering aimlessly and at one point find themselves in a completely destroyed Indian camp. There is no living soul, but only dismembered corpses, when an old woman appears as if out of nowhere to warn the two: one sister will kill the other. The two are still shaken when one of them is caught in a wolf trap; an Indian appears who frees her and cures her wound, after which he takes the two sisters to a fort of the desperate. Little by little, they realize that the people around them are terrified of something loose in the forest, something monstrous and uncontrollable. They learn of continued disappearances and that those sent for help have not returned. Suddenly the girls are attacked by a pack of werewolves, who nearly kill them. Aware of the danger they face, they try to escape the place, but when they least expect it, Ginger is bitten by something locked in the village headman's house. The girl starts to sense something's wrong, and now she's all vicious and bloodthirsty. Her sister's gonna find out she's the one who's gonna have to kill her.

text_a: Brigitte is trying to avoid what happened to her sister Ginger, first turned into a werewolf and then killed by Brigitte herself. The terrible disease is already circulating in his blood, and the only hope of salvation lies in an antidote that, however, does nothing but slow down the effects of the disease. Confined to a rehabilitation center for drug addicts, Brigitte, in a confused state, is deprived of the antidote that curbs her blood lust and prevents her from turning into a werewolf who instinctively kills anyone in her path. Meanwhile, another werewolf prowls the neighborhood, hunting her down. By befriending an orphan, nicknamed Ghost, Brigitte manages to escape and takes refuge in Ghost's old home. There he will try in vain to stop the disease.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of photographer Elizabeth Lee Miller. During World War II, she made a name for herself as a war correspondent for Vogue magazine.
text_a: Lili Smith is a German spy who during World War I performs as a chanteuse to smuggle military secrets to Major Larrabee. Events are precipitated when Smith falls in love with the Major.
text_b: During World War II, Elizabeth Lee Miller a former model would go on to become a renowned war correspondent for Vogue magazine.

anchor_text: Colonel Pembroke was sent by the United States government to investigate the actions of Major Cabot, commander of the Fort Cristobal garrison, who multiplied the abuses and murders of Indians. On the other hand, Cabot kept to himself the $300,000 that the government owed the Indians as a result of the signing of a treaty. Colonel Pembroke is killed by Mortimer's gang, who usually engage in committing murders by putting them on the backs of the Indians that Cabot then executes. Joe Thanks, a friend of the Indians, wants to recover the money and return it to its rightful owners, the Indians who have already been dispossessed of their ancestral lands. He uses Locomotive Bill and Lucy and tricks this whole beautiful world into saving the $300,000 he's giving back to the Indian chief.
text_a: Joe Thanks (Terence Hill), Locomotive Bill (Robert Charlebois) and Jelly, three thieves with the same goal, plan to rob the Fort Granby safe where the $300,000 Major Trevees was supposed to deliver to the Indians on the reservation is kept. Joe convinces his partner Locomotive to impersonate the murdered Colonel Pembroke and seize the $300,000, which they will then distribute. But his surprise is overwhelming when he arrives there and discovers that Jelly has gotten ahead of him. Major Treeves also enters the game by ambushing the fake Colonel Pembroke. They're all thieves, and since there's not enough for everyone, each one steals from his partner.
text_b: The Indian war is raging. Lieutenant Holliway went on a mission to Apache territory and did not return: the detachment that was to lead the Indian chief Tuscos was massacred. Captain Richard Lance is suspected of having sent him to his death on the pretext that he was a rival in love with the beautiful Cathy Eversham. To prove otherwise, he decides with twelve men to occupy Fort Invincible, a position controlling a parade through which the Indian tribes break in...

anchor_text: In December 1942, British intelligence received reports that the Germans were developing new devastating weapons (V1). Professor Lindemann, scientific adviser to Winston Churchill, was the most skeptical about the reality of the information and the Allies concluded a little too hastily to a poisoning maneuver. Nevertheless, scientists under the command of General Ziemann were experimenting with new flying bombs at the secret base at Peenemünde, on the shores of the Baltic Sea. In order to determine the exact nature of the German research and facilitate the destruction of the Peenemünde facilities, three agents are recruited from the Allied forces. Scientists by training and experts in foreign languages, they are tasked with posing as pro-Nazi engineers (now deceased, which the Germans, desperate for ballistics experts, seem to ignore). This film is based on real events (the Operation Crossbow of the title) and will have an unofficial sequel in 1969, Operation V2.
text_a: Germany, World War II; in Peenemünde the Nazis are experimenting with the new V1 and V2 missiles with alternating results, even sacrificing human pilots to learn about their flight and configuration defects. British intelligence has to select three volunteer technicians, equipped with an excellent German and expert in ballistic physics, to infiltrate the factory and Phil Bradley, John Curtis and Robert Henshaw are the chosen ones. Robert is identified and shot before he can infiltrate, while the other two manage to get hired and, thanks to the sacrifice of their lives, they will be able to pilot the bombing of the dangerous base that will be completely destroyed.
text_b: During a research expedition in Antarctica, two American researchers discover a metal object with a Nazi swastika on it. Two men with respirators and hook-and-cross bracelets capture the two. The other researchers, led by Dr. Reistad, set off to search for the two missing people. They're discovering a way under the ice. There is a Nazi camp under the leadership of Dr. Josef Mengele. They arrive at an entrance and are surrounded by Nazi soldiers. Dr. Reistad turns out to be Mengele's secret accomplice, the rest of the research team is captured. Reistad is to assist Mengele in his attempt to bring Adolf Hitler back to life. They succeed in doing so. Hitler's head is attached to a robot that allows him to move freely. With UFO-like flying objects, the Nazis intend to bombard every non-Aryan country with flesh-eating bacteria. Mengele, Reistad, Hitler and the crew take off in the UFO, break through the ice sheet and start the bombing. Two fighter jets fly by, track down the UFO, and start shooting at it. The device repels all attacks and destroys both planes. Hitler releases the first bombing, but before it is carried out, the flying object of Reistad is blown up. Two researchers survive the explosion and are attacked by the Hitler robot. The scientists destroy the robot with a dose of the flesh-eating bacteria, which they inject into the oxygen tube through which Hitler breathes.

anchor_text: During a cricket match that takes place in a mental institution, writer Robert Graves meets Charles Crossley, a strange inmate presented as highly intelligent. While the two of them are in a cabin counting the points of the game, Crossley begins to tell him his story. A great walker, he says he traveled for eighteen years in Australia where he learned magic from an Aboriginal sorcerer and acquired a terrible power, the scream of terror that causes instant death. Crossley, a mysterious traveler invades the lives of a young couple, Rachel and Anthony Fielding. Anthony is a composer who experiments with sound effects and various electronic sources in his secluded studio in Devon. The couple extend hospitality to Crossley but his intentions gradually turn out to be more sinister. He claims to have learned from an Aboriginal shaman how to produce a "cry of terror" that can kill anyone who hears it without protection.
text_a: Elizabeth Fielding, wife of writer Lewis Fielding, leads a comfortable but emotionless life and, during a solitary stay in Baden-Baden, meets Thomas, a seductive German poet. Upon returning home, she tells her husband, who is working on a script and who, not only for professional reasons, but above all because he suspects a possible relationship between the two, decides to invite him home. The young man's stay, however, produces the consequence of the materialization of the relationship between him and Elizabeth: surprised by Lewis, they are both removed from home. The two decide to settle in a house on the Côte d'Azur, but, running out of money, the relationship between them is also extinguished and Thomas's real business comes to light, that of drug courier, on the run after losing a shipment. Swan, his boss, manages to discover his hiding place and, after finding him, takes him away, presumably to kill him, but before that happens, Thomas warns Lewis, who goes to France and, after seeing him as the henchmen are loading him into a car, forgives Elizabeth and returns home with her.
text_b: At a cricket match that takes place in a psychiatric institution, writer Robert Graves meets Charles Crossley, an intern stranger presented as highly intelligent. While the two are in a booth counting the points of the match, Crossley begins to tell his story, in which he claims to have traveled for eighteen years through Australia, where he learned magic from an Aboriginal witch and received a terrible power: the cry of terror that causes instant death. Although Graves takes the story as a fantasy, in the course of the match something happens that makes him question his own mental health.[2]

anchor_text: Crossley's telling a crazy story of his own. He tells it in the parking lot of a psychiatric hospital to a non-inmate. Crossley says that one day he went to see the musician Anthony Fielding. Fielding lives with his wife in a country house by the sea. Fielding works and composes with sounds: he works with everyday sobs, the noise of the wind, the swarming wasps. Fielding is also organist at the nearby church. Crossley has a strange magic that he learned from the Aborigines that can kill anyone who's not protected with sounds, with horrifying screams. He's welcomed as a guest by the Fieldings, but he's also nestled in their marriage. It begins to crackle erotically and the drama in the developing triangle relationship creates more and more shock and horror. Small, inconspicuous objects become threatening tools when Crossley holds them in his hands. Crossley, with his shamanistic delusions, becomes the couple's nightmare. It's only when Fielding manages to enter the magical realm that he can defeat Crossley. He desperately searches for a certain stone that symbolizes Crossley's soul and smashes it when he finds it. Crossley senses, as he expresses it, that his soul is split into four parts, and his shamanic power instantly disappears.
