anchor_text: The lives of five Lebanese women intersect. Layale works in a beauty salon along with two other women, Nisrine and Rima. Each has a problem: Layale is trapped in a relationship with a married man, Nisrine is no longer a virgin but her future husband is Muslim, Rima is attracted to women. They're joined by Jamale and Rose. Jamale, a regular customer and aspiring actress, is worried about growing old; Rose, a seamstress with the shop next to the salon, is an elderly lady who has devoted her life to caring for her older sister, Lili, who is mentally ill.
text_a: A student of Sufi poetry and oriental dance in Cairo, Dunia aspires to be a professional dancer and find herself. During a competition, she meets the seductive Dr. Beshir, illustrious Sufi thinker and man of letters. With him, she will experience the pleasure of the word in her quest for ecstasy through Sufi poetry, and discover, in his arms, the pleasure of the senses. However, she must confront the tradition, which destroyed her ability to feel pleasure, in order to free her body and dance with her soul.
text_b: Caramel revolves around the lives of five Lebanese women who meet at a beauty salon. Layale (Nadine Labaki) works at a beauty salon in Beirut along with Nisrine (Yasmine Al Masri) and Rima (Joanna Moukarzel). Each of them has a problem: Layale maintains a futureless relationship with a married man; Nisrine is no longer a virgin and is about to get married in a society, the Muslim, in which sex before marriage is not accepted; Rima is attracted to women; Jamale (Gisèle Aouad) is a regular customer who wants to be an actress and who worries about aging; Rose (Sihame Haddad), a tailor who has a shop near the salon, a lady who finds her first love after devoting her life to caring for her older sister Lili (Aziza Semaan), who suffers from a mental imbalance. The film does not refer to any political problem or the recent war situation in which Lebanon has been plunged, but to ordinary people with common problems.

anchor_text: In a beauty and hairdressing salon in Beirut, five women work and meet each other, whose daily life and love affairs the film follows. Three of them work at the Si Belle salon: Layale, who has an affair with a married man, Nisrine, who is engaged and whose wedding preparations progress as the story progresses, and Rima, who is not indifferent to a beautiful customer with long black hair. Layale, the owner, is a Christian (a procession of the Virgin Mary can be seen making a stop in the living room), while Nisrine is a Muslim. Rima is a little missing boy Nisrine is going to force him for once to wear a skirt to participate in her wedding, but homosexuality is never explicitly mentioned. Layale waits for the man she loves to leave his wife to join her. One day, her competitor shows up at the salon to get her hair done. Layale inflicts a brutal and painful waxing with burning melted caramel to satisfy her vengeance. Jamale, a regular customer and frequent visitor to the salon, is so anxious about growing old that she fakes menstruation to hide her menopause. She's auditioning for commercials and raising her kids on her own. An elderly neighbor, Rose, a seamstress, who cares for her older half-mad sister, Lili, occasionally visits them. She is visited by an elegant and attractive old gentleman who asks her to mend clothes. Rose is falling for it.
text_a: Dunia is the daughter of a well-known belly dancer from Cairo. She has just finished her literary studies and is preparing for the entrance examination as a belly dancer. With literature professor Beshir, she begins a relationship marked by both mutual erotic attraction and intellectual engagement with Arabic literature. Beshir believes that love lyrics and their sensuality are an essential part of Arab culture. But this sensuality is denied by the conservative forces in the country. Through the belly dance, Dunia discovers the pleasure of her own body. And that was taken away from her because of the circumcision of her foreskin. She describes her situation as if she were setting a cold stove on fire. The preoccupation with literature and belly dancing leads Dunia to the decision to marry her lover Mamdouh. At the end of the film, Dunia learns that her cousin has been circumcised in her absence at her mother's request. Furious, she insults her mother, claiming that she slaughtered her cousin. In the trailer of the film, it is pointed out that according to estimates by Amnesty International, 97% of Egyptian women are circumcised.
text_b: Caramel revolves around the lives of five Lebanese women who meet at a beauty salon. Layale (Nadine Labaki) works at a beauty salon in Beirut along with Nisrine (Yasmine Al Masri) and Rima (Joanna Moukarzel). Each of them has a problem: Layale maintains a futureless relationship with a married man; Nisrine is no longer a virgin and is about to get married in a society, the Muslim, in which sex before marriage is not accepted; Rima is attracted to women; Jamale (Gisèle Aouad) is a regular customer who wants to be an actress and who worries about aging; Rose (Sihame Haddad), a tailor who has a shop near the salon, a lady who finds her first love after devoting her life to caring for her older sister Lili (Aziza Semaan), who suffers from a mental imbalance. The film does not refer to any political problem or the recent war situation in which Lebanon has been plunged, but to ordinary people with common problems.

anchor_text: Famous writer Barbara Broadcast (Annette Haven) is interviewed by reporter Roberta (C. J. Laing) in an elegant New York restaurant, resulting in various pornographic scenes. Later, Barbara Broadcast visits her lover in his office, while Roberta seduces a cook in the kitchen. After that, the plot shifts to a nightclub.
text_a: Female Pleasure deals with the oppression of women and in particular their sexuality. The documentary follows five women with different religious backgrounds in various regions of the world in their struggle to have an equal relationship between the sexes.
text_b: The beautiful Barbara Broadcast, best-selling author on sexuality, exiled from Puerto Rico for political reasons is interviewed by a journalist in an elegant Manhattan restaurant, where in addition to food, on the menu there are also various sexual practices available to the clientele. Soon, in a sort of surrealist "Buñellian" atmosphere, a collective orgy is unleashed. Additionally, other sexual encounters in an office and a crowded nightclub are shown.

anchor_text: Mary stole a large sum of money from her boss to pay off her lover's debts. She's resting in a motel run by Norman Bates, an enigmatic man who lives under the spell of his mother. After a brief conversation between them, Mary changed her mind and decided to return the money. Following his disappearance, his sister Lila sets out to find him, aided by Detective Milston Arbogast.
text_a: In June 1816, at the Villa Diodati on Lake Geneva, Lord Byron, Percy Bysshe Shelley, their lovers Claire Clairmont and Mary Shelley, and Dr. John Polidori, Byron's physician and personal secretary, after a séance on a stormy night, challenge each other to invent stories of ghosts and fantastic nightmares. What follows is a convulsive night in which the passion for mystery and fantasy of the two celebrated romance writers intertwines with the nightmares, the perversions, and the frightening fantasies of the participants, in a continuous play between reality and imagination.
text_b: It was a dark and stormy night; Mary Craine was exhausted, lost, and at the limit of her strength, eager to take a hot shower and find a place to spend the night. When the Bates Motel suddenly appeared amidst the storm, Mary thought it was her salvation. The rooms were old and damp, but they were clean, and the manager, Norman Bates, seemed like a pretty nice guy, if a little strange. Minutes later, Mary met with Norman's mother. And the butcher knife. The nightmare had just begun.

anchor_text: Robert Colomb, married to Catherine, is a reporter who is very fond of women and starts a relationship with Candice. The relationship between the two becomes deeper and deeper, but Robert also has many scruples related to his marital relationship. He decides to leave to follow the war in Vietnam and later realizes that he was wrong, even risking his life. Returning to his homeland, he finds that his wife has reorganized his existence. He's having fun with his friends on a holiday in the Alps. Robert thinks all is lost, so he gets in his car and goes to Catherine, who never stopped loving him.
text_a: Robert Colomb (Yves Montand) is a famous television news anchor, married to Catherine (Annie Girardot), but is continually unfaithful to her. Then he becomes fascinated by Candice. He's taking her on assignment to Kenya and Amsterdam. Then he gets assigned to Vietnam and tells Candice her adventure is over. He finds that he is more than accepted by her, as she is tired of him. Upon returning from a Vietnamese prison, he decides to return to Catherine, but discovers that she has made a new life for herself.
text_b: The Frenchman Pierre shot his wife when he returned from World War II in 1944 and found her with another man. After that, he went to Lisbon to start a new life. He now works there as a taxi driver, but he is still plagued by feelings of guilt. Here he meets the seductive widow Kathleen, who has also unsuccessfully fled to Lisbon from dark memories of her husband's death. They're going to be lovers. When Pierre learns more about Kathleen's secret, they decide to escape to South America together. But the painful truth and the law are about to tear them apart.

anchor_text: The marriage of the renowned Parisian TV reporter Robert Colomb is in trouble. Robert usually overcomes indifference and habit with attractive one-day companions, while his wife Catherine suffers from it, but perseveres. It wasn't until Robert cheats on Catherine with the American model Candice, even on one of those long-awaited trips to Amsterdam to celebrate an anniversary of their wedding, that Catherine gives up and starts a new life. But Robert and Candice will soon have nothing to say to each other. She's going back to the States, and he's going to Vietnam for a big story. Inwardly and outwardly at the end of his strength, he returns repentant. Catherine puts love above pride and forgives him. She does not share Robert's disturbingly casual outlook on life, which is reflected in the film's title: live to live.
text_a: Robert Colomb, married to Catherine, is a reporter who is very fond of women and starts a relationship with Candice. The relationship between the two becomes deeper and deeper, but Robert also has many scruples related to his marital relationship. He decides to leave to follow the war in Vietnam and later realizes that he was wrong, even risking his life. Returning to his homeland, he finds that his wife has reorganized his existence. He's having fun with his friends on a holiday in the Alps. Robert thinks all is lost, so he gets in his car and goes to Catherine, who never stopped loving him.
text_b: The newly married Rebecca (played by the singer Marianne Faithfull) wakes up early in the morning after confusing dreams next to her husband Raymond and decides to drive from their home in Alsace to Heidelberg to Daniel (played by Alain Delon), her lover. Without waking Raymond, she strips herself naked into a tight black leather motorcycle suit, gets on her motorcycle, a Harley, and drives off. On the drive through the countryside, she indulges in memories and erotic fantasies that develop as cinematic flashbacks to Rebecca's relationship with the two men and her obsession with Daniel: Before Rebecca marries teacher Raymond, she works in her father's bookstore. There she meets Daniel, an attractive Swiss lecturer working in Germany. Even though he knows she's engaged, the two begin an affair and he teaches her how to ride a motorcycle. He promises her a wedding present and soon a Harley-Davidson will be delivered to her. Her father strongly advises her to reject the gift, but the unsuspecting Raymond suggests she not return it if it will make her unhappy. So she rides her motorcycle back and forth to visit Daniel in Heidelberg. On a drive back home, she decides midway that she wants to stay with her lover and turns around. She's intoxicated, euphoric, drives fast and reckless. The ride ends abruptly when a truck veers in front of her and rams her. It's thrown into the windshield of an oncoming pickup truck.

anchor_text: While the US President's special agent Cleopatra has a poppy plantation in Asia set on fire by the air force of a friendly state, Mommy (head of the drug syndicate so damaged) uses her absence to have a raid carried out by corrupt officials in the B&S House, a drug withdrawal project, to bring her back to the United States. As requested, the resident, Jimmy Beeker, is drugged and arrested. Now the project Cleo supported is threatened with closure. With the help of brothers Mat and Mel Johnson, the corrupt cop is arrested. Doodlebug, the cheeky-looking thug who's surrounded by a yellow-livery butler, can't get over his attempt to break away from Mommy and start his own crew. Mommy has him murdered by trucks crushing Doodlebug's car on the street. Finally, the showdown takes place in a junkyard, with Mommy taking her own life. In the end, Jones' friend Reuben's project is saved and the innocent Jimmy is freed.
text_a: Cleopatra Jones is a special agent - who drives a 1973 Chevrolet Corvette Stingray "C3" - in the fight against drug trafficking. In Turkey, he destroys a poppy plantation, grown for drugs at the behest of Mommy, who is the head of a powerful drug-dealing organization. Back in the United States, Cleopatra is attacked by some of Mommy's men. Cleo's got the upper hand in karate. Meanwhile, the corrupt police officer Purdy has ordered the closure of the addiction center that Cleo helps and, hearing the news, the girl hunts Purdy and after many adventures manages to expose him and defeat Mommy. In the end, Cleo sets off on new adventures.
text_b: Korean War veteran Lucas Doolin (Robert Mitchum) works in the family's moonshine business, delivering the illegal liquor his father distills to clandestine distribution points throughout the South in his race car. However, Lucas has more problems than trying to avoid the United States federal agents ("Avengers"), led by newcomer Troy Barrett (Gene Barry). Lucas worries that his younger brother Robin (James Mitchum), who is also his mechanic, will be tempted to follow in his footsteps and become a moonshine driver. A well-funded foreign gangster, Carl Kogan (Jacques Aubuchon), seeks to gain control of local independent producers and their distribution outlets and is willing to kill anyone who gets in his way. Things get complicated when an attempt by Kogan to kill Lucas results in the deaths of a government agent and another driver (Mitchell Ryan). In a romantic subplot, Lucas becomes involved with nightclub singer Francie Wymore (Keely Smith). He does not know that one of the neighbors, Roxanna Ledbetter (Sandra Knight), is in love with him and fears for her life. When a series of government raids destroy his hidden stills, Lucas' father and the other local producers suspend production "for a season" to allow the government to negotiate with Kogan during that time but Lucas is forced, by circumstances and his own code of honor, to make one last run.

anchor_text: A young country doctor, Dr. Robert Koch, is desperate because a tuberculosis epidemic is claiming the lives of countless children in his district. Already one in four children is afflicted with this insidious disease, and parents have to watch their little ones die miserably. For years, Koch has been feverishly searching for the cause of tuberculosis. His work is met with disfavor and outright rejection by many of his colleagues. He is viewed as a charlatan, a bigot who is completely off the rails in his conjectures and investigations. There are those who are envious, such as a teacher whose honour has been injured or a representative of a health-care sect, but there are also much more serious opponents - above all, the famous medical colleague and politician, Privy Councillor Rudolf Virchow. This influential member of the Reichstag strongly denies Koch's conjecture that a bacillus is the cause of tuberculosis. Intrigue and slander hamper Koch's relentless quest, but cannot stop it. One day, the young doctor succeeds in proving his suspicions. The Ministry of Health then invites him to Berlin to continue his research undisturbed and with the necessary financial resources. But also in the capital, various forces are trying to torpedo Robert Koch's work.
text_a: Dr. Jack treats his patients with unusual but very effective methods. He is advised to visit a girl, treated by a charlatan doctor. He goes to visit her and finds that the girl, treated like a seriously ill person, forced to take medication and stay in the house in the dark all the time, is doing very well. But her father trusts the charlatan more and ignores Dr. Jack who is very sorry for the way the girl lives, having fallen in love with her and reciprocated. But he decides to stay and help her. When news arrives of a criminal roaming the premises, the girl gets excited and the charlatan orders her back to bed. So Dr. Jack pretends to be the criminal, disguises himself, and scares everyone and they try to hide and run, even the girl. Then she notices the trick and tells her father that she did it to show him that Dr. Jack cares about her. So the father chases away the charlatan and Dr. Jack and the girl kiss.
text_b: A young country doctor, Dr. Robert Koch, is desperate to see a tuberculosis epidemic take the lives of many children in his area. Already one in four children is afflicted with this insidious disease, and parents must care for these dying children. For years Koch has been frantically searching for the infectious agent of tuberculosis. His work arouses the envy and rejection of his colleagues. He is regarded as a charlatan, a fabricator, who by his conjectures and investigations leads astray. The envious are a so-called healer guru of a sect or wounded professors in their honor like the famous doctor Rudolf Virchow who is also a politician. This influential member of parliament strongly disagrees with Koch's hypothesis that the agent of tuberculosis is a bacillus. The intrigues and slanders hamper Koch's tireless zeal, but do not stop him. One day, the young doctor is able to prove his conjecture. The Ministry of Health invited him to Berlin to continue his studies quietly and offered him the necessary financial resources. But even after Koch's arrival in the capital, the evil doesn't stop.

anchor_text: Jackie (originally: Chan Foh To) is an outstanding Chinese auto mechanic and racing driver. The search for Cougar, who ran over a police officer in an illegal car race, leads to a race between Jackie and Cougar, which Jackie wins. After escaping from prison, Cougar kidnaps the Chinese man's two sisters, forcing him to come to Japan and compete in an official race against him. Jackie is working with the employees of his garage assisted by reporter Amy Ip on a race car with which he wants to participate in the race. However, an accident during the qualifying race destroys the car. With the support of the Mitsubishi company, whose boss had stuck Jackie's hand in a car door at the beginning of the film, he can still participate in the race and win.
text_a: Al McCord is having lunch at his favorite restaurant when he crosses paths with an attractive young woman, Ellie, who is seeking to travel to the Mojave Desert, where her mother, Julie, lives. He decides to take her with him. That's when the problems arise. As Ellie begins to fall in love with Al, he, in turn, falls under the spell of his mother, despite the presence of her companion who turns out to be a real nut. Along with these unlikely love stories, strange and funny events occur, for which Al must seek an explanation.
text_b: Alfred Tung, a former Mitsubishi pilot, lives and works as a mechanic in a workshop with his father and two sisters. They modify cars for legal sports racing and work with law enforcement to stop illegal racing of modified cars. One night, during a police check, an international criminal named Cougar crosses the roadblock and is chased by Alfred Tung. He manages to stop him and have him arrested, but will be released later due to lack of evidence. Cougar challenges Alfred to a race, but he refuses and his sisters are kidnapped. The police convince the mechanic to testify and the wanted man is incarcerated. Cougar, escaped from prison, destroys the workshop and kidnaps the sisters. From here, Alfred will have to face his enemies to free the sisters. He's back on the Mitsubishi team and he's in the race. After a frantic race and with strong twists and turns, he will succeed in winning the race and obtaining the release of the sisters.

anchor_text: New York-based best-selling author Joan Wilder receives mail from her recently murdered Colombian brother-in-law. Shortly thereafter, she receives a call from her sister, who has been kidnapped. The kidnappers, Ira and Ralph, demand in exchange for their release the treasure map Joan received in the mail. Wilder's going to Colombia to fulfill the kidnappers' demands. There she meets the adventurer Jack T. Colton, who is willing to help her after negotiating a fee. They are being pursued by Zolo, an officer of the country's intelligence service, who also wants the map. Along the way, they get help in a village where a smuggler turns out to be a fan of Joan Wilder. Using the map, Wilder and Colton find a large emerald called El Corazón. On the run from Zolo, they're separated. In a fortress in the Colombian city of Cartagena, where Ira breeds crocodiles, Wilder hands over the map and is able to see her sister again. Zolo and his men are joining forces with the captured Colton. It comes to the fight for the stone that Colton wore on his lower abdomen. When Zolo picks up the emerald, his hand is bitten off by a crocodile. First, Colton helps the savage threatened by Zolo, then he chases after the reptile. The film ends with a happy ending in New York: Wilder has turned the experience into a bestseller, Colton wears boots made of crocodile skin, he was able to sell the stone and fulfill his dream of a sailboat.
text_a: Gloria Davis travels to the Amazon along with her brother Rudy and her friend Pat, to refute the article of a magazine that assured the existence of cannibalism in a village called Manioca. According to Gloria, cannibalism had been an invention of the conquistadors to justify their murders. In the jungle, they encounter Mike Logan and his partner Joe. Mike tells them that they were attacked by members of that village, and that their Portuguese guide was mutilated by the natives. Upon reaching the village, the youths find it almost empty, except for some indigenous people who are frightened by their presence. Joe, who was dying, confesses to Gloria and Rudy that Mike was actually looking for emeralds, and that he would have mutilated his guide in front of the villagers. The natives catch the protagonists and torture them, as revenge for Mike's murders. The only survivor is Gloria, who returns to New York to receive her doctorate after the research. However, she does not reveal the death of her friends, inventing an accident from which she would have escaped.
text_b: The novelist Joan Wilder has to return to her sister, kidnapped in Colombia, a card sent to her by her husband shortly before he was found dead. The rendezvous point is in Cartagena de Indias, but she's on the wrong bus to the airport. The bus crashes on a small mountain road. While waiting for help on the side of the road, she is ambushed by a strange policeman. She's rescued by an adventurer named Jack Colton, who happens to be there. She hires him to drive her to a phone. Realizing that they are being chased by the strange policeman, who has joined the help of the military, they flee through the virgin forest. Along the way, Jack realizes that the map shows the location of a treasure, which explains the relentless pursuit. At the end of their adventures, they reach a small town, where Joan can finally call her sister's kidnappers. With their commitment fulfilled, Joan and Jack should be separated, but they have become attached to each other. So they decide to go together in search of the treasure: the green diamond.

anchor_text: You know, California. Wade Porter is a family man with a promising future who lives in a lovely cottage with his fiancée Laura and their five-year-old son. One night, a burglar breaks into Porter and Wade's home, and in an attempt to protect the family, hits him on the run with a baseball bat, mortally wounding him. Accused of premeditated murder, he is sentenced to 3 years in prison; during the transfer by bus from the county penitentiary to the maximum security penitentiary where he will have to serve his sentence, he is involved in a riot and for this sent to isolation. In prison, Wade is forced to endure the antics of fellow inmates and guards, particularly Lieutenant Jackson, and to deal with a violent and corrupt system in which the rules of society no longer apply. It will be his friendship with his cellmate, life inmate John Smith, that saves his life and helps him expose the system.
text_a: Wade Porter (Stephen Dorff) is a family man and businessman, a few months away from marrying his girlfriend Laura Porter (Marisol Nichols), mother of his son, Michael, after six years of relationship. One night a thief breaks into his house. Wade chases him into the front yard and hits him in the head with a baseball bat, causing his death. The D.A. is sympathetic to Wade's case. However, he is forced by the evidence to reject his plea of self-defense and conviction of involuntary manslaughter. Wade's subsequent imprisonment forces him to see a world unlike any he had known before. At first he faces the crowded confines of the county jail and then the Special Security Unit (SHU). SHU is twenty-three hours a day in a concrete box with handcuffs, listening to hundreds of commands and not getting warning shots to annoy the cops. The only freedom Wade has left is his head and his backyard. But the courtyard, as he soon finds out, has become a figurative gladiatorial arena with Special Security Unit inmates as the gladiators, forced to fight by the whims of prison officials, here prison politics and state violence. As he struggles to survive inside, Wade discovers two things: the humanity of his cellmate, a notorious serial killer, John Smith (Val Kilmer) and the increasing sadism of Special Security Unit commander Lieutenant Jackson (Harold Perrineau).
text_b: Jess Wade (Elvis Presley), an original member of a gang of fugitives led by Vince Hackett, is led to believe that a former teammate of his and Vince's wanted to meet him in a Mexican saloon. Jess later sees Billy Roy Hackett, Vince's younger brother, summoning Vince and other members of the band into the living room. Jess realizes that he is prepared and orders the bartenders to leave before the first shot is fired. After making a stand in the fire, Jess heads for the gate where he is stopped by another gang member, Gunner, and is trapped. Jess is forced to give up his gun and go with them to their lair in the mountains. Vince later tells Jess that the gang has stolen a gold cannon that belonged to Emperor Maximilian in his ill-fated battle against popular Mexican leader Benito Juarez. Vince tells him that according to the wanted poster put on him, Jess was in the gang that stole the cannon and had a mark on his neck as a result of a gunshot by a guard. Ordering his men to subdue Jess on the ground, Vince takes a hot cattle-marking iron and produces a burn wound on his neck. Taking Jess's horse, Vince and the others leave later. Jess managed to capture a wild horse in the desert and set off from there. The gang's intentions are to force a rescue from the village where they stole the cannon, but the gang also uses the cannon to force the villagers into the bay. Only Wade can save the people of the gang he belonged to.[6][7][8][9]

anchor_text: This is Brooklyn, 1986. Bernard and Joan live in a nice apartment with their two children: Walt, 16, and Frank, 12. If Bernard is a writer on the return that once had its glory hour, Joan, on the other hand, is on the rise. The tension between them is such that they can no longer save appearances. One night, a violent argument broke out between the two husbands. The next day, they announced to the boys that they were divorcing, and set up a split custody: three days at one's house, three days at the other's, and one Thursday at the parent's house. Bernard rents a dilapidated house on the other side of Prospect Park. The two boys lose their bearings, especially since Bernard does not hesitate to tarnish the image of his ex-wife in front of the children. Walt openly supports him, while Frank clings to his mother. Frank rebels by smearing the library books with semen. Walt is dating a classmate, Sophie. Bernard hosts and attempts to seduce Lili, a 20-year-old student of his. who Walt quickly falls in love with. Frank discovers that his mother is having an affair with Ivan, his tennis coach. Walt plagiarizes a Pink Floyd song and wins a talent show. When he's diagnosed, he has to go through therapy. Later, he surprises his father with Lili, and runs away to his mother's. Bernard goes there to get it, and quarrels with Joan. As he left the house, he had a seizure and was hospitalized. Walt decides to stay with his mother.
text_a: This is New York. Charlie meets a girl, Jordan, in the park, but he doesn't have the courage to talk to her. Fate has it that shortly afterwards he finds himself rescuing her from a speeding train because she's drunk. The two begin to date, but her attitude seems rather strange: she seems to almost enjoy complicating his life, and appears and disappears at her pleasure, until she asks him to meet her in the park. At the appointment, both will have to bring a letter, in which they explain how they feel for each other; once there, Jordan asks to bury the letters, without reading them, under a tree with the intention of meeting there in a year and see if fate wants them to be together again. Charlie goes crazy. A year without seeing her is too long. On the advice of his friend Leo, he starts dating other girls, just to make the year go by faster: none of them manage to win him over. But on the fateful day, only Charlie shows up for the date. After waiting four hours, he decides to open the time capsule containing the letters and read Jordan's. The girl reveals her grief over the loss of her boyfriend; her guilt over his death, for which she feels responsible; how she had drawn closer to Charlie because of his resemblance; how she had wanted to repeat every moment of his story, deluding herself that Charlie was him; how she had not been able to overcome the grief anyway. His every move, his every sentence takes on meaning. The next day, Jordan also goes to the rendezvous site, feeling ready to start over, convinced that fate wants her with Charlie, who meanwhile has decided to look forward. Jordan reaches out to her ex-boyfriend's mother, who has arranged a date for her with her nephew, who has just arrived. To Jordan's surprise, he finds out that the man is Charlie.
text_b: In 1986, in Brooklyn, New York, the dysfunctional pair of pseudo-intellectuals consisting of university professor Bernard, who in the past had been a brilliant writer (Jeff Daniels), and up-and-coming writer Joan (Laura Linney) part ways. Bernard is a selfish, stingy and jealous person who rationalizes every attitude of his family and life, and does not accept the uneducated, people who do not read books or watch interesting movies, while Joan is growing as a writer and has no problems with the uneducated. Their children, young Walt (Jesse Eisenberg) and little Frank (Owen Kline), are affected by the separation and take sides: Walt supports his father and Frank his mother, and both develop strange behaviors. Frank starts drinking and dropping semen all over the library books and locker rooms of the girls' locker room at his school. The insecure Walt uses Pink Floyd's song Hey you at a festival as if it were his own and breaks up with his girlfriend Sophie (Halley Feiffer). Meanwhile Joan has an affair with Ivan (William Baldwin), Frank's tennis teacher, and Bernard with Lili (Anna Paquin), one of his students, whom he also invites to move into his house.

anchor_text: In 1964, Henri-Georges Clouzot's ambitious film project LEnfer failed, after the main actor of many already filmed scenes, Serge Reggiani, had to be replaced and shortly afterwards Clouzot suffered a heart attack. By this time, Clouzot, known for his perfectionism and also having a reputation for being "difficult", had already significantly exceeded the shooting schedule. 45 years later, Serge Bromberg and Ruxandra Medrea presented a documentary that includes (in addition to the scenes already shot and tests for color effects and visual effects) interviews with members of the production staff and recreated key scenes.
text_a: Milan, in the present day, police officer Franco Amore is on his last night shift. For 35 years he worked in the police service. Now he is finishing his farewell speech, in which he states that in his entire career he has never fired a gun at a human being. But the following night becomes the longest and most difficult of his entire professional life. It also threatens everything Franco holds dear in his life: his job as a civil servant, his love for his wife Viviana, his friendship with his colleague Dino, and his own life.
text_b: Documentary on L'Enfer, an unfinished film by Henri-Georges Clouzot, he alternates scenes from this film and experimental sequences of artistic effects extracted from the rushes with interviews with people who participated in the shooting. The soundtracks being lost, two actors re-enact some dialogues between Odette and Marcel. We see Romy Schneider water skiing on the lake and Serge Reggiani running on the road to exhaustion. Marcel, who he plays, is morbidly jealous and has hallucinatory visions of his wife with a lover. Every train crossing the viaduct seems to trigger a crisis. The shooting time is limited but Henri-Georges Clouzot, both producer and director, keeps turning the scenes. His relationships with the actors become strained. Then Serge Reggiani, ill, suddenly left the set. It's obviously no longer possible to meet the deadlines but the director continues to shoot secondary scenes while waiting for a replacement... It all ends when Henri-Georges Clouzot suffers a heart attack.

anchor_text: Guadalcanal (Solomon Islands - South Pacific), 1942: the Charlie Company of riflemen of a department of the US Army is sent to conquer a Japanese airfield located on top of a hill on the island. The military group is led by the mild-mannered Captain Staros, under the command of the ambitious Colonel Tall. During the long and bloody assault, the events and inner torments of a group of men forced to confront their duties and above all the madness of war will be consumed, while all around nature, luxuriant and indifferent, seems to crush them and oppose their logic. Private Edward Train describes in thought all his questions about this madness: Among the various events, three strongly emerge: that of soldier Witt, who first deserts and takes refuge among the indigenous Melanesians and then returns to the company and sacrifices himself for his comrades, instilling more than a doubt in the cynical Sergeant Welsh, his direct superior; that of soldier Bell, who cannot bear the forced distance from his wife from whom he will eventually be left by a letter announcing the divorce; finally the clash between Colonel Tall and Captain Staros, who refuses to send his men on a suicide mission and who for this will be relieved of duty and replaced by Lieutenant Band.
text_a: The U.S. Army landed to relieve the Marines and secure the island of Guadalcanal, facing fierce resistance from soldiers of the Imperial Japanese Army present in military camps and bunkers. The idea of the Japanese high command is to establish an air base to bomb Australia, the last stronghold of the South Pacific. The story largely follows the path of Private Witt, played by Jim Caviezel. The story pivots on Witt's fate during the invasion of Guadalcanal while also showing the tensions faced by other protagonists of that invasion, all on the Allied side, in pursuit of their own personal goals (Colonel Gordon Nick Nolte and General Quintard John Travolta) or facing moral conflicts raised during combat actions (Captain Staros Elias Koteas and Captain Gaff John Cusack). All these stories are developed by contrasting the beauty of the surrounding landscape of the subtropical rainforest and the contradictory peace and alienation of the inhabitants of the island and the landscape against the drama of the war that is presented to them.
text_b: During the war in the Pacific, a battalion of U.S. Marines lands on an island in the Philippines occupied by the Japanese. Under the command of Captain McDonald, the soldiers fight their way forward, but suffer heavy casualties in the process. McDonald is wounded in the shoulder, but he continues to lead his men into a patch of forest. They're setting up camp there. The Americans go in search of their enemies. During a pause in the fighting, the soldiers remember their civilian lives. The Japanese soldiers under the command of Captain Tanaka are also thinking of their families and their homeland. McDonald sends nervous young Cliff, whose father is a minister, and womanizer Egan on a scouting mission. They can transmit enemy positions by radio, but they'll be ambushed on their way back. Egan is killed, Cliff and the young Japanese Nakano are badly wounded. Cliff takes pity and gives Nakano his field bottle. In return, Nakano Cliff offers his cigarettes. At this moment, the sadistic Sergeant Honeywell reaches the two wounded and kills the young Japanese. Captain McDonald looks around and laments the madness of war.

anchor_text: One night in a plantation in Sumatra, shots were fired. A man stumbles out of a house, followed by a woman who empties a gun barrel on him. Leslie Crosbie shot and killed Geoffroy Hammond, a friend of the family. After a moment of shock, she calls her husband, a policeman, and the family lawyer. She mimics, with impressive acting skills, the scene that allegedly occurred when Hammond attempted to abuse her. Everybody's ready to believe their side of the story. Leslie is in prison awaiting trial, certain she'll be acquitted. But, in a twist, the assistant attorney informs the attorney of the existence of a handwritten letter from Leslie, inviting Geoffroy Hammond to her house the night of the murder. The assistant then reveals to his supervisor that the information comes from Hammond's Eurasian widow and that she is asking for $10,000. The lawyer refuses first, out of ethics, to buy this document which would greatly threaten Leslie's release, before giving in out of friendship for the husband, who could suffer horribly from a murder charge on his wife. The lawyer manages to get Leslie out of prison in time to retrieve the letter (a condition demanded by the widow). She was acquitted, despite the lawyer's hesitant, uncomfortable plea. However, she will have to confess the truth to her husband in order to explain the expenditure of the ten thousand dollars. Still in love, he forgives her. Leslie, on the other hand, no longer loves him and prefers to take the risk of being killed by Mrs. Hammond lurking in the garden.
text_a: One night in the countryside, Mike Hammer tries to help a stranger, Christina, a frightened woman on the run. They are caught by the pursuers and Hammer narrowly escapes death. When he got out of the hospital, he went back to his shady life as a private investigator. His policeman friend Pat Murphy clumsily draws his attention to the mysteries surrounding his fake accident. Realizing that there is something important behind this seemingly mundane affair, Hammer then conducts his own investigation, with the help of his secretary and mistress Velda. Finding Christina's home, investigating among what remains of her acquaintances as some have disappeared in violent circumstances, Hammer slowly approaches the goal, avoiding several assassination attempts. His investigation leads him to a high-level gangster named Carl Evello. Hammer understands that all the protagonists of the case, the police, the various gangsters and now himself, are looking for an object (a MacGuffin) whose nature he does not know. Meanwhile, informants point Velda in the right direction, complicating the investigation. While Pat has information he doesn't want to give to Mike, Mike manages to track down Lily Carver, Christina's former roommate, who will help him considerably in solving the riddle. But in the shadows, a character pulls the strings, who manages to get his hands on the object first, not without considerable damage to him and to all the protagonists. Mike Hammer is portrayed as a fan of fast cars (a Jaguar XK 140 and then a Chevrolet Corvette first model) that he drives to an open grave. His friend and garage keeper Nick (Nick Dennis) is murdered in a way that is as atrocious as it is dishonest, crushed under a vehicle from which a killer removes the jack.
text_b: The action takes place on a rubber plantation in the British colony of Malaya. In one of the most shocking and memorable beginnings ever filmed, on a full moon night, Leslie (Bette Davis), the wife of manager Robert Crosbie, kills a man at the door of her home with multiple shots, claiming it was in self-defense as the man, known to the Crosbies, had attempted to rape her.[2] Everything seems clear. She is trusted by her husband, Robert (Herbert Marshall), as well as her lawyer, Howard (James Stephenson). But the appearance of a mysterious letter will shake Leslie's version.[3] A letter where Leslie asked the murdered man to leave his Asian lover (Gale Sondergaard) for her. If the letter were to appear during the trial in court, the jury would sentence her to hang. Unless her husband buys it from the strange lady.[4] Robert must go broke to buy the letter, he does so and she is acquitted. The couple decide to move to another country. That farewell night, at a party for her friends, Leslie confesses to Robert that she still loves Hammond, but he forgives her. In the final scene, Leslie goes out into the garden foreseeing the death that awaits her at the dagger of mrs. Hammond, his rival. Like the opening, the final scene has an unforgettable atmospheric charge.[5]

anchor_text: In 1943, Italian-American soldiers were sent on a mission to North Africa to take control of an oasis, awaiting the arrival of Allied troops. While the operation was successful, a division of the Afrika Korps landed on the spot...
text_a: In the midst of World War II, Sergeant Sullivan assembles a group of Italian-Americans disguised as Italian soldiers with the goal of infiltrating an enemy camp in North Africa, which is occupied by the fascists. But to do so, they must land in the middle of the desert and bypass the dangers of nature before facing enemy troops.
text_b: During World War II, an officer is assigned to train a special commando unit, the 1st Special Service Force, nicknamed The Devil's Brigade. The major difficulty will be to cohabitate the members of this commando composed half of exuberant and undisciplined Americans and half of stiff and grooved Canadians.

anchor_text: North Africa in October of 1942. The advance of German troops under the leadership of Field Marshal Rommel towards Egypt seems hardly to stop, only the Italian allies appear to the Anglo-American allies as the weak link in the German forward strategy. Led by the slightly psychopathic American Sergeant Sullivan and the more sensible Italian-American Valli, the Americans parachute with a special force into an oasis near the Italian camp and capture it. Sullivan and his men can surprise the Italians in their camp just outside El Alamein and wait for the Germans in Italian uniforms. They hope to learn more about the German advance plans during the upcoming visit of Rommel's Afrika Korps representatives. For two days, the Americans must endure in Italian disguise, then horror from their own people is to arrive. The nerves of the occupiers are stretched to tear, and the Italians, too, fear a bloodbath should Sullivan's scheme be exposed. But Lieutenant Colonel Heitzel of the Wehrmacht during the visit of his people (including Lieutenant Colonel Rudi, Petty Officer Hans and Sergeant Braumann) after a short time instinctively senses that something is lazy with these "allies". Then, out of the blue, a storm breaks out, resulting in a savage, brutal exchange of fire in which hardly anyone is left alive. When the guns are down and the smoke has cleared, only the German Hans and the Italian Aldo are alive. They throw away their weapons, the war has lost its last meaning for them.
text_a: In Libya, during World War II, Sergeant Sullivan (a veteran of the battlefields) groups a team of Italian-American soldiers into a commando, with the goal of infiltrating, disguised as enemy soldiers, into a military camp of the Royal Italian Army. The commandos penetrate the camp and, after killing a part of the Italian soldiers in their beds and the sentries, take possession of the base. Another part is captured and imprisoned in the base's cellars. The commandos are ordered to keep the camp and the weapons in it present until the arrival of an American battalion: all the while, the soldiers pretend to be Italians, even often hosting enemy troops in transit. Meanwhile, Captain Valli and Sergeant Sullivan become increasingly hostile towards each other. Following the escape of the prisoners with Lieutenant Tommasini at the head and after an adventurous pursuit where only Tommasini will be saved, another German convoy arrives at the base that had been there the previous day for supplies. The officer who commands it is the same as the day before and who had close relations with Captain Valli. But Lieutenant Agen is informed by radio that the soldiers there were actually American commandos and immediately afterwards a real guerrilla war between Germans and commandos begins. Meanwhile, Lieutenant Kasper's tanks arrive and try to wipe out the enemy. At the end of a long truce and battle, where even the tanks will succumb as a result of anti-tank bazooka, only two will survive: an American commandos and a German, who together will bury all the dead.
text_b: In 1941, a year after Italy pledged its aid to Germany against the Allies in World War II, a small group of Italian soldiers is sent for four months as a guard post on a small Greek island in the Aegean Sea. The group consists of an art-loving lieutenant, a macho sergeant, a farmer accompanied by his beloved donkey Silvana, and other quirky soldiers. The soldiers suspect an imminent attack on the island and take a series of seemingly absurd precautions. They observe a bomb attack on the horizon and realize that it was the destruction of the ship that was supposed to take them away from the island. In a supposedly abandoned village, people show up and say they've been hiding. Since the Germans had captured all the men, they were determined to join the Italians. From then on, life begins for the Italian soldiers: the heat, the scenery, and the people draw them into the spell of the idyllic, small island. The local priest asks the lieutenant to restore the large murals of his church. The two soldiers, who are brothers, help a lovely young shepherdess who thinks that three is the perfect number for an affair of pure sexual pleasure. The sergeant begins to learn the traditional dance and the shyest of the soldiers falls in love with the island's only overworked whore.

anchor_text: In the novel, the homosexual or transgendered Molina, sentenced to eight years in prison for homosexuality, and the Marxist revolutionary Valentín Arregui, imprisoned for left-wing extremist activities, sit in a prison cell, and Molina tells Valentín film content of old Hollywood and UFA films from the 1930s and 1940s. In the process, Molina falls in love with Valentín. The two men approach each other, and the rigid gender roles dissolve. In addition, after Valentín was given food poisoned with drugs, Molina nursed Valentín to weaken him physically so that he would reveal information to Molina, who would pass it on to the prison authorities. However, Molina warns Valentín against poisoned food and drink and shares his food with Valentín. Valentín is very grateful for this. Molina is eventually released, but then executes an order for the resistance fighters of Valentín and is shot in the process. Valentín remains in prison, his end is uncertain. In the final chapter, however, he delivers from the effects of the torture.
text_a: Between September 9, 1975 and October 8, 1975, two prisoners, Luis Molina and Valentín Arregui, shared a cell in a prison in Buenos Aires. Molina, an effeminate homosexual, is imprisoned for the kidnapping of a minor, while Valentín is a political prisoner, a member of a revolutionary group trying to overthrow the government. To help them forget their miserable situation, Molina recalls several movies he saw with Valentín. Gradually, the two men, who are opposite in every way, develop a friendly relationship that significantly changes their respective perceptions of the world. Towards the middle of the story, however, the reader learns that Molina has agreed to a deal with the authorities: in order for his sentence to be commuted, he must get as much information as possible from Valentín about his political group. However, Molina falls in love with Valentín, and when Valentín shows affection for her, he tries to deceive the authorities and help the members of the political group. But the police suspect he's playing a double game and they're looking for him when he's released. Molina will meet a tragic end, like the beautiful melodramatic fates of the movie heroines he loved and identified with.
text_b: Political prisoner Valentin Arregui and homosexual child molestation accused Luis Molina share a cell in a prison in Brazil in the 1970s (as opposed to the book's template, in which the prison is located in Argentina). Molina tells his cellmate the plot of old movies, especially a German propaganda film from the Nazi era, starring Leni Lamaison and an officer. Arregui tells about Marta, who he was once deeply in love with, and about his activities as a revolutionary. Molina made a deal with the secret police to spy on Arregui. However, he falls in love with Arregui and keeps silent. When Arregui becomes ill in prison from deliberately poisoned food, Molina takes care of him. Arregui finally thanks them with a joint, last night before Molina's release from prison. After his release, Molina contacts Arregui's revolutionary friends and dies when they are trapped by the security forces. The secret police dump his body on a piece of land covered in garbage. Meanwhile, Arregui is tortured and then taken to the prison hospital. A compassionate doctor gives him a shot of morphine for the pain, and he sinks into a dream in which Marta comes to him, takes his hand, and pulls him out of prison. The two get into a boat on the beach and row out to sea together.

anchor_text: Between September 9, 1975 and October 8, 1975, two prisoners, Luis Molina and Valentín Arregui, shared a cell in a prison in Buenos Aires. Molina, an effeminate homosexual, is imprisoned for the kidnapping of a minor, while Valentín is a political prisoner, a member of a revolutionary group trying to overthrow the government. To help them forget their miserable situation, Molina recalls several movies he saw with Valentín. Gradually, the two men, who are opposite in every way, develop a friendly relationship that significantly changes their respective perceptions of the world. Towards the middle of the story, however, the reader learns that Molina has agreed to a deal with the authorities: in order for his sentence to be commuted, he must get as much information as possible from Valentín about his political group. However, Molina falls in love with Valentín, and when Valentín shows affection for her, he tries to deceive the authorities and help the members of the political group. But the police suspect he's playing a double game and they're looking for him when he's released. Molina will meet a tragic end, like the beautiful melodramatic fates of the movie heroines he loved and identified with.
text_a: The novel tells the story of literary editor María Dolz, who, through the everyday vision of a couple in love in her favorite café, perceives a situation that is much more pleasant to her than the bad novels she faces in her work. From one day to the next, however, the couple disappears and Mary learns what happened from the newspaper. The man was Miguel Deverne, a film businessman who was stabbed to death by the tramp Luis Felipe Vázquez Canella. Maria tries to get close to Luisa, Miguel's widow, and they become friends. In Luisa's entourage is also Desvern's best friend, Javier Díaz-Varela, with whom Maria falls in love, although she quickly realizes that the links of the latter with the young widow are of the most ambiguous. Javier, once a restless womanizer with no need for commitment, is obsessively in love with Luisa and only waits for the day when the mourning for the loss of her husband diminishes. Javier's intense care for Luisa seems suspicious to Maria. As he listens to a conversation, he notices the connection that evidently exists between Javier's death and Miguel's. Javier admits to blackmailing the drifter into killing Desvern, which he justifies by citing Miguel's seemingly terminal illness. After the failed relationship with Javier, María investigates the murder on the internet and is surprised why none of the journalists mention the fateful diagnosis about Miguel. Javier wanted to eliminate a rival? Two years later, Maria participates in a business lunch, and a few moments later Luisa and Javier also appear, wearing identical rings. Maria thinks that Javier has achieved his goal.
text_b: In a prison in Buenos Aires, two inmates share a cell: one is Luis Molina, a transgender woman, while the other is Valentín Arregui, a socialist political prisoner. Despite the considerable differences, the two become closer during the course of forced cohabitation and Molina, who is serving a sentence for "corruption of a minor", tells Valentín the plot of several films to entertain him and make him forget about their imprisonment. Molina is actually a spy hired by the government to steal Valentín's secrets in exchange for a reduced sentence and the hope of being able to see her mother during a prison visit. Molina's feelings for her cellmate are actually genuine and with her kindness she manages to make the tough Valentín fall in love with her, who briefly becomes her lover. Molina's revelations bring her early freedom for good conduct and, on the day of her release, Valentín convinces her to take a message to her fellow revolutionaries. Neither suspects that the secret police are spying on Molina and the agents follow her into the secret hideout of the rioters; here the police break in during their meeting and one of Valentín's comrades, believing Molina to be a mole, kills her. The novel ends with a fantasy of Valentín who, deeply sedated after a particularly brutal torture session, imagines sailing away with his beloved Marta.

anchor_text: France in the year 1563. Calvinistic Protestantism has arrived in France. But the Catholic reaction is not long in coming, and the small but steadily growing Protestant population is suppressed in French society. However, the Huguenots, especially in the south of France and in the small kingdom of Navarre near Spain, resisted. Catherine de' Medici, the actual ruler of France, wants to counter this. Her two sons Charles IX and D'Anjou show weakness in this fight and Henri, the young prince of Navarre, vital strength. Catherine de' Medici is forced to yield and makes Henry a peace offer, which he, tired of fighting, accepts. This peace includes Henri marrying Margot, the Medici's daughter, in Paris. But even during the wedding festivities, the Catholics attack, causing a bloodbath among the Protestant wedding guests. About 30,000 Huguenots are killed on this Bartholomew night.
text_a: At the court of Francis I, the Countess Diane de Brézé came to defend the head of her husband suspected of plotting against the king. She eventually gained his favor by agreeing to take charge of the education of Prince Henry, whose rude manners the king deplored; the young man was only interested in hunting, dogs and horses. At the same time, Francis I arranged the prince's marriage to the young Catherine de' Medici. However, before she arrives at the French court, Diane and Henri are, despite their 20 years apart, already united by a passionate affair, at 38 and 18 years old. From the outset, Catherine of Medici hated her rival, but had to endure her constant presence with the prince, even when the latter, under the name of Henry II, ascended the throne after the death of her father and older brother. Henry died in an accident during a tournament on July 10, 1559. The power rests with Catherine de' Medici. Therefore, Diane's fate is also in his hands...
text_b: French, 1563. Protestants and Catholics fought mercilessly for land and power. Henry IV marched into Paris at the head of the Protestants, ready to engage the Catholics, including his mother's rival, the powerful Catherine de' Medici. Reconciliation between the two clans seemed within reach, when in 1572 the queen offered her daughter Margot in marriage. The wedding is turning into a bloodbath... Henry survived the St. Bartholomew's Day massacre only to be taken prisoner. Imprisoned in the Louvre, he spent four years there before being able to escape. At the death of Francis of Alencon, King Henry III being without offspring, he became the heir to the throne, but the Catholics refused this prospect and delivered him to Coutras, in 1587, a battle in which Henry of Navarre emerged victorious and where the Duke of Joyeuse was killed. Henry III is assassinated in turn, Henry must succeed him but the Catholics locked in Paris resist him: Rosny (the future Sully) advises him to convert again to Catholicism to be accepted as king.

anchor_text: In Egypt, archaeologist Matthew Corbeck and his assistant Jane discover the sarcophagus of Queen Kara, who supposedly possessed evil powers during her lifetime. Corbeck opens the sarcophagus and stares at the mummy's mask. At the same time, his wife gives birth to a baby girl under painful circumstances. Tired of her husband's repeated absences, she disappears with the child. Eighteen years later, the child, now a young woman, wants to meet her father, Matthew Corbeck, and travels to England in search of answers. From then on, a supernatural threat will weigh on the family.
text_a: A group of archaeologists led by Richard Turkel discover in Egypt the mummy of a prince who turns out to be the source of evil. Sir Turkel is just in time to close the sarcophagus before he dies. Decades later, the mummy is found by Samantha Turkel, a granddaughter of Sir Turkel. The mummy will be taken to the British Museum. Flights happen in the city of ritual murders. After an American politician is murdered, the U.S. investigator helps Riley find the killer. The mummy is waiting for a certain constellation of celestial bodies to allow the prince to be reborn.
text_b: In Egypt a passionate and determined archaeologist unearths the sarcophagus of a goddess who supposedly possessed evil powers during her lifetime. At the same time, his wife gives birth to a baby girl in painful conditions. Tired of her husband's repeated absences, she disappears with the child. Eighteen years later, the child turned young woman longs to know her father and travels to England in search of answers. From then on, a supernatural threat will begin to weigh on the family.

anchor_text: The story takes place in the United States in the 1950s. Ten-year-old Michael Laemle's family moves to a small town. Michael suspects his parents of cannibalism and suspects they would also feed him human flesh. He shares these suspicions with his schoolmate Sheila Zellner and the social worker who works at his school, but they don't believe him. In the end, Michael kills his father after he stabbed his mother.
text_a: Michael Laemle is a boy living the typical life of a 1950s child in a suburban American hovel, except for strange nightmares that often come to haunt him, as well as a vague sense of unease he feels towards his parents, and particularly his father, Nick. Michael even comes to suspect that his parents aren't just making classic meat on the garden barbecue. But he's not sure how to talk about it, whether it's to his new girlfriend Sheila or the guidance counselor at his school...
text_b: This is New Zealand, 1984. Boy is an 11-year-old boy who lives in a small town with his grandmother, his little brother Rocky, and their cousins. Orphaned by his mother, Boy grew up with the myth of his father Alamein, who is actually a good-for-nothing criminal and has never been there for the family. Things change when Alamein returns home unexpectedly to look for a bag full of money buried years earlier.

anchor_text: Gangster Chris is released from prison after nine years. His brother Frank, who works as a policeman, gets him a job in an auto repair shop, where Chris meets Natalie, who he falls in love with. After a short time, Chris abandons his resolve to stop being a criminal. During a robbery of a money carrier, he is pursued and shot by Frank. But since Frank has a guilty conscience for hiding as a child during a burglary committed by Chris when the police showed up, instead of warning his brother why he was arrested, he lets Chris, who also has a hostage, escape and does not betray him to his colleagues. However, as a result, he resigns from the police force. After Chris' ex-wife Monica, who runs a brothel for him, is arrested, she enters into a deal to avoid a prison sentence that threatens her because there was also an underage prostitute working in the brothel. She's handing over to the police the bullet she removed from Chris's shoulder after the robbery. Frank, who learns of the arrest planned for the following day, warns his brother. When Chris on the run from his accomplice Mike learns that the gangster Scarfo, who brought Frank to prison and whose ex-girlfriend Vanessa he lives with, wants to kill Frank, he returns and sets out to find his brother. Despite being chased by the police, he manages to get to Grand Central Station, where he shoots Scarfo before he can kill Frank. After that, Chris is arrested.
text_a: When convicted murderer Richard Montgomery escapes from prison, potential contacts are monitored by the FBI, including Montgomery's ex-girlfriend Maria, who lives in Seattle. The night shift of watching Maria from the vacant house across the street is taken over by assistant detectives Chris Lecce and Bill Reimers, who take turns with their colleagues Jack and Phil. Lecce and Reimers have been working together as a team for a long time. After Lecce, disguised as a technician, bugs Maria's phone, he is later accidentally recognized by her in the supermarket and invited to her home for dinner. Lecce falls in love with her. Since the colleagues other than Reimers are not allowed to know about this and Lecce Maria must continue to monitor, several bizarre situations arise. After a chase with the police, Montgomery's car crashes into a river, and the FBI pronounces him dead. The surveillance is broken, and Chris has to figure out how to teach Maria the truth. At this very moment, Montgomery actually shows up at Maria's and meets Chris, posing as an ex-bank robber in distress. Reimers, who had been watching through the window and wanted to come to Chris' aid, is knocked unconscious by Chris himself to keep him from talking and to prevent Montgomery from shooting at him. When Montgomery discovers Bill's police badge, Chris claims he wasn't after Montgomery, he was after him. Montgomery then takes Maria, Chris, and Bill with him on his next escape. A showdown ensues at a sawmill, where Chris and Bill rescue Maria and Montgomery is mortally wounded.
text_b: In New York City, in 1974, Chris was released from prison for good behavior, after serving several years as a result of a deadly settlement. At the prison, his younger brother Frank, a promising policeman, came to pick him up, rather reluctantly. More than their career choices, it was a long-standing rivalry that separated Chris and Frank. Their father Leon raised them alone and always had a marked preference for Chris, despite all his mistakes and crimes. Frank hopes that his brother has changed and gives him another chance: he finds him a place to live and a job. He even helped him reconnect with his kids and his ex-wife Monica. But Chris meets Natalie and, caught up in his past, plunges back into his old ways. Frank definitely feels betrayed and cuts his brother out of his life. He leaves the police force and moves in with Vanessa, the ex-partner of Antony Scarfo, a dangerous robber.

anchor_text: In New York City, in 1974, Chris was released from prison for good behavior, after serving several years as a result of a deadly settlement. At the prison, his younger brother Frank, a promising policeman, came to pick him up, rather reluctantly. More than their career choices, it was a long-standing rivalry that separated Chris and Frank. He leaves the police force and moves in with Vanessa, the ex-partner of Antony Scarfo, a dangerous robber.
text_a: In Seattle, detectives Chris Lecce and Bill Reimers are ridiculed after losing a suspect. Nothing's going well for Chris in the middle of a separation. Bill, the youngest, has a more orderly life with his wife and child. The FBI assigned them to watch Maria McGuire's apartment. The latter is the ex-partner of a dangerous, recently escaped gangster, Richard Stick Montgomery. The two officers took up residence in an empty apartment across the street from the young woman's home. They are installing telescopes, cameras, microphones, and tape recorders. The wait begins. Chris and Bill run the safe house at night, and another team runs it during the day. Pretending to be a repairman, Chris is going to plant a bug on Maria's phones. He gets to know the girl, and the flow is great between the two of them. It's not going to make Chris' job any easier.
text_b: In New York City in 1974, Chris is released from prison for good behavior, having served several years as a result of a deadly settlement. His younger brother Frank, a promising cop, reluctantly comes to pick him up. More than their choice of careers, it's a very old rivalry that has separated Chris and Frank. Their father Léon has raised them alone and has always preferred Chris, despite his mistakes and his crimes. Frank hopes in spite of everything that his brother has changed and gives him a chance: he finds him a lodging and a job. It also helps him reconnect with his daughters and his ex-wife Monica. But Chris meets Natalie and plunges back into his old habits. Frank feels definitively betrayed and pulls his brother out of his life. He quits his job and moves in with Vanessa, the former partner of Antony Scarfo, a dangerous thief.

anchor_text: The American Suzy Banyon comes to Freiburg in the Breisgau in Germany to study in a ballet school. Arriving late at night, she encounters a disturbed-looking girl who flees in panic from the main entrance of the dance academy, speaks incoherent things whose meaning is revealed only in the course of the plot, and is stabbed to death by a mysterious figure and hung from a rope the same night. In the days that follow, Suzy Banyon must find that the mysterious events accumulate: for example, the attic turns out to be infested with white maggots, through spoiled food, after which a scavenger must disinfect the building. In addition, the school administration insidiously mixes sleeping pills with the meals of the new dance student Suzy. More and more of her female comrades are being haunted by a killer at night. When it hits her friend Sandra, she begins researching witches and black magic and sets off in search of the mysterious causes of the murders. Gradually, the American dance student discovers the existence of a satanic cult. She remembers the girl she ran into the night of her arrival, and her seemingly confused words that lead her to a secret door, behind which she meets the 140-year-old Greek witch Helena Markos, who founded the dance academy in 1895 and wakes up in a bed behind a white curtain. Suzy stabs her in the throat with a knife and escapes from the dance academy as it burns to the ground.
text_a: A young woman Peggy Heller (Judy Geeson) must join her new husband, Robert (Ralph Bates), a teacher living in the countryside on the property where the school is located. The night before her departure, she is attacked in her apartment by a mysterious figure, whose face she cannot distinguish, but whose articulated arm she drops while struggling. His landlady believes it's a figment of his imagination. After joining Robert, she discovers the premises, and, as she wanders around the school, she comes across the headmaster, Michael Carmichael (Peter Cushing), with the strange personality, who seems to broadcast recordings of school life in empty rooms. In addition, Carmichael is revealed to have an articulated arm of his own. Later, she is attacked apparently by the same attacker, whose face she still does not see. Robert also thinks of a hallucination, to the point of making her doubt herself, so much so that she refuses to have him call the police. Her torment is just beginning, and she's about to discover a truth she never suspected...
text_b: Suzy Bannion (Jessica Harper), an American ballet student, arrives in the German city of Freiburg to further her studies at the prestigious Tanz Academy. Coinciding with her arrival, a recently expelled student, Pat Hingle (Eva Axén), is savagely murdered in the apartment of a friend to whom, fleeing some grave danger, she had turned for help. Naive Suzy will soon become aware of the strange behavior of the academy staff which, coupled with the terrible deaths that have begun to happen, will make her suspect that something sinister is going on. Later, his partner Sarah (Stefania Casini) will confirm his suspicions by confessing that the late Pat told him that she knew a dark secret related to the academy that she did not reveal to him. Suzy will find out that the Tanz Academy is actually run by a powerful witches' sabbath under the command of the evil Helena Markos, "The Black Queen": better known, thanks to her sequel, Inferno, as the "Mater Suspiriorum".

anchor_text: Dr. Jane Everett of the Brentwood Psychiatric Clinic has found therapeutic methods that allow her to treat more patients, but one day, a new misogynist chief of staff is appointed.
text_a: Three single men and three single women with stressful or unhappy lives sign up for an Italian language course in a Danish village, which helps them cope with their grief at loss or loneliness. When the teacher dies of a heart attack, these students choose the student who knows best to continue teaching the class.
text_b: In a decent psychiatric hospital alternate and intertwine the stories of a misogynist headmaster, a frustrated doctor who tries to seduce his boss's sister and a nurse still not resigned to the death of her fiancé in the war.

anchor_text: The story takes place in an indeterminate year of the Edo period. Ogami Ittō, a samurai serving the Tokugawa shogunate as Kogi Kaishaku-nin, the official executioner, is the target of a plot by the Yagyu clan to usurp his position and replace him with a member of his own family. When his wife is murdered and evidence emerges that he is plotting against the Shogun, the Bushido code requires him to commit Seppuku. Instead, he defies the orders of the Tokugawa Shogun and takes up the sword with his young son against his enemies to become a contract killer.
text_a: The story takes place in 18th century Japan. As a terrible storm overflows the river, many travelers are stranded in an isolated inn. Among these travelers was a masterless samurai (rōnin) named Ihei Misawa, accompanied by his wife Tayo. Ihei, who excelled in the art of swordsmanship (iaijutsu and kenjutsu), participated in prize-winning duels, a dishonourable activity for a samurai but necessary to earn money and food with which he hoped to distract for a moment the difficult life of his fellow casualties. By preventing an unnecessary duel between young samurai, Ihei is noticed by the daimyo Nagai Izuminokami Shigeaki, who offers him to become the lord of arms of his fief. But the path to this appointment is riddled with pitfalls, and his past catches up with him little by little when his expected job is denied. The bride then understands that their life will remain adventurous and free. The couple crossed the river, left the region and reached the shore of the ocean.
text_b: Ogami Ittō, a samurai who serves the shogun as "Kogi Kaishaku-nin" (official executioner) is the target of a conspiracy by the Yagyu clan to take his job and replace it with a member of his own family. When his wife is murdered and evidence seems to show that he is plotting against the Shogun, the Bushido code forces him to commit seppuku. Instead, he defies the Shogun's orders and takes up arms with his young son against his enemies, becoming an assassin for hire.

anchor_text: Former CIA agent Alexis Faraday robs a bank with Kevin Fuller and Raymond Kelly. During the robbery, Alex kills the fourth accomplice when he gets out of control in a drug binge. At the hotel, Alex and Fuller are attacked by four mercenaries led by Mr. Washington. Fuller gets killed, Alex can get away. Mr. Washington is working on behalf of a U.S. senator to recover a memory stick that the bank robbers stole from the safe along with diamonds. Alex is trying to hide at Kelly's. Mr. Washington and his men track her down and kill Kelly. The mercenaries are attacking Fuller's wife and son. Alex can save her and kill two of the attackers. When Alex tries to trade the stick for the diamonds in an abandoned warehouse, she is captured and tortured. At the Cape Town airport, with the help of Fuller's wife, Alex is able to lure the mercenaries into a trap and escape with the diamonds. On the drive, she finds information about an attack the senator is planning in Chicago.
text_a: In London, where murder has become commonplace, some contract killers and serial killers who want to curb their criminal impulses regularly meet with Killers Anonymous, a secret meeting where, in order to cure themselves, they can talk freely about their murderous desires. Their common addiction to crime forces them to support each other to heal together their true bloodlust. But when a U.S. senator is pronounced dead in a bombing, the group, led by Joanna, find themselves at the heart of a web of dark intrigue. Soon after, a new recruit, Alice, will join the group and the participants will begin to wonder if dangerous and powerful people might be hiding behind their confidential meetings. Therefore, all the "anonymous killers" suspect each other because they think the senator's killer is hiding among them. But it turns out that he faked his own death in order to recruit a new talent, the best shooter among them, for the CIA. However, Kyle announces to them that he was not supposed to be shot in his own assassination attempt. But this revelation, along with the arrival of a certain stranger who calls himself "The Man", is going to push all the anonymous killers to face each other to the death...
text_b: Alex Farraday is a professional thief. One of her former teammates pulls her out of retirement to ask her to pull one last heist. In the process, she steals not only diamonds but also a compromising USB flash drive. The young woman, unmasked during the operation, then hides with Kevin, one of his accomplices. But this one's out. She manages to escape while discovering that she has fallen into a trapdoor. Alex finds herself being hunted by the CIA as well as an international criminal organization, led by a Mr. Washington who was hired by a U.S. Senator to retrieve the flash drive. As they must neutralize her, Alex's opponents discover that she is far more formidable than they are.
