text_b: In Cape Town, a gang of high-tech robbers break into a large bank vault to steal a large number of diamonds. The hit succeeds but in the escape two components quarrel until they shoot each other. One is killed, the other, a very beautiful and attractive woman named Alexis Farraday, aka Alex, is unmasked in front of several witnesses. The gang splits but brave Alex and her ex Kevin, who keeps the loot, find themselves in the same hotel. That same night, on the orders of a U.S. senator, a group led by the ruthless Mr. Washington broke into Kevin's room, demanding not the diamonds, but a flash drive from the box they had stolen. The man does not give in to torture, and before he is killed, he cleverly manages to tell Alex where the drive is located. The woman flees, setting off a chase made up of chases by all means and repetitive twists and turns. It turns out that Alexis is an agent who left the service six years ago because in the completion of a very delicate mission the wife and children of the subject to be eliminated were killed, who instead came out alive. Becoming a thief, on this occasion she finds herself involved in a deal that immediately seems very big, which could allow her a personal ransom in addition to avenging the murdered companion. Finally, with Mr. Washington out of the way, Alexis boards a flight to London and can finally see the contents of the coveted flash-card, uncovering a blatant self-destructive plan that would target the city of Chicago and provide the United States with a ruse to start a new war. The U.S. senator who masterminded this plan gives orders to kill Alexis.

anchor_text: Gwendoline, a young and naive girl escaped from a convent, together with her friend Beth leaves for Asia in search of her entomologist father, who mysteriously disappeared during a search for a rare butterfly. The two girls, accompanied by the dashing adventurer Willard, arrive in the subterranean city of Yik-Yak, the undisputed kingdom of a cruel queen who rules over a society of female insects and female warriors modeled on bees. While Willard is conceived by the queen, who plans to kill him after mating him with the winner of a gladiatorial fight, Gwendoline and Beth go through a harsh apprenticeship of erotic trials and torture.
text_a: Gwen married, against the wishes of her father, Burnet, an Indian, Torito, whom she followed into his tribe. An enemy tribe kidnaps Gwen, but the girl manages to escape and is rescued by Johnny, a cowboy who after securing Gwen goes to Burnet to convince him to take his daughter home. Gwen, meanwhile, is found by Torito who leads her to his cabin where they are joined by Burnet and Johnny. Torito refuses to move to Burnet's ranch, and Gwen decides to follow her father on her own. The two are accompanied by Johnny who realizes that Gwen deeply loves her husband and then decides to return to Torito to persuade him to follow his wife. Meanwhile, the tribe of Indians who had kidnapped Gwen attacks Burnet's ranch, who, after a violent battle, is killed with all his men while Gwen is kidnapped again. After several adventures, the girl will be freed from Johnny and Torito.
text_b: Gwendoline (Tawny Kitaen), accompanied by her good French friend Beth (Zabou Breitman) and mercenary Willard (Brent Huff), goes on an adventure in the jungle and then into the desert to find her father, missing during a scientific expedition in search of a rare butterfly. They will eventually find the underground kingdom of Yik-Yak, a predominantly female society led by a queen who plans to kill Willard after copulating with the champion of a gladiatorial fight where he is one of the fighters.

anchor_text: The attractive Gwendoline is shipped to China in a cargo box with her maid Beth to find her missing father. There she gets into trouble with the Chinese Mafia, from which she is rescued by the adventurer Willard. Together, the three set out to reach the fabled land of Yik-Yak in the South American jungle, where Gwendoline's father is to search for a rare butterfly. After learning that the father has died in the meantime, they set out to hunt the butterfly themselves in order to name it after the father. After daring boat trips, battles with pirates, and desert crossings, they finally reach the secret underground city. It is ruled by half-naked Amazons in leather armor. The queen of the state quickly devises a plan to kill Willard. But first, he is to mate with the champion female gladiator.
text_a: Gwendoline (Tawny Kitaen), accompanied by her good French friend Beth (Zabou Breitman) and mercenary Willard (Brent Huff), goes on an adventure in the jungle and then into the desert to find her father, missing during a scientific expedition in search of a rare butterfly. They will eventually find the underground kingdom of Yik-Yak, a predominantly female society led by a queen who plans to kill Willard after copulating with the champion of a gladiatorial fight where he is one of the fighters.
text_b: Gwen married, against her father Sam Burnett's wishes, the Torito Indian and lives with his tribe. After being kidnapped by a hostile Indian tribe, she escapes and is taken to safety by Cowboy Johnny. Johnny's looking for Gwen's father, to whom he intends to turn her over. In the meantime, however, her husband, Torito, took her home. Johnny and Burnett try to convince the couple to move to Burnett's ranch; while Torito refuses, Gwen follows her father there. Johnny accompanies her and realizes that Gwen really loves her husband. He returns to Torito and tries to persuade him to follow his wife. Meanwhile, the Indians, led by their hostage, attack Burnett's ranch. All the inhabitants except Gwen are killed in the ultimately futile defense, the latter becomes a victim of another abduction. Johnny and Torito team up, and they can finally free Gwen.

anchor_text: In the 1950s, 25-year-old Alia returns to her birthplace, a palace of the Bey of Tunis where her mother, Khedija, worked as a maid and concubine. Alia had fled the palace ten years earlier, during which time she had spent burying tormented memories of her childhood. On her visit to pay tribute to the prince's death, Alia wanders through the largely abandoned palace where she finds herself faced with these memories represented as detailed flashbacks to her childhood. She begins to piece together a narrative about her mother's sexuality and sexual exploitation in a space ordered by gender and class difference, and is awakened by her ongoing questions about her father's identity. As Alia negotiates her past, she also confronts her current relationship with her lover, Lotfi, who has asked her to have what appears to be yet another miscarriage. Her development throughout the film contrasts her awakening to a past of sexual and social servitude that many of the maids lived in the palace against her own contested independence fraught with pain, conflict, and uncertainty.
text_a: The story is about a royal family from the kingdom of Kumarpur. There lives the old king with his two wives Dilbahar and Navbahar, who are constantly at war with each other. The situation escalates when a fakir prophesizes that Navbahar will give birth to the king's heir. Dilbahar then tries in vain to get ahead of him and seduce Army Chief Adil. In revenge, she destroys his family and sends him to prison. His daughter Alam Ara is banished and raised by nomads. These later storm the palace, reveal Dilbahar's machinations, free Adil, and Alam Ara marries the prince of the realm she had fallen in love with.
text_b: Set in 1950s Tunisia, the film is about a 25-year-old woman, Alia, who returns to her birthplace, a princely palace in which her mother, Khedija, worked as a servant and mistress of the house.[3] Alia had fled the palace ten years earlier, to bury the torturous memories of her childhood.[4] On her visit to pay her respects to the prince's death, Alia wanders through the largely abandoned palace where she confronts these memories represented as detailed recollections of her childhood. She begins to reconstruct a narrative about her mother's sexuality and sexual exploitation in a space ordered by gender and class difference, and reawakens her lingering question about her father's identity.[4][5] While Alia recalls her past, she also deals with her current relationship with her lover, Lotfi, who has asked her to have what appears to be another abortion.[4][1] Her development throughout the film contrasts her awakening to a past of sexual and social servitude that many of the maids experienced in the palace against her own independence fraught with pain, conflict, and uncertainty.[1]

anchor_text: Around 180 A.D., on the northern border of the Roman Empire, Emperor Marcus Aurelius ends a long war with barbarian tribes. The aging and war-weary emperor wants to finally bring lasting peace to his empire and proclaims the Pax Romana. He knows that his only son Commodus does not qualify to be a good emperor and wants to make the military tribune Livius, Commodus' close friend and lover of his daughter Lucilla, his successor instead. But before he can make it official, he is poisoned by followers of Commodus who learn of this plan without his knowledge. Lucilla, at the request of Marcus Aurelius, despite her love for Livius, has married the king of Armenia in order to secure peace with the East; but her grandiose brother, now in power, is ruthlessly leading Rome towards destruction. Because of the high taxes, there is an uprising in the eastern provinces. Livius is sent east to put down the rebellion. When he arrives there, he must realize that Lucilla is the driving force behind the rebellion. She tries to persuade him to join her, but despite his love for her, he refuses and is able to finish the campaign victorious.
text_a: This feature-length film chronicles the last moments of Roman Emperor and philosopher Marcus Aurelius and the beginning of the reign of his bloodthirsty and violent son Commodus. It is an action and reflection film about the beginnings and causes of the decline of the Roman Empire, at a time when the Empire was still at its peak, radiating from the Eastern Mediterranean to the West to Northern Europe. It's also the story of rivalries within the imperial family and the generals surrounding the emperor. The film deals with themes of power always in search of power and efficiency, and the consequences of this gradual or violent loss of power. Finally, through the story of Emperor Marcus Aurelius and his son Commodus, it is the story of the difficult government of an empire on the verge of political, social, economic and military crisis.
text_b: Commodus succeeds his father Marcus Aurelius as Roman emperor. He also likes to go undercover as a gladiator. Even as such, he has no mercy for his opponents. In order to defeat the Christian hero Ursus, he kidnaps his fiancée and threatens to destroy Ursus' home village. In order to save his people, Ursus accepts the challenge to take on the supposed gladiator in the arena. He has no idea that political opponents of the emperor in the Roman Senate have manipulated his weapon to kill Commodus. When the attack fails, the enraged emperor marches troops against his opponents. The senator's son and officer Septimius Letus, who leads the rebel army, can defeat the Imperial loyalists with Ursus' help. He ascends the throne as Emperor Septimius and peace returns to the empire.

text_b: In the year 180, during a war against the Barbarians in Germany, the elderly Roman emperor Marcus Aurelius is poisoned by his son Commodus, to whom he passes the imperial power. Meanwhile, barbarian peoples continue to press along the borders of the Roman Empire. Soon Commodus will have to answer to his sister Lucilla and her lover, Livius, for his bloody conduct. From the final clash will emerge the death of the emperor and the abandonment to himself of the Empire, since neither Lucilla nor Livius will take up the imperial toga. The plot of the film has some elements in common with the film of The Gladiator, filmed 36 years later.

anchor_text: Arlington Road is a quiet residential street in the suburbs of Washington, D.C. Deeply shaken by the sudden death of his wife in the course of his work for the FBI, Michael Faraday, a professor of history at George Washington University and an expert on international terrorism, struggles to rebuild his life. His new companion, Brooke Wolfe, is doing her best to help him raise his nine-year-old son, Grant. One day, Faraday saves the life of a young boy, Brady Lang, who was seriously injured while playing with fireworks. As a result of this event, he became friends with his new neighbours, Oliver and Cheryl Lang, lovely people, parents of three young children. However, the sleeping expert in him wakes up when he discovers that Oliver Lang has lied to him about some details of his life. Are the Langs really the good people they seem to be?
text_a: Michael Faraday, a widowed history professor, lives in Washington with his infant son Grant. One day he saves the son of the neighbors, with whom he makes friends, from a strange accident. Oliver and Cheryl Lang are polite and nice people, but over time, Michael becomes suspicious because of some strange occurrences. The Langs are not what they seem. Michael is just a pawn in their plan, which hides a terrifying motive, terrorism. So Michael begins to investigate Lang's past and discovers that he had assumed different identities to hide what he was doing against the government. Faraday then tries to figure out what their targets are and thinks he can thwart an attack on the FBI's Washington headquarters when he realizes too late that he himself has been used to carry out the first in a series of terrorist attacks.
text_b: Evan Birch (Guy Pearce) is a family man and respected philosophy professor at a prestigious college. When a student at a nearby high school disappears, the professor's past alliances cause the professor's wife (Minnie Driver) to question his alibi. Detective Malloy (Pierce Brosnan) has even more reason to be suspicious when crucial evidence makes Evan the prime suspect in the student's disappearance. Suddenly, the questions Evan faces aren't just academic, they're a matter of life and death.

anchor_text: In the 1920s in Indochina, in the forests of northern Cambodia, two young tiger brothers are born far from civilization, in the ruins of a once-glorious Khmer temple. As they quietly grow up in their natural habitat, the two little tigers find their destiny upended by the will of men, hunters and looters who came to steal some statues from the temples of Angkor. The two captured brothers will be separated and then sold, one, named Sangha, to a local prince and the other, named Kumal, to a circus. A year later, they meet again in the murderous setting of an arena during a fight between the two grown-up cats. But in the course of this bloody battle, they will recognize each other, and will manage to flee together to their native land.
text_a: In the heart of Africa, in the forest on the southern bank of the Ubangui River, two white explorers discover an unknown people living in the trees. These two heroes, a Frenchman and an American, named Max Huber and John Cort, carefully observe the people to find out if they are human and try to meet their king. The explorers are accompanied by Khamis, a native who serves as their guide, and a black boy, Llanga, whom they have picked up during their trip to the Congo. The voyage was originally intended to accompany Urdax, a Portuguese ivory trader, and the adventures of the Frenchman, the American and their companions will begin upon their return from that voyage. There are episodes in which the characters are successively faced with:
text_b: In the ruins of a forgotten temple centuries ago, in the heart of the jungle of Southeast Asia, two tiger brothers grow up playing under the watchful eyes of their parents. One was shy and sweet and the other brave and daring. But the arrival of a hunter, ready to plunder the temple, will change fate forever. The brave becomes a circus star by the name of Kumal and the timid, destined to be known as Sangah, goes from being the beloved companion of the colonial administrator's son to being a gift to a prince who will teach him to fight. In maturity they meet again in the prince's arena, where they must face each other, face to face, and then reunite to devise a united plan to escape captivity and return to their true home in the jungle.

anchor_text: Michel goes to the racetrack and steals some money from a spectator. On leaving the scene he was arrested, but due to lack of evidence he was soon released. Despite this, Michel continues to steal and partners with accomplices in order to be more effective. He refuses the help of his friend James who urges him to get a job. He deliberately attracts the attention of a police commissioner, professing theories about stealing as a legitimate activity for elite personalities. Feeling threatened, he fled France and returned two years later. When he resumed his activities, he was arrested, but it was only when he was in prison that he was able to connect with Jeanne, his mother's former housekeeper.
text_a: 'Pickpocket' is the story of Michel, a lonely young man who is fascinated by robberies, elevated to the level of art. One day he goes to a horse race and steals money from one of the attendants. Confident that no one has seen him, he is surprised when the police stop him. After this episode, Michel is captured by a network of professional pickpockets, who teach him their way of doing things and invite him to be one of them, and to steal in public areas with many targets. During a robbery, something goes wrong. Michel doesn't realize it, but when he guesses that everything that has happened to him is part of a police strategy to search his apartment, he must flee with the money he has. Michel then decides to earn an honest living, but he has to do it outside his home, to hide from those who pursue him.
text_b: The gangster Simon is preparing, with his accomplice Charlot, what he calls "the first psychological robbery in the history of banditry". Next to the Van Cleef & Arpels jewelry store, on the Croisette, in Cannes, is the boutique of a beautiful antique dealer, Françoise, who attracts the attention of the delinquent. The burglar and the antique dealer fall in love with each other. He is sympathetic but without refinement; she is cultured and independent, but discovers in contact with Simon that the vanity of her family environment weighs on her and she prefers a simple love affair with Simon. The robber's plan is long and far more sophisticated than his seductive maneuvers with Françoise. But it will be less successful.

anchor_text: John May is a city official who lives alone. His job is to find the closest relatives of people who have died in total loneliness. He carries out his work with the most meticulous care and leads a quiet, ordinary life, made up of daily rituals that become obsessive. One day, he was assigned the case of Billy Stoke, an alcoholic who died alone a few feet from his own home. He then starts collecting clues about his life and looking for people he was related to. But because of the economic crisis, he is informed that his service is about to be reduced, that he is overworked and is going to be fired. He doesn't give up and convinces his boss to give him a little more time to finish his last investigation. During his research, he meets Kelly, the daughter that Billy Stoke abandoned in his childhood, and during his travels in search of the people who knew Stoke, John has the opportunity to savor life again. It will not last long and he himself will die in total solitude, like all those he had followed during his work, who, in a dreamlike and fascinating ending, will pay him a grateful tribute.
text_a: Bill Hayward's years as a college athlete and singer are now behind him and as he struggles financially, his wife Ann, a successful lawyer, is promoted to junior partner in his law firm. While she is in Washington, D.C., on business, Bill accompanies her friends June and George to a New York nightclub where they have been hired to entertain the public. He is intimately persuaded to go on stage and sing himself, but resists the temptation to get into a romantic situation with June, a former school girlfriend. June is inebriated and stumbles to an accidental death. Bill, however, is falsely accused of her murder. Ann offers to defend him in court, but Bill can't bear the thought. However, when the case turns against him, Ann provides information that leads to Bill's acquittal and their mutual reconciliation.
text_b: John May is a reclusive London council official whose job is to track down the next of kin of people who have died in total solitude. May carries out her work with extreme meticulousness and leads a quiet and ordinary life, made up of obsessive daily rituals. When he is assigned the case of Billy Stoke, an alcoholic who died alone a few steps from his home, he begins to gather clues about his life and to look for people he was related to. But because of the economic crisis, he is informed that his office is about to be downsized and that he will be fired. John May doesn't give up and convinces his boss to give him a few days to finish his latest case. While researching, he meets Kelly, the daughter of Billy Stoke, who was abandoned as a child. On his rounds looking for people who knew Stoke, John May has a way of savoring life. Unfortunately, however, the man, after meeting Kelly again, decided to go to his father's funeral and interested in getting to know John better, dies hit by a bus. His funeral will be held on the same day as Billy Stoke's, and he will be surrounded by his daughters, his ex-girlfriend, his niece, his former comrades-in-arms, his co-workers, his comrades-in-suffering, all of whom John May tracked down. The moment all these people walk away, as if by magic, the spirit of Billy Stoke and all those whom John May followed in his work, in the end, rise from the graves and pay homage to him.

anchor_text: John May is employed by the City of London. His job is to provide for the burial of the deceased alone. He carries out this task with great care and dedication. Usually he is not only the only funeral guest, but he has also written the funeral speech given by the respective priest. At night, in his lonely apartment, he painstakingly glues pictures of the buried to his private album. Mr. May is working too slowly for his boss. That's why his job is terminated, he's fired himself. But he's still allowed to work on his last case, William Stoke, known as Billy. Using findings from Stokes' apartment, Mr. May can trace Stokes' love affair with Mary, their daughter together, and another daughter. In addition, Mr. May has found veterans of Stokes's service in the Falklands, co-workers, the homeless, and sports friends. Tragically, Mr. May is killed in a bus accident. He will be buried at the same time as his last client, William Stoke. But while at his funeral all the people Mr. May had found will attend, no one will stand at his grave until the spirits of all his funeral patrons step on his grave.

anchor_text: Gaspare Aversi, an aging man who has earned his living in shady businesses, is killed by Don Vito, who is acting on behalf of the Mafia. His son Rico ends up in prison trying to avenge him. Released after two years, he embarks on the pursuit of Cirano, his father's former companion, who now works for Don Vito. With the help of the beautiful Scilla, he manages to seize 500 million and several diamonds, the product of a transaction between the head of the mafia and a Marseillais. He must now not only escape the vengeance of the Marseillais and the killers Don Vito sent him, but he must also get rid of Cirano, who claims the booty for himself. Rico's family is murdered by gangsters before he himself wins a duel with the boss, but at the cost of his life.
text_a: Gaspare Aversi, engaged in shady dealings, is killed by Don Vito, who takes over his mafia organization. Young Rico, imprisoned for trying to avenge his father Gaspare, after two years is incited by his mother to repeat the attempt. Allying himself with a former friend of Gaspare, Cirano, then in the service of Don Vito, Rico steals five hundred million and a batch of diamonds, the result of an operation between the boss and a Marseillais. Having managed to escape from the betrayal of Cirano, who wants all the loot for himself, and from the revenge of the Marseillais, Rico, after Don Vito to lure him into a trap, has him kill his mother, sister and brother-in-law, confronts the boss in his lair and kills him, but is also killed by the boss's assassins.
text_b: Enlisted at the age of fifteen, Sergeant Vittorio was discharged after spending more than twenty years in the army. Towards the end of the civil war in Angola, he stepped on a land mine and was amputated. With one leg amputated, Vittorio returns to the city to start a new life. To do that, he has to get a prosthesis and get a job. Manu, a young teenager raised alone by his grandmother, hopes to find his father, who has been at war since his childhood...

anchor_text: In 1431 Joan of Arc was put on trial for heresy before the ecclesiastical court of Rouen. The trial, which took place without Pulzella being able to have defenders, is presided over by the Bishop of Beauvais Pierre Cauchon, and is strongly influenced by British power. A woman of strong personality and deep faith, after four months of humiliating interrogations, the pulzella of Orléans is accused of heresy, for having believed that she could communicate with God directly and without the mediation of the Catholic church. Joan claims to speak with St. Catherine and St. Margaret and to receive comfort from St. Michael. She was condemned to death and burned at the stake on May 30, 1431.
text_a: In 1431, Jeanne, a French peasant, is imprisoned for heresy and brought to trial in Rouen. Despite the rigorous interrogations of the judges and the constant persecution of the prison guards, his faith remains unwavering. The incessant theological questioning and argument in court is broken only by an ineffective attempt at torture and an examination to prove her virginity. The English, who are eager to destroy the legend that is already being built around her, scoff at Joan's insistence that her military ventures were ordered by God.[3] In a moment of weakness during the trial, Jeanne recants her faith. She was sentenced to life imprisonment, but when she recanted her earlier confession, the court decreed that she be burned at the stake as a witch.[4]
text_b: Diderot tells the story of Suzanne Simonin, but does not make any inroads and does not deviate in the sequence of events. Denis Diderot had a sister, Angélique Diderot (17201749), who had joined an Ursuline order and died there at a young age in a state of mental confusion. Perhaps this event inspired his work. The nun Suzanne Simonin tells the story of her life in letters. She is forced by her parents to become a nun against her will, as she lacks the necessary financial means for a proper marriage, since she was an illegitimate child of her mother's former lover. She hates the monastic life, although she initially enters into a relationship with an understanding superior, her love of freedom remains unbroken. Thus, under a new, fanatical and cruel abbess, she becomes the target of reprisals and harassment by her and fellow sisters. In three monasteries, she is confronted with the power of convention and money, as well as hypocrisy and religious fanaticism.

anchor_text: After Edgar Allan Poe, confused and repulsed by nightmarish experiences in an inn, meets journalist Alan Foster, who doubts the veracity of his stories, he challenges him to spend the next night in the castle of Lord Blackwood, his companion; yet no one has left alive the next morning. Foster's going along with it. As the night progresses, he gets deeper and deeper into a whirlwind of supernatural experiences, witnessing numerous murders in the past that took place in the rooms of the castle. He falls in love with Elisabeth Dollister, whom he meets and seems to be the only real being. Led by Carmus, who is a ghost himself, Foster gradually understands the whole story of vampirism, murders and accidents, but increasingly loses touch with reality. At the last moment, he can escape with Elisabeth from the building to the cemetery of the estate before she too turns out to be a delusion. With the first rays of the sun of the day, he reaches the gate, being pierced by an iron hook.
text_a: American journalist Alan Foster meets the writer Edgar Allan Poe one night in a drunken state, and begins to argue with him about his horror stories. Poe claims that the stories he writes and the nightmares are real and manifest to man, so he makes a bet with the journalist proposing to spend a night in a nearby castle. The journalist, who is a pragmatic man of science, accepts the challenge, but as soon as he enters the castle he is seduced by a woman, Elizabeth, who claims to be the sister of the owner of the castle, but who soon reveals herself to be a ghost. Throughout the evening other terrifying apparitions, including vampires and the undead, will plague the unfortunate, until he meets his fate at sunrise.
text_b: Amid an aura of horror tale, pregnant with supernatural elements, unfolds the story of Allan Gray, a young man absorbed in the study of demonology and vampire traditions. His interest in the extravagant ideas of past centuries turned him into a dreamer and a fantasizer, lost in the borderline between the real and the supernatural. One day, wandering aimlessly late at night, he arrives at an isolated inn near a river in the village of Courtempierre. Little by little he discovers that strange things are happening in the village: murders, sudden and unexplained illnesses... plus the presence of strange creatures. Allan is required to help one of the daughters of Bernard, the castle's owner. After a blood transfusion to save the young woman, a debilitated Allan begins to suffer hallucinations, seeing himself being buried alive. Recovered, he discovers that it is all due to the presence in the village of an old vampire witch, who is stabbed in the chest while sleeping in her graveyard tomb.

anchor_text: The life of Sayat-Nova, 18th-century Armenian poet, in eight chapters: At the beginning of the film, a card explains Paradjanov's intentions: Dear audience, do not look for in this film the life of Sayat-Nova, the great Armenian poet of the 18th century. We have only tried to convey by means of cinema the imagined universe of that poetry of which the Russian singer Valeri Brioussov said: "The Armenian poetry of the Middle Ages is one of the brilliant victories of the human spirit inscribed in the annals of our world".
text_a: The film tells the life of the 18th-century Armenian troubadour Sayat-Nova, from childhood to the royal court, from retirement to a convent to death. The author himself states in an introductory table that the film is an attempt to explain Sayat-Nova's life in a poetic way, with images inspired by his works as well as his life. The film is divided into chapters introduced by panels featuring passages from Sayat-Nova's poems: Childhood, Youth, The Prince's Court (where he falls in love with a Tsarina), The Monastery, The Dream, Old Age, The Angel of Death and Death.
text_b: The film is loosely based on the novel by the Strugazki brothers. It is based on the discovery of an alien planet that, like Earth, has developed a society of humans who, however, have remained stuck at the level of the late Middle Ages. The Earthling Anton was brought to this planet as an explorer and is psychologically broken by his inability to do anything about the suffering of the population, which is kept in its ignorance by a clerical upper class. He travels through the lands disguised as feudal lord Don Rumata and everywhere encounters the horrors that murder all the literati and burn books. Because of his superior abilities, he is considered by many to be invulnerable and godlike, which eventually leads him to realize that being a god is not easy. The film tells fragmentary scenes of this journey, the context of which the viewer can only guess.

anchor_text: This unusual film biography of the 18th-century Armenian musician Sayat Nova consists of 8 chapters that depict his life stages with the help of strongly surreal tableaux. There is hardly any speech, most of the words are found in the intertitles, which in their lyrical vagueness hardly produce a contextual context. Nevertheless, a certain line can be seen: it follows the poet and singer from his childhood to his death at the end of the film. There are many recurring elements, such as dilapidated monastic ruins; shredded old books; washing, slaughtering, eating; old Christian customs; pain and death; sensuality in both art and the brutality of miserable life; holy images; and so on. All these images penetrate deeply into the Christian-Oriental culture of Armenia, are carefully and rigorously composed and their content shines in the colors of blood. These include scenes of sheep being slaughtered and chickens being thrown to the ground in their final convulsions after having their heads cut off. In between, you can see the rigid, tired, hypnotized faces of the people, their bodies stretching and twisting in mysterious poses. Lamentations and barren sounds of craftsmanship and nature are constantly being heard. It is an impressive film in which the images are in charge.
text_a: The film is loosely based on the novel by the Strugazki brothers. It is based on the discovery of an alien planet that, like Earth, has developed a society of humans who, however, have remained stuck at the level of the late Middle Ages. The Earthling Anton was brought to this planet as an explorer and is psychologically broken by his inability to do anything about the suffering of the population, which is kept in its ignorance by a clerical upper class. He travels through the lands disguised as feudal lord Don Rumata and everywhere encounters the horrors that murder all the literati and burn books. Because of his superior abilities, he is considered by many to be invulnerable and godlike, which eventually leads him to realize that being a god is not easy. The film tells fragmentary scenes of this journey, the context of which the viewer can only guess.
text_b: It is a biography of the Armenian ashik Sayat-Nová that attempts to relate his life in a visual and poetic way. The film is presented with few dialogues using active frames depicting the poet's life in chapters: Childhood, Youth, The Prince's Court (where he falls in love with a Tsarina), The Monastery, The Dream, Old Age, The Angel of Death and Death.[3] There are occasional sounds, music and chants, but the dialogue is rare.[5] Each chapter is indicated by a title card and framed through Paradzhanov's imagination and Sayat-Nová's poems. Actress Sofiko Chiaureli plays six roles in the film, both male and female.[6] According to Frank Williams, the film celebrates the survival of Armenian culture in the face of Russian oppression and persecution: There are highly charged specific images: blood red juice pouring from a grenade cut into a cloth forming a stain in the shape of the boundaries of the ancient Kingdom of Armenia, dyers pulling wool flakes from tubs with the colors of the national flag, among others.[7]

anchor_text: During the Bosnian War in 1993, Tchiki and Nino, two enemy soldiers, one Bosnian and the other Serb, are stranded in a no man's land from which they cannot escape, under fire from both sides. The two men tried to find a solution to their problem, using the French Blue Helmets of UNIFIL, although the latter did not have the approval of their superiors. The media gets involved, turning a simple event into an international media spectacle.
text_a: Broken by the loss of his family in a terrorist attack, Officer Joshua Rhodes is driven by revenge. Blinded by hatred, he kills innocent people before joining the Foreign Legion to escape justice. Determined to pursue his revenge to the end, he left the Legion to become a mercenary and fight with the Serbian army during the Bosnian War (1991-1995).
text_b: Two soldiers from two different sides, Čiki and Nino, one Bosnian and the other Bosnian Serb, find themselves trapped between enemy lines, in no man's land, during the 1993 Bosnian war. While Čiki and Nino try to find a solution to their complicated problem, a UN blue helmet sergeant prepares to help them, against the orders of his superiors. The media are in charge of transforming a simple anecdote into an international media show. While the tension between the different parties is increasing, and the press is patiently waiting for news, Nino and Čiki try by all means to negotiate the price of their own life in the midst of the madness of the war.

anchor_text: The story is about two brothers, Lung and Chi-Pei Chan, who belong to the Yin-Yang clan. They want to avenge the death of their father, who was murdered by the enemy devil clan of Heaven and Earth. You must realize that behind the assassination attempt is actually General Yen from the first part, who has risen from the dead. After long battles, they can finally defeat him.
text_a: Two formidable assassins skilled in the martial arts of Heaven and Earth and virtually invincible, seek to avenge the death of their father, a tyrant murdered by three vigilante brothers, by inflicting the same punishment on them and their family members. They will achieve their ends throughout history, driven by their absolute thirst for revenge. Only survivors of the massacre, two cousins decide to punish the killers of their entire family by perfecting their kung fu practice. Later, they will face the murderers of their fathers, uncles, cousins, the Vaurian friend.
text_b: A boy accidentally dies in a fire set by two boys from a rival gang. Three of his relatives - his twin brother and two of his friends - recovered from the tragedy in different ways. The twin brother, Jacob, affected by a wine stain on his face, is unable to forgive and ruminates on revenge.

anchor_text: Marianne, a 38-year-old overweight woman, works as a gravedigger at the funeral home Ruhesanft. Her life consists of her work, eating and watching TV in bed. When she hears the driver's voice in the Munich subway, she immediately falls in love with him. She finds out everything about the much younger Huber in detective work. At home, she likes to listen to Sugar Baby by Peter Kraus. When Huber's wife is away for two weeks because of a death, Marianne puts her plan into action to win Huber's heart. At a candy machine at a subway stop, she offers him chocolate and goes to have coffee with him. She invites him to dinner the next day. They're having an affair. Based on a song by Peter Kraus, they call themselves Sugar Baby. However, when Mrs. Huber returns after two weeks and finds a note with Marianne's address in the apartment, she pursues the two and puts them at a rock 'n' roll dance party to the song Zuckerbaby. She beats Marianne down and kicks her husband out. The film ends with Marianne again dressed up, seduced by chocolate, at the candy machine of a subway station.
text_a: Munich in the mid-1980s. Marianne, a powerful thirty-eight-year-old undertaker, single, spends her days between work, swimming in the pool and a passion for good food and sweets. Fat, shaggy, masculine, doesn't seem to have any interest in men. But one day, on the subway, she is fascinated by the voice of Huber, a driver announcing stops on the loudspeaker. With a clever ploy, she approaches him, and taking advantage of a sudden departure of her rude and uncouth wife, she invites him to her home. Marianne, in preparation for the date, buys makeup, sexy lingerie, and short dresses. The two soon become overwhelmed with passion, and Marianne glimpses a stable relationship. But his wife, returning unexpectedly, surprises them in a ballerina, thus attacking Marianne. Despite the clear physical superiority, Marianne is savagely beaten and left lying on the floor, while the man is dragged away by his wife. In the final sequence of the film, Marianne, recovering from severe trauma, will try to find Huber again to try again.
text_b: On Christmas Eve, as every year, Frank drives to his in-laws with his wife Laura, son Richard, daughter Marion, and Marion's boyfriend Brad. With time running out, he decides to take a shortcut while the others sleep in the car. Soon, he passes a strange woman in white with her baby on the side of the road, apparently shocked. Marion gives up her seat to the stranger, who obviously needs care. When they find a house on their way, son Frank and his wife descend to get help. So Marion's boyfriend is left alone in the car with the woman in white. It tells her that the baby, Amy, is already dead. Brad looks at the baby, screams. Shortly afterward, Marion, who had set out on foot, saw an old black car pass by with Brad in it. Then she runs back to her family. Brad was later found dead, apparently crushed. As time passes, different members of the family suffer the same fate. Marion is alive and was picked up by a passerby who is taking her to the city for treatment. From then on, everything seems to go wrong...

anchor_text: Jordan O'Neil, a female lieutenant in the U.S. Navy, wants to prove to herself and others that she can successfully survive the brutal training program of the U.S. Navy SEALs as the first woman. A challenge, in particular, because it is supported by the ambitious US Senator DeHaven, who interprets the promotion of women in a very special way. Despite all the resistance and hard training by her instructor, Master Chief John James Urgayle, O'Neil achieves her goal. During a combat mission, she eventually saves her trainer's life.
text_a: With a divorce behind her, Giulia Benjamin decides to remarry but the second time is even more unlucky than the first: her husband dies of a heart attack a few hours after the wedding. Julia interprets the incident as a sign of fate: married life, obviously, was not meant for her. So without giving up, he decides to join the army. Under her arms are captains of all colors, but the real test comes when Julia meets Henri, a charming French doctor, with whom she naturally falls in love. The third marriage is looming, but, on the very day of the wedding, some strange behaviors of the "husband" induce Julia to let it go and return to arms.
text_b: The U.S. Senate Armed Services Committee, led by Senator Lillian DeHaven, wants to launch an experimental program to open certain positions to women in the U.S. Army Special Forces. Theodore Hayes, Secretary of the Navy, is fiercely opposed to the idea and will attempt to scuttle it. Among several candidates, Senator DeHaven chose the young Lieutenant Jordan O'Neil, a specialist in topography. She will be the first woman to undergo the Navy SEALs' rigorous and selective training at the Catalano Naval Base in Florida. But there are many who would like to see it fail. However, Jordan is not asking for any passes or exceptions. In particular, it contributes to the terrible SERE. Determined, she doesn't want to throw in the towel.

anchor_text: Jordan O'Neil, a female lieutenant in the U.S. Navy, wants to prove to herself and others that she can successfully survive the brutal training program of the U.S. Navy SEALs as the first woman. A challenge, in particular, because it is supported by the ambitious US Senator DeHaven, who interprets the promotion of women in a very special way. Despite all the resistance and hard training by her instructor, Master Chief John James Urgayle, O'Neil achieves her goal. During a combat mission, she eventually saves her trainer's life.

Julia interprets the incident as a sign of fate: married life, obviously, was not meant for her. So without giving up, he decides to join the army. The third marriage is looming, but, on the very day of the wedding, some strange behaviors of the "husband" induce Julia to let it go and return to arms.
text_b: Due to pressure from United States Congresswoman Lillian DeHaven (Anne Bancroft), Lieutenant Jordan O'Neil (Demi Moore) becomes the first female member of a United States Navy SEAL unit, called U.S. Navy combined reconnaissance team. No one expects him to succeed under a tremendously harsh training regimen, which barely half of the male recruits manage to complete. However, Jordan O'Neil is willing to prove everyone wrong.

anchor_text: The film takes place in a dilapidated hotel in Salta, where a conference for ENT specialists is being held. Helena, a divorced hotel owner, is attracted to the attractive, sensitive Dr. Jano, one of the convention delegates. Jano, however, has a secret weakness for young girls. During a theremin concert, he approaches an underage victim and apparently rubs against him accidentally. Back at the hotel, he discovers to his horror that the girl is Amalia, the daughter of the hotel owner. In a mixture of religious fervor and adolescent exuberance, Amalia's conviction grows that she must save Dr. Jano. She starts following him around, watching him. She also attends Bible studies with her friend Josefina. The girls spend their free time praying together, fantasizing about sex, and practicing kissing. Finally, Amalia claims to have been molested by Dr. Jano in one of the hotel rooms. However, the calamity that is likely to follow is not shown in the film. In the final scene, Amalia and Josefina are floating in the hotel pool.
text_a: At the Hotel Termas, a dilapidated hotel in the Argentine town of La Ciénaga, teenage friends Amalia and Josefina begin to explore their fledgling sexuality, trying to reconcile it with their fervent Catholicism. Amalia lives with her divorced mother, who owns the hotel, and her Uncle Freddy. At this time, in Amalia's mind, spiritual and sexual impulses are inextricably intertwined. There's a medical conference at the hotel. One day, while attending the performance of a musician playing the theremin, Dr. Jano, a guest of the hotel, sexually harasses Amalia. The girl is shocked, but takes the man's action as a sign that Heaven has given her a mission: to redeem Dr. Jano from his inappropriate behavior. Subsequently, the married and middle-aged doctor becomes the object of desire of Amalia, who begins to spy on him. Finally, as the conference draws to a close, Amalia and Josefina are shown together in the hotel pool, leaving the viewer in uncertainty about what happened between Amalia and the doctor.
text_b: Yoshimi Matsubara is a young divorced woman with a history of mental health problems, who is looking for a home for herself and her six-year-old daughter, Ikuko. She decides to take a house that will drag her into the mysterious and frightening reality of a little girl who died two years earlier within the walls of the same apartment building: from the apartment above their, in fact, once the residence of the girl's family, strange noises come. Yoshimi frequently finds a small red backpack which, each time, she tries to get rid of. The events frighten her to such an extent that she tries to leave the apartment but is dissuaded by her lawyer. One night, after finding the red backpack in his daughter's purse for the umpteenth time, Yoshimi decides to go to the roof of the palace. Suddenly he realizes: the girl who had disappeared two years earlier had drowned in the water tank on top of the building to retrieve her small backpack that had fallen into it. After this incident, the film ends with the sacrifice of the young mother in order to protect Ikuko from the child. Once in high school, Ikuko returns to the house, apparently abandoned, and meets her mother. The mother does not allow her to return to live with her as Ikuko had asked her while behind her suddenly appears the child. When Ikuko senses someone's presence, she turns around and sees no one - both mother and child are missing.

anchor_text: The film focuses on the young medical student Herbert Sperling, who is studying medicine at St. Swithins Teaching Hospital with the three other students Grimsdyke, Benskin (both have already failed the exams several times) and Taffy. The head of the hospital is Sir Lancelot Sprat, a stern, brash, and temperamental (but also fair) man. Despite their efforts, Sparrow and his companions (one more, the other less) often collide with Sprat. In addition, there are love affairs that also distract Sparrow from studying. Finally, however, there is the test...
text_a: Simon Sparrow is a medical student who has just arrived at St Swithin's Hospital in London. Placed in the same rank as three ambitious men more experienced than him, he soon finds himself immersed in medical training as he conceived himself in the 1950s, a training consisting of flirting, drinking alcohol and driving fast sports cars! But Sir Lancelot Spratt, the chief physician, is watching, and is always there to call them to order...
text_b: The main character of the film is the ambitious country doctor Sansfin. He discovers the orphan Lamiel, who has blossomed into beauty, with a farmer and places her as a lecturer in the nearby castle of his ducal patient de Miossens. With their son Fédor, Lamiel soon flees to Paris and marries there, through the mediation of Doctor Sansfin, even a count title, after the country doctor has provided her with nobility and dowry through an adoption. He himself rises to become a sought-after fashion doctor, without, of course, from an interested admirer of Lamiel also becoming one of her numerous lovers. But when his now very calculating protégé, who has never been able to feel a sense of happiness in the midst of many love adventures, really falls in love with the very male burglar and murderer Roger Valbert, jealousy still awakens in him. He challenges the hunted to a dangerous visit to the Paris Opera and the shot fired at the discovered Valbert kills Lamiel.

text_b: Freshman Robert Sparrow, enrolled in medical school, soon makes the acquaintance of three other students: Tony, taken by women and leisure, Tartufo, a player in the school rugby team, and Richard, a cheerful student voluntarily off course thanks to studies paid for by his grandmother. Between crushes with nurses, trespassing, and fights with students from rival schools, expulsion from the faculty is imminent.

anchor_text: A woman accused of a crime of passion risks being sent to the electric chair by a popular jury, led by the uncompromising Edward Weldon. He is faced with a dilemma when his own daughter is also accused of a crime.
text_a: Edward Weldon, president of a jury, has to decide whether or not young Ethel Saxon is sentenced to the electric chair for murder. Finally he shows himself ruthless and condemns the wrongdoer to capital punishment. However, he will have to question his decision when Stella, his own daughter, confesses to killing a man.
text_b: A middle-class family man fights the Admiralty all the way to the High Court of Justice to get his son acquitted of stealing a money order from his military school.

anchor_text: Edward Weldon heads a jury that sentences a woman to death for murder. His uncompromising stance towards the accused makes him very unpopular and even his family doesn't seem to share his choices. But Weldon defends his opinion to the extreme as a rigid defender of social rules. But when his daughter Stella shoots a lover who has betrayed her, Weldon begins to question his own rigour.
text_a: Sensitive to the cause of oppressed women, brilliant lawyer Portia Merriman decides to defend in court the unmotivated Elizabeth Manners, on trial for the murder of her partner Earle Condon. During the debate, unexpected truths emerge that directly involve the lawyer herself due to an episode from her past.
text_b: Edward Weldon, president of a jury, has to decide whether or not young Ethel Saxon is sentenced to the electric chair for murder. Finally he shows himself ruthless and condemns the wrongdoer to capital punishment. However, he will have to question his decision when Stella, his own daughter, confesses to killing a man.

anchor_text: The story takes place in the 1960s, the life of a family mother Rachel Flax, her little troubles and her two daughters Charlotte and Kate. Rachel takes them everywhere with her, from town to town, to the rhythm of her tumultuous break-ups. All three moved to East Port, New England. As soon as Rachel arrives, she becomes a sensation and befriends Lou Landsky, the friendly owner of a shoe store. The same goes for his eldest daughter Charlotte, who's rather withdrawn, who's about to meet Joe, an employee of a convent...
text_a: Rachel is a middle-school teacher in her 40's, divorced, and childless. Although she is happy and satisfied with her life, she begins to reassess her position after falling in love with Ali and meeting her daughter Leila. Rachel spends a lot of time with the child and treats her as her own, even though she knows that her relationship with the child could break down at any time or be compromised by Alice, Leila's mother. A desire for a child of her own arises, accompanied by intense anger.
text_b: It's 1963. Single mother Rachel Flax arrives in a small town in Massachusetts with her two daughters, born to two different fathers and fed up with her mother's constant failed relationships, who decides to change towns each time. The eldest daughter, Charlotte, is in her mid-teens, aspires to be a nun and soon falls in love with the bell tower keeper, while Kate is a sweet little girl who dreams of becoming a professional swimmer. The two daughters try their best to get their mother to marry Lou, a shoe salesman who seems to be perfect for her, so that she won't have to move to another city again.

anchor_text: The train conductor Klaus Schneider is admitted to a hospital during the First World War. There, the doctor, Dr. Bettelheim, discovers that the tailor, who was being treated for head injuries, has a clairvoyant gift. After the war, he meets again Captain Nowotny, who during the war provided entertainment for the soldiers with a front theater, and who now becomes his advisor and manager. After changing his name to Erik Jan Hanussen at Nowotny's suggestion and launching a successful career as a variety star, he suddenly becomes famous for predicting the sinking of a cruise ship. After an arrest and subsequent trial for embezzlement, he is acquitted as he demonstrates his special skills in court. In Berlin, where he moved with his girlfriend Valery (called Wally) and Nowotny, he moves in the "upper ten thousand". When the prediction that Adolf Hitler would be appointed Reich Chancellor actually comes true, Nowotny and Wally make serious accusations against him, as he has always insisted on being an apolitical person. When he foresaw the Reichstag fire, he was declared a traitor by the National Socialists, although many thought him to be a National Socialist, and was murdered by the SA on the night of 24 to 25 March 1933, which probably contributed to his Jewish origin.
text_a: During World War II, in August 1941, a shipment of gold bars destined for the Führer's coffers mysteriously disappeared. Former German Colonel Furben, who learned of the treasure by chance from former non-commissioned officer Hans Schmidt, assembles a team of "specialists" who move with him to Berlin to discuss preparations for the recovery operation. These include: former Major Harry Morgan, an enterprising English luxury lover, formerly a prisoner of Colonel Furben in Germany; the expert burglar Sly Welis of Amsterdam; Schmidt himself, a good actor, currently unemployed; Nurse Erika; Chief Sergeant William Prior, currently on duty at Siegfried Prison where Nazi criminal Reinhard Holtz is imprisoned, the only one still alive who can remember where the gold cargo ended up, having authorized its transfer at the time. Thanks to the information and connivance of Sergeant Prior, as well as the accuracy of the organization, the group manages to replace the prisoner in his cell with his doctor for 24 hours and make him talk, drugging him and comparing him to a revived Hitler played by Schmidt. Then, with the connivance of a Russian officer, he manages to retrieve the gold in a secret bunker located in East Germany, near the villa that was Holtz's, simulating the operation of disarming a war device to displace the inhabitants of the building under which the bunker is located and penetrate it with the explosive. The operation seems to end happily, when the Soviet officer betrays them trying to steal all the treasure.
text_b: At the end of World War I, Corporal-in-Chief Klaus Schneider was wounded in the head. At the military hospital, Dr. Bettelheim notices his patient's special abilities as a hypnotist and would like to use them to care for the sick. But Captain Nowotny offers him the glittering world of variety shows. They perform first in Vienna where the former chief corporal appears under the name Hanussen, then in Berlin. Here he is successful not only with his hypnotic shows but also with divination (it is not known exactly how much of it is luck and how much is fraud). While it's clear that the Nazi party is growing stronger, he predicted Hitler's rise to power. The new power notices him, courts him, invites him to parties. For a while, he seems to be able to control his destiny and find ways to escape, but at the same time he is in constant uncertainty because he senses danger. The head of Nazi propaganda used Hanussen and made him predict the Reichstag fire. After that, there is no way out: Hanussen becomes dangerous, he must disappear. He is taken, and in the autumn forest, simply shot.

anchor_text: Dr. Bock is the chief of medicine at a teaching hospital in Manhattan. Bock's personal life is in disarray; his wife has left him, and his children no longer speak to him. He's also struggling professionally. Two colleagues, a doctor and a nurse, died unexpectedly. There are also protests from residents who want their homes demolished so that an annex can be built for the hospital to treat drug addicts. The residents want to get the hospital administration to provide them with other homes. Dr. Bock suffers from erectile dysfunction, which makes him contemplate suicide. But his acquaintance with Barbara Drummond, a patient's daughter, gives him a boost. It becomes clear that both deaths were caused by Edmund Drummond, Barbara's father. Edmund wanted to draw attention to the inhumanity of modern medicine. His victims would have survived if they had received prompt and appropriate treatment. To cover for Drummond, Barbara and Dr. Bock stage another fatal accident at the hospital, which Dr. Welbeck suffers. Bock helps Barbara and her father escape, but stays behind.
text_a: At a university hospital in Manhattan, the life of Dr. Bock, the chief of medicine, is a mess: he's left his wife, his kids won't talk to him, and his once-beloved university hospital is falling apart. The hospital is dealing with the sudden deaths of two doctors and a nurse. These are attributed to coincidental or unavoidable failures to provide accurate treatment. At the same time, administrators must deal with a protest against the hospital's annexation of an adjacent, decrepit apartment building. The annex will be used for a drug rehabilitation center; the current occupants of the building are demanding that the hospital find them replacement housing before the building is demolished even though the building was condemned at some point before. The Dr. Bock admits to impotence and suicidal thoughts, but falls in love with Barbara Drummond, the daughter of a patient who came with her father from Mexico for his treatment. This temporarily gives dr. Bock something to live for, after Barbara defies him and gets involved with him. It is discovered that the deaths were caused by Barbara's father as retribution for the "inhumanity" of modern medical treatment. Drummond takes no personal responsibility, claiming that his victims would have been saved if they had received prompt and adequate treatment, but they did not. Dr. Bock and Barbara use the eventual accidental death of a doctor at the hospital to cover up Drummond's misdeeds. Barbara makes plans to fly with her father back to Mexico. Dr. Bock initially intends to go with them, but at the last minute, driven by his sense of duty, insists on staying in the hospital so that it doesn't become total chaos.
text_b: U.S. hospital doctor Kevin Riley is on vacation in the Bahamas with his wife and five-year-old son. The idyllic summer vacation takes a dark turn, however, when the motorboat chartered by handshake after visiting an uninhabited island, of which there are hundreds, fails to start and threatens to dry up the family. At the last moment, two destitute local fishermen pass by, mistaking the stranded American tourists for rich men and demanding a million dollars in exchange for their rescue. The American manages to negotiate the two down to a realistic amount of US$193,000, but holds the blackmailer's wife and child hostage on the island. After receiving the money, they drown the potentially dangerous witness in a prepared boat. Unbeknownst to them, their victim survives the assassination attempt. In order to save his family, whose lives are in danger, Kevin Riley contacts the police as well as the American consulate. When he is denied the help he needs, the panicked surgeon takes matters into his own hands. A scar on the upper arm of one of the blackmailers leads him to a doctor at the local hospital. In yet another dramatic twist, the hunter suddenly becomes the hunted.

text_a: The film depicts life in a Manhattan ICU and focuses on Dr. Bock (George C. Scott), the medical director, whose life has become a mess: his wife has left him, his children do not speak to him and the ICU that was so dear to him is falling apart. With all this, the ICU sees a number of strange deaths, both among doctors and hospital staff, and all of this eventually drives Dr. Bock to the brink of madness. While on the verge of a nervous breakdown, he falls under the spell of a patient's attractive daughter (Diana Rigg), and not only does she give him a reason to live, but she may be the only person who can help him get his life back on track and give it meaning.
text_b: U.S. hospital doctor Kevin Riley is on vacation in the Bahamas with his wife and five-year-old son. The idyllic summer vacation takes a dark turn, however, when the motorboat chartered by handshake after visiting an uninhabited island, of which there are hundreds, fails to start and threatens to dry up the family. When he is denied the help he needs, the panicked surgeon takes matters into his own hands. A scar on the upper arm of one of the blackmailers leads him to a doctor at the local hospital. In yet another dramatic twist, the hunter suddenly becomes the hunted.

anchor_text: Laura Mars (Faye Dunaway) is an American photographer acclaimed and disliked because her eloquent photos denounce the violent, greedy and sexist world around us. Laura's happy, though, until one day she starts having strange visions. She watches a psychopath murder her family and friends one by one. The problem is that these visions are simultaneous, Laura Mars sees the crimes as they're being committed... A handsome detective (Tommy Lee Jones) tries to help him...
text_a: Laura escaped from a satanic cult as a young girl. Now she's a mother herself. But when some members of her former cult break into her home and try to take her 8-year-old son, David, and the police arrive, no trace of the men is found. Some time later, however, the boy suddenly becomes ill. The attempted kidnapping somehow changed him completely.
text_b: Laura Mars, a fashion photographer who makes extensive use of violent and provocative imagery for her work, is appalled by the extrasensory sightings of the murders of models and collaborators. The affair attracts the particular attention of Lieutenant John Neville, who forms an emotional bond with the woman.

anchor_text: During World War II, an evangelical nurse cares for war wounded in the Belgian Congo. This is where their Christian worldview clashes with that of the natives. The colonel of the nearby garrison, Colonel Henry Derode, falls in love with her and asks her to be his wife. But Rachel refuses. One day, a plane crashes nearby, but the pilot, Paul Wilton, can be saved. Paul and Rachel fall in love. Rachel decides to continue her missionary work and provide for the natives until Paul is recalled. She becomes pregnant, but soon Paul is called back into the army. Torn back and forth and feeling remorse for what he had done to her, Rachel becomes ill. Eventually, though, she manages to deliver the baby. Paul returns to America, where he is promoted to colonel. When Rachel and Paul are reunited, she renounces the continuation of her mission.
text_a: Rachel is 40 years old. Although she is single and has no children, she is satisfied with her life. She works as a teacher in a middle school, has a large circle of friends and also maintains friendly contact with her ex-husbands. She also takes guitar lessons. When she falls in love with Ali one day, his four-year-old daughter Leila enters her life. Rachel takes care of the infant, nurses it and soon loves it as her own. But she must realize that her close bond with Leila also carries potential risks, especially with regard to her biological mother Alice. Rachel doesn't want to settle for being just an extra in Leila's life. Similarly, time is working against her if, in her old age, she still wishes to have a child of her own.
text_b: During the last world war, Rachel Cade cared for indigenous people in a missionary hospital in Congo as a nurse. Here he clashes with the shaman's beliefs in contrast to the missionary's (Protestant) Christian religion and Western medicine. The colonel of the garrison in the meantime falls in love with her and proposes marriage, but Rachel refuses. Then a plane crashes and the pilot escapes. It turns out that he is a doctor and with Rachel decides to treat the natives until his recall. It's love at first sight between the two, but shortly after that comes Paul's call to arms, while Rachel has become pregnant. Worn out by remorse for what she did, contrary to Christian teachings, Rachel falls ill, but eventually manages to have the baby. Paul is warned by the Colonel and returns to bring both Rachel and the baby to America, but she gives up for the sake of the mission and a new feeling for the Colonel.

anchor_text: During World War II, an evangelical nurse cares for war wounded in the Belgian Congo. This is where their Christian worldview clashes with that of the natives. The colonel of the nearby garrison, Colonel Henry Derode, falls in love with her and asks her to be his wife. But Rachel refuses.
