text_a: Rachel Cade, a young nurse, arrives at a small mission in the Belgian Congo during World War II. She was greeted by the local administrator, Colonel Henry Derode, a widowed and disillusioned man. A devout Protestant, Rachel Cade clashes with tribal traditions and the power of Kalanumu, the high priest of the mountain god. Despite Jacob's opposition, Rachel operates on a child with appendicitis. Henry Derode strives to protect Rachel and is sensitive to her charms. An American plane crashes near the village and the only survivor, Paul Wilton, a doctor, falls under Rachel's spell.
text_b: Rachel is 40 years old. Although she is single and has no children, she is satisfied with her life. She works as a teacher in a middle school, has a large circle of friends and also maintains friendly contact with her ex-husbands. She also takes guitar lessons. When she falls in love with Ali one day, his four-year-old daughter Leila enters her life. Rachel takes care of the infant, nurses it and soon loves it as her own. But she must realize that her close bond with Leila also carries potential risks, especially with regard to her biological mother Alice. Rachel doesn't want to settle for being just an extra in Leila's life. Similarly, time is working against her if, in her old age, she still wishes to have a child of her own.

anchor_text: Diijon makes his living as a vaudeville performer with cheap tricks and gags. His much younger wife Victoria, fed up with Diijon's ill-treatment, begins an affair with a musician. Offended and driven by a creeping madness, Diijon begins to study hypnosis. As a result, he almost succeeds in influencing his wife to shoot her lover in a trance.
text_a: A young woman just committed suicide. Near the body, under the gaze of the old maid, the husband wondered and recalled their past. A poor girl, she often came to his pawn shop. He loved her, insisted on marrying her. Soon, wounded by his coldness and jealousy, she shut herself up in silence...
text_b: The magician Dijon (Erich von Stroheim) is interested in hypnosis and uses his wife Victoria (Jeanne Bates) as a guinea pig. The latter wished to leave him to join the troupe of producer Tony Holiday (William Wright).

anchor_text: Little orphan Jane Eyre lives a hard life, neglected by her wicked Aunt Reed and subject to the ill-treatment of Mr Brocklehurst's gloomy boarding house... becoming governess to little Adele, at Thornfield Manor, she soon meets the landlord, Edward Rochester. But the latter hides some secrets...
text_a: The orphan Jane Eyre is housed in a castle to educate the daughter of the rich Edward Rochester, who proposes to her to marry him: discovering that he already has a crazy wife, she flees. After his wife dies in the fire she hanged herself and blinded Rochester, Jane who could never forget him will return to him.
text_b: Antoniette, a young landowner in Jamaica in the 1840s, marries an English lord to avoid losing her lands. Everything seems to be going well between the two of them, but the woman is hiding a secret about her childhood and her mother. To complicate matters, Amelie, a young, mixed-race servant born to a Jamaican mother, will do anything to drive a wedge between them.

anchor_text: Marty Pascal is a student in New York City in 1983. He's engaged to a waitress named Lesly. Marty's taking his fiancée to visit his family in Washington D.C. for Thanksgiving. There, Lesly meets his twin sister Jackie, who is fascinated by former U.S. First Lady Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis. Jackie mocks Lesly by asking her about her home state of Pennsylvania. She asks questions like, "Do you have any paintings in Pennsylvania?" Then Jackie gets hysterical when the ice cubes for the drinks run out. The electric light goes out, candles are lit. Jackie and Marty's mother is going to bed. She says she can foresee where the conversation will lead. It turns out that Marty and Jackie had an incestuous relationship in the past. Jackie thinks incest is normal and wants to renew the relationship. There's going to be an act of love between Jackie and Marty in the presence of Lesly.
text_a: It's 1983 and, for Thanksgiving, Marty and his fiancée Leslie travel to Washington to visit family. Marty is reunited with his mother and brother Anthony, but most of all his twin sister Jacqueline. The quirky sister calls herself "Jackie-O" and loves to dress up in the same pink dress Jacqueline Kennedy wore the day her husband was murdered. Mother and daughter take a dim view of Leslie, and, coupled with a thunderstorm, the air in the house becomes increasingly tense until family secrets come to the surface, such as the morbid and borderline incest relationship between Marty and Jackie. While Anthony tries to seduce Leslie, Jackie and her mother will do anything to keep Marty from leaving again.
text_b: Martha Marie/Marty Talridge lives simultaneously in New York City and the South of France. When she goes to sleep in one life, she wakes up in the next with full awareness of events in the other world. In New York City, she's a successful publisher. In the south of France, she's a widow raising her children alone. In both worlds, Marie/Marty seeks counseling from a psychiatrist. She tells the psychiatrists about the sessions with each other's doctors. In New York, she meets literary agent Aaron Reilly, with whom she falls in love. In the south of France, she meets the writer William Granther, with whom she begins a relationship. When she tells the men about their different lives and their different relationships, they get jealous. At the end of the film, Marie/Talridge in both worlds says goodbye to their respective life partners and the other people close to her.

anchor_text: Diderot tells the story of Suzanne Simonin, but does not make any inroads and does not deviate in the sequence of events. Denis Diderot had a sister, Angélique Diderot (17201749), who had joined an Ursuline order and died there at a young age in a state of mental confusion. Perhaps this event inspired his work. The nun Suzanne Simonin tells the story of her life in letters. She is forced by her parents to become a nun against her will, as she lacks the necessary financial means for a proper marriage, since she was an illegitimate child of her mother's former lover. She hates the monastic life, although she initially enters into a relationship with an understanding superior, her love of freedom remains unbroken. Thus, under a new, fanatical and cruel abbess, she becomes the target of reprisals and harassment by her and fellow sisters. In three monasteries, she is confronted with the power of convention and money, as well as hypocrisy and religious fanaticism.
text_a: In the 18th century, shortly before the French Revolution, orphan Henriette Gérard accompanied her blind foster sister Louise to Paris. The two girls hope to find a doctor who will cure Louise of her blindness. Unfortunately, Henriette is kidnapped by the Marquis de Presles, a coward who has decided to make her his plaything. Louise is no more fortunate than her sister: left to herself, she falls into the hands of Mother Frochard, an alcoholic hag who will not stop humiliating and tormenting her to force her to beg. Everything seems to be arranged with the intervention of the Chevalier de Vaudrey and the Comtesse de Linières, when the Revolution breaks out...
text_b: In 18th-century France, a girl named Suzanne Simonin is forced by her parents to take vows after completing her novitiate; for alleged financial reasons, they preferred to have the teenager locked up in a convent. The reality is that the mother hopes to make amends for a youthful sin, since the girl is an illegitimate child. In the community of the Clarisse nuns of Longchamp she meets an elderly mystic who enters into a close friendship with Suzanne shortly before dying; but the period of happiness and satisfaction ends soon for the protagonist of the story, with the arrival of the new mother superior, Sister Christine. Aware that Suzanne wants to break her vows, Sister Christine sues her in front of the entire community, at the same time beginning a real work of both moral and physical harassment against her. The young woman's defender, however, touched by her anguish, gets her transferred to the convent of Saint-Eutrope. Here Suzanne suffers a failed seduction attempt; unable to endure the cloistered regime any longer, she manages to escape from the convent. The ending, only sketched, makes us understand that Suzanne remains in hiding waiting for help from the Marquis de Croismare, the recipient of her memorial - a real character, who frequented the same Diderot - living in constant fear of being caught.

anchor_text: Nightclub singer Ivy Stevens has a relaxed lifestyle and doesn't take morality too seriously. Currently, she is the mistress of action-traveler Howard Palmer, who sexually exploits her without offering her any lasting perspective. Eventually, Palmer marries his boss's daughter. Desperate Ivy tries to commit suicide, but is saved at the last moment by Carl Loomis, a member of the Salvation Army. His kind, courteous manner impresses Ivy, who wants to start a new life. About a year later, Ivy, who is now working for the Salvation Army, and Howard Paler, who is in a boring marriage, meet again. Instantly, Ivy becomes his mistress again. After much remorse, the young woman realizes that strength of character and a moral way of life are much more important than the fleeting fulfillment of erotic desires. Carl and Ivy getting married.
text_a: Nightclub singer Ivy Stevens leads a relaxed life with little regard for morals. At present, she is the mistress of a good-for-nothing, traveling salesman Howard Palmer, who takes advantage of her sexually without offering her any lasting prospects. Eventually, he marries his boss's daughter. In desperation, Ivy attempts suicide but is saved at the last second by Carl Loomis, a member of the Salvation Army. She was so impressed by his kindness and consideration that she wanted to start a new life. A good year later Ivy, who has since been working for the Salvation Army, meets Howard Paler who is bored in his marriage. It doesn't take long for her to become his mistress again. After many pangs of conscience, the young woman finally realizes that strength of character and a moral life are far more important than the fleeting fulfillment of erotic desires. She's marrying Carl.
text_b: Ivy lives as a black domestic servant in a household of wealthy, white businessmen. She's fine, but only on the outside. With intrusive kindness, the family grabs Ivy into their midst. The young woman will do anything for the whites. And yet she is cruelly left alone. She can't and won't see that you have to live and die alone. The care of the family is exposed by the domestic worker as pure selfishness that denies her the right to life and love. That's how the supposed caring turns into complete incomprehension and outright hatred as Ivy seeks to take on a life of her own. A young, sympathetic black man, first used as a tool of this sophisticated slave-holding in the golden cage, tears with Ivy the threads of fake love. In doing so, they gain true love and freedom.

anchor_text: Stanton Carlisle is a trickster who over the years has managed to create a rigged show where he pretends to be able to read minds with the complicity of a fortune teller and a psychologist. His fall will be as spectacular as his rise when his wife denounces him to the press and the public. From then on, he will be a pariah with no choice but to work in traveling circuses.
text_a: Stan Helsing is a video store clerk who arrives on Halloween with his friends in a town where parodies of Freddy Krueger, Chucky, Leatherface, Jason, Pinhead, and Michael Myers are rampant. He learns that he is a descendant of Abraham van Helsing and must try to survive.
text_b: Stanton Carlisle is a young man who works at a traveling carnival show. She collaborates on Zeena's fortune-telling trick, but after the death of Pete, Zeena's husband, she learns the code on which the trick is based. After seducing Molly, a fellow clerk, he goes out on his own, displaying his act in the salons of the upper class. His goal will be to trick some millionaire into plucking his feathers.

anchor_text: On a beautiful sunny day, Thomas from class 4a has to take a station on the Berlin underground to get to school, as he always does. But it's too beautiful a day to spend on the school bench. So he decides to take the subway a little further and wander through Berlin. He discovers a house on fire and alerts the fire department. Thus, he becomes a lifesaver for two small children who were in the apartment. When the fire department wants to take his name, he runs away. The next day, the newspaper reports on the unknown boy's heroic deed, and Thomas's teacher talks about it in class. Thomas, however, cannot appear because he lied to the teacher about not being able to come to school the day before because of a toothache. In the end, the fire department can locate him along with the two rescued children. They want to thank him for what he did, and now Thomas admits to skipping school and even lying to the teacher.
text_a: On a beautiful sunny day, as always, Thomas of the 4th class has to take a train on the Berlin underground to reach the school. But it's too beautiful a day for school. So he decides to go a little further on the subway and walk around Berlin. It detects a fire and alerts the fire department. So he becomes the savior of two small children who were in the apartment. When the firemen want to take your name, run away. The newspaper reports the next day a report on the unknown boy's heroic act and even in Thomas's class the matter is discussed with the teacher. Thomas, however, cannot reveal that he is the unknown hero because he lied to the teacher, telling her that he couldn't come to school the day before because of a toothache. Eventually, firefighters manage to track him down with the two rescued children. They want to thank him for his action and Thomas then admits to skipping school and even lying to the teacher.
text_b: In New York, in a very large building. It's a hot summer night in the Buddy apartment. The son of the house, Buddy, unable to sleep in his room, settles for the night on the landing of the emergency staircase on the upper floor. In his sleep, he sees, in the upstairs neighborhood, a murder... When he tells his story, his parents don't believe him because it's his habit to make up stories. Even the police don't take it seriously, the Kellersons dumped the body and they look like a couple with no history. But the neighbor eventually finds out that the kid saw his gesture...

anchor_text: Having just graduated from university, young Sola Fajure must face the harsh realities of the job market. However, when his search is unsuccessful, Sola decides to join the military together with his longtime friend Femi and his secret love Mona. During an exercise in the forest, Sola and Femi find a small wooden figure inhabited by the mythical deity Araromire. Over the next seven years, the three young people are pursued by fortune: Sola and Mona marry, Femi becomes a successful businessman. Until the spirit of Araromire makes life hell for the ignorant.
text_a: The daughter of Pastor Olsen, Laure is a beautiful and unassuming 17-year-old girl who lives in Manila. One day, she accidentally meets English documentary filmmaker Nicholas and decides to join him on an expedition to the island of Emelle among the mythical Mara people, whose sexual customs they learned from Professor Morgan. Before embarking on this exotic adventure, Laure and Nicolas decide to get married, because they have discovered that they share the same belief that to love means to enjoy the freedom of the partner.
text_b: In the ancient Yoruba land, a priest introduces the cult of Araromire, the goddess of good luck and fortune who grants seven years of wealth. Two young friends find a statuette on an abandoned altar while doing military service at a camp near the town of Araromire. They stop selling drugs in Lagos to become two successful businessmen with the normal problems of life: friendship, love, family. But what happens next?

anchor_text: In the ancient Yoruba territory, a priest introduces Araromire, the goddess of luck and fortune, who ensures the wealth of all for seven years... After finding a figurine in an abandoned chapel while doing their military service in a camp near the town of Araromire, two young friends leave their lives as drug dealers in Lagos to become successful businessmen facing seemingly normal stories of friendship, love and family. But what happens after that?
text_a: In the ancient Yoruba land, a priest introduces the cult of Araromire, the goddess of good luck and fortune who grants seven years of wealth. Two young friends find a statuette on an abandoned altar while doing military service at a camp near the town of Araromire. They stop selling drugs in Lagos to become two successful businessmen with the normal problems of life: friendship, love, family. But what happens next?
text_b: The daughter of Pastor Olsen, Laure is a beautiful and unassuming 17-year-old girl who lives in Manila. One day, she accidentally meets English documentary filmmaker Nicholas and decides to join him on an expedition to the island of Emelle among the mythical Mara people, whose sexual customs they learned from Professor Morgan. Before embarking on this exotic adventure, Laure and Nicolas decide to get married, because they have discovered that they share the same belief that to love means to enjoy the freedom of the partner.

anchor_text: Viridiana will soon take her vows and lock herself in a convent. Before that, she comes one last time to greet her uncle, Don Jaime, a wealthy bourgeois. He seduces her and asks her to marry him. She refuses, horrified, and runs off the property. Don Jaime commits suicide by hanging himself in the garden. Heiress of the estate with her cousin Jorge, natural son of Don Jaime, Viridiana renounces the cloister and decides to devote her life and property to the poor who beg at the exit of the church. On a night when the masters are away, the people she has gathered invade the house, throw a banquet, get drunk, and turn their party into an orgy. When Viridiana and Jorge return prematurely, most flee, but two of the beggars try to rape their benefactor. Rescued by her cousin, who convinces one of the beggars to kill the other, Viridiana succumbs to Jorge's charms and ends up living with him and their maid in a threesome.
text_a: The novice Viridiana (Silvia Pinal), about to take the habits, must leave the convent to visit her uncle don Jaime (Fernando Rey), who has paid for her studies. During his visit, Don Jaime, impressed by the resemblance between Viridiana and his deceased wife, puts her to sleep and attempts to rape her, but finally does not dare. Subsequently, he tries to detain her when she wants to return to the convent; he lies to her by telling her that she can no longer profess as a nun, because he has possessed her while she was asleep. This further alienates Viridiana from her uncle, who, after her departure, commits suicide. Viridiana, who feels guilty about her uncle's death, resigns from being a nun and stays in the mansion to practice Christian charity, welcoming a group of vagabonds, whom she provides shelter and food but who are, in the end, the ones who will attack and rob her. The arrival of Jorge (Francisco Rabal), natural son of Don Jaime, will definitely change the fate of the young woman.
text_b: Young Carlos arrives at a remote orphanage during the Spanish Civil War. There he befriends the other boys and learns of a ghost that supposedly haunts the place. The orphanage is run by Dr. Casares and Carmen, who sympathize with the Republicans and have gold in custody for the Republican cause. Behind this gold is the angry employee Jacinto, who was raised in the orphanage. As we later learn, one night he was working on the large vault in the kitchen where the gold was hidden, when he was surprised by the orphan Santi. In the basement, Jacinto confronted the boy and pushed him roughly, whereupon Santi hit his head and was mortally wounded. Jacinto tried to cover up his act by tying stones to the dying boy and plunging him into the building's supply pool. The ghost of Santis has haunted the orphanage ever since, prophesying to Carlos that many residents of the home will die. As hostilities escalate near the orphanage, Dr. Casares plans to evacuate it. Jacinto, who had just been chased off the premises after trying to take the gold, then sets the kitchen on fire with gasoline. The explosion kills several children and the headmistress Carmen. Dr. Casares also died of his injuries shortly thereafter. The next day, Jacinto returns to the orphanage to look for the gold. He finds it, but is thrown by the surviving children into the supply pool in which he once drowned Santi. The gold he carries pulls him down. Just as he's trying to get rid of it to re-emerge, Santi appears behind him and drowns him.

anchor_text: An American woman, who decided to leave her country, her boyfriend and her job without interest, fails in London because the ticket was $99, her only fortune. She's going to be approached by three random men, the first of whom is a successful music producer with no cultural reach, the second of whom is a self-centered failed actor, and the last of whom is a bridge teacher who's a little smarter than the previous two. How surprised he will be to learn that these three strangers are childhood friends, each shooting at the feet of his rivals without knowing who the other two are! Her favorite bridge instructor will be assisted by a high-flying "psychologist"; but the suit does not make the monk.
text_a: Martha, a 20-year-old American, tired of her life, leaves her Minneapolis and takes the first flight to London, with no definite destination and no one to know, determined only to change her life and put the past behind her. Arriving in the British capital, the young woman meets, in three different moments, Laurence, an introverted bridge teacher, Daniel, a very rich and arrogant record producer, and Frank, a failed theater actor, cynical and spiteful. Martha does not know that the three are bound by a deep friendship since childhood and, unknowingly, begins to frequent them until the three discover the truth and then try to win the heart of the American who, in the end, even more disoriented, must choose between the three.
text_b: Daniel is a young professor in Hamburg. He's planning on staying in town for the summer. At a market, a young jewellery seller, Juli, promises him that he will meet love in the day if he wears the sun-shaped ring she sells him. At the same time she invites him to join her for the evening. Unfortunately for her, Daniel falls in love with an attractive young Turkish woman, Melek, a little lost in Hamburg who is going to Istanbul; Daniel decides to go to join her. Juli then decides to hitchhike where the first person to stop will win. And it's with Daniel that she embarks on a road-movie to the Bosphorus...

anchor_text: Chicago police narcotics chief Tony Church (Burt Reynolds) organizes an operation against drug dealers that turns into a disaster and forces him to resign. Returning to work as a security guard in a store, he is visited by Della Roberts (Liza Minnelli), a young prostitute who witnessed the failed operation. Considered an embarrassing witness by the traffickers, she is targeted by Dancer (James Remar), a contract killer. She asks Church to protect her and he agrees, prompting him to resume his investigation.
text_a: Unlike many of his colleagues on the Phoenix Police Department, Harry Collins is a good cop and, despite his very particular value system, an honest man. However, his compulsion for gambling led to a large debt to a gangster bookmaker in Chicago. With only 48 hours to pay off his debt, Harry is offered a contract: to murder Joey, a young prisoner who can provide the police with information about the bookmaker, and the debt will be canceled. Harry cannot contemplate such an action and prefers to offer Joey some life-saving advice.
text_b: In a Chicago hotel, due to a ruthless killer named Dancer, prostitute Della Roberts finds herself embroiled, against her will, in a shootout between drug dealers and a group of undercover police officers. In the shootout unfortunately some policemen lose their lives. Among the agents who escaped death is Church, who, however, due to the failure of this mission, is removed from the police. Having faced the killer who killed some of Church's colleagues, Della feels threatened and decides to hire him as a bodyguard. Together they will uncover the underbelly of the case, where corrupt cops are actually "working" for a crime boss.

anchor_text: The action takes place in 19th century British India. The film's prologue features a group of Thugs, a cult of assassins worshipping Kali, who disguise themselves as travelers to follow a British patrol, whom they massacre during the night by strangling them. The telegraph link to a village in the mountains is interrupted, the General Staff sends a detachment to repair the telegraph line, commanded by three British sergeants, MacChesney (Victor McLaglen), Thomas Tommy Ballantine (Douglas Fairbanks Jr.) and Archibald Cutter (Cary Grant). The small party is followed by the humble Indian water carrier Gunga Din. The British find a deserted village, and begin repairing the line, when they are attacked from the rooftops. After fierce resistance, the survivors of the detachment retreated across the river with the help of Gunga Din. Back at their base, the three sergeants resume their activities: MacChesney tends his elephant, Cutter notices that Gunga Din is secretly attending military exercises because he wants to become a soldier, and teaches him to make the military salute. As for Ballantine, he announces that he will leave the army to get married, a catastrophic abandonment for his two comrades-in-arms who will deploy all their tricks to get him to sign his re-enlistment. The trio is assigned a new mission, to restore the telegraph, with a larger and more vigilant detachment...
text_a: With the help of the Iroquois tribe, led by their chief Magua, white bandits attack the Mohicans and cause a bloodbath. The heavily wounded chief of the Mohicans, Chingachgook, makes his way to his son Unkas and his blood brother Hawkeye, a famous forest runner. Unkas vows to avenge his father. A cash shipment is attacked by the bandits and the Iroquois, but the soldiers manage to retreat to the nearby fortified Munroe farm. Colonel Munroe is assisting them in their defense. However, he is worried about his two daughters Cora and Alice, who are on their way to the farm with Captain Hayward and some soldiers. With Captain Hayward, Magua poses as Munroe's messenger, tasked with leading the entire command on safe routes to the farm. In the canyon of the Black Vulture, the troop is attacked by the Iroquois, but Unkas and Hawkeye come to their aid. But you can't stop Magua from escaping with Cora as a hostage. Unkas pursues Magua and captures him. But to save Cora's life, he must let Magua go and return to the farm with her. There, in the meantime, the attack of the Indians and bandits is escalating. At the last minute, Hawkeye can come to the rescue with soldiers from Fort Westerhill. Once again, Magua has succeeded in abducting Cora. But Unkas is after him again. Unkas defeats Magua this time, but is mortally wounded in the battle. The last of the Mohicans dies with him.
text_b: In India, at a British army outpost, Gunga Din, the water carrier, dreams of becoming a soldier. Meanwhile, the regiment is alerted by news that the telegraph wires have been cut. Cutter, MacChesney and Ballantine, three sergeants very friendly with each other, are sent to investigate. They discover that the culprits are members of a sect of thugs, fanatical killers devoted to the goddess Kali. Escaping the thugs, the three sergeants make it back to base. Ballantine then announces that he has decided to leave the army to marry Emmy, his fiancée. The comrades, on hearing that he will become a tea merchant, are horrified and try to change his mind. Captivated by the idea of discovering a treasure, Cutter is led by Gunga Din to a temple that they discover to be the holy place of the thugs. Gunga Din returns to call for reinforcements but MacChesney and Ballantine, convinced that their friend is being held captive by some priests, rush to his aid on their own, only to be captured as well. Powerless, they watch the Scottish troops who are about to fall into the trap of the thugs: to save the soldiers will be the heroic sacrifice of Gunga Din who, embracing the trumpet, will sound the alarm, but in this way exposing himself to the blows of the enemies who will bring him down. The battle is won, the soldiers return. After the announcement that Ballantine would no longer leave the army, the regiment attends the solemn funeral of Gunga Din who is buried with full military honours and with the rank of corporal in the British Army.

anchor_text: Marcy Tizard works for U.S. Senator John McGlory and organizes his campaign in Massachusetts. Since the senator is dependent on the votes of Irish-born voters, she travels to Ireland to search for his relatives living there and evidence of his ancestry. There, in the small town of Ballinagra, she meets rival matchmakers Millie O'Dowd and Dermot O'Brien. In Ballinagra, the regularly scheduled courtship rituals are about to begin. One of the villagers, Sean Kelly, is bidding for Tizard.

anchor_text: In his introduction, Leo Tolstoy refers to his work "My Faith", published ten years earlier: "In 1884 I wrote a book entitled "My Faith!" In this book I have truthfully stated what I believe". The contents of the book are summarized at the end of the work under the following headings:" Chapter 1: "The doctrine of non-resistance has been known by the minority of men since the founding of Christianity and is now known by the minority of men" Chapter 2: "Judgments on the non-resistance of believers and non-believers" Chapter 3: "The Misunderstanding of Christianity by the Believers" Chapter 4: "How Scientists are Misunderstanding Christianity" Chapter 5: The Contradictions of Our Life and of Our Christian Consciousness Chapter 6: "The Attitude of the People of Our World to War" In Chapter 1, Leo Tolstoy addresses authors who had responded to his work "My Faith" in a supportive manner, particularly those from the United States such as the Quakers, as well as Christian pacifist writers William Lloyd Garrison and Adin Ballou. He also quotes extensively the Czech theologian and writer Petr Chelčický as a representative of the Bohemian Brethren. In Chapter 2, Tolstoy refutes arguments made against the absolute validity and applicability of nonviolence (non-resistance) by critics of his book "My Faith", dividing the counterarguments into 5 categories: 1. "Violence is not contrary to Christianity". 2. "Violence is necessary to restrain evildoers". 3. "Violence is a duty in the defense of one's neighbor". "The violation of the commandment of non-resistance is recognized as a simple weakness". 5. "The avoidance of the answer by the pretext that the question is completely solved".
text_a: The author began this work in 1890 and finished it in May of 1893, working hard on it. It is divided into twelve chapters, to which Tolstoy intended to add an epilogue, but did not write it. He confided to Čertkov: "Never has any work cost me so much effort". Banned by the tsarist government for its criticism of the state and the Church, the work nevertheless circulated clandestinely in Russia, and was immediately translated abroad. It was published in French in 1893 by the Librerie academique Didier Perrin et C. - Paris. From this text was conducted in 1894 the translation into Italian of the Fratelli Bocca di Roma, reprinted in 1988 thanks to the collaboration of the publishing houses M. It lacks Genoa and Publiprint of Trento. The work, which expounds the doctrine of non-resistance to evil by means of evil, had a considerable influence on Gandhi, who read an English translation of it in 1894. The Mahatma will write: Leo Tolstoy Jr. considered The Kingdom of God Is Within You and Of Life as his father's "capital religious works".
text_b: In 1910, the young intellectual Valentin Bulgakov arrives at writer Lev Tolstoy's estate in Jasnaja Poljana, near Moscow, and is hired by Tolstoy as his personal secretary. For a long time the Russian writer has approached an anarchist-Christian faith and non-violent belief and has embraced vegetarianism, even giving up his wealth and copyright on his works. All this has triggered a struggle involving his children, his beloved wife Sofya, who despite jealously loving her husband does not accept his decisions, and Vladimir Chertkov, leader of the Tolstoyan movement who is inspired by the writer's ideas. Young Valentin finds himself in the middle of this struggle, unable to handle the events. To escape family strife, Tolstoy finally leaves his residence to go to a secret place where he can continue his work undisturbed. Sofya, feeling abandoned, tries to commit suicide. During the journey Tolstoy falls ill and dies near the railway station of Astapovo, where Sofya joins him to see him one last time.

anchor_text: The Henderson family from Seattle is vacationing in the Rocky Mountains. Father George gets blinded by the sun on his way home and hits a big, hairy creature with his car. Only on closer inspection does the family realize that it is the fabled creature Bigfoot. With George's anticipation of appearances on TV shows and a lucrative business for discovering Bigfoot, they simply pack the lifeless creature onto the roof of their car and drive it back to the city. But Bigfoot just passed out and woke up in the Henderson garage at night. Unable to navigate the unfamiliar surroundings, Bigfoot destroys the family home. He is horrified by the many furs and stuffed animal trophies in the house and buries them in the garden. At first, the family fears Bigfoot, and George, who works in a hunting business, wants to shoot the animal. But eventually he has compassion and recognizes the human traits of Bigfoot, who is basically a harmless giant. The Hendersons befriend Bigfoot and George gives him the name "Harry". Attempts to keep Bigfoot as a pet fail because he simply isn't cut out for the human world. So the Hendersons decide it's in everyone's best interest to put Harry back in the wild. The plan is complicated by the big-game hunter Jacques LaFleur, who is closely following Harry. text_a: The film claims to be based on true events. It describes the existence of the Fouke Monster, a six-foot-tall Bigfoot-like creature said to have been spotted by local residents since the 1950s. It is described as completely red-brown-haired, thirteen years old, and unpleasantly smelly. The residents, who claim to have seen the monster in the past, tell their story of the encounter in the film, often appearing under their own names. Among other things, the monster is said to have killed a dog and several large pigs and easily jumped over a fence with a 300-pound specimen under his arm. In another scene, a cat is shown "scared to death" at the sight of the monster. The Fouke monster has been shot at several times in the past, but it has escaped every time. When hunters try to hunt the creature with dogs, they fail because the dogs refuse. Strange howls are heard around Fouke, such as in Boggy Creek. There, a police officer reports that the monster ran across the street in front of his car one night when he was on his way home. A later scene, based on a newspaper report that inspired the film, shows the creature threatening a family living in a secluded house in the woods. After the monster is shot, it attacks the house and its inhabitants. In the process, the creature causes the family to flee their home and "overturns several flower pots", the narrator records. This event, too, does not result in the creature's capture, and so it is said to still roam the swamps and bayous of southern Arkansas, where residents claim to see it at regular intervals.

anchor_text: The two halves of the body of murdered prostitute Elizabeth are found at two locations several miles apart in Charleston, South Carolina. Officers Edward Kennesaw and Phillip Braxton are leading the investigation. The businessman's son, James Walter Wayland, whose phone number was found on the body, is the only witness. In the interviews, Wayland admits to meeting the dead woman in the park that night, but says he never saw her again. Extremely intelligent and well-educated from his studies in psychology, Wayland, who suffers from temporal lobe epilepsy, leads the investigators on the wrong track. After being able to manipulate the polygraph tests during the first interrogations under the influence of drugs, after three days of interrogation, he shows a videotape showing Edward Kennesaw and Elizabeth together. Kennesaw forces the prostitute to play his wife. Faced with this videotape, Detective Edward Kennesaw agrees to submit himself to a polygraph test, which yields ambiguous results. Kennesaw calmly denies the question of whether he is the murderer of Elizabeth. He becomes restless when Wayland asks him if Kennesaw murdered his wife. But his wife is alive. Kennesaw then pressures Wayland until he admits to finding and dismembering the murdered Elizabeth to deflect suspicion. He's still in denial about a murder. Shortly thereafter, he collapses unconscious after swallowing two pills to induce a faint; his death is erroneously determined. A year later, the living Wayland appears in a park and speaks to a woman.
text_a: The film centers on the interrogation of billionaire Wayland by two detectives in an attempt to get him to confess to the murder of a prostitute. It's going to turn into a long, exhausting game. A wealthy textile industry upstart, James Wayland, is suspected in the murder of a prostitute known as Elizabeth, whose body was found cut in half in two locations a few miles apart. Wayland is a heavy drinker as well as a compulsive liar, who suffers from violent outbursts as well as memory lapses. The investigation is now in the hands of tenacious Detective Kennesaw and his less-than-spoiled assistant, Braxton. In the course of the investigation, the suspect in turn obtains information and evidence about the dark secrets of his interrogators, discovering that the detective had frequented Elizabeth to avenge a marital betrayal, while his assistant is in trouble for contracting gambling debts. With these elements of psychological pressure in hand, Wayland almost gains the upper hand against the inquisitors, managing to manipulate the reactions of the truth machine and even inducing Kennesaw to undergo the polygraph test himself. While eventually admitting to dissecting and dispersing the prostitute's corpse (but not admitting to killing her), Wayland later succeeds in faking her death by ingesting pills. At the end of the movie we see him, a year later, approaching a stranger in a park.

anchor_text: In India, Tom Ransome, a high-ranking bourgeois official leads an opulent life between his conquests and his former wife Lady Edwina Esketh. She meets a young Indian doctor, Major Rama Safti, and falls in love with him. The city of Ranchipur is devastated by an earthquake, floods and a plague epidemic. Lady Esketh renounces her middle-class life and decides to get involved alongside the sick, while staying with Rama Safti whom she loves deeply. Unfortunately, after a moment of inattention, she is infected with a virus. Affected by the disease, she dies. Rama Safti will become the ruler of the Kingdom of Jharkhand. Tom Ransome and a young missionary named Fern became friends.
text_a: Tom (Franco Nero) is a gold prospector who receives a message from a friend in his hometown asking him to return. When he does, he finds the place dominated and feared by the landowner Lord Scott (Giuseppe Addobbati) and his bloodthirsty son Jason (Nino Castelnuovo), with no one even daring to question their methods. All the people who could provide him with information, including the friend who sent him the letter, are murdered with impunity one after the other. In view of the situation, Tom decides to investigate what is happening in the town and enters the Scott mansion along with his brother Jeff (George Hilton), who has become a drunk.
text_b: Tom Ransome, who has lived in Ranchipur for years and is a close friend of Rama Safti, who represents the new India, is joined by Lady Edwina, a former lover now married to Lord Esketh. The woman tries to seduce Rama Safti but slowly falls in love with him. Meanwhile, Fern Simon, the young daughter of a missionary, falls in love with Tom. An earthquake first and then a flood devastates the city of Ranchipur where a cholera epidemic breaks out. Lord Esketh dies and his wife Edwina decides to help Safti in providing support and care to the exhausted population. Unfortunately, she too falls ill and dies while Tom accepts the love of young Fern.

anchor_text: The film follows on from the first and begins with the return of H. (pronounced Hatché ) (Mario Casas) after two years abroad in London. He hopes to rebuild his life after separating from his first great love Babi (Maria Valverde). But on his way, he will meet Gin (Clara Lago) who will give him a new impetus, full of joy, spontaneity, smile. But in H.'s heart, Babi is still there and continues to torment him.
text_a: The film tells the story of a young man, Luke, between his friendship with his father, his studies and his love life. Received at the École Boulle in Paris, he finds himself caught between two romances, the one with Djemila whom he met in the capital and his girlfriend Geneviève who stayed where he grew up with his father.
text_b: After spending two years in London, Hache returns home to Spain. During his absence, he has never forgotten the intense love affair with Babi but, when he meets Gin, he thinks he can fall in love again. Forgetting the first love, however, is not easy and the chance encounter with Babi collapses Hache's precarious balance, involved in a love triangle against his will.

anchor_text: The cartoonist Dino Romani meets on the beach of Trieste the young, beautiful Nicole, who has just been saved from drowning by two men. Nicole visits Dino at his house the same day, where they immediately have sex. Dino is fascinated by Nicole's beauty and the two begin an affair. But soon Dino realizes that something is wrong with his girlfriend. She loves provocation, prone to hysteria and jealousy. Dino spies on her and finds out that Nicole suffers from severe mental disorders and lives in an open mental institution. On the one hand, he's obsessed with the beautiful woman, feels rejected, annoyed, but also wants to help her. An amour fou develops between the two and Nicole's mental problems intensify. She's becoming increasingly deranged and self-destructive. The film ends with the bald-headed Nicole she cut her hair to test if Dino still desires her going into the sea. Whether she commits suicide or not remains open.
text_a: The cartoonist Dino Romani meets on a beach in Trieste the young and sculptural Nicole, who has just been saved from drowning by two men. Nicole visits Dino the same day at his home, where they immediately have sex. Dino is fascinated by Nicole's beauty and the relationship between the two continues. However, Dino soon notices some strange ways in Nicole. She loves provocation and tends to be hysterically jealous and self-destructive. Dino ends up following her and discovers that Nicole is staying in a mental institution. As his troubles persist or even worsen, Dino grows more and more in love with her...
text_b: Dino is unsuccessful at work and also as a representative for encyclopedias and lives at the expense of his partner Elsa. He has an eight-year-old son whom the mother, from whom he is separated, has not allowed him to see for five years. Now he gets to spend an entire Thursday with him. In order to make the best possible impression on little Robertino, he rents an imposing crossroads with money borrowed from Elsa. They're taking it to different places in Rome. Dino tells the boy adventurous stories about his alleged war captivity and also reveals much else, which the clever boy soon realizes. On the beach, they meet Elsa, who has been able to find Dino a steady job. But Dino declines because he still wants to pursue his dream of making a lot of money in a big business instead of doing boring office work. Angry, she tells him that she no longer wants to see him. As he continues to spend time with the boy, Dino realizes that his son is suffocating in the noble spheres in which his mother moves, and teaches him something about life. He lets us eat with our hands instead of cutlery. Eventually, Dino runs out of money to fill up the gasoline-thirsty car and has to leave it parked. At the end of the day, he confesses to Robertino that he told a lot of lies and it didn't work out. Robertino has closed his heart to his father and wants to see him again. Dino calls Elsa and says he'll take the job.

anchor_text: In 1880, in Colorado, a poker game ends in death when one of the men is lynched by his opponents for cheating. Only one of the players, Van Morgan, is trying to stop the murder. When Reverend Rudd, the dead man's brother, shows up a little later, the perpetrators are gradually killed by him. The last survivor is the innocent Van Morgan. In a final confrontation, he kills the Reverend.
text_a: A disloyal employee ends up in prison, and from there he takes revenge by killing the brother of the boss who turned him in by a prisoner about to be released. But the chain of crimes continues until justice is done.
text_b: In 1880, in a Colorado town hall, during a poker game, one of the players, an outsider, is discovered to be cheating, so his rivals, outraged, hang him. A while later, all the people who took part in that card game are being killed one by one.

anchor_text: In 1880, in the town of Rincon, Colorado, a gambler was caught cheating during a poker game. Outraged, the other players decide to hang the cheater. Van Morgan (Dean Martin) is the only one who tries to calm them down but is knocked unconscious by one of them, who is none other than Nick (Roddy McDowall), the hateful and sadistic brother of Nora Evers, Van Morgan's fiancée. As the town welcomes a new pastor (Robert Mitchum), the lynchers are killed one by one by an unknown vigilante who has decided to avenge the cheater, and to whom a mysterious member of the gambling group reveals the identity.
text_a: On Grandpa Ted's death, Monty Mulligan has a chance to inherit a large fortune, but only if they live together for at least six months. Although the two have very different habits and characters they are united by the fact that they are squatters. Waiting to receive the inheritance, they embark on criminal adventures with unexpected outcomes: first they kidnap a banker's daughter in order to demand a ransom but he is very happy not to see his daughter again. They attempt an assault on a gold shipment but encounter another gang with the same objective. Ted is hit, falls into the water and is swept away by the current while Monty, after annihilating the rival gang, is welcomed into town as the defender of the gold cargo and as a reward he can marry the girl from whom he too would like to escape. Fortunately, Ted reappears, saving him from a dangerous marriage.
text_b: In a saloon in Colorado, a stranger is caught cheating during a poker game. Nick Evers, with the complicity of the other players, decides to execute the thief and hang him. The only one among them who opposes it is Van Morgan. After a while, preacher Jonathan Rudd, a skilled gunfighter, arrives in the village. In reality Rudd is the brother of the thief, who has come to avenge his death by searching one by one the players involved in the episode. After the murder of the first two, Van Morgan links the events to what happened at the poker table. Tension mounts as the killer hunts down the others responsible, until Morgan ends up clashing with Rudd and winning in a shootout to the death.

anchor_text: While Tommy and Tuppence have just bought a new house, Tuppence notices that in one of the books that has been there for generations, an edition of Robert Louis Stevenson's The Black Arrow, certain letters are underlined. These letters, placed side by side, form a most intriguing sentence: Marie Jordan did not die of natural causes. One of us killed her. I think I know who. This sentence, written by Alexander Parkinson, a young man who lived long before this discovery and died at the age of only fourteen, would prompt Tommy and Tuppence to investigate what had happened at that time.
text_a: By the early 1970s, Tommy and Tuppence Beresford had decided to retire to private life. The couple moves to a quiet village. While arranging their newly purchased home, they stumble upon a book containing the following message: "Marie Jordan did not die of natural causes. One of us killed her. I know who did it". Who wrote this message? Who was Marie Jordan? Who killed her? These are the questions the Beresfords are trying to answer by re-investigating this cold case dating back to the First World War. Returning to the adventurous life they had lived, they discover it concerns the poisoning of a spy woman.
text_b: In the spring of 1940, at the beginning of the Second World War, the close-knit couple Tommy and Tuppence Beresford, memories of their adventures during the previous world war, scattered because they wanted to have a significant role in the events involving the British Empire. Tommy is thus contacted by their old boss and is tasked with discovering the two German spies who are organizing a Fifth Column in Britain, collaborators known only by the initials of N and M. But the assignment is only for him, not for Tuppence, who will have to stay at home. Tommy agrees and leaves for the town of Leahampton, where he finds lodging in the boarding house believed to be the hiding place of one of the two spies. Here he discovers that Tuppence is already a guest at the boarding house. Together, the two of them will be able to uncover the German spies and sabotage their invasion plan.

anchor_text: Now in the '70s, Tommy and Tuppence decide to move again. They want to live in a quiet English village. The house they bought still contains some furniture and parts of the library of the numerous previous owners. Among them is an extensive collection of English children's books. Instead of cleaning up further, Tuppence gets stuck in the books because they remind her of her own childhood. As Tuppence sifts through the books, she discovers in one a coded message: "Mary Jordan did not die of natural causes" and "It was one of us". First of all, Tommy isn't thrilled with his wife's newfound detective spirit. However, he soon becomes infected and plays detective himself. Among other things, he goes to the old village cemetery, accompanied by his dog Hannibal, looking for clues, looking through old archives and making contact with former intelligence colleagues in London. Both of them are now on the lookout for traces of Mary Jordan. You delve into a German-British espionage affair from the time before the First World War and can partially solve the murder of Mary Jordan, an agent in British services. The couple's 90-year-old gardener, Isaac, is also murdered in the course of the novel because he knew something about the conspirators. Ultimately, the case remains unsolved in all its dimensions. The Wireheads are a small group of British fascists. The description of Hannibal's character and behavior occupies a large space in the plot of the novel. Extensive passages are also devoted to the description of the old children's books that Tuppence discovered. Favorite book is called The Black Arrow by Robert Louis Stevenson.
text_a: The plot closely follows the story published in Fotorama in the spring of the same year, 1910: Anna, a young Danish girl, reads in the newspaper an advertisement offering an interesting job in England. So she leaves for London, leaving her friend Georg alone, saddened by her departure. As soon as she crossed by boat, she was spotted by white slave traders. Arriving on British soil, these men welcome her, but as soon as she falls into their traps, she is kidnapped and locked in a brothel. With the help of a cleaning woman who works there and sees with increasing discomfort what the bandits are doing, she manages to get a letter to her father. He immediately goes to the police in his own country, but the police declare that they have no jurisdiction in a case that takes place abroad. At this point, Georg intervenes; he is determined to save his friend. He, in turn, travels to London and enlists the help of a detective. During the crossing, he recognized on the ship a man he thought he had already seen when Anna had boarded and he quickly began to piece together the story. The detective and Georg secretly follow him and he leads them to the brothel. They both managed to make contact with Anna. Using a sheet, she escaped through the window of the room where she was being held prisoner, but was later captured again by human traffickers. The detective notifies the police who arrive and search the brothel, unfortunately no trace of Anna. But it is then that the police receive crucial information from the housekeeper: Anna is being held prisoner on a boat! We find her there, we release her and she goes back to her country with Georg.
text_b: The elderly retired couple Tommy and Tuppence Beresford buy an old house in a small coastal town in England, and when the necessary reforms are ready, they decide to move in. The purchase of the house included an attic full of old books that Tuppence, a lover of reading, decides to organize. The books remind her of her past and she, with great pleasure, rereads some fragments of works that she always admired. In doing so, he finds a message composed of a few words underlined in red on the page of a book, Marie Jordan did not die of natural causes. It was one of us. Tuppence begins to investigate the past of the house and the town, relying on the memory of the old men in the nursing homes and the elderly ladies. It is hard work to investigate events that took place 50 years earlier, but Tuppence, with the support of her husband Tommy, manages to find out who Marie Jordan was, why she was killed, and by whom. As it cannot but be in a novel with Tommy and Tuppence, the solution of the case is related to espionage.

anchor_text: Lindsay and Frank are two strangers invited to Frank's half-brother's wedding in Paso Robles, California. Once there, they realize that they are more than strangers and share an equal level of irritability and grief towards the world. However, under the thick cloud of exasperation, the desperate romantics fall in love with each other.
text_a: Frank and Lindsay are meeting at the airport. After a very brief alchemy that seems to have been created, they begin to fight furiously. The relationship between the two seems to end right away, but fate has something else in store for them. In fact, they'll be sharing the same plane headed, both of them, to the same wedding. The groom, in fact, is nothing more than his brother and her ex. The meeting will allow both of you to discuss the idea of love, human relationships and life in general.
text_b: While shopping for their respective families at Christmas, architect Frank Raftis (De Niro) and graphic artist Molly Gilmore (Streep) cross paths and confuse their Christmas gifts. What begins as a pleasant friendship becomes a romance. They both feel that what they're doing isn't right, they're two good people, they don't want to hurt anyone, yet they love each other in spite of everything and everyone. Their marriages have long ceased to exist. But they're still in love and they won't be able to fight that love.

anchor_text: James Silva is a very intelligent man but troubled by his many emotional and behavioral disorders. He nevertheless became an expert agent and an elite U.S. intelligence officer, working primarily for the CIA. With his Overwatch team, he participates in a mission to neutralize Russian FSB agents operating on American soil. The mission ends with the loss of an American agent and the assassination of all the Russians present. Sixteen months later, in the Southeast Asian country of Indocarr, James and his team are tasked with exfiltrating Li Noor, a local police officer who holds information about the location of quantities of cesium 139 (a fictitious isotope). But they'll be hunted by an army of local assassins all along the 22 miles that separates them from the plane that will allow them to leave the country.
text_a: Two CIA employees, a brilliant data analyst and a shortcut-seeking time-waster, undergo a test to be admitted to the operational ranks of the famous Langley Agency, but the latter's grotesque attempts to cheat and copy condemn both of them to be unknowingly chosen as bait to be sacrificed in a suicide mission, so as to allow "real" agents to go unnoticed. The two are first assigned to an unlikely training course and then sent to Pakistan from where, after escaping capture by the Soviets, they travel to Afghanistan and are mistaken for doctors; finally, with the help of local guerrillas, they infiltrate Tajikistan, where they are reunited - unexpectedly - with the real CIA operatives sent to the scene. The purpose of the mission, as it turns out, is to take control of a TEL base and its Soviet ICBM so that U.S. forces can intercept and destroy it with a sophisticated laser system, under conditions totally similar to those of a real nuclear attack. The system fails and, faced with an unexpected nuclear impact on the United States, World War III now seems imminent also because of the determination of the generals, ready to sacrifice humanity in order to guarantee the American way of life; from the lost Soviet steppe the two "spies like us" have the minutes counted to avoid the nuclear disaster now imminent.
text_b: In the wake of the disappearance of four kilos of radioactive isotopes of Cesium139, Li Noor (Iko Uwais) an ex-military, shows up exhibiting a disc in his hand at the United States Embassy in Indonesia to ask for an immediate extradition from the country in exchange for information about the whereabouts of these elements, a fact that strains the relationship between the governments of both countries, more when the local authorities claim Li for espionage. The covert operations group Overwatch is tasked with researching the truth about Li Noor, who in exchange for the information requests its shipment to the US on a military plane, if it is not taken, the disk it carried will self-destruct. However, during the transfer they are fiercely attacked by unknown suicide extremists who attempt to assassinate Noor. In the midst of the skirmishes, Silva puts his trust in Noor and allows him to defend as well, however Silva's teammates fall one by one leaving him without members of the field team. To obtain the information, they plan an escape through the covert operations group Overwatch, led by ex-marine James Silva (Mark Wahlberg), having to leave him at a specific point; Mile 22. As Noor board the plane, he exchanges a series of decontextualized phrases that Silva interprets at the end of the ride with fatal results for Overwatch.

anchor_text: In episodes set in Budapest, London, New York, Paris, Madrid, and Munich, neglected housewives console themselves with the neighbor, the psychiatrist, a relative, a taxi driver, and an athlete.
text_a: Budapest, London, Madrid, New York, Paris, and Munich: A reporter goes to meet married women who are more or less satisfied with their marital and extra-marital lives.
text_b: The film depicts the lives of numerous people in a Tokyo slum in the form of individual episodes without a continuous plot. The partly very surreal film shows the life in poverty and hopelessness, with which the inhabitants still manage to cope. The protagonist of the film is the mentally handicapped Roku-chan.

anchor_text: Young Grover just graduated and wants to be a novelist. He is still in love with the beautiful Jane, but she has moved to Prague to study. Their relationship isn't working because of the distance. The aspiring writer is unhappy, but he's not the only one living in a kind of emotional and professional limbo. Each of his friends around him shares the same restlessness and existential dissatisfaction.
text_a: Young Grover has just graduated from college and wants to be a novelist. The boy is still in love with the beautiful Jane, who has moved to Prague for studies, and is unable in any way to overcome the distance. The aspiring writer is not alone in living in a kind of emotional and professional limbo; each of the friends around him share the same existential restlessness and dissatisfaction.
text_b: Lee, a writer, tells the story of his life to expatriate American students in Mexico City. He wanders aimlessly through the gay bars of the city, but his destiny is about to change the day he crosses paths with young Allerton. He sets his sights on the young man he falls obsessively in love with but he rejects him. However, despite their complex relationship, the two friends set out together in search of a hallucinogenic plant that would confer telepathic gifts.

anchor_text: Jakob Lieblich (originally Jason Sweet), a professional gambler, wins a flock of sheep playing poker. He arranges for the animals to be taken by train to Powder Valley, which is noted with displeasure by the local population. With McCall, the toughest and strongest guy on the ground, he starts a fight, which he wins. He's also proven himself to be an excellent gunner. He falls in love with Dell Payton, who doesn't know what to think of him, but falls in love with him anyway. Jason's sidekick is the cattle baron Steven Bedford, who he knows from earlier days. Bedford has given himself a new identity. Before he came to town, he was Johnny Bledsoe, a cheater and a gunfighter. Bedford/Bledsoe realizes that the residents are turning away from him after Jason has made it clear to the people that he wants to buy up the whole country for himself. He's hiring a professional hit man to take out Jason. Jason can defeat the killer, Chocktaw Neal, with the help of Dell and Milt Masters. This leads to a showdown between Jason and Bedford/Bledsoe, in which Jason is the fastest, but is still wounded. He hooks up with Dell, but to her surprise, he sells the flock. He's settling down as a rancher.
text_a: The United States in 1880. Jason Okay wins a flock of sheep at poker and decides to take them to a small town in the west where the only business is cattle ranching. Jason, imagining that it will not be easy to get him accepted, uses all his tricks to show everyone right away for what he is, that is, a great gunman, a skilled puncher and an extremely determined and determined person. Dell Payton is the first person to run into Jason and be attracted to him. The arrogant newcomer, however, soon clashes with his fiancé, "Colonel" Steven Bedford, a kind of plenipotentiary revered by all citizens. Jason actually knew him when he was with him in Texas and his name was Johnny Bledsoe. The "colonel" understands that Jason is a threat to the position he's built up over time with lies. Bedford initially manages to convince the entire population to side against the man who wants to raise sheep, but he does not give up and instills in the inhabitants the well-founded doubt that their champion is actually cheating them. Bedford then calls professional gunfighter Chocktaw with whom Jason already has an open account. Jason confronts Chocktaw whose two henchmen are disarmed by Dell Payton and Milt Masters, now turning on the Colonel. Frozen Chocktaw and then Bedford/Bledsoe, Jason Okay, though wounded, is the victor and the savior of the country. To Dell's surprise, Jason sells his flock of sheep to buy cattle, and then sets off for the farm he will run with her.
text_b: Jason Stillwell (Kurt McKinney) is a young karate student who trains at his father's dōjō in Los Angeles. One night after the training is over, the dojo is visited by members of an organized crime syndicate in order to recruit Jason's father to be their thug. Jason's father is attacked and wounded by Russian boxer Ivan Kraschinsky (Jean-Claude Van Damme), a hit man for the mafia boss. Jason's father decides that the family moves to start a new life in Seattle, away from the dangers and threats of that mob. Jason has trouble adjusting and is constantly harassed by a group of boys one of whom is the assistant of the national karate champion, brother of the girl with whom the protagonist unknowingly starts an affair. However Jason is an unconditional admirer of Bruce Lee and tries to practice martial arts like him. One night after being beaten up at his girlfriend's birthday party and having a critical argument with his father, he is visited by the ghost of Bruce Lee (played by Tai Chung Kim). After being trained by Bruce's specter, Jason goes from a low-level opponent to a surprising and invincible martial artist. Practitioners of different martial arts organize a local tournament, a perfect opportunity for Jason to settle scores with himself, Ivan and the mafia.

text_a: Player Jason Sweet (Ford) has won a flock of sheep during a poker game and decides to transport them by train to the cattle region. Once there he has to confront the local thug, Jumbo McCall (Mickey Shaughnessy) and tips him a beating. Sweet also stands out for being an expert gunman. Dell Payton (Shirley MacLaine) doesn't know what to do with him, but they're drawn to him. Her suitor is the local cattleman, Colonel Steven Bedford (Leslie Nielsen). When Bedford finds himself losing his battle for dominance, despite having the entire city behind him, he brings in a professional gunfighter, Chocktaw Neal (Pernell Roberts), but Jason Sweet has little trouble dealing with him. The final showdown comes down to Bedford and Jason Sweet. Sweet is faster and smarter and Bedford ends up dead. Sweet keeps the girl, and then, to his amazement, sells the sheep. He only kept them because he refused to be pressured by anyone.
text_b: Jason Stillwell (Kurt McKinney) is a young karate student who trains at his father's dōjō in Los Angeles. One night after the training is over, the dojo is visited by members of an organized crime syndicate in order to recruit Jason's father to be their thug. anchor_text: Tom Fowler and Darcy Rivera are getting married. They reunite their two families to celebrate their wedding on a private island in the Philippines. As the couple argue, they are taken hostage by pirates who demand $45 million from Darcy's wealthy father, Robert. The latter refuses to pay until he is assured that Darcy is alive. Darcy and Tom manage to escape their captors by killing one of the pirates. As they try to escape the Reconquest, Darcy and Tom express their respective doubts about marriage.
text_a: Darcy, a brilliant lawyer, and Tom, a declining baseball player, are about to get married on a private island in the Philippines, surrounded by intrusive relatives. As their relationship seems on edge, a gang of Indonesian pirates land on the island and take the guests hostage, with the intent of blackmailing the bride's father, a well-known millionaire. The combativeness of the two newlyweds, however, seriously jeopardizes the success of their plan.
text_b: Rachel White is a single, attractive lawyer who works at a New York law firm. On her 30th birthday, after having a few too many drinks, she reveals to Dex, her best friend's future husband, that she's had a crush on him since college. The two end up in bed that night. What seems like a simple night of passion turns into something more complicated when Dex reveals to Rachel that he is in love with her.

anchor_text: Darcy and Tom are about to enter into the covenant of marriage. So they invite their families to a big wedding party in the Philippines. The stubborn families make sure that the family blessing hangs crooked. When Darcy's ex-boyfriend Sean shows up, the two of them get cold feet. As if this were not bad enough, the party guests are also taken hostage by pirates and everyone's life is in danger. As Darcy and Tom work together to save their beloved families, they rediscover the true reason for their love.

anchor_text: Following the Napoleonic wars, parts of Britain are marked by poverty, unemployment and famine. Against this backdrop, a massive protest movement arose in the Manchester area in 1819. Their main demand is an electoral reform that systematically favours wealthy citizens and allows the poorer sections of the population only a minimal political voice. Finally, the local authorities deploy troops, including a mounted Hussar unit, against a large demonstration. The arrest of the leaders leads to a bloodbath in which many protesters lose their lives.
text_a: In 1863, Peter was a slave on a plantation in Louisiana. After being savagely beaten by a foreman, the man decides to flee north. During the American Civil War, he joined the Union Army against Confederate troops. Photographs of the injuries inflicted on Peter will be published in the press and will serve abolitionists as a symbol of the atrocities committed in the southern states.
text_b: Joseph, a young trumpeter, returns from the Battle of Waterloo to Manchester, where he finds his family and the entire population ruined by the economic crisis and the greed of the landowners. He mounted the protest and in 1819 a large demonstration was held under the leadership of some radical reformers, notably Orator Hunt, demanding electoral reform and the abolition of the Corn Laws. Despite the peaceful nature of the protest, the demonstration is bloody repressed by the army as if it were a riot. The event will go down in history as the Battle of Peterloo, with ironic reference to that of Waterloo.

anchor_text: Hillary O'Neil is a 23-year-old young woman, very beautiful, ambitious but unemployed. She just broke up with her boyfriend who cheated on her. Looking for a job, she responded to a small ad in the newspaper for a job at the home of a person requiring a little experience as a nurse. Going to the address, she entered the home of a very wealthy businessman seeking permanent help for his 28-year-old son, who had been suffering from leukemia for more than a decade. The father refuses the job to the young woman, estimating that she lacks the necessary experience and that she has no recommendations. But the son, Victor, immediately recalls her from the street by his butler as she returns home, immediately reveals her illness, and decides to offer her the job without hiding from her that she will necessarily have to experience extremely trying times in her supporting role, especially during the terrible side effects of chemotherapy. Then begins a poignant story in which the young woman's feelings progress from compassion to a true deep love shared between her and the young man.
text_a: The love story is set in the 1980s against the backdrop of an age-old cinema called the Empire Cinema on the coast of southern England. Its receptionist is lonely Hilary, a middle-aged woman suffering from bipolar disorder. Hilary is being sexually and emotionally abused by the theater owner, Mr. Ellis, and that's not going unnoticed by her co-workers. Still, they're coming to terms with it, as Hilary has become much calmer since Mr. Ellis has been asking her into his office more and more. When Stephen is joined by an eager new employee at the theater, Hilary takes him under her wing and introduces him to the daily chores. In the process, the young man and she become closer. They eventually begin a romantic relationship, though Hilary struggles with an ominous past and her mental health. Stephen, on the other hand, is an Afro-European subject to racism because of the colour of his skin. Both are imbued with uncontrollable aggression and have different plans for the future. Soon, Hilary and Stephen will have to face the fact that their relationship won't last.
text_b: Hilary, a young country girl, when she finds out that her boyfriend is cheating on her, decides to change her life and starts looking for a new job. Presented to an interview with Mr. Richard Geddes, for a job as the personal nurse of his son Victor, she is rejected, but Victor himself will later want to meet her and hire her as a private nurse. The boy, afflicted with a severe form of leukemia, is subject to violent seizures caused by the chemotherapy sessions, but Hilary with her tenacity, holds on and stays by his side, helping and supporting him. Victor is an educated and refined boy, while she is a poor and ignorant girl, but as time passes, the two will grow closer and closer until they fall in love. In fact, they move to a small fishing village where they will spend days of love and tranquility. Unbeknownst to Hilary, Victor has stopped treatment and tries to hide the pain with morphine injections, but the seizures multiply and Hilary, having discovered the deception, calls Mr. Geddes to have Victor admitted to the hospital. Finally, it is she who convinces him to resume treatment, promising to stand by him through thick and thin.

anchor_text: The bachelor Tripp is 35 years old, boat broker and still lives with his parents Sue and Al. He's not interested in long-term relationships with women. As a last resort to get rid of his girlfriends, the womanizer invites them to his home. After that, at the latest, the women don't want anything to do with the nest-hocker. His best friends confirm his lifestyle because they, too, still live with their parents. His parents are fed up with their landlord and want to live their own lives, so they hire the attractive Paula to convince Tripp to move out of his parents' house. Paula is a professional "education assistant": she pretends to men the great love to persuade them to change apartments, and then leaves them. She's being paid by Tripp's parents to do this. Tripp teaches Paula how to sail, and she pretends to be in love with him. Paula actually falls in love with her client and sleeps with him. That creates some problems. Meanwhile, Tripp's boyfriend Ace blackmails Paula and demands that she convince her attractive roommate Kit to go out with him. Kit reluctantly agrees, and the two become a couple. When Tripp learns through his friend Demo that Paula has been hired by his parents, he breaks up with her in an argument and moves out of the house. Paula's depressed and wants to leave town. She wants to move back to Colorado because she's still in love with Tripp, but he wants nothing to do with her. Paula's girlfriend and Tripp's buddies help change her mind and get the two of them back together. At the end, you see the lovers, Tripp and Paula, on a boat.
text_a: Bachelor Fred Williams and his three married friends George, Doug and Howard are commuters from Connecticut who work in Manhattan. The four of them decide to rent an apartment in New York. There's supposed to be an attractive blonde waiting for her. When a newspaper advertisement appears, young and pretty Cathy agrees to the terms. The four friends do not know that Cathy is studying sociology and is working on her doctoral thesis entitled "The Immature Sexual Behavior of the American Male in the Suburban Suburbs", which she wants to deliver to her lecturer, Dr. Prokosch. Prokosch isn't convinced she'll find men willing to work with her. With skillful maneuvering, Cathy can get the men to suppress their sexual urges. Although the nights they spend with Cathy are innocent, the three married men lie to Fred about the course of their nights with Cathy. Fred is jealous and proposes to her, which Cathy accepts. Meanwhile, the three wives have found out what's going on in the apartment through private investigator Bohannon. Angry ladies show up there. Cathy's letting everyone in on her true motivation. Angry Fred wants to disappear, but the women and also his mother Ethel can persuade him to return to the apartment. Fred becomes the fourth member of the group of married men who spend one night a week in the apartment together with their wives.
text_b: Tripp is a successful 35-year-old who still lives with his parents Al and Sue. The exasperated parents would do anything for their son to become independent and leave the maternal nest, and on the advice of a couple of friends, they hire the hot Paula, an expert in such cases, to make that happen. In fact, Paula is convinced that men who do not leave the maternal nest do so because of a lack of self-esteem. The woman then takes care of raising the self-esteem of men through a series of stratagems: Paula acts incognito, making men fall in love with themselves and forcing them to leave the parental home to avoid a fake crisis that is created during the relationship. However, Tripp's profile doesn't match any of the ones Paula had faced up to that point. In fact, Tripp has an intense social life and no self-esteem issues. The reason why a man cannot leave his parents' home is the tragic end of a past relationship. In fact, after the death of Tripp's last girlfriend, the man had found in his parents the only support to move forward. Paula's curiosity soon turns to love for her "client", who also reciprocates her feelings in abundance. However, when Tripp discovers that Paula actually works for his parents, he immediately breaks off the relationship and, offended by Al and Sue, finally decides to move in. Later Tripp will find the strength to forgive his parents, but not Paula. Only through the intervention of Demo, Ace and Kit will reconciliation between the two be possible.

anchor_text: In the suburbs of Vienna, a few years after the end of World War I. Taken by an impression of déjà vu, a man realizes that he is in the café Gluck (corner of the Alserstrasse), renovated since, where he used to meet the bookseller Jacob Mendel, while he was looking for documents to do research on mesmerism. He then learns from the mouth of Madame Sporschil, the old woman of the washbasins, the pitiful end of the man, who in his lifetime was a real living catalogue, capable of recording and retrieving the references of an immeasurable number of books. Too old to have been drafted, and not reading the newspapers, Mendel was unaware of the outbreak of war. Therefore, he continued his correspondence with the enemy countries, complaining, among other things, that he did not receive the catalogues of collections he had requested. Summoned by the secret police, he turns out to be a Russian citizen, and spends much of the war in a concentration camp, before being released because of his high connections. But if this charge is dropped, he will never be able to do his job as before, and he will eventually die penniless. The last work he had in his hands, and which would eventually remain in Mrs. Sporschil's pocket, was the second volume of Hayn's Bibliotheca Germanorum erotica et curiosa.
