The young woman, horrified, soon finds herself drawn into a world where cunning would be the most effective weapon at hand.

anchor_text: After more than 80 years as a journalist, George Seldes became one of the most vocal critics of the U.S. press, questioning its methods and viewing its moral and ethical values as a threat to freedom of information.
text_a: The film looks at the life of controversial journalist George Seldes, known as a muckraker and investigative journalist who fought injustice.
text_b: The film documents Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales' travels around the world promoting free knowledge. It also features interviews with proponents and opponents of Wikipedia. They include Richard Stallman, Larry Sanger and Noam Chomsky.

anchor_text: The plot loosely follows the manga Familiar Strangers by Jirō Taniguchi, who also has a brief guest appearance at the end. The film is about 50-year-old Thomas, who happens to pass by his childhood home and takes the opportunity to visit his mother's grave. There, he jumps back in time 36 years and is suddenly 14 years old again.
text_a: Thomas, a Frenchman in his fifties, passes through the city of his childhood and takes the opportunity to visit his mother's grave. This return to the footsteps of his youth will turn into a leap in time. He will be transported at the time of his fourteenth year, with the appearance he had then.
text_b: The film opens with a pregnant woman telling her unborn baby that his father is now dead. Change of scenery. It begins the love story of 9-year-old Rebecca and 10-year-old Thomas, whom she meets at her grandfather's. But soon after, Rebecca and her mother move to Tokyo. Twelve years later, upon her return, she sets out to find Thomas. When she finally finds him, however, their happiness is short-lived: Thomas dies in a car accident. Desperate at the loss of her great love, Rebecca decides to have Thomas cloned and carry him out herself. But the older Tommy gets, the harder it gets for Rebecca.

anchor_text: It shows the professional and personal lives of seven people in their thirties living in London. Daphne and Frank recently met. Graham and George have been together for a long time and are overcoming the difficulties of their relationship. The pregnant Jules and Will want to get married, but Will allows himself side trips. Billy, Will's friend, can't find a partner, and his relationship with a single mother is a disaster. Eventually, Billy and Jules become a couple. Daphne makes a suicide attempt.
text_a: The film, set in London, tells the stories of a group of thirty-somethings struggling with love. Daphne and Frank met recently. Graham and George have been together for a while and have overcome difficulties within their relationship. Jules and Will want to get married, but Will's cheating on her. Billy, Will's friend, can't find a partner until he meets a young American woman named Dodie.
text_b: Maxime visits his cousin François in the countryside, but he had to leave and it is his partner, Daphne, who is three months pregnant, who welcomes him. For four days, Maxime and Daphne get to know each other as they tell each other their recent love stories with many twists and turns.

anchor_text: Commissioner Montalbano is investigating the death of a high-ranking Sicilian politician who apparently died of heart failure while visiting an open-air brothel. At first, however, Montalbano does not believe in a natural cause of death and in the course of the investigation finds evidence of the involvement of a young Swedish woman, Ingrid Sjöström, in the matter. However, it turns out that the evidence of her involvement (the discovery of a piece of jewellery and a bag with her initials on the crime scene) was fabricated in order to link her father-in-law, also a politician, to the crime. Although Montalbano is able to solve the case and find out that the deceased died of natural causes (namely in a sexual adventure with his nephew) as well as revealing the involvement of a political companion in the affair, his investigation has no judicial repercussions. Upon learning of the plot to kill his beloved uncle, the nephew murders the man responsible and then takes his own life. The title of the book refers to a story from the childhood of the widow of the deceased. This one tells Montalbano of a childhood friend who once asked her about the "shape of water". Their answer was that water has no form of its own, but always takes the form you give it. Through this parable, she expresses her belief in the involvement of her husband's political opponents, who try to use the circumstances of his death for their own purposes.
text_a: Pino Catalano and Saro Montaperto, two geometricians who by necessity do the work of munnizzari in a notorious area, the mànnara, a usual place of meeting of prostitutes, find inside an automobile the corpse of a man with untidy clothes that they know well, being a well-known local political figure: the engineer Luparello. One of the two, Saro, later finds a valuable necklace that he hides because he promises to sell it to raise money to cure his sick son. They think, in order to win their favours, to warn by telephone, even before the police, the lawyer Rizzo, friend and political adviser of the deceased engineer and his foreseeable successor to the post of political secretary: this one, however, strangely does not seem surprised by what the two report to him but simply invites them to call the police. Arriving on the scene, Commissioner Montalbano receives confirmation from the grumpy medical examiner Pasquano that Luparello's death occurred during a sexual encounter. A handbag with Ingrid Sjöström's initials was found at the scene, and the necklace found by the cleaner, as the Commissioner will know, also belongs to her. The murder of the engineer, because Montalbano is convinced that this is the case, has been constructed in such a way that it assumes a particular form, like that of water in a container, so as to make the responsibility fall on the beautiful Swede whose in the course of the investigation the commissioner, heroically resisting the reckless profferings of the young woman to remain faithful to his fiancée Livia, becomes a friend but not a lover. In the end, it will be the two women who make Montalbano solve the intricate case.
text_b: The body of a transporter named Albini is found hanging from a crane in the port of Genoa. Commissioner Nicola Sironi, separated from his wife and hated by his son, a young militant of the radical left, begins to investigate and discovers a price control in the wholesale market of the Ligurian capital exercised by the mafia that intimidates reluctant retailers. The commissioner's trying to find witnesses, but to no avail. Sironi soon comes into contact with Pino Mancinelli, an old acquaintance from the prostitution world, but Mancinelli prefers not to say anything for fear of reprisals against him. Nevertheless, Sironi is able to extract valuable information from him by means of trickery. Mancinelli, considered a collaborator, is executed by Mafia killers. The next murder is that of a winegrower who reacted by filing a complaint with the police after mafia men threw a bomb in his store. But some languages are starting to come apart. Sironi thus discovers the identity of the harbour master and the city's mafia: the industrialist and newspaper owner Pier Paolo Brera. However, the systematic disappearance of probable witnesses and the corruption within the police itself make the commissioner's task insurmountable. Deputy Commissioner Martin, with whom Sironi has been working since the beginning of the investigation, eventually regrets his dishonesty and proposes to plot a trap to bring down the leaders of the mafia through intercepted and recorded conversations. He will be murdered. Sironi then realized that he could no longer act according to the law and decided to take justice into his own hands.

anchor_text: The film is set in Czechoslovakia in the early 1950s. Karel Cernik runs a circus that has been in the Cernik family for generations. But since the Communists took power in Czechoslovakia, the circus has taken a turn for the worse. Although Cernik is still allowed to run the circus privately, he is exposed to the harassment of those in power. Cernik has come down to the brink of ruin. Now Cernik is forced to hand over the circus to the state, but to continue running it. The circus continues to tour the country. Stripped of his freedom, he must watch as his daughter Tereza moves away from him. She is in love with lion tamer Joe Vosdek. Cernik's young wife, Zama, considers her husband a coward who yields unresistingly to the power of the state. But Karel Cernik has a plan, which is jeopardized by spies inside the company. First, he suspects that Joe Vosdek is the spy. Then it turns out that Krofta is the informant for the state policeman Fesker. When the circus on its tour comes close to the German border, Cernik wants to put his plan into action. From a circus parade, he leads the entire circus across a border bridge to freedom. Cernik escapes, but is shot by Krofta, who in turn is shot by a circus dwarf. However, under the leadership of Joe Vosdek, the circus is set free, and Cernik's wife, Zama, must realize that her husband was a hero.
text_a: In 1952 Czechoslovakia, circus man Karel Černík struggles to hold together his beloved Cirkus Černík, which belonged to his family before it was nationalized by the communist government. The government allows Černík to run the circus, but he deals with the deteriorating conditions at the circus, the loss of his workers in the state and the tension with his daughter Tereza and his young second wife Zama, whom everyone suspects of being unfaithful to him. Černík wants to end a budding romance between Tereza and Joe Vosdek, who has only been in the circus for a year. Černík is interrogated at the headquarters of the S.N.B. state security in Pilzen, about why he is not showing in his circus the acts of Marxist propaganda imposed by the government. Černík explains that the sketches were not funny, and that the audience prefers his usual act. The head of the S.N.B. orders you to resume the required act and dismiss a veteran member of the circus who calls herself "The Duchess". Propaganda Minister Fesker casually asks him about a radio in his trailer, alerting Černík that there is a spy among them. Černík is fined and released, although Fesker believes he is a threat to the state. Černík, inspired by a recent escape, decides to escape by crossing the Bavarian border.
text_b: The story takes place in the 1930s, in the Tetrallini circus on tour across Europe. Hans, a person with dwarfism (pronounced lilliputian ), illusionist, engaged to the equestrian Frieda, also a dwarf, falls in love with the tall and beautiful Cleopatra, the trapeze artist. At first, amused, she gently mocks him, accepting his advances and especially his gifts, under Frieda's jealous and helpless eye. Meanwhile, Cleopatra secretly cultivates her relationship with the handsome and strong Hercules, the muscle man of the circus. So when they learn that Hans has inherited a fortune, what was just a gamble turns into a Machiavellian plan. The circus troupe, made up of people with various deformities, victims of the mockery of the beautiful Cleopatra, will revolt and eventually take revenge.

anchor_text: In 1971, David Bowie, along with publicist Rob Oberman, embarked on a transformative journey across the United States. There are moments on this journey that inspire Bowie to reinvent himself, to truly become himself, his iconic alter ego Ziggy Stardust.
text_a: A nuclear physicist, Armstrong, breaks up with his fiancée and assistant, Sarah, before allegedly going to Stockholm. Intrigued, she follows him and discovers that he is actually going to East Berlin. She follows him, he's furious to see her on his heels. Armstrong seems to want to make his discoveries available to the East Germans.
text_b: In 1971, David Bowie was at a turning point in his career. While making his first trip to the United States, he met his publicist for Mercury Records, Ron Oberman. The latter urges him to imagine a character. It will therefore be Ziggy Stardust, which the singer uses notably for the album The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars.

anchor_text: Madame de Dumeval, the Duke of Thesis and the Duke of Wand, libertines expelled from the puritan court of Louis XVI, seek the support of the legendary Duke of Walchen, seducer and free-thinking German, isolated in a country where hypocrisy and false virtue reign. Their mission: to export to Germany libertinism, the philosophy of the Enlightenment based on the rejection of morality and authority, but also, and above all, to find a safe place to continue their deviant games.
text_a: The film takes place in 18th century France. Madame de La Pommeraye, a young and beautiful widow, who is proud of not having been in love, ends up giving in to the advances of the Marquis of Arcis, considered a libertine, who courts her diligently. After a few happy years, she discovers that he has grown tired of her little by little. Hurt and wounded in her pride, she takes revenge by humiliating the marquis and leads her to marry Mademoiselle de Joncquières, with whom he has fallen in love, but who does not know that she and her mother have become prostitutes as a result of a deception by the mother's husband. The Marquis, more honest than fickle, finally assumes the situation and keeps his new wife with him.
text_b: In 1774, in a forest somewhere between Potsdam and Berlin, three French aristocrats, Madame de Dumeval, the Duke de Tesis and the Duke de Wand, libertines expelled from the court of Louis XVI, lose themselves at night in search of the Duke of Walchen, legendary seducer and free-thinking German: they will decide to give vent there on the spot to their perversions.

anchor_text: In 1774, a few years before the French Revolution, Madame de Dumeval, the Duke of Tesis and the Duke of Wand, Libertines who had to leave the puritanical court of Louis XVI, seek refuge with the Duke de Walchen. The freethinker lives somewhere between Potsdam and Berlin, and in his country there is hypocrisy and false virtues. He and his friends experience sadistic pleasures in games of pain, torture, humiliation, hoping to achieve a form of erotic nirvana.
text_a: In 1774, in a forest somewhere between Potsdam and Berlin, three French aristocrats, Madame de Dumeval, the Duke de Tesis and the Duke de Wand, libertines expelled from the court of Louis XVI, lose themselves at night in search of the Duke of Walchen, legendary seducer and free-thinking German: they will decide to give vent there on the spot to their perversions.
text_b: The film takes place in 18th century France. Madame de La Pommeraye, a young and beautiful widow, who is proud of not having been in love, ends up giving in to the advances of the Marquis of Arcis, considered a libertine, who courts her diligently. After a few happy years, she discovers that he has grown tired of her little by little. Hurt and wounded in her pride, she takes revenge by humiliating the marquis and leads her to marry Mademoiselle de Joncquières, with whom he has fallen in love, but who does not know that she and her mother have become prostitutes as a result of a deception by the mother's husband. The Marquis, more honest than fickle, finally assumes the situation and keeps his new wife with him.

anchor_text: The central figure of the plot is psychology professor Peter Boyd, whose reputation suffers from the fact that his father was in prison. That's why he's trying to teach a chimp human morality. He wants to prove that it's the environment that shapes a man's personality, not heredity. To do this, he hires a woman, Jane Linden, to be the mother of the chimpanzees. He himself assumes the role of the father and uses the parenting methods of the 1950s. When the monkey is accused of stealing the painting, the professor almost goes to jail.
text_a: Peter Jones, an English diplomat in France, learns from a private detective that his wife Margaret goes to an apartment every day. Jones is not shocked (he has a lover too) but soon discovers that his rival is Max, a chimpanzee. Then a zoologist, a psychiatrist, a prostitute intervene: the story will end with an unusual threesome.
text_b: Valerie, the daughter of a university dean, is engaged to Peter, a professor of psychology and a colleague of the dean. When the dean discovers that Peter is the son of a former criminal, he forbids the marriage, declaring that Peter's blood is tainted, in line with his firm belief in heredity as an influence on character. As Peter believes just as firmly in the opposite environmental theory, he sets out to prove that he can raise a chimpanzee as you would raise a human child in a law-abiding home.

anchor_text: It's the story of a professor, Peter Boyd, who conducts secret experiments to prove a theory he approves. To do this, the professor will use the chimpanzee department of his faculty.
text_a: Bonzo, a small chimpanzee, is raised as a baby by Peter Boyd, a psychology professor whose father is a notorious thief, to prove that environment determines character more than heredity.
text_b: Almost nothing is known about its contents. At the center of the plot is an extremely jealous artist who wears a monkey costume and kills all the men who get too close to his wife, an artist.

anchor_text: Eleven-year-old François lost his parents in an accident and withdraws into himself. Also, he hasn't been in a car since the tragic incident. He lives with his aunt Florence and his uncle Philippe in the country. One day, the young Englishwoman Wendy Sinclair shows up, the ex-girlfriend of his beautiful cousin Olivier. Her refreshing manner brings the boy out of his isolation. He secretly takes a bottle of perfume from the young woman and cuts off a lock of her hair while she sleeps. A friendship develops between the two, which even gives François the courage to get into a car. Wendy promises to take him to London. But then she leaves without fulfilling her promise. François is retreating back into his world. He pours the perfume on his face.
text_a: After surviving a terrible car accident that claimed the lives of his parents, eleven-year-old François is taken in by his uncle and aunt in their country home and locks himself confusedly into a world of loneliness and rather morbid daydreaming. But the arrival of a beautiful young English woman, Wendy Sinclair, who is also the mistress of the owner of the house, abruptly changes the situation: an unexpected complicity develops between the child and the young woman that will gradually free the former from her isolation and the latter from the grip of men who covet her.
text_b: A young widow, finding her sixteen-year-old prom notebook, searches for what became of her knights twenty years ago. From disappointment to denial, she realizes it's best to let the past go. But just as she may be about to give in to despair, she learns that the one boy she may have truly loved, Gerard, lives not far from her. And that he just died. She's going to take Jacques, her 16-year-old son, under her wing, make him her godfather and... take him to his first ball.

anchor_text: Chauvel's film uses introductory, staged scenes depicting the mutiny, followed by anthropological-style documentaries about the descendants of the mutineers on Pitcairn Island. Chauvel also used footage of Polynesian dancers and footage of a submarine he had filmed with a glass-bottomed boat, which he assumed was the Bounty, but probably wasn't. This was Chauvel's first "talking film", and he clearly hadn't learned to work with actors at that point: the dialogue is very rigid and amateurish. The use of long sections of voice-over documentaries combined with staged scenes resembles the mixed forms of silent and sound films produced during the transition to sound. This is also in line with the interests of the director, who returned to documentary film towards the end of his career with the BBC television series Walkabout. Despite the poorly written dialogue, the documentary sections retain their excellent quality. The return to sequences at the end of the film with a modern scene in which a child suffers because there are no regular ship visits that could have taken the child to the hospital should probably make the film a useful mouthpiece for the Pitcairn Island community who generously contributed to the film. The film mixed reenactments with documentary footage and focused less on the mutiny itself and more on its aftermath.
text_a: Based on Chile's relationship with water, the film evokes Chilean historical episodes such as the story of the Patagonian Indians (Kawesqar, Tehuelches, Selknam, Mánekenk, Yagan) who were massacred, deported, decultured and of whom only a few descendants remain today, and the September 11, 1973 coup d'état and the subsequent repression during the military regime of Augusto Pinochet. The film offers an intriguing journey on the theme of water, in the manner of Gaston Bachelard (Water and Dreams, 1942), or in the manner of Chris Marker, but it is to better evoke two episodes founding Chile today, the disappearance, hidden and forgotten, of indigenous peoples, and the most noisy of the lead years of 1973-1990. It was no doubt for this unconventional script, not corresponding to the conventions of narrative cinema, that the film received the prize for the script in Berlin. The following list attempts to highlight the various sub-themes covered:
text_b: Chauvel's film uses staged introductory scenes showing the mutiny, followed by an anthropological-style documentary footage of the descendants of the mutineers on Pitcairn Island. This was Chauvel's first sound and clearly at this stage he was not yet very familiar with directing actors: the dialogue is rigid and amateurish. The use of long sections of documentary footage with a voice-over, combined with acted scenes, is similar to the hybrid and talking images that were produced during the transition to sound. It also represents the combination of the director's interests returning to documentary towards the end of his career with the BBC television series Walkabout. Despite the poorly written dialogue, the documentary sections retain their quality. The film, mixing re-enactments with documentary footage, focused less on the mutiny itself and more on its aftermath.

anchor_text: Fresh out of a mental asylum, Charlie takes his daughter Miranda on a search for a treasure hidden by the Spaniard Juan Garces during his exploration of California. After several weeks of searching, it is found buried under a supermarket. Nothing will stop Charlie from getting it back...
text_a: After two years in a mental institution, Charlie returns to his life. Waiting for him is his daughter Miranda, whose quiet life is disrupted by her father's antics, on top of his obsession with a buried treasure that the man believes lies beneath a shopping mall. Involved in her father's adventure against her will, Miranda will get to know him better and finally establish a relationship after years of distance.
text_b: Charlie (Dane Cook) suffered as a child a curse that has now become for some a "gift". After sleeping with a woman, she always finds her true love in the next man and marries him. So Charlie, nicknamed "Lucky Chuck", is sought after by a multitude of single women. But when Charlie meets the woman of his dreams, Cam (Jessica Alba), he has a "little problem": he can't sleep with her because if he does she will find another man and lose her.

anchor_text: The reclusive Adrian Lester is leaving New York for the holidays to return to his old home in Texas. The man, in his mid-20s, hasn't been to Fort Worth in three years and wants to use Christmas to tell his family he's HIV-positive. His mother, Eileen, almost overwhelms him with attention and affection, while his younger brother, Andrew, distances himself.
text_a: In present-day Vienna, Alex is an ex-con who works for a brothel owner planning to escape with his girlfriend, a beautiful young Ukrainian prostitute. The man's dream was to rob a suburban bank, turning his life around. But everything goes wrong, because of an accidental death by a policeman. Alex gets the loot, but in exchange for that product of a gunshot, the young woman dies. Heartbroken, Alex takes refuge on his grandfather's farm, not far from the crime scene. As the days go by, he gets deeper and deeper into the life of the cop and his wife, as his obsession with revenge for the crime grows.
text_b: This is Texas, 1985. During the Christmas holidays, Adrian, a successful New York advertising executive, returns to his hometown after a three-year absence, reuniting with his younger brother Andrew and his very religious parents. In reality, his visit is a final farewell, in which Adrian will have to settle many outstanding accounts with his family, before telling them that he is dying of AIDS.

anchor_text: The first part of the film presents in the manner of a documentary the effects of the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki on the Japanese population; the narrators of this chapter are the protagonists who have not yet been introduced, and who are exposed in the second part of the film. A French actress travels to Hiroshima to make a film about peace. There she meets a Japanese architect, a former conscript whose family disappeared in the bombings, who briefly becomes her lover, but also her confidant. As they prepare to part, he exposes his life to her and repeats to her that she didn't see anything in Hiroshima. She told him about her adolescence in Nevers during World War II, her love for a German soldier and the humiliation she suffered at the Liberation of France when she was shaved. Their exchanges on the themes of memory and forgetting are presented to us in fragments throughout the 36 hours of the digestion.
text_a: The film opens in Hiroshima on August 6, 1945. People are going to work. Suddenly, a white lightning flashed across the sky. His light is accompanied by a terrible blast and hell is unleashed. There are mutilated ghosts wandering through the piles of ruins. The film focuses on Yasuko. At the time of the explosion, the girl was on a boat on her way to her uncle's house. A black rain falls on the sea and on the passengers. Those who have been touched by this rain, contaminated, do not yet know that they have been irradiated. A few years later, Yasuko lived in the country with her uncle and aunt. The war is over, life has returned to normal. And it's in an atmosphere of serene, soothing nature that death slowly settles in. Yasuko can't seem to find anyone to marry. We fear his illness. The victims of the bomb became the hibakusha. Not only did they suffer physically, in the absence of adequate treatment, but they were also ashamed of having been defeated. Suicides have been very high. Yuichi, a traumatized ex-soldier, is the only being she could share her existence with.
text_b: After shooting a pacifist film in Hiroshima, twelve years after the end of the Second World War, a young French actress spends her last night in a hotel in the company of a Japanese architect. The two are strangers, but what could be a fleeting one-night stand turns into an intense romance. In the wake of this experience, she recalls an impossible love she experienced in Nevers years earlier, when she fell in love with a German soldier in the same war conflict. The love relationship between the actress and the young Japanese man then becomes an introspective process through which she reconstructs her past and reveals her most intimate feelings to her new partner.

anchor_text: The story takes place in the northern United States in the state of Michigan in the small town of Hortons Bay. Liz Coates works in the kitchen of a small diner and has a crush on Canadian immigrant Jim Gilmore, who recently bought a local blacksmith shop. She falls in love with his looks and all the things that make him special to her, and she wishes he would talk to her. One evening Gilmore and Liz's employer DJ Smith go hunting and when they return in the evening, they drink together to celebrate their hunting success. When Liz and Gilmore are alone, Gilmore attacks her by groping her and openly declaring his interest in the young woman. They go for a walk together and finally have intercourse at a fishing pier. Whether this is a rape has been hotly debated in the literary world since the story was first published, because despite her initial infatuation with Gilmore, she tries to stop him and tells him that he shouldn't, but despite her pleas, Gilmore continues. After it happens, he falls asleep and she starts crying because she wants to go home. Finally, she covers him with her jacket and goes home alone.
text_a: The story is told from the perspectives of three women: Rachel, Megan, and Anna. Rachel Watson, 32, is a London woman with serious alcoholism problems, caused by the fact that she cannot accept the end of her marriage to Tom, especially since the latter has married Anna, her lover since before they broke up. The result of this union is also the birth of a girl, Evie, an event that sharpens Rachel's pain that she had not been able to have children. Due to her alcoholism she has recently been fired, however she continues to take the train every morning, pretending to go to work to save appearances with her friend Cathy, who hosts her in her home. During his daily train journeys, to escape boredom and loneliness, he observes people's lives through the window. His attention is focused on a couple whose house he passes by every day and to whom he gives the fictitious names of Jess and Jason, idealizing them as the "perfect couple". Their real names are Megan and Scott Hipwell, and their existence isn't as perfect as it seems. One day Rachel sees Megan through the window with another man and a few days later the woman disappears into thin air. The night of Megan's disappearance coincides with the night that Rachel raised her elbow too high and was injured, moments of which she has only vague and hazy memories. Thinking she's somehow involved in Megan's disappearance, Rachel begins investigating on her own, uncovering a baffling truth.
text_b: Jim Gilmor, newly arrived in Horton Bay, manages to buy the blacksmith's shop and open his own business; Liz Coates is a young woman who works as a waitress for the Smiths and has a crush on Jim. Meanwhile, along with DJ Smith and Charley Wyman, Jim sets off on a deer hunt. After dinner at Smith's, Jim goes to the kitchen and, after petting Liz a bit, asks her out. So they go for a late-night walk together towards the end of the pier and there, in the evening breeze, the young man begins to explore the girl's body with his hands: she is frightened and begs him to stop. Jim rapes her and then falls asleep; Liz, unconscious, tries to wake him up by dragging him a little but then, seeing that it is all useless, leaves him there after carefully covering him with her coat. The girl, sad and cold, returns home alone to go to bed.

anchor_text: Ned Fleming is a father of two and the owner of the Fleming Company printing company in Grand Rapids, Michigan. Along with his wife, Barb, and 15-year-old son, Scotty, he plans to visit his daughter, Stephanie, who is studying at Stanford University. When the family arrives, they are surprised to find that Stephanie is dating a young millionaire entrepreneur named Laird Mayhew. This one soon turns out to be completely boorish and impudent, which causes Ned to develop a veritable hatred for him. Nor can Stephanie's insistence that Laird is basically a nice person and that he makes her happy change Ned's mind. Then, when Laird asks Ned for his daughter's hand in marriage, Ned's hopes are dashed and he goes to great lengths to prevent the wedding. Laird, in turn, tries to impress his potential father-in-law by any means necessary so that he can still marry Stephanie. He's buying Ned's printing business so he can pay off the debts he's accumulated. On Christmas Eve, he gets Ned's consent to marry Stephanie. She does not accept Laird's proposal, but wants to continue to meet with him. The print shop is being transformed into a very successful plumbing factory that is involved in Third World projects.
text_a: Ned Fleming (Bryan Cranston) and his wife Barb (Megan Mullally), along with their 15-year-old son Scott (Griffin Gluck), come to visit their eldest daughter, Stephanie (Zoey Deutch), where she introduces them to her boyfriend, Laird Mayhew (James Franco), a rich and famous young man whose use of profanity, bubbly personality, and honesty about personal matters are a bit overwhelming for Barb and Scott, and are the causes of Ned's disdain for him, though Stephanie insists he is a good person and makes her happy. But when Laird reveals that his plan is to propose to Stephanie in the next five days and asks for a chance to prove himself worthy of her love so that Ned can give them his blessing, Laird goes out of his way to win over Barb and Scott, while Ned tries to make sure Laird doesn't.
text_b: Ned Merrill, a theatrical impresario, visits a friendly family, swims in their pool, and decides to walk back to his villa in a bathing suit, bathing in all the pools he encounters along the way. At the beginning of the film, Ned appears as a successful man: rich, respected and a happy family man. He is accompanied on the journey by Julie Ann, a young woman who feels a filial affection for him; but when Ned tries to kiss her, the girl runs away. From that moment on, the film takes on a darker tone and the bitter truths that Ned has removed emerge: the man is full of debts and is despised by neighbors and his own family. Ned has a painful encounter with Shirley, a woman with whom he has had an affair and who confronts him with his selfishness and hypocrisy; he finally reaches, while a torrential rain is unleashed, his villa and finds it closed and deserted for a long time.

anchor_text: Florence Fallon is the daughter of an elderly itinerant preacher. She opposes the bigotry and narrow-mindedness of his denomination, which prefers a young, dynamic priest to an experienced man. The faithful flee in panic, but Bob Hornsby senses a deal. He convinces Florence to profit from her ability to speak. She soon becomes Sister Fallon, who, in her temple of bliss, performs alleged miracles in front of the numerous poor and helpless and receives ample donations in return. One day, she meets John Carson, a former pilot who has been blind since a plane crash. Desperate Carson was about to commit suicide when Sister Fallon's radio broadcast brought him back to life. He attends one of Florence's sermons and is initially skeptical, especially when Sister Fallon speaks to the people from a lion's cage. Later, the two meet and Florence falls in love with the shy young man who is only able to express his feelings with the help of a ventriloquist doll. After several entanglements, Sister Fallon finally confesses her deceptions and finds happiness and contentment in John's arms.
text_a: Florence Marr is an aspiring singer who occasionally performs in some club, supported by her friend Gina. He currently works as a handyman for the Greenberg family in the Hollywood Hills, to whom he devotes himself entirely. Florence stays home alone when Phillip Greenberg's family with his wife and children go on vacation to Vietnam. Free from the obligations of the Greenberg family, you can think a little more about your personal life. Phillip's brother, Roger, soon arrives at the Greenberg home, in the midst of an existential crisis. He moves from New York to Los Angeles to fix some things at his brother's house, as he is a carpenter. Roger's a 40-year-old single guy who's a little lost. With this lifestyle, he approaches Florence, with whom he begins a sexual relationship. All intertwined with reuniting with his old friends who now have lives of their own, particularly Ivan Schrank his former bandmate and his ex, Beth. The story continues with a growing relationship between Roger and Florence, in which there are quarrels and misunderstandings, but also confessions and tenderness. In the meantime, several situations are articulated, including the illness of Mahler, the family dog, the hospitalization of Florence who has an abortion after discovering that she is pregnant with her ex-boyfriend, and a party at Greenberg's house, organized by Sara and a friend of hers. The day after the party the girls have to leave for Australia and they convince Roger to go with them, but at the last he remembers Florence and comes back to pick her up from the hospital.
text_b: Florence Fallon, a preacher's daughter, just lost her father. At the church during the funeral, she climbed into the pulpit and lashed out at the audience for having replaced her father as pastor because of her old age. A swindler, Hornsby, who was present at the scene, convinced her to use her oratorical gifts to preach the gospel in a house of worship. Florence entrusted Hornsby with the financial side of the business, and each evening the preacher drew more and more worshipers to a temple. The house of worship becomes a real show, Florence even delivers her message in a cage surrounded by beasts and performs so-called miracles in front of an ecstatic crowd. It's a very lucrative business, especially for Hornsby. Later, she met a young blind man named John Carson, who was saved from suicide by hearing Florence on the radio. They fall in love and decide to run away to Monte Carlo. Hornsby, save face by explaining that the preacher went on a pilgrimage. Upon returning, Florence is determined to stop the show by revealing that everything is done only to bring back money. Hornsby, determined to prevent Florence from revealing the deception, causes a short circuit on the night of the revelations to plunge the room into darkness but a fire breaks out and Hornsby dies in the flames. Florence and John will leave together.

anchor_text: Robin Cavendish, a brilliant and adventurous man, sees his life take a tragic turn when he is paralyzed by polio at the age of 28. However, he will be able to count on the unfailing support of his devoted wife, Diana.
text_a: The official synopsis as released by Apple TV+ is as follows: After years in the spotlight, Selena Gomez has achieved phenomenal stardom. But after this sudden ascent, an unexpected event plunged him into darkness. This authentic and intimate documentary chronicles her six-year journey to find the light.
text_b: Robin Cavendish, who was given just three months to live after being paralyzed from the neck down by polio at the age of 28, becomes a pioneer in disability rights advocacy. He and his beloved wife, Diana Blacker, have traveled the world for many years hoping to make a positive difference in the lives of others like him.

anchor_text: Phileas Fogg is a London gentleman, an elegant person, extremely precise and habitual. One day he is involved in a bet by some members of the Reform Club, a gentlemen's club he attends, who are discussing the possibility of making a trip around the world in just eighty days. Fogg accepts the bet, whose stake is £20,000, and leaves immediately, accompanied by his butler Passepartout. During the journey, full of surprises and setbacks, the two will use the most varied means of transport (trains, steamboats, hot air balloons, pirogues and elephants) and will be involved in lively adventures, such as the rescue of the Indian princess Aouda, who will follow Fogg to London and become his wife. Throughout the journey, Fogg is pursued by Detective Fix, who believes him to be responsible for a robbery at the Bank of England. The investigator arrests him and holds him for a day, which was not on the schedule; Fogg then believes he has lost the bet. But by always traveling eastward, he gained a day over the expected eighty days, enabling him to show up at the clubhouse at the perfect time and win the bet.
text_a: In 1871 Phileas Fogg, a true English gentleman, has made a bet with his 4 friends at the Reform Club, assuring them that he is able to circumnavigate the world in 80 days. He's bet £20,000 that he'll get it in such a short time while his skeptical friends have bet £5,000 each against him. Meanwhile, a thief has stolen £55,000 from the Bank of England and the police think it's Phileas Fogg because he looks like him. So they come for him as he makes his journey with his servant Picaporte. Finally, after many adventures and having cleared up the misunderstanding with the police, who later discovered that it was someone else, Fogg manages to win the bet and also gets Princess Aouda, whom he met along the way, as his future wife.
text_b: Two friends, Arthur and Franz, lovers of the easy life and unlikely to look for a job, roam around Paris and its surroundings in their SIMCA convertible. One day they raid a house on the outskirts of Paris where they plan to carry out a robbery. In fact, Odile, a classmate of theirs in an English class for adults, overlooked the fact that her aunt Victoria's lodger, the landlady, has a large sum of cash hidden in the attic. To convince Odile to let them into her aunt's house, the two court her in turns, passing her love notes and taking her dancing; when they decide which of them will have to spend the night with the girl, she cheats so that Arthur wins. Arthur has a fight with his evil cousin, who wants to take part in the heist. Finally Odile gives in and takes the friends home, they tie her up so as not to compromise her, but they cannot go upstairs because the door is closed. They then try the coup again the next day, this time tying and gagging Mrs. Victoria, but the money is nowhere to be found. Arthur searches the house until he finds the trash can in the doghouse. He overtakes his cousin, causing a shootout in which both he and Arthur are killed. Convinced that the aunt has died asphyxiated by gagging (in reality she has only lost consciousness), Odile and Franz escape without loot, and decide to emigrate to South America.

anchor_text: English gentleman Phileas Fogg bet members of his Reform Club that he could circumnavigate the globe in 80 days. He leaves at once, taking with him Passepartout, his French valet, former clown and man of means, and carrying, as luggage, a bag full of banknotes. Various means of transport (balloon, boat, train, elephant, etc.) It allows them to go through a very long series of stages: Paris, Spain, the Suez Canal, Calcutta, Hong Kong, Yokohama, San Francisco, New York and London. From Suez, Fix, a policeman, suspects him of robbing the Bank of England. The two heroes arrive on the coast of England to catch the train to London when Fix has Fogg imprisoned for a few hours, but later admits his mistake. Fogg thinks he's lost his bet. But Parrot realizes that they arrived on time, on a Saturday, having gained 24 hours thanks to the change of date line. Fogg arrives on time at the Reform Club in front of the stunned members.

anchor_text: Boyd Mitchell and his wife have to spend Christmas with his family that he hasn't seen in a very long time. Realizing that he has left all of his son's gifts at home, he sets off with his father in an attempt to make the round trip before sunrise. But not all is going to go as planned since he's been angry with his father for many years.
text_a: Boyd Mitchler has to spend Christmas with his misfit family. Realizing that she has left all of her son's gifts at home, she sets off on the road with her father and brother in an attempt to make the 8-hour round trip before dawn.
text_b: Fred Claus (Vince Vaughn) and Nicholas Santa Claus (Paul Giamatti) have been brothers for many years. In theory, Fred is arrogant, bitter and unlucky and Nicholas is kind, honest and affectionate. Now Fred must forget his differences with his brother, who believes blindly and takes care of him; an efficiency expert (Kevin Spacey) advises the work at the North Pole and does everything to leave out Santa Claus, Fred will do his best to help his brother save Christmas.

anchor_text: Mary Dwight Stauber works as a nightclub hostess for the notorious gangster Johnny Vanning. She befriends Ralph Krawford, who confesses to her that he doesn't have the money to pay off his gambling debts. Mary warns him that he is in great danger. She is shocked, but not surprised, after learning shortly afterwards that Ralph has been murdered by Charlie Delaney, an ally of Vanning. On the advice of District Attorney David Graham, Mary and the other women have refused to press charges against Vanning. They fear his vengeance and are powerless to free themselves from his influence. Mary's younger sister Betty is coming to visit. Unaware of the dangerous situation in which she finds herself, she acts recklessly against the advice of her older sister. When she's killed, Mary agrees to testify against Vanning. She is beaten and disfigured by his thugs, as the title of the film (Marked Woman) suggests. Nevertheless, this strengthens her resolve to testify. Since the other women know that they can only live freely if they also testify, they agree with Mary and also testify against Vanning.
text_a: The film tells the tragic story of brave high school students who, after consuming cannabis, rape, drive irresponsibly, commit suicide and go insane. The film is about Mae Coleman and Jack Perry, among others, who sell marijuana to children and teenagers. Bill Harper and Jimmy Lane visit Jack, who gives Jimmy a joint. This causes Jimmy to drive away in his car shortly afterwards, picking up a pedestrian, causing him to commit hit-and-run, while the pedestrian dies shortly afterwards. At first, though, this crime seems to have no consequences for Jimmy. Bill begins an affair with Blanche, while Mary, Jimmy's sister, is offered a marijuana cigarette by Ralph Wiley, a mentally ill dealer. A short time later, Ralph attempts to brutally rape her. At this moment, Bill comes out of the bedroom after drug-enhanced, promiscuous intercourse with Blanche and grabs Ralph. A fight breaks out, resulting in a gunshot that kills Mary. Jack puts the gun in the hand of Bill, who is drowning in marijuana, and wakes him, who believes he killed Mary. He's put on trial, and even though Ralph tries to testify on his behalf, he's convicted because Ralph's marijuana use has made him insane. In the process, Jack, driven by marijuana-fueled hatred, intends to murder Ralph so he can't reveal his guilt. When he visits him, however, Ralph gains the upper hand and beats him to death in a marijuana frenzy. When Ralph, Mae, and Blanche are arrested, Blanche jumps out of a window and kills herself. Ralph will be locked away as a criminally insane for the rest of his life, while Mae's future life will not be considered. Finally, the words Tell Your Children appear on the screen.
text_b: Mary Dwight Stauber, an entertainer who works with four friends in a club run by gangster Johnny Vanning, meets a young man one night who confides to her, in the taxi that is taking them home, that he does not have the money to repay the gambling debt he has accumulated during the night. When the police recover the body, the girl is charged with murder by District Attorney David Graham, who has been unsuccessfully trying to frame Vanning for a long time, and who hopes that the fear of going to jail will prompt the girl to cooperate. On the contrary, following the instructions of the skilled lawyer hired by the boss, Mary plays a double game and succeeds in discrediting the accusation in the eyes of the court. Although cleared of all charges, the scandal caused Mary to lose the trust of her younger sister Betty, whom she paid for her education and wished a different future for. It will be the tragic death of Betty, who naively tried to follow in the footsteps of her older sister, to make her aware of her mistakes and finally convince her to side with the prosecutor against Vanning. Beaten by Vanning's henchmen and disfigured by a hideous scar, her will will grow strong enough to convince even her friends to testify.

anchor_text: On December 5, 1492, a gang led by former bishop Niklas is killed by villagers who no longer accept the gang's looting and murders. In years when the gang's death date falls on a full moon, they return as murderous ghosts. The public is unaware of this and celebrates the annual Sinterklaas-Fest on December 5th, with adults who don't believe that Sinterklaas exists, but who make the children believe that he exists. The Black Pietes are not black because of their race, but because of the fire in which they were killed. Niklas's crowbar has sharp edges and is a weapon. On December 5, Sinterklaas and the Zwarte Pieten do not stand out because many people are dressed for the festivities and therefore look like them. The last time the ghostly gang came back was in 1968. Hundreds of people were killed, including the entire family of a little boy, Goert, who is now a policeman. The authorities downplayed the incidents, and like the Roman Catholic Church, kept Niklas' possible involvement a secret. When December 5, 2010 is also full moon, Goert is very concerned, and recommends the ban of all Sinterklaas activities and increased surveillance, but he is not taken seriously and sent on leave. As Goert predicted, the ghost gang returns and kills hundreds of people. Since the gang is immune to bullets, it is eventually chased away by fire. Besides Goert, the student Frank also plays a heroic role in the film.
text_a: On December 5, 1492, a band led by the former bishop Nicholas was decimated by villagers who wanted to avenge the plunder they had suffered. From then on, every year when St. Nicholas Day coincides with the full moon, the gang returns, St. Nicholas at its head, to carry out murder and desolation. The last time the Ghost Band came back was 1968. Hundreds of people were killed, including the entire family of a little boy, Goert, who is now a policeman. The authorities downplay the incidents, as does the Catholic Church, which intends to keep the true nature of St. Nicholas a secret. In 2010, as there is a full moon on December 5, Goert is very concerned and recommends banning all activities related to St. Nicholas and increasing surveillance, but he is not taken seriously and is put on official leave. As Goert predicts, the gang reappears and kills hundreds of people, the confusion is at its peak because many townspeople are disguised as St. Nicholas, a young man is thus arrested by mistake but manages to escape, he meets Goert and together they manage to defeat Saint Nicola and his gang by fire.
text_b: A heterogeneous group of young people move in scattered order within the Paris metro, throughout an entire afternoon, according to a plan that does not seem very clear. Some of them immobilise security officers, one of them visits the Minister of the Interior, some of them plant charges, then one of them kills the president of HSBC Europe in cold blood. Finally, at 7:00 p.m. sharp, charges explode simultaneously blowing up a wing of the Ministry of Interior building, a bank in the Defense District, and a row of cars parked in front of the Stock Exchange. In addition, the statue of Joan of Arc in the Place des Pyramides is burned. The common matrix is confirmed by the fact that the children gather together in the same place, a shopping mall in the middle of the city, which they will occupy entirely as soon as the closure is decreed and the security is rendered harmless. The appeal lacks two elements. Fred was killed during a D.O.D. cover-up. Greg, after murdering the minister, ran away armed and won't join the others until later. The nervousness of the wait is broken by late night news footage reporting that they have discovered where the terrorists have taken refuge. Trapped, the youths first improvise a form of defense, then succumb to the onslaught of special forces who mercilessly kill everyone. Among the casualties were two innocent homeless men who were prowling the unattended mall with the complicity of the young rebels.

text_a: In 1910, the fictitious village of Vandorf has for five years witnessed a strange series of murders whose corpses have become stone statues. The discovery of the last victim, the fiancée of the artist Bruno Heitz, drives him to suicide, thus leaving the authorities to believe that he was the culprit. Her father, convinced of her innocence, encounters such hostility from the locals that they attempt to burn his house to the ground. He travels to the ruins of Borski Castle and, faced with the gorgon Megaera, senses that he is gradually turning to stone. Before he dies, he manages to write a last message to his older brother Paul, who, with the help of his mentor, Professor Meister, will try to unravel this mystery at the risk of his life.
text_b: The Netherlands. On the night of December 5th every 36 years, an ancient and hidden legend comes to life and the Amsterdam-Zuid neighborhood is violently sacked by Sinterklaas who reveals himself in search of revenge. Living in the Middle Ages, St. Nicholas in 1492 cursed the world when he was burned alive on his boat by the enraged population after he had fallen from grace, he had put himself at the head of a gang of Zwarte Piet looters and raiders. Today, paying the deadly consequences of its curse is a group of teenagers. But the young Frank manages to escape from an ambush and, with the help of detective Goert (who in 1968 as a child came across the evil bishop but no one ever believed him), will try to put an end to the troubles of the ghost and his servants forever.

anchor_text: At the beginning of the 20th century in Hong Kong, the incorruptible Sergeant Dragon, having put the pirate San Po out of harm's way, is entrusted with the surveillance of the most dangerous district in the province of Hong Kong. Once on the scene, he must stop dangerous criminals but faces the dramatic corruption of the local police, who are trying to get rid of this new overzealous colleague as soon as possible.
text_a: Hong Kong in the 19th century. Dragon Mao has returned after freeing the seas from the dreaded Pirate Lo. Once on the ground, he's assigned to a police station to clean up corruption and crime in the suburbs. In time, he is approached by a group of Chinese patriots seeking supporters for their revolutionary cause. The Chinese government obviously tries to eliminate those who pose an obstacle, even if they are part of the police...
text_b: Tiger and Dragon are two orphans welcomed by a Sifu into his martial arts school. When Tiger betrays the school for money and is kicked out, Dragon decides to go looking for him to redeem him and put him back on the right path. However, Dragon is mistaken for Tiger by the police chief and tries to defend himself with rocambolesque fights first, and escape attempts later. At some point he is found innocent, but to truly free Tiger he will be forced to face the fearsome leader of the bandits in a duel.

anchor_text: Hong Kong in the 19th century. Dragon Mao has returned after freeing the seas from the dreaded Pirate Lo. In time, he is approached by a group of Chinese patriots seeking supporters for their revolutionary cause. The Chinese government obviously tries to eliminate those who pose an obstacle, even if they are part of the police...
text_a: The sequel continues with the pirates on the run, claiming they must kill Dragon Ma to avenge their deceased captain. On the recommendation of the Chief of the Sea Force, Dragon Ma is transferred to be in charge of the Sai Wan district after Superintendent Chun is suspected of being organizing his arrests. Chun, however, has an excellent record and the "criminals" he has been involved with are shot to death, so there is no evidence against him.
text_b: Tiger and Dragon are two orphans welcomed by a Sifu into his martial arts school. When Tiger betrays the school for money and is kicked out, Dragon decides to go looking for him to redeem him and put him back on the right path. However, Dragon is mistaken for Tiger by the police chief and tries to defend himself with rocambolesque fights first, and escape attempts later. At some point he is found innocent, but to truly free Tiger he will be forced to face the fearsome leader of the bandits in a duel.

anchor_text: A criminal clan led by the head of the family Vittorio Manalese, a Sicilian who emigrated to Paris, makes the gangster Roger Sartet escape, who in prison came into possession of the scheme of the security system of a large jewelry exhibition in Rome and proposed a coup to the gang of Sicilians. Vittorio asks for the cooperation of his old friend, boss of Cosa Nostra, Tony Nicosia, but, realizing the impossibility of overcoming the anti-theft system, he has the idea of hijacking the plane that will carry the jewels to transfer the exhibition to New York. Meanwhile, Commissioner Le Goff is on the trail of Sartet, who falls in love with Jeanne, Manalese's daughter-in-law, but the two are discovered by the family. After the success of the coup, the younger members of the clan are arrested and it is the patriarch who executes revenge by killing the two lovers. When Vittorio returns, he finds Commissioner Le Goff waiting for him.
text_a: Commissioner Sarti makes Er Monnezza, a petty thief, escape with the intention of getting help to track down Brescianelli, a dangerous bandit who has kidnapped a seriously ill child. Brescianelli, in addition to being a fugitive, has also changed his connotations with a facial plastic surgery. Er Monnezza, after an initial reluctance, decides to cooperate with justice and calls three of his robber friends, the Calabrese, Er Cinico and Vallelunga, to help him. The search for Brescianelli leads them to several leads, including the one that leads to Mara, Brescianelli's girlfriend, and that of Tunisino, a known drug dealer. Also thanks to the knowledge of the Monk in the underworld, the circle closes and while Monnezza, who pretends to be a shepherd of sheep, finds the den where the child is detained, Brescianelli and his family see it with Commissioner Sarti. The Calabrian is killed in the firefight, while Sarti with the launch of smoke bombs manages to hunt down and kill both the kidnappers and the Cynic himself, who intended to appropriate the ransom money. Sarti saves the girl and takes her to safety, then enters the den and finds the nun injured. Loads it into a car and drives to Rome. Sarti gets out of the car to call a doctor, and when he turns around Monnezza is gone: the man only pretended to be injured, so as not to have to go back to prison.
text_b: Roger Sartet, murderer and thief is released from prison by the clan of old Vittorio Malanese. He and Startet decide to steal the jewels from an exhibition to be held in Rome. The jewels are to be flown from Rome to New York. The coup is a success, Vittorio returns to Paris with his family, while Roger remains in New York. By chance, Vittorio discovers that between Roger and Jeanne, his daughter-in-law, there has been a relationship: The old man brings Roger back and kills him, but both he and his children fall into the hands of Inspector Révert, who had been following him for a long time.

anchor_text: Felicia, a teenager from Ireland, goes to Birmingham to find the boy who got her pregnant. She accepts help from an elderly man who seems friendly but hides heavy secrets.
text_a: Felicia, an Irish teenager, travels to Birmingham in search of the boy who has impregnated her. He accepts help from an older man who seems helpful, but who hides big secrets.
text_b: Colm is a 46-year-old married man who has spent much of his life working as a longshoreman in Dublin. As his life unravels following his father's firing and death, Colm decides to explore his sexuality with a 19-year-old gigolo named Jay.

anchor_text: Husband and wife Lucy and Sam Bell want to have a child. Numerous attempts fail, the doctors cannot help the couple, even the magical rituals of their friend Drusilla prove ineffective. Sam is increasingly dissatisfied with his work as an editor at the BBC. His friend Druscilla gives him the idea to write a comedy about a childless couple. The final impulse comes from his boss when Sam rejects a script that he thinks is idiotic. The boss insults him and mockingly asks him about his own scripts. Lucy doesn't like the idea of the script. She forbids her husband to carry them out. Sam still writes the script, using sentences and phrases he finds in his wife's secretly-read diary. When Lucy visits the hospital where the shooting is taking place, she recognizes some phrases. She leaves her husband and has an affair with the lead actor in the film. The film will be a success. In the end, Lucy and Sam are back together. Sam is trying hard to get his wife pregnant.
text_a: Sam and Lucy, a young couple from London, England, had everything they needed to be happy. They were in love, newly married, and both had successful careers. All they want is a child, but it's a long time coming. Then comes the time of doubts and trials, infertility tests, visits to specialists, and attempts at in vitro fertilization. In this difficult situation, Lucy, until then faithful, begins to notice other men; Sam leaves his job as a producer at the BBC and realizes his dream of becoming a film screenwriter. Out of inspiration, he set out to write a screenplay based on the true story of a couple who want a child but can't.
text_b: The movie starts at the end. Lucy, a journalist in New York, shortly before her marriage to a mysterious man, tells a friend about the events that led her to the altar. The scene changes and the story goes back to the moment when Lucy, after being dumped, decides to go in search of the perfect man. So she gives herself the luxury of dating five different men at the same time so that she can choose the one that suits her best. With each of them Lucy behaves differently: she gets drunk on her first date with Doug, the entomologist; she is unprejudiced in bed with Gabriel, the screenwriter; with Bobby, the baseball player, she brings out a bad character; she spends her first date with Barry, the owner of an online store, also with his parents; and finally with Luke, the doctor, the date takes an embarrassing turn when she discovers that another woman is interested in him. The film continues with Lucy's experiments, discarding some, insisting on others, until one of them wins her over. Who he is is revealed only in the finale, when Lucy gets married and the mystery man's identity is revealed.

anchor_text: As Commissioner Dreyfus had gone mad as a result of Clouseau's misconduct, his subordinate (cf. previous films), he was admitted to a psychiatric clinic. Considered cured, he's about to be released. Unfortunately, a catastrophic visit from Clouseau causes him to slip back into madness and become a dangerous criminal. Dreyfus kidnaps a scientist, inventor of a destructive laser beam, and threatens to use it if Clouseau is not eliminated quickly. Secret agents from every country are tasked by their governments to kill Clouseau.
text_a: Inspector Clouseau's former boss, Charles Dreyfus, goes insane and threatens to destroy the world if the authorities do not hand over Clouseau so that he can get rid of him and end all his nightmares. As a guarantee that he can do it, the UN building disappears. Frightened, several countries send their best assassins to kill Clouseau, but all fail. Finally, Clouseau (accidentally) makes Dreyfus and the castle he inhabited disappear.
text_b: Having survived sixteen assassination attempts, Inspector Clouseau has become a legend. The French Connection, to show its power to its customers, is seeking to eliminate this inspector. He's an escaped felon who's mistakenly killed in his place. Clouseau, considered dead, is entitled to a state funeral. Inspector Dreyfus, former superior and intimate enemy of Clouseau, is released from the psychiatric asylum where he was interned (cf. previous episodes) in order to conduct the investigation of the crime. Meanwhile, Clouseau, aided by Kato, is also investigating. They're all meeting in Hong Kong.

anchor_text: On the day of John Willis' wedding, before the wedding of the psychiatrists, Dr. Harry Blair appears at Willis's estate and asks for a one-on-one with the groom. In it, he reveals to Willis that his fiancée Nancy is Blair's mentally unstable ex-wife, who has already ruined the lives of several other men. In a series of complex, interlocking flashbacks, Blair tells the story of Nancy, the daughter of a domestic servant, who as a young girl was innocently accused by her mother's employer of stealing a valuable medallion. As a result of this severe trauma, Nancy became a compulsive liar and a kleptomaniac. She may even be the real culprit in a murder case for which a likely innocent man was executed. Feeling guilty for not preventing the execution by testifying, Nancy's ex-boyfriend, Norman Clyde, committed suicide. Willis refuses to believe Blair's story and sends him away. Afterward, as Nancy dresses for the wedding ceremony, her future mother-in-law enters the room. It turns out that this is the former employer of Nancy's mother. Unaware of Nancy's true identity, she hands her the fateful medallion as a wedding gift. Overwhelmed by the memories evoked, Nancy suffers a nervous breakdown as the bride moves in. Completely insane, she is committed to a sanatorium.
text_a: In New York, during his pre-wedding party, John Willis is greeted by a man, Dr. Harry Blair, who warns him not to marry. Blair warns Willis against the sweet, sweaty Nancy, having been a victim himself. In a lengthy narrative, Blair lays out his troubled history with Nancy, including the story of his former partner, artist Norman Clyde, who is then the person who discovered what is perhaps the key episode in the woman's life. In fact, as a child, she was unjustly accused of stealing a medallion from the woman her mother worked for and whose daughter was her best friend. Thrown out with her mother and losing the dear friend that that medallion gave her before her mother forced her to back down because of its immense value, Nancy became a cleptomaniac, a liar and, perhaps, even a murderer. Willis doesn't believe what he's been told and, not at all alarmed, decides to get married. Nancy, already in her wedding dress, receives from her mother-in-law that much-desired medallion. John is, in fact, the brother of her childhood friend, who died a few years earlier. Reliving the trauma she suffered as a child, however, triggers an emotional crescendo that causes Nancy to have a psychological breakdown that causes her to faint before reaching the altar. After the shock Nancy has regressed to a childlike state but Willis, despite everything, decides to stay close to her.
text_b: Colin is a young teacher who also owns a boarding house in London. The young man can't cope with women, he just can't get through to them. His tenant Tolen, on the other hand, is kind of a ladies' man. Tolen's even organizing a gathering of all his girlfriends at the Royal Albert Hall. Another tenant, actor Tom, has a calming influence on both men. Colin comes up with the idea of getting a brass bed to impress the ladies. He and Tom go to a junkyard to look for one. As they roll the found bed home through the streets, they encounter the innocent Nancy Jones. Nancy comes from the provinces and is looking for a Y.W.C.A., the Y.M.C.A. for women. The men offer to help, but in the end it is Nancy who helps them move the bed. In doing so, however, they cause traffic accidents and hysterical attacks on passersby. At the boarding house, Tolen wants Colin to demonstrate his flirting technique. Tell Colin to hit on Nancy when she's in her prime. But Colin stubbornly passes up his chance, leaving Nancy with Tolen on a motorcycle. Colin and Tom are going after them. In a park, they meet a hysterical Nancy. She accuses Tolen of rape, which he denies. Colin takes care of Nancy, the two become fast friends.

anchor_text: Dr. Sullivan Travis (called "Dr. T".) is the gynecologist of choice for the women of high society in Dallas. He finds his idyllic life falling apart when his wife Kate begins to suffer from a rare childhood regression and is admitted to a psychiatric clinic. Bree, a golf instructor, falls in love with Dr. T, reciprocates, and offers him comfort. Dr. T's loyal secretary, Carolyn, is also secretly in love with him, but the feeling is not mutual. Dr. T's sister-in-law, Peggy, gets into every situation she can get into. Dr. T's eldest daughter, Dee Dee, faces preparations for her own wedding despite being secretly a lesbian and having a secret relationship with Marilyn, the bridesmaid. The younger daughter, Connie, is a rebel with a passion for conspiracy theories. The epilogue is rocambolesque and ends under a flood where all the stories are completed.
text_a: Diana Sullivan, photojournalist for Cosmopolitan Magazine, leaves her sophisticated New York penthouse and travels with her rebellious 16-year-old daughter, Grace, to the swamps of the Louisiana Delta, determined to make a story about that world of extreme poverty and isolation, and especially about her family roots. Once they arrive with many difficulties at their destination, the two women have to deal with Ruth - the young widow of Diana's great-uncle - a rude and despotic woman who has kept the three sons away from the city for years, raising them in a climate of superstition, hardship and mutual oppression. The cohabitation will reserve small and large misadventures, more or less bloodless, until the final revelation of what really happened to Ruth's husband, figure of absent that also hovers menacing and disturbing on all the characters, like an indistinct silhouette in the fog of the swamp.
text_b: The Dr. Sullivan Travis (also known as "Dr. T") (Richard Gere) is a Dallas gynecologist who treats some of the wealthiest women in Texas, and who finds his idealistic life falling apart when his wife, Kate (Farrah Fawcett), suffers a nervous breakdown and is committed to a psychiatric hospital. The eldest daughter of Dr. T, Dee Dee (Kate Hudson), is planning to move on with her life despite the secret that she is a lesbian and is in a relationship with Marilyn (Liv Tyler), the maid of honor. The youngest daughter of dr. T, Connie (Tara Reid), is a conspiracy theorist who has her own agenda for everything, while Dr. T's secretary, T, Carolyn (Shelley Long), has feelings for him, which are not mutual. The sister-in-law of dr. T, Peggy (Laura Dern), meddles in every situation she stumbles into, while a woman, Bree (Helen Hunt), a golf instructor, is the only one who offers her comfort and salvation. Towards the end, it looks like Bree's seeing one of her golf buddies.

anchor_text: Dr. Sullivan Travis, "Dr. T" to the in-laws, is the favorite gynecologist of the upper-class ladies of Dallas. His human and professional qualities, his discretion and natural elegance have attracted many loyal customers. His office keeps filling up and Dr. T is perpetually overwhelmed, despite the efforts of his assistant Carolyn, who is secretly in love with him. In Dr. T's private life, things get rushed: his wife Kate, who fell into childhood, has to be hospitalized a week before the wedding of their daughter DeeDee; his sister-in-law Peggy, doped with champagne and whose husband has just left, invites him home with her three unbearable little girls; his other daughter, Connie, reveals to him that Marilyn, DeeDee's maid of honor, is having a guilty affair with her. To escape all these problems, Dr. T takes refuge in his golf club, where he meets Bree, a young and charming teacher who, for a while, will make him forget his troubles. Big changes were coming for Sullivan, changes he didn't expect.
text_a: Dr. Sullivan Travis (called "Dr. T".) is the gynecologist of choice for the women of high society in Dallas. He finds his idyllic life falling apart when his wife Kate begins to suffer from a rare childhood regression and is admitted to a psychiatric clinic.
