text_b: Pianist Stephen Orlac is the victim of a railway accident, which severely injures his head and deprives him of his hands. The famous and controversial Dr. Gogol grafts him with those of a newly guillotined murderer. From then on, Orlac wondered if he wasn't a Mr. Hyde with inherited criminal tendencies. He seems to be suffering from hallucinations, slipping into depression. His wife investigates to save him, but the couple is caught in a conspiracy. Mysteries and crimes abound around Orlac.

anchor_text: The Hours chronicles a pivotal day in the lives of three women from different eras, whose fates are intertwined by Virginia Woolf's novel Mrs Dalloway. In her 1922 diary, she wrote: I sketch a study of madness and suicide; the world seen by reason and madness side by side. The film retranscribes this juxtaposition of ordinary life and uncommunicative moral suffering.
text_a: The film sees itself as a plea for the liberation of female sexuality in sensual and equal relations between the sexes in the 21st century. He accompanies five young women from different cultural backgrounds who oppose the repression of female sexuality in their cultural and religious communities. The film reveals similarities between the various protagonists and shows their struggle for self-determined sexuality. In addition, it questions patriarchal structures and the ubiquitous contemporary pornography.
text_b: It's based on the lives of three women at three different times in the 20th century. The film revolves around the novel Mrs. Dalloway. It focuses on the lives of three women in search of meaning in their lives. Despite living in different times they are united by their longings and fears. In addition, the film has as background lesbianism and its social impact in 3 different eras of history. The fates of the three women are bound together in some uncertain way.

anchor_text: Mason marries his longtime secretary Della Street, but must cut short the honeymoon to defend a woman accused of murder.
text_a: When Gordon Evers, a World War I veteran, meets Sarah Cazenove, they are both in loveless marriages to different people.
text_b: Notorious attorney Perry Mason is getting ready to marry his secretary, Della Street. But the honeymoon comes to an abrupt halt when Mason is asked to sponsor the defense of a woman accused of murder.

anchor_text: On the eve of a big family celebration, Chuck "Tiger" Warsaw and his father come to blows. Shots are fired and Tiger must flee. Fifteen years later, longing drives him back to his hometown. He realized that the only way to live was to make peace with his family. Karen, his childhood sweetheart, is the only one who still stands by him and confesses her love to him.
text_a: After 15 years of absence, Chuck returns home. Fifteen years earlier, he left, leaving his family in the throes of tragedy, breaking up his sister's marriage. Since then, his father has lost his mind. With the help of Karen, his high school sweetheart, he's going to try to find a second chance.
text_b: Chuck Scott, a war veteran with mental problems, is hired as a driver by Eddie Roman, a gangster. When he finds out that Eddie is keeping his wife Lorna under house arrest, he frees her and flees with her to Cuba.

anchor_text: Sergeant Ernest G. Bilko of the Fort Baxter Regiment has nothing to do with military regulations. To him, the army is just a facade, and when no one's looking, the seasoned gambler he is resurfaces. As a result, these men are more adept at playing cards, roulette, and other games of chance than they are at marching a battalion. Even under the nose of his Colonel who really sees nothing of the tricks of this sergeant unworthy to wear the uniform. But the day Fort Baxter is threatened with closure, Bilko will have to use all his talents to save the fort.
text_a: Sergeant Bilko is officially in charge of the motor pool at Fort Buxter. In fact, with the help of his loyal subordinates, he runs a very organized "entertainment center" for the residents of the base. What he doesn't know is that he's about to be inspected by his archenemy, Major Thorn, who, in revenge for a past wrong, will do anything to transfer him from his beloved base.
text_b: In Lanford City, Manuel's gang, working for the crooked Inspector Klinger, is terrorizing local farmers. As a secret agent, Army Captain Jackson, himself from Lanford, is sent to the city; he has five days to fight the outlaws. Klinger, meanwhile, is in possession of a map that leads to a silver mine; because of it, he has killed Burton Prior and kidnapped his daughter Mary, Jackson's former girlfriend, whom he carries with him as a pawn. Jackson manages to take out most of the bandits and follows Jackson, who flees to Mexico, where he is imprisoned after clashes with the arrogant but rabbit-footed Manuel. However, when he manages to escape, Captain Klinger can face and defeat him in a final duel.

anchor_text: The now-elderly Victor and the sexy Betty are two funny, cheeky con artists at a convention. When the case brings them face-to-face with the real underworld that uses violent methods that end in murder, they reevaluate their existence.
text_a: Maurice is a French teenager who leaves France and starts working at a Brooklyn hype club called BIZARRE. He immediately becomes something of a mascot for this incredible club. The owner and the artists who play him love him, but Maurice can't love because of his past and his secrets.
text_b: Betty, a beautiful young woman, and her companion Victor tour France and neighboring countries in a caravan. They live off small scams and swindles that they commit at thematic congresses, taking advantage of the power of seduction of women. But, their life gets complicated when they try to cheat the treasurer of a multinational company.

anchor_text: Art restorer Amanda Pierce finds her boyfriend in her apartment having sex with another woman. As a new roommate, she moves into an apartment where four models already live: Canadian Jade, Russian Roxana Milla Slasnakova, Australian Candi and African-American Holly. Women like to party. Pierce finds her neighbor Jim Winston attractive. Her roommates are helping her conquer Jim. One day, however, she watches through the window as Winston apparently murders a woman. The alerted police can't find a body. Later, it turns out that Winston, whose real name is Bob Smoot, is an undercover FBI agent. The alleged murder was postponed for tactical reasons. Amanda and Jim are getting back together.
text_a: A hypochondriac woman, confined to her home, tries to get her husband on the phone, but the switchboard operator makes a mistake and switches to a line already in use. She overhears two men discussing a murder one of them is going to commit that very night. She then called the police, who explained that they could not do anything because there were no details on the alleged crime scene. As she receives new calls about her husband, then from her husband himself long prevented from reaching her because the line was busy and she intercepts the information, she eventually discovers that it is she who must be murdered. Trailer for the film
text_b: Amanda Pierce is a restoration artist who specializes in paintings. One day, she comes home early to find her boyfriend in bed with a model. She then decides to move and, reading an advertisement, rents a room in an apartment occupied by four disheveled models. Soon between five, a friendship develops that involves the girls when Amanda loses her mind to a neighbor, Jim Winston, with a mysterious life. Between misunderstandings and funny situations, Amanda also gets involved for a short time in the world of fashion. One day she thinks she's witnessing a murder and begins to suspect that the man she loves is the culprit.

anchor_text: Byvalov, a careerist leader and bureaucrat from a small provincial town, dreams of settling in Moscow. He receives a telegram from the capital to present teams of amateur musicians to the Musical Olympiads in Moscow, but feeling that there is no talent in his small town, Byvalov does not want to send anyone. There are two amateur orchestras in town, one of classical music, the other of popular music. They try in vain to convince the head of the municipal soviet. Eventually Byvalov agreed to go by boat on the Volga to Moscow with the classical orchestra. The other team, led by Dounia (Lioubov Orlova), travels on their own and arrives before the others.
text_a: Byvalov, director of a handicraft business, dreams of a place in Moscow. He is in charge of preparing, with local companies, a festival of amateur artists. In the town there are two amateur companies: the choir of folk songs, animated by the peasant Dunja Petrovna called Strelka, and the symphony orchestra directed by the accountant Alexa Trubykin. Byvalov is going to Moscow on the mail ship "Sevryuga" with Alexa's orchestra. They will be joined by Strelka on the sailboat Lesorub. The wind blows away the score of the song on the Volga composed by Strelka. Once found, the song is broadcast on the radio and Byvalov, upon his arrival in Moscow, is acclaimed as the song's composer. But the jury won't be fooled and awarded the prize to Dunja Petrovna.
text_b: The plot revolves around the failed military campaign of knjaz Igor' Svjatoslavič of Novhorod-Sivers'kyj (a city that was part of the Principality of Černihiv in ancient Kievan Rus') against the Polovcy (also known as Cumans) who lived in the southern part of the Don region in 1185 (Battle of the Kajaly River). Other historical figures of Rus' are also mentioned such as the bard Bojan, Prince Vseslav of Polotsk, Jaroslav Osmomysl of Halyč, and Vsevolod III of Suzdal. The author appeals to the rebellious principles of Kievan Rus' to cease the internal skirmishes and face the threats coming from the Turkish East in a united front. An interesting aspect of the text is the mixture of Christianity and ancient Slavic religion. Jaroslavna, Igor's wife, in a famous part of the play invokes the forces of nature from the walls of Putyvl. Another peculiarity that distinguishes him from contemporary Western epic poems is the continuous description of nature as a force that plays a fundamental role in the lives of human beings.

anchor_text: In 1978, an American journalist was sent to work in Rome, then plunged into the lead years. He begins to take a close interest in the bombings committed by the Red Brigades, in the context of the kidnapping of Aldo Moro, and finds himself caught in a murderous machination.
text_a: The kidnapping of the chairman of the Italian Christian-Democratic Party, Aldo Moro, by members of the Red Brigades terrorist organization on 16 March 1978 plunged the government into a paralyzing inability to act. As Moro faces a so-called people's court for the kidnappers, the Moro family's hopes that the government can secure their release are dashed. All attempts fail in a negotiated situation; Moro is murdered.
text_b: David is a journalist who is writing a novel about the Red Brigades, an Italian terrorist group. To finish the book he travels to Rome where he meets a lovely photographer with whom he enters into a love affair, unaware of the danger that awaits him in the Italian capital.

anchor_text: Shurik is a student who often finds himself in ridiculous situations, but always manages to get out of them in different ways. The film consists of three independent parts, The Teammate (Naparnik), The Hallucination (Navajdenié) and Operation "Y" (Operatsia Y).
text_a: Chourik is a student who often finds himself in ridiculous situations, but always comes out of them by different means. The film is composed of three independent parts, The Teammate (Naparnik), The Hallucination (Navazhdeniye) and Operation "Y" (Operatsiya Y).
text_b: What's more important than controlling the Tokyo area? Otomo, a gangster specialized in handling the dirty work of his bosses and tasked with becoming a "crazed piece of shit" and starting a feud between the most powerful Yakuza clans in the city, knows this well. But something will get out of control, and that will be the beginning of the end.

anchor_text: Ahmad, who spent several years in Germany and is newly divorced, is on holiday in Iran. With some friends, he spends a weekend at the Caspian Sea, where his friends introduce him to Elly, who praises them in high tones. After one of the children nearly drowns while playing in the ocean, the group notices that Elly has disappeared. It is suspected that she was trying to save the child. Even at nightfall, Elly still hasn't shown up. The mood is upside down. You worry that she might have just driven off without saying goodbye. Later it turns out that she's already engaged. In notifying the fiancé, the friends engage in lies. Eventually her body is discovered, she has indeed drowned.
text_a: In the film, Kheiron tells in a humorous way the story of his family, who fled from Iran to France during the Iranian Revolution. The story begins in Iran in the early 1960s, where Hibat, one of twelve siblings, grows up in a rural area of southern Iran and learns to stand up to his siblings. Later, as a law student, together with two of his brothers, he is sentenced by the Shah's regime for sedition to a ten-year prison sentence, during which he is held in solitary confinement and tortured because he refuses to eat a biscuit handed to the prisoners on behalf of the Shah. When he is released from prison, he meets the nurse Fereshteh and falls in love with her. He tricky convinces her father, who is obviously very traditional-conservative, that the two can get married. They have a son and flee the aftermath of the Islamic Revolution as a family under the motto "Just the three of us together" on an adventurous route across Kurdistan to Turkey. Eventually, they are granted political asylum in France, where they are housed in a run-down suburb of Paris. There, the experiences of escape help to unite the previously divided inhabitants and to enhance the neighborhood.
text_b: Ahmad, an Iranian who has lived in Germany for years, returns to his native country from vacation. His university friends celebrate his arrival with a three-day excursion on the Caspian Sea. Her friend Sepideh, who knows that she has just divorced a German woman, invites Elly, her daughter's teacher. The next day, Elly disappears without a trace, and everyone blames Sepideh for inviting her. Little by little, the truth about Elly is finally coming out.

anchor_text: Mia is 30 years old and single. She lives and works in Stockholm and travels to Dalarna to visit her parents as her father is celebrating his 70th birthday. There's a surprise waiting for her. Her parents want to give Mia a piece of land by the lake so that she can build a house there and move back "home" to her family and relatives. This leads to discussions between Mia and her two older sisters Eivor and Gunilla, who feel disadvantaged. Both have stayed in Dalarna to take care of their parents, while Mia can participate in the big city life in Stockholm. Mia just walked away, and the sisters had to sacrifice themselves.
text_a: Mia, an aspiring architect, is about to marry Tim, her business partner. Her father Lasse is also engaged to Elizabeth. During Lasse's birthday party she meets Frida, the daughter of Elisabeth. Mia and Frida exchange many glances, presaging mutual attraction. Mia and Frida both visit the new house that Elisabeth and Lasse plan to share. Mia kisses Frida and then regrets it. Their feelings change, but Mia is torn between Tim, her fiancé, and her ever-growing attraction to Frida. The choice is left to Mia to decide whether she prefers to forget her love for Frida or break off her engagement with Tim and start her new life.
text_b: Mia, who has been living in Stockholm for a long time, returns to her hometown for her father's 70th birthday. Here he meets the two older sisters he hasn't seen in a long time. A situation of misunderstanding immediately arises between them, which becomes friction when her father decides to host her in a cabin by the lake, which is also highly coveted by her sisters. During her stay, she meets other people she knew in her youth, none of whom seem to be happy with the life she leads.

anchor_text: The film is about physicist Tom Buckley, who is on the trail of a successful medium. We're talking about Simon Silver, who used to be a star, and now he's making a comeback. Even the harshest critic of such stage shows (in which parapsychological phenomena are sold as real skills), the supervisor of the young Buckley, the psychologist Margaret Matheson, could not prove that Silver was the only one in his profession who was not a charlatan. In their search for the truth, scientists are delving into ever more mysterious abysses. Matheson dies during the course of the investigation. Increasingly plagued by nightmares, Buckley, after a private visit with the blind Simon Silver, sets out to prove his cheating on supernatural abilities during a prestigious test before Silver's abilities are confirmed to be genuine. Meanwhile, he begins a relationship with one of his students, Sally Owen, who helps him with his work. Eventually, an analysis of the video recordings made during the tests proves beyond a shadow of a doubt that Simon Silver faked his blindness. In the showdown, conducted as wordy dialogue on and off the stage of a packed theater, it is implied that Tom Buckley himself possesses psychic abilities.
text_a: The scientist Dr. Exorcio seeks world domination with the help of demons and wants to ally with the vampire Count Sartana. However, the vampire hunter Dr. Seward tracked Sartana down in the Carpathians and killed her with a silver nail. So Dr. Exorcio and his servant Morpho find in Sartana's castle only a bat impaled. The scientist revives the vampire by drowning him in blood. Sartana now becomes a slave to Dr. Exorcio, constantly supplying him with new victims who are vampirized by electric shocks. Dr. Seward learns of the vampires' activities, but is severely wounded by a monster created by Dr. Exorcio. Finally, the gypsy Amira, whom Dr. Seward nursed, curses the demons shortly before her death and incites the werewolf against her. Eventually, all the demons will be destroyed by Dr. Exorcio.
text_b: Margaret Matheson and her assistant Tom Buckley are two of the country's foremost expert paranormal researchers and as such the foremost exposers of charlatans, healers and mediums. Dr. Matheson has never confirmed a single paranormal event in her career, and the most high-profile case of all, that of blind clairvoyant Simon Silver, is the obsession that almost killed her. Anyone who tries to expose Silver ends badly, and now the self-styled psychic is planning to stage one of his crowded psychic shows for the last time. Despite warnings not to initiate any investigation against Silver, Buckley independently decides to pursue the case. During his research, however, Dr. Matheson dies, and he speculates that it was the work of the clairvoyant. Eager to discover the truth about what happened, Tom will continue by any means until he comes to a final confrontation with Silver, during which he will not only be able to unmask how the self-styled medium is an impostor, but he will also come to understand that he himself is a person endowed with paranormal powers, which, during his investigations, have caused unexplained phenomena initially attributed to Simon.

anchor_text: Georgina, 67, went to Guantánamo to visit her niece Gina and her childhood sweetheart Cándido. Georgina's sudden death forces Cándido, Gina, and her husband, Adolfo, a government official, to take Georgina's body home by road. To do this, they must follow the new state plan designed to save money when transporting the deceased.
text_a: The film tells the story of a group of passengers who meet at a bus terminal in a small Cuban town. But none of them can make their journey because all the vehicles that pass through the terminal are full and no longer take passengers. To reach their destination, they all set about repairing a vehicle that was in poor condition.
text_b: Cuba: Adolfo, a funeral home owner, is married to the beautiful Gina. The death of an aunt prompts him to try a miraculous plan: a funeral home relay to deliver coffins from one side of the island to the other. Many adventures and misadventures of a journey revealing life, death and the incredible vitality and philosophy of today's Cuba, strangled by the embargo and an often inhumane and harassing bureaucracy.

anchor_text: This is Los Angeles, 1948. Desmond Spellacy is an ambitious monsignor assistant to the archbishop, while his brother Thomas is a tough, impulsive cop investigating the murder of a dismembered prostitute. Desmond is more inclined to securing the material well-being of the archdiocese than to mysticism and, even if he does not derive any personal benefit from it, he does not disdain accepting the offers of Jack Amsterdam, an ambiguous entrepreneur with a life full of shady areas. Thomas manages to solve the murder case but raises a scandal that invests the archdiocese and marks the downfall of his brother's career ambitions. The latter, however, recovers the spiritual dimension of his ministry and, after many years, reconciled with himself and on the verge of death, thanks Thomas for it.
text_a: Catholic priest Andrews is caught at the scene of a murder with the victim's blood on him. The police will arrest him. Another priest, Daniel Clemens, visits Andrews in custody and learns that Andrews knows the identity of the killer. This cannot be revealed because Andrews feels bound by the secrecy of the confessional. A short time later, Andrews is found hanging in his cell. Clemens wants to prove Andrews was innocent. He investigates the priest's former parish, defying the will of the church hierarchy. Journalist Madeline Finney, who he dated before he became a priest, is helping him. They discover a group of homosexual Catholics who have nothing to do with the crime. Clemens comes into conflict with a woman who secretly records the confessions of her foster children; she slanders him to his superior. Clemens, who collects donations for the church organization, is assigned to visit a donor in another city. His superior tells him that he tolerates his insubordination only because Clemens is a capable fund-raiser. Since Clement refuses to travel, he is suspended as a priest; he is to leave the tabernacle. Nevertheless, he continues to search for the murderer and finds him. The perpetrator commits suicide after the alerted police appear. The film ends with the reinstated Clemens celebrating a Mass in memory of Andrews.
text_b: In 1948, Archbishop Desmond Spellacy is a young and ambitious Catholic prelate of the Archdiocese of Los Angeles. His older brother Tom is a homicide detective with the Los Angeles Police Department. Desmond is related to construction magnate Jack Amsterdam, a Catholic who uses his ties to the monsignor for his congregation's benefit, but primarily for his own. One day in Los Angeles, a young woman is found brutally murdered and her body sliced in half in a parking lot. Tom Spellacy and his partner, Frank Crotty, are in charge of the case. His wife, Lois Fazenda, is labeled by the local press as "the vagabond virgin" because she appears to be Catholic and a prostitute, becoming a sensationalist case. The details of the case that will come to light will test Tom's relationship with his brother Des and his brother's relationship with Jack Amsterdam.

anchor_text: James King is a high-paying hedge fund manager on the cusp of success and on the verge of marrying the attractive daughter of his super-rich boss. However, due to a conspiracy that is completely obscure to him, he, although completely innocent, is sentenced for embezzlement and investment fraud to a ten-year prison sentence in a maximum-security prison, which he must serve in thirty days. Fearing the conditions at the prison, he hires the black car wash operator Darnell Lewis to harden him for life in prison. He hires him because he has the later proven false assumption that Darnell, as a typical African-American, has had some experience in prison. Darnell, who doesn't have a criminal record, lets him in on it because he desperately needs equity to finance a house so he and his family can finally move out of the ghetto. The training program for James includes self-defense, oral sex, and other survival skills for prison life. In the end, however, they work together to uncover the plot and convict the real fraudsters. Since James was carrying an unregistered firearm, he still has to go to jail for six months. At the end of his sentence, Darnell picks him up. He's become a friend of Darnell's, and he's invested in Darnell's laundromat.
text_a: Businessman James King (Will Ferrell) wakes up in his mansion, while another man, Darnell Lewis (Kevin Hart) wakes up in his. They appreciate their two routines on two sides, while James goes to his father-in-law's company to make money, Darnell takes his daughter to school and talks to his bank to try to buy a house. James' father-in-law, the head of the company gives him a lesson on the importance of hard work and gives him the blessing to marry his daughter. Then, during the party where he announces his wedding to the daughter (Alison Brie) of the company president, while playing a song with the electric guitar they gave him, James is arrested for tax fraud, although he is innocent and tries to say it, but no one believes him. After the trial, he's sentenced to 10 years in prison, while everyone he allegedly conned tells him everything. On one occasion, while picking up his car from the car wash he runs into Darnell, who is the man who washes his car. Using statistics, James deduces that Darnell is an ex-convict and promises to give him money if he helps him survive in prison. Darnell initially refuses, as he's never been to prison. James offers him money and Darnell puts in the figure of $30,000, which is what he needs to buy the new house. James agrees and so does Darnell. Darnell, having no idea about prison, calls a cousin of his who's been there, to tell him what it's like. The next day, Darnell begins training James, turning his home into a prison. As they progress, James improves his skills and becomes a member of a gang run by Darnell's cousin, where he falls in love with one of its members.
text_b: Steve is a 17-year-old African-American boy who faces the prospect of spending his entire youth in prison for a crime he says he did not commit. Steve is accused of being a stoolie during a robbery on behalf of an acquaintance and a third man, but he claims he was just in the wrong place at the wrong time. No one seems to believe him except his family and his lawyer. The latter rejects an offer from the prosecution and therefore proceeds with the trial without a plea bargain. Between hearings, memories, and prison time, Steve's life and personality are gradually eviscerated. As a young man with a passion for cinema and intending to study to become a filmmaker, Steve has always shown creativity and talent and has received support from family, friends and above all from his film teacher. On the other hand, the boy had to deal with the difficult reality of his neighborhood, approaching the charismatic James King in the hope of obtaining a more prestigious role in the neighborhood. Pressured by life in prison, where only one other inmate is close to him, and by a heated accusation that has called him a "monster" and involved various criminals to testify against him, James ends up testifying in person at the suggestion of his attorney. The boy repeats his version of events and manages to convince the jury: in the end King is convicted of the crime committed, however the boy is at the same time acquitted and can return to his life and his dreams. The reality, however, is only part of the story: although not fully aware of what he was doing, Steve actually helped the robbers by being a stick. Now the boy can return to his life and his dreams, yet he does so with some remorse and continues to wonder if the prosecutor was actually right in calling him a monster.

anchor_text: While Annabeth and Percy cross Tartarus, facing many monsters and gods of the oldest to find the Gates of Death on the side of the Underworld, the others travel with the Athena Parthenos, in order to rally the House of Hades, an underground temple in Epirus, which is the location of the Gates of Death on the mortal side. By means of a sacred fire, Annabeth will, from Tartarus, send a message to Reyna to convince her that she must go to the Old Lands to retrieve the Athena Parthenos and return it to the Half-Blood Colony in order to end the hostilities between Greeks and Romans. It will not happen until much later, when exhausted, the Seven emerge from the House of Hades having once again repulsed monsters, giants and sorcerers; having found Percy and Annabeth who came out of Tartarus alive and having broken the chains of the Gates of Death and closed them.
text_a: Pelias misinterpreted a prophecy of Zeus and seized the throne of Thessaly by killing King Aristo and numerous members of his family. In doing so, he incurs the wrath of Hermes and Heras, who will henceforth protect his nephew Jason. Jason, spurred on by Pelias, sets off in search of the Golden Fleece to secure his rule over Iolkos. Pelias hopes that Jason will not survive the journey. Jason's journey has been joined by many heroes, named after Jason's ship, Argo, the Argonauts. On an island, the Argonauts must contend with a bronze Titan (Talos). King Phineus, blinded by the gods, consults the Argonauts in Phrygia, where they also succeed in capturing two Harpies. As they cross the Strait to the Black Sea, they narrowly escape a rockslide by a lucky amulet of Phineus. In Colchis, Jason defeats the Hydra and must fight the skeletal warriors created from their teeth, managing to escape. Jason loses several companions and makes the journey home to Thessaly with Medea and the surviving Argonauts. On Olympus, Zeus informs Hera that he will soon call Jason back for new deeds.
text_b: Percy and Annabeth are plunged into Tartarus and the other five demigods of the prophecy (Jason, Leo, Piper, Hazel, Frank) plus Nico Di Angelo must arrive in Greece to close the Gates of Death, to prevent the infernal creatures from crossing them, facing a thousand pitfalls and dangers in the Ancient Lands. In the meantime, Percy and Annabeth must get to the heart of Tartarus to close the Gates of Death from the inside, or the work of the others will be in vain. All this must be accomplished before the festival of the goddess Spes, the Hope, on the first of August, the day when Gaia will awaken and attack the gods and their children. Moreover, a war is about to break out at Camp Half-Blood between the Greeks and the Romans, led by Octavian, a descendant of Apollo. Only the Athena Parthenos, the statue of Athena stolen from the Greeks by the Romans in ancient times, can bring peace. But the statue is on Argo II, in Greece, and at the end of the book, once the gates of death are closed, and once Percy and Annabeth are out of Tartarus, Nico will volunteer along with Reyna, who by then had joined the group, to take the statue to Camp Half-Blood to try to restore peace between the two camps.

anchor_text: The film is about the incestuous relationship between siblings Elisabeth and Paul. Together, they share a room in the Paris apartment of their sick, bedridden mother. Paul has a crush on his school friend Dargelos. The film begins when Paul is hit by a snowball thrown by Dargelos and in which he has hidden a stone. Paul is confined to bed, and his slightly older sister, Elisabeth, has to care for him. In the process, the relationship between the two siblings becomes more and more intimate. After the death of her mother and a trip to the Mediterranean with young Gérard and his uncle, Elisabeth invites Gérard to move in with them. Quarrel and intimacy between Elizabeth and Paul replace each other. "The chamber theater began at 11 o'clock at night", says the commentary. Gérard is a spectator of these theatrical scenes. As the dispute escalates, Elizabeth takes a job as a mannequin. There she meets the young Agathe. She too is invited to move in with him, and it turns out that Agathe bears a striking resemblance to Paul's former school friend, his unforgettable Dargelos. At her job at the fashion salon, Elisabeth meets the wealthy American Michael; they marry; Michael is killed in an accident, and Elisabeth becomes the heiress of a huge city palace near the Arc de Triomphe. The four of them - Elisabeth, Paul, Gérard, Agathe - move in together. But Paul isn't happy there and sets up a room in the foyer that looks exactly like the one in her old apartment. At first it remains unspoken, but it becomes clearer and clearer: Paul has fallen in love with Agathe and Agathe has fallen in love with Paul. Elizabeth, jealous of her brother's possible happiness without her, devises a plan that ends in suicide for both of them.
text_a: In a high school snowball fight, Paul (Edouard Dermithe) is injured by a stone thrown by his friend Dargelos, whom he idolizes, and has to stay in bed. Elisabeth (Nicole Stéphane), his sister, who has always been very protective of him, devotes all her time to him, recreating with him an obsessive, fantasy and secret world in the room where the two live secluded. After her mother's death, Elisabeth finds work as a model at the Dior house and marries Michael, a wealthy American businessman. But he is killed in a car accident the day after the wedding, leaving her his vast fortune. Paul, Agathe (Renée Cosima), a friend of Elisabeth who bears a great resemblance to Dargelos, and Gérard (Jacques Bernard), a childhood friend of the brothers, move to live with Elisabeth in the mansion she has inherited from her husband. There they re-create their imaginary world far removed from reality, until Elisabeth sees how this could be jeopardized by the burgeoning love between her brother and Agathe. Caught up in jealousy, he plots a diabolical plan to separate his friend from his brother, a plan that will lead to a tragic end.
text_b: Around 1905, ten-year-old Marcel is to prepare for the examination for high school. As a reward, he'll be staying at the cottage in his beloved hills of Provence. At the suggestion of the mother, the family also spends the weekends in the cottage. Marcel meets the girl Isabelle and falls in love with her. But Isabelle soon sets herself up to rule over him. Lili and Paul are watching the two and informing the parents. After the bitter disappointment of his first love, he turns again to his friend Lili. After a former student who is now a sewer guard illegally gives his father a key to cut their way to their vacation home and they are caught by a supervisor, his father is put in a precarious position. The mother collapses. But the canal keeper can solve the situation with two friends. After five years, the childhood idyll ends with the death of the mother. His childhood friend Lili is killed by a bullet in the war in 1917. His brother Paul, who later worked as a shepherd in the hills, died at the age of 30. When Marcel grows up and buys a castle for filming, he discovers that it is the castle that played an important role in his childhood. This is where they were caught by the supervisor.

anchor_text: Brothers Elisabeth and Paul live almost in seclusion in their bedroom which has been transformed into a kind of sanctuary full of "treasures" small objects even of little value to which they give a special meaning. Even though they fight all the time, they can't stay away from each other. Elisabeth gives in to Michael's insistence and marries him but soon becomes a widow without consummating the marriage and moves into her husband's large villa. Immediately her brother joins her, bringing his friends Gérard and Agathe with him. In particular, she could be the female version of Dargelos, a schoolmate whom Paul idolizes. As soon as Elisabeth realizes that Paul and Agathe are developing romantic feelings for each other, she devises a diabolical plan to separate them and prevent her brother from abandoning her. The plan works but the unexpected happens: after breaking up with Agathe Paul she poisons herself. Elisabeth, not wanting to live with the remorse of having killed her brother, commits suicide by shooting herself in the temple.
text_a: In a high school snowball fight, Paul (Edouard Dermithe) is injured by a stone thrown by his friend Dargelos, whom he idolizes, and has to stay in bed. Elisabeth (Nicole Stéphane), his sister, who has always been very protective of him, devotes all her time to him, recreating with him an obsessive, fantasy and secret world in the room where the two live secluded. After her mother's death, Elisabeth finds work as a model at the Dior house and marries Michael, a wealthy American businessman. But he is killed in a car accident the day after the wedding, leaving her his vast fortune. Paul, Agathe (Renée Cosima), a friend of Elisabeth who bears a great resemblance to Dargelos, and Gérard (Jacques Bernard), a childhood friend of the brothers, move to live with Elisabeth in the mansion she has inherited from her husband. There they re-create their imaginary world far removed from reality, until Elisabeth sees how this could be jeopardized by the burgeoning love between her brother and Agathe. Caught up in jealousy, he plots a diabolical plan to separate his friend from his brother, a plan that will lead to a tragic end.
text_b: Around 1905, ten-year-old Marcel is to prepare for the examination for high school. As a reward, he'll be staying at the cottage in his beloved hills of Provence. At the suggestion of the mother, the family also spends the weekends in the cottage. Marcel meets the girl Isabelle and falls in love with her. But Isabelle soon sets herself up to rule over him. Lili and Paul are watching the two and informing the parents. After the bitter disappointment of his first love, he turns again to his friend Lili. After a former student who is now a sewer guard illegally gives his father a key to cut their way to their vacation home and they are caught by a supervisor, his father is put in a precarious position. The mother collapses. But the canal keeper can solve the situation with two friends. After five years, the childhood idyll ends with the death of the mother. His childhood friend Lili is killed by a bullet in the war in 1917. His brother Paul, who later worked as a shepherd in the hills, died at the age of 30. When Marcel grows up and buys a castle for filming, he discovers that it is the castle that played an important role in his childhood. This is where they were caught by the supervisor.

anchor_text: Three women named Cissie Colpitts - grandmother, mother and daughter - drown their husbands in quick succession and are covered by the local coroner Madgett, who rules out murder each time - in the hope of a love reward. Meanwhile, Madgett's son, Smut, is busy numbering and cataloguing animal carcasses he discovers. He is always accompanied by his jumping rope girlfriend, who lists the names of a hundred fixed stars while jumping. Suspicious friends and relatives of the dead band together to prove the women's crimes, but to no avail. The three women drive Madgett out to a lake to scatter the ashes of the dead men. In the middle of the lake, the women pull a plug, so that the boat (number 100), in which the four are, begins to sink. Afterward, the women leave Madgett, who can't swim, alone. Smut's rope-jumping girlfriend is run over by a car, causing Smut to hang himself.
text_a: Three women, mother, daughter and granddaughter, all named Cissie Colpitts, drown their respective husbands (a gardener, a businessman and an unemployed plumber), one after the other, in a bathtub, in the sea and in the pool, respectively. Despite the suspicions of the families of the deceased, they make the deaths look like accidents thanks to the complicity of eccentric coroner Madgett, a family friend, who spends time with his teenage son inventing strange games and styling macabre death statistics. Madgett expects each time to be rewarded by their feminine graces, but is only repeatedly deceived (she is the youngest and transgressive of the three to grant him a minimal satisfaction, but then she also pulls back), until finally, during the nightly ceremony of the dispersion of the ashes of the three husbands, she remains a victim in turn of the ruthless tercetto.
text_b: This is a story of the Brothers Grimm (the film invents details that have nothing to do with the real life of the real Brothers Grimm). They both try to make a living by cheating the poor locals. They tell them stories of curses, spirits and ghosts that haunt them, to later save them through exorcisms and various rituals in exchange for some money. Together they traveled many roads collecting and spreading tales of fairies, beasts and diabolical witches until, on one occasion, accused by the French authorities of being swindlers, they are forced to go to a small town where several girls have disappeared, forcing the parents to dress them as boys. At first they think it's other con men like them, but the problem arises when they discover that a real curse is hanging over the village, related to an enchanted forest in which the girls have disappeared. With Angelica's help, they discover that an evil witch is the one who makes the girls disappear, making them sleep in order to be young and beautiful. In this film, Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Hansel and Gretel, Rapunzel, among other characters who originally appear in the tales of these two brothers, appear in the village.

Meanwhile, Madgett's son, Smut, is busy numbering and cataloguing animal carcasses he discovers. He is always accompanied by his jumping rope girlfriend, who lists the names of a hundred fixed stars while jumping. Smut's rope-jumping girlfriend is run over by a car, causing Smut to hang himself.
text_a: The film's plot centers on three married women, a grandmother, her daughter and her niece, each named Cissie Colpitts. As the story progresses, each woman successively drowns her husband. The three Cissie Colpitts are played by Joan Plowright, Juliet Stevenson and Joely Richardson, while Bernard Hill plays the coroner, Madgett, who engages in covering up all three murders. The structure, with similar stories repeated three times, is reminiscent of a fairy tale, more specifically 'The Billy Goats Gruff', because Madgett is constantly promised greater rewards as she tries her luck with each of the Cissies in turn. The link to folklore is further established by Madgett's son, Smut, who recites the rules of several unusual games that the characters play as if they were ancient traditions. Many of these games were invented for the film, including: In Conspiracy of Women, the counting of numbers, the rules of the games, and the repetitions of the plot are all devices that emphasize structure. Throughout the film each of the numbers from 1 to 100 appear, the vast majority in sequence, often seen in the background, sometimes spoken by the characters.[3] The film is set and was filmed in and around Southwold, Suffolk, England, with key landmarks such as the Victorian-style water tower, Southwold Lighthouse, and the Blyth River estuary clearly identifiable.
text_b: This is a story of the Brothers Grimm (the film invents details that have nothing to do with the real life of the real Brothers Grimm). They both try to make a living by cheating the poor locals. They tell them stories of curses, spirits and ghosts that haunt them, to later save them through exorcisms and various rituals in exchange for some money. Together they traveled many roads collecting and spreading tales of fairies, beasts and diabolical witches until, on one occasion, accused by the French authorities of being swindlers, they are forced to go to a small town where several girls have disappeared, forcing the parents to dress them as boys. At first they think it's other con men like them, but the problem arises when they discover that a real curse is hanging over the village, related to an enchanted forest in which the girls have disappeared. With Angelica's help, they discover that an evil witch is the one who makes the girls disappear, making them sleep in order to be young and beautiful. In this film, Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Hansel and Gretel, Rapunzel, among other characters who originally appear in the tales of these two brothers, appear in the village.

anchor_text: Salome cunningly seeks the power of King Herod. She has eliminated Herod's main rivals and brings about the death of his wife by her own treachery. John the Baptist, who enjoys popular support, denounces Herod and his court state. To prevent unrest, Herod has him arrested and thrown into prison. There, he meets Salome and becomes passionate about him. When John rejects her advances, however, she is outraged and seeks revenge. She is able to thrill Herod with a sensual dance and demands John's head as a reward. But this act will bring death to you. It is crushed between the pointed shield buckles of the Roman legionaries.
text_a: Herodias leaves her husband to marry his brother Herod, king of Judea. The prophet John the Baptist, then, accuses her of being an adulteress and the king has him imprisoned. Herodias, however, continues to listen to the prophet's accusations. Herod, meanwhile, is seized by an insane passion for his stepdaughter Salome, the daughter of Herodias. He promises her anything she wants if she dances for him. The young woman, instructed by her mother who wants to silence John forever, after performing a tumultuous dance for the king, asks Herod for the head of the Baptist.
text_b: During the reign of Emperor Tiberius, the Galilean prophet John the Baptizer preached against King Herod Antipas and his wife, his half brother's former wife, Queen Herodias. She wanted the priest dead, but Herod feared to harm her because of a prophecy that John the Baptizer's death would lead to his own. The beautiful princess Salome, the daughter of Herodias, arrives. The king's growing lust for his daughter-in-law and niece Salome will be used by Herodias to bend the king to her desires. Ignoring the Scriptural account, the film depicts Salome fleeing the palace with a converted Roman centurion and joining the early Christians.

anchor_text: Salome cunningly seeks the power of King Herod. She has eliminated Herod's main rivals and brings about the death of his wife by her own treachery. John the Baptist, who enjoys popular support, denounces Herod and his court state. To prevent unrest, Herod has him arrested and thrown into prison. There, he meets Salome and becomes passionate about him. When John rejects her advances, however, she is outraged and seeks revenge. She is able to thrill Herod with a sensual dance and demands John's head as a reward. But this act will bring death to you. It is crushed between the pointed shield buckles of the Roman legionaries.
text_a: John Prudhomme is a married detective whose partner is Andrew Hollinsworth. John is haunted by the death of his daughter a year ago, which has led him to distance himself from the church. One day he is assigned to investigate a murder in the victim's home. Once there, two details catch his attention: the man's dismembered arm appears, and a message written in blood on the window reads: "He will come". After two more murders in which the victims are missing body parts, he realizes that he is facing a serial killer who uses such parts to reconstruct the body of Jesus Christ before the arrival of Resurrection Sunday. That's why he's also been assisted ever since by federal agent Wingate. However the serial killer knows him and sees him as a threat to his endeavor after he almost manages to catch him during one of the murders. Thus a duel to the death, in which he has the advantage, begins.
text_b: During Roman rule over Judea and Israel, the palatial intrigues and licentious customs of the imperial court scandalized the spiritual leaders of the Jewish people, who viewed the Romans as transmitters of utter moral degeneracy. An example of this was the scandalous conduct of the young and sensual Salome, stepdaughter of King Herod over whom she exercises enormous power.[2]

anchor_text: Patsy finds out that Kate Moss wants to change her PR agency. After hearing this too, Eddy goes to great lengths to win Kate Moss as a client. At a major London fashion event, she attempts to outplay her competitors, including longtime nemesis Claudia Bing, and inadvertently pushes Kate Moss sitting on a balcony railing into the River Thames. After the search for the victim is unsuccessful and Eddie's face appears with the headline of Moss' death on the front page of the tabloid newspaper The Sun, Eddy and Patsy are relentlessly pursued by paparazzi. To escape this, the two settle on the French Riviera and devise a plan to put an end to the escape from the press.
text_a: Edina and Patsy still live the high life they are accustomed to, based on glitz and glamour; spending, drinking and going out in the trendiest parts of London. Accused of causing a terrible incident at a fashion launch party, they find themselves dragged into a media storm and relentlessly pursued by the paparazzi. Fleeing penniless on the Côte d'Azur, a realm of glamour and billionaires, they hatch a plan to make their escape permanent and live the high life forever!
text_b: Jill Cheyne hopes to become a dancer at the Pleasure Garden. But when she arrived in London, she discovered that she had lost her letter of recommendation. Fortunately, Patsy Brand, a cabaret dancer, offered him hospitality for the night. The next day, Jill, who has not given up on her project, meets the boss of the Garden and gets an engagement. Hugh, Jill's fiancé, witnesses her triumph before leaving England for the tropics. But no matter what, Jill got what she was looking for: fame, an easy life and rich admirers... Meanwhile, Patsy marries Levet, a friend of Hugh's, before leaving on their honeymoon to Lake Como. For Patsy, happiness seems within reach, but it's not... and Levet flees, too, to the tropics... where a woman is waiting for him... wife he would drown in a fit of madness before trying to kill Patsy, who, knowing he was suffering, had come to join him.

anchor_text: On a trip to India, young Australian Ruth Barron experiences a spiritual awakening and adopts the teachings of a guru named Baba. Her parents are shocked to learn that she now goes by the name Nazni, has no intention of returning, and believe that she has joined a cult and is being drugged without her knowledge. Ruth's mother visits her in India, hoping to persuade her to return, but Ruth refuses. The mother then makes Ruth believe that she has to see her father, who is supposedly sick, but instead introduces her to P. J. Waters, an exorcist who gets cult members off the streets. The confident Waters wants to distance Ruth from the influence Baba has had on her, but he is sexually attracted to the girl, who encourages him to seduce her, for fun. Eventually, she turns on him, shatters his misogyny, his pride, and opens his eyes to who he really is.
text_a: Rut and her husband Bertil are returning to their home country of Sweden after a vacation by train through Germany, which has been devastated by World War II. Outwardly harmonious, their marriage is actually unhappy, largely because of Ruth's mood swings. She's hysterical, suffers from insomnia and alcoholism. As ruined landscapes and starving, begging villagers pass by the train window, Ruth recalls her former lover Raoul. As a young girl, Ruth had an affair with the older, married officer Raoul. When he got Ruth pregnant, he forced her to have an abortion. The procedure led to health complications, Ruth became infertile, and Raoul turned his back on her. Added to this were physical problems that damaged Ruth's career as a dancer. Her friend Valborg, also a dancer, turned to other women out of disgust for men. Ruth's husband Bertil is pursuing a past affair with the widow Viola, who, like Ruth, is mentally unstable. Parallel to Ruth and Bertil's journey home, the film depicts Viola's escape from a tormenting psychiatrist, her encounter with Ruth's friend Valborg, who makes openly homoerotic advances towards her, and her eventual suicide. Meanwhile, tensions between Rut and Bertil escalate to the point that he kills her in an act of apparent infatuation. He wakes up to find that Ruth's murder was just a dream. The couple decide to give their marriage a second chance.
text_b: Australia: Ruth is a young woman who, after a trip to India with a friend, joins a kind of cult. The friend, back in Australia, tells the story to Ruth's parents, who immediately take action to try to get her back home. Once they've managed to get her back, they trick her into handing her over to a famous "deprogrammer" named PJ, whose job it is to reason with her and get her back to her old self. In the isolation of a shack in the middle of the Australian desert, PJ's inquisitive and rational approach shatters the young woman's certainties. In the anguish of this deprivation Ruth, who has set her Indian sari on fire and presented herself to him naked and incontinent, obtains the tenderness of her rival. But PJ, in revealing to him the animal and social plagiarism that constitutes him as a person, inverts the roles of the de-conditioner and the subject to be freed from outside influences. Overwhelmed by passion, the man submits to the girl to the point of "deprogramming" his manhood, disguising himself as a middle-aged woman with a moustache and exploding in violence in the face of a rejection of the young woman. This of course costs him a hard lesson from his relatives and an immediate return to California from where, married and with a pair of twins, he maintains an epistolary correspondence with Ruth, who in the meantime has returned to India with her mother.

anchor_text: Myth and monster investigator/researcher Sir Lionel Frost sets off on an adventure in the Pacific Northwest to prove the existence of man's legendary ancestor. He is accompanied on his journey by Adelina Fortnight, a free-spirited, independent adventurer who possesses the only known map that could lead her to Link's secret location. With the venture, Frost also wants to gain the recognition of his/her narrow-minded discoverer. When they find what they're looking for, the creature Frost names Mr. Link turns out to be a kind-hearted and surprisingly clever, but also very lonely creature. Mr. Link believes he's the last of his kind, but a rumor gives him hope that in the mysterious place known as Shangri-La, there are other beings like him. Its cold-weather cousins, hairy, blue-skinned Yetis, are said to have built a society far removed from the threat to humanity. Together with the expedition team, Mr. Link sets off in search of this fabled paradise. After being confronted by a Yeti in the Himalayas, they are taken to Shangri-La, where they are quickly thrown into a hole to keep out intruders, especially humans. The group manages to escape. Mr. Link doesn't care about his own kind. He's more attracted to his friend Lionel, with whom he plans exciting explorations in the future.
text_a: Footprints found in the Himalayas suggest the existence of mysterious mountain dwellers. Botanist Dr. Rollason and his wife, Helen, are exploring plants in the Himalayas. The two set up their headquarters in a Buddhist monastery. Dr. Rollason is expecting an expedition led by Dr. Friend to search the mountains for the legendary Yeti. Five of them set off, but the climb is difficult. McNee steps into a bear trap designed for the Yeti. At night, a snowman comes into the camp and they shoot him with a rifle. But Dr. Friend doesn't just have scientific goals. He's greedy for profit. He wants to capture a Yeti alive and use the dead as bait. The snowmen, normally peace-loving creatures, put up a fight, driving the expedition members to madness and death. Only Dr. Rollason and his wife can return.
text_b: Sir Lionel Frost, an English gentleman, once again misses his chance to join the London Adventurers and Explorers Club by destroying his camera containing a picture of Nessie. Upon his return, he receives a mysterious letter inviting him to meet a Sasquatch. Intoxicated by this news, he makes a bet with Lord Piggot-Dunceby that if he brings back proof of the Sasquatch's existence he will be admitted to the club. He travels to the United States where he meets the author of the letter, the Sasquatch in question, whom Sir Lionel names Mr. Link (or Mr. Chaînon in Quebec) and who wishes to go to the Himalayas to meet his family and accompanied by Sir Lionel's former flame, Adelina Fortnight. For his part, Piggot-Dunceby makes sure he doesn't lose his bet and hires Willard Stenk, a hitman, to prevent the trio from succeeding.

anchor_text: Grace (Sandra Bullock), an FBI agent, is anything but graceful. My dear boy, she always expresses herself in a vulgar way. When a mysterious terrorist talks about blowing up a bomb on the day of the Miss USA election, Grace is assigned to handle the situation and infiltrate the contestants. All her colleagues, including herself, are in shock. The young woman will, however, take classes in maintaining (elegance) and speaking in record time. A virulent critic of the beauty contest, she meets passionate young women and then makes beautiful encounters. As for his colleague Eric Matthews (Benjamin Bratt), he eventually falls under his spell...
text_a: Rowena Price is a journalist who begins investigating the murder of her childhood friend Grace Clayton. During his investigation, with the collaboration of his friend and fellow computer expert Miles, he discovers that his friend had a relationship with well-known advertising executive Harrison Hill, and that she was blackmailing him by threatening to expose their clandestine relationship. So Rowena gets hired as an intern at the ad agency under the false name of Katherine to spy on Hill. But not everything is as it seems...
text_b: Gracie Hart, a rough and masculine FBI agent, must infiltrate the contestants of the Miss America pageant to thwart the threats to the girls received by the organization of the contest. To be credible, he must transform his appearance and demeanor with the help of Victor Melling, an English image curator who, to the astonishment of Eric Matthews, Gracie's colleague, succeeds in giving the agent a new image. Gracie will be able to thwart a plot to kill the winner of the pageant, and, thanks to her new and different approach to men, to win over and make Matthews fall in love with her.

anchor_text: Loki and Bartleby, two fallen angels who have been stranded on Earth for centuries, wish to return to Paradise. Today, they find a logical flaw that cleanses them of their sins, allowing them to return to heaven. But God has disappeared from Earth. His deputy, the Metatron, assigns a woman, Bethany, to make them give up their project. He's joined by two sex-crazed prophets, Jay and Silent Bob. They will be accompanied by two heavenly beings, Rufus (who claims to be the 13th apostle) and Serendipity (a former muse turned stripper). On the other hand, the demon Azrael helps the two fallen angels: if they manage to return to Paradise, it will mean that God is fallible in His decisions, and will in fact bring about the end of all things.
text_a: Two angels, Bartleby and Loki, were cast out of heaven by God and banished to Wisconsin. Azrael manipulates them by offering them the opportunity to return home through a Catholic Church teaching called plenary indulgence. If this happens it would mean the end of existence, so Mary's last descendant, Bethany, is sent to stop them. Bethany will be assisted in her mission by the thirteenth apostle, Rufus, by Serendipity, the muse of inspiration, and by two prophets, Jay and Silent Bob.
text_b: Heaven remains divided between the angels who are jealous of man and those who remain loyal to God's will. They send one of their own, Daniel, to impregnate a woman, Valerie Rosales. The child born of the union between the angel and the mortal woman will be a Nephilim, a being with the powers of an angel and the free will of a man, who is mentioned in Genesis chapter 6. Archangel Michael wants the Nephilim or Nephelin to end the war. But Gabriel is freed by Lucifer and returns to interfere with his plans, this time using the young suicidal Izzy.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of Adam Stein, the patient of a psychiatric clinic for Holocaust survivors in Israel in 1961. Former showman Stein was the only member of his family to survive persecution and concentration camps because he agreed to disguise himself as a dog for the amusement of the camp commandant. When a neglected boy who thinks he's a dog is admitted to the clinic, Stein is able to overcome his own trauma during the gradual rapprochement between him and the boy.
text_a: The film is based on the true story of Rudolf Vrba and Alfréd Wetzler, two prisoners from the Auschwitz concentration camp who manage to escape with details about the operation of the camp, including a label on a bottle of the pesticide Zyklon-B, used in the killings there. Once they cross the border into Žilina, Slovakia, the resistance asks them to write their memoirs, which later becomes known as the Vrba-Wetzler report. The pair are introduced to a representative of the Red Cross, who, despite not believing them, agrees to relay the report to the allies.[3]
text_b: In Israel, in 1961, Adam Stein was admitted to a psychiatric hospital for Holocaust survivors. He remembers his past where he was a, before the war, in Berlin, the owner of a circus but also a musician and a magician, adored by the Nazis and the public. Until the day he was sent to a concentration camp with his wife and daughter. As they are sent to their deaths, he becomes the dog of Commander Klein, accepting to be humiliated in order to survive.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of Adam Stein, the patient of a psychiatric clinic for Holocaust survivors in Israel in 1961. Former showman Stein was the only member of his family to survive persecution and concentration camps because he agreed to disguise himself as a dog for the amusement of the camp commandant. When a neglected boy who thinks he's a dog is admitted to the clinic, Stein is able to overcome his own trauma during the gradual rapprochement between him and the boy.
text_a: The early '60s. After being a clown and showman during World War II, Adam Stein (Jeff Goldblum) is now the patient of a psychiatric center located in the Israeli desert and entirely dedicated to Holocaust survivors. Deeply scarred by the traumatic experience of the war conflict, Adam lives in the anguished memory of a past marked by the oppression and madness of Nazi persecution. His sanity seems seriously compromised.
text_b: The film is based on the true story of Rudolf Vrba and Alfréd Wetzler, two prisoners from the Auschwitz concentration camp who manage to escape with details about the operation of the camp, including a label on a bottle of the pesticide Zyklon-B, used in the killings there. Once they cross the border into Žilina, Slovakia, the resistance asks them to write their memoirs, which later becomes known as the Vrba-Wetzler report. The pair are introduced to a representative of the Red Cross, who, despite not believing them, agrees to relay the report to the allies.[3]

anchor_text: Allyson (Sarah Drew), Sondra (Patricia Heaton), and Izzy (Andrea Logan White) are all stay-at-home mothers who have their own challenges. They decide to go out to dinner together in a restaurant and leave their husbands with the children. The evening will not go as planned and the mothers will start looking for a missing baby.
text_a: Allyson and her friends Sondra and Izzy want to spend a quiet evening having dinner and talking without their kids around. To accomplish this, they entrust their husbands with the task of caring for the children.
text_b: Harvey is approaching his 60th birthday, and the Fairchild family is gathering to celebrate. Man is a hypochondriac and is bound to be afflicted with a number of disorders of various kinds. His wife, Gillian, on the other hand, could be really sick -- waiting for a test result, but not telling anyone in the family.

anchor_text: Stressed by her three children and completely overworked, Allyson decides to have a long overdue bridal shower with her friends Izzy and Sondra. The three of them want to relax for a night while their husbands look after the children. After a reservation problem at the restaurant and Allison's sister-in-law, Bridget, discovers that her son, Phoenix, is missing, the evening descends into disaster.
text_a: Allyson Field writes an unhappy blog post about her constant stress and feelings of inadequacy. Though happily married with three children, Allyson is beset by anxiety and finds solace in her best friend Izzy and her mentor Sondra. Allyson's husband, Sean, recommends a night out for his stressed-out wife, so she, Izzy and Sondra plan an evening out at a fancy Chinese restaurant.
text_b: A wealthy and famous Malibuans couple, Harvey Fairchild (Jack Lemmon) and Gillian Fairchild (Julie Andrews), are forced to re-examine their lives and values on Harvey's 60th birthday. Harvey is an architect who managed to make a fortune by flattering his wealthy clients without compromising his ideals. He's got everything he could want in life, but he's still unhappy. Looking in the mirror, he sees a middle-aged man who hates himself and feels that his children don't love him. He thinks he's going to die.

anchor_text: When Kjeld and Vibeke's son moves out, they decide to buy the apartment that happened to be for sale from their student days. Kjeld sets up the apartment as it was in her student days and for a while the two relive their youth. When they wake up one morning, they find that they are actually thirty years younger.
text_a: When their son Esben leaves home, Kjeld and Vibeke decide to move into something smaller. They find out their old apartment is for sale, and they move back in to start over. Kjeld decorated the apartment as it once was and together they will relive their youthful love. But what once defined them may no longer exist, and one morning, events take a turn that none of them could have foreseen as they wake up thirty years younger.
text_b: In Tokyo, 12-year-old Akira Fukushima moves in with his mother Keiko, an irresponsible young woman, in a small apartment in the capital. The boy's three younger brothers - Kyoko, Shigeru, and Yuki - also sneaked in. One day the mother of the four boys leaves to pursue the man she has fallen in love with leaving the eldest son a small amount of money that soon runs out. As time passes, their mother disappears, the bills are not paid, and there is no money to buy food. But Akira will not give up and will look for any way to help his family.

anchor_text: In 1863, Peter was a slave on a plantation in Louisiana. After being savagely beaten by a foreman, the man decides to flee north. During the American Civil War, he joined the Union Army against Confederate troops. Photographs of the injuries inflicted on Peter will be published in the press and will serve abolitionists as a symbol of the atrocities committed in the southern states.
text_a: A film by Antoine Fuqua Inspired by the moving true story of a man willing to do anything for his family, and for freedom. When Peter, a man enslaved, risks his life to escape and return to his family, he embarks on a perilous journey of love and resilience.
text_b: Donald Peters, a little black boy, wanders the black neighborhoods looking for the affection he's been missing. Unwanted, misunderstood, and plagued by inner torment, he soon turned to crime. Imprisoned in the rehabilitation school for social misfits at Wiltwyck School, he gradually rebuilt himself there.

anchor_text: Sasha is a former medical student and taxi driver in Berlin. When he learns from his girlfriend Lina that she is pregnant with his child, his life is thrown into disarray and he makes an accident. At the hospital, he meets 87-year-old Ella Friday, who is about to have an eye operation. As Sascha learns about her medical background and the unnecessary surgery would be too much of a risk, he abducts her from the hospital. At home, Mrs. Ella, as Sasha calls her, learns that Lina and Sasha have separated. Because the police are looking for Sasha for the kidnapping, he wants to take her back to the hospital, but Mrs. Ella refuses. Instead, they drive with Sasha's roommate Klaus to an abandoned hospital in the Berlin area. Here, after World War II, Mrs. Ella had an affair with an American soldier named Jason. Sasha finds out that Jason last lived in Paris and wants to arrange a reunion. The three of them decide to go to Paris. This is where they're gonna hear that Jason moved out. At a nursing home, they learn their new address on the Breton coast. Shortly after Paris, Klaus' car breaks down. During the repair time, Klaus takes Mrs. Ella for a spin on a motorcycle. This leads to a quarrel between Klaus and Sascha, which is soon settled. Meanwhile, Mrs. Ella has gone on to Jason's residence. There she meets Anna, Jason's daughter, who tells her that Jason died the previous year. After a short period of mourning, Ella returns to Berlin with Sascha and Klaus. As soon as she arrives, Ella thanks Sascha for the good time and dies. He's finally reconciled with Lina.
text_a: Sascha is a former medical student and taxi driver in Berlin. When he learns from his girlfriend Lina that she is pregnant with him, he loses his bearings and has an accident. At the hospital, he meets 87-year-old Ella Freitag, who is scheduled for eye surgery. When Sascha learns of her medical history and that the surgery has little benefit and too much risk, he removes her from the hospital. At home, Mrs. Ella, as Sascha calls her, sees the separation of Lina and Sascha. The police are looking for Sascha for the kidnapping, he wants to take her back to the hospital, but Mrs. Ella refuses. text_b: Melanie Pröschle is 27 years old and has just moved to Karlsruhe for her first job as a teacher. Melanie is optimistic about her new life but is soon demoralized by her difficulties making friends and by her students, whom she has difficulty controlling. After a frustrated student throws chocolate milk at her, Melanie summons her mother but she minimizes the incident and Melanie tries to hide it. Melanie manages to approach Tina, a saleswoman who sells her a jacket and turns out to live in the same building as her. After a visit from Tina to her apartment, the two drink and realize that Melanie has seen the inside of Tina's apartment. They end up spying from the window on Tina's ex, Tobias, as he passes by the apartment. Melanie continues to date Tina but things quickly get awkward when Melanie spies on Tina from her apartment and becomes rather clingy. When the new semester begins, Melanie continues to have problems at school and with Tina. She is relieved when Tina invites her to the party for her birthday, but runs into Tobias outside and thoughtfully tells him he'd better not go inside. Tobias then instructed him to give Tina the flowers he had brought her. When Tina realizes that Melanie told Tobias not to come, she asks him to leave and never come to her house again. Melanie's attempts to reconnect with Tina fail and she becomes the target of Tobias and Tina's ridicule when the two catch her spying on their living room. Frustrated, Melanie began to miss classes and skipped parent-child meetings. The next day she leaves the school in the middle of a classroom, gets in her car and drives away crying. She lets go of the steering wheel and climbs into the backseat, where she calmly observes the scenery.

anchor_text: While reporting from a lunatic asylum, a young journalist (Emanuelle) discovers a disturbing case of cannibalism on a young girl found in the Amazon rainforest. She convinced the young ethnologist Marc Lester to mount an expedition. They are joined by a sister (Angela), a sixteen-year-old girl (Isabelle) and an indigenous guide. They meet a hunter (Donald Mackenzie) and his wife Peggy who are searching for a lost plane containing diamonds. The small troop is attacked by the Indians. Sister Angela is devoured, Donald and Peggy horribly tortured, Isabelle is raped by all the men of the tribe. Only Emmanuelle, Isabelle and Marc will escape to civilization.
text_a: Gloria Davis travels to the Amazon along with her brother Rudy and her friend Pat, to refute the article of a magazine that assured the existence of cannibalism in a village called Manioca. According to Gloria, cannibalism had been an invention of the conquistadors to justify their murders. In the jungle, they encounter Mike Logan and his partner Joe. Mike tells them that they were attacked by members of that village, and that their Portuguese guide was mutilated by the natives. Upon reaching the village, the youths find it almost empty, except for some indigenous people who are frightened by their presence. Joe, who was dying, confesses to Gloria and Rudy that Mike was actually looking for emeralds, and that he would have mutilated his guide in front of the villagers. The natives catch the protagonists and torture them, as revenge for Mike's murders. The only survivor is Gloria, who returns to New York to receive her doctorate after the research. However, she does not reveal the death of her friends, inventing an accident from which she would have escaped.
text_b: Emanuelle, a young reporter, decides to be admitted to a psychiatric institution to write a scoop. Once inside the asylum, the woman witnesses a particularly brutal attack. Dr. Mark Lester confides to the reporter that there have been instances of cannibalism inside the facility. Intrigued by this fact, the two set off for the Amazon, in search of an anthropophobic tribe. During the trip, the couple meets Sister Angela, Isabel, and the McKenzies. Emanuelle and Mark, meanwhile, get swept up in various erotic adventures. But the cannibals are lying in wait, ruthlessly killing the McKenzies and kidnapping Isabel to sacrifice her as a scapegoat. The reporter, discovering this, releases the girl with a ruse. Chased by the tribe, they're rescued by Lester.

anchor_text: A young woman wakes up in a coffin in the middle of a morgue. She suffered a head trauma and lost her memory, not even remembering who she was. Various clues lead her to realize that she has fallen prey to a dangerous psychopath...
text_a: A terrifying story of a young girl who wakes up in a coffin with a traumatic head injury and no memory of her identity.[2] She quickly realizes that she was kidnapped by a serial killer and must survive in an isolated rural town at night and outsmart the killer, who is determined to finish what he started.[3]
text_b: The film tells the story of a nameless woman. It's shown at the beginning of the film in a hotel in a Third World country. The actual story is told in flashback. The woman lives in London and enjoys privileges. She's not involved in politics. The woman is going to an unnamed country where terror reigns. There she meets a revolutionary woman who tells her about her life.

anchor_text: Michael Felgate is a young British auctioneer based in New York. But when he proposes to Gena, a young teacher with whom he has a perfect love affair, she refuses and runs away. Michael is stunned and decides to find out more about the young woman's family. He immediately understands Gena's reaction when he meets his father, Frank, a member of a feared Italian-American mafia family, the Graziosi family. Gena agrees to marry him, but on one condition: he must stay away from his in-laws. But for Michael, it's not going to go the way he planned.
text_a: After his latest adventures, Detective Frank Drebin has decided to retire. Now for him, it's housekeeping and cookies, but America still needs him. In fact, Rocco Dillon is planning an attack that will take place during the Academy Awards. When his former teammates ask him to rejoin the brigade, Frank hesitates, having promised Jane that it was all over. But his sense of duty as a policeman prevails, prompting him to resume his duties.
text_b: Michael Felgate is an Englishman who moved to New York, working as a manager for Cromwell's art gallery. Engaged to Gina Vitale, he asks her to marry him but she refuses, explaining desperately that his father Frank and many of his cousins and uncles are longtime gangsters affiliated with a Mafia family and fears that Michael may be involved in their shady dealings. Michael assures her that he will not be involved in any way, but right at the engagement party he is involved in smuggling laundered money and soon the FBI will be on his tail.
