anchor_text: The android Honey Kisaragi works as an office clerk. She's very naive, has human emotions and superpowers. When Sister Jill and her criminal organization, the Panther Claws, emerge to take over the world, Cutie Honey stands up to them. Honey's uncle is kidnapped by the organization, so Honey joins forces with police officer Aki and journalist Seiji Hayami.
text_a: Honey Kisaragi, a very naive office worker, is actually a gynoid capable of transforming into Cutie Honey, the Warrior of Love. When Sister Jill and her criminal organization Panther Claws appear, Cutie Honey opposes them. She'll be assisted by an inspector and a mysterious journalist.
text_b: The year is 1815. Red-haired Ginger and her sister Brigitte arrive at Fort Bailey driven by a native hunter who has saved Brigitte from dying in a bear trap. The fort is inhabited by only eleven men, who live in terror of a terrible danger that exists in this area. When they realize that very strange things are happening, they flee the fort again with the help of the Indian hunter. But it will be too late for one of them, it must be her own sister who kills her if she wants to end a curse that can plague the place forever...

anchor_text: The story relates the encounter between three anthropomorphic bears and a little girl called Golden Earrings or Golden Earring because of her blonde hair. A family of three bears, consisting of a father, a mother and their cub, live in a small house in the woods. One day, while waiting for their food to cool, the bear family went for a walk. Goldilocks accidentally discovers the empty house. Curious, she enters and visits the house. Hungry, Golden Earrings tastes the bowls of gruel and revels in that of the teddy bear, neither too hot nor too cold. Then, wanting to rest, she tries each of the three armchairs, but breaks the bear's armchair, which is neither too hard nor too soft. Finally asleep, Golden Earrings decides to go to sleep, and, after testing the three beds, finally falls asleep in the bear's one, just at his waist. The three bears return home while Goldilocks is still sound asleep. They wake her up and, depending on the version of the story, either kill her or frighten her into flight. In the most recent versions, the three bears unintentionally frighten Golden Earrings, even though they don't mean him any harm. She then runs away; the bear family resumes their interrupted breakfast, after the father bear has fixed their child's chair. In some versions, the bears go so far as to show Goldilocks the right way home. The interpretation of the story may differ as well, but may boil down to the idea that the privacy of others should be respected.
text_a: The story tells of the encounter between three anthropomorphic bears and a girl named Goldilocks because of her blonde hair. A family of bears consisting of a father, a mother and their young son live in a house in the woods. One day, waiting for their oatmeal to cool, the bear family goes for a walk. Goldilocks finds the house empty. Curious, she enters and explores the house. Because he is hungry, he first tastes the oatmeal: he likes the bear's, which is neither too hot nor too cold, drinking it all. Exploring other rooms in the house, he arrives at the room where the bear parents' chairs fell very uncomfortably because of their large size and when trying with the little bear's, he accidentally breaks it. Then she goes up to the rooms, and likewise, the beds of the big bears annoyed her, not so that of the little bear, where she fell asleep instantly. Not long after, the owners of the house arrived and were very surprised to see that someone had drunk from their oatmeal dishes, messed up their living room chairs (leaving the smaller bear's broken) and that the room was also messy. At that moment, they noticed the presence of the little girl who had fallen asleep in the bear's bed, who woke up startled to see them and ran out of the house without looking back, and when she got home she told her parents what had happened that afternoon. That day, everyone learned a valuable lesson: the bears, not to leave the entrance of their house and the girl unsafe, not to enter the houses of strangers and less, without permission.
text_b: The beautiful Cinderella, orphaned of both parents, lives with her stepmother and half-sister on a farm at the edge of the woods. Since her father's death, the young woman is harassed by her stepmother who forces her to be a servant in her own house and perform the most humble chores. One day Cinderella, escaping the hustle and bustle of the King and Queen's visit to the farm, meets the young Prince in the woods on a hunting trip and falls in love with him. The boy is now ready for marriage and the father, tired of his son's frivolity, orders a great ball to finally allow him to find a bride. The stepmother, having obtained the invitation from the King during the visit to the farm, immediately begins the preparations so that her daughter Dora, clumsy and unattractive, is the one chosen by the Prince, and forbids Cinderella to participate in the party, giving her a mountain of chores to perform. But the stepmother is unaware that Cinderella is aided by her pigeon friends and a magical twig of hazelnut, ready with its three nuts to grant her three wishes.

anchor_text: In Southey's story, three male bears - a small, a medium, and a large bear - live together in a house in the woods. Southey describes her as good-natured, trusting, harmless, clean and hospitable. Each bear has its own meal bowl, its own chair and its own bed. One day, they wander through the woods while their hot porridge is supposed to cool. An old woman (who is described in the course of the narrative as impudent, evil, cursing, ugly, dirty, as well as a tramp belonging to a reformatory) discovers the home of the bears. She looks through the window and the keyhole, unlocks the door and enters after making sure no one is home. The old woman eats the porridge of the smallest bear, then sits in his chair, which breaks as a result. On her further wanderings through the house, she finds the beds of the bears and sleeps in the smallest one. When the bears return home, the story reaches its climax. The smallest bear finds the old woman in his bed and shouts: "Someone has laid in my bed" and there she is still! The old woman screams, jumps out the window, runs away and is never seen again.
text_a: The beautiful Cinderella, orphaned of both parents, lives with her stepmother and half-sister on a farm at the edge of the woods. Since her father's death, the young woman is harassed by her stepmother who forces her to be a servant in her own house and perform the most humble chores. A family of three bears, consisting of a father, a mother and their cub, live in a small house in the woods. One day, while waiting for their food to cool, the bear family went for a walk. Goldilocks accidentally discovers the empty house. Curious, she enters and visits the house. Hungry, Golden Earrings tastes the bowls of gruel and revels in that of the teddy bear, neither too hot nor too cold. Then, wanting to rest, she tries each of the three armchairs, but breaks the bear's armchair, which is neither too hard nor too soft. Finally asleep, Golden Earrings decides to go to sleep, and, after testing the three beds, finally falls asleep in the bear's one, just at his waist. The three bears return home while Goldilocks is still sound asleep. They wake her up and, depending on the version of the story, either kill her or frighten her into flight. In the most recent versions, the three bears unintentionally frighten Golden Earrings, even though they don't mean him any harm. She then runs away; the bear family resumes their interrupted breakfast, after the father bear has fixed their child's chair. In some versions, the bears go so far as to show Goldilocks the right way home. The interpretation of the story may differ as well, but may boil down to the idea that the privacy of others should be respected.

anchor_text: Thurgood and his friends Brian and Scarface are forced to trade medical marijuana to buy their friend Kenny out of prison. He had earlier accidentally killed a diabetic police horse with candy. The marijuana was stolen from the lab where Thurgood works as a janitor. They set up a company, Mr. Nice Guy (a nod to Howard Marks, who controlled much of the marijuana trade in the United States in the 1970s and 1980s), sell the drug, and quickly become very popular with customers. Thurgood's life gets into some turmoil when his anti-drug girlfriend Mary Jane (a stage name variant of marijuana) finds out that he works for Mr. Nice Guy. Soon the lives of the others are in danger as well, when local drug dealer Samson Simpson notices that Mr. Nice Guy is ruining his business, including trading with his former clients, including the rapper Sir Smoke-a-Lot (a reference to Sir Mix-a-Lot).
text_a: Thurgood (Chapelle), Brian (Breuer) and Scarface (Diaz) following the arrest of their friend and roommate Kenny (Williams), accused of killing police horse Ranuncolo, are forced to deal marijuana in order to pay the $100,000 ransom. They set up a dealing company, Bravo Boy, selling marijuana that Thurgood recovers at work, a pharmaceutical company that conducts laboratory research for the government and big corporations and, as a result, also works with drugs. Their business becomes very popular, so much so that they find among their clients even very famous people; however, the circumstances for the group prove to be adverse. First of all in private life the three begin to have problems, first of all Thurgood, who is engaged to a girl opposed to any kind of drug and any form of its use, ironically called Mary Jane (English slang for "marijuana"). The biggest problem, however, is the enmity that the group creates with the dealer Samson Simpson, from whom it is stealing more and more customers. Simpson forces them to pay $20,000 a week, threatening to kill them, and the three, desperate, enlist the help of the police, agreeing to release Kenny if they could turn over the powerful drug dealer. After a series of bizarre and controversial events, the trio succeeds: Kenny is released and Thurgood gives up drugs for his beloved Mary Jane.
text_b: A bandit, Joe Burdette, has been arrested in Rio Bravo by John T. Chance, the town sheriff, for murder with the help of his deputies, a murder that Sheriff John T. Chance and one of his deputies, Dude, witnessed it. Nathan Burdette, the wealthy landowner and his brother, wants to save him from the gallows and seizes the city and the city jail with the help of his henchmen. He also murders anyone who wants to help the sheriff in his quest to bring Joe to justice. The sheriff and his deputies notice over time how the pressure from their enemies increases as the day approaches when the state authorities must come to town to take Joe away, realizing that they must stick together and overcome their personal limitations if they are to get out of the situation. Finally, after two failed attempts to free him, his henchmen manage to kidnap Dude and are willing to trade him for Joe. Aware that they can't afford it because the rich landowner would kill them for having witnessed everything that happened so that he could end up saving Joe, they decide to face death against them once they manage to free Dude. After releasing Dude in that exchange and preventing Joe from escaping to his brother, the fight to the death begins and after a long gunfight between both sides the sheriff and his men manage to defeat them and arrest them thanks to the advantage of having dynamite. The victory causes the remaining henchmen of the landowner to flee and thus the siege of the city and the prison end, restoring peace to the city.

anchor_text: Maxim Kammerer, whose career was informed by the books The Inhabited Island and A Bug in the Ants' Nest, is now 89 years old. In his memoirs, he looks back on the events surrounding the "Great Apocalypse", an event that took place toward the end of the 23rd century. The great revelation profoundly shocked mankind and brought about events that no one had anticipated. But most of all, it ends the eerie stagnation that threatens to suffocate the world of "Noon". Kammerer, formerly a free-lance searcher looking for alien civilizations in space, is now a Progressor whose mission is to support alien civilizations and accompany them in their development, and serves on a Commission for the Protection of the Earth. Again and again, Kammerer comes into contact with the effects of human progress, and so the final consequence is not surprising - at least not to him, even if at first he is the only one who recognizes the situation. Many circumstances and motives of the action remain in the dark or are not conclusively justified.
text_a: The novel is written in the form of a documentary (a bit of a diary and a bit of a collection of investigative reports) by a now eighty-nine-year-old Maksim Kammerer. At some unspecified time in the future mankind colonized the solar system and even reached other planets where they came into contact with other intelligent alien races. The tranquillity and peace that has been established are, however, endangered by the supposed influence on human affairs (past and present) of the mysterious "Time Wanderers", whose existence, however, has not been proven. The "Wanderers" are mentioned in many other earlier works by the authors but only in this book does one come to clearly reveal their identity, provenance and purposes. Maksim Kammerer, a member of COMCON-2, the world's security system, is the head of the EI (Unusual Events) Bureau, and entrusts an investigation into the Vagabonds to Toivo Glumov, one of his brightest agents. One of Glumov's first stops is a small town in the Russian Arctic where horrible but harmless creatures have appeared and terrorized the inhabitants. The novel is rich in references to many other earlier works by the authors and represents a sort of final seal. Many characters and settings that have already appeared in other novels and short stories are mentioned. For example, among the many, there are references to Planetary Catastrophe, the Inhabited Island, It's Hard to be a God.
text_b: Two Soviets, Uncle Vova and Ghedevan, Skripač (nickname that in Russian means "violinist") meet a man on the street in Moscow who looks like a tramp. This asks them for the number of their planet and shows a device for traveling through space and time. The protagonists take him for a madman, press the button of the device and immediately find themselves in a sandy desert. The two discover that they have stumbled upon another planet, called Pluk, in the Kin-dza-dza galaxy, populated by humanoid inhabitants, who are able to communicate in any human language. Their civilization is more advanced in many technologies - they fly in UFO-like aircraft, they use very effective weapons - and they have telepathic abilities. But society and the planet are in dire straits. The whole planet is a sandy desert with ruins of rusty buildings and constructions. The society of Pluk has barbarian or medieval relations. The protagonists try to find a way back to Earth, stumbling upon the strange customs of the local society and, encountering no help, are deceived by many people. For starters, they have a box of matches, which are a very valuable thing on Pluk. At one point their fellow plukan Uef and Bi stole their last match, and were immediately arrested by an ecilop (police officer) and put in the ezikh (prison). Later Uncle Vova and Skripac, having the chance to return to Earth, twice decide to stay on Pluk and help their comrades. They don't understand sincere motives, and Uef asks Uncle Vova: - Tell the truth for once in your life! Why didn't you move in with that idiot when you had the chance? What did you want? Raspberry shorts? The PG pool? Tell me about it! The plukani themselves promise to bring back the protagonists only in exchange for many matches.

anchor_text: At the age of 45, the famous novelist Charles Dickens had an unknown love affair with Nelly Ternan, an 18-year-old actress. This adulterous affair would last for thirteen years, until Dickens' death.
text_a: The secret love affair between the writer Charles Dickens and the "invisible woman" Ellen Ternan, also known as Nelly Ternan, which would remain Dickens's greatest secret for thirteen years, until his death.
text_b: Nick Charles and his wife Nora go with their son to the villa of a colonel threatened with death who will be killed shortly after. As always, they'll solve the mystery.

anchor_text: Lagos, in the present day: Bambino and Bawa meet and instantly fall in love. On long walks through the city, the two men develop a deep affection for each other. But in a society where homosexuality is taboo, Bambino and Bawa must recognize the pressures of social norms.
text_a: Two men, Bambino and Bawa, meet at a photography competition in Lagos. They have feelings for each other, but they don't dare express them in the social atmosphere.
text_b: Tiyaa is a young woman from the aristocratic Fulani[2] who studied in Paris and has returned to her native region in the Sultanate of Damagaramr in Niger.[3] She is waiting for the young man she met in Paris, from a prestigious family in the emirate of Maiduguri,[4] to travel to Niger to propose to her. Tiyaa has an opportunity to learn the culture of her country, mainly by talking to other women.[5]

anchor_text: Gary arrives in New York City, where shortly after arriving he rescues drag queen Kid Joey, who is attacked by a group of homophobic people. The newcomer later meets the male porn star Valentino. The friendship becomes a homosexual relationship. Valentino also has a relationship with waitress Mary Carmen. Valentino falls ill with AIDS and is admitted to a hospital, where he is visited by Gary and Mary Carmen.
text_a: This film is a version of the Cinderella tale in a modern setting, with Mary Santiago (Selena Gomez), a high school student with the ambition of becoming a professional dancer, taking on the role of Cinderella. Tammy (Jessica Parker Kennedy), Mary's best friend, as her fairy godmother. Dominique Blatt (Jane Lynch), taking on the role of the stepmother. Britt (Emily Perkins) and Bree (Katharine Isabelle), as the two stepsisters, and Joey Parker (Drew Seeley), now a celebrity who has returned to school for his senior year, and to remember why he started dancing, like the prince. In this story, instead of the glass slipper, what Mary (Cinderella) loses is an MP3 during her high school prom, in which she dances with Joey.
text_b: Valentino, he's a bisexual porn actor who's having an affair with waitress Mary Carmen. When Valentino meets Gary, a passion is born between the two, and Gary and Mary Carmen will compete for Valentino's love, living a sort of ménage à trois. The two of them will have to put aside their grudges when Valentino gets AIDS.

anchor_text: At the end of the 19th century, a young farmer named Snug buys two mules from his employer that no one seems to be able to control. The boss's daughter, Rad, and he become romantically involved. Snug's gonna have to tame all these little people...
text_a: After being orphaned, Eli and Joshua leave Gatling and travel to Chicago to be raised by a foster family. Eli, however, has brought the seeds of evil from the countryside and begins to cultivate corn behind his house with the intent of spreading the worship of "He Who Walks in the Grain" even among the young people of the city.
text_b: On a farm, a man named Daniel, nicknamed "Snug", has to take care of mules while he's in love with the farmer's daughter. Eventually he will be able to tame the animals and win the girl's heart.

anchor_text: It's set in Manhattan. Syd is meeting with the Bateman he's never met before, apparently a friend's contact, to buy cocaine from him. The two meet at a bar and then get in Syd's car to close the deal. Syd invites Bateman to a party and eventually convinces him to come along. It's a going-away party for his ex-girlfriend London, who apparently wants to move to Los Angeles with her new boyfriend. Syd didn't know about this, and she's not invited to the party. Still, he's going with Bateman. The party's at Rebecca's parents' place. Rebecca is a friend from London. When Syd and Bateman arrive, they go straight to the bathroom to coke. An almost half-hour dialogue-laden scene begins, which takes place exclusively in the bathroom. Supporting actors come and go. The red thread is both the drugs that seem to be consumed by most of the partygoers and the conversation between Bateman and Syd. This conversation is about drugs and sexual preference. It's very emotional, full of outbursts. In the course of the conversation, Syd tells Bateman of his sorrow at not being with London anymore, and Bateman reveals that he suffers from impotence. Characteristic is Bateman's statement towards the end of the film: "The best conversation is always with a stranger", by which he means that the deep conversation between him and Syd was a fulfilling mutual exchange. This quote was also chosen as the subtitle for the German DVD version.
text_a: Syd (Evans) wakes up after a drug and alcohol binge to receive a phone call from a friend who informs him that his ex-girlfriend London (Biel) will be moving to California in a few days with her new boyfriend, and that there will be a going-away party that same evening. Although not invited, Syd decides to attend anyway, accompanied by Bateman (Statham), a banker who also sells cocaine. Bateman's carrying a large quantity of the drug. After arriving at the party located in Rebecca's (Isla Fisher) parents' apartment, he and Syd settle into the bathroom, where they inhale large doses of cocaine, while drinking tequila and discussing philosophical topics about love, sex, and emotional damage. Syd was never able to tell London that he loved her, while Bateman is impotent. This party is soon joined by Maya (Kelli Garner) and Mallory (Bryant), who get high with them. When Syd hears that London has arrived, he starts talking and talking until Bateman encourages him to go talk to him.
text_b: New York, the middle of the '60s. Bernardo is a shy, sensitive and somewhat clumsy nineteen-year-old, dependent on his parents. He works as a clerk in the library of his father who, not happy with his work conduct, decides to send him to live alone in a furnished room; despite this, the mother still remains in contact with the owner of the apartment to obtain information about the behavior of her son. Bernardo, still a virgin, one day loses his mind and becomes completely confused by the whimsical Barbara Darlyn, not realizing the attentions of Amy, his father's secretary. So he decides to write a letter to Barbara which invites him to come to the dressing room after the show at the theater. Struck by Bernardo's peculiarity, and after spending a night with him (without concluding anything) she induces him to move permanently to her. In reality Barbara is just an unstable girl and treats Bernard as if he were a puppet in her hands; but Bernard understands that Barbara is just a woman and he is just a man, and after discovering Barbara's "betrayal" with his colleague, he explodes, and goes on all fury trying to burn the bible jealously kept by his father in the library. He spends the night in jail for the above-mentioned stunt, gets out thanks to Amy, who pays the bail, and then now disillusioned but lucid decides to partner with Amy.

The two meet at a bar and then get in Syd's car to close the deal. Syd invites Bateman to a party and eventually convinces him to come along. Characteristic is Bateman's statement towards the end of the film: "The best conversation is always with a stranger", by which he means that the deep conversation between him and Syd was a fulfilling mutual exchange. This quote was also chosen as the subtitle for the German DVD version.
text_a: This is New York. Syd is devastated by the end of his two-year relationship with the sexy and charming London and drowns his despair in the use of drugs and alcohol. One evening, while at the bar, he accidentally learns that a farewell party has been arranged for London who is about to move, a party to which he was not invited. So he invites himself, dragging along Bateman, a drug dealer he met at the bar. The two men, arriving early, lock themselves in the bathroom and fill themselves with alcohol and drugs, with the participation of two girls in turn. The two begin a long conversation with each other: both are in crisis for the same reasons and confess to each other. Bateman informs him of all his sadomasochistic experiences, until he gets to the real reason that pushed him down that road, namely his impotence: his wife told him to his face that the substitute was more virile than him, because even though in love he fails to have relations with the woman he loves and who loves him, and this is the cause of an extreme frustration that leads him to verbal exasperation towards Syd. Syd in turn gets to the knot that caused his personal crisis: never being able to say "I love you" to his woman. In short, the crisis of two men, with a lot of self-analysis and a hopeful ending: Syd finally finds the courage to go downstairs and talk to London. They leave the party after a fight, and in the car he apologizes to her for all his shortcomings and admits his mistakes. They spend the night together but in the morning she has to leave.
text_b: New York, the middle of the '60s. Bernardo is a shy, sensitive and somewhat clumsy nineteen-year-old, dependent on his parents. He works as a clerk in the library of his father who, not happy with his work conduct, decides to send him to live alone in a furnished room; despite this, the mother still remains in contact with the owner of the apartment to obtain information about the behavior of her son. He spends the night in jail for the above-mentioned stunt, gets out thanks to Amy, who pays the bail, and then now disillusioned but lucid decides to partner with Amy.

anchor_text: Jackie is an adventurer who collects antique artifacts from around the world, including pieces of medieval armor, which he auctions off in Austria. But then his old friend Alan asks him to help him. His girlfriend, Lorelei, whom he'd meticulously hooked up with Jackie a few years ago, was kidnapped by a dangerous cult. It demands the parts of the armor of God, both those sold and those of the new owner, as ransom money. So Jackie tracks down the buyer of his pieces of armor - the local Count Bannon - and borrows them from him, promising to return them to him along with the rest of the pieces in the cult's possession. He agrees, but on the condition that Jackie take his daughter May on the dangerous journey. Together, the trio embarks on an adventurous journey through Europe. In Yugoslavia, the adventurer's pack is finally found. At the headquarters of the drug-dealing cult, hidden in a twisted cave system inside a mountain, Jackie and Alan are able to free Lorelei and take out the criminal gang.
text_a: Jackie, a great adventurer, is appointed by Count Bannon, a millionaire antiquities collector, to find the missing pieces of an ancient religious artifact, the Armour of God. Meanwhile, the followers of this religion want their treasure back and kidnap Jackie's best friend, Lorelei, to use it as a bargaining chip. Jackie wishes to borrow the parts of the Armour of God from Bannon in exchange for Lorelei, but Bannon agrees only if his daughter May accompanies him to keep an eye on the Armour. Lorelei's fiance, Jackie's best friend, insists on going with them. Together, they will devise a plan to free Lorelei while maintaining, and even completing, the Armour of God.
text_b: Tomás (Jackie Chan) and David (Biao Yuen) are cousins who run a fast food outlet in Barcelona. The food is delivered by Tomás, who moves around the square on a skateboard. After a clash with bikers, they go about their business as if nothing had happened. They visit David's father, who is in a mental institution, and they meet Sylvia, the daughter of David's father's friend. Tom encourages David to ask her out, cowering and making the excuse that she would have turned him down anyway. Late at night, back at the food stall, Tom runs into Sylvia, who pretends to be a prostitute, being in fact a pickpocket, robbing a man in his room and fleeing to the food stall. Tom and David have fallen in love with her. After allowing her to stay in their apartment that night, they wake up to discover that Sylvia has stolen their money. The next day, they run into Moby, a babbling private investigator, who is also stalking Sylvia. Later they discover that she is the heiress of a large sum, which she tries to steal from a criminal gang. When she is kidnapped, Thomas, David, and Moby band together to save her, infiltrating the villain's castle, defeating them all in martial arts combat. The final scene of the film shows David, Tomás and Sylvia reunited. Sylvia asks them for a summer job, and Moby asks them if they want to work as private detectives with him, something they decline.

anchor_text: Jackie, a great adventurer, is appointed by Count Bannon, a millionaire antiquities collector, to find the missing pieces of an ancient religious artifact, the Armour of God. Meanwhile, the followers of this religion want their treasure back and kidnap Jackie's best friend, Lorelei, to use it as a bargaining chip. Jackie wishes to borrow the parts of the Armour of God from Bannon in exchange for Lorelei, but Bannon agrees only if his daughter May accompanies him to keep an eye on the Armour. Lorelei's fiance, Jackie's best friend, insists on going with them. Together, they will devise a plan to free Lorelei while maintaining, and even completing, the Armour of God.
text_a: Two Chinese immigrants, Thomas and David, run a food truck in Barcelona. When the two meet the pickpocket Sylvia, they immediately fall in love with her and of course also help her when she is pursued by a creepy man. It turns out that this private investigator is tasked with finding Sylvia, as she is the illegitimate daughter of a Spanish nobleman who has just died and left her an inheritance worth millions. The only catch is that Sylvia has to claim her inheritance within two weeks. But since Sylvia's evil uncle is also after the money, he hires some villains to kidnap Sylvia and keep her imprisoned in a castle until the deadline expires. When Thomas and David learn of this, they team up with private investigator Moby and break into the castle to free Sylvia. Although David is captured, they manage to join forces and defeat the villains.
text_b: Asian Hawk, an adventurous mercenary treasure hunter (with a past as the lead singer of the band "The Losers"), on a mission to retrieve the mythological Armor of God, is joined by his fellow adventurer Alan and the beautiful May, daughter of Count Banon. The cult that owns the armor and sword of God, has kidnapped Lorelai, Alan's current girlfriend, as well as Asian's ex. In a castle in the heart of a Slavic forest takes place the final duel between Asian and the perverted monks of the sect.

anchor_text: Vinay is the son of the chief of police and is very knowledgeable about gems. Therefore, he also works for the jeweler Vishambar, whose daughter Anjali soon loses her heart to the young Vinay. But at Anjali's birthday party, Shalini claims she is engaged to Vinay and falls for her 'Amar'. The turbulence won't last long, because Vinay can playfully prove he's not Amar. There does appear to be a doppelganger named Amar, but he's a notorious jewel thief. Soon the jewel thief Amar disguises himself as Vinay and robs Vishambar. This does not sit well with Vinay and decides to capture the jewel thief by posing as Amar. After some time, it turns out that Shalini's big brother Arjun Singh is involved. Once posing as a friend, Arjun tries to get Vinay out of the way and has him drugged out of action so that Vinay really believes he is Amar. Soon Vinay pretends to be in front of Amar and can skillfully arrest Arjun and his gang.
text_a: Ali, a plasterer from Cairo, is tormented in hell by his quarrelsome wife, Fatma, a true Xanthippe. He wants to escape this marital tyranny today rather than tomorrow and dreams of a life at the sultan's court in luxury. One day, he comes into possession of a magic pipe, and all of his desires are suddenly within the realm of possibility. Quickly, Ali leaves home and hearth and travels on a ship as a blind passenger to the Sultan's capital. In the residence, Ali sees the commander, Prince Hussein, returning from a victorious battle, with Prince Ahmed as his personal prisoner. From his hiding place, Ali watches as the Sultan's favorite wife, Sobeide, and his daughter Gylnare raise the beautiful Achmed. When Ali is discovered in his hiding place, he is introduced to Prince Hussein. Hussein takes Ali into his service. But now the little plasterer gets caught up in a series of court intrigues and adventures. He is introduced by the court astrologer to the sultan as a prince from distant lands who wants to ask for the hand of the beautiful sultan's daughter Gylnare. However, Ali soon comes into conflict with Prince Achmed, who himself desires Gylnare and gets away with it. Thanks to his wishes fulfilled by the miracle pipe, Ali now gets into one mess after another; eventually they even want to hang him. Thanks to his magic pipe, Ali and Ahmed escape their terrible fate, and the princely couple is allowed to stay together. When Ali wakes up from his whistle-blowing nightmare due to a sonorous slap in the face from his quarrelsome wife, everything is back to normal.
text_b: Vinay (Dev Anand), is a normal young man who finds himself confused with a jewel thief named Amar who looks just like him. Vinay works with the police to impersonate Amar and end his crime, but it seems that Amar has also decided to impersonate Vinay, and soon their true identities become totally entangled. Vinay lives with his father, a police commissioner and his mother in Mumbai. Vinay's father is very upset with him, as he has not learned any skill and spends all his time examining stones and jewels. The town is being held for ransom by a daring jewel thief, who continues to confuse them endlessly. Vinay's family gets a pleasant surprise when they discover that he has landed a job with a prominent jeweler by the name of Vishamber Nath. Vinay also meets Nath's attractive daughter, Anju, and a romance begins between the two. But this is short-lived when several people greet Vinay as "Amar" or "Prince Amar". Things start to get worse when a young woman named Shalini shows up and claims Vinay is her fiancé. Vinay is able to prove to them that he is, in fact, Vinay, and cooperates with them to locate the elusive Amar, only to find himself embroiled in a devious plan, which robs him of his sanity, causes him to lose his memory, identity, and makes him a target of all the police in India, particularly in the then independent Kingdom of Sikkim.

anchor_text: Gino, a handsome young mechanic but also an unemployed vagabond, stops at a roadside cafe-garage-gas station to eat. The cook of this inn, named Giovanna, is also the young wife of Bragana, the owner of the trattoria, a rich, rugged and handsome man. Between Gino and Giovanna, it's love at first sight. The girl, in love, devises a ruse to keep him with her: she makes her husband think he didn't pay for the meal. Gino, as an arrangement, then offers his help to repair Bragana's vehicle. That night Giovanna and Gino are lovers. Gino proposes to Giovanna to leave with her, but, for fear of falling back into the misery she once knew, she decides to return to her husband, leaving Gino to continue on his way. Gino goes to Ancona and dreams of boarding a ship. On his way, Gino will meet the Spaniard, who will pay for his train ticket. Later, Gino meets Giovanna and Bragana at the fair in Ancona. The boss convinces Gino to return to the inn: on the way back the two lovers kill him and cover up their crime by faking an accident. This murder that was supposed to bring them together separates them: Gino, thinking that Giovanna used him to kill Bragana and thus get the insurance money, leaves with a young prostitute, Anita (met in Ferrara). But soon, they reconcile (in addition Giovanna announces her pregnancy) and, knowing that they are being watched, they decide to flee. While they are on the road, the car skids and Giovanna is killed.
text_a: The vagabond Gino Costa stops at a restaurant for travelers in the lower Padana, becoming the lover of Giovanna, wife of the unknown Giuseppe, owner of the store. Gino can't stand this situation and proposes to the woman to run away with him. Giovanna refuses and he leaves for Ancona, which attracts him by the presence of the port: he hopes to embark and leave behind the story just concluded. During the journey to Ancona he befriends a drifter called the Spaniard. Gino no longer boards, but finds work with his new friend at the May Fair; a new life seems to have begun. During the days of the fair, Gino still meets Giovanna and her husband, who had come to Ancona to participate in a singing competition. The two ex-lovers immediately re-establish their bond and, indeed, decide to kill Joseph through the simulation of a car accident. They soon put their plan into action, but the incident arouses the police's suspicions. After the murder, the story between the two lovers becomes tense: Giovanna collects her husband's life insurance and reopens her husband's trattoria together with Gino. Gino, overwhelmed by remorse and the disappointment of a life he feels stolen, leaves her and leaves for Ferrara where he befriends Anita, a sweet and understanding prostitute. He then sees Giovanna again, who tells him she is pregnant; the two lovers then try to escape, but the car ends up off the road, Giovanna dies and Gino is arrested by the police.
text_b: Luciano and Leonardo are two friends, eccentric owners of a vintage car garage, roommates and partners in erotic adventures. Their daily ménage and their friendship are called into question when Leonardo's favorite car, a Rolls-Royce Silver Wraith, is rented for the wedding of a beautiful girl from Dovera Giovanna, daughter of the commendator Marco Franzoni. Leonardo personally takes the car to Giovanna's house. Between the girl and Leonardo there is the classic lightning strike and the planned wedding goes down the drain. But Luciano doesn't take it well. He begins an intense work of sabotage: first he disrupts a romantic dinner between the two, then he gives defamatory information to Giovanna about Leonardo's account, until Giovanna stops answering the phone, finally he warns Leonardo about the problems of marriage. Leonardo, however, goes to Giovanna, the two kiss and later marry. Once at the home of the two friends for the first wedding night, Leonardo, in the evening, sends Luciano away. Luciano, left homeless, goes to the garage to spend the night. There, he is tempted to take revenge on Leonardo's Rolls, but at the last moment he changes his mind and saves the car and the saloon from a fire he set by mistake. Leonardo and Giovanna are warned by the doctor that Luciano is in the hospital seriously ill with burns. Here they come, and they find Luciano dying. Leonardo, who has made a similar joke to his friend in the past, pretends not to believe in what to him is a set-up. Out of the hospital, sitting in the car with Giovanna, he no longer holds back the tears showing the pain for the imminent loss of his dear friend, when suddenly, from the back seat, Luciano emerges alive and well. Leonardo and Giovanna start to laugh.

anchor_text: The tramp Gino meets at a gas station and Trattoria Giovanna Bragana, the young wife of the already elderly owner. A passionate affair develops between Gino and Giovanna. However, Gino then travels to Ancona: he does not want to continue building on a troubled relationship and the port of Ancona is for him the hope of a better future. But fate brings the two lovers together again. Gino then kills Bragana. But the guilt of murder is unbearable. Gino leaves his mistress and goes to Ferrara. In this town, Gino meets a friendly prostitute. He hopes to put the past behind him.
text_a: Leonardo and Luciano, single men in their mid-40's, operate a vintage car rental business together, live together, and also spend their free time together, often in the company of young women. One day, when the daring Leonardo drives the white Rolls-Royce rented as a wedding car to a mansion to pick up the young bride Giovanna, he spontaneously kisses her. Arriving at the wedding party, he advises her against marrying her chosen one. He drives off with the bride, chased by the groom on a motorcycle that crashes. Giovanna reciprocates Leonardo's strong affection and a love story develops, against which Luciano intervenes by portraying Giovanna in a bad light and telling untruths to Leonardo. However, this ultimately fails, Leonardo and Giovanna marry and Leonardo compliments Luciano from the apartment.
text_b: Giovanna (Clara Calamai) is a beautiful woman married to the owner of a restaurant, Giuseppe (Juan de Landa). Her life will change when she meets Gino (Massimo Girotti), a drifter who breaks into the marriage to become her lover. In the whirlwind of lust, they both agree to murder Giovanna's husband and thus finally find happiness. Nevertheless, remorse 'among other things' must end the couple's future. The tramp will meet the rigour of the law by being convicted of manslaughter after being found not guilty in a real murder case.

anchor_text: At the end of the 19th century, Buck the dog lives in the Santa Clara Valley south of San Francisco. One day, he is kidnapped by his owner's gardener for gambling debts and taken to the Klondike region of Alaska as a sled dog. Throughout history, the "call of the wild" has been heard more and more. After many adventures with constantly changing sleigh drivers, Buck is rescued by John Thornton. The two of them will be inseparable. After the death of its last master, the dog, now unattached to humans, finally follows this call and joins a pack of wolves as its leader. The book found its sequel in Jerry the Islander and Michael, Jerry's brother.
text_a: At the end of the 19th century, in Canada. The gold rush is in full swing, and hundreds of prospectors need sled dogs to get to the snowy sites of the Klondike. One of these dogs was Buck, a dog stolen from a wealthy owner and sold to John Thornton, who was commissioned by the government to deliver mail to these inhospitable areas. Together, they will face a thousand dangers, challenge a particularly hostile nature, be attacked by wolves, fight Indians, and become the best friends in the world.
text_b: Buck is a California dog owned by Judge Miller. He was once kidnapped from his master by the judge's gardener assistant, Manoel, and sold to a sled dog dealer. Soon faced with the brutality of his new life, Buck must find the strength to survive and adapt to the cold of Alaska and the Yukon. When he is confronted by the other dogs in the pack, he learns to steal meat and to fight to survive. He is often sold until he becomes the dog of a respectable master, John Thornton. But when his master is killed by a Native American tribe, Buck becomes a wolf again and kills some of the assassins. Returning to the wild in the middle of the Wild, the great northern Canadian forest, he mingles with a pack of wolves and becomes their leader. Images of death, cruelty, and Darwinian allusions to the "struggle for life" are omnipresent throughout the narrative. London describes the jungle of the Wild as a world dominated by fear ( The salient thing of this other world seemed fear ).

anchor_text: The cartoonist Frenzy lives and works in Munich, lives in a studio-like warehouse and is in a creative crisis. Her sketches are quite pessimistic, which is why she urges her editor-in-chief to draw more optimistic stories, otherwise she risks losing her job. Frenzy's best friend Maischa, meanwhile, has moved her boyfriend Klaus, with whom she was supposed to go to a conference over the weekend. Instead, she wants to use the weekend to go out and celebrate. Both visit an exhibition where Maischa is fond of the handsome René. Despite her unsuccessful flirtation that night, Frenzy gets to know more about René through a friend. So they arrange a meeting for the next day. In the meantime, have Frenzy take care of Mark, a friend of René's, who's staying with him. Although Frenzy initially considers this a waste of time and annoyedly takes Mark under her wing, the day together with him in Munich develops very pleasant and fun. Meanwhile, Maischa, having eaten with René, has landed at this man's house and is sleeping with him. When Frenzy comes home at night and finds Maischa already in her bathtub, she tells of the disastrous sex with René. Frenzy and Mark meet again in the coming days and fall in love. The budding relationship becomes a challenge for both of them, as Mark lives and works elsewhere and is rarely in Munich. Maischa, meanwhile, breaks up with her boyfriend and tries to get along with herself for the first time in her life.
text_a: In a foggy and vague central European city, a crazed strangler is on the loose and in the middle of the night a group of hysterical citizens throw poor, insignificant clerk Kleinman out of bed to join them in hunting him down. He has no idea what to do, nor do the others seem to, but he only hopes never to come face to face with the murderer. Soon suspicion falls on him, and so his night becomes a grotesque and surreal sequence of events where he finds himself having to defend himself from the hysteria of the townspeople. Meanwhile, the circus arrives in the city, where other stories unfold in parallel: Paul, a clown, has a love affair with the trapeze artist Marie, and so his wife Irmy, a sword-eater, decides to pack up and heads to the city, where she finds herself the unwilling protagonist of an intricate series of episodes that eventually leads her to a house of tolerance where she meets a group of prostitutes and is mistaken for one of them by a romantic college student, who pays her the exorbitant sum of 700 dollars for the service; but she is arrested because she does not have a license and there she meets Kleinman, who, chased by the angry mob, finds refuge in the circus, where she later meets the killer. Irmy finally meets her husband, who in the meantime had gone to look for her, finds an abandoned baby girl next to her mother's corpse on the street (another victim of the strangler) and takes her with him. Kleinman, on the other hand, decides to go with the circus where he will assist the magician Armstead, his idol (Kleinman is also an amateur magician).
text_b: The cartoonist Frenzy lives in Munich, she's going through a creative crisis. Her sketches are rather pessimistic, which is why she asks her editor-in-chief to make more optimistic reports, otherwise she is threatened with losing her job. Mascha, Frenzy's best friend, is posing as her boyfriend Klaus, with whom she was supposed to go to a meeting over the weekend. Instead, she wants to spend the weekend hanging out and having fun. They both go to an exhibition where Maisha looks tenderly at the handsome René. Frenzy finds out more about Rene through a friend. So, the next day, an agreement is made between the two women. Frenzy has to pick up Mark, a friend of René's, who was at the exhibition. Although Frenzy considers this a waste of time and takes an annoyed Mark under her wing, the common day with him in Munich turns out to be very pleasant and funny. Meanwhile, Maisha has dinner with René, goes home and sleeps with him. When Frenzy comes home at night and finds Maisha already in her bathtub, she recounts the disastrous sex with Rene. Frenzy and Mark meet again in the following days and fall in love. The relationship becomes a challenge for them, since Mark lives and works elsewhere and rarely comes to Munich. Maisha breaks up with her boyfriend and tries to come to terms with herself in her life.

anchor_text: Just as the couple Linda and Paxton were about to separate, a lockdown is imposed in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic, forcing the two to continue living together in their London home. Eventually, they come up with a plan to pull off a high-risk jewelry heist at Harrods that could also bring them closer together as humans.
text_a: The story of three housekeepers who worked in the Federal Reserve Building and who one day decided to rob the institution, determined to make more money than their night work would allow them to claim. But nothing went as planned.
text_b: In London, Linda and Paxton are in the middle of a separation. But due to the COVID-19 pandemic and the lockdown, they will have to live together for a while longer, between telecommuting and multiple arguments, twenty-four hours a day. So they come up with a plan to rob the jewelry store of the luxury department store Harrods.

anchor_text: Just as the couple Linda and Paxton were about to separate, a lockdown is imposed in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic, forcing the two to continue living together in their London home. text_a: The story of three housekeepers who worked in the Federal Reserve Building and who one day decided to rob the institution, determined to make more money than their night work would allow them to claim. But nothing went as planned.
text_b: Paxton and Linda are an unhappy married couple living in London during the lockdown caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. Paxton is only able to get a job as a delivery truck driver due to an assault arrest 10 years earlier and is devastated by how his life ended.

anchor_text: On their way back to Athens, Theseus and his friend Demetrius save a beautiful young woman, Ariane, from death. Demetrius finds a striking resemblance between Ariana and her queen, Phaedra, who rules over Crete. As for Theseus, he immediately falls in love with her and goes to present her to his father, the king of Athens. But Phaedrus ordered Demetrius to eliminate Ariane. He can't force himself and confides in Theseus. He then decides to travel to Crete to meet with Phaedrus and find out why the queen is so eager to see his beloved dead.
text_a: In Crete, the beautiful and evil Princess Phaedra (Rosanna Schiaffino) sends the officer Chiron (Alberto Lupo) to murder her twin Ariadne (Rossana Schiaffino), who grew up with a peasant family, as the mother of both, Pasiphae (Elena Zareschi), decided to hide her so that she would not be sacrificed to the Minotaur as the Cretan law dictated. However, Chiron fails in his mission when the son of the king of Athens, Theseus (Bob Mathias, in real life a celebrated athlete of the time), and his Cretan friend Demetrius (Rik Battaglia) save Ariadne from death. Theseus brings the young woman to Athens, and the two fall in love, but when Phaedra's plan persists, Theseus, with Demetrius' help, must travel to Crete to defeat his enemies and confront the monstrous Minotaur.
text_b: Hercules' foster parents are murdered, he sets off for Troy himself. There he falls in love with Cassiopeia. Since the godfather Zeus has promised her to King Minos, he is enraged and prevents the faithful togetherness by hurling Hercules into the sea. He then has to deal with the sorceress Circe, a hydra, and the underworld before finally reaching Crete. There, he challenges Minos, who, as he has found out, is also responsible for the death of his parents, to a duel and, after its successful outcome, can save Cassiopeia from the clutches of the Phoenix with the help of a magic sword. After all, all of Crete is burning.

anchor_text: Phaedra, the daughter of the Cretan king Minos, orders her beloved Chiron to track down and kill her twin sister Ariadne, who lives in a village. The assault on the village does not escape Theseus and Demetrius, who rescue Ariadne. Chirone then orders Demetrius' family in Crete to sacrifice to the Minotaur. The two heroes travel to Crete, where they are captured by Phaedra, though she has fallen in love with Theseus. Demetrius and his family are killed, Theseus escapes wounded and plunges into the sea. He is rescued by the goddess Amphitrite and sent back with a sword. He learns that Chiron's men have kidnapped Ariadne and placed her in Phaedra's tomb, whereupon he can enter, defeat the guards, and vanquish the monster Minotaur. Phaedra flees and is attacked by her own animals, while the revolting people kill her ally Chiron.
text_a: In Crete, the beautiful and evil Princess Phaedra (Rosanna Schiaffino) sends the officer Chiron (Alberto Lupo) to murder her twin Ariadne (Rossana Schiaffino), who grew up with a peasant family, as the mother of both, Pasiphae (Elena Zareschi), decided to hide her so that she would not be sacrificed to the Minotaur as the Cretan law dictated. anchor_text: Jeanne Dandieu, after a happy childhood near her parents, married the handsome Julien de Lamare. The latter, cynical and brutal, having made a marriage of money, soon suffocates under the exclusive passion of his wife. He cheats on her openly, under her roof, with Rosalie, the servant who finds herself pregnant. Jeanne has forgiven me. She has a son of her own, Paul. The arrival of the beautiful and sensual Gilberte de Fourcheville will hasten the drama. Having surprised his wife with Julien, Fourcheville will push into the void the cart in which the two lovers sheltered their love. Next to Rosalie, a broken Jeanne will dedicate her life to her son.
text_a: 1819 in the Normandy. Jeanne Le Perthuis des Vauds has just left the convent and is about to start a new life with her future husband Julien de Lamare. He soon reveals himself to be selfish, greedy, and unfaithful. Julien cheats on Jeanne first with Rosalie, the servant, then with a neighbor, Gilberte de Fourville. Having two children is not going to improve the relationship between the spouses.
text_b: At the end of the nineteenth century, Jeanne, after a happy childhood that lived in the affection of her parents, marries Julien, a handsome, cynical and brutal boy who, she soon realizes, has married her only for interest. The man impregnates Rosalie, the young maid. Jeanne also realizes that she is expecting a child and ends up forgiving her husband for his infidelity. But when the beautiful and sensual Gilberte arrives, the drama begins. Her husband, Foucheville, discovers the affair, kills the two lovers by plunging them into the void. Jeanne, now a widow, will devote herself to raising her child with Rosalie.

anchor_text: Ernst Graeber was a German soldier on the German-Russian front at the end of World War II. He and his comrades Steinbrenner and Hirschland were ordered to execute Russian civilians, but Hirschland refused and committed suicide instead. On his first leave in two years, Ernst returns to his village, only to find it bombed to the ground; his parents are missing. Elizabeth Kruse, a childhood friend, daughter of her mother's doctor, tells her that her father has been taken away by the Gestapo. The Allied bombings are relentless and interrupt moments of calm. Ernst and Elizabeth fall in love with each other and have a brief romance. In the urgency of the war, they decide to get married. An old friend, Oscar Binding, a wealthy Nazi who became district party leader, welcomes Ernst into his home and prepares a party for the newlyweds. Oscar tries to help Ernst get news of his parents... In addition, Professor Pohlmann offers his help to young couples if they are thinking of fleeing their country. But Ernst is ordered back to the front. There he finds Steinbrenner again going to execute Russian civilians. Ernst, to prevent this execution, is driven to kill Steinbrenner, then he frees the civilian prisoners. One of the prisoners ignores the fact that Ernst saved their lives, grabs Steinbrenner's rifle and shoots him. Ernst died shortly afterwards when he read a letter from Elizabeth telling him she was pregnant.
text_a: At Frankfurt Airport, an elderly man attacks another. One's going to the hospital, the other's in custody. The attacker doesn't say a word, and he doesn't have any papers on him. Only one forearm tattoo points to a concentration camp past. Attorney-at-law Gabriele Freund is moving all the levers to determine the identity of her client. Only if she knows his motive will she be able to defend him. Eventually, everything comes to light: the man's name is Aaron Reichenbach and his victim is the former commandant of the Neuengamme concentration camp in Hamburg, Arnold Krenn. Aaron's attack was intended to prevent Krenn's departure for South America and to bring the man and his story to justice. Because in 1945, shortly before the British army invaded, Arnold Krenn had 20 children murdered in Neuengamme concentration camp for medical experiments. For this he had only served a short time and, incidentally, later received a prison allowance. Aaron Reichenbach has lost his two sisters, whom he suspects were among the Nazi victims of Neuengamme. He wants to know from Krenn the fate of his sisters Rachel and Ruth. But the old SS man Krenn still has strong allies in Germany: in the judiciary and industry still dominated by old Nazi scoundrels and also among the young right-wing radicals who brainlessly glorify the Third Reich. Gabrielle's struggle for truth and at least a little justice demands everything from her. She feels abandoned even by those closest to her, and threats come not only to her but also to her young daughter. In the end, it comes down to a showdown in court and a surprise for Aaron Reichenbach as well.
text_b: Ernst Graeber is a German soldier stationed on the Eastern Front during the last days of the war. He and his comrades Steinbrenner and Hirschland are ordered to kill Russian civilians, but instead Hirschland commits suicide. He gets his first leave in two years and Ernst returns home to find his village bombed and his parents gone. Elizabeth Kruse, daughter of her mother's doctor, tells her that her father has been arrested by the Gestapo. The constant air raids interrupt any quiet moment while Ernst and Elizabeth enjoy their love. An old friend, Binding, a wealthy Nazi, welcomes Ernst into his home and throws a party for the newly married couple, while a friendly teacher, Pohlmann, offers his help in case they decide to flee the country. Ernst is ordered back to the front where he finds Steinbrenner about to shoot arrested Russian civilians. To prevent their looting, Ernst himself shoots Steinbrenner and frees them. One of the prisoners unmoved by this sentimentality retrieves Steinbrenner's rifle and then shoots Graeber. She dies while reading a letter from Elizabeth telling her that she is expecting her child.

anchor_text: Ernst Graeber, a German soldier engaged on the Russian front, during the retreat receives the happy news of the arrival of his license permit in his hometown, Werden (a fictitious name). On arrival, he finds the city destroyed by bombing, which also razed his house to the ground; in an attempt to find his parents, Ernst meets the daughter of his former family doctor, deported to a concentration camp four months earlier, Elizabeth Kruse. In the meantime, Ernst is staying at the military barracks on leave. After a few days, Ernst crosses the street with an old schoolmate who has become an SS commander, whom Ernst asks for help in finding his parents. In the days that follow, Ernst befriends Elizabeth, eventually marrying her. After a bombing destroys their apartment, Ernst and Elizabeth go to live in a house just outside the city, after Elizabeth receives from Ernst the news of her father's death. Later, Ernst receives a letter from his parents informing him that they are doing well. The leave ends and Ernst is forced to return to the front, where he finds the comrades who continue the retreat. After an aerial bombardment their company finds some prisoners and the Gestapo soldier Brenner gives the order to kill them to Ernst; he refuses and kills Brenner freeing the prisoners, but, as he walks away to read a letter from Elizabeth, one of the prisoners, who had taken Brenner's rifle, kills Ernst in treason, just as he had just read the news of the arrival of a son.
