anchor_text: The musician Rudolf is a good-natured, boyish and sociable guy with big children's eyes, with whom you also like to make your jokes. When one of his friends, the writer Alfred, once had too much fun with him a girl was playing with him, he withdraws deeply affected temporarily into the loneliness of his bachelorhood. One day, Rudolf meets a poor woman who sells matches, and he takes pity on her. Rudolf takes her home and gives her a home and food. In return, the girl as hostess takes care of him and his household so that the self-made man does not go to waste. Rudolf develops deep feelings for those under his protection. Things seem to be going well for Rudolf, especially since he has reconciled with Alfred, whom he had raped. He just had his own girlfriend, Hanni, cheat on him. Rudolf expresses his solidarity with Alfred out of deep friendship and makes it clear to Hanni how much he thinks of her behavior towards his friend: namely, nothing. It's the same moment when his boyfriend has no qualms about cheating on him, Rudolf, with his new flame, the match girl. Rudolf is just a clown's way of saying, "Laugh Bajazzo", even if it makes you want to cry.
text_a: The musician Rudolf is a gentle, sociable boy with large childlike eyes, with whom one likes to joke. When one of his friends, the writer Alfred, becomes too mean to him - while a girl was playing with him - he is deeply hurt and temporarily retreats into the solitude of his single existence. One day Rudolf met a poor matchmaker, and he took pity on her. Rudolf took her home and gave her food and shelter. In return, the girl takes care of him and his household as a housekeeper. Rudolf was deeply moved. Things seem to be going well for Rudolf as he reconciles with Alfred with whom he had had a falling out. His own girlfriend, Hanni, cheated on him. In deep friendship, Rudolf stands by Alfred and lets Hanni know how he feels about his behavior toward his friend. This is the moment when his friend does not hesitate to betray Rudolf's friendship with his new love, the girl with the matches. All that remains for Rudolf is to apply the saying of all clowns: "Ris Bajazzo!... even if you really feel like crying".
text_b: Rudolf, the narrator suffering from Boeck's disease, lives in seclusion in his house in Peiskam, a district of Ohlsdorf (Upper Austria), where Thomas Bernhard owned a square farm. For ten years, Rudolf has been preparing a scientific work on his favorite composer Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, bringing together a huge amount of material, but failing already in the first sentence. At first, he finds himself hampered by the presence of his sister, but even after her departure, the work does not want to succeed. He decides to go to Palma, where he remembers "the young Härdtl", a young woman he met on his last visit to Mallorca. Her husband was killed in a tragic accident. When Rudolf visits the cemetery where the man is buried in a concrete box, he also reads the name of the woman who committed suicide.

anchor_text: Dan Gillis is the sheriff of the small New England town of Potters Bluff, where several new arrivals are found dead. He investigates the murders while his wife Janet, a schoolteacher, regularly leaves the house at night. One day, Gillis runs into an unknown person who is seriously injured the accident victim is obviously of supernatural origin fleeing. The incident is making law enforcement suspicious. He then intensifies his efforts to learn more about its movements. Gillis eventually has the remains of the car's skin examined in a laboratory, which ultimately reveals that the tissue has been dead for several weeks. It turns out that local undertaker William G. Dobbs invented a method to prepare the dead and ultimately resurrect them. The entire local population is made up of zombies controlled by Dobbs. In the end, even the sheriff has to realize that he was once murdered by his wife and is also a revenant.
text_a: New England in the 1890's The undertaker business founded by Hinchley has been in decline ever since his hideous son-in-law Trumbull took over, aided by a servile Gillie whom he blackmails and terrorizes. When Black, his landlord, demands a year's back rent, Trumbull, in order to bail himself out, resumes his disgusting habit of helping wealthy people who have to resort to his services. Phipps, a charming man with whom Trumbull prides himself on having had highly urban relations, is the first of the clients thus recruited, and the basket case of Hinchley and Co is conveniently waiting in the driveway for the discovery of his body. But Mrs. Phipps doesn't show up for the funeral because she's already moved out with all her furniture and luggage. We have to do it all over again. Trumbull then sets his sights on Black himself, thus striking two blows with one stone, and he willingly dies, but resurrects just as easily, actually subject to epileptic seizures that plunge him into a state of total catatonia. A hundred times on the job it is necessary to hand over the work, and prosecutors as prosecuted die in turn, but usually for a short time, while Gillie and Mrs. Trumbull, finally decided to free themselves from her torturer husband, swear to eternal love...
text_b: The peaceful coastal town of Potter's Bluff is the setting for a series of gruesome crimes. It all starts with the murder of a photographer, found burned alive. City Sheriff Dan Gillis (James Farentino) is leading the investigation. He received valuable assistance from the medical examiner, William G. Dobbs (Jack Albertson). He soon discovers that many strangers passing through his town meet a tragic end. But the murderers are not unknown to the viewer: they are respected members of the community of Potter's Bluff! Not to mention that some of the dead reappear, as if nothing had happened... After an investigation involving black magic and serial murders, Dan Gillis is about to uncover the terrible truth.

anchor_text: The film deals with the lives of many gay men in their subculture in the early 1970s. It is not addressed to the oppressors, but to the homosexuals themselves. The film's premise is that gay people are responsible for their plight and that to overcome their fears they should come out and fight for better treatment in the future. Young Daniel, raised in the country, arrives in Berlin where he meets Clemens. The two live an intense love story, go to live together and try to copy a middle-class married life. After four months, they split up because Daniel met an older, richer man and moved into his mansion. Shortly afterwards, Clemens cheats his ex-partner into a party: to him, Daniel was just an object. Meanwhile, Daniel starts working in a gay bar, dresses in the latest fashion and quickly adapts to the ideals of the culture: he spends his free time on the beach and at night frequents gay clubs where he gets used to occasional sexual adventures. He soon discovers the stimuli of encounters in parks and public restrooms where he discovers how older gay men are beaten up. He happens to be in a transvestite club where he meets anyone who hasn't found a partner. Here he meets Paul, who invites him to his gay room. The group discusses with him the problems of gay life and makes him realize how shallow his life is. His job as an emancipated gay man is to assert his status and find other content besides fashion and sex. He is asked to organize himself politically and to reflect together with other gays on the forms of common life.
text_a: Daniel is an outsider and lives in Copenhagen. He earns his meager income by spray-painting love messages on the walls of public buildings. His lifelong friend Roger, nicknamed Grandpa, works as an assistant in a sleep laboratory and invests his spare time in training as a soccer referee. Grandpa has a crush on Franc, who works in a bakery. Because of his shyness, however, he is unable to approach her. When he finally decides to take the plunge, Franc is standing in the store drunk on psychedelic mushrooms. Grandpa loses his nerve and runs away, but Daniel takes her home and comes back a few days later to check on her. After meeting Franc again in a café, he realizes that he is in love and from then on sprays his own love messages on the walls. On a new assignment, he is trapped by the police and sentenced to probation. After having to leave his apartment, he volunteers at Grandpa's sleep lab for a long-term experiment to at least have a bed. One day, it turns out that Franc is pregnant with Daniel's child. Daniel flees to Spain because he doesn't feel up to the task of being a father. When he returns, he takes Franc to an abortion clinic. On the way there, however, in a moment of clarity, he suddenly realizes that it is time to take responsibility and decides against the abortion.
text_b: The young Daniel, arrives from the Province to Berlin and there he meets Clemens. They both live their great love, live together and try to imitate a bourgeois marriage. But, after four months together, they separate, as Daniel has met an older and wealthy man, and moves to live in his village. A little later, Daniel is cheated on with another by his lover at a musical evening. To him, Daniel was just an object. Daniel starts working in a gay café, dresses in the latest fashion and quickly adapts to the ideals of the gay subculture. He lets himself be loved and spends his free time at the pool. At night he goes out to gay bars and becomes increasingly dependent on his various sexual adventures. Soon afterwards he discovers the pleasures of anonymous sex in parks and public baths, where he sees for the first time how older homosexuals are beaten up. A day ends at the last minute in a transvestite bar, where all those who have not yet gotten laid for the night meet. There he meets Paul, who takes him to his flatmate. The group discusses with Daniel the problems of gay life and clarifies that he leads a very shallow life. His mission as an emancipated homosexual should be to recognize himself as such and actively contribute to achieving content beyond fashion and sex. They propose that he join a political organisation and, together with other gays, reflect on the different ways in which people live together.

anchor_text: Young Daniel from the provinces comes to West Berlin and meets Clemens. They both fall in love, move in together, and try to replicate civil marriage. After four months, however, they separate again, as Daniel meets an older, wealthy man whose mansion he moves into. Shortly thereafter, his older boyfriend cheats on him with another man at a music event. To him, Daniel was just a lust object. Daniel starts working in a gay café, dresses in the latest fashion and quickly adapts to the ideals of the gay subculture. He enjoys spending his free time bathing on the beach and being admired by other gay men wearing only a tight bathing suit. At night, he goes to trendy bars and becomes increasingly dependent on constantly changing partners and fleeting sexual adventures. After some time he also discovers the charms of cruising in parks and in public toilets, where he also witnesses older homosexuals being beaten up. One night, Daniel arrives at a transvestite bar late at night, where many men who have not yet found a partner for the night meet. Here he meets Paul, who takes him to his gay commune-style apartment. The residents discuss with him the challenges and problems of gay life and make it clear to him that he leads a very shallow life. His task as an emancipated gay man is to publicly acknowledge his homosexuality and actively advocate for other values and contents of homosexual life than just chasing fashion trends and fast, mostly anonymous sex. The group suggests that he join them in social and political engagement, networking and working with others to fight for a more just society in which homosexuals can develop freely and without discrimination.
text_a: Daniel is an outsider and lives in Copenhagen. He earns his meager income by spray-painting love messages on the walls of public buildings. His lifelong friend Roger, nicknamed Grandpa, works as an assistant in a sleep laboratory and invests his spare time in training as a soccer referee. Grandpa has a crush on Franc, who works in a bakery. Because of his shyness, however, he is unable to approach her. When he finally decides to take the plunge, Franc is standing in the store drunk on psychedelic mushrooms. Grandpa loses his nerve and runs away, but Daniel takes her home and comes back a few days later to check on her. After meeting Franc again in a café, he realizes that he is in love and from then on sprays his own love messages on the walls. On a new assignment, he is trapped by the police and sentenced to probation. After having to leave his apartment, he volunteers at Grandpa's sleep lab for a long-term experiment to at least have a bed. One day, it turns out that Franc is pregnant with Daniel's child. Daniel flees to Spain because he doesn't feel up to the task of being a father. When he returns, he takes Franc to an abortion clinic. On the way there, however, in a moment of clarity, he suddenly realizes that it is time to take responsibility and decides against the abortion.
text_b: The film deals with the lives of many gay men in their subculture in the early 1970s. It is not addressed to the oppressors, but to the homosexuals themselves. The film's premise is that gay people are responsible for their plight and that to overcome their fears they should come out and fight for better treatment in the future. Young Daniel, raised in the country, arrives in Berlin where he meets Clemens. The two live an intense love story, go to live together and try to copy a middle-class married life. After four months, they split up because Daniel met an older, richer man and moved into his mansion. Shortly afterwards, Clemens cheats his ex-partner into a party: to him, Daniel was just an object. Meanwhile, Daniel starts working in a gay bar, dresses in the latest fashion and quickly adapts to the ideals of the culture: he spends his free time on the beach and at night frequents gay clubs where he gets used to occasional sexual adventures. He soon discovers the stimuli of encounters in parks and public restrooms where he discovers how older gay men are beaten up. He happens to be in a transvestite club where he meets anyone who hasn't found a partner. Here he meets Paul, who invites him to his gay room. The group discusses with him the problems of gay life and makes him realize how shallow his life is. His job as an emancipated gay man is to assert his status and find other content besides fashion and sex. He is asked to organize himself politically and to reflect together with other gays on the forms of common life.

anchor_text: Pogo Poole is an international playboy who has been traveling around the world for the past 15 years. When his daughter Jessica gets married, he suddenly reappears with his family in San Francisco. His ex-wife Katharine is now married to Jim Dougherty. Jessica is overjoyed to see her father again and is captivated by his charm and worldliness. However, Katharine fears the worst, especially she fears that Pogo wants to prevent the wedding with the somewhat dull and naive, but very rich rancher Roger Henderson. Jessica also very quickly realizes that Henderson has little format compared to her father and the relationship begins to crumble. When she finally announces that she's cancelling the wedding, it looks like disaster and Pogo is at the finish line. Pogo's just reacting differently than anyone expected. He's making sure that Jessica and Roger get back together and the wedding can still happen. The day after the wedding, he disappears again.
text_a: Biddeford "Pogo" Poole is a restless, wandering man who returns to San Francisco to visit his daughter Jessica, who is about to marry cattle rancher Roger. The girl is fascinated by the father figure and seems willing to give up her plans and follow him on his wanderings through the world. Biddeford tries by all means to convince her but in the end discovers that the daughter wants to please him only so as not to abandon him to the loneliness of old age. Aware for the first time of his condition, he lets Jessica decide her own life and sets off on a new journey, this time accompanied by his first wife Kate's Chinese waiter.
text_b: Poonam is a very beautiful and shy girl, raised by her uncle because she lost her parents as a child. She had a happy childhood, although her aunt, seeing her much more beautiful than her own daughter, never managed to love her. The moment a family friend shows his photograph to the family of a young careerist, his relatives, enchanted by Poonam's beauty, are convinced that their second son should marry her. The young man, Prem, initially reluctant, is overwhelmed by her beauty as soon as he sees the photo. The two meet to get to know each other and decide to unite in marriage. The film follows the slow building of their relationship, from a family vacation to a private meeting on the terrace, from the aunt's complaints that her husband is spending so much on her granddaughter that he won't stay in view of the eventual marriage of his "real" daughter to his response that there are two daughters in that house. Poonam is shocked when she hears a quarrel between her adoptive parents and would give up on the wedding if her uncle didn't convince her that everything is fine and she doesn't have to feel guilty. The day before the wedding, however, a fire hits Poonam's uncles' house. The young woman, in order to save her cousin, is disfigured all over her body, except for her face. She is rushed to the hospital with third-degree burns and undergoes an operation. Prem and his family join them immediately and he, sincerely in love, decides to marry her anyway. Poonam's aunt is moved by the gesture of her niece, who has sacrificed her own beauty in the name of her cousin, and the coldness towards her begins to melt away. The operation succeeds and the two finally get married.

anchor_text: The novel is structured in two parts. At the time of its publication, it was located in a region of the Austrian Alps. The narrator, a 21-year-old young man, accompanies his father, a doctor, on his daily tour. The appalling gallery of torn portraits he discovers leads him to view humans as prey to an intrinsic perturbation (Verstörung), plunging them inexorably into darkness. The second part is a monologue by a particular patient of his father, Prince Saurau, an enlightened aristocrat living in an eerie castle. The character of Saurau is a classic figure in the work of Bernhard, the mature, cultured Austrian, expressing with remarkable ferocity his dislike for his country and his contemporaries. Diseases are man's shortest way to himself, writes Thomas Bernhard. This is what Perturbation is about: the disease that eats away bodies and souls, and gangrenous the social body. # Quote
text_a: Through four breathlessly interlocking episodes, the anonymous narrator, a hungry young writer, writes for newspapers, changes homes or sleeps on the streets from time to time, and meets many different, often mysterious characters, such as the female figure he nicknames Ulayali. The physical and mental decay of the character is completed by his fantasies, his inexplicable outbursts of anger or joy, his faculties at the expense or benefit of his acquaintances or complete strangers, in an impetus sometimes close to hallucination or madness. Driven from a furnished house, the narrator finds himself once again without a home or resources when, on an impulse, he decides to embark on a departing ship, thus freeing himself from wandering.
text_b: The novel takes place, from the first light of dawn until the night of an unspecified day, against the backdrop of a rural Austria. The narrator is a young university student who follows his father, a country doctor, on his many visits to patients scattered throughout the area surrounding a remote gorge in the Styrian mountains. But the rural landscape soon shatters against that of pain: every patient the two visit suffers from some horrible evil through which the doctor intends to expose his son - an idealistic and rational student of science - to the ubiquity of disease, brutality, death. They observe grotesque and depressing characters, from a host whose wife has been murdered to a crippled musical prodigy confined to bed. This procession of human deformities culminates in a compulsive hundred-page monologue, given by an eccentric paranoid prince (Saurau). "...if you listen carefully, you will feel that, in rhythms designed especially for you, it is always your story that is told to you and that they give you to understand". (p. 199) and

anchor_text: Stephen Meyers is the deputy campaign manager for Pennsylvania Governor Mike Morris, who is running for the Democratic nomination in the primary. Unlike the two campaign managers, Paul Zara for Morris and Tom Duffy for his rival (Arkansas Senator Ted Pullman), who are cynical and jaded, Stephen is still an idealist. He is convinced that the candidate for whom he is working, beyond his political agenda, possesses the virtues that will make him a good president. The race to win the votes of the great voters of Ohio is expected to be close. Governor Morris refuses to compromise, while the other side maneuvers to rally Meyers to their cause... or to discredit him. He also began a romantic relationship with Molly Stearns, an intern in the campaign office and the daughter of the chairman of the Democratic National Committee.
text_a: Stephen Meyers (Ryan Gosling) is a young and idealistic political campaign advisor serving Democratic Pennsylvania Governor Mike Morris (George Clooney). Stephen will face the greatest challenge of his professional career as he tries to get Morris elected by the United States Democratic Party to face the Republican candidate in the 2004 U.S. presidential election. Meyers is the top aide to the campaign's director, Paul Zara (Philip Seymour Hoffman). To secure a crucial victory for candidate Morris in the Ohio State primary, they must enlist the support of a corrupt and very influential Senator. Meyers is tempted by Tom Duffy (Paul Giamatti), the campaign manager of the other Democratic candidate, who offers him a prominent position in the opposing ranks, but he refuses. Meyers will find himself involved in the world of politics, full of betrayal, cruel and lying, in which nothing is what it seems; his ideals will try to stay above corruption, without succeeding. A dramatic dilemma between ethics and politics which in the film seem incompatible.
text_b: In an un-nominated New England town, veteran, sentimental and astute iron-fisted mayor Frank Skeffington begins his campaign for a fifth term. Catholic and of Irish descent and always got enough support from the slums and the Catholic church to get elected. His opponents are the Protestant bishop, journalist Amos Fuerce (a former childhood crush), and bankers led by Norman Cass, all of the city's elite and supporters of rookie candidate Kevin McCluskey, a young Catholic lawyer and war veteran. Skeffington calls his nephew Adam Caulfield, a sports journalist who works at the Amos newspaper at the behest of his father-in-law Roger Sugrue, another opponent of the mayor, to observe the development of the campaign, which he thinks will be his last victory ("the last hurrah"). The mayor wants the journalist to record the campaign "old-fashioned", destined to disappear with the increasing use of radio and television. Caulfield accompanies his uncle and watches him do politics, helping poor people, trying to raise funds to reform neighborhoods, and using the public machine to force elites to cooperate. Skeffington's veteran advisers are confident of a new victory, but when the scrutiny begins, McCluskey proves he can win.

anchor_text: The inconspicuous and submissive Muharrem lives alone and as an employee is the "girl for everything" of his superior. Privately, he tries to live strictly according to the commandments of the Prophet, prays frequently he attends the prayer hours of a Sufi order and abstains from any form of sexuality in particular. His devotion attracts the attention of the order's sheikh, who offers him a position as a tenant of the order's properties. This new work confronts him with the temptations of the modern world: alcohol, lying, lewdness. Brother Rauf becomes his interlocutor; he tries to keep him in the balance between conscience and reality. Muharrem is also plagued by sexual dreams. The sheikh attempts to resolve the internal conflict by offering Muharrem his daughter as a wife; Muharrem refuses. He notices his transformation into pride, dominance, and dishonesty that plunge him into ever-greater confusion. His fear of God causes him to go to the sheik to confess to him a embezzlement but he has gone into seclusion. As the business partners pursue more degrees, his mind becomes more and more confused; he wanders the streets and follows the woman he has seen in his dreams home: It is the sheik's daughter. The final scenes show Muharrem lying apathetic in bed and being nursed by the sheikh's daughter, who meanwhile is reasoning about vocation and the dangers of vocation.
text_a: In a village between the Blue and White Nile, a child was born. Mother Sakina takes the newborn boy to a Sufi ceremony where he is to be blessed. On the other hand, there is a dancing dervish who counts before him, but then falls to the ground with the word "twenty" on his lips. Those present interpret this last of his words to mean that Muzamil, as the boy is called, will die at the age of twenty. The prophecy weighs heavily on the family, and Muzamil's father Alnour soon leaves them because he doesn't feel himself as strong as Sakina. So she has to raise her son on her own. In anticipation of his untimely death, Muzamil grows up under the watchful eye of his overprotective mother, who dresses in all black. Even other children call him "the son of death". Although his mother forbids him to play with the other children or go swimming, because Muzamil is an alert and curious boy, she allows him to study the Koran. Muzamil proves to be gifted at memorizing and reciting the suras. Years later, Muzamil works as the village shopkeeper's right-hand man. He forbiddenly supplies alcohol to old Sulaiman, who has returned to the village from abroad after a professional life as a cameraman. With Sulaiman and his girlfriend, the singer and prostitute Set Alnesea, Muzamil comes into contact with other life designs for the first time. Suleiman is showing the boy movies, like Crime Scene... Cairo Central Station and the documentary Khartoum by Sudanese film pioneer Gadalla Gubara. Through these influences, Muzamil begins to doubt the prophecy that has so far shaped his life and torn his family apart. At the age of 19, he finally begins to take charge of his life.
text_b: Muharrem, humble and devout, leads a solitary and meager existence of prayer and sexual abstinence. His extraordinary dedication attracts the attention of the sheikh of a Sufi order in Istanbul who offers him an administrative position as manager of the seminary's properties that support a school for orphans and poor children. Muharrem's new work throws him into a modern outside world he hasn't experienced before. He soon witnesses conflicting attitudes and dilemmas towards alcohol, charity and honesty. He realizes that he himself has become proud, domineering, and even dishonest. To make matters worse, Muharrem's inner peace is distorted by the haunting image of a seductive woman who tempts him in his dreams, both day and night. With his devotion now tested, his fear of God begins to devour his senses. Muharrem remains steadfast in seeking Allah's forgiveness and the guidance of his sheikh, in whom he has complete faith. The sheik is presented as evidence of the corruption of power. Throughout the story, the sheik guides Muharrem on the mystical path, however, Muharrem is confused by the sheik's lack of sensitivity. As Muharrem faces his greatest spiritual crisis, the retired sheikh is unavailable to advise him. The film ends with Muharrem experiencing an excellently portrayed spiritual crisis, driven by his inner piety that clashes with the discordant change he sees in himself, brought about by his new job that pushes him unprepared into the modern world. He ends up catatonic in bed, being cared for by the sheik's daughter, but completely oblivious to her presence.

anchor_text: The story begins in 1986 with the birth of Tomaz. Six years later, the film shows the close relationship, marked by mutual protection and affection, between the two brothers, who grow up with their mother Julieta and her second husband Alexandre. After the death of their mother Francisco and Tomaz are now between 25 and 20 years old the close brotherly relationship becomes a love relationship. When Tomaz, a talented swimmer, receives an offer to train for three years in Moscow in preparation for the Olympic Games, the two brothers are separated for the first time in their lives. The brothers keep in touch through the Internet. During one of the video calls, Tomaz gets a little jealous for no reason. After a young woman from Francisco's circle of acquaintances unsuccessfully tries to seduce him, unaware that he is in a gay love affair with his half-brother, Francisco travels briefly determined to Tomaz. In the last scene, the two of them embrace.
text_a: Julieta is an emergency room doctor and the loving mother of Francisco and Thomás, who are the children of two different men. Francisco is the son of her first husband, the entrepreneur Pedro, who now lives in Argentina and with whom she has remained on good terms, while Thomás is the son of her current husband, the architect Alexandre. Thomas is born in 1986 with his eyes closed and does not open them for several weeks, until one day, suddenly, he opens them to look at his brother Francisco, who is five years older. Since childhood the two brothers have been very close, bound by a very deep bond. In adulthood, when they are 27 and 22 years old respectively, their mother dies, and the two brothers are able to fully live an unusual and intense love story.
text_b: In winter, two young men from Porto Alegre travel to a nearby coastal town that they have known since childhood. Martin was sent by his father to retrieve important documents belonging to his deceased grandfather. However, the relationship between Martin's father and the family of the widowed Marisa and her grandson Rosa has long been strained. Martin unexpectedly encounters years of accumulated rejection. Tomaz accompanies his friend out of quiet affection, even though he's supposed to be at a cousin's wedding. At first, Tomaz is too shy to open up to Martin; Martin, on the other hand, is selfish. He's enjoying himself at a disco after Tomaz isn't allowed in. Tomaz feels out of place after a short time. But slowly the two friends get closer to each other: while celebrating together with friends, Martin discovers that Tomaz is gay and also Tomaz becomes braver. Over time, Martin manages to break free from the burdensome and restrictive influences of his father and build a bond with the previously unknown part of the family. In the same train of inner liberation, the relationship with Tomaz experiences a new and intimate component.

anchor_text: During the winter of 1870-1871, during the Franco-Prussian War, the city of Rouen (Normandy) was invaded by the Prussians. To escape the occupation, ten people take the Dieppe diligence: a couple of merchants, a couple of bourgeois, a couple of nobles, two nuns, a democrat and finally, a prostitute, the patriotic Elizabeth Rousset, nicknamed Boule de Suif. The trip looks like it's going to take longer than expected. The travelers are hungry and only Boule de Suif thought to take some provisions, which she generously shares. The travellers stop for the night at an inn at Tôtes (similar to the swan's inn), occupied by the Prussians. The next day, they could no longer leave, the Prussian officer exerting a blackmail: Boule de Suif had to sleep with him if they wanted to leave, but she refused. At first, all are shocked by the behavior of the Prussian, but, as the days pass and the boredom settles, they put pressure on Boule de Suif who eventually agrees. The next day, the travelers were able to leave. Everybody's avoiding the bowl of soup. At lunch, all of them, except Boule de Suif, were able to stock up on provisions, but none of them would give even a small piece of bread to the young woman. The story ends with Boule de Suif in tears, bewildered and desperate.
text_a: Mexico: Bandits attack a train and steal its safe. Two adventurous friends, an American and a Mexican, Gringo and Lucas, witness the robbery and set out on the trail of the outlaws to take possession of the safe. Gringo and Lucas, however, encounter countless unforeseen events, but a strange individual will save them in the most critical situations. Gringo and Lucas finally manage to get their hands on the gold that was in the safe, but the strange individual who qualifies as a U.S. Army captain tasked with retrieving the stolen gold appears. The captain at this point confiscates the gold and leaves. Gringo and Lucas discover, however, that the captain is just a con man to get hold of the gold, but the two catch up with him. The swindler accepts the proposal to divide the booty, deceiving them again but manages to escape with the gold.
text_b: Fleeing from Ruan a stagecoach is arranged for 10 people who obtain a safe passage to El Havre; in them are arranged three wealthy couples fleeing the Prussian occupation preserving their fortunes. Along with them travel two nuns, a fierce revolutionary (called Cornudet), who hopes to continue his actions in Le Havre, and a well-known lady of the gallant life known as Bola de Sebo. What is expected to be a quick journey is hampered by the passage of troops and other unforeseen events. On that journey the only one who carried food is Bola de Sebo who shares it with the other travelers, despite having felt the criticism and contempt of them during the trip. The carriage has to stop at an inn. There a Prussian officer prevents Bola de Sebo from continuing the journey unless she agrees to spend a night with him; she refuses, which the "distinguished" ladies do not understand because of their profession. The gentlemen gently try to convince her, especially the Count. Finally, the ball of tallow agrees. The next day the journey resumes. Bola de Sebo is once again the center of attention, especially of the ladies. At mid-morning the travelers take out their food to consume. Bola de Sebo, due to the night she spent with the officer, did not have time to prepare anything and none of the travelers offers anything to her, so the rest of the trip is spent sobbing. Cornudet starts humming and singing a republican song to the horror of the three marriages.

anchor_text: Ruby Lee Gissing, twenty-something from Tennessee, moves to a tourist town in Florida to start a new life. There she gets a job as a saleswoman in a store. She meets Ricky Chambers, the son of her boss Mildred Chambers. She meets with him regularly. Later, she breaks up with him and meets the worker Mike McCaslin.
text_a: Ruby left Tennessee for Panama City, Florida, home to a summer camp she had visited as a child. Arriving in the fall, she landed a job at the Chambers Beach Emporium, a souvenir shop run by Mrs. Chambers, despite the previous rejection of his application by the owner. During the year, she keeps a diary in which she philosophizes about events.
text_b: With her husband constantly on the move, Lucia Harper lives with her two children with her stepfather. His daughter Beatrice is falling in love with a very unsavory man, Ted Darby. This relationship rightly worries Lucia, who wants to end it. After a meeting between Beatrice and Darby, Lucia finds Darby dead. To protect her daughter, she makes the body disappear and then becomes the prey of a master singer, Martin Donnelly...

anchor_text: Ruby is a young woman who decides to leave her small town in Tennessee, landing in Panama City, Florida, a summer resort she visited as a child. Despite arriving in the fall, at the beginning of the low season, Ruby finds work at the Chambers Beach Emporium, a souvenir shop run by Mildred Chambers. Over the course of a year, she keeps a diary in which she contemplates the ups and downs of her career, her love life, her past and her plans for the future. Ruby's introspective narration is interspersed with routine scenes between the souvenir shop and conversations with her friend Rochelle and the men she dates: Ricky and Mike.
text_a: With her husband constantly on the move, Lucia Harper lives with her two children with her stepfather. His daughter Beatrice is falling in love with a very unsavory man, Ted Darby. This relationship rightly worries Lucia, who wants to end it. After a meeting between Beatrice and Darby, Lucia finds Darby dead. To protect her daughter, she makes the body disappear and then becomes the prey of a master singer, Martin Donnelly...
text_b: Ruby (Judd) is a young woman who leaves her small town in Tennessee and arrives in Panama City, Florida, a resort town she visited as a child. Although she arrives there in the fall, at the beginning of the off-season, she gets a job at the Chambers Beach Emporium, a souvenir shop run by Mildred Chambers (Lyman). Over the course of a year, she keeps a diary and contemplates the ups and downs of her career, her love life, her past and her future. Ruby's introspective narration is interspersed with routine scenes in the souvenir shop or conversations with her friend Rochelle (Dean), or with the men she dates, Ricky (Mitchum) and Mike (Field).

anchor_text: Based on events that took place during World War II between London and Wales, the film tells the story of poet Dylan Thomas's love for two charming and gritty women, Caitlin McNamara and Vera Phillips. Into their lives will enter an officer of the British army, William Killick. The lives of the protagonists intertwine and the love they feel will inevitably involve them in this great rush of life and emotions, between broken promises, passion, betrayal and love, the shadow of war and the constant threat of imminent death push the protagonists to the limits of love.
text_a: Diana and Maureen are very close friends, living through their rebellious teenage years together, made up of partying, dope and boys, despite their opposite personalities, the one a maverick and the other more peaceful. One quiet morning like so many will forever mark their lives, as they lie in the school bathrooms hearing screams and gunshots coming from the school hallways, suddenly a student named Michael bursts into the bathroom pointing a gun at them. Diana and Maureen feel on the verge of death, but Michael offers them a choice, asking the two girls to indicate to him which of them he should shoot, and Maureen volunteers. Fifteen years after that tragic event, Diana is now a woman, married and the mother of a little girl named Emma, smart but addicted to playing hide-and-seek. Diana's life seems perfect, despite the ups and downs with her husband Paul and the rebellious nature of her daughter, but the memories of that tragic day continue to haunt her.
text_b: In the London of World War II, singer Vera Phillips is reunited with her old love, the Welsh poet Dylan Thomas. Although magic resurfaces between them, Dylan is married to Caitlin, a cheerful woman who slowly becomes Vera's close friend. The war progresses and Vera marries one of her admirers, William, but when he is sent to the front, she decides to return to her native Wales. There she will be forced to choose between the two men in her life and her friend...

anchor_text: Young Amy Cole escapes her monotonous life in Florida to enlist in the Army, with a desire to serve in Iraq. Unfortunately, the young soldier is assigned to the Military Police and sent as a prison guard to the Guantánamo prison camp where torture and repression take place against detainees suspected of terrorism. The cells and inmates are examined and checked very often. After several adjustment difficulties, Private Cole manages to establish a special relationship of friendship with Ali, an innocent Tunisian prisoner arrested in Bremen in Germany and imprisoned there for eight years with the identification number 471. He, besides sleeping and eating, has as a hobby the reading of Harry Potter and the sudoku.
text_a: A young woman (Kristen Stewart) flees a small town to join the army where she is accepted into the Military Police. Her intention is to be deployed to Iraq; but she is eventually sent as a high security guard to Guantanamo, where members of Al Qaeda and similar suspects are detained. In the course of her work and against the rules, she will befriend and establish a relationship with a detainee (Peyman Moaadi) who has been imprisoned for eight years.
text_b: In the south of France, killer whale trainer Stephanie suddenly finds herself confined to a wheelchair after a tragic accident during a show at Marineland d'Antibes. One day she meets young Ali, who works odd jobs and makes ends meet in underground fighting, and her 5-year-old son Sam. The two form a friendship that later, after Ali risks losing her son, develops into something more important.

anchor_text: In Argentina, in Buenos Aires, the dancer Robert Davis (Fred Astaire), in search of a job, goes to the owner of a cabaret Eduardo Acuña (Adolphe Menjou), who, busy, refuses to receive him. Eduardo was worried about his daughter, Maria (Rita Hayworth), who, much to the dismay of her family, did not want to get married. Her father devises a plan to change her mind. He sends flowers and sweet notes to his daughter, making her think they're from an unknown admirer. By a coincidence, Maria is convinced that she has met her "unknown" in the person of Robert Davis and introduces him to her family. Acuña realizes his mistake and to get rid of Robert, he offers him a contract on the condition that he makes Maria hate him. But Robert is already in love with Maria and finds it hard to cope with his father's demands. Eduardo is surprised by his wife writing a love letter and thinks he is writing to his mistress. Robert then confesses to the subterfuge and deeply hurt, Maria vows never to see him again. But Robert following the girl's dreams will take her away, like in fairy tales, on a white horse...
text_a: The story is a feminist variation of Faust, which Fausto Maria Martini wrote in 1915 in a poem. Alba of Oltrevita, an aging noblewoman, promises her soul to the devil, who must restore her youth if she renounces love. Two brothers, Tristano and Sergio, threaten to kill themselves if she does not consider their love. But she is not interested in Sergio at all and is preparing her marriage with Tristano. It is only when Sergio commits suicide that feelings such as love and remorse return to her memory and she accepts the transience of beauty. But Mephisto resurfaces and withdraws his promise to restore her youth, because she broke the contract by finding love again. Alba wanders, dressed in a veil as if she were Sergio's wife, and dies after regaining her appearance as an old woman.
text_b: Maria Acuña is the daughter of wealthy show businessman Eduardo Acuña, who, concerned that his daughter is refractory to the idea of getting engaged, tries to melt her "ice heart" by sending her orchids and love messages anonymously. The girl's heart opens to love, until, by a fate, Robert Davis replaces the usual deliveryman and delivers her orchids, being mistaken by Maria for the mysterious secret lover. Robert is actually an unemployed dancer who, in need of a job, is trying to meet Maria's irascible father by any means, who does not intend to grant him even the time of an audition. Maria falls in love with Robert, but Eduardo Acuña tries in every way to distance the two, even trying to ruin the image of the dancer in the eyes of his daughter. Eventually, Robert will be forced by events to reveal to Maria the staging that originated their encounter, causing the girl to flee, disappointed by the lies although still in love with him. Robert will be forced to do his best to win back Mary.

anchor_text: The German-born emigrant family Dressel, consisting of the resolute Mrs. Dressel and her two sons Federico and Rodolfo, runs a hardware business in Mexico. Against the wishes of his mother, who wants a German woman for her son, Federico marries the Mexican singer Magdalena. In the sequel, the couple is confronted with the hostility of Mrs. Dressel. Magdalena is visited by her former singing teacher Gonzalo, who is in love with her. His mother tells Federico that Gonzalo has invited Magdalena to stay with him. After a quarrel with her mother-in-law, Magdalena accepts the invitation, but leaves again, as Gonzalo has made preparations for a meeting of an intimate nature. At the same time, Federico receives an anonymous letter accusing Magdalena of adultery. Federico and his mother search for Gonzalo, but find him alone. In the face of Mrs. Dressel's hatred and the anonymous accusation, Magdalena wants to separate. However, Federico and his mother apologize, and the couple are eventually reconciled.
text_a: The Dressel family describes the problems of adaptation and rejection experienced by an immigrant family in Mexico. Frau, a German widow, lives with her two children who were born in Mexico. Federico, the eldest son, works in the hardware store that bears the family name and suggests to his mother to advertise the business on a radio broadcaster, there he meets the radio singer Magdalena, his former companion from the German Institute. From that moment they begin to frequent each other and Federico proposes marriage, even though his mother is opposed to him marrying a Mexican artist. At a meeting where Gonzalo performs a melody seems to do only for Magdalena, Federico jealous retreats and Magdalena is alone with the pianist, he invites her to his house where on Fridays friends meet to sing. Federico's mother notifies her son of this invitation. After a fight with her mother-in-law, the young woman decides to accept Gonzalo's invitation, realizing that everything was prepared for intimacy, decides to retire. An anonymous notice arrives asking Magdalena to leave the premises before she is surprised by her husband and mother. Federico and Frau search the house but find only Gonzalo and leave. Both apologize to Magdalena and the misunderstanding is cleared up.[1][2][3][4]
text_b: Vincenzo comes with some friends as guest workers from Italy to a coal mine in the Netherlands. He is instructed by Federico to work with the air hammer. When there's an accident in the mine, they're dumped and rescued days later. Vincenzo decides to leave the underground and return to Italy. Before that, Vincenzo and Federico go to Amsterdam to celebrate their rescue. In the red light district, Federico meets his old friend Chanel and convinces her to spend the weekend with him by the sea. Vincenzo manages to get the cool blonde Else interested in him. He spends the night with her, misses his train, and so the two of them drive to the sea in Elsa's convertible as well. An ultimately harmless boat trip by Else and Federico triggers a jealousy drama, in the course of which Vincenzo throws himself at Federico. The love between him and Else is also put to the test, as is that between Chanel and Federico. Vincenzo is going back to Amsterdam by bus. Else brings him his suitcase to the station, but this time he deliberately misses the train because he wants to marry her. Back in the mine, he reconciles with Federico. They're having a double wedding.

anchor_text: The story is adapted from the life of John Rabe. During the early months of the Sino-Japanese War, this German businessman strove to protect the people of Nanjing during the 1937 massacre by the Imperial Japanese Army. The safe zone he manages to set up saves an estimated 200,000 to 250,000 lives.
text_a: City of Life and Death is set in 1937, shortly after the start of the Second Sino-Japanese War. The Imperial Japanese Army has just occupied the then capital of the Republic of China, Nanking. What followed is commonly known as the Nanking Massacre, in which at least 200,000 Chinese prisoners of war and civilians were killed over a period of several weeks.
text_b: The film narrates the exploits of John Rabe, a German businessman who witnessed the Nanking Massacre by the Japanese army between 1937 and 1938, during the Second Sino-Japanese War, and was among the proponents of the creation of the "Nanking safe zone".

anchor_text: In the winter of 1915/16 the 38th Artillery Battery quartered in Courgenay. At first, thinking that by Christmas the war will be over and the army will be demobilized again, the men are disappointed not to be able to celebrate Christmas in the family circle. Gilberte, the hostess of the Hôtel de la Gare, organizes a Christmas party for these men and quickly becomes the soldiers' idol. She is secretly in love with the gunner Hasler, who suffers from the fact that his fiancée Tilly in Bern does not answer his letters. Tilly's father, the wealthy hotelier Odermatt, intercepts Hasler's letters and hides them in his desk. When a comrade of Hasler has to go to Bern with a sick horse, Tilly learns of the matter and immediately goes to the Jura. She arrives just as the soldiers are playing a song that Hasler composed in Gilberte's honour. Gilberte unselfishly renounces Hasler to give him to Tilly, but cannot hold back a tear.
text_a: During the winter of 1916, the 38th Artillery Battery moved to Courgenay, in the Jura. The men hoped that by Christmas the war would be over and they could go home and celebrate with their families. Gilberte, the waitress at the Hôtel de la Gare, organises a Christmas party for these men and soon becomes the soldiers' idol. She is secretly in love with Halser who suffers because his fiancée Tilly does not reply to his letters in Bern. Tilly's father, the wealthy hotelier Odermatt, takes the mail and hides it. When a comrade of Hasler has to go with a sick horse to Bern, Tilly interrogates him and then goes to the Jura. She arrives just as the soldiers begin a song written in Gilberte's honour. Unselfishly, Gilberte gives up Hasler to return him to Tilly, but not without shedding a tear.
text_b: In September 1918, World War I, which had raged in Europe for four years, was drawing to a close. On the Salonika front, the Bulgarians allied with Germany and the Allies are fighting hard. In the final battle before the capitulation of Bulgaria, the Sokol massif is stormed by the Allies and Captain Conan and his fifty comrades, mostly former prisoners from military prisons, play a crucial role. This is a special task force operating under its own ruthless rules. They feel they're the true heroes, which is why Conan can't understand his former friend Norbert, who's now investigating crimes committed by his own troops for the court-martial. After the armistice is finally signed, Captain Conan's men arrive in Romania. Some are forming gangs, making Bucharest's surroundings unsafe and terrorizing the civilian population. The war has turned men into barbarians. Eventually, the unit is moved to the Russian border to fight the Bolsheviks. When they finally return to their homeland, civic life no longer has a place for them, and they sink into meaninglessness.

anchor_text: Willy is a male orca captured in the Pacific Northwest. She is then sold to a dolphinarium, the Northwest Adventure Park in Portland, to be raised and operated. Some time later, Jesse - a preteen abandoned by his mother - is arrested for vandalism and placed with a foster family who wishes to adopt him, Annie and Glen Greenwood. Jesse's rebellious and opposed to authority, and it's hard for him to fit in. He's also forced to clean up his graffiti at the dolphinarium. There he meets the veterinarian Rae Lindley, Randolph Johnson and especially Willy. Jesse soon manages to gain Willy's acceptance and becomes his best friend. Jesse even becomes a trainer, but the animal does not cooperate with the spectacle that is expected of him. So, Mr. Dial, the owner of the park is going to try to get rid of it to collect on the insurance. With the help of his adoptive parents and some of the dolphinarium employees, Jesse will do anything to save Willy.
text_a: A male killer whale, nicknamed Willy, is captured and separated from his parents, eventually becoming a marine park attraction. Jesse, a 12-year-old street kid, is forced by acts of vandalism to volunteer at that very aquarium. In this place Jesse befriends Willy, and teaches her some tricks and behaviors that even her trainer Rae had failed to teach her. Jesse also befriends Randolph, the park ranger, and begins to trust the Greenwood family who have just adopted him. After an unsuccessful attempt to get the orca to perform, Jesse realizes that the owner of the aquarium would soon have gotten rid of Willy and decides to give the orca freedom. She then devises an escape plan and succeeds in the venture with the help of Rae, Randolph, and her adoptive parents. Unfortunately, the water is 20 feet high and the nets of the fishing boats are all along the coast. So Jesse comes up with an idea to blow Willy over the cliffs. After a few minutes, Jesse convinces Willy to jump. Finally, Willy, reunited with his family, thanks Jesse and his friends with spectacular jumps. They say Jesse will never forget it.
text_b: Jesse, now 17, is working with his friend Randolph on a research vessel for orca whales. In addition, the story of the whaling ship Botany Bay is told. Captain's 10-year-old son Max is taken by his father to sea for the first time to learn the techniques of whaling. Apparently, this profession has a long tradition in Max's family. When he has to harpoon an orca for the first time, there is an accident in which he jumps overboard and is rescued by the orca Willy. From now on, his attitude toward the whales will change. This is reinforced when Jesse spies Botany Bay looking for evidence of a whaler and is caught by Max in the process, which leads to the two becoming friends and Jesse revealing to him the true nature of whales. In the end, they use their research vessel to track down the whaler and ram him before Willy can be killed with the harpoon. Max's father goes overboard and is caught in a fishing net that slowly begins to sink. It seems as if he is drowning as he desperately tries to escape the net with the help of a knife. At the last moment, he is rescued by Willy, as the orca brings the net back to the surface with Max's father. Randolph and Jesse pull him out of the water, whereupon he admits to being mistaken about the subject of "whale". The crew of the whaler is being taken into custody by the Coast Guard, who have already arrived. At the end of the film, it turns out that Willy is going to be a father.

anchor_text: The film revolves around the friendship of young Jesse with the orca (sword whale) Willy. The orca was a juvenile and thus far too old to be captured. After Jesse leaves smears at an amusement park, he has to clean them up, befriending Willy and the park staff in the process. The boy teaches the orca tricks that are considered difficult and is allowed to perform with him. But the performance fails, and Jesse decides to break away from his foster parents to find his mother. When Jesse says goodbye to Willy, he discovers that the park owner wants to kill Willy to get insurance money. Together with Rae and Randolph, the park's friendly employees, he rescues the orca in a dramatic action and throws it into the sea. When his pursuers block his way to the open sea, Willy manages to escape to freedom with a mighty leap.

anchor_text: Sierra Nevada at the end of the 19th century. A lone bounty hunter for the army, Isaac Lemay earned a reputation as a cold-blooded killer by committing cheyenne massacres and, in his spare time, frequented many prostitutes whom he soon abandoned. However, he was the target of a curse by the Great Chief White Thunder, who warned him that one day, one of his children from his one-night adventures would kill him. In order to avoid this fate, Lemay sets out to find all of his descendants and slaughter them...
text_a: A gunfighter named Cain is released from prison after 18 years and wants to start a quiet life on a ranch without ever having to touch a gun again. But no one's giving him a job because of his prior crimes. He finally accepts an offer from a showman named Ruffalo who wants him to play "killer Cain" in his traveling show. However, after 18 years of no practice, Cain is not as skilled with a gun as he once was. Cain then tries to find redemption and some peace when he falls in love with Monica Alton, an artist who has come to the West to paint. But Cain's reputation continues to haunt him again when his old nemesis, Luke Santee, arrives to settle old scores and a young gunfighter, Billy Valence, tries to make a name for himself by killing Cain.
text_b: Set in Sierra Nevada, California in the late 19th century, the story focuses on LeMay, a homicidal villain who was cursed by a horrible vaticinio that haunts his descendants, and who has his son Cal as his next target. With the bounty hunters and the mysterious Sheriff Solomon after them, the situation heads into a fierce game of cat and mouse.[3]

anchor_text: Ex-gangster Gal has chosen to live in Spain with his beloved wife to enjoy a well-deserved rest. But a series of events will upset the man's life, until he meets an old comrade, Don Logan, who asks him to participate in a new robbery, at the expense of a bank's safe deposit boxes. Gal's going to be convinced eventually.
text_a: Jake Lo is a Chinese-American student who was orphaned by his father, a police officer killed during the Tiananmen Square clashes. He unwittingly witnesses an assassination between gangsters involved in the drug trade between China and the United States, operated by their respective local bosses. Put under guard by the FBI, he will find himself threatened not only by the Chinese mafia but also by corrupt agents. But Jake does not fear those who threaten him and fearlessly confronts those who want to kill him, displaying his martial arts fighting skills.
text_b: Having spent a season behind bars, Gal Dove now lives happily retired in a chalet on the Spanish Costa del Sol, with his wife, whom he adores. However, his happiness is marred by the arrival of Don Logan, a former crony who tries to convince him to return to London for one last big score.

anchor_text: Three young friends - Vinnie, Carter and Rosie - organize horse racing scams. When an official discovers the scam, they think they're framing him for sexual blackmail. Twenty years later, Vinnie is still haunted by the ghosts of the past and will try to use the compromising photographs again to get closer to Rose, the unhappy wife of a wealthy man, an alcoholic and who has only affection for the horse Simpatico, a former racing champion who is about to be sold.
text_a: In Detroit, three friends, Sonny (50 Cent), Dave (Brett Granstaff) and Vincent (Ryan Phillippe), are planning a robbery. After the hit, Vincent betrays them, shooting Sonny and Dave (who dies soon after) and escapes with the money. Sonny manages to survive and seeks revenge by allying himself with the city's most dangerous boss (Bruce Willis) to get the money back from the robbery.
text_b: Three young con artists, Vinnie, Carter and Rosie, make large sums of money by cheating. Twenty years later, Carter and Rosie are married and are successful racehorses in Kentucky on the verge of selling their favorite stallion, the Simpatico. Vinnie, meanwhile, is a drunk in Pomona. Vinnie decides to do a play for Rosie, lures Carter to California, steals his wallet, and heads to Kentucky with the original blackmail material. Carter begs Vinnie's friend, a supermarket clerk named Cecilia, to follow Vinnie and retrieve the material.

anchor_text: In a Danish village in 1623, old woman Herlofs Marte is accused of witchcraft. She flees to the house of the Reverend Absalom, where his young wife Anne hides her in the attic. That's where she'll be found by her henchmen. Her trial, including the painful interrogation by torture, is conducted by Absalom, who uses her confession to save her soul and sends her to the stake. A secret love affair develops between Martin, Absalom's son who has just returned, and Anne. Absalom's mother Merete hates Anne, she suspects the emotional bond between the two and warns her grandson, but he can't let go of Anne. Anne imagines how beautiful a life together could be if Absalom were dead. Absalom returns shortly afterwards from visiting a dying man and Anne reveals to him the relationship with Martin. Absalom, who had married her as a child against her will, dies of a heart attack. At the coffin, Merete accuses Anne of having killed Absalom by witchcraft. After Martin also turns away from her and takes Merete's side, Anne confesses her crime.
text_a: In 1623 Denmark, the pastor of a small community, Absalon Perderssön is married to the young Anne. When Anne was still a child, her mother was exonerated of the charge of witchcraft, thanks to the intervention of the well-respected clergyman. Marte Herlofs is an old woman who, accused of being a witch, avoids lynching by taking refuge in Absalon's house. She is convinced that she is receiving protection because she knows Absalom's secret. The old shepherd in fact saved Anne's mother even though he knew she was a witch, just so he could one day have his daughter's hand. Mars enlists Anne's help, making her aware of her witch ancestry, then confesses and is led to the stake, without revealing Absalon's secret. Absalon is increasingly tormented by his past while Anne, who meanwhile shows an interest in the magical arts, falls in love with Martin, Absalon's son from his first marriage. When she reveals the truth to her husband, his desire to be rid of him is realized - Absalom dies suddenly. Merete, the elderly mother of the shepherd, accuses her of witchcraft and ends up convincing Martin himself of the witch nature of his mistress/stepmother. The pain of her lover's mistrust makes it impossible for Anne to fight against unjust and outrageous accusations. So in a tragic and surprising ending, the woman blames herself and, with exemplary courage, prepares herself for the stake.
text_b: Father Michael is an excommunicated priest. He founds from that event a satanic cult that practices clandestinely in a place that, in appearance, is a Catholic convent with Catholic manners. Twenty years have passed since then. Catherine Meddows, an 18-year-old adept has been chosen by him ever since to bring the demon Astaroth, mentioned in the Old Testament, into the world through a sacrilegious ritual. Her father Henry Meddows, a man who years ago gave his daughter to the cult, repentant, seeks the help of the writer and expert in Satanism John Verney to save her, which he gives her. A fierce battle between Michael and Verney begins. To end it Verney reads with the consent of the church the book of Astaroth to save her. Finding a way to save her, Verney goes to the place where the sacrilege is going to be done and with this knowledge he manages to use his rites against the father, defeating him and saving her.

anchor_text: Tun-Shan is sad because he accidentally killed an enemy and doesn't want to fight anymore. Then he destroys his sword. Meanwhile, Su-Chen, who is in love with him, convinces him to fight again.
text_a: Tun-Shan is afflicted for unintentionally killing an opponent and doesn't want to fight anymore. So, he destroys his sword. Meanwhile, Su-Chen, in love with him, convinces him to start fighting again.
text_b: Juang, grandmaster of Tang Shan, wins the annual championship. But Chung arrives too late to compete but insists on fighting. Juang, wounded and beaten, dies humiliated. A few years later, the best student of the great master, Tang, decides to avenge him.

anchor_text: Freshly graduated, the young Dr. Manson arrived in a small mining town in Wales, where he was appointed physician to the Mining Company. There he meets Denny, who becomes his friend, and Christine, who he falls in love with. Idealistic, he faces the disease, but also social injustices, which displeases his employer who dismisses him. He and his wife moved to London to open a practice. There he meets Dr. Lawford, a cynical surgeon who has made a fortune with a wealthy middle-class clientele. With his success, Manson lost sight of his ideals.
text_a: Andrew Manson, a young Scotsman just graduated, becomes a doctor-in-training at the village of Blaenely, Wales, starting as an apprentice to Dr. Page. In love with the village teacher, a young woman who shares his ideals, strong and serene, he marries. Full of noble scientific and humanitarian intentions, his spirit is gradually corrupted when he later moves to London and, thanks to a chance encounter with a fellow university student, Dr. Frederick Lawford, begins to treat wealthy hypochondriac patients. His wife, Christine, will try to get him back on his old ethical and moral path. Manson will finally open his eyes when his best friend, Dr. Philip Denny, is killed by an incompetent and incompetent surgeon.
text_b: The film stars Andrew Largeman (Zach Braff), a young actor who returns home to his home state of New Jersey after a nine-year absence. He does so after his father (Ian Holm) has informed him that his mother has died. Returning home, he meets his old friend Mark (Peter Sarsgaard), who now works as a gravedigger. There are several people with whom Andrew reconnects, and whose memories and feelings he has to ponder. Along with the reminiscences that his stay and tour of the place provoke him, he also has to confront the relationship with his father. The development of events changes, when he meets a girl named Sam (Natalie Portman), while in the waiting room of a doctor's office.

text_a: The Dr. Andrew Manson (Robert Donat) is an idealistic doctor who treats Welsh miners suffering from tuberculosis in the mining village of Blaenely. He has problems with the village brutes and his laboratory is destroyed, so he decides to move to London where he helps working class patients, until a colleague (Rex Harrison) tempts him to work in a clinic for rich hypochondriacs.
text_b: The film stars Andrew Largeman (Zach Braff), a young actor who returns home to his home state of New Jersey after a nine-year absence. Along with the reminiscences that his stay and tour of the place provoke him, he also has to confront the relationship with his father. The development of events changes, when he meets a girl named Sam (Natalie Portman), while in the waiting room of a doctor's office.

anchor_text: The film opens in black and white and the Academy's standard ratio, as travel writer and short story presenter Lowell Thomas appears to discuss the evolution of cinematic entertainment, from the earliest cave paintings designed to suggest movement to the introduction of color and sound. At the end of the 12-minute lecture, Thomas says the words "This is Cinerama" and the screen expands to Cinerama 2.65:1 format with color, as a series of vignettes, narrated by Thomas, begins. The film includes point-of-view scenes of the Atom Smasher roller coaster at Rockaways Playland, then moves to a temple dance scene from the La Scala opera company's production of Aida. There are also views of Niagara Falls, a church choir performance, a performance by the Little Singers of Vienna, scenes with gondoliers from Venice, a military parade in Edinburgh, a bullfight in Spain, and scenes from the Cypress Gardens amusement park with an elaborate water-skiing show. The film ends with a sequence featuring high-flying scenes of natural sites of the American West, filmed from the nose of a low-flying B-25, while the Mormon Tabernacle Choir sings America the Beautiful, Come, Come Ye Saints, and the Battle Hymn of the Republic.
text_a: The film is a tribute to outstanding artists of Latin jazz. These include artists from Brazil (such as the pianist Eliane Elias), from Cuba (such as the saxophonist and conductor Paquito DRivera, the pianists Bebo Valdés and Chucho Valdés), Spanish artists (such as the pianist Chano Domínguez), artists from Santo Domingo (such as the pianist and composer Michel Camilo), from Puerto Rico and the USA (such as Tito Puente and Jerry González) and from Argentina (Gato Barbieri). The film's writers visit the places where the various branches of this genre have developed, especially New York City and Havana. The film mixes rhythms and sounds of Latin American music (such as rumba and samba) with the harmonies and structures of jazz. In the various scenes, the most important artists with their bands are each introduced in detail, usually with one of their pieces. The film's title is derived from the address of Sony Music Studios, where much of the film was shot. These are located in New York City on 54th Street.
text_b: The film begins in black and white and in the standard 4:3. Traveller and newscaster Lowell Thomas appears on screen to discuss the evolution of cinematic entertainment, from the earliest cave paintings designed to suggest movement, to the introduction of color and sound. At the end of the 12-minute lecture, Thomas says the words "This is Cinerama", and following that, the screen expands to the full aspect ratio of Cinerama and color appears. The film includes scenes of a New York amusement park roller coaster as well as an Aida dance, Niagara Falls, a choir, the canals of Venice, a military presentation in Edinburgh, bullfighting practice, a sound demonstration, a Florida amusement park and the performance of an American folk song, America the Beautiful, through the noise produced by a North American B-25 Mitchell bomber.

anchor_text: The film opens in black and white in the usual Hollywood format. Lowell Thomas introduces us to the history of the representation of the world, starting with cave paintings about Leonardo da Vinci and the beginning of film history. With the words Ladies and Gentlemen: This is cinerama, the film is colored and adopts the Cinerama 2.685:1 format. It follows a ride on a roller coaster in Coney Island from the perspective of the first seat in the first car. Afterward, footage is shown of parts of a performance of Aida at La Scala, a helicopter flight over Niagara Falls, gondola rides in Venice, a bullfight and a dance festival in Spain, a meeting of Scottish clans at Edinburgh Castle, a performance by the Vienna Boys' Choir in front of Schönbrunn Castle. After a break, we'll shoot motorboats and water-skiing acrobatics at Cypress Gardens in Florida. It concludes with a longer section, named after the song America the Beautiful, featuring aerial photographs from the cockpit. It shows several American landmarks, such as Manhattan, the Grand Canyon, Crater Lake, various national parks or a flight under the Golden Gate Bridge.
