The film ends with a sequence featuring high-flying scenes of natural sites of the American West, filmed from the nose of a low-flying B-25, while the Mormon Tabernacle Choir sings America the Beautiful, Come, Come Ye Saints, and the Battle Hymn of the Republic.
text_b: The film is a tribute to outstanding artists of Latin jazz. These include artists from Brazil (such as the pianist Eliane Elias), from Cuba (such as the saxophonist and conductor Paquito DRivera, the pianists Bebo Valdés and Chucho Valdés), Spanish artists (such as the pianist Chano Domínguez), artists from Santo Domingo (such as the pianist and composer Michel Camilo), from Puerto Rico and the USA (such as Tito Puente and Jerry González) and from Argentina (Gato Barbieri). The film's writers visit the places where the various branches of this genre have developed, especially New York City and Havana. The film mixes rhythms and sounds of Latin American music (such as rumba and samba) with the harmonies and structures of jazz. In the various scenes, the most important artists with their bands are each introduced in detail, usually with one of their pieces. The film's title is derived from the address of Sony Music Studios, where much of the film was shot. These are located in New York City on 54th Street.

anchor_text: The film takes place in the 18th century and tells the story of an officer named Don Diego de Zama, born in South America and confined to Paraguay at the behest of the Spanish Crown. From a riverbank that feels like the edge of the world, Zama lives far away from his family, waiting for a promotion that never comes. One day Zama realizes all that he is missing and, in an attempt to make himself heard, decides to embark on a dangerous mission to distant lands inhabited by Indians.
text_a: Diego de Zama, an American official of the Spanish Crown, is waiting for a letter from the King to take him away from the border post where he is stuck. Your situation is delicate. You must be careful that nothing clouds that possibility. He is forced to accept with submission any task ordered by the successive governors while he remains. Some years go by and the letter never comes. Realizing that in waiting he has lost everything, Zama decides to join a party of soldiers and leave for distant lands in search of a dangerous bandit. Free of his hopes of transfer and promotion, knowing he is in danger, he discovers that all he wants is to live. Maybe I can make it.[7]
text_b: The young nobleman Don Ricardo Villaverde lives in Mexico, ruled by Emperor Maximilian and led by Governor Leblanche. Zorro, fight for the poor peons and rebels who suffer under the governor and therefore for the ongoing revolution. While waiting for a shipment of weapons destined for the revolutionaries, the Alcalde of the city of San Ramon, Don Herrera Coser, leader of the rebels, is killed; his daughter Donna Conchita escapes with the help of Zorro. Meanwhile, the weapons become the property of the rebels, who equip the peons with them and start the revolution, causing the governor and his wife to flee. Zorro, who made possible and supported all these events, goes to the home of the unhappy married Conchita and reveals himself to her.

anchor_text: Zama is set in the 18th century in what is now Asunción, Paraguay. Don Diego de Zama serves the Spanish Crown as a corregidor, that is, an administrative and judicial official. For a long time now, he has been waiting for a letter from the king finally summoning him from his post and back to his wife and children. His superior governors come and go. Zama, long marked by grief and exhaustion, waits in vain for his transfer. He is suspected of being passed over for promotions as a so-called americano, born not in Spain but in Latin America. Zama tries for a long time to get the attention of different women with little success, has an illegitimate child, is waiting for the remaining salary, has to move to increasingly impoverished housing. Finally, he joins an expedition to hunt the legendary bandit Vicuña Porto and thus still achieve his transfer. With a mercenary force decimated along the way, he drives through the grasslands of Paraguay. In the end, Zama loses himself in a delirium reminiscent of Joseph Conrad's Heart of Darkness.
text_a: In Mexico, ruled by Emperor Maximilian and led by Governor Leblanche as his very pleasure-loving representative, lives the young nobleman Don Ricardo Villaverde, who sees his comfortable, seemingly frivolous life only impaired by his somewhat unhappy marriage to Donna Conchita and by the occasional appearance of the masked son of Zorro, a fighter for the poor, peones and rebels suffering under the governor and thus for the revolution. When a shipment of weapons is on its way to the alcalde of the town of San Ramon, Don Herrera Coser, the transport is attacked by the rebel Garincha and Herrera is killed; his daughter Donna Conchita is able to escape with the help of the Son of Zorro and is hidden by him. They both fall in love. Meanwhile, the weapons fall into the hands of the rebels, who equip the peones with them and start the revolution, causing the governor and his wife to flee. The son of Zorro, who made all this possible and supported it, goes to the house of the unhappily married Conchita and reveals himself to her.
text_b: Diego de Zama, an American official of the Spanish Crown, is waiting for a letter from the King to take him away from the border post where he is stuck. Your situation is delicate. You must be careful that nothing clouds that possibility. He is forced to accept with submission any task ordered by the successive governors while he remains. Some years go by and the letter never comes. Realizing that in waiting he has lost everything, Zama decides to join a party of soldiers and leave for distant lands in search of a dangerous bandit. Free of his hopes of transfer and promotion, knowing he is in danger, he discovers that all he wants is to live. Maybe I can make it.[7]

anchor_text: Ben Jordan is a comedy writer and has been married to crossword puzzle designer Katie for 15 years. But the marriage is rigid: They live more in rituals, mainly for the sake of their children Erin and Josh. While Ben blames his wife for her lack of spontaneity, she claims that her husband is too irresponsible. When the children leave for summer camp, the couple decides to separate. But the separation is more difficult for both of them than they are willing to admit - little things keep popping up that remind them of past events. When a parent visits the summer camp, the two get back together so as not to spoil the children's vacation. They may even see each other from time to time. Katie even engages in an affair with a dentist, but can't get enough of Ben. Finally, at the end of the vacation, when the children are to be picked up at the agreed-upon collection point, Ben and Katie decide to give their marriage another chance.
text_a: Ben Jordan (Bruce Willis) and his wife Katie (Michelle Pfeiffer) have been married for 15 years and seemingly have a perfect life. They share with their two lovely children, Erin (Colleen Rennison) and Josh (Jake Sandvig), a cozy and comfortable home. However, when the children are away, the couple have no choice but to face the harsh reality that at this point neither of them can stand each other. As the school holidays approach, they enroll their boys in a summer camp and, as soon as they leave home, the couple begin making all the necessary arrangements to formalize their separation. During this process, each of you on your own will try to remember the reasons why you fell in love in the first place. Will they find enough reasons to stay together?
text_b: Tom (Ashton Kutcher) is a traffic and weather announcer for a late-night radio show. One day, while playing football with his friends, he accidentally hits Sarah (Brittany Murphy) with the ball. There they realize that they already knew each other through the program when once she had called for help with traffic, but on that occasion they did not get along very well. However, that same day they begin a relationship and a few months later they decide to marry with the illusion that their marriage will be perfect, despite the rejection of her wealthy family. The problems begin right during their honeymoon: Sarah meets a boy with whom she had been unfaithful to Tom and at the same time she learns that Tom had lied to her. This is why they go from being very much in love to not wanting to see each other, realizing with this that marriage is not as easy as they expected it to be. Both will have to pass great trials to prove their love.

text_b: Ben and Kate have been married for 15 years. He's a writer who lives for the day, a fun dad who makes disorganization his lifestyle. She's an editor, as careful and precise in her work as she is in her family. Their two children, Josh and Erin, are the only element that holds them together: taking advantage of summer camp, they separate for a couple of weeks. It will be a test to retrace their history of good and bad, of joy and sorrow. But the result is that she wants a divorce. He'd like another chance, he doesn't want to waste everything, and the two seem to be getting closer, but as soon as they spend some time together, their mutual annoyance takes over, making it clear that the solution is irrevocable. So they will wait for their children to return to tell them the news. But perhaps all is not lost.

anchor_text: Jessie is in her mid-30's and suffers from epilepsy and depression. She lives with her mother Thelma and opens up to her at the beginning of the play in an almost casual tone while sorting the laundry that she plans to kill herself at the end of the day. At first, Thelma thinks this is a bad joke, but she quickly realizes how serious her daughter's intentions are. She then tries desperately to change Jessie's mind. Gradually, it turns out that Jessie had planned her suicide in great detail. Both now face their past, discuss past conflicts, and draw conclusions from their lives thus far. Thelma is unable to dissuade Jessie, who, after carefully weighing all aspects, still believes that life is not worth living. The play ends with the daughter's suicide.
text_a: Jessie is a middle-aged woman who has returned to live with her mother Thelma after a disastrous divorce. The woman suffers from epilepsy, which prevents her from finding and keeping a job, and her only son has just been tried for theft. At the beginning Jessie asks her mother where her late father's gun is and very calmly announces that before the end of the evening she will use it to kill herself. At first, Thelma doesn't believe her, but when she sees Jessie start cleaning up the house and making lists of things to do after her death, her mother realizes she's not kidding. Thelma tries to convince her to go on living and together they do the things they've always loved to do: mother and daughter do their nails, Thelma makes candy apples. But Jessie's decision is irrevocable and, with Thelma unable to prevent it, at the end of the drama the woman locks herself in her room and shoots herself.
text_b: Isabel is a young fashion photographer who thinks only of her career but must take care of her boyfriend and partner Luke's children: their twelve-year-old daughter Anna and their seven-year-old son Ben. Elizabeth tries to get his children to be comfortable with her, but she doesn't succeed, more with Ben than with Anna. To this is also added the continuous disputes between her and Jackie, the biological mother, who considers herself superior in everything. She blames him for everything as she believes that Elizabeth is guilty of having stolen her marriage, but every time Jackie finds herself weaker because of a tumor she has and can not attend to all the things that her children require, therefore, Elizabeth has to take care more of the children and as a result of this loses her job. In the end the two are doomed to understand each other and are finding a balance until finally they become friends, Jackie knows that she is going to die of cancer and decides to tell her a little about the past of her children, since she will take care of their future.

anchor_text: Seven men from different generations who have been devastated by life (depression, professional or family failure, etc.) will regain their taste for life by investing in their synchronized swimming team. In preparation for the world championships in Norway, they are supported by two former champion coaches, Delphine, an alcoholic, and Amanda, a paraplegic athlete.
text_a: A group of depressed men, marked by existential difficulties due to professional or family failures, regain a taste for life by joining a men's synchronized swimming team. These men will regain their self-esteem and train to prove themselves at the world championships in Norway, under the strict guidance of two former champions, alcoholic Delphine and paraplegic Amanda.
text_b: Bertrand, an opportunistic gigolo and writer in vogue after secretly seizing a play from one of his clients, accidentally meets Eva, a mysterious prostitute, at a friend's family cabin where he tries to find inspiration to write a new play. But Bertrand soon becomes obsessed with the poisonous Eva...

anchor_text: Holland, a former assassin, is forced to accept the assignment to kill Dr. Moloch who, in the service of a corrupt Guatemalan regime, commits atrocities of all kinds. So Holland goes to Guatemala, gets rid of Moloch's henchmen and lures him into a mine where, however, it will be the miners themselves, also victims of Moloch, who will exact their revenge.
text_a: Holland is a former CIA assassin living quietly and peacefully in the Cayman Islands. The death of Jorge Hidalgo, a dissident friend and journalist, persuades him to come out of retirement. Hidalgo was killed by Clement Molloch, a Welsh doctor based in Guatemala. Molloch is an expert in the science of torture and sells his knowledge and skills to any government that can pay his price.
text_b: An honest prison official in a fictitious Central American state aspires to rise through the ranks, but to do so he faces a series of power games that, in many cases, are very dirty. To deal with the intrigues of other officials and the military, she devises a plan with the help of her lover.

anchor_text: In 1943, during World War II, the Allies must ship a large cargo of supplies from America to England. But the Atlantic is infested with German U-boats. In order to avoid the failure of the mission, it is necessary to be able to decode the German transmissions, encrypted through the Enigma machine, so as to know the position of the U-boats and avoid them. The British, thanks to brilliant minds like that of Tom Jericho, the protagonist (in reality, Alan Turing), are initially able to decipher the messages produced by Enigma. Also to facilitate the recovery, in a captured U-boat, of an Enigma machine complete with instructions for decryption. Nevertheless, the subsequent developments of the Enigma machine again leave Anglo-Saxon intelligence in the dark about the movements of the Germans. Tom Jericho, who has just returned to Bletchley Park (a place where the best minds in the intelligence service were gathered) after a period of rest due to a nervous breakdown, finds himself faced with the problem. Adding to his problems is Claire, the woman he met there and fell in love with, who has now mysteriously vanished into thin air. So, with the help of Claire's friend Hester Wallace, Jericho investigates to shed light on the mystery of the woman's disappearance and her connection to some German messages that vanished along with her once they were deciphered. The two of them are gonna get in trouble, and they're gonna find out something they weren't expecting.
text_a: In World War II (March 1943), in England, the cryptanalysts of Bletchley Park, Station X, headquarters of the British secret services, face their worst nightmare: unexpectedly, the Nazi U-Boot submarines of the Kriegsmarine have changed the code of the Enigma machine they use to communicate with each other and with the German high command. A convoy of Allied merchant ships crossing the Atlantic with 10,000 passengers and important supplies is in danger of attack. The authorities turn to Tom Jericho (Dougray Scott), a brilliant mathematician and code-breaking expert for the British intelligence services, who had managed to crack the previous code, called "Shark", used by the German submarine fleet. What your colleagues don't know is that Tom Jericho has a personal riddle to solve. Claire Romilly (Saffron Burrows), the woman he has fallen in love with, disappears when the authorities begin to suspect there may be a spy in Bletchley Park. To get to the bottom of both mysteries, Jericho enlists the help of Hester Wallace (Kate Winslet), Claire's best friend.
text_b: During World War II, the British Army sends a small group of four men to a German factory in Berlin that manufactures Enigma cipher machines. The mission is designed to fail, a ruse to pretend the British don't have Enigma yet. However, the executive team is not informed of this. The team is made up of the American O'Rourke, transvestite artist Tony Parker, the genius Johnno and the reluctant Archie. The factory staff is made up entirely of women, so the team members have to dress as women. Dropped in the wrong area, the team must first regain their orientation. With the help of Romy, a sympathizer, they find their way to the factory by a detour. Finally, contrary to the expectations of the British army, they manage to steal an Enigma. When they learn the true purpose of the mission shortly before their return to England, Archie wants to sacrifice himself for the others and remain alone with the Enigma in Germany. Eventually, however, all will be able to return to England, including Romy.

anchor_text: The plane of Dieter Dengler, a pilot of the United States Navy, is shot down in a secret bombing over Laos. He survives the crash, but is captured by a Pathet Lao militia. He is offered clemency if he signs a document strongly condemning the actions of the United States. Dengler refuses and is severely tortured. Dengler is taken to a prisoner of war camp deep in the jungle, where he meets five other prisoners. When they discover that their guards are planning to kill the prisoners in the jungle and make it look like an escape attempt due to food shortages, they decide to escape. Although it was agreed to overpower the guards only to take their guns and road food, Gene leaves with three more prisoners. Dengler eventually goes on the run alone with Duane. The two make their way south through the jungle, their escape marked by many hardships and a steadily declining physical condition. When they encounter a village, the inhabitants are very surprised and attack them. Duane is first severely wounded in the thigh and then decapitated by the villagers. Dengler manages to escape and is now alone in the jungle. He also struggles with himself because he hears voices and is constantly hungry. Dengler is eventually spotted by an American plane and shortly after rescued by helicopter to a military airfield. He's being shielded at the hospital because he's been part of a covert operation, and he's recovering under the watchful eye of the CIA. During an interrogation at the hospital by CIA agents, who apparently decide to take him to Guam, he is smuggled out of the hospital by comrades of his unit and taken back to his aircraft carrier, where the crew cheerfully welcomes him back.
text_a: In 1965, on the eve of the war in Vietnam, the American aviator of German origin Dieter Dengler is sent for the first time on a mission (a secret bombing of Laos). During the operation, his plane is shot down and he is taken prisoner. He is taken to a prison camp in the middle of the jungle where five other prisoners, including two Americans, Eugene and Duane, have already been held for a year and a half. Dieter begins to plan the escape, which will have to wait for the rainy season. Eugene is adamantly opposed to the escape plan, because he continues to believe that they will be released soon. One day the prisoners overhear a conversation in which the guards claim that they intend to take them into the jungle and kill them. The next day Dieter and his companions escape, but things don't go as planned and Dieter continues the escape alone with Duane. The two embark on a harrowing journey through the jungle with no food and no clear direction. After some adventures the two are found by a group of Laotians. Duane is beheaded, while Dieter manages to escape and continues his journey alone. Some time later he manages to get noticed by a passing American plane and is rescued by helicopters. While he's in the hospital, his old comrades go to pick him up and bring him back to their aircraft carrier, where he's given a hero's welcome.
text_b: Twelve years after the end of the Vietnam War, Colonel James Braddock returns to the scene to rescue his presumed dead Asian wife, Lin, and his 13-year-old son from the hands of the Communists. Armed with advanced technology, Braddock is advancing from the coast of Vietnam to the interior. After finding his wife and son, he tries to leave the country with them, but is tracked down by the military; his wife is shot. Braddock and his son will be taken into custody. The two manage to escape later. His son is hiding in Father Polanski's orphanage. The Communists track down Braddock's son and re-arrest him, along with all the Asian-American orphans present and their guardian, Father Polanski. Braddock finds out and can free all the prisoners after a battle with the military. Together they make their way to the rescue border to Thailand and on their escape hijack a transport plane, which is shot at from the ground, causing it to leak. Braddock is forced to make an emergency landing. The plane was heavily damaged during the landing, but all the passengers were able to disembark unharmed and continue their flight to the nearby Thai border. Once there, we must now cross one last bridge. Colonel Braddock resumes the fight with the communist military and achieves the final victory thanks to the support of US combat helicopters called to help.

anchor_text: Earth, in the near future. On an October night, the sky suddenly turns opaque and the stars seem to have disappeared. Humanity soon realizes that it is surrounded by a barrier, which posterity will call Spin, outside of which time passes a hundred million times faster than on Earth. The transformation of the Sun into a red giant is only a matter of decades, condemning the planet to certain destruction. Tyler Dupree, Jason Lawton and his twin sister Diane witnessed the stars disappear. Jason will now have only one goal: to find out who built this barrier and for what purpose. A quest as blind as it is determined, in which Tyler and Diane will play a decisive role.
text_a: A team of scientists discovers a crashed spacecraft frozen in ice near their research station in the Arctic. A group of soldiers called to the rescue attempt to detonate the spacecraft, accidentally destroying it. However, if an unknown creature is frozen in a block of ice, it can be recovered unharmed and taken to the research station. There, the ice melts and the resurrected creature begins a murderous hunt for humans and animals. The scientists discover that the alien is a blood-feeding plant. The creature seems invincible until a group of survivors manage to take down the thing with high voltage.
text_b: One night in October, when he was about ten years old, Tyler Dupree was lying in his garden when he saw the stars disappear. After a flash, lasting a moment, they all disappeared together, replaced by a kind of black barrier, empty and oppressive. Tyler and his best friends, Jason and Diane Lawton, had witnessed what would become known as the great blackout. Through the pages of the book, we'll see these characters grow and adapt to a world that will never be the same, where Mars will never be as far away as it is today.

anchor_text: Accused of the murder of a Burmese prince, Jim Brecan, an American adventurer, is hunted by the men of the Sawbwa and by Inspector Cardigan, a representative of English justice. Taking refuge on the plantation of Gwen Moore, a western landowner who raises elephants, Brecan tries to remain incognito.
text_a: Dr. Stevens, a renowned psychiatrist, discovers that he is the target of a plot to eliminate him and decides to alert the police. But this one won't help him, on the contrary. Stevens is now a hunted man and alone to outwit the trap that is closing in on him.
text_b: Accused of murdering the son of the Burmese rajah, Jim Brennan manages to escape. He is greeted by Jay Moore, a wealthy landowner who trades in timber and elephants. She suspects something, but the attraction is stronger. The arrival of a policeman on the plantation, however, interrupts the idyll and sets off an exhausting jungle manhunt.

anchor_text: Every seven years, an extraordinary competition takes place in an ordinary town, closed to the public. A competition in which 30 of the world's best contract killers will compete for a prize pool of $10 million in cash and the honorary title of "World's Best Killer". The last man standing wins. However, if after 24 hours more than one participant is still alive, all die. The competition will be hosted by Powers, who will be the showmaster for all the celebrities in attendance. They're betting upwards of $100,000 on the contestants, who have been implanted with tracking devices so they can be tracked at any time. In order to follow the event, the images from all the surveillance cameras in the city will be redirected and shown to the viewers. This year's contestants include China's Lai Lai Zhen, Texas' Miles Slade, France's Anton Bogart, Russia's Yuri Petrov, and defending champion Joshua Harlow, who is seeking revenge for the death of his wife, Mary. As the killers battle for the title, a drunken priest, Father MacAvoy, unwittingly finds himself caught in the middle. He accidentally swallowed the transmitter from the Frenchman Anton Bogart and now has to fight for his own life. He gets help from Lai Lai Zhen, who only participates in the competition to be able to retire with the prize money. Joshua Harlow finds out who killed his wife as the film progresses. It was Lai Lai Zhen who had been commissioned by promoter Powers to persuade Joshua Harlow, who had retired after his victory, to return to competition. Harlow blows himself up and Powers at the end of the movie. Lai Lai Zhen and Father MacAvoy are the only survivors of the competition. At the end of the film, Father MacAvoy is seen working as a priest again and has conquered his alcohol addiction.
text_a: Every seven years, somewhere in the world, a competition is held involving the world's top serial killers. This tournament is organized by billionaires, who with the blessing of the security agencies bet on their favorite while the winning assassin can get 10 million pounds from the hand of William Randolph Hearst (Malcom Embree). Each participant is inserted into the abdominal area a tiny tracker that allows the movement of the other killers to be tracked for the enjoyment of the attendees. Coincidentally the device is an explosive that could detonate if in twenty-four hours more than one survives. The film begins with Joshua Harlow (Ving Rhames) after winning the last competition, who is convinced to participate in the next one after being informed that his wife's killer will participate in the tournament. On the other hand, Anton Bogart "The Frenchman" (Sébastien Foucan) manages to withdraw the device thus deceiving viewers after depositing it in a cup of coffee from which Father Joseph MacAvoy (Robert Carlyle) drinks, a priest with alcoholism problems who is not aware that his life is in danger until he meets Lai Lai Zhen (Kelly Hu), a Chinese-born hitman. Aware of the confusion, the two embark on a race against time with the goal of surviving, not only the other competitors, but the devices inside them.
text_b: In an undefined future, for the amusement of the upper class, the annual "Hunger Games" take place, in which residents of the various districts must compete against each other in a life-and-death struggle for survival. After young Petunia is chosen to represent District 12, her sister, Miss Evershot, volunteers to spare her life. She is joined by Peter, who has a crush on her. Kantmiss bravely fights through the various dangers posed by the spectacle on television. She can even take on five opponents at once by exploiting their weaknesses. She and Peter get close in a cave and figure out how to end it together as a winning couple. Finally, she faces a group of Hollywood hotshots, all of whom are shot in the back by the jealous Dale. Now Marco takes Peter hostage, but Kantmiss deliberately kills him with a baguette, which is shot in his eye. When there's a rule change after there can only be one winner, she takes out Peter with an arrow shot. Eventually, the Avengers show up, wanting to invite them onto their team, but they explode upon entering the mined area.

anchor_text: The film starts from the assumption that there is a game that is held periodically somewhere in the world where 30 assassins get together to kill each other, the only one who survives will be awarded the prize money. The game lasts 24 hours during which bets are placed on who will win at the end of the race. After a short introduction where you see the epilogue of an old race won by Joshua Harlow, the film tells the story of the race started 7 years after the previous one took place in England for the first time. Introducing the various participants to the betting group is Mr. Powers, who monitors the progress of the game and ensures that the rules are followed. His goal is to get as much attention and profit as possible. # Rules The rules of the game are: The organization hopes to find a valid excuse for the series of killings, should not involve civilians even if some participants have different ideas about it. # Participants Among the various participants given as favorites at the beginning of the race there are some who will move the next 24 hours:

anchor_text: The farmer Pachom buys a piece of land and becomes a landowner. He's "proud and happy". But his sense of ownership has been awakened. He is at odds with his neighbours over minor damage to corridors along his field boundaries. He's going to get robbed, too. He can't convict the thief, his case is dismissed. Now Pachom was at odds with the judges and the neighbors. The peasants threatened him with the red rooster. So even though Pachom had plenty of room on his land, the congregation became too cramped for him. To the east, inland, good land is cheap to buy. After verifying this rumor, he sells his property and settles four hundred versts east of the Volga. Pachom now lives ten times better than before. But there are farmers richer than him. In his urge to increase his size, he attacks his neighbors here as well. Then he hears from a traveling merchant that good steppe land can be bought cheaply from the Bashkirs, even further east. Pachom travels with his servant five hundred Werst to the people of the steppes. He is welcomed into their tent camp and allowed to buy as much land as he can walk from sunrise to sunset. However, Pachom overestimates his strength by assessing his future possession. He collapses from exhaustion after finally circling a very large piece of land, because the last time he ran, in the setting sun, he was desperate. The servant took the axe, dug Pachom a grave, just as long as the piece of earth that he covered with his body, from the feet to the head, six cubits, and tightened it.
text_a: Deserted land is open to anyone who wants to develop it. The rule is that one will be awarded for an agreed sum of thousand rubles - an area of reasonable size, corresponding to what he is able to walk around in a day, between sunrise and sunset. If the buyer doesn't return in time, his bet is lost. Pakhomm, an ambitious farmer, is running. He sold all he had to get the money and, accompanied by his servant, went to meet the Bashkirs who were selling their land. He is assigned landmarks to demarcate his future domain as he sees fit. Man is initially organized, planning his changes of direction in order to get as much land as possible in the allotted time. Every time he changes his route, however, he finds reasons to do so a little later: this water point, it would be a pity not to include it; this piece of forest would be interesting in the field, etc. As his ambitions increase and his journey lengthens, so does his delay. So he moves on. As the day draws to a close, he accelerates his pace to get home in time, only to find himself further away from the rendezvous point than he expected. He paces as fast as he can as he watches the sun begin to set. Thinking for a moment that he had lost because the sun was no longer visible, he remembered that the meeting place was on a hill and that from there the sun could still be seen. Running now with the energy of despair, he arrives in extremis, in a state of extreme exhaustion. Congratulations!, he was told, you gained a lot of land! while he is dying from his overwork. The man dies of exhaustion in front of the witnesses. Then one of them threw a spear at the servant and said, "Bury him! This place occupied by the unburied is just what the man of earth needs".
text_b: An ex-soldier wanders the great desert as a nomad to forget that he lost his family in the war. On his way to an inn, he meets his old combat instructor and army-day friend Cord. He tells him that he has now been appointed the new Peacemaker of the Valley. Shortly thereafter, the two are ambushed by a squad of armed men, with the soldier being poisoned and Cord being killed by a contract killer. The nomad survives the poison attack and then continues to travel the great desert to track down his friend's killer until he comes across the farm of Kasha, a landowner and war widow of the Meridian settlement. She lives there with her son Jux, Tark the foreman, and two other farmers. The nomad doesn't tell her his name and works for her as a farmer for food and lodging. As it turns out, Kasha has a secret, clean and inexhaustible source of water on her property, which she wants to supply the entire valley with a pipeline. But the farm is repeatedly attacked and sabotaged by Damnil's people, who, for lack of a peacemaker, feel they are the rulers of the valley. Only the nomad can successfully oppose his people. After Damnil's unsuccessful attempt to win over the nomads to drive the peasants away, he re-engages Sho, also an ex-soldier and a ruthless killer who has already murdered Cord on his behalf. He kills in a fight with the nomad Tark, who rushes to his aid and kidnaps Kasha's son Jux, leading to the decisive battle in which Sho and Damniel are killed. After the valley is pacified and can be greened to build a city, the nomad decides to move again through the great desert, with the promise to return.

anchor_text: Hibakusha Kane, who lost her husband during the atomic bombing of Nagasaki, spends the summer of 1990 at home with her four grandchildren, Tami, Minako, Tateo, and Shinjiro. Their parents are visiting relatives in Hawaii at the same time. The four youths help their grandmother with daily chores and keep her company, and at the same time try to persuade her to go with them to Hawaii to visit their dying granduncle. The eldest grandson, Tateo, has also discovered his grandmother's old, out-of-tune harmonium and wants to repair it. When the four of them go shopping in Nagasaki, Tami shows the other children the memorial to the victims of the atomic bomb. The next night, their grandmother tells them horror stories directly related to the dropping of the atomic bombs. The next day, Kane's sons Tadao and Yoshie visit her and tell her about their experiences in Hawaii. But their interest is only in the material wealth of the American relatives, which Kane sharply criticizes. The parents, in turn, are incensed that Tadao mentioned the dropping of the atomic bomb in his correspondence with Kane's nephew Clark, which they see as a faux pas towards an American. To everyone's surprise, Clark travels to Japan on short notice, just in time for the funeral of Kane's brother. However, it turns out that the American Clark is not offended, participates in the funeral ceremonies and wants to reconcile with Kane. Tateo also finds out that the piano is suddenly out of tune. However, Clark's stay in Japan comes to an abrupt end when he receives a telegram with the news of his father's death and has to leave. The next day, Tadao and Yoshie also want to leave with their families, but Kane is confused and during a thunderstorm seems to experience the bomb dropping a second time.
text_a: Shūkichi and Tomi Hirayama, in their early seventies, decide to visit their children in Tokyo. So they leave their hometown, Onomichi, near Hiroshima, and prepare to embark on a long train journey back to the capital. Arriving in Tokyo, they find hospitality first in the home of the eldest son Kōichi, a pediatrician, and then in that of the eldest daughter Shige, a hairdresser. They realize, though, that their children are busy with work and family, so they have little time for them. The only one who really cares for the two elderly spouses, although not related to them by any blood ties, is Noriko, the widow of the second son Shōji, who died in the war. Although eight years have now passed since the tragic event, Noriko has chosen to remain bound to her deceased husband and not to remarry. Shūkichi and Tomi, a little disappointed by the children's coldness but encouraged to see them active and healthy, decide to return home. During the train journey, however, Tomi begins to experience the first signs of a mysterious illness, which will bring her almost to the end of her life upon arrival in Onomichi. All of the couple's children, then, including Keizō, the third son, and the inevitable Noriko, will go to their parents' bedside summoned by a funeral telegram from the last born Kyōko, who still lives in Onomichi, only to witness the death of their mother helplessly. After the funeral, Noriko will be the only one to stay in Onomichi for a few more days, while everyone else will return to their daily commitments. Old Shūkichi will thank her wholeheartedly, also on behalf of the late Tomi, for all the kindness shown to them, far superior to that of their own children, and will urge her to create a new life for herself.
text_b: Rhapsody in August is the story of three generations and their responses to the atomic bombing of Japan. Kane is an elderly woman whose husband was killed in the bombing of Nagasaki. Then, come his two sons and their spouses, all of whom were raised in post-war Japan, as well as his cousin Nisei Clark (Richard Gere), who grew up in America. Finally, Kane has four grandchildren, who were born quite some time later. Kane's grandchildren come to visit her at her rural home in Kyushu during the summer while her parents visit a man who may be Kane's brother in Hawaii. While in Nagasaki the children visit the place where their grandfather was killed in 1945 and become aware of the atomic bombings for the first time in their lives. Little by little they come to have more respect for their grandmother. In the meantime they receive a telegram from their American cousins offering the parents a job managing their pineapple fields in Hawaii. When matters are complicated, in their response, the grandchildren mention the attack, which infuriates their parents. To smooth things over, one of the Japanese-Americans (Clark) travels to Japan to be with Kane for the anniversary.

anchor_text: Lucy Hill is a young executive from Miami. Single, beautiful, elegant, ambitious, dedicated to her work, she finds herself managing a major renovation of the food plant in the backwater town of New Ulm, Minnesota. When she reaches the village she finds a harsh and very cold winter and is welcomed by the locals although in a very kind manner. After initial misunderstandings, Lucy comes to appreciate the openness and industriousness of the people of New Ulm and even falls in love with Ted Mitchell, the Minnesota union leader. The objectives of the parent company in Miami have changed in the meantime and the decision has even been taken to close the plant, the only source of employment in the area. It will be Lucy, disobeying her superiors, who will revive the plant by converting it to the production of tapioca pudding, a specialty of her secretary Blanche Gunderson.
text_a: Lucy (Emily Browning), a young college student with a bit of a limp, lives off small jobs. One day, she shows up at a mysterious ad. Discretion, class, and femininity are the order of the day. It's about putting yourself at the service of a network of very wealthy people eager for high-end eroticism. For her initiation, Lucy must serve at the table in fine lingerie, worn by older girls who, in turn, are naked, adorned with fetishistic lingerie. Lucy is proving to be a perfect candidate for the next step. Especially since the pay is extremely high, enough to move into a luxury apartment overlooking the city. Clara (Rachael Blake), the beautiful female director of the network, explains to Lucy that some men dream of sleeping with beautiful young girls. But the sleep must be real. For that, Lucy will have to take a powerful sleeping pill. Afterwards, Lucy finds herself naked in the bed of a luxurious room where an elderly man soon arrives, accompanied by Clara who reminds her of the rules: no penetration. Alongside these sleepless nights of which Lucy has no memory when she wakes up, we follow elements of her life: a violent and alcoholic mother, an old friend doomed by illness, outings to nightclubs where she does not hesitate to have a very direct conversation with men. One day, out of curiosity, Lucy buys a micro camera and hides it in the bathroom. But this time, the man had chosen to spend his last night with her and Lucy wakes up next to a dead body. Seeing this, Lucy starts screaming in pain and disgust.
text_b: Lucy Hill (Renée Zellweger) is an ambitious, up-and-coming executive living in Miami, delighted with her lifestyle in the bustling city. He loves his shoes, he loves his cars, and he loves climbing the corporate ladder. When she is suddenly offered a temporary position far from her hometown, in a small rural town in Minnesota, to restructure a factory, Lucy immediately accepts, knowing that a big promotion is not far off. But what begins as a simple job ends up being a life-changing experience, as Lucy finds a greater meaning to her existence and also, unexpectedly, the man of her dreams (Harry Connick Jr.).

anchor_text: Anna M. is a lonely girl who lives in seclusion with her mother and her dog. As a book restorer in the French National Library, she is popular with her colleagues, especially the young Éléonore is very fond of her. But Anna longs for love, and in her desperation, she throws herself in front of a car. Injured, she is taken to a hospital, where Dr. Zanevsky treats her lovingly. Anna falls in love with the handsome doctor, but he doesn't notice. Every little attention he gives her is only proof of his love for Anna and increases her unrealistic desire for the happily married man. She gets more and more involved in the lives of Dr. Zanevsky and his wife, to the point of obsessive stalking, which eventually leads her to the psychiatric ward. There, it is determined that Anna is pregnant. With Albert, a security guard, she had a lovemaking night in a hotel where she had previously, in her delusion, thought she had an appointment with Dr. Zanevsky. Her colleague Éléonore is now the only person who still supports her. Together they start a new life and want to be loving parents to Anna's little daughter.
text_a: Anna, an erotically frustrated young woman, attempts suicide by throwing herself under a car. She gets away with a few leg problems. At the hospital she is treated by Dr. Zanevsky and therefore misunderstands the surgeon's relationship with her. She falls madly in love with him. Anna will use all means to try to get into Zanevsky's life, following him, spying on him, provoking him and even getting hired as a babysitter in the apartment above his own so that she can get in. Meanwhile, Anna decides to find some compensation by sleeping with a stranger, a station guard. As the case takes on dramatic and dangerous proportions, Anna's mother decides to have her committed. Anna pretends to be cured, and when she gets out, she has only one idea in mind: to find Zanevsky, when she has just learned that she is pregnant by the guard... But Zanevsky moved in with his wife and no one wants to tell her. She's having a seizure at the hotel. The final scene shows Anna and her child in the countryside, two years later. Zanevsky and his wife walk away.
text_b: In the early 1970s, Anna and Erik move in with their daughter Freja in the old but large villa that Erik's father left them. At Anna's urging, the architecture professor and the news announcer decide to form a commune. Anna enjoys the variety of community life, while Erik becomes increasingly distant from her. The mood changes when Erik falls in love with one of his students. Anna wants to keep the difficult situation under control and invites the young Emma to move into the commune. Unexpectedly, however, Anna breaks up at the apparent end of her marriage. In front of her daughter and the inhabitants of the commune, she lives out her jealousy and grief and tends to excessive alcohol consumption. Eventually, she loses her TV job, too. Anna's only support is 14-year-old Freja, who discovers her first love with an older classmate. When the residents cannot agree on Anna's departure, Freja is asked to decide whether her mother should stay or leave. Freja tearfully decides to move out with her mother, as she is not doing well in the commune. At the funeral of Vilads, the seriously ill son of a couple from the commune, the group finally finds itself together again, without Anna, who has moved out.

anchor_text: Popeye Doyle, the ruthless New York City cop, is still in pursuit of French drug lord Alain Charnier, whose American network he has just dismantled. He's the only one who can identify it. Arriving in Marseille, Doyle made contact with Inspector Barthélémy. But he was greeted rather coolly by his French counterparts who prohibited him from carrying a weapon. Exasperated, not speaking the language, he nevertheless continued his investigation stubbornly, not realizing that the French police were using him to trap Charnier.
text_a: Corey (Alain Delon) has served his sentence in a Marseille prison and must face society again, but together with Vogel (Gian Maria Volonté), a fugitive from justice, and to avenge his imprisonment, he moves to Paris to rob Rico, the head of the gang to which he belonged before finding his bones in prison; of course, his former boss will try by all means not to get away with it. They are also pursued by the policeman Mattei, who with the collaboration of Santi, the owner of a nightclub, sets a trap for them.
text_b: Popeye Doyle arrived in Marseille to capture the drug trafficker Alain Charnier who had managed to escape during his previous confrontation in New York. Always in search of the drug trafficker Alain Charnier, whose network in the United States had dismantled and as the only person who can identify him, 'Popeye' Doyle, ruthless policeman from New York, arrives in Marseille where he comes into contact with Inspector Bartholomew. However, he is received rather coldly, his French counterparts even prohibit him from carrying weapons. Isolated, unable to speak the language, he nevertheless stubbornly continues his research. Unaware that the French police are using him to catch Charnier.

anchor_text: Nat and Josh, respectively ambitious executives and stubborn writers, are willing to do anything to prove to their loved ones that their marriage can last more than a year. Will the strength of their union withstand the advances of Guy, Nat's new client, and Chloe, Josh's ex-girlfriend?
text_a: Anna is British, Jacob is American. They've been madly in love since they met, but when the girl's visa expires, she decides to stay, thus going against the law. A subsequent ban from the U.S. affects their love.
text_b: Nat (Rose Byrne) and Josh (Rafe Spall) are a newlywed couple who remain united and happy despite their differences, yet their friends and family are not convinced that they can last more than a year. The film highlights their struggles during that first year of marriage, making constant flashbacks as they are in a consultation with a marriage counselor.

anchor_text: John Randall is an Army cadet stationed at West Point. Paul, his younger brother, is an ensign at the Naval Academy. John's helping his brother get back together with Nancy. Every time Paul tries to impress Nancy, he fails because of a faux pas. In a football game, he can impress Nancy with the deciding touchdown.
text_a: Paul Crewe (Burt Reynolds) steals a Citroen SM and eludes the police. He was caught and subsequently sent to prison. There, he organized a prisoners' American football team to face off against the guards.
text_b: The challenge for the coveted football cup that is put on the line, annually, between the Army and Navy teams, is the backdrop to the rivalry between Cadet John, grandson of a major general, and Paul, aspiring Guards Marine and grandson of a Rear Admiral. The rivalry and challenge is passed down from generation to generation, until it is resolved (perhaps) through a football match.

anchor_text: The story is told in flashbacks: Barber Assistant Joe is in love with Sally, a manicurist who works with him in the same barbershop. But she is not particularly interested in him and therefore resists his attempts to approach her. When farmer Harry from Dartmoor comes into the shop to get his nails done, she finds time to spend more time with him. Joe reacts jealously and follows the two as they visit a movie theater. But one day, when Sally comes to work with an engagement ring on her finger, Joe can't take it anymore. Unfortunately, at this very moment, Harry comes into the shop and asks Joe to shave him. He threatens him with a razor blade at his throat, and he is arrested and thrown into prison for attempted murder. While Joe is serving his sentence in the secluded, high-security prison of Dartmoor, Sally and Harry live in his cabin and have a young son. When Joe manages to break free, he unexpectedly reappears with them after fighting his way through the bleak landscape of Dartmoor. Sally feels something of remorse for her role in Joe's conviction, and offers to hide him. There is also an awkward but honest reconciliation with Harry, culminating in Harry offering to help him escape. But when the time comes, Joe gives up and his behavior draws the attention of the police, who open fire on him. Mortally wounded, he dies in Sally's arms.
text_a: Joe Gower is a quiet librarian, eccentric in character, and good friend of police officer Abe Washington; when the police department puts on a musical show with real officers, Abe, who wants nothing to do with it, convinces the recalcitrant Joe to impersonate him at the audition and get himself kicked out; whereas Joe meets police officer Rachel Rareham at the tryouts and becomes infatuated with her. So he decides to persist and be admitted. Abe does not see it well, but Joe manages to convince him to let him continue; in order not to arouse suspicion, he pretends to be a policeman even outside the rehearsals, in order to get close to Rachel; but in his way is Rachel's ex, Pete Peterson, also a policeman; a long series of gags and misunderstandings bring the story to the end. Joe at one point confesses the truth to Rachel, who remains wounded; Joe contemplates quitting the show, but during a commission in the bank is taken hostage with the other customers by two clumsy robbers; his fake name leaks and Abe ends up in trouble, and must go to the bank hoping to save the salvageable (on horseback, as a punishment decided by his superior); but Joe manages to distract and disarm the robbers with a dance step. Eventually, Joe and Rachel attend the successful show, and they reconcile.
text_b: Joe just escaped from prison and fled into the moor. Meanwhile, in a nearby cottage, Sally puts her child to bed. When she hears the alarm at the nearby prison, she goes downstairs and finds herself face-to-face with Joe. She recognizes him and screams in fear. Some time before that, Joe and Sally worked in the same barbershop, and he was secretly in love with her. One day, Harry, a local farmer, walked into the living room and he and Sally fell in love. Joe became so jealous that one day he tried to cut Harry's throat with a razor. Joe, after his escape, voluntarily chose to meet Sally and Harry in their home. She tries to escape, but she meets the prison guards. Joe's ready to turn himself in, but Sally won't turn him in. She goes to the baby's room where she hid Joe, who then asks her to forgive him. Harry returns home, goes upstairs and discovers Joe. He agrees, however, at Sally's request, not to warn the guards to look for the prisoner. Joe can then escape, wearing a coat given to him by Sally. But when he finds a picture of Sally in his pocket, he returns to the cottage where he is shot by the guards. He then dies in Sally's arms.

anchor_text: In 450, the Huns came from Asia under the sign of paganism led by the most inhuman of conquerors, the plague of God, Attila, ravage the borders of the declining Roman Empire. The two capitals Rome and Constantinople were weakened by rivalries between the emperors Valentinian III and Theodosius II. A Roman centurion, Marcian, carrying a message from Valentinian to Theodosius is captured by the Huns. Attila appreciates his bravery and thrift, so that he can teach the Huns how to fight the Romans. Marcian escapes, goes to Constantinople to inform Emperor Theodosius of Attila's plan to destroy the city of Rome. Theodose refuses the requested help. But Marcian has won the heart of Pulcheria, the emperor's sister, and she takes him into her service. A palace revolution forced Theodosius to abdicate. Pulcheria replaces him on the throne. Attila and his army are camped outside Rome. He has a meeting with the Pope and has given up on attacking the city. Marcian and the Roman troops fight the Huns. Attila is killed by a slave. Marcian marries Pulcheria.
text_a: Roman Emperor Valentinian III drinks himself drunk at banquets, while Aetius, his general, negotiates a peace treaty with Bleda, the king of the Huns. Attila, Bleda's brother, does not accept this peace with the Romans and has Bleda murdered in order to take power himself. Emperor Valentinian III, who also rejected the treaty, accused Aetius of high treason. Attila is preparing for war against the Romans, who are also beginning to prepare for it. Valentinian's sister Honoria turns to Aetius, but he refuses. Thanks to her skill, Honoria manages to become Attila's new mistress, unfortunately she is unmasked and killed on the battlefield, where also Attila's son, still a child, dies accidentally. Her father is inconsolable and badly beaten. Will Pope Leo the Great, whom no other power has so far been able to stop, be able to appeal to Attila's conscience and turn him back?
text_b: The Roman Empire is divided into two parts and is attacked by Attila. The Roman centurion Marcian must carry a message to Constantinople when he is captured by the Huns. Attila likes him and spares his life to learn more about Roman tactics. Attila marries Princess Ilduco after killing her father. Marcian steals a horse and flees to Constantinople. In Constantinople, Marcian befriends General Paulinus but finds that both emperors are corrupt and cowardly politicians, unable to stop Attila's advance. When the Huns besiege Rome, Attila consults a wizard who has a bad feeling. With the help of Paulinus, Marcian forces the emperor to abdicate and installs his sister Pulcheria on the throne and announces his plans to travel to Rome. Attila receives the strange visit of Pope Leo I that puts fear in his body, also discovers that his daughter has become a Christian and furious kills her. That night he has nightmares and frightened orders the Huns to lift the siege and retreat. Marcian pursues them and finds Attila, but it is Ilducus, full of hatred against her cruel "husband" and murderer of her father, who stabs him in the chest with a dagger. Attila dies with the hilt of the dagger forming the shadow of a cross on the ground. Days later, Pulcheria named Marcian as emperor.

anchor_text: Mike Mascarenhas is Portuguese by birth and lives in San Diego. He is a fisherman and hunts mainly tuna. On a fishing trip, his boat gets into trouble at sea. Mike and his crew have to leave the ship. A shark attacks Mike and bites off his hand. The following year, a crew member, Manuel Silva, goes overboard and is killed by the sharks following the cutter. Mike brings the news of Manuel's death to his daughter Quita. The beautiful Quita is now alone. Mike, who had no previous relationship with women, takes in the young woman and provides her with money and food. Mike is proposing to Quita. Quita doesn't love Mike, but Mike doesn't care. He accepts her for who she is. Mike's generosity touches Quita, who agrees to the marriage. Quita, however, falls in love with Pipes Boley, who is Mike's boatman. Pipes wants to leave town when he finds out about Quita's feelings for him. However, he is seriously injured by a fishing hook while at work. Mike's taking him to his house to recuperate. Quita takes care of Pipes, and their love for each other deepens. When Pipes is well again, Quita accompanies him and her husband on a fishing trip. Overcome with emotion, she embraces Pipes, even though Mike is there. Mike gets mad, beats Pipes to a pulp and loads him into a leaky outrigger. Mike watches the sharks circle the boat. His foot gets caught in the line and he's pulled overboard. The sharks attack him, his men drag him and Pipes back aboard. Mike, badly wounded, asks Pipes and Quita for forgiveness and dies.
text_a: Surviving a shipwreck, Portuguese-born fisherman Mike Mascarenhas loses a hand to a shark. Some time later, Manuel, one of his crew, falls into the sea and is killed by the sharks. Mike, then, takes care of Quita, Manuel's daughter, by falling in love with her. When he asks her to marry him, the girl confesses that she does not love him but then accepts his proposal of marriage, moved by the man's generosity and the love he shows her. Quita, however, falls in love with Pipes, her husband's best man. When she tells him she loves him, he decides to leave the country but, before he can do so, he is injured by a fishing hook. Mike takes him to his home where Quita takes care of him. Their love grows ever deeper, and one day the woman inadvertently reveals her feelings to her husband when he, unseen, sees her embrace her friend during a fishing trip at sea. Furious, Mike punches Pipes into a capsized boat that's about to sink, surrounded by sharks. Mike, however, loses his balance and falls into the water but is recovered, though badly injured, by his fishermen. Before he dies, the man reconciles with his wife and Pipes.
text_b: A group of wealthy tourists sets off on a boat trip with crew members, including sailor Manuel, who just before departure has an argument with a woman, ending up beating her. Ella, who is actually a practitioner of voodoo rituals, angered by the offense received and the rejection with which Manuel has responded to her proposal of marriage (being pregnant), decides to curse him with esoteric rites. Because of Manuel, the tourist boat ends up on fire and only he and another woman, Jennifer, manage to swim to an island. Manuel's immediate interest in the woman ends up being reciprocated, until Manuel himself finds and rescues Jack, the husband that Jennifer thought was dead. Jack is immediately aggressive with his rescuer and jealous of his wife, so much so that he forces Manuel to leave the hut built by the sailor himself. Manuel, however, does not want to give up on Jennifer, and when Jack discovers that he has indeed been betrayed, he organizes an escape on his own with a boat found at the bottom of the sea. But it's a ruse: After rigging the boat and getting the two to steal it, Jack thinks he's gotten rid of them. The two lovers, however, manage to swim back, but Manuel, exhausted and sick, is killed in the forest while fleeing from Jennifer's husband. The latter, after spending a year or more on the island with her murderous husband, is found by tourists who take her to safety while Jack, who meanwhile was underwater fishing, begs from afar not to be left on the island.

anchor_text: Mike Mascarenhas is one of San Diego's most famous fishermen. With his friend Pipes Boley, whose life he once saved, he runs a boat and trades tuna. One day, one of the sailors, Manuel Silva, dies during the campaign. On the way back, Mike goes to his daughter, Quita, to tell her the news. He falls under the spell of the latter and decides to marry her...
text_a: Mike Mascarenhas is a great Portuguese tuna fisherman who once lost a hand while trying to save his friend Pipes Boley. Mike is happily married to Quita Silva, the daughter of a sailor who served as shark fodder, but really Quita is in love with Pipes.
text_b: After John and Ariel get married, Max finds himself more alone than ever. Jacob tells him he should find someone. Hostile to the idea, Max quickly changes his mind when he meets the beautiful Maria, the new manager of the fish shop by the lake, who wants to make it an Italian restaurant. John and Max are going to do everything they can to get her to leave, but Maria is going to make war on them...

anchor_text: Donald Peters, a little black boy, wanders the black neighborhoods looking for the affection he's been missing. Unwanted, misunderstood, and plagued by inner torment, he soon turned to crime. Imprisoned in the rehabilitation school for social misfits at Wiltwyck School, he gradually rebuilt himself there.
text_a: A psychotherapist on duty at the Wiltwyck School, a school for the re-education of disadvantaged minors, takes to heart a Harlem black boy with a painful family situation who is emotionally disturbed, misunderstood, misunderstood, and tormented inside.
text_b: A young boy, Peter Flood, witnesses the tragic disappearance of his little sister. Their families are trying to get revenge. Thirty years later, Peter is still haunted by the trauma. While his cousin and half-brother, Michael, who runs his small Irish mafia ring in Philadelphia sees his position jeopardized by competition, a new cycle of betrayal and revenge awakens old family demons.

anchor_text: Juan Quin Quin is an adventurer from a poor background. He earns his living as a church servant and circus performer, performs as a bullfighter, and works on a coffee plantation. Cheated of his earnings by the plantation owner, he plays Jesus in a street theater. After the outbreak of the revolution, Juan, along with his friend Jachero, joined Fidel Castro's revolutionary army. On a reconnaissance patrol, Jachero is killed by government soldiers. Juan remembers their experiences together and their struggles together.
text_a: Juan (Alexis Díaz de Villegas) is forty years old, and has lived in Cuba doing absolutely nothing. That's his way of life, and he's willing to defend it at any cost, accompanied by his partner and sidekick, Lázaro (Jorge Molina), who is just as lazy but twice as clumsy. Juan's only emotional bond is with his daughter Camila (Andrea Duro), a young and beautiful girl who doesn't want to know anything about her father because all he does is get into trouble. Suddenly a series of strange events begin to happen: people become violent and attack one another. After some hesitation, Juan comes to the conclusion that they are zombies and decides that the best way to deal with the situation is to thrive with it and starts a business with the slogan: 'Juan de los Muertos: Let's kill your loved ones'.
text_b: Juan Quin Quin and his friend Jachero multiply the adventures and the most bizarre trades (clowns, fair demonstrators, bullfighters, chorus children...). Persecuted by the authorities under the reign of Fulgencio Batista, they eventually joined the revolutionary guerrillas. But isn't that, again, the continuation of their unconventional adventures?

anchor_text: Islamic terrorist Nagy 'Altar' Hassan and a group of terrorists hijack Oceanic Flight 343, a Boeing 747 flying from Athens to Washington, D.C., demanding the release of a prominent terrorist held in the United States of America. Intelligence analyst David Grant, however, is convinced that the real objective is to fly the plane over the capital and detonate a chemical bomb made of nerve gas. Only doubt about Nagy's true intentions prevents the authorities from ordering the downing of the plane and the deaths of the more than 400 passengers on board. Instead, it is decided to send a team of Green Berets aboard the aircraft by means of an experimental Lockheed F-117 Nighthawk equipped for docking in flight. The commando transfer fails after only a few members of the team, including David Grant, have entered the Boeing. Unable to communicate with the Pentagon's crisis unit, the team has only hours left to neutralize the terrorists and avert the danger. The operation will be successful thanks also to the collaboration of the hostess Jean.
text_a: Terrorists have hijacked an Oceanic Airlines Boeing 747-200 en route from Athens to Washington D.C. C., demanding in exchange for the hostages the release of the terrorist El Sayed Jaffa, who had recently been captured while attending his daughter's wedding in Nicosia (Cyprus), and made available to US justice. David Grant an intelligence expert believes that Nagi Hassan (Jaffa's right-hand man) planned the Jaffa kidnapping to take over the leadership of the terrorist group. Grant also learns that Ahmed Rasjami (an explosives and weapons expert working for Jaffa) stole a Dz5, a nerve gas, and handed it to Jean Paul Demou, a former Franco-Algerian nuclear engineer who builds a bomb and puts it on the plane to blow it up in Washington D.C. The Pentagon, after learning this, orders a special forces team to board the plane with the help of an aircraft equipped with stealth technology to stop the terrorists and deactivate the bomb, but during the transfer Oceanic Flight 343 Jumbo descends due to a change of wind and with the eddy causing the plane to shatter. In addition, they lose much of the equipment and some of the soldiers die. Additionally, the soldiers are isolated in the hold of Flight 343. Still, under these dire conditions, they initiate a plan to find the bomb and disarm it, and also locate Hassan and his men to finish them off. They know that if the Pentagon doesn't get the call from the soldiers that the plane has been controlled before the plane arrives in the U.S. the United States, will bring down the plane, which would mean their deaths and the deaths of the 400 innocent passengers on board.
text_b: An Airbus A319 flight from Berlin to Paris is hijacked by a group of Islamic terrorists; the co-pilot manages to barricade himself in the cabin, while the criminals take the passengers and crew hostage. After the death of the captain, a student and a flight attendant (the co-pilot's partner), the passengers rebel, but two terrorists manage to enter the cockpit and take control of the plane, immobilizing the officer, who was preparing for an emergency landing in Hanover. The two threaten to crash the plane over Hanover, but the youngest of the terrorists changes his mind, kills the accomplice and frees the co-pilot. The young man allows the co-pilot to make the planned emergency landing. Once on the ground, the passengers are disembarked, while the co-pilot remains in the cabin, hostage to the only surviving terrorist. The criminal demands that the plane be refueled so that it can take off again. Despite several attempts to convince him to surrender, the boy continues to threaten the co-pilot, eventually being shot by a sniper, who seriously wounds him, ending the affair.

anchor_text: In 1762, the Austrian Empress Maria Theresia was confronted by a letter from the front with the question of how to proceed in the war against Prussia. She chooses to end the war and make peace, even though she finds it difficult to concentrate on her affairs of state. She has private concerns, so she suspects her husband Franz I. Stephan is back with his mistress, the Countess Aliano. Maria Theresia is spending the night alone. The next morning, her son brings her a locket that the father lost. The portrait in the piece of jewellery shows the Countess Aliano. Maria Theresia retreats to the uninhabited part of the Hofburg and loses herself in memories. At a masquerade ball at Schönbrunn Castle, she had met the beautiful Countess Aliano and shortly after made her her youngest lady-in-waiting. When she suspected a relationship between the Countess and the Emperor, she married her to Prince Trautperg, who died shortly afterwards in a hunting accident. Maria Theresia felt guilty and let the widowed Countess Aliano know that she was welcome at court at any time. When a secret dispatch for the Empress arrives at the Hofburg, Count Kaunitz searches in vain for her. The Emperor spent the night not at the countess's, but at the gambling table. He learns that his wife is nowhere to be found and discovers on her desk the medallion with the portrait of the Countess. His search leads him to the uninhabited part of the Hofburg, where he meets Maria Theresia. They both speak out and realize that they still love each other. Together they return to the official part of the castle. Maria Theresa opens the secret message it is the news that the war with Prussia is officially over and there is now peace.
text_a: In 1762, Austrian Empress Maria Theresa received a letter from the front that confronted her with the question of whether to attack Prussia. She decided to end the war and make a peace treaty, even though it was difficult for her to concentrate on state affairs. text_b: Vienna, 1815, during the Congress of Vienna. At that time, Vienna was the most important metropolis in Europe, where the rulers of the world met. The young Christel Weinzinger is a glove maker. She advertises her business by throwing a bouquet of flowers with her business card into every Monarch carriage that passes by. In the process, she accidentally hits the Russian Tsar Alexander in the head. The bouquet is initially mistaken for a bomb and Christel is accused of attempted assassination. For insulting Majesty, she is finally sentenced to 25 lashes on the bare buttocks, but released just in time at the behest of the Tsar. The Tsar falls in love with Christel, and the two spend the evening in the vineyard. A romance arises, which Prince Metternich wants to use to his advantage, as it is important to him that the Tsar stays away from the meetings of the Congress. Pepi, Metternich's secretary, who is in love with Christel, is now supposed to support the Tsar's "women's stories". Christel tells her friends about her experience, but they do not believe her until she is picked up by a fancy carriage to her new villa. The Tsar himself, who does not allow himself to be involved by Metternich, however, shines by absence. Instead, Uralsky, his official doppelganger and stand-in for boring social engagements, causes some confusion for both Metternich and Christel in the further course of the plot. The romance ends with Napoleon Bonaparte's escape from the island of Elba and his march on Paris. Alexander is leaving as quickly as any other ruler. Christel is left unhappy, but Pepi is happy to have her for himself again.

anchor_text: François Coste is an art dealer with a well-stocked address book, but whose personal life is a failure. After buying a Greek vase on a whim, jeopardizing their shared art gallery, his partner Catherine challenges him to present his best friend to her before the end of the month, or else the vase will be returned to him.
text_a: Summer of 1943, in a place of occupied France, not far from the Spanish border, lives an old and famous sculptor, tired of life and the madness of men. However, with the arrival of a young Spanish woman who has escaped from a refugee camp and who will serve as his muse, the desire to return to work and sculpt his latest work is reborn in him. In the mountain workshop, as they work, the model and the artist speak with simplicity about everything around them.
text_b: François Coste is an art dealer with a busy schedule, but a failure in his personal life. After impulsively purchasing an expensive Greek vase, endangering the municipal art gallery, his partner Catherine presents him with a challenge. François must present his best friend before the end of the month or return the vase.

anchor_text: The episodic novel tells the story of Ferdinand Bardamu, who escapes the turmoil of World War I and returns to Paris after stations in Africa and America, where he studies medicine, makes his way as a poor doctor and eventually becomes the acting director of a psychiatric institution on the outskirts of the city. Again and again, the tragic and unscrupulous figure of Leon Robinson appears and gives the plot the decisive twists. The stations are associated with Bardamus' loves as characteristic female figures.
text_a: The work, with an autobiographical background like all Céline's novels, follows the events of Ferdinand Bardamu. A young soldier in the First World War, he then went to colonial Africa, where he realized firsthand the modus operandi of the French colonizers, and in the United States after the First World War, where he witnessed the prodigies of Fordism and mass society. In the second half of the opera he returns to France, where he becomes a doctor and opens a practice in a run-down suburb of Paris (the fictional La Garenne-Rancy), and then ends up working at a mental health institute. The disparate elements of the novel are interconnected by recurring encounters with Léon Robinson, an unfortunate but ambiguous character, whose experience is in some ways parallel to that of Bardamu.
text_b: The novel tells the story of Yusuf, a twelve-year-old boy born in the fictional town of Kawa in Tanzania in the early 20th century. Yusuf's father is a hotelier and is in debt to a rich and powerful Arab merchant named Aziz. At the beginning of the story, Yusuf is pawned on account of that debt and must work as Aziz's servant without receiving any remuneration. Yusuf travels with Aziz's caravan through Central Africa and the Congo Basin, areas that have not been traded with for many generations. The caravan encounters hostility from local tribes, wild animals, and difficult terrain. When he returns to East Africa, World War I begins and Aziz witnesses how the German Army recruits Africans as soldiers by force.

anchor_text: Gerda learns with her friend Karin that Aunt Trude from Buxtehude has deposited a fortune of one million D-Mark (German marks) in a safe deposit box of a bank in Salzburg, which she is to receive on her 21st birthday. But on her wedding day, she receives nothing but a huge suitcase with ten dresses. Only when the disappointed Gerda has already sold some of the dresses, she learns that in one of the dresses must be the key to the safe. Gerda, Karin and their friends, the naive detectives Rudi and Moritz, hurry to look for the sold clothes, unaware that Moritz has already found the right key and thoughtlessly inserted it. The trail leads to Kitzbühel, where the boutique owner Toni has already resold some of the clothes. So the buyers have to be searched, which leads to considerable difficulties. Eventually, a gangster gets wind of the matter and steals the key. In the bank, the gangster with the key appears first, then a fake Aunt Trude appears three times, and finally the real heiress Gerda, who can prove everything and becomes a couple with Toni.
text_a: Three young people living in a small coastal town on the Black Sea spend their time unplanned. One day, they discover a black Gucci pin-striped suit in the window of a fashion store in the big city, which they can only reach by ferry. Now they're working their asses off to get that suit so they can look like successful, confident adults. After a few setbacks, they manage to acquire the suit. They agree to wear it alternately. Shtyr uses the suit to enhance his appearance. In his expensive suit, he walks undisturbed into the champagne reception of a luxury cruise ship. When his mother is in desperate need of help, he tries to contact his father in the suit, who abandoned the family long ago and works as a successful tailor in the city. Geka wears the suit in hopes of gaining the respect of his attractive stepmother Asya, who is apparently cheating on his father by dating other men. On the one hand, he detests Asya for her more or less obvious lightness, on the other hand, he seems to be attracted to her. Dumbo puts on the suit to impress Dina, a beautiful Jewish fishmonger with whom he has a one-sided crush; Dina is visibly delighted by Dumbo's appearance, but she uses him primarily to shake off an intrusive admirer named Arthur. The experiences of all three youths with the suit end in tragedy.

anchor_text: Harry Keach, a construction engineer, lives alone with his son Howard. As a result of an accident at work, he is fired. Affected morally, he gradually becomes unpleasant and difficult to live with.
text_a: Widowed factory worker Harry lives with his aspiring writer son.
