Believing that he is going to die, he recalls his past and his romantic adventures, especially during the Spanish Civil War, and cannot help but confide in his wife.

anchor_text: Footprints found in the Himalayas suggest the existence of mysterious mountain dwellers. Botanist Dr. Rollason and his wife, Helen, are exploring plants in the Himalayas. The two set up their headquarters in a Buddhist monastery. Dr. Rollason is expecting an expedition led by Dr. Friend to search the mountains for the legendary Yeti. Five of them set off, but the climb is difficult. McNee steps into a bear trap designed for the Yeti. At night, a snowman comes into the camp and they shoot him with a rifle. But Dr. Friend doesn't just have scientific goals. He's greedy for profit. He wants to capture a Yeti alive and use the dead as bait. The snowmen, normally peace-loving creatures, put up a fight, driving the expedition members to madness and death. Only Dr. Rollason and his wife can return.
text_a: Myth and monster investigator/researcher Sir Lionel Frost sets off on an adventure in the Pacific Northwest to prove the existence of man's legendary ancestor. He is accompanied on his journey by Adelina Fortnight, a free-spirited, independent adventurer who possesses the only known map that could lead her to Link's secret location. With the venture, Frost also wants to gain the recognition of his/her narrow-minded discoverer. When they find what they're looking for, the creature Frost names Mr. Link turns out to be a kind-hearted and surprisingly clever, but also very lonely creature. Mr. Link believes he's the last of his kind, but a rumor gives him hope that in the mysterious place known as Shangri-La, there are other beings like him. Its cold-weather cousins, hairy, blue-skinned Yetis, are said to have built a society far removed from the threat to humanity. Together with the expedition team, Mr. Link sets off in search of this fabled paradise. After being confronted by a Yeti in the Himalayas, they are taken to Shangri-La, where they are quickly thrown into a hole to keep out intruders, especially humans. The group manages to escape. Mr. Link doesn't care about his own kind. He's more attracted to his friend Lionel, with whom he plans exciting explorations in the future.
text_b: It was the mid-1950s. English botanist Rollason (Peter Cushing), his wife Helen and his assistant are guests at a monastery in Tibet. An American expedition soon arrives with the aim of discovering the legendary Yeti by climbing the Himalayan heights. Rollason decides to join them for a scientific purpose, despite the Lama's warnings. The group soon reaches the Highlands and the naive botanist learns that the Americans want to shoot the creature for purely commercial purposes. But nothing goes as planned, and accidents and deaths multiply due to lack of oxygen, avalanches, and snow creatures roaming the mountains.

anchor_text: A villain pursues a hotel barber's waiter who accidentally came into possession of a large diamond he had stolen. The boy, pretending to be 12 years old, will eventually succeed in bringing the real culprit of the theft to justice.
text_a: Floyd Gambino, convicted of grand theft auto, is released from prison after serving five months. His boss Joe Malcomb offers him a jewelry store heist, and Floyd immediately agrees. The robbery succeeds, but leaves behind the death of a jewelry store customer. Floyd hides in a motel, but is robbed by Michelle, the owner's wife, who takes the stolen car.
text_b: A barber's apprentice gets involved in the theft of a diamond and to throw off those looking for it will disguise himself and pretend to be a 12-year-old boy.

Rice reviews her notes from her sessions with Bynum and begins her own investigation. At the same time, he falls in love with the enigmatic Brooke, despite her increasingly suspicious behavior. anchor_text: Leona Hart, who lives in Baltimore, loses her little girl. She employs the African-American Clara Mayfield as a housekeeper, whom she met in Jamaica. Clara takes care of Leona's son David, who is considered an outsider, as if he were her own son. Leona and Bill's marriage is on the rocks. After the divorce, Leona, David and Clara move to the country. David sees his father on the weekends, sometimes he and Clara take trips to Baltimore. Clara often openly accuses Leona of not performing her role as a mother well enough. She also refuses to be intimidated by Bill when she interferes with the ex-spouses' discussions about David's future. Clara's past is shrouded in mystery. David is going through Clara's things to find out. Clara becomes angry, but tells a little later that she once had a son. He raped a 15-year-old girl. When Clara confronted him, he also raped his own mother and killed himself. Leona meets a new partner who wants to move to California. David wants to live with his father in Baltimore so he can stay close to Clara. Clara realizes that she's become too much of a surrogate for him. She quits and finds a new job at a hospital. A little later, David comes to the hospital and thanks Clara. Clara tells him that their friendship will always be with him.
text_a: The film tells the story of a family in crisis. The mother, Leona (Kathleen Quinlan), flees Jamaica to mourn the loss of her daughter, Edith. Meanwhile, she meets the friendly housekeeper Clara Mayfield (Goldberg). Clara takes the depression out of Clara with a blunt senseless style. Leona is so impressed with her that she takes Clara back to her home in Baltimore to be housekeeper and nanny to the youngest son David (Harris). At first he resists and sees her as an intruder, but the parents are completely wrapped up in their own grief and with the marriage about to end, David trusts Clara and depends on her. Clara harbors her own dark secret, which, when revealed, serves to reaffirm the bond between these two very different characters, though full of affective sensitivity.
text_b: The setting is London, the time is now. The prostitute Leonora lost her daughter a few years ago. When she meets the neglected and emotionally unstable Cenci, she is amazed at how much the girl resembles her drowned daughter. Cenci is similar to Leonora, because she resembles her mother, whose death she has not yet been able to process. Cenci takes Leonora into her home and develops a symbiotic relationship with her, in which the two women repeatedly give in to the illusion that the other is the lost object of love. The situation takes a threatening turn when Cenci's stepfather, Albert, shows up. Albert was banished from the house after Cenci accused him of sexually assaulting her. A short time later, she accuses him of raping her. Albert, on the other hand, tries to convince Leonora that Cenci is psychotic and, on the contrary, has tried to seduce him. Leonora finally believes him and tries to confront Cenci with this reality. However, Cenci rejects her, goes to Albert, sleeps with him and commits suicide shortly afterwards. When Leonora and Albert meet at Cenci's funeral, she stabs him with a knife.

anchor_text: In British India, the young Toomai learns the trade of roe deer from his father who regularly participates in large hunts organized by the white "sahibs". During one of them, led by Peterson, Toomai's father is killed by a tiger. The elephant he cared for, Kala Nag, is now in the care of another antelope. The child who dreamed of succeeding his father, escapes into the forest with Kala Nag and soon, attends the "elephant dance"...
text_a: Toomai is the son of a kornak (that is, an elephant driver). Orphaned during a hunting trip, to prevent his father's elephant from being given to another kornak, Toomai decides to run away with the pachyderm into the forest, where he watches the elephant dance...
text_b: An Indian maharajah commissioned Tarzan to rescue a herd of elephants threatened by the construction of a dam. Despite the accumulated obstacles, the ape-man manages to carry out his mission thanks to the help of a princess and a little hornet...

anchor_text: Mona Dearly is a resident of Verplanck, New York. In the first scene, she leaves her house and drives away in her car. Her car drives into the Hudson River later, and Dearly dies in the process. The city's police chief, Wyatt Rash, suspects that Dearly's car was tampered with and is conducting an investigation. It turns out that many of the locals were hostile to Dearly and are considered suspects. That includes Bobby Calzone, who Dearly threatened the night before she died. Calzone is about to marry his pregnant fiancée Ellen Rash, the police chief's daughter. The previous events are shown in flashbacks. Rash promises Calzone that he won't betray him. In the end, Calzone and Ellen get married.
text_a: The year is 1866. Josephine, seduced by the family photographer, now carries an illegitimate child. Disgraced by her relatives, she had to leave her home, abandon her newborn and flee to the American West. His prospects are extremely limited. As a woman, she is very often viewed with suspicion and suffers many sexual harassments. So she is forced to disguise herself as a man in order to work honestly and survive. At a mining camp in Ruby City, she met Percy who recommended her for a job as a stable boy. However, she sees scenes in which Percy displays a deep contempt for women. Feeling insecure, Jo, as she now calls herself, eventually became a sheep farmer for farmer Frank Badger. After properly saving up, Jo separates from her employer and buys her own farm. Soon, she hires a young Chinese man, accused of "taking the work of others" and threatened with lynching. This one becomes his cook and his housekeeper...
text_b: In Verplanck, a small town in upstate New York, the whispery and extremely unpopular Mona Dearly drowns after driving her car into a lake. Police Chief Wyatt Rash opened an investigation after discovering that the car's braking system had been tampered with. In the course of the investigation, however, the number of suspects gradually grows, as there were many who did not feel sorry for the very unpleasant Mrs. Dearly, starting with her future son-in-law Bobby Calzone, convinced himself to be the murderer of the woman.

Feeling insecure, Jo, as she now calls herself, eventually became a sheep farmer for farmer Frank Badger. After properly saving up, Jo separates from her employer and buys her own farm. Soon, she hires a young Chinese man, accused of "taking the work of others" and threatened with lynching. This one becomes his cook and his housekeeper...
text_b: Somewhere north of Manhattan lies the peaceful little town of Verplanck. No events have ever attracted media attention to this town. Yet Verplanck has his stories. One day, Mona Dearly, one of the local celebrities, drives her son's Yugo and falls into the river. His death leaves no one indifferent. The murder is then quickly confirmed with the discovery of evidence that the vehicle was sabotaged. Police Chief Wyatt Rash then attempts to unravel the crime by meeting a gallery of characters who gravitate around Mona. But the investigation could take a long time, because none of the residents liked Mona. Even her own family hated her. She led a hard life to her son Jeff and his partner Bobby Calzone; she did not take care of Phil, her husband; she hated Ellen Rash, Bobby's fiancée and Lieutenant Feege.

anchor_text: The unnamed asthmatic boy in the film is persuaded by his mother to throw away some broken and old toys, including his favorite stuffed animal Kooky. In the boy's imagination, the teddy bear comes to life and is able to escape at the last second from the landfill where it landed. Convinced that he doesn't belong here, he embarks on an adventurous journey home through a fairy-tale forest. There he meets various fantastic creatures of nature, such as the friendly Captain of Hergot, the guardian of the forest, and Anuška, who intends to become the new guardian through intrigue.
text_a: Little Ondra is six years old and has asthma. His mother, to protect him, decides to get rid of Kooky, his teddy bear. Ondra then dreams of Kooky's journey from the junkyard where he is to Ondra's house. A journey that will take him through a forest populated by mysterious beings, including the guardian of the forest, Bondieu, who will help Kooky get rid of three bad guys who want to take him back to the landfill.
text_b: Sarah is passionate about fairy tales. One night, she finds herself forced to babysit her younger half-brother Toby. Trying to soothe his tears by telling him a story about a goblin king who fell in love with a human girl, she utters a fateful sentence that transports the baby to a fantasy world ruled by Jareth, the androgynous and troubled goblin king. She must follow him into this fantasy world of goblins, goblins and fairies and, to prevent the boy from becoming a goblin himself, overcome in less than 13 hours the trials of Jareth's labyrinth... for which she cannot help but feel a certain fascination.

anchor_text: The main character Rick is a successful Hollywood screenwriter who lives in a sugarcoated world of villas, resorts, beaches and nightclubs, where the pursuit of pleasure is the most important thing. He travels between Los Angeles and Las Vegas in search of love and himself. In private life, however, he is a tormented man: he has lost a brother and feels responsible for the latter's death. She's had a failed marriage and doesn't know what to do with her life. In his personal stories, various images of women, events and flashbacks of his childhood follow each other, in a continuous flow that he cannot tame. Each episode of Rick's life is associated with a particular Tarot card: The Tower, The Hermit, The Hanged Man, Death, and so on, indicating a possible reading of events. All in all, Rick seems to resemble the protagonist of a fairy tale that his father told him when he was a child: a prince, son of the king of the East, set off to the West in search of a pearl; when he reached his destination, the prince drank from the cup of oblivion and forgot who he was and the reason for his journey, falling into a deep sleep. Just like the prince, Rick can't seem to remember who he is. The pearl, the meaning of his life, eludes him all the time and it's useless to look for this deep meaning in the women he meets and who love him. Perhaps, as his father himself tells him, Rick is destined to remain a wandering pilgrim on this earth, never to find his place in the world.
text_a: A screenwriter - Rick (played by Christian Bale) - living in Los Angeles tries to find meaning in his life. Rick is a slave to the Hollywood system. He's addicted to success, but at the same time he's desperate for the emptiness in his life. He's at home, he's in a world of illusions, but at the same time he's pursuing a real life. Like the tarot card that gives the title its name, Rick is bored and needs outside stimulation. But the "cup horse" is also an artist, a romantic and an adventurer. In Terrence Malick's seventh film, the camera slips and accompanies, once again, a tormented hero in his search for the meaning of life. A voice-over overlaps with images that also seek their own authenticity. And once again, Malick articulates a world that is tottering. His symphony of images contrasts with cold, functional architecture and the timeless beauty of nature. Rick's inner monologue is joined by the voices of the women who cross his path; women who represent different principles in life: while one lives in the real world, the other embodies beauty and sensuality. What path will Rick choose? In the city of Los Angeles and the surrounding desert, will he find his own way?
text_b: Rickard is what you'd call a loser. His wife's dead, and his son Eric hates him. During the day, he hangs out with his buddy Geko in her filthy apartment. They're sitting in front of their computers, gambling, talking nonsense, trying to kill time, doing amateur porn with Tess. This one is 21 years old and has dreamed of making sex movies since she was 12. Recently, she even had her labia minimized. While the three of them indulge in sexual excesses on camera, Eric sits in a darkened room listening to loud music. He occupies himself in the realm of his thoughts with God and the world, doing nothing else, and devoting himself in the meantime to his breeding of worms. This chaotic symbiosis of the Plattenbau-Vier-WG gets out of hand when one day the two men want to drive Tess with motorcycle helmet, ski mask and baseball bat to a brutal kind of porn. Tess flees the apartment, but the very next day she returns, packed with a huge purchase of food and drink: what follows is the big meal... So their escape attempt remains episode and none of the roommates really seems to be able to counteract the desolation of their life together.

anchor_text: Samuel Makin, a tough cop, is officially killed in the line of duty. In reality, his death was faked by a secret organization to recruit him. The purpose of this organization is to protect the U.S. Constitution without respecting it. Now known as Remo, our hero becomes the arm of this organization, having been trained by an old oriental, Master Chiun. The latter belongs to an ancient line of Korean assassins and undertakes to teach him the Sinanju ( pressure on the nerve centers ), ancestor of all martial arts. As Remo learns more and more, he gains extraordinary abilities...
text_a: An army veteran, the sole survivor of a decimated American patrol, meets a young South Korean, Short Round, as well as others left behind by the war. He leads them to an unoccupied Buddhist temple, which they turn into an observation camp. But when they discover that they are in the immediate vicinity of a North Korean communist camp, the troop prepares for the possibility of a fight...
text_b: Sam Makin (Fred Ward) is a tough Brooklyn street cop and Marine Corps veteran of the Vietnam War. He is reluctantly recruited as an assassin for a secret U.S. organization, CURE. The recruitment is done through a strange method: his death is faked and he is given a new face and a new name. Renamed "Remo Williams" (for the name and location of the urinal manufacturer in Makin's hospital room), his face is surgically altered and his elderly, mocking and unfeeling Korean martial arts master Chiun (Joel Grey) trains him to be a human killing machine. Although Remo's training is extremely accelerated by Chiun's standards, Remo learns seemingly impossible skills, such as dodging bullets and running over water and wet cement. Chiun teaches Remo the Korean martial art called "Sinanju". Remo's instruction is interrupted when CURE sends him to investigate a corrupt weapons procurement program within the U.S. Army. the United States.

anchor_text: During a night patrol, familyless cop Makin is thrown overboard with his car by recruiter Mac. Left for dead, with a facial plastic and a new name, Remo Williams, he is trained by Chiun, master of Sinanju, to serve as the eleventh command in an organization headed by Director Smith, who is concerned with evicting and punishing the perpetrators of state-protected mischief. The training will be hard but Remo manages, not without irony, to win the trust of the master (despite not being Korean like him), and to learn special skills such as dodging bullets, walking on fresh concrete, not being afraid of dizziness and not needing weapons. All of which will come in handy when, aided by Major Fleming, an intellectual like himself, he must challenge the bad guy on duty, George Grove, who builds flawed weapons for the government.
text_a: Sam Staziak (Daniel Quinn), a rookie cop from Los Angeles, is also a "scanner", a person born with telepathic and telekinetic abilities who as a child was saved by police officer Peter Harrigan because he lived with his father in conditions unsuitable for his development as a person. His new foster father raised him and enlisted him at a young age in the Los Angeles Police Department. When a string of murders begin to destroy the police department, his foster father as chief of police sends "Sam" to use his mental powers to investigate the unexplained deaths. "Sam" facing sensory overload and possible madness, uses his powers to find Karl Glock (Richard Lynch) and his fellow soothsayer Zena as responsible for these murders.
text_b: Sam Makin (Fred Ward) is a tough Brooklyn street cop and Marine Corps veteran of the Vietnam War. He is reluctantly recruited as an assassin for a secret U.S. organization, CURE. The recruitment is done through a strange method: his death is faked and he is given a new face and a new name. Renamed "Remo Williams" (for the name and location of the urinal manufacturer in Makin's hospital room), his face is surgically altered and his elderly, mocking and unfeeling Korean martial arts master Chiun (Joel Grey) trains him to be a human killing machine. Although Remo's training is extremely accelerated by Chiun's standards, Remo learns seemingly impossible skills, such as dodging bullets and running over water and wet cement. Chiun teaches Remo the Korean martial art called "Sinanju". Remo's instruction is interrupted when CURE sends him to investigate a corrupt weapons procurement program within the U.S. Army. the United States.

anchor_text: The film is set on the Italian A90 motorway, the peripheral belt of Rome, abbreviated GRA (Grande Raccordo Anulare). Rosi shot for over two years, while the editing of the film required eight months of work. According to the director, the film is inspired by Italo Calvino's novel, The Invisible Cities, which imagines Marco Polo telling Kubilai Khan's court about his travels.
text_a: The film depicts life around the Grande Raccordo Anulare, the great highway that circumnavigates the city of Rome.[4] Rosi spent two years on the filming and the editing required eight more months.[5] According to the director, the film is inspired by Italo Calvino's novel The Invisible Cities (1972), in which the explorer Marco Polo explains his travels through the domains of the Chinese emperor Kublai Khan.[4][5]
text_b: Francesco, Sergio and Antonio are three privileged young men who live in the provincial town of Minervino Murge, located between Puglia and Basilicata: the film is a portrait of their lives too imbued with apathy and provincialism to make them want to go to other destinations. Indeed, when Antonio's aunt, an apathetic academic, proposes to him to go live with her in Rome, transferring his enrolment from the University of Bari to that of the capital, after a short time, Antonio gives up and returns to his village, unable to abandon the prejudices, clichés and rituals of his native province, irreversibly rooted in his being.

anchor_text: In the summer of 1956, Colin Clark works as an assistant on the British set of the film The Prince and the Showgirl, starring Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe, who is also on her honeymoon with her new husband, playwright Arthur Miller. When Miller leaves England, Clark gives Monroe a tour of London life and, after spending a week together, falls in love with her, allowing the actress to escape the routine of Hollywood and the pressures of work.
text_a: The film is about the adventures and misadventures of a group of visitors at a luxurious Los Angeles hotel. Among them are British actress Diana Barrie (Maggie Smith) and her husband, homosexual Sidnet Cochran (Michael Caine), who come to town for Diana's Oscar nomination for best actress; Hannah Warren (Jane Fonda) and her former husband Billy Warren (Alan Alda), who meet at the hotel to discuss their daughter's situation; doctors Gump (Richard Pryor) and Panama (Bill Cosby), who come with their wives to relax; and Marvin and Millie Michaels (Walter Matthau and Elaine May), who go to a bar observing the mitzvah, and he ends up with a prostitute.
text_b: In the summer of 1956, Colin Clark works as an assistant on the set of the British film The Prince and the Showgirl, starring Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe, the latter is on her honeymoon with her new husband, playwright Arthur Miller. When Miller leaves the country, Clark introduces Monroe to the British lifestyle and they spend a week together, during which time she escapes her Hollywood routine and the pressures of work.[5]

anchor_text: Little chimney sweep Tom falls into a river after meeting the rich girl Ellie and being chased out of her house. There, he dies and is transformed into a "water child", as a cockroach tells him. His religious education begins. A series of adventures and lessons begins for Tom. After proving himself to be a moral being, he enjoys the company of other aquatic children. His spiritual guides in his new world are the elves Mrs. Doasyouwouldbedoneby (Doasyouwouldbedoneby) and Mrs. Bedonebyasyoudid (Bedonebyasyoudid) as well as Mother Carey. During the week, Tom is taught by Ellie, who also fell into the river. His old master Grimes is drowning, too. In his latest adventure, Tom travels to the end of the world to help Grimes, who is being punished for his misdeeds. As Tom showed his willingness to do even things he doesn't like, simply because they are the right things to do, he is transformed back into a human being. He will become a "great scientist" who can design "railroads, steam engines, telegraphs, boxes, and so forth". Tom and Ellie are back together.
text_a: The book is about the adventures of a little scavenger named Tom. The latter leads a difficult life, made of toil and hardship, under the ferocity of his master, the cruel Mr. Grimes. His courage and good character enable him to overcome the trials he endures every day. But, after accidentally meeting a little girl from a good family, Ellie, Tom is accidentally led to fall into a river, where fairies turn him into a water baby. This passage into another reality will result, for the little boy, in both physical and psychological transformations. The encounter with creatures as strange as they are varied, as well as the life and moral lessons distilled by the fairies (including the remarkable sisters Mrs Bedonebyasyoudid and Mrs Doasyouwouldbedoneby), will lead Tom to make the best of himself, getting rid of the bad habits and values of his former life.
text_b: Tom is a lonely introverted and troubled teenager who lives with his mother Elaine with whom he has just moved into an inherited house. As Tom explores the nearby seasonal lake, fed by a mysterious and dangerous underground river that flows intermittently, he discovers the remains of a newborn in the water. Elaine, finding herself in trouble, needs the help of neighbor Ward who jealously watches over his daughter Holly, a manipulative girl who has a crush on Tom. While Ward will do anything to keep people from knowing about the newborn, Holly, sensing that Tom has found the remains, will push him to kill his father.

anchor_text: In November 1959, Truman Capote travels with his girlfriend Harper Lee to the small town of Holcomb, Kansas, to investigate the cold-blooded murder of farmer Herbert W. Clutter and his family. First of all, Capote can't find anyone who wants to talk to him at all. It is only after he shares his experiences in New York society at a dinner with Alvin Dewey and his wife that he receives one invitation after another. Slowly, Capote is gaining insight into the events surrounding the murder. He even manages to get permission to talk to the main perpetrator Perry Smith and his accomplice Dick Hickock in prison. What was planned as a short detour becomes more and more of a long process, in the course of which he even occasionally bumps into his girlfriend Harper Lee. Increasingly, Capote develops a very special relationship with Perry Smith. When Smith learns that he intends to deal with the crime in a novel entitled In Cold Blood, he sexually assaults Capote in his cell. After Capote explains to him that despite the title, it is mainly about the background to this murder, Smith agrees to further talks. On April 14, 1965, Smith and Hickock are executed at the Lansing Correctional Facility in Lansing. Truman Capote will be present at the execution. First, Hickock is hanged, but it takes a very long time for him to be dead. When Smith is hanged, Capote leaves in horror, shaking up the execution site. His novel In Cold Blood becomes a bestseller, but after its publication, Capote is burned out. Capote receives a box containing Smith's estate. Among other things, it contains a guitar and many drawings. Among some children's drawings, Truman Capote also finds a drawing showing him.
text_a: Kansas, November 1959: Perry Smith, who had been convicted of manslaughter, and Dick Hickock plan to rob the Clutter family's home. They believe Mr. Clutter has a large amount of cash in a vault. After gradually realizing that there is neither a safe nor larger amounts of cash in the house, Smith kills the couple and two of their teenage children. The bodies of the victims are discovered the next day. The police investigation will begin immediately. The attending psychologist doesn't believe in a robbery-murder. All that's missing is a few checks, a radio and $43. The two fugitives try to evade capture, but when they pay with the stolen checks, they are arrested. The police interrogate the two and confront them with incriminating evidence, such as a bloody boot print that matches exactly the profile of the boots of one of the two. Refusal to answer delays the interrogation. They claim they made a mistake and left a witness. In the end, Hickock confesses to avoid a death sentence. A grand jury finds Smith and Hickock guilty and sentences them to death by hanging. In 1965, they are executed in Lansing.
text_b: We're at the El Morocco nightclub, New York, 1959: A diminutive, homosexual man named Truman Capote (Toby Jones) slowly enters the arm of an elegant, high-society lady named Babe Paley (Sigourney Weaver). A long mink coat is entrusted to the wardrobe girl, a match is lit, a cigarette is lit and... Manhattan high society walks the premises. Caviar is served while a lovely and beautiful singer, Kitty Dean (Gwyneth Paltrow), reminisces about Cole Porter. After a night at the city's most famous and prestigious clubs, Truman Capote reads a short article in The New York Times newspaper, November 16, 1959, with the following headline: "A Rich Farmer and Three Members of His Family Are Murdered". The author decides to investigate a little more about the case and thus be able to start a new book, under the title of "In Cold Blood", which would later become his masterpiece. From here, a turning point in the writer's life occurred that would mark the beginning of the fascinating six-year period of the novel's creation. During those years Truman Capote investigated in the town of Kansas with his friend Harper Lee (Sandra Bullock) what happened in the tragic murder. Asking the cops Alvin Dewey (Jeff Daniels) and even the killer himself, Perry Smith (Daniel Craig), about what attracts him more than just knowing why he committed the crime.

anchor_text: Interior designer Eve and lawyer Arthur are an elderly married couple. Her three daughters, Flyn, Joey and Renata, have left home and are living their own lives. Arthur decides to temporarily separate from his wife in order to gain clarity about his future goals in Greece. The decision causes the established family structures to be thrown into disarray. Joey quits her job as an editor and is determined to abort her unborn child. Renata, who has to endure internal family rivalries with Joey, gets into a marital crisis as her husband is dissatisfied with his work as a writer. The abandoned Eve makes an unsuccessful suicide attempt. Arthur reveals to his daughters that he wants to divorce Eve and marry the somewhat naive mid-60s Pearl. Pearl is not accepted by the daughters Joey and Renata in the family. Nevertheless, Arthur marries her in the presence of his daughters. On the night of the wedding celebration, tragedy strikes when the abandoned Eve enters the house and talks to Joey. Eve then disappears in a hurry, ending her own life in the surf of the sea.
text_a: Elderly ex-Marshal Steve Judd is hired by a bank in Sacramento to transport gold from a mine high in the mountains to the city. His former traveling companion Gil Westrum and his young companion Heck, working as showmen in Sacramento, offer Judd to accompany him, which he accepts. What Judd doesn't know is that Westrum and Heck are determined to steal the gold. On their way to the mountains, they meet young Elsa, who is seeking to escape her strictly religious father in order to marry gold-digger Billy Hammond. Elsa joins the group. Arriving in the rough gold-digging town, however, Elsa must find that Billy and his brothers, with whom he lives, are unscrupulous and violent. When Billy tries to rape Elsa on their wedding night, she flees to Judd and his companions and asks for protection. The group that received the gold shipment takes Elsa back to Sacramento against Billy's wishes. When Westrum and Heck attempt to steal the gold, they are disarmed and handcuffed by Judd, who has sensed the betrayal. Chased by Billy and his brothers, they try to reach Elsa's father's house. Judd is aided in this by the perceptive Heck, who has vowed to surrender to the Sheriff upon arrival. But the pursuers are faster and lure the group, lurking in the Father's house, into a trap. Judd is badly wounded, until finally Westrum, plagued by his conscience, leaps to his old friend's side. In a duel in the front yard of the house, Judd and Westrum shoot the Hammond gang before Judd is struck down again. Westrum promises to deliver the gold to the dying Jew in Sacramento.
text_b: Arthur is a lawyer married to Eve, an interior decorator. The couple have three daughters: Renata, a poetess married to struggling writer Frederick; Flyn, an up-and-coming actress who is mostly away from home making low-budget films; and finally Joey, wife of journalist Mike, who fails to build a career and resents her mother's excessive affection for her sister Renata. One day Arthur unexpectedly announces that he wants to separate from his wife and live on his own. Eve, depressed and mentally unstable, attempts suicide. The shock of these two events causes a rift between the sisters. Arthur returns from a trip to Greece with Pearl, a vivacious woman he intends to marry. The daughters are upset that Arthur ignored Eve's suicide attempt and sought out another woman, whom Joey describes as vulgar. Arthur and Pearl are married in the family summer residence, and the three daughters attend the wedding. Later in the evening, Joey gets mad at Pearl after she accidentally breaks one of Eve's pots. Frederick, drunk, unsuccessfully attempts to rape Flynn. Meanwhile, Joey finds Eve at home, and sadly explains to her what she has done for her mother, and how contemptuously she has been treated. At this point, Eve walks onto the beach and into the open sea. Joey, Mike and Pearl unsuccessfully attempt to save her, and in the attempt Joey almost drowns, being saved by Pearl herself.

anchor_text: Arthur and Eve had been married for many years and had three daughters. Eve thought she would give her life meaning by creating the most harmonious home possible for those she loved. However, her obsessive taste is weighing on her family, as well as the severe depression she is going through. When Arthur couldn't take it anymore, he decided to part ways for a while. During a trip to Greece, he fell in love with another woman and wanted to marry her. The father introduces his new partner to his three daughters and causes a family drama. Divorce is a test for them, and they're going to have to solve each other's problems.
text_a: A family of three sisters is seen in the painful process of their parents' separation. His father's separation from his mentally unstable, obsessive, controlling mother and his marriage to a "normal" woman. With well-constructed characters who relate to each other in beautifully decorated interiors but empty and icy as the soul of the central character (the mother who works as an interior decorator) and in the style of playwright Anton Chekhov, (another of the objects of desire of the New York filmmaker) it is precisely in this fact in which the emotional load of the characters intensifies to the limit.
text_b: The story is told from the perspectives of three female characters: Rachel, Anna, and Megan. Rachel Watson is a 32-year-old woman who has fallen into depression and alcoholism since her divorce from Tom. Her ex-husband has since moved back in with his mistress, Anna, and their daughter in the couple's former home. In order to keep her days occupied and deceive her roommate about losing her job, Rachel commutes to London daily by train, passing near her old address. Sitting in the same seat every day, she sees through the window a neighboring couple of Tom and Anna, whom she has nicknamed Jason and Jess and whom she imagines as a happy, straightforward couple. One night, she sees Jess with another man, and learns a few days later of the disappearance of this woman, whose real name is Megan Hipwell. Convinced that she saw something regarding Megan's disappearance during a drunken night out, Rachel approaches Scott/ Jason, and Tom and Anna cross paths again.

anchor_text: Twelve teenagers and one adult remain trapped inside Tham Luang Nang Non, a cave in Chiang Rai province. The monsoon rains raise the water level and the Thai authorities must rescue the 13 people trapped before they drown.
text_a: The film chronicles the events of the 2018 Tham Luang cave rescue, in which a Thai youth football team and their coach were trapped in a cave for a period of 18 days.
text_b: The film covers the events of the 2018 cave rescue, focusing on several people who contributed to the rescue effort, including water pump manufacturer Nopadol Niyomka, retired Thai Navy SEAL Saman Kunan, and, most notably, Irish cave diver Jim Warny.

anchor_text: When the "Wild Boars" soccer team and their coach make a detour to the Tham Luang Cave during a hike during the monsoon season, they are surprised by the water there, caused by a heavy rain. When the team doesn't show up for a celebration, the parents fear the worst. The boys and their coach are trapped in the tunnel system, and they're in danger of running out of oxygen if they're not rescued soon.
text_a: In June 2018, twelve young footballers from a football team and their coach were trapped in a cave in Doi Nang Non, Thailand. Due to a sharp rise in water, the rescue operation takes time, despite the enormous resources deployed. Many foreign experts are assisting the Thai authorities, including British divers Rick Stanton and John Volanthen of the Cave Rescue Organisation.
text_b: The film covers the events of the 2018 cave rescue, focusing on several people who contributed to the rescue effort, including water pump manufacturer Nopadol Niyomka, retired Thai Navy SEAL Saman Kunan, and, most notably, Irish cave diver Jim Warny.

anchor_text: Androcles, King of Thebes, Hercules, his son Illo and the faithful Timothy leave after Greece is threatened by a sinister power. The galleys carrying the warriors capsize and King Androkles is taken to the "Chamber of Oblivion". Hercules is banished to an island where he can defeat a monster and save Ismene. Ismene is the daughter of Antinea, queen of Atlantis, to whom they come. In the palace, they will find Androcles. Hercules survives several assassination attempts by the queen and finds a magical stone in a cave, upon which sunlight falls due to Hercules' action, releasing forces that destroy Atlantis.
text_a: The king of Thebes Androcles and his inseparable companion Hercules set off for an unknown destination with the aim of aborting a war prophesied by a Sibyl. After not a few problems, they will reach Atlantis, where the perfidious Antinea, who plans to conquer the world, does not hesitate to sacrifice her own daughter, which must also serve her so that the prophecies are not fulfilled.[1]
text_b: Its plot is related to the classic Hercules chained peplum. Likewise, here the hero arrives in the kingdom of Lydia and cannot resist living a life of pleasure alongside Queen Omphale. However, she is not in love with Hercules but with Inor, the son of her own mother's bitter enemy, Nemea. Forced by her mother to accept Hercules, Omphale uses a subterfuge that the Oracle of the High Goddess, who declares that Jupiter will give his consent to the marriage only on the condition that Hercules manages to defeat the strongest man in the world: Samson.

anchor_text: The fortune-teller Tiresias reveals to the king of Thebes, Androcles, that a terrible calamity is about to befall Greece. Androcles then summons the council of kings to convince them to unite, but all refuse. Hercules and Androcles embark on a galley with Hyllos and the dwarf Timotheus to an unknown destination, which turns out to be Atlantis.
text_a: The king of Thebes Androcles and his inseparable companion Hercules set off for an unknown destination with the aim of aborting a war prophesied by a Sibyl. After not a few problems, they will reach Atlantis, where the perfidious Antinea, who plans to conquer the world, does not hesitate to sacrifice her own daughter, which must also serve her so that the prophecies are not fulfilled.[1]
text_b: Its plot is related to the classic Hercules chained peplum. Likewise, here the hero arrives in the kingdom of Lydia and cannot resist living a life of pleasure alongside Queen Omphale. However, she is not in love with Hercules but with Inor, the son of her own mother's bitter enemy, Nemea. Forced by her mother to accept Hercules, Omphale uses a subterfuge that the Oracle of the High Goddess, who declares that Jupiter will give his consent to the marriage only on the condition that Hercules manages to defeat the strongest man in the world: Samson.

anchor_text: An Egyptian police orchestra from Alexandria is invited to a cultural festival in Israel. However, when the musicians arrive at the airport in Tel Aviv, there is no one to greet them. Colonel Tawfiq Zacharya and his musicians decide to take the bus to their destination. When buying tickets, however, band member Khaled mispronounces the destination (Arabs can't pronounce a hard "p"), and so the orchestra ends up in the sleepy Beit Hatikva provincial nest instead of Petah Tikva. When the musicians ask Dina, the owner of a small restaurant, about the Arab cultural center, they realize that they are in the wrong place. Recognizing their plight, Dina invites them to spend the night with her and two friendly families. The Egyptians and their Israeli hosts initially encounter reluctance and fear of contact; due to cultural differences and language barriers, there are various misunderstandings, sometimes funny, sometimes tragicomic. During the course of the evening and night, however, intense conversations and eventually a friendly relationship develop, especially between the serious Tawfiq and the cheerful Dina. The next day, the orchestra leaves for its actual destination.
text_a: A group of six anarchist musicians, led by Sanna Persson and a conductor named Magnus, decide to swarm to make music with objects that are not normally associated with music. They're planning a concert at four different venues in the city, each with humorous names. Before doing so, however, they carefully planned which objects could be used for the sounds. All the while, the group is watched by an unmusical policeman named Amadeus Warnebring, who was born into an up-and-coming musical family. He himself hates the sound of music. The group begins their concert in an operating room, where they also use, among other things, a well-known TV host as a sound object, who is in the hospital for hemorrhoids. The next part takes place in the counter and waiting room of a bank. Among other things, the group shreds bank notes to imitate the sound of a bass. The next play takes place in front of the opera house, where the group uses bulldozers to push them into a well. For the crescendo, the fountain statue is overturned, causing it to fall to the ground. The last piece is played on high-voltage cables that hang down and are played like violin strings. Warnebring eventually comes to the realization that he no longer perceives the sounds played by the musicians. At that moment, he devises a plan to get the musicians to use the entire city as an instrument, because he controls the energy supply rhythmically. The plan succeeds: Persson notices that the city noises around her are slowly becoming musical. Warning, on the other hand, is freed from all sounds. The film ends with the musicians fleeing the city and performing as a lounge act while Warnebring enjoys a quiet concert somewhere.
text_b: Members of the Egyptian Alexandria Police Band fly to Israel to perform at the opening of an Arab cultural center. There's no one waiting for them at the airport, and the despotic director Tewfiq doesn't want to ask the embassy for directions. The young Khaled asks for information at the airport, but, distracted by the beautiful women, does not quite understand the indication given to him, and the group gets off the bus to a lost village in the middle of the desert, where of course there is no Arab cultural center. Without a means of transportation until the next day, and therefore unable to reach the right destination, the members of the group must accept the hospitality of the restaurateur Dina, who welcomes Tewqif and Khaled into her home, and his colleague Simon, who together with his (not enthusiastic) wife hosts the rest of the gang. At first, there seems to be a lack of communication, but then, during the evening, everyone opens up to confide, especially through music, their fears and hopes.

anchor_text: The Alexandria Police Band is invited to Israel to inaugurate an Arab cultural center. Arriving at the airport, the small, self-contained troop takes a wrong turn and mistakenly arrives in a small, lost town in the Negev, Beit Hatikva ( the house of hope), a newly built town, dotted with grey HLMs and surrounded by boredom, a place isolated from the world, forgotten of old dreams of humanism and democracy, barely identifiable on a map, instead of Petah Tikva ( the door of hope). She is forced to spend the night there, getting along well and badly with the Israeli inhabitants, including an energetic restaurant owner...
text_a: Members of the Egyptian Alexandria Police Band fly to Israel to perform at the opening of an Arab cultural center. There's no one waiting for them at the airport, and the despotic director Tewfiq doesn't want to ask the embassy for directions. The young Khaled asks for information at the airport, but, distracted by the beautiful women, does not quite understand the indication given to him, and the group gets off the bus to a lost village in the middle of the desert, where of course there is no Arab cultural center. text_b: Amadeus Warnerbring, police officer, was born into a family of great musicians but strangely enough, he is bitterly allergic to music. That's when six drummers decided to play music illegally using the city as an instrument. Their performances create disturbances and damage. Warnerbring absolutely must find these terrorists who are spreading panic and making far too much noise for his liking... It's imperative that everything returns to calm and quiet. But to do that, Warnerbring is going to have to get closer to the musicians and the world of music that he hates so much...

anchor_text: During World War II, in the North Atlantic, Captain Jarvis' ship is torpedoed by a German submarine. He succeeds, with the support of his second-in-command, Lt. Rossi, to keep the crew's spirits up and get the ship back to an American port. Barely rested, they were given a new assignment to escort an Arctic convoy of Liberty ships carrying Lend-Lease equipment from Halifax to Murmansk on a new ship. They'll have to thwart the attacks and tricks of their underwater enemies...
text_a: # General presentation The commander of a U.S. nuclear submarine, James Ferraday, is ordered by the Secret Service to leave for the Arctic with the Soviet Westbound Boris Vasselov and U.S. officer Leslie Anders. They have to rally Zebra, a British polar base. Their mission is to recover a secret camera and its films. During the mission, many sabotages take place that endanger the submarine and its entire crew. In each case, the aircraft and crew were saved in extremis, at the cost of human lives. Who's the traitor on board, especially one who's willing to sacrifice his life to ensure the success of his mission to destroy the submarine? # Detailed synopsis
text_b: In the midst of World War II, an American ship is attacked by a German submarine: the ship goes down and many are the victims but Captain Steve Jarvis survives along with Lieutenant Joe Rossi and part of the crew. The survivors are picked up by another boat after 11 days in the middle of the ocean on a lifeboat. The same men soon find themselves on board a ship bound for Murmansk; soon this too remains isolated due to heavy German attacks but also thanks to the help of Russian aircraft the convoy manages to reach its destination.

anchor_text: Bertrand, the narrator of the story, is studying pharmacy in Paris. Together with his friend Guillaume, a student of political science, he meets Suzanne, who works at the National Tuberculosis Committee and attends evening courses in Italian, at the Café Le Luco. Guillaume invites Suzanne to a party at his mother's house. In the course of the evening, he deals exclusively with Sophie, a student from Ireland, while Bertrand withdraws and observes the two in particular Suzanne's reaction. After the party, when only Bertrand and Suzanne are left, Guillaume says he's too tired to go back to Paris. He urges Suzanne to go to bed, and then says goodbye to Bertrand, saying that he must console poor Suzanne. The next morning, Bertrand is the first to leave the house, he feels uncomfortable in his role as an accomplice. Suzanne goes to Bertrand to get back in touch with Guillaume. Bertrand is not enthusiastic about this, as he is not interested in her; but he allows her to invite him to a party. Guillaume finds out about it, and for a while, he puts up with her. After returning from the Easter holidays with his family, Bertrand hides in a book some money for a new suit. Guillaume takes the bills, but Bertrand suspects Suzanne. When Bertrand, who is now in a relationship with Sophie, learns that Suzanne is going to marry a fellow student, his image of her changes: he now finds her attractive.
text_a: Paris - Bertrand is a young man who earns his living as a caregiver for an elderly writer who dies suddenly by appropriating a play by the latter. Having become famous and wealthy thanks to the remarkable success of the work of which he is the author, he finds himself pressured by his manager so that he can achieve success. Bertrand realizes that this is an impossible task, until one evening, in Annecy in the French Alps, he meets Eva, a mature and charming high-end prostitute. A customer relationship will arise, the purpose of which is to draw inspiration for a subject that is certainly not original and to be able to extend the dialogues from her speeches. Bertrand, however, goes further, deluding himself of confirming himself a great author and of entering the life of the woman, showing himself intrusive and arrogant. Even Eva, who is gradually becoming attached to Bertrand, hides a secret, that of her husband Georges in prison, for which she practices the craft to be able to release him. One day Caroline, Bertrand's fiancée, found out about Eva. Shocked, she escapes in her car and falls into a ravine to her death. Eva, in order to punish Bertrand, makes him meet with his Georges who beats him savagely sending him to the hospital. Some time later, the young man notices Eva sitting in a Parisian bar, with her husband and other people. The woman gestures to him in denial. A friend asks her who she is, and the answer is "nobody".
text_b: Bertrand, a young student of pharmacy, timid and studious, has a classmate, Guillaume, who studies Sciences Po, and whose rather vulgar ease and so-called donjuanism he admires. The two friends approach Suzanne in a cafe. She's a young, independent, dark-skinned woman who's studying to be an interpreter while working to pay for her education. She quickly succumbs to William's cavalier advances, who quickly treats her with rudeness and eventually avoids her completely, while taking care to humiliate her every time he meets her. Perhaps in order to reconnect with Guillaume, she goes to Bertrand, who is unwilling to date this girl who seems uninteresting to him. He, on the other hand, covets Sophie, a beautiful young woman who proves to be unattainable. Suzanne invites him to a student party. Hearing this, Guillaume decides that Suzanne will support them, and for a while, she pays for their outings, until she is completely broke. Some of the money that Bertrand hid in the pages of an uncut book in his room is stolen. He blames Suzanne, who spent a night in his apartment she no longer had money to take a taxi, but Sophie rather blames Guillaume, just as suspicious, and who we saw snooping in the books... Bertrand, who persists in seeing Guillaume as a friend and Suzanne as a poor girl, is astonished to learn, at the end of the year, that Suzanne will marry a handsome boy, whose charms, he thought, had kept him away from Sophie. Then he admits that maybe Suzanne wasn't such an insignificant person.

text_a: Bertrand, a medical student, is seduced by his friend Guillaume. So when he, on the pretext of a séance, takes advantage of Suzanne, a young working student he meets in a Parisian bistro, and gets rid of her a few days later, he does not disapprove of his conduct. Afterwards, it is the woman who is the object of his indifference and contempt, even when, through repeated invitations and attempts, he shows his interest in Bertrand. He does not even hesitate to address her suspicions about the disappearance of a sum of money from the room of the boarding house where he is staying and where the young woman spent a night with him, once again ignored. In fact, Guillaume was the perpetrator of the robbery. Bertrand is attracted to Sophie, a woman of fierce beauty, who proves inaccessible to his approaches. It's the comparison with Sophie's idealized image that makes the other mediocre and vulgar. Only when, some time later, he finds her happily married to a former friend of Sophie's, is he forced to reconsider his evaluations of young Suzanne.

anchor_text: Charles Swan III is a successful graphic designer whose fame and fortune have made him irresistible in the eyes of women. Because of this success with women, his fiancée Ivana leaves him and he, obsessed with the woman he loves and has lost, ends up with his car in a swimming pool. Waking up in the hospital, the man begins to come to terms with his life and enters a state of deep doubt that drives him to elaborate delusional fantasies that often involve some of his past relationships. Charles will soon realize that he let the only woman he ever cared about get away.
text_a: Charles Castle is a war photographer who continues to be haunted by the loss of his wife, killed in the line of duty. After the war, he analyses his photographs, still impressed by an image shown to him by a woman who involuntarily portrayed a fairy. Determined to understand us better, his search leads him to Burkinwell, a quiet community that seems to be hiding secrets...
text_b: In the 1970s, successful graphic designer and womanizer Charles Swan III (Sheen) is dumped by his girlfriend Ivana (Winnick), which brings his life crashing down. He doesn't know if he loves her, hates her, wants her back or never wants to see her again. Along with his best friend, Kirby (Schwartzman) and his manager, Saul (Murray), Charles begins to suffer from nightmares, fever, dreams of past relationships and hit rock bottom as he tries to recover from the recent breakup.

anchor_text: Catalina Cruz is the intrepid captain of a cheerleading squad whose world is turned upside down when her family moves from the bustling streets of East Los Angeles to Malibu, a cozy city of sun and beach. At her new school, Lina confronts Avery, the ultra-competitive captain of the Jaguars, while falling in love with the handsome Evan, Avery's brother. Lina has always risen to the challenge, but will she be able to form a new elite team and surpass Avery in the championship while living out her love affair with Evan?
text_a: Lina Cruz is a tough Latina girl who leads a talented cheerleading squad, with dreams of winning the All-Stars Spirit Championship. However, her world is turned upside down when her mother remarries and, due to this decision, Lina moves from the urban streets of East Los Angeles to the luxurious neighborhood of Malibu. There she meets her new half-sister, Sky, who is very insecure and shy, but has a good heart and from the beginning tries to win Lina's friendship, but she doesn't pay much attention to her. The next day, at her new school, Lina has a clash with Avery, the competitive captain of the Jaguars, winners of the previous Spirit championship, in addition to falling in love with Evan, Avery's brother. With the help of Sky and her friends, Gloria and Tryvonetta, Lina decides to create a new cheerleading team and prove who is the best.
text_b: Destiny is the captain of the cheerleading squad called the Rebels, which is top-notch. The three-time national champions are challenged by the new team, The Truth, to a global competition. The Rebels take up the challenge and compete in an online challenge hosted by the Cheer Goddess against cheerleading teams from around the world. However, the other teams all seem to be plotting against Destiny and her teammates, but they're not giving up. To have a chance at the trophy, the captain must put aside her selfish behavior for the good of the team and find out who her true friends are. Integrating Blake and his street dancers into her team initially proves difficult.

anchor_text: Lina is upset when her mother remarries a wealthy man with a daughter named Sky and is forced to move to Malibu. Sky and Lina's relationship is strained at first, but they manage to become great friends and with persistence Sky's cheerleading team, The Sea Lions, manages to convince Lina to become the captain. So, Lina with the help of her friend Gloria manages to get a great team by coaching her. Meanwhile, Lina is courted by Evan, Avery's brother, a snobby All-Star girl who doesn't want to accept this reality. In fact, Avery had a grudge against Lina all along. In the end, the Sea Lions manage to win the All-Star championship by going into the final against Avery's team.

anchor_text: During the Napoleonic Wars, Valentine Brown, a young doctor from an English village, volunteers for service. Phoebe, who was expecting a marriage proposal from him, is on the verge of despair. When Valentine returns ten years later, he finds her changed and doesn't invite her to the dance. To punish him, she disguises herself as the young Livvy, her own niece. And it's to her that Valentine confesses his lifelong love for Phoebe, who he thinks is his aunt.
text_a: England, the year 1805. Phoebe Throssel, who lives with her sister Susan on Quality Street, has met the handsome Dr. Valentine Brown and awaits his visit home, believing he will propose to her. However, Brown only wants to tell you that he has enlisted in the army and has to leave for the front. Ten years later, made captain, he returns unexpectedly and finds Phoebe much changed: for the purpose of rejuvenation, he poses as a non-existent character: Olivia, his own niece. Brown succumbs to the charms of young Olivia.
text_b: Phoebe is a restless teenager scarred by the death of Faith, her adorable older sister who died while traveling across Europe. Determined to uncover the truth behind Faith's death, Phoebe embarks on a journey across the Old Continent, following in the footsteps of her sister and her boyfriend Wolf. Reliving the European political situation of the 1960s, Phoebe over time discovers several clues that allow her to decipher her sister's life.

anchor_text: In the 1800s, a young girl named Phoebe Throssel thinks that Dr. Brown who kissed her one night is going to ask her to marry him, but he's going to war. Ten years have passed, Phoebe and her sister have opened a school and when Dr. Brown returns, he has unfortunate words about the tired and "aged" look of the young woman. This one, pricked in the head, decides to take on the role of Livvy, a young niece of Phoebe who turns the heads of all the young men and takes revenge on Dr. Brown.
