anchor_text: The film is inspired by the Haditha massacre, which occurred three months after the Battle of Haditha in the Iraq War. On November 19, 2005, in Haditha, a city in the western Iraqi province of al-Anbar, 24 unarmed Iraqi men, women and children were killed by a group of U.S. Marines following an incident in which an IED killed one Marine and seriously injured two others. Since the film's release, the U.S. military has controversially dropped all charges against all the Marines involved. The Marines' names were changed in the movie, while the Iraqi civilians kept their real names.
text_a: This is the first time that the Commission has put forward a proposal for a directive on the protection of workers from the risks related to exposure to carcinogens at work. Iraqi insurgents bomb a convoy of U.S. Marines and kill the officer most beloved by his comrades. Enraged by this loss, his young companions carry out a brutal retaliation. Their violent house searches result in the killing of 24 people, many of whom are women and children, tragic victims of a war they are unable to control. Marines are also victims: they are attacked, wounded and forced to respond in the way they have been taught.
text_b: The president of the United States has just ordered the dismantling of a nuclear weapon system. During the Christmas season, a group of terrorists led by a powerful android stormed the nuclear base, intending to launch a missile at Washington. Three people present at the scene now hold the fate of thousands of human lives in their hands. It's an employee of the factory, his son and a repair technician. To avoid the drama, they try to stop the operation from the inside but the terrorists are armed to the teeth and determined not to let anyone stand in their way...

anchor_text: The artist Oliver, after the death of his mother, finds himself faced with an unexpected event. His 75-year-old father confesses to him that he's gay. Initially surprised, Oliver is unable to understand the reasons for this coming out so late and finds himself observing the radical change of the man, who begins to frequent clubs, organize parties and manages to get engaged to Andy, a gay guy much younger than him. Oliver, from his father's choice, understands the redemption of many years of deprivation and, accompanying the last period of life of the parent, sick for a long time with cancer, manages to give a new value to his own existence, trying to commit for the first time in a serious relationship with the actress Anna and living courageously and ironically life, as his father taught him in his last years.
text_a: The film tells the story of Victoria Thompson, who lived in the 19th century in New Zealand. Victoria, an orphan, marries wealthy Oliver Thompson at the age of 18. The husband later turns out to be despotic and stingy. Victoria then falls in love with George, Oliver's brother. She wants to make a fresh start with him, but the plans fail. Victoria poisons Oliver, who dies very slowly and cruelly. However, she is not convicted of the crime in court and is acquitted. But she doesn't have the courage to start a new life with George.
text_b: The film is structured as a series of interconnected flashbacks. After the death of his father Hal, Oliver reflects on his relationship with him and the death of his mother, Georgia. Shortly after her death, Hal had made his homosexuality public to his son (Oliver) and began to explore this aspect of his life. Hal finds a boyfriend, Andy, and surrounds himself with a circle of gay friends. Meanwhile, Hal is diagnosed with terminal cancer. After a long illness, in which Oliver helps care for him, Hal dies. Several months after Hal's death, at a party, Oliver meets Anna, a French actress, with whom he begins a relationship. Oliver's unresolved feelings surrounding his father's death and their parents' life together, along with Anna's mixed feelings about her emotionally unstable father, initially interfere with their relationship, but, nevertheless, they are determined to stay together.

anchor_text: During a dinner with his wife, Cal learns that she wants a divorce, and a little later that she has cheated on him. At worst, he drowns his grief in the bottom of a glass in a bar. He realizes that in all these 25 years of marriage, he's forgotten the game of seduction. So Jacob, a successful seducer, offers to become his personal coach. But in this family, there's more to it than that. There is also the story of Robbie, who is very much in love with his babysitter, who tries to break her down by any means, but without success, however, because she is actually in love with Cal, Robbie's father. Then there's Hannah waiting for her boyfriend to propose, even though her best friend insists he doesn't deserve her. The stories of all these characters turn out to be intimately intertwined.
text_a: Cal Weaver is a man who has everything in life: a beautiful home, a good job, a lovely wife and children. However, his world crumbles miserably the moment his wife Emily suddenly asks him for a divorce. Disgusted and moved into his old apartment, Cal starts going to a nightclub every night, until he attracts the attention of a young man named Jacob Palmer, a charming womanizer who offers to teach Cal the art of seduction. With Jacob's help, Cal is able to form a new self-image and begin interacting with the opposite sex again, successfully seducing many women, including Kate, Robbie's son's teacher. Meanwhile, Cal finally decides to declare his love for Jessica, his babysitter, who, in turn, is madly (but secretly) in love with Cal. Meanwhile, Jacob is forced to question his playboy lifestyle when he falls in love with Hannah. The stories of each of the protagonists will eventually intertwine in unexpected ways and lead the characters to reflect on themselves and their personal relationships.
text_b: After being bullied, teenager Cal Chetley joins the school wrestling team in Oklahoma, also to reunite with his estranged and rebellious brother Mike. His mother, Sharon, is initially not supportive of Cal's decision, especially when she finds out that Mike is coaching him and after she receives a call from the prison where Mike is being held, arrested for a fight. Cal, like his father and brother, will also become a wrestling legend, and he will be able to reunite his brother Mike and his mother, 10 years after his father's death.

anchor_text: Jakob Zuckermann, aka Jaeckie Zucker, a former sports journalist in the GDR, is saddled with gambling debts and is counting on a pool competition to get his life back on track. He finally learns that his mother has just died and is counting on her inheritance. But there was a stipulation in the will: in order to receive the inheritance, Zucker must reconcile with his brother during the seven-day mourning period. So Zucker is joined by his very devout family. He himself has abandoned the Jewish religion but tries to make his family believe he has not. He also faked a heart attack to go to the pool tournament.
text_a: Commissioner Bellamy goes on holiday to Nîmes, to his wife's family home: he wants to give himself a month of tranquility but the arrival of his half-brother Jacques, an alcoholic, adventurous and coward, complicates his life. Not only that, but a man often sneaks into the backyard of the house and creates unrest. Bellamy loves food and good wine and wants to enjoy a time all to himself, but he will nevertheless listen to the motives that drive the stranger to hang around his house.
text_b: Jakob Zuckermann, aka Jaeckie Zucker, is Jewish, but claims to have nothing to do with "that club", since, when he was young, his mother and younger brother left him behind the Iron Curtain in East Germany. When he learns of his mother's death, he cares nothing, but his younger brother visits him with his family, because according to Jewish tradition they must observe the seven-day mourning period of Shiva and the mother's will requires them to reconcile in the presence of the rabbi and the family. If they fail, your assets will be returned to the Jewish community in Berlin and not to them.

anchor_text: Alex, thirty years old and unemployed, gives computer lessons to Pierre, eighty years old, who then decides to register on a dating site, but with the photo of his young teacher and becoming fifty years younger. Peter, struggling to come to terms with the death of his wife two years ago, is unaware that Alex is his granddaughter's companion. Pierre will seduce a young physiotherapist from Brussels, and will force Alex to play his role for the first date. But it's the fling between Alex and the young woman...
text_a: An 83-year-old widower, class of 1934, named Pierre, lives alone, very bored and sad. Sylvie, the daughter, gives him a laptop connected to the Internet. Thirty-year-old Alex a fledgling writer with dreams of becoming a TV screenwriter is her computer science teacher, unaware that he is her niece Juliette's new boyfriend. Soon, Pierre learns to sail, spurred on by curiosity. Eager to meet new people, he chooses an online dating site, matching his nickname (Pierrot98) with a photo of Alex. Shortly afterwards, Pierre becomes infatuated with the user profile of Flora63, a young woman interested in meeting him in person, so similar to his late wife Madeleine.
text_b: The Frenchman Pierre Roubier, after being tried in France and acquitted of the murder of his wife, became a taxi driver in Lisbon. This is how he meets the attractive Katleen Dinver, widow of a lord, and falls madly in love with her. But Pierre soon discovers that Katleen is being spied on, suspected of murdering her husband. About to leave together to start their lives elsewhere, the truth will separate the lovers forever...

anchor_text: In the 1970s, Miklo, who was of mixed American and Mexican descent, lived in Las Vegas with his American father. When Miklo hits his father, he immediately returns to his home neighborhood in East Los Angeles. There he is reunited with his mother's family, mainly Paco and Cruz, his cousins. Despite his white skin, Miklo remains a Chicano, but when before the end of his probation he wants his tattoo Vatos Locos and that for this he must prove himself, he does not hesitate to attack a rival gang, which will generate a war and the separation of the three Chicano.
text_a: Sixteen-year-old Link and his younger half-brother Travis, who grew up with their violent father Arvin in a trailer park in Nova Scotia, set off in search of their mother, a Mi'kmaw they thought was dead. On their journey, they encounter 18-year-old Pasmay and Smokey, who, along with other natives, help them in their search. In the process, Link also rediscovers his Mi'kmaw heritage and that he is a Two-Spirit himself.
text_b: Miklo is a young man, almost eighteen years old, the son of an acid building contractor and a Mexican woman. After a bad experience working in construction with his father and some legal trouble, he decides to go back to East Los Angeles. Between pranks and pranks with the Los Vatos Locos, a neighborhood gang of friends and family, Miklo ends up in trouble again. The doors of San Quentin Prison will open for him. The story will also be divided on personal events related to Miklo's family, between betrayals, reconsiderations and reconciliations.

anchor_text: Successful black architect Flipper Purify begins an affair with Italian Angie. Purify's wife leaves him and he moves in with his father. Angie gets beaten up by her father. The couple decides to move in together. In the end, however, the relationship breaks down because of the conflicts that arise.
text_a: Flipper Purify is a black architect from Harlem. Besides having a perfectly successful career, he's happily married to Drew. However, he begins an extra-marital relationship with Angela, his temporary secretary, a white Italian-American. Then everything is called into question. Drew kicks him out of the house and his affair causes a scandal, fueled by racial prejudice. His love for Angela overwhelms him, and Flipper returns to his wife.
text_b: The film tells the story of the social network created around the Zurich-based homophile Der Kreis magazine which was published from 1943 to 1967 and distributed internationally. The film focuses particularly on the story of Ernst Ostertag and Röbi Rapp in the 1950s and 1960s (with documentary interviews of real people).

anchor_text: Flipper Purify is a black architect from Harlem. Besides having a perfectly successful career, he's happily married to Drew. However, he begins an extra-marital relationship with Angela, his temporary secretary, a white Italian-American. Then everything is called into question. Drew kicks him out of the house and his affair causes a scandal, fueled by racial prejudice. His love for Angela overwhelms him, and Flipper returns to his wife.
text_a: Zurich, late 1950s: The young, shy teacher Ernst Ostertag becomes a member of the Swiss gay organization Der Kreis. There he meets the travestie star Röbi Rapp and falls in love with him. Röbi and Ernst witness the heyday and destruction of the organization, which is regarded throughout Europe as a pioneer of gay emancipation. Ernst has to choose between his bourgeois existence as a teacher at a girls' school and his commitment to homosexuality. For Röbi, it's about the first serious love relationship, the love relationship that will last a lifetime.
text_b: The story focuses on the interracial romance between a successful African-American married architect, Flipper Purify (Wesley Snipes), and an Italian-American woman, Angie Tucci (Annabella Sciorra). The two meet at a New York architectural firm where Flipper works and Angie is temporarily hired as a secretary. As a result of their relationship, both suffer intense pressure from their friends and family. The film's title refers to the sexual attraction between Caucasians and African-Americans. A subplot follows the troubles of Gator (Samuel L. Jackson), the brother of Flipper, a crack addict.

anchor_text: A group of explorers led by German Otto Gerran arrive on the Arctic Bear Island south of Spitsbergen. However, the main actors of the film, most notably the protagonist Frank Lansing, almost all participate in the expedition only superficially because of their research activities. Rather, the island is the focus of interest because of its role in World War II. According to the film plot, it served as a base for the German submarines, which was destroyed in the last days of the war by an allied bombing attack. Lansing tries to clarify the fate of his father, who was stationed on Bear Island as a submarine commander, and stumbles upon a treasure trove of gold that is still stored in the bunker. It is this treasure trove of gold that triggers a flood of sabotage and attacks between parties that are at first vague, but later become more and more obvious. A group of Nazis operating around the world and their mysterious leader, code-named "Zelda", the Norwegian secret service, a greedy American, a self-righteous German and countless others compete for the treasure. Lansing and Dr. Linquist find out with the help of the supposedly dead Larsen that Jungbeck and Heyter are Nazis and Paul Hartmann is "Zelda". Hartmann shoots Gerran and seriously injures him because he exposed him. On the expedition's research boat, a final battle takes place between Lansing and Hartmann, during which Lansing shoots Hartmann in the back with a flare rocket, causing him to fall overboard.
text_a: The Bear Island, in northern Norway, there's a hidden Nazi treasure, the Norwegian gold that they confiscated during World War II and that's hidden in a German U-boat base that was destroyed in an aerial bombardment just before the end of the war. That island is subsequently declared a no-go zone by NATO, so it cannot be stepped on until a scientific expedition organized by Ott Gerran makes it possible. In it, a German-born American biologist named Frank Lansing wants to find his dead father, who was the commander of a local submarine, to find out who he was, since he died in that place in that bombing before he could meet him. However others want to use the expedition to find the gold, among them a group of Nazis, who want to use the gold to blow up the Western economy and who are willing to go over corpses for it and to also cover their tracks while the Norwegian secret service is aware of what is happening and have sent an agent, Heddi Linquist, against them who discovers that the previous agent who discovered that was murdered. Lansing discovers his presence after he and a colleague of his, Judith were the victims of an assassination attempt, in which she died, when he wanted to look for his father in that submarine base. She informs Lansing about what's going on in exchange for him keeping his cover. He agrees and, realizing that they have a common enemy, the two come together to finish them off while also having to face others. A life-and-death struggle on the island begins.
text_b: News of the discovery of gold in Alaska sends the nation into an uproar, sparking the Klondike Gold Rush. Many men leave their homes and move north to try their luck. Samuel Foote's moving out, leaving his family behind. Sneaky Lars Petersen, a lumberjack in Michigan, is leaving his wife. The preacher Salvation Jim is coming from the Nevada desert to Alaska. The Bulkeys, on the road with their relative Berna and their blind grandfather Henry Kelland, want to open a restaurant on the Klondike. On the way from Dawson to the gold fields, the grandfather dies. At the finish, everyone is horrified by the harsh conditions and ruthless people, especially the unscrupulous saloon owner Jack Locasto. While Berna falls in love with adventurer Larry, the Bulkeys pack up their belongings and plan to leave Alaska. Berna can convince Larry to go back with her, but first he wants to look for a promising position. Lars, Larry, Jim and Samuel actually find and discover a gold vein. Larry and Samuel guard the place while Lars and Jim file the claim. The freezing cold makes it hard for the men to work. Samuel gets greedy and wants to get rid of Larry, but is attacked and killed by a pack of hungry wolves. Larry's making it into town with all his might. Affected by the fact that Berna has to work as a saloon girl, there is a fight with Locasto and his men. Larry throws a kerosene lamp at Locasto and sets him on fire. Locasto dies in the fire, the saloon burns down. Larry is rescued from the flames by Berna. Larry, Berna, Lars and Jim decide to return to their normal lives.

anchor_text: Silvia is a pretty and charming worker in a factory specializing in underwear. She's in love with José Luis, the son of the company owner. When the latter's mother discovers that the two want to get married, she decides to intervene and create a plan to destroy the couple. He hires a model, Raul, to seduce the main character. The matter is complicated when the young bud shows genuine feelings for Silvia.
text_a: José Luis (Jordi Mollà) is the son of a wealthy couple who own a men's underwear factory where Silvia (Penélope Cruz), his girlfriend, works. When she becomes pregnant, José Luis promises to marry her, contrary to her parents' decision. José Luis's mother (Stefania Sandrelli), not considering Silvia good enough to marry her son, decides to hire a young aspiring bullfighter, Raúl (Javier Bardem), to seduce the young woman. Everything will start to get complicated when Raúl falls in love with Silvia and she discovers that everything was planned by her boyfriend's mother.
text_b: The young Gloria accidentally meets the rich Francisco Galván at the mass. He falls in love with her and convinces her to marry him. However, she soon discovers, on their honeymoon, that he is afflicted with pathological jealousy and paranoia. So he sticks a needle in the lock in case the roommate spies on them. But it's only the beginning, and Gloria's life is about to become an ordeal.

anchor_text: Ten-year-old Devon, who suffers from a heart condition, grows up in a typical American suburb, where everything is shiny and flawless at first glance. The front gardens are freshly mowed by Trent, a young man from a poor background. Devon and Trent are becoming friends. Trent hides his friendship with the girl from the residents of the settlement because he fears further discrimination. Devon, too, is aware of the suspicion and distrust of the suburban population. The last time we went on a trip together, Trent hit on a dog. This one gets hurt so bad that Trent beats him to death. Disappointed by this brutality, Devon tells her parents about the now ended friendship with Trent. Devon's father and other residents of the settlement search for Trent and beat him up. Devon, fearing for Trent's life, shoots one of the attackers. Trent fears he'll be accused of the crime and flees. Devon tells how a fairy-tale hero protects himself from the pursuit of a witch. This will be backed up with corresponding images of Trent's escape.
text_a: The protagonist, Laura, narrates how her life goes from placid and quiet to bewildering and frightening when Carmilla, a beautiful and enigmatic young woman, appears. Laura, the protagonist of the story, is a young woman who lives with her father and servants in an old castle in the outskirts of Styria, Austria. Often, she remembers when she was a little girl and she was asleep in the arms of a young woman and woke up to the feeling of two needles in her chest. Their lives take a turn when, in front of the castle where they live, a lady's carriage suffers an accident, and her daughter, who was also traveling in it, is knocked unconscious. As the lady could not waste time and had to continue her journey, the young lady is received at Hollis Castle until her mother returns to fetch her. Laura and the young woman, Carmilla, strike up a friendship, despite the fact that the new tenant shows oddities in her behavior: she wakes up after noon and locks herself in her room without giving any sign of being in it.
text_b: The story of a friendship between a man living on the fringes of society and a 10-year-old girl from a wealthy family. This friendship will be misunderstood by the residents of the neighborhood. It's the story of the relationship between Devon, a ten-year-old girl who's lively and too smart for her age, and Trent, a lonely young proletarian who mows the grass for the wealthy middle-class. A bond of trust and friendship develops between this delusional little red-hat and his gentle wild wolf, which is viewed very negatively by the inhabitants of the hamlet. Built around a tale by the presence of a tale narrated by Devon, there is a princess, a wolf, a turtle, a castle (the village is called Camelot) and enchanted forests. Yet the reality is very clear, the princess lives in the midst of big bourgeois, cloistered in their pastel bunkers, who humiliate the poor guys who work for them, while turning their children into obedient clones. Little Devon, touched by death and mortified by the boredom of this too-sweet world, and a five-year-old boy who works for petty thieves. Their innocent actions will have dark repercussions.

anchor_text: Kirsty Cotton is now married to her husband, Trevor. However, he planned to kill his wife, whom he had cheated on with three other women, along with an accomplice in order to get Kirsty's inheritance. When Kirsty and her husband are on the road together, an accident occurs and their car plunges into a lake. Your husband can save himself, but Kirsty's missing. Traumatized and plagued by nightmares, her husband tries to find his way back to life, only to be suspected by the police of Kirsty's murder. Trying to prove his innocence, he stumbles upon a conspiracy. Because Kirsty's not dead. Instead, she's made another alliance with the Pinhead to save her own life. Because after Trevor gave her a mysterious cube, the Zenobites were back on Kirsty's trail. To save herself, she betrayed her husband, his accomplice and the lovers to the Pinhead in exchange for her own life. Trevor's experiences come out at the end of the film as part of his torture by the Pinhead. The car accident that made Kirsty disappear was actually caused by Trevor getting shot in the head by his wife. When the police fish the car out of the lake, they find the gun that also shot the other people, and Kirsty can make the police believe that her husband, after confessing to her the acts, committed suicide while driving. Finally, a policeman returns Kirsty's wedding anniversary gift, which was also in the car: the cube.
text_a: Lynn stabs her father with a knife when he tries to rape her. The loss is driving her crazy. She refuses to accept her father's death and ends up in an insane asylum, where she is treated with shock therapy. Soon she escapes and drives off into the wilderness in a stolen car. There she finds the café of old Zambrini, a hermit. She'll get a room and a job there. Zambrini seems nice, but he's keeping a secret. Soon Lynn discovers that Zambrini feeds his guests as well as corpses he retrieves from the cemetery to his pigs. When Lynn has an intrusive admirer on her mind, Zambrini helps her eliminate him. In the end, Lynn goes completely insane, kills Zambrini and supposedly feeds himself to the pigs. At the end, you see her driving her car down the country road. She's taking a hitchhiker with her.
text_b: Kirsty Cotton got married. While on the road with her husband, an accident occurs and the car falls into a lake; the husband is saved, but Kirsty is missing. Traumatized and haunted by nightmares, he tries to remember his past, but is investigated for murder, accused of killing his wife. As he tries to prove his innocence, he finds himself on a psychological journey into the depths of his nightmares. It turns out that Trevor was actually a petty man and after he had intentionally given Kirsty the strange box, the Cenobites had returned with bad intentions towards Kirsty who had escaped them more than once. Kirsty's relationship with Trevor is unstable, and her husband cheats on her more than once with different women. Kirsty discovers this and once she receives the box from her husband, she uses it to her advantage, making a deal with Pinhead one last time: the salvation of her soul against the sacrifice of five others, the three women Trevor cheated on her with, a friend of his hired to kill her and inherit her estate, and finally Trevor himself.

text_a: The story begins when Trevor and Kirsty Cotton (protagonist of the first two films of the series), recently married, are walking down the road talking affectionately. Suddenly, Trevor makes a maneuver to avoid colliding with a pickup truck and the car falls into a lake. Trevor tries to save Kirsty but fails, from this moment the police begin to consider him a suspect in her murder. Trevor tries to move on with his life, but women he meets constantly insinuate themselves to him and he begins to suffer from terrible headaches. Throughout the film, every time Trevor starts having sex with a woman he has a vision of the death of the woman in question and then wakes up on a bus on his way home from work. Trevor also sees a recording of him handing Kirsty the Lemarchand Box and she becomes disturbed. Eventually, the detective investigating Kirsty's disappearance arrests Trevor and takes him to an underground police station. Trevor walks through it until he reaches the morgue, where the cenobit Pinhead explains to him what has happened. After Kirsty received Lemarchand's box she opened it releasing Pinhead and made a deal with him: five souls instead of her own. So, Kirsty used Trevor's revolver to kill his accomplice, and his three mistresses, and then while they were arguing in the car she shot him in the head. Kirsty is cleared of suspicion as she states that Trevor committed suicide while driving with her on board and suspicion for the murders remains on Trevor. All that makes up the movie is the explanation of the hell that Pinhead prepared for Trevor.

anchor_text: In New York, the French businessman, Mr. Devereaux, is a pervert and womanizer who parties and participates in group sex with women. When he tries to rape an employee in his hotel room, the woman reports him to the police. Devereaux is arrested, which also affects the life of his wife Simone.
text_a: Devereaux is a powerful French businessman who heads a major international financial institution in New York. His wife, Simone, encourages him to run for the next French presidential election. Devereaux is, however, also a man with an unbridled sexual appetite that leads him to partake in numerous feasts in luxurious hotel suites. The day after one of these feasts, he assaults a cleaning woman before leaving the hotel to fly back to Paris. He is then arrested at the New York airport.
text_b: Inspired by a true story that shocked Louis-Philippe's France, this film tells the story of a housekeeper in love with her employer who is suspected in the murder of her boss's wife.

anchor_text: In Texas, Mike Norton, a U.S. Border Patrol officer, accidentally kills Melquiades Estrada, an illegal Mexican immigrant who works as a cowboy. The superiors decide to cover it up and give the impression that Melquiades fired first and forced the agent to defend himself. The man's friend, Pete Perkins (Tommy Lee Jones), learns the truth, kidnaps the policeman and forces him to accompany him to Melquiades' home village to bury his corpse there.
text_a: Eddie Macon was wrongfully convicted of a crime. During a prison rodeo in Huntsville, Texas, he escapes and heads for the border, hoping to join his family in Mexico. Carl "Buster" Marzack, a police officer, goes after Eddie, determined to put him back in jail. Eddie, after a long walk, ends up in the woods, where he almost gets killed. He meets Jilly Buck, a bored rich girl who agrees to help him because it's "just a boring Wednesday".
text_b: Mexican Melquiades Estrada (Julio Cedillo) is shot dead at the U.S. border by Border Patrol agent Mike Norton (Barry Pepper) and is buried, first in the West Texas desert and then in a local cemetery. His best friend Pete (Tommy Lee Jones) kidnaps Mike and forces him to dig up the body, embarking on a quixotic and dangerous horseback ride to bury - for the third time - his best friend in a place Melquiades has told him about on several occasions.

anchor_text: In modern-day Texas, near the Mexican border, a young border guard accidentally kills Melquiades Estrada, an illegal immigrant cowboy. After a quick investigation, his friend, Pete Perkins, decides to take his body back to his village in Mexico, forcing his murderer to accompany him.
text_a: Eddie Macon was wrongfully convicted of a crime. During a prison rodeo in Huntsville, Texas, he escapes and heads for the border, hoping to join his family in Mexico. The man's friend, Pete Perkins (Tommy Lee Jones), learns the truth, kidnaps the policeman and forces him to accompany him to Melquiades' home village to bury his corpse there.

anchor_text: Young Fahim and his father leave their native Bangladesh, leaving the rest of the family behind. On French soil, their request for political asylum is not granted, and they find themselves in an irregular situation. Fahim, already a young chess champion in his country, pushes the doors of the Créteil club, and meets Sylvain, a chess coach (a character inspired by FIDE master Xavier Parmentier). Overcoming a difficult first contact, and now accompanied by Sylvain, Fahim qualifies for the French youth championship. Fahim doesn't know it yet but this competition will allow his father to avoid expulsion and regularize their administrative situation.
text_a: Forced to flee his native Bangladesh, young Fahim (Assad Ahmed) and his father leave the rest of the family behind and go to Paris. Upon their arrival in France, they begin a real obstacle course for political asylum. Thanks to his talent at chess, Fahim meets Sylvain (Gérard Depardieu), one of the best coaches in France. When the French league starts, the threat of deportation puts pressure on Fahim and his father.[1]
text_b: In the ancient Yoruba land, a priest introduces the cult of Araromire, the goddess of good luck and fortune who grants seven years of wealth. Two young friends find a statuette on an abandoned altar while doing military service at a camp near the town of Araromire. They stop selling drugs in Lagos to become two successful businessmen with the normal problems of life: friendship, love, family. But what happens next?

text_a: Fahim and his father Nura Mohammad, two refugees from Bangladesh, embark on a long journey to Paris, where they find refuge and seek political asylum from the French authorities. The initial response from the French government is not positive and they start a life as illegal immigrants with the constant threat of being expelled from the country. But there is one hope: Fahim's chess prowess. His talent is noticed by chess coach Sylvain, who will make him reach and win the national championship, the ideal step to obtain political asylum together with the whole family.
text_b: In the ancient Yoruba land, a priest introduces the cult of Araromire, the goddess of good luck and fortune who grants seven years of wealth. Two young friends find a statuette on an abandoned altar while doing military service at a camp near the town of Araromire. They stop selling drugs in Lagos to become two successful businessmen with the normal problems of life: friendship, love, family. But what happens next?

anchor_text: After becoming heir to a multinational corporation, Winch decides to sell the company and donate the proceeds to charity. However, he finds himself entangled in an old dirty story from his father's time, concerning a massacre of civilians in Burma; he will have to fight to avoid being indicted by the UN prosecutor Diane Francken (Sharon Stone) and prevent the company from ending up in the hands of a shady Georgian arms dealer.
text_a: Largo is a young orphan who is adopted by Nerio Winch, a Bosnian-born billionaire, to be his heir. Years later, Nerio, founder and main shareholder of the Group, is found drowned. Largo escapes from a prison in the Amazon and sets out to find the killer to avenge his adoptive father and defend his property.
text_b: Becoming the head of a major company after the death of his adoptive father, Largo Winch (Tomer Sisley), he decides, to everyone's surprise, to sell it and use the profits to create an ambitious humanitarian foundation. But on that very day, he will be accused (by a mysterious witness) of crimes against humanity. To prove his innocence, Largo will have to retrace the steps of his past life, in the heart of the Burmese jungle.[1]

But on the day of the signing, he finds himself accused of crimes against humanity by a mysterious witness. To prove his innocence, Largo must retrace the steps of his past life deep in the Burmese jungle.

anchor_text: In 2009, Iranian-Canadian journalist Maziar Bahari was interviewed by Daily Show correspondent Jason Jones. Subsequently, he was imprisoned and tortured in Evin Prison for more than 118 days. The film's title, Rosewater (literally rose water ), refers to the smell of one of Bahari's interrogators. He was usually blindfolded during interrogation sessions, and rose water was the only way to identify his executioner.
text_a: In 2009, a London-based journalist named Maziar Bahari (Gael García Bernal) was detained in Iran for four months because of an interview regarding the country's presidential election. Bahari was arrested, and while his pregnant girlfriend waited for him, he spent four months in prison, during which he was brutally interrogated (always blindfolded). The only distinguishing feature of his interrogator was that he smelled of rose water (which is why Bahari nicknamed him Rosewater).[6]
text_b: This is Iran, 2001. In the Shiite pilgrimage city of Mashhad, Saeed Hanaei, an ordinary family man, construction worker and veteran of the war against Iraq, goes out at night to lure prostitutes and kill them, strangling them with their veil, as sinners whose presence defiles the city. Investigating the murders is a journalist from the capital, Rahimi, who clashes with sexist prejudice and an apathetic police force. Rahimi finds himself collaborating with local reporter Sharifi, as news of the crimes of the so-called "Ragno" begins to spread among the population, provoking mixed reactions.

anchor_text: In the 2009 Iranian presidential election, challenger Mir Hossein Mousavi is running against incumbent President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad. Also in the country is 42-year-old journalist Maziar Bahari, who travelled from London to meet and interview Moussavi. When Ahmadinejad declares himself the winner of the election hours before it ends, there are protests and attacks on the streets of Iran. In the course of these riots, Bahari, who is recording the whole thing with his camera, is arrested by the police and detained for 118 days. In prison, he is repeatedly interrogated under brutal torture until his wife, with the support of Western media, succeeds in obtaining his release.
text_a: In Mashhad, Iran's second largest city, 50-year-old war veteran, small businessman and family man Saeed Hanaei murdered a number of street prostitutes in the early 2000s. He sees it as a spiritual mission to cleanse Imam Reza's holy city of immoral women, many of whom are addicted to opium from nearby Afghanistan, by driving them home on his motorcycle and then strangling them with their hijabs. Although the newspapers refer to him as the "spider killer", he is increasingly angry at the lack of public interest in his actions. In 2001, Rahimi, a journalist on assignment from her newspaper in Tehran, enters this world and quickly discovers that the police are in no hurry to solve the case. Courageous and supported only by her colleague Sharifi, she acts as a decoy herself. In fact, she succeeds in luring the murderer into a trap, narrowly escaping him. After murdering a total of 16 women in two years, he is arrested, convicted and executed; however, he is considered a hero to his family, but also to large parts of the population.
text_b: In 2009, a London-based journalist named Maziar Bahari (Gael García Bernal) was detained in Iran for four months because of an interview regarding the country's presidential election. Bahari was arrested, and while his pregnant girlfriend waited for him, he spent four months in prison, during which he was brutally interrogated (always blindfolded). The only distinguishing feature of his interrogator was that he smelled of rose water (which is why Bahari nicknamed him Rosewater).[6]

anchor_text: At the beginning of the 18th century, Jonathan Green leaves England to map the east of Russia. Eventually, however, he must travel to China to meet the Dragon King and take on the Russian Czar in the Iron Mask, who has escaped from the Tower of London.
text_a: Set in the 18th century, the film follows the ongoing exploits of cartographer Jonathan Green as he embarks on a scientific and supernatural journey that takes him from England to China.
text_b: Princess Yasmine is kidnapped because of her country's relations with France. The efficient Chief Inspector Dreyfus is determined to find them. So he sent the gendarme Jacques Gambrelli, the son of Maria Gambrelli and Inspector Clouseau.

anchor_text: At the beginning of the 18th century, Jonathan Green leaves England to map the east of Russia. Eventually, however, he must travel to China to meet the Dragon King and take on the Russian Czar in the Iron Mask, who has escaped from the Tower of London.
text_a: After the events in The Legend of Viy, cartographer Jonathan Green travels to China with young Cheng Lan to confront a witch who holds a village under her spell. At the same time, Tsar Peter the Great, who was held prisoner in the same cell as Chen's father in the Tower of London, managed to escape with the help of Miss Dudley, the wife of Jonathan Green.
text_b: Princess Yasmine is kidnapped because of her country's relations with France. The efficient Chief Inspector Dreyfus is determined to find them. So he sent the gendarme Jacques Gambrelli, the son of Maria Gambrelli and Inspector Clouseau.

anchor_text: Set in the gambling and entertainment city of Las Vegas, the time is now. Dancer Fran is a woman with a deep-seated fear of bonding with a person who might leave her. She found temporary balance in the affair with the businessman Lockwood, who can not leave her, but is not ready to divorce for her. This arrangement is jeopardized when Fran encounters bar pianist Joe Grady, a compulsive player whose naïve emotionality and affection, however, attracts her. She sleeps with him and eventually even lets him move into her apartment, but agrees with him that there should be nothing but sex between them. She doesn't realize how much Joe means to her until Lockwood shows up and surprisingly announces that he's divorced his wife. Fran's letting him go for good. But the happy ending comes when Joe wins a fortune in craps and decides to quit gambling.
text_a: A famous American dancer, Jerry Travers, while staying in a hotel with his manager, meets by chance a charming hostess, Dale Tramont, and falls in love with her. His attentions, however, are not reciprocated by the pretty maiden. After an insistent courtship, Jerry manages to make her fall in love with him, but by mistake the girl begins to believe that he is the husband of a close friend of hers. So, convinced that he is an adventurer, she dismisses him with disdain. Jerry is very disappointed. One day, after a successful performance in a musical, Jerry is persuaded to go to Venice, only to find out that his manager's wife wants to introduce him to a girl, who happens to be Dale. Thus begins a hilarious game of misunderstandings and name-calling, since her friend's real husband is Jerry's businessman. But eventually it'll all come together, and Jerry and Dale will finally find love again.
text_b: In the city of Las Vegas, United States, Fran Walker (Elizabeth Taylor), a retired dancer, meets Joe Grady (Warren Beatty), a bar pianist and compulsive gambler, while she is expecting another man, her lover Thomas Lockwood, a businessman from San Francisco. After five years of waiting, he has to tell her that he's finally divorced. Just as Lockwood keeps his word and is free to marry his mistress, she discovers that she has fallen in love with Joe, who has finally accumulated enough money to realize his dream of moving to New York and starting a new life there. Joe will have to choose between a possible career in Manhattan or marrying Fran.

anchor_text: Johnny Dalton, a bank teller, for trying to help a bookie out of a predicament, finds himself in a similar predicament. Only his best friend Emile can get him out of this dead end.
text_a: In this non-musical comedy, Frank Sinatra, who plays Groucho Marx's sidekick, is a bank teller suspected of embezzlement. The dance numbers were choreographed by Gene Kelly, with his classic inexhaustible energy. As usual, Sinatra always arrives prepared, without ever having been to rehearsal.
text_b: The film is based on the best-selling autobiography by Polly Adler, a legendary madame of the Roaring Twenties. It follows the life of Polly as she transforms from being an immigrant worker to becoming the friend and confidante of underworld heavyweights, social leaders, businessmen, politicians, writers and artists.

anchor_text: Yoshika is a clever, 24-year-old accountant who has never kissed in her life. She works very long hours. She spends her free time on the Internet on Wikipedia and informs herself there mainly about extinct animals. Otherwise, she imagines conversations with real people and dreams of her school crush, whom she calls Ichi (No. 1). She's planning a class reunion in hopes of winning his heart. But suddenly her emotionally charged colleague, whom she calls Ni (No. 2), confesses his love for her.
text_a: Yoshika Etō has reached the age of twenty-four without ever having a boyfriend or even a stable relationship, which has led her to have as a fixed thought the reunion of her high school crush, whom she calls "The One". At the workplace, however, she meets a boy, Kirishima who is immediately nicknamed "Two" who is sincerely interested in her. Yoshika thus finds herself choosing between pursuing her own fantasies or being with a boy who truly loves her.
text_b: Yoshimi Matsubara is in the midst of divorce proceedings, living with her 6-year-old daughter, Ikuko, struggling every day to find a job, get a decent place to live, and retain custody of her child. Sometimes on the verge of a nervous breakdown and struggling with her husband's beatings, Yoshimi finds in Ikuko the strength to cope. She thinks she'll finally see the end of the tunnel when she finds a new job and a new apartment. However, she is soon disillusioned, because strange phenomena will soon manifest themselves in the sinister building: moisture stains on the ceiling, cloudy tap water... and a mysterious presence, like a shadow, like a little girl... with an unfathomable face.

anchor_text: In Oklahoma, in the month of August in Osage County. Beverly Weston is an alcoholic poet, while his wife, Violet, suffers from oral cancer and a drug addiction. Beverly hires a Native American cook and nurse for Violet. Shortly after the hiring of this domestic helper, Beverly disappears, causing part of the family to gather to look for him, but his body is found a few days later: he committed suicide. The couple's daughters, Barbara, Karen, and Ivy - the only of the three daughters who live in the area - come with their families to attend their father's funeral. A series of conflicts ensued. Hidden secrets will be revealed, stored grudges will be expressed, and no one will be spared.
text_a: The Weston family is reunited in the wake of the father's disappearance and subsequent suicide. It's August, and the heat makes it tough. At the mother's house in Oklahoma, all the members of the family are reunited after a long time. The mother, a cancer-stricken and bitter woman (Meryl Streep), is addicted to pills. Barbara, the eldest daughter (Julia Roberts), faces problems in her marriage and the difficulties of a teenage daughter. Karen (Juliette Lewis) tries, for the umpteenth time, to have a stable relationship; Ivy (Julianne Nicholson), the only daughter who remained in the house, plans an independent life at the age of forty. The Westons' conflicts, exacerbated by the death of their father and the August heat, set the stage for each of the characters to put their respective truths on the table.
text_b: The story is the almost autobiographical one of the Alcott family, who became March in the book: the four sisters, Margaret, Josephine, Elizabeth and Amy are the protagonists of the story, which revolves around the domestic events that happened in the year when the father is away from home because in the army during the Civil War. In this year, which the girls live as a game, they try to work on their flaws and look for small jobs for daily sustenance, accompanied by other characters such as their mother, loyal maid Hannah and friend Laurie. The happy ending is a must and also the reflection on moralistic arguments, which the writer proposes to her readers seriously but without being boring.

anchor_text: Section 1 The narrator, a merchant, relates the case of a giant mole that appears in a village, is admired by the surrounding people, and its existence is published by a village schoolteacher in a small printed matter. The village schoolteacher, however, is unable to arouse the interest of the scientists. One scholar concludes by suggesting that the black, fatty soil is to blame for the giant growth. The village schoolteacher added this and other bad experiences and hardships to his original writing. Section two Reading this supplement, the narrator is so moved by the fate of the village schoolteacher that, without knowing more about the mole and the first printing, he begins his own investigations. He wants to give the village school teacher the scientific reputation he deserves. Section 3 is amended as follows: But this does not lead to fruitful cooperation. The village schoolteacher does not trust the merchant, misunderstands him, and sometimes has legitimate objections. The merchant, who was originally a philanthropist, becomes entangled in the subject of the mole like his counterpart and now believes only his findings to be correct. Section 4 is amended as follows: He fails just like the village school teacher. He also published a paper and later a disappointed appendix to it. He now wants to withdraw from the case. At the last meeting with the village schoolteacher, to whom he was initially so helpful, he just wants to have this as something very annoying quickly out of his apartment.
text_a: World War II veteran Richard Dadier takes a job teaching English at a high school. In this school, the students are in charge, and the teachers have given up on controlling the students and teaching them the material. But young Mr. Dadier, whose wife is expecting a child, tries, guided by his idealism, to teach the students something. At first, students behave as expected, disrespectfully and aggressively towards the new teacher. Dadier, however, sees in the young African-American Miller a leadership personality who seems to him not only willing to learn, but also able to motivate her classmates. Miller initially refuses, but does not go as far in his resistance as the gang leader West, who makes life as difficult as possible for the new teacher and incites other students against him. This even leads to Dadier being beaten up by the gang and his wife suffering a premature birth after West anonymously claims to her that her husband is having an affair. Despite some setbacks, Dadier keeps reminding himself of his ideals and does not give up trying to make his class a group of decent students and teach them something. Through conversations, Dadier manages to get Miller on his side, so that he assists him in a kind of showdown when West tries to stab Dadier in the classroom. Dadier overwhelms West and the other students turn their backs on West and decide to stop boycotting classes in the future.
text_b: The narrator about the presence of a giant mole in a distant village, and the community school teacher's attempt to bring its existence to public attention, but with the only result of becoming the laughing stock of official scientists. Without knowing the schoolmaster personally, the narrator tries to defend him and his honesty as a researcher in a document concerning the existence of the giant mole; he wants to help the teacher gain his well-deserved scientific reputation. However, the cooperation cannot be very fruitful or successful, since the teacher does not trust the merchant too much, misunderstands him and also raises objections that are largely justified, so much so that the merchant ends up wishing to withdraw from the case. In a Christmas-time conversation between him and the teacher, they discuss each other's motivations and the different outcomes they have had compared to what they both expected. The story ends in the middle of the two men's conversation.

anchor_text: In 1968, the governor of Arkansas nominated Henry Brubaker as the new warden of Wakefield Penitentiary. He's there to push through the new legislative reforms. To that end, Brubaker allows himself to be smuggled into the facility as an inmate. Criminologist Brubaker learns firsthand that corruption, rape and torture are part of prison life. In the fields, prisoners work up to fifteen hours a day until they physically collapse. Henry Brubaker, for example, is stunned to discover that prisoners continue to be held in prison long after they have served their sentences. Brubaker then decides to take up the fight against the abuses - not only in the prison he manages, but in the American penitentiary in general. During a hostage situation, Henry Brubaker reveals himself as the new warden and begins to fight the inhumane conditions in his own way, firing corrupt officials and implementing appropriate reforms. His actions and revelations are increasingly arousing the distrust of the governor and the prison board, who are increasingly criticizing him for his activities. When Brubaker finally exhumes a mass grave of some 200 prisoners who have died in suspicious circumstances over the decades, he is released under a pretext. But he leaves a legacy with the prisoners. They learn from him how to make the penal system more humane and thank him for his work with a public applause. So he can go in peace, no matter what. In the aftermath, it says that a group of inmates sued the prison two years after Brubaker's release and that the court ordered a fundamental reform or, alternatively, the closure of the facility.
text_a: Brubaker, the new warden of a farm-style prison in the southern United States, dresses like a prisoner to get an up-close look at the conditions in which they live. He observes the corruption, the bad state of the place and the existing violence on the part of the guards and officials. He also observes the business they do at the expense of prisoners by subjecting them to slave labor for the benefit of local businesses. When he goes public and starts taking steps to curb corruption and violence and improve the situation there, he meets with resistance from the entire prison guard corps. The issue transcends even the state prison administration, whose officials are also not willing to tolerate Brubaker's actions, as they are also corrupt and have taken advantage of the prisoners, as well as the surrounding companies who have also done the same. Finally, when Brubaker discovers a clandestine cemetery with 200 bodies of prisoners murdered in one of the fields of the farm, which have accumulated there over time, the administration, to cover it up, and in an attempt to return to the corrupt past, expels him under an excuse, relativizes the discovery and abolishes all the reforms he brought with him using an excuse for it. Yet he leaves a legacy. Through him the prisoners learned how to make the jail more humane, which leads to their rebellion two years later, when they denounce the situation before the Supreme Court, which judicially orders in its final verdict regarding the unconditional reform of the jail under the threat of closing it as an alternative for being unconstitutional the functioning of it.
text_b: In 1883, during a robbery in Arizona, the bandit Paris Pitman made off with $500,000. However, as a precaution, he hides the money in a snake pit. When he's arrested in a brothel for a crime he didn't commit by Sheriff Woodward Lopeman, he's the only one who knows the whereabouts of the loot, and he doesn't think twice about revealing his knowledge. He is sentenced to 10 years in prison, to be served in the Arizona State Penitentiary. LeGoff, the corrupt prison warden in the penitentiary, offers Pitman a deal to cut himself a piece of the bait. He's actually planning on having Pitman tower. Pitman agrees to the proposal, but before it gets to that point and Paris can imprison Pitman, LeGoff is murdered by the Chinese convict Ah-Ping. When former Sheriff Lopeman becomes the new warden at the prison, Pitman senses his chance, despite the old enmity. He strives for good leadership and continuously helps Lopeman to improve conditions in prison. One day, the governor himself visits the penitentiary to learn about the new modern measures in place. That's when Pitman saw his chance to escape. In the chaos of a daring escape attempt that kills several fellow inmates, Pitman manages to escape and heads straight for the money stash in the middle of the Arizona desert. Lopeman's closing in on the son of a bitch, though. Upon arriving at the hiding place, Pitman shoots at what appear to be all the snakes in the pit in order to get to the money safely. When he reaches for the money in the bag, he is bitten by the last rattlesnake that has made its home there. Pitman knows he's not gonna spend the money anymore. Lopeman still finds the dead bandit. But instead of returning the money, the former sheriff keeps it and rides off with the loot into the sunset toward Mexico.

text_b: Henry Brubaker is a reformed criminologist and former army captain who is assigned to run the Wakefield Penitentiary in Arkansas, where there are no prison guards and internal order is maintained by so-called "reliable" inmates. In order to realize the real problems of the institution, the man enters the prison pretending, for a few days, to be a prisoner himself and thus discovers the dramatic conditions in which the prisoners find themselves: the physical and psychological violence, the constant abuse, the exploitation to which they are constantly subjected by the merchants of the city and, not infrequently, death. Once his identity is revealed, he begins an action of radical change of life inside the prison, trying to eliminate its injustices and clashing with the corrupt members of the committee for the management of the penitentiary. In his work the new director is supported by Lillian, the woman who wanted him to lead Wakefield, but his support comes to an end at the moment when the clash with the bureaucracy and especially with politics emerges in all its evidence when Brubaker refuses to stop the excavations in a field where the murdered prisoners were buried inside the prison. Dickie, an inmate of the trusted group, is initially skeptical of the new warden, but slowly realizes the value of his work, until he pays him full tribute when he, just removed from office, is about to leave the penitentiary.

anchor_text: Alex is a waiter at Bastards, a fancy restaurant. He's black, he's gay, he's clumsy and he's annoying. When he has an argument with his more popular co-worker Mark in front of the guests, Alex is fired. He joins forces with some other freaks to start a revolution against the system. His goal is to take revenge on his tormentors, the bastard's employees and guests. He and his new friends storm the bastards and kill the employees and guests. These he and his colleagues process into food for the next guests. Bastards is renamed Eat the Rich and seems to continue to run as a fine dining restaurant. The guests consider it to be the last hurrah, as they are insulted and humiliated there and (supposedly) served human flesh. The guests think the whole thing's a fancy marketing gimmick. The subplot tells the story of Nosher Powell, the proletarian interior minister in the election campaign, his spoiled wife and Commander Fortune, a KGB agent who, together with the rock musician Spider, sells weapons to terrorists.
text_a: The film begins at an upscale restaurant called Bastards, where the protagonist, Alex, works as a waitress. Subject to the daily contempt and disgust of upper-class customers, Alex is fired for being obnoxious and rude to the clientele. After witnessing a terrorist act at an embassy, he robs an office and flees. The film also introduces a lame politician who always solves problems with violence. He's the favorite of the voters, the press and the beautiful Fiona. He was also the one who put an end to the terrorist situation that Alex witnessed earlier. However, he has enemies, including the sinister Commander Fortune, General Karprov and Spider, who plan to derail the politician's campaign to become Prime Minister.
text_b: Former CIA agent Alexis Faraday robs a bank with Kevin Fuller and Raymond Kelly. During the robbery, Alex kills the fourth accomplice when he gets out of control in a drug binge. At the hotel, Alex and Fuller are attacked by four mercenaries led by Mr. Washington. Fuller gets killed, Alex can get away. Mr. Washington is working on behalf of a U.S. senator to recover a memory stick that the bank robbers stole from the safe along with diamonds. Alex is trying to hide at Kelly's. Mr. Washington and his men track her down and kill Kelly. The mercenaries are attacking Fuller's wife and son. Alex can save her and kill two of the attackers. When Alex tries to trade the stick for the diamonds in an abandoned warehouse, she is captured and tortured. At the Cape Town airport, with the help of Fuller's wife, Alex is able to lure the mercenaries into a trap and escape with the diamonds. On the drive, she finds information about an attack the senator is planning in Chicago.

anchor_text: Dublin, in 1985, Conor Lawlor (Ferdia Walsh-Peelo), a 15-year-old high school student, is forced to change high school because his parents, affected by the economic crisis, no longer have money to pay for his education. In this new establishment, he suffered the disastrous atmosphere of the religious high school Synge Street. Inspired by the legendary bands of the time such as The Cure, Duran Duran and A-ha, the young boy forms a band with the help of his new classmates and his older brother Brendan (Jack Reynor), to seduce the beautiful and mysterious model Raphina (Lucy Boynton).
text_a: It is a triptych of three films that follow the same time period, but are told from different perspectives; on the one hand, there is that of Connor Ludlow (James McAvoy) and that of Eleanor Rigby (Jessica Chastain), a young married couple living in New York. He looks at the relationship from Connor's angle, while Ella follows Eleanor, and they transpose the couple's perspective. Connor spends his days working at his restaurant while his wife Eleanor goes back to college to continue her studies. During the course of their daily lives, the couple encounters an event that changes their lives and threatens the stability of their marriage.
text_b: In the 1980s, fourteen-year-old Conor lives with his parents in Dublin and tries to escape family and economic problems through music. Conor also has to adapt to the change at his school, entering a large complex run by Catholics. In the new high school, the protagonist faces bullying and the professors immediately begin to hate him because of the different vision of progress between him and them. After meeting Raphina, Conor decides to bring his talent to fruition by forming a musical group started with the sole purpose of conquering the girl, but then turned into a real passion that allows him to escape from the cruel world of everyday life.

text_a: Sing Street takes us to Dublin in 1985, where the economic downturn forces Conor to leave the comfort of the private school he attended and has to survive in the public school in the city centre, where the climate is much more tense. He finds a ray of hope in the mysterious and cool Raphina, and in order to win her over, he invites her to star in the music videos of the band he wants to form. She agrees, and now Conor must keep his word. He changes his name to Cosmo and immerses himself in the vibrant trends of rock music of the 80s, forms his band and devotes himself to composing songs and recording music videos. Combining the warmth of Carney's direction, vibrant humor and an unforgettable soundtrack with hits from The Cure, Duran Duran, The Police or Genesis, Sing Street is an electrifying film that will fool any music fan.
text_b: It is a triptych of three films that follow the same time period, but are told from different perspectives; on the one hand, there is that of Connor Ludlow (James McAvoy) and that of Eleanor Rigby (Jessica Chastain), a young married couple living in New York. He looks at the relationship from Connor's angle, while Ella follows Eleanor, and they transpose the couple's perspective. Connor spends his days working at his restaurant while his wife Eleanor goes back to college to continue her studies. During the course of their daily lives, the couple encounters an event that changes their lives and threatens the stability of their marriage.

anchor_text: The brother of a Ugandan mafia boss, Richard, who leads the violent Tiger Mafia drug ring, is arrested by the army. Richard blames one of his wives, Ritah, for his brother's arrest and shoots her to death. The officer in charge, Captain Alex, is seduced by one of Richard's female agents and lured into an ambush. When the gangsters, led by Russian mercenary Puff, attack the army camp where Richard's brother is being held, a shot is fired and Alex is killed. The gangsters, however, deny that they killed him. His brother Bruce U, a Ugandan Shaolin monk and kung fu master, vows revenge and meets Ritah in the jungle, who survives with severe injuries. She leads him to a warehouse, which is then stormed by the army. In retaliation, the Tiger Mafia sends the mercenary Puffs to steal a military helicopter and use it to bomb Kampala to the ground. It's a bloody showdown between Bruce U, the Tiger Mafia and the Ugandan Army.
text_a: Captain Alex, the top soldier in the local army, is sent on a mission to destroy the Tiger Mafia, a group that controls the city of Kampala, led by a man named Richard. After his brother is captured by Captain Alex, Richard decides to take revenge. Next, Captain Alex is found dead, but no one knows who killed him. Captain Alex's brother, a Ugandan Shaolin monk, arrives in Kampala to try to find the culprit. She befriends one of Richard's wives, who lost her memory after her husband shot her. The final action sequence includes numerous helicopters, a myriad of explosions and an immense number of deaths. After a long ambush, a chain reaction of events leads to Richard being wounded and taken into custody but in the end no one yet knows who really killed Captain Alex (not even the film's director).
text_b: Yamamoto is a reclusive member of the yakuza. Defeated in a war against a rival family, during which his boss was assassinated, and abandoned even by his most loyal "brother", he has no other purpose than to find out the whereabouts of his younger brother, Ken, who left Japan to study in Los Angeles. So Yamamoto goes to Los Angeles to look for him. Following some tracks, he finally finds Ken. He discovers, however, that his younger brother has become a drug dealer along with three of his friends. With the passage of time, he manages to form his own gang that gradually establishes itself as a fierce criminal organization, capable of exercising strong control over the territory, until he finds himself facing a Mafia family. The Mafia's attacks are relentless and soon Yamamoto and his men will find themselves entangled in a disastrous and irreversible situation.

anchor_text: The criminal organization Tiger Mafia and its leader, Maleficent Richard, control and terrorize the city of Kampala in the shadows. To put an end to their actions, Captain Alex is sent by his superiors to dismantle and destroy the organization and its leader. During this carefully planned mission by the captain, one of the men closest to the leader of the Tiger Mafia is captured and several criminals put out of harm's way. It is the latter's brother who decides to take revenge. To carry out his revenge, Richard sends a spy to seduce Captain Alex so that the gang members can capture him. What was done. But later that night, Alex is mysteriously murdered by an unknown man. It's time for Alex's brother to go and avenge his brother.

anchor_text: Locomotive driver Sisif rescues a little girl from a train wreck and decides to take the child in and raise her. As she grows older, Sisyphus falls in love with his stepchild, but tries to hide his affection for her. Once he sees her rocking in the front yard, he finds no other way out of his burning excitement than to close the curtains. He later became blind and could hardly work on the mountain railway, and twice threatened to derail a train out of desperation. A long time later, shortly before Sisyphus's death, the two are reunited and Sisyphus has the opportunity to enjoy his last days close to his stepchild.
text_a: Paul Verdier is a modest chocolate salesman who hasn't found his ideal wife. Despite the fact that he was always in a bad mood and was a troublemaker at home and in business, Juliette found Paul to be a loving, loyal, hardworking, and ideal husband. Then, during his tours, chance will put him in a rather ambiguous situation: a young stranger will ask him to pretend to be her husband so that she can safely return to her native farm. The shy representative, surprised at first, finally agrees. At first, everything goes wonderfully, a great feast is held, the neighbors and the priest rush in. The next day after Paul sleeps alone in the barn the deception is discovered, but forgiveness comes quickly. Paul will always remember her kindness and the tenderness of Mary.
text_b: Sisif, who works on the railways, in a train accident, picks up little Norma, who lost her parents and makes her grow up with his real son Elie. When she grows up, Sisyphus falls in love with her, but an engineer takes her away to marry her. On Mont Blanc Elie, who in turn has fallen in love with Norma, falls into a crevasse and dies. Sisyphus, blinded, does not accept Norma's help and dies.

anchor_text: Zazie is a rebellious girl, who comes to Paris from the countryside to spend a few days with her uncle Gabriel, a man who is a transvestite dancer by trade. Zazie's main desire is to see the subway, but a strike prevents her from doing so. Not resigned to the idea of seeing the people of the French capital, the little girl does not hesitate to escape from the house where she is staying, to meet a series of surreal characters: pitiful and diabolical policemen, satirists, dreamy cobblers and consoling widows who crowd Queneau's Paris.
text_a: Zaza is a celebrated actress on the stages of Paris, celebrating one success after another in the music halls of the French metropolis. One day she meets a man and seduces him with all the tools of a woman. It's about the diplomat Bernard Dufresne, who quickly falls for the beautiful artist. Dufresne takes her to his villa-like estate, where they both experience a time of intense passion. After being together for a long time, Zaza learns through the indiscretion of a jealous sidekick that her lover is already married and has a family. In spite of the pain of love, she leaves him. It's not until years later, when his wife dies, that the two of them are reunited.
text_b: The events are set in Paris, over the course of two days and during a strike of the subway system.[1] The protagonist, named Zazie, is visiting her uncle Gabriel's house. Zazie is a naughty young woman, and she doesn't pay much attention to moral conventions. Because of the strike, Zazie is forced to cross Paris partly by taxi and partly on foot. Thus, in his walks, he wanders through various neighborhoods of the city where he meets characters from the street and engages in highly original conversations. [1] [2]

anchor_text: Jack Sadelstein has a life that others can only dream of. He's a successful commercial producer for the Dunkin' Donuts company, has a wife and two kids. But every year on Thanksgiving, he gets a visit from his eccentric twin sister Jill. Actually, Jill only wanted to stay for the weekend, but she decides to extend her stay. Jack's annoyed because he's got other things on his mind. His company is desperate to make a commercial with Al Pacino, and Jack has to convince the movie star to do it. He drives his sister to a basketball game, where they meet Al Pacino and Johnny Depp. Al Pacino, who initially refuses, is Jill's flame after the first encounter. Now, Jack's visiting his sister isn't so wrong anymore, but there's a catch: Jill isn't thrilled with Pacino. A week later, the Sadelsteins take Jill on a cruise to Europe. Jack tries to convince Jill to go on a date with Al Pacino, but she refuses. Finally, Jack sees no other way out, so he disguises himself as Jill and goes to see Pacino. The real Jill feels humiliated and leaves. When she arrives home, she is surprised to find Jack with his family. While attending an alumni party, Jill gets into a hand-to-hand fight with an old schoolmate. In the midst of the commotion, Al Pacino unexpectedly appears in a theatrical costume, deeply in love. Jill explains to Pacino that her heart is already forgiven, that she loves the Mexican gardener who tends Jack's garden. Al Pacino channels his disappointment into a savage stickfight with the ceiling fan. In the end, Jack recognizes the big heart beneath his sister's rough shell and Jill finds a man who loves her with her quirks.
text_a: Jack is a handsome 30-year-old New Yorker, single, wealthy and in a very good position in the advertising industry. His is a successful life, based on a solid and precise routine, but extremely boring. Until, by chance, he meets Jill, newly arrived in town and looking for a place to stay. Jack decides to help her by taking her into his home and the two, day by day, end up getting closer and closer to each other. Together, they craft a decalogue of rules, a manifesto to follow, the first and most essential of which is: "Be honest". However, their relationship seems to be in crisis due to Jill's habit of disappearing for long periods of time without giving any explanation: when Jack forces her to confess, she reveals that her separations are due to the treatment she has to undergo for cystic fibrosis, a degenerative disease. Jack angrily accuses Jill of breaking the first rule of their relationship, and leaves her. However, after talking to his father Norman, Jack goes back on his feet. So he contacts Lucy, an eccentric friend of Jill's, and convinces her to help him win Jill back.
text_b: Jack Sadelstein is a successful advertising executive who lives in Los Angeles with his wife Erin and two young children. He leads a quiet life, but it is upset every year at Thanksgiving by the visit of his twin sister Jill who lives in the Bronx. From an early age, Jill's intrusive and extremely needy personality exasperates Jack, bringing turmoil into his life. As long as it is a matter of living together for a few days the two manage to survive, but when the cohabitation is prolonged for a month the relationship becomes unsustainable. Jack's job is to convince Al Pacino to star in one of his commercials, and knowing he'll find him at a basketball game, he joins him to meet him and brings his sister with him. Al has a crush on Jill and tells Jack that if he can get her to marry him, he'll do the commercial. On a New Year's Eve cruise, Jack, unable to convince his sister, disguises himself as Jill and meets Al Pacino. He compliments Jill/Jack a lot, telling her that he thinks her brother doesn't understand her. Jack realizes how lonely his sister must feel and that he has always mistreated and neglected her. So he goes back to the ship to apologize, but she's back in the Bronx for New Year's. Jack and his family join her along with the gardener who has in the meantime developed a love for Jill.

anchor_text: Rocky Barbella (Paul Newman) grew up in New York City in the late 1930s as part of a gang of petty criminals. He's been in and out of juvie. A deserter from the U.S. Army, he joined the gym of a boxing manager (Everett Sloane). When the authorities caught up with him, he was imprisoned in a military prison. This strong head is held in the hand by a sergeant, and makes him understand that he has gold in his hands and that it is better to put his strength and courage in becoming a real boxer than in the service of evil. Returning to civilian life, he devoted himself entirely to the career of professional boxer and gradually climbed all the ladders until he became world champion under the name of Rocky Graziano.
text_a: Jack Jefferson is a boxer who deals with racism and hatred against African Americans in the predominantly white United States in the early 20th century. Not only is he the first black heavyweight champion, but he's also in love with a white woman. Jefferson had to contend not only with the hatred of the whites, but also with the ostracism of some members of the black community who believed that Jefferson had sold them out. As a black boxing champion, he and his white entourage must learn to survive because the white leadership of the boxing organizations is also looking for ways to eliminate him.
text_b: Rocky Graziano had a difficult childhood and was beaten by his father. He joined one of the neighborhood gangs and began a long criminal career. He went to prison where he became a rebellious figure against authority. After his release, he was drafted into the army but ran away. With economic necessity, he became a boxer and found his natural talent before the army dishonorably discharged him. With a whole new life and married to Norma, he rises to the top but loses the title to Tony Zale.

anchor_text: A team of scientists discovers a crashed spacecraft frozen in ice near their research station in the Arctic. A group of soldiers called to the rescue attempt to detonate the spacecraft, accidentally destroying it. However, if an unknown creature is frozen in a block of ice, it can be recovered unharmed and taken to the research station. There, the ice melts and the resurrected creature begins a murderous hunt for humans and animals. The scientists discover that the alien is a blood-feeding plant. The creature seems invincible until a group of survivors manage to take down the thing with high voltage.
text_a: Scientists discover a spacecraft trapped in the Arctic ice sheet. When they try to extract it with heat bombs, they destroy it. Nevertheless, they detect an alien body beneath the ice. They take the specimen back to their base, frozen in a block of ice. When the latter accidentally melts, releasing the thing at the same time, the entire population of the base is threatened.
text_b: The Legion of the Winged Serpent originally from the planet Alpha in the constellation Orion are a group of humanoids with telepathic powers who are commanded by Captain Rameses (Christopher Lee). Because his planet will be destroyed by an eminent supernova, Rameses guides a group of flying saucers in order to look for some habitable planet for them. He discovers that Earth is a viable planet so, after experimenting with several abducted humans, he decides to take over the planet.

anchor_text: In 2079, Emilie Warnock, the daughter of the President of the United States visits the space prison MS One, where dangerous criminals are held. During this visit, a mutiny breaks out and the prisoners take control of the space station. An ex-CIA agent, Snow, is sent to the scene to rescue the young woman.
text_a: In 2079, Snow, a former CIA agent convicted of a crime he never committed, is offered his freedom in exchange for rescuing Emilie, the daughter of the U.S. President, from the orbital prison MS One, which has been raided by its inmates. Recovery will be hampered by Alex and psychopath Hydell.
text_b: Agent Jonathan Cold has accepted a final contract to personally transport and deliver a package to Mr. Van Haken. But the mission takes a turn for the worse when a number of assassins set out to intercept the precious and mysterious package.

anchor_text: Mash Donovan is the leader of the bandits who hold an entire region of the west. In a bar shooting, Sam Wallash is also wounded, while his friend is killed. Sam, taken in by a family, heals his wounds and sets out to find the gang. The noise comes to the ears of Donovan, who also employs Fanny in his saloon.
