She's starting to sense that Donovan is nervous and he's abusing her. Sam walks into a saloon, where Sturges, who doesn't recognize him, treats him like a stranger who can be mocked with impunity, but not killed. In a rigged boxing match, the boxer who had been intimidated into losing refuses to play the game and defeats Donovan's candidate. The latter's henchmen settle accounts with their opponents. Sam finds Sturges and makes him confess the whereabouts of Donovan, a respectable saloon manager by the name of Mash Flanagan. When Sturges reports him to Donovan, he methodically organizes the search for Sam, who, however, surprises the gang's plans about an inhabitant who doesn't pay Donovan enough. He gets rid of the men who were supposed to kill him and makes Donovan think he's dead. Meanwhile, he prevents the planned racket and eliminates part of Donovan's gang. Sam returns to town and breaks into Fanny's room. This one doesn't refuse to help him. But Sam has one of his anxiety attacks when a door slams open... reminding him of Donovan's massacre of his parents. When the latter enters the room, a little suspicious of having heard the noise, Fanny calms him down and he talks about his plans, which does not escape Sam. The next day, Sam goes to the location of Donovan's planned ambush. With a machine gun hidden in the attacked wagon, he eliminates Donovan's gang, before taking revenge on Donovan.
text_a: Mash Donovan is the leader of a vicious gang that rules unchallenged in a small western town, in one of their bloody shootings in an inn, Sam Wallash is also wounded, while his friend is killed. While Sam, who in the meantime has had his wound treated, is on the band's trail for revenge, Donovan in turn learns that Sam is not actually dead, but only injured, and asks his gang to eliminate him, hoping among other things for the help of Fanny, a woman who works in the saloon owned by Donovan. Sam, arriving in the village, enters a saloon to feed himself, but meets a man from Donovan who, not knowing his true identity, challenges him and with the help of his other accomplices is also robbed of money. Meanwhile, a boxing match is organized in the village, Donovan tries to convince the challenger to lose the race because he has bet a lot of money on the other opponent, intimidating him that in case of victory he would eliminate him. The challenger, however, does not accept and wins the race, while he is intent on running away to avoid being killed, two men enter his room and kill him. Sam, meanwhile, manages to get into Fanny's room and she tries to help him, explaining that she, too, is tired of him and would like to return home to her family. As Sam hides in the room, he is reminded of the true reasons for his grudge against Donovan, who years earlier when he was still a child, had eliminated his parents. The next day, using a ruse, he manages to gather the whole gang in an isolated place. From far away comes a carriage with two puppets on it: while the gang is gathered in a group, from the carriage emerges Sam who armed with a large machine gun kills the whole gang leaving Donovan last, finally fulfilling his revenge.
text_b: Two brothers with supernatural powers, whose parents were killed by a mob attack on them, use their powers in contrast one as the hero Samaritan to help people, the other as the villain and his bitterest opponent Nemesis to take revenge on people. After an epic battle, both are presumed dead. Young Sam, a big fan of the hero Samaritan, 25 years later does not believe that his hero has died, and meets the garbage man Joe, who can no longer hide his supernatural powers from Sam after Joe rescues him from thugs and who, after being deliberately run over, heals his severe injuries in front of Sam. Joe, however, repeatedly evades Sam as soon as he speaks to him about the past, and wants to continue to live an inconspicuous life incognito, until the leader of a gang, Cyrus, after the robbery in a warehouse captures the helmet and the powerful weapon of Nemesis and begins to spread chaos and violence as a new Nemesis and the calls for a return of Samaritan get louder and louder. Cyrus kidnaps Sam, leaving behind his wounded mother, who informs Joe, who rushes over. Joe goes up against Cyrus to save Sam. In his flashing memories of his brother's death and the ruthlessness with which he fights and defeats Cyrus and his men, Cyrus realizes that Joe is not the surviving Samaritan, but Nemesis, who tried to save his brother from death in their bitter fight at the last moment, but could not save him. In a similar situation, however, he deliberately plunges Cyrus into the deep. Unbeknownst to the media, Joe is actually Nemesis, and Sam encourages him to take up the fight against evil as a returned Samaritan in his brother's place.

anchor_text: Helen Ferguson is a young woman abandoned by her lover. Pregnant, she met on the train to California, a young married couple Hugh Harkness and his wife Patrice. The two women sympathize on the train, but an accident occurs that kills the Harkness couple. Helen Ferguson survives, but at the hospital where she is being treated, she is mistaken for Patrice Harkness by the family of the deceased husband. She then assumes the role of the deceased wife, and enters into a relationship with Bill Harkness, Hugh's brother. But her former lover finds her threatening to reveal her true identity.
text_a: During a train journey, two women who are both pregnant but do not know each other are injured in a train accident. One dies with her husband, the other ends up in the hospital but is mistaken for the deceased. She decides not to reveal the truth to ensure a good future for her child and shows up at the home of wealthy in-laws she had never met. Welcomed kindly, everything seems to be going well until her former lover, the child's father, who had previously betrayed and abandoned her, shows up. The man knows her true identity and blackmails her for money.
text_b: Surgeon Eugene Ferguson is held hostage by a group of soldiers in the midst of an insurrectionary movement against dictator Ferrago. The man eventually manages to free himself, but is warned that the politician is gravely afflicted with a brain tumor and needs immediate surgery. Meanwhile, the rebel Gonzales kidnaps Ferguson's wife, threatening to kill her if her husband performs the operation.

anchor_text: In 2001, American Samoa lost to Australia by 0:31, the highest defeat ever for a national team. The next 30 international matches were also lost. Nevertheless, the team wants to qualify for the 2014 FIFA World Cup. To assist, the Football Federation American Samoa has hired Dutch-born American football coach Thomas Rongen. For the next three weeks, Rongen trains the team, bringing in new players who no longer live on the island until they're strong enough to compete in the World Cup qualifiers. Jaiyah Saelua, part of the squad since 2003, is the first Faʻafafine to participate in a men's World Cup qualifier.
text_a: In 2002, the national football teams of the Himalayan Buddhist kingdom of Bhutan and the Caribbean island of Montserrat, which was largely devastated by a volcano between 1995 and 1997, were ranked in the bottom two positions (place 202 Bhutan and place 203 Montserrat) in the FIFA world rankings. On 30 June 2002, the day of the 2002 FIFA World Cup final between Brazil and Germany, the two teams faced each other in front of around 25,000 spectators at the Changlimithang Stadium in the Bhutanese capital Thimphu. The match ended 4-0 (1:0) for Bhutan. The Dutch director Johan Kramer documented this international game and the preparations in the participating countries and on the part of the organizers. The film shows the enthusiasm of players and fans from completely different cultural backgrounds. On one side was a quiet Buddhist hill tribe, on the other a colourful band of calypso- and reggae-crazed islanders with a British passport away from the stadiums that are usually the centre of football interest.
text_b: In 2001, the American Samoa national team, with a team decimated by problems with the transfer of players, lost 31:0 to Australia in a match valid for the Oceania qualifiers to the 2002 World Cup. The result is the biggest in the history of international football, where in addition the Australian Archie Thompson broke the record of most goals scored by a single player in a single match. In the context of this, the American Samoa national team had lost every match they played from 1994 to 2011. As a result, the American Samoa Football Federation signed Dutchman Thomas Rongen for the 2012 OFC Nations Cup qualifiers, to be played in Samoa between 22 and 26 November 2011. There, the American Samoan team finally defeated Tonga by 2:1, while it was about to qualify for the Oceanic continental tournament but after falling to Samoa 1:0 in the final match, it was eliminated.

anchor_text: Witches exist, and they have only one goal: the elimination of all the children they naturally loathe. Tired of eliminating one child at a time, the High Witch (Anjelica Huston) devises a plan: in all candy stores in Britain, give away chocolate laced with Formula 86 Retard/Mouse Mutorium a magic potion that turns children into mice within two hours. But Grandma Helga a Norwegian expert on witches and her grandson Luke happen to be at the same hotel in the UK where the Witch Conference is taking place (disguised as an organization that wants to protect children from abuse), where the High Grand Master Witch presents her plan. Luke has been hiding in the conference room to train his two white mice as the witches unmask (they take off their wigs under which they are bald). Enlightened by his grandmother, he immediately recognizes who he is dealing with. The witches have lured a boy named Bruno Jenkins with the promise of chocolate, to whom they have previously given chocolate with "Formula 86", and turn him into a mouse. Then they'll discover Luke and turn him too. The boys, however, retain their human minds. I'm sure they'll reach Grandma Helga's room and take up the fight against the witches. Luke manages to steal a vial of the potion and pour it into the soup for the witches' gala dinner. In the end, the witches are turned into mice and slaughtered by the hotel staff, clearing Britain of witches. But a good witch (the former assistant to the chief witch who left her due to her incompetence) survives, transforms Luke back into a boy and promises to do the same to Bruno. Luke and Helga decide to go to America and fight the witches there.
text_a: A man tells a part of his childhood. In 1968, when he was just a young boy, he became an orphan after his parents died in a car accident. He was then taken in by his maternal grandmother who lived in Demopolis, Alabama. The young boy struggles to find his footing because life is so different from what he had in Chicago. As he eventually gets used to all these changes, he discovers that witches do exist and that they live nearby. His grandmother knows a lot about the subject and teaches him everything there is to know. They then take refuge in a huge and very fancy hotel, The Grand Orleans Imperial Hotel, in the Gulf of Mexico where the grandmother has a cousin. There, however, the boy meets witches, who frequent the same hotel. He's going to have a real hard time unmasking them because, contrary to popular belief, these witches don't wear brooms and pointed hats. He'll have to face them, including their leader, the Greatest Witch.
text_b: The Great High Witch has a diabolical plan to get rid of every child in England. First, his kind of witches will take over all the candy stores. Then they'll sell poisoned candy and chocolate that turns children into mice! So make way for the mousetraps... This dreadful plan is overheard by a boy, the young narrator of the story. Luckily, her grandmother knows a few things about witches. Unfortunately, before he has a chance to consult her, he is transformed into a mouse himself. He escapes from their clutches and searches for his grandmother in the hotel. Not surprisingly, she blames herself for not being able to help her grandson. Because this is an old story between the Queen of Witches and the latter: old enemies. After a few tribulations, Luke, still a mouse, takes advantage of the dinner to poison the witches with the potion that turns them into mice. That's how you get what you think you're going to get. The guards exterminated them all except one, the queen's right-hand man. From a witch, she becomes a fairy and turns Luke back into a little boy.

anchor_text: In San Francisco in 1882, card cheat Charlie Malloy, nicknamed the Dirtwater Fox (George Segal), stole $40,000 from a gang of tough guys and was about to embark for Australia to start a new life. While waiting for the boat, he meets cabaret performer Amanda Quaid (Goldie Hawn), who also dreams of starting a new life as a governess in a Mormon family with many children. To get that job, she needs to make herself look neat. Using Mallone's briefcase with money, Amanda buys a dress, poses as an English duchess, gets a job and travels to Salt Lake City. Malloy goes after him.
text_a: In San Francisco in 1850, the young and beautiful Mary Rutledge became the mistress of the owner of a gambling house, Luis Chamalis. Some time later, she met a friendly gold prospector, Jim Carmichael, who was attracted to gambling tables and the hope of becoming rich. But Jim doesn't know that Chamalis is rigging the game, and he loses all the capital he had accumulated. Mary is going to help him get his money back and escape, with it, escaping Chamalis' revenge.
text_b: Charlie Malloy, a disgraced con man, is about to be hanged but a bandit saves him, forcing him, in exchange for saving his life, to participate in a robbery. After the robbery, Charlie abandons his accomplice and, after taking possession of the loot, escapes to a small town where in a saloon he meets Amanda who, in turn, steals his money and flees. Charlie manages to catch up with her but they are both followed by the bandit and, not without difficulty, they will be able to get rid of him by staying together.

anchor_text: In Shermer, Illinois, Gary and Wyatt are two teenagers who are teased at school and unsuccessful with girls. While watching the movie Frankenstein, Gary gets the idea to create an artificial woman using Wyatt's computer. The result is Lisa, a beautiful young woman, who turns out to be very delusional and super-powered. In order to get them to get their act together and regain their self-confidence, Lisa leads the two friends on a series of fantastical and wacky adventures, which include the near-destruction of Wyatt's parents' home, and a fight with mutant bikers who escaped from Mad Max 2: The Conjuring and The Hill Has Eyes.
text_a: Diana and Maureen are very close friends, living through their rebellious teenage years together, made up of partying, dope and boys, despite their opposite personalities, the one a maverick and the other more peaceful. One quiet morning like so many will forever mark their lives, as they lie in the school bathrooms hearing screams and gunshots coming from the school hallways, suddenly a student named Michael bursts into the bathroom pointing a gun at them. Diana and Maureen feel on the verge of death, but Michael offers them a choice, asking the two girls to indicate to him which of them he should shoot, and Maureen volunteers. Fifteen years after that tragic event, Diana is now a woman, married and the mother of a little girl named Emma, smart but addicted to playing hide-and-seek. Diana's life seems perfect, despite the ups and downs with her husband Paul and the rebellious nature of her daughter, but the memories of that tragic day continue to haunt her.
text_b: Gary and Wyatt are two very clumsy teenagers, especially with girls, and are often and willingly made fun of by their peers Ian and Max. One day, after watching a Frankenstein movie on TV, they decide to create the ideal woman using Wyatt's computer; so out comes Lisa, a beautiful and practically perfect girl, who will also turn out to be endowed with extraordinary powers. Thus begins a series of exciting adventures for Gary and Wyatt that will lead them to mature and gain self-confidence, succeeding, with Lisa's help, in winning the hearts of two of their peers and gaining popularity among their schoolmates. Eventually it turns out that Gary and Wyatt's popularity comes "magically", because Lisa has used her superpowers to help the boys fit in.

anchor_text: The brothers Danny and Ody are very different: While 18-year-old Ody is grounded and mature and has built an existence in Athens despite his Albanian origin, 16-year-old Danny is boisterous, adventurous, gay and still lives with his mother in Crete. When his mother dies, Danny wants to move in with Ody in Athens so he can plunge into the nightlife there. After an incident during the night, the two decide to search for their long-lost, wealthy father, who is said to be in Thessaloniki. Once there, they stumble upon the casting for the "Superstar" singing competition. Ody has the hope of finally realizing his dream of a musical career.
text_a: When Miriam breaks off her relationship with her rich lover, the woman is forced to move with her fifteen-year-old son Michael to a poor and multiracial neighborhood in Berlin. The boy makes friends with the new neighbors, but at school he soon becomes a victim of the bully Errol and his gang. In search of easy money, Michael enters the world of drug dealers and in exchange for their protection begins to work for them as a drug courier.
text_b: The protagonist of this story is called Dany and is a homosexual teenager born in Albania, although raised in Greece, with his mother. After the boy's mother dies, he must find his brother to tell him what happened. Now, you're both facing a serious problem. Having been born in Albania they can no longer continue to live in Greece, unless they obtain Greek nationality. Dany and his brother Odysseas will have to make a journey from the city of Thessaloniki to Athens, to look for their father, whom they have not seen since they were very small. Xenia is a film of search and rediscovery of oneself, where topics such as homophobia and intolerance that still exist in Greece are addressed.

anchor_text: In 1327, when Christendom was divided between the authority of Pope John XXII and that of Emperor Louis IV of the Holy Empire, the former inquisitor William of Baskerville went to a Benedictine abbey, located between Provence and Liguria, accompanied by his young secretary Adso who is the narrator of the plot. In a climate of theological conflict between the Franciscans and the papal authority over the poverty of Christ - serving above all as a facade for the political conflict between the pope and the emperor - the former inquisitor must investigate, at the request of the abbot, the suspicious death of one of the monks. Soon, what many seemed to regard as suicide is taking on increasingly disturbing proportions. At the pope's request, the Dominican inquisitor Bernardo Gui went to the abbey and interfered with the inquiry, but this did little to improve matters. The Name of the Rose is a story in seven chapters, a symbolic number that represents the number of days and stages of the investigation as well as the approximate number of deaths. The story is bounded by the account of the discovery of the manuscript which the author purports to translate, and by the conclusions of the aged narrator.
text_a: A fictionalized biography of St. Francis of Assisi. Son of the wealthy merchant Pietro di Bernardone, he chose to fight in the papal troops alongside his friend Paolo di Vandria. But he hears the voice of God, defect and is imprisoned. Freed by his friend Chiara, he renounces his possessions and wears a poor suit, gathering some confreres and finding the opposition of Paul, who has become a knight. His rule is approved by Pope Innocent III as he begins to preach for Italy. He travels to the Holy Land to preach the gospel to the Saracens and the Sultan of Egypt and is hosted by them. He returns to Assisi almost blind and finds his brothers have modified the rule. He retires to a hermitage on Mount Verna and there he receives the stigmata. He will die at the Porziuncola, attended only by the friars, by Chiara and by Paolo who has just repented of having insulted his friend and of having led a life in the service of weapons and war.
text_b: It's the Middle Ages in the vicinity of the winter of 1327 under the papacy of John XXII. The Franciscan William of Baskerville and his disciple, the Benedictine novice Adso of Melk, arrive at a Benedictine abbey located in northern Italy and famous for its impressive library, which has strict access rules. William must organize a meeting between the pope's delegates and the leaders of the Franciscan order, in which the alleged heresy of the doctrine of apostolic poverty, promoted by a branch of the Franciscan order: the Spirituals, will be discussed. The celebration and success of this meeting are threatened by a series of deaths that the superstitious monks, at the behest of the blind ex-librarian Jorge de Burgos, consider to follow the pattern of a passage from the Apocalypse. William and Adso, often evading the rules of the abbey, try to solve the mystery by discovering that, in fact, the deaths revolve around the existence of a poisoned book, a book that was thought to be lost: the second book of Aristotle's Poetics. The arrival of the papal envoy and inquisitor Bernardo Gui begins an inquisitorial process of bitter memory for William, who in his search has discovered the magnificent and labyrinthine library of the abbey. William's scientific method is confronted by the religious fanaticism represented by George of Burgos.

anchor_text: One night in the countryside, Mike Hammer tries to help a stranger, Christina, a frightened woman on the run. They are caught by the pursuers and Hammer narrowly escapes death. When he got out of the hospital, he went back to his shady life as a private investigator. His policeman friend Pat Murphy clumsily draws his attention to the mysteries surrounding his fake accident. Realizing that there is something important behind this seemingly mundane affair, Hammer then conducts his own investigation, with the help of his secretary and mistress Velda. Finding Christina's home, investigating among what remains of her acquaintances as some have disappeared in violent circumstances, Hammer slowly approaches the goal, avoiding several assassination attempts. His investigation leads him to a high-level gangster named Carl Evello. Hammer understands that all the protagonists of the case, the police, the various gangsters and now himself, are looking for an object (a MacGuffin) whose nature he does not know. Meanwhile, informants point Velda in the right direction, complicating the investigation. While Pat has information he doesn't want to give to Mike, Mike manages to track down Lily Carver, Christina's former roommate, who will help him considerably in solving the riddle. But in the shadows, a character pulls the strings, who manages to get his hands on the object first, not without considerable damage to him and to all the protagonists. Mike Hammer is portrayed as a fan of fast cars (a Jaguar XK 140 and then a Chevrolet Corvette first model) that he drives to an open grave. His friend and garage keeper Nick (Nick Dennis) is murdered in a way that is as atrocious as it is dishonest, crushed under a vehicle from which a killer removes the jack.
text_a: Mike Hammer is a private investigator in Los Angeles, brutal, lover of women and violence and very corrupt (he lives by blackmailing people who have something to hide). One night he gives a ride to Christina, an attractive hitchhiker dressed only in a raincoat, who blocks him in the middle of the road. Forced to stop, he forces her into a car on a lonely country road. After passing a police checkpoint, which stops cars looking for a woman who escaped from a mental hospital, some cars cut them off, causing them to go off the road. Hammer, in a semi-conscious state, sees Christina being tortured to death. The criminals then, to disguise the crime, throw them (Hammer unconscious and Christina dead) with the car down a cliff. Mike wakes up after three days in the hospital, watched by his secretary. After a while, he gets better, partly out of revenge, partly because he thinks there's something bigger going on, so he decides to pursue the case. At the end of a series of events, Hammer discovers that the reason for a long series of deaths is contained in a small briefcase containing a luminous and dangerous substance.
text_b: One night, while in the car with his girlfriend and two friends, Chris Pratt performs the stunt of driving with the headlights off, causing an accident in which two of them lose their lives. He survives, but remains crippled in his mental faculties, as he can no longer put events in sequence. Plagued by guilt, he goes through a rehabilitation program and lives with Lewis, a blind man who works as his assistant. Relations with his wealthy family are also difficult. One day he meets Gary, a former schoolmate who wants to involve him, even with the help of a girl, in a criminal project; to carry out a robbery in the bank vault where Chris performs cleaning work. After some resistance, Chris, now convinced that he can receive no help from family or others, is persuaded to participate in the crime. At the last moment he wants to back off, but in the meantime the theft is discovered by a policeman, a friend of Chris, who loses his life along with two of the thieves in the ensuing violent shootout. Chris manages to escape with the rich loot, but the surviving robbers kidnap his blind friend, threatening to kill him if he does not hand over the stolen money. With one last shot, Chris, who hasn't forgotten the lessons he learned from hunting as a young man, manages to To prevail against the bandits and save Lewis. Due to your state of mind, you will not be charged for your participation in the robbery.

anchor_text: Bobby Funke is a sophomore who's not very popular. His ambition is to attend Northwestern University to become a journalist. However, although he claimed to be the best, he never completed an article for his school newspaper (Saint Donovan). The editor-in-chief, Clara Diaz, asks him to write an article about Paul Moore, the student president and the most popular boy. Bobby tries to interview him but to no avail and he's even intimidated by Paul's friends. One day, the school's headmaster, Jared Kirkpatrick, discovers that some test papers in his office have been stolen. He then summons the students from his blacklist as well as Bobby, in order to question them. Although the group turns out to be innocent, the leader warns them that they are now under surveillance. When Francesca Facchini asks Bobby to track down the thieves, he conducts a wide-ranging investigation linking Paul to the theft and writes an article denouncing the latter. The principal then forces Paul to open his locker and finds the subjects under examination. Clara decided to send the article to Northwestern University. Bobby then becomes a popular student and gains the respect of all. Although Bobby's popularity grows by the day, he begins to have suspicions about Paul's guilt, who claims his innocence. Bobby wonders if he's the victim of a conspiracy.
text_a: Alex Jurel is a teacher at a high school with over 2,000 students, where other teachers are demotivated or corrupt. He gets involved and temporarily assumes the duties of a school psychologist. In this capacity, he takes care of student Eddie Pilikian. There's a lawsuit against the school. Attorney Lisa Hammond, a former student of Jurel's, has been assigned by the plaintiff to find out why a student passed school despite being illiterate. Jurel notices the corruption behind this case Eddie is illiterate too. He realizes that the school's appearance of educating the children is more important than the education itself. That's why he's trying to reform the school. Principal Dr. Donna Burke and the majority of the teachers feel threatened by Jurel's actions. So Burke offers him his resignation and threatens to fire him. The students and Hammond, who has also noticed the corruption, stand up for Jurel. He then turns against her.
text_b: Bobby Funke (Reece Thompson) is a sophomore aspiring journalist who is assigned to do a story on student body president Paul Moore (Patrick Taylor) for the school newspaper of private St. John's High School. Donovans. What are you talking about? While following Moore to interview a popular senior boy, the team's leading basketball player and boyfriend of the most popular girl, Francesca Fachini (Mischa Barton), Principal Kirkpatrick (Bruce Willis) discovers that the SAT exams, which most seniors take, have been stolen and Bobby begins investigating at the request of Francesca, with whom he is in love. After interrogating those who might be the prime suspects, he receives clues that lead him to Moore, and these would supposedly confirm his guilt in the facts. By making the report and printing it, Funke becomes a popular guy when everyone reads the report and Director Kirkpatrick finds the exams in Moore's locker. After this and being acclaimed by his peers from the school newspaper, the newspaper's editor and Bobby's sentimental interest, Clara Diaz (Melonie Diaz) decides to introduce him into the journalism program of a prestigious university, while Moore leaves his position as president and assumes Francesca's stepbrother and vice president of the school, Marlon Piazza (Luke Grimes) as president. After receiving a call from the head of the journalism program, he asks Funke for evidence to confirm that his story is true and he begins to investigate after Paul Moore reveals that he was not the culprit. Bobby discovers that Francesca was an accomplice of Marlon Piazza, because one night when he goes to his house he finds them in the bathtub and begins to doubt her, finally ends up discovering her, being she who steals the exams and puts them in Paul Moore's locker.

anchor_text: After a failed operation of the Secret Intelligence Service in Czechoslovakia, which he led from Vienna, the young secret agent John Shay returns to the headquarters in London. Although his superiors don't believe him, John is certain that there is a double agent at the head of the intelligence service, who must have also betrayed his action to the enemy. Through his mistress, John obtains the files on all the failed operations of the last few years. His suspicions are finally confirmed, but he is suspended from the service for the theft of files. His circumstantial evidence was rejected shortly afterwards by a commission of inquiry. Still, John does not give up, even though he narrowly escapes an assassination attempt. He'll soon be able to liquidate the double agent. The last round of the dangerous game also goes to British intelligence, which, with John's help but without his knowledge, succeeds in inflicting a crushing defeat on Soviet intelligence in Greece. At the end of the day, John still gets promoted because his actions fit the concept of top intelligence.
text_a: In 1981, Oleg Ivanovich Zajcev, a KGB officer working in the Kremlin's communications center, learns of a Soviet operation to assassinate Pope John Paul II, guilty of wanting to help the Polish people in their difficult political and social situation, with the support of the Bulgarian secret services, the KDS. Zaitsev, however, could not accept that an innocent man such as the Pope should be killed for purely political purposes and therefore decided to defect, after having made contact with one of the American agents of the CIA in Moscow, in order to inform the Western secret services of this operation and prevent its implementation. Jack Ryan, CIA analyst, gets involved in a field operation for the first time. His job is to support the British secret service team, MI6, which has the very delicate objective of expelling "Red Rabbit" Zajcev and his family from the Soviet Union. As valuable intelligence gets into the hands of the Americans and the British agents are deployed on the ground, and even what should be a simple analyst ends up on the front line to give all possible support. The Italian police forces are not informed to avoid leaks of news and the operation is carried out; in fact Ali Ağca will succeed in shooting the Pope, but the Bulgarian colonel who should have killed the Turk immediately after the attack to eliminate any connection between him and the DS, and consequently with the KGB, is stopped by Jack and after being interrogated, he is "disabled" and left in a car.
text_b: John Shay, a British agent of the Secret Intelligence Service, suspects that a security breach was the cause of the failure of British operations in Vienna. He persuades his girlfriend Polly, a secretary at the headquarters of the Intelligence Service, to facilitate access to secret files. The information he uncovers leads him to suspect Agent Adam Booth (with whose wife, Sarah, he had an affair) of being a double agent for the Soviet Union. When Shay reports his suspicions to his superiors, they refute what they believe to be baseless accusations and suspend him from office. Nevertheless, Shay is continuing to investigate. On his way to Istanbul to gather more evidence against Booth, he was the victim of an assassination attempt. Overwhelming information given to him by British scientist Philip Crawford leads him to execute Booth and finds in one of his pockets a plane ticket to Athens. Shay takes Booth's identity and sails to Athens with Sarah. In Greece, they are captured by Soviet agents to serve as bargaining chips against Philip Crawford. The couple are rescued by a CIA agent, Colonel Scott, who reveals to them that Booth was indeed a double agent, but was working for the British to relay false information to the Soviets.

anchor_text: A successful fashion designer is assaulted at home by his nagging wife and at work by his competitors. His life becomes even more complicated when one of his clients, a future bride, abandons her wealthy husband and comes to him in search of a job.
text_a: An heiress leaves her boring boyfriend to become a model and soon finds herself in love with the owner of the fashion house who hires her, except he's married. The poor guy's got his own problems, though. His wife's artistic ambitions drive him to invest too much money. But it'll all work out for the best, he'll leave her and marry his young model.
text_b: In a hot town on the Florida coast, mediocre lawyer Ned Racine (William Hurt) begins an affair with Matty Walker (Kathleen Turner), the wife of an older businessman. Walker convinces Racine to get her husband out of the way, and the two plot a murder. However, Racine's fate will be very different from the plans.

anchor_text: A young girl, Letty, arrives by train from Virginia to work on her cousin Beverly's ranch. On board she meets Whit Roddy, a man who trades along the railway line, who tells her that the area where she is going to live is particularly windy. He tells her that this wind drives people crazy, especially women. Arriving at her cousin's house, Letty must endure the jealousy of Cora, Beverly's wife. Two men, Sourdough and Lige, propose to her, but she rejects them. Cora urges him to leave the ranch, having no money, she wishes to marry Whit Roddy. She then discovers that he is already married and wants to make the girl his mistress. She ends up marrying Lige. Discovering that she does not love him and that the wind is tormenting him to the point of unbearability, he leaves in order to earn enough money to send her back to Virginia.
text_a: Young Vergie Winters falls in love in 1910 with the attractive John Shadwell, who comes from a family of the better society. His relatives break up the relationship and John marries the cold-hearted Laura according to custom. Vergie, meanwhile, has become pregnant by John and is giving their child, John and Laura, up for adoption. Again and again, John Vergie offers to divorce her, but she refuses, nobly and courteously, to build her fortune on the ruins of a failed relationship. Instead, she becomes the successful owner of an elegant fashion salon and soon counts the very best society among her customers. John continues to maintain a relationship with Vergie over the years, and at the same time tries everything in his power to get Laura to divorce him. When he finally finds a leverage to get rid of his wife, she shoots him in a fit of jealousy. For some inexplicable reason, Vergie Winters takes the blame and goes to prison in Laura's place. It's not until years later, on her deathbed, that Laura confesses the truth and Vergie can finally hold her daughter in her arms.
text_b: Letty, a young girl from Virginia, goes to live with her cousin Beverly in a valley in a desert in the United States where the wind blows relentlessly. The relationship with Cora, the cousin's wife, quickly becomes strained and Letty will be forced to leave the house, but to do so she will have to marry. Tied to Lige, a man she doesn't love, and the victim of a wind that drives her crazy, things can't get any better for Letty. Yet it all comes crashing down with the arrival of her old crush Roddy and ends in a tragic ending, but one that leads the girl to love again, giving her hope.

anchor_text: A few years after the Civil War, the James-Younger gang terrorized the Midwest. This gang of criminals is led by Jesse James and Cole Younger. They commit many robberies together. Mr. Rixley, of the Pinkerton Detective Agency, is in charge of capturing these outlaws.
text_a: A bitter struggle has just begun between the railroad owners and the ranchers who view with a very bad eye the passage of the iron horse on their fertile lands. After killing the representative of the Railroad Company, who had come to expropriate his family, Jesse James fled to the hills and organized a gang that ransomed and attacked passenger trains.
text_b: The year is 1866. It is the story of the notorious Jesse James gang of brother Frank, the Younger brothers and the Millers who, after the war between the North and the South of the United States, continued in Missouri to rob banks, trains and Union coaches.

anchor_text: Long Riders depicts the lives of the various members of the James-Younger gang, though it can be argued that the film is basically historically accurate. The film actually begins in the middle of the gang's career, and does not deal with the background of the individual gang members who fought as guerrillas against the Union troops in the Civil War. However, the film does not only deal with the gang's robberies, but also tries to give a little insight into the private lives of the gang members: the marriages of Frank and Jesse James, as well as love affairs and relationships. Also depicted are very private aspects, in addition to the above-mentioned weddings, the funeral of the murdered youngest James brother and the recovery of a farmer friend who had sheltered the gang and was killed in a police action. Also noteworthy is the discussion of Cole Younger's relationship with Belle Starr. In addition, the extraordinary interest of the press in the James-Younger deeds is reflected, which manifests itself again and again in the encounter of a reporter with Allan Pinkerton. Furthermore, the behavior of the supporters of the former warring parties is shown Union loyalists support Pinkerton in the search and pursuit of Jesse James, but this is usually unsuccessful, as the former supporters of the Confederacy repeatedly provide shelter to the gang. Finally, the film ends with the murder of Jesse James by Bob Ford, which happens in a family idyll (Jesse still gives his son a goodnight kiss before being shot), and the voluntary resignation of Frank James, who surrenders to the authorities.
text_a: The film is about the James-Younger gang, which performed in Missouri after the Civil War and the lives of each of them. They robbed banks and trains. In time, they became the most notorious bandits in the area. As pro-Confederate guerrillas during the war, they were sheltered by those who supported the Confederacy, as they were motivated by a feeling of hostility towards the opposing side they had previously fought during the war, an event in which they learned all about it. At the same time the man who was looking for them, Mr. Rixley of the Pinkerton Agency, was supported by those who stood on the side of the Union. By making mistakes, the Pinkerton agency fails to capture the gang, as those mistakes only put those who harbor them even more on their side. However, in 1876, the gang tries to rob a bank in the north of the country, which is discovered earlier by the locals. That way you can receive them properly prepared. Much of the gang is killed, while the wounded Younger brothers are arrested. Only the James brothers can escape and then form another gang. However Jesse James is later betrayed and murdered by the Fords, new members of the gang, who killed them in exchange for money from Mr. Rixley, and Frank James then hands himself over to Pinkerton in exchange for him being able to give his brother a proper burial, which he does.
text_b: The story begins with the railroad company's dishonest purchase of land from poor Missouri farmers. Among these poor peasants was the mother of the Jameses who, defended by these against the intimidation of the railway thugs, does not sign the sale contract. But the man in charge of the purchase of the land not content with a refusal prepares an assault on the house of James that ends with the death of the mother. These to avenge the death of his mother begin a series of robberies always being the railway the victim of these crimes. The struggle of the underprivileged against the all-powerful railroad makes the James brothers gain great popularity throughout the American West. But over the years the rebellious youths have become outlaws and have a whole army behind them ready to capture them. While Jesse has married and had a child, tired of the life of criminal prepares a big bank robbery that fails and loses much of the gang and the James brothers are saved by a miracle. Willing to retire and lead a new life, Jesse is visited by former bandmate Bob Ford and his brother, who offer him a new deal, but it is nothing more than the excuse to kill him treacherously when he is unhooking a painting.

anchor_text: Twenty-three-year-old Ann lives with her husband and two young daughters in a trailer in her mother's backyard. She had her first child at 17, dropped out of school, and works as a night janitor at the university. Her life changes abruptly when she learns that she has ovarian cancer and has only two to three months to live. She refuses treatment and decides not to tell anyone that she has to die, not even her family; she seems to go on with her life as before. She sits down in a coffee shop and makes a list of things she wants to do before she dies. In addition to seemingly mundane routines like changing her hairstyle, she wants to have sex with another man for the first time, since her husband Don is her first and only partner. And she wishes someone would fall in love with her. Similarly, she regulates the time after her death: she seeks a new wife for her husband and discusses tapes for the birthdays of her two daughters up to their eighteenth birthday.
text_a: Ann is only 23 years old, yet her life already seems to run on perfectly defined tracks. Two daughters and a husband she loves and feels loved by, a perfect routine that comes wiped out one day like so many. Ann gets sick, goes to the hospital, and after a long series of tests, she's diagnosed with a disease. It was incurable. She learns the news in an unusual way, a doctor sits down next to her and, without looking her in the eye, tells her that she has only a few weeks to live. Ann does not react in despair, but with extreme detachment and awareness, faces death avoiding looking her in the eyes to avoid dragging her last moments into the abyss of despair. She doesn't talk about her illness with anyone, not her mother, not even her husband. With cold lucidity she plans the goals she hopes to achieve before her death, to give meaning to a life without dreams that has worn her out far more than the disease will be able to do. A long list of trivial yet true things, in which is encapsulated the essence of what his life has been. She plans to make love to other men, she who has had only one love, to make someone fall in love with her, and to send her daughters birthday wishes until they are 18 years old, recording her voice on tapes that her doctor will deliver to them from year to year. He'll be able to fulfill every wish, and then he'll die peacefully.
text_b: The terminally ill Ann Lord, who is being cared for by a nurse, calls her daughters Constance and Nina to her. She tells them of events 50 years earlier, when she was still living under her birth name Ann Grant, and of a previously unknown Harris Arden, whose existence was a secret for 50 years and whom she loved more than anything. In 1954, she had traveled to Newport to become the bridesmaid of her college friend, Lila Wittenborn. In flashbacks that blend with the present, it is told how the young Ann Grant then assists her friend at her wedding because Lila actually married the wrong man, a man named Carl. In reality, however, she is in love with someone else: Harris Arden, a doctor who is a friend of the Wittenborn family. But Harris never reciprocated Lila's feelings. He, on the other hand, is immediately fascinated by the arrival of the beautiful Ann. Before long, the two become close, dance together, kiss, and spend a night together. This, in turn, does not tolerate Lila's brother Buddy, with whom Ann is close friends, and gets drunk. The night ends with a tragic accident that Ann will never forget. These memories haunt old Ann on her deathbed. Harris was the love of her life, but after that passionate night, they parted ways again. Ann has had several unfulfilled marriages, given up her singing career, and raised her children. Harris now calls them mistakes because he never let them go. In the present, Lila Wittenborn comes to visit her sick friend. She provides background information to her daughter Nina who is now researching her unknown husband.

anchor_text: A strange asteroid crashes into Earth and Professor Murai's team goes to investigate. Meanwhile, a strange woman claiming to be a Martian announces that if mankind does not repent, it will be destroyed. The Martian prophesies the return of Godzilla and Rodan, as well as the coming of a space monster called King Ghidorah. The prophecies are fulfilled and Ghidorah spreads terror. The only solution: Godzilla, Rodan and Mothra must agree to cooperate in order to defeat the space monster.
text_a: A Himalayan princess is possessed by the spirit of a female inhabitant of Venus, who escaped from her planet before it was ravaged by the monstrous King Ghidorah, a powerful space-faring dragon who travels through space with a meteorite. The princess, who someone intends to assassinate, tries to warn the powerful of Earth, but is not listened to. Only in the priestesses-fairies of Mothra can he find help. At the same time, however, Godzilla and Rodan also make their appearance, who begin to attack Japan. But when King Ghidorah arrives, the two monsters, along with Mothra (in the larval state), will give him battle, and they will succeed in defeating him. The princess can thus return to her homeland, while Godzilla and Rodan watch Mothra return to her island.
text_b: Unscrupulous businessman Clark Nelson captures the two tiny Shobijin fairies on an expedition to Infant Island and uses them in a circus show. To save them, Mothra, still in its larval stage, will rush to their rescue, destroying much of Tokyo. The army would kill it, but the larva, pooping on Tokyo Tower, will turn into a beautiful moth. Finally, in a gunfight, Nelson is killed and the fairies can return to Infant Island with their goddess.

anchor_text: The ancient strongman Samson (originally: Hercules) on his visit to Nisia, a kingdom, learns that the despotic Menisto has taken advantage of the king's death to enthrone himself as his former advisor. Officially, however, the daughter reigns, carrying out the will of Menistos and oppressing the people. As the queen falls in love with Samson, Menisto fears for his influence and tries to overcome his anxiety by assassinating him. They fail, and Samson allies with the local fighters who carry out a rebellion against Menisto. Menisto may be defeated in battle, but the queen is also killed.
text_a: The island of Hermosa is ambushed by the pirates of the Black Border who do not miss an opportunity to launch attacks and their accomplice Rodrigo Sanchez, a cowardly advisor to the governor. Samson, captain of the Spanish soldiers, confronts the evil and restores order, also obtaining the love of Rosita, daughter of the governor
text_b: Hercules of Thebes, during his journey to the land of the King of Misia, is joined by Daria, a servant of Queen Nissia, who informs him of the taking of the throne by Menistus, who has established an oppressive and cruel government of the country towards the inhabitants. Hercules decides to immediately put himself at the head of the oppressed to provoke a reaction and bring down the tyrant. Menistus then kills Daria in revenge, but will ultimately be executed by Hercules. Eridon, the leader of the rebels, assumes power and Hercules can resume his journey.

anchor_text: A plane crashes in the middle of the African savannah, with five Americans on board who came to observe the animals. Its occupants owe their survival solely to Tarzan's intervention. He offers to guide the survivors through the jungle. But Hawkins, a white hunter, joins the group, with the goal of delivering them to the chief of a nearby village, in exchange for the indigenous people's stock of ivory...
text_a: A plane crashes in the African jungle and only a few passengers survive including the beautiful Diana and a doctor. Tarzan takes care of them, but Diana is unexpectedly kidnapped by the Opar tribe who want to sacrifice her to their lion god. The ape-man manages to save her just in time and take her with him to the doctor. Tarzan trusts him very much, but the man has actually made deals with the enemy Opar and soon Diana finds herself again in the balance between life and death.
text_b: An elderly man decides to settle permanently in Kenya, in a cabin lost in the woods, with his adult granddaughter and some animals. They live a joyful life, in a primitive state, far from everyone except a tribe of nomads, with whom they trade, until a plane crashes in the nearby forest, and one of the passengers, due to the impact, loses his memory. He heavily influences their lives, and he gives rise to a long series of unexpected events.

anchor_text: Three girlfriends live in Eastwick, a quiet and Puritan village in Rhode Island. Jane, the redheaded one, is divorced and a music teacher; Sukie, the blonde one, is a journalist and a mother of six little girls who were abandoned by their father; and Alexandra, the brunette one, is a sculptor and a widow. The three of them get together every Thursday night to talk about anything and everything but often to express their fantasies. On a stormy night, the three friends, quite drunk, have fun making the robot portrait of the ideal lover. The next day, Daryl Van Horne moved into Eastwick's oldest and most sumptuous residence, where the village's founding fathers once burned witches. Nothing is known about this handsome man who is surrounded by a somewhat evil aura of mystery. So he's going to get close to the three women and start a rather special relationship with them, revealing them to themselves. Quickly, the man begins charming the three beautiful young women, one after the other. At the same time, they will begin to develop strange abilities...
text_a: The story is set in the fictional town of Eastwick in Rhode Island and deals with the magical abilities and personal growth of Alexandra Medford (Cher), Jane Spofford (Susan Sarandon), and Sukie Rougemont (Michelle Pfeiffer), three bored women who discover they have magical abilities. His power is small at first, but expands when he summons the devil, Daryl Van Horne (Jack Nicholson). The mysterious Daryl seduces each of the women and, in return, teaches them how to increase their powers, but the sexual antics and magic employed by the witches end up creating a scandal in the town. The women realize that Daryl is corrupting them and the city, and they decide to cut him out of their lives.
text_b: Two English nobles, father and son, go on holiday to Paris where they will both have a romantic adventure. John, the son, tries to foster a relationship between his father and a beautiful widow, Sylvia Gilbert; the father, Sir Edgar Fraser, thinks that the right girl for John might be the poor young Lisette. They'll both fall in love, but with the woman they've chosen for each other. When the holidays are over, they'll all be at Mr. de Colville's country mansion whose daughter, Suzanne, is in love with John. The girl declares to the young man that she realizes that the affair with Sylvia was only a fleeting and romantic encounter, while Suzanne could be the woman of his life. For their part, even Sir Edgar and Lisette acknowledge that theirs is an impossible affair and part as good friends. In Paris, the girl is awaited by her lover who comes to pick her up in Vespa: she abandons the golden world of castles and luxury sports cars to return to the modest life of every day, while Sir Edgar, perhaps, will weave a relationship with Sylvia.

anchor_text: Lenny Bruce is a comedian famous for his improvisational talents. He created a new style, later called stand-up. In the 1950s and 1960s, he would fight to preserve his libertarian and satirical tone in a conservative America. It's going to get him several convictions but it's going to allow for the advent of greater freedom of speech for a whole generation of comedians.
text_a: Lenny Bruce is a caustic comedian who works in low-class nightclubs. Shortly after meeting Honey, an exuberant stripper, he marries her. For years they maintain a tumultuous relationship that includes drugs and unconventional sex, but they eventually break up. Lenny's fame as a showman is on the rise, but so are the denunciations and trials he must face due to the subversive nature of his humor.
text_b: Lenny Cantrow, a sporting goods salesman, has just married Lila Kolodny. He meets and falls in love with Kelly while on his honeymoon. As soon as they arrive at the hotel, Lenny realizes that everything Lila says or does annoys him. One day he was walking alone on the beach when he met the beautiful Kelly. This meeting confirms his fears: he does not love his wife. Lenny wants to divorce his wife to seduce Kelly, but he bumps into her father.

anchor_text: Comedian Lenny Bruce's rise to success is continually hampered by obscenity complaints due to his provocative humor and vulgar and explicit language, misunderstood by the puritanical America riding through the fifties and sixties. What he considers a bigoted persecution affects his private life: the continuous quarrels with his wife, unarmed stripper, the betrayals and adventures without feeling, the unregulated life and the abuse of alcohol and drugs lead him to a tragic death.
text_a: Sportswriter Lenny and his wife, Amanda, adopt young Max, who grows up to be a smart, witty, and resourceful child. One day, Lenny can no longer control his curiosity and sets out to find the boy's birth mother. This one is the prostitute Linda, who also does not correspond to Lenny's idea in any other way - especially in terms of her intellect. Nevertheless, they become friends, and Lenny tries to put them back on the path of virtue. He buys her off her pimp for basketball cards and tries to set her up with a decent guy. When his marriage is in crisis, Lenny sleeps with Linda, and the two have a daughter, which he never learns, just as Linda never learns that Lenny's son Max is her biological child.
text_b: Lenny Bruce is a caustic comedian who works in low-class nightclubs. Shortly after meeting Honey, an exuberant stripper, he marries her. For years they maintain a tumultuous relationship that includes drugs and unconventional sex, but they eventually break up. Lenny's fame as a showman is on the rise, but so are the denunciations and trials he must face due to the subversive nature of his humor.

anchor_text: Sartana is accused by the bank's director of stealing $300,000 and killing all the employees. In reality, he is innocent, and it is with the help of a thief, Buddy Ben, that he comes to town, avoids all the traps and discovers the real thieves.
text_a: Sartana is accused by the bank's director of robbing $300,000 and killing all the employees. But he's actually innocent, and with the help of the thief Buddy Ben, he goes into town, avoids all the ambushes that are thrown at him, and discovers the real culprits.
text_b: Two crooked bankers cause diligence attacks to offer themselves substantial financial benefits. Eager to pit the two gangs they use against each other, they discover that the latter two have been neutralized by a mysterious man in black named Sartana.

anchor_text: Colonel Cantwell may have escaped the battlefields of the Second World War, but the war hero is marked by the horrors. He retreats to Venice for the weekend and lets his driver take him to his favorite spots in the Italian city. He meets Renata, a young woman who rekindles the spark of life in him.
text_a: During World War II, Allied forces land on the beaches of Anzio unopposed, but instead of moving inland their commander decides to dig trenches. A war correspondent, already hardened by previous battles, borrows a jeep, drives to Rome and returns without encountering any of the German forces. However, his report on the absence of the enemy is considered of little account by the Allied High Command. When they finally decide to advance, the Germans arrive in force and a prolonged and bloody struggle ensues.
text_b: Richard Cantwell is a war-torn U.S. Army colonel who confronts the news of his terminal illness with stoic indifference. A chance encounter with a young woman begins to rekindle in him the hope of renewal.

anchor_text: Doug Holt is 15 years old. He lives with a schoolteacher mother and Jacey, his older brother. Doug is a longtime friend of Pamela Abbott, the daughter of one of the city's wealthiest families. As for Jacey, he decides to start his own business with the rebel Eleanor Abbott in an attempt to climb the social ladder. But Mr. Abbott, the patriarch, doesn't see it that way.
text_a: They share the same birth month, which is why the orphanage calls them the December boys - Map, Spit, Spark and Misty - they have a lot in common. With no hope of being adopted, they form their family obligations. Suddenly an unexpected news that a young couple could adopt only one of them, will turn them into rivals for being chosen.
text_b: In 1950s Illinois, the relationships between the wealthy Abbott family and the poor Holt family are intertwined, especially when brothers Jacey and Doug court the beautiful Abbott sisters, but there is a closely guarded secret between the two families.

anchor_text: Doug Holt is 15 years old. He lives with a schoolteacher mother and Jacey, his older brother. Doug is a longtime friend of Pamela Abbott, the daughter of one of the city's wealthiest families. As for Jacey, he decides to start his own business with the rebel Eleanor Abbott in an attempt to climb the social ladder. But Mr. Abbott, the patriarch, doesn't see it that way.
text_a: They share the same birth month, which is why the orphanage calls them the December boys - Map, Spit, Spark and Misty - they have a lot in common. With no hope of being adopted, they form their family obligations. Suddenly an unexpected news that a young couple could adopt only one of them, will turn them into rivals for being chosen.
text_b: The film is set in a small town in Illinois in the year 1957. It tells the story of the lives of five teenagers. The working-class Holt brothers are very different from the beautiful daughters of the Abbott family, an upper-class family with a great reputation in their town. The three beautiful Abbott daughters seem to have it all and are the envy of the Holt brothers. The Holts and the Abbots will bond, facing the challenges of love and sex, unaware that both families are hiding a secret in common.

anchor_text: A crazed killer is terrorizing Crippen High School. Since the murderer cannot be caught, the school must be closed. Years later, a film crew led by director Arthur Lyman Kastleman decides to film the story of the gruesome series of murders. But gradually, during filming, some actors and crew members disappear without a trace. Not only are the police at a loss, but some of them are soon to fall victim to the unknown killer. As two of the actors struggle to get to the bottom of the matter, suspicion arises that the knife-wielding psychopath from back in the day has returned.
text_a: This story, which serves as a parody of action movies, brings together two policemen with very different personalities who will have to work on the same case. An agent from the Los Angeles Police Department (Estévez) has the help of a veteran on the verge of retirement (Jackson) who is fed up with the police bureaucracy that must be followed. The difference of characters and the latent weariness will not prevent both of them from joining forces to solve the murder of the girl from the cookies of the Páramo.[3]
text_b: In 1982, the town of Crippen was rocked by a series of murders at the local high school and the killer was never caught. A few years later Cosmic Pictures, the company of producer Harry Sleerik, has arrived in Crippen to shoot a film as cheaply as possible about the murders. But it looks like the killer is still out there, and the film's cast and crew are disappearing one by one. It's up to alumni Steven Blake and Callie Cassidy to get to the bottom of this.

anchor_text: Lili Heiser, a ski instructor, works at a luxury hotel in the Swiss Alps. She falls in love with a man who goes skiing every morning thinking he is just an ordinary tourist but he turns out to be a prince trying to escape the pressures of the etiquette of royal life.
text_a: In New York City, Doris Walker, an employee of the Macy's department store chain, is desperately looking for someone to play Santa Claus to liven up her store during the holidays. She eventually hires Kris Kringle, a sympathetic old man who claims to be the real Santa Claus. Faced with the scepticism of his employer, but also of his daughter, Susan, Kris decides to go to court to publicly prove his identity.
text_b: In a luxurious hotel in the Swiss Alps, lives and works a young ski instructor, Lili Heiser. The girl falls in love with one of the guests, a tourist who shows up on the slopes every morning to ski with her. In truth, the one who introduces himself as Rudy Miller is, instead, Rudolph, an incognito prince who has left his court to escape the harsh responsibilities of his rank.

anchor_text: Lili Heiser is a ski instructor in a Swiss hotel. She's fallen in love with the shy young man who comes to her class every morning, not knowing it's Prince Rudolph. Little do they know that the world's press is watching their romance unfold. The hotel management takes advantage of the publicity and gives Lili the lead role in a spectacular ice-cream revue. In the end, Lili Heiser becomes Princess Lili and she and Rudolph live happily ever after.
text_a: The young and destitute Lilli tries to steal the wallet from Toni, an employee in a amusement park who has just arrived in Munich, but the man falls in love with her and decides not to report her. Lilli also attracts the attention of Franz, who performs as an acrobat and who offers her to work in the traveling company. On the night of the first performance, Franz asks her to marry him, drawing the wrath of Toni who kills him by faking an accident. Lilli will get him to confess at the risk of her own life, waiting for the happy ending.
text_b: In a luxurious hotel in the Swiss Alps, lives and works a young ski instructor, Lili Heiser. The girl falls in love with one of the guests, a tourist who shows up on the slopes every morning to ski with her. In truth, the one who introduces himself as Rudy Miller is, instead, Rudolph, an incognito prince who has left his court to escape the harsh responsibilities of his rank.

anchor_text: Around 1905, ten-year-old Marcel is to prepare for the examination for high school. As a reward, he'll be staying at the cottage in his beloved hills of Provence. At the suggestion of the mother, the family also spends the weekends in the cottage. Marcel meets the girl Isabelle and falls in love with her. But Isabelle soon sets herself up to rule over him. Lili and Paul are watching the two and informing the parents. After the bitter disappointment of his first love, he turns again to his friend Lili. After a former student who is now a sewer guard illegally gives his father a key to cut their way to their vacation home and they are caught by a supervisor, his father is put in a precarious position. The mother collapses. But the canal keeper can solve the situation with two friends. After five years, the childhood idyll ends with the death of the mother. His childhood friend Lili is killed by a bullet in the war in 1917. His brother Paul, who later worked as a shepherd in the hills, died at the age of 30. When Marcel grows up and buys a castle for filming, he discovers that it is the castle that played an important role in his childhood. This is where they were caught by the supervisor.
text_a: The son of a teacher from Marseille, Marcel Pagnol spent his early years in the school world where he naturally showed himself to be a good student. As a child, during the great holidays of the summer of 1904, he discovered the garrigues surrounding Marseille and the Garlaban, to which he would remain attached for the rest of his life. The old bastide rented by the family becomes the center of a kind of paradise populated by picturesque characters including Lili des Bellons, a young peasant who becomes her friend and initiates her to the mysteries of the hills. His father and uncle engage in hunting and it is a hunting feat, a double of bartavelles, that becomes his father's glory.
text_b: Every weekend and in summer, the young Marcel Pagnol spends his childhood holidays with his family, at the Bastide Neuve de La Treille, in the Garlaban hills, above Marseille. Marcel can't wait to meet his little brother from the hills, Lili from the Bellons. But it's a long way from the Phocian city. Marcel's father, Joseph, then decides to illegally cross the banks of the canal to shorten the walk of several kilometers, which is not in the taste of Augustine, his wife, anxious to cross private property. The Pagnol family is soon caught in the gardens of the Château de la Buzine belonging to a former military man. Marcel soon falls in love with the pretentious Isabelle Cassignol, daughter of a supposedly rich poet on holiday in La Treille. Blinded by love, the young boy, manipulated by Isabelle, soon abandons his family and his friend Lili.

anchor_text: Aliokhine goes into considerations about love, its reasons, taking the example of her servant, the beautiful Pelageya, who loves Nicador the cook, a "mouth of a bitch" who beats her when he is drunk. He tells of his youth, his settling on the family estate, where to redress a critical financial situation, he began to work the land as a farmer. His only trips out of town are to sit on the bench as an honorary justice of the peace. He sympathizes with the deputy chairman of the court Dmitri Luganovich and is immediately attracted to his wife, the young and beautiful Anna. In time, he becomes a familiar of the family, he is uncle Pavel to the children, he loves him, she loves him, but nothing will happen between them, the weight of habits, fear of the unknown. Then there's the separation. Dmitri is appointed in the Urals. At the station, he jumps into the carriage, kisses her and goes home alone. He let go of the love of his life.
text_a: Alëhin Konstantinyč, the landowner encountered in the story The thorn grape, tells his guests Ivan Ivanyč and Burkin, his love story. Returning home after brilliantly completing his university studies, Alëhin realizes the poor economic situation of his family and the need to engage in the management of his agricultural holdings. Forgetting his intellectual interests, he begins to work hard in the countryside, like a farmer. His only recreational opportunities are trips to towns where he serves as an honorary justice of the peace. In the city he strikes up a friendship with Dmitrij Luganovič, the deputy chairman of the district court, who takes him in and invites him to his home. Alëhin then meets Anna Alekseevna, the young and beautiful wife of Dmitry. Alëhin is attracted to Anna and has the impression that his feelings are reciprocated by the woman. Alëhin assiduously attends Dmitrij and Anna, he is well-liked by their young sons, but suppresses his feelings. In fact, he realizes that a possible union with Anna would lead to the unhappiness of Dmitry, Anna herself and their children. Eventually, an event interrupts their courtship. Dmitri is appointed court president in a governorate in the Urals; Anna will follow him to the new headquarters after a period of rest in Crimea for health reasons. Alëhin accompanies Anna to the station. However, he leaves with her; on the train the two kiss passionately; then Alëhin gets off at the first station and walks home alone, without the woman of his life.
text_b: Anna Karenina is the wife of Alexei Karenin, a cold-blooded politician, more interested in his career than satisfying his wife's emotional needs. Called back to Moscow by her brother, Countess Anna meets Countess Vronsky on the night train. Both speak of their children, the Countess Vronsky is the mother of a very handsome young man, Count Vronsky. The latter shows up at the station to take his mother home, and immediately falls in love with Anna. He courts her shamelessly, wounding poor Kitty Scherbatsky, one of his suitors. Following Anna to St. Petersburg, Vronsky lets society know that he is her lover. Russian society begins to talk about the affair, and even Anna's husband finds out about it. More concerned about his political position than his wife's relationship, he puts her in front of a choice, either Vronsky or his family. Anna will eventually decide to give up her family. She will become pregnant with her new mate, but the child will die in childbirth and she herself will risk death. Alexei allows Vronsky to visit Anna during her convalescence. Embarrassed by the scandal, Vronsky tries to kill himself, but fails. Anna tries to live with Alexei again, but eventually leaves him and goes to live in Italy with Vronsky. He will also leave Vronsky and eventually realizing that he has lost everything, he commits suicide by going on the tracks and letting himself be hit and run over by a train.

anchor_text: Occasionally, Alyokhin's work on his inherited estate in Sofjino is interrupted by trips to the county town. He had been elected a justice of the peace and in this capacity worked with Luganowicz, the deputy president of the district court. Alyokhin meets and falls in love with Luganowitsch's 22-year-old wife Anna. The "beautiful, kind, intelligent and charming woman" has a six-month-old child. Alyokhin can not forget Anna at home in Sofino. On recent visits to the city, he is often seen at the Luganowitsch House. Gifts from the Luganowitschs are presented to the despite incessant work in Sofjino but actually poor Alekhine. He returns with gifts of poultry, butter, and flowers. Alyokhin can't understand why Anna fell for the much older Luganowitsch and not him. Alyokhin tells:...we did not confess our love, but hid it shyly and jealously. Years later, Anna already has a second child from her husband, the strange love couple separates forever. During the farewell, both Alekhine and Anna lose the strength they had built up over the years. He hugs and kisses his beloved. Anna presses her face to his chest. He confesses his love and realizes his mistake. Alyokhin did not "begin from the Most High", but had constantly convinced himself that loving a married woman was a sin.
