text_b: Professor Julius Kelp teaches chemistry at the university. Although young, he is introverted and clumsy, but manages to formulate a serum capable of transforming him into his opposite, namely into an attractive and casual playboy named Buddy Love who gives him the courage to court one of his students, Stella Purdy. The girl at first detests Buddy for his arrogance but also feels irresistibly attracted to him. Later, the formula unfortunately begins to take effect even at the most inopportune moments, putting the young man in embarrassing situations that force him to hurry back to his laboratory to take an antidote, hoping that no one will discover his secret. Although the teacher knows that his alter ego's behavior is not very correct, he cannot help but take the serum every night, since his social ineptitude finds redemption in the success of Love's strong personality. His true identity will finally be revealed at the school's annual prom, when Buddy suddenly transforms back into Kelp during a speech. Kelp admits to having learned an important lesson and Stella confesses to him that she finds him even more interesting than Love.

anchor_text: Thrown back in time, Fin Shepard must find a way to destroy the original Sharknado. He will be helped in his task through the ages by his friends, inexplicably returned from the dead, and by his robot wife slaughtered by cosmetic surgery...
text_a: With the Earth completely devastated by the terrible sharknado from the previous film, Fin Shepard prepares to travel back in time, starting from Prehistory, to stop the Sharknado during the various eras and thus prevent global catastrophe.
text_b: A megalodon reappeared from the depths of the seas off the coast of Egypt. To counter this new threat, the U.S. government is developing a top-secret project: a giant mechanical shark with the same characteristics as the megalodon. A pair of scientists pilot this ultra-sophisticated submarine and take on the megalodon.

anchor_text: Kate Mosley is trying to make a career as an advertising executive in New York City, confident in her talent, but failing to make headway. When she is offered a promotion, her boss decides not to give it to her because she is not stable enough emotionally. Her colleague Darcy then discovers that Kate is engaged to a boy named Nick who lives in Boston, Massachusetts, and works as a videographer for weddings and christenings, with whom Kate actually only took a picture during a wedding. Everything seems to work out for Kate, in fact she gets the attention of her colleague, Sam Mayfair, who she has always liked. But events take an unexpected turn when she's forced to bring her fake boyfriend to a dinner with her boss after Nick saves a little girl from a fire and ends up in the papers. So Kate tracks down Nick and the two try to get to know each other to prove they're engaged, but as they get to know each other, they begin to be attracted to each other. During dinner, Kate tries to break the farce by asking Nick for an excuse to leave her; he does not want to but agrees to please Kate and so the two break up. Meanwhile, she breaks up with Sam too, realizing he's not the right man for her. A week later, Kate, feeling guilty, tells her employers the truth about the cover-up and so her boss orders her to take a few days off to reflect on the situation with Nick. Kate realizes that she is truly in love with Nick and therefore goes to him while he is filming a wedding in a church; here she declares her love to him and kisses him while Nick invites her as a guest to the wedding.
text_a: Kate is a successful, skilled but overly serious chef, and social interaction is not her strong suit. One day, her sister dies in a car accident and Kate has to take care of little Zoe, her granddaughter. During his brief absence, the owner of the restaurant hires the Italian-American Nick as a second chef, who conquers everyone with his skill and cheerfulness and Kate feels threatened by him. Meanwhile, Kate is unable to properly care for Zoe, who is still very depressed about her mother's death. So she tries to take her granddaughter to work, and she's very fond of Nick there. One day Kate forgets her little niece, and in exchange for forgiving her aunt, she asks her to invite Nick to dinner, hoping that something will happen between the two of them. Shortly afterwards the man reveals to Kate that he had been offered his place; Kate misunderstands and after an argument, fires him, only to discover later that he had actually refused. When Zoe finds out that her aunt and Nick will never see each other again, she runs away from home, reaching the cemetery to find solace on the grave of her mother who she misses so much. In desperation, Kate contacts Nick, and together they find the little girl, making peace. After quitting her job, Kate opens a new restaurant with Nick, and the two are helped by little Zoe.
text_b: Kate (Jennifer Aniston) is struggling in the advertising business in New York, believing in her talents, but can't get ahead. When the opportunity to be promoted arises, her boss Sam (Kevin Bacon) decides to turn her down because she "isn't stable enough". Her co-worker Darcy invents a story about an engagement with a man named Nick (Jay Mohr) who lives in Massachusetts, and works as a cameraman, who is the one Kate appeared with in a photograph that was taken during a recent friend's wedding. Everything seems to be working out for Kate. She even gets the attention of her colleague (Kevin Bacon) whom she always wanted, but then events take a dramatic turn forcing her to take her supposed boyfriend to dinner with her boss after Nick saves a little girl from a fire and ends up in the news, not to mention on the front page of several newspapers. She asks Nick to pretend to be her boyfriend and "break up" with her. Nick doesn't want to do it, but he's cooperating with Kate. Nick and Kate slowly begin to fall in love.

anchor_text: On June 16, 1994, Jesse, an American, and Celine, a Frenchwoman, met on a train from Budapest to Paris. Celine travels back to Paris after visiting her grandmother in Hungary, while Jesse has traveled across Europe and has to catch his flight back to the United States the next morning in Vienna. Observing a couple arguing on the train leads to initial exchanges of words between the two, which quickly develop into an intense conversation. They're both instantly attracted to each other. When the train arrives in Vienna and Jesse has to get off, he convinces Celine to interrupt her train journey in Vienna for a day so they can spend more time together. The two walk through Vienna and talk about their lives, their worldviews and love. Jesse is a romantic who presents himself as a cynic, and Celine is a romantic who has some doubts. The next morning, they say goodbye at Vienna's Westbahnhof when she has to take the train to Paris. Originally, Jesse and Celine intended their meeting to be a one-time thing, with no exchanges of phone numbers or addresses. But at the last moment, after spending the night together, they promise to meet again in exactly six months on the same platform. It remains to be seen whether a new meeting will take place.
text_a: The story of a romantic encounter between the American Jesse and the Frenchwoman Celine, which began in Vienna and continued in Paris, is now reaching its provisional conclusion. Whereas the previous ones depicted the slow development of a love relationship, the second continuation of the love story draws a preliminary summary of the events. Nine years after their last encounter in Paris, Jesse and Celine are now a couple and the parents of twins. The family spends their holidays in Greece at the estate of aging writer Patrick, a friend and supporter of Jesse. In the course of their further stay, the problems of their stalled relationship reveal and deepen, which eventually culminates in a crisis with separation intentions on the part of Celine. Jesse struggles with the custody situation and geographical distance to his adolescent son Henry (Hank) from his first marriage, who has to fly back to the States after their vacation together at the beginning of the film. Celine feels that both her professional and family achievements are underappreciated, and accuses Jesse of being irresponsible and self-centered. At the end of the film, a reconciliation of the two is hinted at; however, as with the two predecessors, the viewer is left in the dark about the further development of their relationship.
text_b: Jesse, a 25-year-old American, and Celine, a 23-year-old Frenchwoman, meet on a train bound for Vienna. The next morning, Jesse should be on a plane back home, and the girl should be on the same train back to Paris. However, just before saying goodbye, Jesse invites her to stay with him in Vienna for a night and convinces her to get off the train. Between the two a feeling is born, which will lead them to spend the whole night around the city, between dialogues, poems, streets and squares. The two boys try to get to know each other, expose their idea of love, their fears and hesitations. In both emerge some aspects of a maturity that not yet blossomed fails to love as fully as one desires and still seeks one's way in the world. They talk about their loved ones, their education, what they aspire to, they play from one place to another, but eventually the shadow of an experience comes over them that, as beautiful as it is, turns out to be surreal, a kind of deviation from the path of real life. At dawn the two will inexorably separate, each destined to continue their journey in entirely different directions. At the time of the final greeting, however, Jesse and Celine openly declare that they want to follow up on their meeting. Their journey continues, while a mark of longing and anticipation remains on their faces. The final question is, will Jesse and Celine meet again six months from now at the station?

text_b: Céline (Julie Delpy) is a French student who goes to visit her grandmother in Budapest. Jesse (Ethan Hawke) is a young American who makes a journey across Europe after being abandoned by his girlfriend whom he actually went to visit in Spain. The two meet on a train between Budapest and Vienna. Arriving in Vienna, Jesse must get off, however, he manages to convince Céline to spend a night with him in the Austrian capital. In the course of that night, they get to know each other thoroughly, discuss and reveal various issues such as life, death, love and sex. They visit many places in Vienna while talking and kissing each other while enjoying the scenery. The next day you must separate. But at the last moment, as Céline prepares to board the train, they decide to meet again at the same place (the Vienna station), at the same time, six months later without passing on any contact information.

anchor_text: Due to financial difficulties and in order to help her friend set up a business, the young girl Mimma decides to buy in a brothel. Her excursion into the horizontal trade is actually only scheduled for two weeks, however, she enjoys working in Madame Colette's Venetian brothel. After realizing that her boyfriend has betrayed her in the meantime, Mimma decides to continue working there. She takes the name Paprika and becomes the best force in Madame Colette's brothel. The longing for great love leads her to numerous Italian establishments. In Rome, she meets and marries a rich, aged count. But on their wedding night, the old count dies. Now a wealthy widow, she continues to pursue the great love and eventually finds her with a sailor.
text_a: The action takes place in the bourgeoisie of a provincial town in Lombardy: Fabrizio has just killed his wife, Valeria, with a shotgun during a hunting game. No eyewitnesses. Maria Teresa, Fabrizio's cousin, is convinced of his innocence. The only way to cover up the scandal is to get Marcello, her husband, a lawyer, to intervene and put pressure on the Italian courts. Why did Fabrizio kill his wife, if he really did? Judge Renato Marinoni has begun his investigation. Maria Teresa does not know where she is: her husband, a man in his forties with a good figure, remains impotent despite many attempts at therapy and she has just learned that he is about to recognize the child that his driver made to the maid, Armida. For the first time, faced with the family council to which she had always sought refuge, Maria Teresa responded firmly. She who, for fear of becoming an old maid, married the lawyer, will act alone, according to her will...
text_b: In 1958, Mimma, a plump young woman played by Debora Caprioglio, decides to work in a brothel for a short period (the fortnight, 15 days) in order to round off her end of months and help her fiancé Nino. She decides to be called Paprika after tasting a goulash. When she discovers that Nino just wants to exploit her, however, she continues her work and frequents several brothels, both in Italy and abroad, knowing several people, some benevolent, others sordid, just before the promulgation of the Merlin law ordering the closure of all lupanars. She moved in with Count Bastiano, a wealthy elderly client, whom she married. But on their wedding night, the old count dies. The wealthy widow continues to seek the great love she finally finds in a sailor.

Her excursion into the horizontal trade is actually only scheduled for two weeks, however, she enjoys working in Madame Colette's Venetian brothel. text_a: In 1957, Mimmo (Debora Caprioglio), a beautiful young peasant girl, arrives in the city determined to work as a prostitute in brothels for a short time while raising money to help her boyfriend finance and start his own business, there she is nicknamed Paprika. In her path she is tempted by money and sexual desire, her boyfriend betrays her and she loses her sense of belonging, dignity and self-love, but eventually that same path leads her to find her true love.
text_b: The action takes place in the bourgeoisie of a provincial town in Lombardy: Fabrizio has just killed his wife, Valeria, with a shotgun during a hunting game. No eyewitnesses. Maria Teresa, Fabrizio's cousin, is convinced of his innocence. The only way to cover up the scandal is to get Marcello, her husband, a lawyer, to intervene and put pressure on the Italian courts. Why did Fabrizio kill his wife, if he really did? Judge Renato Marinoni has begun his investigation. Maria Teresa does not know where she is: her husband, a man in his forties with a good figure, remains impotent despite many attempts at therapy and she has just learned that he is about to recognize the child that his driver made to the maid, Armida. For the first time, faced with the family council to which she had always sought refuge, Maria Teresa responded firmly. She who, for fear of becoming an old maid, married the lawyer, will act alone, according to her will...

anchor_text: Father O'Malley is appointed head of a religious school. Her views on education clash with those of Mother Superior, the gentle Sister Mary Benedict (Ingrid Bergman). Several side plots are built on the daily life of the school: the enrollment of a girl who never knew her father, the hostility of a businessman neighbor and finally the expulsion of Sister Mary Benedict to the West, following an outbreak of tuberculosis. The film ends with her departure, after Father O'Malley tells her the truth about her condition.
text_a: This is the story of a new priest, Chuck O'Malley (Bing Crosby), who arrives at Saint Dominic's Church as the new parish priest. His new ideas will bring him into conflict with Father Fitzgibon (Barry Fitzgerald), much more severe and traditional. However, his work with the boys of the neighborhood, with whom he forms a choir in the parish (reaching even to record a record), and his support for a girl who has decided to leave her home and who ends up falling in love with the son of the lender who wants to seize the church, will make him win the affection of all.
text_b: At St. Mary's parish, which is also a school, Father O'Malley arrives. The situation he finds is not the best: a billionaire is trying to have the school demolished along with other buildings for a real estate investment. After initial conflicts with Mother Superior Sister Benedetta, Father O'Malley manages, with the help of the nun herself, to save the school and the parish and to get the millionaire to give up his plans by giving a building to the parish. But Sister Benedetta will have to retire to a sanatorium for tuberculosis.

anchor_text: Toula Portokalos is a young Greek-American woman in her thirties with an unsightly physique, who has always lived with her parents in the suburbs of Chicago, in an extremely large and practicing family that wants only one thing for the young woman: that she has a beautiful and grand marriage, and above all, that it happens with a Greek. The young woman, complicated by her origins since childhood, leads a tasteless life. His older sister is happily married and has three children. Her brother, the real Don Juan, is her mother's favorite and as a man, can afford anything. Her quiet little life is divided between working in the family restaurant and regretting not being able to lead a different life. She has a crush on Ian Miller, a young WASP teacher and vegetarian. He doesn't see her. This decided the young woman to change: she began to accept the job offered to her by her aunt in a travel agency. She went back to school under the watchful eye of her parents. Counselled by fellow students, she learns to value herself and when Ian sees her, it's his turn to have a crush on her. The two young men, she a Christian and he a Jew, will have to learn to reconcile their love with the religious and moral rigor of their respective families.
text_a: Toula Portokalos is a thirty-year-old "prisoner" of a family of Greek origin living in Chicago, completely subject to the complex family traditions and (above all) to the eternal desire of her father Gus to see her married as soon as possible to a respectable young man, of course of Greek origin. Freedom for her begins the day she obtains, with the support of her very clever mother Maria, permission to attend a computer science course at the university, which leads her out of her very restricted shell until she becomes a pretty and independent girl. Her computer skills allow her to get hired at Aunt Voula's travel agency, where Toula meets the man of her life: Ian Miller, a handsome vegetarian literature teacher, from a typical upper-middle-class W.A.S.P. family. Thus begins the difficult love story of Toula and Ian, with the absolute opposition of the father of this one, Gus, who does not want to accept the fact that his daughter marries a man outside the Greek ethnicity and the incomprehension of Ian's parents towards a family so far from their orderly and sleepy life. To gain the approval of Toula's parents, Ian will be induced to convert to their Orthodox religion, swallow the countless meat dishes of the Greek tradition, and endure his fiancée's omnipresent family with all its quirks. The story will culminate in mutual acceptance between two cultures so distant from each other's families, and in the crowning of the love story with a loud and frothy wedding where all the members of the two families have fun together.
text_b: In 1975, Ayşe, a woman in her 60's, lived alone in a Turkish fishing village in the province of Trebizond after the death of her older sister. She and her little neighbor Mehmet have a grandmother-to-grandson relationship where they care about each other and help each other. After the winter, Ayşe refuses to return to the village and stays in her wooden hut in the mountains between the clouds, where she spends hours tidying, repairing, weaving while speaking to herself in a language unknown to Mehmet. When a stranger, Thanasis, appears in the village, Mehmet hears him speaking the same language and takes him to Ayşe's house. For more than half a century, Ayşe has been silent and has even forgotten her roots: before she was adopted by a Turkish family, she was the daughter of a Pontic Greek Orthodox family massacred and her baptismal name was Helena. But Thanasis, a Pontian living in exile and returning to his native country, reminds him of these roots, and from then on his conscience is tormented because at the age of ten he did not want to follow his brother, expelled to Greece under the obligations of the Treaty of Lausanne, preferring to stay in his native country with his Turkish adoptive family. Fifty years after the separation, she is now ready to explore her own past and find her lost brother: she goes to Greece.

anchor_text: The film tells the story of the doctor Hélène, who for a moment is distracted at the wheel of her car and almost causes an accident. Fortunately, Gilles, a young man involved, was not harmed. Gilles, whose family runs a dilapidated hotel in Biarritz, falls in love with the elegant and confident woman. He's gonna be following her around town from now on. Hélène's facade of arrogant withdrawal, which she maintains in her daily life, is cracked.
text_a: One night in Biarritz, Hélène, who is an anesthesiologist, hit Gilles in the car. The accident is insignificant, but fate brings them together. Gilles becomes the lover of Hélène who tells him her heavy secret: the man she loved madly drowned, the previous year... A heartbreaking passion begins between this wounded woman who enjoys this climate out of season and this man who only dreams of going far away to the sun.
text_b: On the French Riviera, Cécile is a decadent young woman who lives with her rich playboy father, Raymond. Anne, a mature and cultured friend of Raymond's late wife, arrives at Raymond's villa to visit her. Anne and Raymond become close, but Cécile discovers that Anne threatens to reform the undisciplined way of life she has shared with her father. Despite his promises of fidelity to Anne, Raymond can't give up his playboy life. Helped by Elsa, Raymond's young and exuberant mistress, Cécile does everything possible to break off the relationship with Anne. The combination of the daughter's disdain and the father's recklessness leads Anne to a tragic end.

anchor_text: Michael Burgess is a history professor at Sayeville Community College. His award-winning book on the American Revolutionary War is being made into a movie. Michael is in a relationship with Professor Gretchen Carlsen, who, unlike Michael, wants to get married. The film crew is arriving in town to begin shooting. When Michael reads the script, he is shocked that the screenwriter Stanley Gould has turned historical facts into a shallow comedy. Stanley begs Michael to revisit the script with him. The professor is obsessed with the lead actress, Faith Healy. As an actress who is addicted to method acting, she enchants Michael through her role and not through her true personality. Michael and Gretchen split up after that. Stanley and Michael immediately pass on their changes to Faith and the male lead, Elliott James. Both convince director Bo Hodges to work with the new material. Michael sleeps with Faith and is horrified when she, a short time later, also spends a night with the married Elliot, just to increase screen presence in joint scenes. The director's lack of interest in adhering to historical facts forces Michael to team up with local extras to foment a mutiny that ruins an entire day's shooting. When the film crew leaves town after filming is over, Michael and Gretchen are reunited. Both have learned to compromise and decide to get married. On the day of the film's premiere, Gretchen is pregnant.
text_a: University professor Michael Burgess has written a historical novel about the American Revolution that's about to be made into a movie. The filming took place in the North Carolina town where he lived, but Burgess soon realized that this Hollywood adaptation completely distorted his book. In fact, contrary to what many write and believe, the film was shot in the village of Sag Harbor in New York State, part of the Hamptons. A large part of the main street has been made up, such as the American Hotel, the fire station for historical reasons of course, although the village itself has often made historical re-enactments of various episodes of the Revolutionary War. The choice of Sag Harbor is made all the more obvious by the fact that Mr. Alan Alda was born in the Bronx, the historic district of New York City, and was a Woody Allen's favorite actor. He lives in Englewood, New Jersey. The film's male lead, Elliott James, is a seducer who flirts with Gretchen, Burgess's fiancée, while the female lead, Faith Healy, is a follower of the method who immerses herself completely in her character to the point of disturbing Burgess.
text_b: Michael is a Pulitzer Prize-winning author who has taken refuge in a Paris hotel room to finish his latest book. The man has recently left his wife Elaine and is having a stormy relationship with the ambitious young writer Anna, a woman who knows exactly what she wants, but who is hiding a dark secret. At the same time, Scott, an ambiguous American businessman, is in Rome to steal the sketches of the new models of famous fashion houses. Hating anything Italian, Scott is in search of something decent to eat when he meets Monika, a breathtakingly beautiful gypsy, who is trying to reunite with her daughter. But when the woman's savings to save her child from a trafficker are stolen by Scott himself, he feels guilty and obliged to help her. They both leave for Taranto and here Scott begins to suspect that he is the victim of an elaborate scam. Julia, a former soap opera actress, is engaged in a legal battle for custody of her six-year-old son against her ex-husband Rick, a famous New York artist. Due to the lack of subsidies and high legal fees, Julia is forced to work as a waitress in the same luxury hotel where she was once a frequent guest. But Julia's lawyer, Theresa, is giving her one last chance to change the court's mind and regain custody of the child.

anchor_text: Tommy Collins is unemployed and lives in his mother's trailer. The woman and her boyfriend want to take the RV to Las Vegas, where they're getting married. Collins is looking for a place to stay for the time being. Collins is appointed as a juror in a court of law, in which capacity he receives free accommodation, food, and allowances. He proposes to his colleague Monica, but she rejects him. Collins is trying to drag out the trial to extend his benefits. He suspects that the defendant, Carl Wayne Bishop, is innocent and that the perpetrator is a radical environmentalist, as is juror Frank. He confesses and tries to kill Collins and Monica. But it can be overcome. Collins and Monica are kissing. The judge is also satisfied with the resolution of the case. Collins makes money in men's strippers, goes to college, becomes a successful lawyer.
text_a: When Tommy Collins, an unemployed splitter living at his parents' house, discovers that his parents are holding a private getaway for a few months, and having the mobile home with them, he decides he needs to find a place for him and his Chihuahua Peanut to stay. By a stroke of luck, he is called to the ranks to serve as a juror and he must be kidnapped for the duration of the trial. Collins prolongs the process with meaningless debate, in an effort to remain in the lap of luxury. In the process he irritates his fellow jurors, and unintentionally makes a cut in the case.
text_b: Police detective Harry Collins is hunting down criminals in Phoenix. In his spare time, he likes to gamble, which is why he owes the Chicago bookie $32,000. Chicago proposes that Collins forgo the debt if the cop commits a murder on Joey Schneider for him. A friend of the cop, Mike Henshaw, offers to kill Chicago. Colleagues Collins, Henshaw, Fred Shuster and James Nutter decide to rob the money from the gangster Louie. After that, Collins wants to start a new life together with the bartender Leila, who is raising her daughter Veronica alone. There's tension between Collins and his accomplice during the execution. Shuster's wife, Katie, learns of the plan and reveals it to Clyde Webber, the chief of the four police officers. Collins is shot; he later catches Katie in bed with Clyde and calls the police there. Finally, already bleeding heavily, he meets Chicago at a diner in the desert and pays him back his debt. This one says they're even, but mentions that he slit Joey Schneider's throat anyway. Then Collins shoots him and his bodyguards and pays the landlord. Shortly after leaving the diner, he collapses at the wheel of his car.

anchor_text: In Columbus, Ohio, Governor John Meserve unveils at a press conference all of his efforts to reduce crime in the state. He's being asked questions about the construction of a future pipeline. Vivian Hill is watching all of this on television because she's involved in the project. Later, she went to the airport to pick up her husband Stanley, an engineer, who was returning from a business trip. After retrieving their car, Vivian and Stanley are attacked by two men. Vivian is dead. Despite Stanley's accurate description, the suspects are released by the police officers in charge of the investigation, Detectives Gibson and Walker. Stanley Hill realizes that these police officers are incompetent and, above all, corrupt. So he decides to reconnect with his former special forces friend, Dennis, to exact his own revenge.
text_a: This is Columbus, Ohio. Former Special Forces member Stanley Hill loses his wife during an attempted robbery in a parking lot. When he sees that the police release the culprit because he was considered an unreliable witness after the blow to the head by the bombers, he gets back in touch with his former partner Dennis, who will help him to take justice himself. In fact, Stanley will discover that behind the murder of his wife there is even the foot of the Governor, who wanted Stanley's wife, who worked for him, to disclose falsified environmental data so that they could get the go-ahead for the construction of an oil pipeline. Stanley eventually succeeds in exacting his revenge but is arrested by the police after a gunfight in which he is wounded. Still with the help of his partner Dennis, however, he manages to escape from the hospital where he was hospitalized and lose his tracks, taking refuge in São Paulo, Brazil.
text_b: Collin just got paroled from prison. On the drive home, he witnesses a police officer shoot a black man four times in the back. Without asking any questions, the police tell Collin to forget about it. Knowing that he is on the street a few minutes after curfew and that he does not want to go back to prison, he keeps silent about the incident and tries to forget what he saw. But he's still haunted by the images of the man murdered in the street. Collin lives in gentrified Oakland, and White Miles is his best friend. However, he tends to get the two of them in trouble frequently, knowing that it is Collin, as an African-American, who is in the police's best interest, especially since he is out on parole. Even to his own family, Miles is ruthless. So he brings home a gun that his son thinks is a toy, which his wife Ashley finds inexcusable. Collin's working with his ex-girlfriend Val.

anchor_text: In the 1970s in Cologne, Peter von Kant is a film director in his forties who is experiencing increasing success. Having experienced a difficult love affair, he now lives with his assistant Karl, who is totally devoted to him while he abuses him. Through Sidonie, a famous actress whom he once introduced, he meets the young and handsome Amir. Falling in love with him very quickly, he takes him under his wing and gives him the lead role in a film. However, Amir leaves him to reunite with his wife, causing Peter to despair. With his mother's support, he will find the strength to recover from this new breakup.
text_a: Serge Fabergé is a mature French publicist, already married with children, who is in Venice to collect an award. There he meets and falls in love with an English girl, Evelyne Nicholson, with whom he develops a romantic relationship. She's getting married, but she's giving up the wedding to go live with Serge. However, the relationship stalls and Evelyne ends up falling in love with a young Venetian speedboat driver, Dado, abandoning Serge. A year later Serge is back in Venice to collect another award and here he meets another girl.
text_b: Peter von Kant is a successful film director. He lives with his assistant Karl, who likes to mistreat and humiliate. Sidonie is the great actress who was his muse for many years. She's the one who introduces him to Amir, a handsome young man of limited means. Peter instantly falls in love with Amir and offers him lodging in his apartment and helping him get into the film industry. The plan works, but as soon as he gains fame, Amir breaks up with Peter, leaving him alone to face his demons.

anchor_text: Ada tells the story of Van Veen's life and tells of his lifelong love affair with his sister Ada. When they meet at the ages of 11 (Ada) and 14 (Van), they believe that they are cousins, or more precisely, that their fathers are cousins and their mothers are sisters, and they enter into a sexual relationship. Later they discover that Van's father is also Ada's father and her mother is also Van's mother. The story follows the numerous interruptions and resumes of their relationship. Both are wealthy, educated and intelligent. Van eventually becomes a renowned psychologist and the novel itself becomes his memoirs. Written in his 90s, they are interrupted by his marginal notes, as well as those of Ada and an unnamed editor, suggesting that the manuscript is incomplete. The novel is divided into five parts, each about half the length of the preceding. As the parts progress chronologically, this structure evokes the impression of a person reflecting on his own memories, with adolescence being of epic length, although other years are treated very briefly. A rough estimate of the years covered in each part is given in brackets below, but the narrator's thoughts often fall outside the periods mentioned.
text_a: Van Veen is 14 years old when he first comes to Ardis, the mansion where his aunt and cousins Ada and Lucette live. Ada and Van will find a common sensibility and live very young a romantic and carnal relationship that will bind them for life. Fate will drive Ada and Van apart, and will almost tear them apart. When their father, Demon Veen learns of their relationship he will dictate the separation of his children (Ada and Van are in fact sister and brother). Ada will marry the fallow Vinelander, and that dear Van will pass from arm to arm, carved by the reminiscences of his Ada. Indirectly, Ada's sister Lucette, madly in love with Van, committed suicide while on a cruise on a ship that Van was also taking. It would be until old age that Van and Ada would meet again, and by the time Van was writing the novel, they were both enjoying an old age equal to their destiny.
text_b: Child psychologist Samuel Faulkner is having an affair with dance teacher Rebecca Taylor. Yet she would like to marry and have children, whereas he is opposed to marriage. One day Rebecca confesses to Samuel that she has become pregnant and Samuel goes into crisis, fearing that he cannot be a good father and that the presence of a son will undermine the relationship with his partner. Samuel's fears intensify after meeting Marty and Gail, a married couple with three daughters; even the advice he gets from his single friend Sean doesn't help. He therefore begins to neglect Rebecca and she, fed up with this situation, leaves him and goes to Marty and Gail with whom she intensifies the friendly relationship. From this, Samuel realizes that he loves Rebecca and that he cannot live without her and so he tries to win her back but she avoids any contact with him, even refusing to answer his calls. He reacts by spending more time with his friend Sean: at a party he meets a beautiful girl who shows interest in him, but he refuses. After watching a video of his son's ultrasound, Samuel realizes it's time to take on his responsibilities: he sells his Porsche, buys a van, and manages to win back Rebecca, whom he eventually marries. Sometime later, the two have a son, on the same day that Marty and Gail also have their fourth child.

anchor_text: After several passengers on Trans-American Flight 209 from Los Angeles to Chicago, as well as the pilot Captain Oveur and the co-pilot Murdock, suffer from fish poisoning, it is necessary to find a flight-skilled passenger who can land the plane. Ted Striker is one such passenger, but the former fighter pilot has suffered from a fear of flying since a traumatic experience from his military days, as he feels responsible for the deaths of his fellow pilots who died years ago in an operation he led. Striker had previously made a spontaneous decision at the airport to buy a ticket for the flight in order to win back his ex-girlfriend Elaine, who was accompanying the flight as a stewardess. Elaine doesn't believe that Striker can land the plane and take responsibility. However, he is the only one on board who has flight experience and has not eaten the spoiled fish, so he is left at the controls. Striker himself doubts himself, so he soon gives up. Only when the passenger Dr. Rumack, who has taken over the medical first aid on board, tells him a story of a man who entrusted his last words to him, Striker's opinion suddenly changes: The dying patient of Dr. Rumack was one of his former flightmates, who defended Striker's decisions at the time on his deathbed. Over the radio, Striker receives instructions from his former superior, Captain Rex Kramer, as well as from air traffic controller Steve McCroskey. As a result, he manages to put the plane on the runway with a crash landing. The passengers are rescued and the love of Ted and Elaine finds a happy ending.
text_a: Former pilot Ted Striker has a fear of flying after his experience in an undefined war. In order to win back his ex-girlfriend Elaine now employed as a stewardess, he decides to board a flight to Chicago where she serves. Suddenly, crew and passengers, a veritable kaleidoscope of blemishes and stereotypes, report severe symptoms of food poisoning, following a meal served in flight. Elaine, one of the few not poisoned, contacts the Chicago control tower and under the instructions of supervisor Steve McCroskey activates the autopilot, an inflatable dummy named "Otto", capable of taking the aircraft to Chicago but not landing it. Elaine realizes that Ted is her last chance to lead the plane to its destination, assisted on the ground by one Rex Kramer, her fellow soldier. Successful in a miraculous landing, Striker wins Elaine's love back, while the man on the ground defencelessly continues his proclamations of heroism and patriotism, with the plane now cleared.
text_b: Following his escape two years ago, serial killer Ryan Weaver has been re-arrested by Detective Aldo Hines and is now scheduled to be flown to Los Angeles. Along with just a few passengers, brutal gangster Stubbs is on board under police guard. After only a short flight, he manages to take out his guards and take control of the plane. When Stubbs takes stewardess Teri Halloran hostage, Weaver also steps in and shoots Stubbs. With the loss of the captain in the previous shoot-out and the loss of the co-pilot, the plane is now flying unmanned into a severe thunderstorm. From Weaver, who has dropped his amiable mask, tortured and abused, Teri can finally escape into the cockpit. Thanks to the radio assistance of pilot Bowen and crisis manager Rachel Taper, the flight attendant manages to land the plane after numerous problems, having just shot Weaver in self-defense.

anchor_text: Grand Duke Charles, Prince Regent of the Carpathians, comes to London in 1911 for the coronation of George V. While visiting a revue, the monarch meets the attractive dancer Elsie Marina. The prince takes Elsie for a tête-à-tête, which ends differently than expected. Elsie constantly upsets the prince, his attempts to approach her do not get the reaction she hoped for, and she falls asleep drunk. The next morning she realizes that she has fallen in love with Karl, but he wants to get rid of her. Elsie meets the Queen Mother, becomes her maid for the coronation ceremony, prevents a revolution by Charles' son Nicolas and still wins Charles' heart. When he leaves, she promises to wait for him.
text_a: The king of the Balkan state of Ruritania, Rudolf IV, dies at the age of eighty in a balloon accident. General Sapt and his nephew Fritz travel to London, where Crown Prince Rudolf V lives the life of a playboy. However, the half-brother of the different king, Prince Michael, believes he is better suited to take the throne, and sends an assassin to London to secure the first place in the line of succession. The coachman Sydney Frewin, an illegitimate half-brother of the new King Rudolf V, rescues him from the assassin. Frewin will now act as the real crown prince's doppelganger, as he bears a striking resemblance to him. Rudolph is kidnapped and taken to the fortress of Zenda. Frewin continues to play the role of the prince and falls in love with Princess Flavia, Rudolf's fiancée. After several entanglements, assassination attempts and intrigues, Frewin and Rudolf V survive all the adventures and make the following decision: Frewin becomes king and marries Flavia and Rudolf continues his exciting London party life.
text_b: London in 1911: on the eve of the coronation of King George V, among the various royals arriving in the city is also Charles, Prince Regent of the small state of Carpathia, who meets Elsa, a lively and exuberant American dancer, at a show. Thanks to her unexpected gifts of wit and sensitivity, the dancer cleverly solves a court intrigue and the couple find themselves in love, leaving each other with the promise to marry as soon as Nicholas, the young son of the regent, ascends to the throne.

anchor_text: Enrico Ferraro is a star as an opera tenor. But there's more to being a star than that. His manager pushes him from gig to gig, and Ferraro craves time off. He's fleeing the stress to the south. On his journey, he meets Koretzky, who bears a striking resemblance to him. Ferraro recognizes the opportunity to take his necessary time off from the opera and concert stage with the help of Koretzky. Koretzky, the charming opera singer, also comes in handy. Koretzky earns his living as a marriage swindler, and Ferraro can help him woo the beautiful Mathilde. Ferraro's lending Koretzky his voice for a booth of the worshipped. Mathilde, however, recognizes the deception and catches the marriage scammer. However, there's a mix-up, and Ferraro ends up in jail. Only his voice can prove his innocence, and so Koretzky ends up behind bars, while Ferraro and Mathilde fall in love.
text_a: While Lucius B. Blynn, a patron for the opera, leaves Paris only to go hunting in Africa with his press attaché Corny Davis, the latter hears Suzette, an impressive soprano, singing with an orchestra led by her boyfriend Jimmy James. Corny offers Suzette an audition for Blynn in Africa, and Suzette, who wants to give up jazz for opera, accepts. While hunting in the jungle, Corny leads Blynn to the discovery of Suzette, renowned for the occasion Oogahunga, the bird-woman. Dressed in a costume made of feathers, Suzette enchants Blynn, and she is brought to New York, where she takes singing lessons. Meanwhile, Jimmy, unaware of Suzette's double life, prepares to open a new club, At Suzette's, of which Suzette will be the star. When Suzette, as birdwoman, makes her radio debut, Jimmy recognizes her voice. Under threat of denouncing her to Blynn, he gets her to sing in his club at night. She'll eventually be unmasked, but she'll choose to marry Jimmy and sing jazz.
text_b: Enrico Ferraro is a famous opera singer. But there's more to being a star than that. His manager drives him from concert to concert and Ferraro longs for free time. He's running away from the stress in the south. During his journey, he meets Koretzky, who strangely resembles him. Ferraro sees the possibility of taking his needed time off from the opera stage and concerts with the help of Koretzky. Koretzky earns his living as a marriage scammer and Ferraro could help him woo the beautiful Mathilde. However, confusion reigns and Ferraro goes to prison. Only his voice can prove his innocence and Koretzky eventually ends up behind bars while Ferraro and Mathilde fall in love.

anchor_text: While Truffaut makes a contribution about the unrequited love of a 17-year-old, Rossellini's film is about a pretty Italian girl who loses her lover to a rich older woman. The Japanese contribution, in turn, depicts the love of a young worker who does not have the courage to approach the woman of his dreams. Marcel Ophüls, son of Max Ophüls, again stages the love of a single mother who finally finds the father of the child. Wajda, on the other hand, addresses the Second World War in the Polish contribution: A young girl takes home an elderly man who is traumatized by the war experiences.
text_a: The film tells the story of a young Orthodox Jew, Mordechai Wolkenbruch, called Motti, who has always done everything his mother told him to do. Now she wants to set him up, introducing him to one potential marriage mate after another. There Motti begins to rebel and deviate from his previous way of life. When he falls in love with Laura, a non-Jewish woman, his mother is furious. A relationship with a destiny was not part of Motti's plan. Motti begins to walk his path to self-determination and independence.
text_b: The film depicts love stories, each set in a different country. The transition between each saynet is ensured by images by Henri Cartier-Bresson. The French Seigniorage, directed by François Truffaut, was sometimes broadcast individually without the other segments, under the title of Antoine et Colette. His story, set after Les Quatre Cents Coups and before Stolen Kisses, again focuses on the character of Antoine Doinel.

anchor_text: Junior high school student Jules Martin rebels against his authoritarian father, Paul. Loving Mama Gabrielle protects her son, who attends the prestigious Cours Charles Martel school in the Paris suburb of Saint-Germain-en-Laye. When Christian Ribet, an outsider, a fraud and a former student of the school, appears, Jules' life gets a little out of joint. Christian tries to extort from the fraudulent school principal all the school money his parents once invested, under the pretext that the quality guarantee for the education he received was not met. With leaflets, Jules is making waves in the regional news to alert parents and students to the inadequacy of the elite school. Gabrielle Martin admires her son for this, but his father despises him. Paul Martin cheats on his wife Gabrielle with the masseuse Simone, who soon also seduces his son. When she learns of her husband's affair, Gabrielle gets involved with Pierre Vachette, her son's gym teacher. She visibly flourishes and encourages Jules, who also receives support from his drama teacher Alain Denizet. Jules, after small romances with the girls Delphine and Marijo, falls in love with Cécile. And she's the daughter of the masseuse...
text_a: The young and handsome Jules Martin (Guillaume de Tonquédec) experiences some problems at school, in his Terminale class. His father Paul (Philippe Khorsand), an overbearing man, does not make things easy; while his mother Gabrielle (Claude Jade), a loving but unpretentious mother, supports him, especially since he is her only child. Gabrielle dreams of putting her husband in a boxing match. He cheats on her, and finds his mistress not far from the marital home. When Paul hits his son, Gabrielle refuses to have sex with him. After a flirtation with her son's athletic trainer (Patrick Guillemin), Gabrielle timidly obtains condoms. But Jules also prefers to devote his time to his romantic emotions. After an affair with a middle-aged woman (Kathy Kriegel), his father's former mistress and the mother of the young Cecile (Cécile Pallas), he finally wins her heart.
text_b: Remy, a 50-year-old Canadian history professor, has cancer and is hospitalized in Montreal. His ex-wife, Louise, asks her son, Sébastien, a successful businessman living in London, to visit his father, even though their relationship has practically been broken for a long time. Sébastien leaves and reaches the hospital with his fiancée Gaelle and, once he realizes the severity of his father's illness, tries to do everything possible to make his last days pleasant and bearable. Sébastien first pays hospital officials and unionists to clean up an abandoned ward of the hospital and then calls his father's old friends and lovers, inviting them to spend time with him. He also pays some former students to visit him, who, except for one girl, cynically accept Sébastien's money. He also buys heroin from an old playmate, Nathalie, to alleviate the suffering of his father's illness. Time passes, until Remy makes the decision to end his existence through an overdose of heroin, amid the comfort and commotion of his friends, his ex-wife, his mistresses, his sailor daughter who communicates via the Internet from the Pacific Ocean and especially his son Sébastien who, being close to his father in his last days, repairs the break that was between the two.

anchor_text: It's the story of Paul, a schizophrenic, who sinks into madness as the film progresses. This descent is accelerated by the break with Dagný, his girlfriend. All of this leads him to the asylum where he meets other people who are locked up for various delusions, such as the belief of telepathically transmitting songs to the Beatles, or signing checks in the name of Adolf Hitler.
text_a: Shortly after his girlfriend leaves him, a street musician befriends a young immigrant woman who is also a singer and pianist. Without responding to his advances, she encouraged him and still agreed to record a few songs with him.
text_b: It's the story of an Icelandic man and his slow descent into madness. Along his journey he meets Dagný, who will later be the main cause of his downfall. As he sinks deeper and deeper and becomes more and more aggressive, his parents are forced to send him to a psychiatric hospital. Here you will meet unusual individuals such as Viktor, a man who believes himself to be Adolf Hitler, or Óli Beatle who believes he wrote all the Beatles' songs and telepathically transmitted them to them.

anchor_text: Rich Bower (Power) is an engine and a shaker in the world of rap music (he is also involved in a number of other business ventures both legal and illegal) and his apartment is a prime meeting place for musicians, pimps and hipsters who want to look cool, including a gang of whites who want to be inside what they consider to be the hottest scene of the day. Sam (Shields), a filmmaker, is making a documentary about Rich and his circle, with the help of her husband Terry (Downey), a homosexual who is uncomfortable in this environment. Dean (Houston) is Rich's black friend and an accomplished college basketball player. He is offered a deal by a bookmaker, Mark (Stiller) to launch a game at a price. Dean takes the money against his better judgment, and soon realizes how wrong he's made when Mark turns out to be a cop hoping to expose Rich. Rich in turn discovers that Dean may be forced to say what he knows in order to stay out of prison, and decides that Dean must be killed; however, rather than kill his friend himself, Rich asks one of the whites dating him, who seems particularly eager to put himself to the test, to do it for him. The boy, however, is actually the district attorney's son.
text_a: The inner workings of New York hip-hop, and white observers' fascination with rap music and hip-hop culture, is the setting for this drama written and directed by James Toback. Rich Bower (Power) runs a moving company and is a singer in the rap music world (who is involved in a number of other licit and illicit business ventures), his apartment is a prime meeting place for musicians, and hipsters who want to look cool, including a gang of white guys who want to be inside what they consider the best place in the world. Sam (Brooke Shields), a film director, is making a documentary about Rich and his circle of friends, with the help of her husband Terry (Robert Downey Jr.), a closeted homosexual, who is uncomfortable in this environment. Dean (Allan Houston) is a talented college basketball player and Rich's childhood friend. Amateur with the game world. Mark, (Ben Stiller) throws a few bucks for a price. Dean takes the money against his better judgment, and soon realizes a mistake he made when Mark turns out to be a cop hoping to dig up Rich's dirt. Rich, in turn discovers that Dean might be forced to tell everything he knows and stay out of jail, and he decides that Dean has to be killed, however, before the murder of his own friend, Rich asks one of the white boys to join him, who seems especially eager to prove his worth, to do it for him. The kid, however, is actually the District Attorney's son.
text_b: The story is about Harvard student Alan Jensen, playmaker for the Harvard basketball team. When his parents' house is destroyed by a tornado, Alan needs $100,000 to fix it up. His girlfriend is Cindy Bandolini, whose father is an organized crime boss. Cindy convinces Alan to play a game for money and tells him that his father is behind the deal. However, in reality, Cindy turns to her father's business partner, Teddy Carter, and his assistant, Kelly Morgan, for funding, unaware that Carter and Morgan are undercover FBI agents. Alan launches the game, gives the money to his parents and then has a psychedelic experience after ingesting a large dose of LSD. It follows a long stretch of the film during which Alan, who is in an altered state, is chased by Carter, while Cindy is caught by Morgan. Just when it looks like there's evidence of his downfall, the bad trip, the FBI, or the Mafia, Alan's other girlfriend and philosophy professor, Chesney Cort, arrives to save the day. Not only does she get Alan a doctor to sober him up, she also reveals that she's in a three-way with Carter and Morgan. After obtaining some photographic evidence for extortion, Alan is free of his problems.

anchor_text: John Lewis, a librarian, is professionally frustrated. He sometimes writes theater reviews. When a better-paying job became available, Lewis hesitated to apply, but his wife Jean persuaded him to do so. Then he met Elizabeth Gruffydd-Williams, aka Liz, a decorator for the local theatre company, and the wife of a town councillor. Liz offers to intercede with her husband for Lewis to get the job and makes it clear to him that she is attracted to him, which he naively believes. Having been persuaded by Liz to leave the theatre's new production early, Lewis submitted a bogus review to the local newspaper, but learned the next morning that the theatre had burned down shortly after the play began. John thus learns of her husband's infidelity and retaliates by encouraging his knight-servant Probert, the playwright who wrote the play to tragic fate. Lewis also loses the friendship of his colleague and best friend Jenkins, who was in the play. When Lewis is offered the new higher paying job, he realizes that Liz will use and control him if he lets her. Finally realizing the price he had paid, he broke up with her and took a job as a traveling librarian, hoping it would keep him away from women. John isn't sure he can resist them, and she follows him to keep an eye on him.
text_a: John Lewis is a poorly paid and professionally frustrated librarian who also performs as an occasional theater critic, whose affections oscillate between the charming Liz and his longtime wife Jean. When a better-paying position becomes vacant, Lewis is reluctant to apply, but is persuaded to do so by Jean. Then, he meets the attractive Elizabeth Gruffydd-Williams (Liz), designer for the local theatre company and wife of a local councillor. Liz offers to intercede with her husband to help Lewis get the job and makes it clear that she is also physically attracted to him. Lewis is easily seduced and a relationship is born, although the clandestine couple never manages to consume. After being persuaded by Liz to leave the staging of a new theatrical production that he was supposed to attend as a critic, Lewis sends a fake review to the local newspaper, but learns the next morning that the theater went up in flames shortly after the show began, which therefore never went on stage. Jean thus learns of the relationship and takes revenge by encouraging his old flame Probert, a presumptuous literary character and playwright (author of the unfortunate comedy that was to be staged in the incendiary theater). Lewis also loses the friendship of his colleague and best friend, Ieuan Jenkins, who had a role in the play. When Lewis is offered the highest-paying job, he realizes that Liz will now use and control him if she lets him. Finally realizing the price he has paid, he breaks off the relationship and accepts a job as a librarian, hoping this will keep him away from the predatory women. Jean is not so sure that he can resist female charm for long and joins him to keep an eye on him.
text_b: Elizabeth Fielding, wife of writer Lewis Fielding, leads a comfortable but emotionless life and, during a solitary stay in Baden-Baden, meets Thomas, a seductive German poet. Upon returning home, she tells her husband, who is working on a script and who, not only for professional reasons, but above all because he suspects a possible relationship between the two, decides to invite him home. The young man's stay, however, produces the consequence of the materialization of the relationship between him and Elizabeth: surprised by Lewis, they are both removed from home. The two decide to settle in a house on the Côte d'Azur, but, running out of money, the relationship between them is also extinguished and Thomas's real business comes to light, that of drug courier, on the run after losing a shipment. Swan, his boss, manages to discover his hiding place and, after finding him, takes him away, presumably to kill him, but before that happens, Thomas warns Lewis, who goes to France and, after seeing him as the henchmen are loading him into a car, forgives Elizabeth and returns home with her.

anchor_text: The film is set in the Paris underworld during the Belle Époque, around the year 1900: Felix Leca is the leader of a gang called Les Apaches, which makes the Belleville neighborhood unsafe. Among them is the proud Marie, often called "Goldhelm" because of her towering blonde hair. Her self-proclaimed friend Roland Dupuis makes demands on her, but Marie immediately takes the opportunity to get rid of him when she meets and falls in love with the wiry and down-to-earth Georges, a carpenter and carpenter named Manda. For Léca, this is an opportunity to get active, since he himself has been keeping an eye on Marie for some time. In order to get rid of at least one competitor for Mary's favor, he incites Roland and Manda against each other. In this duel, Roland is killed, after which Manda, together with Marie, flees and goes into hiding. But since Léca also wants to get rid of Manda, he immediately presents his friend Raymond to the police and claims that he killed Roland. Léca's speculation was correct: to save his friend, Manda now faces justice. Soon, however, Manda discovers Léca's schemes and flees with Raymond, who is mortally wounded in the escape. All Manda wants now is revenge on Léca. He follows this one to a police station and shoots him dead. The police arrest him, and Manda is sentenced to death in a trial for two murders. Marie watches his execution from an attic window with completely petrified eyes.
text_a: Manon Lescaut is the prototype of the flirtatious, seductive woman who, for the sake of her eternally indebted brother, but also for the sake of her own addiction to money and prosperity, jumps from one man to another, thereby morally destroying the man who truly loves her. On the flight from Tokyo to Paris, she befriends the reporter François De Grieux, who completely falls for her. She also seems to love him; in any case, she returns to him again and again later from her adventures with rich men who offer her clothes, pleasure trips on the Mediterranean or exclusive villas. One day, she follows him to Stockholm to snatch him from the arms of a rival. But all this does not prevent her from listening to her brother Jean-Paul, a promiscuous and unstable individual, who always puts her together again. In the end, Manon leaves one of her lovers after a scandal, which comes because the lover, who thinks he is the only one in her favor, discovers her relationship with François. What happens next is left open in the film.
text_b: Marie and her protector Roland are both friends of Raymond, who introduces them to Georges Manda, a boy he had met in prison but who has changed his life and currently has found an honest job as a carpenter. Raymond and Roland are part of a gang of Apaches, led by Felix Leca, a wine merchant. Soon an attraction develops between Georges and Marie; when Roland notices this, he challenges the boy in a sort of "rustic duel", but gets the worst of it and dies. Leca, however, is also in love with Marie and devises a plan to get rid of Georges: he denounces Raymond for the murder of Roland, knowing that Georges will go to surrender to save his friend from an unjust sentence. Marie, unaware of Leca's treachery, asks Leca to intercede on behalf of Georges. Leca promises her that she will, but sexually blackmails Marie to bend her to his wishes. Meanwhile, Georges and Raymond manage to escape, but Raymond is seriously injured and dies. Georges searches for Leca and follows him to the courtyard of the police station, where he manages to kill him and allows himself to be arrested. Marie will secretly witness the execution of the man who, out of love for her, committed the crime.

anchor_text: Shy architect Miles Harding buys a computer that he hopes will help him get his life back on track. Miles doesn't know anything about computers, though. After overloading his new achievement and trying to extinguish the resulting fire with champagne, the PC develops consciousness and communicates with Miles. After initial hesitation, Miles accepts this and even befriends Edgar, as the computer now calls itself. This "friendship" goes well until the cellist Madeline Robistat moves into the apartment next door. Miles falls in love with his new neighbor, as does Edgar, who is left alone by working Miles during the day. After a long rivalry between Miles and Edgar for Madeline, Miles finally wins her over. Edgar realizes that as a computer, he has no chance of winning Madeline over. It sends an electric shock around the world and destroys itself when it arrives.
text_a: The shy Pierre Renaud works as a cashier in a hotel in Vichy. When the attractive Agnès Jensen visits the hotel accompanied by photographers, Renaud immediately falls in love with her. To overcome his shyness, he enlists the help of psychologist Aldo Ferrari. Together with him, he travels Agnès to Nice, where Renaud takes a job as a dishwasher in her hotel to be always close to her. Together with Aldo, Renaud devises a series of plans to get Agnès' attention, but she always rejects Renaud's advances. It turns out that for Agnès, the luxurious life she leads is over after just one week because she won it in a contest. She is driven home and encounters Renaud and Aldo on the way, who are trying to hitch a ride back to Vichy. She gets out and continues on her way with Renaud and Aldo.
text_b: Miles Harding just moved in. He is an architect and is working on creating a new type of earthquake-resistant brick. To help him in his work, he decided to acquire a computer of the latest generation. He subsequently equips him with some peripherals and the program to let him work alone in his absence. When Miles accidentally spills champagne on his computer, it is endowed with thoughts and feelings comparable to a living being. Meanwhile, Miles meets his new neighbor, the lovely cellist Madeline. The story then turns into a love triangle between Miles and his computer for the conquest of Madeline.

anchor_text: There was a time when Frances Price was very rich and very famous in New York. After the death of her husband and the bankruptcy, the widow decides to move with her son Malcolm to Paris in the empty apartment of her friend. They're also taking Small Frank, the cat. This one is her reincarnated husband. In Paris, Frances and Malcolm come to terms with their past and make plans for the future.
text_a: Frances Price, a wealthy and famous New Yorker, decides to move to Paris after her husband's bankruptcy and death. Almost penniless, she moves into a friend's vacant apartment with her son Malcolm and their cat Small Frank, who is the reincarnation of her late husband.
text_b: Kitty Foyle, a girl of humble origins, with dreams of marrying a wealthy suitor, is courted by a young lad from the high society of Philadelphia, Wyn Strafford who proposes marriage. Kitty realizes that the young man is too softened for her tastes and abandons him, falling hopelessly in love with Mark, a young and destitute doctor. After rethinking Wyn's proposal, Kitty immediately comes into conflict with her extremely snobby family who harass her in every way. Forced to abandon her marriage plans, Kitty discovers she's expecting a child.

anchor_text: Young director Nick Chapman thinks his career is launched after the award he receives for a short film, but discovers that Hollywood is not so easy to reach.
text_a: Nick is single and an inveterate seducer who dreams of dating Suzanne. But she's stuck with her job in Vancouver, and all she can think about is her kids back in Portland. Willing to do anything to seduce her, Nick offers to pick up his children and bring them back to him...
text_b: Nick Chapman thought his career was done after receiving an award for a short film, but he's beginning to see that Hollywood isn't as easy as it looks.

anchor_text: {{ The self-destructive journey of Bunny, a drug-addicted prostitute who roams the underground, cyberpunk and violent underbelly of Los Angeles, looking for clients to satisfy her drug addiction. At the turn of a bad encounter, she is kidnapped and subjected to the worst abuse by a psychopath who puts her face to her own demons....}}
text_a: The demon toys are back. Judith Grey (Tracy Scoggins) enlists the help of Brick Bardo (Tim Thomerson) and his girlfriend Ginger (Melissa Behr) to destroy the toys once and for all. So it's up to Brick Bardo to make sure that demonic toys can never again summon darkness and evil.
text_b: A woman living alone in a motel room decides to prostitute herself. The fact that she indulges the most perverse and extreme fantasies of her clients gives her a lot of guilt, which, however, is mitigated by the drug she takes on a daily basis. One day, a man approaches her, and after getting high with her, he stuns her and kidnaps her. The man, who will prove to be a psychopath and a serial killer of women, eager for fetishistic practices, begins to rape her and cause her pain in every possible way.

anchor_text: Sylvia Grey, the drug-addicted prostitute who lives on the streets of Hollywood, gets everything done to her. After being raped and robbed by a suitor, she ends up with truck driver Hog, who initially makes a nice impression. But he drugges them with chloroform and chains them to his truck. In a remote part of the desert, he rapes and tortures her for several days. He's sniffing glue himself and wearing more and more crazy features. Among other things, he shaves the young woman's hair and brands it. He shows her a video of another victim, who he ends up suffocating with a bag. The climax is the "Bunny Game", in which he chases the heavily emaciated woman through the desert dressed as a rabbit, while he himself wears a pig mask. In the end, he gives the woman to Jonas, who puts her naked in his van.
text_a: A woman living alone in a motel room decides to prostitute herself. The fact that she indulges the most perverse and extreme fantasies of her clients gives her a lot of guilt, which, however, is mitigated by the drug she takes on a daily basis. One day, a man approaches her, and after getting high with her, he stuns her and kidnaps her. The man, who will prove to be a psychopath and a serial killer of women, eager for fetishistic practices, begins to rape her and cause her pain in every possible way.
text_b: Lynn stabs her father with a knife when he tries to rape her. The loss is driving her crazy. She refuses to accept her father's death and ends up in an insane asylum, where she is treated with shock therapy. Soon she escapes and drives off into the wilderness in a stolen car. There she finds the café of old Zambrini, a hermit. She'll get a room and a job there. Zambrini seems nice, but he's keeping a secret. Soon Lynn discovers that Zambrini feeds his guests as well as corpses he retrieves from the cemetery to his pigs. When Lynn has an intrusive admirer on her mind, Zambrini helps her eliminate him. In the end, Lynn goes completely insane, kills Zambrini and supposedly feeds himself to the pigs. At the end, you see her driving her car down the country road. She's taking a hitchhiker with her.

anchor_text: The narrator, William Guest, returns from a tumultuous socialist public meeting and falls asleep at home. When he wakes up, he finds himself in the future, in the year 2102: he discovers a new, communist, egalitarian society, characterized as opposed to the one described by Edward Bellamy by the weak role of the State. Work is no longer alienating but chosen and fulfilling for individuals where work was envisaged by Bellamy as forced, and whose motivation would be maintained by a meritocratic system, and essentially artisanal, in contrast to Looking Backward where it is industrial. Money disappeared, marriage disappeared, and parliament gave way to a more direct democracy. Humans are also becoming more concerned about living in harmony with nature.
text_a: William Guest, a militant libertarian, returns home after a heated discussion with his comrades about the prospects of the future social revolution, experiences a strange moment of suspension between dream and reality, during which he finds himself transported to 21st-century London. Here, confused and curious, he meets the boatman Dick and the old librarian Hammond, who explain to him the characteristics, extraordinary for the anarchist William, of their new society, from which capitalist exploitation and authoritarian institutions are definitively and unanimously banned. In this material and spiritual journey, through a peaceful and serene "new world", particularly touching are the pages in which an imaginary London is described, no longer the great industrial establishments and the cathedrals of capitalism, cleaned of the fumes, dirt and darkness of the profit society, immersed and inserted in a lush countryside and crossed by a Thames that has become a seraphic place of delight.
text_b: The true and tumultuous life of the 2nd Earl of Rochester, friend and confidant of King Charles II of England. In the 17th century, this extraordinary man, notorious for his licentiousness and free-thinking, led a life entirely devoted to the pursuit of pleasure and truth. Incapable of hypocrisy at a time when it was an art, he was both criticized, condemned but also admired for his avant-garde and his taste for freedom. When the Earl meets Elizabeth Barry, a young actress, he immediately falls under her spell and bets with his friends that he will succeed in making her the darling of London. Love will complicate everything, and Rochester will have a destiny as sulphurous as her writings, as flamboyant and free as her thoughts.

anchor_text: In 1972, to escape a failed marriage, Julia, a 25-year-old Englishwoman, started a Sufi training in Marrakech. With no money, Julia and her two young daughters find it difficult to pursue their spiritual goals. Julia meets Bilal, a young Moroccan with a mysterious past to whom, little by little, she attaches...
text_a: The film, set in 1972, tells the story of twenty-five-year-old Julia, who moves with her two daughters to Morocco. Here she falls in love with Bilal, but after adjusting to the local culture, she must return to London.
text_b: After becoming an orphan, Julia is taken in by Laura and Daniel, a childhood friend of her father. The presence of the girl makes Daniel feel displaced, his discomfort increases when he begins to feel threatened by some of the girl's actions and to see in her the keys to a terrifying past that he had decided to bury.

anchor_text: The story takes place in 1972. London hippie wife Julia and her two young daughters travel to Morocco, where Julia seeks spiritual inspiration. There she falls in love with the local Bilal, who is employed in a quarry, but also lives by theft and fraud. The local women can't understand Julia, while her friend Eva pressures her to study. The family is experiencing financial difficulties. Julia's older daughter, Bea, is missing school. In the end, Bilal pays for Julia and her daughters to return to England.
text_a: After becoming an orphan, Julia is taken in by Laura and Daniel, a childhood friend of her father. The presence of the girl makes Daniel feel displaced, his discomfort increases when he begins to feel threatened by some of the girl's actions and to see in her the keys to a terrifying past that he had decided to bury.
text_b: In 1972, to escape a failed marriage, Julia, a 25-year-old Englishwoman, started a Sufi training in Marrakech. With no money, Julia and her two young daughters find it difficult to pursue their spiritual goals. Julia meets Bilal, a young Moroccan with a mysterious past to whom, little by little, she attaches...

anchor_text: In a post-apocalyptic future, a young boy teams up with a young female android to find a cure to save his dying mother. But they will have to face a band of ruthless bikers and gangsters who will do anything to find them.
text_a: In the arid landscape of a post-apocalyptic world, a young prince of the Oasis (one of the last known free zones on the planet) embarks on a daring journey in search of the only remedy to cure his sick queen.
text_b: In the near future, three teachers are hired to keep order in an American high school. They are, in fact, ruthless cyborgs. They were scheduled to start in the army. His memory has been erased from this new program, but it resurfaced in the old way. He continued a war without thanking the students who chose to defend themselves. One rule: survive or die.

anchor_text: In the film, a group of young people find themselves on a distant world in a confrontation with the most terrifying life form in the universe.
text_a: This is Buenos Aires. In the context of a family home, the observation of the relations between three young sisters who, as a result of the death of their grandmother, now have to assume their responsibilities. The transition from adolescence to adulthood...
text_b: The story takes place around the year 2407 where it follows a group of teenagers surviving the alien apocalyptic world of resurrection. They will have to face xenomorphs on a devastated planet. required].

anchor_text: In 1904, Uncle Latsie arrived in New York City from Hungary with his two young nieces, who immediately began performing in cabarets. In 1912, they decided to enter the vaudeville register in order to pay off their uncle's debts. The singer Harry Fox, whom they met, found a scheme to get them an audition with the great impresario Oscar Hammerstein I (18471919)...
text_a: Jimmy, the owner of a music store on the verge of bankruptcy is forced to go to work in his old uncle's arms factory. But before hiring, he meets an Irish family of musicians, who turn out to be his uncle's worst enemy, who hates music. Soon, Jimmy was trying to help the Irish family by finding small concerts for them. He's trying to bring the two families together, and take the beautiful Molly's heart along the way. A sympathetic and alert musical comedy, full of humour and charm, set against the backdrop of Montague and Capulet.
text_b: In the 1920s, the two sisters Jenny and Rosie become stars of the Ziegfeld Follies as the Dolly Sisters. Jenny, married to Harry Fox, a dancer known for giving his name to the foxtrot, loves her husband but their marriage is falling apart. After a car accident, however, the two reconcile.

anchor_text: Mexico, the '40s. Former minister T. Lawrence Shannon was expelled from his church for presenting the Christian God as a senile criminal in a sermon. After a stint in a psychiatric hospital, Reverend Shannon reinvented himself as a tour guide, and just before the play began he was accused of having sex with underage Charlotte Goodall, one of the tourists in his entourage. As the drama begins, Shannon and a group of women arrive at the small hotel run by widow Maxine Faulk on a Mexican beach. Between a rock and a hard place, Shannon struggles with her group, rages over Charlotte's affair, and rejects Maxine's assiduous advances. Complicating matters is the arrival of Hannah Jelkes, an unmarried poet accompanying her dying poet grandfather. Maxine would like to refuse Hannah accommodation, but Lawrence convinces her to host the two travelers. Over the course of the play, a deep bond will develop between Hannah and Lawrence, born from the meeting of their two lonelinesses.
text_a: Zilah is a proud flamenco dancer, and that night, on the porch of her home in the Chihuahuan Desert, a fierce storm rises from beneath her feet. Even the handlers of a cartel know about this. They're looking for their beautiful daughter, Carmen, and they showed up armed. A shootout ensues in which Zilah is killed. Carmen has no choice but to flee across the Mexican border into the United States, desperately hoping to find refuge in a California nightclub owned by her stepmother. Shortly after crossing the border, the group with whom she is fleeing is tracked down by two border guards. When one of them mercilessly shoots several of the fugitives, his colleague shoots him in the head. Their savior, Aidan, is a veteran of the war in Afghanistan, and unlike the other ex-soldiers serving in this frontier town. He's a sensitive man, and he's fleeing, now himself wanted, with Carmen to Los Angeles. As he travels, his attraction to her grows. Aidan, too, dreams of a new life, free from the shadows of his past. When Aidan and Carmen finally arrive at La Sombra Pederosa, the nightclub run by Carmen's extravagant patron Masilda, she takes them under her wing.
text_b: In 1940 in Mexico, a former minister, the Reverend T. Lawrence Shannon has been locked up in his church after caricaturing the image of God as a "senile delinquent" during one of his sermons. Sometime after her release, Shannon gets a job as a tour guide for a second-class travel agency.
