Shortly before the play begins, Shannon is accused of a crime of rape against a sixteen-year-old girl, named Charlotte Goodall, who is accompanying her current group of tourists. As the curtain rises, Shannon arrives with a group of women at a cheap hotel on the coast of Mexico, which had been run by her friends, Fred and Maxine Faulk. The former had recently passed away, and Maxine Faulk assumed sole management of the establishment. Shannon tries to cope with his emotional conflicts, caught between the group of tourists, who have turned against him for having sex with the minor, and Maxine, who is interested in him for purely carnal reasons. Adding to this chaotic situation, Hannah Jelka, a single woman, appears with her dying grandfather, Nonno, who despite his condition, aspires to compose his last poem. Jelka, a painter and cartoonist, is soon at Maxine's mercy. Shannon, who exerts considerable influence over Maxine, offers Hannah Jelka shelter at night. The main axis of the work focuses on the deeply human bond that is born between Hannah Jelka and Shannon Lawrence. Like the iguana, captured and tied to a pole by the Mexicans in the play, Hannah and them have reached the end of their rope.

anchor_text: Aurore, 50, divorced, losing her job, preparing to become a grandmother, and coping with the hot flashes of menopause. Fortunately, she can count on her daughters and her best friend.
text_a: Detective François Manéri is hired to investigate a girl, Rachel Siprien, who has been missing for six months. His research will lead him to his best friend, stepfather, ex-boyfriend and acquaintances.
text_b: Aurora, a 50-year-old divorcee, loses her job, prepares to become a grandmother, and has to endure hot flashes due to menopause. Fortunately, she has her daughters and her best friend.

anchor_text: In April 1975, the Lebanese Civil War broke out; Beirut was divided along a line separating the mixed-faith (Muslim-Christian-Jewish) West Beirut from the almost entirely Christian East Beirut. After the creation of the line, Tarek discovers that he lives in West Beirut (Tarek is of Islamic faith). He goes to high school and makes Super 8 movies with his friend Omar. At first the war is taken lightly: the school is closed (because it is in East Beirut no longer accessible to residents of West Beirut), the violence is fascinating, going from West to East is a game, and it is at this early stage that Tarek meets and spends time with May, an orphaned Christian girl who lives in his building. Unlike his son, his worried mother wants to leave the country, but his father refuses because he believes the crisis will abate as it has happened before. During a demonstration, Tarek accidentally ends up in a notorious brothel in the war-torn Zeytouni neighborhood, meeting a legendary lady, Oum Walid, the owner of the brothel, where he will later take Omar and May. Family tensions mount, and later, as Tarek becomes of age, the war will inexorably shift from adventure to national tragedy.
text_a: In April 1975 civil war broke out; Beirut was divided along a line between Muslims and Christians and into East and West Beirut. After the creation of the line, Tarek now lives in West Beirut (the Muslim part) and is in high school, making Super 8 movies with his friend Omar. At first, the war is a joke: the school has closed (and it is located in the east of Beirut, which is no longer accessible to Muslims, as Christians build walls to defend themselves), the violence is fascinating, going from West to East is a game. His mother wants to leave the country, but his father refuses. Tarek spends time with May, a Christian orphan who lives in his building. By accident, Tarek goes to a famous brothel in the Olive District, where he meets his legendary madam, Oum Walid. Tensions between families begin to rise. As young people grow up, war moves inexorably from adventure to national tragedy.
text_b: 2006: Lebanese woman Zeina Nasrueddi grew up in Lebanon but has lived for many years in Dubai with her husband, an architect, and her son Karim. Her son spent the summer with her sister Maha in Lebanon, but then the war between Israel and Lebanon took her by surprise. Zeina immediately traveled to Beirut via Turkey, but she doesn't arrive until after the ceasefire and doesn't find her son. Nor is anyone prepared to take them to southern Lebanon. Eventually, though, Tony, a taxi driver, agrees to drive her for a considerable sum. In their search for Maha and Karim, they encounter both the destruction of war and themselves. Zeina learns that Tony is Christian, whose brother served in the South Lebanese army and now lives in Israel. After all, Zeina needs to know that Maha is dead. Someone claims to have seen Karim taken to a monastery. Zeina is on the phone with her son and she's going to the convent. The boy she meets there may be wearing Karim's jacket, but he is a different person.

anchor_text: On the Italian island of Ischia, a seventeen-year-old orphan girl named Scampolo works as a tourist guide. She also does laundry for Marietta. In the process, she meets the penniless young architect Costa. Although Marietta warns her about the men, a love begins to germinate between Scampolo and him. Costa is only able to stay afloat thanks to the support of his wealthy friend, photographer Andreas Michaelis. He hopes to win a prize in an upcoming architecture competition. Scampolo, who doesn't know about Andreas at first, lends Costa some money so he can pay for the laundry. They approach cautiously, and Costa introduces the inexperienced Scampolo to the upper classes via Andreas' connections. At a party, she dances with the Minister of Housing, and with naïve candor criticizes the false statements of the mayor who bragged to the Minister. When it turns out that because of a postal accident, Costa's designs for the competition have not arrived, Scampolo takes them personally to the Minister. In the end, Costa and Scampolo found each other.
text_a: On the island of Ischia, 17-year-old orphan Scampolo works as a tour guide and for a woman named Marietta. He meets a penniless young architect named Roberto Costa. Although Marietta warns her about the attitude of men, a love begins to germinate between Scampolo and him. Costa can stay afloat only thanks to the support of his wealthy friend, photographer Andreas Michaelis. He hopes to win a prize in an upcoming architecture competition. Scampolo, who doesn't know anything about Andreas at first, lends Costa some money so he can pay for the laundry. They cautiously approach each other, and Costa introduces the inexperienced Scampolo to upper-class people. At a party, she dances with the Minister of Housing and speaks naively against lying in politics. When it is discovered that Costa's designs for the competition have not arrived due to an accident at the post office, Scampolo takes them personally to the minister. Eventually Costa and Scampolo begin a relationship.
text_b: Guido Massaccesi, a Roman banking executive, informed by his sister Oriana that his father Armando is seriously ill, leaves his wife Elisa with his son and leaves in a car, an Alfa Romeo Giulietta, to reach Rosignano Solvay, their hometown. Determined to make the journey in sweet company, Guido goes to visit Jennifer, his mistress whom he has not seen for months. The girl's clear refusal to follow him prompts him to bring with him Anita Watson, a twenty-six-year-old American, who has come to Rome to meet an Italian architect known in Chicago. The girl, unaware of the real reason for the trip, reacts to the bullying of her companion and causes a traffic accident. After a stop in Orbetello, the two bond more and make a trip to the deserted Isola del Giglio. Arriving near Rosignano Solvay where in the meantime Armando has died, assisted by his wife Adelina and sons Teo, Omero and Oriana, the unconscious Guido unloads Anita at a boarding house and joins the family. After befriending the operator Sandra and waiting in vain for an explanation of Guido's strange behavior, Anita accidentally discovers the truth and joins him as the funeral procession is about to leave for the cemetery. The arrival of the American causes a scandal and Guido takes revenge by revealing the presence of Noemi, his father's lover for 18 years. Anita refuses to make peace with Guido and leaves on her own for Pisa.

anchor_text: Italy, probably the late 1940s. Several officers and non-commissioned officers of the Italian Navy meet at a naval base at an invitation. Here it is explained to them that a special enterprise is planned against the Royal Navy in Crete, for which highly qualified personnel are needed. Everyone is volunteering. In a specially prepared training area, the volunteers train in the use of miniature torpedo boats or fast boats of the M.T. type against the British. The boats only carry one torpedo. The tactic is to sneak up on the enemy ships at night and then approach the target at full speed. Shortly before the collision, the speedboat pilots, wearing diving suits, are to jump backwards from the boats. It is expected that the British will then rescue the voluntary shipwrecked. As the training officer emphasizes, this is not a kamikaze enterprise. The unit is being transferred to the Greek island of Leros. But before the operation against Crete, an emergency occurs. An Italian submarine is attacked and sunk by two British fighter-bombers. However, since the wreck is in shallow water, there is a possibility of rescuing part of the crew, who are in an area of the boat separated by bulkheads. In a highly dramatic rescue operation, the members of the command first manage to put an oxygen pipe into the submarine and then open a hatch, so that the crew, who are extremely nervous, can finally get out of the sunken wreck and be rescued. After that, the command attacked Souda Bay at night. Under the most difficult conditions, sea barriers can be overcome. Although they are spotted by the British guards and under heavy fire, the attack on the British units succeeds. The torpedo men who jumped in time are rescued by the British and become prisoners of war.
text_a: The Second World War, the Mediterranean. The Italian Navy has established a special unit, the Decima Flottiglia MAS, which is internally named after the constellation Big Bear. These are so-called torpedo riders; divers who carry out underwater sabotage operations with miniature submarines. The first deployment of the unit will take place in Gibraltar. In neutral Algeciras, Spain, they set up a secret base aboard an Italian merchant ship with the help of Agent Marion, who works as a singer in a nightclub. From here, the torpedo boats will operate against Gibraltar. Although the British tried to prevent the invasion of the divers by constant bombardment of the harbour waters, they succeeded in blowing up Allied merchant ships. A second operation takes place in Alexandria, where they succeed in blowing up a British battleship. After the armistice of 8 September 1943, the unit fought on the side of the Allies. When three members are to be awarded the gold medal for this, a British admiral whose battleship was sunk in Alexandria personally places the medals on their chests.
text_b: Italy, probably in the late 1940's. Several officers and non-commissioned officers of the Regia Marina meet by invitation at a naval base. They are told that a special operation is planned against the Royal Navy in Crete, for which highly trained personnel are needed. They all volunteer. In a specially prepared training area, the volunteers trained to use miniature torpedo boats or M.T. type boats against the British. These boats only carry one torpedo. The tactic involves stealthily approaching enemy ships at night and then moving toward the target at full speed. Just before the collision, the shuttle pilots, who are wearing diving suits, have to jump to the back of the boats. It is expected that the British will then rescue the voluntary shipwrecked. As the training officer points out, this is not a suicide mission. The unit is transferred to the Greek island of Leros. But before the intervention against Crete could take place, an emergency arose. An Italian submarine is attacked and sunk by two British fighter-bombers. But since the shipwreck is in shallow water, it is possible to rescue some of the crew, who are in an area of the ship separated by partitions. During a very nerve-wracking rescue operation, the commandos first manage to install an oxygen pipe in the submarine, then open a hatch, so that the crew, extremely nervous, can finally emerge from the sunken wreckage and be saved. The commando then attacked the bay of La Sude at night. With great difficulty, they manage to cross sea mine barriers. Although they were discovered by the British guard teams and caught under heavy fire, the attack on the British units was successful. The torpedo men who jumped in time are rescued by the British and taken as prisoners of war.

anchor_text: A young woman is abducted by her heterosexual boyfriend to a gay disco. Because no one there is interested in her, she cuts her wrists open in the bathroom. An attentive visitor rescues her and takes her to a pharmacy. She offers him a deal; she'll pay him for it if he looks at her "where I'm not handsome". The gay guy's going along with it. The man accompanies the woman to her apartment in a secluded house. She takes off her clothes and spreads her legs so he can look at her vulva. Through his "neutral" (not heterosexual) gaze, she hopes to find a positive attitude towards her own body. His thoughts as he contemplates are recited by Breillat's voice. After a few nights, they sleep together. The intimacy does not last long because her menstrual blood evokes a childhood trauma in him. The man's hoped-for appreciation fails because he is increasingly disgusted by her vulva and speaks in patriarchal contempt of women. When she's asleep, he paints her lips, labia and anus with red lipstick and then penetrates her anus. Then he puts the stalk of a hack into it. He goes to a bar and leaves the money he received from the woman there. In a dream sequence, he pushes her off the cliffs into the sea.
text_a: In one of the gay clubs, where people mingle without meeting each other and where the movements of the bodies are punctuated by techno music, a man dances without caring about what is going on around him. Stranded in the midst of this community, a young woman is gripped by despair. She goes into the school bathroom and, with a razor blade, slashes open her veins. A stranger enters the premises at this moment and rescues the unfortunate woman. After recovering, the young woman gives a blowjob to her rescuer and then offers him a strange deal: in exchange for payment, he must, for four nights, look at her where she should not be looked at. In fact, she pays him to look at her and listen to her talk about her, her desires, the female body, and the fears she arouses in men. Despite his initial aversion, man participates physically in this contract.
text_b: Frédéric is a partner in a small but successful law firm in Paris. His wife Hélène is an English teacher and is expecting her second child. Although he loves her, the young family man feels constrained by marriage. He regularly takes the early afternoon off to indulge his daydreams in a café. These are mostly about women he watches on the street and finds all beautiful since they are unattainable for him. One of his fantasies is to possess a magical amulet that will turn any woman to his will. One day an old acquaintance appears in his office, Chloé, who once twisted the head of a childhood friend and now works in a nightclub. At first, Frederic is reserved because he thinks she's only looking out for herself. But the more they see each other, the more he enjoys the regular encounters with the attractive woman. Although he keeps the meetings that always take place on his free afternoons a secret from his wife, he does not feel guilty of adultery because there is no sexual intercourse. Rather, it is the fulfillment of a long-cherished dream for Frédéric to live two separate lives at the same time. Chloe's affairs with other men continue to fail, and as her desire for children becomes more pressing, Frédéric is the only father she can think of. He becomes increasingly uncertain whether he will be able to resist her temptation. Finally, she invites him into her apartment and presents herself naked to him in bed. Upon moving out, Frédéric becomes aware of the situation and flees the apartment in panic. He's taking time off at the office because all he wants to do is go home to his wife. When he spends the afternoon with Hélène for the first time in a long time, she bursts into tears.

anchor_text: Matteo Scuro, a widower from Sicily, was abandoned during the summer holidays by his five grown-up children, who live in different towns on the Italian mainland. Despite the criticism of his neighbors, he remains optimistic, his children are hindered because they are very busy in their responsible professions. Matteo decides to pay each of his children a surprise visit. His journey by train takes him from south to north, via Naples, Rome, Florence, Milan to Turin. He finds each of his children different from what he had imagined, and each has something of those opera characters after which he named his children as an opera boy: Tosca after Puccini's Tosca, Canio after the Pagliacci of Leoncavallo, Norma after Bellini's Norma, Guglielmo after Rossini's Guglielmo Tell and Alvaro after Verdi's La forza del destino. When he returns to Sicily, he visits his wife's grave and tells her about the children: "Everyone is fine".
text_a: Matteo Scuro is a retired Sicilian widower, formerly employed in the civil service of a town hall. A lover of opera, he chose for each of his five children, living in the four corners of Italy, the name of an opera hero or heroine: Tosca (Tosca by Giacomo Puccini), Canio (Pagliacci by Ruggero Leoncavallo), Norma (Norma by Vincenzo Bellini), Guillaume (Guillaume Tell by Gioachino Rossini) and Alvaro (La forza del destino by Giuseppe Verdi). He is aware that he has not been a very involved father in the upbringing of his children and, in order to reestablish contact, he invites them for the next summer vacation. Each, always with good reason, declines the invitation. Matteo then decided to surprise them by going to visit them in Naples, Rome, Florence, Milan and Turin, their cities of residence.
text_b: The rich Lombard family of the Recchi consists of Emma and Tancredi and their children Elisabetta, Edoardo and Gianluca. Between bourgeois rites and social conventions in the heart of Milan, the stories of the various members of the family are consumed, grappling with the affairs of the family business and the roles that their position imposes on them. Elderly Emma finds comfort and love in the arms of the young cook, Antonio. Emma reveals to him her past and how Tancredi has been her Pygmalion, taking her under his protective wing and even changing her name. The passion that erupts between two totally different souls over the mountains of Sanremo, such as Emma and Antonio, breaks instead bonds and well-established conventions, bringing the two to come into contact with their true nature. The same family business, which at the beginning of the film is passed into the hands of Tancredi and his son Edoardo, undergoes a radical transformation. Emma's life takes a decisive turn with the tragic end of Edward.

anchor_text: On the edge of the Volga, Aïst, a photographer in a paper factory and a memorialist of a forgotten people of Finnish origin, the Merians, buys two yellow ruants (Овсянки, Ovsyanki) at a market. But when he arrives at the factory, his boss and friend, Miron, tells him the tragic news of his wife's death. In order to cremate his wife's body on their honeymoon, Miron set off on a journey with Aïst... and her two birds.
text_a: After the death of his wife Tanya, Miron asks his friend Aist for help in preparing her burial according to the tradition of the Merya, an ancient Russian people living near the Black Lake. The two friends continue the journey to the burial in the company of his wife along with two caged birds, during which Miron confides with Aist about his wife's life.
text_b: The central character is a peasant named Nikita, who seduces and abandons a young orphan Marinka; then, the charming Anisija murders her own husband to marry Nikita, who impregnates her new stepdaughter and then, under the influence of his wife, murders her baby. Finally, on the day of her stepdaughter's wedding, she turns herself in and confesses to the police.[1]

anchor_text: Niccolò is a film director and is currently researching for a new film. In search of the ideal woman, he begins a relationship with Mavi, a noblewoman who attended a boarding school in Wales. Despite sexual intercourse, Niccolò is unable to convey his love to her adequately. A car trip to the countryside in the fog, in which Mavi is very anxious, turns out to be a fiasco for Niccolò. After a while, the contact between the two breaks down. Through visits to Mavi's acquaintances, he learns something about their bisexuality. When Niccolò visits his sister, a gynecologist, he sees her son playing with a stamp collection. This one shows Niccolò his favorite stamp, a Soviet stamp depicting a space station. Niccolò then reflects on science fiction. In a small town, Niccolò made an appointment with an acquaintance from the countryside. She has to go to the bathroom, but she doesn't want to go to a bar. She prefers the theater. There Niccolò meets Ida, an actress, whom he later visits. Although Niccolò has sex with Ida, she realizes that he does not love her unconditionally. When Niccolò invites Ida to Venice, she confesses to him that she is pregnant. Ida, who was happy before, is sad from one moment to the next. She also confesses to Niccolò that she has a problem with Italian directors. At the end of the film, Niccolò reflects again on a science fiction film he might want to make.
text_a: The director Niccolò, in search of a new cinematographic subject and a suitable female face, meets Maria Vittoria called Mavi, an assistant of his gynecologist sister, with whom he establishes an exciting relationship. Unfortunately, their relationship is proving fragile. Her interest in him is strongly opposed by the woman's friends, people of higher class - and in particular by a mysterious young man, who, after an appointment at an ice cream parlor, urges him to end the relationship. Niccolò assumes that this may have been sent by Mavi's real father, whom she herself has always hated and considered as a mere family friend. Upon returning to the city after a trip to the countryside, during which Mavi accuses Niccolò of not knowing how to love her, she disappears, losing any trace of herself. A few days later the man receives a visit from his nephew, his sister's son, who suggests that he make a science fiction film.
text_b: Domenico Soriano (Marcello Mastroianni), a wealthy Neapolitan seducer, meets a prostitute, Filumena Marturano (Sophia Loren), whom he offers to live with him in his house. In fact, Filumena is reduced to the role of maid for Domenico's mother who is in a vegetative state and housekeeper. In addition to these household duties, she takes care of Domenico's pastry shop while he, free of all constraints, spends his time traveling. After 22 years of this ungrateful life, Filumena suddenly falls ill and has only a few hours to live. To ease his conscience, Domenico agrees, at the request of the priest, to marry her In articulo mortis. However, after the marriage is announced, the bride suddenly rises from her bed, revealing her ruse. She tells Domenico that she has three sons, one of whom is his, but without specifying which one. The marriage is annulled and Filumena leaves the house. After unsuccessfully trying to find out which of the boys is his son and finally realizing that he loves Filumena, Domenico marries her. It is only on the wedding day that he discovers that he is, in fact, the father of the three boys.

anchor_text: In the mid-1990s, in the small village of Grimley in northern England, miners and their families fought against the closure of their mine as part of the UK's national programme to close coal mines. Among them was a brass band called "brassed off" led by Danny (Pete Postlethwaite). They are torn between their love of music and their hopes of competing in the national brass band championship finals, and the prospect of losing their jobs and seeing their community fall apart. It is the arrival of a new girl, Gloria (Tara Fitzgerald), the only woman in the orchestra, who will give them the desire to play again and the hope they had lost. But the successive events will come to ruin their hopes little by little, like Phil (Stephen Tompkinson) abandoned by his wife who sees only suicide as a solution, Danny to the health weakened by silicosis, who almost dies when he learns that his mine and his brass band are doomed and Andy (Ewan McGregor) pulled between his love for Gloria and the fact that she works for the commission responsible for liquidating the mine. However, their friendship, as well as their desire to show the UK their pride in being miners, will allow them to reach the final at the Royal Albert Hall in London, and save their honour in the absence of their jobs.
text_a: Earth passes through the path of a comet's tail which causes animal and human life to dehydrate into red dust. Regina (Catherine Mary Stewart) and Samantha (Kelli Maroney), two teenage sisters, save their lives by spending the night inside a metal container. They wake up to find that the world has been devastated and those who weren't fully exposed have become hungry zombies. To survive, they learn self-defense and acquire firearms. They hear the voice of an announcer from a radio station and come for help. Along the way they run into Hector Gomez (Robert Beltran), another survivor. His surprise is great when he arrives at the station and discovers that it is a programmed audio tape player. They use that medium to send a message of help. His message attracts the attention of some scientists from a think tank of some kind of underground government, who by not taking proper precautions are partially affected, so they seek out healthy survivors to obtain their blood and prepare an antidote. Regina is kidnapped and freed by her friends, who kill some scientists and manage to rescue other healthy survivors. The group consolidates as a family cell, except Samantha leaves with a boy her own age named Danny Mason Keener. Danny is the one who has always surpassed Regina's score in the video game Tempest to which she is addicted, as shown at the beginning of the story and it is until now when the true identity of the initials DMK is discovered.
text_b: With the closure of the mine, the historic local brass band is also at risk. The musician miners are disillusioned. During the day they take part in strikes to avoid the closure, but now with little conviction, being deeply scarred by the defeat of the strike of 1984. In the evening they gather to rehearse, but with the knowledge that once the mine is closed they will be forced to cease their musical activity as well. The entry into the gang of Gloria, a native of Grimley but graduated and employed in the direction of the mine as a "white collar", arouses friction and ambivalent feelings, especially in Andy. But her skill with the soprano horn, and the fact that she's the daughter of the former director of the brass band, makes her acceptable to the band. Driven by the determination and passion of their senior conductor, Danny, the Band is spurred on to go on and compete in the finals of the National Brass Band Championship, which takes place in London's prestigious Royal Albert Hall.

anchor_text: In the early 1990s, young Gloria is sent to her home town in northern England to investigate the economic viability of a coal mine for the company's management. She starts playing with the in-house Bergmanns brass band, led by the older but combative Danny. Gloria hides from the band members that she works for corporate management. The woman even begins a love affair with young Andy. When the mine is supposed to be closed, the band members no longer see any point in their leisure activities. Gloria and Danny renew the self-confidence of the miners and train the band to participate in a competition at London's Royal Albert Hall. But then Danny collapses from the effects of his dusty lungs and is hospitalized, after which his heavily indebted son Phil attempts suicide. Nevertheless, the band continues, travels to London, and surprisingly wins the competition. Danny refuses to accept the award and criticizes the government's economic policies in a speech.
text_a: As a result of a comet passing close to the Earth, almost all of the world's population is decimated. Regina and her 16-year-old sister, Samantha, survive and take refuge in the local radio studio, which continues to broadcast. There they meet Hector Gomez, a truck driver who was protected by the steel cab of his vehicle. In a ruined world where there are no more rules, young girls will quickly give in to their instinct to buy and visit the big shopping malls in order to change their wardrobe. But it will be without radiation survivors who have become aggressive, and a team of scientists trying to create a serum that can cure radiation victims, starting with themselves.
text_b: In the mid-1990s, in the small village of Grimley in northern England, miners and their families fought against the closure of their mine as part of the UK's national programme to close coal mines. Among them was a brass band called "brassed off" led by Danny (Pete Postlethwaite). They are torn between their love of music and their hopes of competing in the national brass band championship finals, and the prospect of losing their jobs and seeing their community fall apart. It is the arrival of a new girl, Gloria (Tara Fitzgerald), the only woman in the orchestra, who will give them the desire to play again and the hope they had lost. But the successive events will come to ruin their hopes little by little, like Phil (Stephen Tompkinson) abandoned by his wife who sees only suicide as a solution, Danny to the health weakened by silicosis, who almost dies when he learns that his mine and his brass band are doomed and Andy (Ewan McGregor) pulled between his love for Gloria and the fact that she works for the commission responsible for liquidating the mine. However, their friendship, as well as their desire to show the UK their pride in being miners, will allow them to reach the final at the Royal Albert Hall in London, and save their honour in the absence of their jobs.

anchor_text: Matthew Saunders (Luke Wilson) is employed in a design office. Secretly in love with his co-worker Hannah (Anna Faris), he has no love life and eventually lets his friend Vaughn (Rainn Wilson) convince him to approach a woman apparently as shy as him on the subway. The young woman is then robbed of her purse, and he chases the thief and eventually retrieves Jenny's (Uma Thurman) belongings. She is very touched by the gesture and quickly begins a relationship with Matt, to whom she reveals her secret: she is none other than G-Girl, a superheroine with unlimited powers. Matt soon discovers that Jenny's impulsiveness and jealousy frighten him. He also realizes that he's in love with Hannah. When he leaves Jenny, Matt then faces the vengeance of his former girlfriend, and fears for his life as much as Hannah's. It is then that he is contacted by Professor Bedlam (Eddie Izzard), a super-villain enemy of G-Girl, who knows how to deprive the heroine of her fantastic powers.
text_a: Matthew, a former photographer and now an established advertising executive, is leaving on a business trip to China. During a business dinner attended by his fiancée, he walks away from the table to make a phone call but finds the booth occupied, so he heads for the bathroom. As soon as he leaves, he realizes that the one in the cabin was Lisa, his ex-girlfriend who had broken his heart, but he immediately loses sight of her even if he finds the key to her room; this allows him to meet her. So Matthew decides not to go to China, although he makes everyone believe it, but to stay so that he can see Lisa again, with whom he is still in love. She discovers that the room belongs to Alex who had done everything to keep them from meeting, she was and still is in love with Matthew. It turns out that Lisa had not run away but had left for a dance tour that went out at the last moment and had asked Alex to deliver a letter to Matthew; she had also handed her a copy of the keys to his apartment, but once Alex entered the apartment she simply deleted all of Lisa's messages from the answering machine. Matthew and Lisa finally embrace back at the station.
text_b: Matt Saunders (Luke Wilson) is an attractive guy in his mid-30s who has yet to face a serious date in his life. Although he possesses a charming clumsiness, he doesn't seem to find a girl for himself. That's when he meets Jenny Johnson (Uma Thurman), an attractive but somewhat eccentric girl who looks nothing like any other girl Matt has ever met before. Matt feels like the luckiest man on earth when he finds out that his girl is actually G-Girl, the most fantastic superheroine who ever lived. You can't even imagine the possibilities these superpowers can bring to your relationship! They have wonderful times together and push their sex life to the limit... But after the initial thrill, Matt breaks up with Jenny, and that doesn't sit well with her and her big neurotic vein. However, she soon realizes that a discarded superhero can be the one who suffers the most. G-Girl unleashes her superpowers to humiliate and torment him, but she won't disappear from his life until she sees him turned into a living hell.

anchor_text: A four-man SWAT team gets caught between two criminal gangs during a routine training exercise. It's going to be a fight.
text_a: Young delinquents, responsible for the suicide of one of their own in prison, are sent to a deserted island for a survival training. They are then attacked by rabid dogs...
text_b: An exercise for the LAPD SWAT team goes terribly wrong when they find themselves having to deal with two rival gangs while trapped in an abandoned hangar.

anchor_text: Grete works in a manicure salon and lives with the former theater actress Mrs. Seidelbast as a sublet. During the day, when Grete works, the room is rented to Hans, who is a waiter in the nightclub "Casanova" and only comes to sleep in the morning. Grete and Hans have never met. One day, the two meet and fall in love, unaware that they're already sharing a room. Until enlightenment and happy endings, there are numerous confusions.
text_a: Greta (Hilary Duff) is a 17-year-old, beautiful and sunny, but also quite rebellious due to her turbulent family life. After her mother Karen (Melissa Leo) remarries for the third time, Greta is sent to live with her grandparents for the summer. She's not happy about it, and neither are her grandparents. The girl informs him that before the end of the summer she intends to commit suicide and so begins to write, in a notebook, a list of possible ways to commit suicide.
text_b: Greta is a manicurist who works in a beauty salon and lives in subletting from a former theater actress, Frau Seidelbast. In fact, the room is only sublet at night, because during the day Hans, a waiter in a nightclub, sleeps there. Greta and Hans have never met. One day, by chance, the two meet and fall in love with each other, but without knowing that they share a room. Before they find out, and before the happy ending, they'll still run into a series of misunderstandings.

anchor_text: Three years after the Dayton Agreement (1998), the small Bosnian town of Tešanj is thrown into turmoil by the announced visit of US President Bill Clinton. The inhabitants hope that his visit will improve the quality of life and increase prosperity in the war-torn town. That's why they're doing their best to show themselves and Teshanj from the best side, trying to cover up the city's shadowy sides. Years of corruption, crime and ethnic strife must be overcome within a week. So the Bosnians, paradoxically, decorate the U.S. flag with red stars instead of white stars. This brings us back to the old communist regime in the SFRY. As a model, Pjer Žalica used the play The Reviewer by Gogol.
text_a: The plot takes place in the small town of Tešanj in the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, two years after the Bosnian War. The city is dominated by corruption, prostitution and organized crime. The people of Tešanj live in peace, although the scars of war are visible everywhere in the city, as well as in the souls of the people. After the war, the population of Tešanj is composed almost exclusively of Bosniaks. Ethnic Serbs now live in the surrounding villages. It is announced that the President of the United States, Bill Clinton, will pay a visit to the city. Western bureaucrats arrive in Teshanj to supervise the preparations for the visit. Everything in the city must be in order, including the false brotherhood between Bosniaks and Serbs.
text_b: Luka, a Serbian engineer (Slavko Stimac) from Belgrade, wants to turn the small Bosnian town he has moved to into a tourist destination. He wants to build a new railway line in the village in the middle of nowhere. The coach is accompanied by his wife Jadranka (Vesna Tribalic) - who sings operas - and his son Milos (Vuka Kostic) - who dreams of being a great football player. Dedicated entirely to his work, Luka ignores the persistent rumors of war. However, one day conflict breaks out. The wife suffers an attack of madness and is admitted to a psychiatric hospital, while the son is called up to the ranks in the army. During the farewell to his son his wife runs away with a musician, during that period Luka suffers from loneliness and learns that his son has been captured. Meanwhile Luka has been appointed guardian of Sabaha, a young Muslim nurse who has been taken hostage. And although he ends up falling in love with her, fate decides that she must be exchanged for a Serbian prisoner, his son Milos.

text_b: In 1995, the small Bosnian town of Tešanj is shaken by news of the impending visit of U.S. President Bill Clinton. In a few days, the inhabitants will have to dispel the ethnic hatred, genocide and corruption that are spreading in the community, pretending peace and harmony to attract the president and get out of a situation of destitution. Many comic touches, the most famous of which occurs when red stars are sewn on the American flag, made to decorate the country. The only one who hasn't been touched by Bill Clinton's arrival is a man who doesn't resign himself to the loss of his son, killed in the war.

anchor_text: In Los Angeles in the late 1990s, police officer Dave Brown, a Vietnam War veteran, works in a division of the Los Angeles Police Department that has repeatedly been at the center of corruption cases. The agent operates according to a personal line of conduct, made up of violence and abuse, to the limit of legality. When he beats up a simple suspect in front of the cameras, the officer is thrust into the middle of a thorny investigation, where he does not renounce his harsh and macho ways, despite pressure from the department. In his life on the edge, made of alcohol and fleeting encounters, the agent will have to fight not to be overwhelmed by professional and personal events.
text_a: The film centers on the story of two Los Angeles Police Department patrol officers, Brian Taylor and Mike Zavala. Their friendship is very deep, and they know they have to protect each other, trying to make it to the end of the shift. Their job is to disrupt organized crime in an area of Los Angeles, tracking down small-time drug dealers and other criminals in the area. While their advancement in the workplace is shown, there are also changes in their personal lives. Zavala will have a child with his wife Gabby, while Taylor will meet Janet, whom he will marry. In the course of their work, however, they end up getting involved in bigger deals than expected, finding themselves up against a ruthless local gang. The latter, in revenge for their interference, will attempt to kill them by luring them into a trap. The plan works and the two officers are riddled with bullets after killing several members of the gang, who will then be killed by the officers who intervened at the scene of the firefight. In the end it turns out that only Agent Zavala is dead; Taylor had been shot, but not to death, and his partner had defended him with his own body from the majority of the blows.
text_b: It tells the story of a veteran police officer in the Rampart police station corruption case in the late 1990s.[2] Los Angeles Police Department officer Dave Brown (Harrelson) is discovered "doing the dirty work" and finds himself in the middle of a vicious scandal. The era of being above the law is over. Now Dave's in the crosshairs and he's gonna get his revenge. Nothing is what it seems as Dave explores the underbelly of Los Angeles and imparts his own sense of justice.[3] A homicide detective (Cube) is sent to investigate Brown.[4]

anchor_text: This is Los Angeles, 1999. As the Los Angeles Police Department is scarred by a scandal involving the Rampart Division, Officer Dave Brown has no intention of changing his methods. Violent, alcoholic, misogynist, and racist, this Vietnam veteran is doing just fine after 24 years of service. He is, however, an expert in jurisprudence. Dave also tries to maintain family ties with his two daughters and squats with his two ex-wives, Heche and Nixon, who are also sisters. After brutalizing a man in front of cameras, he causes a new controversy for the Rampart Division. The video quickly went viral and his superiors asked him to retire. Fired, Dave wanders aimlessly and lost. During one of his many outings to bars, he meets Linda. He also found support from a former police officer, Hartshorn. Dave feels that in the shadows people want to bring him down. An old case of a rapist killed in a grocery store has resurfaced. Everybody thinks Dave did it. He finds himself being followed by a prosecutor's investigator, Kyle Timkins.
text_a: Brian Taylor and Mike Zavala are two officers from the Newton Division of the Los Angeles Police Department. Very close to each other, they patrol the streets of South Central together every day. A former Marine, Brian is passionate about film and wants to film himself at work as part of a personal project. He and Mike then film each other using small cameras attached to their uniforms. One day, Mike confronts the loyal Tre, a member of the Bloods. Mike manages to control it and win her respect. Later, Tre and some Bloods are targeted in a drive-by by Sureños. A Blood is killed. The next day, several police officers are called for a nighttime riot at a party given by Sureños. Brian and Mike meet Big Evil and LaLa, gang members seeking recognition from their superiors. Meanwhile, Brian - very picky about women - begins dating Janet, while Mike and his wife Gabriella are expecting their first child. Later, Mike and Brian rescue children from a fire and receive the Medal of Valor from the Los Angeles Police Department. After arresting an armed suspect carrying drugs in his car, the two police officers go beyond their roles as officers and begin to investigate the man and his entourage. Through their actions and discoveries, including human trafficking, they end up disturbing the Sinaloa Cartel, which puts a contract on their heads.
text_b: In Los Angeles in the late 1990s, police officer Dave Brown, a Vietnam War veteran, works in a division of the Los Angeles Police Department that has repeatedly been at the center of corruption cases. The agent operates according to a personal line of conduct, made up of violence and abuse, to the limit of legality. When he beats up a simple suspect in front of the cameras, the officer is thrust into the middle of a thorny investigation, where he does not renounce his harsh and macho ways, despite pressure from the department. In his life on the edge, made of alcohol and fleeting encounters, the agent will have to fight not to be overwhelmed by professional and personal events.

anchor_text: To recover from a love disappointment, Joe is trained by his friends Eric and Hung in a life-size role-playing game. Unfortunately, the players will have to become the heroes they play when one of them accidentally summons a demon...
text_a: Joe will rescue a girl named Alicia Sanbord, who is none other than Professor Sanbord's daughter. Joe will not only have to face off against Burke's savage ninja horde in order to free Wild Bill's men, but also Tojo Ken, Burke's assistant and a ruthless martial arts master.
text_b: To distract himself from his recent breakup with his girlfriend, heavy metal singer Joe is convinced by his friends to attend a live role-playing gathering. But during the "battle" the group accidentally conjures up a bloody succubus. Armed with only fake weapons, the group joins forces with other LARP players to defeat the evil creature they have unleashed.

anchor_text: To recover from a love disappointment, Joe is trained by his friends Eric and Hung in a life-size role-playing game. Unfortunately, the players will have to become the heroes they play when one of them accidentally summons a demon...
text_a: Joe will rescue a girl named Alicia Sanbord, who is none other than Professor Sanbord's daughter. Joe will not only have to face off against Burke's savage ninja horde in order to free Wild Bill's men, but also Tojo Ken, Burke's assistant and a ruthless martial arts master.
text_b: Three best friends addicted to role-playing games go into the woods to recreate dragons and dungeons. The problem arises when a spell book acquired from the internet turns out to be a genuine grimoire and they unconsciously conjure up a bloodthirsty succubus from hell.[4]

anchor_text: The kidnapping of the chairman of the Italian Christian-Democratic Party, Aldo Moro, by members of the Red Brigades terrorist organization on 16 March 1978 plunged the government into a paralyzing inability to act. As Moro faces a so-called people's court for the kidnappers, the Moro family's hopes that the government can secure their release are dashed. All attempts fail in a negotiated situation; Moro is murdered.
text_a: The film travels chronologically through the 55 days of Aldo Moro's kidnapping, dwelling on the events that characterized those two months in the life of the country. A special moment are the scenes dedicated to the contacts between the State and the Italian criminal organizations (camorra, Banda della Magliana, etc.).
text_b: David is a journalist who is writing a novel about the Red Brigades, an Italian terrorist group. To finish the book he travels to Rome where he meets a lovely photographer with whom he enters into a love affair, unaware of the danger that awaits him in the Italian capital.

anchor_text: Alex Bernier is a member of an esoteric order of priests known as the Carolinians. After a series of unexplained murders, including that of the leader of this secret order, Alex is sent to Rome to investigate the mysterious circumstances of the murders. The priest's body bears strange marks on the torso that could be the signature of an expiator of sins, William Eden, a renegade who offers absolution and ultimate rituals, religious practices not recognized by the Church. Alex finds himself plunged into a death spiral. His investigations will lead him to the Purifier, an evil being, a mythical figure of the secret order.
text_a: Alex Bernier, a young New York priest belonging to an ancient order on the fringes of the Church and almost extinct (the "Carolingi"), travels to Rome to investigate the mysterious death of his mentor Father Dominic. Investigating together with Thomas Garrett (also a member of Alex's order), he discovers the truth: Father Dominic has been murdered and the murderer is William Eden, a "devourer of sins", an obscure character expelled by the "Carolingians", who, with a particular ritual, ensures, for a large fee, the purification of sins, even the most nefarious, to the dying who do not intend to repent.
text_b: The Basque priest Angel, after a long study of the Bible, determines the time for the arrival of the Antichrist. At Christmas time, he travels to Madrid to come into contact with the Antichrist by committing many sins. He meets Jose Maria, a death metal fan working in the music store, and ends up in his mother's boarding house. Together, they seek out Dr. Cavan, the host of an occult TV show. Together they go hunting in Christmastime Madrid to prevent the rebirth of the Antichrist.

anchor_text: Vienna, Austria Alex Linden (Art Garfunkel) and Milena Flaherty (Theresa Russell), a young American couple, are living there with a tumultuous relationship. One night, the young woman is admitted to the hospital in serious condition for a suicide attempt. Inspector Netusil (Harvey Keitel) is tasked with discovering the reasons for this hospitalization and whether they are judicial in nature. All night long, he's trying to get Alex to confess to an attempted murder, but by dawn, the young woman is off the hook: since there's no body, there was no murder.
text_a: Alex (Art Garfunkel) is an American psychoanalyst and professor in Vienna, Austria. He meets a fellow countrywoman, Milena (Theresa Russell), with whom he begins a passionate relationship. But she's very independent and doesn't want an exclusive relationship, so their infidelities escalate, exacerbating Alex's possessive desire. Months later she takes an overdose of barbiturates that bring her to the brink of death. Inspector Netusil (Harvey Keitel) must find out if it was actually a suicide attempt, or a foiled murder. The color.
text_b: In present-day Vienna, Alex is an ex-con who works for a brothel owner planning to escape with his girlfriend, a beautiful young Ukrainian prostitute. The man's dream was to rob a suburban bank, turning his life around. But everything goes wrong, because of an accidental death by a policeman. Alex gets the loot, but in exchange for that product of a gunshot, the young woman dies. Heartbroken, Alex takes refuge on his grandfather's farm, not far from the crime scene. As the days go by, he gets deeper and deeper into the life of the cop and his wife, as his obsession with revenge for the crime grows.

anchor_text: In Santa Fe, Edward Scott meets and falls in love with the "half-breed" Juanitta, an "opium addict". He marries Juanita and takes her to Europe. At a meeting with his friend Axel van der Straaten, the conversation also comes to Juanitta and Axel claims that a "half-blood" like Juanitta is not equal to a man: one can have her as a lover, but not marry her. Edward does not contradict him, and Juanita, who secretly overheard the conversation, now despises her husband. She ignores him and provokes his jealousy until he is committed to an insane asylum. Juanitta now gives herself to Axel van der Straeten, wooing and seducing him until he finally falls for her. She takes him to a gambling den, where he loses his fortune. Through her influence, he becomes an impostor and Juanita betrays him to the police for revenge. But Axel can evade arrest. Juanita wants to flee to Mexico with the stolen money and her new lover, the half-breed. Axel, however, realizes that Juanitta only played with him for revenge, and shoots her.
text_a: In Santa Fe, Edward Scott meets the half-blooded Juanita, an opium-addicted prostitute. They quickly fall in love with each other and get married. Edward then took Juanita back to Europe. During a conversation with his friend Axel van der Straaten, it is learned that Axel is also a half-breed like Junita. But human beings are not on an equal footing: if they can love equally, they still cannot marry. Edward did not contradict him, and Juanita, who had secretly listened to the conversation, abhorred his love from that moment on. She ignored him, arousing such jealousy in him that he was eventually committed. Juanita now devotes herself to Axel van der Straaten, making his eyes soft, teasing him so much that he falls madly in love with her. She takes Axel to a casino where he loses his entire fortune. Under her influence, he becomes a cheat; Juanita also advises him to take revenge on the police. Axel's arrest, however, allows him to escape Juanita's control. Juanita wants to flee with the proceeds of the scams with her new love, the Métis (Mestizo), to Mexico. Axel, however, realized that Juanita had simply used him to get revenge and killed her with a gun.
text_b: María, a member of Madrid's upper class, and her lover Juan, a university professor, carelessly hit a cyclist with their car. Fearing that their affair might be exposed, they commit hit-and-run. Later, Juan learns from the newspaper that the accident victim has succumbed to his injuries. Distracted by the news, Juan blames the failed examination on the student Matilde. While there are protests by students demanding Juan's dismissal, and María is blackmailed by the newcomer Rafa, who suspects a connection between her, Juan and the accident, Juan begins to torment the bad conscience. He seeks, without being recognized, to enter the block of flats in the working-class district where the killed cyclist lived, and tries to reconcile with Matilde. Together, María and Juan return to the scene of the accident, which was once the scene of a bloody battle in the Spanish Civil War. Juan tries to persuade María to go to the police with him, but she wants to go abroad with her husband Miguel. María kills Juan and is herself the victim of a traffic accident on the way back to the city.

text_b: Edward meets the half-breed Juanita in Santa Fe, a prostitute from an opium den. He falls in love with her, marries her and takes her to Europe. In a meeting with his friend Axel van der Straaten, the conversation shifts to Juanita and Axel reproaches Edward for marrying her: "You can certainly have a half-breed as a lover, but you certainly don't have to marry her". Juanita overhears the conversation, despises her husband for not defending her, and vows revenge. She provokes Edward in a thousand ways. Eaten up with jealousy, he'll die in a madhouse. Axel's going to jail. Trapped and seduced by the woman, he frequents the underground gambling hall run by her and another half-breed, her new lover. He gambles and loses his fortune. Denounced by Juanita herself, he'll be arrested. Juanita wants to escape with the loot and her lover to Mexico but fate does not allow it: she is hit by a bullet fired by a defrauded client and dies.

anchor_text: In 1825, India is under British control. At that time, fear was spreading in India over the existence of a group of killers who were systematically killing people and committing ritual murders. William Savage, an English officer, accidentally discovers these events and decides to fight them against the will of his superiors, who do not wish to interfere in the affairs of the Hindus. He manages to arrest one of these killers and discovers that they belong to a sect called the Thugs or Deceivers. They worship the goddess Kali and kill for her. With his help, he infiltrates this group and learns their habits. He will witness how they commit crimes in the name of Kali and how English officials cover them in exchange for bribes. However, the deceivers are getting closer and closer to the truth about him, and as he submits to their rituals, he becomes more and more like them. Eventually, they discover who he is and try to kill him. Calling him the greatest deceiver of all, they try to destroy him completely, but he manages to escape and call for help. That's how he manages to save himself and expose their schemes. When the East India Company, which governs India on behalf of Great Britain, realizes how dangerous the sect is, it decides to commission William Savage to exterminate them throughout India, which he then does systematically, but the events then traumatize him in a lasting way.
text_a: Bombay is controlled by gangster boss Jahangir Khan, known as Abbaji. Not only the corrupt cops Purohit and Pandit work for him, the politicians also depend on him. Miyan Maqbool is his right-hand man and a reliable gangster. Abbaji's young wife Nimmi has already cast an eye on him and is starting an affair with him. She doesn't want to have to hide her love anymore, so she puts him in front of a choice. Either he kills Abbaji or she kills him. When Abbaji's future son-in-law Guddu is put forward as his successor, Maqbool sees the danger and kills Abbaji in his sleep. Now Maqbool becomes the head of the clan. However, the necessary loyalty is not shown to him, since everyone suspected the murder. The politicians, policemen and rival gangsters all tear under Maqbool's rule, driving him to despair. Nimmi is also haunted by delusions. One murder chases the next until the police officer Pandit's prophecy comes true and Maqbool faces death.
text_b: In 1825 India is controlled by the British. During that time fear is spreading across the country. The followers of a mysterious religion strangle people, make human sacrifices, and kill anyone who opposes their beliefs. When William Savage, an English tax inspector, accidentally discovers these killings and with them these followers, he decides to investigate them to finish them off. His investigations lead him to the stranglers, also known as thugs or forgers. They're a cult that worships the goddess Kali and kills for her. With the help of one of the followers, who he managed to catch, he manages to infiltrate them and study them. Thus it bears witness to their rituals and beliefs and to their killing and wickedness. He also discovers, that there are English officials who cover them up in exchange for bribes. However the stranglers are getting closer and closer to discovering who he is, while his presence among them makes him more and more one of them by having to submit to their malevolent rituals. Eventually they discover him and want to kill him, but with the help of others, whom he managed to call, Savage is able to escape and stop them. When the English find out all about the sect through him, they decide to end it and put Savage in charge of it, who acts accordingly. Yet the experience leaves him traumatized in a lasting way.

anchor_text: Young Chasan Yussel pursues a career in the music business against the wishes of his strictly religious father and his wife. Under the name Jess Robin, he travels to California and meets the manager Molly Bell, a woman who shares his dream with him. Jess, despairing of his newfound fame and the reaction of his father, who pronounces him dead, runs away and wanders across America unrecognized for a while.
text_a: Yussel is a singer by day in a synagogue; he dresses in black to perform at night as Jess Robin in Harlem's jazz clubs. Her formidable voice offers her the possibility of a career, but her father, a conservative Orthodox Jew, believes that her gift should be reserved only for God, and their relationship is poisoned.
text_b: The promising and talented young Red Nichols arrives in New York in the 1920s to work as a trumpet player in Wil Paradise's band. He marries one of the group's singers, Bobbie Meredith, and manages to have his own "Dixieland" style orchestra, called "The Five Pennies" (named after one of Nichols' songs, at the time a nickel coin was worth five pence). At the height of his popularity, Red's daughter, Dorothy, contracted polio and he, blaming himself for leaving her in boarding school while touring with the band, interrupted his career and moved to Los Angeles, working as an operator in a shipyard during World War II.

anchor_text: In 1889, in Oklahoma, Jed Cooper (Clint Eastwood), barely saved by Marshal Dave Bliss after being lynched by Red Creek residents who falsely accused him of shooting a cattle owner to rob him, is found not guilty by Judge Fenton. This makes him a marshal, partly to prevent Jed from engaging in personal revenge. The judge orders him to bring back alive the men responsible for his lynching so they can be properly tried. Energetic and resourceful, Jed travels through Oklahoma's lawless territory and carries out his duties with formidable efficiency. At the same time, Jed falls under the spell of a young, attractive widow who examines each prisoner with a persistence that intrigues him. She tells him she's looking for the bandit who killed her husband.
text_a: Jed Cooper (Clint Eastwood) is about to be hanged for cattle rustling by a group of nine men who are taking justice into their own hands. However, they make two mistakes: hanging the wrong man, and not ending his life. At the last moment, he is saved by a commissioner on the orders of Judge Fentom (Pat Hingle), who is aware that he is innocent of the crime for which he was tried for murder, since at the same time the real criminal had been caught. Cooper is advised by the judge to forget the past and is offered a position as a commissioner in the Oklahoma Territory, handling the more complicated missions and bringing the accused to be tried by the judge himself. Cooper's life, however, is marked by the scar left by the noose on his neck, and one day he is attacked by three of the nine men, being badly wounded. Raquel (Inger Stevens) treats him and asks him to find out who killed her husband and then insulted her. When he recovers from his wounds, Cooper is authorized by the judge to take charge of the arrest of the three bandits who attacked him.
text_b: Philip Vermeer is accused of the murder of Patriarch Samuel Saxon, which he did not commit. On the run from bounty hunters looking to collect the $3,000 put on his head, he gets help from former Sheriff Clayton. He claims to know the real killer and wants to help Vermeer prove his innocence. The two travel to a town in Arizona ruled by the patriarch's three sons: David, Adam, and Eli Saxon. It turns out that at the trial, David Saxon bribed the judge to get his hands on Vermeer's silver. Vermeer is captured and is to be hanged. When the verdict is read, Clayton shows up and admits to shooting the patriarch himself. In the ensuing duel, Clayton manages to kill all three brothers with Vermeer's help.

anchor_text: Melanie Cattori, a trained opera singer and the daughter of a music professor, falls in love with Freddy, the drummer of a jazz band living in the same house. Unfortunately, Melanie's father is strictly opposed to jazz music. So Freddy tries to convince a theater director to hire the jazz band for a revue. The jazz musicians are supported by the confectioner Franz Huber, whose daughter Gretl participates as a dancer. However, it is only when the theatre author forces the director to listen to the band through an artificially induced car breakdown and further difficulties are overcome that the performance can be arranged. Even Father Cattori allows his daughter Melanie to participate. The show is a success, and in the end, three couples find each other.
text_a: Melanie Cattori, a trained opera singer and daughter of a music teacher, falls in love with Freddy, the drummer of a jazz band who lives in the same house. Unfortunately, Melanie's father is strictly against jazz music. So Freddy tries to persuade a theater manager to hire the jazz band for a revue. The jazz musicians are supported by the general merchant Franz Huber, whose daughter Gretl is a dancer. But it is only when the director forces the conductor to listen to the band after an artificial car breakdown and other difficulties are overcome that the show can take place. Even Father Cattori allows his daughter Melanie to participate. The show is a hit and three couples are reunited.
text_b: At the age of ten, Mélanie Prouvost is a child endowed with an excellent talent for the piano. However, during an audition to enter the conservatory, a distraction caused by the chairman of the committee affects the test. The disappointment is enormous for Melanie, so much so that she decides to give up music forever. Ten years after the incident, the girl, now grown, is hired as a babysitter for the son of the lawyer Jean Fouchécourt and his wife Ariane, who turns out to be the woman who ten years earlier had caused, albeit unintentionally, the abandonment of Mélanie's musical career. The girl, after getting into the lady's good graces, becomes her face during concerts. A very intimate relationship develops between the two women, and Mélanie, obsessed with the pianist and eager to avenge the abuse she suffered as a child, seduces her and then abandons her, finally revealing the affair to her husband.

anchor_text: Women's hero and self-righteous Rafael González works at Yeyos, a department store in Madrid, as the head of the women's fashion department. In the competition for a managerial position in the department store, he is defeated by his rival Don Antonio. When this is made public, Don Antonio uses his new position as floor chief to pay Rafael back for his past humiliations. A fight ensues between the two, resulting in an accident that kills Don Antonio. The process is observed by the seller Lourdes, who from then on blackmails Rafael and completely controls his future life.
text_a: Rafael (Willy Toledo) is a seductive and ambitious guy who likes beautiful women, stylish clothes and the select environment. He works at a department store and wants to be the new plant manager. His only rival is Don Antonio, whom he accidentally kills in a heated argument. The only witness is Lourdes (Monica Cervera), who decides to blackmail him to make him her lover first and her husband later. There comes a point when he can't stand the situation anymore and comes up with a plan that's just "fuck".
text_b: The young Mexican nobleman Ricardo, hired as a courier, eliminates the consequences of some robberies. Even for Maria, whom he loves, he runs the risk of confronting the gang behind it, is captured and freed again with the help of a gypsy girl. Then he can expose the culprit of the misdeeds and the backer of the crooks, Don Rafael.

anchor_text: In the prologue, the student Rebecka is staged in the shower. She then begins a farewell letter, which she rejects, and she eventually falls from the roof of the school building during a speech by the headmistress she dies. A year later, life at the boarding school is back to normal. Sara's researching a project paper on the local history of a farmer who killed three boarding school students about 100 years ago. Legend has it that the farmer's ghost still wanders. A farmer's granddaughter provides Sara with valuable information about the long-ago criminal case. The farmer's 14-year-old daughter, Elvira, was raped and murdered by three students at the boarding school. Her father took revenge by killing the guilty. It turns out that one of the perpetrators was an ancestor of Måns. Sara investigates that Rebecca, who took her own life at the beginning of the film, was a victim of abuse, just like the farmer's daughter 100 years ago. Rebecca's father escapes from a closed facility. Felix turns out to be Rebecca's brother. A group of students are planning to throw a party on the grounds of the now-defunct farm. This event sets the stage for the showdown. A chase ensues, and several persons lose their lives. Towards the end of the film, a skeleton is fished out of a nearby lake. In the final scene, Sara and Leo are celebrating their high school graduation.
text_a: Graduate student Angie is hired by Jim and Violet Stanton to care for their son Sam. She had a car accident on the way to the Stanton farm. So Angie asks her new college friend Rick to drive her to the Stanton estate. When she finally arrives, she receives final instructions from Jim and Violet. Then, during the night, she receives several strange phone calls and hears noises. Terrified, Angie calls Rick and the police. The police chief calms Angie down and assures her that he will stop by later. In the night, a heavily scarred priest comes and attacks her. Terrified, Angie knocks him unconscious with a golf club. She notices that Sam has horns on his head. When Jim and Violet come home, they discover the priest. Jim kills the priest and beats up Angie. In the basement, he's got Angie and an unconscious girl tied up. Jim reveals that Sam is the son of the devil and eats women's flesh. Jim is cutting out all kinds of body parts from the unconscious and preparing to do the same to Angie. When the alarmed policeman arrives, Jim assures him that Angie is already on her way home, prompting him to return. Meanwhile, Angie breaks free, but is captured. The policeman returns and disarms Jim, however Violet slaps him in the back with an axe. Meanwhile, Angie can grab the gun and shoot Jim and then Violet. As Angie and the badly wounded officer try to return to the car, Sam appears and slits the officer's throat. Angie gets out of the car and runs away from Sam. In a barn, Sam attaches himself to a chain, which allows him to be overpowered and beaten by Angie. She drives off, wakes up in the hospital. Another cop informs them that Sam hasn't been found. The final scene shows Sam with his adoptive mother, who again puts a babysitter ad on the blackboard.
text_b: In the Swedish school Hallestad, a student dies after jumping from the roof for no apparent reason. A fantastic story runs through the minds of the students: the ancient legend of the Drowned Ghost. It is said that at the end of the 19th century, a local farmer brutally killed three students without any explanation. Then he killed himself in a lake and his body was never found. Since then and on those same dates, his ghost reappears in the school. Another student, Sara, sets out to investigate the legend and the origin of these crimes, discovering that the farmer did have a motive for committing these murders: the rape of his own daughter, a student at the same time in school. Sara discovers that one of the boys involved, Mans, belonged to the Weine family, one of the most important and prosperous in the region as well as the main contributor to the funding of the center. Following this discovery, Sara encounters strong opposition from the school principal to continue her research. Two new facts give even greater gravity to the situation: the strange disappearance of a student and the appearance of a letter from the deceased student, where the figure of the Phantom seems to acquire real meaning.

anchor_text: In a village in Upper Provence, a newly settled baker discovers one morning that his young wife has left with a shepherd. He decides to go on a bread strike until his wife returns. The village is mobilizing to find its bakery.
text_a: The film tells the intersecting fates of seven lives, set in a town on the edge of the Atlantic, during the off-season. Seven people who are going to cross paths, or miss each other. Seven lives that will be forever changed by their separation.
text_b: Recently arrived in a small village in Haute Provence, a baker discovers one day that his wife has left with a shepherd. The man then decides to go on strike and stop supplying the village with bread until his wife returns home. The whole village is mobilizing to get the bakery reopened.

anchor_text: The two mafia bosses Antonio Marchesi and Peppino Scalise compete in Palermo in the field of construction speculation; Scalise emerges as the winner: he has the rival killed. In the murder committed by the two gangsters Johnny De Salvo and Turi Nannisco, Marquesis is also killed some time ago by a certain successor, Federici. The case is being investigated by Commissioner La Manna, who is trying to get Federico's widow to testify against Scalise. After another murder, some mafiosi are also charged, but acquitted. Unwilling to give up, La Manna comes into contact with a vengeful former employee of Scalise who is willing to testify for a new identity. He too dies before he can fulfill his promise. Escaping an assassination attempt by Johnny and Turi, in which they both die, La Manna discovers conclusive documents in the Marquesis safe, with the help of which he can have Scalise arrested. As Scalise is being transported in a police car, he is shot by an unknown Mafia assassin. The honourable company remains intact.
text_a: Antonio, a Sicilian immigrant, finds himself in the clutches of the Mafia in the United States, who are terrorizing their compatriots in New York City. With muted approval, Tonio kills the murderer of his best friend Salvatore after he was murdered by a protection fund collector. Tonio is then welcomed into the "family", to which he is tied even more firmly by the murder covered up. He falls in love with the so-called professional named Angela and saves her from a club run, but in doing so violates the rules of the family. For Antonio and his beloved, the air is getting thinner and thinner. Lieutenant Joseph Petrosino is also becoming increasingly critical of the organization.
text_b: Two mafia bosses, Don Antonio Marchesi and Don Peppino Scalise, are competing for the field of building speculation in Palermo. The confrontation ends with Scalise's victory, which kills the rival. The murder, carried out by two of Don Peppino's assassins - Johnny De Salvo and Turi Nannisco - was witnessed by the foreman Orlando Federici, in the service of the boss Marchesi, who was also assassinated some time later. In charge of the investigation, Commissioner La Manna tries to get Federici's widow to testify against Don Scalise. The only way she does that is when the boss's hit men kill her little boy, targeting her. The trial, however, ends with the acquittal of the mafioso and his assassins. Determined not to give up, La Manna comes into contact with a prejudiced man who, wanting to take revenge on Don Peppino, promises the commissioner, in exchange for a fake passport, important revelations against the boss. The man, however, is killed before he can keep his promise. The commissioner, who escaped an attempt by Johnny and Turi, who let you live instead, will finally succeed, thanks to important documents found in a safe deposit box of the late Marchesi, to arrest Don Scalise. As he prepares to board a police van, however, the boss is killed by an unknown Mafia hit man.

text_a: Two mafia bosses, Don Antonio Marchesi and Don Peppino Scalise, compete in the field of real estate speculation in Palermo. The confrontation ends with the victory of Scalise, who murders his rival. The crime, perpetrated by two hitmen of Don Peppino Johnny De Salvo and Turi Nannisco is assisted by the foreman Orlando Federici, who works on behalf of Marchesi. Some time later, however, he too was assassinated. Commissioner La Manna, in charge of the investigation, tries to persuade Federici's widow to testify against Don Scalise. He only gets what he wants when the mob boss's hit men murder his grandson as a warning. However, the trial ends with the acquittal of the mafioso and his hired killers. Determined not to give up, La Manna comes into contact with a criminal who wants revenge on Don Peppino. This scoundrel promises the commissioner, in exchange for a fake passport, to make important revelations against the mafioso. But he gets himself killed before he can keep his promise. The Commissioner narrowly escapes an assassination attempt by Johnny and Turi, and manages to kill them instead. The commissioner finally manages to arrest Don Scalise thanks to important documents found in a safe belonging to the late Marchesi. But just as he's about to board a police truck, the mafioso is killed by an unknown Mafia hit man.
text_b: Antonio, a Sicilian immigrant, finds himself in the clutches of the Mafia in the United States, who are terrorizing their compatriots in New York City. With muted approval, Tonio kills the murderer of his best friend Salvatore after he was murdered by a protection fund collector. Tonio is then welcomed into the "family", to which he is tied even more firmly by the murder covered up. He falls in love with the so-called professional named Angela and saves her from a club run, but in doing so violates the rules of the family. For Antonio and his beloved, the air is getting thinner and thinner. Lieutenant Joseph Petrosino is also becoming increasingly critical of the organization.

anchor_text: Thunder is a Native American who went to the police. Among his colleagues is former Deputy Rusty. Tensions from earlier days immediately flare up, especially since Rusty is deeply involved in the local drug trade and fears that the new colleague could cause him trouble. He arranges the evidence so that Thunder is sentenced to five years of hard labor as the murderer of drug dealer Max. Even the skilled lawyer Thomas Ruder cannot refute the false evidence. After eleven days in solitary confinement, Thunder escapes to the mountains to join his pregnant wife, Sheela. He gets Ruder to reopen the case, only to be chased by Rusty in a helicopter. When Sheela loses the baby, Thunder recalls his Native American roots and goes to war with a bow and arrow. He manages to meet the now-suspect Rusty alone in an interrogation cell. Thunder, however, is reluctant to kill him. With the help of the kind-hearted Sheriff Roger, Thunder can reach the border.
text_a: Believed to be dead, Thunder returns to the city and is recruited into the police force where he meets Deputy Rusty, with whom he had previously had a falling out. The latter is actually a drug dealer and manages to trap the young Indian who is locked in a penitentiary where he is subjected to brutal treatment; having managed to escape, Thunder hunts down Rusty, who had also made himself responsible for the death of his son, and once face to face with his enemy instead of killing him decides to spare him. The sheriff decides to close the matter by letting Thunder escape, asking him not to return to the city, and once the Indian drives away with his wife he pulls out a sniper rifle, aims at the cabin and fires.
text_b: The criminal Jim Lang is called Thunderbolt. He's wanted for robbery and murder. With his girlfriend Ritzy, he frequents a nightclub in New York City's Harlem neighborhood. At the club, Ritzy explains to him that she's leaving him. Shortly thereafter, when the police raid, Thunderbolt narrowly escapes. He's got Ritzy watching from his gang. He's informed that she's staying with Mrs. Morgen. Her son Bob is a bank clerk who fell in love with Ritzy. Ritzy's worried about Bob's safety. She informs the police, who want to set a trap for the gangster. Although Thunderbolt manages to escape this trap, he is soon arrested and put on trial. The judge sentences him to death. From his death cell, Thunderbolt orchestrates a robbery of the bank where Bob works. I want Bob to be considered an accomplice to the robbery that's supposed to kill people. In fact, Bob is arrested and taken to Thunderbolt's next cell. Thunderbolt's attempts to reach his rival are thwarted by the guards. Bob is also sentenced to death, but still marries Ritzy on death row. The gangster comes to his senses and shows remorse. He confesses to the trap Bob fell into.
