He drops the plan to kill Bob the night before his execution. On the contrary, he patted his rival on the shoulder in a friendly gesture.

anchor_text: Pat Garrett and Doc Holliday are old friends. Their friendship is threatened when Billy the Kid steals Doc's horse. What follows is a game of cat and mouse between the three men and a love-hate relationship between the Kid and Doc. All the protagonists are charmed by Billy while repeatedly trying to kill him... himself seemingly indifferent to everyone and everything... except "Red", the stolen horse from Doc. Jane Russell's character, Rio, is a real supporting role. She wants to kill the Kid first to avenge her brother. Later, we find out that she's Doc's girlfriend. She cares for the Kid during his convalescence to the point of sneaking into his bed and marrying him in the early morning, then she tries to drown him; the Kid gives her little interest, abuses her, but they both eventually leave after many twists and turns. The turkey in the farce is Doc who loses both his horse, and Rio, and life.
text_a: 1906: Buffalo Bill and his circus tour Europe. During his stay in Rome, he and his assistant Red visit the estate of Countess Stefania; they are looking for suitable foals. There they meet the Countess's stepdaughter, Maria, with whom Red falls in love. However, she is promised to the steward Pietro, whom she does not love. So she takes advantage of one of his jealousy bouts to get rid of him. From this dispute, a rivalry develops between the shepherds led by Pietro and the cowboys of the circus, who side with Reds. Pietro is an excellent cleaner, which is inferior to Red. Nevertheless, Maria returns his love; at a night meeting they are surprised by their father, who is killed in the resulting quarrel. Pietro intervenes in the fight; Red tries to kill him, but wounds Maria and is himself stabbed by the jealous Lolita, whom he had left for Maria. Maria begs Pietro's forgiveness and returns with him to the countess' estate.
text_b: The plot of the film The Outlaw begins with the arrival of Doc Holliday (Walter Huston) to the town where his friend Pat Garrett (Thomas Mitchell) serves as sheriff. Doc goes in search of a stolen horse, which turns out to be in the possession of Billy the Kid (Jack Buetel); however, the two become friends, to the detriment of the relationship between Doc and Pat Garrett, Billy the Kid's stalker. In turn, the friendship between Doc and Billy deteriorates when Doc's girl Rio (Jane Russell) falls in love with Billy. The complicated relationship between the four characters unfolds against the backdrop of a Native American attack, until it reaches a violent outcome.

anchor_text: During a fight, Billy kills a man and, to prevent Sheriff Pat Garrett from arresting him, Doc hides him, badly injured, in a cabin and entrusts him to the care of his woman Rio. Billy and Rio have an unavoidable feeling for each other, but Doc is forced to keep his mouth shut because the three of them are on the run from the sheriff. When Garrett reaches them, Doc resists Billy's arrest and is killed. The other two manage to chain the sheriff and leave together.
text_a: The plot of the film The Outlaw begins with the arrival of Doc Holliday (Walter Huston) to the town where his friend Pat Garrett (Thomas Mitchell) serves as sheriff. Doc goes in search of a stolen horse, which turns out to be in the possession of Billy the Kid (Jack Buetel); however, the two become friends, to the detriment of the relationship between Doc and Pat Garrett, Billy the Kid's stalker. In turn, the friendship between Doc and Billy deteriorates when Doc's girl Rio (Jane Russell) falls in love with Billy. The complicated relationship between the four characters unfolds against the backdrop of a Native American attack, until it reaches a violent outcome.
text_b: For accidentally killing a man during a prom, quiet cowboy Tod Lohman is hunted by the victim's father and brother. Eager to save his life, David becomes a fugitive. He is pious, abstinent and fiercely non-violent, yet he is an astonishing marksman. This allows him to get rid of every one of his pursuers. He meets Amos Brady and his daughter, and ends up meeting them at their ranch. When he is joined by the last of his pursuers, Tod Lohman is determined to exercise his right of self-defense. After a confrontation ends without bloodshed, the victim's father calls for an end to the violence.

anchor_text: The strong Jewish blacksmith Zishe Breitbart is brought to Berlin from his Polish Schtetl in 1932. There he performs as a power acrobat in the variety show "Palace of the Occult". It's run by hypnotist Hanussen and mostly attended by National Socialists. With anti-Semitism on the rise, Breitbart's doubts about the value of his involvement in the variety show are growing. Moreover, the visit of his younger brother Benjamin enhances his pride in his Jewish heritage. Zishe consults a rabbi who is convinced that he has been chosen by God to warn his people against the Nazis. Breitbart dissolves his masquerade on stage and informs the audience that he is a Jew. He is hailed as a hero by the local Jews. Hanussen is kidnapped and murdered by the Stormtroopers. Breitbart senses that the Jewish population is about to suffer, so he travels back to Poland to warn others of the impending Holocaust. But no one believes him. To prove his strength, he bangs nails into a thick board with his bare hands, with a very long rusty nail penetrating and injuring his knee. He ignores all advice to see a doctor. It is only just before the burn leaves him unconscious that he seeks medical attention. In a delirium, he sees his brother Benjamin fly to safety and then dies, two days before Adolf Hitler comes to power and the disaster takes its course.
text_a: In 1943, in Rome during World War II, Matilde, Cencio, Fulvio and Mario are four attractions of the Mezzapiotta circus, owned by a man called Israel. They each have supernatural powers. Matilde produces electricity, and anyone who touches her gets an electric shock. Cencio, albino, can control all the insects. Fulvio, the beast-man, suffers from hypertrichosis and is endowed with superhuman strength. And Mario, who has dwarfism, has a body that acts like a magnet to metal objects and can move them through space. The worsening of the conflict - especially the beginning of the Italian campaign - put an end to the circus performances and made the lives of these five protagonists precarious. Israel then proposes that he and the four freaks go to America. Instead, Fulvio suggests finding work at the prestigious Berlin Circus, a lavish show put on by the Nazis. The Zircus is the realm of Franz, a German pianist with six fingers on each hand and who possesses powers of clairvoyance. Among other things, he predicted the suicide of Adolf Hitler and the arrival of four supernatural beings. Franz thinks they can save the fate of the Third Reich.
text_b: The story begins in March of 1932. Seigmund Breitbart (Jouko Ahola) is the son of a blacksmith from a village in Poland. He's very strong, partly because of his work. A bar owner saw Breitbart's incredible strength after a fight with a bunch of drunks and convinced him to go to Berlin to put on a show of strength. Upon arriving in Berlin he starts working in the cabaret of Hanussen exhibiting his strength. On his first appearance on stage Hanussen disguises him as the Nordic hero Siegfried to hide his Jewish origin, as much of the audience was Nazi. When his younger brother Benjamin visits him, Breibart puts aside his character of Siegfried and reveals his origin to the audience, showing himself as the new Jewish Samson, and the audience reacts scandalously. After a time Hanussen is tried and accused of forgery, is ordered to execute and the cabaret closes. In December 1932 Breibart returns to his hometown in Poland and warns that the Nazis are a serious threat to his people and that everyone must become strong like him. Later, when a nail is nailed to a piece of wood, it is pierced to the knee and becomes infected. His leg is amputated and Breibart dies on January 28, 1933, two days before Hitler came to power in Germany.

anchor_text: The widowed coachman Jona Potapow has only his daughter Anissya left after his son Kusma Jonytsch died in the hospital less than a week ago. Grief-stricken Jonah is desperate to talk to someone. However, none of Jonah's passengers want to hear more about it, not the officer who drives Jonah to the Wyborg side and not the three young men he carries to the police bridge. One of these young men even punches the coachman in the neck during the journey when he mentions the death and the passengers are moving too slowly. Jonah, who hasn't even earned the oatmeal for his mare today, makes the one-and-a-half-hour drive home. At home in the village, a young coachman wakes up as he enters the coachman's cabin. Jonah tells of his grief. The boy falls asleep over it. Jonah goes to the stable and takes care of his horse. The coachman starts talking and tells him everything.
text_a: Hannah gives a ride to a preacher with car trouble. The preacher was on his way to Gatlin. An introduction where he explains the origins of her name, she suddenly disappears, and Hannah, in astonishment, bursts out and bangs her head. When she wakes up, the sheriff is waiting for her, and after a checkup where she sees that she's the one who's meant to be, he convinces her to go to Gatlin Country Hospital. There she meets Dr. Michaels, who dismisses her and warns her to leave Gatlin. As she is leaving, she is chased by a delusional patient from the hospital (who later turns out to be Jack and the sheriff's brother) who captures her and takes her to a room where there is a person in a coma. This person turns out to be the husband of Cora the sheriff and thanks to the contact with Hannah he wakes up and his wife tells him that 19 years have passed and that she has a son. The sheriff's husband's name is Isaac. Hannah wanders around the hospital having visions and meets a boy where she confides that she is here to get information about her mother.
text_b: The coachman Iona Potapov has been waiting under the snow for several hours. An officer finally gets on the sleigh Vyborg Street! The sleigh starts slowly. He doesn't hold his right hand, and passersby berate him. Potapov tells the officer that his son died this week. He wanted to talk, but the officer closed his eyes. Waiting again, an hour, two hours... Three young people get into the sleigh: Coachman, to the police deck! The young people are thirsty. They complain about the slowness, and one of them hits Potapov. It distracted him for a moment. Wait some more. For her son was dead, and he is alive again: Death hath passed through the door, and he cometh into his chamber. He wants so much to talk, to tell stories, to empty himself. He goes to the stable and tells his horse everything.

anchor_text: Because one of the publishers to whom she sent her first novel gave her an appointment, Louise, a beautician in Le Mans, landed in Paris for three days. She's staying with her sister Martine, who can't stand her. They have nothing in common. The candid Louise is a bit of a nunchuch but has a heart this big. Martine, on the other hand, is just a block of bitterness. She lives an idle middle-class life in a gorgeous apartment in a beautiful neighborhood, alongside a rich husband, Pierre, whom she does not love, and a little boy she is not interested in. Between small quarrels and big fights, living together isn't going to be easy.
text_a: Martine (Isabelle Huppert) is an upper-middle-class bourgeois who lives in Paris in a nice apartment, knows how to dress well, has a very active social life and a husband whom she affectionately detests. Yet, deep down she feels that something is missing and would like to find a job to support herself. Her younger sister Louise (Catherine Frot), a beautician in the countryside, lives in a quiet country house in Le Mans and glamour is not part of her life. She is a direct and spontaneous woman who feels happy in her monotonous and simple existence next to her husband with whom she is in love. But Louise has written a novel and she has to go to the capital to meet with a publisher so she takes the opportunity to stay at Martine's house whom she hasn't seen in three years. The two sisters' worlds will collide head-on for three days: laughter, tears, recriminations and jealousy will cause the roles of the two to be exchanged.
text_b: Sarah Dedieu, an actress in her forties, is at the peak of her career. It has just been awarded a prestigious prize for interpretation. Going to rest at her house in the Var, she invites three other actresses. The four women, of different ages, will be exchanging views on their respective professions. And also about men, especially how men affect their lives and their art.

anchor_text: Seymour Moskowitz is a parking attendant in New York. Tired of that city, he decided to go to Los Angeles to do the same job. There he meets Minnie Moore, an employee at a modern art museum, as she tries to escape a suitor.
text_a: It's the story of a young girl who became an orphan as a result of a grenade accident that also left her deaf, dumb and blind. She is first entrusted to a wicked woman, then the village priest intervenes and asks a wealthy woman to take her to a rehabilitation center, Esther gradually regains her abilities thanks to the friendship between the two women.
text_b: Seymour Moskowitz is a young man who parks cars in a New York City parking lot. Tired of this town he decides to go to Los Angeles to do the same job. There he meets Minnie Moore, a single woman who works as a clerk in a modern art museum. Minnie has a secret relationship with a violent man who is married and has two children.

anchor_text: Seymour Moskowitz is a parking attendant in New York. Tired of that city, he decided to go to Los Angeles to do the same job. There he meets Minnie Moore, an employee at a modern art museum, as she tries to escape a suitor.
text_a: It's the story of a young girl who became an orphan as a result of a grenade accident that also left her deaf, dumb and blind. She is first entrusted to a wicked woman, then the village priest intervenes and asks a wealthy woman to take her to a rehabilitation center, Esther gradually regains her abilities thanks to the friendship between the two women.
text_b: Minnie, no longer young and employed in a museum, meets Moskowitz, a mustachioed parking attendant and hairdresser. Although they are very different, they fall in love.

anchor_text: Memphis, the "American", and "Moskito", who is actually Dino Bianco, rob a jewelry store while Dino's hippie girlfriend Maria waits in the getaway car. The action is poorly prepared and the execution fails, which causes Memphis to react brutally. On their escape, they find the British consul's 13-year-old son in a stolen car and take him hostage. The American's actions become increasingly confusing and brutal; he shoots Maria and forces Mosquito to drown a German family so that they can get to safety. Mosquito befriends Lennox, the kidnapped boy, and tries to stop the American from doing what he's doing. A few meters from the border, they are stopped by the police and found dead.
text_a: Memphis, the American, attacked a jewelry store with Moskito, whose real name is Dino Bianco, while Dino's hipster friend Maria is waiting in the getaway car. The action is poorly prepared and the execution fails, which makes Memphis react brutally. During their escape, they find the British consul's 13-year-old son in a stolen car, whom they take hostage. The American's actions become increasingly confused and brutal; so he shoots Maria and forces Mosquito to drown a German family so that they - Memphis is now wounded - can escape to safety. Mosquito befriends Lennox, the kidnapped boy, and tries to stop the American from doing what he's doing. A few meters from the border, they are caught by the police and killed.
text_b: A dangerous kidnapper stalks the streets of a small, middle-class neighborhood. In it lives Tim, an eight-year-old boy whose life is about to change, when one afternoon he and his mother are kidnapped by the dangerous Bob. At that moment, the young man's life will turn into a real nightmare, when he witnesses the brutal murder of his mother and is kidnapped by Bob becoming his slave. In order to be released from his punishment, the young man must make a life-or-death decision.[1]

anchor_text: After a long absence Sadko returns to his native Novgorod: he is taken by the beauty of Liubava, to which he is committed, while the misery of the people saddens him. He lashes out at the rich merchants, who leave their riches unproductive, instead of using them to give the people work. Chased by the merchants, Sadko goes to the sea and here Ilmen, daughter of the king of the oceans, appears to him, who promises him for the next day the fishing of the goldfish. With this, in fact, he returns to the city and persuades the merchants to entrust him with their merchandise and to arm three ships, with which he sets off in search of the Phoenix, the mythical bird of happiness, captured in a distant country. After passing terrible trials, Sadko manages to capture the fabulous bird. On the return journey the God of the sea, whom Sadko has neglected to propitiate, unleashes upon him a terrible storm. Relying on a raft, Sadko ends up at the bottom of the sea and is welcomed by the king and queen of the oceans, who would like to force him to marry one of their daughters. But the beautiful Princess Ilmen, though she loves her, helps her escape. So Sadko can return to Novgorod, where he is greeted triumphantly by the people, the merchants and Liubava, where he will find happiness, that happiness that he has vainly sought in the wide world.
text_a: After many years spent on long voyages, the protagonist sets sail from Java as a passenger on a cargo ship bound for the Sunda Islands. After traveling for several days, one evening the boat, which had hitherto been in the midst of good fortune, is suddenly hit by a storm. A huge wave hits the ship, sweeping all those on the deck into the sea; the narrator and an old Swede are saved, while the captain and a few seconds die drowned in their sleep in their cabins. The ship is heavily damaged, even the rudder is smashed so that the ship is no longer navigable by the survivors. A few days pass, and as the ship is driven toward the South Pole the sun gradually loses its rays and eventually dies out, while the stormy sea strangely produces no more foam or the phosphorescence typical of tropical waters. The ship roams in darkness, threatened by giant waves. Finally, over one of these huge waves, a gigantic black galleon rises, plunging and catapulting the protagonist over the mysterious ship. He immediately hides, but the crew of decrepit men seem unaware of his presence. Accustomed to life on the ghost prison ship, pushed further and further south by the currents, he begins compiling a handwritten memoir that closes shortly before the ship is swallowed by a vortex. The manuscript will be found inside a bottle.
text_b: This story is based on legends told in antiquity in the ancient Russian city of Novgorod (the capital of the Novgorod Republic). The merchants of Novgorod are feasting in a beautiful palace. A young gusli interpreter named Sadkó boasts that he can bring to his land a sweet-voiced bird of happiness. The merchants mock him for his bravery and tell him that his quest is impossible. Sadko, however, sets out on a journey to bring the bird of happiness to Novgorod. The daughter of the Ocean King offers to help him: she is hypnotized by Sadkó's singing and is in love with him. Sadkó visits many lands in his search for the bird, including India, Egypt and other countries. Sadko couldn't capture the bird of happiness and returned empty-handed. But upon his return to Novgorod, Sadko realizes that there is no better land than his homeland, and there is no need to go far in search of one's own happiness.

anchor_text: Jim Bennett is a highly indebted and gambling professor of literature. He borrows money from several loan sharks and gambles it away. His mother Roberta gives him $260,000 to pay off his debts and save his life. He gambles away that money, too. One of the criminal moneylenders finds out that basketball player Lamar is in Bennett's class and forces Bennett to get Lamar to win the next game by no more than 8 points. Bennett borrows money again and bets anonymously on the basketball game. But even the large profit is not enough to pay off his two main creditors, who are now threatening his life. He meets the two of them at a gambling establishment controlled by an even more powerful underworld figure, so that neither of them can forcibly take his money. At the roulette table, he puts all his money from the basketball bet on black, wins, and for the first time doesn't just play on. He leaves the winnings on the table for his creditors and leaves the gambling hell with not a penny in his pocket. Outside, he throws his jacket into a garbage can and rejects the offer of one of the two moneylenders to pay him his six-figure surplus from the roulette game. At dawn, he runs free across the city and ends up knocking on the door of his student Amy, who has fallen in love with him over the course of the film.
text_a: Coming from a very wealthy family, having never lacked for anything, Jim Bennett is an associate professor of contemporary literature and he tries in vain to teach existentialism to students who seem more concerned with collecting their points than with bits of literary culture. Dramaturge who does not know, he committed in 2007 a literary success that brought him some notoriety. Nothing since has brought him any excitement other than gambling, in which he draws adrenaline, the fuel necessary to continue his suicidal lifestyle, the only thing that gives meaning to his existence. Over-indebted, he owes considerable sums - which he has no intention of repaying - to the bosses of the Caucasian, African-American and Korean pimps, who are sympathetic to him. However, some are threatening to his mother and his girlfriend, one of his students, the only one who seems to demonstrate a certain literary talent and who finances her studies by working as a waitress in the underground gambling hall of the Korean underworld. At the same time, their relationship gets complicated. The girl is much bolder than Jim first imagined. But nothing could keep him away from the gambling tables. To lift the contracts that weigh on his loved ones, but not only, by paying off his debts, he agrees to participate in the organization of a rigged basketball game involving one of his students.
text_b: George is a teenager in New York City who is experiencing a peculiar phase of his existence. Based on the axiom that man is mortal, he believes that homework and schoolwork are useless pastimes, all of which occupy only a limited time in life. Only his great talent in art, which he expresses by scribbling something as little as he can, manages to shake him a little from this torpor. As the school year draws to a close, George has managed to make it almost to the end without ever really studying; even during art lessons, the only subject he should be interested in, he proves to be absent and lazy. A few months after graduation, he spends most of his time alone, and often wanders away from the institute, repeatedly risking expulsion. He has no interest in his future - neither in finding a college to enroll in later, nor in making friends, much less in finding a girlfriend. One day, he impulsively takes the blame for a cigarette smoked on the roof of the school, thus defending the prettiest girl in the class, Sally, popular and from a rich family. In return, she begins dating him and introduces him to her circle of friends, opening the door for George to an extremely worldly life of partying, museum visits, and idle walks around the big apple.

anchor_text: Carol White is a housewife who lives in a wealthy suburb of Los Angeles. She spends her days doing activities like gardening and aerobics. Her marriage and family life seem stable but shallow, and her friends are polite but distant. After renovating her home, Carol suddenly developed physical symptoms when she was near certain everyday chemicals: she coughed uncontrollably when she was driving and inhaled exhaust fumes from a nearby truck; she had trouble breathing at a baby shower; and she suffered a nosebleed when she went to a hairdresser for a long haircut. As her symptoms worsen, the chemicals that trigger them seem to be ubiquitous. Eventually, it collapses in a cleaning process that is fumigated with pesticides. The doctors have no idea how to cure or help Carol. Her psychotherapy sessions aren't showing any signs of success either. She realizes that she is alone with her illness, while her surroundings are indifferent to her; her life can no longer function like this. Carol leaves behind her house, her possessions, and her old life. Without her husband, she moves to Wrenwood, a creepy new-age community for people with environmental illnesses in the middle of the desert. Wrenwood, which has sectarian overtones, is run by a man whose relentless motivational speeches amount to "psychological fascism". Even in a community of people with similar health problems, Carol becomes increasingly isolated. She moves into an igloo, isolated from the rest of the community. The movie ends with Carol looking in the mirror and saying to herself, "I love you".
text_a: After escaping from a satanic cult as a child, kindergarten teacher Laura's past comes back to haunt her when, one night, she spots several cult members around the bed of her eight-year-old son, David. Since their appearance, the latter seems to be infected with a rare disease that makes him thirsty for blood, especially as he vomits blood. As for his body, it is now covered with burns. Arrested by the home invasion, a detective, Paul, doubts the woman's complaint because it seems that she is paranoid because there is no scientific evidence of the existence of these people. Plus, she's discredited because Laura's living under a new identity as if she's desperate to leave no trace so they can't find her. Convinced that Paul does not believe her and that the doctors who are dealing with the alarming case of her child are part of the sectarian clan that wants to own it, Laura immediately flees with David whose hunger grows more and more, a kind of vampire who must kill to survive...
text_b: In 1987, Carol and her husband Greg were living with their young son Rory in a luxurious mansion in the San Fernando Valley, a suburb of Los Angeles. Carol, a conscientious and hardworking housewife, suddenly has breathing problems when she finds herself in the middle of traffic and becomes sensitive to all kinds of chemicals and synthetic materials until she has severe reactive seizures. After medical examinations and tests by an allergist, it appears that she suffers from an immunological disorder (a chemical hypersensitivity) also called "20th Century Disease". With no improvement, Carol became interested in New Age therapies. She signed up for one of these treatments and became a patient at Peter Dunning's specialized non-violent Wrenwood Center in a remote desert area of New Mexico. She develops friendships with other patients and will be the next occupant of the "igloo", a tiny bungalow, a kind of "bubble" mounted away from the centre. When she moves in, equipped with her respirator and oxygen bottle, Carol seems to find serenity in solitude and silence: now that she is safe and detoxified, she loves...

anchor_text: A valuable piece of jewelry belonging to the family of Thailand's Prince Gulab has been stolen from a museum in Bangkok. Immediately after the robbery, the Thai thief is shot by a Western-looking man who takes the gold chain. The gold chain is adorned by a black ruby. The chain has great symbolic value for Gulab; it proves that he is the rightful ruler of his dynasty. According to legend, any unlawful possessor of this piece of jewelry will be met with violent death. When all the Thai police investigations come to nothing, Prince Gulab enlists the help of international private investigator Rolf Torring. This one's bringing his buddy Hans Warren and his black sidekick Pongo. Right on the way from the airport to the city, Warren narrowly escapes an acid attack. Obviously they know about the arrival of the Europeans and want to sabotage their work from the beginning. Accompanied by a hand-tamed monkey, the three follow the few remaining tracks. Joining them is Mitra, the beautiful assistant to the museum director, who eventually turns out to be a real Thai princess and heir to the throne. The hunt for the jewels soon turns into a life-and-death struggle in Bangkok and the Thai wilderness, including unpleasant encounters with tigers, snakes, and gun-toting killers. Eventually, it turns out that a geologist named Perkins is behind the whole thing. Perkins sees the arrangement of the gems as the key to the uranium deposits he's desperate to get his hands on.
text_a: Van Damme plays Christopher Dubois, an acrobatic clown and pickpocket who lives on the streets of New York City in 1925. After stealing from the mob, he must escape the city as a stowaway on a boat. He is eventually discovered by the crew and imprisoned by smugglers and pirates. He is rescued by a British mercenary named Lord Edgar Dobbs (Roger Moore), who then sells him as a slave to an island where Dubois learns the Thai fighting style of muay thai. Dobbs helps (and to some extent exploits) Dubois, buying his freedom so that the now expert can represent the United States at a kumite held in the lost city of Tibet, where representatives from different countries such as Germany, Soviet Union, Scotland, Spain, Turkey, Brazil, Siam, Korea, Greece, France, China, Japan, Okinawa, Africa, and Mongolia battle it out for elimination, and the winner of the tournament receives a valuable statue of a pure gold Dragon. He is followed along the way by American reporter Carrie Newton (Janet Gunn) and heavyweight boxing champion Maxie Devine (James Remar). As the tournament progresses, Dobbs and his partner Harry are sentenced to death for attempting to steal the Golden Dragon. Ultimately, Dubois negotiates the lives of Dobbs and Harry, promising that if he does not win the tournament, he himself will spend the rest of his days in the lost city of Tibet, in exchange for the freedom of Dobbs and Harry.
text_b: A valuable jewel belonging to the family of Prince Gulab of Thailand is stolen from a museum in Bangkok. Immediately after the robbery, the Thai thief is shot by a Western-looking man who takes the gold chain. The gold chain is adorned with a black ruby. The chain has great symbolic value to Gulab; it proves that he is the rightful ruler of his dynasty. Legend has it that any illegitimate owner of this jewel will die a violent death. As all Thai police investigations come to nothing, Prince Gulab enlists the help of international private investigator Rolf Torring. He brings with him his friend Hans Warren and his black companion Pongo. On the way from the airport to the city, Warren narrowly escapes an acid attack. Of course, we know the Europeans are coming and we want to sabotage their work from the start. Accompanied by a tamed monkey, the three follow the few remaining tracks. They are joined by Mitra, the beautiful assistant to the museum director, who turns out to be a real Thai princess and heir to the throne. The jewel hunt in Bangkok and the Thai wilderness quickly turns into a life-and-death struggle, including unpleasant encounters with tigers, snakes, and trigger-happy killers. Eventually, it turns out that a geologist named Perkins is behind the machinations. Perkins sees the arrangement of the gems as the key to the uranium deposits, which he desperately wants to get his hands on.

anchor_text: Django, a bounty hunter, leaves Stonecity to track down Le Corbeau, a bandit who terrorizes the area. Meanwhile, another bounty hunter named Sartana arrives. In Django's absence, his younger brother Steve is instructed by the bank manager to offer Sartana money to dissuade him from robbing the bank. Sartana is offended by this proposal and dismisses poor Steve. Meanwhile, the bank manager is found dead by his men, and his niece is kidnapped. One of the men who found the director testified that his last words were Steve and Sartana. The safe at the bank was also found empty, which points to Steve as the culprit, especially since he was seen with Sartana. Steve is found drunk in a brothel, and he's lynched instantly. When Django returns to town, he finds his brother's body still hanging. He doesn't believe a word of the accusations against Steve and is looking for Sartana. When the two bounty hunters meet, they fight hand-to-hand, and Sartana manages to make Django understand that he has received a bizarre proposal from Steve. They open the casket of the bank manager and find it empty. They both go to Mexico, where they hear about a very rich gringo who recently arrived. They realize that it is indeed the bank manager, who has gone off with his mistress and is keeping a close eye on his niece. Django gets hired by the warden's men, and tries to free the niece, the only witness who could exonerate Steve. But Django has been recognized. He was captured and tortured. Sartana finally intervenes from outside the fazenda, rescues Django and retrieves the loot. The revenge ends with the execution of the director, as he attempts one last time to kill Django. The two bounty hunters then went their separate ways.
text_a: Django, a bounty hunter, leaves Stonecity to search for "The Crow", a bandit who terrorizes the area. Meanwhile, Sartana, another bounty hunter, arrives in town. Since Django is out of town, Steve, Django's younger brother, is instructed by the bank manager, who fears being robbed by Sartana, to offer him money. Sartana is offended by this offer, and sends Steve away. Meanwhile, the bank manager is found dead by his men, and his niece is kidnapped. One of the bank manager's men says his last words were "Steve" and "Sartana". Also, the bank is robbed, so for all the citizens, who had seen Steve and Sartana together, a manhunt begins. Steve is found drunk in a brothel, and lynched on the spot. When Django returns to the city, he finds his brother still hanging from the gallows, and, not believing the charges against him, goes in search of Sartana. Trovatolo begins a hand-to-hand combat, but Sartana reveals to him that he has received an offer from his brother, so Django begins to believe in Sartana's good faith. To find out which of the two is actually right, they open the bank manager's grave and find it empty. So they go to Mexico, where there's talk of a newly arrived gringo who's very rich. When we get to the farm, we find out he's actually the bank manager. Django then frees the kidnapped niece, the only one who could exonerate his brother's memory, but is captured and tortured. Sartana intervenes from outside the farm, saves Django and recovers the loot from the robbery. Completing the revenge with the murder of the director, whom Django had at first spared, save then to shoot him down after an extreme attempt by these to kill him, the two friends leave the farm, going each on their own way.
text_b: The film opens with a scene where Ricky, one of the men who participated in a robbery, is tortured by five of his men in order to make him confess where he is supposed to meet Django. He is tied with ropes to the horses of men, and when he refuses to speak, they drive him away. Django arrives too late at the scene picks up a piece of each rope and vows to avenge his friend. Django first finds Domingo, gets him to talk and gets three names, the fourth is unknown. It is then learned that the gang led by Mendoza robbed the safe of a bank, but that Mendoza died in the collapse of the building. Django in his quest for revenge will successively kill a professional card cheater who has just sold his girlfriend Jane to a supporter, he then kills the supporter and befriends the young woman, then despite the fact that he is injured, he will defeat in a duel another man and try to make him confess before he dies, the identity of the last comparse, but in vain, it is then that he discovers hanging on his neck the medallion of Mendoza. Django then realizes that he has faked his own death and will hunt him down in the galleries of a sulfur mine. A marshal on the trail of the stolen treasure will help him get out and let Django and Jane go free.

anchor_text: Young FBI agent Ray Levoi is sent from Washington to the Redskins' Badlands reservation in South Dakota to solve the murder of an Oglala named Leo Fast Moose. Sent by his Sioux ancestry, he is joined in the investigation by a veteran Frank "Cooch" Coutelle who, along with government operative Jack Milton, agree that the culprit is a certain Jimmy Double Look activist already in the crosshairs. There is in fact a civil war going on between the native traditionalists and the governmental ones led by Milton, and it is precisely in this scenario that Levoi receives the unexpected help of the indigenous policeman Walter Cavallo Esultante and the activist teacher Maggie Aquila Bear who, together with the shaman Grandfather Sam Reachers, is oriented into a world that belongs to him more and more because of the Native American blood that flows in his veins. Finding out in the end that Jimmy isn't responsible for the murder and that there's more to this act than meets the eye.
text_a: Ray Levoi (Val Kilmer), a young and efficient FBI agent, the son of a deceased alcoholic father, is sent to the desert lands of South Dakota to take charge, along with Frank Coutelle (Sam Shepard), a more veteran and experienced policeman, of the investigation of a murder case, committed on a Sioux Indian belonging to a resistance group that fights other pro-government Indians because of the discriminatory measures imposed by the government for the Indians living on the reservation. The policeman has a dark secret that he has never revealed to anyone, because he is ashamed of it: through his veins also runs Indian blood, paternal heritage. His work will clash with that of his colleague, who emphasizes that officially the matter is only of interest to the FBI to get a slice of it and never to solve the truth. So much so that he will be faced with the dilemma of whether to obey his superiors or to heed the voice of his Indian conscience.
text_b: In 1880, rancher Pop Jones, along with his daughter Sierra Nevada and cowboy Nat Collins, drove his huge herd of cattle from Texas to new pastures in Montana. On their way north, they're ambushed by Indians and Pop is killed. Sierra and cowboy Nat come to a village of Blackfoot Indians. But the chief of Colorado lets the two whites go free. The peaceful leader of the Blackfoot is convinced of the peaceful coexistence of Indians and whites. Sierra can make her way back to her late father's new pastures. There, however, she learns that wealthy rancher Tom McCord has illegally taken over the land. But Sierra can't prove that she's the rightful heir to the land. But cowboy Farrell comes to her rescue. Farrell's working undercover as a cowboy for McCord, but he's investigating McCord.

anchor_text: I was in Baltimore in 1954. The United States is entering a new era, and the Kurtzman family is about to go through a lot of changes, too. Ben, the youngest son, has always lived in an exclusively Jewish neighborhood. He then falls in love with Sylvia, the only black student in his high school, against the advice of her father, because of Ben's skin color (white). Meanwhile, Ben's older brother, Van, is fascinated by the beautiful and aristocratic Dubbie. As for the father, he's trying to save his little music hall.
text_a: Fifties, the Jewish Kurtzmans family, consisting of Nate, the head of the family who runs a burlesque theater and a lottery ticket counter, his wife Ada, a housewife who takes care of the house, and their two sons, Van and Ben. Ben develops a relationship with a black girl, which was frowned upon in those days, while Van develops a relationship with a high-class girl. The two brothers will have to navigate a world of racial and class barriers.
text_b: New York in the mid '80s. Bernard is a creative writing professor, once a successful author; he is married to Joan, who is tired of her husband's heavy ego. When the latter experiences a resounding literary success, the two decide to separate. Sons Walt and Frank become caught up in the centrifugal force of their decision, finding themselves emotionally unprepared.

anchor_text: Annie and her husband Philippe lead a peaceful married life. One day, a newspaper article led her to ask herself the question: "Is she up to it?" It says the modern bride must be erotic. Annie wonders, especially since Philippe is concerned about the tax audit of his company's accounts and seems distant. She's going to do everything she can to become the hot wife the press has been talking about...
text_a: The lives of industrialist Philippe and his wife Annie are threatened by routine, as well as the unexpected visit of Inspector Butor of the tax office. Annie considers herself neglected by her husband and embarks on a seduction play following the suggestions of advertising. The result is not what he hoped for, also because Philip is on the verge of despair because of the tax. After an unsuccessful attempt at adultery by Annie, peace seems to have returned between the two. Filippo liquidates the inspector and goes on vacation with his wife, but the couple's misadventures are not over.
text_b: Annie Braddock, a young woman from New Jersey with a degree in ethnology, unable to find a job, took a job as a babysitter for a wealthy family on the Upper East Side of Manhattan, New York, to care for their son. Very soon, the girl will feel that she is not really welcome, especially since the mother of the family makes it clear that her job is mainly to meet her smallest requirements...

anchor_text: A bailiff named Joe Tyler is assigned to a strange case. An attractive New York client, Sara Moore, wishes to divorce her husband Gordon, who is also her partner in a large Texas farm. According to Texas law, if Gordon hands Sara the divorce papers first, he gets away with it. On the other hand, if Sarah had sent the papers to Abraham first, she would have become the sole manager of the farm. The young woman also offered the bailiff one million dollars in exchange for him to play the role of a messenger to Gordon. Joe then sees a golden opportunity to leave his employer and quit his boring job, but things take an unexpected turn and he soon falls in love with his charming client...
text_a: Alex Lee is an employee of a bank in San Francisco. She has a large mortgage to pay off. Her boss is pressuring her to escort one of her clients into his leisure time, even though it's clear that the client wants to have sex. Lee then works part-time as a prostitute, known to her clients as Johanna. As a representative of a colleague, she visits the entrepreneur Bruno Buckingham, with whom she subsequently meets regularly. She meets his wife Virginia Chow, with whom she begins a lesbian affair. Buckingham's driver Tony turns out to be an undercover FBI agent. He demands that Lee help him with his investigation, and threatens to sue her for prostitution. Buckingham wants Lee to help him cover up a financial transaction. Lee is blowing Tony's cover. Buckingham flees; he wants to take Lee and his wife with him, but the women elude him. Alex and Virginia are going to Mexico together.
text_b: Joe is a law enforcement officer who invents ingenious ways to do his job. His next victim is Sara Moore, partner and wife of Gordon, a wealthy cattle rancher, who asks Joe's boss to send Sara a notice of divorce. But she doesn't want to know and does everything to avoid being found until she decides to fight for what she deserves. After doing his job anyway, Joe makes a deal with Sara by deciding not to notify her, but Gordon...

anchor_text: 1890 in Paris. Georges Duroy is an ambitious young man, who has to settle for a job at the railway office and living in a filthy attic. One night, he meets the prostitute Rachel, but also Charles Forestier, a former classmate from Algeria. The latter, who became political editor at the newspaper La Vie française, allowed him to meet influential women of the capital, including Clotilde de Marelle, Madeleine Forestier (the wife of his friend) and Virginie Rousset. Using his charm and intelligence, Georges goes from poverty to wealth by becoming the lover of Clotilde and getting hired by Monsieur Rousset, director of La Vie française. In a world where politics and the media scramble for influence, where sex is synonymous with power and celebrity is an obsession, Georges will stop at nothing to succeed...
text_a: In 1880 Georges Duroy returned to Paris after a stay in Morocco. There he meets his friend Forestier who gives him some money, enough to go to the cabaret where Rachel performs the song Bel Ami. A spectator interrupts his performance by whistling and shouting "To the gallows!" Georges Duroy silences him by punching him in the face. Rachel then approaches him as she continues to sing and identifies him as Bel Ami. Duroy will end the night with Rachel. Then Forestier brought Duroy into La Vie française as a journalist. Duroy has an idea for an article (Casablanca, White City but fails to write it. Madeleine, Forestier's wife and also Laroche's mistress, came to his aid, titled the article Le Maroc vu de mes propres yeux and had it signed by Duroy. Other women would help the arrival Georges Duroy in his ascent, until he became a minister. This is followed by a romantic boating party that degenerates into a domestic scene on the water... Madeleine divorced Forestier and married Duroy. The Minister of the Colonies, following speculation in Morocco, resigned and was replaced by Laroche. Duroy will eventually dismiss all his conquests and marry Suzanne, Laroche's daughter.
text_b: Georges Duroy (Robert Pattinson) is a man who has just completed his military service in Africa and arrives in Paris with no money. Protected by his old partner Charles Forestier (Philip Glenister), he manages to enter as an auxiliary reporter in "La vie française", newspaper owned by Monsieur Rousset (Colm Meany), in which Forestier has become chief, with the help of his wife Madelaine Forestier (Uma Thurman), a beautiful and intelligent woman who writes for him. At a dinner with the Forestiers, Duroy meets Clotilde de Marelle (Christina Ricci), who eventually becomes his mistress, and throughout the film proves her love and help. When Forestier dies, George marries his wife despite her questionable behavior. Madelaine "protects" George just as she did Forestier, so Duroy ends up taking the position his friend held at the newspaper. Duroy, unscrupulously, in search of profit seduces the wife of the newspaper's director, Virginie Rousset (Kristin Scott Thomas) but soon gets tired of her, finding her to be immodest. However, nothing prevents him from seducing the couple's daughter, Suzanne Walter (Holliday Grainger), with whom he ends up marrying when he divorces Madelaine after catching her committing adultery.

anchor_text: On the occasion of the birth of her child, expectant mother Chrissy DeWitt reunites with her old childhood friends Samantha, Teeny and Roberta in her hometown. The quartet goes to a former gathering place, a tree house, and reminisces about earlier times. By the summer of 1970, the four girls were inseparable, spending their vacations swimming, cycling and playing truth or dare. At the same time, they were drinking and playing pranks with four brothers from the neighborhood, and their interest in the opposite sex was growing. Roberta received her first kiss from one of the brothers, trying to cope with the death of her mother years earlier. Samantha, meanwhile, witnessed her parents' divorce. The big event of the summer, however, was the attempt to solve the murder of 12-year-old Johnathan Simms 25 years earlier by conjuring up ghosts. The film ends with the birth of Chrissy's daughter, who has been delivered by Roberta, among her friends.
text_a: In 1991, four childhood friends reunite in their hometown of Shelby, Indiana. Samantha Albertson (Demi Moore), a science fiction writer, narrates the story, as a teenager (played by Gaby Hoffmann), Samantha was considered the "rare" girl who liked to perform séances and was interested in science fiction and the supernatural. Roberta Martin (Rosie O'Donnell), a doctor, was a tough person (played by Christina Ricci) whose mother died when she was four. Chrissy Dewitt (Rita Wilson), who lives in her childhood home, is married and about to give birth to her first child, as a naive young woman (played by Ashleigh Aston Moore), her mother was overprotective of her. Tina "Teeny" Tercell (Melanie Griffith) is a successful Hollywood actress; as a child (played by Thora Birch), she had always dreamed of fame. Teeny and Samantha haven't visited their hometown in years. The story goes back to 1970, when the girls had two goals: to save enough money to buy a tree house and to avoid the Wormer brothers.
text_b: It explores the individual experiences of nine women told in a single sequence. Sandra (Elpidia Carrillo), is in prison and desperately wants to see her daughter who has gone to visit her. Diana (Robin Wright) is suddenly faced with a past relationship, when she is already pregnant with her new husband. Holly (Lisa Gay Hamilton) can't go on living until her stepfather acknowledges the damage he's caused. Sonia (Holly Hunter) reveals an intimate secret to her closest friends. Teenage Samantha (Amanda Seyfried) is caught in the middle of the destructive relationship between her parents. Lorna (Amy Brenneman) tries to comfort her ex-husband after his wife's suicide, only to find herself implicated in the tragic death. Ruth (Sissy Spacek) considers walking away from her married life during a meeting at a motel. Camille (Kathy Baker) faces the limitations of her illness. Maggie (Glenn Close) allows her own life to be overshadowed by her young daughter Maria (Dakota Fanning). The Director delves into the lives of these nine women who face life's joys and disappointments with an attitude that is both hopeful and heartbreaking.

anchor_text: The story of the film revolves around five strangers linked together by a series of events that happen on Christmas Eve. Rose (Susan Sarandon) is a divorced publisher who cares for her mother who suffers from Alzheimer's disease. Nina (Penélope Cruz) and Mike (Paul Walker) are a young couple on the verge of breaking up because of his jealousy. Artie (Alan Arkin) is an elderly waiter, who thinks of his late wife Angelina every Christmas and is convinced that she has reincarnated into Mike. Finally, Jules (Marcus Thomas) is a boy who deliberately injures his hands so he can spend the holidays in the emergency room, the only place that can bring back happy moments from his childhood. The adventures of the five characters are joined by the mysterious Charlie (Robin Williams) who comes to Rose's aid to invite her to take better care of herself, rather than just worrying about others.
text_a: It explores the individual experiences of nine women told in a single sequence. Sandra (Elpidia Carrillo), is in prison and desperately wants to see her daughter who has gone to visit her. Diana (Robin Wright) is suddenly faced with a past relationship, when she is already pregnant with her new husband. Holly (Lisa Gay Hamilton) can't go on living until her stepfather acknowledges the damage he's caused. Sonia (Holly Hunter) reveals an intimate secret to her closest friends. Teenage Samantha (Amanda Seyfried) is caught in the middle of the destructive relationship between her parents. Lorna (Amy Brenneman) tries to comfort her ex-husband after his wife's suicide, only to find herself implicated in the tragic death. Ruth (Sissy Spacek) considers walking away from her married life during a meeting at a motel. Camille (Kathy Baker) faces the limitations of her illness. Maggie (Glenn Close) allows her own life to be overshadowed by her young daughter Maria (Dakota Fanning). The Director delves into the lives of these nine women who face life's joys and disappointments with an attitude that is both hopeful and heartbreaking.
text_b: The film revolves around five characters, whose difficult lives intersect in a series of unexpected events that take place on Christmas Eve in New York City. The main character, Rose (Susan Sarandon), is a lonely publicist who struggles to cope with the illness of her mother, a hospitalized Alzheimer's patient, whom she has been caring for over ten years. Meanwhile, Nina (Penélope Cruz) and Mike (Paul Walker) are a young couple on the verge of breaking up due to Mike's increasingly jealous behavior. Elsewhere, Artie (Alan Arkin) is an old waiter who searches for his deceased wife every Christmas Eve. Finally, Jules is a young man who deliberately injures his hand so that he can attend a Christmas party in the emergency room of a hospital, as it constitutes the only happy memory of his childhood. In addition to the five main characters, the mysterious Charlie (Robin Williams) - a terminal cancer patient incarnated as an angel - is presented as the person who may be able to help Rose finally realize that she should live her own very happy life.

anchor_text: The caveman Atouk falls in love with the hot Lana, the wife of his clan chief Tonda. The jealous Tonda banishes Atouk from the clan. In the wilderness, he meets the simple Lar and the beautiful Tala. Together they go on various adventures and battles against dinosaurs. Atouk discovers the benefits of walking upright and the effect of certain plants as a sleep aid. Along with his new companions, he discovers the usefulness of fire and also invents music.
text_a: Wedding ceremonies are being prepared in a Bedouin village in Israel. Jalila (Ruba Blal) must welcome the second wife, much younger, of her husband Suliman (Hitham Omari). During the celebration, Jalila discovers that her eldest daughter, Layla (Lamis Ammar), is having an affair with a boy from her university, which is strictly forbidden and would bring shame to the family.
text_b: Atouk (Ringo Starr) is in love with Lana (Barbara Bach), the wife of the Tonda chief (John Matuszak). He offers her gifts, but she gives them back to her companion. After using a ruse to abduct Lana, he fails and is banished from the tribe. Atouk meets Tala, a beautiful blonde who falls in love with him, but he insists on winning Lana over. After several adventures, including the discovery of the use of fire (and roasted chicken), Atouk used new weapons and succeeded in defeating Tonda in single combat. Lana finally offers herself to him, much to Tala's chagrin. But, at the last moment, Atouk divorces Lana and proclaims his union with Tala.

anchor_text: The novel is about twenty-year-old Jimmy Rabbitte, who at the end of the 1980s with some friends founds the soul band The Commitments and becomes its manager. (Roddy Doyle later said that the characters in the novel were inspired by the young people he taught as a teacher. And the fictitious town of Barrytown was in part his own home town of Kilbarrack, Co. Dublin.) The novel describes the band's coming together, their first gruesome rehearsals, the first gig, the band's gradual rise to local hero and finally their breakup on the very night that a Dublin record company signs a recording contract. Jimmy's love of soul music, his dry sense of humor, his acceptance of failure with girls, beer in the pub, and Irish puns are his trademarks. For him, the band is a way to cope better with poverty and the threat of unemployment. When The Commitments finally ceases to exist, Jimmy decides to start over. This time he wants to start a punk band that plays country music.
text_a: Buddy, a young musician from the country of Nellofselek (read backwards), arrives in the city of Ankh-Morpork and gets a strange stringed musical instrument that saves him from dying in a performance and catapults him to fame. He decides to form a band with the dwarf Odro, who plays the horn, and the troll Cliff, percussionist. Soon they attract the attention of the merchant Y.V.A.L.R. Weirdly, he discovers that he can make a lot of money with them thanks to odd occurrences like people being willing to pay to wear T-shirts advertising the group. It's not the only strange behaviour the citizens of Ankh-Morpork are showing: "rock music" is invading them. Simultaneously, Death has disappeared from the face of the Discworld and his powers and responsibilities are being transferred to his granddaughter Susan Sto Helit, daughter of Mort and Ysabell from the novel Mort. Susan is soon drawn into Buddy's story and between the two they must investigate the origin of the strange music and Death's disappearance. Also revealed in Soul music is the fate of Mort and Ysabell. The magicians of the Invisible University also have a certain prominence in this novel, both in the role of the most extravagant aficionados that "music with rocks" can have and in that of researchers of the paranormal phenomenon that it supposes in the Mundodisco. Including the orangutan who also takes a small role apart from the magicians as a pianist in a concert
text_b: Two friends, Derek Scully and "Oustpan" Foster, come together to form a band, but soon realise they have no idea how the music business works and that if they continue like this, they won't get much further from their small neighbourhood in the north Dublin area. To solve this problem, they decide to recruit their school friend, Jimmy Rabbite, to be their manager. The old friend agrees, but on the condition of making radical changes in the band, adding a synthesizer. After this, Rabbite proposes a name change and calls the band "The Commitments". The manager turns the band into a synth-based group in what he would later call the great Dublin soul revolution.

anchor_text: A love story between the writer Iris Murdoch and John Bayley, from the beginning of their relationship, when the young girl had a very sociable character, while Bayley was very shy, to the years of their old age, when the husband took care of the elderly wife affected by Alzheimer's disease.
text_a: An illiterate cook working in a commercial cafeteria tries to attract the attention of a young widow. As they get to know each other, she discovers her inability to read. When he loses his job, she takes it upon herself to teach him to read in her kitchen every night.
text_b: It chronicles the life of the philosopher and novelist Iris Murdoch from her youth, focusing on the love story with her husband John Bayley, her academic breakthroughs and acclaimed successes and, above all, her coping with Alzheimer's disease, which took her life in the 1990s. He died on February 8, 1999.

anchor_text: Will Francis is a young English architect who leads a detached, routine life in London with his Swedish partner Liv and her autistic 13-year-old daughter Bea. The girl suffers from sleep and eating irregularities, as well as having, at times, antisocial behavior, and seems interested only in her artistic gymnastics lessons, so much so that the relationship between Will and Liv itself is in crisis. Complicating matters is the fact that he's not Bea's biological father, and he feels excluded from Liv's bond with his daughter. Liv and Will are also seeing a family counselor, but their estrangement continues.
text_a: Will (Jude Law) is a London-based urban architect whose life revolves around work and family. The daughter of his girlfriend suffers from autism, which makes life unbearable for the couple because it does not let them sleep at night and they are helpless in the face of the situation, causing conflicts between the two. A series of robberies in Will's office breaks with the monotony of his life and plunges him into a spiral of self-exploration where Will himself will deal with the miseries and grandeurs of the human condition all framed in the context of a modern London where the values (or lack thereof) of the materialistic and impersonal society of a large city are reflected in the interpersonal relationships of the protagonists as well as their concerns and longings.
text_b: Will Freeman (Hugh Grant) is a handsome and wealthy bachelor from London, whose priority in life is to evade any kind of responsibility. Will doesn't need to work since he lives off the royalties from a song his father wrote. When his imagination leads him to create an "imaginary 2-year-old child who cares like a single father" to meet single mothers and have dates with them, Will takes a big life lesson, as he meets a hopeless and rejected 12-year-old boy named Marcus Brewer (Nicholas Hoult), who is bullied at his school by the older kids. Also, while Will teaches Marcus to become popular, Marcus will teach Will that it's never too late to mature.

anchor_text: The world was destroyed by a nuclear war. Tony Lamont and Ruby, caught in a dust storm, try to reach Rick and Louise Maddison's remote ranch. Louise's father, Jim, refuses to let them in because there's only enough food for three people. But Tony breaks into the house. With that done, Jim finally welcomes them. He had them change their clothes and wash in clean water. Meanwhile, Rick found a radiation victim, Radek, who's in very bad shape. But against all odds, Radek recovers very quickly and shows abnormal behavior. He doesn't seem to be eating, but he's in great shape. Soon, it turns out that Radek is a mutant, who hunts for survival and feeds on raw rabbits. He even shows himself to be a cannibal by devouring an old prospector who had taken refuge in a mine. The survivors are trying to eliminate Radek. But he's not the only mutant out there. Their situation gets worse until the miracle falls from the sky. The rain, once pure, clears the radiation from the mutants.
text_a: In the aftermath of an atomic war that wiped out most of humanity and spawned radiation-affected mutant humans and animals, a group of people, including a geologist, the criminal Tony and his ex-stripper girlfriend Ruby, try to survive in the mountains sheltered from the same radiation in a shelter built by Jim, a former military man. Difficulties are not few even among the survivors themselves because of the lack of food and because of Tony who continues to create problems with his bullying and irresponsible behavior. At one point they are confronted by a monstrous being who manages to make telepathic contact with Louise, a survivor of the group.
text_b: The plot takes place in a village after a nuclear war caused by a computer error and the failure of the operator to prevent the launch of the missiles managed to realize the problem, but shocked failed to give the necessary orders to avoid the disaster. The place was destroyed and contaminated by radioactivity. The state of siege has begun and the selection of the strongest and healthiest to enter the Central Bunker has begun. The main character, played by Rolan Bykov, is a Nobel Prize-winning physicist, sheltered in the basement of the Museum of History along with other scientists, some of its officials and a small group of children from an orphanage who with their muteness punish the supreme stupidity of adults that led to the destruction of the world. The physicist spends most of his time writing letters to his son Erik, who evidently died in the nuclear disaster. Along with the rest of the survivors, they discuss the meaning of the hecatomb and whether this is the end of humanity. After the death of his wife and the suicide of one of the refugee scientists, he decides to move to the central shelter, from where he escapes to take care of the orphaned children and give them the last hope, thus restoring their speech. Upon his death, the twelve surviving children return to the surface of the devastated world, believing his last words: "Remember. The world is not dead... Go and walk until your strength is exhausted. Because the man who walks always has hope.

anchor_text: Niko, a young man from Berlin, has been living day by day since he left law school two years ago. The film, which chronicles the events of one day and the following night, shows Niko's wanderings through Berlin, and his sometimes tragic, sometimes comic encounters. He finds himself confronted by a variety of people: his father, who has just cut off his food, a failed actor, his lonely and desperate neighbor, a fastidious psychologist who withdraws his driver's license, a former classmate who continues to struggle with childhood traumas, drunken teenagers who come to his aid, and, late at night, a mysterious old man who collapses in front of his eyes, and dies in the hospital during the night.
text_a: Niklas (Gustaf Skarsgård) arranges his departure to London to study writing, thus escaping the grip of his mother (Li Bråhde) who has raised him alone since his father's disappearance. But on the day of his departure the boys from the clique of Annelie (Tuva Novotny), a troubled student involved in a robbery, come to assault him in the middle of the forest until they kill him, considering him responsible for the betrayal of this robbery. The next morning, Niklas comes out of the forest and goes to school, but everyone considers him invisible and they realize that he has become a ghost, so he must solve the mystery of his death before he dies permanently. And the only person who can help her is Annelie...
text_b: Niko Fischer is a 20-year-old man going through a very different time in his life. After he leaves the girl and drops out of college, due to little interest in either, his father cuts off his monthly support and encourages him to make a life for himself, earning it. But Niko's "no day" continues, between a psychologist who confirms the suspension of his license, a neighbor in need of an outlet, a former schoolmate full of grudges (but also capable of so much "psychological" truth, achieved through suffering, and not devoid of a certain aggressiveness), the failed life of a talented actor and other encounters, including a reflection, indirectly, on the meaning of death.

anchor_text: In the beginning, Seiji Kashiwada creates a new cursed video that is completely different from the original video. You can see him being killed by Sadako Yamamura herself. Sadako can't be seen in the video, so viewers think it's a suicide. Akane Ayukawa is a high school teacher. She learns from the students that a suicide video exists. If you watch this video, you're committing suicide. Akane Ayukawa doesn't believe the story at first. Suddenly one of Akane's students dies. Akane and her friend Takanori want to find the cause of the mysterious death. The two of them find out that Seiji Kashiwada has something to do with it. Seiji wants to use Sadako to throw the world into chaos. Akane must stop Seji and Sadako before the world falls into chaos.
text_a: Ryuji Takayama's body is examined by his friend and rival, pathologist Mitsuo Andou. Inside Takayama's stomach, Andou finds a note with a cryptic message "a gift for you". Meanwhile, Reiko Asakawa and her son Yoichi Asakawa, Takayama's wife and son, die in an accident. Andou begins to put the pieces of the puzzle together and learns about the cursed videotape, which kills him seven days after viewing it. Andou, disinterested in his own life, after the death of his young son, watches the videotape without caring too much about the curse. However, Sadako's vengeful spirit seems to have other plans for him.
text_b: Seiji Kashiwada creates a new cursed video, completely different from the original, where he himself is killed by Sadako Yamamura, but who is invisible in the video, so viewers think it's a suicide. This video was created by him specifically for the evil spirit because he wants to return to the world and the girl seems to be looking for someone where she wants to reincarnate. Akane Ayukawa is a beautiful young professor who learns the story of this cursed video from her friend and boyfriend Takanori. The latter is sought after by every teenager interested in watching this video that really shouldn't even exist. There are a few casualties, but it's believed to be a suicide. But one of Akane's students, who had been looking for the video for many, many days, dies.

anchor_text: In 2011, during a space shuttle mission in space, there is a mysterious incident with a sudden appearance of a nanoparticle swarm, in which an astronaut is killed and the space shuttle is severely damaged. Another astronaut, Brian Harper, loses his job because no one at NASA wants to believe his story. Not even astronaut Jo Fowler can confirm his report, as she fainted during the events. Ten years later, Harper meets the pseudo-scientist K.C. Houseman, who believes the Moon is a megastructure and has detected a deviation of its orbit towards Earth. With increasing evidence of gravitational distortions and catastrophic environmental anomalies, Harper believes him and invites him to NASA. This one reactivates the retired space shuttle Endeavour in an emergency situation. Harper, Houseman and Fowler take off with an EMP bomb aboard due to a tidal wave rushing towards the moon. A rover takes them to the moon's interior, where they're attacked by the particle swarm. You can escape to a sort of hangar, where the history of mankind is presented to Harper by means of a projection. So there was a peaceful civilization billions of years ago, but the artificial intelligence that served it turned against it. So it destroys electronic objects that support organic life. The trio finds the rover, damaged on landing, repaired and flies through the megastructure again according to Harper's plan. Eventually, Houseman sacrifices himself and is captured by the swarm along with the bomb. The bomb explodes and the two surviving astronauts find themselves on the devastated Earth, where they embrace their children again.
text_a: On January 12, 2011, astronauts Brian Harper, Jocinda Fowler, and Alan Marcus are on an orbiting mission aboard the shuttle Endeavour to repair a satellite. A mysterious force attacks them and causes Marcus' death. Brian manages to bring the shuttle back to Earth, but he is held responsible for the accident because no one believes him, not even Jocinda. This is going to profoundly affect Brian, his career and his personal life. Ten years later, Brian lives alone and is deep in debt. He is divorced from Brenda, who is now in a relationship with Tom Lopez, and their 18-year-old son, Sonny, is in serious legal trouble. Then Brian meets K.C. Houseman, a conspiracy theorist who is convinced that the Moon is actually a megastructure. The astronomer tells him that the moon has suddenly changed its orbit and is about to crash into the earth. Jocinda, now a NASA executive, made the same discovery. After an expedition to the Moon ends dramatically and confirms Brian's revelations, Jocinda thinks she has the solution to prevent this catastrophe, which would cause the extinction of humanity in 3 weeks, but which forces her to work with Brian again despite their grudge.
text_b: Three astronauts - an American, a Russian, and a Briton - land as the first humans on the moon. But to their amazement, they discover a British flag and a document from 1899. The UN is looking for Arnold Bedford, whose name was on the document. The UN people find the man in a nursing home in England. People think he's crazy because of his stories about a trip to the moon. This time he tells the story to the officials: the writer Arnold Bedford moves with his fiancée Kate to the house of a relative. The two of them meet Professor Joseph Cavor. Arnold and Joseph become friends and now begin researching together. The professor has discovered an element to counteract gravity. Coated with a kind of ointment, any object can now overcome gravity. He also designed a capsule to take him to the moon. The indebted Arnold wants to accompany the professor. Kate is forced into the capsule just before launch. The take-off, the flight and the landing are going smoothly. On the moon, they discover a highly evolved insect-like species called the Selenites. The professor's thirst for knowledge is reawakened, while Arnold feels threatened and tries to arm himself. Arnold and Kate can finally leave the moon, the professor stays behind voluntarily. The astronauts now discover another surprise on the moon. The subterranean vaults collapse as you enter. The astronauts can only save themselves now. The Selenites have fallen victim to the viruses that the professor brought with a cold.

anchor_text: Depressed worker Sandra is set to be fired from her solar panel company after a staff vote. In return, the workers would receive a bonus payment. Sandra can get the staff to vote again. In personal visits to her colleagues, Sandra tries to convince them not to follow such a vote against her. Reactions to their attempted influence vary widely. In the re-vote, only half the staff votes for Sandra's job. The boss, however, is impressed by Sandra's action and offers to stay anyway. Surprisingly, Sandra refuses, since her workplace would not extend another employee's temporary employment contract.
text_a: Angie, a 30-year-old British woman from a modest background and the mother of a young boy, has been accumulating unrewarding, low-paying menial jobs in London. She works for a British temporary employment agency that recruits cheap labour from the countries of the former Eastern bloc. After being unfairly fired, she decides to open her own temporary work agency with her roommate Rose, clandestinely without money, initially using the backyard of a friend's pub as a gathering point and their apartment as an office. They promise to completely regularize their situation as soon as their first profits and serious clients are acquired. Large clients promptly urge Angie to provide them with undocumented immigrant workers to exploit by explaining the huge profits to be made and the ease of abusing poor people without recourse in a mafia market that the government turns a blind eye to. Angie promises to quickly regularize her business, but the lure of large and easy gains in unhealthy environments eventually overcomes her ideas of human justice.
text_b: In Seraing, an industrial town near Liège, in Belgium, Sandra (Marion Cotillard) is a young wife and mother who works in a small solar panel factory. Suffering from depression, she is forced to take a leave of absence from her job. During their absence, their co-workers realize that they are able to carry out Sandra's work shifts by working overtime, and the factory management then offers them a bonus of 1,000 euros each if they agree, even if it means Sandra's dismissal. After discharge, she discovers that her job depends on the decision of her 16 colleagues, so she has to visit each of them over the course of a weekend to convince them to give up the salary premium that, however, most need to support their families. Sandra will then have to fight a hard battle to keep her job before the final vote on Monday morning.

anchor_text: As World War II raged in Europe, John Flaherty was ordained a priest. Before leaving to serve as a military chaplain, he celebrates the wedding of his best friend Ludovico. At the front, he was almost captured by German forces after watching a dying soldier. To save his life, he opens fire on the enemy soldiers, inadvertently launching a decisive counterattack. In high places, the priest's decision to use a weapon went badly, but the Vatican's liaison officer with the Fifth Army, Bishop Walkman, brought him to Rome to become the church's financial adviser. With the help of his friend Ludovico, who was still a soldier, he tried to save the Church, which was almost bankrupt in these troubled times.
text_a: Father Flaherty is an American priest who fought in World War II. During his ecclesiastical career, in order to get something into the church coffers, he agrees with the mafia with whom he shares the black market of cigarettes, while his purchase of a bank allows him to launder dirty money more easily. The man falls in love with a nun and deceives her to seduce her; when the woman discovers the truth, she breaks off the affair. His career leads him to become a cardinal and to be betrayed by a mobster who robs him of $250 million. Even if the traitor is killed immediately Flaherty faces a trial, but the Pope's death will be his salvation.
text_b: The film follows the career and rise of Stephen Fermoyle, a young American of humble origins. Convinced of his vocation, Fermoyle was ordained a priest in 1917. Fermoyle feels so close to the Catholic Church that his dogmatism prevents his sister Mona from marrying a Jew. Years later, when Mona falls seriously ill during her pregnancy, Stephen decides that the life of the unborn child comes first: Mona dies in agony. Tormented by guilt, Stephen leaves his priesthood and works as a language teacher in Vienna. Here he falls in love with the beautiful student Annemarie. But Stephen's calling to the priesthood ultimately proves to be stronger. Innerly matured, he entered the service of the Vatican until he returned to Vienna in 1938 in a papal mission. Stephen is supposed to help prevent National Socialist terror. But politics turns out to be much more powerful and Stephen has to stand by while Annemarie is held captive by the Gestapo. In 1939, just before the outbreak of World War II, Stephen was made a cardinal.

anchor_text: During World War II, the Vatican ran into financial difficulties. Because of his skills as an accountant, Father Flaherty, a young and ambitious priest from the United States, is sent to Rome in 1944. Father John Flaherty has a head for numbers, but he also believes that the end justifies the means. To raise money for the church, Flaherty soon makes deals with the corrupt Sergeant Verese of the U.S. Army, who trades goods on the local black market and is connected to the Sicilian gangster Don Appolini. Appolini is willing to finance Flaherty's black market cigarette sales through a Swiss bank account. In addition, disguised as an officer, he also begins a romantic relationship with the aspiring nun Clara, who is in a crisis of faith. They begin an affair without Flaherty revealing his true identity to her. One day, Clara sees Flaherty in priestly robes escorting the Pope. This shatters Clara's love and trust. Because of Flaherty's great success in raising funds, his methods are overlooked and he rises to the rank of monsignor and later cardinal. But when he loses millions in stock trades, he has to pay the price for his actions.
text_a: As World War II raged in Europe, John Flaherty was ordained a priest. Before leaving to serve as a military chaplain, he celebrates the wedding of his best friend Ludovico. At the front, he was almost captured by German forces after watching a dying soldier. To save his life, he opens fire on the enemy soldiers, inadvertently launching a decisive counterattack.
