In high places, the priest's decision to use a weapon went badly, but the Vatican's liaison officer with the Fifth Army, Bishop Walkman, brought him to Rome to become the church's financial adviser. With the help of his friend Ludovico, who was still a soldier, he tried to save the Church, which was almost bankrupt in these troubled times.
text_b: In 1917 Stephen Fermoyle, the son of a Boston streetcar driver, after studying in Rome became a priest; then he returned to Boston as a parish vicar. Here he sends a manuscript to his cardinal archbishop who, however, finds him ambitious and sends him to a small poor parish, where he finds the parish priest very sick. On the death of the parish priest, the archbishop hires him as secretary and takes him to Rome for the conclave as his assistant. Meanwhile in Boston she is faced with the choice of choosing the spiritual path or the human path when her pregnant sister is due to give birth and the lives of mother and daughter are in danger. He decides to save the unborn child. While in Rome he reveals to the cardinal his priestly crisis and then a period of priestly suspension is decided that he will spend in Vienna. There he becomes a language teacher, and almost falls in love with a student; nevertheless at this point he decides to follow his vocation. Returning to Rome, he was promoted to the Vatican Secretariat of State, and was entrusted with various duties, which he carried out successfully. Consecrated bishop, he witnesses the entry of the Nazis into Austria and finds the young student, arrested by the Gestapo. Returning again to Rome, he was named cardinal and archbishop of Boston.

anchor_text: At the beginning of the 1980s, the newly elected Polish Pope John Paul II (often mentioned in the book only as Karol, in allusion to his civil name Karol Wojtyła) threatens the Soviet power apparatus around Andropov with the resignation of his office in order to return to Poland and support his oppressed people. At the same time, the relatively young Jack Ryan moves with his family to a new house near London to work as a CIA analyst with the British Foreign Intelligence Service (SIS) on matters of the political leadership of the Soviet Union. A key role in the book is played by Ed and Mary Pat MP Foley, a CIA agent couple stationed on the ground in Moscow. One day, by chance, Ed meets Oleg Zaitzev, a KGB agent who has valuable information to share with him, but he makes one condition: he wants to get to the West safely with his family. Zaitzew gets a code name from the CIA, Rabbit. It must be smuggled out as soon as possible. This succeeds and, based on the information provided, the attack can be partially thwarted.
text_a: In 1981, Oleg Ivanovich Zajcev, a KGB officer working in the Kremlin's communications center, learns of a Soviet operation to assassinate Pope John Paul II, guilty of wanting to help the Polish people in their difficult political and social situation, with the support of the Bulgarian secret services, the KDS. Zaitsev, however, could not accept that an innocent man such as the Pope should be killed for purely political purposes and therefore decided to defect, after having made contact with one of the American agents of the CIA in Moscow, in order to inform the Western secret services of this operation and prevent its implementation. Jack Ryan, CIA analyst, gets involved in a field operation for the first time. His job is to support the British secret service team, MI6, which has the very delicate objective of expelling "Red Rabbit" Zajcev and his family from the Soviet Union. As valuable intelligence gets into the hands of the Americans and the British agents are deployed on the ground, and even what should be a simple analyst ends up on the front line to give all possible support. The Italian police forces are not informed to avoid leaks of news and the operation is carried out; in fact Ali Ağca will succeed in shooting the Pope, but the Bulgarian colonel who should have killed the Turk immediately after the attack to eliminate any connection between him and the DS, and consequently with the KGB, is stopped by Jack and after being interrogated, he is "disabled" and left in a car.
text_b: The head of US intelligence in Europe, General Bruce, is to prepare an exchange of prisoners with his Russian counterpart on the German-German border. On the way to the rendezvous point, he recalls three cases he supervised. The first case involves an Italian scientist who has developed a special type of rocket fuel. The scientist doesn't want to make the fuel available to either the Russians or the Americans. The double agent Perego offers the Russians to kidnap the scientist by helicopter. At the same time, it shall notify the Italian authorities of the project. With the help of US agents, the kidnapping can be thwarted and the scientist convinced of the pure intentions of the Americans. In the second case, the aim is to avert a possible Russian attack on two submarines conducting manoeuvres off the coast of Somalia. French underwater expert Laland can locate the Russian submarine base. The third case involves American agent Koulov, who, after 17 years in prison, manages to escape with classified information for General Bruce. He arrives at the rendezvous point a day earlier than scheduled. The betrayal of the Berlin head of the CIA Petchatkin reveals Koulow's hiding place. Koulov will be killed, but he can give the general a clue to the traitor. When Bruce meets his Russian counterpart and the exchange takes place, the two officers begin to philosophize. Bruce comes to the conclusion that war is hell on earth.

anchor_text: Vittorio, a young racing driver, does not know whether he should marry the rich widow Diana or the innocent young woman Adriana, which will force both women. Just as he was about to make that decision, Vittorio was killed in a car accident.
text_a: Adriano is getting ready. After a somewhat turbulent life, he marries Marcella. But the recent past and the beloved women of the past don't go away easily.
text_b: Vittorio, is a young motor racing driver, undecided whether to marry the rich widow Diana or the innocent girl Adriana. This brings suffering to Diana and Adriana; then Vittorio dies in a car accident.

anchor_text: Two narrators portray, in their own way, the lives of suburban American youth. A white student has documented her sexual experiences with her black teacher in a story that has caused a stir in her class, and a director is working on a documentary about American teenagers, following a lethargic student and his dysfunctional family.
text_a: Storytelling is divided into two separate parts telling two unrelated stories, Fiction and Non-fiction. Fiction: In a writing class, a student (Selma Blair) has an affair with her teacher. Non-Fiction: A reporter (Paul Giamatti) decides to film a teenage boy and his family.
text_b: Alex is a debt-ridden writer. He has to write a novel within a month to have the money to pay them off, and finally live with a modicum of financial security. But he has too little time to finish the book, so, with a ruse, he contacts the stenographer Emma and hires her to help him in the undertaking. Little by little, imagining the story taking shape, a relationship develops between the two that goes beyond the professional one.

anchor_text: During the journey to Uranus, the rocket crew is controlled by a foreign power. The crew then awakens as if from a dream and realizes that an inexplicably long time has passed. After landing on the icy and hostile Uranus, the crew strangely finds an area of forest with a breathable atmosphere surrounded by a mysterious barrier. When the crew member puts his arm through the barrier, Karl freezes to death. The zone inside the barrier is constantly changing its appearance. So a village appears, and even attractive women familiar to the astronauts of the past. They encounter strange animals, including a giant one-eyed rodent. The crew realizes they've fallen victim to a mind-controlled entity that seems to make their thoughts, desires, and fears come true. The alien brain plans to take over the bodies of the astronauts and then rule the Earth. The crew, especially Captain Graham, recognizes the danger and begins to defend themselves against the mental presence of the alien brain. The alien exploits their deepest fears until it comes to the decisive confrontation, which ultimately takes place in the consciousness of the astronauts themselves. This gives some parallels to the much more significant 1972 film Solaris, based on the novel of the same name by Stanisław Lem. The "Third Expedition" from the Mars Chronicles also has the motif of the illusions created by extraterrestrial beings.
text_a: Dr. Chris Kelvin (George Clooney) is tasked with investigating the behavior of a small group of scientists aboard the space station Prometheus, located on the distant planet Solaris, consisting of an immense ocean. When the rescue expedition arrives at the scene, they find a bleak picture. Almost all the members of the base are dead, and those who are left look crazy. The same thing could be happening to Kelvin, as he wakes up next to his wife, who died a few years ago. The plot is based on a novel by Polish writer Stanisław Lem, already adapted for television by Nirenburg (Solaris) in 1968 and cinema by Andrei Tarkovsky (Solaris) in 1972, whose central plot describes contact with extraterrestrial intelligence, the efforts of humans to communicate with an immense living being that they cannot understand: the ocean of the planet. Soderbergh has focused on the romantic story: the protagonist's reunion with the woman he loved, played by Natascha McElhone.
text_b: A crew is sent to Uranus by the UN on a mission of space exploration. During the voyage an alien presence briefly takes control of the crew's minds. The astronauts wake up normally but notice that a long period of time has passed, inexplicably. Upon landing on Uranus, the crew members find themselves in a forest eerily similar to Earth's, instead of the cold, desolate world they expected to find. The forest is surrounded by a mysterious barrier, over which one of the crew members pushes his arm, which remains frozen. The crew then comes into contact with a village, whose inhabitants, human beings, are all old acquaintances of the astronauts, who soon realize that this is all an illusion created by a very powerful alien mind, which has the ability to materialize the thoughts and memories of space travelers, pleasant as beautiful women to the male members, but also their fears and their worst memories. The crew gradually realizes the dangerous intentions of the alien mind and begins to fight it in order to return home.

anchor_text: A young father is hired to keep an eye on a stylist whose boss suspects her of selling her designs to competitors. He discovers that, at night, the stylist prostitutes under the pseudonym of China Blue. Fascinated, he continues his espionage. A regular peep-show guest, dressed as a priest and armed with a Bible and a Godemiche, repeatedly harasses China Blue, claiming to be his savior and redeemer. One day, the young father, who thought he had a happy marriage, is faced with the disintegration of his marriage.
text_a: Daniela Ansalmi is a beautiful young prostitute, seventeen years old, whom everyone calls "Blue Jeans", because of her blue jeans that she always wears. In the first part, she walks through the city while everyone looks at her, and she decides to seduce a man who was taking her in his car, but the police find them and realize that she was a minor, for which the gentleman is locked in prison. Daniela does not have a cleared address and therefore her behavior is inappropriate, for which in prison she clarifies that Dr. Anselmo Carlini is her father. Dr. Anselmo denies it to her, and says that he is not her father, but he is forced to take her with him to take care of her and take care of her.
text_b: Joanna Crane, an established fashion designer by day, leads a double life, becoming China Blue, a consummate prostitute by night. Two men discover her double life: a diabolical priest, who would like to redeem her with a vibrator-shaped dagger, and an electrician, who has become an industrial spy to improve the family budget.

anchor_text: Ruth, a Jewish woman originally from Berlin, escaped the Holocaust as a child and came to the United States after the war ended. The memories of her childhood are too painful for Ruth, and in time, she chose to remove them. So when, many years later, she is widowed and dislikes her daughter Hannah's non-Jewish boyfriend, Hannah finds this irrational. Hannah, with the help of her mother's cousin, begins to reconstruct the story of little Ruth and travels to Germany to interview Lena, the woman who saved her. Through Lena's memories of taking part in the Rosenstrasse protest, Hannah recovers her memory of the past and is finally able to marry with her mother's blessing.
text_a: Based on a true story, the film deals primarily with the Rosenstraße protests in 1943. The film begins at the funeral of the husband of Ruth, an elderly German-Jewish woman living in New York who has been acting strangely with her children since the death of her husband. His daughter Hannah thinks, after the appearance of a mysterious woman at the wake, that this behavior is due to some kind of trauma that the mother suffered when living in Berlin, at the time of the Second World War. In this way, she contacts first with who happens to be the mother's cousin and through a Jewish Center manages to find the woman who had saved her mother's life, whom she will visit in Berlin posing as a historian. Thus, very old Lena will tell you the vicissitudes that had to happen in just seven days and will help to put white on black in the explanations that Ruth never wanted to give.
text_b: An American journalist, a former CBS correspondent, arrives in Berlin just after the end of World War II. Hoping to find Lena Brandt, his German mistress whom he once passionately loved, Jake Geismer investigates the death of a G.I. in the Russian sector and discovers that he was Lena's lover. Lena's husband, a brilliant scientist, is he really dead, what was he manufacturing in Dora and under what conditions, why are the Americans and Soviets in such a hurry to get their hands on Emil Brandt and his works, how did Lena, a Jew whose entire family died in the camps, survive until 1945, that her file is in the hands of the Americans, why is she in a hurry to leave Berlin, who is manipulating whom? Jake will uncover the truth in successive layers until the final, abominable revelation on an airport tarmac.

anchor_text: New York, these days. Ruth Weinstein just lost her husband. Her daughter Hannah is in love with Louis, a Nicaraguan and will marry against the consent of her mother, who opposes this marriage without valid explanations. Hannah decides to go to Berlin to try to understand the reasons for this stubbornness. Investigating her mother's life, she discovers the truth whose origin dates back to 1943, in the Rosenstraße.
text_a: Ruth, a Jewish woman originally from Berlin, escaped the Holocaust as a child and came to the United States after the war ended. The memories of her childhood are too painful for Ruth, and in time, she chose to remove them. So when, many years later, she is widowed and dislikes her daughter Hannah's non-Jewish boyfriend, Hannah finds this irrational. Hannah, with the help of her mother's cousin, begins to reconstruct the story of little Ruth and travels to Germany to interview Lena, the woman who saved her. Through Lena's memories of taking part in the Rosenstrasse protest, Hannah recovers her memory of the past and is finally able to marry with her mother's blessing.
text_b: In Berlin at the end of World War II, journalist Jake Geismar (George Clooney) investigates the case of his former lover: Lena Brandt (Cate Blanchett). Your husband has disappeared and is being sought and captured by the U.S. and the U.S.S.R.

anchor_text: This is West Virginia. Rosalee Futch is a simple girl who works as a clerk in a shopping mall along with her two best friends Pete and Catylin. Like all the girls in town, Rosalee falls for Tad Hamilton, a Hollywood bachelor who's always surrounded by beautiful girls. One day Rosalee wins a contest, and as a prize she is given the opportunity to date Tad Hamilton himself. During the date, Tad is struck by the girl's simplicity and decides to follow her to West Virginia. This upsets Pete, the boy who has always been in love with Rosalee and was about to ask her to move with him to a nearby town. Rosalee decides to go with Tad to Hollywood, where he has to shoot a movie. Pete then declares his love to Rosalee by kissing her, but she, embarrassed and stunned, says nothing. It's only later, on the trip, that she realizes that Tad has a bad habit of acting out at all times and being in love with Pete, so she decides to go back.
text_a: Rosalee Futch (Kate Bosworth) works at a West Virginia ultramarine store, and dreams of one day meeting her movie idol, Tad Hamilton (Josh Duhamel). The opportunity comes in the form of a contest; the grand prize is a date with Tad Hamilton. Rosalee wins the contest, much to the chagrin of her friend and co-worker Pete Ames (Topher Grace), who secretly deeply and desperately loves Rosalee. The "Win a Date" contest is, in fact, a gimmick devised by Tad's agent, Richard Levy (Nathan Lane) and his manager, who is also named Richard Levy (Sean Hayes), to wash Tad's golfing image a bit. But the idea turns against them. When Tad meets Rosalee and discovers what he's been missing out on in the real world, he decides he wants to repeat the dish and moves to Virginia. Rosalee's dream come true becomes a nightmare for Richard Levy and, most of all, for Pete.
text_b: Priscilla Chase, a working woman, lives in Cleveland. She's been married to her biology teacher, Jack, for ten years, so things have settled down between the two of them. Priscilla has never felt fulfilled in her sex life, so on the advice of a couples therapist, she buys a vibrator. Jack is burdened by the frigidity of his naturally extremely attractive wife and is drawn to the high school student Kristen Taylor, whom he teaches. His ego is deeply offended that Priscilla can achieve an orgasm using a dead object, which he was unable to do in the 10 years of their marriage. He's getting involved with Kristen and finally feeling like a real man again. Priscilla, on the other hand, becomes almost addicted to her vibrator, so Jack can't stand it and eventually leaves her. While he gets his own apartment, Priscilla dreams of a swimming pool. She meets with pool builder Wayne, who invites her to his house to test a pool. Although Wayne is considerably older, shorter, and fatter than Jack, she has sex with him and is naturally satisfied for the very first time. From then on, she meets regularly with Wayne, for whom Priscilla is a veritable fountain of youth. When he then sees her casually conversing with Jack, he becomes a little jealous and struggles to make an indirect marriage proposal to her. Priscilla promises to give it some thought.

anchor_text: Katie Gregerstitch, naive little country girl, has a life planned out for her. While she is about to marry her childhood sweetheart, he postpones the marriage to devote himself to his modeling career, leaving their small hometown for New York. Seeing her little girl's helplessness as Valentine's Day approaches, her grandfather offers her a plane ticket to join his fiance. But when Katie arrives unexpectedly and discovers that her boyfriend is cheating on her, her world comes crashing down...
text_a: Katie Holstrom leaves her father's home in Minnesota to study nursing in the nation's capital. She is taken to her destination by a fence painter, a friend of the family, who gets drunk on the way, leading to an accident. Katie arrives in the capital penniless and has to take a casual job at the home of Mrs. Morley, whose son, Glen, is a congressman. Gradually, Katie is gaining the affection of everyone and is encouraged to take sides in the political struggle. He beats his opponent and his campaign is off to a flying start. But a problem arises, as the painter appears willing to recount his journey with Katie.
text_b: Katie Gregerstitch leaves her small Oklahoma town and comes to New York to be with her boyfriend. Arriving in the metropolis, however, Katie has a bitter surprise because he cheats on her with another woman. Undeterred, beautiful Katie feels that the time has come for her to leave her provincial air behind. He gets a job with a construction company and hopes to make a career in business. In his climb to success he will also find a new love.

anchor_text: Ben Crandall (Ethan Hawke) is a young boy with a passion for aliens, living in suburban Maryland. He likes to watch old science fiction movies. He has strange dreams every night. He sees a giant printed circuit board. With the help of his friends Wolfgang (River Phoenix) and Darren (Jason Presson), they manage to replicate this circuit and discover that it is used to develop a force field. Using this and materials salvaged from Darren's father's junkyard, they build a spaceship. They're calling it Thunder Road, after the Bruce Springsteen song.
text_a: Ben Crandall, a boy who loves science fiction movies, has very vivid dreams of flying in a space-like place, and shares his visions through drawings with his best friend Wolfgang Müller, a young genius, who is able to translate his dreams into a complex circuit board that actually works. After numerous experiments, they discover that the circuit board creates a sphere of generated electricity. By using a computer to guide it, it can be made to move around anywhere in three dimensions, it can even pierce through solid walls. With the help of their new friend Darren, they create a homemade spaceship out of elements from a dumpster, name the ship "The Thunder Road", and embark on an adventure to another galaxy where they find that things are not always as they seem.
text_b: Ben Cronin is a guy who has everything in life; a good family, he's loved at school, he has a beautiful girlfriend and a career as a swimmer. One day, the mysterious Madison enters his life, determined to seduce him, but what for Ben was just a fling turns into a nightmare. In fact, Madison suffers from borderline personality disorder, and she'll do anything to get Ben.

anchor_text: Oslo police detective Harry Hole is a shadow of his former self. He's an alcoholic looking for a purpose in his career. It was then that he met a new recruit, Katrine Bratt, who had just moved from Bergen. She investigates the disappearance of a married woman and mother of a little girl, whose scarf is found wrapped around a sinister snowman. Harry Hole, who also received a mysterious ornate letter from a snowman, is interested in Katrine's investigations. Katrine and Harry then discover that several women have disappeared under similar circumstances.
text_a: In Oslo, after a traumatic childhood, where he loses his mother, the famous detective Harry Hole (Michael Fassbender) investigates the disappearance of a young girl's mother. The only clue we have is that his scarf was found hanging from a snowman. With the help of a new police team, Harry will have to connect this new case with other old ones to uncover the truth before the next snowfall, as he is suspected of being a serial killer.
text_b: Kat is a typical 17-year-old with a boyfriend, friends, and a father and mother who don't really love each other. One day, Kat's mother disappears. For Kat, this is the beginning of big questions. She realizes that she doesn't really miss her mother. She's especially outraged by her mother's selfishness in leaving without telling anyone. So she gets used to living without her and even dates the detective in charge of investigating her mother's disappearance, because of a bet she made with her friends. And then he has nightmares, always about his mother. One big question haunts her: where did her mother go?

anchor_text: Oslo police detective Harry Hole is a shadow of his former self. He's an alcoholic looking for a purpose in his career. She investigates the disappearance of a married woman and mother of a little girl, whose scarf is found wrapped around a sinister snowman. text_a: Harry Hole is an alcoholic detective who investigates a murderer who signs his murders by drawing snowmen at the time of the crime. While investigating in the city of Oslo, he finds on his street Katrine Bratt, a young policewoman determined to take revenge on the industrialist Arve Støp, with whom he has an unfinished business. In the background the story is intertwined with a case many years earlier in Bergen, never fully solved.
text_b: Kat is a typical 17-year-old with a boyfriend, friends, and a father and mother who don't really love each other. One day, Kat's mother disappears. For Kat, this is the beginning of big questions. She realizes that she doesn't really miss her mother. She's especially outraged by her mother's selfishness in leaving without telling anyone. So she gets used to living without her and even dates the detective in charge of investigating her mother's disappearance, because of a bet she made with her friends. And then he has nightmares, always about his mother. One big question haunts her: where did her mother go?

anchor_text: In a Bulgarian concentration camp, Walter, a German sub-officer, tries to escape Ruth, a young Jewish woman, with whom he has fallen in love.
text_a: Set in Bulgaria during World War II, the film tells the story of a German officer who falls in love with a Jewish girl he is escorting to the camps. She'll die and he'll go with the partisans.
text_b: A group of Polish officers, former prisoners of war, settle in a deserted German town, except for the doctor and his daughters.

anchor_text: François Marin is a young French teacher of a 4th grade class in a reputedly difficult college in the 20th arrondissement of Paris. He will have to face his students: Esmeralda, Souleymane, Khoumba and the others. François does not hesitate to go beyond the academic framework and push adolescents to their limits in order to motivate them, sometimes even taking the risk of excess.
text_a: In a very small rural commune in Auvergne, the primary school is run by a single teacher, Georges Lopez, who has about fifteen children in his class ranging from kindergarten to sixth form. The documentary focuses on the teaching and living conditions in this particular setting, the relationship between pupils and their teachers, and the relationship between teachers and pupils' parents. The teacher's pedagogical approach is emphasised. Unlike most documentaries, this one has no voice-over commentary.
text_b: The film covers an entire academic year at a high school. François teaches French in a classroom where students come from very different backgrounds, cultures and attitudes, and he also acts as a tutor for these students. From the beginning the students take the school and the classes as a joke, the arrival of new students makes Professor Marin elaborate a dynamic to get to know each other. Later on, students become interested, for various reasons, in the way in which the teacher leads the class, with dynamism.

anchor_text: Justin lives in a kingdom where bureaucratic rules govern the lives of its inhabitants. The days of brave and heroic knights are long gone. Justin dreams of becoming as great a knight as his late grandfather once was. Justin's father, Reginald, the Queen's counsel and attorney, wants Justin to follow in his footsteps and become a lawyer. After a conversation with his grandmother, who encourages him in his desire and gives him a golden key from his grandfather, Justin sets out on a journey through the kingdom against his father's will. He wants to fulfill his big dream and become a knight. He also decides to search for his grandfather's lost sword. He passes his great love, Lara, who accidentally throws a sock at him. He thinks it's her going-away present to him. Along the way, he meets Talia, the courageous and strong waitress at a restaurant. There he meets the sorcerer Melchidares, who shows him the way. After a long search, he finds the tower where a knight, a wizard and a monk live: his grandfather's best friends. He is trained to be a knight, but the wizard sends him home because it is "too dangerous". He returns to Lara's sweet 16 party and tries to save her from kidnappers who serve a lord who wants to re-create chivalry. Together with Talia, who resigned, they're trying to free Lara. In the end, Justin fights the man who killed his grandfather on a giant wheel. Justin can win back his grandfather's sword and the evil one plunges into the depths. In the final scene, the queen restores the knighthood and his father signs the bill. Justin returns Lara's sock, but she thinks he wants to marry her and embarrasses herself in front of the whole church. Then Justin proposes to Talia, and she accepts.
text_a: The story begins with what appears to be a great battle between Kull and several of the king of Valusia's elite legionnaires. But in reality it was all a test to join the legion. Despite passing the physical tests, Kull is rejected because he has no blue blood. Shortly afterwards a messenger from the Royal Palace notifies that King Borna has lost his mind and murdered all his sons. The legion leaves with Kull for the palace where they find the king covered in blood and all his sons dead. The leader of the legion, Taligaro, asks the king what has happened, but the king, still mad, attacks him and when he is about to kill him, Kull intervenes to prevent him and after a struggle, the king is seriously injured. The dying king gives the crown to Kull knowing that many will now seek revenge against him.[2] Among those seeking revenge are Taligaro and his nephew, both of blue blood and potential heirs to the throne. Taligaro and his nephew decide to try to bring an evil witch queen back to life so she can finish off Kull and they can reclaim the crown. Queen Akivasha once brought back to life, seduces Kull and marries him. On the wedding night he poisoned him and accused a slave and concubine named Zareta, but Kull survived and awoke in time to prevent Zareta's execution, and together with her and Ascalante, the concubine's cousin priest set off in search of Valka's breath, the only thing capable of ending Akivasha. After achieving it, they return to the kingdom and begin a battle against Akivasha who had become a monster, after making him ingest the breath by a kiss, dies, reinstating Kull as king. Once installed as king, he reformed the legislation to give more voice to both the people and women.[2]
text_b: Justin is a young boy who lives in a country whose knights have been banished: the country is now ruled by bureaucrats. Since childhood, Justin has dreamed of becoming one of the Knights of Courage, like his ancestor Roland. But his father, Reginald, who is a Queen's Counsel, expects him to follow in his footsteps and become a lawyer. In order to make a decision, Justin visits his grandmother, Gran, who tells him the true story of his grandfather Roland: he was the best knight in the kingdom, until he was assassinated by one of his comrades-in-arms, the treacherous Sir Heraclio. She then gives him two options, either to become a bureaucrat like his father or a knight like his grandfather. Justin then decides to leave everything, even his friends Abuela and Lara, and run away to become a knight. During his tribulations, he meets a young waitress, Talia, who surprises him with her skills as a warrior. He also deals with Melquiades, a wizard afflicted with a personality disorder in the service of Sir Antony, and the handsome and boastful Sir Clorex. Justin continues his quest and arrives at an abbey where three people, Blucher, Legantir and Braulio, agree to take charge of his training as a future knight. The monk Braulio pits him against a dragon that is not really a dragon, but a hapless crocodile suspended from a remote-controlled flying machine equipped with a flamethrower. Blucher, on the other hand, is a former Knight of Courage, and provides him with valuable training in the use of weapons. Legantir, who runs the abbey, is well versed in the magical arts and watches over him with a stern eye. Meanwhile, shadows gathered over the kingdom: sire Heraclio and his sidekick Sota raised an army in order to carry out a coup against the Queen.

anchor_text: In November 1899, in Paris, the Apollonide is a high-end brothel. Madeleine (Alice Barnole) the Jew, a prostitute, dreams that her most regular client (Laurent Lacotte) proposes to her. She told him her dream, lying on the bed. He ties her wrists to the bedpost, slides the blade of his knife over her body, then puts it in her mouth and cuts off her cheeks at the corner of her lips. March 1900, the wound leaves a scar on Madeleine's face in the form of a melancholy smile. The Apollonide is managed by Marie-France (Noémie Lvovsky), whom its residents call Madame. They include: Léa the Doll (Adèle Haenel), Samira the Algerian (Hafsia Herzi), Clotilde Beautiful thigh (Céline Sallette) and Julie Caca (Jasmine Trinca). All of them are bound by the debts they have accumulated. Madeleine, who can no longer practise, is kept away from clients and takes care of the housekeeping. Familiars of the house have their favorite and their usual fantasies. The film chronicles the life of Apollonides until the dawn of the 20th century, a theatre of dramas, habits and life. It's a kind of closed door where the characters are the prostitutes and their clients.
text_a: In November 1899, in Paris, L'Apollonide is a house of prostitution for the bourgeoisie. Madeleine (Alice Barnole), the Jew, a prostitute, dreams that her most assiduous client (Laurent Lacotte) asks her to marry him. One day she dares to tell him her dream when she is lying in bed, but he hits her hard and injures her face. A few months later, in March 1900, Madeleine remains in the house of tolerance and we delve into the daily life of the L'Apollonide brothel, run by Marie-France (Noémie Lvovsky), whom her pupils call Madame. They include Léa la Poupée (Adèle Haenel), Samira l'Algérienne (Hafsia Herzi), Clotilde Belle cuisse (Céline Sallette) and Julie Caca (Jasmine Trinca). They all stay in the comfortable house because they have old debts with the boss. Madeleine, injured and sick, will no longer be able to work and her mission is now to take care of the housekeeping so that each client, who has a prostitute of reference, feels at home.[2]
text_b: The plot takes place in Paris in the 1930s in a loose-jazzy Viennese operetta version: Marcel, who works as a shop window decorator, surprisingly receives a ticket for the theater from a stranger for him. To dress appropriately, he 'borrows' a coat from his employer's stock at his own discretion. At the theater, he meets Madeleine,* a chocolate saleswoman, who immediately falls in love with the elegantly dressed and extremely charming young man. To be his equal, she confides in her friend Lily and the revue star Yvonne, who, with the help of the clothes from the theater fund, transform Madeleine into a lady of society. Marcel, in turn, becomes aware of the lovely young lady and courts her. So it is that after the show is over, the two of them go to a dance hall together. A found business card leads to Marcel being mistaken for Baron de Monteuil. That's why he has to give the baron's address on the card on the way home in the taxi. He takes Madeleine to the villa where, fortunately, no one else lives, and the young couple spends happy hours there. The next morning, however, disaster looms as Baron and Baroness Monteuil, who had been on a journey, return to their home. The baroness, entering her bedroom, encounters Madeleine, who is still in bed. She now believes her husband is having an affair. Marcel sees only one way to clarify the matter. He tells us what the connections really are. Madeleine's reaction, which Marcel did not necessarily expect, makes him happy, because it lets him know that she prefers a husband from her own circles to a nobleman. * in the French version Simone; ** in the French version Colette and Mariette

anchor_text: The city of Aran is being destroyed by the mole people, strange creatures who can't stand the sun and are therefore only active at night. Its ruler is Queen Halis Mojab. To avenge the death of the King of Aran and to free his son and his fiancée, Lot and Saliurà, from slavery, the strong Maciste sets out with his faithful black friend Bango in search of the mysterious realm. They enter the winding corridors of the underground labyrinth, quarrel with the High Priest Ka'ab, and face numerous dangers, including battles with lions and great apes. Eventually, they succeed - after the queen also falls in love with Maciste - to destroy the empire with the help of the slaves of the mole people: Maciste causes the center of the empire to collapse, so that the sun shines into the depths, which kills the mole people.
text_a: A volcanic eruption accompanied by a devastating earthquake destroys an island and kills many of its inhabitants. Under Maciste's leadership, the survivors can reach the mysterious Urias. There, Maciste meets Queen Amoha, whose people are hiding in the forest for fear of headhunters, savages bent on taking Amoha's throne. Maciste takes the side of the queen, who is captured by the headhunters and is to marry their chief. After many battles and feuds, Uriah's people are victorious. After a long duel with Maciste, the head hunter dies; the winner is rewarded for his efforts with the love of Amohas.
text_b: The city of Aran is destroyed by a race of individuals called "mole men". Maciste intends to avenge the king's death and free Loth, the crown prince captured by the mole-men and enslaved. Along with his black friend Bango, Maciste begins to search for the mysterious people, but when he finds them he cannot prevail and is captured. They are taken as slaves to the underground city but manage to escape. Later Maciste returns to the underground kingdom and after having fought several times against the enemies takes advantage of his superhuman strength to collapse the supports of the city's vault, burying all its inhabitants alive. The men of Aran manage to save themselves and return free to rebuild their city, while Maciste renounces glory to set off for new adventures and new ventures.

anchor_text: Touko Laaksonen returns from the front of the Second World War. In Helsinki, homosexuality is hardly more tolerated than in the countryside where he comes from. Besides her work as a graphic designer, Touko unleashes her fantasies in drawing, depicting muscular and highly erotic men. They are of such quality that his dancing partner pushes him to find a way to publish them.
text_a: In a small town in rural Poland in 1989, Andrzej knows that he is not like his peers, but only several years later, having become a loving husband and father in the meantime, will he admit to himself and others that he is transgender, embarking on a journey riddled with sacrifice and prejudice.
text_b: Touko Laaksonen returns home after serving in the Second World War. In postwar Helsinki, he made a name for himself with his homoerotic drawings of muscular men. Before finding fame, she encounters challenges from her sister and Finnish society due to her art.

anchor_text: A professor (Pierce Brosnan) and his wife (Susan Sarandon), as well as their youngest son Brian, are faced with the death of their eldest son, Bennett, who was killed in a car accident. His girlfriend (Carey Mulligan), who survived the accident and is pregnant with the missing man, moves in with them. Everyone's trying to grieve with difficulty.
text_a: Jean (Heather Locklear) has to move town every time she suffers a new love disappointment, but her daughter Holly (Hilary Duff) is already tired of never having a "home" and always moving back and forth with her mother and younger sister Zoe (Aria Wallace). So, Holly, you create the perfect man so that your mother is happy, so you don't have to move, but you're in trouble and you have to choose the truth.
text_b: Allen Brewer and his wife, Grace, are trying to cope with the loss of their eldest son, Bennett, following a fatal car accident. When a young woman named Rose contacts them to tell them she is pregnant with Bennett's child, the Brewers invite her to stay in their home. At first, Rose's presence threatens to ruin the battered family ties, but eventually her interaction with each of the Brewers turns out to be exactly what they need to become the united family of old.

anchor_text: Three radical environmentalists decide to team up to blow up a hydroelectric dam. But the death of a camper on the shores of the lake due to an unexpected flood makes them doubt the validity of their action.
text_a: A father and his 13-year-old daughter live in the woods of an Oregon nature reserve without anyone knowing and without contact with the modern world. A small mistake puts the authorities on his trail and his hitherto idyllic existence at risk.
text_b: The three environmentalists Harmon, Dena and Josh decide to join forces to make the most sensational gesture of their lives: blowing up a hydroelectric dam symbolic of the capitalist industry that consumes energy and precious natural resources. Their goal, however, is not to harm people but to change the world for the better.

anchor_text: On the East Side of New York City, luxurious buildings are slowly emerging from underprivileged neighborhoods. Thus the opulence shamelessly adjoins the dilapidated buildings, inhabited by a miserable population. At the end of a street that ends abruptly on the banks of the Hudson, plays the ordinary theater of social misery. Drina takes part in a strike at her factory for higher wages; Joel, unemployed, works odd jobs and regrets the failure of his career as an architect; a gang of kids play rough and learn the barbaric rules of the gangs. The notorious gangster "Baby Face" returns to the neighborhood incognito to reunite with his mother and his childhood sweetheart. His disappointment will be like this street with no prospects for the future.
text_a: The protagonist of the film is a street on the East Side of New York where two models of American life side by side; one is that of the rich inhabitants of the "good" buildings, the other is made up of the miserable constructions (slums). It is the story of a gangster's return home, which turns out to be a failure, as his mother rejects him and his old flame now practices prostitution. Desperate for his now anonymous presence, he then decides to carry out a super hit that will, however, have the most tragic consequences.
text_b: The film tells the story of a woman who, coming from the criminal milieu of New York, comes into conflict with her old friends when she rescues a neighbor boy whose family is murdered in cold blood by gangsters. At first, she clumsily tries to get rid of the boy. When the killers reappear to retrieve the boy, she turns against them. A wild chase begins, ending at the gangsters' headquarters. At the heart of the film is a character study of an aging gangster bride who desires nothing more than to escape the milieu to spend the rest of her life in peace with her savings. The boy, the son of a Puerto Rican friend, is forced by her when the gangsters are already in the house to kill the family. It relaxes. A conflict emerges between the disoriented boy who has just lost his family and the grumpy woman whose life has also been thrown upside down by this event. When she realizes the futility of running away and takes the initiative, this also leads to a greater closeness between the two protagonists.

anchor_text: Alex Gates is a fine wine merchant from Miami who, in addition to being heavily in debt, also has to support his alcoholic wife, Suzanne, and stepson Jason. Alex decides to steal a very valuable necklace with the complicity of his friend Victor, to get out of debt, but during the theft Suzanne and Jason will also be involved.
text_a: Three boys (Kevin is a clerk, Rob repairs appliances, and Sam) delight in planning perfect robberies that they do not execute. One day, they are pursued by the notorious criminal Leo Gillete who steals a plan to rob a jewelry store and forces them to work together to seize a treasure trove of bonds stored in a bank. At first the young people decide to cooperate, but the understanding will gradually become more complicated.
text_b: With his stepson Jason, Alex Gates runs a small imported wine company that caters to most of the millionaires in a select Miami neighborhood. For work reasons, one day he meets Gabriela, a domestic servant of a wealthy couple who owns a very valuable diamond necklace. Alex decides to steal the jewel and escape from the city with Gabriela, but fate prepares a surprise that will completely change the course of events.

anchor_text: Nothing predicted that Alex Gates, a successful wine merchant in Miami, would one day rob one of his best customers... A million dollars in a diamond necklace. With the help of Gabriella and an old accomplice, Victor Spansky, Alex masterminds his robbery. All he has to do is run to New York with the beautiful Gabriella in order to get the necklace, but Suzanna, his wife, finds nothing to stop him but to knock him out. She ran off with the suitcase where the necklace was hidden! Victor and Alex set off in pursuit...
text_a: Three boys (Kevin is a clerk, Rob repairs appliances, and Sam) delight in planning perfect robberies that they do not execute. One day, they are pursued by the notorious criminal Leo Gillete who steals a plan to rob a jewelry store and forces them to work together to seize a treasure trove of bonds stored in a bank. At first the young people decide to cooperate, but the understanding will gradually become more complicated.
text_b: Alex Gates is a fine wine merchant from Miami who, in addition to being heavily in debt, also has to support his alcoholic wife, Suzanne, and stepson Jason. Alex decides to steal a very valuable necklace with the complicity of his friend Victor, to get out of debt, but during the theft Suzanne and Jason will also be involved.

anchor_text: While the President of the United States is being held hostage on the Vegan base on the moon, a clone replaces him in the White House. Detective Richard Dick Dix investigates on behalf of the FBI, but naively, he has no idea that he's in the middle of a huge conspiracy.
text_a: A Concorde supersonic jet acquired by an American company makes a flight from Washington to Paris to Moscow. During the journey he will suffer an attack with a missile towards Paris and a sabotage on his way to Moscow, interweaving several stories of some passengers.
text_b: A conspiracy to supplant the President of the United States is afoot and Agent Richard "Dick" Dix will have to travel to a moon base on a mission to rescue the President and avert a global catastrophe.

anchor_text: President Bill Clinton is kidnapped and replaced by an alien clone. Police Chief Halverson assigns Marshal Richard "Dick" Dix, along with Cassandra Menage, to free the President from the clutches of Dr. Griffin Pratt. This one's on a lunar base doing cloning research. Bradford Shitzu and Captain Valentino Di Pasquale are helping Dix and Menage. Dix encounters Dr. Künstler, who wants to seduce him to get to the real president and kill him. Dix and Menage flee with the president from the moon to Earth, where they plan to exchange him for the wrong man at an opera performance.
text_a: Dick Hudson (whose name in the original English is Dick Dix), a clumsy investigator who claims to have been part of the Marines, is given the task of conducting a top-secret operation: to save the President of the United States of America Bill Clinton, who has been replaced by aliens with his clone. Despite Dick Hudson's lack of understanding of the true nature of the mission (he believes it is normal for the President of the United States to be a clown), the former Marine accepts the assignment. During a party Dick Hudson meets Captain Valentino Fumagalli, with whom he later travels to Paris to see a Three Tenors concert. After the latter performed songs by the Village People, Hudson and Fumagalli manage to replace the cloned president with the original, putting an end to the alien machination.
text_b: A talented engineer and shrewd businessman, Kevin Harrison is a wealthy man, admired by American society for the powerful weapons systems he designs for the military. But Kevin Harrison is also an unscrupulous man who does not hesitate, to enrich himself, to sell his sophisticated weapons to countries unfavourable to the United States. Compromising information about his actions reaches his own mistress, a journalist who escapes him by boarding the newly acquired Concorde by Eli Sands' airline, and who makes a connection to Moscow as the 1980 Summer Olympics approach. Determined to eliminate this troublesome witness, Harrison arranges a series of bombings to destroy the supersonic in flight. But he counted without the French commander Paul Metrand and his partner, none other than Joe Patroni. While the two pilots have no idea where the attacks are coming from, their skill and composure have saved the day on more than one occasion.

anchor_text: In the Norwegian city of Tromsø, the Swedish and Norwegian police are cooperating in the search for the murderer of a 17-year-old girl. When police officer Jonas Engström accidentally shoots a colleague in pursuit of the perpetrator, he tries to blame the murderer. Because of his guilt and the midnight sun, Engström can no longer sleep (hence the film title Insomnia).
text_a: After Jonas' parents are killed in a tragic car accident, the high school senior moves to an island to live with his recently widowed grandfather Friedrich Hansen. He approaches his classmate Nina. But the arrival of his new German teacher, Helena, turns his world upside down. She recognizes his talent for writing and thus builds an intimate relationship of trust with him. Jonas, however, has no idea what Helena's plan really is.
text_b: Jonas Engstrom, a Swedish police officer stationed in Oslo, is sent with his partner to a small village in northern Norway to investigate the murder of a young teenage girl. He accidentally kills his teammate in a chase with the original killer. Later, he gets a call from the killer claiming to have seen him kill his partner. The detective must then deal with the insomnia that gains him in the land where the sun does not set for two months, in addition to the witness killer who begins to play with him.

anchor_text: In 1942, the Reich Protector Reinhard Heydrich is killed by the surgeon Dr. Frantisek Svoboda. Svoboda's escape is supported by history professor Stephan Novotny, himself under Nazi suspicion, and his daughter Masha. In retaliation for the assassination of Heydrich, 400 citizens of Prague, including Novotny, are to be executed as hostages if the assassin is not handed over to the Gestapo. The brewery owner Emil Czaka, apparently a supporter of the resistance, but in reality a Gestapo spy, helps to compile the hostage lists. The executions are about to begin. The Czechoslovak resistance movement, with the help of falsified evidence and witness statements, gets Czaka himself suspected of Heydrich's murder.
text_a: On 27 May 1942, during the Nazi occupation of Czechoslovakia, Reichsprotektor Reinhard Heydrich was assassinated in the streets of Prague by surgeon Franticek Svoboda. But the driver of the car in which he was to flee was apprehended, Svoboda fled on foot, hiding under a porch, he saw a young woman voluntarily pointing the Germans in the wrong direction, then walking home. Not knowing where to hide as the curfew hour approaches, Svoboda rings the doorbell at the young woman's (Macha) home and her family welcomes her in for the night. The next day, the Gestapo arrested Macha's father and young students in order to make them hostages who would be shot daily until Heydrich's murderer was found. At the same time, the Resistance network to which Svoboda belongs is centered on a wealthy brewer, Emil Czaka. Through a complex series of events and the active complicity of many Prague citizens, the Resistance succeeded in making it appear that Czaka murdered Heydrich, but not before the Nazis executed a large number of hostages.
text_b: In 1929, young German Navy officer Reinhard Heydrich is dishonorably discharged for having an affair with a woman by refusing to marry her. He'll marry another, Lina, who'll get him into the Nazi Party, not yet in power. Heydrich will be put in charge of counterintelligence for the SS. In 1942, when the Third Reich is at the height of its power, the Czechoslovak resistance in London decides to plan an ambitious military operation, called Operation Anthropoid. Paratroopers Jozef Gabčík and Jan Kubiš are sent to Prague to assassinate Heydrich, now governor of Bohemia and one of the most ruthless Nazi generals.

anchor_text: In 1942, the Reich Protector Reinhard Heydrich is killed by the surgeon Dr. Frantisek Svoboda. Svoboda's escape is supported by history professor Stephan Novotny, himself under Nazi suspicion, and his daughter Masha. In retaliation for the assassination of Heydrich, 400 citizens of Prague, including Novotny, are to be executed as hostages if the assassin is not handed over to the Gestapo. The brewery owner Emil Czaka, apparently a supporter of the resistance, but in reality a Gestapo spy, helps to compile the hostage lists. The executions are about to begin. The Czechoslovak resistance movement, with the help of falsified evidence and witness statements, gets Czaka himself suspected of Heydrich's murder.
text_a: Prague, 1942: the present-day capital of the Czech Republic is occupied by the Nazis. The ruthless governor Reinhard Heydrich is shot during a partisan action, and the Germans decide to impose curfew and heavy repression. The bomber, Dr. František Svoboda, took refuge in the home of a patriot named Štěpán Novotný. To avoid further reprisals on the already tortured people of Prague, the Resistance manages to hand over to the Nazis the brewer Czaka, a collaborator infiltrated among the patriots and responsible for the death of several of them.
text_b: The story reconstructs the precise development of a plan that was to lead to the assassination of Reinhard Heydrich, the deputy imperial protector in Bohemia and Moravia. The British government instructs Czech nationalists to assassinate this leading representative of the hated Nazi occupation regime. At the center of the resistance are Karel Vavra and Jan Hanka, who after the assassination are chased and confronted by the police forces of the occupiers and finally give themselves a merciless firefight that lasts for many hours in the Prague Church of St. Cyril and Methodius. As a result of the assassination, the SS and other police forces take terrible revenge and commit a massacre in the small Czech town of Lidice as a retaliatory measure. 172 men or boys over the age of 15 are shot immediately, 195 women are deported to German concentration camps. About a third of them do not survive the hardships of the camp. Eventually, Lidice is razed to the ground. And that's the end of the movie, which was very current at the time of its release.

anchor_text: One Man's Hero is set against the backdrop of the Mexican-American War in the 1840s, and follows the story of the Saint Patrick's Battalion, a battalion of Irish deserters. They were members of the U.S. Army, but when the U.S. Army confronted Mexico, they chose, for religious reasons (they were Catholics like the Mexicans) and because of the ill-treatment they suffered, to join the Mexican Army.
text_a: In 1836, one hundred and eighty-five unruly American civilians in Mexico, including the legendary Davy Crockett and Jim Bowie, sacrificed themselves to face the onslaughts of the first columns of men from the regular Mexican troops while awaiting the arrival of the Texan army.
text_b: The film tells the story of John Riley and the St. Patrick's Battalion, a group of Irish Catholic immigrants who deserted the Protestant United States Army to cross over to the Catholic side of Mexico during the Mexican-American War of 1846-1848. The story focuses on Riley and a group of men from his battalion, who are flogged for their mutiny after crossing the border and attending a Catholic Mass. So they flee to Mexico to find a ship and return to Ireland, only to find that war has broken out between the United States and Mexico and if caught they would be hanged.

anchor_text: After the Axis-led Battle of Kozara (as part of Operation Western Bosnia), the majority of the Serb population ended up in the concentration camps (KZ) of Ustasha in the Independent State of Croatia. Ten-year-old Dara Ilić marches alongside her older brother Jovo and asks him why others are not gathered as well, even though they look the same. A woman marching in the prisoners' column, carrying her crying baby, makes eye contact with one of the young Croatian peasant women working in the fields along the march path. She decides to leave the baby with the farmer before returning to the deportees. At a railway station with swastika flags, Miroslav Filipović separates the sick and elderly from those able to work, who are forced into cattle cars. He watches as those who are left behind are shot at the behest of Filipović. The train takes Dara, her mother and two brothers to the Jasenovac concentration camp. While Dara wonders where her father is, he is forced to dispose of the bodies. During a visit of German Nazi officers, the Ustasha camp supervision makes a trip to Jerusalem by concentration camp prisoners on one evening to entertain the visit. Witness the execution of all those who lose the game. When the families are separated, Dara's mother refuses to part with her older son. Both are then shot by an Ustasha. From then on, Dara has to take care of her younger brother, who is assigned with her to Stara Gradiška Concentration Camp, a camp for women and children. She makes it her personal mission and goal to ensure her younger brother's survival. With the help of the Jewish prisoner Blankica, Dara plans an escape attempt.
text_a: After the Axis-led Kozara offensive, most of the local Serb population ends up in the Nazi and Ustasha Croat concentration camps. With no information on the whereabouts of his father, 10-year-old Dara, his mother and two brothers are transported by train to the Jasenovac concentration camp. Dara is separated from her family and sent to a special children's camp with her two-year-old brother Budo. His mother and older brother are killed by Ustashas. Dara's personal mission and goal is to ensure his younger brother's survival. At an undisclosed location nearby, Dara's father, Mile, is assigned the task of disposing of the bodies. He is devastated when among the corpses he finds his own wife and eldest son. He also hears rumors that Dara and Budo are still alive and are thirty kilometers downriver, within the same Jasenovac concentration camp complex. Dara witnesses police cruelty in the countryside and is shocked by the violence. One night, the guards invented a set of musical chairs as entertainment for visiting Nazi German officers. The loser of each round gets sliced open or hit with a hammer. With the help of a Jewish prisoner, Blankica, Dara plots a way to escape.
text_b: # Intrigued In 1995, Aïda, an English teacher in Srebrenica, worked as an interpreter for the Dutch Blue Helmets, who guarantee the security of the city. Despite an ultimatum ultimately not carried out by the UN, the Serbs take the city. Its inhabitants, protected by the Blue Helmets, take refuge in the old factory which is the camp of the Blue Helmets. Even more are left outside, blocked at the entrance. While carrying out her task of translating the officers' instructions to the refugees and their remarks to the Serbian officers, Aida tries to take advantage of her status (she is protected as a UN worker) to save her husband and two sons, whom she hides in the factory. When the Serbs loaded the buses with all its occupants (women and children on one side, men on the other), including General Mladić himself who swore that they were going to Kladanj, in the Bosnian area, the UN soldiers handed over the three hidden men, applying the regulation to the letter. They were executed in cold blood along with the other occupants of their bus, as were most of the Bosniaks arrested by the Serbs. A few years later, Aida comes to recognize their remains after the discovery of a sarcophagus. She is preparing to return to teaching in a city where executioners and survivors now live side by side.

anchor_text: This is Ferrara, 1938. The policy of the fascist regime is thickening threatening clouds over the local Jewish community, which is gradually pushed back into a moral ghetto of humiliation and bullying. Davide Lattes, a sensitive and educated university student, seems to present - unlike his own family (who is of Jewish origin) - what is being prepared for Italy and Europe. The boy lives a love affair with Nora, a Jew like him, which ends with her abandonment, decided to get baptized and marry a fascist hierarch, in order to escape a fate now more than likely. But David is, at the same time, a witness to the story of Dr. Fadigati, a respected doctor who, as a homosexual, is taken by the passion for Eraldo, a young blonde, David's university classmate. anchor_text: The two main characters Hero Robot No. 1 and Hero Robot No. 2 appear as robot alter egos from Daft Punk. After some time, Hero Robot No. 1 activates a self-destruct mechanism by its companion and explodes after a countdown. Hero Robot No. 2 continues to wander, but after some time collapses and wants to destroy himself as well. However, it cannot activate the mechanism on its own. Instead, he destroys his helmet, exposing the circuitry behind it. Using a fragment of the helmet's visor as a fire glass, he lights his hand in the setting sun. The film ends with the burning robot slowly walking through the darkness of the desert.
text_a: The world is populated by two types of robots with Daft Punk masks that live in a similar way to humans. text_a: Wandering crook Frank Chambers is hired as an errand boy by gas station and restaurant owner Nick Papadakis. This one is married to the young, attractive Cora. She's the main reason Chambers stays at his job. While Papadakis is in town for shopping, Chambers harasses Cora, who is engaged in an affair. Back at the gas station, but they can't keep their hands off each other. Cora suggests that there is only one solution, which is to kill her husband, which they succeed after a first failed attempt. The drunken Papadakis is beaten to death and driven off a cliff in his car. Chambers and Cora are charged with felony murder, but acquitted with the help of a clever lawyer. However, when he tries to steal a horse, he is caught, but can buy his way out of the loot with a gold nugget. The two of them are on the run.

text_a: Johnny Marco is a famous American movie star who lives in the legendary Chateau Marmont. His days are filled with booze, women, fast cars and crowds of fans. anchor_text: 1906: Buffalo Bill and his circus tour Europe. During his stay in Rome, he and his assistant Red visit the estate of Countess Stefania; they are looking for suitable foals. There they meet the Countess's stepdaughter, Maria, with whom Red falls in love. However, she is promised to the steward Pietro, whom she does not love. So she takes advantage of one of his jealousy bouts to get rid of him. From this dispute, a rivalry develops between the shepherds led by Pietro and the cowboys of the circus, who side with Reds. Pietro is an excellent cleaner, which is inferior to Red. Nevertheless, Maria returns his love; at a night meeting they are surprised by their father, who is killed in the resulting quarrel. Pietro intervenes in the fight; Red tries to kill him, but wounds Maria and is himself stabbed by the jealous Lolita, whom he had left for Maria. Maria begs Pietro's forgiveness and returns with him to the countess' estate.
text_a: The year is 1906, and Buffalo Bill's famous equestrian circus is in Rome. Buffalo Bill, accompanied by the unlikable Red, his assistant, visits the neighborhood with the intention of buying horses. So they arrive in Maremma, at the estate of the wealthy Countess Stefania. The daughter of the guardian of the estate, Maria, falls in love with Red and breaks off relations with the farmer Pietro, her promised husband with whom she is not in love. A challenge ensues between the local butchers and the cowboys in which Pietro excels, while Red is beaten. Maria, however, continues to be in love with Red and, while they are alone in the countryside at night, the two young men are surprised by the old father of the girl who, in the ensuing clash, is killed by Red. At that point Peter arrives and Red, in an attempt to kill him as well, injures Maria instead. It will be Lolita, Red's betrayed lover, who kills him with a knife. Mary then apologizes to Peter and returns to his arms.
text_b: The story takes place somewhere in a Russian province. In a great loneliness, a postmaster runs a relay post. That's where he lives with his daughter, the beautiful Dounia. One day the cavalry captain Minsky stops at this place and speaks in a tone of wonder of St. Petersburg. It is imperative that Dounia come to the Tsar's city. She falls in love with this handsome man and accompanies him to St. Petersburg, convinced that she will marry him. But once there, there is no question of marriage and Dounia drowns in that great city: she establishes herself as a prostitute. Only the young Mitia loves her with an honest love. When the old postmaster receives news of his daughter's debauched life, he sets off for St. Petersburg. If the rumor is true, he intends to kill his daughter and the cavalry captain with her. Although deep down she would rather be with Mitia, Dounia persuades cavalry captain Minsky to pretend to be married to her in front of her father. This comedy convinces the postmaster who leaves quietly, accompanied by Mitia who breaks up with Dounia. Completely alone and disillusioned with her own life, she commits suicide.

anchor_text: While his wife Grace is on assignment in Iraq, Stanley has to provide for his two daughters on his own. Reports of continuing attacks in the Middle East undermine domestic tranquility forcing insomnia on Heidi, the eldest daughter, and prompting Stanley to join a support group for military relatives deployed overseas. One day two soldiers show up at Stanley's door and sadly inform him that his wife has been killed in an encounter. The man, initially struggling to accept the news, gradually begins to think about how he can tell his daughters. Destroyed by grief, he is unable to find the appropriate way and taking his time finally decides to take the girls to a distant amusement park, imagining that this will somehow alleviate their suffering. The journey will be marked by a thousand small revelations and will end up bringing Stanley closer to the girls.
text_a: Isabel is a young fashion photographer who thinks only of her career but must take care of her boyfriend and partner Luke's children: their twelve-year-old daughter Anna and their seven-year-old son Ben. Elizabeth tries to get his children to be comfortable with her, but she doesn't succeed, more with Ben than with Anna. To this is also added the continuous disputes between her and Jackie, the biological mother, who considers herself superior in everything. She blames him for everything as she believes that Elizabeth is guilty of having stolen her marriage, but every time Jackie finds herself weaker because of a tumor she has and can not attend to all the things that her children require, therefore, Elizabeth has to take care more of the children and as a result of this loses her job. In the end the two are doomed to understand each other and are finding a balance until finally they become friends, Jackie knows that she is going to die of cancer and decides to tell her a little about the past of her children, since she will take care of their future.
text_b: Stanley Philipps (John Cusack) has just learned that his wife, a top soldier in the Iraq War, has been killed by enemy fire. Stanley completely collapses and his whole world falls apart, but he cannot afford to cry as he has to keep his composure in front of his two daughters, Heidi (Shélan O'Keffe) and Dawn (Gracie Bednarczyk). Her friends and family like John (Alessandro Nivola) try to find the best way to communicate the news to the little ones. He finally decides that the best thing is to take them on a trip and go to an amusement park, so that the girls are entertained and happy and when they learn of their mother's death they suffer less. But the opportunity doesn't just present itself, and Stanley endures his pain, disguises himself, dodges questions and makes up stories for them. But him, who's going to console him?

anchor_text: In 1966, Professor Marx discovered a means of controlling people telepathically. In the year 2087, cyborgs are created in human form and controlled by the medium. Some cyborgs have managed to escape control. They send one of their own, Garth, back to 1966, just before the professor makes his discovery. Have Garth convince Marx to keep his discovery a secret. The enemies of the free cyborgs send two men, also cyborgs, after Garth to kill him. Garth enlists the help of Sharon, the professor's assistant. She's supposed to help him remove a mechanism from his body. This mechanism helps his pursuers track him down. Sharon's friend Dr. Zeller successfully operates the mechanism from the cyborg's body. Garth and Dr. Zeller attempt to electrocute the pursuers, but the attempt is unsuccessful. Garth telepathically instructs Sharon to bring the Professor to him. He can overpower his persecutors and travel with Marx into the future. Marx realizes what his discovery will do and agrees to keep it a secret. As a result, Garth disappears, while the people who helped him can no longer remember him.
text_a: In the year 2080, humans and cyborgs will populate the Earth. They largely replace human labor and are programmed to serve their owners. Each of these cyborgs, however, has a strictly limited lifespan, a kind of "expiration date". When their time is up, they are eliminated. Officer R is a member of Task Force MP, which specializes in eliminating defective cyborgs. Because of minor errors in the system, some robots suddenly develop a life of their own. Thus Cyper, one of these defective cyborgs, learns of the invention of scientist Dr. Croy, which makes it possible to implant robots' chips into humans. R has fallen in love with a cyborg named Ria, but unfortunately, her expiration date is only a few days in the future. So R and Cyper are hunting the prostitute Cyon because her human body can serve as a replacement body for Ria and Cyper due to their DNA. Cyper invades the control room of a science complex and kills all the scientists present. There he tries to take over the body of the Cyon he kidnapped. The Task Force's attempt to storm the building fails, as Cyper uses the reprogrammed MP combat cyborgs, which are far superior to the task force. The building's self-destruct mechanism is activated to thwart Cyper's plans. Before the complex explodes, R still manages to kill Cyper and free Cyon. R asks Cyon to bury Ria, since he had to leave her alone. R himself stays behind and waits for the research complex to self-destruct in order to find his death along the way.
text_b: Garth, a cyborg from 2087, travels back in time to 1966 to prevent Professor Sigmund Marx from revealing his new discovery in the field of radiotelepathy, a practice that in the future will make mind control possible and that will be responsible for a tyrannical society in Garth's time. Two counter-agents, also cyborgs, are sent on Garth's trail, programmed to eliminate him. Garth enlists the help of Dr. Sharon Mason, Marx's assistant, to have a scientist friend, Dr. Zeller, operate on him and eliminate the radiant cosmos, a transmitter device the size of a capsule placed inside his chest, which allows two cyborgs sent from the future to locate him. Meanwhile, the local sheriff is taking an interest in the case. Eventually Garth manages to defeat the counter-agents and convinces Professor Marx to keep his discovery a secret. Then, with his future wiped out by the changing course of time, Garth ceases to exist, and the people who helped him no longer even have a memory of him.

anchor_text: In 2087, the Cyborgs, half-human, half-machine beings, revolt against the dictatorship and send one of their own, Garth A7, in 1966 with the intention of changing the future. He must prevent Professor Sigmund Marx, the inventor of radio telepathy, from revealing his new invention which, more than 120 years later, will plunge the world into total chaos. To achieve his goals, Garth is surrounded by Dr. Sharon Mason and Dr. Zeller who will help him get rid of a tracking system implanted in him. Because Garth A7 is being stalked by two Tracers, two evil cyborgs programmed by the dictatorship of the future to eliminate him before he succeeds in his mission.
text_a: Garth, a cyborg from 2087, travels back in time to 1966 to prevent Professor Sigmund Marx from revealing his new discovery in the field of radiotelepathy, a practice that in the future will make mind control possible and that will be responsible for a tyrannical society in Garth's time. Two counter-agents, also cyborgs, are sent on Garth's trail, programmed to eliminate him. Garth enlists the help of Dr. Sharon Mason, Marx's assistant, to have a scientist friend, Dr. Zeller, operate on him and eliminate the radiant cosmos, a transmitter device the size of a capsule placed inside his chest, which allows two cyborgs sent from the future to locate him. Meanwhile, the local sheriff is taking an interest in the case. Eventually Garth manages to defeat the counter-agents and convinces Professor Marx to keep his discovery a secret. Then, with his future wiped out by the changing course of time, Garth ceases to exist, and the people who helped him no longer even have a memory of him.
text_b: Two police officers, R and Noma, are hunters of rebel cyborgs. Cyborgs perform a wide range of tasks in society, from military cyborgs to "dolls", female cyborgs designed for company. Cyborgs have a life span of three years, but a technology is being developed on the black market to transfer the artificial intelligence of a cyborg into the mind of a human host. This revolutionary invention leads R to find Cyon, a young orphaned prostitute, who could serve as a host for the mind of his doll Ria, who he is deeply in love with and has only a few days left to live. Eventually R will have to choose between leaving the colony with Ria to spend her last days together on a paradise planet, or rescuing her friends when a rebel fighting cyborg takes over the police headquarters.

anchor_text: The widow Iris King single-handedly provides for her children Kelly and Richard by working in a large bakery. There she meets the chef Stanley Cox, with whom she becomes friends. Iris is struggling to cope with the death of her husband eight months ago and with the problems of her growing children. Stanley, on the other hand, has to cope with the death of his newly estranged father, with whom he lived until recently. After Stanley's boss finds out that he can't read, Stanley gets fired. Iris teaches Stanley how to read, and in doing so, she brings him closer to humanity. She enters into a relationship with him, allowing her to overcome her grief over her late husband, while allowing Stanley to develop professionally.
text_a: Well-known author Iris Murdoch is preparing a new book when she begins to show symptoms of Alzheimer's disease. Unable to bring the word "puzzle" to her lips, she questions its meaning. Afterward, without realizing it, she repeats bits and pieces of words over and over again. After medical tests, the then greatest English novelist and her husband John Bayley are faced with the diagnosis of Alzheimer's. As Iris's mental and physical condition deteriorates, her devoted husband, John, does not want to give up his great love and takes over her care. The intelligent and articulate writer, portrayed in flashbacks as a young woman hungry for life and sexually open and experienced, is losing more and more of her mind due to the progressive dementia. Unable to cope with the demands of his wife's intensive care, John Bayley had Iris committed to a home, where she died four years after the onset of her illness.
text_b: Iris King (Jane Fonda) is a widow to whom her husband has left two children and little else. To support her family, she works as a labourer in a bakery. But even so, the money she earns is not enough to maintain her children in basic conditions. On one occasion, as he leaves the factory, a stranger tries to steal his bag and a cook who works in the same factory helps him pursue the thief. This is how she meets Stanley Cox (Robert De Niro), a kind, but distant and mysterious man, who awakens in Iris a strange restlessness, which encourages her to take an interest in her life. Gradually she discovers that she is as lonely a person as she is, though the worst comes when she finds out that Stanley can't read or write. From that moment on, all of Iris' efforts are directed towards her new friend's literacy.
