text_b: In a small New England town, introverted and shy Stanley Cox has a chance to befriend Iris King after coming to her aid during a brawl. Both work in the same candy factory: Iris is an energetic widow dedicated both to raising her two children, rebellious teenager Kelly and docile 11-year-old Richard, and to supporting her sister Sharon and husband Joe, both of whom are unemployed. Stanley is illiterate, and therefore insecure, completely taken over by the care of his aging father, who will die in a nursing home. Fired because he can't read and forced to do the most humiliating jobs to survive, Stanley is comforted by Iris who teaches him to read and write. Becoming confident, Cox is hired in another city making an unexpected career, but the distance sharpens in both the desire for love that already bound them for a long time: overcome the obstacles and worries, Iris and Stanley, after being reunited, decide to marry.

anchor_text: In Valparaíso, Chile, a powerful earthquake hits, and a group of tourists soon learn that reaching the surface is just the beginning of their nightmare. The catastrophe led to the collapse of social norms, including murder, rape, looting and other chaotic activities. After the earthquake, a tsunami forms...
text_a: A group of six young people from different countries (Chile, the United States, Russia and Hungary) are in Chile spending a vacation, after meeting in a disco. On one of their trips to the city of Valparaíso, while they are at another disco, an earthquake occurs that turns into a total chaos to the people and the city. During the earthquake, prisoners escape from a nearby prison, unleashing chaos that turns into tragedy.[4]
text_b: Eight survivors of a nuclear attack take refuge in a bunker and manage to survive on a stockpile of supplies. But tensions arise within the group, and two of them eventually proclaim themselves "leaders" and seize all the food available...

anchor_text: The Game Changers follows the journey of James Wilks, an elite Special Forces trainer specializing in self-defense and winner of The Ultimate Fighter 2009, who, injured in both knees during training and wanting to hasten his recovery, travels the world seeking to find out whether meat and protein are truly necessary for health and physical strength, including that of high-level athletes.
text_a: The documentary follows former Ultimate Fighting Championship fighter James Wilks in his research on nutrition. Wilks travels the world discussing his findings with other elite athletes who follow plant-based diets, including Arnold Schwarzenegger, Patrik Baboumian, and Dotsie Bausch.[3][9]
text_b: The film advocates the health effectiveness of a diet that, unlike typical American habits, excludes foods of animal origin and instead relies on vegetable and whole foods, to promote the prevention and treatment of numerous diseases.

anchor_text: The Game Changers follows the journey of James Wilks, an elite Special Forces trainer specializing in self-defense and winner of The Ultimate Fighter 2009, who, injured in both knees during training and wanting to hasten his recovery, travels the world seeking to find out whether meat and protein are truly necessary for health and physical strength, including that of high-level athletes.
text_a: James Wilks, a UFC fighter and self-defense instructor, uses the time he is forced to rest due to an injury to study the most effective method to regain his fitness. This led him to conduct research on the effects of a vegan diet on physical performance, during which he interviewed various athletes and doctors who claimed its benefits.
text_b: The film advocates the health effectiveness of a diet that, unlike typical American habits, excludes foods of animal origin and instead relies on vegetable and whole foods, to promote the prevention and treatment of numerous diseases.

anchor_text: Fandor, a journalist from Le point du Jour, publishes a fake interview with Fantomas, in which he introduces the famous outlaw to a delusional plan to conquer the world and other similar amenities, seasoned with a bizarre and laughable Weltanschauung. The mendacious article has caused a sensation around the world, so much so that even Commissioner Juve is forced to issue a statement to the nation regarding such an interview. However, the real Fantomas, portrayed by all the mass media as a furious madman, decides to take revenge on the journalist who has so vociferously defamed him, assuming his appearance and carrying out various misdeeds with his face. Commissioner Juve falls into the trap of camouflage and no longer doubts: that of Fandor is the true identity of Fantomas! Juve then joins the hunt, but the chameleon Fantomas immediately commits a crime by wearing a mask in the likeness of Juve. In the end, Fandor, Juve and Fandor's girlfriend Hélène go in pursuit of the great criminal, some by car, some by helicopter, but it is all useless, because the man in a thousand masks finally manages to escape using a submarine.
text_a: Fantômas is a man of many disguises. He uses theatrical makeup as a weapon. It can impersonate anyone using a variety of masks and is capable of creating endless confusion by constantly changing its appearance. In the first episode of the series he is upset with Fandor, following a fictitious interview the journalist wrote about him. He takes revenge by kidnapping Fandor and threatening to kill him. Then he uses his formidable makeup skills to commit a spectacular crime while disguised as Fandor. When Commissioner Juve joins the pursuit, the chameleon Fantômas soon commits a crime in a mask in which he resembles Juve. In the end, Fandor, Juve and Hélène, Fandor's girlfriend, follow the trail of the master criminal, but all is in vain as the man of a thousand masks finally manages to escape.
text_b: Naples, where the underworld rules. Commissioner Betti, who works with unconventional methods, is mainly after the hitherto untouchable 'General', assisted by a number of specialists. Despite Dr. Gervasi's anxious silence, he is able to shed light on the events surrounding the torture of his wife, although false traces are left by burglaries and peasant casualties. A car dealer has to pay with his life for not wanting to pay protection money. Despite his best efforts, however, the general is forced to confront his rival, Capuano, who is also using brutal tactics to get his piece of the pie. A gang of bank robbers led by Casagrande is also causing further confusion. Finally, Betti can use a secret meeting with two mafiosi to kill them and make it look like their responsibility.

anchor_text: The story is said to be based on a true event that took place in 1939 and 1940. It is set in Shanghai during World War II and the Second Sino-Japanese War and describes a group of Chinese students who, led by the student Kuang Yu Min, found a patriotic theater group. They find out that a Mr. Yee is an influential and dangerous collaborator of the Japanese army, and they want to assassinate him. To do this, they use the beautiful Wong Chia Chi as a sociable decoy, who is supposed to befriend Mrs. Mak with Mrs. Yee to enter into a relationship with her husband. However, the murder of a blackmailer by the theater group and the transfer of Mr. Yee to Shanghai cause the protagonists to part ways. In 1941, during the Japanese occupation, Wong meets the leader of the theater group Kuang Yu Min in Shanghai again, where she is persuaded by him to join the Chinese resistance and from her position to once again act against the well-guarded and extremely cautious Mr. Yee. Due to her strained financial situation and unquenchable love for Kuang, she joins the resistance. However, she seems to be falling for the cool head of intelligence. At the jeweler's, she warns Yee at the last moment of the planned assassination with a "Go, now!" Yee escapes into his car and alerts the security services, who immediately block the roads and eventually capture the theater group. Yee is reluctant to question Wong himself. As the resistance fighters kneel in front of a rock face in a quarry, ready for their execution, Yee signs the execution order with mixed emotions. When the precious ring, his gift to his beloved Wong, is handed to him, he claims not to know it. You can't see or hear a gunshot. Yee returns to his family every day.
text_a: In China at the end of the 19th century, the Chinese folk hero and martial arts master Wong Fei-hung (1847-1924) fights against the British, French and Americans who exploit the country as foreign rulers and receive support from criminal Chinese gangs. Wong Fei-hung's (non-physical) aunt Yee, who returns from America completely westernized, awakens in him romantic feelings, which he only reluctantly admits to himself, since for the conservative Chinese society of that time such a connection is not sustainable. Wong's opponents are the members of the Shaho Gang, who are strongly opposed to him and his school. Leung Foon, an aspiring kung fu student, unsuccessfully looking for work and in love with Yee, also constantly comes into conflict with Hung. Even the Chinese governor opposes Hung. Again and again, the opponents meet each other and engage in fierce battles that always result in Wong winning, but he loses the school and most of his students are interned. Aunt Yee is kidnapped by the Shaho Gang and taken to an American restricted area and port grounds. Foon is joined by Ironclad Yim, a success-hungry and impoverished master. He's colluding with the Shaho Gang to force Won Fei-hung, the city's best fighter, to fight him. Along with his followers, Wong is now searching for Yee. Along with many other kidnapped women who were to be taken to America to be enslaved in prostitution, Yee is freed from the hands of the Shaho Gang. After the gang leader and Yim are defeated in battle, Leung joins martial arts master Wong, who has learned to accept him.
text_b: In 1938, in Hong Kong, during the Japanese occupation of China, six idealistic students, who met while performing a patriotic play, plot to assassinate politician Yee, who collaborates with the occupier. They are naive, without real political conviction and their conspiracy is more amateur theater than terrorism. They will be brutally confronted with the real world and forced to sacrifice far more than they were prepared to. Young Wong Chia Chi, who must become Yee's mistress to lure him into an ambush, is still a virgin. To be credible in her role as a femme fatale, she allows herself to be deflowered and perfected with a fellow student. Shanghai, three years later. Their attempt failed but an opportunity to pursue their plan presented itself as Yee is now a minister in Wang Jingwei's collaborationist government. They're recruited by the secret service of the Kuomintang. In order to enter into the intimacy of his target, Wong Chia Chi befriends his idle wife and manages to catch the husband in his traps. The latter, courteous and refined in public, unleashes his domineering impulses on her. Though initially suspicious, he is won over by the girl's gentleness and composure. The consenting victim finds herself unwillingly bewitched by her tormentor. In order not to be discovered, she immersed herself so deeply in her role that she could no longer distinguish between her real self and the one she was pretending to be. When she reports to her superiors, it is with a voice without tone, which masks the passion that she displays during the wild fights with her lover. When it becomes apparent that Yee is genuinely in love with her, she will have to choose between two loyalties, her country or the man who has metamorphosed into her contact.

anchor_text: Set in 1940s Japanese-occupied Shanghai, the film tells the story of a female student who joins a group of college boys to carry out an assassination on a director of the secret service of the Japanese puppet government. By pretending to be a sophisticated lady, she will become the lover of the traitor, but by pushing herself further and further into this relationship, she will end up loving him.
text_a: Once in China tells the story of Wong Fei-Hong (1847-1924), a historical figure who became an emblematic figure of the People's Republic of China. A descendant of Shaolin monks and a doctor, Wong Fei-Hong must fight not only bandits, but also the British and Americans who increasingly interfere in China. Succeeding many talented actors, Jet Li (Wong Fei-hong) established himself as the ideal embodiment of the hero in 1991, before giving way to Chiu Mang Cheuk for episodes 4 and 5.
text_b: During World War II, college student Wong Chia Chi (Tang Wei), who participates in a theater group, meets Kuang Yu Min (Lee-Hom Wang) who belongs to the Chinese resistance and proposes to pretend to marry Mr. Mak. His intention is that I have a friendship with mrs. Yee (Joan Chen), wife of an important collaborator of the Japanese, Mr. Yee (Tony Leung) and try to assassinate the latter but first Wong Chia-chi must become his lover.

anchor_text: The famous writer Philippe Malaiseau dies in a car accident. The young author Marc Lancelot (Jacques Weber) is to complete his last work on Charles the Bold. The writer's assistant, Julie Schmidt (Claude Jade), immediately seduces him. In addition to Julie, Malaiseau's widow Marianne (Anny Duperey), his cousin Christine (Cécile Vassort), the Italian ex-girlfriend Melisa (Nicoletta Machiavelli) and the mother of the deceased (Mary Marquet) also live in the Villa Fontbonne. Also living with them is the young son of the deceased, Jean-Philippe (Eric Najsztat). Marc begins to suspect that the five women murdered his predecessor. He learns that Malaiseau was in love with a publisher and wanted a divorce. He dares to interrogate and blackmail the women individually and collectively. But his malicious pleasure is unrequited by the conspiratorial women.
text_a: When Philippe Malaiseau, a writer and historian, dies before completing his studies on Charles the Bold, his publisher sends the young writer Marc Lancelot, a ghostwriter fascinated by Malaiseau, to Fontbonne to finish the book. In Fontbonne there are five women: Julie, a beautiful secretary who seduces Marc, Malaiseau's old mother, Marianne, a distant widow, Christine, her cousin, and Melisa, an Italian friend. The four women are all connected to Malaiseau; was the man's death simply an accident? Marc begins a kind of investigation in the arms of Christine, Julie and Melisa, who, one by one, give in to his charm and confide in him details that reinforce his belief that Philippe has been murdered.
text_b: In Algiers lives Mrs. Brisson who leads a double life, during the day she is an elegant lady, but at night she becomes Musa Samarra manager of a notorious club. The woman, in order to preserve her daughter Adrienne, had her study in a boarding school in the south of France, but now the ragza is returning to Algeria to find her mother. During the crossing, the girl accidentally meets Molescu, a shady individual, who collaborates with her mother. Prosecutor René Cadillac is also on board, investigating the affairs of Mrs. Brisson and Molescu. The meeting between mother and daughter is full of emotions and Mrs. Brisson decides to abandon her double life in order to take full advantage of her daughter. Molescu, worried about business, wants to stop him by any means and decides to kidnap Adrienne to blackmail her mother. The woman manages to save her daughter but Molescu stabs her to death during a fight. Meanwhile, the man is stabbed in turn by the brother of Mira, a girl he raped. The film ends with the prosecutor René Cadillac, who brings to safety Adrienne, with whom he has fallen in love.
